Faith: Believe in and pray to the God of grace who makes families out of the lonely

Holiness is not compulsion but a result. To say 'be holy' is not to say you must qualify to become God's child. Because you have become God's child, you naturally become holy as His child. Therefore, let us stop trying to prove ourselves and pray to be moved and transformed by His grace.

Look to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith—Hebrews 12:2. Therefore, holy brothers and sisters who share in the heavenly calling, think deeply on Jesus, the apostle and high priest of the faith we profess—Hebrews 3:1
To those asking 'When?', God asks a more important question back. It is your 'identity.' If you cannot become yourself, everything else you enjoy is meaningless.

How to overcome evil with good: To overcome evil with good means to speak and act by the essence and standard you hold—Jesus—toward those with wounds and low self-worth around you. Ultimately, essence overcomes packaging.

When you drink living water, the party ends. He said, 'Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.' The water Jesus gives becomes a spring welling up to eternal life. Without this eternal spring, one can only drink the corrupted, salty water of the world. Those who drink the world prove they are not drinking Jesus. Stop the false claim that you know the Lord while separated from the world. If you become truthful and say, 'Lord, I did this because I had no Lord,' the Lord will save you.

Let essence overcome. Even with tremendous blessings and prayers, without Jesus it is only fake. Even without any threatening force, without clinging to the cross, you will surely fall. What God asks is not to fret over circumstances and reactions, focusing on the non-essential, but to concentrate and labor on the essence—which must be Jesus. What is truly urgent for yourself is not external 'favor' or 'threat,' but 'Can I take up my cross?' Therefore, together, holy brothers and sisters called to the heavenly calling, think deeply on Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our faith—Hebrews 3:1

The alabaster jar of perfume: Life is ultimately a series of moments, each one potentially the last. That is why believers in faith pour out everything in each moment as if it were their last worship. As if today were the last, as if the last were eternal....
Stop worrying and pray. In the time spent on human worry that yields no answers, entrust yourself to the Lord and pray. That is what it means to be a person of faith.
Are you at peace now? Absolute peace is the most basic duty of a believer. Without peace, it means God is absent, and consequently, you abuse yourself and trouble others.
What the Lord desires now: What the Lord desires of me now is to humble myself before Him. Furthermore, what He desires more is to humble myself even further.

Faith: Living by faith means living with conviction. Especially, since it begins with believing in God's existence and character, it means stepping out from my situation and my problems, and approaching them with the Lord's eyes and the Lord's character. There should be no me; only my redeemed Lord should remain.
Worship and ministry: Ministry is not done by excelling greatly nor pursued because potential is visible. It is done simply because worshiping God is sufficient.

Consistency is power. Sometimes in novels and movies, scenes appear of spirits sharing friendship and love, but that is purely romantic imagination. The forces of darkness possess tremendous consistency—to kill. For the past 6,000 years, they have never deviated from their focus or compromised even once. Yet people so easily turn away from Jesus. Consistency is power.
Essence: You must decide to be faithful to essence alone. There is no need to get upset over matters unrelated to faith.
A loving rebuke: Love means bearing shame in another's place. Look at Jesus.

How to transcend routine: When you love, you pray. Not praying means not loving. Having the inclination to pray but being unable to pray also means lacking love. Apostle Paul thought only of Jesus Christ, and thus could remain unchanged before the many years and challenges, racing toward the goal.
Everyday my faith in you is renewed.
True wisdom: Genuine wisdom is placing God first in life. That is why fearing God is called the beginning of wisdom.

True faith: I cannot understand how there can be 'star pastors' and 'influential believers' in God's kingdom. So everyone rushes to succeed and be recognized. Then inevitably, human evaluation and gaze become important, and you must be praised for doing well. Noah built the ark not because his shipbuilding skill surpassed others. The Bible says Noah built the ark on the mountain by faith, and it came solely from fearing God. If you skillfully build a fine ship for yourself based on greed, that is nothing but excrement, not faith. True faith comes only when you act with reverence for Him alone. By faith Noah, being warned of things not yet seen, in reverence prepared an ark for the saving of his household, by which he condemned the world and became an heir of the righteousness which is according to faith—Hebrews 11:7

True danger: Not working hard is not the serious crisis. Not acting by faith is what is truly dangerous. If it is God's work and you stake your life with a martyr's spirit, but it is your own greed, then you are committing sin with your life at stake. Please stop worshiping and praying to fulfill your desires. Faith means fearing Him alone.

The reason for quarrels: Families with much wealth share blood but still fight. Behind quarrels, there is always the issue of vested interests and money. The early church in Acts was a community that scattered and gave to those in need. If you try to accumulate what is yours, crisis consciousness leads to quarrels. But if you share, the kingdom of heaven comes.

Kingdom Church: The true church is not organization and system, but a community purchased by the blood of Jesus and conceived by the Holy Spirit. It is not a fortress anxiously built with dikes and walls, but a place where the blood and life of Jesus flow over, overwhelming the waves of sin. The Kingdom Church God desires is an apostolic church focused on the command Jesus gave His apostles. When you are ready for martyrdom and stake your life on Jesus, the church is established not only in church but in the world, on campuses, and even on the streets.
If you desire to save your life, you must kneel.
Jesus said 'I am the bread of life'(John 6:35). Without the bread you feel hunger deep inside your heart. Jesus satisfy your deepest hunger in making relationship with God to be restored.
Christianity is first about relationships rather than rules

The standard of beauty: There was once a time when a woman's foot size was the absolute standard of beauty, but now the size of the face matters. It will change again in the near future. More important than appearance that follows trends is that every person has the right to be respected and possesses value as a being. Judging people by appearance is called uncivilized.
How to have a good day: When you wake up, simply trust that God is worthy of granting you a good day this morning. A good day you yourself do not believe in cannot exist.
Why trust is lacking: Because you use your head more than your heart.

Unemployment: When you quit your job, you realize something. That you have an enormous amount of time remaining, and that you truly become aware of your solitude. And that you must possess a faith and confession so genuine that you would have no regrets living such a life forever.
Why change is difficult: Because you have not repented with true desperation.
Skilled labor: In God's kingdom, there can be no 'skilled labor.' There are only those who repent.

Believe in the Lord. Trust in the Lord. When you have faith, you can obey. Before the sovereignty of One wiser than my decisions and wiser than my thoughts, I will entrust all things and dwell in peace.

Trust makes obedience easy: The person who obeys the Lord's will is a servant who acknowledges and trusts in the Lord's lordship. When you trust, obedience becomes easy. 'I am the Lord's servant; let it be to me as you have said'—and the angel left her—Luke 1:38

An overflowing life: There is a way to obtain by envying what was not given, by being false and snatching. And there is a way to receive and enjoy gifts given liberally and abundantly. When you trust, you are not only at peace but enriched.
For believers and saints to share tea and a meal together is a great thing. All of this happens through God's grace and gifts.
Your true faith: Saul resorts to human shortcuts when anxious, but David sings to the Lord in crisis. Their results reflect their choices.
Serving is discovering: You can know God only after serving with love. God is love. The reason He called you is to belong together, to contribute, and to serve with love.
Voice phishing: Satan's voice phishing is giving you not the word you should hear but the word you want to hear.

The ability to be misunderstood and the ability not to misunderstand: Though my heart may consume what it will, ultimately all things are set right by God. Though injustice seems briefly to prevail, eventually all returns to its rightful place, leaving behind needless worry and misunderstanding. Humility and uprightness are what God expects of me.

The thorny path, the path of the cross: With rough feet that have walked the thorny way, I wish to tread the golden path. With hands roughened by toil and without fair form, I wish to hold the nail-pierced hands of the Lord. I wish to be embraced in the warm bosom of the Lord with shoulders that ache from exposure to cold, a heart wounded by loneliness, and a stomach hollow from hunger. In heaven, boasting lies not in living well, eating well, and dressing well in this world, but in the difficult past of suffering and shedding tears for the Lord. We deserve contempt, yet the Lord is almighty but faced mockery. We deserve no hospitality, yet the Lord healed the sick but was stoned. We deserve no remembrance, yet the Lord gave Himself but was hated. We deserve to be foolish, yet the Lord took up the cross but became a byword. We deserve to be deceived, yet the Lord forgives but was sold for thirty pieces of silver. We deserve to be struck, yet the Lord reached out His hand and was nailed. Do not forget the difficult past of suffering and shedding tears for the Lord. Live while depending on the living God.... —November 2, 1991, Seoul

You are the champion: God sometimes causes you to reap what you did not sow. He also sometimes reduces to naught what you labored hard over to prevent you from being reckless. This is so that you become a victor—not someone who rejoices in success and despairs in failure, but one in whom Christ is fully magnified whether you live or die. I want Christ to be exalted in my body, whether by life or by death—Philippians 1:20
Do not fear what you may lose. The Lord both gives and takes away, but all of this is so that you might eternally gain what is eternal. Thus my soul is kept safe in the Lord.
The great principle of action: I can do nothing by myself unless the Lord is pleased.
Recovery: If you desire recovery, begin praying now. If you refuse to pray, you do not yet desire recovery.

Love song: To love means to choose only one, and to choose means to cross a river. After crossing that river, not to worry about what will remain with me and what will scatter is because it is for Him. If you have loved, you do not fear. There is no fear in love; perfect love casts out fear, because fear involves punishment. Whoever fears has not been made perfect in love—1 John 4:18

Responsibility to manage and responsibility to supply: If something necessary is absent, it is for one of two reasons: either the person responsible to supply did not supply, or the person who received did not manage it properly. There are two reasons why Jesus becomes scarce.
Merry Christmas: The characteristics of those distant from Jesus are pride and doubt. The spirit of Christmas is humility and joy. It is the origin point of the Gospel.

A moment is prepared for by a lifetime: Jeroboam received consultation from several experts over three days with prudence and analyzed precise information from the time of his father Solomon, yet made the most foolish and cruel decision, ultimately splitting the 80-year united kingdom of Israel that David had built through blood and sweat. The Bible says this came from God. The king harshly answered the people, rejecting the counsel of the elders and following the counsel of the young men, saying to them, 'My father made your yoke heavy; I will make it even heavier. My father scourged you with whips; I will scourge you with scorpions.' The king did not listen to the people, for this turn of events was from the Lord, to fulfill the word the Lord had spoken to Jeroboam son of Nebat through Ahijah the Shilonite—1 Kings 12:12-15

* True Mental Strength does not waver under any threats or circumstances. Because Jesus becomes our hope. That we possess this hope is like an anchor of the soul, strong and steadfast, entering within the veil (Hebrews 6:19)
* Freedom of Expression On the surface, it may appear that people are struggling, circumstances are evil, and luck is bad, but the true meaning is that God's hand is working and intervening.

* Reformation If Christianity abandons money and becomes honest, they say revival occurs. Since humans cannot be satisfied with the cross, they chose such things, but how can a person who cannot be satisfied with the cross, even after abandoning money and honor, not fill that empty space with something more evil and provocative? Religious acts without the cross are merely splendid vices.

* My Responsibility What is certain is that I will die. Perhaps when I die, I will be placed in a small coffin, people will come to bury me, and those who loved me will shed tears. Though I cannot avoid that death, how I die well or poorly is my responsibility. I can die beautifully and holily, or I can die shamefully and greedily.
* Leader vs. Boss 2 A leader's life-giving influence is leadership, but when a boss boasts of leadership, it is nothing but coercion.

* Leader vs. Boss 1 If you find the problem in yourself, you are a 'leader,' but if you find it in your subordinates, you are a 'boss.' A boss uses people below him to raise his own value, but a leader sheds his own blood to honor people.

* The Power of the Blood There is nothing in this world that can wash away sin. Sin will certainly find us and remain. Only the blood can wash away sin. That sin can only be washed by the blood means that any terrible sin can be washed away by the blood.

* How to Eat the Tree of Knowledge You can make the fruit of the tree of knowledge into apple pie or juice, or put it in a salad or eat it as a pizza topping, but that doesn't change the result. A sick pursuit of self takes only different forms, but the result is always sin.
* The Message to Proclaim Delivering the Word well is a matter of gift. The gift does not save people. The cross saves.

* Vision Having a vision is not fulfilling my desires and ambitions, but the heavenly worship of seeing the Lord's glory in the kingdom of the Lord is the vision. When the vision is correct, the subjects of pain and sorrow change.
* True Friend The counsel of a beloved person gives 'courage,' while flattery that only watches for opportunity gives 'crisis.'

* Put Off the Works of Darkness Works of darkness such as debauchery, drunkenness, lewdness, lustfulness, envy, and strife (Romans 13:13) cannot be removed by human ability. But when we clothe ourselves with Jesus Christ, they are separated at the speed of light. Let us walk properly as in the daytime, not in reveling or drunkenness, not in lewdness or lustfulness, not in strife or envy, but put on the Lord Jesus Christ and make no provision for the desires of the flesh (Romans 13:13-14).
* Do Not Fail In every test that comes upon you at this moment, resolve to be silent, cease complaining, and respond only in faith. Because I do not want you to fail.

* The Greatness of Psalm 23 The Lord became my shepherd not because I lack nothing, but because God is the shepherd, I lack nothing. When we come as children of God, we truly lack nothing. Identity is ability.
I did not call you for revival. I called you only to obey. - May 2009
* The Lord Who Comforts the Brokenhearted The way God comforts the brokenhearted is not by subduing the adversary or gaining recognition, but simply by being with them.
* The Greatest Miracle How loved you are can be known by how much you change. Therefore, love is the greatest miracle.
* Pass Through Winter This Way Remember the Lord's help in Ebenezer and anticipate the Lord's sovereignty in Philip where despair prevailed.
* Contradiction I will remove civil servants who are too conscious of people's reactions. - President (April 21, 2014) This is the statement that makes one most conscious.
* To Be Happy, Discover Yourself By seeing what I seek, I can know who I am.

* Moving Church Just as a singer is a person who breathes through song and a climber is a person who breaks through the summit, a person of God, wherever they go, reveals the glory of God that cannot be imitated through the shameless gospel they carry.
* The Reason for Joy It is not because the result is good. It is because we attempted what was right and exerted effort.
* Conversion When we first believe in Jesus, we return to the eternal Father. That conversion is needed at every moment. That is faith.
* The Secret to Victory It is continually proclaiming the clear gospel and dwelling in the community given by the Lord.

* The Word We Must Always Hear If we do not hear the proclamation of the gospel, no matter how much a minister of the gospel lives by the gospel as a profession, they cannot avoid desolation and collapse.
* God's Grace That a sinful deficient person has come this far is because of God's infinite mercy, and that the weak me is upheld and moves forward is also because of the Lord's boundless grace.

* The Reason for Repeated Falling The reason we repeatedly fall is because we made a wrong first choice, and the reason the first choice fails is because we are a wicked and lazy servant, and the reason we become a wicked and lazy servant is because we lack affection, and the reason we lack affection is because we do not truly love, and the reason we do not truly love is because we regret but do not repent truly, and the reason true repentance does not occur is because the 'me' that God sees and the 'me' that is reflected to people are different. If we do not let go of everything and repent thoroughly, collapse will always repeat. Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand (Matthew 4:17) Stumbling → Priority Problem → Wicked Servant → Absence of Affection → Absence of Love → Superficial Repentance → Hypocrisy and Pretense
* Morning Prayer Today, I rely not on my ability and strength, but on the Lord's mercy and compassion.
* Jacob's Blessing To join two hands together, you must release what is clenched. To behold the Lord, you must become blind to the world and lame to temptation.
* The Power of Humility Who can stand against one who humbles himself?
* The Reason for Calling God called me not because He needs me, but because I need God.
* What Is Needed Now God's wisdom, God's guidance, God's discernment, God's passion, God's character.
* True Deficiency Deficiency does not mean blaming oneself for lacking. The truly critical deficiency is feeling that you lack while not petitioning God.
* Believe and Expect God's Work When God stirs the teacup, a spiritual tsunami occurs in us. May you enjoy peace in the Lord's work.
* What I Must Confirm It is foolish for me to try to confirm my own future. I am to confirm my faith in Him, and God is to confirm all my life.

* True Calling The ultimate nature of calling is to know and believe in the true God and His Son Jesus Christ and walk together with Him. Therefore, if we have faith but do not enjoy the Lord, we merely subsist and live unable to die.
* Meditate on Your First Calling When you think of your first calling, you understand His intention and cannot help but truly give thanks.
"Where there is resistance to the times, there Christ is." - Martin Luther

* A Rotten Rope Is Not Useless But Evil If there had been no rotten rope, we would have found another path of life, but because we grasped even a rotten rope, we merely subsist for a while, and when it breaks, the collapse is severe. It is not about working hard; the direction must be right.
* The Reason for Stinginess The reason we cannot intercede and help is not because we lack means. It is because we do not enjoy living eternally with God.

* Do Not Depart From Jerusalem By ignoring essence and dwelling only on methodology, true change does not occur. When? How should we do it? What should we do? To these questions, He says these are not your decisions. I must recover and dwell in my Jerusalem. Only then do we go to the ends of the earth. If we go without dwelling, we cannot reach Judea and Samaria and will only collapse into selfishness and humanism. And being assembled together with them, He commanded them not to depart from Jerusalem, but to wait for the Promise of the Father (Acts 1:4).
* Heal Your Discomfort By seeing why I fear, become angry, feel uncomfortable, and rejoice, I can know what I am serving. I must be healed from humanism to the cross.
* The Reason for Fear It is the fear of loss. True peace and boldness come when we can let go of everything except Jesus.

* Without Insight, One Boasts Only with Pretense When one boasts with pretense, the situation worsens. Pretense is carelessly speaking about results without God as the cause. We must hold to truth, not pretense.
* It Is Not Bad Luck It is not bad luck; it is the Lord's love. He interferes with affection, desiring to prepare us and make us lower.

* The Genuine Does Not Depend on Packaging It is not that we are cursed for poor packaging. We are cursed because we package too well. We must not call a matter of faith and longing an issue of image making.
* God's Guidance To receive the guidance of the perfect God, I do not need to be perfect. Since we are infinitely lacking, the perfect One guides us abundantly.
* The Secret to Not Growing Weary The secret to not growing weary is to serve and love as if in love.
* Inducing a Reaction Is Evil Faith is that I believe in God and respond, not that I bind another person. To believe and trust in myself is faith.

* Be an Alternative Paul neither engaged in unnecessary wasteful arguments nor turned away from problems. He had a solution—supplying what should be there in the place where it should be. Yet indeed I also count all things loss for the excellence of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord, for whom I have suffered the loss of all things, and count them as rubbish, that I may gain Christ (Philippians 3:8) I am crucified with Christ; it is no longer I who live, but Christ lives in me (Galatians 2:20).
* The ability to humble ourselves: The ability to lower ourselves further and serve comes because the Lord's high purpose and strong hand sustains us.
* Displaying faith: When we desperately need salvation in our lives, that is when we display our faith to the Lord. Faith is revealed when we raise the level of our prayers.
* What must be remembered: Remember the Lord's almightiness. The Lord rules.

* Reasonable complaints cannot change the world. Worldly authority does not want to acknowledge new excellence. Therefore, new challenges become frustrated before suffocating traditions and lodge reasonable complaints. However, excellence is blocked not because of wrong practices and systems, but because we were not excellent enough. Stop complaining and become more prepared and more excellent. Overcoming even that is true excellence.
* Eyes to see truth: Truth is always seen within the peace that God gives.
* Why prayer and meditation deepen: That is the nature of love itself.

* The freedom that peace gives: When patients leave the hospital having found health, none of them cling to hospital gowns or become attached to them. When I fear losing something and truly meet Him, I worry instead that I cannot let it go. Only within His peace do I become truly free.

* When ministry becomes beautiful: If we can listen to the Lord's voice and advance with that heart, it becomes beautiful when we abandon success, recognize all things as the Lord's, and lay down all fear.
* Fear of loss: To anxiously worry about losing something is unbelief. This is because the fruit of the knowledge of good and evil—thinking something was originally mine—is at the starting line.
* Revival: Revival of nations and churches comes from individuals. If numbers increase without me drawing near to Christ, it is not a blessing but merely spiritual obesity at the end of days.

* Transcending circumstances: There is what we do when circumstances are good and what we do when they are bad. When circumstances are good, we work diligently. When circumstances are bad, we serve what we have been doing with greater meaning so that circumstances do not control us. Not being shaken is power.
* What do you earnestly desire: Whatever you earnestly desire, you will express. If you truly desire earnestly, show your wish through prayer.
* The Lord's servants who are rejected: Not because they could not be exalted, but because they could not be humbled.
* When you awake: Never overlook the invisible world. No matter what, the flesh must die and the spirit must live.
* The ability to win simply: Hearing the gospel daily and confirming it in your life.

* To all friends: In the Lord, together with Him, without regard to means or methods, persevere. If this does not happen, will you not lose even if you fight a hundred times? Victory comes from the power of His presence, fellowship, and perseverance.

* Enter boldly into the furnace of affliction: Daniel's three friends confessed, 'Even if He does not' (Daniel 3:18) and boldly walked into the fire seven times hotter. God did not extinguish the fire or exempt them from suffering, but He entered with them. The king spoke and said, 'Look! I see four men unbound, walking around in the fire, unharmed, and the appearance of the fourth is like a son of the gods.' —Daniel 3:25

* How to fight sin effectively: 1. Wash sinful acts (Sins) in the blood of Jesus. 2. Crucify the sinful nature on the cross. 3. Do not fear falling into sin; believe in the Lord who will keep you. 4. Look to the glory that cannot be compared with the pleasure of sin. 5. Always look to the Lord who is with me and will come again. Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires. —Galatians 5:24

* Complete victory, not mere enumeration of good words: 'For I know the plans I have for you,' declares the Lord, 'plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.' (Jeremiah 29:11) 'And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose.' (Romans 8:28) 'I am the Lord, the God of all mankind. Is anything too hard for me? Therefore, this is what the Lord says: Ask and I will answer you and tell you great and unsearchable things you do not know.' (Jeremiah 33:2-3)
* Love in the time given by God: In the precious time given by God, serve, give, share, rejoice together, and love.

* The conclusion of suffering: Please do not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God. If you worry about bread, you will lose bread and lose other things too. But if you hold onto His presence and fellowship, you will live by His word. He humbled you, causing you to hunger and then feeding you with manna, which neither you nor your ancestors had known, to teach you that man does not live on bread alone but on every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord. —Deuteronomy 8:3
* Do not hate yourself, but repent: Rather than hating yourself for being weak, you must repent for not relying more on the Lord above all else.
Word of grace received today: 'Obey the builder.' Even obey an evil builder. —Gunny Kim
* How to keep faith from falling: 'But I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith may not fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.' —Luke 22:32

* True authentication: When meeting someone, preparing food, or in situations where others should recognize you, we take authentication photos. But we want to avoid suffering, and giving up rights is burdensome. However, everything we bear for God's sake, God authenticates. It becomes glorious because of Him.
* Shameful boasting: A person walking with Jesus cannot boast of anything except Jesus. Because before Jesus, the act of trying to prove oneself with perishable things is quite shameful above all.
* The only checkpoint: A life that checks reactions of people, ownership, and evaluation is miserable. Simply stand yourself before the Lord and only check whether I am in Jesus.
* The ability not to fear: If you give your first fruits, give beforehand, give gladly, give good things, and willingly give precious things, you will not be afraid.
* Two things the Lord commanded: To believe and to proclaim that belief. He will rule all things and be responsible for all things.
* First-mover advantage: What you do first, you will continue to do. To do it first is power.
* Christian crime: Not to be moved with wonder. True faith is moved with wonder every day, every moment through faith.
* Do not fear disappointing others: Do not anxiously worry about disappointing others. You will not disappoint. That is why Jesus went to the cross.

* A teacher is not an inspector: A teacher teaches those who desire to learn. Learning means being incomplete and having many shortcomings. It means enabling what cannot be done to be done, making what seems impossible possible, and making what is unwanted become desired. This is qualitatively different from simply saying, 'You must do well! Why can't you?' and evaluating.
* Youth mission trip current location: We are currently passing Motorway M42, so we expect to arrive at church around 6 o'clock.

* Foolish preparation: If a soccer player practices only 'goal celebrations' throughout the off-season and no other training, he will truly be a liability on the field. If you score beautiful goals frequently, celebrations will naturally follow. Please sow the seeds first, even if you shed tears.
* Conditional dedication of the faithless: 'If the Lord guarantees, then I will dedicate.' But faith is dedicating to what the Lord will guarantee in the future, though it is not seen now.
* Speak your wishes: By this you can know who you are.
* Be bold: It means to act by faith.
* How to obtain peace: If you cling, you will add torment; but if you entrust, you will have peace.
* Those who argue and those who marvel: Let those who argue argue. I will marvel at God's grace.
* Rather happiness: It is happiness to have no other hope. Because the Lord alone becomes your hope.
Food taken and grace increase together almost equally.

* The content of faith: When you hear His voice, you must not be hardened. You must believe that He speaks through me and works through me. Israel, though God was with them before the Anakim of Canaan, was rejected because they bound themselves to their own judgment of being like grasshoppers and denied God's work. But Joshua and Caleb, who confessed, 'God uses me and receives glory,' received what they believed. Without faith it is impossible to please God, because anyone who comes to him must believe that he exists and that he rewards those who earnestly seek him. —Hebrews 11:6
* Believe and pray: Through me, through my lips, the Lord alone is exalted.
* Victory by faith: Winning by faith means winning by looking to the Lord. If you want to continue winning greatly, you must continuously look to the Lord.
* Everything done not by faith is sin: Not doing by faith means not looking to the Lord. Zeal without looking to the Lord is empty, and passion without proper focus is only frightening.
* Invincible: A humble person has eyes of wisdom opened and sees Him, so there is nothing to fear.

* The gospel we preached to you: The first word we gave was the cross. And the word we preached again was the cross. And the word we will proclaim in the future is also the cross. But even if we or an angel from heaven should preach a gospel other than the one we preached to you, let them be under God's curse! —Galatians 1:8
* The foolish and the wise: Foolish deeds are those where you deliberately start a fire then quickly cover it with the soil of forgetfulness and laziness. The true competition begins now.

* The definition of heresy: Requiring something other than our Lord Jesus Christ. Galatians 1:8: 'But even if we or an angel from heaven should preach a gospel other than the one we preached to you, let them be under God's curse!'
* To be happy, above all else you must receive understanding and love. These come from the Lord. Then, without begging, you will love and become beautiful.

* Victory in Life: Life's victory is the victory of faith. Faith is not about the trivial matter of what you did or didn't do. Rather, it is about the priority of 'what?' and whether you can 'sustain it?'

* Choices Without Regret: If you sell something worth one million pounds for fifty pounds, the person introducing it should not be begging. Rather, it is an act of kindness. Throughout my life, I have never met anyone who regretted attending a retreat. This is because it is God's choice.
* Why We Cannot Continue to Seek: Because we seek things that perish. Perishable things are either added when seeking eternal things, or they simply disappear.
* The Lord is Greater Than Content: The Lord's work cannot be limited by anyone. The reason for having a planned content is for souls, and ultimately it is God who transforms souls.
* My Identity: You can know it by what I always desire.
* Believe in Victory: No one expects to fall into heresy after worship today. And consequently, they do not fall. Do not believe you will fall into filthy sin. Victory comes from faith.
* If You Truly Want to Win: Receive the anointing alone.

* The Attitude of a Leader: A leader is not merely a conveyor of information. Many people do not understand that the Word and prayer are important and that the fullness of the Holy Spirit is important. That is why leaders were appointed. When you meet a true leader, you should gain the power of prayer, desire to read the Word, and feel God's presence. Woe to a leader who only speaks.

* Why the Cross is Heavy: The cross Jesus bore was rough and heavy. Therefore, Jesus found it difficult and fearful to bear it, but until the end He bore that detested cross and drank the cup of wrath. The reason was so that when He comes again to this world, He would treat me with that great love.
* Do Not Try to Give the Right Answer: A leader is not someone who gives the right answer to every problem. Rather, one awakens the path already known.

* Transcend Reality and Awaken from Delusion: What is as it is, we call reality; what we choose to believe is delusion. Faith is believing in God's sovereignty.
* Boast in Weakness: You will experience the Lord's sovereignty.
* Weakness Becomes Hope: Not by my unqualified strength, but standing by the blood of Jesus alone. Then your weakness will become your hope.

* The Beginning of Suffering: It is difficult because I try to become myself by my own effort. I have become by God's grace.

* Guidance: We will die anyway when the time comes. We can die trembling, or we can die boldly singing praise. It is only this difference, but the results are very different.

* The Ability of a Clay Vessel: What a clay vessel can do best is to hold. This is called receptivity. When you hold and receive the precious 'Jesus,' you become the most powerful being.

* I Am Neither an Angel: Nor am I a demon. Sometimes I seem like an angel and sometimes like a demon—such is humanity. But I exist solely by grace.

In the Lord, by existence alone, we are most beautiful.
* Evidence of Being Blessed: To know that each person given to me is a blessing beyond words, and to thank this infinitely.
* Why I Am Not Lonely: Because I am comforting others.

* Whenever you try to have faith, powerful challenges inevitably come. That is why you want to abandon everything and run away. Yet, faith is not believing in that emotion, but still believing in the Lord's sovereignty who rules over all things.
* Do not receive grace in vain. There is nothing more foolish than returning to the state before receiving grace after having received it.
* The technique of spiritual warfare. Fight with faith. You will overcome with truth.

To hope for His presence and revival without bloodshed?...that is nonsense. 'Martyr' comes from the Greek word 'Martus,' meaning 'witness' or 'one who bears witness.'

* The death of righteous Abel. The Bible says that the reason righteous Abel died was, in short, that he pleased God by offering a better sacrifice. (Hebrews 11:4) If he had not pleased God, there would have been no reason for him to die. His name, Abel, means 'vapor,' and he seemed to vanish as vapor, but God did not leave him. Rather, He said, 'Though he is dead, yet he speaks through his faith.' (Hebrews 11:4) There are those whose very existence speaks without words, and there is One whose absence conveys a message. Even if given another choice, He testifies eternally in faith, 'I will only love and rejoice in God,' never fleeing from destiny.

* Deep condolences. Deaconess Park Kyung-ja, the mother of brothers Kim Ju-in and Ju-hyun, has passed away this afternoon at St. George Hospital. Please pray for the family and all. The mother of our beloved Ju-in and Ju-hyun was also my mother.
* The only boast is the Lord's mercy. Because there is nothing good in me...
* What the Lord does. It is the Lord who turns me back to seek Him, who causes me to turn from sin and be restored to Him. That the Lord is my Lord is also grace.

* If you love first, the will is revealed later. God desires that we believe in His faithfulness and His love now, rather than hastily prejudging His will. When you know His love, His will is revealed.

If it is not the Lord's will, it is undesirable even if given freely. If it is what the Lord desires, even being a doorkeeper is good.
* When collapse is severe. When you have collapsed much, it means you should begin with the Lord's compassion.

When Jesus is important and everything else is disregarded, peace comes. Looking always upon the cross is faith and religion.

Would I then clench my fist and crush it? Or would I provide shelter and warmth, food and protection?
* What 'fear not' means—When God repeatedly says 'fear not,' it is not merely empty words of comfort. He also provides a solution. That solution is giving Himself.
* What do you fear? The object of fear reveals what you boast in and rely upon. Those who revere the Lord have nothing to fear. The treasures and peace buried deep in the heart cannot be taken away.

* God's solution—God has never overwhelmed by sheer quantity. Rather, He chooses one person and accomplishes His will. The person God seeks is one who simply offers himself for the Lord. A person is the alternative.

* The worst deed—The worst thing in the world is when a saint does not receive grace.
* Humility—It is boasting in the Eternal One. Thus before Him I naturally become lowly.
* Pride—Pride is boasting in what is vain and perishable.

* Grace upon the unqualified—Therefore, we can only give thanks.

* State over content—When the state is wrong, a simple opinion appears as attack and rejection. Grace matters more than content.

* If you do not fear, you conquer—Everyone becomes enslaved to what they fear. Whatever we obey becomes our master. Therefore, when freed from fear, there is no need to obey that object, and we will not become slaves to sin.
* A tree with deep roots does not shake. Even when everyone in the world rejects and abandons me, it does not shake me. Because the most precious One dwells in the deepest place of my heart.

* The only foundation—That I obtain life and receive salvation in distress is not due to other conditions in me. The only foundation of my salvation is the body and blood of Jesus given as the Passover Lamb.

* The reason He called—He did not call us to do well. He simply called us to be faithful. If we strive to do well, we will ultimately flee, blame others, and collapse. If we obey, we will give glory to God.

* The reason—It is not because there are many sins, nor because wrongs have grown severe. It is not because of misfortune, nor even because ordinariness itself that others enjoy is a luxury. It is because God loves.
* True faith—Even when it is extremely difficult to look to God, offering my heart and focus to the Lord.

* Rather than the burden being heavy, it is the lack of hope—If devotion feels difficult and burdensome, it is not because the burden is heavy. It is because you have not seen God's glory.

* Let faith be your conclusion—A flea-infested and dead dog of Israel becomes an incomparable great king. Because he received anointing. The valley before you will be filled and the mountain will be lowered. 'The king of Israel has come out to search for a flea, as though he were hunting a partridge in the mountains.' 1 Samuel 24:14

* The perfect prescription—The Lord's grace is perfect for me. Therefore at the cliff's edge, seek grace rather than money and solutions. Receiving grace, you are already a victor. The Lord is sufficient for me and gives grace that is abundant.
* Holiness and the Holy Spirit—One must be sanctified to receive the Holy Spirit, but also one needs the grace of the Holy Spirit to be holy. Not by my strength, but only by the Lord's power.

* Transformation is glory—No matter how stubborn and tumultuous, once transformed, the game is over. In family, in relationships, in ministry, the stones that block the way will also be transformed if the Lord rules over them. Believe, pray, and proclaim.

* The intent of rebuke—Be cautious of sin's evil, but believe in the power of the gospel. If this does not happen, we repent while falling into despair. The cross is eternal power for all.
* The Lord's concern—Jesus' concern is 'Who do you say I am?' Therefore, be clear about who your Master is. See what you are looking at, who fills you, what brings you joy and wounds you.

Four years ago—Afterward I ate leeks and garlic and became human, while Yerin and Misun received grace and became women.
* Victory of Faith - Victory of faith is victory of perspective. I urge you to interpret this situation with the Lord's perspective. What a caterpillar calls death, God calls a butterfly.

* If you love, you will make it known. The Bible says that you can know a person through love. A person you cannot love is fake. A motive that does not come from the Lord is counterfeit faith, like counterfeit currency. By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another. -John 13:35
* A minister is one who, more than anyone else, knows the truth and therefore trembles before God with greater fear.
* Letting Go - An apt metaphor. When you let go, you win. Letting go means... well, like when a leader dances at Christmas, you let yourself go.
* Judgment and Forgiveness - When you look only at results, you judge. But when you look at motives, you forgive as the Lord does.
* Expectation Without Shame - It is holding the expectation of the Father.
* The Power of Meekness - A person of faith can be meek because they are a victor. If you cannot bow your head or extend your hand, it is because you are defeated.

* The Persistent Gratitude of Paul - Like Paul, a person who is goal-oriented and persistent finds it more appealing to instigate system criticism or complaint rather than gratitude. The fact that gratitude overflows in every line of all his letters is because he experienced the secret of gratitude and God, who is the object of that gratitude. Therefore, he strategically and persistently gives thanks in a fearful manner.

* The Secret to Gaining New Strength from Helplessness - It is thanksgiving. Your soul will be renewed and you will experience the peace of faith. You can discover that in Apostle Paul's letters, the word gratitude appears far more than our everyday life.

* Weak Yet Victorious - People are sinners and must crumble. People are defeated, but faith wins. Everyone born of God overcomes the world. This is the victory that has overcome the world—our faith. -1 John 5:4
* We Become What We Behold - We become what we behold. Worship is concentration and beholding. When you worship, you become like the object of your worship.
* Absolute Faith and Absolute Sovereignty - Do not fear, but quote the Word in confidence and pray with thanksgiving. The Lord's reign cannot be opposed by anyone.
* Unity is Not Merely a Good Method - In faith, becoming one in love is not merely an effective method to achieve a purpose, but unity itself is victory and revival.
* Perfect Love Casts Out Fear - To be seized by fear is to lose to the devil. Fear is the emotional denial of God's existence. How can there be an answer when you deny God?

* Peace and Answer Are Synonyms - In the Bible, peace and answer are used as synonyms. When the answer comes, peace comes, and when there is peace, the answer comes. Hold onto the truth and be at peace.

* Why We Do Not Become Discouraged - We do our best with diligence, but we do not become discouraged. This is because we do not rely on our own zeal but only on the Lord. Without grace, zeal is in vain.

* Not Probability but Promise - Abraham does not know where he is going, but he goes forward believing the Word. When we abandon probability, which is calculable and predictable, and hold onto the promise, we will see God's glory.

* All In - When you break through halfheartedly, it really hurts. If you do not have complete faith, everyone suffers. The person who suffers the most is yourself for not going all in. You must believe completely.

* What Grace Is - Grace is precious because of "who gives it" and "with what heart it is given." The Almighty God gives it with the love of giving up His Son. Therefore, His grace will take responsibility.
Snow White vs. Princess Fiona - A true life is not about specs.

When you look at a loved one, you should not look with a mirror held up; you should look with your heart. When you judge people by conditions, even if God's Son is incarnate before your eyes and performs miracles, you cannot believe. (John 8:15) You judge by human standards; I pass judgment on no one.
Abandon and overcome. Only through great reverence for the Lord... Amen.
I want to live in a world without law. ^^
How to live with unchanging faith - Keep the flame burning. The power of the Lord's blood is eternal. If it becomes my words forever, that is enough.
My unchanging conclusion: The Cross.
Change is not by chance. It is the price paid by tears and the Lord's complete grace.

When Potiphar's wife tempted Joseph, he rejected her saying, 'How could I do such a wicked thing and sin against God?' In Joseph's eyes of faith, God was visible, but not to that woman. If you cannot see, you live recklessly. People live by what they behold.

Let the gospel determine me.

Yet I am not ashamed, because I know whom I have believed, and am convinced that he is able to guard what I have entrusted to him for that day.' (2 Timothy 1:12)
The Lord who called will surely take responsibility. The Lord does not call and then default. This is because His faithfulness is a quality that cannot be measured by the world's credit rating.

My parents have always wanted me to live a life that is ordinary enough to boast about to others, even slightly superior to others—a predictable life. But in Jesus, the Lord never lets us live ordinarily. The Lord wants to be the foundation of faith and trust in uncertainty.

My parents always say to eat well and tell me to end calls quickly because the phone bill is too high. When I realize that my parents' faces are getting more wrinkles and their shoulders are becoming narrower, I understand that those annoying words mean love, and they are asking me to visit their face when I have time. The Lord misses us so much that the Holy Spirit comes and makes us think of Jesus. The Father, who has spoken through the prophets, finally speaks through Jesus in the last days. Jesus is the highest expression of God the Father's love. God so loved the world...
* Strong People - Strong people do not care much about others' evaluations. This is because what I am is by the grace of God.

Do not be content with the grace that is poured out, but long for the grace that wells up and overflows from within me. Do not live only on the grace received through others, but live with a heart that Jesus alone is sufficient. We will see each other at the Saturday gathering.
Because there is so much lacking, so much sin, and so many shortcomings, I look to the Lord. He came not to call the righteous but to call sinners.

If you have knowledge of the resurrection but have not experienced the risen Lord, you will not be transformed. When you meet the Lord, open your heart, and He pours out His Word, your heart becomes warm, and a U-turn in life appears. Change comes from faith. It is because a vision that sees a better value and a decision to choose it is faith.

* Meekness is being angry at what should anger you and entrusting what should be entrusted. Only those who are convinced in their bones of the Lord's existence, only people of faith, can take this attitude.

Amidst busy work, complicated matters, and things that provoke anger, maintaining the heart the Lord gives is living by faith. If it is not 'living for myself,' which is the nature of Adam, but 'living for the Lord,' then you must view everything through the Lord's eyes.

A person who has been touched by God's fire is destined to run. The problem is that from the devil's perspective, there is no effective way to stop it. The only chance a tortoise has to win is if the rabbit is sleeping. A rabbit that does not become careless and is watchful will never lose. Just like in the classic story...

Disbelief is also a kind of faith. It is having excessive trust in something negative and untrue. All thoughts that do not come from the Lord must be abandoned. When the Spirit of truth comes, true freedom comes.

Unbelievers say this: the Bible is contradictory. Yes! That is correct. Calculus is contradictory to an elementary school student. Arbitrary interpretation and a sense of victimization come from immaturity.

The goal of unbelievers is survival. They are people who will survive somewhat if they beg in front of a fiery furnace. On the other hand, those whose purpose is glory move forward with faith and the cross. Even when the flames are overcome and the nation changes and all those in authority are purged, they continue to be used.

* The Most Pitiful People - If we had no resurrection, if we had no cross, we would be the most wronged and pitiful people. God will repay. Therefore, we wait silently, looking to Him. Holding onto the cross of resurrection, shedding tears, nodding our heads, and enduring saying, 'We can endure more.' The Lord is alive. If only in this present life we have hope in Christ, we are of all people most to be pitied. -1 Corinthians 15:19

* The Weariness of All Things - When you view the world as a problem, everything is full of landmines, and with my own strength and ability, I cannot possibly navigate through the misunderstandings and wounds. This is why the Lord's grace is needed in this indescribable mess. If it were possible by my own strength, why would Jesus have borne the cross? All things are full of weariness; words cannot express it. The eye is not satisfied with seeing, nor the ear filled with hearing. -Ecclesiastes 1:8

* The Word Is Not Difficult - God's will is not difficult. Only, although God's Word has been heard in many ways, people lack the willingness to bend their own will and obey. The command I am giving you today is not too difficult for you or beyond your reach. -Deuteronomy 30:11

* The Majesty of One Verse of Scripture - Tim Tebow, who has exerted the greatest influence in American sports history, presents only one verse of Scripture: John 3:16. You do not need many verses to obey. One word changes not only a life but history itself.

* Faith is a choice. Choice changes destiny. Therefore, life can be said to be a collection and continuity of choices. However, to make a choice, a value comparison must occur, and to have the ability to make such comparisons, one must have discernment. Hebrews says that we make such value comparisons through faith. Choosing where I must stand, choosing what I must bear—that is faith, and through it destiny is changed.
You must be uncomfortable. Very uncomfortable. There must be no peace. Until you seek centered on Jesus.
Comparison brings unhappiness. Do not compare yourself with others; see and interpret through the Lord's eyes. That is reality and living by faith.

* Undefeatable faith No matter what circumstances threaten, believe that the Lord is working even at this very moment, and even if He does not, give thanks and worry about nothing. Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus. — Philippians 4:6, 7
* Heaven is a delicious place.
When you stand blocking the wind and rain, a following emerges. Those who look only to Jesus will gain Jesus' people.

* The Power of the Word The power of God's Word is far stronger than our stubbornness. It is not that people interpret the Word, but the Word interprets people. When you can hear the Word that is meant to be heard and obey the Word that is meant to be obeyed, you will not be ashamed. When I pay attention to all your precepts, I will not be put to shame. — Psalm 119:6

* What is more sorrowful? Do not grieve over not being loved; grieve more over not being able to love. It is more sorrowful to be unable to obey the Lord's commandment, and His commandment is to love.
* Trust, not control Unbelief is the belief that you can control your circumstances. It is accomplished not by your ability but by the Lord's grace.

The principle of flight is not about focusing on the balloon pulling upward or the force supporting from the ground. When you have 'thrust' despite resistance and challenges, the resistance actually provides 'lift' and you fly. The thrust of life is the power to hold onto the cross. Before the cross, everything becomes an insignificant problem.

When a plane flies, it does not fly believing in vague possibilities. It flies based on a kind of law, a promise. For a plane to take off, it needs 300km of thrust and must travel about 1800 meters. It must dash at a certain speed beyond a certain distance. People decide their investments based on possibilities. However, a person of faith holds onto promises and invests absolute time and concentration instead of inferiority complex and greed. Today is a day when we have a Saturday gathering. I want to invest absolute time and concentration. The Lord will intervene.

When you serve without light and without name, you become stronger. Because you become free. Freedom is being unbound, looking only to the One who calls and holds you, and running forward. John 8:31-32 KJV So Jesus said to the Jews who had believed in him, 'If you abide in my word, you are truly my disciples, and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.' Only when you abide in that Word and obey, then afterward you become free.
* The ultimate form of foolishness Limiting what is truly precious to what is truly trivial.

Satan can never overcome us. Because we have *'that love.' No matter how strong the devil is, he cannot overcome our love. He can never endure it. Spiritual warfare is won if you do not give up. Satan has a nature that can never persevere. *'Love' is here: This is love: not that we have loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son as an atoning sacrifice for our sins. — 1 John 1:10 Love is patient, love is kind. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. — 1 Corinthians 13:3, 7

The Apostle Paul said he was not ashamed of the gospel. This confession can only come when you have the conviction that only Jesus' cross is enough. Because I have Jesus of the cross, I have in Jesus all the joy that life can offer. If after having what is called the gospel, there are still many things in this world you want to have, where is the gospel in that?
* True wisdom Rather than the wisdom of the loser who wisely gives up, embrace the foolishness of the victor who stubbornly perseveres.

The Apostle Paul said he had no interest in being loved and treated well. He said he never dreamed of gaining attention and recognition. He only desired that Jesus be honored in him. The subject of 'I' disappears and the Master 'the Lord' rules. I pray that you may be filled with the measure of love given to you. If you continue to love regardless of response and attitude, you will become the Lord's true disciple. You were not called to 'judge' but to 'love.' A new command I give you: Love one another. As I have loved you, so you must love one another. By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another. — John 13:34-35

Do not despair over who you are before people; discover who you are before the Lord and be strengthened. We finished the Sunday gathering twice in a row at 7:30. Let me leave it as a gift from a constrained minister and have a valuable and leisurely time. I seem to really love.

When you compare person with person, I become quite a decent person. However, before God, no one is free from lust and violence, greed and hatred, jealousy. Standing before Him is faith. That is why repentance bursts forth.

Just as people sometimes suffer, the church can also suffer. When we were young, our parents always seemed like mountains, but sometimes they are ill. However, there would be no one blaming them for becoming weak and not being what they were before. The church is like my mother.

* Why we fear even while loving If you feel fear while loving Jesus, it is because you still have much to lose. True love comes when you can lose everything except Jesus, and it casts out fear. There is no fear in love. But perfect love drives out fear, because fear has to do with punishment. The one who fears is not made perfect in love. — 1 John 4:18
* If I protect myself, it becomes sin. Trying to protect myself is sin. That is why pride becomes sin. On the other hand, when God protects me, it becomes self-worth.

People erase the past, pretend to count, and pretend to have. In conclusion, it is because they want to hear that 'they are a decent person whom no one can ignore.' So they 'bluff' and 'pump themselves up.' You do not need to exaggerate or pretend to be something. It is because you do not know yourself well. You do not know God well, so you do not know yourself well. Do not bluff or pump yourself up; receive love. I will love you warmly too. Big Hug!! ^^ Happy Valentine.

People think they will be unhappy because of what they have not accomplished. That is not so. Most people feel more unhappy because of what others have accomplished. No matter how much possession, recognition, and evaluation you receive, unhappiness does not cease unless greed departs. Greed compares. Comparison becomes hell itself. But faith is gratitude. Gratitude is heaven.

People are frustrated. Because they do not speak of essentials. If the cross gospel of Jesus disappears from the saints, it will be very frustrating. If there is one thing the church can uniquely have that the world does not, it is the gospel of Jesus' cross and blood. Let us repent.

People experience warm grace, shed tears, and change. We call it corruption. They are corrupted in a month, or corrupted in six months. Some people are corrupted in just a few years. Everything in the world rots. But the Lord is eternal. Those who hold only to the Lord can never change.

People are anxious, so they try to control situations. However, those of faith are called to exercise self-control and be sober-minded. Then you will see the Lord's glory, who governs and sustains all things.

* Envy does not mean defeat It is not envy that makes you lose. It is fear that makes you lose. Because you lose control. Boldness is not arbitrary conviction but belief in God's rule and sovereignty in the midst of circumstances.
People commit sin. They do it to be happy, to be joyful. But instead, deep regret and suffering remain. Holiness and purity are power and happiness.
People wander around looking for happiness, going here and there. But true happiness can only be enjoyed when you are fixed on Jesus.

People increased efficiency by recycling cattle remains as cattle feed and felt proud, but through the worst investment, mad cow disease emerged. Yet the Lord recognized Mary of the alabaster jar who made a holy waste. Waste for the Lord is the greatest investment in the world.

People imitate. Because they want to obtain even a little of the reputation that the original has. Seasoned ribs seem like they would be more expensive than fresh ribs, but they are cheaper. Even though more seasoning and effort went in, why is it cheaper? Because it is not fresh. That is why seasoning is used to supplement the taste. I hope you become a person without need for seasoning. Become a person of faith who recovers the power of essence.
All that people possess and boast of are like straw that will be burned and disappear. But invisible faith, hope, and love are like jewels that never change and will shine even in heaven.
* When faith is needed Faith is not when conditions are good shouting 'Hallelujah~,' but what is truly needed in times of trial.

People's loyalty and dedication are decided only after gauging my loyalty and dedication and obtaining hints. They themselves only exhibit that degree of ownership. Only when I sacrifice for that vision do they feel the value of sacrificing. Whether I am a hired hand paying only a modest price, or a soldier of the Lord giving everything and pleasing my commander—people are very curious about which I am. And as time passes, it will become clear to all.

No matter what people say or what the world does, look only to the Lord. If you are hurt, it means you were not looking only to the Lord. It means you expected and desired something other than the Lord.

When people desire to walk with the Lord but it does not go well, it is because they deny that they died with the holy Lord at the cross. A person nailed to the cross cannot live that way. A person nailed to the cross can only live this way.
* Identity Trial tells you who you are.

Do not seek recognition from people; seek to see the Lord's glory. A life pursuing the prize to which the Lord calls from above toward the goal is glorious. When the Lord's glory comes before everything else, that person is a pioneer.

Everyone has a void in their soul. It is a bottomless pit that is not filled by anyone's attention or love. Ultimately, unhappiness comes not from having less or lacking, but from deciding to be unhappy. I hope you enjoy the joy of blessing that makes you yourself. From now on, decide to be joyful, grateful, and happy. When attitude changes, you can be victorious with nothing.

* The unprofitable Professional A servant of God keeps what the Lord entrusted in any circumstance and quietly withdraws. Because they are a professional. Being called an unprofitable servant is nothing more than doing what must be done. — Luke 17:10
Do not be so heartbroken over people; rather, have a showdown with God.

A person draws closest to what is most important. They always carry objects, people, status, and such. An unremarkable life. When you draw near to God, the fragrance of God emanates and the power of God appears. This is precisely the 'power of proximity.' The essence of faith is that when you draw near to Jesus, you are transformed into Jesus' people, and when you draw near to those of faith, you are transformed into such a company. Mary's power was not a great deed but drawing near to Jesus, standing at Jesus' feet.
* When promise and circumstance differ Many people are disappointed. Because there is no grace.
A person can mature only through weakness, not through strength.

A person feels secure through calculation. But although human calculation seems to prosper, ultimately it collapses. No matter how much you calculate two fish and a few barley loaves, the answer does not come out. Let us make room for the Lord to work beyond our human calculation.
* When trials come, faith is revealed When a storm comes, an eagle rides the wind and soars higher, but a chicken buries its head in its wings and avoids. Do not be a chicken; be an eagle.

A person feels secure when following the majority. But if there are 300 who please the One who called them, they will defeat an enemy army of 135,000. (Judges 7) What kind of path are you walking now? Are you walking because others are walking it? Or are you walking the path of mission?
A person's heart becomes so sore that only then do they look upon the Lord's face. Are you in pain? It is time to look upon the Lord.

* By grace alone we enter. What a truly grateful saying. We enter by grace alone. This is so that no one can boast. By this grace you have been saved through faith, and this is not from yourselves, it is the gift of God—not by works, so that no one can boast. -Ephesians 2:8,9

People have a fear of empty spaces. Therefore, they unconditionally seek to fill them. Through various attempts, they try to manipulate people and solidify relationships. Only the Lord can fill my hunger. I must see what I truly long for. Jesus said, 'I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.' -John 6:35 If you are thirsty and hungry, now is the time to stop wearing false masks and return to the Lord.

People are born to love, and to love means to give time. And that time is now. If time spent with the Lord is squeezed out and filled with worldly things, you are not loving the Lord. Love is not productive or efficient at all. It is waste. A holy and happy waste.

Only when a person faces a death sentence and passes through death does the essence of the faith they serve truly reveal itself. Will you serve people well? Or serve God well? If I seek human approval, I am not a servant of Christ. The process of becoming refined gold is not easy, but it will be glorious.

* The reasons for hardship: First, it means you are saved. Second, it is because you love your fellow workers. Third, it is because you love souls. Without these, there would be nothing to struggle with at the fundamental level.

People do not commit new sins. They unfailingly repeat the same sins they committed a year ago. When seeing the same problem about a hundred times, there should be times when you see it well, but it seems that is not the case. Faith becomes easy when pursued fully, but when pursued precariously, it becomes very difficult or even impossible.
* The scope of worry: We are not permitted any scope for worry. To worry is to expect something other than the Lord. When you look only to the Lord, you do not worry.

People strive for success, but God does not desire people's success. He seeks people who break their own will and obey the Lord's Word. To obey is better than sacrifice, and to listen is better than the fat of rams. For rebellion is like the sin of divination, and arrogance like the evil of idolatry. -1 Samuel 15:22

People often cannot actively express their true feelings, and there are many misunderstandings due to insufficient and distorted expressions. More important than being misunderstood is that I do not misunderstand others.

* Do not be anxious about anything, do not be anxious about anything, do not be anxious about anything. Rather, in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus. -Philippians 4:6,7
People are not beautiful because they are beautiful. They are beautiful because a beautiful Being created them. When you embrace a beautiful heart, you become a beautiful person.
* Desires are fulfilled by grace alone. Whether it is something impossible like a miracle or something small and humble, it can only be received through God's sovereignty and grace.

People serve boasting. Whether refined or crude, the moment you boast in it, you come under its dominion. A person who served and boasted in perishable things has their direction and essence changed through the cross. When I look only to the Lord, the world becomes dead to me. Yet I boast only in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. Through the cross, the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world. -Galatians 6:14

It is common for people to pursue themselves and live for their egos. This means there is nothing lacking regarding others. However, when the Holy Spirit comes and a calling is given, seeing obstinacy brings tears without ever having received anything, and compassion arises without ever having shared blood.

People think that if they obtain what they want, they will be happy. But this is absolutely not possible. In reality, what they mistakenly think they want is not what they truly want, but standards given by the world. Appearance seen on TV, academic credentials recognized by society, and economic power become attractive in this world. Without faithfulness and honesty that God desires, and without intimacy with Him, you can never be happy. God designed us that way from the beginning.

People sometimes mistake obsession for love. It is not about pursuing what I want, but filling what the other person needs. If I like flowers, I will pick them, but if I love them, I place them in the sunlight and give them water.

People gather many things to be happy, and to do so, they want to overcome and trample on others to rise above them. They live boasting of what they obtain this way, but can never be happy. When I share bread with the people I love, share the Lord's grace, and worship, I experience happiness that is not envious of anyone in the world. Thank You, Lord, truly. For giving me such precious people of God...

People yearn for happiness. But the more they yearn, the more they feel thirst. I hope you change the content of your yearning. This is my desire, to know as I am known To see as I am seen, to love as I am love

Distinguishing between people is a great evil. Because people always place themselves on the good side and form their own group to compensate for damage. They avoid rather than confront. The most commonly used phrase is, 'That person is just like that.' It is difficult to acknowledge spiritual transformation. Because I must always be good and others must always be evil. Do not judge one another with evil thoughts. Are you not showing favoritism? -James 2:4

* What is more important than the situation is focusing on the Lord. When engulfed by circumstances, you cannot see the Lord. When you focus on the Lord, circumstances do not worry you. Lift the eyes of faith and look to the Lord. In distress, He will give you rest.
Distinguishing between people is committing evil. People are objects of love.
A person who seeks the Lord's joy rather than satisfying people—you are the champion.

* Beginning is never half. If the work begun is a good work, that is, saving lives and building up souls, be at peace. It is the Lord's work and the Lord will accomplish it. What I need is faith. Being confident of this, that he who began a good work in you will carry it on to completion until the day of Christ Jesus. -Philippians 1:6
Looking to people means plucking out one eye. Then you will be shaken and tested. Looking to the bronze serpent brings life. Looking to people brings death.
Do not deal with people, but through prayer, exercise authority over the spiritual realm. The God who leads history within providence will make it beautiful.
* Why weakness becomes strength: Standing before a problem, I am helpless and unable to handle it. Therefore, grace must be poured out. Become a weak person who needs the Lord's help.

When introducing people, we mention their school of origin, occupation, and even salary and appearance, which are never omitted. Because these are the sensitivities of worldly people. But Jesus is introduced by His mission and relationship. 'Look, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!' -John 1:29 (Mission) A voice came from heaven: 'You are my Son, whom I love; with you I am well pleased.' -Mark 1:11 (Relationship with God) Do not boast or be sensitive about perishable things, but run toward eternal belonging and praise.
* The most painful thing is wanting to take responsibility but being unable to do so. Wanting to take on everything and step back, but not being permitted to do so. This is why there is repentance.

Pray that you walk only in the Lord's way, not in human voices, human expectations, or vain perishing standards. Faith is a choice, and the continuation of that choice brings blessing and the Lord's guidance.
When caught up in human approval and popularity, surprisingly, we can become indifferent to God's approval.

* Faith is a collision of desires. Faith is when the longing for Jesus overwhelms the desires of the world. 'If my opponent wins, it is because their determination was stronger, and if I win, it is because my determination was stronger.' - Tsubasa Tenko 2011

Do not be proud of human approval or discouraged by human criticism. We must become people sensitive to God's evaluation. Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus. -Philippians 3:13,14
Woe to those who expect human approval. Blessed are those who expect the Lord.

* What should you look to? Do not doubt grace because of circumstances; interpret circumstances through the lens of grace. Faith is both interpretation and choice. Your interpretation and choice determine your destiny.

A person's essence, their character, is not understanding. Understanding fades and the body perishes, but affection continues to remain. We go to eternity with that affection. The affection I feel pain and joy through tells me who 'I as a person' am. Spiritual growth means my affection has changed. If Jesus is the highest peak of my life, then I am truly a mature and happy person.

People's highest concern is 'Until when?' (Psalm 13:1) But the Lord's timing does not belong to my domain and has never been permitted to people even once. So He simply says to be filled. That is the option permitted to me. Psalm 13:5 But I trust in your unfailing love; my heart rejoices in your salvation.
If a person's actions are transformed to please the Lord, the difficult tasks will be resolved. It will become a miracle.

* A contest without regret: Whether living or dying, it is for the Lord. Whether succeeding or failing, it is for the Lord. Therefore, there is no regret. Life is ultimately a true contest. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord. -Romans 14:8

The illness that people have cannot be known by appearance alone. Everyone carries a fatal illness. Even if you go to another hospital, no diagnosis comes out. For a life separated from God, indescribable agony covers it. This cannot be covered by money and fame.

When people suffer and are wounded, they become selfish. Their emotions dry up, becoming harsh at trivial disputes with immediate reactions. This is because there is no space. Even in the agony of death on the cross, Jesus thought of us and obeyed unto death. With His pierced hands, He touches us.

If a person pleases God through their actions, even enemies are reconciled to them. Proverbs 16:7. According to this, God is the sovereign of relationships. Self-initiated calculations for relationship improvement only worsen the situation. Restore your relationship with God, and the rest will naturally resolve itself. Do not hurry to harvest; focus on the roots. You will reap the fruit.

* When a thorn was embedded: When I was five years old, a cactus thorn became deeply embedded in my hand, and 38 years later, that bone-piercing pain still feels vivid. My aunts encouraged me, saying I was brave, and my mother suffered with me. But my father coldly removed the thorn. It was unbearably painful. Encouragement and empathy are helpful, but if we stop there, the hand can rot and need to be amputated. After enduring the pain and coldly removing the thorn, it is not too late to wrap it and cry. Love is not all the same.

The fundamental reason people desire, sin, and rush toward death is not because temptation is strong. To be honest, it is because they hate God. The fear of the Lord is a fountain of life, turning a person from the snares of death. -Proverbs 14:27 Fear God.

When people encounter an extremely difficult, closed-off situation, sometimes they can only laugh. But a Christian has reliance on the Lord and heavenly composure in that moment. Because they know that only the Lord can resolve it. 'Do not worry!!'

'So then, men ought to regard us as servants of Christ and as those entrusted with the secret things of God. Now it is required that those who have been given a trust must prove faithful.' -1 Corinthians 4:1-2 The most important virtue of a leader is 'faithfulness.' David, Moses, and Jesus exemplified this. But what must be preceding is knowing 'God's secret.' Many people become caught while being faithful without knowing this secret. I hope you become one who knows the secret.
What is man that you are mindful of him? What is mankind that you care for him?

* The reason I am alive even when it seems like I should perish and proclaim the Lord's work (Psalm 118:17): It is God's living Word. This word is my comfort in my affliction; your promise preserves my life. -Psalm 119:50 If your law had not been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction. -Psalm 119:92
People are not transformed but corrupted because they do not yearn. If the spirit is not poor, people become corrupted.

Is it really impossible for a person not to hate another person? Can they only smile on the outside while hiding their heart and continuously hate? I do not think so. Receive the heart of the Lord whose hands were nailed. That was His prayer the night before the crucifixion. 'I will remain in the world no longer, but they are still in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, protect them by the power of your name—the name you gave me—so that they may be one as we are one.' -John 17:11
Responding to God rather than people is faithfulness. The Lord looks at whether you are faithful in small things, wealth, and things of others.
