* Losing the great for the small - It is foolish to covet small things and lose great things. For us, the great things are His Kingdom and His Righteousness, and the small things are Myself and My Pride. When you have what matters, other things are added to you.

There are many reasons why people do not move, but taking the church as an example: 1. Change does not occur. 2. Change has two main fields: the general congregation and existing believers. 3. Change in the masses and congregation stems from 'communication,' while change in existing members requires 'grounds for action' to be provided. 4. Communication for the general public must secure 'understanding' (intellect), 'empathy' (emotion), and 'decision' (will) across all dimensions of the person. When there is spiritual empathy, motivation for life change comes. 5. The grounds for members (Acts) must have examples and samples. If the leader does not move, followers will not move through lecture (words only) and urging. There must be 'experience' in seeing and hearing. 6. Communication that transforms the general public must happen only once, within worship. To gain gentiles, one must approach in the language of gentiles. The masses grant only 2 hours per week (Sunday worship). 7. Existing members must be given ministry to share. The best example is when the martyr (sacrifice) occurs. Without blood shed, there are no followers. 8. If the target (congregation or members) is unclear, methods and approach cannot be appropriate. You must know where the pain is to prescribe and treat. 9. In many cases, numbers replace core values. That is why insight into problems is important. 10. Looking beyond illusions to core values—wisdom is power. 11. Action←Change←Communication and grounds←Understanding, empathy, decision (details) and grounds←Inspiration and effective approach←Selection and focus←Insight←Wisdom. True wisdom comes from affection for souls and the relinquishment of rights.
As a person speaks with his friend, so the Lord spoke with Moses.

Though it appears that a person earns as much as he works, the giver is God. God's principle of giving material and gifts is not by my ability but by need. When you know God, you can see that all things are accomplished by His will. Even the flying bird does not fall unless it is the Lord's will.
* Eternally Undefeated (永遠不敗) - No matter how dark it appears, God does not make mistakes, and through His faithfulness, He will receive praise among all nations and peoples. I believe in Him.
Though a person may boast that he has no flaw in himself, God sees even the depths of his heart. -Proverbs 21:2
There comes a time when a person shows his faith. That is precisely when he chooses.

* To love, one must not be shaken. To love, one must become strong. How strong? Strong enough to endure this hardship without dying, to live and practice steadfast love. The secret to becoming strong is to humble myself before the Lord.

A person is most vulnerable to sin when afraid. When afraid, he becomes angry, cannot hear His voice, and loses wisdom and gentleness. When he comes to his senses, many wounds remain. That is why the Lord, when giving a mission, says, 'Do not fear.' He does not desire a leader who sins.
* What is grace? It is the awareness that you are so lacking that you cannot even exist for a moment without the Lord's mercy.

There is no fear in love; perfect love casts out fear. Fear has punishment; whoever fears has not been perfected in love. -1 John 4:18. When there is confidence in God's love, you do not fear. Fear is a matter of punishment, not optional. It must be cast out. Through the Lord's love...

Love and hate, faith and unbelief, hope and despair are one path. Only the direction differs. That is why repentance is a far greater transformation than making a checklist of items to discard. It is a change of direction.

To those hungry for love and recognition... Do you truly wish to become a true servant of God? 'Then become completely free from the gaze and judgment of people.' 'Now, should I please people or please God? Should I seek the approval of people? If I were still seeking the approval of people, I would not be a servant of Christ.' Galatians 1:10

* How a leader loves - by becoming strong. Like Jesus standing on the boat in the midst of raging waves saying 'Be at peace,' I must stand firm with conviction so that the souls entrusted to me are not shaken, becoming strong in a new way. That is how I can save the souls I love.
One must pray because of love and love through prayer. Then true grace from the Lord comes upon you.
Because of love, one can become cowardly, and that is the greatest courage.

* Faith is interpretation. Faith is knowing that the small grace I received means all that has been given me is sufficient grace, and trial is judging through a few negative things that all the grace I had received was worthless. Faith and unbelief are separated by a sheet of paper.
Love is not giving what I want. That is why love without wisdom becomes a disaster.
'Love' is not because of something but rather love despite everything.
Love is not giving something but giving time and doing something meaningful together. That is why a selfish life knows neither happiness nor experiences happiness.

Love must manifest in the form of prayer. Since love is not the nature of man but the attribute of God, love cannot be accomplished without prayer that relies on the Lord's power, nor can it be sustained. Love through prayer and pray through love. The Lord prayed and loved to the end.

Love is inefficient. For a one-hour meeting, eight hours and much finance are consumed. Even thoughtlessly, one schemes to pour out love. Why? Because the Son of God taking up the cross for me is the greatest inefficiency and unfair transaction. Love inefficiently. The world calls that 'crazy.'
Love is not persuaded. True love is revealed. False love is pretended, but real love is poured out.

Love is patient. The reason it must be patient is that true love is not accomplished without patience. Love must overcome challenge and resistance and must also be tuned. Love is patient and -1 Corinthians 13:4

* The peacemaker - One who makes peace is truly blessed. Because in relation to God, they attain the closest distance, the position of a son. Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.
Love is a cure for loneliness. Do not wait to be loved; pour out love now.

Love gives courage. And it makes one challenge for the person loved. One may fail and fall, but without love, one would not do it. Even if things do not go as planned, the concern alone already means loving with all one's heart. If you loved with all your heart, you will not be ashamed or afraid.
Love needs no proof. Love speaks for itself. Moreover, it cannot be hidden. Because of love, we pray.

Even carrying thorns with love, if held with bleeding, they become roses. That love is not human philanthropy or pity but the love of God's Son dying on the cross. God desires that we become ministers who bleed from holding thorns today so that not a single person entrusted to us is lost.

One must receive much love. Love from the Lord and love and understanding from God's people who reflect the Lord's love. Then the disorder within me becomes still and I enjoy peace. That is the grace God pours out.
* Before giving up - When people abandon what they have started, they have certainly forgotten this: why they started it.
Without praying with the motive of love, you cannot receive answers.
Love decides. Since love is what I continuously do, love changes everything.

When love grows cold, expectations disappear, and you cannot feel—this is called a crisis. Yet that is a crisis only from the human perspective. Throughout history, there has never been a crisis from God's perspective. He even said He led the disobedient Israelites in the wilderness as a father embraces his son.

To be called to a place without love, filled with hatred and envy, without worship, with only dryness and competition—that is glorious. Because the ground I tread will be covered with the Lord's blood. I believe that.
Even running with love, life is short. Do not despair; rise up and do the work the Lord has given you. Then the Lord will be glorified.

* What I truly fear - Being criticized, being misunderstood and walking alone for life, even everything failing—I do not fear these. I fear only being unfaithful and disloyal to the One who has entrusted all this to me today.

The heart to love is given by God. That is why being loved according to God's will is a gift and grace. It is not because I am good but because the grace the Lord pours out is deep and great. Therefore, I must truly give thanks and love with that heart.

I think about what those I love will do, intercede, and pray. If I pray with love and tears, God will surely bless and grant the wonderful blessings He has prepared—this I believe and anticipate. God will surely work.

That those I love love the One I love is inspiring. And that each person I love loves one another and becomes one is moving. The one who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly at the bridegroom's voice. I am filled with this joy. John 3:29. The saints on earth are precious; all my delight is in them. Psalm 16:3

* I am not failing to see that person; I am failing to see Peniel. When a person becomes inconvenient and stressful, it is not because the other is wicked. It is because I am not seeing God's face right now. When you see God's face, even enemies and sinners seem like seeing God's face. Therefore, Jacob called that place Peniel (the face of God), saying, 'I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.' -Genesis 32:30. When I saw my brother's (Esau's) face, it was like seeing God's face, and he also accepted me. -Genesis 33:10
* Do not worry. I am. Entrust to me. Look only to the Lord.

* Change and corruption - When you receive Jesus's cross in your heart, change occurs. When you turn away from that cross, you fall into temptation, doubt, and corruption. Since the cross is eternal salvation, those who hold the cross do not change and complete the race. -Genesis 32:30

* The victory of overcoming trial - Though years pass and times change, truth does not change. Though there are challenges and tribulations, there is truth that pushes me not to draw back. The power of the Lord's blood prevails.

* The harbor of hope and the place of safety - Trusting in what is unbelievable leads to anxiety. When you rest your security in Him and trust the Lord's work, there is true peace. Peace that can never be taken away.
You said to love, so I ran for the Lord. In a poor heart becomes pain, and as tears well up in both eyes, I run more hotly. Toward the Lord...
Love makes you pray. If you say you love but do not pray for someone, you love only with words.

When you love, toil is not toilsome. Rather, toil becomes joy and suffering becomes glory. Why? Because love has the nature of payment. The more things precious are paid without reservation, the happier love becomes.

When you love, without saying anything, when in pain you share the pain, when together you are at peace without fear, and the more you give all you have and sacrifice, the happier you become. That is why Jesus shed blood on the cross. In the end, one who loves cannot help but be a person who, as I decline, prays with all my heart.

* Benevolence is undefeatable (仁者無敵) - Those who love have no adversary. If one loves souls without falsehood and lived faithfully in faith, there is no need to fear, for there is no one to oppose them. If you pursue love and faith, regardless of the world's and people's recognition and misunderstanding, you possess the greatest authority and kingly power as a priest. The way the Lord went is a narrow way, not one understood or recognized by people.
To love is to await. We love through action, truth, and prayer.

To love is not merely to possess information about someone. It is to have your heart completely filled with thoughts of that person. You go to school, work, and sleep, but that beloved person covers your heart. I pray that person becomes the Lord.

To love is to cover the other person with the blood of the cross. Because you desire to give good things and are willing to sacrifice. All good things must begin with the blood of the cross, and for that, I too must shed my blood.
If you love, you pray. Without love and faith, you cannot even ask for prayer. I pray that we become servants of the Lord who understand the value of prayer and can ask for it.

If you love, do not postpone for something better. It is more important to be together now, in this moment, in this form—not a slightly better version. Because love is not a condition; it is a matter of the heart.

* Circumstances cannot be adjusted. They can only be governed. The circumstances I face cannot be adjusted as I wish. I have neither the ability nor the authority to do so. But when I move forward with gratitude, I will experience the Lord's amazing works and wisdom. I believe in the Lord.
If you love, you must be given.

Using what you should love, and loving what you should use—this is called 'fall.' Forgetting what is essential and wasting resources on peripheral and non-essential things is where the fall begins. Jesus and souls are essential.
A heart to love, a heart to pray—the heart the Lord gives.
Regretting having loved means you did not stake your life on it. If you loved with all your heart, even if you part, you will not regret.
* Courage standing at the cliff's edge. Those not prepared are fearful and anxious. Especially because they are not prepared to lose, they cannot receive.
God who brings rivers in the desert and protects us—we look only to the Lord who will do great works within us.

Because the power of death is broken, the devil can only deceive in the end. When facing any problem, if we just commit it without being hasty, it resolves itself. Most problems worsen when Christians 'imagine' them. We must stop writing fiction.
Before the calling, he denied everything. Loyalty comes from the 'depth of affection' for Him.
The authenticity of a calling is known by the response, not the result. If it was for oneself, after success there will be emptiness, or after failure, self-condemnation.
* My place. What I can do clearly has limits. But when I do it faithfully, power comes and miracles happen. I ask that you come to the place where you do what you can do.
Those with a calling stumble but never fall completely. It is not that they bear the calling, but the calling bears them.
* In truth, many people considered bad are not actually bad; their hearts are hurting. They are friends who need so much love. Only God's love can build them up and make them whole.

* It is not expectation but promise. If things go as expected based on my position, status, and current circumstances, why would everyone in the world sigh as they live? Would not everyone who buys lottery tickets and invests in stocks and businesses strike it big? We must not have vain hopes because what happens is not according to circumstances and expectations, but through the hand of the one who gives.

* It is unconditional, unconditional. People try to find reasons in their own excellence. It seems they need to discover reasons even that way for comfort. But all things that happen are the Lord's unconditional grace.
* What is love? It is not only wanting to be together, but praying and preparing so that the other can be happy even in my absence.

Putting down ministry after five years—a guitar I had not touched even once, as dull and worn as my own voice. It accompanied many prayer meetings and even now I think it is good enough for prayer. Still, after a long time, I sharpened the strings and patched broken places.

* It is not feeling, so it can be done well. Joseph was respected and loved by people not as a skilled person (A line), but with sincerity. He was a person who never forgot respect and consideration not only toward subordinates but also toward slaves and even prisoners, and especially he could understand with eyes of compassion the unspeakable wounds from broken families. This is because Joseph was a slave and a prisoner, a wounded healer.

To the leader loved by a minister—One with a calling is not someone trying to succeed. Why? Because they have already succeeded in the Lord. The Lord has given me the best, and if He causes me to take on something, He will supply the power according to His good purpose. What we need is unwavering trust like a rock.

* To offer thanks. When I seek first His kingdom and His righteousness, I have faith and trust that God will add all these things. Also, after seeking His kingdom and His righteousness, I cannot place my hope on all these things, but I can only offer thanksgiving and worship to Him.

A minister to young people—If you think it cannot be done, countless reasons and plausible excuses arise. But I want to say this: please concern yourselves only with whether this is the right work the Lord desires and whether it is something that must be done for souls. If you think it is right, act on it. And so no one can stand in your way. Sometimes I meet members who are tormented, possessed by demons. At such times, I sometimes dialogue with evil spirits. I say this: 'You know me, don't you? That I will not give up and will go to the end. And that you will not last long.'
A minister is like a waiting room. The role is to welcome, be with, and send off. Though we cannot be together forever, we keep that place and rejoice in the joy of birthing people into heaven.

A minister has a harsh and bitter side. To those in real difficulty, they say, give even more. To those whose hearts are truly burdened, they say, advance toward souls. To those in desperate loneliness, they say, look only to the Lord and worship. Why? Because it is truly words that stick in the throat, but from my experience, it is the way that brings the most happiness and life. The happiest moment of life was when the Lord made me weep.

Ministers and church members are all sinners. But when God anoints and works are done in that anointing, the Lord will regard it as the Lord's direct work. When imperfect humans obey the Word and advance in anointing, God promises to show His authority in the insufficient human life and pour out His power.

The posture of a minister: Do not alienate any souls. Above all, do not leave anyone out of the intercessory prayer list. Even someone who has not come to church for six months is persistently sought out and prayed for.

* Are you joking? Wherever you go, whatever you do, even if you disappear, just intercede. God's network is anytime, any network, and works faithfully in any circumstance. When you pray for joking kids, something not joking happens in their lives.

After ministry, I prayed with the young people and the Lord, and was welcomed by my daughters at home. When are young people most refreshing? When the blood of the cross flows through their eyes, and this happens when they yearn for the Lord with their own hearts. Pour out more, more....

* When the purpose is clear, life becomes simple. If I have done what I must do, it is enough. When the calling is clear, life becomes simple and worries decrease. Freedom from meaningless misunderstandings and imaginations comes because preaching, praying, and witnessing is already too full. Preach and pray—witness daily - Hymn 268

One reason King Saul perished was because he feared people. A person who fears people cannot have deep friends, no matter how kind. Proverbs 29:25 The fear of man brings a snare, but whoever trusts in the Lord is safe.

Saul gathered all his abilities in war to catch David. David, in great trouble and distress, lifted his head, praised the Lord, and sought His face. Life is decided right there. The objective military strength is not the issue; what matters is how meaningfully one lives. Therefore Saul stopped pursuing David and returned and went to fight the Philistines, so that place was called Sela-hammahlekoth—1 Samuel 23:28

Saul said to Samuel, 'I have sinned. For I have transgressed the commandment of the Lord and your words, because I feared the people and obeyed their voice'—1 Samuel 15:24. Those who pursue recognition, reputation, and popularity will never be used by God.
* Prayer in difficult times: God, make me even lower, I pray.
Photography is affection for the subject. It increases after early morning worship.

I take many photos not because I like photography but because I love. So please take a natural pose when saying something good. Don't worry about silly photos that come up. Anyway, something will go up.

When opposing Satan—with the power of the Holy Spirit, the blood of Jesus, and the holy name Jesus, bind darkness and release peace. Oppose with the recorded Word (Eph 6), the sword of the Spirit. Oppose with reverse spirit (Matt 5). If the opponent comes at you with life and death while you receive it as a joke, you yourself will suffer great loss. You must be strong to save yourself and others.

Satan has a classic, time-honored traditional approach. 'If you just disappear, that's all.' Many have been eliminated this way. This proposal is so sweet because it seems all problems can be solved at once. All scenarios stem from fleshly desires and self-will. If disappearing were the Lord's will, it would make Jesus's cross void. The Lord's will is to stand in that place, receive power, hear His voice, and destroy the devil and circumstances.

Satan does not always condemn. Sometimes he allows praise. Not to restore self-esteem, but to make 'self' swell so one cannot depend on God. Satan rushes like a roaring lion at those separated from God.
* Ribeye, clam miso soup, razor clams

If I were as useless and should disappear as Satan says, there would be no need to be so persistent. That Satan condemns me as a useless person, someone who should disappear, is opposite to the truth when viewed against Satan's position, his purpose, and his schemes based on lies. Do not agree with evil; walk in the light. If there is one who always lies, then if you do only the opposite, you do well.—Opposite spirit

* A sinner's prayer. Feeling peace when praying means the sinner's prayer is received through 'the merit of the blood,' and that 'He heard,' so 'according to His kindness' He will accomplish it. Prayer is not penance but a privilege like an ambassador with full powers.

A person injured on a mountain meets a rescue team and leaves the mountain. The wood chips, frozen food, and torn clothing they valued so much are all discarded, and without hesitation they board the helicopter. How happy I am comes down to how much of my rights I have surrendered before Jesus. Faith is not wordplay; it is life. Whether you have truly met Jesus or just pretend to have met Him is told by the items you have given up.

Living, there are times when you can do neither this nor that. So you reach a point in the middle where you do nothing but simply 'wait.' That is when you pray. God is blocking other things and telling you to pray.

When your heart hurts too much while living, say this: 'God, my heart hurts so much. Like my heart striking the earth, my heart hurts so much. My heart hurts so, it hurts. X 100.' And call out like this: 'He set me free from sin, I gained victory over temptation...'

Life has God's way, God's method. No matter how busy, I have time to fellowship with the Lord and keep it holy, serving from what is mine and worshiping. By calculation it is 'loss' by worldly terms, but by faith it is 'victory'.

The thirst of life should not be sought through worldly wisdom, but by longing for the Lord. This is the qualification of those who will enter Canaan. Every time thirst becomes a reality in life, we must choose not some clever scheme, but God Himself.

The intensity of life is like a strategic position in war. It is an important strategic location where the owner changes several times a day. Do not give up after fighting once or twice and collapsing from repeated sins. The more you must persistently fight back immediately. Then the enemy will retreat in fear. You have sinned, but rise up immediately, repent and worship. To the enemy, it is terror itself. The expression may be awkward, but it will appear almost zombie-like.
* The Reason for Moving Forward: Although I am weak and insufficient, the Lord's love compels me and pushes me forward endlessly. Though I am lacking, He will receive me... -Hymn 545
Samson brought storms upon his own life, but God never abandoned Samson. Neither do we abandon. God does not forget the reason He created us, but remembers it.

* Living in repentance makes us humble, lets us let go, and makes us earnest. Most importantly, it allows us to see the truth, not be deceived, and walk in the path of life. Do not judge, but repent is the Lord's desire.

The other side is coming at you with their life on the line, yet many people think it is 'friendly.' In all matters related to life, you must stake your life. Moreover, to mature and nurture life, you must again endure the pain of crossing the line of death. I am again suffering birth pangs for you... this was Paul's dedication and desire.
* The Common Appearance of Saints: When Satan attacks with cunning, the saint plants faith in prayer today as well.

* The Core Problem: Loving Jesus is only possible when you hold the cross. To say you love Jesus while turning away from the cross is like getting married because you enjoyed the honeymoon. All side effects are because we cannot hold the cross. It is not ignorant new members, but myself as a servant, leader, or officer who cannot hold the cross that is the problem. The cross means that Jesus was nailed to death for my sins, and that I also died nailed to the cross in union with Jesus. I say Jesus lives in me, but my self is living vividly, so family and colleagues cannot see Jesus. I have been crucified with Christ; and it is no longer I who live, but Christ lives in me... -Galatians 2:20

* More Beautiful Than Flowers: People are beautiful just standing there. It is because we are made in God's image. Rather than being dazzled by conditions, meet many people who love your appearance, your pain, just as you are. It is not something you see when you stop, but something you see when you let go.

Wounds come from one word—rejection. However, rejection is not the root cause of the wound. Essentially, before the issue of rejection, it begins with losing your identity: 'How valuable am I in the Lord?' It is not the environment and people that are the problem, but not knowing 'who I am in the Lord.' -From a leader meeting. Little children, I am writing to you because you know the Father. Fathers, I am writing to you because you know Him who has been from the beginning. Young men, I am writing to you because you are strong, and the word of God abides in you, and you have overcome the evil one. -1 John 2:14

* I Must Experience Frustration: Do I come to my senses only when frustrated to death? You must experience frustration. You must experience things being blocked. You petition the Lord but receive the answer, 'What does this have to do with you?' (John 2:4). You must realize that although the Lord is present and received, when He is a guest, He cannot help even if He wants to. Until my self dies and the Lord becomes the complete Lord who rules, until He commands as the host of my Canaan wedding feast, such deprivation and suffering in me must never stop. Therefore we have been buried with Him through baptism into death, so that as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too might walk in newness of life. -Romans 6:4
The most difficult thing in circumstances is myself. The most difficult thing is my own heart thinking it is hard and difficult. Believe in the Lord and try it. Really, that will work.

There is no need to be sorrowful because circumstances and conditions are not good. Even if life experiences drought, a person who believes in Jesus is not hindered at all. Because we are 'a tree planted by the stream.'
Circumstances are watching me. If I change, circumstances change too. If you only look at circumstances, you will continue to take the same test in that same place.
Even if circumstances have not changed, if you believe they are in the Lord's hands, you will obtain true rest. If you are in the Almighty God, what is there to fear, even in the worst case?
* Simple Faith: Ultimately, worldly desires and longing for God collide. Worldly desires live a life of envying the world, but longing for God brings shame to the world. Yearn. You will win.
Do not look at circumstances but believe in the conclusion. Truth is the conclusion.

A person who twists situations through clever schemes, calculates, speculates, imagines, and is led by emotions cannot be trusted. Become a fool who knows nothing but Jesus Christ. You will become the wisest person.
If people cannot control circumstances, they become anxious. But circumstances are handled by the Lord. Looking at circumstances with eyes of faith is a holy beginning.

When circumstances are extremely difficult and you want to give up... 1 Kings 17:12: She said, 'As the Lord your God lives, I have nothing baked, except a handful of flour in a jar and a little oil in a jug; and behold, I am gathering a couple of sticks that I may go in and prepare it for me and my son, that we may eat it and die.' To those broken people who had given up and despaired, leaving only a handful, wanting to pack up and return to Korea or quit and be disappointed, the Lord says: Give even that and look to the Lord. It means if you cannot change your current circumstances with a handful, stake your life. When circumstances become extremely critical, obedience becomes rather easy. 17:14: But the Lord, the God of Israel, said to her, 'The jar of flour shall not be exhausted, nor shall the jug of oil be empty, until the day that the Lord sends rain on the face of the earth.'

Circumstances do not dictate me. Rather, my faith turns circumstances upside down. Faith pushes out anxiety and calls forth the Lord's miracles. The faithful God will give what is best. When Jacob prayed earnestly, Esau's heart changed, and when Esther fasted and prayed for three days, a series of miraculous coincidences appeared. If you believe, you will see the glory of God.
No matter how difficult circumstances are, we cannot deny the Lord's faithfulness and grace.

That circumstances are not good is an interpretation from the human perspective. God is the One who provides the path in the wilderness and springs in the desert even in this frustrating situation. Yesterday's sigh will change to song. I hope you trust and believe only in the Lord.

* Do Not Speak Lightly: From experience, speaking persuasively better than others and being excellent at grasping logic often led to much being spent in conflict. Trying to listen more to the perspectives others hold is mature leadership.
In the new year, there is work to do. To hear the voice of Jesus. To look to Jesus. To hold the hand of Jesus. And to walk on water without hesitation.

* Do Not Be Ashamed of the One Who Gave Grace: Everything given to me is God's grace. Because He is perfect, the grace He gave me is also perfect. Complaining and murmuring about what He gave me and the relationships is making the One who gave me grace appear shameful. Because the Giver is good, what is given cannot help but be good.

He gave a new commandment, which is to love one another. Our love is Jesus's desire and the heavenly Father's dream. To those who do it, God pours out power and grace abundantly. Criticism exists for the one who criticizes. Power exists for those who love.

* How to Live Without Shame: Do not hang your life on what the world envies; pursue a life that brings shame to the world. You must become a life that overturns and opposes the turbid currents of the times. People who sacrifice themselves to feed others and reduce their desires to serve someone are not lives that make the world envious but glorious lives that bring shame to it.
A new youth group is being established. What is the most important ministry?

* True Leader: Even without recognition, bearing the cross, paying the price, putting down pride, walking alone on the narrow path that everyone abandons and leaves, you rejoice and quietly maintain infinite loyalty. Because you are a professional.

You do not need to wear a mask and become someone else instead of being renewed. Living as the 'self' that God accepts and created is doing it best. Wanting what the Lord does not give is greed. Many people cannot live 'their own life' because of this greed and try to mimic 'another's life.' You are already beautiful enough as you are.
* True Worship: The heavens open and you hear the Lord's voice like a trumpet sound and see the Lord's heavenly glory. Do not count your wounds; long for such worship.

Do not suddenly become an excessive sinner or righteous. From the beginning, through boundless grace you were forgiven and declared righteous, so live with humble heart and the consciousness of a debtor, and He is pleased. Realize that you are forgiven and that you need the Lord for life. Then it is so.

There is a bird that is not caged in a cage. The world makes a cage and confines you. Through the specifications, standards, recognition, and competition the world demands, it threatens you that unless you live this way, you will become a loser in life. It says that if you do not live the world's way, you will fall behind and something terrible will happen. We are not beings to be caged. Robbing freedom from a bird flying in the open sky is a great sin. Take flight. You are someone who cannot be caged.
* How Can I Not Give Thanks?: How can I not give thanks that it is the Lord who moves me? What we follow is not people but only the Lamb who is the Shepherd, Jesus.
If you work thoughtlessly, you will suffer; if you work from experience, you will be ordinary; but if you are led by voice, miracles will happen.

If you think too much, you cannot pray properly. Because there are many things to worry about and many things to enjoy. The apostle Paul said he knows nothing but Jesus Christ and the cross. The devil says to do it moderately, but we give wholeheartedly.

There is only one life. It means you cannot be reborn just because you have ruined it. But through the power of the blood that cleanses my soul and gives me a new day, I can live a new life every day. How amazing is this! Hallelujah!

Life abundant is obtained by having a thought and intention against Satan who hates giving life abundantly more than death. That is, victory comes when you abandon the desire to keep my possessions and the grace given to me, and actively share. When we do this, we can enjoy and experience God more abundantly.
To obtain life, I must die. When I die, the life of Jesus appears. The moment I die, hatred, disappointment, resentment, and anguish all disappear in an instant.

If you cannot be satisfied with the living water, the Lord, you will inevitably seek dirty sewage. If you find life in a pigsty, it is the result, not the cause, of leaving the Lord. The cause begins from not looking to the Lord. My people have committed two evils: they have forsaken Me, the fountain of living water, and hewed for themselves cisterns, broken cisterns that can hold no water. -Jeremiah 2:13. And even as they did not see fit to acknowledge God any longer, God gave them over to a depraved mind, to do those things which are not proper. -Romans 1:28. I must decide whether I want to fear God or not. It is an age when many want God's gifts while rejecting God.

* Sufficient Grace: Satisfaction in the world is acquiring and monopolizing everything. That is why there is competition and conflict. But God wants to give us grace. That grace is abundant, rich, full, and sufficient grace. Because He wants to give Himself.

* Daily Grace: I must ask for the grace prepared for me today. That grace allows me to live as a saint in faith. Without that grace, I misunderstand on my own, get hurt by emotions, and my life is ruined. That grace holds me forever.

* Only One Thing to Confirm: Confirm only one thing—that my heart and passion for the beautiful Lord cannot stop. Ignore the rest. Since I have nothing to lose, I have nothing to fear. The Lord alone is sufficient.

* Servant, What Is It That You Truly Desire?: To love the Lord even more. To see the Lord's glory. To see revival in this land. To taste and know the Lord's goodness, all else can be lost. Then you will be satisfied even if you lose everything. When desire changes, faith becomes easy.
* No Need to Fear Anymore: If you pray that the Lord's will be accomplished and believe it, what else is there to fear?

* 德必有隣 (Virtue Has Neighbors): One with virtue will not be lonely. Pursuing excessively and exclusively beyond the scope and purpose given by God is greed for monopoly. Because what God gave temporarily for its proper use is misused, ironically, those who seek to monopolize power and wealth, relationships and attention, are lonely in crowds and live glorious yet miserable lives. Of course, such monopoly permitted in society and its members creates dead-weight loss, excess burden, and allocative inefficiency. When you know that One and the truth, you are free from monopoly and attachment, and living the life of bearing the cross, you give life abundantly not only to yourself but to all. We who are strong ought to bear the weaknesses of those without strength and not just please ourselves. Let each of us please his neighbor for his good, to build him up. -Romans 15:1-2

* The Reason for Discouragement and Pride (Freedom from Mood Swings): We do not become discouraged when things are difficult nor become arrogant when things become slightly favorable. The reason we consistently maintain the given path is not because we trust ourselves but because we trust the Almighty God and the completeness of God's grace.

* Intellect Alone Finds No Answer: Really, going all the way to the end, there are times when even using your mind finds no answer. By understanding the context before and after, there is no way it should go well, yet you see cases where grace is poured out powerfully. That I receive salvation and blessing is grace that is utterly incomprehensible. That is why I live by grace.

* The Ability to Rejoice: God wants us to become people who live in joy. But to rejoice, you need ability. One is 'forbearance' and the other is 'prayers of thanksgiving.' If your relationship with people and with God are broken, you can never rejoice. Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say, rejoice! Let your forbearing spirit be known to all men; the Lord is near. Be anxious for nothing, but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God. -Philippians 4:4-6

* The reason for anger: We become angry when things don't go our way. The problem is that such a heart is self-centered. Therefore, a self-centered person cannot gain wisdom and discernment because of anger. Become angry when God's will is not fulfilled. This is called righteous anger.

* Do not be discouraged. Everything has its time, season, and purpose. Of course, the same applies to me. Do not be discouraged because you were not welcomed under the spotlight you desired. It is God's hand extending the water of life to someone thirsting whom God sends.

* Discernment comes when we are emptied. When you play baduk or chess, you often see better when you're observing than when you're playing. True discernment is given when you let go and have no need to cling. Therefore, those whose purpose is the Lord have nothing to lose, so they discern all things but are judged by no one. Every nation has a time to decide; when truth and falsehood battle, which side will you stand on? - Hymn 521 The spiritual person judges all things, but is themselves judged by no one. - 1 Corinthians 2:15
Washing one another's feet is the Lord's will. Blessed are those who know this and do it.

Whether it's Seoul, London, or a remote impoverished area, life is the same everywhere. My happiness comes from standing where God has called me. The Lord's guidance must be experienced through waiting. It is impossible to explain something without tasting it.
* A spiritual servant does not give mere compliments. Good words that people speak become compliments, but when God's people speak, they become prophecy.
If you thought you were standing firm, you have already fallen. Before seeing the results, look at the heart. Faith is not done by words—whether it's Jesus to the bone or merely foam.

* His love embraces even thorns. There is no need to struggle to remove your shortcomings or become someone else. If someone cannot love you as you are, their feelings mean little to you. God loves you as you are—yesterday, today, and forever. "But God demonstrates his own love for us in this: While we were still sinners, Christ died for us." - Romans 5:8

* The condition for happiness: To be happy, one must have peace. To have peace, one must be grateful. To be grateful, faith must be presupposed. Faith is a gift, and gifts are given by grace, not by price. Therefore, grace is what God gives us first when we are sinners.

* Beware of new ransomware. Never click on suspicious email attachments. These days there are viruses coming through email attachments with names like Fedex, DHL, Outstanding invoice. Ordinary viruses are okay because you can recover some data, but a nasty one called Cryptolocker encrypts files even after the virus is removed, ultimately destroying all photos and documents on your PC. If you clicked on it, immediately switch off and find an expert. Once files are encrypted, they cannot be recovered with current technology. Especially for those writing essays—back up and work using public clouds like Google Drive. * See FBI link - under investigation http://www.fbi.gov/washingtondc/news-and-outreach/stories/cryptolocker-ransomware-encrypts-users-files

* Beware of new ransomware. Never click on suspicious attachments. These days there are viruses coming through email attachments with names like Fedex, DHL, Outstanding invoice. Ordinary viruses are okay because you can recover some data, but a nasty one called Cryptolocker encrypts files even after the virus is removed, ultimately destroying all photos and documents on your PC. If you clicked on it, immediately switch off and find an expert.
* Worship is knowing God's worth, looking to Him and becoming like Him, laying yourself down before Him, and following Him as your shepherd. - Revelation 4, 5, 6, 7

* If you pray to love, you will be loved. If you pray to be loved, you will earnestly seek but still feel anxious. However, if you pray to love, despite many challenges, you will become stronger in grace and peace. "And as for you, Simon, I have prayed for you, that your faith may not fail..." - Luke 22:32 "If we love one another, God lives in us and his love is made complete in us." - 1 John 4:12

* Believe in the Lord's joy, not your own: To please someone, you must know that person—what they dislike and what they like. To please God means seeking joy within God's will, not conforming to your own joy. Sometimes God even requires the opposite of your joy. This is painful, but ultimately it is for your benefit, so He allows it. God will never fail you!

* Fear what comes easily: When you reach a certain level, you begin to coast by mere inertia without paying a price. That is not a blessing but a curse. Because the bubbles of pretense and compromise will eventually burst, and since you have never labored with the wings of true ability, you will fall. The higher you climb, the lower you must humble yourself in fear.

* The reason we need not fear trials: Stephen shone like an angel with a bright face, looking toward heaven in the place of death. The trial did not shake him because he looked only to the Lord. Paul also died having carried the cross in his heart—the endless grace of the Lord who gave up His own Son on the cross. Therefore, he could run for the Lord alone without regret. "Look, I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God." - Acts 7:56

* Victory over trials: Love means not changing your heart even as time passes. Rather, you become stronger to protect them. "So we do not lose heart. Though outwardly we are wasting away, yet inwardly we are being renewed day by day." - 2 Corinthians 4:16

* The beginning becomes everything, not just half: God begins good and righteous works within me and does not abandon them; He completes and perfects them. This is not because I am good, but because the One who acts is good. Therefore, we must trust the Word regardless of circumstances and feelings, and not abandon souls. God's grace is limitless. "Being confident of this, that he who began a good work in you will carry it on to completion until the day of Christ Jesus." - Philippians 1:6

* Spiritual warfare: The prayer of the righteous is very powerful. Therefore, when praying, repentance must follow thoroughly. If you submit to the Lord, you will overcome the enemy. "Submit yourselves, then, to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you." - James 4:7
* Beloved, I pray that you may prosper in all things and be in good health, even as your soul prospers.

* Knowing the truth makes things so easy: Even without knowing that 'all things work together for good,' prayers solve problems. The only difference is that without knowing, you wander in the darkness until you nearly perish, then barely survive, looking exhausted. It's like mistaking a rerun of a Korea-Japan game broadcast for a live broadcast. "And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him, who have been called according to his purpose." - Romans 8:28

* David's path: Just as Jacob had Bethel and the disciples had Galilee, there is only one way to live—to flee to God. No matter how cleverly the world advises you to flee like a bird to the mountains, or says the foundation has crumbled and there is no place for the righteous to stand, ultimately you must return to God. "God said to Jacob, 'Go up to Bethel and settle there, and build an altar there to the God who appeared to you when you were fleeing from your brother Esau.'" - Genesis 35:1 "In the Lord I take refuge. How then can you say to me: 'Flee like a bird to your mountain.'" - Psalm 11:1

* Love over confirmation: When I finally call my parents in my homeland after a long time without contact, they are always happy and worried about me. If this were a romantic relationship, it would be 'get lost!' But a mother does not fall into temptation. Her heart is so full of love to give, not love seeking confirmation.

* A disheartened spirit: One of humanity's and faith's greatest enemies is 'feeling disheartened.' If this feeling of discontent did not exist, the church would have been one, and world evangelization would have been accomplished much earlier. If the devil has 'discontent' as his secret weapon, God's people have 'gratitude' as asymmetric power. Spiritual life is the collision of these two.

* Prayer that overcomes worry: You're told not to worry, yet you hear people say worry comes automatically and it's hard. Of course, worry comes. That's why we pray and make requests, and then the power of thanksgiving comes, and ultimately we receive the answer in peace. Pray enough so that worry does not arise.
* Do not grieve over unfulfilled desires, but be grateful for the sufficient grace already given to you. Hell will transform into a heavenly life.
* The Lord's mercy: He knows my nature and remembers that I am dust. Therefore, He keeps me from death and lets me live to see God's glory.

* The gospel John proclaimed: In the Gospel of John, John tells unbelievers to believe in Jesus. Also, in John's epistles, he exhorts believers to love. In Revelation, he tells people who have faced the last days to 'overcome.' Believe the gospel, gain eternal life, enjoy that eternal life, and be victorious.

* The reason for mercy: God the Father shows us mercy as a father shows to his children. (Psalm 103) Because He remembers. "For he knows how we are formed, he remembers that we are dust." - Psalm 103:14

* Jesus' clear purpose: Jesus had a very clear purpose for coming to this earth—to give life and bring salvation. Apart from the purpose of the cross, Jesus was not concerned with other things. However, when His purpose to die, His reason for bearing the cross, was opposed, He became angry. "Jesus turned and said to Peter, 'Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me; you do not have in mind the concerns of God, but merely human concerns.'" - Matthew 16:22-23

* My symptoms, God's prescription: The Lord acknowledges that we may have temporary sorrow because of our present difficulties. However, He wants us to know that they cannot compare with the glory of God we will see. "I consider that our present sufferings are not worth comparing with the glory that will be revealed in us." - Romans 8:18
* The reason it's unexpectedly difficult: Let go so that the Lord's grace may work within you. (1 Corinthians 15:9-10) Then you will experience Him holding you and carrying you.
* Zeal vs. letting go: Aren't you confused by the potential conflict between these two concepts?

* What we need more: What we need is not confidence in success. It is that even if we fail, we do not deny the Lord but rather glorify Him through faith. Shouldn't we live testifying that 'what I am has become by God's grace'?

* Present choice reveals future results: Everything is given by grace. We respond in faith and receive it. Even what seems difficult and impossible, we must go forward believing in the Lord's almighty grace. Let grace and faith, not I, decide.

* Faith works now: The eunuch Arioch favored Daniel and showed him kindness, not because he was a good person. In ancient Babylonian royal court intrigue and schemes, how could someone who survived and gained status risk his life for the requests of captive orphans? (Daniel 1:10) It was grace. Because Daniel was a person of faith. Faith even works on unbelievers. "Now God caused the chief official to show favor and compassion to Daniel..." - Daniel 1:9

* Faith's Pioneer and Perfecter: When loss is expected to increase, cutting losses early to minimize harm is called 'management.' But knowing you will die and yet hoping for resurrection is called 'faith.'

* The unshaken captain: In a ship about to sink during a storm, experienced fishermen fall into panic. But Jesus is not at all shaken. Not because He is the Son of God, but because as a human who must be weary and sleep, He had 'faith.' "Jesus was in the stern, sleeping on a cushion. The disciples woke him and said to him, 'Teacher, don't you care if we drown?'" - Mark 4:38

* The power to rise again: Resurrection is life that presupposes death. Therefore, the power of resurrection is experienced only by transcending one's utter death. How can there be resurrection without the cross?

* How to give glory: It's easy to think you're giving glory to God when you stand at awards ceremonies with plaques and flowers saying 'Glory to God,' or when you achieve worldly success and go around giving testimonies. But true glory is given when, in circumstances where everything has collapsed, faith does not weaken. "Without weakening in his faith, he faced the fact that his body was as good as dead—and that Sarah's womb was also dead. Yet he did not waver through unbelief regarding the promise of God, but was strengthened in his faith and gave glory to God..." - Romans 4:19-20

* When faith is needed: Faith is not needed when making money, getting into school, or gaining popularity in the world. It is needed precisely when you must 'overcome.' Overcome. Overcome through faith.
* Real collapse: Even in collapse that seems so certain, believe in the Lord's work. This is the situation where faith is truly required and the faith the Lord desires.

* One consoling thing: When I cannot grow weary, one consoling thing is that God has given me a partner in prayer. I have been that for someone, and someone is that for me. When you are alone and sorrowful and your heart breaks, someone prays for you—someone prays for you.
* Faith: Faith is not hearing what you want to hear but hearing the Word you must hear. That is truth.
* Unshakable faith: A person with a firm heart is not shaken by strong winds but has peace. Most look at appearance, but God loves the one who works with the heart.
* True evaluation: Even when disappointing evaluations are given, do not be bothered. More important is that even when receiving good evaluations, do not rely on them. Rely only on the Lord.

* Do not extinguish the fire on the altar: Israel is not defeated by disease or war. Even in idolatry and corruption, they have turned things around and brought about revival. But when the fire on the altar was extinguished, no matter how strong they were, they perished. In any case, for any reason, do not extinguish the fire burning on the altar. The enemy will relentlessly pursue precisely this even at the cost of everything. "The fire must be kept burning on the altar continuously; it must not go out." - Leviticus 6:13

* Limitless grace: Grace is precious but received without payment. What is so valuable cannot be reciprocated. So it is received by grace. Therefore, the only fitting response is simply to be grateful with emotion.
* How to protect what is truly important: If you cannot lose it, you cannot keep it. "'Take your son, your only son, Isaac, whom you love...'" - Genesis 22:12

The characteristics of fallen humanity after the fruit of knowledge are 'blame-shifting' and 'self-centeredness.' Thus we have become beings who pursue a victim mentality and selfish gain even in small matters. The cross begins with acknowledging one's sinfulness and repentance, and nailing one's selfish self to the cross.

* Christian Success Believing in Jesus means being saved from the thought that we must succeed in the world. It is being amazed by salvation in Jesus to the point that success or failure means nothing.

Disappointment and complaining are truly difficult subjects. If we could just pass these two courses well, world evangelization would have been accelerated by about 1,000 years. Let us believe in God and love souls. Then at least we will not be wounded.

Moses, a former prisoner with a fiery temperament; Gideon, with inferiority complex; David, a man with fatal moral flaws—all are God's works. God's power is never limited by human shortcomings. Whether God uses me or not is not a matter decided by my weaknesses, but purely God's sovereignty. He is One who places treasures in broken earthen vessels. We have this treasure in earthen vessels, so that the surpassing power may be of God and not from ourselves. —2 Corinthians 4:7
I often see people boasting about reading the Bible 500 times. That's impressive. Like boasting about eating 500 bowls of rice...
In the Bible, there is not one person who went knowing their own destiny. Their lives were contrary to their desires, but they lived by God's vision, God's level of vision, in God's hands.
The Bible does not disparage personal effort. However, the work of the Lord is not something we do but something that is done to us. It is not accomplished by effort alone but achieved by grace.
* God's Gaze While everyone else notices the capable ones, God receives the truly weak and seemingly worthless and receives glory.
The Bible does not say, 'Do not commit sin, and then you will become a saint.' Instead, it says, 'Because you have become saints, do not commit sin.'
The Bible speaks of life as a vessel. The Lord is asking, not 'Who are you?' but 'What is inside you?' The material of a vessel is not what matters; the contents are what matter.
When I read the Bible, David seems like me and King Saul seems like my adversary. But wise people read it differently. We must learn of our own evil from King Saul. All evil begins with pride.

* There is nothing that cannot be done... If one wing is broken, we should keep the remaining wing and use it with single-minded loyalty to the purpose given us. If the other wing is also broken, we should gather our weakened wings, cover our insufficient face, and look only to the Lord.

The Bible speaks of us as vessels. The sole function of a vessel is to contain. If a vessel does anything other than contain, it is not a vessel. To receive, to believe, to welcome Jesus—this is the vessel's purpose.

Success is having more than others. It becomes sin when we fail to have what was given us. To do it because we are moved is victory. Even if there is nothing left, if it is the Lord's will and we do it, it is obedience and true victory.

* Interpretation is faith. The situation is not making life impossible for me; I am interpreting it that way. Falling leaves fall thoughtlessly, yet they become romance for some and sorrow for others.

We do not grasp the Lord's precious blood to succeed. If we are grasping the Lord's precious blood, regardless of the outcome, we have already won. What comes out when we examine our lips' confession and our heart? The precious blood? Or something else?

* God's Grace God's grace rules over me, makes me new, sets me free from sin and death, transforms me, and fills me completely. Because God is almighty, His grace is also almighty, and I firmly believe He will surely do this for me and my fellow workers.

Saints overcome through confession. It is powerful ability to recover from deep wounds and reverse crises in community. We must speak words that change circumstances and claim through the blood. 'They overcame him by the blood of the Lamb and by the word of their testimony; they did not love their lives so much as to shrink from death.' —Revelation 12:11

When saints experience tribulation, their true nature emerges. They do not lose freshness even in difficulty. Worldly glory and recognition are not their motivation; rather, the strength God supplies sustains them. Those who root themselves deeply in the Word are never overcome by worldly mockery.
A soldier recruited does not entangle himself with the affairs of this life, in order to please the one who enlisted him.
Saints do not avoid trials by luck; they overcome and pass through them. When you acknowledge this from the start, you can overcome boldly.

The word 'saint' means being transformed into holiness. When a barren place becomes holy, the cause of tears and suffering changes. It is because the Lord dwells in the place where I used to live. When the Lord becomes the master, I am bound to hear His Word.

The fire of the Holy Spirit and the Word makes us boldly abandon what we treasured. Abandoning and restraint are power. God's people are like lions grasping their food—they have absolute time and concentration.
The Holy Spirit does not work through any sincerity, threat, or flattery. He works only in the name of Jesus.
By the anointing of the Holy Spirit, the Lord will accomplish it.

* Toil and Heavy Burden If you feel toil and heaviness, it has not come from the Lord. It is not a gentle and humble burden. The reason Jesus' yoke is light enough to give rest and peace is not that we carry ourselves as a heavy burden, but because we die with Him in humility and gentleness and walk together. Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy and my burden is light. —Matthew 11:28-30
Without the fullness of the Holy Spirit, worship and service become mere burdensome religious practices. We will pray earnestly and prepare, and God will give the promised Holy Spirit.

Being filled with the Holy Spirit is not receiving gifts and displaying power. It is confessing and seeking that Jesus alone is sufficient. If it is true that you died on the cross, you will seek only Jesus as your life.

KakaoTalk Question 1: In today's devotion, God said He regretted appointing Saul as king. But wouldn't God, who is omniscient, have known beforehand? Can He feel regret? The word regret seems to imply something unforeseen. When the Bible speaks of God's regret (nacham), in cases connected to human stubbornness and depravity, it means 'to grieve deeply,' 'to lament,' 'to be emotionally wounded.' In this case, it means the Lord patiently endures sinners and waits, but when the time comes when He must discipline them, He grieves and laments. When regret (nacham) is connected to already-suffering people of God, it can mean two things: First, seeing His suffering people, He 'feels compassion for them.' Second, seeing the intensity of their suffering, He 'changes His mind' and withdraws the discipline. This means the opposite of the first case—the suffering people's discipline period has ended and the time of grace has come again. Simple example: 1. The extreme approach that God feels regret because He is not omniscient; 2. The opposite extreme, cold approach that God is omniscient, so even if humans perish miserably, it is settled (destiny); 3. God is omniscient so He has no regrets, but as a God of love, He deeply laments the destruction of sinners as a result of their stubbornness. This expression is theologically called anthropomorphic language. It is a way of expressing God's incomprehensible depth through human emotional expression.

* Equally Important to Passionate Prayer Just as important as passionate prayer is the thought that matches that prayer. The saying 'believe that you have received what you pray for and it will be yours' is not mere lip service but actually visualizing with your heart what you have prayed for, giving thanks and blessing it.
A mature person is sensitive to their own wrongs and sins but insensitive to others' wrongs and sins. Those who are not mature are the opposite.

We live in an age that makes light of sexual temptation. Joseph not only refuses but even goes to prison. What he confessed was, 'How can I sin against God?' We must not take lightly the sin that Joseph guarded so carefully even unto imprisonment. Nothing done in the name of love or youth can ever be rationalized. Saul is a man who perished through rationalization. If you fall into sexual sin, that sin will certainly, certainly find you and destroy you. God will certainly judge with justice. All transgressions are forgiven before the cross. But rationalization bypasses forgiveness. God's wonderful plan is aborted because of arrogant pride, and pride means self-justification, blaming others, and seeking human approval more than God's approval. I pray that we become like Joseph—loving God, repenting, and turning from sin—to save the world and ourselves.

* A Life Not Calculated The world teaches that life is probability. So we waste ourselves in the wrong place—calculating probabilities and imagining outcomes. If things happened according to probability, the Bible would be full of mathematics and statistics. We live by faith. Only my righteous one lives by faith.

* Futile Attack Trials and tribulations can come, growling and tearing at my weak flesh and heart, leaving me broken and wounded. But do not fear. The old me no longer exists, and the new me is in the Lord. Satan only confirms the victory.

We learn more through mistakes than through achievements. So even when directed by the Lord, 'famine' can come. It means we were not misdirected. Every sitting and rising of the Christian, every tear and meditation, is not futile but will be recorded and remembered and bring about good.

Achievement brings joy. For a moment only. So we keep setting new goals. My target is not achievement but His presence and companionship. What I have been apprehended by in Jesus. That experience changes life and causes us to continually pursue that direction. That is revival.

* When Faith Wavers, First Period Begins The Lord seeks people of faith at this time. And He asks, 'Do you really believe in me?' The storm has purpose, and that purpose is to test complete faith. Will you look at circumstances and calculate, or will you stand at the cliff's edge and look only to the Lord whether living or dying? If there is no peace, you are already in trial.
Never agree with worldly counsel. If you never agree to the end, you will live according to God's counsel. No matter what anyone says...

There is a reason for every 'repetition' in the world. There is a reason you keep stumbling in certain temptations. That is your weakness and what needs to be corrected. The subject changes, but the same type of recurring 'emotional state' and 'victim mentality' is your problem, not someone else's. Before God gives great blessings and revival, healing and recovery, He first gives as a gift the repentance that says, 'I am a sinner.' No one sees the Lord without repentance.

In the eyes of worldly people, living by faith looks unsophisticated and incomprehensible. Of course. How can people living on earth understand the lifestyle of one living out heaven? Those for whom credentials matter can never understand the depth of a story. We have His story (history).

* Sweet Anxiety and Sublime Knowledge Worry and anxiety have power and quickly take hold of the heart and circumstances, pulling them in negative directions. They are so strong that it is difficult to refuse; they make us discouraged, unwise, and cause us to avoid situations. There is only one ability to refuse such a strong and sweet request. The sublime knowledge of knowing Jesus transforms all relative values into the level of excrement.

Worldly people chase and rush about, never able to wait. People of faith consistently live the path they must walk. Because they prayed, they can wait. Because they know that He will accomplish it, they have the 'power of waiting.'

We do not make the compromise that worldly people and many believers make (harmony of faith and desire). Why? Because even if we wanted to, mixing these things is fundamentally impossible. When the value and treasure of Jesus come into the heart, everything else becomes worthless and falls away.

* Reason Not to Be Anxious The Lord commands us not to be anxious. Much worry and concern means I am focusing on the part I cannot do. That is where the Lord must intervene and give grace, yet I calculate and imagine. Anxiety is not hearing the Lord's voice but surrendering the heart to Satan. So decisions that follow anxiety are nothing but garbage that leads to regret.

Worldly thoughts and people's thoughts are non-existent thoughts without empathy. They are just thoughts. But Jesus' thoughts make His presence real and cause His people to experience God coming Himself in their longing.
When worldly temptation and trial come, the best and proven way to navigate is to follow the Spirit and make the choice of heaven and live accordingly. Then you will not bear the fruit of darkness.

Those who seek worldly approval will shipwreck their faith. Worldly approval is pride and a great temptation hard to overcome. But those living by faith receive comfort from heaven. Whatever they face, they will not fear and will overcome. —Hymn 434
Worldly wisdom calculates. There is always fear of dangers to avoid. But people of faith believe God's wisdom and are bold. The original Creator finished all calculations before creation.

The world and Satan challenged Jesus about His being the Son of God. They said, 'Prove it if you can.' Living a life of proving things is exhausting. Jesus received the testimony of God the Father, so there was no need to prove anything to the world. My identity, when I remain with the Lord, naturally becomes evident. 'Be still and know that I am God.' I pray you become people who testify rather than prove.

* Superstition and Faith Wanting God to bless a decision I have made is superstition. Choosing what God blesses even when difficult is faith. If you live by superstition, you will certainly perish, but if you live by faith, you will see glory. Aaron took what they handed him and cast it into a mold, and made a calf. Then they said, 'These are your gods, O Israel, who brought you up out of Egypt.' —Exodus 32:4
When the world and darkness approach, I urge you to cry out like this: I am the one who loves Jesus the most!

* The impulse to lose focus is what the devil wants to do—steal, kill, and destroy. There is no reason to agree with such intentions. What tries to make us escape the cross from every angle does so because it knows the consequences better than anyone. The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full. -John 10:10
Even though there are many contradictions in the world and within us, the only way to resolve them is through love.

We must surrender the advantageous positions that worldly people think are favorable and move forward trusting only the Lord. When I cling more to advantages than to the Lord, God tells me to let go. When you release the advantage, you hear less of the reputation you so desired. Yet the question remains: is the Lord alone sufficient for you?

When worldly people speak evil and harsh words, there is no reason to focus on them. No one meditates while holding garbage. (How dirty would it be? What would it smell like?) Just throw it away. Cast away garbage-like thoughts and words and follow the words of truth.

* Grace casts out fear. To fear is to deny that what has come until now is God's grace. The God of Ebenezer who has helped us until now will help us now and will help us in the future. Be at peace before the God who helps. Then Samuel took a stone and set it up between Mizpah and Shen. He named it Ebenezer, saying, "Thus far the Lord has helped us." -1 Samuel 7:12
* Faith means that I do not demand of God what I treasure, but rather I treasure what God treasures.

Testifying to God in the world means relying on the Almighty God in my life. If we live as though God has passed away or retired, the world will misunderstand God that way. The God we believe in is the Almighty God.

There is no neutral zone in the world. Either we are used by the devil or we must be used by God. We must become people whom the devil cannot use and whom God can easily use. The reason Judas Iscariot was used was his heart pursuing vanity (greed, the devil) and his willful ignoring of Jesus' words and warnings. After dipping the piece of bread, Satan entered into him. So Jesus said to him, "What you are about to do, do quickly." -John 13:27 As one of the Twelve, Judas Iscariot, who would betray Jesus, said, "Why wasn't this perfume sold and the money given to the poor? It was worth a year's wages." He did not say this because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief; as keeper of the money bag, he used to help himself to what was put into it. -John 12:4,6
* Gift Today is not something obvious but a special gift from God. That is why the remaining days are precious.

When we encounter urgent matters in the world, or are at peace, or seek to accomplish something with hope, we must first look to the Lord's cross. This is so that all God intends in those matters may be accomplished. For any joy or achievement not intended by God is sin.

* When you love with precious love, everything becomes grace and emotion. A person who lives with the Lord's love knows what to do and feel—they are a prepared person. Being free from being loved, the love they receive becomes overwhelming gratitude and boundless grace. When you have the ability to give, you gain the capacity not to fear or feel awkward when receiving.

In the world, people think becoming rich, graduating from good schools, getting good jobs, and getting married means 'prospering.' But the most important element of prosperity is 'Immanuel'—'Is God with us?' The prosperity of a person of faith is only one thing: 'God is with me.'

The foolish thing in the world is seeking recognition from people. It is as foolish as seeking water in the desert, and those who pursue it grow distant from God and ultimately reap only emptiness. All recognition comes from the Lord.

* Labor and heavy attachment When you abandon God and desperately seek to be loved by the world, let go of that heavy labor and attachment. Come to the humble and gentle Jesus and learn from Him, and you will find rest. Clinging to reactions and fearing them is laborious, heavy, and tiring. But rather than taking up my own desires, following Jesus in love makes for an easy and light yoke. I have already received an eternal and immeasurable love. "Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy and my burden is light." -Matthew 11:28, 30

A capable salesman in the world deeply trusts the product he introduces. At least, he must appear to trust it. Who would sell a product they themselves cannot trust? Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you and your household will be saved. -Acts 16:31 'It's hard even with faith,' 'It didn't work even with faith,' 'Some other method is easier anyway.' Such faith has no work of God. In the Bible, everything the Almighty God has spoken does not change by even a stroke of a pen. The problems we face are enormously complex. Yet the condition for salvation is simple: 'Believe in the Lord Jesus.'

In the world, the one holding the sword is called superior and the one wavering on the other side is called inferior. That is why people work so hard to become superior. Whether you hold the sword or stand at its point, all are in the Lord's hands. Though you stand on the ground, look to heaven.
In the world we may face tribulation, but take courage! I have overcome the world. If the Jesus who overcame the world is within me, I do not fear.

* Give & Give rather than Give & Take If you love and serve seeking to be loved in return, you will soon feel misery. Because you have received immeasurable love, you love. That is why you can be happy.

In the world, when you meet, it seems so wonderful, but when you return, your heart feels empty. It is because of show. You do not become dignified by the thickness of your packaging—rather, you become even more empty.

In the world, effectiveness and efficiency are very important. That is why we use numbers. The Almighty God is not particularly interested in such things. What God sees is our attitude. In the five loaves and two fish, what was needed was not numbers but small dedication and faith.
* Priority determines the outcome The devil tells Jesus to eat and drink. Jesus says to do the word first, then eat. Jesus won through the cross, and the devil perished through the cross.

We so desire worldly achievement and gain it, but we end up distancing ourselves from God. When we grow distant from God, the pressure of holiness and life within us decreases, and eventually worldly turbidity and sin flood in like a tidal wave. Whether believers or unbelievers, those who distance themselves from regular intimacy with God have not escaped habitual sin.

The world is not cut off—it is cut away. When you truly meet a precious person, filth becomes hateful to you. When knowledge of Christ becomes most sublime, all else becomes refuse. Between the world and me, there is no gap, and I see only my Redeemer. That is why worldly things are cut away.
The world urges you toward frustration, but I urge you to hold fast to hope.

The world seeks success and happiness through much learning and research. What such pursuit overlooks—what cannot be seen or heard by worldly eyes—is our relationship with God. True victory and happiness is our relationship with God. It is the most basic and irreplaceable high calling.

The world is a place where simple authenticity competes with elaborate fakeness. That is why disputes about values arise. If we could at least focus on opportunities to prove the value of Jesus—in worship, prayer, and time in the Word—much more salvation would occur. Modern believers are too busy away from the cross.

The world imposes limits on us through media and custom. But people of faith do not work within limits. Working by faith, they overturn all limits. The power to break limits lies only in prayer. Pray alone in your room and pray in unity at worship, letting power overflow.

The world lives as desire leads. Within people there is what emotion desires and what calling leads to—they are different. Living by calling, you will live a different life. Truth is solitary but grows bolder each day.

* More important relationship Relationship testifies to value. That is why we strive so hard to enter relationships we think so valuable and are anxious about them. Worldly relationships prove temporary and empty value; the relationship with the Lord proves eternal and glorious value.
The world gets us to cling to good things. So we miss the best. What looks good will perish; what is best is eternal.
The world tells you to stop. It urges you that enough is enough. It threatens and persuades, making deals using the parts that hurt most. But we cannot do this. Because Jesus is most precious.

The world gives conditions. It says love is because of this and that. When those conditions disappear, the love disappears too. But the Lord loves despite everything. That is why Jesus died on the cross for us when we were still sinners. This is incomprehensible through give and take.

The world is certainly uncertain, and in times of crisis there is no alternative. In moments of absolute peril, Christians save the world. The Lord prepares people of faith with anointing, holiness, and expertise like thick walls of tradition.

Do not fear the world. The one who gives power is God. If God does not permit it, nothing can be done arbitrarily. He is greater than professors, bosses, anyone. Overcome. (1 John 4:4) "You, dear children, are from God and have overcome them, because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world."

If Christians fight by imitating the world's methods, they lose every battle. Christians have God's way, God's wisdom, God's faithfulness. If David had imitated Goliath's armor, he would lose every one of a million battles. What the other side finds important and cannot abandon, I who believe in Jesus can release—that is not a handicap but rather a gift and ability. My wounds become my power. Being maligned becomes the salvation that will rescue them.

* The tree of knowledge is attractive but the humble tree of life gives life. Happiness is not seizing what I desire but beautifully keeping what the Lord permits. Adam took what he desired but became unhappy and feared losing what he was given. The fruit of life is not as attractive as the tree of knowledge—not pleasing to the eye, not pleasant to look at, not desirable for gaining wisdom—yet it leads to life. He grew up before him like a tender shoot, and like a root out of dry ground. He had no beauty or majesty to attract us to him, nothing in his appearance that we should desire him. -Isaiah 53:2,3
