Changing the world is not easy because you must change first. It means we need about three times more of what we now love and forgive. The standard God requires is somewhat high—about three times what it is now.

The reason we look to the world and to people is to be happy. But after some time passes, there is only intense thirst and disappointment. There is nothing there. If we look only to the Lord, everything becomes ordered and we can reach true, balanced happiness.

Living in the world, we often encounter situations that are no one's fault. Such things are inevitable. Stop blaming and arguing about responsibility, step back, and see with the Lord's eyes. Then you will see paths and ways not visible before, in compassion and love. At the Jerusalem Council, the apostles said this: It seemed good to the Holy Spirit and to us not to burden you with anything beyond the following requirements. -Acts 15:28 Simply put, hold to what is essential, what is important, what is core value. We will not demand much. That is the will of the Holy Spirit, the will of God. God does not want us to be misunderstood, but He even more does not want us to misunderstand. Forcing perfectionism is violence to both ourselves and others. Hold only to what is important. And cover the rest with grace. That is what the Lord desired.

* Love is not a luxury but a skill. Looking back, we could love warmly when we secured truly absolute prayer times and determined opportunities for united prayer. Love is not pleasant entertainment; it is the essence that flows inevitably when we deeply encounter God and are filled. Without prayer, love is nonexistent, and only self-centered greed remains.
Seeking liberation from sin while serving the world makes no sense. Choosing the right master will produce the right life.
In the spiritual world that dominates the world, the ultimate martial art is—to continue.
* The nature of hatred and love There is no good hatred. And love has never been bad. Take what has been given, do your very best, give thanks, and love.

The world's rule is that for comfort, you must survive fiercely in competition. If you fall short of that standard, you truly think you will be a failure. But a person living by God's standard lives by the Word. They trust the Lord's help and gladly suffer with Christ rather than enjoy wickedness. They rejoice in suffering with Christ more. When Moses had grown up, he refused to be known as the son of Pharaoh's daughter. He chose to be mistreated along with the people of God rather than to enjoy the fleeting pleasures of sin. -Hebrews 11:24-25
We run not for the glory of the world but for the prize called from above.
* The insight of grace For a hardened heart, everything is troublesome and doubtful, but for one living by grace, everything is an unforgettable memory and gift.

Worldly desire causes change. But it is never satisfied and never brings happiness. That does not mean we should turn away from the world—it is not the Lord's will. We must enter the world. No, we must charge into it. Not to be assimilated into the world's form and enjoy it, but to serve with a distinguished form. We must have light and salt influence, deep and wide. To do that, we must study much. We must earn money with integrity. Then we can serve more.
When people say 'that's just how the world is,' I hope we become people of faith who can say the world is not like that.

* The secret of receiving grace Grace means receiving something undeserved but given with gratitude. That is why I am the most grace-receiving person, and that grace can only be expressed as immeasurable.
Be bold in the Lord who is the Lord of the world. Be reverent before the Lord who is the King of all kings.
It is God who establishes. A life that maintains focus is powerful. The Lord is your focus.
As time passes, you meet stronger opponents. Just a few months ago, enemies challenge more fiercely. That is why I decide to become weaker. In the Lord...

* Ignorant yet brave(?) answer Moses says to Pharaoh, 'Let this people go.' Pharaoh naturally refused and was destroyed by ten plagues. Because he did not know God. If you do not know God, you cannot be free, nor can you obey. Pharaoh said, 'Who is the Lord that I should obey His voice to let Israel go? I do not know the Lord, and I will not let Israel go.' -Exodus 5:2
* More important than misunderstanding More important than being misunderstood is that despite it, I do not misunderstand. The work of Satan ends there, and life and grace begin to work.
Rejoice in hope. Do not become a life shaken by people and the world, but become a person of faith who fears only God.

There was a person who, hearing just a rumor, risked their life to follow Jesus and touched the hem of His garment. The Lord's power flows. But when countless proclamations of the Word are heard and recognized yet remain parched, even when the Lord Himself lays His hand, nothing happens. Matthew 5:21-34 When she heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his garment, for she said, 'If I only touch his garments, I will be made well.'

* Not solution but freedom This problem ends when you are free from it. But you become entangled by the other's words, rumors, even imagining their innermost thoughts. That is not my business, and I should focus on the truth. 'You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.' -John 8:32
Do not worry too much about rumors and words. The world passes away like the flower of grass, but the Word of God remains forever.
A sense of belonging relieves our anxiety. Sometimes it even gives pride. But God has no interest in what you belong to. What matters is what kind of person you stand before the Lord as.

In the midst of being excluded, when self-pity, anger, rejection, and victimhood rush in, David chooses worship. When the presence comes in worship, all negative influences withdraw, and the Lord builds you up through His reign.

* Satan's professional spirit Satan never stirs up conflict or opposes himself. This is not because Satan's nature is gentle or good. It is because Satan is not that foolish. At least in war, he knows what to do. If you don't have faith, you should at least have a business mind. 'If Satan drives out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then can his kingdom stand?' -Matthew 12:26
What you desire reveals your faith. What do you truly want?

* Are you happy? People fight to gain more. Whether it is material or emotional, they fight to gain more, envy, deceive, and are deceived. They even deceive themselves and God through justification. Are you truly happy? If not, something is hidden. Something you do not want to lose, do not want to let go of. Are you happy? You should be happy. Because you have given up so much to claim that place. -Dae Jang Geum http://youtu.be/EJmKyoB8Nxg?t=1m50s
When you lose something precious, your faith expands.

Sometimes you know you are being deceived yet would rather be deceived. This is called self-pity. True faith is needed here. You must think about who called you first. 'You must believe that He exists and that He rewards those who earnestly seek Him.'

* Why God becomes strong when we are weak God uses the weak. Because He transforms and uses them. A coward became an apostle, and a persecutor and pursuer became a grace-filled person seeking only the Lord's honor, whether alive or dead. Transformation is glory, that is why.

Worshiper, not customer Do not act like a customer ordering at a restaurant before the throne of grace. Rather, you must humble and deny yourself endlessly, rely on the blood of the cross and the guidance of the Holy Spirit, and receive the Lord. The throne of grace is not a place to fill desires and seize, but a place to let go and be lowered to be exalted.

Despite having nothing in my hands, I believe in Jesus, rely on the merit of His blood, and can pray to Him—what great comfort is this? It is not only peace of mind but an amazing grace manifested in our actual lives.
Not wanting to lose, hating senseless loss is contrary to the spirit of the cross. Like Jesus, when asked to go one mile, go two, and rather preach the Gospel and bless.

* Dear saints, were you greatly startled? The patient's family may be startled, but the doctor is not. Even if the patient is one's own family, this must be so. Do the work of saving lives and entrust everything to God. Panic comes from a place that is not my responsibility. 'Do not fear, for you will not be put to shame; do not be dismayed, for you will not be disgraced.' -Isaiah 54:4 (New Translations)

Solomon sought wisdom in his youth, but in his later years sought truth. If wisdom brought excellence, truth from the midst of all success shows me hidden vanity before the Lord and turns me back. True blessing is not denying the Lord, obeying, and living.

Solomon's excellent wisdom is 'the listening heart' (lev shomea). The wisest person is one who hears God's voice. Not analytical intelligence but a heart that can listen. 'Give your servant a discerning heart to govern your people and to distinguish between right and wrong.' -1 Kings 3:9
When Solomon seeks power for the community, God gives wisdom. When the magician Simon seeks power for popularity, he is cursed. The motive I pursue tells me who I am. Who are you?

Toil, time, and lack of proper recognition and evaluation—this is called 'waste.' Avoiding this waste is human nature. But without this waste, I would not have been saved. The most holy waste—God's Son Jesus Christ giving His life in place of creation bound up in sin.
Come to me, all you who are weary and heavy laden.

* Different destination means different role Tell the devil this: You do your work of envy, division, cunning, and lust. I will do my work. 'Whether I live or die, Christ will be exalted in my body.' -Philippians 1:20
If the retreat is a great success, there is nothing for the cell leader to do. Because grace covers everything. Is it hard, leader? Receive grace and give grace.

* What do people live by? Without God's grace, we cannot live. That is why there are two things to do: believe and acknowledge that without God's grace, everything is futile work, and pray earnestly looking toward it. So people live by grace, and the method is believing and praying. Pretty easy, right?
* Why professionals are shaken Even a seasoned warrior of faith who shook the gates of hell can collapse. When Basic is ignored. Use your faith.
* Grace that overwhelms disappointment and injustice You live by faith but receive evaluation from people without faith. There is no need to be disappointed because God's grace is incomprehensible.

A moment seems like nothing. But to a person of faith, every moment is precious. When you do your best in each moment, ordinary times of trusting the Lord gather to form a glorious day like a huge wave.
You give long time to guard the moment. A brief meeting with God's people is better than a thousand days in the world.

Pure gold does not need plating. Only fakes get plated. A person who discovers they are a jewel in the Lord does not lie to prove their worth, nor does he envy. Because he is a jewel life that needs no plating.

* Why human recognition is not needed Because you see through situations with truth. Jesus did not entrust himself to them, because he knew all people. He did not need any testimony about mankind, because he knew what was in each person. -John 2:24-25
When God requests obedience, it is not oppression but protection of us.
There are times when it is difficult to make the decision to obey. There are times when you fear letting go of what is familiar. But the Lord says He will walk with you through it.

For the obedient soul, the light of that revelation becomes ever clearer. Because when God reveals His will, He shows it 'as a command requiring immediate obedience.' When you obey one step, you will see only the next step. That is all you need to see. When Paul was converted in Damascus, the Lord did not tell him he would become a great apostle, an incomparable missionary, the greatest theologian. He only said, 'Get up and go into the city, and you will be told what you must do.' -Acts 9:6 When you walk the path of obedience, you come to know God's will for your life.
* The victory that overcomes temptation The power of the blood. You discern the enemy's schemes, are not deceived, obtain freedom, and gain victory over temptation.

When you obey, God's voice is heard clearly. Those who would do God's will, namely those who would obey, discern what is God's teaching and word. 'If anyone chooses to do God's will, he will find out whether my teaching comes from God or whether I speak on my own.' -John 7:17

Drunkenness and debauchery, and the world is full of all corruption. God prepares a new people for new work. Just as Abraham built an altar in faith and called upon the 'Lord' in an age without fear of God, we too must call upon the holy name of the Father in this land called London. The hope of this nation begins with my repentance.

* God's grace Without what the Lord gives, you have nothing. Do not focus on what is lacking; give thanks for what is given. Whether great or small, God gave it because of life and intended to use it for good together. 'No one can receive anything except what is given them from heaven.' -John 3:27

In schools, workplaces, and communities full of drunkenness and debauchery, let us proclaim the sovereignty of the Lord. That too is the Lord's dominion and still ruled land. I pray that we declare and raise our hands in prayer. The gateway where the Lord's rule begins is the church, but also the gateway through which that rule extends to the world is the church. Let us proclaim and pray that God's kingdom comes to workplaces, schools, homes, and communities.
When it was not easy but you did not give up, you see the Lord's glory. That glory is transformation.

Before the great and small challenges that are not easy, you must come out to the place of prayer. So the demons that dwell in the neighborhood should have this nickname: 'Don't touch her; if you do, she prays.' Therefore, become a saint who gives the devil depression. -1/3/2010
* Gratitude All of this was done by God's grace and always an undeserved love. You confirm that love at the cross.

Having a smartphone does not make you smart. Rather, it is easy to become a foolish life without concentration. When you exercise self-control, you become smart (wise). There must be self-control in all things.

A person who boasts about himself is foolish. Because after 10 years, he will not have changed. Because he sees others' faults and does not perceive his own beam. Hearing what you want to hear and seeing only what you want to see is human nature, and seeing what the Lord wants is faith. You will be freed only after receiving the Lord's recognition. 'Let the one who boasts boast in the Lord.' For it is not the one who commends himself who is approved, but the one whom the Lord commends. -2 Corinthians 10:17-18
If you do not choose your own actions and only react to people your whole life, the vigor of your life weakens.

* The ability to take comes from the authority to let go. Those who cannot let go have no right to take. 'I have authority to lay down my life and authority to take it up again. This command I have received from my Father.' - John 10:17,18

Only Jesus can say 'Peace' to trembling people behind locked doors, while those who killed their teacher boast with confidence. In this moment I face, Jesus is the only One who can speak peace over my life and soul.
It is not by flipping your style or personality that good results come. When oil is poured over your insufficient self as you are, then power is manifested.

Stress removes margin and casts a spell that says 'I cannot solve this.' Problems appear larger and my self-worth diminishes. Christians need humor. And we need friends like that. Alone we fall, but together we can endure. Delusion enlarges problems through imagination and we suffer from what doesn't exist. Let us look to the Lord who calls things that are not as though they were.
* The reason for doing your best: Not doing your best leads to regret. Because today could be your last. The best you can do is to 'love.'

In sports, what matters is controlling the flow of the game. King Nebuchadnezzar tries to control the situation by threatening with a furnace seven times hotter. Breaking these worldly challenges requires: 1. 'Faith' that the Lord saves, and 2. 'Sacrifice'—the cross—to remain unchanged even if He does not. When you trust the Lord completely and bear the cross saying 'let me die,' you experience the Lord overwhelming your enemies and controlling circumstances. God has put all things under the authority of Christ and has made him head over all things for the benefit of... God has put everything under the control of Christ. Ephesians 1:22
* Fate: When you fear the Lord, you need not fear fate. You fate, bring it on!
* Under control: The evidence of controlling circumstances is 'gratitude.' When you give thanks, you will not be controlled but will control.

A blind man lost his way on a familiar path. The tree that helped him locate his position had been cut down. After wandering for hours, he received help from someone. He spoke warmly and tried to express gratitude, but the other person thanked him instead. 'Actually, I was about to commit suicide on that bridge earlier, but meeting you and walking while talking gave me courage to live again. I am grateful to you.'
* Difficulty: The greatest difficulty must be given for it to become a test. That is why the Lord asked Abraham for Isaac, not Sarah.
I offer my time, heart, and soul. If it were for myself it would be futile, but if it fulfills the Lord's purpose it will be glory.
Those who make time: those who wait for God, those who make the Lord their master and entrust all problems to Him—upon them the wind of the Holy Spirit blows.
God hears even the brief prayer when we pause and close our eyes for a moment. Prayer changes life. Because it changes my heart.
* Be faithful unto death: Faithfulness ensures that time is not wasted.
As time passes, memory fades. But we love Jesus more. Because we think of Him constantly.

Things we regret as time passes include loving less. Another is being stingy in expressing love even though we loved. In our youth given to us, may we love without regret. I hope for love that can share pain and tears together, love that encourages one another and prays for each other.

* The attitude of receiving curses: King Saul feared people, so through religious ceremony he was sensitive to the people's response. David feared God, so he did not react to the curse of man. David said to Abishai and all his officials, 'If even my own son seeks my life, how much more this Benjamite! Let him curse, for the Lord has told him to.' - 2 Samuel 16:11
Joy does not come from possessing many things or from people's recognition. The greatest joy is living with the Lord. That is the treasure and the pearl.

* Why rejection is not fearful - Psalm 3:5,6: Even if all the world turns away from me, I am not afraid because the Lord upholds me. I lie down and sleep; I wake again, because the Lord sustains me. Though an army besiege me, my heart will not fear. - Psalm 3:5,6
It has begun. The season of the Lord's power.

* Boasting in weakness: I do not have sufficient grace because I somewhat manage the trial. I have enough because the Lord's grace is sufficient. The Lord has given abundant grace, and His power will work perfectly in my weakness.

Psalm 1 says the blessed person does not follow, does not stand, and does not sit. Blessing is not achievement but attitude and position. Keeping your place and standing as the Lord directs is blessing. Blessed is the one who does not walk in step with the wicked or stand in the way that sinners take or sit in the company of mockers, but whose delight is in the law of the Lord, and who meditates on his law day and night. - Psalm 1:1,2
This night Psalm 23:4 keeps me calm. Church barbecue fills my belly, and I write one thing down. ^^

Through the Psalms, David confessed that what pained him most was not betrayal by blood relatives or abandonment by close friends. It was distance from God. For David, who fled from King Saul for over ten years, God was his strength, ability, and hope, making Him the most important in his life. As a king and poet, this truly broke his heart. 'Do not forsake me, O Lord; do not be far from me, my God.' - Psalm 38:21

* Insufficient me, yet sufficient grace: In the midst of extreme suffering, just before liberation, there is something we realize. Through that realization, bitterness and defeat vanish, and suffering transforms into glory. He said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.' Therefore I will boast all the more gladly about my weaknesses, so that Christ's power may rest on me. - 2 Corinthians 12:9

The purpose of trials is not to bring shame but to rely on the Lord before trials and achieve victory. Through this long tunnel, God sees us not as evidence but rejoices in our wounds. 'Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a great cloud of witnesses, let us throw off everything that hinders and the sin that so easily entangles. And let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us, fixing our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and perfecter of faith.' - Hebrews 12:1-2

I pray intensely for those members taking exams, assignments, and projects. Pour sincerity and passion, wisdom and ideas into them. Let them be assured of the Lord's love and not be afraid. (1 John 4:18) Add inspiration and ability in their practice, study, and work. (Exodus 31:1-11) Let them reveal the Lord through example and excellence in company and society. But Lord, if possible, I personally request that much overflow from where they study. ^^;

* True freedom comes when you know the truth. The free walk through storms with confident, peaceful expressions. Be free from attachments, fear, and anger. God has never failed and cannot fail. God is truth.
Do you face trials and obstacles? Break through them with prayer. Trust in the faithful and almighty Lord. He will help you at dawn.
When facing trials, we think it is because the other person is evil or I am weak. No. The fundamental cause of trial is not believing in the Lord's work. Unbelief is the cause.

* Why breakdown comes: Some people smile but anxiety shows through. Such people inevitably face breakdown soon, lack ability to explain, so they disappear or wear masks and project the source of negative emotions onto others. The farther from God, the more they seek to guarantee their existence through 'self-satisfaction' and 'relational validation,' and are always anxious. If you truly believe in what Jesus did on the cross, you will not experience constant breakdown from vain pursuits but will live in 'glory' with unashamed hope. Love never fails. Love never ceases. - 1 Corinthians 13:8

He said to keep watch and pray so you do not fall into temptation. But what if you don't pray? Don't worry. Soon the rooster will crow twice. Of course, you will realize you have already fallen into temptation.
The reason for trial is not that the situation is evil. Simply put, it is because you do not love the Lord most.

Trials separate the precious from the worthless. They reveal the truth of what we said during times of grace. The claim 'I am satisfied with only the Lord' will be validated in trials. Whether it was false or true, you will know when you walk through trials.

When trials come, do not panic but think, 'Now it is time for God to work.' There are two truly absurd cases: 1. You go to a hospital because you really hurt, but you feel no pain before the doctor, and 2. Your computer breaks so you call an engineer, but it works perfectly. In professional terms, we say 'going crazy and jumping eight feet.' What is not exposed cannot be fixed.
A trial makes the obstacle before me appear larger. Look to the great God. Seeing the Lord as great is prayer.

* The reason He called me: He did not call me to do well. That is the Lord's domain. He called me only to be faithful. If you have been faithful and sincere, you have nothing to be ashamed of. The one entrusted with something is expected to be faithful. - 1 Corinthians 4:2
Pay no mind (it's not your business) and look only to the Lord. You will receive power and become a witness. Faith is simple.

* Why we are not ashamed: Paul was not ashamed. Because he had a hope that was not shameful. And hope does not put us to shame, because God's love has been poured out into our hearts through the Holy Spirit, whom he has given us. - Romans 5:5

* Why we hate it to death but are not afraid: Poverty is detestable but not feared. For Abraham, loss of relationship like Isaac can be heart-wrenching pain but also not frightening. Only because we 'hope' in the Lord and enjoy 'security' under His rule and reign.

* Love pierces through fear: We are not tempted by what we do not fear. We do not fear what we do not hope for. Thus vain hope is the source of fear of loss and rejection. Those who hope only in the eternal fear only God's departure and are not afraid or tempted by anything else.
* The initiative of temptation: It is not trials coming that make us afraid. We fall into temptation because we are afraid. Job says that what he feared came upon him.
The bridegroom's friend who stands and hears the bridegroom's voice is filled with joy. I am filled with this joy. - John 3:29
Faithful God, looking to You, have mercy on us.
* Dust-like me: By the Lord's grace I have lived through today. What more can this infinitely insufficient and weak one desire? The Lord's grace has been enough for me.
From my experience of faith, the glorious path we so desire to walk together always seems to have stragglers. Yet I dare to hope: the desire to reach that place without losing even one person.

There is nothing as fair as faith. This is because there is judgment, and furthermore, that judgment is just. Faith is not something you cannot do because you lack money, nor because you lack air conditioning or even a building. You simply cannot do it because your heart is absent. If your heart is present, you will see the Lord's glory even on a deserted island.

* The Root of Fear. Fear exists because I am afraid my expectations will be shattered. That expectation is to be accepted and not rejected, but in trying to avoid and defend against emotional pain, people choose anger, frustration, depression, obsession, addiction, or isolation, and ultimately become even more lonely. When you change your expectations, fear changes too. Paul's expectation was that 'whether I live or die, Christ will be exalted in my body.' Before this expectation, all trivial values become garbage and filth. Just throw away the garbage. 'According to my earnest expectation and hope, that I will not be put to shame in anything, but that with all boldness, Christ will even now, as always, be exalted in my body, whether by life or by death.' - Phil 1:20

Living a life of faith is not easy because it requires perseverance. True ability is in perseverance. Good scores one or two times have not yet been validated. Love is patient and long-suffering. The devil's attacks through relationships without love cannot last long.

Living a life of faith is not something you squeeze out. It is something that overflows from fullness. There is no power other than the Holy Spirit of life to deliver us from this body of death. Scheming must stop. There is no path of faith without a price.

How to live a life of faith well: 1. Set your goal (Jesus Christ). 2. Run toward it. 3. Dismiss petty resistance and suffering. 4. Dismiss even major resistance and suffering. 5. Be gripped by Christ. Hallelujah!
* Free from Worry. Only Jesus can touch my heart. This is not a problem to worry about but a problem to be healed. So now let it go and be at peace. The Lord reigns.

Living a life of faith well is like swinging on a trapeze at the circus. You boldly leave your own swing, fly through the air waiting, and are caught by your partner's swing. There's really not much to do. You leave, wait, and are caught.

Living a life of faith is simple: 'beholding' and 'running.' Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a great cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight and the sin which so easily ensnares us, and let us run with endurance the race that is set before us, looking unto Jesus, the author and finisher of our faith. - Heb 12:1-2. To run, you must not let your vision be diverted elsewhere. That's why you must look to Jesus, who ran ahead of us. (In Heb 6:20, the Lord is spoken of as the forerunner.) That is the secret. To overcome the law of sin and death, the law of the Spirit of life must become stronger. This becomes possible when you are under grace. When we look to the Lord and run, we are under grace. The answer is found in Romans 6:14: 'For sin shall not have dominion over you, for you are not under law but under grace.' - Rom 6:14

The most heartbreaking thing while living a life of faith is not loneliness, being forgotten, or being hated. Rather, it is that the Lord's glory is obscured because of me. With a repentant heart, obeying what I have asked the Lord is the way to follow Jesus, though I am an inadequate disciple.

Faith certainly has an element of warfare. You must not fear or avoid fighting. Presupposing that you will actively fight against sin and darkness, enjoy peace in the Lord. Do not be discouraged or run away, but overcome evil with good.

The most difficult thing in faith is doing nothing. From the perspective of unbelief, prayer seems like doing nothing. But when you lift your spiritual eyes, the greatest and most significant work is happening in the heavens.

The most difficult thing in faith is standing still. When you acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty and remain still, the Lord makes you know that He is God and displays His glory. Let us stop our labor and enter into the ability to abide. So that worship becomes true worship.

Faith is a relationship. And the most important relationship is with the Lord. Relationship is power, wisdom, and everything. That is why interaction with the Lord at every step is important. I must advance relying on Him, not on my own wisdom and ability.
Faith is not something you have to live; it is something you cannot help but live.

Faith is the life of a sojourner. A sojourner leaves behind their comfortable established home and, amid the pain of broken relationships, clings only to the Lord. Not settling down, not being greedy, and continuing to advance through the wilderness.

* The Power of Cleansing. To chase away flies or pests, you clean up the garbage. But some people cover the garbage with a blanket, spray perfume, and say there's no problem. Everyone has bitter roots. The question is whether you will wear a mask over it or whether you will clean it. 'See to it that no one comes short of the grace of God; that no root of bitterness springing up causes trouble, and by it many be defiled.' - Heb 12:15
Faith is not hard labor. It begins when you let go of your efforts. My defense, my pride, my reputation, my zeal—when these are frustrated, only then can the Lord help me.
Faith is concentration. Looking only to the Lord so that nothing else catches your eye.

* The Cause of Internal Conflict. After sin entered, it did not just produce thistles and thorns on the earth. The human heart also became dry, longing to be loved but unable to be satisfied. That is why people desire and envy. When you crucify the self's desire to become god, you will experience salvation coming to all areas of my life where I seek loneliness to avoid fear, addiction, and rejection. 'What is the source of quarrels and conflicts among you? Is not the source your pleasures that wage war in your members? You lust and do not have; so you commit murder. You are envious and cannot obtain; so you fight and quarrel. You do not have because you do not ask. You ask and do not receive, because you ask with wrong motives.' - James 4:1-3

Expertise is required even in guiding faith. Just because someone has handled meat for thirty years at a butcher shop does not mean they can perform heart surgery. How much more so when one is entrusted with souls—one must treat life with prayer and tears.

Losing the passion of faith is a disaster. One of two reasons is losing focus: 1. not fearing God and not delighting in Him forever (Paul called this 'refusing to have God in their knowledge'), and the other is 2. not becoming one as the Father and Son are one. When you stake your life on revering the Lord—one of God's two desires—and become the hands and feet of the Lord to bless and seek your brothers and sisters, God will not abandon you. If you live and die not for your own glory but for the Lord's eternal name, there is no reason God would not support you.

The measure of faith is not the quantity of what I have accomplished. It is the distance—'how close am I to God?' Power and wisdom come from distance and relationship. Without relationship with the Lord, no matter how much work you do, the Lord will say 'I never knew you.' - Matt 7:23
* How to Hear God's Voice. Deny yourself, humble yourself, do not follow greed, and find the cause in yourself. Of course, doing the opposite will cause you to hear another voice.

Spiritual maturity in faith is when the center shifts from self-centered interpretation to the Lord's interpretation, from selfish requests to loving consideration. When you stand before the Lord at every moment, this will happen.

It is very easy for faith to improve, especially when you take on a position. However, it is truly difficult within the family. True faith is when fire is kindled from the hidden depths within me, and I am purified.

* The Way of Love 7. I loved the meeting called Ebenezer, where I came to believe in Jesus, so much that I firmly decided, 'I must live for this gathering.' But later I was misunderstood, and in the community I loved, I was accused of being 'demon-possessed' and came forward and apologized(?) without knowing why. And as I walked down alone from the prayer house that everyone had left in groups of twos and threes, I changed the hasty decision I had made. 'Before, I said I would live for this gathering, but from now on, I will die for this gathering I love.'

Improving in faith means getting to know the Lord better. The God David experienced is One who has no misunderstanding of me, knows all things, and it is spiritually impossible to leave the Lord beyond space and time. Ultimately, realizing that each moment I breathe is a moment held in the Lord's hand, I come to treasure His word and examine myself and seek His guidance.
Saying faith improves is saying the subject of suffering changes.

Receiving blessings as a believer does not mean exemption from various challenges and trials. Rather, it means experiencing the Lord who is greater than such problems. The Lord's blood is thicker than my pain, and the Lord's grace is greater than my problems.

* The Way of Love 6. I love with all that I have. And what I do not have, I love through prayer. And I place all expectations and hopes only in my Father in heaven. Only He will record and judge all of this.

The most important thing for a believer is humility. When Joshua took Jericho and saw the small city of Ai and momentarily took off the garment of humility, when Samson's hair was cut and the Spirit of God departed from him, they simply thought God was working as before. A proud person is someone who does not know that God's power has departed from them. Bow down.
* Mission. It is doing what God told you to do where He told you to stand. And it is being faithful and continuing until the final whistle blows. Everything else that happens is not my concern.

Believers struggle because they fear loneliness and difficulty and try to avoid them at all costs. So what if I'm a bit lonely? So what if it's a bit difficult? I dislike loneliness and don't welcome difficulty, but that doesn't mean I'm afraid of them. If the Lord is exalted within me, even death is gain.

It is very natural for believers to be wounded in a world of lust and rebellion. Even Lot's righteous spirit was grieved. But it is more important to come before the Lord and be healed and restored from that natural wound. Do not try to win in battle but win the war. To win the war, you must be continuously supplied. This war is not mine but belongs to God.

* The Way of Love 4. If you love with the Lord's love, you will be truly joyful. So joyful that you do not look for or expect anything else. That love is real. If you desire much, that is not love but simply an investment you have made and managed. Love is this: 'By this is love perfected with us, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment; because as he is so also are we in this world. We love, because he first loved us. In this is love made perfect with us, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment.' - 1 John 4:10
Believers worry that their lives will become a loser. This means worrying that the Lord will make a mistake. What a disrespectful concern.
Religious action can never be a boast. For example, it is like boasting about doing good 300 times. If you really want to boast, you should boast that 'I truly loved.'
If God came in the form of God, we would all have been judged and perished. God came in a human body. That is Jesus, and so we are saved.

Being disappointed always means you had expectations. If you seek what is vain, you will certainly be disappointed. It will wither away soon. Those who pursue eternal things will obtain glory that does not fade.

* The Way of Love 2. It is shameful that I cannot eat like Jesus, cannot sleep like Jesus, cannot pray like Jesus, and cannot weep like Jesus. It is because Jesus extended His hand but was rejected, healed the sick but was ignored, bore the cross but was made a sinner, and loved but was hated.

Saying you are disappointed means your expectations were high. Desire always brings disappointment. If you cannot be satisfied with the Lord and Jesus is not enough, you will be disappointed more often.
Many members are following in real time. ^^ Should we stay up tonight?

* The Way of Love. It is difficult because you try to return only what you have received. Love is when you give and give and give everything, and yet wish you could give more. You are not acknowledged for loving, nor is it recognized by anyone, but the act itself fulfills the purpose, and He asked for it, so you do it. Just as waiting is good even without meeting as its purpose.

In reality, what ability we have does not matter at all. No matter what trial or challenge we face, what matters is not my strength but 'He who strengthens me.' No matter how steep and rugged the mountain, if the One who strengthens me says 'you can do it,' then I can do it without worry or fear. 'I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me.' Receive this word in faith and declare it with boldness.

When tangled problems are unraveled like tight cords, when sin and debauchery overflow and holiness comes forth, and when crises turn into opportunities—this is accomplished when the Lord reigns. Take off your sandals and follow the Lord. That land will be made holy.
When failure comes, everyone is tempted to flee and abandon that place. But as time passes, something becomes clear: that I love the Lord. That failure does not change this fact. 'Do you love me?'
Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. - Matt 5:3
* Sufficient Gospel. The gospel and prayer are sufficient. True gospel always brings change.
Just as the heart beats, what must be continually confirmed is the presence of the Lord within me. Without His presence, there is NOTHING.

A person with a steadfast heart and a person who trusts in the Lord are the same person. Those who commit their hearts to the Lord at every moment and every second are held by the Lord and guarded with peace. 'Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee: because he trusteth in thee.' - Isa 26:3
* Reasons for Happiness Are not all these things to be grateful for rather than complaining about?
Judgment is fearful. However, grace is the only power that can overcome judgment. When sin is washed away by grace, the body and soul, and failures are restored and come alive again.

Judgment begins in the house of God. For the Almighty God who called Gideon, military power, the enemy's circumstances, and weapons systems could not be issues. He desired only to first break the idols in Gideon and his household. God's people must become pure so that He can transform the world. God's concern is with 'me.' The moment I change, Esau and King Saul who tormented me are no longer needed.
The secret to excellence that transcends ten years is obedience. When you attempt obedience, a voice is heard, change occurs, and things are accomplished that a nation cannot do.

* Reasons for Peace and Boldness The love of God is incomparably stronger than the devil's instigation and schemes. Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword? Yet in all these things we are more than conquerors through Him who loved us. (Romans 8:35-37)
While proclaiming the gospel of the cross, what I constantly feel is that without fail, the work of life, recovery and healing occur. @Kingston University
When the cross becomes dim, the Lord allows loss. When we lose what we cherish, we come to know who the true owner is. Loss is not about losing something, but about making what to focus on clear.
* A Light in Darkness I pray that in the darkness, only the Lord becomes the lamp of my hope.

The cross is not a game. The cross is not a hobby. The cross is the highest conclusion God can give and an eternal solution. If you hold fast to the cross, you can embrace all, and by the power of the cross, you love all. There is no problem that cannot be lifted before the cross. If there had been a problem the cross could not solve, the Lord would not have borne the cross from the beginning. The spirit of the cross is 'I cannot bear to see you die; even if I abandon everything, you shall live.' How can those who behold the cross wander? How can they put emotions before their calling? The cross is not frivolity. It is truth that takes precedence over every decision and precedes every response. Resolution at the Lord's level—before the cross there is nothing impossible and nothing to fear. Outside the cross there is nothing to boast of. Therefore, I will love all that has been given to me to the end. And I will not lose even one person.
* The Victory of Faith Do not be weak but use your faith. Continue to look forward and advance in faith to the end.
Do not cling to things that perish, but fix your heart on the Lord's love, which is unseen but eternal. The Lord comes.
* Only You The one who touches the heart is only God. That fact is such great comfort and peace to me. O Lord, there is none like You; You are great and Your name is great in power. (Jeremiah 10:6)

Oh, by God's grace, I cannot understand why He redeems this useless servant. What I believe and depend on is that the Lord, who knows all my circumstances, will always protect me—I am certain of this. (Hymn 310) I really don't understand—the Lord's grace.

Ah, Seo-young said Gyeong-yong's friend was ordering chicken, so I rushed over, but when they heard I was coming, all the young adults scattered like wind. I think I need to improve my relationships. Please have mercy on me. Sob...

A baby is peaceful and safe because it is completely dependent on its mother. What I must always confirm is whether I am dwelling in the Lord. Then I will bear fruit, and in Him who gives strength, nothing will be impossible.
I have no regrets because there is nothing I spare. Holy consumption occurs. Because He called and sent sinners, I praise the Lord with my last breath. The chief of sinners received great grace.
Son, what is your desire? Lord, my desire is to love You more. T.T

* The Most Important Question One can tell whether people truly love or are merely going through the motions. True love shows me where I came from and where I am going. That is why the Lord asked Peter, among many questions: 'Do you love Me?' Because it is truly important. We know that we have passed from death to life because we love the brethren. He who does not love abides in death. (1 John 3:14)
Beautiful places become more beautiful because of beautiful people. Even those with poor memory wish to keep this moment eternally.

* How Not to Be Anxious To not be anxious, you must believe in the Almighty God. But the question is whether that Almighty God is interested in my life and problems. That is why He tells us also to believe in Jesus. That Almighty God has come as Emmanuel, not as a stern Judge but in human form, beside us. Do not let your hearts be troubled. Believe in God; believe also in Me. (John 14:1)

As it says in Amos 3:3, 'How can two walk together unless they are agreed?' (The Correct Version), the secret of Enoch's walking with God was that Enoch's heart and God's heart became completely one. When God looks at people all over the world and sees that they all commit sin and give off a stench of evil, causing Him distress, but when He sees only Enoch, who obeys God's Word and remains uncontaminated by the sinful world, His heart is pleased. At the age of 365, He did not let him die but took him up alive into God's kingdom. (Genesis 5:24, Hebrews 11:5-6)
Do not be anxious about anything. I will prepare and guide you.
Even with nothing and powerlessness, there is something we can do. It is to forgive. It is to give thanks. It is to bless. That is what we do best.

* Wilderness Trial 2—Subtle Challenge, Yet Complete Response 1. What the devil persistently tempts Jesus with is to 'prove Himself.' (If You are the Son of God...) 2. The anxiety of not filling the assurance of being loved (bread) and being confirmed through people's responses (recognition). A life that I must confirm is anxious, and ultimately leads to disappointment, anger, and despair. That is why a tested life wants to give up, run away. 3. Jesus did not come to prove Himself or gain recognition, but to come to love with the cross and give His life. So He does not respond with His feelings and emotions but answers with truth like this: 'It is written...' Be victorious by truth. The gospel has never been defeated. If You are the Son of God... (Matthew 4:3, 6) And Jesus answered Him, It is written... (Matthew 4:4, 7) Be of good cheer; I have overcome the world. (John 16:32)

Believe that no one can harm you. It is only if you do not harm yourself. Just do not be deceived by Satan who brings fear. Believe that truth clearly and certainly. If you earnestly do what is good, who is he who will harm you? (1 Peter 3:13)

No matter how rough and hard the work, if you bear it with love, there is no wound. All achievements and abilities will eventually pass away and disappear, but love is eternal. It is foolish to stake your life on means. Stake everything on the purpose. Love is an unfading purpose, a meaning the Lord recognizes.
To those who are discouraged before a bleak reality that cannot be resolved no matter how hard they try, I would like to say this: That is why Jesus came.

* True Faith Believe that what you ask for, you have received. Do not doubt but believe that it will come to pass. Especially believe that the Lord rules over this complex situation with His power and authority. Therefore I tell you, whatever things you ask when you pray, believe that you receive them, and you will have them. (Mark 21:24)

No matter how beautiful a being is, if it does not maintain its place, it decays, rots, and stinks. But if one takes on the position one should hold and the attitude one should have, it becomes a truly beautiful life.
* Overcoming by Truth Draw the sword of the Spirit, truth. Reveal the true identity. Then they will flee without being chased.
No matter how good the food, you cannot eat it when you are full. That is why you must not be satisfied. A poor person always longs. Such a person is blessed.
No matter how excellent a person is, there is absolutely no possibility. The only alternative is Jesus Christ. Flourishing without Jesus is a prelude to destruction. Romans 3:21

No matter what difficulty comes, do not be discouraged. God does not give up or make mistakes. This trial you face now comes with strength and wisdom to endure it, and He has also prepared a way of escape. If you set your attitude right before the Lord, all things will work together for good. Faith overcomes all things.
No matter how hard, difficult, or even impossible it seems, believe that God's faithfulness is working. The Lord never gives up or makes mistakes.

A thoughtless word spoken casually can wound. Therefore, what is important is not to be wounded by such words. And even more important is to give encouragement in difficult moments that could cause collapse—encouragement to stand.
Happy birthday, Father~ I sincerely love you and am always grateful. ♥

To rejoice in what the Father desires and to shed tears at what the Father grieves—that is the highest happiness. Whatever the Father does not give must be rejected, no matter how good or attractive it appears.

* Why It Is Hard It is hard because you seek to be loved. The calling in the Bible is to love. Love one another as I have loved you. When you love, it becomes heaven, but the moment you seek to be loved, it turns into hell.
The Father loves a lacking son more than a perfect servant. May you have the authority not to become a perfect servant but to be a lacking child.

If the Son glorifies the Father, the Father will glorify the Son. The glory the Son gives is the death of the cross, letting go, and the glory the Father gives is resurrection. We can give glory when we bear the cross, deny ourselves, and love. God gives us the new life of resurrection and glorifies us who died in sin. Jesus said these things, and lifting up His eyes to heaven, He said, Father, the hour has come; glorify Your Son so that the Son may glorify You. (John 17:1)
The heart of the Father...

The foolish child who lacks the wisdom to recognize the Father's love will face the misery of a pig in a place without the fruit of the jujube tree given to it. Only then does one come to recognize the Father's worth, repentance comes, and recovery happens. When God lets go, it should feel a bit refreshing. You will deeply feel the benefit of suffering.

Was Abel righteous and Cain wicked? No. Both were sinners born to Adam, a sinner. Abel offered a better sacrifice because he understood that he, as a 'sinner,' must die and go up to the altar, and he came to the Lord relying on His mercy. When we stand before the Lord, we come to know who we are.

* Knowing Whom I Serve as the Object of Joy Living through today, what brought such joy and what caused concern? Even if 7 billion people surround me and hate me, if my worship offered today was accepted by the Lord, that is happiness. The one who sustains me is God.

Though Abel died, through faith he still speaks. What faith can restrain is not death. Pain, emotion, and disappointment cannot restrain faith. Before Jesus Christ, all values and priorities are reset. Even if you lose everything, rise with a burning heart and run forward.
Abraham is led by faith. By faith he achieves intimacy with God—becoming His friend. Faith brings about a better choice.

When Abraham believed the promise, God counted it to him as righteousness. The person who believes the promise is the most righteous. What is God's promise to me? When I believe in Jesus, the eternal Promise, that is the highest 'righteousness' not made by man but imparted. With 'righteousness,' we approach God's throne with boldness.
Abraham offering Isaac is a wonderful blessing of offering his heart. For some people, all of God's Word is burdensome.

In the trial and moment of crisis when Abraham bound Isaac, there was something he believed and confessed. He believed that the Almighty and faithful God would certainly make the child of promise He had given an heir. However, he did not know the specific method. (It seems he believed something like even if Isaac dies, God can raise him—as in Hebrews.) The Lord will glorify me and make me laugh. But He will not do it my way. Do not imagine the method but simply trust.

* The work of the Lord is not about worrying but about crying out to Him. Believe that what you have asked for, you have already received. If you do not doubt within the Lord's will, it will surely be accomplished.

When electricity first came to Ireland, the Irish people were truly amazed and grateful. Because in the darkness, turning on electric lights and lighting candles was truly convenient. It is foolish to live a life that only moves when cornered. Use your faith.

* If you take shortcuts, you end up back where you started. If someone else gets on the bus first and you don't wait for your bus but board theirs instead, that is not something that happens by going along with it—you must come back again. That is why repentance is best when done quickly. There is no point in stubbornly refusing; only you suffer the loss.
If you are still breathing, know that the Lord has given you a mission and strength. Run toward the Lord. Those who have not completed their mission do not die.

* The foundation of boldness: The basis for our boldness is not mind control, but that the Lord has overcome the world, the desires of the world, the temptations of the world, and the world's standards. To reveal this to you: that you may have peace in me. In the world you will have tribulation, but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world. -John 16:33
While praying in the morning, a text message arrives! 'Oppa~ let's eat ice cream ^___^^' Thank you God for today too. Love love <3

There is someone whose phone battery is not charged from morning, leaving only 5%, unable to text and struggling, and there is someone who is fully charged and continuously recharges in spare moments, even charging wirelessly. Be filled.
* My heart, be victorious! The gospel has never lost once. Therefore, a heart that believes the gospel will surely be victorious. Let your heart win. For I am not ashamed of the gospel. -Romans 1:16

Reading Ephesians in the morning, I feel that the phrase 'in Christ' appears very frequently. The two problems of the Ephesian church are: 1. The discord between Gentiles with wrong backgrounds and Jews, and 2. Moral issues. All of God's salvation and all good things are possible only 'in Christ.' And these abilities are accomplished when we think, act, and pray 'in Christ.' How great a phrase is this? 'In Christ...' For reference, chapter 1, verse 10 can be called the theme. The cause and solution are also only 'in Christ...' If you cannot forgive, return again 'to Christ.'

When you are sick and suffering, you should pray to be made even lower. Only then will the hidden darkness in your life that you did not know appear, and light will seep in. This was all to humble you and test you, that in the end He might do you good. -Deuteronomy 8:16

When shown a luxury Mercedes to an African native, they will be amazed. No matter how scorching the weather outside is above 50 degrees, if set to 22 degrees, it always maintains 22 degrees. The navigation screen also operates, and when bored, videos can be watched. So that car is parked in front of the chief's house, and when people walk a long distance and it gets hot, they go in to rest and come back out. This is the tragedy of believers who do not understand grace in detail. Grace is not merely comfort of the heart; it is God's actual, powerful working in reality.
You can love as much as you suffer, and you can suffer as much as you love. Therefore, not just anyone suffers without permission, nor is anyone served without permission.

Sick members, pray for healing. In particular, you must hold on to God's promises and pray. After thorough repentance, ask a person of faith to pray for you. Therefore confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective. -James 5:16
If you do not feel pain, you do not love. Without love, you ignore and just live on.

I hope you do not cause pain. The more you love, the more... you must be sincere and sufficiently specific. We can love God to the extent we know Him, and God is the one who reveals Himself. The greatest tragedy in human history originates from imagination (Gapdol~): imagination.

* The wilderness test: The devil tempts Jesus in three ways: priority, worldly glory, and self-satisfaction—in the form of lust of the flesh, pride of life, and lust of the eyes. But from Jesus's perspective, if summarized, it is one: what does He truly desire? Therefore, the answer is also one: the cross.

Do not repay evil with evil or insult with insult. Rather, repay with a blessing, because to this you were called so that you may inherit a blessing. -1 Peter 4:9 This is the truth that Peter, who drew the sword before the Lord, proclaimed in his later years. When you know who you are, you also know how to live.
You must overcome evil. You must overcome it with good. The goodness that the Holy Spirit dwells within and gives.
Do not be distressed by the prosperity of the wicked. God does not judge by outward appearance but by the love in one's heart.

* The reason for being filled with the Holy Spirit: Not for success. Success is not eternal. Not to avoid failure. Failure is not fatal. Being filled with the Holy Spirit means that a person separated by sin is reunited with God.
Do not lose heart looking only at closed doors. Work diligently at what is open. No matter how harsh, there is always one bosom that loves me. That is the Lord.

* The reason for boldness: When you confess and love, you gain boldness. Since the one who loves you is God, who can condemn you? Who will bring any charge against those whom God has chosen? It is God who justifies. Who then is the one who condemns? Christ Jesus who died—more than that, who was raised to life—is at the right hand of God and is also interceding for us. Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall trouble or hardship or persecution or famine or nakedness or danger or sword? -Romans 8:33-35

* The idols to be burned: An idol is not attractive because we worship before it. An idol is the manifestation outward of coveting that exists in my heart. Such people complain, become impatient, fear, and while saying they worship God, practice dual worship by setting their hearts on their lusts.

* When benefit (利) precedes righteousness (義), that is idolatry. God does not deny bread or loneliness. However, the world's teaching is that one can be satisfied and full with benefit (利). But the Bible says that is not possible. Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness (義), for they will be filled. -Matthew 5:6

Do not retreat saying it cannot be done. Look in faith and attempt it. Even if you sink while walking on water without faith, the Lord will hold your hand and you will walk. That there are no failures means you have never attempted anything. Faith is an adventure.
There is a saying that a doomed person is doomed. At that very moment when someone seems doomed, God holds their hand. What is impossible for humans is possible for God.

How to live a life of faith while being reluctant, how to gain everything without letting go, how to please the Lord while acknowledging yourself without denying yourself—true faith has no such way. There is no other evaluation or method besides the path where the flesh dies and the spirit lives. Everything else is useless.
* Rest is the power of restoration. Also, enjoying rest is itself an ability. Rest comes not through circumstances but only through faith. Only through faith.

* The scope of worry: It is not the Lord's will to worry about what is not something I can handle and have a face full of anxiety. That is a work the Lord must bless, and the time and method are in the Lord's hands.
Take heart. It is I; do not be afraid. Be assured. I pray for you.

* True security: Not the approval of people and the satisfying contentment represented by numbers, but hearing the voice of the Lord and being completely united with God is true security. Therefore, my soul is safe in the Lord.
If you only pursue stability, you will not live the best life but only a second-best life. Take the adventure of faith. ^^
* Your resume: If someone asks, how would you answer? But by the grace of God I am what I am. -1 Corinthians 15:10
The secret to a poor minister doing good work: filling yourself with the Lord alone.

* Knowing yet not being able to do it well is faith. Therefore, there are no experts in faith. Faith is living, and to live it you need not only knowledge but also competence. That competence is His presence and companionship.

* The reason for being overly sensitive: Being overly sensitive to others' gazes is due to pride. Do not stand as the image you must appear to be; honestly acknowledge your true self before the Lord. God will surely respond. Therefore humble yourselves under God's mighty hand, that he may lift you up in due time. -1 Peter 5:6
Do not bring forward anxiety about future matters or worry. The trouble of each day is sufficient for that day. Be sober-minded, and then worry will leave.

* The glorious gospel: The gospel is glorious. The gospel is God's highest and was complete for us. Because Jesus is the gospel. Since He gave Himself to us, no greater evidence exists. I am not ashamed of the gospel, because it is the power of God that brings salvation to everyone who believes. -Romans 1:16

When the future appears dark and you cannot see, do not strain to see. Look only to the Lord who makes a way in the desert within me. The Lord is a refuge for those who flee. You are the beacon, My song in the night.

Before Israel at the Red Sea with the Egyptian army coming, what stood in the way and led the way in the wilderness was the pillar of fire and pillar of cloud—God's protection and guidance. The glory and anointing I must save are not about sustaining and maintaining, but His presence that the enemy and adversary witness and fear, even without advertisement. Look to the highest things.

Pharaoh, the king of Egypt, responded to Moses's request to go according to the Lord's will by demanding more 'production from the people,' making them powerless and creating division between the people and Moses. This is 'Pharaoh's system'—the world's way. Though the world provided the cause, the evil standard it presents within that system makes people envious of one another. Living by that evil standard feels like you will die, but it is deception. If you don't live that way, you will be eliminated, ignored, and become a failure—this is the lie. If you have the mind to give everything and live and die for the Lord, it is nothing. That is why Christians shine brighter and have more peace in crisis situations. A truly good player demonstrates their training in critical situations.

When children do wrong, they skillfully avoid their mother's eyes or close the door. If you do not seek His kingdom and His righteousness, you will have desires but much worry and anxiety that wounds your own heart. If you are in the Lord, what should you fear?
Kids, I'm off today. I'm going to the swimming pool now. With the kids.

Greetings without affection and gestures without soul are merely burdensome. When you receive what the Lord gives, you are truly happy. What the Lord does not give, no matter how much you gather, will not bring happiness at all. Therefore, sharing small things with 'the heart of Jesus' in the Lord is truly happiness.

* God's intention: You must believe that God's intention toward you is to give you peace and hope for the future. But that is an intention you experience when you come and cry out in prayer and seek the Lord with all your heart. 'For I know the plans I have for you,' declares the Lord, 'plans not to harm you but to prosper you and not for evil but for good, to give you hope and a future. Then you will call on me and come and pray to me, and I will listen to you. You will seek me and find me when you seek me with all your heart.' -Jeremiah 29:11-13
Affection reveals who that person is. What you pursue is your essence, and you will meet eternity where you pursue your object of pursuit.

* Rules of engagement: When you do not know what to do or how to handle something, follow these rules: 1. Regarding matters of concern, pray and make requests abundantly, but do so with thanksgiving. 2. When you present your concerns before God as specifically and thoroughly as possible, peace will come. 3. Believe that God's thoughts toward you are not for evil but for peace, and be confident in His answer. Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus. -Philippians 4:6-7
For Jacob to possibly lose Benjamin is grace. Though it seems frightening, difficult, and impossible, when you dwell in the Lord, all attachments are let go. The ability to let go—that is grace.

* Overcome fear: Because of a sick self, you fear and cling, and you reject yourself, ultimately becoming depressed. You can see this by looking at what you truly live for. Tear down everything and start again on the blood price of Jesus. You will be free. And free those who all their lives were held in slavery by their fear of death. -Hebrews 2:15
Study and work not for ambition, but for calling.

* It is not difficulty but fear. Your faith does not waver because life is hard. It wavers because you do not love Jesus. Evil does not come like a tsunami from the start; it creates a tiny gap and eventually causes you to fall. If you are not filled with Jesus, consider yourself already in sin.
Weaknesses are overcome when wounds are healed. When a scar is held in the Lord's hand, it becomes a shining gift (star).

Are you weak? At the moment you look to the Lord, you become spiritually strong. Therefore, always look to the Lord. That is the beginning and that is the completion. Look to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of faith. -Hebrews 12:2

* Church upgrade The reason the world looks down on the church is not because it lacks, but because it clutches what it has. Because such things are no different from the world. If we lose everything and suffer loss to hold onto Jesus, the world will truly be afraid.
The church is not weakened by weak people. Rather, the Lord's power is manifested through weak people. When a person becomes strong, the Lord's power is hidden.
When you are weakest, you are strongest. When you lack most, you receive the most love. At that time the Lord's power is made perfect in us. Sunday intercession prayer 1:00 PM @Choir section
Blessed is the life that has nothing to boast of except weakness, humility, Jesus Christ, and the cross. How thankful we should be that everything is abandoned to hold onto Jesus...
When you see weakness, you must see weakness correctly. The first is another's; the second is your own. There is purpose when He reveals it.

To become weak means to completely break your own righteousness and cling to God's grace. When a father becomes weak, the home becomes happy. When a mother becomes weak, the home becomes happy. When a saint becomes weak, the church becomes happy. When a leader becomes infinitely weak, cell members feel loved and grow. Strength is the problem, and saying 'I did well' becomes arrogance and returns. In strength there is self-righteousness, boasting, and pain that wounds others.

When the Lord separates sheep from goats, He will judge based on what was done to the least of these—the weakest and most marginalized—using the standard of what was done to Him. One soul is more precious than the whole world.

* Simple but powerful living To escape is truly difficult. But if you joyfully endure persecution for godliness and willingly choose solitude to hold onto truth, it becomes not just easy but peaceful. When you try not to die, you experience that agony of dying.

Living as sheep in this dark world itself is not safe living. The only safe way to live is to stay close to the Shepherd and hear the Shepherd's voice. Because sheep are helpless, the Shepherd cares for and cherishes them more.

Sheep hear and follow the Shepherd's voice. Whether the path is rough or seemingly impossible, if He says stand, they stand; if He says go, they go; if He says cross, they cross. At the end of that path they will see the Lord.

Sheep hear the Shepherd's voice. When you obey the Lord's Word, the Lord's image appears in me and I become His disciple. But when you listen to the desires of the flesh and the voice of the world, the Lord disappears and only sorrowful multitudes remain troubled. King Saul faded away like that into the shadows of history. He spent most of his strength and years proving himself and pursuing David.
* The heart young people must have is simple. Have 'absolute faith' and 'infinite loyalty.'
Wherever you go, you engage in ministry like warfare. Life is not about choice. Obedience is power. Do what He desires.

What if one day a pastor didn't come and the reason was he faced trials? How ridiculous would that be? Don't ministers experience pain and difficulty? Of course they do, but the Lord's love is greater and conquers. Compared to the Lord's love, all else is insignificant.

Nowadays people in our time do not ask 'Who am I?' They only ask 'What do others think of me?' It is hard to even live out my own one portion of life, yet I live the life others and relatives expect. A meaningless life is miserable. Because it is not my own life. You must decide whether to see yourself through God or through your possessions.
Darkness tries to dominate people through fear. But when we lay down all circumstances and the future to almighty God, true peace covers us and Satan can no longer use fear.
Darkness tries to block it, but God's word of truth keeps running. Just as the Lord broke Himself and offered Himself as a sacrifice of truth to the world, the gospel's march continues. Friday 2013

* Will you die slowly wearing a mask? Or will you die now and live by faith? Will you pretend to be filled with the Holy Spirit, or will you obey the Holy Spirit's guidance that is actually present? That is the choice to discard what was beneficial to me like garbage to gain Jesus. That is why it is called the narrow way.

What darkness hates most is saints gathering to pray. Other service, other faith activities are tolerated somewhat, but prayer it strongly opposes. So we must pray to be able to pray. Especially when it challenges so strongly as to not give time to pray, we must pray to be able to pray. We must ask for the conditions and opportunity to pray.
If darkness opposes and tests something so much, we should welcome it with both hands raised. There is the enemy's fatal weakness. Spirit of opposition -

* Wearing a mask blinds you When no one is watching, you see your true self. If you appear completely different when people are watching, that is wearing a mask and pretending, and the Holy Spirit does not work in harmony with acting.

Some indulge in something and boast of living happily. That is trying to distract from a fatal wound through other stimulation. A sick person in a hospital cannot boast of relative health or present other conditions—a patient remains a patient. The patient must see a doctor and receive treatment. That person is Jesus.

If anyone or any event steals my gaze from the cross, I see the storm and am being engulfed by it. I hope you hold the cross firmly and love it until final victory. The cross is not some convenient accessory to put on and take off. It comes before every decision and circumstance.
No one goes through life knowing it. They do not have the ability nor are they permitted. So we do not go knowing, but believing.
* Self-diagnosis Are you truly at peace this moment?
No matter what problem or challenge comes, there is no one to look to but Jesus. I hope you look to the living Jesus beyond problems and circumstances.
Some say a man in London sold sushi and made 100 billion won. Hmm, let us sell the gospel and harvest souls.

* Tears not light in weight Abraham, who was the source of blessing, received the glory of becoming God's friend and became the ancestor of all believers today. But many overlook the four days of bloody tears he must have shed. 'My son, God himself will provide the lamb for the burnt offering.' -Genesis 22:8
Despite any circumstance and lack, God never abandons you. Because my hand became one with Your hand at the cross.
When you do not know what choice to make, trust the Lord. He becomes a refuge for those who flee to Him.

Even in paradise where one could forget all sorrow, people can turn that place into hell with these three things: 1. When comparing—without exception, one immediately becomes unhappy. 2. When wanting only to receive love—you struggle and leave, unable to go either way. Then it deepens into greater wounds. 3. When relationships break—you see the bottom of life. Conversely: 1. When not comparing but finding yourself in the Lord, 2. When trying to love with the Lord's heart, 3. When maintaining balanced relationships in the Lord—even in Achor Valley or any empty field, heaven's kingdom is there. - During a leader meeting
If you do not accept a weak person, God's training is postponed. Whether it is 40 days or 40 years depends on the attitude of the one being trained.
Remember what grace you received, what love you experienced, where you were saved. We have no other 'way' but Jesus. Let us hold onto faith now.

* Either/or When choosing between two options, the joy of choosing the other should be greater than the pain of giving up one. A person of faith has a different standard for seeing value. They regard what is permanent over what is temporary, what is glorious over what attracts attention and popularity, suffering with the Lord over what dominates in the world.

When looking back on something, sometimes you think 'this is a problem I cannot possibly avoid given my level and circumstances.' That means you must abandon trying to solve this issue yourself. It is a matter to entrust to the Lord's sovereignty, and you simply obey as He directs.
Even if tribulation comes and the heart collapses, if at this very moment you can cry out 'the blood of the Lord has power,' that alone is already sufficient.
Do not worry about 'what should I do'; hold the cross and do not let go. The cross is eternal and Jesus is the reason. A person with a clear standard does not wander.
When facing difficulty, obedience to forgive brings a holy chain reaction.
When facing difficulty, though my faith is small, I depend more on my Lord whom I trust. As time passes, there is only One to depend on. Whatever I face, I depend on Jesus. - Hymn 342 -

* It is truly foolish to contend with people. Because it is opposing God. 'David said to Abishai and all his officials, 'My own son, who came from my body, is trying to kill me. How much more, then, this Benjamite! Leave him alone; let him curse, for the Lord has told him to. It may be that the Lord will look upon my misery and restore to me his covenant blessing instead of his curse today.' -2 Samuel 16:11-12
When facing difficulty, though my faith is small, I depend more on my Lord whom I trust. As time passes, there is only One to depend on. Whatever I face, I depend on Jesus. -Hymn 342
When facing difficulty, though my faith is small, I depend more on my Lord whom I trust. -Hymn 342
Even in difficulty and tribulation, if I can live for the Lord, that is great grace.
Difficulty prolonging means you have not changed. If you cannot change, at least let go. Then the Lord can change you.
When you think something is difficult, ignore it and try. Making the impossible normal.
* Self-diagnosis If you think it is someone else's fault, you are surely sick. Satan will never let go of that gap.
A foolish person - gets angry at people over what the Lord has humbled; A ridiculous person - looks to people's eyes over what God has exalted
A foolish person thinks one interruption is insignificant. However, a wise person works with elasticity, acceleration, and passion.
*Grace calls forth grace. When you have received grace, you deeply feel that everything you have is by God's grace.

A child does childish things. Easily pleased and easily offended, clinging to trivial matters. Being immature means not knowing what is truly important (core values). If you know how precious God's love is and understand your own value of existence, you cannot live so anxiously. Do not be choked by human affection; love the Lord. Then what is incomplete will become complete. The love of Jesus is the most perfect completeness. When I was a child, I spoke like a child, I understood like a child, I thought like a child; when I became a man, I gave up childish ways. For now we see in a mirror dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part; then I shall know fully, even as I have been fully known. - 1 Corinthians 13:11-12

A child continually confirms mother's love in the midst of their problems. A baby who is loved develops faith that relies on mother's complete love. At every important work of Jesus, the Lord heard God's voice. (On the Mount of Transfiguration, at the Jordan River, in the upper room) That voice said 'I love you' and 'I am pleased with you.' In this moment of crisis, what you truly need is 'confirmation of love.'

If you give children candy and a check for 100 million won, they choose candy. Because they are immature. When grown, they choose 100 million won. Because 100 million won contains enough candy to eat until death. Everything is contained in Jesus.

Even small children worry about losing mother's embrace, food, or toys. It is the nature of humans who lost the Garden of Eden. Such a sense of loss cannot be filled by anything in the world. It is not compensated by academic credentials, honor, possessions, or boasting. Rather, it worsens. If the sense of loss comes from losing the place where you were with the Lord, there is only one place humans must return to. The Lord's embrace.

*Your gaze is your influence. When you hope only in the Lord, you can love most intensely and give the greatest influence. Because the Lord acts. It is God who works in you to will and to act in order to fulfill his good purpose. - Philippians 2:13

When I was young, I played with handcuffs from a police officer I knew, and they tightened so deeply that I got scolded. At first it was play, but later I panicked so much my face turned pale. But that officer's friend just laughed. He had the key and from experience knew this much was okay. Joseph also wore handcuffs and was wounded, so he likely said this: 'This is no joke.' Even if wounded and bloodied, falling deeper into the abyss, everything works together for good in the Lord's hands. He sent one before them—Joseph, who was sold as a slave.
