His feet were hurt with shackles, his neck was put in a collar of iron. - Psalm 105:17-18

When I was young, I resented things. But living on, I realized there were far more things I should be resented for than things to resent. Enough to make my face burn with shame... And I came to realize that those people I resented back then were also doing their best in their circumstances. So I deeply remind myself again and again. That I am a sinner.

From childhood we have learned tendencies that can lead us astray in our journey of faith. These are legalism and works-righteousness. Understanding faith as give and take. The most typical example is a vending machine. Money goes in, drink comes out. If money (merit) goes in but what I want doesn't come out, in most cases people hit it with their hands and kick it with their feet. God is not a vending machine.
*Knowing God. A holy person is not a good person, but one who has tasted and knows God's goodness. Taste and see that the Lord is good. - Psalm 34:8

When offered half-heartedly, it burdens me and frustrates others. But when I offer myself before the Lord's altar, heavenly fire ignites and burns everything. Only when one's self dies can the Lord fully minister as my King.

*Confirm your faith. The Christian life is not about well avoiding storms, but about well facing them. Whatever the world says, holding onto the Lord is true faith, and this is proven only when storms come.

How an immature leader saves souls: responding to the coming and going of souls at the level of spiritual warfare. Spiritual enemies wage war, but if we keep clinging to non-essential things and ignore spiritual warfare, everyone becomes weary. If your sibling was beaten and came home, go together and declare in the Lord's name so you don't both get beaten.
Yesterday I felt like I was watching 'I am a revivalist.' Now I'm picking up the preacher-pastor.
It could be good yesterday and lose momentum today. But do not be alarmed. Only the righteous live by faith, not by circumstances and evaluations.
Freedom from yesterday's trials today is a shift in focus. I do my part with diligence, and the Lord does His part.

*Be clear about whether you will or won't. A person clear about what they must die for is also clear about what they must live for. According to my earnest expectation and hope, I will not be ashamed in anything, but with all boldness, as always, so now also Christ will be magnified in my body, whether by life or by death. For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain. - Philippians 1:20-21
How can I not give thanks? For the ability to pray and bless...
How can I not rejoice? That I can look to the Lord...

*Prayer is not an excuse. 'I will try praying' does not mean making a decision that currently causes no harm to me. It means pursuing the Lord's will whatever the cost. Yet not my will, but yours be done. - Luke 22:42
How can I not rejoice? In the Lord's presence...
Whatever schemes prevent you from looking to the Lord, firmly reject them. The Lord promises that if you love Him and know His name, He will honor, answer, and glorify you.

*When attitude changes, life changes. The reason for frustration is futile expectations. Because you expect excessive happy endings. A person prepared to walk through even narrow valleys will find every day to be a bonus and cause for gratitude.

When did you feel most loved? On dates at nice restaurants and beautiful scenery? No. When sharing sorrow and pain and being unable to help. When you wish you could bear the pain instead but can't—that is when love becomes overwhelming. Most people seem to turn away when they become peaceful and prosperous.

*Obedience accomplishes completion. Obedience and prayer are our part. But the outcome rests with God. Many seek favor from the Sovereign, but a man's steps are established by the Lord. - Proverbs 29:26
Always and everywhere with deep, deep grace...
Always, still, eternally, only, surely, even so..., serving only the Lord.
Someday you will not resent 'my weakness' but will truly give thanks. In that weakness you will see and be amazed at God who perfects you and works powerfully.

*The servant who fulfills purpose. What the Lord's servant brings forth through the pain of labor is not mood change or deepened human relationships. It is what Jesus wanted to give. I have come to bring fire on the earth, and how I wish it were already kindled! - Luke 12:49
Prostrate yourselves with your faces to the ground. This is not a time to be cheerful and laugh. It is only a time to be humble and turn from evil sin. I long for this land to be healed.

Even if you pray for your face to become smaller, to become the youngest-looking, and your body to become thin as chopsticks, God will likely not answer unless there are special circumstances. These so-called standards of happiness are abnormal Korean society's particular demands in 2013, unrelated to God's will. Others' gaze cannot bring you happiness. Only the Lord is my strength and shield.
*The most powerful performance. The strongest power is love. Where the Lord's heart is, the Lord Himself works. Without love, it is useless and meaningless.

*Repentance is transformation. Through sharing many messages, I learned what friends like. It is messages of peace and comfort in worry and anxiety. But true transformation does not happen by adding on. It happens when you have heart surgery—tearing open your heart and abandoning greed. But such messages are not widely welcomed. Then transformation will not occur, and even after ten years you will be in the same place. Because from the beginning there was no intention to let go of greed. Rationalization without repentance is not a mistake but a character issue.
How grateful am I? That I can look to the Lord...

There are times when steel is heated to extreme temperatures. This is when transformation is needed. When heated, you must strike. When passing through affliction's furnace, if prayer increases, the fastest and most complete transformation occurs.

More important than achievement and recognition is dreaming dreams. Dreaming Jesus' dream. Worshiping Jesus and thinking deeply of Him. Salvation belongs to our God who sits on the throne and to the Lamb.
*The great mistake. The mistake itself is not wrong. Rationalizing it is the great wrong. Be courageous and say 'I am sorry.' God will transform it beautifully.

The ups and downs of faith's struggle are ultimately a contest for perspective (view). Having truth's perspective is paramount. Though the world discriminates people by money, position, beauty, and education, God reveals that I, bearing His image, am already sufficiently dignified. That cross is the evidence. You are already sufficiently attractive and dignified.
Do not become cynical about what you lack; be grateful for what you have. You will become happy and happiness will open wider paths. God does not fear nor negotiate.

By walking daily with the Lord, Enoch's heart gradually distanced itself from earthly things. Like Paul, he died daily to this earthly life, and in his spirit he was lifted to the heavenly realm. But while living on this earth, Enoch fulfilled all his responsibilities. He cared for his family. He worked, ministered, and dwelled. But he was not bound to the world. No earthly problem prevented him from walking with God.
*Wounded Healer. A true servant must be one whose wounds have been healed. Otherwise, that wound becomes a great weapon against others.

Through difficulties and sorrowful problems that arise here and there, what we must feel is not the severity of my own situation or the negligence and coldness of those around me. We must see at that very place our infinite insufficiency and weakness. Rather, we must hear the Lord's voice telling us to humble ourselves and serve on our knees. We must become lower and more humble, becoming God's servant whom people trust without needing proof, and become a harbor of peace to many wounded people, responding to the Lord's request. Let us all pray together. Father God! Please humble me further.

Looking at many things means being scattered and becoming weak. That is why the Lord restricts us. Because then you can concentrate on one thing. When God restricts and removes, it means concentrate on what matters most. One thing only—forgetting what lies behind and straining toward what lies ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus. - Philippians 3:13-14
Looking at many problems, I feel infinitely lacking. So I pray and fall to my knees. Being able to see your own insufficiency is a blessing. Because it makes you depend more on the Lord.
Due to various reasons, there will inevitably be people who turn back at the end. Do not fear; only believe. God is faithful.
* Rather than frustration, if you have faith to move forward but are not living by faith, think simply. The Lord is awakening you to the fact that you must hold onto the cross more immediately.
You will realize it later, much later. How great a work you have accomplished in the Lord..., God is wonderful.

You are people who have been chosen by God, gained new life, and received God's deep love and care. So treat others warmly and kindly with humility, gentleness, and patience; extend grace and always forgive others without harboring resentment. Remember how the Lord forgave you and forgive others as well. Most importantly, live a life overflowing with love; love is the bond that perfectly unites all into one. Let the peace you received from Christ always dwell in your heart and life. This is both your responsibility and privilege as members of Christ's body. And always live with gratitude. Keep Christ's teachings in your heart so that His word enriches your life. And through His word, become wise and teach and admonish one another. -Paul
* The Ability of Declaring Everything - Confession makes the weak become great.
When you lack leisure, other things may appear wrong. I urge you to have margin. They are simply different. The Lord makes the decision. Entrust it to Him.

* True Assessment - Ministry cannot be known by numbers and titles. Even after leaving that position, it is difficult to say you know. Eventually, you will know when you stand before the Lord. That is why you must serve with reverence.

A compass is vital to a traveler's life. Yet that compass points to only one direction—true north. It teaches only north, but automatically south, east, and west become visible. When you fix your eyes on the Lord and make Him your standard, other decisions become easy to grasp in accordance with that standard. Before the Lord, there is no complexity or difficulty.
* How a Leader Loves - through intercession with tears and running toward the Lord on bended knee. Because this is so important, everything else becomes invisible and you become blind to it.
The Lord establishes the steps of a man and delights in his way. Though he falls, he will not be utterly cast down, for the Lord upholds him with His hand. -Psalm 37:23,24

The Lord is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in Him, and I am helped. Therefore my heart greatly rejoices, and with my song I will praise Him. -Psalm 28:7 David had much gold and silver, people and power to rely on. But he relied only on the Lord. For one who has God, there is no other greater or more wonderful power. The world is a society that promotes newer and more amazing things, but there is no other name by which salvation has been given to mankind except the name of Jesus. Do you love Jesus? Then there is nothing more to seek.
Why History Stopped - Most do not deny the Holy Spirit, but many greatly limit Him. It is because I become the master and the Lord becomes the guest.

History repeats itself because human nature does not change. But if people who have nailed themselves to the cross begin to be raised up, even history will change. I hope you become a person who changes world history.
* Dangerous Self-Esteem - For a leader to have low self-esteem and inferiority complex is very dangerous. Only those who respect themselves can respect those they lead.

Jesus is the standard that divides history. Jesus is the standard that divides people. Jesus is the standard that divides life and death. Without knowing Jesus, people worry only about earthly things—'what will we eat, what will we wear?'—and die. Those who know and believe in Jesus seek His kingdom and righteousness and live. When a person meets Jesus, they live their life to the fullest, burn completely, and consume themselves entirely.

Indeed, I always feel the devotion of serving the Lord with all your best and living it out. Challenged, envious, and grateful... Looking at the main photo, you seem to have lost weight... But you look healthy, which is wonderful. I will pray for you... for your ministry, health, and all that you do~!!

To undergo refining, to face challenges, to be driven into the wilderness—these are emergencies. My situation has become dire. But a wise person does not mind and advances boldly. When advancing in the wilderness, one receives remarkable progress and power. A person with faith gives more in offering than a rich person. A person who is truly busy, cannot even eat properly, and rushes about is more faithful than one with much time. Advancement does not come when conditions are right. It comes through struggle, real breakthrough thinking, challenging your limits and moving forward. To advance in the wilderness is to move forward.
If the purpose of love is pleasure, only wounds remain. One purpose of romantic love is to know God's will.

* Overcoming Distance - Focusing excessively on the distance from those who are closer and being distant from those who are not close is not the way of the Lord's leader. That you worship with me means the Lord trusts me, expects me, and has entrusted me to you. Whatever you did for the least of these among my brothers, you did for me. -Matthew 25:40

Sing When You Are Weak - When I am weak, I must not despair but rejoice in the greatness of the Lord who becomes strong in my weakness. My strength is not in my power but in my joy in the Lord; that is my strength. Therefore I take pleasure in weaknesses, in insults, in hardships, in persecutions, in difficulties for Christ's sake; for when I am weak, then I am strong. -2 Corinthians 12:10
The revelation of weakness is not because I failed at self-management but because there is a part that needs to be healed. The Lord desires to break down emotions that do not come from Him.
Inferiority comes from not grasping the gospel. Low self-esteem cannot love others, and without love, evangelism is impossible.

* Man's Way, God's Way - 'Declaring' is what I do, and 'keeping' is what the Lord does. Declare it! Then He will keep it. Be anxious for nothing, but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God. And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus. -Philippians 4:6,7
How to Be Zealous - You must labor with all your strength according to the work of Him who works in me with power. Zeal without Him working through me is not only futile labor but evil.

It is not because there is a lack of zealous and capable servants that there is difficulty. Rather, it is because there are no useless servants. They distance themselves from the cross to receive recognition for their zeal. The way of the cross is the immeasurable love of the Lord that humbles us even through irrational circumstances. He made you walk the wilderness... He humbled you...
* Paul's Thought Structure - Not considering that I have already attained, but forgetting what lies behind and reaching forward to what lies ahead.

The meaning of praying earnestly is not offering my sincerity but relying on Him alone. This difference is crucial: moral reward becomes self-righteousness in faith and is useless. Rather, listening and believing, praying because you believe—this is the way to obtain true grace.
The conclusion of living zealously is that zeal alone is not enough. Even lazy people do not reach this conclusion. If God does not help, diligence and labor are futile and empty.
* Busy at work, Thanks Misun Hong
* The Proof of Love - I love God's people given to me. But I do not argue or overstate to prove that love. Love cannot be hidden, and most importantly, God knows.
If you worked hard and had a sense of accomplishment but missed prayer, it is normal that repentance comes. I hope there is a simple life where your life priorities are established.
A generation that offers passion and intelligence. I bless you with burning logic.

Despite such imperfection, God works through the grace He pours out on the church and the maturity through the Holy Spirit to overcome problems and become glorified. The church and family were the irrevocable decision of the Father, the foundation of wisdom and glory.
Passion also has levels. Jesus becomes my passion. Therefore our passion must be first-rate.
You seem to have much passion. 'You will get used to it.'
* Not Popularity but Glory - Do not seek confirmation; testify. What makes us run is not popularity but glory. Therefore do not watch reactions but hold to the truth.

Let us suppose someone researched how a train could run without rails. After several decades and astronomical budgets, they succeeded in making a heavy train move without rails. That heavy train moving without rails...!! When is this amazing? But a train without rails is slower than a regular train, costs much more, and most importantly, it does not help much. Just let it run on the rails... Prayer is the rail of the train.
Glory and honor and power—even if I fade and am forgotten, receive such glory and honor.
* Anxiety Under God's Sovereignty - To say the Lord rules means my worries and anxieties leave me and melt away under His glory. Our God is a consuming fire. -Hebrews 12:29

Living forever, eternal life, is not merely the continuation of time. It means the qualitative life of having intimate oneness with the Lord. Everyone will exist for eternal time. What faith you have determines where you spend eternity, and how much you love Him determines what you will do eternally.

When you raise your spiritual altitude higher, you can be free from temptations and clouds of reactions that hold your ankles. Raising altitude is the assurance that 'I will serve only the Lord.' Rocom Meri Khost!
Satan concentrates his attack on those with spiritual laziness and slack tension.

The secret to victory in spiritual warfare is maintaining peace. It is not panicking or being mentally broken. The Lord never panicked or was flustered while working, and He maintained peace. He even slept in a stormy sea. The most effective victory is not being shaken as the devil shakes and not responding. Do not respond. That is the famous ability to ignore.

Spiritual warfare is precisely a battle of perspective. To deal with greed and sin extends to a battle of values in which greed arises. In Philippians, Paul confesses that before the value of Jesus, all other values that were so impressive become 'dung.' Before the revelation and illumination of the Holy Spirit, things that were so impressive become mere 'excrement,' and for such people, even when temptation and the world come, they only smell.

* Focus Over Response - Repeatedly seeking confirmation of relationships through the other person's response comes from fear. This anxiety stems from 'anxiety about one's existence.' Not knowing God, one does not know one's existence and purpose, and attachment creates empty pursuits that twist life.

* Glory Overwhelms Worldliness - Rather than being pressed by relationships and worrying, be concerned that you cannot let go of yourself and cannot look to God properly. This is pursuing glory rather than pursuing the art of getting along in the world.
If you try to have influence, side effects appear. True influence from God comes from those who concentrate on God and are moved.

Soul, be victorious; heart, recover. To be victorious, you must recover. God asks you first to recover your 'first love' before health, finances, or social status. The fatal deficiency that the Lord diagnoses is irreplaceable 'first love.' To recover it, you must repent, turn around, find what was lost, change it, remove it, simplify, and focus. This is changing your interest and affection, and renewing what was once beneficial to me when I did not know Jesus by the surpassing knowledge of knowing Jesus Christ. But whatever things were gain to me, these I have counted loss for Christ... I count all things loss for the excellence of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord. -Philippians 3:7,8
There is no news more joyful than hearing that a soul has been saved. The Father is still working even now.

In the film A Beautiful Mind, Professor John Nash overcomes what he wants to see and the hallucinations that result from it, and wins the Nobel Prize. After his acceptance speech, as he goes out with his wife, three figures from his hallucination are there. But he does not choose them. Living by faith is not being free from all temptations. Rather, you simply do not choose. Faith is a choice.

If the neighboring house has water but mine doesn't, it's not Thames Water's problem. Water is sufficiently supplied, but my pipe is the issue. If faith is the problem, I cannot receive the supply. Have good faith.

When Jeremiah spoke of the destruction of the superpower Babylon of that time, no one believed him. Yet it was conquered by a foreign nation with different values, worldview, and culture. We must become foreigners in the eyes of the world. That means living in this world with a different attitude—a spirit of opposition. I will send foreigners to Babylon to winnow and empty that land... Jeremiah 51:2

The expression 'watching worship' needs to be corrected. Only unbelievers watch worship as spectators. Worship must be offered at minimum, and I myself must become part of that holy work. It's not watching fire from across the river, but that fire entering into me and burning. A life without fire is truly an extinguished life. Come to pray.

Those who do not know the preciousness of worship are like Esau, who sold his birthright as the firstborn for a bowl of lentil stew. We must not become people who fail to see precious things as precious and cannot keep our own place given by the Lord.
Worshipers must rejoice. Because they are victors. David was victorious in all his wars. It was because he was a victor in worship.

To have the ability to worship and worship where you are called, you must receive power. Before going where called, you must focus and seek the Lord until you are clothed with power. To those who have, more will be given; from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.

Even believing in Jesus and praying, one can walk through the dark valley of the shadow of death. The quality of the path is not important; who you walk with is what matters. Walking with the Lord who drank every bitter cup for me—that is faith.
Believers in Jesus are not destroyed because trials are great. They are destroyed because they do not bear their cross.
Those who believe in Jesus do not act by compulsion. They walk under God's guidance—His overflowing guidance.

The joy of believing in Jesus and the obedience of following His Word are blocked by worldly pleasures and desires. Even when we nod at the Word, desires cling to our hearts like gum and hardly fall away. Yet when a pearl merchant finds the greatest pearl, he sells all his pearls and buys that great pearl. When the joy of knowing Jesus comes, everything else becomes worthless and falls away.
Those who believe in Jesus are not pursued. Rather, they are those who pursue and lead.

Outside of Jesus, even what is splendid and high is a flower in a vase without roots. If we had remained in Jesus, even the smallest thing is everything and happiness. Because Jesus loved us to the end (John 13:1), we do not fail. Because we did not love first, we never fear.
We came here by Jesus's love. And by Jesus's love, we will go there too.
If you want to become close to Jesus, self must be surrendered. It is impossible to become close to Jesus without denying yourself.
Revival occurs when we focus on Jesus. That is why being unable to look at anything else but the Lord is grace. True blessing is losing other things to gain Jesus.

Jesus said, 'Live as salt,' and 'Have salt in yourselves and be at peace with one another.' Salt is influence. With only 2.82%, it keeps the great ocean from rotting and is the only source of saltiness. Salt is the only thing that prevents decay and gives saltiness. So when salt loses its taste, it becomes completely useless and is trampled by people. How can we live as salt? Living as salt means living through surrendering rights, denying self, and infinite forgiveness. (Luke 14:33) Any of you who does not give up everything he has cannot be my disciple. (Luke 14:34) Salt is good, but if it loses its saltiness, how can it be made salty again? (Luke 14:35) It is no longer good for the soil or the manure pile; it is thrown out. Whoever has ears to hear, let them hear. Salt is discipleship. Wherever disciples go, the Lord is proclaimed and God's kingdom is established.

Jesus alone is sufficient, they say. But it doesn't seem that way. If we live by the Gospel, we are happy even if we die today. Are you sufficiently happy? If not, then Jesus is not sufficient for you.

Those who live by Jesus do not have complete absence of temptation from sinful nature; it always exists, but they rule their flesh by the spirit. If we serve the flesh, we will die; but if we kill and rule the flesh by the spirit, we become people receiving God's guidance. If by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live... - Paul
* Absolute peace without rejection and loss. This peace that wells up in my heart is a hidden treasure. Who would take away my treasure? Be at ease. - Hymn 469

A person explained only by Jesus, if I must answer what my existence means, I can only say it is God's grace. I hope to be like a clay vessel, broken and chipped, so that the precious Jesus shines through. My worth is only the worth of Jesus.

Jesus has almost no record before beginning His public ministry at age thirty. He simply grew, lived as God's Son, and had no achievements. Yet God the Father said of Jesus, 'This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased.' This evaluation was based not on any work, achievement, or accomplishment, but solely on intimate relationship with Himself.

* Reasons to be strong. 'I' myself am far weaker and more limited than I think, while God's grace is incomparably greater and eternal than I can imagine. Who will separate us from the love of Christ? Will tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword? - Romans 8:35
Jesus can never ignore my cry. The soul purchased with the blood shed at the cross cannot be ignored or deprived of blessing. Prayer is answered not by great spirituality but only by the cross.
Jesus is my strength.

* Why lies are powerful. Ahab receives guidance from a lying spirit. He heard from it through the false prophet Zedekiah. He can never see truth, only the lies he wants to see. What causes fights and quarrels among you? Don't they come from your desires that battle within you? You desire but do not have, so you kill. You covet but you cannot get what you want, so you quarrel and fight. - James 4:1,2
Jesus is not only on the high Mount of Transfiguration. He is also in the low schools and workplaces, in the places where breath gets stuck. The Lord works there too. Let us pray.

Jesus made tremendous sacrifice to give you authority. If you surrender it, the evil one will seize it and attack instead. When you become one with Jesus, His abundant grace and power also work through me.
Jesus was led like a silent lamb to the slaughter. But in the temple, He was angry for His Father's name.

Jesus was silent. Not because He had nothing to say, but to drink all the cup of suffering. In the moment of agony where even groans had to be swallowed, the Lord was silent while picturing the faces of us who would be saved. If Jesus's surrender of rights gave us salvation, why should we sinners be boastful about surrendering our rights?

Jesus prayed all night and established people. That Jesus labored so hard to build us up means that as disciples, our shortcomings and weaknesses are not our responsibility but 'Jesus's responsibility.' He will do it. The One who keeps me is God, not I. The One who opens my path is God, not I. David, who became a prosperous king, makes this confession: 'Because the Lord is my shepherd, I am safe.' Greater than not sinning is to completely trust the Lord.

Jesus marveled at the faith of the centurion. So He healed the sick with a word as the centurion requested. His confession of faith was rare even in Israel because it was true faith that acknowledged Jesus's sovereignty. There are many believers, but few acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty.

* The glory of humility. Even people who seem weak and hate getting shots will not hesitate to undergo bone-scraping surgery to be beautiful. They want to be beautiful. Believers are not originally strong people. But because the glory of the Lord they experienced is so great, they can become infinitely humble and pray to be humbled more. Having humbled himself by becoming obedient to death—even death on a cross! Therefore God exalted him to the highest place. - Philippians 2:8,9

Jesus told fish-smelling fishermen that just as God the Father sent Him, so He sends us into the world. The only way for this world to receive forgiveness of sins, return to the Lord, and be saved is for us to testify to the Gospel. Because the Lord called us and sent us.
Jesus could not pass by the tears of the Canaanite widow whose son died. Jesus remembers our tears. 'I have heard your prayer and seen your tears.' - 2 Kings 20:5
* Broken-hearted love. I will love with a heart that breaks, having received love so great it breaks.
Jesus knew that only the Father's will would be accomplished, so He was neither pursued nor anxious. Accomplishing His will in His time begins with believing.

In John's Gospel, Jesus revealed that His disciples are hated by the world because they belong to a different kingdom. * What to remember in darkness. What God reveals to us in prayer during darkness is that there are more for us than those against us. Jesus extends to us an 'invitation to happiness.' There is no threat or condemnation in the Lord's invitation. He is never disappointed. Jesus drew a clear boundary between His disciples and the crowds. For those who love Jesus and follow Jesus, miracles become common sense. Jesus said a servant is not above his master, nor a disciple above his teacher. Yet He washed their feet. The purpose of God's glorious Son coming into this world was to wash away filth. By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another.

Jesus went up to the Mount of Olives to pray on His last day on earth. To accomplish the Father's will in heaven also on earth. Those who meet God experience transformation, and that transformation comes as free grace.

Though Jesus was the Son of God, He was falsely accused in the court of sinful humans and received the death sentence. It is because when He rises and returns, He will expose all things with truth and crush Satan's head.
Jesus never pressed me. Because my wrongdoing is not something I can bear. Rather, I desperately need Jesus in my life, and Jesus bore the cross for that.
* The best discernment. Rather than sensing the power of darkness, long for the Lord's presence. That is true discernment.
Without Jesus, we can do nothing. But why should we be disappointed that we can do nothing? Pride in relying on ourselves and others makes us disappointed. A humble person is never disappointed.

Believing in Jesus means looking only at the Lord and being moved with gratitude because of the glory that is no longer evaluated by the world's standards. My choices and responses prove what I trust, love, and value. Do you love Jesus?

Believing in Jesus is not wish fulfillment. Paul says because of Jesus he can be delivered over to death. (2 Corinthians 4:11) He also desired to know the fellowship of sharing in His sufferings. (Philippians 3:10) That is because when you truly love, you want to sacrifice instead.

When you believe in Jesus, God immediately dwells with you. However, you must be transformed by renewing your mind. God must come from my inner sanctum (spirit) to the sanctuary (thoughts) and fully govern even the outer court (body). We must not seek the Holy Spirit elsewhere. When you look to Jesus, you gain the courage to rise again without giving up.

* Finding the True Culprit What kind of extremely wicked people nailed Jesus to the cross? Mark says that the people who lacked God's Word and Pilate who sought to satisfy the people killed him. Matthew testifies that the high priests captivated by jealousy killed him. John and Luke record that the people who were disappointed in believing Jesus as the nation's savior, and Judas, as well as the Roman soldiers with malicious hearts, did this. To us who tsk our tongues at such people, Paul says this: I am chief among sinners—1 Timothy 1:15. Not being able to see yourself means you are not seeing the Lord before the Lord.

To see Jesus was to see the Father. That is why all that I have loved appears in my image. Therefore, faith can be inherited. John 14:9 'Anyone who has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, 'Show us the Father'?'
Decide only to love Jesus. Then renounce and refuse to be remembered or explained by anything else.
Loving Jesus. Looking to Jesus is the most crucial work.

Only when you kneel before the Lord and weep until you can weep will the storm not cease, and it should not cease. Now my light must go out before the Lord's glory.
To love Jesus is to die for Jesus.
Those who love Jesus are bold. They live for the Lord, work for the Lord, suffer for the Lord, and die for the Lord. To the slain Lamb, we give all glory and honor.

Those who love Jesus do not waver. But in truly difficult moments of life, they can waver. However, those who love Jesus even more do not waver. But when faced with truly difficult circumstances, they can waver. However, those who truly love Jesus cannot waver. How can you say that those who love Jesus can waver? Ministry is not a game and calling is not play. Jesus shed His precious blood, the Lord bore the cross. That is why no one can stop you. Whether we live or die, we live for the Lord and die for the Lord. Living or dying, we are the Lord's. Those who hold only to the cross, those who enjoy the precious blood, cannot be bound by their own work. Even if they want to be bound, they cannot. There is fire in the heart...
Do you love Jesus? Those who love Jesus are those who die for Jesus. In a meeting in 1989...
I think of Jesus. I love Jesus. I look only to Jesus. What else should I look to?

* Faith That Does Not Avoid As we live in the world, we encounter unavoidable circumstances. Because they are things that must not be avoided. These are things that must be prayed about with faith and areas requiring endurance. While avoiding the time of trial is grace, sometimes facing it is also grace.

When knowledge of Jesus reaches its highest nobility, things that were once beneficial no longer attract me. If people claim to believe but show no sign of believers and continue in excess, it means the transformation of values has not yet occurred. If you do not love Jesus, you remain in sin. A person's affections reveal what their true nature is.

Those who have testified of Jesus marvel at that joy and anointing. The anointing is poured out when we love and bless. Since the greatest blessing is proclaiming Jesus, the Father cannot remain idle. When we bless and speak of love in Jesus' name, the power of heaven comes. Upon both unbelievers and those with the dynamic of faith...
If you truly love Jesus, you can be free from everything that easily binds. Because life is not lost but becomes clearer.

When first believing in Jesus, you realize that I am a sinner and that Jesus bore the cross because of my sins. After believing in Jesus for a long time, you realize that I am the chief among sinners and that Jesus' cross continues.

Jesus' fervent prayer was not only that His disciples be one as the Father and Jesus are one, but also that those who hear their words and believe in Jesus would be one. When we become one, Jesus' desire is fulfilled and the kingdom of heaven becomes reality.

* Crucifying Jesus Through Jealousy Making judgments based on speculation is extremely foolish and can leave many wounds. Jesus asked the Pharisees who hated Him to testify what was wrong, but they simply hated Him. (John 18:23) But underlying that hatred was this disposition: He knew that it was out of jealousy that the chief priests had handed Jesus over to him—Mark 15:10. The jealousy of wanting to be in control, of not wanting to lose my authority, is what nailed Jesus to the cross.

Jesus' yoke is easy and His burden is light. (Matthew 11:29-30) It is not what I do (to Do) but through being with the Lord and becoming like Jesus' image (to Be), the Lord accomplishes the work. My breathing, heartbeat, everything comes from God. Only the Lord should be the subject of my joy.

Jesus' commands have power. When He says 'Pick up your mat and walk,' even a man paralyzed for thirty-eight years rises. I hope you will hear the Lord's command. In it there is compassion and healing, restoration and the power of fulfillment.

Seventy percent of Jesus' parables are about money, and the Bible speaks of finances in about 3,000 ways. From God's perspective, money is the most common thing, and there are few who are worthy of receiving its abundance.

Jesus' life had a consistent intention that transcended circumstances and emotions. To fulfill Scripture. Do not respond to the devil's request and intention; respond to the Lord's will. What is the Lord's request?

The final temptation in Jesus' crucifixion work was the temptation of 'self-vindication.' 'If you truly are the Son of God, come down.' Being able to come down but choosing not to is true grace. Do not vindicate yourself when misunderstood or in pain; pray like Jesus. The Lord will work all things together for good.

* Rest & Recovery Do not try to fix the problem; be fixed by God. It is not my work to solve problems, and the more you try to solve them, the more you get lost in a maze. My work is holiness and entrusting; God's work is healing and revival.

Many touch the hem of Jesus' garment now and in the past. But not all receive salvation. When faith reaches out a hand, the Lord does not take offense but rather bestows salvation. He healed the woman with the issue of blood.
* Grace Being loved by someone is not evidence that I am a good person, but evidence of God's infinite grace.

If you truly know who Jesus is, what came before becomes dung. A person with a truly transformed value system is truly transformed. But whatever things were gain to me I counted loss for the sake of Christ. More than that, I count all things to be loss in view of the surpassing value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord, for whom I have suffered the loss of all things, and count them but rubbish so that I may gain Christ, and may be found in Him, not having a righteousness of my own derived from the Law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness which comes from God on the basis of faith—Philippians 3:7, 9

All the solutions Jesus taught in parables and teachings ultimately point to the cross. The cross has two properties: complete trust in the Lord and renunciation of one's own rights. If anyone would come after Me, he must deny himself, and take up his cross daily and follow Me.

* Time to Blow Out the Candle It is sad that you are not prepared, because your heart is full of the world. If there is joy in being filled with Jesus because of my insufficiency, then weakness becomes a boast. Before the rising sun, the candle must go out.

Jesus declared that He has overcome the world and its prince. And He spoke that word to us. The reason He spoke it is 'so that in Me you may have peace. In the world you have tribulation, but take courage; I have overcome the world.' From our perspective, we must hear that word He spoke. Those who hear the Lord's voice have peace and victory. Those who hear the voice of the world and people remain in darkness and stumble.

Jesus did not come to improve attitudes or morality. He came to give life in God to us sinners who would helplessly serve the devil due to sin and the law of death that the devil wields. Through the cross, the Holy Spirit came, and because this Helper gives the life of God and gives the power to live in that life, He is called 'the Spirit of life,' and further 'the law of the Spirit of life' (Romans 8:2). If I dwell in the flesh, I cannot please God, but 'in Jesus,' when I dwell in the Holy Spirit, I live not in the old sinful life but in God's life and God's power. Faith is believing that.

* The Blessing of Helplessness With every step I walk, with every breath I take, there has never been a moment when I do not need the Lord's grace. The more I depend, the more I have to depend on, and whatever situation I face, I rely on Jesus.

The way Jesus wants us to rejoice is not to rejoice in Performance but to rejoice in Identity. Incentive is how companies motivate employees; Love is how God treats us. The cross is love. 'But do not rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are recorded in heaven'—Luke 10:20

If you cannot truly be satisfied with Jesus, then you should not be satisfied with anything else. But the problem is that there are sometimes or often cases where you are satisfied. It is because they are counterfeits.
Saying that Jesus is sufficient does not end in words. It is proven by the subjects of my pain and through the Lord becoming the master of my heart.
If you have Jesus, you have the best thing. There is no reason to be discouraged over other things. An earthen vessel is glorified only by the treasure it contains.
To believe in Jesus is to have peace. Because you look only to Jesus. Honestly, saying you believe is not something you can say easily. It means He monopolizes you.

Is it true that if Jesus is enough, such sufficiency must be proven? Even the value of good food and good luxury brands, when you learn and enjoy them, need not be reminded but rather you yourself seek out good things with a terrible obsession. When you truly experience 'Jesus is sufficient,' there is no reason to say 'do this' or 'do not do that.'
In Jesus, all things are possible.
Do not hesitate by hoping to accomplish something with anything other than Jesus. If you just run for Jesus alone, you will see glory.
* True Expert Rather than becoming an expert who understands the situation and quickly provides solutions, you must become someone who first examines your own heart. God uses only humble people.

I remember a hymn I sang when I was in college leading prayer meetings. It was a song made purely for prayer gatherings. The praying soul will be blessed in all things; because the Lord does not abandon, even if trials come or there is a threat to life, I will pray to the end. The praying soul, like the Lord, will cry out loudly with tears to the Lord; because the petition is made to God alone, it will be completely resolved. I will remember the Lord who, though He had every desire, learned obedience and was made perfect through obedience. The praying soul is...

In the past, it seemed I had never bowed. I could not bend. But after realizing what Jesus did on the cross, I have become bent. Now it is almost automatic. To save others, I must die. If I die, I live and others live. Conversely, if I live, everyone dies. One way of dying is fasting. Anger does not accomplish God's righteousness.
What was once shame has now become praise from my lips through God's love. Even when enemies come, I do not crumble. I believe in God and fear nothing. I will surely live to see God's glory.

* Anxiety A person's anxiety cannot accomplish God's righteousness and causes the pain of withering bones. When entrusted to the Lord's hands, after a while you forget, and being troubled in heart over matters of such weight that fade from memory is not the Lord's will. If I focus on holiness and my work, the Lord will do His work and add all these things to me.

The essence of faith is no different now than before. In the past, if you did not deny the Lord, you would be thrown into a lion's den. Now, too, if you do not have greater affection for another being than the Lord, you are threatened with failure and despair. Still, to overcome such things, you must cling to the cross with blood shed. Determine to shed blood. You will rather have peace.

An old saying goes that it is not people who lie, but money that lies. It means the true heart is not so, but the harsh reality and circumstances drive people that way. If I do not have spiritual assets, Jesus, I easily become angry, frustrated, and wounded by the slightest stimulus. Without the risen Jesus, every other problem is merely a consequence of not having Jesus. I desire that the Lord be with your spirit; grace be with you—2 Timothy 4:22

* Two Hearts What I am concerned about and care for is my purpose. If it is my own desire, it is greed for a purpose; if it is God's desire, it is a calling. A person cannot serve two masters, and if you say you serve one master while pursuing other desires, you are unstable with a divided heart. God seeks those who fear Him.

How important spiritual authority is can be seen in the fact that Satan does his best to prevent spiritual authority from being established. When this is set right, God's influence floods into my life.

In today's house church where everyone prays, I want to kindle the fire of prayer with the heart that the Father gives. No one asked for it, and even if only the last person remains, the mission of the saints is to pray. Prayer continues.

* The rest that is the source of supply does not mean doing nothing. True rest is obtained only when God grants rest. If we continually pursue vain things when we should rest, that is, when we should meet the Lord, the Lord will surely make us rest. Ultimately, we must receive strength, and that comes from God. God's calling is to give new strength to us who are exhausted. You are my strength, O Lord, and I love You. -Psalm 18:1

The reason many churches today face difficulties cannot be attributed to a lack of faithful people. Rather, it is because there are many. After being faithful, many people demand 'their share.' The Lord rejoices with tears over our dedication and labor, but He desired to call us 'unprofitable servants' in our attitude. (Luke 17:9)

* Do not despair but fear the Lord. The reason for despair is that you relied on yourself. The reason for separation is that you relied on people. The reason for ruin is that you relied on material things and circumstances. Only those who fear the Lord will shine like the stars of heaven.

This morning, before you get out of bed, as soon as you open your eyes, confess this: I want to know God more and love Him better than anything else today. Life is preparation for eternity. The present life is practice for what we must do eternally in the eternal kingdom of heaven. Faith, hope, love, peace, gentleness, self-control, intimacy, praise, worship....

* The reason for frustration Frustration testifies to two things: that you are not looking with the Lord's heart, and that the possibility of using human shortcuts has greatly increased. It is better to hold onto the cross of Jesus and perish. Whether we live or die, we live for the Lord or die for the Lord; whether we live or die, are we not His?
* The source of anxiety Anxiety comes from greed. If you are content, you will experience the power of peace. Contentment comes from trusting in the Almighty Lord.

* Self-denial Do not try to satisfy your desires; pursue your calling. If you do not deny your desires, you will achieve shame, but those who pursue their calling will gain life and honor and see infinite glory.

Did you pray before leaving home today? Did you pray for the grace you will receive today? This is a gospel song that was popular in the 1980s. When writing emails, using SNS, making phone calls, meeting friends, turning on a computer at work, I urge you to pray. Pray that He would govern your words and actions, thoughts and emotions, keeping you not only from sin but also manifesting the righteousness of God through your conduct. Especially during conversations, ask the Lord and pray that the desires that people pursue would be abandoned and the will of the Lord would be accomplished.

* The reason for joy What could a person in prison be happy about? Yet Paul is a man who laughs in his sleep if it is about the 'gospel.' When he sees the disciples in Philippi having fellowship in the gospel and living by the gospel, his chains could not hinder his joy. Every time I make a request, I always make requests with joy on behalf of all of you because of your partnership in the gospel from the first day until now. Philippians 1:4-5
The crisis in families today is not due to lack of health, material goods, or children, but due to the absence of prayer.

Even today darkness keeps saying 'have more,' 'possessions are good,' 'own more,' and even tempts us with the productivity of making bread from stones to become winners in the world. To overcome Satan, we must surrender ourselves to God and abandon our lusts.

* The reason for persecution 'I consider that our present sufferings are not worth comparing with the glory that will be revealed in us.' -Romans 8:18 The reason Paul says the world causes us to suffer is because the world hates us, because 'we are 1. heirs, 2. sharing in His suffering, 3. sharers in His glory, and 4. receivers of the testimony of the Spirit.' We must maintain the Maginot line of glorious heirs amid suffering and be victorious. The Lord's glory is great.

* Acceleration that does not stop When the heart beats, it beats. When breathing is possible, we run. For those running to accomplish their calling, the words 'God is good' are sufficient. We do not believe people's claims that they have 'abandoned foolishness.' Those who truly look to the Lord speak through their lives. If we run without regret until the time when the Lord tells us to run, to the place He tells us to go, that is the glory of this humble servant.

Sleep does not come this morning. It is not insomnia but because each person, one by one, feels so precious. I too had my youth. There were seniors in faith who extended their shoulders to me. I will also unreservedly extend my shoulders to young people and have them step on them and move forward. When their vision is fulfilled, that is precisely when my vision is fulfilled. That is why He causes us to pray for souls.

If I stand before God today and review my day, I will ask for love. To be able to love the souls He has given me to the end, to become a person blinded by love so that their strengths and weaknesses are not visible.

Come, he said, and Peter got down out of the boat and walked on the water toward Jesus. -Matthew 14:29 Faith is jumping out of the boat without Jesus. We must abandon the boat without Jesus and jump into the water where Jesus is, even if it seems like certain death. What I boast in, what I rely on, what my heart desires—I believe in it and serve it.

Over a long time, the wound of the heart like a rose thorn that hurt just from being touched will someday become a precious and beautiful memory. This is because pain, too, is a valuable part of my life.

The secret things belong to the Lord our God, but the things revealed belong to us and to our children forever, that we may follow all the words of this law. -Deuteronomy 29:29 Secret belongs to the realm of God, and Revealed belongs to the realm of humans. We must entrust to God what we cannot digest, and obey what has been Revealed. Do not lament over what you do not know; keep what has been revealed.

* Good loss There are people who do as much as they can, but their faith declines. They sense their lack and know that they must change to live, but looking back, they are always stepping backward. The reason is that they have not resolved to lose. A higher value can only be obtained after losing a lower value. You cannot have both revival and the world. Fix your resolve and boldly abandon what must be abandoned. Only then will you receive the blessing of having all these things added to you.
Only through His Son can we come to the Father, and only through the influence of the Holy Spirit can we believe in the Son. Neither by strength nor by might.
Only two choices are possible: flee or stand and fight. If you run away, it is easier, but you will spend your whole life fleeing. If you stand and fight, you will see the path God has given.

Only all your attention and thoughts must be filled with Jesus. You must think of Jesus 24 hours a day. Living in obedience is more important than preaching the Word. The reason we do not live as we preach is not that the truth is not truth, but because we have not met Jesus, have not walked with Him, have not become one with Him, and have not thought of Him. When you open your eyes from your bed in the morning, who do you think of? (Hebrews 3:1) Therefore, holy brothers and sisters who have been called to the same heavenly calling, fix your thoughts on Jesus, the apostle and high priest whom we confess.

* The greatest reward God told Abraham He would give him a very great reward. Yet that reward was invisible. It was because He Himself was invisible. He wanted to give not the blessing of a passing moment but Himself, who is eternal.
Only a loving heart comes from the Lord.
Only the righteous live by faith. Receiving the sprinkled blood of Jesus and the confidence of being children of God—that is 'righteousness.'
* Stand right only before the Lord. I urge you to work hard to stand right only before the Lord. Remember: it is only the Lord. Not people. Trying to stand before people becomes a whitewashed tomb.

* Faithfulness where no one dedicates The reason we go out into the world is to build strength so we become people whom no one can despise. But even if you serve diligently in the church, no one acknowledges you. Because no one acknowledges you, you can run. Because you see only the goal.
Let us look only to the Lord. When we look to the Lord, the things I could not let go become like dung and are discarded.
Only let the Lord be able to call me; stand nameless and without light. Become a secret.

Only those who love the Lord can break through this tribulation and this trial. Because those who love have no fear. Love the Lord, and then all problems will become unimportant before His love. I lean upon You and run into enemy territory; I trust in my God and leap over a wall. -2 Samuel 22:30

* Persistent humility Humility is not seeing my own lack but seeing the Lord's glory. An attitude based on myself is always like a roller coaster, but when the Lord is my rock, I am not shaken. My lack is not what matters; what matters is who the Lord is.
Only God does the realm of grace and transformation. I see many miracles around me. What were originally robbers who didn't even carry swords are now wingless angels.
It is by God's grace alone. Think about it. There is nothing I can do.

* Overlooked, so overlooked Young people have great visions and dreams. But what they overlook is that by missing small things, they lose big things. Depending on perspective, small things can easily be overlooked, but they are also matters of immediate obedience. Small things like eating habits create habits, and habits determine destiny. Daniel resolved not to defile himself with the royal food and wine, and asked the chief official for permission not to defile himself. -Daniel 1:8

An orchestra musician must focus on the conductor. You must not let your attention be taken by the gestures of people sitting in the audience, their glances, their whispers and evaluations, and even the expectations of the family members who came with them. If you start to be conscious of these things, you perform differently and ultimately give an unhappy performance. Sometimes you may not understand, but follow the conductor. Jesus is my only conductor. Living the life the Lord has given requires faith and courage. You must reject living someone else's life.
Even if all the world abandons me, I will not abandon Lord Jesus, and He cares for me to the end. I do not fear water or fire or spear or sword; the Lord is my high fortress and my shield.
When we look to the Lord with all our hearts, dying souls and withered hearts come alive. We must look to the Lord with all our hearts so that we receive the Lord's anointing.

* You cannot run into enemy territory with common sense People seek two kinds of paths: which path will others recognize, or which path is the most stable. For this, they gather information from many directions. Living this way is bound to end in resentment. You end up resenting others, blaming yourself, or living in regret. But a life lived by asking the Lord is not the choice of others or of yourself (greed), so you do not regret and are glorious. Therefore, you must choose: either slowly sink at the level of common sense or rely on the Lord and run into enemy territory.

* Gentleness is toward people; faithfulness is before the Lord. If you are faithful before people, it is not faithfulness but hypocrisy. Faithfulness without expecting a response is the right thing. Appear lonely for the Lord; the Lord will fill you.

The gentle person (one who can control reactions) is blessed. They will inherit the land as a possession. It is not 'if' you might be wounded, but 'when' you will be wounded that you must prepare for. We must meet wounds with love.
Incorrect interest is called obsession. It is a strong warning that there will be no peace. It is also a mistake that chooses misfortune.

* The secret of relationships The sharp intellect and decisive judgment of my relationship will keep it smart—this is only my delusion. Relationships are a blessing and God must guard them. After sin and fleshly desires entered the world through Adam, relationships are destroyed first. Relationships are guarded by the fruit of the Spirit. I say to you: Live by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh. -Galatians 5:16
Choose only what is right. Never regret. It is doing work that glorifies and blesses.

A person with many options does not rely on the Lord. God's people not only live simply but also do not have much variety to choose from. If a complicated cockpit is an unbeliever's, then having just one switch is the believer's. The name of that switch is 'faith.' Use faith. Hope and believe.

* Humility Everyone worries that they might be humbled, that others might despise them, and they boast. A person of faith should rather worry about the pride of the heart. We must lower ourselves as much as possible in obedience to the Lord's will. Only then can the Lord use us.
How to consume wine
A perfect painting does not come from a perfect sketch. Faith is not following your own stubbornness but following God's guidance.
I came and went. Have a happy day today. If I get the chance, I will definitely go to England ^^^^

I came and went~ It seems like our country is still a nice place to live~ Someday I'll go to England~! I want to go to America too ㅠ If the opportunity is given, I want to go then. Have a pleasant evening~~!!

*When the path is not visible, when darkness covers all, the Lord becomes my path. Many people in the world forcefully point out the direction of the path, but the only path is Jesus. I am the way, the truth, and the life...
A priest-king does not fear people. Neither does he hate them. This is because he knows that he has been called to something far greater.

The word 'king' means being first in that field, and 'ruling' means continuously exercising broad influence. We must become irreplaceable people with high authority and influence in a specific realm. That realm is life itself. Romans 5:17
*When faith in Jesus wavers, it is because there is no faith. It is because you do not know who God is and do not believe in Jesus. When you love Jesus, you will not live that way.
My King, my God, I will exalt You and bless Your name forever.
Why do you study? Why do you work? Why do you labor so hard? Why can you endure so much? Why do you pray, fast, and reach out your hands even while crying tears? Because my dream is love.

*Clear and Simple solution! If there is disorder in life, there must be a solution that matches it. That is a clear goal (apprehended by Christ Jesus) and burning passion (I press on). This is the response that appears when you know Jesus. Know Jesus. Know Him more. Continue~~ Not that I have already obtained this or have already become perfect, but I press on to make it my own, because Christ Jesus has made me His own. Philippians 3:12

Why do Christians live in the world without power? First reason: because they treat God's grace as merely fire insurance... God's grace fills you with power greater than Daniel or John the Baptist. Grace is what is needed in every field.

Why the power of the blood is the only way to overcome all trials and challenges that humans face is because there is no other name given under heaven by which salvation can be obtained. Only Jesus is the way, the truth, and the life. All schemes and calculations that do not hold fast to Jesus are only suffering like kicking against the goads. The Lord is the answer to all problems, the source of all happiness, and the Savior worthy of praise from eternity to eternity.

Why do you worry and deliberately seek anxiety that you were told not to have? Because you cannot be grateful for what has been given. Trust in the Lord's sovereignty and be bold. The Lord has already given you much. My grace is sufficient for you.

Why can't you die to yourself and are dragged along by worldly values? Because you have not resolved to lose everything. Because you have not nailed to the cross all things except the Lord so they cannot lead you. Unless you have the heart to give up everything to gain only the Lord, the roller coaster of faith will continue. But those who seek to lose their life will gain it. I invite you to Saturday gatherings.
If you ask why I am in this place, I would answer Jesus. Since you asked the reason, I tell the reason. Jesus is the only reason for my life.

Why do you waver? When the foundation is not solid, the higher you go, the more you shake. Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God - Ephesians 1:1 "I am an apostle of Christ. I am an instrument of Christ. I am a spokesman proclaiming God's will." This is Paul's confession. That is why he does not shake in prison, being stoned, robbed, in river danger, or beaten with 39 lashes. Do you lack conviction? Then you will shake. Hold fast to truth and calling and move forward. If you lack conviction, pray until you hear. If you lack conviction, hold fast to God's Word until you hear. You are Christ's irreplaceable conviction in this world and the only hope.
There's something that makes me bow my head to speak informally... Why do I keep feeling guilty?...
When loneliness challenges you, you must choose one of two things. Either sympathize alone in solitude, or lift your eyes and empathize with the needs of others...
If you are lonely, spend time with those who are even more marginalized and in the shadows. The Lord Himself is there.
*The name of failure. Failure is a powerful signal that I must advance further toward the Lord and be completely filled by Him. It is so precious. The fact that I need the Lord...

Othniel, a left-handed judge, killed King Eglon and saved Israel, and there was peace for 40 years. Resolution in life and peace in relationships come from the Lord. It is possible for a nation, a people, and an individual to have peace and be redeemed when you act in faith.
Cooking is affection. Toward the other person...
I do not like cooking. I am impatient by nature, finding unnecessary and inefficient things bothersome. So I do not cook. I only do what the people I love like.

*The weight of hatred and love. The warm love from important people who understand me well is far more precious to my heart than the wound given by unimportant people who do not even know me well. Focus on what matters and let the chaff blow away. That is love. Pursue love - 1 Corinthians 4:1

For Joseph, life in prison was simple. In the midst of monotonous daily routines, Joseph's only option was to look to the Lord. To look in faith to God who guides me, holds my life, and never makes mistakes. Only then did peace come to him even in the suffering of prison, and he even began to have composure. Then he began to read the expression of the chief cupbearer and he came into view. That was the beginning.

Joseph looked to and was directed toward the Lord from his position in prison. David pursued the dry wilderness and was directed toward the Lord through poetry. Abraham in his barren, lifeless age saw the heavens that the Lord showed him and was directed toward the Almighty Lord. Direction is more important than speed.

Joseph did not consciously try to dream. Because the Lord gave it. But he treasured it in his heart. By renewing the mind and being transformed, we discern what God's will is, abandon greed and ambition, and redeem the time. Those with foolish hearts cannot pass puberty even after middle age.

Joseph was not a person preoccupied only with his own problems. When he observed the cupbearer's face, he found the opportunity of life and the clue to solving his own problems. Lift your eyes and serve. Through my problems and the service to others, their lives are set free.

Joseph's life was full of problems. There was no ability to solve them or understand them. When the confusion that appeared only as misfortune passed and he advanced with faithfulness and reverence, he finally realized that all this was God's intention to save lives. The Lord's thoughts are different from my emotions.
*Why grace is needed. The more sinful, flawed, and weak I am, the more it proves that I need Jesus. Do you feel lacking? You must draw near to Jesus. He will give abundantly and will not rebuke.

Joseph's dream was great. The sun and eleven stars bowed to his star, and his brothers' sheaves of grain bowed to his sheaf. Simply put, 'only I succeed.' Right after giving this dream comes the threat of death from his brothers, becoming a slave, going to prison, becoming a convict. In that place of suffering and failure, Joseph learns loyalty and learns complete trust. Prison and failure are not the end but become a jewel where he discovers God's will and returns again. To fully realize the dream..., suffering is a blessing.

If Joseph had believed 'I will go home this year,' his faith would have been shipwrecked. Joseph believed in the God who was with him. That is why he could be led on a path he had never traveled. Do not force your desires to be realized, but look back at whether the Lord is with you. The Lord asks you: Where is your faith?
*Faith is a gift. Faith is a gift, but it grows as you use it.

When Joseph interpreted the cupbearer's dream, he thought he had found an opportunity that would open the path for the rest of his life. At that time, the Lord instead gave him two more years of silence in prison, endurance, prayer, and seeking only the Lord. You know what 'steaming' is when cooking rice, right? This is what makes rice become rice. Without steaming, you eat uncooked rice.

Joseph did not curse his circumstances because the one who allowed that environment was God. Sometimes high love may cause pain because we cannot immediately understand it, but in His time, everything will be accomplished.

These days from Deuteronomy devotions, I share what might be curious. The law is not made of one thing. The law consists of: first, ethical law - law in which God's character and intention are directly manifested (e.g., the Ten Commandments); second, civil law; third, ceremonial law; fourth, health law. Among these, the laws abolished by the cross include civil law of the Israelites and sacrificial law prefiguring Jesus' sacrifice. Civil law is the spatial and temporal application of the Ten Commandments, so we no longer need to keep the external formal forms specifically explained to Israel, but the principles have not been abolished. Also, laws that cannot be abolished are: health law, which is unchangeable as long as human cells do not change, and the ethical and moral law of the Ten Commandments, which is unchangeable as long as human nature does not change.
Facebook has been quiet these days ^^ It's not because of the Olympics, is it...? You're doing well, right?
These days when we have Sunday meetings..., for some reason I feel a bit like I'm a 'master of the craft.'

These days it's a smart era where we can confirm each other from afar and communicate each other's news. But Christians have for thousands of years, though physically apart, understood each other's situations in real-time through love and intercession for one another. When there is love, even though separated, we become one. When we become one, the world sees the glory of the Lord.
Requests and prayers must be sought with love as motivation. The Bible says that is the better way.
Desire + Anxiety = Obsession. Purpose + Passion = Focus. Life - Faith = Worry. Life + Faith = Hope. 2Life = Worry + Hope (Life is either hoping or worrying)
If you look to the Lord beyond greed and ambition, you will surely see the Lord.
Rather than pursuing your desires, pursue God's will. He will accomplish the purpose and give you peace and glory.
Pursuing desires will make you anxious, but pursuing your calling will make you earnest. Escape anxiety and remain in peace.

*Unrelenting worry. Do not love money but consider what you have as sufficient. He Himself said: I will never leave you or forsake you. Therefore we can boldly say: The Lord is my helper; I will not be afraid. What can man do to me? - Hebrews 13:5,6. Simply put: 'Please stop worrying so much! Haven't I said I will help you?' That's the meaning.
You must not pursue your desires but fulfill the Lord's will. To fulfill the Lord's will, you must become a person who pleases God's heart.
Job's three friends tried to explain easily. Job's suffering was a size they could not handle. Suffering also comes seeing the person. Before giving answers, please first empathize.

The forgiving one VS the one who hates. 1. To his brothers, Joseph was not so much a younger brother but a son favored by their father, so they wanted to kill him. 2. Joseph recognized his brothers as family given by God, and he forgives his brothers who gave him an indelible wound. -> 'It is my son's robe' VS 'I am your brother Joseph'. 3. Many people realize: 'Ah, Joseph was wounded but forgave because of God.' Yet in reality, no relationship improvement occurs. There is a problem in application. 4. I was the brothers. The fault is mine for not recognizing them as God's people given to me. So I hated, envied, and was jealous of many Josephs beside me and wanted to kill them. If I recognize myself as Joseph, it's a pleasant realization, but when I discover that I am the brothers behind the veil of hypocrisy, weeping and repentance burst forth. Have you seen change without repentance?

*Victory over success. Daniel was a successful top political figure who transcended regimes and nations, a wonderful superior, and a loyal subordinate. His career and influence extended beyond the ancient Near East to other nations. However, what Daniel wanted was not such rosy success but the 'present holiness' of setting his purpose (Daniel 1:8). When you are holy, the Lord establishes you. The Lord needs holy people.
You are not forgiven and understood and then loved. You forgive and understand because you love.
Forgive. Otherwise you will become like that person. And you will marry such a person.
Our beloved young people are now starting Saturday gathering praise. Though in Korea, my heart swells. Our Lord God is worthy of all glory.

The enemy that blocks the amazing plan of God for our lives is the sin of stubborn pride. We can see this in King Saul. 1. He blamed others; 2. He concealed his own faults; 3. He justified himself. And above all, he was more concerned about how he appeared to people than what God thought of him. Compare the confessions of David and Saul. It becomes clear. 'God, don't take Your Holy Spirit from me! All I want is to please You. I know I have failed You but please forgive me. Cleanse my heart.' - David from Psalm 51. 'Don't take my kingdom from me. Please stand with me so I won't look bad in front of my people.' - King Saul from 1 Samuel 15:22-35. Pride leads people to destruction.
If what we pursued earnestly in our lives was love, we have no regrets.

Before our almighty God, nothing about me is hidden or unknown to Him. Moreover, He has faithful and wonderful purposes and plans for my life, and all the days of those plans are recorded in the Lord's book before they even begin. This means the Lord is with me not only beside me but also in my heart and thoughts.

* The courage to stand at the cliff's edge in the workplace - With a junior colleague's request, stand at the cliff's edge with courage. Do not let the forced conditions of this world hold you; acknowledge that your very being is by God's grace, entrust all things to Him, and do not fear fate. Believe that in all matters there is God's permission and that the God who turns evil into good is working behind all of this. Storms are not overcome by rowing; problems must be solved with humility and reverence. The purpose of the storm is humbling and obedience. Fear not, entrust all things, and like Daniel, draw a spiritual line and act consistently. Do not fear those who merely breathe; stand calmly at the cliff's edge. Daniel purposed in his heart... -Daniel 1:8
Our good Father God gives us grace. One of those graces is 'opportunity.'

Even when we don't know the path we're taking and it seems dark, we can rejoice because the Holy Spirit is present. When the Holy Spirit is with us, regardless of our own ability, God's purposes are poured out and begin to be fulfilled. We rely on the Holy Spirit.

We cannot prove that we are valuable and meaningful beings. Many people in the world are busy trying to prove this, expending their energy. Our value as human beings is discovered in God. After leaving behind the joy of a full catch, when Peter stood before Jesus, he saw his true self and became a fisher of men. The qualification to live in our true form, not clinging to meaningless or worthless things, becomes possible only when we stand before the Lord.

When we pray, sometimes God permits both paths. This is because God sees the hidden motivations within us. This is His consideration for my weakness. The truly wise person here returns to their original heart and prays the question anew.

If we love someone, we cannot turn a blind eye to their pain and difficulties. We would grieve and, if we could help, willingly suffer in their place. God's love overcomes all things. I bless you to possess the tears of the Lord.

* The power of persistence - If you don't stop, you win. Because Jesus becomes the purpose and love for Jesus becomes the passion. I do not say that I have already obtained it, nor that I have already been made perfect, but I press on to take hold of that for which Christ Jesus took hold of me. -Philippians 3:12

We are not permitted to wound anyone, but there is one being to whom it is uniquely permitted. The devil. Remind and declare the devil's destination to him. Restrain him so that the one already deposed cannot accuse us. The more we do this, the devil's heart is hurt and he must attend an inner healing seminar, so he bows his head and flees. ^^

The grace we enjoy is not a given. Behind it lies a leader with a pained heart who bleeds. They labor for the flock as if they themselves are debtors. I give thanks to God for giving us good pastors and churches.

The peace we enjoy is a peace that the world cannot know or understand. Because one must know Jesus to know that peace, yet the world has no interest in and does not know Jesus. I leave you my peace; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. -John 14:27

The reason we lack boldness is that we distance ourselves from Jesus's suffering. That glorious Son of God, to save us who had no possibility whatsoever, was dragged along by those led by sinners, limping and in quick steps, having to be beaten much. That the very image of God's substance was dragged to His death in this way. If this suffering is in your heart, it means you cannot continue living like this. There is nothing to be proud of, nothing to boast about. There is nothing but the cross...

What we take for granted and receive is, in fact, all the boundless grace of the Lord. Having money and leisure does not automatically make the journey happy. The peace and joy the Lord gives are entirely grace and therefore must be sought through prayer.
The sufferings we endure are not worthy of comparison with the glory we will enjoy in the future.
* Not chance but necessity - It is not meeting the age wrongly or having bad luck; the Lord has guided us thus. It means we must become lower and obey humbly. The Lord is almighty.

There is a reason why we should not have anxiety in our hearts: it is the declaration that the almighty God dwells among us as Immanuel. Because we believe in God and He has confirmed 'believe in Jesus,' we can approach His throne without any fear.

* When working hard but nothing changes - We work hard, make time, do our best until the last moment, but there is no change and we remain in the same place. It is because we do not transcend our limits. It is not a matter of diligence; we must change our class.

When we have many spiritual experiences and receive smooth guidance and receive praise and support from other believers, with everyone cheering and rejoicing, if we turn away from the cross-bearing Jesus who stood silently at the edge of that cheering crowd, that will be the most tragic failure and a nauseating thing. More important than the gift is the One who gives the gift.

If we live by faith, even when heaven collapses, we find a way to live. Among people of faith dwells another. At the Last Passover meal, the disciples ate Jesus's life, flesh, and blood, but Jesus drank the cup of judgment and wrath toward all humanity without remainder. When Daniel's three friends were thrown into the fiery furnace, a fourth person was among them. We are not alone, and among us is Jesus who drinks a different cup for us. The king said, 'Look! I see four men walking around in the fire, unbound and unharmed, and the fourth looks like a son of the gods.' -Daniel 3:25 This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you. -Luke 22:20
