When we share the grace we have received, sometimes we discover new grace. Because when I become a channel of grace, that is God's will, and therefore I experience God's help. The pipe of grace never runs dry.
We cannot help but speak of what we have seen and heard.

Our busyness comes from the desire to show a perfect image. The Lord earnestly desires that we offer our entire being to Jesus without reservation. A clean person is one who frequently offers their dirty, foul-smelling feet to Jesus. Those who hide become dirty, and those who reveal themselves become clean. The power of holiness is the power to reveal.

* Faith that leaves familiarity - He commanded us to leave what was familiar and what we depended on, and to go forward without showing us where to go. This means God Himself will guide us to our destination. Leave your country, your people and your father's household and go to the land I will show you. -Genesis 12:1

We have survived not because we are strong, but only because of His endless patience and compassion. Speaking loudly and taking pride are mere appearances; we survive only because of His unfailing love.

The promised land we hope for is not a place where conditions are good and milk and honey flow by themselves. It is a land where we advance and conquer through the relationship between God and me. Since we will surely die without God's help, we confess that we will die and move forward.

We say 'What shall we do?' Acts 2:37 When our heart is pricked, we cannot simply maintain our dignity; it will rot and produce poison. Naturally, repentance asking 'What should we do?' must come. We hope for humble repentance in worship, in the place of God's Word, in the place of grace, in the place of prayer, and in the place of fellowship, where we bow down and prostrate ourselves. This is what God desires. When people struck Stephen to death, they too had pricking in their hearts, but they ignored it and instead became persecutors, murderers, and enemies of the cross. Never ignore being pricked or God's Word.
Spiritual growth begins when we face our true selves. It is very painful. But since it is something that needs to be dealt with, it cannot be skipped.
We cannot be saved more than once in this world, but that revelation must come afresh into our hearts every day. I believe, Yes I believe.
As we live our lives, we must seriously consider: Will we first build the ark of salvation according to the Lord's will? Or will we first build the Tower of Babel for our own glory?
* Humility - Humility is becoming low before God. But the verification of that humility is also becoming low before people.
The only reason we can deny ourselves and take up the cross is because Jesus bore us and took up the cross.
We are not defeated by enemies because we are weaker than them. It is because we oppose evil with the same evil spirit they possess. Overcome evil with good.
Even when we don't know the Lord well or misunderstand Him, the Lord knows us very well. You have searched me, Lord, and you know me. -Psalm 139:1

* Love is... When Peter was asked if he loved the Lord, he hesitated. To say he loved seemed too insufficient, and to say otherwise seemed ambiguous. Love is to be captured. That is why it moves us. What doesn't move is not love.

If we live being chased, with worry and anxiety, it is because we live only for ourselves. A life that lives by bread alone is always hurried and difficult. A life that lives by God's Word, a life that lives only as the Lord gives grace, is balanced and abundant through the Lord's sovereignty.

The most reasonable deal we can make with God is to glorify God while taking care of ourselves to some extent. But the Lord refuses any such transaction. He sees hidden motives and judges according to deeds. To the humble He gives grace; the proud He turns away.

* Love is... Love possesses me. That is why when we see pain, we can know love. If we hurt because of that brother, then that brother exists; if we cry because of her, then she exists. What fills me when God has not given is called greed and covetousness. Therefore, covetousness is called idolatry. Covetousness is idolatry because it takes the place of the Lord, the place that fills me. -Colossians 3:5

The first goal of receiving God's guidance is to lead us into an ever closer relationship with Jesus. All other goals should be secondary to that. If we do the Lord's work but depart from the Lord's glory and concern, everything must cease and we must return to the cross.
We can never satisfy someone who loves us. We will even disappoint them. But behind that refining comes salvation for all and glory to the Lord.

* The blessing of the rocky field - More blessed than coming to fertile soil and easily enjoying much is to till the rocky field. The valley of Achor is not where cursed people go; it is a privilege that one called by the glorious God can receive. Then we gain the ability to not seize all honor, glory, and authority and instead return them to the Lord.
We do not promote ourselves or boast about ourselves. We are not nailed for the sake of the world; Jesus was nailed.

* Focus is guidance - If we lose focus on the Lord, our attention is stolen by our circumstances, we calculate situations, and ultimately fall into fear. It seems we will be outdated and become losers, so we cling to this and that and pursue what is vain. When we offer even our last self-esteem before God and hope only in the Lord, only then will we experience the Lord using and guiding us. If I pursue, I only fall further away.
We have limits in loving others. That is, we can only love 'as yourself.' If the Lord does not accept me as He received me, I cannot love others.
We become low. We will continue to become lower until we weep. We will weep until we break. Then we will discover the true peace that the world cannot give.

* Calling - I am not used because I want to be used. I am used when the Lord calls. Worrying about circumstances and boasting about popularity are nothing but futile fantasies. Only the Lord is the one and only One who can open my path.

Regardless of the titles we hold and others' evaluations of us, at every moment we must choose whether we will be real or fake. The standard is determined by whether my life's motivation is the Lord or not. If we can gain the Lord and lose all things, this decision becomes easy and, on the contrary, we will gain all things.

We are too thirsty for victory. We must win in academics and work, in relationships and in the world. We must surely survive and win. This only victory comes from faith alone. That is why the righteous concentrate on faith. Faith wins. Everyone born of God overcomes the world. This is the victory that has overcome the world, even our faith. -1 John 5:4

When we remember someone, we do not recall information about that person. What we remember more and wish to remember is the meaning in that person's heart. So the information will fade but the impressiveness will deepen. In God's heart are my thoughts entirely. From anyone in this world, how can we receive such a heartfelt, moving love?

* True opportunity comes only through humbling - Do you believe in the Lord's sovereignty and that Jesus is Lord? Then stand fearlessly at the cliff's edge. Only through humbling are we used, and only through dying do we live.

We don't express it openly, but sometimes we harbor an irreverent complaint that the Lord does not supply sufficiently or resolve matters. When we can't take even one more step, we may explode saying, 'What more do you want from me?' But the Lord quietly speaks: 'My grace is sufficient for you.'
We must seek stronger power. To sacrifice more and love more...

* The power of choice to become a shining star Daniel was an exceedingly glorious person among God and people, believers and unbelieving colleagues, and received the evaluation of shining like the stars of heaven (Dan 12:3). This was already determined when he was taken captive long ago (Dan 1:8). Make a firm decision and determine your future now.

We become like two types of people. First, we become like those we love, because we constantly think about and cherish them in our hearts. Second, we become like those we don't forgive, because we hold them in our hearts with hatred even while refusing to forgive them. That's why we feel attracted to familiar people and eventually marry them.
* A grace leader is one who extends grace. If this is not happening, everyone will thirst. Therefore, a leader must become a repentant person, one who bleeds.
We will surely face trials. We cannot escape them, because this faith must be shown to be real. When trials come, it becomes clear who we truly believed in and loved.

We must serve humbly toward the neglected and uncharted places. For this, we must prepare much and polish ourselves. If we are lazy during the time of preparation and polishing, later when we must humble ourselves and serve, we will not serve sufficiently. Even serving humbly requires skill.
* Sin is not endured but discarded. Jesus is so good and lovely—worthy of having sin discarded for Him.
We want to gain all the fruit. Then we must sow. Even if we weep, now is the time to sow.
We worry and fear. Will people look down on me? Will they blame and isolate me? Will I become society's outcast? We fret in anxiety. But the Lord asks us this: Where is your faith?—Luke 8:25

* The fear of judgment, the peace of love. If we compare and calculate evaluatively, we will certainly have anxiety and fear because we are full of unfulfilled things. But if we let go of everything and love, we will have peace because we are fulfilling the most important purpose. Loving is doing the very best.
We must fight a spiritual war to enter the kingdom of heaven. That war cannot be fought with emotions, logic, power, or popularity. We can only be victorious through God's Word, which is truth.
If we give only our own things, we give only impurities. That is why we must first seek 'Him' to come and fill us.

We have an instinct to protect ourselves. But we can never protect ourselves, and it only makes relationships difficult. Only the Lord touches my heart. As people dead and risen in the Lord, we no longer need to defend ourselves.

* To obtain Isaac, Abraham obtained his son twice. Once at age one hundred, and again tens of years later on Mount Moriah. The first time he obtained him through patience, but the second time through surrender. To obtain Isaac, you must give Isaac.

We are weak people unable to protect ourselves. How futile is the attempt to protect ourselves by our own strength. True peace and resolution come when we entrust ourselves to the hand of the Almighty.

We are not called to distribute information (rumors). That is not what an anointed person should do. We are called to testify to the cross and make disciples, and receiving the anointing gives us power. In this sense, regardless of age or position, we are teachers.

We resort to human methods to avoid pain, but we do not know that such cleverness brings greater pain. Standing upright before the Lord is difficult at first, but it becomes the most peaceful and happy way.

* The power to advance. When life doesn't open up and we mark time, we become discouraged. It's a matter of priorities. One thing is clear: repentance comes before advancement. If we are not changed, we cannot change others.
We often disagree with or criticize others' opinions. The Lord also often does not agree with our opinions, but He is always with us.

We desperately need the Lord's interference and rulership. Acknowledging the Lord's sovereignty means abandoning our strength and praying. Not mere formality in prayer, but overflowing, abundant prayer. 'Lord, reign over us.'
* The Lord's Omnipotence. Humble yourselves and repent. And wait. This is the Lord's Word.

We must determine our future now. If we live by the flesh, we will surely die; if we put to death the flesh by the Spirit, we will surely live. For this to be possible, we must have a heart that fears the Lord. We must worship.
We are called to bless. Moreover, the Lord has given us such authority and power. When we bless in the Lord's name, simply 'being together' can make us truly happy.

We tend to entrust big steps to the Lord. Not so. Before we take even one step, we must already entrust it, and after we take it, we must accept the circumstances the Lord permits. Fight where He told you to fight in faith, and let go where He told you to let go.
We must believe and pray that God's kingdom—that is, God's most powerful rule—comes upon us and our land. We hope to enjoy that rule and presence 14 hours from now. The Lord does this.

We want solutions. And we want them right now. But God has a slightly different perspective. What God wants from us is to 'make efforts to know God.' By crying out in problems and hearing His voice, we come to know the greater God the Father more than the solution itself. Make efforts to know God.

We do not make the improvement of works our primary purpose. Change in life is a result of change in essence. So we don't forcefully impose life. True freedom doesn't come from my satisfaction but depends on where I belong. In Jesus, there is no condemnation. Also, did you not read in the law that the priests in the temple break the Sabbath and are guiltless?—Matthew 12

Gentleness is the attitude of regarding God's way of dealing with us as good, not doubting or resisting. Circumstances are also under the Lord's sovereignty, and through them we will learn what we must learn. Let us entrust everything to the Lord's hands.

What sustains us is not our job, education, or career. It is the Lord who sustains me. Then who indeed should we fear? Therefore, believers in Jesus are not intimidated by such deals. We can commit our lives to the Lord and go.
We do not read the Bible to change God's will toward us. We read the Bible to change our thoughts about God.
* Obstacles to His presence, masks. We must come before God naked. A packaged appearance, a masked self, cannot meet God. God does not meet with fakes.

God's passion for us has never been abandoned or cooled. From eternity to eternity, His passion for us will continue, and the enemy who came to harm will be ashamed and retreat. Though your hand be lifted high, Lord, they will not see; when they see your zeal for your people, they will be put to shame. And fire will consume your adversaries.—Isaiah 26:11

For God's power to be fully manifested in us, our self must die. How much must we die? We must be humbled to the level of an earthen vessel. So the apostle Paul says that when he was weak, God's power was perfected in him. If anything besides Jesus remains to trust in us, it must be completely discarded.

* The calling of a minister. The minister's calling is to flow God's Word so that people love Jesus. Also, to make those who reject the cross uncomfortable by stretching out their legs, making them unable to sleep. Peace without the cross is sin.

Prayer partners are extremely important to us. Having someone who can pray for you and share grace is like having a conduit for the Holy Spirit. The Holy Spirit works through love and forgiveness. Hudson Taylor, a missionary to China, said: Even if you fast, read the Bible, and pray all day long, without prayer partners, you will eventually collapse.
The Lord has not permitted us everything. When we try to give answers to everything, we leave deep frustration and many wounds. Say yes to yes and no to no. If you force anything, it will break.

* The infallibility of Scripture vs. my fallibility. There are times when Scripture meets me. But there is absolutely no possibility that Scripture will change. Since creation, it has never happened, and it never will. Ultimately, what must change is not Scripture but me. God never apologizes or compromises.

Our continued pain and wounds are not because we don't know God. Rather, it is because we think we know God too well and try to take His place. So small things become great disasters. Do not fear. Shall I take God's place?—Genesis 50:19. God turns evil into good. Joseph is not someone who opposes this but someone who obeys and follows. God changed it to good, and as it is today, He intends to save the lives of all people.—Genesis 50:20. It is not easy for a wounded person to speak like this. Only someone whose wounds have become glory through the Lord's love can speak such words.

Everything that happens to us has a reason. Behind that reason, He stands. Through that reason, we must return to God. Do not confront the problem through the problem, but approach Him who speaks through that problem.

Among the things that happen to us, there are many unfair and incomprehensible things. Such things discourage us and make us abandon the values we hold. But if we overcome prejudice with tolerance, oppression with compassion, and hatred with love, we will see God's glory.
* The definition of achievement. My hard work is not achievement. Achievement is what God accomplishes.
What we need is not a response of love but to love with the Lord's heart 'simply' as is. That is our calling.

We have unavoidable suffering. It is suffered for the Lord and for the saints. When we feel the strength fading and we can no longer bear it, at that critical moment overcome by God's help, we will see the Lord's glory. Be courageous; much is prepared.
We have been given many instruments and gifts for God's great purpose. But if we are not careful and those strengths and gifts take the Lord's place, that would be a sad thing.

* Direction comes from continuity. A community progresses gradually but consistently in one direction because there is a leader aiming toward that goal. If there is a clear purpose and goal sufficient to overcome all obstacles, eventually the community will move toward that direction. It is not easy to abandon other things to gain Jesus, and much resistance is encountered, but ultimately the person who goes all the way becomes a true leader. Because it is the way we must go, and there is no other way.
Our deepest life message sometimes comes from our deepest pain. That is why God sometimes uses pain to fully accomplish the purpose He has set in our lives.
Our greatest mistake is not allowing God to do what only God can do. An even greater mistake is to demand and resent that God-level work from family, friends, colleagues, and ourselves.

We desperately need salvation in our relationships, finances, character, work, studies, health, and vision. This means we need God's kingdom. But this salvation and heaven, which feel so distant and seem to require 40 days of fasting and prayer, are unexpectedly near. When I turn from sin, God's kingdom and God's rule are near. From that time Jesus began to preach, saying, 'Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.'—Matthew 4:17. Those who want to experience God's rule and salvation today, come to repent. 7:30 PM as held.

* God is Your Keeper. There is no being who is not precious before God. Especially for humans who bear God's image and are redeemed by His Son's blood. Even sparrows live within God's mercy. How much more precious am I than a sparrow?

* The Blessing of Relationships. When life's focus becomes distorted, unnecessary imaginations arise and relationships ultimately crumble through misunderstandings. There must be a peace in looking to the Lord and being willing to lose everything, and only then can we protect all things. Do not be afraid.

Our concern is to remove difficulties. However, the Lord's concern is that we be transformed through them and trust and depend entirely on Him alone. At least for now, such tribulations and sufferings will not be removed. This is because it is a higher priority that my pride, stubbornness, unbelief, and hatred be removed. Before I was afflicted I went astray, but now I keep Your word. -Psalm 119:67

Not everything we hear a voice say is correct. Prophecy must be according to the measure of faith. We know in part and prophesy in part, solely for edification. The only One to hold to is Jesus. Let the speaker be cautious and the listener be discerning.
Discern the voice. A man hears a voice: 'Kiss me.' A woman also hears a voice: 'I heard it too.' This is not the voice of the Holy Spirit but the 'voice of hormones.'

If you drive while drinking, some countries execute you. It means never to take the steering wheel while drunk. When there is worry, anxiety, and concern, you must not make any decisions or take action. You kill yourself and others. When excited or afraid is not the time to decide or act, but the time to pray and stand silent before the Lord.

Despair comes when we look only at circumstances and calculate. The worst way to calculate circumstances is by comparing with others. When faith is lost, the situation is always exaggerated and comparison always ends in despair.

Famine can come even in the promised land after receiving answers. It would have been difficult for Joseph, whose hands and feet were wounded in stocks and whose heart was infinitely broken, to believe that this was the Lord's guidance. Yet the Lord faithfully and ceaselessly continued His work and will faithfully accomplish His purposes. Behind all of this was Him. -Genesis 45:5 God was behind it.
When discouraged, eat, drink, and rest. This was God's first remedy for Elijah.

Practicing righteousness and becoming strong in weakness is not done by someone who has some enlightenment or strong execution ability. It just happens~. Only by faith..., living by faith. 'By faith they...performed acts of righteousness...received promises... became mighty in war... grew strong in weakness' -Hebrews 11:33
The righteous do not live by emotion. Only the righteous live by faith.
I hope you understand these three things. First, living according to God's will is our duty. Second, taking responsibility for such people is God's duty. Third, therefore there is nothing to fear.

Think about whether the devil would fear if this prayer were answered. Even if my desires were fulfilled and I became prosperous, the devil would not fear and tremble, but when the saints deny themselves and relinquish their rights, the powers of darkness crumble. The cross is power.
Choose either to resent this problem or to entrust it to the Lord's hands. The results are as different as heaven and earth.
Tonight, let us pray for all the members who desperately need prayer. Let us look only to the Lord who makes rivers flow through the dry and barren life of the wilderness.

No one in this world can change the past. But the Lord is the only One who can cover the ashamed person and completely restore them. The reason is that Jesus is the King of kings. There is no king in this world who wears such a thick crown of thorns and sheds blood for sinners. The Lord broke the power of death and Satan through the cross, forgave sins, and gave me life.

What was the most intense work of Satan in world history? World War II? 9/11? Neither. It was at Golgotha, the event of crucifying Jesus on the cross. Sinners defining the sinless Son of God as a sinner and nailing Him to the cross—there is no greater evil in history than this. Then what was the greatest good work in world history? It was precisely the event of Jesus being crucified on the cross at Golgotha. God transformed the most heinous act that Satan or those deceived by Satan could commit into the greatest good in this world. In soccer, this is called an own goal.
Do not fear whether I can bear this task. This work is not something I do nor my business. I am obeying what He will accomplish.

There is no one in this world who will not tremble before judgment. How terrifying it is that in Revelation the rich and kings wanted the mountains to collapse to hide from the gaze of the Lord. At the last day of judgment, there is no act or religion that conquers judgment, but 'mercy' is the only thing that conquers judgment. Show mercy now. Then the Lord will also show mercy to you on the day of judgment.

The love that best reflects God's love in this world is the love of parents. In childhood, my father seemed so strong and great, but as time passed, he became physically weak parents. The Almighty God became weak for us. He came in the form of sinful flesh to embrace sinners. That is the love of the Almighty. The love of the Almighty is for the strong to become weak, the One who does not hesitate to fall into the mud pit together for me covered with filth, the One who gives freedom so that He does not use force because of love but chooses to love of His own accord.

Blessed is the one who accomplishes the will of God concerning oneself in this world before leaving. Before Jesus breathed His last, He said, 'It is finished.' Because He bled and loved without regret.

The saints are not torn down by any attack and hindrance in this world. This is because there is no one who can separate us from the Lord's love. God Almighty is love, and that love is not abandoned. Neither height nor depth nor any other created thing can separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord. -Romans 8:39
The most certain thing at this moment is that we are truly grateful to God and that all things are immeasurable grace. That is sufficient. Other things hardly matter.
I pray that all souls who desperately need prayer at this moment may have God's peace.

On this late night, those who cannot yet sleep, or those in Korea where it is a clear morning but whose future and heart are still in night, spend this night this way. He alone knows the path I go; when He has tested me, I will come out as pure gold. Job 23:10 The Lord reveals His sovereignty through trials, grants repentance and forgiveness, and gives us fellow-laborers like Elihu.
Do not fear that this goodness will soon fade away; just be simply grateful. That is the complete response that pleases the Lord. The most powerful prayer is thanksgiving.

The only way to survive in this barren and dangerous wilderness-like world is to hear the Father's voice. To hear that voice and obey it. To hold to only the Lord's Word beneath all false signposts, obey, and go forward.

'If you do this, you'll be happy; you were unhappy because you didn't have this; if you just have this, no one can ignore you. You won't fail again and will stand confidently before others.' Satan says, 'Well done!'

If this work is for me, then I should fear and worry. But if it is given by God, regardless of circumstances and results, I will complete this race by faith. Even if the enemy comes to exploit weaknesses, threaten, and take everything away, I will not abandon this path because originally it was not done for my satisfaction and success.

Here we engage in fierce spiritual warfare, and in the busy preparations for the retreat, there is one thing we do not forget. That is you. Especially I will not forget and will pray for those of you in Korea. We will have a time at the retreat to pray specifically for those in Korea. Live in a manner worthy of the gospel. We will cry out in fervent prayer for you. Let my weakness not be the conclusion but the cross of the Lord be the conclusion. That is the truth. Truth has never changed.

Everyone who strives to overcome is restricted in freedom. Park Tae-hwan wore only swimming trunks in a narrow pool and entrusted himself to the water, and later became an Olympic gold medalist who enjoys everything.

Those who strive to overcome practice self-control. To achieve victory, we choose and concentrate. If you don't pray and don't ask others to pray even though you desire it, I assure you it will absolutely not be accomplished. You must truly believe what you desire. When the Holy Spirit comes, self-control follows.

Prayer for selfish purposes cannot continue. Because it is shameful. But some prayers pour out fiercely like fire. Prayer that comes from my spirit, receiving the Lord's heart, burning with emotion. Pray in the Spirit.
Show faith at such times. When will I take a single shot for the Lord? It's only a few days out of the year that I can devote to the Lord.
If there are so many hurting hearts, more must be offered.
Stop theoretical talk and live out the Word. -February 26, 2015

For those who already believe in Jesus and have become church members, what to hold is the cross and what to believe is the gospel. I believe in the gospel that saves all people, all things, all generations. For Greeks or Jews, for Christians or unbelievers...
This retreat had a value worthy of lifetime memory. All because of Jesus. Hallelujah~

This week's prayer meeting is at 7:30. This will probably be the last week of prayer. Prayer meetings will be suspended, but I hope you become warriors of prayer who continue to pray. Last week the Lord had us gather to pray almost daily for 12 weeks, overcame tests, and healed many areas. From next week, cell circulation will begin and Sunday evening leader meetings will probably handle series rather than prayer meetings. For example: Acts expansion - inner healing version, short-term mission project, LTC, FDTS, Youth Alpha Course, etc.

This week's devotional 1 Peter summary - Summary for those who missed this week's devotional. Please definitely do your devotional; Lauren Cunningham also leads 12,000 full-time missionaries through morning devotionals. God speaks through the Bible. 1 Peter is Peter's 'epistle of suffering' and 'epistle of hope and promise' written to suffering Christians. 1 Peter 1 - Purpose of suffering: So we won't be shamed that day but receive praise and glory. (1 Peter 1:7) The genuineness of your faith—more precious than gold that perishes though refined by fire—may result in praise, glory, and honor when Jesus Christ is revealed. 1 Peter 2 - Example of suffering: Follow our Boss, the Lord, as your example. (1 Peter 2:21) To this you were called, because Christ suffered for you, leaving you an example, that you should follow in His steps. 1 Peter 3 - Blessing of suffering: Suffering is not a waste. Hidden blessing—maturity and grace. (1 Peter 3:14) But even if you should suffer for what is right, you are blessed. Do not fear their threats; do not be frightened. 1 Peter 4 - Meaning of suffering: Stop drinking and living recklessly; live godly. (1 Peter 4:1) Therefore, since Christ suffered in His body, arm yourselves also with the same attitude, because whoever suffers in the body is done with sin. (1 Peter 4:2) As a result, they do not live the rest of their earthly lives for evil human desires, but rather for the will of God. 1 Peter 5 - Reward of suffering: Not a withered cabbage leaf but a crown that never withers. Both leaders and followers obtain a crown through suffering. (1 Peter 5:4) And when the Chief Shepherd appears, you will receive the crown of glory that will never fade away.
We will hold the dedication service this week and want to maintain Saturday meetings normally. However, it would be nice to have meals together and then hold the meeting. Or do it afterwards.
Starting this Friday through Saturday, I'm responsible for grace. Please entrust it to me.
Strangely, oppa's posts aren't uploading to Facebook well lately... Why is that......
Counsel on dating - Do not pray for 3 billion different men; pray for the one man prepared for you.

The characteristic of a proud person in this world is that they rely on themselves, and the response is fear. That is why the Lord says not to fear. A person who fears is a proud person. - It is this: Israel says, 'My hand has saved me.' Now tell the people, 'Whoever is afraid and trembling, let him turn back and leave Mount Gilead.' -Judges 7:2,3

The Israelites wore only one set of clothes and shoes for 40 years of wilderness life. They were unsophisticated nomads who never went near a school. In contrast, the Canaanites were an agricultural people with more advanced centralized royal authority and experience and skill. God tells us not to envy or be intimidated by any of these things. He also told us not to accept idols trying to learn them. (At that time, the custom required idol exchange to be a prerequisite in trade and commerce.) The Lord protected and multiplied a people who had no army but had Levites, no powerful chariots and refined temples but had 'worship.' The world envies academic credentials, wealth, and fame and wants everything to go well. But God's people hold to one important thing. That one thing makes our weakness strong and our strength weak. That one thing is holding to Jesus.

Israel was not a nation guarded by an army. It was a nation kept when priests were faithful. Even though the people sinned and the king fell into corruption, as long as the priests' eyes were alive, that people had hope. If my thanksgiving is offered and living sacrifice is presented, we are already victorious.
How precious is the power of the blood? The hymn about the blood is so wonderful. But we must not sing it carelessly without meaning. When we sing it with deep confession, we become so grateful.
The reason is not that I am spiritual, but because Jesus took my dirty sins and carried them away.
Those who pursue profit cannot bear loss. Those who pursue honor cannot endure shame. Those who love the Lord and love Him with all their life can bear anything.

I have erased from memory what I did in the past. I do not even dream of saying I have fully achieved it now. Even if believers have heard about holiness, repentance, and judgment, yet rationalize their sins and uncleanness according to their own standards and do not change, I will not grow weary or disappointed. The Lord sees, the Lord hears, and the Lord knows me. I will only become lower and more prostrate as the Lord desires, and become holy like Jesus. I do not say that I have already obtained it, nor that I have already been made perfect, but I press on to take hold of that for which Christ Jesus took hold of me. Brothers, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus. —Philippians 3:12-14
Now I run toward the end. I ask for your intercession that the healing be completely finished to the end.
Now cease speaking and come before the throne.
I just saw the message now. I had a good birthday. Thank you for taking care of me. Have a joyful day today too ^^

The president of Egypt stepped down through eighteen days of civil unrest. However, thirty-five hundred years ago in Egypt, when the people of Israel ate lamb and sprinkled blood, the tyrant Pharaoh fell. What does my problem have to do with catching lamb? By the world's eyes, what relevance does a death-row prisoner Jesus from two thousand years ago have to my problems in the twenty-first century? Yet that is God's method. Come to the altar and become the offering and die. When rights are surrendered, you will see God's glory.

Business is better than economy, and first class is better than business. However, if it is a falling airplane, before the matter of class, you must fundamentally ride an airplane that does not fall. Jesus never falls.
Love without understanding is violent love. We can understand as much as affection, and love as much as we know.
A familiar place seems safe, but it is the most dangerous place. Because we cannot experience God there.

All relationships between humans—husband and wife, teacher and student, parent and child, friends who stake their lives—combined, can only barely illustrate our relationship with God. The oneness and completeness cannot be fully expressed because there is nothing comparable to God's love.
For humans, abandoning idols is an impossible thing. It is something that must be done while holding the cross and obeying, something that must be done by receiving the Holy Spirit and being burned.

Humans are corrupted and decayed beings. Though Moses and Paul are great people, that I am I is by God's grace. God is great. Therefore, the church does not produce heroes. Too much praise and encouragement is not fitting. We give praise only to the name of Jesus.

The maximum that human wisdom can say is 'think rationally.' It is a word frequently used by securities analysts. But the problem is that such rational conclusions are almost never realized. The plan prepared by the Lord is fulfilled.

One of the most foolish things humans do is waste their short lives trying to possess much. Yet the more they do this, the more impoverished life becomes. After having to vomit out what they accumulated trying to recover wasted health and relationships, they feel they have arrived at life's end at an awkward time. Therefore, Satan's strategy is not to block but to let them live moderately.

The 'righteousness' humans possess is relative 'ragged righteousness.' Ultimately it is determined by 'convenience' related to one's profit and subjectivity. It is not about truth but about what helps me, so allies are created and enemies are created. When we take off our shoes before God and become servants of the Lord, we will be freed from 'self.'

From a human perspective, when we face Abraham's test, we look sideways at the 'ram' caught in the thicket early on, hoping to gain some evidence. If there were some more certain sign on this path I walk, it would be much easier. But the Lord does not reveal the ram's existence until the final moment. If Abraham had gone knowing that the ram was prepared beforehand, he could not have become God's friend. Shall I hide from him what I am about to do? Genesis 8:17

Human help cannot build God's kingdom. When we work hard in the place the Lord has appointed, the Lord receives glory and brings revival. Just as not everyone who laughs is really laughing, not everything that looks good is actually good.
Guidance. The Bible uses guidance and anointing together. All who are led by the Spirit of God are sons of God. —Romans 8:14

When life stands stubbornly before us, if we perceive it as an obstacle, we will become discouraged. It is not so much an obstacle or deficiency as it is the Lord's request to increase the depth and level of prayer. How can a life of enjoying without prayer be the same as a life of pouring out tears, fasting, and breaking through? If you have a more precious household, you will be given more prayer topics, and if you have a more precious gift, you will want to pray more and receive and serve it.
Life and grace are in real time. After the Wednesday service.

In life there is inevitably war. But the most important true enemy is the war with anxiety within me. Anxiety makes us overly sensitive to reactions and circumstances, steals peace, makes us make excuses about results, and causes us to cross lines we should not cross. King Saul became sensitive to the Philistines through anxiety, was consistent in making excuses to Samuel, and tried to use worship to achieve his own purposes.

In life, there are days when things do not go my way. In such times, you can say, 'Today is a day when things don't go my way.' We do not live by emotion but by faith. David also experienced a downcast heart, but did not allow himself to live that way. Do not hide it but reveal it and confess it. That is normal.
In life, what matters is not frequency. It is meaning.
Life is panic and tough. But it's okay. If the Lord gives peace, everything is okay.
Life never goes as calculated. It goes as entrusted and trusted.

Life must pass through night to bloom flowers. We are made to experience betrayal and nights of frustration and failure so that the Lord removes the poison from our souls and purifies us. We keep wanting to succeed, but because we become proud and evil, the Lord makes us pass through night. To see morning, first remove the poison.

Life concludes in two ways: either following the flesh's desire to death, or following the Spirit to life. I cannot understand why this simple truth is made complicated in a roundabout way. Life is a choice. The natural person chooses to follow the flesh. But when we pray, we make a choice of faith and our destiny changes. Faith determines to obtain a better reward.
Life is not a simple thing. It does not end with one college entrance exam. Life is to question intensely and do without regret what I truly believe is meaningful.
In life, as we walk toward purpose, there are things that are obtained and enjoyed. But we do not walk through life in order to obtain and enjoy. I hope the Lord becomes your purpose.

Life's happiness and success are not determined by visible possessions and comparisons. What is invisible is eternal, and true happiness and success are determined through hidden roots. Those roots are the soul and relationship with God.

Life is much shorter than we think. What fills those short moments is an individual's choice. If we fill it with misunderstanding and conjecture, life will be so futile. If we fill it with love and forgiveness, it will shine like stars in heaven.
Life is not solved by one test. The Lord opens my way and sustains my feet.
If you think of life in terms of ranking and speed, you will always be frustrated. But if you accept it as direction and mission, understanding and interpretation begin.
When living life, there are times when you have nothing to say. In such times, there is something to say: 'For eternity, I have nothing to say but Jesus' blood.'

As I live life, I see things not going my way and becoming slightly twisted. There is someone adjusting it from behind—God. Trying to go but cannot, trying to open but it does not open. At this time, if I die to myself and obey the Lord, I save many people.

To maintain faith during the turbulent 20s and 30s of life, having only fun and fellowship is not enough. That level of fun exists plenty in the world, and there is more, so there is no reason to stay in church. That is the crisis of youth. Only those whose blood vessels and bones are filled with Jesus, whether young or mature, will not stumble while walking all day through dry land.
If the goal of life is human recognition, it is a life of decline. When my longing is only to see the beauty of the Lord, then truly becomes a glorious life.
When facing trials in life, the path of life becomes clear. I hope you become people of faith who choose the good at that moment.

The beautiful and happy moments in life are when I have been faithful before the Lord. No matter how much fortune and abundance there were and wishes were fulfilled, if I acted wickedly before the Lord, I always lived in anxiety and fear. Revival happens when we act faithfully (be faithful) and continue (keep going). I hope you overcome and win against the wicked enemy that prevents us from being faithful and from continuing.

When facing difficulties in life, it seems like wasting time. But it is not a wasted place but a waiting place. The Lord will eventually show His way to those who wait for the Lord and endure in patience by walking with them.
Faith is not believing in myself and not placing security in conditions.

The true victory in life belongs to those with a soft heart. With good specs but without God, you become Goliath. But if weak yet relying on the Lord, you become David. I hope you become like David and achieve victory.
The focus of life must not stop at the problem. Beyond the problem, we must concentrate on God, on Him.

At life's halftime, Abraham leaves the 'land shown' and not the 'land to be shown,' setting aside a somewhat stable life and embarking on a journey. The path of vision is certainly not easy. But a sadder life is one where we settle in anxious reality, fearing failure, and attempt nothing.

In life's voyage, we must navigate by looking at light so we do not run aground and reach our destination completely. But among people, there are those who follow the lights of other moving ships. It is foolishness. That light passes away and cannot be a standard. We must only follow Jesus, the lighthouse of truth, who is always present and eternal. When God is the standard, the power to overcome the world comes.
In difficult moments of life, the Lord opened the way. He made humans out of those who could not act as humans. Such a Lord will continue to work before me.
People say life is all like that. Be clear about this. Life is certainly not like that. God does not leave a person with eyes of faith to live only in the routine path that others take. Rise up.

The reason life is painful is because we choose wounds and are stingy with grace, and we have superhuman memory regarding wounds, but amazing amnesia regarding grace. Life is something to be grateful for.
If you truly want your life to change, you must redesign your life and change your priorities in how you use your time. Without such a decision, you do not truly want your life to change.
When life is mistaken, God sounds the alarm. It is not by your strength but only by His grace, and without prayer, such things do not depart.

When life becomes difficult and trials pile up, I urge you to look to the Lord. And I earnestly ask you to pray that you may see this situation through the Lord's perspective. That the Lord would allow you to see this situation as He sees you.
Without forcing it artificially, God's guidance alone is abundantly rich and beautiful.
They say on the internet, people express themselves through writing. More important than spelling is maintaining courtesy.

In ordinary circumstances, faith is not evident. In extraordinary situations and moments of crisis, true faith is revealed when we rely on the Lord and gain strength. When the people were grieved at heart because of their children and wanted to stone David, David was greatly distressed, but he strengthened himself in the Lord his God. —1 Samuel 30:6

The question I asked before was about how in Britain, the social system makes it difficult to pioneer churches like in Korea, and regarding free will versus predestination, I wonder what British Christians believe. Are they like Korean Christians where predestination is mainstream, or given that it's Wesley's homeland, would it be more toward free will?
They say the early bird catches the worm. Then someone always says the early worm gets eaten. The biggest problem lies in the identity you have of yourself—thinking you are the worm.

If something is forgotten, it is not love. Love cannot be forgotten no matter how hard you try to forget it. What is not truth and love is not remembered. If my time has not come, I cannot grasp it. If it is not remembered, it is not love. Remember the Lord.
It is foolish for you to acknowledge yourself. You must become faith that the Lord acknowledges. That is a person after God's own heart. The Lord accomplishes His will through such people.
When you humble yourself, people will forget you. Then God will find you.

If you do not love and forgive yourself, you cannot lead anyone. Just as God has received me, I too must receive myself. I hope you become a person of faith who sees the Lord's mercy as greater than your own shortcomings.

A person who restrains and humbles themselves has stability and is not anxious or worried. A self-controlled person is wise, knowing what to focus on and what to restrain, so they do not fear. Reverence for God is self-control, and knowing Him is wisdom.

You can only seek grace when you do not trust in yourself. God seeks such people. You must become someone who collapses without grace. The Lord is near to all who call on Him, to all who call on Him in truth. —Psalm 145:18
Prepare yourself; the Lord will work among us.

Prayer for personal desires and glory cannot be sustained nor easily answered. Prayer for His kingdom and His righteousness becomes a joy to God, thus granting the strength to pray and receiving answers. We need prayer where God's rule is established where I stand, prayer seeking that kingdom and His righteousness, prayer for nation and people, and intercessory prayer for others.
Do not believe your psychological state; believe the conclusion God gave through the cross. Believing and accepting that victory is what brings victory.

Those who lament their circumstances, who despair that their image is not a jewel, saying 'Everyone else is doing well (comparison), so why am I struggling?'—life transformation is not achieved by reshaping yourself like a jewel or upgrading your specs. What matters is that I become a true earthen vessel and focus on the Lord who is precious. He never demanded that I become a jewel, nor can I. Jesus alone is the jewel. The secret to overcoming both despair and pride together is knowing this truth: I am an earthen vessel, the Lord is the jewel. But we have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the surpassing power may be of God and not of ourselves. —2 Corinthians 4:7
Thinking you will love by your own strength is arrogance and delusion. How absurd is it for a branch to live independently apart from the vine?
You think you cannot do it because you are lacking. This severely limits the Almighty God. The Lord is listening right now and seeking. He is guiding.

What makes you uncomfortable reveals where your greed lies. A dog with two bones does not grow angry or jealous. It is because the dog and human are on different levels. But God said He loves sinful humans even to the point of jealousy. This is nonsense. That is why God's love could never be measured by human language from the beginning.
When you feel diminished, behold that the Lord made me. God doesn't make junk.

People with low self-esteem are happy when things go well and depressed when they do not. They cannot be satisfied by their existence, so they seek satisfaction through numbers. Our identity is Jesus.
Falling frequently is proof that you are relying on your own strength. If you relied on the Lord, you would not fall. Because the Jesus in whom I believe and trust never falls.
Moving forward without regard is faith.

Small decisions change the course of life. Therefore, choices determine destiny. Making the decision that the Lord would not make, not the one your flesh desires, is 'faith.' A faith decision is the better choice in the Lord's thinking. Thus through that choice, God makes your life glorified.
There will be small challenges, but also great trials. Yet the principle of overcoming remains constant: walking by faith. Because of Jesus, shame and tribulation become of no concern.
Do not scheme or lament; seek in faith and be bold. Whether it is scheming or faith can be discerned by the degree of 'impatience.'
How are you doing? You came... Have a happy day today too ^^

When you pause for a moment, you see much. When you step back, what was invisible becomes visible. That is why compassion is possible. And with the same heart, shedding tears, you can pat their back. Not the love you desire, but love for that soul, so it is embraced and restored. That is giving love. Not coercion...
Reckless words are like sword thrusts, but the tongue of the wise brings healing. —Proverbs 12:18

The problem is that Jesus is not in their heart, and more seriously, they mistake what is not there as being there. A person produces good from accumulated good and evil from accumulated evil. If Jesus is present, pain and joy will be reversed, and they will do the works of Abraham. Obedience and faith.
More important than having talent, ability, or credentials is faith. Look at Jabez—he had nothing, yet he believed in the Lord's help.
Only when all my strength and ability have collapsed does the Lord accomplish His work.
I am a minister. I belong to those who work diligently. Yet our young people, without me, would find it hard but would not perish. Because God is with them. The righteous live by faith alone.
I am confident that the Lord will bestow much grace in this retreat. Because I see the members serving with hearts ready for martyrdom, having humbled themselves.
If you wish to be happy at least, the most important thing is to look in the same direction at one most important thing. The same truth, the same Lord, Jesus Christ.

The Son, Jesus, who was in the perfect love of the Almighty Father, showed His greatest love through the precious blood He shed. Through the power of the blood, the authority of sin is shattered, all temptations are overcome, and we are not deceived by the enemy's lies. The power of the blood is the greatest victory and power both from God's side and for people.
I believe in the Almighty God and in His only Son, Jesus Christ. I believe in the Holy Spirit and that He forgives sins and grants eternal life.

Professional traders perform enormous analysis, calculation, and comparison. Because they seek to avoid financial risk. Thus humans, sinful by nature, instinctively analyze, calculate, and compare to avoid fear, and soon become anxious. Such human defense mechanisms are not easily abandoned. Only when a greater value is discovered are they abandoned. But whatever things were gains to me, these I have counted as loss for the sake of Christ. More than that, I count all things to be loss in view of the surpassing value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. —Philippians 3:7, 8, 9

When you wholly rely on the Lord, the Lord works. Seeking the Lord wholeheartedly requires no qualifications, grades, or reputation. You seek because you are lacking, because the basics are not in place. When you deeply feel your lack, the Lord's work begins.
A revelation that came in despair: Christ alone.

Even when a young person's heart breaks, they pray and run, even if tomorrow's sun seems not to rise. Living by Jesus, not by circumstances—that is the faith of such a person as the world could not do without, as spoken of in Hebrews.
To know someone, look at the person they love. In the disciple who prayed and shared the Word together, there are unmistakable traces of the life of one who made disciples.
The answer is, of course, 'Jesus Christ.'

It is wisdom to abandon what will perish in order to cling to what is truly precious. Faith is to look only to the Lord and maintain the path given to me, regardless of circumstances and reactions. It is loving Jesus. Then you will be free from all trials.

Whatever you earnestly desire, you will move to accomplish and obtain it. Not earnestly praying for what you desire means you do not truly want it. Try to see what you want now and what you wish to see.

No one thinks it wasteful to make reservations and research restaurants to spend sweet time with someone they truly love. The most valuable thing in the world is prayer. Worldly people cannot and will not understand. Because it presupposes that the Lord is alive.

What is truly amazing is that when I lay down my pride and acknowledge what I dislike about myself, my shortcomings and failures, the Almighty Father's work begins. Under the precious blood, this is why a Christian's failure itself becomes hope.

Do you truly believe in Jesus? Then the real crisis has already been overcome. It is not about tomorrow, but the Lord's work. We labor and toil as stewards, but all responsibility and resolution belongs to the Owner, the Lord.
Do you truly believe in the Almighty God? I believe in that God. And I believe He will work.

Truly, the vast majority of young people have been transformed. It is a miracle and God's irresistible grace. Even after returning to Korea, their resolve to give their all does not change and they continue to exercise good influence. Therefore, they will never abandon that high standard. That standard is the heart. So when others praise the young people by different standards, I challenge them. Abandon idols and give your lives.

If you truly want to have good faith, you must abandon expectations. Throw away even the slightest hope of being recognized and remembered, and run toward the Lord. This is the way of absolute faith and infinite loyalty.

When you are suffering so much you could die, when just moments ago you could not breathe and were nearly at death's door, the reason you can cry out 'one more time!' is because you believe in and live for the Lord. Before the Lord, everything is reset.

I hope you have the conviction of faith to skip less important things in order to speak what is truly important. Jesus had much to rebuke the Jews about, but He spoke of what was more important. 'I have much to say and to judge concerning you, but He who sent Me is true; and I speak to the world those things which I heard from Him.' (John 8:26)
If I ignore your greeting or greet you awkwardly when we meet, please report it. I went to see Mi-sun recently and she said everyone had an awkward experience with me before. ㅠㅠ

As I have done the Lord's work until now, there is almost nothing that succeeded because I did it well. God uses our weaknesses for His good through His power. I hope you experience the power of becoming strong in weakness.
The most frustrating time is when you cannot go mad because of Jesus. A person of faith has nothing to fear except the absence of Jesus.
Please leave some good, strong words that will help in the coming month. You're the pastor who loves and respects Jesus. Hahahaha
To be moved by loving the Lord unconditionally, to be fascinated by His beauty—that is faith.

If you confuse conditions with results, there is no answer. Resolution, fruit, phenomena, and victory are results, not conditions. The condition and cause is Jesus. There must be a cause for there to be a result. A result without a cause is hypocrisy.
Please come a little early. It seems there will not be enough seats.
Not being anxious is faith. When you make up your mind 'If I die, I die; if I lose, I lose,' all conditions disappear and only the Lord becomes your reliance. That is faith.
A person of faith does not live extraordinarily, but simply has a clear purpose.

Being able to respect one worthy of respect is an ability. Not everyone can do it, nor is it permitted to everyone. Recognizing one sent by God is great spiritual discernment. Become a blessed person. Please...
To be more precise, Peter doubted but did not sink. Because Jesus caught him as he was sinking. We are insufficient, but when we come to Jesus, the Lord holds us fast.

To like something is easy. But to love is difficult. A cat likes a mouse because the mouse has something beneficial to the cat. But that is not happiness for the mouse. A person who loves is grateful for weakness. I am grateful that person is as insufficient as I am. Because that person lacks something I have, I can enter that empty place and become part of them, and because that person has what I lack, they can come to me and become part of me.

Behind a good leader are good followers. A good follower eventually becomes a good leader. Everything done when you were a follower is returned to you doubled when you become a leader. Obedience to a leader does not end in obedience. It bears fruit in obedience to the invisible God. A life without obedience is merely a mongrel.

It is very difficult to distinguish between what is good and what looks good. But there is a fundamental difference: whether you pay the price. Good-looking faith and good faith can be known through paying the price.
Have good faith.
There is no need at all to pretend to be a good person. Because in the sight of the Lord, you are already sufficiently good and beautiful.
Good question. Q. When heart and mind differ, does heart come first or mind? A. Faith comes first.

It is truly difficult to abandon sin. Sin has power that transcends human will. But when you truly believe in Jesus, sinning becomes difficult. Because when you truly love Jesus, what cannot be broken is broken. When you love Jesus, everything else can be abandoned like excrement. 'Yet indeed I also count all things loss for the sake of the excellence of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord, for whom I have suffered the loss of all things, and count them as rubbish, that I may gain Christ.' (Philippians 3:8-9)

The secret to overcoming sin and conquering the evil world is to fear the Lord. When you continually look to the Lord with all your heart and rely on His blood, your stumbling life becomes a life of praise and testimony.

* Reasons you should not be disappointed in yourself. That I am I is by God's grace. To be disappointed in myself and live wearing a mask is to disrespect the God who made me. If I am God's reason for being, there is no need to live differently, nor any need for others' approval. When I live as my true self, it is the most beautiful and aligns with God's purpose. If someone dislikes that version of me, I do not need to struggle either. Expecting all relationships to be perfect is greed. Rather than someone who likes me, someone who loves my heart is true gold. 'By the grace of God I am what I am.' (1 Corinthians 15:10) 'Though all the world forsake me, the Lord Jesus does not forsake me. He looks after me to the end.' (Hymn 88)
We can be bold even when sin challenges us by knowing and believing that the Lord holds us. The Lord never lets us go. The cross conquers sin.
No one is free from the temptation of sin. That is why we must rely on the blood of the Lord. There is no sin that cannot be washed by the blood, and no sin that is washed except by the blood.
When the temptation of sin is strong, do not grit your teeth and endure, but pray as follows: 'Lord, keep me under grace. I am about to fall now. By Your cross, to the throne of grace, lead me now.'
The only reason I can stand is His endless mercy.
Live by faith. Do what is permitted, in the way He desires.
The blood of the Lord is powerful. There is nothing more to say. No other name has been given to mankind by which we can be saved.
God does not turn away those who flee to Him.

Three functions of confession: 1. It shows us who God is. 2. It shows us who we are through the Lord's eyes. 3. Knowing God and beholding ourselves, all tribulation and enemies are bound and we are victorious. At every moment, confess that you believe and love the Lord and are loved. That will be your strength and power. Whenever the enemy touches a wound, confession becomes an opportunity to draw near to the Lord. Hallelujah.
The Lord will speak through His Word. There will come a time when you hear that voice, and when you hear it, you are made alive.

The love the Lord pours out is so great that no falsehood or deception can prevent it, and no tribulation or suffering can oppose it. Nothing is stronger or more eternal than love, and because of it, we can run without stopping and without regret. I love you.
When you entrust and trust yourself to the Lord's hands, there is no need for tricks. The Lord's hand will hold you. He is the reason.

The evidence of being in the Lord is not being anxious or driven. To control the situation myself, I must be anxious, but when I entrust myself to the Lord, I not only have peace but also the best decisions and the Lord's will are accomplished.

Paul threw away the seat of a praised rabbi like dust. Because he saw the real in Damascus. Faith is not something you choose not to go to; it is something you cannot go away from. It is because you have never seen truth that would captivate you.

'Once saved in the Lord, past, present, and future sins are forgiven' does not mean you have a license to sin as you please. It means that because I am in grace, not law, sin can no longer rule over me. (Romans 6:14)

In the Lord, sad things do not end in sadness, and fearful things do not end in fear. Life makes people proud when favorable and desperate when unfavorable, but a person of faith trusts only the Lord who knows and vindicates them in any circumstance. 'The Lord is my helper; I will not fear. What can man do to me?' (Hebrews 13:6)
To those who bow humbly before the Lord, He will show Himself ruling over problems that cannot be expressed in human words. Before the Lord's sovereignty, all problems melt away.
To expect any other good without bowing before the Lord is fraud.

When you stand before the Lord in boundless insufficiency, the Lord gives you mission and new strength. Do not become someone who hides your true self. Whether it becomes death or rice, when you stand before the Lord in truth, even if it is confessed as a sigh from our lips, we believe that when the Lord acts, it is done.

When I faithfully do the small work I can do before the Lord, the Lord does great work on His part. Two loaves and five fish are indeed small, but when placed in the Lord's hands, they feed five thousand and give life. Plant in good soil by faith.
Paradise without the Lord becomes more terrible than the original hell.
Conversation without the Lord has no meaning. Until the Lord intervenes, human life is a closed-down state with no solution.
Zeal without the Lord is worse than laziness. More important than zeal is essence. A life with the Lord as the essence is blessed.
The Lord's work is not done by everyone who receives support or by perfect people. It is done by those who have received grace and forgiveness.
Grant that the Lord be God and love, and grant revival. What I desire is not evaluation but to love in the Lord and obtain life.
Being satisfied with the Lord alone is a confession that comes after paying a great price. It is never merely makeup to hide my greed.

A deep illness of twelve years was healed by merely touching the Lord's garment. No matter how deep the sighs and suffering, from the Lord's perspective it is merely a matter of faith. Make your confession and declaration of faith. 'If I just touch his clothes, I will be healed,' says Mark 5:25.
To walk with the Lord, two things must be presupposed: 1. One is faith, and 2. the other is holiness. - Joshua 1:8

To pray to the Lord means simultaneously having the faith that He surely hears and answers, and being grateful even if He does not. 'Know that He will surely deliver me from the king's hand, and even if He does not, I will not bow to the image.' - Daniel 3:18
Without giving your heart to the Lord, without the Lord's grace, a life that cannot move forward even one step is blessed.

Let us look to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith. - Hebrews 12:2. Not constantly looking to Jesus is already sin and a wound in the heart. Therefore, Jesus is not an event. The heart must always be cultivated with the Word of truth.
Walk by faith in the path the Lord has called right. Even if it is like striking a rock with an egg, do it by faith. Rather than staking life on probabilities, become one who lives by the Word.
For the perfect promises the Lord has given to appear not only in our afterlife but in our present reality, we need faith and absolute obedience to His Word.

When you look only to the Lord, you will feel there is no room to be conscious of anything else. David sinned when he had much leisure and took a nap. Empty spaces of time should be filled with the most precious things.

A person who fears only the Lord has nothing to boast of or show off. Having nothing to boast of, he holds no regrets. Without regrets, he does not cling to position. Not clinging, he does not carelessly abandon responsibility.

You must look only to the Lord. You must love the Lord. You must praise only the Lord completely. With all your heart. Otherwise, woe to those who look to people, love people more, and are more conscious of people. 1 Corinthians 16:22 KJV 'If anyone does not love the Lord, let him be cursed. Maranatha.'
Pray that the Lord alone becomes your hope. Though it is self-evident, it only becomes clear when you are desperate.
If you cannot find happiness in the Lord, nothing else has any meaning. Find your answer in the Lord.

The Lord desires thanksgiving. Not only for what He has done for me, but also for what has not yet been accomplished. Therefore, thanksgiving is done by faith. Jesus gave thanks to His heavenly Father before the miracle of the five loaves and two fish took place.
The Lord is salvation.

The Lord said do not let your hearts be troubled. These are the words He spoke to His disciples as He was about to leave them after they had followed Him for three and a half years, living in complete dependence. The Lord gives faith as the basis for escaping worry: 'You believe in God; believe also in me.' This means faith. Do not seek far away; believe, and you will be freed from trouble. - John 14:1
The Lord was with the lowly. Remember those with broken hearts. There the Lord who cares for the marginalized is present.
Sometimes when groundless anxiety comes, there is something to do. It is to hold fast to Jesus, the most certain reason. Faith is a life that precedes feelings.
The Lord commands us to pray for souls and serve in humility. Though no one may desire it, it is not something you can do by wanting to. It happens through the Lord's grace and compassion.
The Lord was almighty, yet showed love by giving His own body. Not knowing this fact leads to reckless action.
The Lord is a good Lord. Believe that the Lord fights for you. Whether your suffering is of your own choosing or caused by others, the Lord does not turn away from us.
The Lord now touches your wounded heart through His Word and the Holy Spirit.
The Lord has never taken His eyes off me for a moment. Even when I was an unbeliever, His eyes were upon me.
What happens if you do not expect the Lord and do not believe in His work? Answer: You will never hear. You will become parched.

Become one who follows the Lord. Follow even in fear, follow even when it is difficult. Even when your opinion differs, if the Lord goes, follow without hesitation or second thought. That is what a Christian is. The Lord works.
Faith in the Lord conquers all things. Even death. Be bold. The Lord has overcome.
You couldn't do it without me, I wouldn't do it without you. You are so special to me. Keep going.
To give up something in order to hold fast to the Lord is the greatest investment.

If you love the Lord completely and truly act in that love, there is nothing left to fear. Because you have done what God commanded regardless of the outcome. What the Lord desires is not success but obedience.
I relied on the Lord and proclaimed the gospel with all my heart. When I had the opportunity, I watched and shared the Bridge video. I entrust this to the Lord.

The characteristic of one who trusts in the Lord is that he remains silent. One who trusts in God's intervention does not easily become excited or handle matters emotionally. 'I am like the deaf; I cannot hear. Like the mute, I do not open my mouth. I wait for you, Lord; you will answer, Lord my God.' - Psalm 38:14-15
I have no choice but to trust in the Lord. That is why I pray.

Determining right and wrong over a problem is truly foolish. Because what is more important than the surface cause of the problem is my response to it—that is, my faith. The Lord desires to see my faith when the storm arises. 'Where is your faith?' - Mark 4:40

One who does not see the Lord's love cannot see his own sin either. My sin is not accidental wrongdoing in the past but inherent in my fundamental attitude of 'self-love.' It is only in the light of that love that I come to realize this. That is why I put my hand to my lips and sigh, 'Woe is me! I am ruined!' I am a person of unclean lips...' This happens when Jesus has brought me to God's heart. - Isaiah 6:5
From those who have nothing to hope for but the Lord's love, I see the greatest strength. Beyond strength, I feel an admiration worthy of envy. That is faith. It is nothing but that.
Before the Lord's cross, every problem is small. Rise up and hold fast to the cross.

Seeking the Lord's glory means unwavering faith. Although you may have sought the Lord's glory, impossible situations and things you truly wish to avoid may still come upon you. Yet without any significant change, continuing to seek only the Lord's glory, being able to rejoice even if you die there, saying 'Glory to the Lord'—that is faith.

The most basic way to hear the Lord's voice is an attitude of obedience to any word He speaks. The Lord never reveals His will to those who pick and choose, listening to what is good and discarding what is displeasing.
Obey the Lord's guidance with courage. You will not die. Rather, you will experience the Lord's work.
Believe in the Lord's omnipotence and wait quietly. Humans always make situations worse because of fear. Yet the love and grace of God who reverses such situations is always amazing.
The greatest hindrance to relying on and trusting in the Lord's omnipotence is not the enemy but the effort of my own self. Believing in the Lord begins with acknowledging my helplessness.

There are two ways to respond by faith. One is obedience. It is not something that comes naturally; even Jesus learned obedience through suffering. The second is trust. Trust without obedience is fanaticism, and obedience without trust is blind faith.
There is something the Lord gives to the despondent through His mercy. It is opportunity.

When the Lord has compassion, death loses its power and retreats in shame before that love. - Luke 7. When the Lord has compassion, my prayer is heard and answered faithfully. When the Lord has compassion, 'His heart went out to you' (Luke 7:13)—that is, the Lord's heart is fixed on you. Luke 7:13. When you ask for mercy, the Lord truly has compassion.
If the Lord had not given life, we could not move our little finger as we wish. In the Lord, there is no easy or difficult task. There is only the work that Jesus living within us does.
If it were not for the Lord, I would not have come this far. The Lord is my reason.
Determine to be humble and lowly until the Lord works. That is the most certain and quickest way.

Even if you say the Lord is everything, the Lord is never deceived. If words could accomplish it, who couldn't do it? One for whom the Lord is everything is not disappointed by judgment. Because the Lord is everything.
One of the consolations the Lord gives is that He uses us. Through our inadequacy, He glorifies His gospel and draws the attention of unbelieving souls to our small lips. It is amazing.
If the Lord did not give it, we have received nothing. Do not concern yourself with many variables; I pray you find peace in the Lord. The Lord will never abandon us.
If you simply accept what the Lord has done, people who stubbornly refuse to accept truth as truth and rely on emotions will suffer greatly.

Believe and accept that what the Lord has permitted is the best thing. And what you have received is not just yours alone—there is surely a portion meant to be shared. Though your life is like five small loaves and two fish, when placed in the Lord's hands, it will feed many people.
* Performance or Promise? "Of all those who put money into the treasury, this poor widow has put in more than all the others." Faith is not Performance but Promise.

*Faith is believing 'just like that' in a situation where you cannot wrap your head around it no matter how hard you try. No matter how much I calculate, there is no answer, but the Lord has already reached His conclusion. He said He would establish descendants through Isaac, yet commanded Abraham to offer Isaac as a burnt offering—a contradiction. This contradiction has no answer in my ability and logic, but the answer lies only with Him. Abraham, when tested, offered Isaac by faith. Being one who had received the promise, he offered his one and only son. God had said to him, 'It is through Isaac that your offspring will be reckoned.' Abraham reasoned that God could raise the dead, and figuratively speaking, he did receive Isaac back from death. - Hebrews 11:17, 19

The curse of the sorcerer Balaam did not work against Israel because God's chosen people are beyond the range of their enemies' attacks. Do not be intimidated by attacks but draw near to the Lord. Then you will naturally be removed from the enemy's reach. Above the world's jurisdiction stands God's sovereignty. We are in His hands.
Do not complain about options the Lord did not give. Instead, do your best with what He has given you now. That is fullness.
He who is in me is greater than he who is in the world.
Lord, I came with empty hands and will return with empty hands. Though I possess nothing, Jesus dwells in my heart. Thank you, Lord.

Sunday Leaders Meeting: 1 Corinthians 12 - Gifts are given for edification, not for praise. Bear fruit through your gifts. Prophecy is the Word (preaching). Do things in order, and to gain instruction, prophecy (give advice) one by one. Gifts are given to those who eagerly desire them. If love is not the motivation, it is useless. Daily Quiet Time James 2:14~ Salvation comes through faith alone. The works in James represent the matured state of faith. Faith and obedience are synonymous. Faith is expressed in word and action. If your words and actions differ, what do you believe? James finds Rahab's righteousness in works. The Hebrew writer finds Rahab's righteousness in faith in chapter 11. They see the same thing but express it differently. Because true faith produces obedience as its result. If someone confesses true faith in words but disobedience comes, that is false faith.

If I had died, I would live and others would be transformed. But it is not as easy as it sounds. Dying is a power—the power of faith. Without the belief that the Lord is the risen Lord, one does not want to live or die.
At the moment of death, only one thing remains: 'relationship with God.' This is called 'righteousness.' It means faith.
Blessed are those who are prepared when preparation is needed. The reason God cannot pour out His blessings is that we are not prepared. Pray to be prepared. That is holiness and purity.

Give the greatest love you can give. That is the level of love the Lord has entrusted to me. If you love with that love without regret, the Lord will pour out His love upon you. Then I will be satisfied.

The way to escape addiction, helplessness, and anger is to find the Lord alone sufficient. The Lord said to the woman who anointed His feet with perfume that because her many sins were forgiven, she showed great love. Repent on your knees humbly so that many sins will be forgiven. You will become one who loves the Lord.
The Lord who looks at the heart is not deceived. When the Father prunes me, it is because He is good. The Father's character is the highest good. Therefore, what He does to me is the highest good.
If complaints arise that there is nothing to rejoice about, look to Habakkuk. Though no harvest or profit was visible, he rejoiced because of Jehovah alone.
The term 'former moderator' means clearly that he is not the current moderator. In conclusion, it has little significance.
We're eating kimchi stew at church right now (half meat, half kimchi). Come by if you can, friend.
Ultimately, it is the Lord's grace rather than human attention.

Determine your future now. Time passes anyway, and if your goal is clear, make your life's conclusion in this very moment. This happens not by maintaining your present self but by breaking it down and choosing to see the Lord more. Make a decision.
Earnestly request that the Lord's mercy come upon you at this very moment. The mercy the Lord gives and extends is the only power and unstoppable history that saves me.
What is needed at this very moment is to confirm the Lord's love. Then touch your unseen wounds. Stand upright with sincerity.

Is the current place important? - Shin Bo-ra says (from 'Discovery of Life') 'Is the current situation important?' - Attitude speaks. The Lord does not look at the size of the problem but at your attitude.
