Please offer the shortest but most powerful intercession. Handle it—that person. Prayer is simple, but the Lord who applies it is amazing, turning the entire earth if needed to make a person.
The last word Jesus, whom they loved dearly, spoke to His disciples was to watch and pray so as not to enter into temptation, and that He intercedes for them so their faith may not fail.

*Prayer tips for your spouse! 1. Wherever they are, fill them with the Holy Spirit; if they don't believe, guide them to faith. 2. When we meet, help us recognize each other so we're not led astray by emotion or misled by calculation. 3. Make me feel attracted and make me someone worth loving, so I discover what others cannot see inside, and that I become that kind of meaning to them too.
* A minister's calling: Pray that they live for the Lord and die for the Lord, or if not, intercede so that lukewarm people who are neither this nor that do not die peacefully.

*Spiritual play: Airplanes are made to fly. But if you lose absolute speed and distance (prayer), they're worse than bicycles. Going to Tesco, flying around, and causing trouble with big wings when parking. The devil's challenge is so strong that we get hit hard once and enter temptation, crashing—what a fuss. You've never flown with the absolute speed of prayer, so what crash? You're still circling in the parking lot, hit by stones thrown by neighborhood kids. One hour of prayer hasn't even been tried—what spiritual warfare? You've just gotten into spiritual play.

When passing through a painful wilderness, pray. Pray like this: 'Make me even lower.' This is most effective and will quickly fulfill the wilderness's purpose. (Deuteronomy 8:16) 'He humbled you, causing you to hunger and then feeding you with manna, which neither you nor your ancestors had known, to teach you that man does not live on bread alone but on every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord.'
Show me what you pray for, not what you want. I'm interested in that. Because that will be answered.
When you pray in the wilderness, change happens. The greatest beneficiary is yourself. Your stubbornness is broken, the Lord is revealed, and you are humbled.
I know what I want, therefore I pray again.
If you don't pray, you become a deceiver.

The word contains 'opponent.' But a person of faith does not oppose the opponent. We must always face the eternal Father, our only concern and solution. Then regarding challenges from people and rumors with no substance, you will experience the Lord's rule. God said David was a man after His own heart. He was someone who fell before problems and appealed to God in prayer. 'I am a friend to all who fear you, to all who follow your precepts. But I pray all the more.' (Psalm 109:4)
Rather than having a particular method of prayer, just do it. If there's a method of prayer, do it right now. Then you will receive the Holy Spirit's guidance.
In prayer, restoration comes before request. That's why we first worship and repent, then give thanks and make supplication. ACTS (Adore, Confess, Thanksgiving, Supplement).
Some people pray for grace in the face of difficulties. But few receive grace and give thanks and praise. The power to persevere is compelling love, overflowing gratitude, and willing praise.

It's not because my will is weak, but because I don't love the Lord enough. If you love the Lord, you must make time and kneel. Jesus told Peter to watch and pray so as not to enter temptation. Without prayer, eating, drinking, and breathing all belong to the devil.
My not feeling the answer doesn't mean God isn't working. When I groan, it sounds like thunder to God. That's why I must continue to pray.
Kneel and lift your hands and cry out like this: 'Let Your will be done, Lord.'
*Intercession request: Today there is a service at Kings Cross Church. Please pray that the Lord's will be accomplished.

Moses stakes his soul and desperately asks for the people's sins to be atoned for. But that heart's beginning comes from God the Father. An intercessor is one who knows the Father's heart. Prayer answers and frequency at one's own level do not frighten Satan. When the Father's heart is poured out and becomes intercession, darkness crumbles and God's will is done on earth as it is in heaven.
I am a sinner and insufficient, so I stand on trial daily. Also, being the chief of sinners and weak, I call upon the Lord every moment. Amen, Lord Jesus.
Look only to the Lord who will touch your hardened heart and raise you up again

In the last days, in the position of the last runner. Being awake, being sober-minded, and praying are the same thing. Love without prayer is a lie. 1 Peter 4:7-8 KJV The end of all things is at hand; therefore be self-controlled and sober-minded for the sake of your prayers. Above all, keep loving one another earnestly, since love covers a multitude of sins.
When your heart is dark and troubled, please think of the Lord. The Lord, who had nowhere to lay His head, bore our suffering in our place.
The only competitiveness a helpless lamb has is 'walking together' with the Shepherd. We can only live by being filled with the Holy Spirit. Wake up and pray.
Grant me young people who love the Lord, and then I will turn this world back to You.
*Confession Confess like this: 'God, I have fallen but I will not give up. I will continue to fight. I will continue to seek Your face. I will continually hold onto the cross.' You are a victor.
*Time After all, when we look back, everyone lacks time. Since time is lacking anyway, kneel before the Lord. You will experience God, who created time, filling it.

*Kim Yeon-ah's Jump When you overlay Kim Yeon-ah's practice competitions with actual competitions, they look almost identical. Because she practices one jump ten thousand times. We want to succeed in faith while failing in prayer. Please hold the rope of prayer. An unbroken rope creates unbroken victory in faith. The victory of the day is almost decided in the morning prayer time. Forget what is past and keep going without regard.

*The Result of Prayer People pray to change situations they cannot handle. However, the more they pray, the more they experience being changed themselves. Ultimately, life is a battle of transformation, and that transformation comes through Christ, who became life and was resurrected.

*Pray Like This The reason prayer is not answered is asking wrongly to use it for lust. Such prayers cannot be sustained and have no power. Do not hope for benefits and luck without paying a price, but ask to be made to pay the price. That is why we pray fervently for God's will to be accomplished. Not my selfishness...

Sometimes what happens to me is startling and surprising. When something completely unexpected befalls me, a believer in God, I must not fix my gaze on external things but on the Lord within me. This is a time to pray. Going a little further, He fell on His face to the ground and prayed, saying, 'My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass from Me; nevertheless, not as I will, but as You will.' Matthew 26:39

There are times when what is clearly not worth getting angry about, yet anger explodes, and not even an opportunity to explain is given. When thinking of the Lord and turning back, it is frustrating and the heart is hurt, but I hope you will pour it out before the Lord: 'My beloved child, I know your heart and the Lordship of this problem belongs to Me. I will make it beautiful.' That is why in the Psalms, lament of a wounded heart comes together with thanksgiving at the end. I trust the Lord.

*Prayers That Are Answered, Problems That Are Solved Despite much faith and prayer, God's work may not appear in daily life. Miracles begin to happen when we receive Jesus as Lord. Whenever the disciples acknowledged, believed, and confessed that Jesus was Lord, they unfailingly experienced miracles and power. Powerless Christianity occurs when we dilute the Lordship of Christ. Remarkably, all the questions of Jesus in the four Gospels are condensed into one: 'Who is your Lord?' The wine that ran out, the five loaves and two fish, the boat in the storm, the sick daughter, the demon-possessed child, the thirsty life—who is truly the Lord? Therefore, do not cling to the problem but check who is the Lord of the problem. Then you can have peace. His mother said to the servants, 'Whatever He says to you, do it.' John 2:5

If you resolve to pray, God will come and pour out His heart to you and give you His heart. And He will show you the state of the church and yourself. And then He will break you. Only after that breaking, through the price paid by poverty and mourning, will He allow recovery. All ministry, relationships, and intentions without prayer must collapse. That is why God does not stop the devil's plans—because He loves us so much. Decide your future right now.

*Discern Like This The secret to knowing whether a prayer is answered is whether you can wait. Even emotional leading can sound incredibly like God's answer. Because emotions are so strong. God's answer is peaceful, not anxious, not rushed. Because it is given by Almighty God and all warfare belongs to God, there is no anxiety, and you do not try to accomplish it yourself by scheming. If you cannot wait for the Lord's time in peace, it is not an answer. May the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God and into the steadfastness of Christ. 2 Thessalonians 3:5 If guided (answered), you will surely develop patience.

*God's Gift We are not beings who pursue pleasure but beings who ask even small things of the Lord and receive answers. Do not calculate for the situation you want but pray simply, be grateful for what is given, rejoice, and live. Then you will enjoy God's gift that cannot be compared with anything.

1. A person seeking God earnestly cries out and begs the Lord for mercy. Then the Lord answers like this: 2. 'Hear, O LORD, when I cry aloud; be gracious to me and answer me! When You said, 'Seek My face,' my heart said to You, 'Your face, LORD, do I seek.' Psalm 27:7-8 3. Seek My face. That was God's answer. The face that Moses longed for so, the face to which David cried out like this, the presence that Job called for so painfully. 4. 'He is the radiance of the glory of God and the exact imprint of His nature, and He upholds the universe by the word of His power. After making purification for sins, He sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high.' Hebrews 1:3 5. God's answer, 'Seek His face,' is Jesus Christ. 6. In Him alone is complete union with God and the solution to that burning thirst.
Do not try to move God to fulfill your joy, but rather may the Lord's will become your joy. That is the secret to a life that receives answers and a prosperous life.

There are always problems, but do not wait for them to be solved; I desire to always rejoice. Because the one closest to you, more than anyone, is God, the chosen must respond to God first. Rejoice always, pray without ceasing, give thanks in all circumstances. 1 Thessalonians 5:16

Moses saw Israel's problems as God's problems. A problem merely provides an occasion. It is an occasion and opportunity to take the right stance before God. The people quarreled with Moses and said, 'Give us water to drink.' Moses said to them, 'Why do you quarrel with me?' Exodus 17:2

If you try to accomplish your own will, you will be frustrated. But for those accomplishing the Lord's will, everything is only prayer and gratitude. Leading God's people is different from leading my people. It is enough to lead His people according to His will. Reporting all things in detail and beholding the Lord is the mission.
We are ready for your grace.
We live by the Word flowing. That is why we must pray and strive so that the Word can flow. When it overflows in and overflows out, the work of life happens.

For a person who cannot decide between A or B..., it is not meaningless that even after long deliberation there is no answer. Being able to pray about one problem for several months is a blessing. How can a life decided by probability without such years of hardship be the same weight as a life decided through prayer in the absence of answers?

When you want to pray but prayer does not come, it is because you have forgotten. You must remember. Only then will prayer open. After supper He took the cup, and when He had given thanks, He said, 'This cup is the new covenant in My blood; do this, as often as you drink it, in remembrance of Me.' 1 Corinthians 11:25-26 Please remember the Lord and His death and His coming again.

My prayer is not that I be recognized, succeed, and accomplish my will. What I earnestly ask for strength is so that I can complete the race appointed for me and run as a servant who does not hinder the Lord's revival.

1. Christians who lead powerless faith may not experience answers to prayer. 2. The Bible says the devil submits, but such things rarely seem to happen in reality. 3. Why does the devil not submit? Submission happens on the battlefield (on site), not at a desk. 4. Because you do not go to the battlefield, there is no submission and no power is manifested. Just like stacking weapons in a warehouse and checking them daily—nothing happens. 5. Do what the Lord desires where the Lord desires. Then the Lord will follow and confirm His word with accompanying signs. Become not eloquent experts but mighty warriors who set fire to lives. 'Lord, even the demons submit to us in Your name.' Luke 10:17 The disciples went out and preached everywhere, and the Lord worked with them and confirmed His word by the signs that accompanied it. Mark 16:20 Peter and John, speaking boldly as they were filled with the Spirit, and the apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people, and by the hands of the apostles many signs and wonders were done among the people. Acts 14:3

Do not worry too much about what you cannot do, but be glad and grateful for what you can do. Anxiety is not God's will. Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus. Philippians 4:6-7

Before prayer, the way seems to be nowhere. But when you cry out, a way appears even in the sea where there was none. The ability to see the process rather than just a part comes after refinement and prayer.
Do not be afraid; only believe. It means to entrust everything. Cast all your anxiety on Him, because He cares for you. 1 Peter 5:7
What I desperately need is deep communion with the Lord. That is what is most important right now.

The mountain I cannot cross by my strength, the valley I cannot fill with my ability—when I make the Lord my Lord and invite His presence, He comes with His Holy Spirit and rules all things by the Lord's omnipotence. The Lord rules.

After praying, there should be no anxiety in your face. If you are still afraid after praying, you must pray more for faith and assurance. Believe the Lord who rules all tangled situations. Use your faith. And proclaim by faith what will be, and be at peace.
If you want a holy generation, then become a holy person. Now kneel down and pray for the fire from heaven to come.
Do not be afraid; only believe. Do not be pressed down by problems; break through by prayer. Jesus heard it and said to them, 'Do not be afraid; only believe.' Luke 8:50

The influence I have is like mist, and the power of the influential relationships I have is less than a tissue paper in the Lord's sight. If you try to use advantage, you will be ashamed. When you depend on the Lord on your knees, you will see the Lord's work without side effects.
I pray. To brothers and sisters who work and study according to the Lord's calling in unbelief and barren land, grant them strength and wisdom...

Prayer so overflows that there is glory when you find a restroom and weep, sobbing with an aching cry. Without regard to time and place, you must call upon the Lord and can only depend on Him—that person is glorious and the strongest.
We should ask, Lord, what lessons I should learn at the moment?

Tears are mostly water with the rest being salt, lactoferrin, and lysozyme. The composition is similar for most people, but the reasons are all different. Every tear shed before the Lord, the Lord remembers and repays. Since the Lord has counted my tears, 'Put my tears into Your bottle. Are they not in Your book?' Psalm 56:8

There is very little I can control. But when I submit myself and bow before the Lord, I see the Lord's work in areas I cannot bear and cannot reach. If I work, only I work, but if I pray, the Lord works.

I must entrust the parts beyond my domain to the Lord. I must do my utmost with all my strength in what I must do and ask for it in prayer. 'The LORD lives...' I cast off the consciousness of trying to respond to everything and entrust the parts beyond my domain to the Lord because the Lord lives. Let me do the work I must do.

Continuing to pray and praise, to love and forgive is faith. The reason we cannot move mountains is because we do not try and do not persevere. One drop of water is a small amount, but flowing continuously, it pierces rocks and fills seas. Respond to challenges like this and continue what God desires. So what? The power to brush it off.

Seeing members who greatly need prayer, I continue to pray. Those sensitive to achievement, those ashamed of their appearance, those unjustly hurt by unfairness, those for whom even one step is difficult, those who think only they are victims, those who wander not knowing the way, those in difficulty due to relationships, those whose bodies hurt as if breaking, those whose hearts are so busy their hands cannot work, those struggling from spiritual attacks, those who must advance but are withered, those in difficulty due to family... May you be free at the foot of the Lord's cross by the grace of the blood. Amen

The first gift given to those who ask for their needs with gratitude is peace. Through irresistible peace that makes one forget even the needs one has asked for, He works so that what one can be grateful for becomes more abundant.

Will you still hold fast your integrity? Job 2:9 To all the young adult members who passed through London over the past 12 years, now growing older, becoming homemakers or parents, and living with responsibility and burden in society, I send my greetings. If you ask how we are living, I would say, 'By the grace of the Lord.' As time passes and we age, we pray more and more and hold onto the Lord's cross even more. Because the 'grace of the cross and blood' we cried out is not a joke. As we live in this harsh world, if someone asks, 'Will you still hold fast your integrity in youth?' I hope you will answer, 'Not only will I hold fast, but I am sorry I cannot give more.' 'Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever.' Hebrews 13:8

Moses was not present at the time of the creation of heaven and earth, but by the inspiration of the Holy Spirit, he recorded the creation that had passed. John prophesied the future world not yet come in Revelation. When you truly love in the Lord, there is a sound heard without speaking and a heart heard without words. That voice becomes an earnest prayer in my heart.

I hope you will pray for friends who need prayer. Even if not seen for a long time, remember and call their names, and continue to pray as the Lord leads for members whose minds and bodies ache and are under fire.
Hoping for something without praying is like expecting mail that was never sent. You either don't receive it or receive a counterfeit.
Help us by your prayers for us also. -2 Corinthians 1:11

When a beast bites an opponent's throat—their fatal weakness—it is to suffocate them or rupture the carotid artery to cause brain death. The devil knows weaknesses better than anyone and attacks accordingly. He knows where your Achilles heel is. Because it is your weakest point, he persistently touches that hidden wound. You must stand before the Lord. Be sober, be watchful. Your adversary the devil prowls around like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour; resist him, firm in your faith. So be watchful and pray. -1 Peter 5:8
Suffering makes you fall to your knees. Your knees turn suffering into a door for you.

Rather than worrying about things that are struggling, focus on things that are opening and becoming. Sometimes certain things require time. If you love the Lord and know the Lord, He will counsel you.

How can you not have peace when you can pray? Today, all who meditate on this Word will have God's wonderful peace. Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus. -Philippians 4:6-7

My people, who are called by My name, will humble themselves and pray and seek My face and turn from their wicked ways, then will I hear from heaven, and I will forgive their sin and will heal their land.

Moses brought Israel's problems before God. Even in the midst of conflict, you can see God's glory manifesting. Do not focus on the conflict but only on the Lord. Then you will see the Lord's glory as He dissolves the problem. Jesus said to him: 'Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind.' This is the first and greatest commandment. -Matthew 22:37

The power that breaks down strongholds is prayer, and prayer becomes more powerful through our attitude. When my life becomes God's joy, and when I fear the Lord, 'the will of heaven will be accomplished on this barren earth.' When that person's deeds please God...

When problems and worries overwhelm you to the point of your head bursting, the Lord simply says, 'I love you.' He knows the depths of people's hearts, yet He will rescue me who loves the Lord and lift me, who knows the Lord, from the ash heap. You must trust and beseech the Lord with all your heart—the Lord who answers and transforms tribulation into glory. God said, 'Because he loves me, I will rescue him; I will protect him, for he acknowledges My name. He will call upon me, and I will answer him; I will be with him in trouble, I will deliver him and honor him.' -Psalm 91:14-15

When you are in distress, you must look upon the Lord's face. Saints who are afflicted by the world must fix their eyes on the Lord, our Comforter. When you are sad, the Lord comforts you, and the Lord of love will surely give you rest. 'Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.' -Matthew 11:28

Even if problems disappear immediately, you will never find true rest. True peace is not the absence of work and issues, but when the Lord grants you rest. It is not difficult because there are many trials; it is hard because there is no true rest. 'Rejoice in the Lord always. I will say it again: Rejoice!... Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.' (Philippians 4:4, 6-7)
When praying, look to God who hears your prayers. Then you will truly hold onto what is precious.

Holiness and sanctity, resolute determination, sacrifice and service, hope of heaven, loving your enemies, unceasing prayer and thanksgiving—all these things are not accomplished but become real in us.

Do not seek magic but seek mercy. What I need now is for the Lord to have compassion on me. And one of them, when he saw he was healed, came back, praising God in a loud voice. He threw himself at Jesus' feet and thanked him—and he was a Samaritan. -Luke 17:15-16

Where there is no revelation, people cast off restraint. -Proverbs 29:18 If the Word is not heard, there is no vision; if there is no vision, there is no hope and no mission. Then people live as they please and feel very empty. The Word must be heard and prayer must overflow. Then we live.
Discharged in 21 days; we continue to push forward through prayer.

Brother Park Han-seok has improved remarkably, and after his final scanning test, the doctor gave a review that discharge will be considered. Please continue to pray. As the kingdom of the Lord expands, darkness will be shattered and life will bloom wherever it touches.

It is futile to cry over those who took what was mine, over the merciless past and environment. The world has the power to take away but not the power to restore. To cry out to the Father—that is power.
**Today is London Saturday gathering (Fire Thursday). ** Please clarify what you hope for and desire from God. The Lord is the God of those who prepare and expect.

Without setting your purpose, you are swayed by others, caught by your weaknesses, doing only urgent things, and end your life with emptiness, squandering your life and time in vain. But those who pray set their purpose and live with the balance the Lord gives.

Daniel was a truly faithful employee to his boss, King Nebuchadnezzar. The king had authority over Daniel's life, his promotion—everything. Yet Daniel was free even before the pressure of such a superior. When threatened with the lion's den and the fiery furnace, he maintains composure and follows the truth. Prayer gives insight and vision to see that God is greater than those who appear great in the world.
That disappointment and hurt when my prayer was rejected, that brokenness where I felt shame and pain—the Lord tells me this is my 'calling.'

He's improving a lot. If we continue to pray, reduce medications, and he doesn't have seizures, we may see good results faster. Together with relay fasting, retreat preparation, and ongoing prayer for Brother Han-seok. The Lord reigns and the powers of darkness retreat.
Please pray. We are in battle. Darkness retreats before the Lord's sovereignty.

Prayer itself does not guarantee God's intervention. After Jacob prayed, he made gifts and divided his people into three groups (Genesis 32:7). This portfolio approach diversifies to reduce risk. When you pray this way, the Lord makes you break your hip again at the Jabbok River. If you say 'if I die, I die,' you will surely live.

Put your life into prayer. It will surely be answered. A person who has nothing but the Lord will surely receive an answer. When everything to rely on is gone and only the Lord remains, that is when your golden age comes.
Prayer is like laying rails on your life. A train running on rails is not being restrained but running faster. May we become saints who lay KTX rails on our lives.

A person of faith must not hastily interrupt or stop. 'All those nations try to scare us, saying, 'Their hands will get too weak to finish the work.' (Nehemiah 6:9) When there is obstruction and obstacle, may we become mighty warriors of faith who advance in faith. Now strengthen my hands. Nehemiah prayed this prayer and then held a tool in one hand and a weapon in the other, continuing to push forward.
Make the barren place holy; let me seek You there.

When told to watch and pray, people just lay down and sleep; when told to serve, they fight over the highest seats. But when the Holy Spirit came upon them, they were transformed into powerful believers. Is there anything impossible? May we become people who break through limitations with overflowing prayer and pull out the stakes that bind us.
Please ask that I be used more powerfully in the work I am entrusted with now. That is wisdom.
The reason I pray is that I believe God is faithful and the Holy Spirit works regardless of my feelings.
Please continue to pray for Brother Park Han-seok. His symptoms have recurred and he has been moved to the ICU. It seems he will have an MRI test on Monday.

If you have the passion to offer the very best to the most precious One, if you desire to move forward as a loving apostle served with greater preparation, you must pour more time and devotion into prayer meetings before worship. If you truly long for the Lord's glory...
'In the early church of Acts, 10 days of prayer and 10 minutes of preaching resulted in 3,000 being saved. But today, 10 minutes of prayer and 10 days of preaching result in 2 being saved.'

A believer who grudges the time spent in prayer is not worthy of precious things. Such a person is foolish, unable to discern what is most important. That is why many people live like beggars, merely prolonging their existence. Pray. You are priests and kings.

Power and transformation, the collapse of darkness, revival in this land—these are not achieved through movement. It is paying the price and kneeling where you stand. No one fails to do these things from ignorance. While knowing, they are deceived; while aware, they do not live according to truth. So prayer and intimacy—relationship—make truth truly true. If you proclaim truth without the Lord being the answer, you catch people. Do not judge; give life. Give them something to eat. -Mark 6:30-44
Prayer is not about filling what I want. Jesus knows our needs but wants to give us the most important thing—Jesus Himself. Prayer must therefore grow.

The end of all things is near; therefore be clear minded and self-controlled so that you can pray. Above all, love each other deeply, because love covers over a multitude of sins. -1 Peter 4:7 The final round of a match is truly fierce. Players pour out all their remaining strength to gain one more point. The Bible tells us we are running the final round. It says we must be the final runner in the final age, giving our all. A Christian giving their all means bringing a war already won to its true victory. Being clear-minded and wise with self-control, being awake and praying, and above all, using love that destroys all schemes to save souls.

* We respond today (fight back). Tonight 9:30 PM at the Mission Education Building. Content: Prayer to break through challenges. Target: Break through, Prayer, Healing, Response to the challenge. Plan: tonight only

If there is bitterness in your heart, do not boast about it (James 3:14). If there is hatred, jealousy, envy, and an unforgiven heart, God does not hear your prayer. No matter how great the work, it means nothing. James sounds a warning bell to those called spiritual elites who say they have great faith. No spiritual achievement can stop such bitterness and jealousy, nor will God acknowledge it. So if such bitterness is stirring within you, it is not something to boast about; rather, you must lay your face on the ground and bow down. The typical enemy of self-satisfaction and the cross within me is jealousy and envy. Recovery and miracles happen only through those who love.

'The end of all things is near; therefore be clear-minded and self-controlled so that you can pray. Above all, love each other deeply, because love covers over a multitude of sins.' 1 Peter 4:7-8 Saturday gathering runs on.

If a person of faith does not pray, even an ordinary life is difficult. Others walk easily through life, but I must stake my life just to take one step through suffering. I cannot understand why I chose this path. Though I cannot understand, that path is glorious and joyful. It is the narrow way.
A person you cannot forgive and hate is not wicked but pitiable. If you cannot respect them, love unconditionally; if love is difficult, then pray and have compassion. That is right.
From my living experience, the only option I can choose is gratitude and prayer.

One of two ways the Holy Spirit helps during prayer is by giving me strength to pray and assurance of answers through peace. The other way is by creating discomfort and a sense that prayers won't be answered, redirecting my prayers back on course. Ultimately, I must rely on the warm Holy Spirit who leads me from uncomfortable prayers to peaceful ones.

When baseless fears overwhelm me and anxiety about the worst that could happen confronts me, I ask that You remind me that only Your will is accomplished and that Your will is good for me. You know the path I walk. Please let go of worry and anxiety.

1 Timothy 4:5 - sanctified by the word of God and prayer. A life where nothing is wasted, a life that is accepted with gratitude, unfolds through the work of holiness in God's word and prayer. I ask that you hold fast to the word and commit yourself to prayer.
The greatest work is intercession and prayer.

We are still thirsty, pour your spirit out Lord. Never be satisfied with just this. God has prepared greater clouds and rain. As a minister now, my intercession is not a prayer asking God to use us on the European continent, but rather that our youth group be refined through praise before God who is already using us and will surely use us, and that we remain humble while giving glory to the Lord to the end. I pray that we become young people who hold only to Jesus and seek the Lord's joy until the end. We will become admirable young people who lay down our crowns before the Lord.
If there is something that frightens me, please rebuke me. If the path I walk is the Lord's path, who can stop me? When He rebukes and corrects me, darkness and fear retreat.
Many failures begin because we pray and start. We must pray before we prepare. Then God will show us. God is not One who supplements us, but One who helps us make a right beginning.

In the last days, many of God's people are needed. Through people who respond in faith and pray in love, the Lord will bestow miracles and power. I earnestly desire to become God's designer, God's engineer, God's teacher, God's son, God's daughter.

The most foolish and ignorant person is one who walks numbly along when the blade is pointed at them. How can you know when the blade is pointed at you? If you neglect prayer, you are already standing on the blade's edge. Repentance and confession before shame comes is the highest life. Repent.
Prayer always receives answers. If you endure long with patience, you will surely see the fruit.
When other paths are blocked, it means the path of prayer is open. If there is no one to meet, it is what the Lord wants to see.

Only the prayer of faith receives answers. Faith is beholding. Then it becomes reality. If you behold what does not exist as existing, believe it, and confess it, you will throw an egg and break a rock.
Luke 18:1 - Always pray and do not lose heart. To not lose heart, you must set a time and follow the habit of praying always. Have you lost heart? Begin praying at set times.

Heavy darkness and sin in the world capture many souls and lead them to death. What is remarkable is that within this London, and within campus, this fallenness has never received a proper challenge in the Lord's name. Like King Jehoshaphat and his people before the Aramean army, though Israel appears helpless, when we proclaim, praise, and advance in the Lord's holy name, this city will know who its Master is.

In Matthew 7 and Luke 11, Jesus speaks commonly of praying and asking. But Luke has a verse Matthew doesn't have. God will give to those who ask. But won't He give the Holy Spirit to those who earnestly seek? God can give all and will give all. But what He truly wanted to give was 'Himself.' His love that gives Himself... I hope you become young people who grow strong in that love.
Be assured that He who caused me to pray will also accomplish it. Prayer needs no words; it is me bowing down and asking.

But Jesus answered and said, 'Do not fear, for those who are with us are more than those who are with them.' And Elisha prayed and said, 'O Lord, I pray, open his eyes that he may see.' So the Lord opened the young man's eyes, and he saw; and behold, the mountain was full of horses and chariots of fire all around Elisha. - 2 Kings 6:16-17. Prayer is a token that calls forth the horses and chariots of fire from heaven. Not praying is despising God. Those who pray are protected and guarded by heaven's chariots of fire. Those who pray have their eyes opened to see heaven's chariots of fire. Today, are you surrounded and attacked spiritually, facing trials and difficulties? Pray with your life at stake. Then you will behold the heavenly chariots of fire that surround you.

When problems appear large, it is the devil's work. When I look to the Lord, problems are not problems but prayer topics and merely tools through which the Lord sanctifies me. We are under the Lord's mighty hand and under the Lord's precious blood.
We won victory through prayer. I believe in the Lord's omnipotence.
If there is nothing to accomplish through prayer, there is nothing to accomplish through anything else. The spirit must live so the flesh dies.
Through the storm You are the beacon, My song in the night. In the shelter of Your wings, Hear my heart's reply.

In the prayer meeting we meet the Lord and see His face. And we receive comfort without judgment or condemnation. I give thanks. Seeing the beautiful face of the Lord, my face is bowed down. The Lord's two hands touch me and comfort my body and soul.

**Gather at the place of prayer where God Himself calls.** I sent word to him, saying, 'The things you are saying are not being done, but you are inventing them in your own mind,' for they all wanted to make us afraid, saying, 'Their hands will grow weak from the work, and it will not be done. But now, O God, strengthen my hands.' - Nehemiah 6:8-9. Disturbance begins to arise in desolate Jerusalem. Those who build God's wall and those who hinder the wall building. Where there is the work of life, there is naturally the work of darkness too. But to those who know the truth, nothing can be a threat. God, who though unseen is most real, and prayer offered to Him—nothing can shake those who pray with that faith. We desire God to strengthen our hands. Mission Education Center 7:30 PM.
I worry much because I hold on. I must not hold on; only the Lord holds all things.
The enemy wants to bring a spirit of slumber to our life and cell but we must wake up, watch and pray! Mission Education Center 7:30 PM Tonight.

Even after victory in prayer and worship, anxiety can come upon returning home. That is entirely natural and a normal process of sanctification. We may be shaken, but we do not return to before. Because we have made peace with God and been transformed, we can never collapse. Because we are under the Lord's precious blood.

Faith is yielding that place to the Lord in every impossible and desolate situation. And delighting in Him forever. This morning we cover our members with precious blood. We bless you in the name of the honorable Lord.

If there is a bitter time in the heart, do not boast. If there is hatred, envy, jealousy, and unforgiveness in the heart, it means God does not hear prayer. The power of precious blood works through those who love.

For people who turn my heart inside out, I must certainly write a card and give a gift at year's end. Because of that person I prayed, and because of that person I came before the Lord's altar, didn't I? That person spent his own money and carried around lunch boxes while teaching me prayer and worship. Be grateful.

When I receive grace while praying and repeatedly ask joyfully, I receive answers. On the other hand, if my heart is heavy while praying and I give up after trying a few times, I almost never receive answers. Selfish prayers for personal gain and desires cannot be done with joyful, repeated anointing. Abandon prayers of the soul and offer prayers of the spirit.
Be bold. I pray for you.

Even though I prayed, is it frustrating that the Lord is not changing the situation? The Lord too is frustrated that people do not let go and grip tightly. Surrender and obedience must come before change and resolution.

It is the power of precious blood that freed me from sin. It is the power of precious blood that gives victory in great troubles. This night I pray that all the power of precious blood covers our members.

Is there anyone thinking, 'This is too hard. I cannot go any further'? Resist. It is a thought from the devil. Strive to gather and heighten the concentration of prayer. Now block the current and call upon the Lord's name.
I love the Lord who touches our hearts of stone and raises us up again. I only look to the Lord who opens rivers and paths in the desert and does new things within us.
Satan has asked for you, that he may sift you as wheat, but I have prayed for you, that your faith should not fail. Luke 22:31-32. Dear young members, you are always in my prayers. Be bold.
As important as taking up the full armor of God against the devil is praying always and without ceasing. Prayer makes power truly powerful.

If My people, who are called by My name, humble themselves and pray and seek My face and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven, and I will forgive their sin and will heal their land. God, cause hidden sins to be confessed, and let us humble ourselves, become low, and bring our prayers before the Lord. - 2 Chronicles 7:14.
Pour out your heart to Him and in turn He pours out to you!
If worrying won't solve the problem, please come to pray. When human problems are solved physically, side effects only result. Please solve life's problems spiritually.

God drives those who do not pray into situations where they must pray, and confines those who do not look to the Lord in a well where they can only look to the Lord. The best position is to be moved and reverent right where I stand now.
When you are in distress, see the Lord's face. Look to the Lord of peace. The Lord gives strength and protection to all who call upon His name.
If success came without prayer, that success will ruin you.
Cause us to stand again before the gospel and serve with God's heart and tears. May Your will be accomplished, O Lord.

* A special song never before heard in the world - 'Freed From Sin' - we sing. Please listen to it repeatedly and saturate yourselves in this song. And through cell meetings, please also sing this song. Pray that the power of precious blood may cover Africa, Asia, and Europe.

In 2007 when Samsung and LG were building specs and technology while eagerly chasing Nokia, Apple changed direction and rushed forward. Those who understand life through speed always chase, become flustered, and compare.
I pray that because of you, your community receives blessing.

I hope you seek grace to stand before the gospel again. Standing before the gospel does not mean never making mistakes again, but rather not using the Lord for selfish desires, and it means that I die and the Lord lives before Him who gave me life.
When serving the Lord with humility, there is much suffering, but O Savior, grant me strength to bear it well - Hymn 347

Do you feel frustrated and disappointed? When there is something urgent for me to resolve and it is not resolved, first I feel frustrated, and then I feel disappointed with God. Paul said that when the Holy Spirit comes, He enables us to call God 'Abba.' Beyond complex interests, the most important request is to call 'Abba' and run into His arms. Then you will experience God coming to find you.

The ugly duckling does not take revenge on the ducks that ostracized him because he has already become a swan. When you love your enemies and even pray for them, you are called a child of God. (Mt 5:45) The swan no longer needs to deal with the ducks. I hope you fly higher.
If you kneel in prayer, you will not stumble.
A Christian on his knees sees farther than an astronomer standing on tiptoes.
If you succeeded without prayer, you will be destroyed by that very success.

Please entrust to the Lord's hands the timid child within you, the child hurt by others' expressions, the discouraged child, the child without confidence. When the Lord embraces you, a healthy self enters within you.
Worrying does not solve anything. Anxiety does not improve things. Please entrust them to the Lord. The one who works among us is the Lord.
It seems like a dream that I can share prayer and the Word with such precious people. They are truly precious children of God. We love our young people.
Before asking to see something more mysterious and higher-dimensional, pray to see your true self.
When you face problems or trials, pray that you may see them from the Lord's perspective.

Bring all your problems to prayer. Then your problem is not solved by you but by God. Even problems from when you were young and ignorant, or even problems that existed before you were born—if you bring them up, He will solve them all.
Christ does not discourage the earnest seeker. From Acts 6 and the cry that was answered
Believe that what you asked for, you have received.
When you are in great pain, you must pray asking the Lord to humble you more.
You who run while praying are not alone right now. Hold the Word and be bold; intercession continues.

Please do not calculate too much. No matter how much you do, you cannot understand, and even if you could, there is no solution, which makes you more frustrated. If the situation is permitted by the Lord, there is surely a way of the Lord. Do not panic but pray.
When your heart aches, entrust everything to the Lord.
I pray that the Lord who rescued me on the day of my distress and humbled me on the day of my trial will personally bless all our young people.
Prayer meeting..., I offer myself before the Lord's altar.
Day has broken. Pour out grace and power. Hallelujah.
Please encourage each leader to attend both the morning and afternoon meetings today.
A person who sees problems and criticizes has not entered from darkness. A person who sees problems and weeps in prayer is part of God's kingdom family.
Worse than laziness is having only greed. Living holy without prayer is greed. Seeking happiness without seeking God's face is evil.

A leader must draw on authority. Authority is given to those who pray more and obey the Word. To the inexperienced Joshua, the Lord commands meditation on His Word and obedience. Even without charisma, the Lord gives power to those who obey.
When I earnestly seek the Lord, He meets us. The fire from heaven comes and burns our hearts and the hearts of all. Then the 'passion' that was dead comes alive again.

[Acts 9:11] The Lord said, 'Go to the street called Straight and inquire at the house of Judas for a man from Tarsus named Saul, for he is praying.' 9:12 'In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias come and place his hands on him to restore his sight.' When Apostle Paul was converted and praying, the one who delivered the answer was none other than Ananias, a faithful disciple on Straight Street. When laying on of hands, God's will that was prepared and made ready, the answer to prayer was accomplished as a miracle. When we seek together in unity, the kingdom of heaven unfolds in this dry land. When we stretch out our hands to bless, the will of heaven is accomplished.

We must be awake to pray and pray to stay awake. In joy or sorrow, able or unable, we must pray. With many words or few words or no words at all, we must pray. We will surely receive the answer that gives peace.
I believe that if I pray not to earn money, nor for fame, but because of that glory shown in our youth, and for a life worthy of the calling, there is no reason God would not work.
For the great ministry, be with family
When the Lord gives a signal to pray, you must pray with all your heart. I hope you pray without fail the prayer that overcomes temptation and brings victory.

What we must be most cautious about is becoming accustomed to serving God. We must go forward with tears, as when we first met the Lord, so that the world and I have no common ground. And from that place, pray asking the Lord to revive my worship.
This is the last week of prayer meeting; Thursday will likely be the final day.

Countless unseen messages fill the heavens through mobile signals. The intercessory prayer of Christians is far more meaningful, immeasurably more powerful as comfort, and actually moves the hand of the Almighty who works. One of the most important things is that coverage is actually infinite compared to mobile. ^^
I wish that outings were not at all inferior to prayer meetings, and hoped we would become stylish people with such rest and peace.

There must be assurance in your heart. Does Jesus truly dwell in me? If not, you must come before the Lord as soon as possible. You may need counseling with a minister and should also request prayer. Because this is the most important work.
Though the meeting has ended, the Lord's touch continues. That is how I will pray.

To say 'I will pray' is not mere courtesy but something that must be embraced with knees and heart. I understand this. Please request prayer from the minister. It is neither improper nor disrespectful. Those who become close to those in authority accomplish God's will.

Personally, being invited to a nearby dinner lasting three hours is far worse than quickly eating instant noodles and leading a three-hour prayer meeting. To pray, you must design your life. When you look at the design and structure, the content and power of that life emerge. I hope you become young people who stake your lives on the fire from heaven.

Please prepare the list of attendees and fasting prayer regarding the retreat on the 19th. I pray that on that day you will hear the Lord's voice and that ships that have been still will move forward gracefully.
I pray. Even with just the strength to crawl out, I crawl out and pray. Even if the great mountain collapses and the earth sinks, prayer continues.

When eating manna in the wilderness, faith must be strong. Without faith, you starve. But when entering Canaan, even without faith, we lived storing grain. When we do not need to live by faith, prayer, the Word, and worship easily disappear.
Just a moment before prayer, and prayer makes everything good. The essence comes alive in conversation and God becomes the master of the home.
That deep sorrow becomes great joy; the Holy Spirit will come to give that freedom at the prayer meeting.
Why worry when you can pray? Even after ministry, when you go home, intercessory prayer continues. The husband came, but the wife is so busy praying she doesn't even look out.
A song I want to sing for everyone. I returned with one life buried in my heart, having entrusted everything to the Lord.
Prayer cannot be done anytime. You must distinguish the time and the heart. That is why you must pray to pray.

When we do not pray, we often do not realize how desperately prayer is needed. Once prayer begins, we come to deeply feel that we cannot live without it. We pray hoping to improve, then we realize that prayer is necessary just to live.

Daniel was a civil servant. Not a ninth or seventh rank official, but the Prime Minister of the world's greatest superpower. Yet he prayed toward the temple three times a day, and everyone knew he was praying. Are you busy? Then there is one more reason why you should pray more.

The author of the masterpiece 'A Christian Living on His Knees,' an anonymous Christian, wrote in the book's preface that the root of all problems among Christians is not meeting God sufficiently, that is, not praying enough. Let praise go deeper, let the Word be delivered simply and clearly, and let prayer be done abundantly.
I often mentioned 'fulfilling my calling' as a prayer request. I truly wish to fulfill that calling.
If one candle is granted in my life, I will burn it through prayer. Then it can give necessary light to those who need it most.

One of the most foolish things is to criticize others. When we criticize, both the criticized and the critic become miserable together. Instead of spending time criticizing others, repent. God will exalt you. Let us go to build an altar of prayer.

The distinction between those who pray and those who do not pray is fair rather than discrimination. In fact, it could be said that it is deception that a person who prayed for two hours becomes ordinary.
Still keep going, pray, pray and pray
It is not stubbornness that receives blessings. God's blessings are poured out by God. Awake from deep sleep, now rise up and lift holy hands and pray.
The praying soul shall be blessed in all things; since the Lord does not forsake me, even if there is a trial or a threat to life, I will continue to pray to the end.

There are two ways to solve a problem. If you solve it with clever tricks, you suffer greatly and return to the same place after 40 years, but if you solve it through prayer, you fulfill God's will and become glorious. Experience the glory of the Lord.

Not asking about a path you have not traveled means losing your way, and not praying about a life you have not lived means wasting time and committing sin. Walk together, and then you will not fulfill the desires of the flesh.
If you pierce through with eyes and pray, God listens. I should go to Saturday gathering to pray.

Increase the concentration of your prayer. Sometimes God uses our deep pain as a channel for His wonderful plans. Because pain causes us to kneel against the flesh and moves us beyond human-level desires to seek the face of the Lord, advancing to a place of deep, earnest prayer. Even now the Lord surveys all the earth and seeks those who cannot live without Him (those of wholehearted devotion), pouring not human desires but God's desires, and challenges them to reach that level of prayer. He does not answer the prayer of one seeking to bear a son, but brings Samuel to usher in a new dawn of history, causing us to pray that prayer.
Hairdresser ma'am, I asked before if you were pregnant... you said you didn't know then... Please tell the hairdresser ma'am that we pray for you here too.
We must eat to live. We must pray to live.
When the purpose is clear, the method becomes sharp. Early morning on knees continues. Breakfast after dawn worship.
Suffering_and_Humility: * Comfort My People - "You did well to do that" explanations accomplish less than "How much does it hurt?" empathy. Sometimes loveless answers hurt people more than regrettable helplessness.

* How to Address Wounds - Others' wounds may leave marks on us, but we can respond differently. The nature of wounds is self-justification and situation control. Love and respect are shown by not being controlled or sympathizing with that anger, but by maintaining a consistent and continuous relationship before the Lord while truly empathizing with the other person without consenting to their demands.

* Why It Hurts - The reason the heart aches so deeply is spiritual slothfulness that compels us to seek the Lord. It is why a stiff spirit that only thought and did not know how to pray can kneel, prostrate itself, weep bitterly, and cry out to the Lord.

* Never Stop Praying Even When Your Heart Is Wounded - Sometimes God uses our deep pain as a conduit for His marvelous plans. Because pain causes us to kneel against the flesh and move to a place of deep prayer that transcends human-level wishes to seek the face of the Lord. Even now, the Lord searches throughout the world for those who cannot live without Him (those of whole heart), and challenges them so that God's desires rather than human desires are poured out and prayers of that level abound. He does not answer the prayer of many women seeking to bear sons, but causes them to pray the prayer that brings Samuel, who ushers in the dawn of history.

* The Suffering of the Cross - Historically, the reason the cross was particularly difficult is that prisoners were completely stripped of their clothes. But Jesus was not ashamed. Therefore, true love is bearing shame. "Let us fix our eyes on Jesus, the author and perfecter of our faith, who for the joy set before him endured the cross, scorning its shame, and sat down at the right hand of the throne of God" (Hebrews 12:2).

* "I go about mourning all day long" (Psalm 38:6) - The causes of grief shown in Psalm 38 were health problems and relationship issues. Yet this passage says all these peripheral problems stem from one's relationship with God. If there is a problem with God and there is no sadness but rather joy and gladness, this is truly a cursed life. For those whose happiness depends only on the cross, who are satisfied with the Lord alone, even a slight shift in life's focus causes them to go about mourning all day long. We will sorrow often ahead, but may we become young lions that sing of the Lord's salvation and help at each moment. "I am bowed down and brought very low; all day long I go about mourning" (Psalm 38).

* Last Christmas - Why did the Lord call at that time, in that manner, in that way...? And why make the path so thorny...? Though I cannot understand, God remains good, truly good, and faithful. The happiest moment of my life was when God made me weep... That God seeks me again. The power of the Lord's blood is sufficient. - May 2007
* The Greatest Cry - God's love spoken in complete silence from the cross.

* Courage for the Discouraged - In a world that cynically says everything is like that, we need people who can shake their heads and say the cross is not like that. It is not about struggling through faith without result, but dying to self while holding the cross.
* Power - The greatest power is being with the Lord. It is the power to be lonely for the Lord's sake. Most withdraw at this stage.

* Good Community - When organic matter (minerals, plants) undergoes temperature and pressure in the ground, it becomes coal. But when paying a greater price with higher temperature and pressure, it becomes diamond. Rather than lamenting that souls are not transformed, the mission of the earthly church should be to raise spiritual temperature and pressure.
* When Collapse Is Severe - Do not torment yourself with guilt. Rather, repent that you have not enjoyed the Lord.
* Maturity Does Not Happen Automatically - Maturity and revival necessarily involve bloodshed. Where God's glory exists, there is a bleeding leader.
* One Question - If later you are blamed and misunderstood for something you did not do, can you think of me and be "gentle"? - 2011 Staff Meeting
* Ministry Is Not a Picnic - Ministry is not playing around or going on a picnic. It is going to die. The problem is not that it is hard, but that we begin without resolving to die.
* A Strong Person - One who has determined to suffer hardship along with the Gospel.

* God Uses Overwrite Rather Than Delete - God's work is not to remove or exempt us from suffering. Suffering simply remains, but God displays greater glory and overwhelms suffering with that glory. "I consider that our present sufferings are not worth comparing with the glory that will be revealed in us" (Romans 8:18).
* When Distress Comes - If you try to remove or solve distress, the situation worsens. Respond with happiness more powerful than that distress. That is why Jesus is the answer.

* A Broken Heart - A broken heart has always existed and will continue to exist. Do not make that broken heart a desire of sin, but offer it as a sacrifice the Lord desires. The sacrifice the Lord desires is a broken heart.

* Why Does God Allow Variety Live Broadcasting? - Because He wants you to see yourself in that collapse and lift your eyes to behold the Lord. He desires that you truly experience the Lord in unspeakable distress.
* Trials Cannot Damage Happiness - Because this moment is so moving and worthy of thanksgiving.

* Intercession Request - Brother Jang Byeong-hwa, a member of the young adult group, lost his father. Today at 5:00 PM they will remove life support. Please pray for his mother, Deacon Kang Ji-sa, and Brother Byeong-hwa.

* Truly, until one seeks God's peace on their own, everyone walks the wilderness and encounters suffering. While walking that path, many wretched souls glance back at vain remnants of the old life. I ask that God grant such pitiful souls not shallow tricks but truly deep suffering, so they become souls that seek only the Lord's peace.

* It Is Not Because You Don't Know the Answer - You are not wandering because you don't know the answer. Storms and wilderness always have reasons and purposes, and when those purposes are fulfilled, they stop immediately. Wanting resolution while refusing to change means you have not yet humbled yourself enough. The highest prayer of those meeting tribulation is "lower me further." The problem of life is not lack of answers but a matter of obedience. "Remember how the Lord your God led you all the way in the wilderness these forty years, to humble and test you in order to know what was in your heart, whether or not you would keep his commands. He humbled you, causing you to hunger and then feeding you with manna... to teach you that man does not live on bread alone but on every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord" (Deuteronomy 8:2-3).
* Severe Disability - In cases of serious illness, either there are no warning signs or one passes through critical danger multiple times but easily forgets and returns to old habits.

* When You Face Trials - Do not think it strange. What should come has come, and simply respond as God expects. Give thanks and pray with confidence. "Consider it pure joy, my brothers, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith develops perseverance. Persevere so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything" (James 1:2-4).
* Suffering Beyond Myself - God does God's work. Therefore, when God is working, people should not worry but praise and be at peace.

* Uncontrollable Suffering - When facing suffering you cannot control, you must remain calm. If what happens occurs outside your realm, the Lord will handle it, and it is about establishing us who are on the margins as the center.

* Crisis - Has there ever been a crisis that was not a crisis? When it is hard, it becomes so hard, and when it is easy, we become careless and hardship comes. It is the famine land where we carry the cross anyway; like Jesus, let us become one grain of wheat.
* True Concern - At this hour, think of brothers and sisters in tribulation and suffering and pray earnestly. If you only seek relationships as you wish, how is that better than unbelievers?
* Why the Lord Came - He came to be rejected, abandoned, nailed to the cross, and die. So living as we follow Jesus, is that not more glorious than being welcomed?

* How Can We Not Give Thanks? - My friend was martyred in Iraq ten years ago. Because he believed in Jesus. To believe in Jesus unto death, how much more can we not give thanks for unavoidable obstacles on that path?

* When Facing Temptation - It means you are not giving thanks, you have not stayed awake in prayer even one hour, you are not loving, and you are thinking entirely self-centeredly. The Lord prayed that faith would not fail. Please respond with intercession and faith.
* I Am Sorry - My heart hurts so much and my tears do not stop. I pray for the bereaved and everyone.
* Two Certain Facts - First, life is difficult. Second, God is good.

* If Dying Is Hard, Living Is Also Hard - Life is hard because we struggle not to die. The standard of living spoken of in the world is roughly "Do you have a job?" "Are you married?" "Did you graduate from university?" "Do you have wealth, ability, beauty?" If you struggle to live, you die more; but if you let go enough to be profitable even in death, each breath of life becomes thanksgiving and emotion. Rather, hold to Jesus and die. The Lord has given enough, so do not expect another voice from the place of martyrdom. What more should you fear?
* A Precious Person - A precious person leaves behind a conspicuous empty space.

* Why Forgiveness - We can forgive not because it is bearable or we have good character, but because we are victors. And we come to understand that holding fast the rough cross until final victory is what is happy. A defeated person can never forgive.

* Side Effects of Unearned Income - Being happy with points others gained for you is brief, but living while seeing you can never surpass your own score created by the nation in your lifetime is a terrible punishment.
* If You Cannot Let Go, You Have No Mind to Let Go - If you are not prepared to lose, trials and tribulations will continue. For those who resolve to lose, only the Lord will be reward.

* Beyond Disappointment Begins the Main Point - Even God's faithful servants in Scripture felt severe disappointment when their desire to be understood by others regarding their faith and deep spiritual communion was rejected. But only by surpassing this does one participate in the Lord's main point and comes to enjoy the Lord's glory rather than seeking recognition. "Though my father and mother forsake me, the Lord will receive me" (Psalm 27:10).
* The Cross Is Heavy Enough - The cross is sufficiently heavy. But when waves are rough, it becomes a sturdy bridge.
* You Will Face Trials - You will be tested. What makes it a difficult trial is not the attempt to overcome, but that it shows you are already the victor overcoming it.

* The Way of Escape Is Provided - God does not allow trials beyond what you can bear. Around the time you cannot endure, He provides a way of escape. Usually this is thought to be some relief of suffering, but it is rather the only refuge, the way to come to Jesus, who is "the way."
* Reason for Pain - When this darkness passes, this darkness later becomes a message for you and others.
* What Must Be Done When Suffering - Going lower. Initially you lower yourself as a reaction to suffering, but the result is humility that saves life.

* Repentance Through Pain - When young people struggle, it pierces the heart of a minister with pain. Through that pain, we hear what He speaks: "Strive! Repent! I rebuke and discipline those I love..."
* Why Pain Is Pain - It is painful because you are not walking your own path. When you clarify the goal and correct your course, what is difficult becomes joyful.

* Closer Than Pain - After entering deep suffering, you realize Jesus is closer than the suffering itself. That is why suffering is a blessing. "Before I was afflicted I went astray, but now I obey your word" (Psalm 119:67).

* Gospel of the Cross When we testify to the gospel of the cross, we face criticism. In the midst of that suffering, the gospel of the cross is witnessed more powerfully and exerts greater influence. However, compromise to avoid blame will bring death to the church.
* Sorrow gives birth to compassion and lovingkindness. God is great in lovingkindness and boundless in mercy because He has experienced a deep sorrow that no one else could have.
* All In When breaking through, hitting halfheartedly truly hurts. If faith is not complete, everyone suffers. The most suffering person is oneself for not going all in. You must believe completely.
When you want to defend yourself, when you want to make excuses, simply remain silent. Just as Jesus entrusted His soul to the Father and remained completely silent.

When betrayed, when cursed, when treated unjustly, the heart aches. But what hurts more is when we fail to look upon the Lord correctly. When such pain comes, confess this: 'I am one who loves the Lord.'
Let go, and you will be free from suffering. Entrust it, and the Lord will resolve it.

We go to the hospital not to do what we want, but to endure pain to cure evil illness and save our lives. We come to the Lord's community not to have our desires fulfilled and our preferred situations created, but to be humbled and lowered so that the Lord's life becomes abundant. This is a place where we repent, shed tears, humble ourselves, and turn back to the Lord.
Though there are many fiery trials, let us not fear. For the Savior's power is great, and we shall overcome and have victory. Hymn 376

* Why does He allow suffering? It is to make us look only to the Lord. Because we keep placing our hope in people, He tells us to look only to the Lord. When in pain, see the Lord's face, behold the Prince of Peace. - J.W.P. 'Cast your burden upon the Lord, and He will sustain you.' - Psalm 55:22
* What is a Leader? A leader is one who carries fussing children, smiles with a red face, and does not show the countless arrows pierced in their back.

* The Most Pitiful If we had no resurrection, no cross, we would be the most wronged and pitiful people. God will repay us. Therefore, we wait silently, looking to Him. Holding on to the cross of resurrection, shedding tears, nodding our heads, and enduring saying, 'I can endure more.' The Lord is alive. 'If in Christ we have hope in this life only, we are of all people most to be pitied.' - 1 Corinthians 15:19

* Weariness of All Things If we see the world as a problem, everything is filled with landmines and with my strength and ability, I cannot possibly navigate through misunderstandings and wounds. That is why we need the Lord's grace in this mess beyond words. If it could be done by my strength, why would Jesus have taken up the cross? 'All things are wearisome; Man is not able to tell it. The eye is not satisfied with seeing, Nor is the ear filled with hearing.' - Ecclesiastes 1:8
Although there are many painful things, the reason I smile is because I have a vision that the Lord has given me. - 1999 New Year's Conference, Second Evening

Although nothing remains for me, if the Lord cares for His servant and grants me a final heartbeat to not give up, I will run again for the Lord's glory. If my strength runs out as I run, I will gather my last breath and call upon Jesus. Then I will truly be happy.

Philippians 1:29 reads 'For to you it has been granted for Christ's sake,' and up to here, the heart leaps, anticipating what grace God will give. But the second half says, 'not only to believe in Him, but also to suffer for His sake.' Christians have an inevitable struggle. The greater the struggle, the greater the victory. Hallelujah. 'This struggle is not a half-day sporting event where we go out briefly, win easily, and quickly forget. This is a long-term warfare—fighting to the end against the devil and his forces, a matter of life and death.' (Ephesians 6:12, The Message)
The Apostle Paul suffered for the Lord and died for the Lord. Because he chose what is most important. What is the most important thing for you now?
* The Season When Faith is Needed Faith is not needed when conditions are good saying 'Hallelujah~' but in the midst of trials when it is truly necessary.

The suffering given to people is constant. The Law of Conservation of Suffering. If the pain is the same, what determines one's life is love in responding to the pain. The amount of love for the Lord differs for each person.
People shed tears and beat their chests, but in the end, they walk in the direction they desire. This is truly sad and heartbreaking.
In the moment of loss, we truly believe in whatever we depend upon. When the Lord allows the pain of loss, please look quietly upon the Lord.
Life is not becoming increasingly difficult; rather, I am being prepared to walk increasingly in the way the Lord desires.
It was not that the other person was wrong, but that I needed refining. Look and interpret through the eyes of faith, not wounded feelings. Interpretation is power.
I pray earnestly for all friends who walk around sad all day because of loss, health, and relationship problems.

Wounds wound our hearts. A wounded, unhealthy heart produces the fruit of sin. Search your memory. When do I become weak regarding sin... Is it not when I am angry, wounded, and lonely, when I am captured by wrong emotions?
'I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory that is to be revealed to us.' - Romans 8:18

* Why Trials Are Not Frightening Stephen, in the place of death, shone with a face like an angel, looking toward heaven. The trial did not become a trial because he looked only to the Lord. Paul also breathed his last with the cross—the boundless grace of the Lord who offered His own Son on the cross—held in his heart, so he could run for the Lord alone without regret. 'Behold, I see the heavens opened up and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God.' - Acts 7:56

* My Symptom, God's Prescription The Lord acknowledges that we may grieve for a time because of the difficulties we currently face. But He desires us to know that it is not worth comparing with the glory of God we will see. 'I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory that is to be revealed to us.' - Romans 8:18

There are times when sad tears flow and deep sighs are breathed. It is a calling to lay everything before the Lord and pray. The most foolish thing is a soul that fails to recognize that signal and sleeps through it.

Trials and challenges will not stop. And they have never been easy. But neither am I afraid. What is there to cling to for one who has already died on the cross? Even if everything is taken away, the truth remains and His kingdom is eternal. Hope is strength.

When you meet a trial, you should regard it with joy. For the testing of your faith produces endurance. When endurance has its full effect, you will be complete and mature, lacking in nothing. Enduring without direction is difficult. That is why guidance is needed. With the wisdom and insight that last month we were attacked but this month we will reap a harvest, we can endure present suffering. Trial → Maturity, endurance requires wisdom. If you pass through the tunnel, go through boldly. God will surely reward you. 'Consider it all joy, my brethren, when you encounter various trials, knowing that the testing of your faith produces endurance. And let endurance have its perfect result, so that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing. But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all generously and without reproach, and it will be given to him.' - James 1:2-5

When you meet a trial, what the Lord commands when you meet a trial is endurance. Endurance makes me complete. The wisdom of looking to the Lord's promise will aid endurance. The Lord surely rewards those who hope in Him. 'Consider it all joy, my brethren, when you encounter various trials, knowing that the testing of your faith produces endurance. And let endurance have its perfect result, so that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing. But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all generously and without reproach, and it will be given to him.' - James 1:2-5
If trials accomplish hope, break through courageously. For tribulation produces perseverance, perseverance produces character, and character produces hope.

There are various difficult factors and trials in faith. There are two ways to go that path. Either go filled or barely go. Environmental problems are solved by being filled, but spiritual poverty is not solved by circumstances. Simply be filled.

Being blessed in faith does not mean exemption from various trials and hardships. Rather, it is experiencing the Lord who is greater than such problems. The Lord's blood is deeper than my pain and the Lord's grace is greater than my problems.
If we can overcome in disappointing situations and endure criticism and mockery with hope as our reason, we can receive the grace God desires to give.
The cross does not exempt us from life's storms. Rather, it enables us to walk upon the storms.

The cross is the highest conclusion the Lord has given. If there were a trial that the cross could not nail down, the Lord would not have instructed it from the beginning. The spirit of the cross is, 'I cannot bear to see you die; I will pay any price to save you.'

When you are in pain, you must speak of it. Even if I was punished for my own wrongdoing, when I shed tears of pain, the Lord binds up my wounds, restores me, and glorifies me more than before. 'O people, pour out your hearts before Him...'
How sad it is that I cannot give more... When in pain, we can give most powerfully, and in darkness, we can long for the Lord most earnestly.
Do you shed tears of mourning? Truly, you are blessed. Genuine tears are like a bomb that breaks through blocked paths. 'Blessed are those who mourn.' - Matthew 5:4
* Testimony of Suffering The reason one's heart is pierced like with a sword is because God has made me honorable and is working within me.
Weakness is the true strength that enables us to experience the power of Christ. 'My grace is sufficient for you, for power is perfected in weakness.' - 2 Corinthians 12:9

* Schedule A certain Christian known for his piety was revealed to be involved in a crime. Moreover, he was a perpetrator caught at the scene of attempted rape and received a heavy sentence, going to prison. While he served his sentence, some said that human hearts are incomprehensible, and others said the God he believed in so much was a fraud. His name was Joseph. (Genesis 39) Persevere. God's schedule and my schedule may differ somewhat.

One day, a puppy reads a book titled 'The Power of Positive Thinking' and is convinced it can fly. It climbs high and jumps off, seeming to fly for a moment. Later it wakes up in the intensive care unit of an animal hospital. While thinking, maybe if I pray more intently and fast more, it will work. But it absolutely cannot. The only way is to ride like an airplane in the master's arms.

* Tears Not Lightly Shed Abraham, the source of blessing, received the glory of becoming God's friend and became the father of all believers. However, many overlook the four days of blood-tears he must have shed. 'My son, God will provide for Himself the lamb for the burnt offering.' - Genesis 22:8
Whatever tribulation comes and whatever collapse of heart occurs, if at this very moment you can cry out 'The blood of the Lord has power!' then that is already sufficient.

* Human Life Comes with Suffering as a Default Option When God does not work according to my will, it is easy to blame others. That is because we try to walk in the air apart from reality. Life is not taking a vacation; it is paying the price and holding onto holiness.

Until we are refined, even when we receive grace, we are more like rough stone than a jewel. Angels cannot refine us. That is why He refines us through people. When our interpretation of life changes, 'enemies' transform and become our 'life teachers.'

* When Meeting Injustice We will meet unjust situations anyway. We may be misunderstood about things we did not do and our intentions. There are two choices: If we simply rage, there is no resolution, and both we and others die. The other choice is to think of the Lord and His glory within that situation, bear it, and become beautiful. There will be great glory for those who think of the Lord who has allowed all this. 'It is good for me that I was afflicted, that I may learn Your statutes.' - Psalm 119:71. 'For this finds favor, if for the sake of conscience toward God a person bears up under sorrows when suffering unjustly.' - 1 Peter 2:19

* Tears of the Same Composition but Different Reason A person feels pain because of what is within themselves. If pain comes from people, then people are filling you; if pain comes from worldly conditions, then the world is filling you. A person of God feels pain because of God. That is why His tears become my tears.

When I was twenty years old, with a shattered and torn heart, I spent the entire night in prayer at an all-night prayer vigil, not knowing what I was praying for on that Friday.
