Around 2 a.m., the late Pastor Kim Jong-han, who was seventy-three years old, spoke these unexpected words: 1. I understand all the pain in your heart. 2. And you walk the path of the Lord. God understands me better than anyone else. And He encourages me to walk the Lord's path. A person of faith does not fail in the Lord's work because of personal circumstances. For the past twenty-four years that I have kept this, it is a rule and the minimum courtesy toward the Lord. By faith alone! I can do all things through Him who gives me strength.

The essence of faith is no different now than before. In the past, they said they would throw you into a lion's den if you did not deny the Lord; now they threaten with failure and despair if you do not have greater affection for other beings than for the Lord. To still reject these things, you must shed blood and cling to the cross. Resolve to shed blood. Rather, you will have peace.

* The Secret to Building Life To build a blessed life, you must remove many things. Among them, if possible, you would especially want to remove the causes of suffering and be free. One of the greatest sufferings is the pain of childbirth, yet no one tries to abandon the cause of that pain—life itself. Suffering is not a problem to be avoided but a work to be perfected.

Only the Lord holds me. Even when I stumble, He still holds me. If Your law had not been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction—Psalm 119:92 This word is my comfort in my distress; Your promise has preserved my life—Psalm 119:50

* The True Gospel The world is not moved by the church's buildings, high positions, or power. It desires to hear the true gospel. A certain pastor's wife preaches the gospel to a taxi driver. He is unmoved. But then she adds this: 'I am on my way back from the hospital where I have just received a cancer diagnosis. Driver, please believe in Jesus. After death, heaven certainly exists. You must truly believe in Jesus.' The taxi driver hears her. The greater glory and influence of a believer is not 'seeking blessing' but 'overcoming'—setting one's heart... (Daniel 1:8)

The king's heart was very grieved, and he went up to the chamber over the gate and wept. As he went, he said, 'O my son Absalom! My son, my son Absalom! If only I had died instead of you, Absalom my son, my son!'—2 Samuel 18:33 Those who have the heart of the Lord, participate in the Lord's mourning.

Like Job in great suffering, when I have lost everything and am in dark darkness, if the Lord were to ask what I desire, I would ask that all the land I tread upon would worship Him. I would ask to see it covered by the Lord's blood. Make this place where I stand holy, and fill it with the fragrance of the Lord.

* Why the Storm is Fierce If a test is too easy or too difficult, it loses its discriminative power to measure ability. Everyone can take it, but it is never easy; the higher the standard of the test, the higher the difficulty. Therefore, the wicked cannot endure judgment and fall away, but the blessed and the righteous endure. The storm is fierce because not everyone is permitted to walk that path, and that place is prepared only for me. The wicked are not so; they are like chaff driven by the wind. Therefore, the wicked will not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous—Psalm 1:4-5

We are shocked when people we consider famous suddenly become people of the past world in an instant. Trials did not kill those people. Most of them 'willingly' gave up before trials and fear. Trials and fear have never voluntarily or actively caused people to be destroyed or die.

When we pass through a tunnel, what we need most is to become lowly. This is so that 'He humbled you and tested you to know what was in your heart, whether you would keep His commandments or not'—Deuteronomy 8:2

When suffering comes, we search for its cause, and when we cannot find it, we blame God. We think God gave us suffering or at least did not prevent it. Then we lose. We are not permitted to know the cause of most suffering. We must only approach God, who can save us by faith.

When we reach the bottom of life, we see another side of ourselves there and meet the Lord. When we see the Lord from different angles of life, especially difficult angles, I grow deeper and come to know the Lord more intimately.

We are made by the Lord and are in His hands. Therefore, we do not force things. We believe that entrusting ourselves to Him is the most right and good path. The Lord always gives me the best. If He did not give something, it is because it was not worth giving. What we lack is more beneficial. To Satan, the Lord boasted of Job's faith. Because of this, Job's trials began. So people think suffering is the subject of courage. The conclusion is that Job changes. He becomes quite a respectable person of faith into a person of faith who sees the Lord firsthand. It seems the suffering began from a wager between God and Satan. It is not so. It is the Lord's providence to let us abandon what is good and advance to what is best. Therefore, the theme of Job is grace. Through refining, Satan's quarrel is transformed, and we become like refined gold. The ability to work all things together for good belongs to Him.

We are patients with a grave illness. When we undergo such an enormous surgery, we may go almost to the point of death. It is truly merciless surgery. Through this major operation, God draws out the sick self within us. The scalpel and forceps that God uses are none other than the authority figure who knows how to penetrate our wounded hearts and weaknesses so well—one just like us.

* Suffering as a Discriminating Test When a college entrance exam is too easy, everyone scores high and cannot be evaluated. Suffering is difficult because it narrows the entrance, not allowing just anyone to enter but only those who have received grace—a place prepared for me alone. If it is difficult, it is prepared for you.

We endure tribulation and overcome challenges. However, enduring and overcoming is not the goal. Through them, we unleash God's rule and dominion in our sphere. You will be slandered and attacked—Luke 6:26. Yet the suffering we now receive is not worthy to be compared with the glory we will enjoy—Romans 8:18. To unleash the Lord's rule in healing, prophecy, love, restoration, politics, economics, and sports, we must fight, endure, and not give up. Then God will use us as a light to the nations—Acts 13:47

Our spiritual predecessors could not deny the power of the blood and were imprisoned in dungeons and jails, and had to lay down their lives. We ask that you proclaim again that cross and blood. Since humanity's problem is the problem of sin, only the blood can solve it. They delivered that gospel as a matter of life.
If you understand the meaning, you can endure. Moreover, you can rejoice. Meaning gives the power to love. Love bears all things and endures all things.
I pray that you become powerful young people who awaken souls in spiritual lethargy, seeking dedication without commitment and a crown without suffering, compromising with reality—Winter 1995

Pray this way. You will see the Lord's glory. When glory is great, it more than overcomes suffering. 1. Present your requests with thanksgiving—Philippians 4:6, Psalm 50:23 (Paul's prayer) 2. Pray to see glory—Exodus 33:18 (Moses' prayer) 3. Ask for a hearing heart, that is, wisdom—1 Kings 3:9 (Solomon's prayer) 4. Pray that your will be broken and the Lord's will be accomplished—Mark 14:36 (Jesus' prayer)

When you pass through the pain of life, please pray this way: God, please lower me further. That is the fastest way to pass through the tunnel. Humble me. God rejects the proud and permits no such thing to anyone. Remember the forty years the Lord your God made you walk in the wilderness to humble you and test you, to know what was in your heart, whether you would keep His commandments or not—Deuteronomy 8:2
Life is difficult because we do not shed blood. Rather, when we willingly shed blood for Jesus, we are most happy and glorious.

The stronger a person's self-management, the longer and harder the refining is. The 'self-defense' reaction to protect ourselves becomes a poison. It becomes easy when we acknowledge and entrust ourselves before the Holy Spirit.
If you want to boast, you must boast in weakness. God's power abounds all the more in our weakness. In weakness, God's power, unmixed with impurities, becomes complete.
The greatest reason people are wounded is not from others, expressions, or words of rejection. The true reason is selfishness that protects ourselves.
When my determination and plans fail and I become discouraged, only then does the Lord work. When I am weak, I am made strong.

I see, today, many believers living lives without the power of Christ and without life. This is because there is no power of life. Why is there no power of life? Because life comes from the power of resurrection, and there is no resurrection without the cross. If you refuse to take up the cross, the work of life does not happen.

Despair is the process of going to rest. The greater the despair, the greater the scope of the Lord's work. Despair is seeing your true self and essence; rest is seeing the Lord's righteousness, salvation, grace, and mercy.

Come forth as refined gold. Only One knows the way I take—God. Only after God has refined me do I become refined gold. Refined gold is valuable not because of its use but because its very essence is precious. The purpose of refining is to obtain pure gold. When impurities are removed from me, only Jesus remains. What is more important than Korean church problems and contemporary demands is 'you doing well.' The owner of refined gold is only God, and refined gold cannot be told what to do. But he knows the way that I take, when he has tested me, I will come forth as gold—Job 23:10

When it is so hard I feel I am dying, even though just a moment ago I could hardly breathe and was nearly at death's door, that I can cry out 'once more~' is because I believe in the Lord and live for the Lord. Before the Lord, everything is reset.
The disciples rejoiced when persecuted. Being resisted in the world meant they had become worthy of heaven.

'Lord, shape me,' is a prayer where the Lord gives just a brief sweet period of courtship and then crushes all my stubbornness and makes me pass through a furnace of fire. If you prayed 'shape me,' then being shaped is done. It is the fastest way. The Lord says: 'For I know the plans I have for you, plans not to harm you but to prosper you and to give you hope and a future. You will call upon me and come and pray to me, and I will listen to you. You will seek me and find me when you seek me with all your heart'—Jeremiah 29:11, 13
There is no one without problems. The choice in that storm is whom you will hold onto. The storm has a purpose, and when that purpose is fulfilled, it will become calm even if you say it should not.

I held fast to a member who was rapidly becoming distant from the Lord and preached the gospel to them. 'You were so moved by God's love, weeping and saying how good the Lord is—why have you become like this?' My friend's answer was: 'What has God done for me? Everything is blocked from life.' From my perspective, what my friend wanted was ambition and desire for relationships the Lord did not guide, and success in what they wanted. Poet Song Myung-hee once writes a poem in the voice of the Lord. How painful God's heart must be—He gave His only Son, yet humans complain of lack. How painful the Lord's heart must be—He gave Himself, yet people deny and curse Him.

In heartache and pain that you can never tell others, let only the Lord be the light in your darkness. With the resolve to die if need be and to lose everything, cry out, 'Come on, fate!' and it will tuck its tail and slink away.
I live for the Lord, work for the Lord, toil for the Lord, and will die for the Lord.

The Lord gives us exceedingly beautiful places. He has allowed family and coworkers, gifts and service. He has also given us tears and anguish that tear at our hearts. Through these, He will make them beautiful. The Lord's zeal will surely be accomplished. Blessed be the Lord who gives pain and anguish. His steadfast love endures forever. The portion marked out for me is in pleasant places; indeed, my inheritance is beautiful to me—Psalm 16:6
The Lord is a good Lord. Believe that the Lord fights for you. Whether the suffering you have chosen or suffering caused by others, the Lord does not forsake us.

Following the Lord, especially in ministry, means knowing losses beforehand. Therefore, spiritual loss, material expense, and the pain of broken relationships are not feared but considered a necessary payment that must be made. Those who have decided to follow the Lord do not regard such things as terrifying pain. Why? Because they have seen the Lord's glory. Personally, poverty and loneliness are difficult, but I am not afraid.
If there is a challenge so weak and dark that I can only look to the Lord, bright enough to whiten the darkest night, then it is the Lord's 'proposal' to meet me personally and intimately.
Suffering is when I decide what the Lord should decide. After refinement, I become devoted unto death in the small work the Lord has given me. The Lord sees.
Do what the Lord desires. Compared to the glory we will enjoy, the suffering and price we pay now are not worthy of comparison.

If Your law had not been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction. I will never forget Your precepts, for by them You have preserved my life—Psalm 119:92-93 The only reason that levels every valley of difficulty and every mountain of anguish is God's Word. It is the cause of life that gives me life.

Exercise is lifting dumbbells against gravity. Muscles are built by overcoming the resistance of gravity. What is created by avoiding gravity is yellowish fat. Do not fear suffering and try to escape it. Rather, find and obtain the treasure hidden within it.
If anguish now presses upon your heart, confess this: I am dead. Then you will see what the Lord does.

Have you recently made an unfavorable choice for the Lord? Faith is not following 'benefit' but following 'righteousness.' So sometimes there is 'disadvantage.' Blessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness.
Now it is calamity, but if it turns around later, it becomes great blessing.

Only persistence bears fruit. What does not persist is merely a waste of time and consumption. Do not throw away good work; cling to the Lord to the end and surely bear good fruit. Trials make fruit beautiful.

True joy is not acquisition or achievement. It is bearing the cross and looking to the joy coming before us in the future. That joy is able to overcome present lack and suffering. Through Him who...endured the cross for the joy set before Him—Hebrews 12:2

The truly frightening diseases are those with no warning signs. Without pain, we would not notice our bodies rotting away. To escape spiritual leprosy, we must experience ruthlessly sharp suffering firsthand. That is a blessing.

The genuine and the fake are revealed when damaged, when tested. Sandalwood releases more fragrance when cut. Imitation seems plausible, but from the start it has no honey to give or sacrifice. The genuine does not fade in color, and its subtle lingering essence continues.

The reason God comforts my hurting heart and becomes a good friend when I am lonely is because God has unspeakable circumstances. When God causes us to shed tears of blood unable to possess the blessings others have, it is because He desires to give us Himself, who is the true blessing. That is why it hurts. Isn't it worth thanking Him for?

At first, we pray not to collapse. It is a feeling of grasping at straws. But when we encounter the almighty God amid the storm, recognizing our boundless weakness, we think it is fine to collapse. We gain not only peace but even boldness.

What youth must do is pass through fire, be refined, and undergo tempering. Then we can nail down ambition masquerading as calling and surrender it to the Lord. That is one of the most essential refinements. What I reflect on most painfully at my age is the fact that I need far more tempering and correction than I realize. I seek the Lord's compassion.

When David fled from Saul, he did not overcome his trials by clinging to the vision of a bright future throne. He simply endured. Clinging to the Lord, praising through tears, as if today were the last day.
When addicted to pleasure, even wings become buried.

Quo Vadis, Domine—Lord, where are you going? These words appear twice. Once directed to the Lord bearing the cross, and another time, not recorded in Scripture but in Roman history, to the Lord bearing the cross who appeared before Peter fleeing persecution. Through the great surgery of the cross, Jesus took all our sins and poured out God's grace upon us. There is nothing more we can offer. The cross alone is perfect redemption.

After great spiritual victory comes strong spiritual challenge and decline. Peter chose to return to his former life as a fisherman in that decline, yet gained nothing. We overcome trials through endurance (James 1:3). Endurance means thinking of the Lord and waiting for Him. And in that waiting, it is important not only to wait for others but also for myself to wait for myself. Especially if I can bear long until I can be transformed, I will gain life through the pain of childbirth. 'By your endurance you will gain your souls.' Luke 21:19

Passing through a tunnel is a painful thing. It is dark, and we do not know how long it will last. However, rejecting and avoiding the tunnel is greater suffering. We do not welcome tunnels, but a tunnel is also the fastest shortcut. Do not refuse the shortcut; run hard through it.
Do not become somewhat comfortable in captivity, but fight the good fight fiercely. The glory of the saints cannot compare with present sufferings.
Being tired and exhausted, suffering—these are not always things we dislike. What matters more is for whom these are endured. If it is for the Lord, we go without regret.
God, if I wait like this... I will truly die. God: Hmm~~!? ...., (Still not dead... what a remarkable one...) Children~~ raise the level higher
God makes me hurt because He loves me. Because there is something special He wishes to tell me. That is: love as much as you hurt.
God sometimes brings us down infinitely, down to the roots of mountains beneath the sea. There, repentance bursts forth, and the Word begins to be experienced alive.
Though I lack much and face hardship, God understands the depths of my sorrow and is with me. He holds my hand, gives me strength, and says to me: Still, you must walk the Lord's way. Amen~

God reveals our hidden darkness through pain and suffering. It is difficult for hidden things to be revealed, but we cannot avoid it when we approach before the Lord in holiness and completeness. We become complete to the depth of our pain.
God disciplines and refines us in the wilderness. When people pass through the wilderness, what they must do is remain silent.

God gives those He loves most unimaginable conflict and anguish. This is because He wants us to completely let go of everything and look only to the Lord, clinging only to His Word. You encounter tribulation in the world, but be courageous. Jesus has overcome the world.
God awakens our sleeping souls through 'problems.' However, He does not immediately solve those problems. He waits a little longer until we truly desire transformation.
God transforms us to use us, and for that transformation, He blocks the very thing I rely upon. He makes mink into dog fur.
God makes us shed tears to mature us, but in fact God's heart aches more. You can confirm this by seeing how much He blesses us when training is complete, to the depth of that pain. God is faithful.
God refines us faithfully. When He deals with me, He refines me through difficult paths—through what I 'cling to.' When you let go of your attachments, you will quickly pass through the tunnel.
A pain in one's chest means I must seek the Lord more. Because it hurts, I am a believer.
God responds to our groaning. Because He is merciful. But He does not support all weak people. If we complain when weak, we will fail. Rather, if we praise, He will give us strength.

God does not realize our selfish desires. Jesus came as the suffering servant, and though Jesus is sinless, He came as the suffering servant in my place to wash away sin and make us holy—not to be a servant we command at will. Rather, we must stand as holy servants serving Jesus. Just as the Lord did, we must be transformed into holy lives of anointing and purity.
If necessary, God changes suffering into miracle, gives strength to overcome it, or continues to permit it according to His deep purposes.

There is a reason for not meeting God. It is because of wrong premises. It is because of the hypothesis: 'God will be glorified through my success.' Gold medals, prestigious universities, good jobs, and social status are the only things that glorify Him. This is wrong thinking. Scripture says that believing is glory. Standing against evil is already a blessing. Crying out in pain amid suffering is the glory of the saints. I pray that we become believers who hold fast not only to One who is with us, but to One who grieves with our hardship and difficulty, shedding tears, yet does not give up with the absolute solution of the cross.
God rejoices most over those who ache with God's ache.
God's grace and love do not wound us. That we receive wounds frequently, well, and more excellently than others means that incomparable love has been poured out exceptionally abundantly.

It is grace when God permits hardship at times. Because that is when we become strongest. When we kneel in tears and fall face down on the earth, it appears truly weak, but in reality, it is the moment we become most solid and strong. 'When I am weak, then I am strong'—2 Corinthians 12:10

Why God regarded Abraham as a friend: On the day Jesus died on the cross, though many angels and saints were beside God in heaven, none could understand God's heart like Abraham. Abraham, who truly understood and grieved with his heart the painful feeling of killing his beloved son and offering him. Abraham and God had shared the same boat, experienced the same suffering, and that is why God unhesitatingly called Abraham His friend—His companion. For God, a 'friend' is defined as one who can share pain. Are you sharing pain now?
If there is sadness and suffering that God did not give, I pray you refuse it. If that is difficult, I pray you seek 'the power to ignore it.'
When you are struggling, you are not alone. God loves and watches over you, and He presses another soul to share that heart and kneel earnestly.

After one difficulty passes, another stands waiting. Yet it does not matter, because those running for the Lord's glory will never give up or stop. Believing in the Lord's omnipotence and that He works all things together for good, I believe it will be accomplished.
One day's excitement becomes another day's abandonment and regret, and one day's pain of letting go becomes another day's glory returning. Genesis 40:23 ...he forgot Joseph.

If you believe that all things work together for good, you will know that through the wounds the Lord permits you, the Lord also speaks. Bearing pain, let the Spirit of the Lord come and comfort those who suffer, and ultimately God's righteousness will be accomplished.
When chocolate receives sunlight it melts, but mud becomes harder. Some people become stronger facing the same trial. Just a little more gaze, until it becomes sharp as steel...

In modern times, there are many teachings and expressions. Many interpretations and solutions for numerous difficulties are presented. How did believers of old confess and move forward with these?: 'I will lay down my life for the Lord. I have nothing to boast of. Without suffering, there is nothing to fear in Jesus. Before a martyr, there are no problems. For one who values his life, everything is a problem.'

Reality is that we may weep even while eating, but the Lord makes us dream beyond walls. Confess and declare before the Lord and the world: 'I shall not die but live, and declare the works of the Lord.'

Grant clear wisdom and the spirit of revelation amid confusion, and give new strength, emotion, and inspiration to our weary bodies and hearts. Send those who can preach the gospel to family in our homeland, experts and helping hands who can untangle complex legal problems and tangled issues. Grant healing to our bodies, especially pouring the Lord's healing oil upon our knees. Fill the emptiness and purposelessness in our hearts with the Lord's truth and meaning. Let us rest in the Lord, not driven by workplace and visa issues. Grant healing to wrists and joints, and cover us with love. Fill loneliness with the Lord's time and cause all our work to look only to the Lord and entrust it to Him. Overcome human ambition with calling and act with neither light nor name. Let judgmental and condemning thoughts depart, and serve with love, compassion, and compassion like the Lord. Reverse the situation in our studies, let us meet helping hands, work miracles. In situations seemingly impossible by human eyes, bestow needed and prepared power. Guide my path whether to serve the Lord in England or Korea. Pour out the strength to bear this work. Bring harmony among parents and open doors of economic provision in the home. Refine me and establish me as a leader serving souls, and arm me. Let anxiety and worry in my heart melt away before the name of Jesus. Deliver me from the temptation and greed of sin and satisfy me with the Lord alone. The God of power, pour out the strength I can bear. I pray earnestly for friends these days with a troubled heart.
Walking in tribulation humbles me and causes me to keep the Lord's law.

Often, when trying to prove one's love, one gives excessive gifts or plans events to bring emotion. But all of this can be imitated in a moment. However, 'sharing pain' and 'continuously empathizing with that pain without change' cannot be imitated. Only when we become one does his pain become my pain.
When it is hard, you should pray like this: God, deal with me and bring me lower.

When it is hard, giving up is the easiest and most convenient choice, but also the most verified worst choice. The Lord did not give up at the cross but accomplished it. 'Finished' means endured well. When difficult, having faith brings victory, and most faith appears in the form of endurance.

People say they will give up because it is hard. Before saying it is hard, you must try praying and then speak. That is not being shot down while flying but falling down on your own while waiting on the ground. Without prayer, you do not even have the right to say it is hard.
When strength fails, look to the Lord. The Lord of comfort will give you rest.
On days too difficult, rather than asking why it is hard, it is enough to know we can return to Jesus and that the Lord embraces us as we are. Jesus gave us everything.
It seems the strong will survive. But those who survived are the strong. Endure. Then you will become strong and return.
I intercede for our young people facing tangled problems like Seltzer that cannot be solved by words. Be courageous.

The source of faith is Jesus. As we come to know Jesus more deeply and understand His works, we develop stronger faith. The power to overcome difficult suffering and unwanted trials is found in knowing that there is greater joy for us. We can understand this when we behold Jesus. Let us look to Jesus, the author and perfecter of our faith, who for the joy set before Him endured the cross, scorning its shame, and sat down at the right hand of the throne of God. Hebrews 12:2

Fasting while crying out and weeping is a painful experience. However, the result is transformation of life. When we sow seeds with tears, we will surely reap in joy. That harvest will be returned to us not as wealth, but as life.

Simply put, there are two kinds of trials that I face: trials I am able to bear, and trials I am unable to bear—when God provides a way out. Believe in this truth and be assured and thankful for overcoming and victory over trials.
When my heart is deeply wounded, I rely on the precious blood of Jesus. When I rely on His blood, pain transforms into praise.

If someone rides a roller coaster at an amusement park and complains constantly as though it will never end, it will look pathetic. When the time comes, it ends, and we should simply enjoy it while it lasts. The Lord has given us only two options: 1. I will allow only trials you can bear. 2. When a trial is unbearable, I will provide a way out. Therefore, we should not panic but approach with thanksgiving and worship.

The difficulties I experience and the wounds I receive are not actually the reality. First, I must look back and ask why such pain came and who permitted it. The reality is that I am not looking at the Lord properly but avoiding Him. It is not the other person who has become my adversary, but the thorn that the Lord places beside me to call me to repentance.

Rather, be thankful. Without that situation, I could have suffered less and avoided facing uncomfortable truths. But I would have remained a fool, seeing only what I want to see and pursuing only convenience and comfort. Especially, there is reason to give thanks for that One's faithfulness and sovereignty who permitted all of this.
Also, for young lambs in difficulty, pray for effective intercession to govern them. When the Lord governs that soul, He brings rivers in the desert and makes flowers bloom in the wilderness.

When passing through painful wilderness, you must pray. Pray like this: 'Lord, humble me further.' It will most effectively and quickly accomplish the purpose of that wilderness. (Deuteronomy 8:16) He humbled you, causing you to hunger and then feeding you with manna, which neither you nor your ancestors had known, to teach you that man does not live on bread alone but on every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord.
That the Lord has granted me obstacles and challenges means He has poured out His love. Because without passion and perseverance, we cannot break through.

When I am tempted in my heart and burdened with heavy sorrow, the Lord is with me at those times and always helps me. He always loves me and always gives me new life, and I always look to the Lord for the hope of glory. —Hymn 456. Do not lament that darkness is dark, but look to the Lord and gain hope. The Lord is with us with immeasurable love and grace.

* Misplaced rights, grateful consideration. The blessings we enjoy as a matter of course are actually God's faithful care. Sometimes He withdraws that care for a time in a place called wilderness. The person 'I' often confuses 'consideration I should be grateful for' with 'rights I am entitled to.' I pray that God's grace may overflow for all those who think of God and endure suffering at this very moment. Suffering is a reminder that draws us to God.
When your heart is dark and troubled, think of the Lord. The Lord, who had nowhere even to lay His head, carried our suffering in our place.

*Overcoming and conquering trials. Extreme trials that are difficult to endure come to an end when we nail the self to the cross. If we take up the cross and die, the trial ends there. When Judah, who had sold his brother, took responsibility for Benjamin and sought to save his family, all the tests ended beautifully. 'Now then, please let your servant remain here as my lord's slave in place of the boy, and let the boy return with his brothers. -Genesis 44:33'

*Reason to be bold when facing tribulation. In the world we are afflicted and experience indescribable pain and tribulation beyond imagination. And though we do not know what tomorrow holds, we lead our weary bodies to the place of worship. It is happiness. Now only the Lord and I exist.
*The cost of love. To love requires paying a price. Therefore, when we truly love, we face many challenges, just as Jesus came to this earth.
*God's offering. God's offering does not complain or feel wronged. Like the quiet Lord, it becomes a perfect offering. He did not make excuses.

*The weight of pain. The heaviest pain people experience is being misunderstood with no way to resolve it. But greater pain than that is when the Lord is silent in that moment. Yet the Lord speaks even in His silence. Faith hears that silence. There is no suffering in God's love in Jesus that could separate us from it. If now I love the Lord, this is enough. 'Lord, receive my soul. Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall trouble or hardship or persecution or famine or nakedness or danger or sword?' —Romans 8:35

*What people realize when in distress. When people are in distress, they realize two things: that I cannot control this, and that I must look to the Lord's face. This is the same as saying I must surrender and find rest. Let us look to the Lord's face.
Nevertheless, healing must continue, the cross must continue to be held, looking only to the Lord must continue, and the gospel message must be witnessed without ceasing.

**God's Solution. 1. Many things happen simultaneously in the church. 2. The foremost concern is that believers grow distant from worship and from God. 3. There are many reasons for this: relationships, heartbreak, self-pity, victimhood, self-blame, doubt, family, and sometimes when circumstances don't cooperate. 3. What we call a capable leader is someone who effectively resolves all these problems. Then one would become a minister targeted for position zero in inviting to large churches. 4. However, the person God desires is not that kind of person. God desires one who brings all problems before the Lord in detail and holds onto the cross. 5. At this very moment, standing on the rock struck by the rod, pouring out His cross blood, that One's salvation is accomplished for us. (Acknowledging that my problem is not my own) The people quarreled with Moses and said, 'Give us water to drink.' Moses replied, 'Why do you quarrel with me? Why do you put the Lord to the test?' —Exodus 17:2 (When you cry out to the Lord) Moses cried out to the Lord, 'What am I to do with these people?' —Exodus 17:4 (The Lord accomplishes salvation through the cross) 'Take the staff, and you and your brother Aaron gather the assembly. Speak to that rock before their eyes and it will pour out its water.' —Exodus 17:5-6

When praying, some cry out, 'What else do You want me to do?' But looking back, that moment will also become the most grateful time. Just as the moment Joseph was sold to Egypt by his brothers' jealousy became Joseph's most grateful moment, this time of solitude now will become your most grateful time.

A few things that praise me, strengths I find impressive—I mistakenly think these still sustain me. No. Rather, what I regarded lightly, what I truly confessed as my weakness, is what sustains me. (2 Corinthians 12:10) Therefore I glory in my weaknesses, for Christ's sake, for when I am weak, then I am strong.

There are times when the Lord permits difficult problems I cannot handle. When every method and path is blocked, what God originally desired is for us to shed tears, fall down, and hold onto the cross.

You will surely be disciplined and refined. If not, you are not being loved. The Lord will surely intervene in my rationalizations. And He will not compromise or give up until you obey. Now there is hardship and tears, but later there will be faith like gold.
Sometimes the Lord permits tests that do not make sense. It is when love has become an idol. Faithful obedience in faith conquers the test.
My feet hurt a lot. But I am very happy. Because of our young people. That is the blessing of this insufficient one. To meet such precious friends.
It is a blessing to have trials I can endure.

We shed tears only after experiencing failure and rejection. These tears do not flow outward but overflow inward, making it difficult for others to participate. Though tears have similar composition, their reasons and meanings differ. In these tears, I am broken, see my limits, and finally see the Lord. God's hammer breaking my shell is very frightening, painful, and sorrowful, but after refinement, it bears fruits of righteousness and peace. 'No discipline seems pleasant at the time, but painful. Later, however, it produces a harvest of righteousness and peace for those who have been trained by it.' —Hebrews 12:11

Everyone is merry at the party, and I too sit in an unavoidable place among loud music wearing an awkward smile. Because of the Father's heart, and with the Father's heart, I ache endlessly. Because of the voiceless cry of the flock given to me and His heart.
Even when my heart is troubled and my mind is in pain, I continue to look to God and trust because God makes no mistakes. I entrust all of this to the Lord. Only the Lord governs me.
Paul was a man whose handicap became his boast. Through that weakness, the Lord's power is made strong. In the Lord, one who is endlessly weak becomes the strongest person.
If my tears dry up, the rainbow of my soul does not appear. Without a rainbow, I cannot reach dreams beyond dreams but remain in greed.
I am in his hands, Why not me?

Seeing members in need of much prayer, I continue to pray. For those sensitive to achievement, those ashamed of their appearance, those wronged by unfairness, those struggling with even one step, those who see themselves as sole victims, those wandering without direction, those struggling with relationships, those whose bodies ache as if breaking, those whose hearts are so busy their hands cannot work, those suffering from spiritual attack, those who must advance but are parched, those struggling because of family—may they be freed by the grace of the blood before the Lord's cross. Amen.

The hardest part of the journey of faith is the final moment. When you want to give up, when life seems wrecked, when God's voice is no longer heard—you must endure that moment. Because the last half hour is the hardest part of faith. Some members may now feel they 'should give up.' But I want to say this: Please endure just a little longer. When facing problems of the moment, hold onto the promises of eternity.

The Son of God did not bear the cross of suffering so that I would be crushed to death by this problem. The Lord's cross is meant to save me. Because He was determined to save me at all costs, He bore that difficult cross. Therefore we must be bold.

It seems others go easily, but I go with difficulty. The way ahead is dark, and when I cry out in prayer, He shows me barely half a step. The most difficult thing is that He does not give me a schedule. I don't know when it will end, I'm not even asking for something big—I don't know when I'll arrive and live like a person. What my family wants is simply a normal but somewhat better school than others, a job that pays slightly better, a life with a spouse appropriate to our level. The Lord insists on making me walk the wilderness. After becoming deeply humbled and obedient to the Lord's voice, He feeds me manna and makes me drink from the rock. If I eat manna and drink from the rock, I will come out as refined gold. 'Remember the long road the Lord your God led you on in the wilderness for these forty years, testing you to humble you and find out what was in your heart, whether or not you would keep His commands. He humbled you and let you go hungry, then He gave you manna, which neither you nor your ancestors had known. He did this to teach you that man does not live only by bread, but by every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord.' —Deuteronomy 8:2-3

I hope you will pray for friends in need of prayer. Even if they do not appear for a long time, remember them, call them by name, and continue praying as the Lord leads, for members whose hearts and bodies are in pain and under fire.
The refinement and pain the Lord requires even of David—whom He did not require this from—is because the Lord does not wish David's mistakes to be repeated. It is love.

You will surely suffer. You will also be tested. Your vision proves this. If the mission is great, resistance will also certainly be great. Overcome it. 'The righteous will live by faith. And if he shrinks back, I will not be pleased with him.' —Hebrews 10:38

In David's Psalms, being delivered from tribulation includes perseverance. David prayed without ceasing, but had to spend many years with only a bare survival kind of salvation. It was not because King Saul's power was strong, but because David needed to be transformed. The problem is not the environment but me. In tribulation, the Lord carefully observes our response and attitude. We can advance as much as we are transformed. Saul will merely be used for a while, and when his refinement is complete, he cannot be found even if searched for. (Psalm 119:71) 'It was good for me to be afflicted so that I might learn your decrees.'

There is no suffering without purpose. Suffering has meaning. There is a voice of God I must hear. If I respond to that voice, I will live. But if I ignore it, I will die. This is the truth. 'My son, pay attention to what your father taught you; do not forsake the teaching of your mother. My son, pay attention to what I teach you; keep my words close to your heart. Follow my instruction and you will live.' —Proverbs 4:4
Suffering makes it impossible not to kneel. Kneeling makes the suffering become a door for me.

I must be hurt by things that hurt. I must mourn at not being able to look to the Lord. The Lord seeks such a person. Tears may have similar composition, but I must shed tears from a different source.

When distressed, you must look to the Lord's face. Saints afflicted in the world must be fixed on the comforting Lord. When sad, He brings comfort, and the Lord of love will surely bring rest. 'Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.' —Matthew 11:28

The furnace of suffering does not seek to kill me but rather frees me and makes me walk. Though that fire appears painful, it harms nothing good; it only burns the bonds that bind me, the chains of sin, and the deeply corrupted self—this is a blessing. Walk through the fire; you will never die, and you will behold God's glory and Jesus. 'He ordered the furnace heated seven times hotter than usual and commanded some of the strongest soldiers in his army to tie up Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego and throw them into the blazing furnace.' —Daniel 3:20 'Then King Nebuchadnezzar leaped to his feet in amazement. 'Weren't there three men that we tied up and threw into the fire?' He asked his advisers. They replied, 'Certainly, Your Majesty.' He said, 'Look! I see four men walking around in the fire, unbound and unharmed, and the fourth looks like a son of the gods.' —Daniel 3:25

I am troubled but not unhappy. I am troubled but happy. Because the Lord heals the brokenhearted and gives true rest. 'The Spirit of the Sovereign Lord is on me, because the Lord has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim freedom for the captives.' —Isaiah 61:1
The greater my distress, the greater the Lord will help me. The Lord is your stronghold and your shield.

When suffering comes, do not think of the depth of that pain but focus your heart on its meaning. The devil makes us meaningless and tests us; the Lord makes us aware of our calling and holds us. We are spiritual beings. Therefore, we are beings who pursue meaning.
The disappointment and pain from when You rejected my prayer, the brokenness I felt with shame and hurt—the Lord tells me this is my 'calling'.
Suffering makes people dry and barren; success makes people arrogant. But the Lord makes people abundant and humble.

If you don't understand the meaning during suffering, you become frustrated. Wisdom helps me understand the meaning and enables me to overcome. Why do I suffer? 'My grace is sufficient for you, for My strength is made perfect in weakness.' 2 Corinthians 12:9

Suffering makes me a bearer of God's mission and makes me bold. Suffering discovers 'my value' and transforms me from a chick staying in the nest into an 'eagle' that rejects temptation. I pray you may be exalted in the midst of suffering.

When I commit my path with my whole body to the Lord, I receive it back as faith refined like gold. (Job 23:10) After that, I—who once knew others' testimonies and experiences only theoretically—directly encounter the living God and become a glorious person. Departing from the values and paths the world speaks of is itself suffering. But suffering is profitable for me. (Psalm 119:67) 'I have heard of You by the hearing of the ear, but now my eye sees You.' - Job 42:5 Because suffering is another revelation of God.

Whether I am genuine or fake is evaluated in the midst of suffering. Some people become stronger through suffering, while others become negative and dry. A dry person recognizes nothing else except 'my reaction' and 'my wound.' In a psychiatric hospital, everyone only talks about their own story. It's not that people around me have become cold. It's not that God has become distant. But when I become dry, it feels that way. When it's difficult, I must cry out to God saying it's difficult; when it hurts, I must say it hurts. Then I discover God comforting and helping me in my pain. That is what suffering does.
If pain and conflict linger in my heart, it means I must look only to the Lord more in this moment.
Please continue to pray for brother Han Seok Park. His symptoms have recurred and he has been moved to the ICU, and he will likely have an MRI on Monday.
Do not try to become too capable. Rather, try to become a fool who is weak in the Lord. 'My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness' (2 Corinthians 12:9).

The way to extinguish a fierce wildfire is to set a 'backfire.' You deliberately set fire to where fire hasn't yet reached so there's nothing left to burn. What has already burned cannot burn further, so it doesn't progress. When I am completely burned at the altar, trials cease at my boundary. I pray you accomplish the death of self on the cross.

If a person of faith does not pray, even an ordinary life is difficult. While others walk easily, I must stake my life to take even one step in pain. I cannot understand why I chose this path. I cannot understand it, but that path is glorious and joyful. It is a narrow path.
David was greatly distressed...., But David found strength in the LORD his God. 1 Samuel 30:6

When relationships begin, people commonly celebrate 100 days and remember Valentine's Day and White Day. Without love, neither the giver nor the receiver experiences such hardship or torture. Without love, everything is a wound and everything is only tiring. The situation is critical—like being struck by stones—but Stephen was happy and glorious. Because there is love. When there is love, you are not deceived, you don't collapse, and you even devastate the forces of darkness.
Every day I encounter my limits, and through those limits I meet death. I see the Lord again in that death. How thankful that limit is.

A person drenched in sudden rain doesn't fear the rain. It is the person less soaked who is afraid. The person who is willing to die will live. Don't struggle with what you're anxious about; if you die cleanly, you will see glory. 一死覺悟

To those whose everything has collapsed and who have lost hope to live, as a minister I would counsel: even if you die, give what remains to the Lord. Then the Lord will handle it whether it's to cook porridge or something else. This is what a minister can say. Let me cast a vote for this advice worth being stoned for. Elijah asked the widow who said she would eat and then die for her last meal.

If you encounter a bear, you'll be panic-stricken and run for your life. But to a hunter, a bear is tuition for his children, rent for his house, an opportunity for his family to eat out after a long time, and income that allows him to change his old car. Become a sufferer-hunter.

Become a sufferer-hunter. When you encounter suffering, don't be frightened and retreat; rather, cry out, 'Suffering, I've met you well!' Give it a try and it's nothing. Suffering is not hard because it is suffering but because your heart collapses before suffering. In Psalm 119:71, 'It is good for me that I have been afflicted, that I may learn Your statutes.' A person who discovers the hidden meaning in suffering is the person the devil fears most. Before Almighty God the Father, no accidents happen to us. There must certainly be meaning there that I must learn.

To me who seeks to accomplish everything easily and move on, the Lord gives time for maturity and ripening, saying become a person of 'authenticity.' Though sometimes difficult, after being refined, you become a person like fine gold.

When others' faults appear and criticism arises in my heart, I am full. Full of worldly desires, I cannot see God. Just as one who suffers in the body ceases from sin, when I bear the Lord's cross, I can only feel sorry and grateful.
Though He slay me, yet will I trust Him.
When walking in the wilderness, I hope you laugh. So that only the Lord receives my deep tears and blood....
There will be pain. If you don't look toward heaven....
The happiest time in my life is when the Lord made me weep.
Look into the source of my suffering and pain. I see my desire. If it's not tears toward Jesus and those who act as enemies toward Jesus, then I'm clinging to what will decay.
My weakness will ultimately be helpful to both me and the community. Our disappointment is another language of God's calling.
Suffering itself is not miserable; collapsing before suffering is miserable. The Lord surely gives suffering you can bear, so being in the confidence that you will surely bear it is the truth.
Speak through perfect silence like Jesus, who walked the quiet path of death.
When my trust in obedience to the Lord's will is greater than the suffering I endure, I can overcome suffering.
When serving the Lord humbly, troubles are many, but Savior, give me strength to bear them well. - Hymn 347
If you are troubled, humble yourself. The fastest way to pass through life's tunnel is to lower yourself.
Many faithful people testify: what I thought was fate was actually refining.

When the Lord gives suffering, He intends to save you. If God intended to destroy, He would not give suffering. He abandons those destined to perish. I hope you become grateful in suffering and be transformed. Hold onto the Word and resolve to die, and you will live.
You cannot carelessly say suffering has meaning. But it is fact and truth.

The difference between my desire to have my problems solved and God's wish to fundamentally change my life determines the duration of my refining. In the end, there is only one place to return to, and the difference is how much I am beaten. A soccer ball, no matter how it bounces, eventually goes into the goal.

When David was being chased and came before the king of Gath of the Philistines, David drooled, rubbed himself against the wall, pretended to be mad, and barely escaped to hide in the cave of Adullam. I wonder if that was when David reached rock bottom. Even in the life of David, a great hero in human history, when you examine just one section of an era, there was such a miserable time.
The Lord desires a broken heart. Because through that broken crack, the Lord's glory will shine more brightly.
To be nailed, I must want the Word to become a sword and pierce within me. Being pierced is painful and hurts, but only through that piercing, from the blood that flows out, new comfort is poured.
When you encounter problems or trials, pray that the Lord's perspective helps you see them.
Even if my whole heart collapses and breathing itself becomes difficult, if I can live for the Lord, that is a blessing to me.

The reason endless troubles keep coming toward you is because there is an unchanged self that must be transformed before that trouble, and the Lord is persistently demanding it. When endless trouble comes, be transformed and respond.
When you are too much in pain, you must pray to the Lord to lower me further.
I pray that the Lord who rescued me in my day of trouble and humbled me in my day of refining will personally bless all the young people.
When serving the Lord humbly, troubles are many, but I trust the Lord I rely on even more.
Those who look to God with faith while preparing for loneliness and desolation cannot be stopped by anyone. In times of deep solitude, the Lord meets us deeply.

Suffering and discipline have nothing in common except that both are very painful. Suffering is enduring for righteousness and thinking of the Lord, while discipline is painful affliction brought by being led by one's own desires. Look into the cause of your pain. Is it desire or righteousness?

Eric, once called a god by others, made a vow before God to quit drugs and debauchery after his son was born, but repeatedly failed. In 1991, his four-year-old son fell from a 55-story apartment and died. He then donated 7 million pounds to an island to build a drug rehabilitation center and created this song: 'If you see me in heaven, will you know my name? Will you hold my hand? I must be strong, and carry on Cause I know I don't belong here in heaven.' Because he did not fall into self-pity, he was able to continue his life. Self-pity is idolatry and sin. Psalm 62:8 We must come before the Lord continually and pour out everything. Then we become wounded healers.
Since I've been in Jesus, even though there have been lots of toils and snares I always believe God is good, God is love, God is faithful, I never doubt those, never...

When we meditate on the source of our suffering, we find that greed lies at its heart. We are not in this place because we are the most excellent in this field. We must do what the Lord has led us to do and be faithful to what He has permitted. We must not greedily strive to do too well. Remember the Lord's tears for that place. Please remember: the Lord did not call us because of our ability, but He empowers those He has called for His mission.

When fighters and boxers first train, they learn to take hits. Especially when a punch comes, they learn not to close their eyes. If you take a hit unknowingly, you get knocked out. But if you know it's coming and take the hit, you can endure it. Various people exist. Understand the other person and take the hit anyway. Pain can eventually even feel endearing when you go beyond it.
The Holy Spirit who gives freedom, where that deep sorrow becomes great joy, comes to our prayer gathering.
The greatest problem with leprosy is the inability to feel pain. If there is no hunger, thirst, or pain, then one is seriously sick.

Deepen the concentration of your prayer. Sometimes God uses our deep pain as a channel for His wonderful plans. This is because pain causes us to kneel against the flesh and move us to seek the Lord's face, transcending human-level desires, to a place of profound prayer. Even now the Lord surveys all the earth and seeks those who cannot live without Him, those of wholehearted devotion. To them He pours out not human desires but God's desire, and challenges them to rise to that level of prayer. Rather than answering the prayer of one seeking to have a son, He causes them to pray the prayer that brings forth Samuel, who ushers in a new dawn of history.
Self_and_Identity: Horses and Chariots - The reason for boasting about horses and chariots, which God forbade from Egypt, stems from an inferiority complex.

Repentance, Not Correction - When you try to change others, you only leave wounds and lose people. The Lord called and established us to humble ourselves most and repent. If I do not live according to His Word, no matter how good my words or how reasonable my logic, no one will be transformed. Living according to His Word is only possible when we think deeply on and behold Jesus. (Hebrews 12:2-3)

Social Media and Love - When you begin to love, you will discover yourself doing social media frequently and excessively. However, when you live with love, you will also discover yourself having no need for social media.

A Precious Life - How precious is a life lived only once? Therefore, our lives must be happy, and to be happy, we must live a valuable life. We competed fiercely for happiness, but looking back, is life not vanity? A precious and valuable life is not about climbing to high places but about descending to low places.
Reset & Reloaded - Before the cross, regardless of any reason or circumstance, we are reset by Jesus' love. There is nothing more prioritized than knowing Jesus and making Him known.
When You Ask 'When?' - To those who ask 'When?', God asks in return a more important question. That is my 'identity.' If I cannot be myself, then all else I enjoy is useless.

The End of 'Abuse of Power' - Haman in Esther was a man who owned the whole world, yet he became furious because of one Jew. Had Haman not reacted and let it pass, history would have changed. Therefore, the persistent 'obsession' of one who possesses more is not authority but 'judgment.' After these things King Ahasuerus promoted Haman, the Agagite, the son of Hammedatha, and advanced him and set his seat above all the officials... When Haman saw that Mordecai did not bow or pay homage to him, Haman was filled with rage. (Esther 3:1, 5)

Overcoming Evil with Good - To overcome evil with good means to speak and act through the essence and standard that I hold—Jesus—regarding the wounds and low self-esteem around me. Ultimately, essence overcomes packaging.
Misaligned Questions - 'How much longer?' versus 'Who do you say I am?' - When focus is misaligned, life cannot move forward.

Losing the Great for the Small (小貪大失) - The End of Abuse of Power - One person who grasped the whole world responds to the smallest matter and crumbles in resentment and anger. We should appreciate what is given and give thanks, but instead, we pursue perfection in what is not given, gain the small and lose the great. Rather than gaining more and rising higher, if we humble ourselves and give thanks, we become happy and can make others happy. If we have truly obtained what is important, we must be able to let the small pass away. After these things King Ahasuerus promoted Haman, the Agagite, the son of Hammedatha, and advanced him and set his seat above all the officials... When Haman saw that Mordecai did not bow or pay homage to him, Haman was filled with rage. (Esther 3:1, 5) But whatever was to my profit I now consider loss for the sake of Christ. What is more, I consider everything a loss because of the surpassing worth of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord, for whose sake I have lost all things. I consider them garbage, that I may gain Christ and be found in him. (Philippians 3:7-9)

The Source of Power - How much others are with you matters less than whether the Lord is with you; that is life. Even with the backing of influential people, without Jesus it becomes nothing, and even surrounded by ten million, if the Lord is with you, victory comes.
Emptiness - When you do God's work without God, that is what happens. In God, no work is ever meaningless.

Filling the Form - Life is far more important than justification. On the battlefield where bullets rain and shells pour down, saving and surviving matters more than appearing polished. Hollow glamor and formality are not only human desires but constitute sin before the matter of life.

The Victor is Not the Survivor; The Survivor is the Victor - Good faith, celebrated belief, is not being proud of people's reputation or becoming the protagonist of various testimonies. It is looking only at the Lord and going to the end. Many are praised and many are recognized, but those who run to the very end are truly rare.

Let Essence Overcome - Even with tremendous blessings and prayers, without Jesus it is merely fake, and even without anything threatening you specifically, if you do not cling to the cross, you will surely perish. What God asks is not to anxiously worry about circumstances and responses and focus on the non-essential, but to concentrate on and strive for the essential, which must be Jesus. What is truly most urgent for you is not external 'favor' or 'threat' but 'Can I take up my cross?' Therefore, holy brothers and sisters, who share in the heavenly calling, fix your thoughts on Jesus, whom we acknowledge as our apostle and high priest. (Hebrews 3:1)

When You Feel Infinitely Small - When you feel like nothing but let out a sigh, you must behold and confirm the glorious Lord and that you are in Him. If Satan reminds you of your past, remind Satan of Satan's future.
If You Desire Excellence - If you desire excellence, you must distinguish. The most excellent area in life is to serve the Lord. That is holiness. Moving Worship Christian Organisation

The Final Evaluation - God's evaluation differs greatly from people's conclusions and praise. That is why we will be surprised when we reach heaven. There will be those who seemed glorious but will be deeply ashamed, and those who seemed disgraced but will be highly honored.

The Protagonist - The protagonist of Job is not Job but God. In life, it is not important what position I am in or what I do, but what matters is that God does His good pleasure. When the Lord is the protagonist of my life, my soul is at peace.

On That Day - On that day, many friends experienced not only freedom and strength but also happiness regardless of problems. Because of God being God and Jesus' cross, graduation, employment, job, and marriage could no longer hold them back. Psalm 1 testifies that a simple, focused, and God-approved life is a victorious life. - February 8, 2014, Gaveston Hall.

The Price Paid - It is tragic to live only for graduation, employment, marriage, stability, and peaceful retirement, then stand before God. Rather than enjoying much, it is clinging to one thing and living clearly; rather than continually accumulating and clinging to a stable life, it is laying down my life and planting a cross on this earth. That is holy consumption.

Addiction and the Gospel - A person addicted to gaming cannot quit no matter how hard they try, but if they play a more addictive game, they will quit. Why can't people break free from empty addictions? Because they have not found something more fun and interesting. What is more powerful than worldly addiction and gives true satisfaction is 'Jesus Christ.' He alone is the way, the truth, and the life of this broken generation. Call upon Jesus. Then you will live. Jesus answered, 'I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.' (John 14:6)

Thoughts of the Flesh - Thoughts of the flesh without exception will surely kill me. If you live by the flesh, you will surely die, but if you follow the Spirit, you will not gratify the desires of the flesh. Those led by God's Spirit are God's children. So if you follow your own desires, you will feel empty and ashamed, but if you hold to your mission, glory will come. I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh. (Galatians 5:16) For those who are led by the Spirit of God are the children of God. (Romans 8:14)

Reformed - Calling Jesus 'Lord' means we are Jesus' servants. We are someone's servants, and when we are Jesus' servants, we become citizens of the kingdom. Therefore, you are 'a servant of the Lord,' and where you stand now, you are a pastor. A servant of the Lord is not someone who uproots themselves to attend seminary, but someone who, knowing Jesus is Lord, obeys inevitably.

Faith - Living by faith means living with decision. Especially since it begins with believing in God's existence and character, we step away from our situation and problems and address them with the Lord's eyes and character. There must be only the Lord who redeemed me, not me.

How to Know Myself - What makes me sad and what makes me glad moves me. What I desire reveals who I am. One thing I ask from the Lord, this only do I seek: that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to gaze on the beauty of the Lord and to seek him in his temple. (Psalm 27:4)

Called from Ordinariness to Purpose - Peter's occupation was a fisherman catching fish, and his dream was a full catch. Paul's dream was to be a rabbi continuing Gamaliel's line. But the Lord called them to be fishers of people and a light to the Gentiles. There were people who naively risked their lives to this calling, and because of them, I am a believer in Jesus, who is Life.

In the Reality of Fierce Living - To small ones living fiercely in harsh reality, to young Gideons who had no choice but to be cowardly to survive in cold reality and fierce competition, God called them 'mighty warrior.' The reason a cowardly Gideon worrying about employment, relationships, and the future can be a mighty warrior is one thing: God wanted to be with him. Judges 6:12 - 'The angel of the Lord appeared to Gideon, saying, 'Mighty warrior, the Lord is with you.'

The Only Competitiveness of a Christian Worker - Cannot drink alcohol, not good at flattery, cannot exaggerate or package, and does not gossip about others behind their backs. The only competitiveness is faithfulness and wisdom. Look at Daniel. (Daniel 1:20) - In every matter of wisdom and understanding about which the king questioned them, he found them ten times better than all the magicians and enchanters in his whole realm.
Fear Not - People fear becoming weak. But they do not know that the only time they become weak is when the Lord works and makes them strong.
What Matters - Character is more important than ability. Truth is more important than miracles. It is okay to live with a little lack. If you stand before the Lord, you are already a victor.

Unbeatable - Rather than building up, seek to share; when gathered, seek to scatter; rather than drawing together, seek to give; worship the Lord everywhere, and it is enough that I am broken and scattered while revealing the Lord fully.

Kingdom Church - A true church is not organization and system but a community bought with Jesus' blood and conceived by the Holy Spirit. It is not a fortified city anxiously building walls but a place where Jesus' blood and life flow abundantly, overwhelming the tide of wickedness. The Kingdom Church God desires is an apostolic church focused on the command Jesus gave to His apostles. When you resolve to martyrdom and risk your life for Jesus, the church is built not only in church but on campuses, streets, and everywhere in the world.

The Reason for Sin - Sin means missing the mark (target). It is a state where the purpose of making God king—Lordship—has a problem. Therefore, sin always intensifies in excessive success-seeking and self-realization desires disguised as ministry.

The Power of Loss - A gospel that cannot move me is not the gospel. If desire, people, recognition, and relationships move me, they are my gospel and what I serve. When I lose them, I see my true nature and know what my true gospel really was.
When the Gospel Becomes Power - When I live according to the gospel, the gospel's power works. The more I live it out, the more the gospel becomes truly gospel.
True Gospel - Do not try to imitate; come before the true gospel that can change me.
How to See My True Nature - When I stand alone before the Lord, many filters fall away and my true nature begins to appear.
God's People - God's people are those who pour out the joy and life that belong to heaven into this barren earth.

The Standard of Beauty - There was a time when a woman's foot size was an absolute standard of beauty, but now the size of the face matters. In the near future, it will change again. More important than appearance that follows trends is that all people have the right to be respected and are beings of value. Judging people by appearance is called uncivilized.

When You Stop the Show, He Will Guide - The way to be guided begins with being entrusted with the smallest things. Then you trust that without masks and show, you can live a more abundant life, and ultimately you experience the Lord guiding your entire life.
The Condition of Happiness - Stop defining your happiness by others' reactions. Furthermore, stop imagining based on that reaction. Doing so is neither truthful fact nor God's intention.

Addiction - When you healthily long for intrinsic beauty, you will be freed from twisted and distorted addiction. If the bright moon is beautiful without shame, you are sufficiently happy without secret possession or obsession.

Unemployed - When you quit work, you realize something. There is an immense amount of time left, and you finally realize you are alone. And you must have a faith and confession that would have no regrets even if you lived such a life your whole life.
Elite Personnel - In God's kingdom, there is no such thing as elite personnel. There are only those who repent.

The Restless and the Worshipers - When facing Jesus, the ambitious become 'restless,' while the hopeful rejoice and 'worship.' Where is he that is born King of the Jews? for we have seen his star in the east, and are come to 'worship' him. When Herod the king had heard these things, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him. (Matthew 2:2-3)
Anointing - There is a reason for anointing and responsibility follows.
A Hungry Socrates or a Satisfied Pig? - Would you rather be a hungry Socrates or a satisfied pig? I will just be faithful in the form the Lord called me to. ^^

Do Not Be Discouraged - Ultimately, the sinful world flows to one place, and there is one path for the soul to walk. Therefore, if you glorify the gospel in your life, it is authority superior to everything.
People Who Attract People - Those faithful with David were not with him because of higher salaries than Saul's people. Those who obey God have the attraction of heaven.
* Calling - Calling is not something someone can order you to do or stop you from doing. It is what happens when you stand before the Lord.
* Meeting - There are those who make precious time meaningless, and there are those who make a moment valuable.
* Second Law of Spiritual Dynamics - You find the greatest peace when you humble yourself before the Lord.
* Inferiority and Pride - Inferiority slanders the faithful person as arrogant, and pride despises the complete person as inferior.

* Athletes Recognize Athletes - A person of faith recognizes faith, and a person of glory recognizes glory. If a carnal person acknowledges you, it is evidence that you are being destroyed. Rather, when you receive the hatred, resistance, and jealousy of worldly people, that is when you know you are walking the right path. Therefore, take heart.

* Anxiety About Recognition and Joy in Being Used - If you seek recognition, you will be afraid and pursued. But if you are satisfied with being used by Him, you will have peace and completeness. What matters is not the size of your position or the results, but Him who desires to use you. Say the Lord will use you. - Matthew 21:3

* Bread Comes from the Oven - When a delicious bread is baked from the oven, it becomes a finished product that can be eaten immediately. Yet everyone boasts that they are that finished product that has passed through that hot oven. But they have not even been kneaded yet.
* The Subject to Proclaim - Delivering God's Word well corresponds to a spiritual gift. It is not the gift that saves people. It is the cross that saves.

* Great Power Brings Great Responsibility - If a person without ability has a corrupt character, they do not cause many problems. However, if a person with ability in a high position is like that, it becomes a serious problem.

* A Respected Leader - To know what kind of person someone truly is, ask not the people they serve, but the people they have ruled over. It is an age that thirsts for leaders who are recognized by their subordinates.
* Nouveau Riche People - A person who seeks to be treated well in front of others and excessively scolds subordinates in public places is a pitiful person who must demonstrate their power.
* The Reason for Lament - It is not that we failed to gain people's hearts or achieve desired accomplishments. True faith is lamenting that we have not given more to the Lord.

* The Duty of the Strong - The privilege of the wealthy is not tyranny. It is the ability to serve that God has temporarily lent. Just as the potential energy of water changes to generate electricity, God's glory is revealed in proportion to my humility.

* The Greatness of Psalm 23 - The Lord became my shepherd not because I lack nothing, but because God is my shepherd, I lack nothing. When I advance as a child of God, I truly lack nothing. Being a child (Identity) is ability.

* The Reason for Disappointment - What I thought I had has disappeared. But it was never mine to begin with. When I understand the greatness of the Lord and realize my smallness, I escape disappointment and become at peace without fear.

* GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) Free - When we see transformation, we experience glory. When we see glory, we can go even unto death. That transformation is not manipulation or coercion, but something welling up from within to without. Guard your heart above all things, for it is the wellspring of life. - Proverbs 4:23

* An Arrogant Crown Becomes Shame - For a person who does not reflect on themselves, a crown becomes shame. Just as anything becomes foolish and even dangerous when you don't know its nature and purpose, no one has ever gained glory while ignorant of their own nature. They reverse things and lower others as they please, but ultimately they reverse their own life and lower themselves.

* The Responsibility of Those Entrusted with Iron and Fire - Iron sharpens iron, and fire kindles fire. If you miss the precious opportunity of meeting iron and facing fire, all your efforts become futile. Therefore, those who handle iron and fire bear great responsibility.

* Fear Not - Those who fear failure ironically also fear the changes needed for victory. So most choose to safely and slowly perish rather than confront challenges, becoming stubborn and too afraid to acknowledge reality. This is not God's will for the young and inexperienced leader Joshua. Be bold.
* Do What You Are Called to Do - Don't be arrogant; humble yourself as Jesus humbled Himself. That is what He asks.

* A Mountain is a Mountain and Water is Water - The ability to see a mountain as a mountain and water as water is no small thing. Even those who boast of knowing the Gospel often fail to see believers as believers and the church as the church. Believers and the church are creatures that are only revealed as a new creation when I die and live through Jesus.

* Youth Sunday Part 2, 2014.11.23 - We Are the Lord's Moving Church - Just as a singer breathes through song and a climber breaks through the summit, God's people reveal God's glory in a way that cannot be imitated, wherever they go, through the shameless Gospel they carry. https://vimeo.com/113124637
* Battle of Priorities - Winning the battle of priorities determines victory or defeat. It reveals what I serve and who I am, and shows where I am heading now.
* The Reason I Was Called - God did not call me because He needed me, but because I need God.

* A Good Community - When organic matter (animals, plants) underground is subjected to temperature and pressure, it becomes coal. But if you pay a greater price and endure higher temperature and pressure, it becomes a diamond. Rather than lamenting that souls are not being transformed, raising the spiritual temperature and pressure should be the mission of the earthly church.
* The Reason for Sadness - Let It Go - It is not sadness from lacking something. Sadness comes when you have what you should not have.

* Why the World is Disappointed in the Church - Not because the church abandoned their joy. The world is disappointed because the church abandoned God's joy, and they see no difference between the church and themselves. When we become servants of the world rather than servants of God, the world grows angry beyond disappointment. Should I please people or please God? If I seek people's pleasure, I am not a servant of God. - Galatians 1:10

* Do Not Be Greedy; Be Faithful in the Margins - Greed is the desire to take what the Lord has not given. So people seek others out, deceive, and are deceived. What we seek that the Lord has given is called calling, and advancing with gratitude for it is called faithfulness.

* True Calling - The ultimate nature of calling is to know and believe in the true God and His Son Jesus Christ, and to walk with Him. So if we claim to have faith but do not enjoy the Lord, we merely prolong life without truly living.

* Freedom as Maturity - Maturity is in one sense freedom. It is laying aside childish things and feeling no need to react, not being damaged by less important and less precious things because of what is more important and more precious. If you mature in grace in Jesus, you will be free. When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child. When I became a man, I put childish ways behind me. - 1 Corinthians 13:11

* True Courage - Courage to look only to the Lord and be forgotten. Letting the Lord alone receive all glory, honor, and power. Should I please people or please God? If I have been seeking people's pleasure until now, I am not a servant of Christ. - Galatians 1:10

* The Generation that Advocates Bullying, A Mature Response - When playing in a playground and ostracizing and discriminating against one person, people experience a kind of pleasure and security. But the problem is that the ostracized person is at the level of a university student. Children are not bullied for being excluded from children's games. When believers raise their standards and deepen their fellowship, many problems solve themselves.

* The Secret to Victory - Not hiding greed and disguising it with justification, but laying bare weakness and holding fast to promises. When there was a long war between the house of Saul and the house of David, David grew stronger and stronger, while the house of Saul grew weaker and weaker. - 2 Samuel 3:1
* The Reason for Stinginess - The reason we cannot intercede and help is not lack of resources. It is because we do not enjoy living eternally with God.
* Why I Do Not Prove Myself - I do not prove or pursue myself because my soul is already safe in the Lord.
* The Reason for Fear - Fear of loss. When you can lay down everything except Jesus, true peace and boldness come.
* Inducing Reaction is Evil - Faith is when I believe God and respond, not when I bind another person. Faith is what I believe and what I depend on.
* God is the Reason - We struggle because we try to see God through results. Results are a tool to humble me; God is my reason.

* When You Lose Your Taste - There was a time when school-front rice cakes were sold for 100 won for 10 pieces.
