The only ingredients were gochujang and green onions, yet that taste is hard to find now. If the taste has changed, you must know the reason. You cannot insist that a changed taste is the same taste. It is not the rice cakes choosing people; people have the right to seek the taste they want.

* When Purpose is Lost, There is Only Coercion - When you become fixated on what (results), you lose precious people. When you obsess only over how (method) to gain results and evaluation, you lose essence and identity. When you determine the why, people who are willing to die for it will emerge.

* Do Not Be Swayed by Judgment - It is not futile. I do not judge myself, so what does the judgment of others matter? The only One who will judge me is the eternal Judge, God. Being judged by you or by any human court is of very little importance to me. I do not even judge myself. - 1 Corinthians 4:3
* Inferiority and Superiority - Inferiority views the obedient person as arrogant, and arrogance views the complete person as inferior.

* Jesus's Choice - Before the Passover Feast, Jesus knew that the hour had come for him to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them to the very end. - John 13:1
* A Difference in Class - Do not boast that you do not do evil or act corruptly. Rather, you should truly grieve at not being moved and not yearning for it.
* One Question - If later you are blamed and misunderstood for something you did not do, could you think of me and be 'meek'? - 2011 Staff Meeting
* Stand at the Margins - Many want to stand at the center, but no one wants to linger at the edges. Yet the neglected and shadowed place is the Lord's place.
* To Speak Like Jesus - To speak like Jesus, be silent like Jesus.
* Control Your Greed - No matter how much greed tempts you, confess that ultimately nothing can be accomplished except the Lord's will. For I belong to the Lord.
* Jesus Deficiency - All problems begin in one place. Especially, it is a great problem that what should exist, and more importantly serve as the source, is absent.
* Ministry is Not a Picnic - Ministry is not playing around or going on a picnic. It is going to die. The problem is not that it is difficult, but that we begin without resolving to die.
* What makes life meaningful is not dazzling lights, but purpose. Who that purpose is for determines my life.
* The airplane of life has no reverse gear. There is no need for reverse in the sky. When you hold a high calling, you do not go backward.
* When a new country you visit for the first time is not so interesting. It is because the country you already belong to is already meaningful enough.
* Why faith becomes difficult. People do first what they love and ultimately continue until the end. What do you love?
* Evidence of the absence of Jesus. When Jesus becomes faint, people do not repent of themselves but blame others. Before Jesus, anyone will burst into repentance.
* The clear difference between religion and Jesus. Religion dictates and forces, but Jesus set an example and influenced through His character.

* When the temporary becomes joyful. You can find joy in what is temporary. However, when you find joy in what is not the eternal rock, you must be humble. So as not to lose Christ, who is the eternal rock.

* A sad masquerade wearing the mask of holiness. Unless you acknowledge that you are existentially a sinner and that picking a few fruits of sin does not remove the root, the laborious and sad masquerade will not end. It is not a matter of 'will' but of 'dying before the gospel.'

* When essence is lost, suffering follows. When a nation, company, or family loses its essence, everyone suffers. The church is no exception. When the cross is lost, there is no wickedness one cannot commit.

* The war of concentration. Whether obsessed with the world or with Jesus, ultimately success or failure is determined by how crazy you are for whom. That is why dilution is not acceptable. The ability is in how you maintain the pure concentrate.

* Glass fragments become diamonds. When a relationship of faith begins, it is fragile like glass, but as it passes through years of tears and joy, it becomes a beautiful driving force like a diamond. God's people are not hastily assembled.
* True glory. The only reason an earthen vessel can be glorious is not because the vessel's material is excellent or has an attractive shape, but because it holds a precious gem inside.
* Change. Not 'therefore' but 'nevertheless...' and all the more I am sorry and apologetic.
* Why I am not ashamed of the gospel. The gospel confronts and collides. It tells you that is not the way and gives you the way to live. That is why the gospel is truly radical.

* What evangelism is. When a community continuously becomes healthy and experiences deep presence, young people will develop a happy sense of identity about the community they attend and naturally invite friends and souls seeking meaning to the gathering.
* The rules of the game. It is Revival, not Survival.
* The reason for rejection. It is to receive true welcome from truly precious people.

* The only begotten in the Father's embrace. There is no envy, jealousy, ambition, or vain goals there. Jesus came from there and was like that, and we too, if we come from the deep embrace of God, will be like that. That is why we do not work with information and data.
* The fear of loss. To be anxious about losing is unbelief. Because the fruit of the tree of knowledge of good and evil—'this is mine'—is at the starting line.
* When awakening from sleep. Never overlook the invisible world. No matter what, the flesh must die and the spirit must live.

* To the question 'What are you doing here now?' There is no need to be obsessed with influencing the world and people. You only need to become intimate with God. That is the true purpose. Do not grieve for not becoming first; stay by the Lord's side and become beautiful. You will become even stronger. 'You have searched me, Lord, and you know me. You know when I sit and when I rise; you perceive my thoughts from afar. You discern my going out and my lying down; you are familiar with all my ways. Before a word is on my tongue you, Lord, know it completely.' —Psalm 139:1–4
* Position is not holiness. A holy position does not automatically make one holy. Because it is a place of serving God in holiness, you must discipline yourself more strictly.

* What is truly important. If what you think is important keeps changing, it was never important. By worldly standards, it may be disregarded, but what is eternal in that kingdom is truly important. Even just moving to another country for immigration changes one's life.
* Why you become generous. Because you have already received so much, you do not cling greedily. Because you are in the Lord and receiving precious love from loved ones.
* The only checkpoint. A life that checks people and reactions, possessions and evaluations, is pitiful. Stand yourself before the Lord and only check if I am in Jesus.
* An excellent leader. Lay down the attempt to become excellent and simply do faithfully what the Lord has commanded. You collapse because you are trying too hard to become excellent.
* What God does. To me who is rough, weak, and struggling, God's word is, 'I will use you.'
* I have nothing to give. That is why I am happy. 'Silver or gold I do not have, but what I do have I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk.' —Acts 3:6

* Do not confirm yourself through ministry. You become a servant of people, not a servant of God. You will frequently experience emptiness and pride. My existence is by God's grace. That means the cross that saved a sinner like me is my identity.
* Tell me your wish. Then I will know who you are.
* Why there is emptiness even though you worked hard. All ability and meaning, the value of my existence, comes from relationship. Seek Him.
* Reject comfort. My value determines my actions. Refuse and pray.
* Why I stand here. Because God has overcome for me. 'You deceived me, Lord, and I was deceived; you overpowered me and prevailed. I am ridiculed all day long; everyone mocks me.' —Jeremiah 20:7

* Do not envy their league alone. The reason Jesus came as an unremarkable stem in this dry land is to bring life to the marginalized who could not join 'their league alone.' True influence that breaks the gates of hell and saves souls comes from renouncing rights and acknowledging sovereignty. It means bearing the cross and going to the dry land.
* When you meet beautiful people. You discover yourself hating sin and staying far from it. Those already happy have nothing more to seek.

* A life that needs no encouragement (infinite power). A mother does not need to encourage a child to love. Because it is life. If you move by encouragement and praise, you have not yet moved beyond the beginning. Those who move by life, not encouragement and slogans, and are faithful are called those with calling.
* The church is not far away. Faith is not burdensome because the church is far. The church is near. It is only that home is far.
* Look back. That I came this far was because of the Lord's encouragement and help, and that encouragement was not because of my excellence but because of my weakness.

* When lonely, behold the Lord. 'Unlikeable appearance and personality, manner of speaking will not be a problem, and an unremarkable shabby background and chronic weakness, none of these will be a problem. I made you and I will also make you.'
* The deep shadow of death. It is because you try so hard to do well and want to be told you do well. As pride and frustration intersect, you will experience hell. Do not try to do well; love.
* God did not call for success. God does not want success. He wants awe.

* Lose pride and gain self-worth. When you abandon empty and perishable pride and place stability in the Lord, you realize that you are truly valuable and loved, and no longer need to cling to or prove your worth through worthless things.
* Why you should neither be afraid nor proud. Because the one who uses is the Lord. Leave the place that is not you and faithfully do only the work the Lord uses you for.

* The solution in crisis is basic. When you meet crisis, you must hold to the basic. Do thoroughly again what you have been doing, live humbly, and a way will appear. Do not compare with others; hold fast to your Jesus.
* What I must always remember. It is the relationship between me and the Lord, and the position of the Lord and my position.

* The worship God receives. 'God is spirit, and his worshippers must worship in the Spirit and in truth.' The Father seeks those who worship in simplicity and honesty before Him, just as they are. —John 4:24

* Worship team attire. 1. Garment of holiness: Psalm 29:2 Ascribe to the Lord glory and strength; ascribe to the Lord the glory due his name; worship the Lord in the splendor of his holiness. 2. Garment of respect: Respect the congregation and church culture. If you say personally there is no issue, so it is fine, this is immaturity. 3. Garment of propriety: A short skirt on the high platform; a tight-fitting shirt like this that burdens brothers. Even esteemed praise teams in foreign countries pursuing free worship maintain the most conservative standards when it comes to clothing. 4. Do not be sensitive to trends. 'Nowadays everyone wears skinny jeans.' Then it would be better to go to a karaoke room. The reason we must be beautiful is not because we follow trends but because we follow calling. You are the face of the church. 5. Prepared clothes rather than expensive brands: Even with the same amount of offering, I think God receives differently when I see the attitude of giving. Check your attire by doing your best to prepare. 6. Wear character: 'Clothe yourselves with compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness and patience.' —Colossians 3:14
* The major of believers. To love to the very end and then depart.
* Boldness comes from humility. Not being bold is because you have lost your place. When you pray, you become bold. Because humble recognition of your place comes upon you.

* Worship team prayer meeting. Why do we pray before worship? Is it to make a final sprint, gain shining power, and have worship go well? It is remembering that I am before God and moving to the correct 'position' as a true worshipper.

* Dangerous Promise - If you had to choose between a stable life with someone you dislike and a risky promise with someone you deeply love, what would you choose? Live each day meaningfully. This is about recovery and continuity.

* Continuous Seeking - When you continuously and repeatedly petition before the Lord what you have already sought, you become a person of the Holy Spirit. This means becoming someone who loves all people without fear.
* My Identity - You can know it by observing what you constantly desire.
* Do Not Struggle With It - God did not call you for that purpose. Hold onto the highest calling, and you will find freedom and peace.
* Regrettable Research - It is attempting to do well without kneeling.

* Seek Why Rather Than How - Do not ask how to fulfill your duty well; pray about why you should fulfill it well. When you abandon selfish motives and discover your mission, the method becomes sharp and focused. When your purpose is clear, the way becomes clear.
* The Useless Becomes Trampled - A disciple without self-denial is salt that has lost its flavor.

* Why We Must Not Curse - We should not curse, but especially when a believer curses, they spread gracious words on social media while denying themselves through cursing in actual life, thereby committing the sin of rapidly defiling themselves, the temple of God. Become someone whose inside and outside are the same.
* Weakness Becomes Hope - Not through my unqualified strength, but standing only through the blood of Jesus. Then your weakness will become hope itself.
* Do Not Find Misunderstanding Strange - Do not regard misunderstanding as strange. God never misunderstands.
* A Life Without Regret - Not a life of fulfilled desires, but a life that delights in the Lord.

* Crisis - Has there ever been a time when crisis was not a crisis? When it is difficult, it is very difficult, and when it is easy, we become absent-minded, and then suffering comes. After all, in a land of famine carrying the cross, like Jesus, we must become a single grain of wheat.
* Focus on the Essence - The essence of faith is to pay attention to and live before the Son of God who daily loved me and gave His body for me.

* Anxiety Does Not Come From Mistakes - I will not fail to hear the Lord's voice or receive His guidance because of my mistakes. I do not lose the Lord's guidance because of my mistakes or weaknesses, but wrong priorities mislead me. When even the wealthy and powerful who appear perfect in everything take wrong priorities, Jesus says, 'How sad.'
* Proclaim It, Do Not Be Intimidated - Proclaim it easily. Evangelism and missions are not begging; they are offering a privilege.
* Repentance - I was a far worse son than I knew myself to be. God showed patience and love toward myself, which I alone did not know. That is why we repent.
* True Leadership - A leader is not someone who gives solutions. A leader is someone who presents the Lord, who is the only way.
* Do Not Imagine, Humble Yourself - Do not imagine; humble yourself and resolve to serve. If you imagine, you will collapse, but if you humble yourself and serve, He will build everything up.

* A Prayer to Start the Day - Today, with the breath the Lord has given me, make me humble and serve, remembered by people, yet enable me to stand properly before the Lord, fixing my eyes only on You each moment.
* What Is Shameful - It is not shameful to be cursed at and criticized. What is shameful is abandoning the truth to be welcomed and recognized.
* The Beginning of Suffering - It is difficult because I try to become myself by my own effort. I am what I am by the grace of God.
* My Desire - God, enable me to humbly serve in places where I am needed, without name or recognition.
* Being Loved - You were not called to receive love from people. Because you are one who receives God's love, you are free to love and be loved.
* A Leader's Ability - It is not receiving attention but giving attention to the marginalized.

* Immaturity Leads to Loneliness - If the spirit remains immature even as the body ages, one cannot help but clamor for recognition through various methods, displaying themselves, becoming sensitive to others' reactions, inflicting wounds while disregarding them, and engaging in self-centered thinking. Maturity is seeing 'I' and 'you' through the Lord. Empathy is the ability.
* The True Reason for Anger - It is because you are bound by the world's evaluation. Be bound to God.
* Fear - In the Garden of Eden, Adam and Eve hid because they were afraid. What they feared most was their naked, exposed self being rejected.
* Do Not React to Fabrications - Do not respond to things that are not facts and not truth. This is because the truth that 'I am a precious being' is being shaken.

* The Person Jesus Disliked - Surprisingly, Jesus disliked the Pharisees. The pious movement that began with earnestly serving the Lord became boastful about themselves and contemptuous and arrogant toward others.

* An Unavoidable Sin - A car collision is not sin. The real cause is driving a car with faulty brakes in the wrong direction on a highway. You should have prevented the beginning. Both sin and holiness are matters of the first button.
* True Happiness - It is not because you have acquired various things you wanted to have. Happiness comes because the Lord reigns.
* Do Not Guess; Humble Yourself Further and Serve.
* The Ability of a Humble Vessel - What a humble vessel can do best is to hold. This is called receptivity. When you hold and receive the precious 'Jesus,' you become the most powerful being.

* Influence - Influence occurs when there is a clear difference. Like a dam that generates tremendous electricity when water falls from a height, when a person living in God's deep presence serves and humbles themselves, they become 'fragrance' and 'flavor.'

* The Power of Focus - To truly concentrate on valuable work means saying 'Yes' to that one thing. This means having the courage to say 'No' to everything else. Only the courageous can concentrate. According to my earnest expectation and hope, I will not be ashamed of anything, but with complete boldness as always, now as before, I will live so that Christ will be exalted in my body, whether by life or by death. - Philippians 1:20

* The Power of Simplicity - Simplicity is far more difficult than complexity. Clear thinking is not something everyone can do. I ask you to abandon the lie that you are a victim and accept the calling to be a healer. Love is restoration.

* The Ability to Let Go - Jacob, who lived his life as a struggle, gained much and lost much. But when he let go, accepting that he could lose, he no longer lost, and instead recovered what he had lost. Those who are prepared to lose are the strongest. If I lose my children, so be it. - Genesis 43:14

* Boasting Is Empty - When people boast, they seek to have their existence recognized. But looking back, how empty such boasting is, realizing that no one acknowledges me through such boasting. Stop vain boasting and find peace in humility.
* Evidence of Unbelief - Being conscious of people. Be conscious only of God. Then you will be free from people's gaze and pretense.
* Maturity Shows Consideration - Because it transcends self-centered interpretation and perspective. When mature, we put away childish things. That is love and completion.
* Self-Justification Becomes Frozen - Do not justify yourself; testify only of Him. You will experience becoming a warm person, I who was cold for the purpose.
* I Am Not an Angel - Nor am I a demon. Sometimes we are like angels, sometimes like demons, but I exist only by grace.
* From the Lord, Not East and West - Do not be discouraged by not being recognized, nor struggle to be recognized. All recognition comes only from the Lord.
* A Leader's Qualification - Whether you are carrying the cross.

* Beautiful People - It is also a slogan introduced by a certain airline. Why must you become 'beautiful people'? To appeal to customers. So they work hard to become 'beautiful people,' provide service, and advertise. This morning there was no such advertisement and no special achievement, yet just remembering this makes the thought of 'beautiful people' come to mind. It is truly wonderful. In the Lord, by existence alone, you are most beautiful.

* The Fundamental Sin - Not 'stealing fruit' from the tree of knowledge, but 'selfishness'—wanting to be God instead of Him and not placing God in that heart—and 'idolatry'—serving the self. He died for all, that those who live should no longer live for themselves but for Him who died for them and was raised again. - 2 Corinthians 5:15

* Expanding Perspective - Seeing others' shortcomings is not insight but seeing my own weaknesses in the other person. When your perspective expands this way, you must see the weakness within yourself rather than criticize others. A player recognizes a player.
* Everything for the Lord - Joy is for the Lord, and delight is for the Lord. Likewise, loneliness is for the Lord, and sorrow is for the Lord. I will praise the Lord with everything within me.

* The God Who Called Me - The God who called me did not call me to do this work excellently and be recognized for it. We do the work, but the Lord bears responsibility for the results. We are only responsible for being faithful unto death. I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has counted me faithful, putting me into the ministry. - 1 Timothy 1:12
* Why Jesus Came - He came to be rejected, abandoned, and crucified on the cross. So is not following Jesus and living this way more glorious than being welcomed?

* Thoughts Leading to Death - Fleshly thoughts lead to death. Fleshly thoughts refer to self-centered thinking. This does not mean to outwardly please others and conform to their expectations, but rather to abandon living for yourself and live through Jesus. For the mind set on the flesh is death, but the mind set on the Spirit is life and peace. - Romans 8:6
* The Difference Between Saint and Silly - A saint applies understanding to themselves; a foolish person applies it to others.
* It is foolish to be angry at people when God has humbled you. It is absurd to be concerned about people's opinions when God has exalted you.
* Focus on What Truly Matters - What truly matters is not 'Am I receiving recognition from people?' but rather 'How am I loving the people God has given me?' Do not fear; only believe.

* Appearing Spiritual Yet Missing the Point - It was not truly spiritual faith, but rather loving people and enjoying people. True faith is not about liking people but liking Jesus, and finding joy when Jesus is revealed in fellowship because of Jesus. The simple way to discern this is to ask: who am I witnessing to?
* The Reason You Were Called - Do not merely sustain life; live eternally. Eternal life means achieving victory through the fullness of life in every dimension of life.

* Envy Reveals Your Level - You mistakenly think that if you acquire certain things, people will envy you. A truly mature person is not attracted to such things. Only a goldfish with an IQ of 10 would respond to earthworms.
* The Blessing of Humbling Yourself - Those who exalt themselves for their own sake will be humbled, and those who humble themselves for the Lord's sake will be exalted.
* Identity - What a person desires reveals who that person is.
* Live Your Own Life - I, who love God, am far more important than this world that hates me.
* Do Not Be Concerned About What Will Not Gain You Life - All outcomes are decided in one place: 'Do I truly have God's anointing?' Focus only on what will gain you life.
* A Person Against Whom Threats Do Not Work - Threats work only when the other person controls something you do not want to lose. Therefore, threats do not work against Christians.

* If You Cannot Let Go, Hold On - When you want to let go of something but cannot, if the knowledge of Jesus becomes exalted, what was once precious and could not be abandoned becomes refuse. Refuse will be discarded even if you are told not to discard it.

* Too Much Concern - Honestly, others show me so little concern, yet I worry excessively about others' opinions of me. Your neighbor is loved at the same level as your own body, and others can only find peace when you find peace.

* Hidden Desires and God's Kingdom - Do not be angry that your hidden desires are not fulfilled; instead, grieve that heaven's will is not done on earth. A cracked cistern cannot hold fresh water, and you cannot make anything other than the Lord your source of living water.

* What is More Important Than Solutions - In modern times, solutions are commodities. Methods for making money, maintaining relationships, and doing ministry receive attention. The world asks 'how?' but God asks 'why?' If your reason is clear, the method will follow.

* Outside the Gate - Jesus suffered outside the gate because He came as the Lamb to accomplish forgiveness and salvation. Therefore, we too must take up the cross of Jesus, reject worldly glory and human popularity, and go outside to gain life. Let us therefore go to Him outside the camp, bearing His reproach. - Hebrews 13:13
* The Art of Love - One is prayer, and the other is becoming holy by growing to resemble the Lord. Then those who belong to you will truly be blessed.
* God's Special People - One who knows how much God loves people in this way cannot easily despise or hate others. It is because God loves them specially.
* Be Free From Comparison and Confirmation - Decide not to compare and not to seek confirmation. God will give you perfect blessings that cannot be compared and require no confirmation.
* Change - When your purpose changes, your smile changes. You will become far happier.
* Reversing Circumstances - To reverse circumstances, you must release a heart opposite to the situation. Release trust amid distrust, love amid hatred, and sacrifice amid selfishness.

* Do Not Defile God's Temple - If anyone defiles God's temple, God will destroy that person. Therefore, do not defile yourself, God's temple, through immorality (1 Corinthians 3:16), and do not despise God's worship. (1 Samuel 2:17) Do you not know that you are God's temple and that God's Spirit dwells in you? If anyone defiles God's temple, God will destroy him. For God's temple is holy, and you are that temple. - 1 Corinthians 3:17

* Decide Beforehand - The reason I walk this narrow path even now is that my past self decided beforehand, no matter how harsh this path might be. Decide beforehand not to turn back. The cross is not a beginning but already a conclusion.
* Look at People and the World Much - You will realize there is never satisfaction.

* Why You Feel Frustrated Despite Working Hard - You work so hard, even beyond your strength, truly for revival, yet you feel frustrated. This is because you are trying to confirm yourself through responses and recognition. From the beginning, you have strayed from testifying to the Lord, and although people do not know, God knows.

* When You Really Love a Garment - If you truly love a garment, you naturally diet for it. If love is truly good, you become like its object. Thus, when you love beautifully, the lusts of the flesh are forgotten.
* Life Becomes Difficult Because You Seek to Receive Love - You were sent to give love, yet...
* Freedom From Fear - One who does not fear death does not fear life.

* Why the Chef Adds Water to Kimchi - When there are too many flavors, there is actually no distinct flavor to taste. This is why professionals become simpler as they mature. It means ornament and pretense disappear.

* Why People Don't Speak When They Feel Hurt - Most people don't explain when they feel hurt because they themselves think it is embarrassing to be hurt by such a thing. Yet they are still angry, so they become upset over other matters. Often, the cause of anger is not the actual situation but self-imagined misunderstanding.

* Why You Feel Anxious - Your hopes and expectations have become rotten and shameful. You already know that such anxious hopes always have a course of return. That course ends in shame. Whether I live or die, I want Christ to be honored in my body as always, with full boldness, not being ashamed of my earnest hope and desire in anything. - Philippians 1:20

* Me - There is the 'I' that I and others know, the 'I' that only I know, and the 'I' that others know but I don't. However, if you know the 'I' that God loves, the other versions of 'I' are merely changing garments.

* Being Hated - If a Christian must be hated, let it be for the sake of truth. Jesus was as well. When you are hated for this reason, do not be ashamed but rather be proud. The world does not hate you; it hates Me, because I testify that its works are evil. - John 7:7
* Fellowship With Those Who Have Beautiful Souls - Your attachment to sin and greed will disappear. How much more when you behold the glorious Lord?
* Be Humble - Knowing that your faith has been over-packaged, tear your heart. When you are alone, who are you truly?

* The Right Time to Distinguish - It is when you offer your heart. Do you have such time and such a prayer closet to offer one sincere heart once? If it is rare, it means you are merely a nominal believer.
* Why You Cannot Acknowledge Your Mistakes - Because you are a cowardly person without courage.

* When Your Pride Is Suddenly Hurt - The real reason you become angry is not that the seemingly unreasonable evaluation of you doesn't match who you are, but that it matches you so well that you fear it. Because you cannot accept your own value, you react so sensitively to others' evaluations. When the relationship with God, not worldly reputation and success, becomes your standard, you become a firm person.

* You Must Go Beyond Saul to Become David - The reason you want to nitpick and fix someone, see through their intentions clearly, and feel your stomach turn is because you see in them, like a mirror, your own weakness that you truly deny.

* The Comfort of the Dead - One who must not die has no choice but to be defensive. However, one who is already dead is free. In childhood, what is good for you is good; in adulthood, what benefits you becomes good; in maturity, what is right becomes good. If you stop here, the community will always be divided. You must choose what the Lord desires. This is a value that transcends the question of right and wrong.

* Anger - If you examine anger closely, it is an explosive method of excessively defending yourself against uncontrollable circumstances and your own helplessness. Facing unfair treatment and the situation of not being recognized, when you cannot control it, it is an attachment appealing to your existence value and an emotional method trying to eliminate the cause. You may make a thoughtless choice and regret it your whole life. One recognized by God can control emotions and circumstances. Gentleness comes from healthy self-esteem.
* Unfinished Adolescence - Because you are conscious of people rather than the Lord, pretense emerges. When you long for only the Lord, people too will long for the Lord.

* Life Without Seasonings - At a healing camp, someone asks in bewilderment whether other seasonings need not be added when cooking. If you simmer long enough, the original essence of the ingredients naturally seeps out. A life of focus needs no seasoning.
* If There Is Anointing - Everyone can recognize it, and if there is none, that person themselves acutely feels the emptiness.

* How to Stand as the Lord's Servant - God's plenipotentiary ambassador fulfills the Lord's will and thus finds peace and boldness. However, if you seek your own purpose and glory, you become anxious and fearful. Your righteousness is like filthy rags.
* Peace Comes When You Fulfill Your Purpose - You experience true peace when you worship God. It is because you fulfill your life's purpose.
* The Beauty of Repentance - When you return to the root, you become most human and beautiful. It is returning to the Father. This is called repentance.
* Love Overcomes Sin - When you love, you not only find peace but naturally strive to gather together. Having received precious heaven as a gift, worldly lusts like dregs can only be counterfeits.
* Two Simple Questions Explain Everything - 1. What do you truly desire in your heart? 2. If you truly desire it, do you have the eagerness to gladly pay its price?
* The one blocking my path and obstructing my way is none other than 'myself.' I am a thief and robber because I desire much while unwilling to pay the price.
* Coram Deo—living with the consciousness of God. In all fields....

* How to live amid shifting standards: True evaluation is not how I appear or am perceived, but who I truly am. History is not made by people's understanding, scores, or rankings, but by passion and love that cannot be measured by these things. Therefore, some decorate history while others create it.

* The value of the alabaster jar presented by the beloved is not diminished—regarding Kim Yuna: The woman who broke the alabaster jar is condemned, yet the value of her love is not diminished. Because the most important One recognized it. The fact that those who deny Kim Yuna and those who recognize her are as divided as the Red Sea means her blood and tears are not tarnished upon the gold-plated medal.

* The side effects of unearned income: Being happy with scores others have given you is temporary, but living while realizing you can never surpass your own score that society has created for you is a cruel punishment.

* The nation that has Kim Yuna possesses the class to be unconcerned with a questionable gold medal. Rather than resentment toward biased judging, we are happy that the beautiful Kim Yuna represented Korea to the end. When one value excels, all else becomes mere waste, and I feel deep sorrow for those who cling to such waste.
* Catch up with me if you can: Ideas and methods can be copied, but passion cannot. You may be able to follow a ministry, but dying for souls is not something you can immediately replicate.
* God does not desire reformation. 'He has shown you, O mortal, what is good. And what does the Lord require of you? To act justly and to love mercy and to walk humbly with your God.' Micah 6:8

* Human joy and God's joy: If a servant of God seeks human joy in the church, he is not a servant of God. Even if he seeks God's joy in the world, he is a servant of God. Whatever moves me is my master. If what moves me is not the Lord, such an idol must be broken and burned until it no longer tempts me.
* Why the cross is lost: Because there is much to do, especially unnecessary things. Doing just two or three things, or even just one, is enough.
* The most terrifying film: A person of God appearing without love.

* Love makes you hear: At the climax of Jesus's ministry, in the moment of crisis, what was needed was not the assurance of help saying 'I will help you,' but the assurance of love saying 'This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased.' In crisis, what is needed most is the assurance of love.

* True holiness: It is a great misconception to think it merely means praying a bit and reading Scripture. True holiness is when what I desire changes. Commonly, people think Christianity is holy if the method of desire is Christian, not the object of desire itself.
* The reason for boldness: Because I know what I desire and also what I can afford to lose. Those who long for eternity cannot be stopped by anything.

* When unable to be humble: If someone despises and hates another for not having much or not graduating from a prestigious school, it is because they have no other way to prove their existence value except to criticize and compare weaknesses. People strongly deny, condemn, and project onto others when they discover their own weaknesses in them.

* True investment: What worldly people so desperately crave are ultimately things they cannot hold onto forever. Yet on the other hand, there is also what can never be lost. Those who let go of pleasures, self-satisfaction, and material things that they ultimately cannot hold onto in order to pursue what can never be lost are not foolish people at all.

* Why success brings anxiety: When people win silver, everyone celebrates, but Korean athletes weep. In a society that remembers only first place, everyone tries to succeed. Yet even if you achieve part of what you and your family want, you remain anxious. Because you are not a victor. This is why taking up the cross brings true peace.
* The reason for not getting drunk: Not to obtain salvation. It is no longer necessary because salvation has already been received.

* What you desire determines your quality: If what you desire is only this life, you are truly pitiful. Because it will all disappear. Desire eternal things. Only Jesus and what is offered to Jesus are eternal. If in Christ we are hoping only for this life, we are of all people most to be pitied—1 Corinthians 15:19
* Just as a body without a soul is lifeless, if there is no grace in logic, all believers become fighters. They are neither smart nor beautiful, merely like zombies.
* If you cannot let go, you miss what is important: Good faith is when lesser desires before the Lord are surrendered. When you focus simply on the Lord, sin decreases and holy influence strengthens.
* The person who is angry: The angry person especially is not looking at the Lord's eyes. A person fixed on the Lord does not respond to the desires of self.

* Absolute happiness: Paul says that even loss is gain. Because the original purpose was not to gain. When Christ living in me is the original purpose, loss does not become sorrow and acquisition does not become pride. 'For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain'—Philippians 1:21
* The present: You are not turned away from people's welcome and gifts; you are waiting for a truly precious gift. You do not need 'a present' but 'The present.'
* Hypocrisy: Boasting of the eternal One while wrapping up your own decay inside and packaging it.

* The opposite of faithfulness is not laziness: The opposite of faithfulness is trying to do many things well yourself. Since God is the One who accomplishes excellently, He called us expecting faithfulness and integrity. Adam tried to become like God, Saul tried to gain recognition from people, and Israel wanted quick settlement in Canaan. Trying to be recognized for doing well, many worries pierce your heart.

* Boasting in vain leads to surrender to emptiness: The irony is that you fear what you boast about so much. If you fear it, you serve it. Boasting in vain ultimately leads to disappointment and fear you cannot bear, your heart collapses and your life falls apart. Fear only the Lord.
* A tree with deep roots does not sway: Even if all people in the world reject and ignore me, I cannot be shaken. Because the most precious One dwells in the deepest part of my heart.

* The burning bush is not consumed: Fire was set to the burning bush but it is not consumed. God does not use us as a resource. He simply desires to be with us and to reveal His own glory. Therefore, when you remove your claims and spirit of doubt before the Lord, God's holiness and glory appear.

* Prophesy: 1. My small flutter of wings becomes a tsunami in heaven that covers the world. Do not despair. 2. The One who gave me femininity and charm is God, and when I believe this, I am most at peace and most beautiful. Do not comfort yourself with small wishes when looking at yourself, but desire the most beautiful wish the Lord gives. 3. My weakness does not cancel God's promise. Do not know yourself through others; when you see yourself through the Lord's eyes, you need not hide in a cave. 4. God, the Giver of peace leading me to the Lord through pain, causes me to comfort the brokenhearted. 5. The 'leisure' given to me now is so I may be grateful and serve; through me my community receives blessing, the most glorious life. 6. That I am myself is God's grace and guidance, and the Lord's intention is not disaster but peace. Do not be hasty; entrust it to Him. In collapse where no answer comes, cry out, 'Come, Holy Spirit.'

* Make faith your conclusion: Israel's flea and dead dog becomes an incomparable great king. Because he was anointed with oil. The valley before you will be filled and the mountain will be made low. After whom has the king of Israel come out? Whom have you been pursuing? A dead dog or a flea?—1 Samuel 24:14
* The core: To bear good fruit, simply become a good tree. That is what matters most.
* The Lord's interest: Jesus's interest is 'Who do you say I am?' Therefore, I must make clear who my master is. I must see what I am looking at, who fills me, and what brings me joy and wounds me.
* He notices insignificant me: Among the multitudes, even VIPs, the Lord notices insignificant me. God is interested in the anointed.

* Not a choice but a lifeline: Gratitude is not an optional luxury done when you feel like it. Without it, you die; it is a lifeline. When you miss gratitude and complain, you cannot see truth and your heart is stolen by falsehood. Gratitude is the perspective that makes God truly God and makes me truly me.
* The victory of faith: The victory of faith is the victory of perspective. Interpret this situation with the Lord's perspective. What the caterpillar calls death, God calls butterfly.

* The recovery of a struggling father: Though family support is difficult and company work is hard, bright children saying 'Dad, I love you' makes him forget all worries and rise again. What is undeniable is the confession from these young people that they love Jesus, a confession that cannot be fabricated. This is enough.

* If you love, you will know: Scripture says you can know a person through love. A person you cannot love is fake. A motive that does not come from the Lord is counterfeit faith like counterfeit currency. 'By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another'—John 13:35

* The voice crying in the wilderness: When asked 'Who are you?' John the Baptist says he is 'the voice of one crying.' He is truly great because he can introduce the Lord and become a voice that disappears. 'I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness: Make straight the way for the Lord'—John 1:23
* A minister is one who fears before God more than anyone because he knows the truth.

* A person who lives only in Jesus and not in himself: There is no conflict in life. Conflict is not evil. But it is evil for a servant to seek human joy when he should seek God's joy. 'Am I now trying to win the approval of human beings, or of God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a servant of Christ'—Galatians 1:10

* Method versus reason: We obsess over How, but God asks Why. Why do you do it, why must you do it, why are you joyful, why does your heart hurt? You must ask truthfully. Because behind that reason you will know who is King, who is Master. I truly pray that God alone becomes the reason and motive. 'So whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord. For this very reason, Christ died and returned to life so that he might be the Lord of both the dead and the living'—Romans 14:8
* Grace: That I am myself is grace, but my obedience is also grace. It is not by me but by the Lord's grace that I do it.

* By motive you will not be deceived: Seeing appearance is sensation, seeing results is logic. But seeing motive is love. Therefore, God sees motive. 'The Lord does not look at the things people look at. People look at the outward appearance, but the Lord looks at the heart'—1 Samuel 16:7
* Expectation without shame: Holding the expectation of the Father.
* The power of gentleness: A person of faith can be gentle because they are a victor. If you cannot bow your head and cannot extend your hand, you are a loser.
* What matters most: You must seek it. When you seek the Lord's face, you have sought what matters most.
* Why pain is pain: Because you do not walk your own path, walking becomes painful. Set a clear goal, correct your course, and walk—it becomes not difficult but joyful.
* Why stumbling repeats: Because you hold a glimmer of hope that you can be happy with people, casting glances at vanity. With a clear goal, the way you must go will be brightly illuminated.
* We become what we behold. We become what we behold. Worship is focused longing. When we worship, we become like the object of our worship.
* A lion does not eat rotten meat. Do not settle for what decays. Have shameless hope and expectation—that Jesus be honored within me. There will be a joy that even death cannot prevent.
* Who knows? In the end, it is God who knows me and calls me. Only He knows me and calls my name. Those who respond to His calling will shine like the stars of heaven.

If you don't like rice, eat ramen; if that doesn't appeal, eat bread. But if breathing becomes burdensome and your heartbeat becomes tedious, that is a serious problem requiring help. That breathing itself, that heartbeat—these are not problems to be covered over with something else. A baby must fall thousands of times to take even a few steps. If we gave a baby a wheelchair for life because walking is difficult, such a person would deserve to be cursed. The structure should be that those whose spirit lives are moved by wonder while those without it grieve. Conversely, if those moved by wonder are restricted and cynical people gain prominence, the church suffocates. There are things to compromise on and things not to. The point is that juice cannot be an alternative when a baby cannot drink milk.
When you want to defend yourself, when you want to make excuses, I urge you to simply be silent. Just as Jesus entrusted His soul to the Father and was wholly silent.

Do not live a defensive life, but live a life of dying on the cross. Saul lived defending himself and destroyed himself, but David lived surrendering himself and became a good shepherd of Israel. When I give up guarding myself, the Lord begins to guard me.

It hurts when we are betrayed, when we are cursed, when we are treated unjustly. But what hurts more is when we do not look rightly upon the Lord. When such suffering comes, confess this: 'I am someone who loves the Lord.'
Snow White vs. Princess Fiona—true life is not about specs.

Why did Snow White's stepmother, the wicked queen, die so miserably? Because she was deceived by the mirror. Because she judged Snow White and herself by conditions. When we look at those we love, we should not hold up a mirror but look with our heart. If we judge people by conditions, even if God's Son became incarnate and performed miracles before our eyes, we cannot believe. (John 8:15) 'You judge according to the flesh; I judge no one.'
King Saul was in the hearts of the people. But David was in the heart of God. Do not seek recognition, but seek grace.

There are things that should be discarded but are not discarded: pride, greed, lust, envy, jealousy, inferiority, anger. These things are ingrained in the body throughout life, have a subtle charm, parasitize the flesh, and are not discarded. But before the knowledge of Jesus, they all become excrement. Excrement can only be discarded. If you cling only to Jesus, faith becomes not 'asceticism' but 'romance.'
Going to Virginia Water is not an obligation, so feel free to do so according to your own schedule. You have already borne many yokes in the world; I hope you will be free.
When standing before God's Word as if naked, the Bible is not just a thick book but an experience of 'God.'

Even when criminals gather, there can be truth there, and even when only saints gather, there can be pettiness. To cut in line at church is evil, but to show consideration to newcomers is good; to encourage and build up brothers and sisters is good, but if others feel excluded through this, it is evil. Good faith is not extreme. That is why we are not judges. Only the Lord judges.
On the day Peter achieved his greatest success in life, he left everything and followed Jesus.

Peter discovered himself as a sinner before the Lord and asked Him to depart. But Jesus stayed with him instead. Even in the disappointing moment when his chief disciple denied him and in his weakened flesh at the end of life, He never left. The Lord does not leave us. Never.
* A sufficiently happy way—It is far happier to be misunderstood while humbly serving than to be welcomed thinking a thief is Santa Claus. Happiness does not come from response but from existence.

Walking alone through a winter forest without stars, there is thirsty poverty on a long road, yet we are happy because of one beautiful star buried in our heart. Do you have that one beautiful star that turns all problems into excrement and enables us to abundantly overcome on the shores of life's suffering? I understand my friends who came to Saturday meetings.
The characteristic of a sick person is that they try hard to prove themselves and always condemn. When infected with the fatal disease of sin, one inevitably becomes self-centered.
To boast in a hospital is foolishness. All patients are simply patients. If everyone stands before the Lord as someone needing healing, there would be neither pride nor comparison.

We go to a hospital not to do what we want but to bear suffering and heal the evil disease and save our life. We come to the Lord's community not to be magnified and lifted up but to be diminished and lowered so that the Lord's life may be abundant. It is not a place where we hear what we want to hear and situations we want to see unfold, but a place where we repent, shed tears, humble ourselves, and turn to the Lord.
* Undelivered mail—Regarding the message of gratitude on a Christmas card, I look back wondering, 'Is this really the right message for me?' but it is so awkward and does not fit. It is simply grace.

* Pray with quiet tears. We are not called to seek human approval. We are called only to love. This is what we received from God, so receiving God's approval is enough; trusting that God will take responsibility, we pray quietly with tears.

Potiphar's wife tried to harm Joseph and had him put in prison. But even if it had not been for Potiphar's wife, Joseph would have gone to prison. The people and circumstances that tried to harm me ultimately could not, and they actually helped fulfill God's providence. That I am myself is by God's grace.

* Following greed brings anxiety. To satisfy greed brings anxiety. Because you steal what was not given to you. Pursuing your calling brings joy. Because you are pleasing the One who gave you your calling.

A jewel falling into mud does not scratch its value. A person with a strong essence is not shaken by any attack. Glass fragments cannot make a diamond into glass no matter how much they attack. You are a jewel born of the Lord.

* Good is not always good. There is a saying that good is good. Following this saying, we can be convenient, not criticized, and avoid relational risks. But good is not always good. What is right is good. To hold onto what is right, we sometimes cannot conform and become outcasts. But the Lord is with those who are right.
Even modest talents, if persisted in endlessly, are used as great power. Persisting is being faithful.

By 'discovering' treasure, fleeting glory becomes 'excrement,' exerts 'influence' like leaven, possesses 'vitality' like a mustard seed, and by 'recognizing' the bridegroom's worth like the five wise virgins, prepares oil, and 'remembers' the mission and received grace like the faithful servant entrusted with talents—I pray you live such a life.
Gospel 6—God's original form, young people, go into the world and become fire.
The gospel is perfect and God is perfect, but I say I am not. Rather, because of the gospel's perfection and God's perfection, I become insufficient. It is because the Lord chose me that I become.
There is no discrimination in the gospel. Whether Jew or Greek, or even someone whose life seems like the bottom by people's evaluation.
The gospel must be proclaimed to me every moment. That is the way to live.

Leaving the gospel does not mean great corruption and debauchery. If Jesus is not in your heart and there is no wonder, no matter how outstanding a religious appearance you maintain, you have already left. Regarding this essential question, the Lord does not give up on the youth group, and the Lord will use youth who are humble before this question.

The most basic character of the gospel is that it is grace, not my merit, and transformation. A gospel that cannot transform me is not the gospel. If God's Son gave His life and saved me, such transformation must follow.
Let the gospel decide for you.
Had it not been for the gospel, it would be merely an event with nothing in common, but standing before that gospel, these souls become family.

A forced external change without essential transformation is called 'hypocrisy.' Change your inner person; then without external pressure, you will serve the Lord with a transformed appearance. There is pure gold, but there is also plating. Do not be plated faith but pure gold faith.

Do not be ashamed but be bold instead. The reason is Jesus, who paid the price with His blood. 'For this reason I also suffer these things; nevertheless I am not ashamed, for I know whom I have believed and am persuaded that He is able to keep what I have committed to Him until that Day.' (2 Timothy 1:12)
Becoming His servant is easy enough, but being one can be very rough.

I see young people confessing that clinking cups, dazzling lights, and intense beats no longer satisfy them. Congratulations. You have experienced God's glory. Now you will always be hungry and thirsty for everything else.

My parents always wanted me to live a life that was ordinary enough to boast about to others, slightly superior to others, a predictable life. But within Jesus, the Lord never lets us live ordinarily. The Lord wants to be the basis of faith and trust within uncertainty.
* The strong person—A strong person is not much concerned with others' evaluations. That I am myself is by God's grace.
When my parents go to the mart, I don't worry about snacks. When my parents are wealthy, I don't worry about money for school supplies. A person whose Father is God does not fear the future.
Even if I am broken, I will do it to the end until the day God says to stand.
* Why I am sorrowful when looking at the Lord—Because I did not look only at the Lord.
In the insufficient disciples, I ultimately see the image of Jesus. Because Jesus bled to love them.

* Why it is hard—Because you try too hard to do well. But that realm is not given to you. What is given to you is the realm of faithfulness. Faithfulness is guarding your position. To such a faithful person, grace to do well and succeed follows.

Certainly at that ball there were many women displaying their greatest beauty, but the prince's eyes saw only Cinderella. The ability to look only at Jesus while living in the world, the power of sight, comes upon those who have deeply experienced the Lord's beauty.

* The power of discernment—The basic principle is that ability recognizes ability. Elisha's excellence begins not from doing great things but from recognizing Elijah. The most fundamental beginning starts with respecting those worthy of respect. Discernment is ability.

Living by faith means maintaining the mind that the Lord gives you amidst busy work, complicated matters, and things worthy of anger. If it is not living for yourself, which is the nature of Adam, but living for the Lord, then you must see all things through the Lord's eyes.

* Called and Used: Everyone is called, but not everyone responds. And even if they respond, not all are used. To be used as a weapon of righteousness in God's kingdom, there must be dedication that breaks oneself and a change of direction called self-denial. To carry the cross without self-denial...? Such a falsehood does not exist.

* Mission: What I am to do is not to receive the most votes in a popularity contest. That is why I was not called as an entertainer. Go bearing contempt, scorn, and the cross. Honor, glory, and all authority must be received by the Lord alone.

* To deviate from purpose is to fall. Please maintain the original purpose for which you were called. When you know the reason, hold fast to it, and run to accomplish it, you will mature, become glorious, and gain life. Mission is to carry out what you were called to do.

* Do not be conscious of people. If you are conscious of people, you will 'pretend,' and if you are conscious of God, you will be 'faithful.' When I become conscious of God, people will also become conscious of God.

* Foundation of Life: If it was possessions, pride, and relationships, it would be shaken. But if it is the Lord, it will not be shaken even if other losses come. Trials will show what you have built your life upon. Stand on the rock. The Lord gives and the Lord takes away; may the name of the Lord be praised. -Job 1:21

If you do not change even when uncomfortable, you will remain stuck in the same place. Even if you avoid uncomfortable people you do not want to face, you will meet another similar person. Why? Because your spiritual level has become fixed. If you know God and have experienced Him but do not obey at critical moments, the awkwardness in such relationships will not cease. If there is a depressing and troubling relationship, at the core of that problem is my self that has not died. Even an earthworm writhes when stepped on. Because it was not stepped on properly. I cannot kill my ego by myself. The only way is to invite Jesus to the throne of my life. Then Jesus takes troubled me and goes to the cross. There I can feel the Lord's love flowing toward me.
Comparison makes you unhappy. Do not compare with others; see and interpret through the Lord's eyes. That is reality and living by faith.
Do not become dejected by comparison; count the grace you have received. That I have received and enjoyed and have come this far is God's boundless grace.

To live a life without comparison, you must abandon the wrong plumb line of your heart. I am a person living in response. It is entirely God's role to exalt and humble people. Trials come to destroy all emotions that do not come from God. They come within God's permission and providence. God destroys everything and rebuilds us anew to heal us. -Saturday Worship

Whether criticized or praised, it is difficult to say that is your essence. Conversely, my judgment toward others is the same. We know partially and speak partially. Only in the Lord do I know who I am. I am loved by God and cleansed by the blood of His Son.

More difficult than criticism is praise. Many great believers in faith have collapsed there. Therefore, before the course of praise and commendation, there are many graves of the Lord's servants. Beginners struggle to overcome criticism, but the skilled overcome praise. 'Do not regard man, whose breath is in his nostrils; for why should he be esteemed?' -Isaiah 2:22 Stop regarding man, whose breath of life is in his nostrils; For why should he be esteemed?
When you criticize, you become evil in a worse form than the one you criticize. Jesus never rebuked the weak. When you love with the Lord's love, not your own strength, miracles happen.
Though foolish, I am not so foolish as to exchange the Lord's glory for perishable glory. Even if I know nothing else, if I know the Lord, I know what is truly important.

* Attitude is more important than the problem: When facing situations, if you prove yourself with a human heart and return hurt for hurt, you wound and are wounded. This is because you do not know the nature of the struggle, the method of struggle, or God's intention. Our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the powers of this dark world and against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly realms. -Ephesians 6:12
When you stand against the storm, a following comes. Those who look only to Jesus will gain Jesus' people.
Ambition wrapped in vision and calculated kindness...that is Absalom.
When criticized, acknowledge it. As your shortcomings and weaknesses become visible, say that you need the Lord more. Each time our heart beats, we need the Lord's grace.
I came with empty hands so I will leave with empty hands. I will take only what I have loved with tears and labor. It is foolish to stake your life on something you will not even remember later.
It is not important to run fast, but important for whom you run. If the One who gave talent is the Lord, when you decide for whom to use that talent, it can be the most beautiful or the most futile.
* The ultimate foolishness: Limiting what is truly precious to what is truly trivial.

Do not be intimidated by the showy power, money, and beauty of the world. What Pharaoh held was a scepter made of gold and jewels, but before Moses' dry staff it was shattered. The power the Lord gives is not wrapped in glamour, but it is more glorious beyond compare to Gandalf's staff. What matters is not the material, but who holds it.

The fact that we have no control over events is correct. However, we can control our own response to them. If we respond in the Holy Spirit, we are doing very well. Love, joy, peace...patience, self-control—if we face events and objects this way, that is 100 points. If you are responding by instinct and maintaining an emotional state immediately after an event, that is not 0 points but continuing to maintain 0 points. If you get the wrong problem wrong again on the next test, the teacher will keep asking that problem.
* A Beautiful Life: Everyone wants a beautiful beginning. Like a wedding. But it is truly beautiful when the process and ending are chosen beautifully.
Apostle Paul suffered for the Lord and died for the Lord. Because he chose what was most important. What is most important to you now?

Apostle Paul said he was not ashamed of the gospel. This confession can only come when there is certainty that only Jesus' cross is needed. Because I have obtained Jesus crucified, I have gained in Jesus all the joy I can have in life. If even after having what is called the gospel, you still desire many things in this world...where is the gospel in that?

* Apostle Paul's boldness: People's evaluations of me are of little concern. I do not even feel the need to condemn myself or fall into self-pity. Why? Because the true evaluation will be made by God on that day. To judge me by you or by others is a very small thing to me; I do not even judge myself. I am not aware of anything against myself, but this does not make me righteous; the One who judges me is the Lord. -1 Corinthians 4:3-4
* The True Problem: Before God, the problem is not the true problem. The true problem is my attitude toward the problem.
Do not stand before people; stand before God. Then you become a person of honor.
* Need to be angry & Right to be angry: If I am not wrong, there is no need to be angry; if I am wrong, there is no right to be angry.

Do not despair about what kind of being you are before people; discover who you are before the Lord and be strengthened. Sunday gatherings ended at 7:30 for two weeks in a row. Regard it as a gift from a blocked minister and have valuable and leisurely time. I guess I really do love.

Living as a supporting character while watching people's faces is not the life the Lord designed. In your own life, only the Lord is Lord, and I live as His masterpiece. The world's concern is popularity and recognition, but the Lord's concern is in 'relationship.'

When I compare people with people, I become a fairly decent person. But before God, no one is free from lust and violence, greed and hatred, jealousy. Standing before Him is faith. That is why repentance breaks forth.
Do not grieve over people and circumstances, but grieve together with what grieves God's heart. Our purpose is not people and circumstances.

* Why fear even when loving: If you fear when loving Jesus, it is because you still have much to lose. Only when you can lose everything except Jesus do you have complete love and it casts out fear. There is no fear in love; perfect love casts out fear. For fear has to do with punishment, and whoever fears has not been made perfect in love. -1 John 4:18
What is important is not whether you are recognized by people, but whether you are being used by the Lord.
People cannot reach eternity through meetings where they wear masks and perform. Acting eventually closes the curtain. A meeting where I become most myself is a good meeting.

People pursue love based on value. They never reach a conclusion and it ends without beginning or end. But the love the Father gives us is love that bestows value. It builds up the weak, heals the sick, enriches the poor. Do not pursue value; become people of love who bestow value.
* If I protect myself, it becomes sin: Trying to protect myself is sin. That is why pride becomes sin. On the other hand, when God protects me, it becomes self-respect.
People want to be moved. The world is busy, dry, and without truth. Jesus is eternal awe and wonder to all people. When moved by Jesus, you become a life that moves others.

People erase the past, pretend it is counted, pretend it exists. Ultimately, they want to hear, 'You are a decent person I cannot ignore.' So they 'exaggerate' and 'embellish.' You do not need to exaggerate or pretend to be something. You do not know yourself well. You do not know yourself well because you do not know God well. Do not exaggerate and embellish; receive love. I will love you warmly too. Big Hug!! ^^ Happy Valentine's.

People think they will be unhappy because of what they have not accomplished. That is not true. Most people feel greater unhappiness because of what others have accomplished. No matter how much possession, recognition, and evaluation you receive, if greed does not leave, unhappiness will not cease. Greed compares. Comparison becomes hell. But faith is gratitude. Gratitude is heaven.
* Strong Love: When you become a mother, you become strong. Because you protect and nurture life with love. Be strong. So love.
People live by the rules of the jungle in an age of infinite competition, eat and are eaten. But Christians live bearing fruit. That is the purpose of life and what He intends.

People long for the world's dreams. That is tragedy. To be surrounded by good environment, wealth and comfort, guaranteed security, and slowly have your heart hardened should not become the dream. Was Stephen's death and Paul's devotion tragedy? Those who dedicated their entire lives so that the name of Jesus Christ would be praised among people did not live tragedy; they lived glory.
People sin. They sin to be happy and to be joyful. But instead, deep regret and pain remain. Holiness and purity are power and happiness.
People look around and wander here and there seeking happiness. But true happiness can only be enjoyed when fixed on Jesus.

People imitate. Because they want to gain even a little of the reputation the original has. Seasoned short ribs seem like they should be more expensive than fresh ribs, but they are cheaper. Although more seasoning and effort are used, why are they cheaper? Because they are not fresh. That is why seasoning is used to make up for taste. May you become a life that does not need seasoning. May you become a person of faith who recovers the power of substance.
* Basis of Joy: If it is about me and mine, it will be sorrowful. But if it is about Him and His, it will be joyful.
My sincere confession to the Lord is more important than people's incomplete evaluation of me.
Rather than becoming a head among people, I hope you will become a servant of the Lord.

The amount of suffering given to people is constant. It is the law of conservation of suffering. If suffering is the same, what determines life is the love with which we respond to that suffering. The total amount of love for the Lord differs for each person.
Being misunderstood by people is not very important. If I do not misunderstand until the end, the devil will return empty-handed and God will gain one more of His people. Overcome evil with good.
People naturally have gaps, and if you try to hide them by force, you become a frustrated person. In life, gaps make us free, leisurely, and easier to approach.

People are deceived for hidden desires. They deceive others, deceive themselves, and even attempt to deceive God. When we reveal ourselves with honesty, we see our true selves, and we begin to distinguish between what we should seek and what we should let go of.
People become like what they boast about.

Do not fear if you cannot become a source of joy for people; rather, strive to become a source of joy for the Lord. Jesus endured the cross for the joy set before Him and sat at the right hand of God.
Human life is crumpled by the pursuit of self. The ironing that can smooth it out is 'obedience.'
Woe to those who expect recognition from people. Blessing is upon those who expect the Lord.
Whatever a person sows, that will they reap. Whether they sow when others are watching or when no one is watching—the result is the same. When you are alone, who are you?
When a person pours their soul into a work, a masterpiece emerges. When the Spirit of God is poured into a person's body, it becomes a work of art of God's level. You are God's Masterpiece.
If your heart is filled with sorrow and pain because you were not loved, you will have little to be happy about for a lifetime. Love is what the Lord gives, and I love and serve with that love.

*Living in communion with the Lord means living with the Lord. The reason people live by their own will is because they do not know Jesus well. A person who lives with Jesus cannot live by self-centeredness and selfish desires.

Praying for a calling can be powerful, but it is also dangerous. This is because a calling can sometimes deteriorate into ambition, becoming self-pursuit in the name of the Lord. A calling is certainly important, but it is important because of the One who gave it. Therefore, prayer is not about achieving but about doing it out of love.
When you experience failure in ministry, you can truly know whom you pursued. It becomes clear whether you sought honor, glory, and all authority for yourself.

Do not rejoice in ministry; do not rejoice in successful ministry and visible results, but rejoice in eternal relationships and love. Rather than rejoicing that we could do something for God, we should rejoice more in what God has done for us. 'Nevertheless do not rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.' —Luke 10:20

Ministry is not a livelihood. If the Lord says to stop, that is where the calling ends. Neither the 'I' that I evaluate nor the 'I' that others evaluate provides any justification. Only He knows my path and sets my time. Moses, who fulfilled his calling but did not enter Canaan, is truly an admirable person.

Saul erected a monument to himself on Mount Carmel after his victory, but Samuel erected Ebenezer between Mizpah and Shen, saying 'Thus far the Lord has helped us.' Your focus reveals the entirety of your life.

The most effective way to resist Satan is to keep your place. Since he is a fallen spirit who did not keep his original place, he fears believers who faithfully keep their place. No matter how glorious and desirable a position may be, if it is not the place given to me, it is far better to stay home and cook ramen. 'And the angels who did not keep their proper domain, but abandoned their correct dwelling place, He has kept in eternal chains under darkness for the judgment of the great day.' —Jude 1:6

As we live, we often think about wanting to please people's hearts. True servants in God's kingdom are those whose hearts align with His. We need people like David—those who so please the Lord that they trust to fulfill His will. Though David suffered greatly, he ultimately abandoned his own will and self-centered thinking to accomplish God's will.
*The way to transform the world is to do what I must do and entrust what the Lord must do to Him. What I must do is repent and be transformed. Revival begins from my own spirit.
You are the reason for life. You are the song of life. With all my heart.
Samson was physically strong but morally weak. We also have seasons when we are physically strong but morally weak—our youth. Strength without self-control brings about a fall.
When you love the other person, you can see your true self. However, when you hate, you only see one cruel, wounded person.
When there is no understanding of the other, people can become very cruel, especially to themselves.

Wounds hurt our hearts. A wounded, unhealthy heart produces the fruit of sin. Look back on your memories. When do I become weak regarding sin? Is it not when I am angry, wounded, and lonely—when I am captivated by wrong emotions?

Wounds essentially come from one thing: rejection. However, rejection is not the root cause of wounds. The fundamental issue is the loss of identity in the Lord before rejection even occurs—not understanding 'how valuable I am in the Lord.' The problem is not the environment or people but not knowing 'who I am in the Lord.' —From a leader's meeting. 'I write to you, little children, because you know the Father. I write to you, fathers, because you know Him who is from the beginning. I write to you, young men, because you are strong, and the word of God abides in you, and you have overcome the wicked one.' —1 John 2:14

*Those who support slavery. Slavery means barbarically determining a person's status based only on skin color they prefer or dislike, regardless of the character and image of God that a person possesses. If you judge souls with prejudice, it is not much different from supporting slavery. Every soul is one for whom Jesus died on the cross.

When you become a new creation, new desires are born. One of them is the constant desire to gather together. Do you hate gathering as a Christian? You need healing. 'Endeavoring to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.' —Ephesians 4:3

Instead of being renewed, there is no need to wear a mask and become someone else. Living as the 'self' that God accepts and created is what you do best. Desiring what the Lord has not given is greed. Many people cannot live 'their own life' because of this greed and instead try to imitate 'someone else's life.' You are already beautiful enough as you are.
*The reason you cannot stop. Tribulation, heart trials, wounds, harsh words, and ridicule cannot stop the saints. It is because it is not I but Jesus living in me who moves forward.

A thoughtless person chooses gifts by price. A gift is my heart toward the other person. Therefore, a gift reveals the other person's image. A single rose means you are beautiful, and a small keychain means I will walk with you. 'It's the luncheon meat spam you used to like. It's quite expensive compared to others. Good, right?' means 'You are my comrade.'

*The grace of acceptance. Does the Lord love such people? Yes, of course He loves them. I know because He loves even someone like me. And I know because He bore the cross not only for me but also for that person.
*Jesus does not cling. Clinging comes out when we are afraid and anxious. Jesus loves without clinging. He loves not our outward appearance and image but our heart.

*Gratitude changes destiny. King Saul is tested by David's fame and the people's cheers. Praise refines people. If, just if, Saul had thanked God at that moment for giving him such an outstanding talent as David, the course of history would have changed.

*The definition of being lifted up. The Lord has lifted you up high and will continue to do so. The true meaning of being lifted up is being made like the lowly Jesus and raising up souls. The height to which you are lifted will create a proportional depth of humility.
*For whom do I live? 'For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain.' —Philippians 1:21

After the Fall, the characteristic of fallen humans is 'shifting blame' and 'self-centeredness.' Thus, they have become beings who pursue victimhood and selfish interests even in small matters. The cross begins with acknowledging one's sinfulness and repentance, nailing one's selfish self to the cross.

What appears in those who sinned through the Tree of Knowledge is 'self-pursuit.' Because of this, people envy, quarrel, and even commit murder (James 4:2). Killing the self is not achieving enlightenment or gaining understanding through spiritual discipline. If the death of self were possible on the human side, then Christ's cross would be in vain. It is only believing in what God predetermined on the cross. It is not something we 'do' but 'take,' 'enjoy,' 'consider.' In the Lord, 'consider' and 'believe' (reckoning). 'We know that our old self was crucified with Him so that the body of sin might be rendered powerless, that we should no longer be slaves to sin. For anyone who has died has been set free from sin. Now if we died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with Him. In the same way, count yourselves dead to sin but alive to God in Christ Jesus.' —Romans 6:6, 11

*Those whom I respect and love, whose outstanding ministries, gifts, and positions are admirable. But toward a heart that loves Jesus, the head naturally bows in respect. It is so lovely that faults are hidden.

*To burn and scatter like flames. Every moment as if it were the last, every meeting as if it were a farewell—everything must be given wholeheartedly. Before the Lord without regret, for what I am is by God's grace, and He alone is the eternal Judge. 'For I am already being poured out like a drink offering, and the time for my departure is near. I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith. Now there is in store for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will award to me on that day—and not only to me, but to all who have longed for His appearing.' —2 Timothy 4:6, 7, 8
*Interpretation is faith. The situation is not grinding me down; I am interpreting it that way. Fallen leaves fall without thought, yet to some they become romantic and to others melancholy.
*Heart, overcome! Ask yourself: Are you afraid? If you are afraid, you must overcome it. If you are not afraid, you have already won.
Live by response, not by achievement. Worldly victory makes people proud, but the answer the Lord gives causes us to radiate the fragrance of the Lord.

Worldly people make choices by avoiding challenges and fears and preferring personal gain. But people of faith stand where the Lord said to stand, in places He deems right despite disadvantage. Those prepared to die fear nothing. Yet we are already people who died in the Lord and live in new life. Beyond the line of death, we are not merely bold but at peace.

The world and Satan challenged Jesus about His identity as the Son of God. They said, 'Prove it if you can.' Living while trying to prove is tiring.
