The Lord received the testimony of God the Father, so He had no need to prove anything to the world. My identity naturally manifests when I abide with the Lord. 'Be still, and know that I am God.' Live a life of testimony, not proof.
The world's and darkness's challenges fade with time. Because they are not truth, they lack staying power. But the Lord's love is the same yesterday, today, and forever.

You must abandon the advantageous position that worldly people think valuable and advance trusting only the Lord. When I cling more to advantage than to the Lord, God says to let it go. When you release advantage, you hear less of the praise you so desired, yet the question is: Is the Lord alone sufficient?

Do not abandon 'calling' while abandoning worldly 'ambition.' You do not study and work only for your own enjoyment. You do it to serve. Reject status, wealth, and qualifications for self-enjoyment, and obtain status, wealth, and qualifications for serving.

There are impossible things in the world, yet many people constantly attempt one thing: serving two masters. This is absolutely impossible. While there may be genuine affection for both, there cannot be wholehearted service to both. Ultimately, you serve the one who prevails.

The hardest thing in the world is pretending. Those who do this well are called comedians. Living as something you are not is exhausting and the life the Lord most dislikes. Be careful not to practice your righteousness before others to be seen by them. —Matthew 6:1

I hope you become messengers who choose victory in the Lord over success in the world. Rather than envying those who build their resume through self-development, I hope you become young people who overcome evil with good through genuine 'competence' that comes with the Lord's presence. The era of competing with mere appearance has passed; in this world like a live broadcast, I hope you compete with substance, not lip-sync or marketing.

* Mission over efficiency. Rather than striving to utilize your time efficiently, you must do what you ought to do. As naturally as we breathe and our hearts beat, those who love God and have received that heart stand where they should stand, do what they must do, and remain with those they should be with. However, such a life and decisions frequently face criticism from lust and worldly values.

The world boasts of things that will perish, but there are truly two kinds of strong people. One is a strong person who has been humbled in Jesus, and the second is a weak person who is bold in Jesus. Let the lowly brother boast in his high position, and let the rich brother boast in his low position, because he will pass away like a flower of the grass. —James 1:9-10

Do not ignore what is more important for the sake of the success the world speaks of. Do not be caught by success, but be captivated by the light within me. Though you go slowly, you will not fall backward, and ultimately you will become what God dreams of.
Become salt and dissolve away. Deny yourself and take up your cross daily. You will disappear and be forgotten, but the Lord will certainly find you.
Belonging relieves our anxiety. Sometimes it also gives us pride. But God is not interested in where you belong. He says what matters is what kind of person you stand before the Lord as.

Though marginalized and threatened, though hope was cut short and he was disappointed, though he stumbled in sin, he was one who relied on God and was a king beloved and aligned with God's heart. David. I hope you rise up like David.
Your desires reveal your faith. What do you truly desire?

* Just a moment; let me think of our sister. What you want to think of now is not a negative situation that defiles me. Think of and hope for good things. I hope you will embrace God's heart and proclaim, envisioning the impact that flows out and spreads heaven. Surprisingly, I see Christians unlock difficult situations starting from trivial places. Not 'Meeting that person will be hard!' but 'Whether Christ in me lives or dies, He will be exalted, God will surely help!'

A retreat does not change you through the power of godliness and then provide a changed environment. There is a world of difference between a place with no signals and no corners to sin, and the world. For the difference between that place and here to diminish, inner transformation must occur. Coming to early morning prayer after going to Korea is not because I am diligent but temporarily due to jet lag. Inner transformation and being with people of faith become power. Furthermore, flee the passions of youth and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace along with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart. —2 Timothy 2:22

A grenade is said to be a great weapon. If thrown and hit directly, it causes concussion; even a grazing blow causes bruising. This is foolish repurposing. You were not called to dig, but to conquer with overflowing prayer.

Shame (Humiliation) is a feeling only humans experience. A dog can be happy, angry, sad, and joyful, but it does not feel embarrassed. This is because shame came as a result of sin. When a person experiences shame, it inflicts internal wounds that make them either violent or so powerless they want to give up life. Only the Lamb has the power to remove shame and instead make us dance in a crown of glory. All my shame has disappeared through the blood of Jesus. —Hymn 282
The passion that burned all of young me, never stopping, never pausing to run—what did I so desperately want to see? Jesus, only Jesus.
* Tailor-made. Who would tell you to do it? Who would stop you? The calling is unique to each person.
Victory depends on how you fill time and empty spaces. Will you fill it with Jesus? Or surrender it to laziness and meaninglessness?
To not regret later when time passes, you must live well. Living well is focusing on the Lord and not fearing people.

Even when something you don't know comes up during a test, set it aside and keep moving forward. We are not called to be perfect but to obey. God is not pleased with those who retreat and sink into despair. That difficult problem in elementary school becomes easy in middle school.

* Why mental breakdown comes. There are those whose laughter is not really laughter but reveals anxiety. Such people will inevitably experience mental breakdown soon, and lacking the ability to explain, they either go silent or hide their weaknesses by wearing a mask and projecting the cause of negative emotions onto others. The further from God, the more they try to guarantee their existence through 'self-satisfaction' and 'relational validation,' always anxious. If you truly believe in what Jesus did on the cross, rather than constantly having mental breakdowns through futile pursuits, you will live in 'glory' with shameless hope. Love never fails. Love never falls away. —1 Corinthians 13:8

* Judges and audience. Athletes who repeatedly focus on the audience's gaze or rely on their own feelings cannot perform excellently. Only the judge makes the judgment. There is nothing more foolish than judging your existence by the self-feeling that comes from the audience's reaction, and nothing more stupid than giving up and running away from the competition because of it. Now henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will award to me on that day, and not only to me but also to all who have loved His appearing. —2 Timothy 4:8

* Knowing myself. David was the eighth child never introduced to important guests, was not trusted enough by his father to be asked to bring evidence, and habitually heard harsh words from his brother Eliab. And he himself confessed that his mother conceived him in sin. Yet he was faithful, steadfast, and bold because he discovered himself in relationship with the Lord. Abide in me, and I in you. —John 15:4 Though my father and mother forsake me, the Lord will receive me. —Psalm 27:10
Faith is not something that must be lived that way, but something that cannot be lived any other way.
Religious action can never be a boast. For example, it is like boasting of seeing goodness 300 times. If you truly want to boast, you should boast, 'I truly loved.'
* No matter how weak, no matter how poor, it is thankful that there is something to give. It is not that we cannot help because we are weak, nor that we cannot give because we have nothing.
* If there is no peace, it is not circumstances or people that are the problem—it is that you are not being thankful. Gratitude alone divides a heavenly life from a hellish one.
* If your heart is beating, you have a calling. If time and life are given to you, you have God's calling, and it is love. Please serve with what the Lord has given you.

Leaving the Father's embrace and serving idols means losing sonship and living imprisoned in a pigsty. Those who lose themselves and live imprisoned boast at most of collecting many carob pods that pigs eat. Do not merely survive; soar like an eagle toward the goal. Do not tear your garments, but tear your hearts and return to the Lord your God. —Joel 2:13 Do not try to appease the Lord through changed behavior; experience the transformation of being that comes from returning to the Lord and turning your heart.
A father does not need to be socially high or wealthy. Often he is more limited and weak than you. Yet he is a good father to me. Because he was limited and weakened for me. Treat your parents well.

* It is not calculation but love. Life is unknown when calculated. Life seems like just riding a roller coaster. But the more you love, the more you realize how short life truly is. Especially, even if you live loving alone, life is never enough. Do not waste life (calculate) and then regret.
Redesign your painful youth with God's dream and passion. You were not that kind of person. The unchanging fact is that you are God's person, called by God Himself.
I do not want to become an evil minister. Nor do I hope to be called a decent minister. Apart from the Lord, I become an empty shell, nothing.

* When Benefit precedes Righteousness, it is idolatry. God does not deny bread or loneliness. But the world's teaching is that you can be satisfied and satisfied by benefit. However, the Bible says you cannot. Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled. —Matthew 5:6
* Looking toward the goal, thinking deeply, pursuing and running means that the goal you are striving for is so precious that you are indifferent to other things.

* True love. Being loved is truly wonderful. But when you only receive, you do not understand well. As you love, you come to understand. When you see what God the Father paid the price for in showing how much He loved us, you understand. See what kind of love the Father has given us, that we should be called children of God, and so we are. This is why the world does not know us, because it did not know Him. —1 John 3:1

A change in the inclination of affection is not living by force of law. Enduring and enjoying are fundamentally different. Forced giving and giving as joy are different. Struggling to forgive and being in a state of hatred are fundamentally difficult things that are different. Suppressing the impulse to sin and hatred of sin and evil are different. It is not easy to distinguish between those who do things forcibly and those who do not. One tip: those who do things forcibly lack ease and condemn others who do not meet the standard. Jesus never condemned anyone.

Obtaining the promised land is not about entering Canaan and registering property or investing well in prime real estate. The only ownership that makes you the owner of that land comes from your relationship with God. Canaan is a region where rain plays an absolute role. Your relationship with God determines everything about you. Relationship is authority and power.
Hide weaknesses and exaggerate strengths. That is the principle of marketing. But we boast in our weakness instead. Through our weakness flows the Lord's power. So when weak, it becomes strong.

Are you weak? In that moment you look to the Lord, you become a spiritual strongman. So always look to the Lord. That is the beginning and that is the completion. Look to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith. —Hebrews 12:2

God's gaze and touch rest on the weak. Do not boast of things that will perish, but boast in weakness instead. Then Christ Himself will visit and dwell with you. 'And He said to me, My grace is sufficient for you, for power is perfected in weakness. Most gladly, therefore, I will rather boast about my weaknesses, so that the power of Christ may dwell in me.' —2 Corinthians 12:9
* Testimony of suffering. The reason your heart aches as if pierced by a sword is because God created you with honor and works within you.

Now, people in our age no longer ask, 'Who am I?' They only think, 'What does others think of me?' Even though it is hard to live my own one-person life, I live the life others and relatives expect. A meaningless life is unhappy. Because it is not your own life. You must decide whether to see yourself through God or through what you own.

Someone asks like this: Even if I cannot climb high, cannot I take off one garment and give it to someone without clothes? The answer is this: That is why the Lord says to live diligently. Do not just receive help while naked; help those who need help by taking off your clothes. Since you are taking off clothes anyway, take off several and help. The Lord does not quench the dimly burning wick or break the bruised reed. But at the same time, the Lord does not want me to become a dimly burning wick or bruised reed either. I hope you become like a torch and cedar wood.

* Either-or choice 2. If you must abandon one thing, then for holding on to the Lord, the things you love and cherish so much must be abandoned without attachment. Those who truly love souls will attempt to love with the highest love. That is choosing 'voluntary self-isolation' as loneliness to draw living water from a deep well. Being called as a channel of grace is because holiness is life. Truth is lonely but grows bolder day by day. —Reverend Choi Chun-seon
An adult can match the child's pace, but a child cannot. That is why the mature one is the servant and the understanding one.

Even if clumsy, a meeting and fellowship where you kept courtesy toward the other person and acted truly without shame before the Lord leaves no regret even when parted. Years pass, and when you unexpectedly meet again on some street or place, rather than your heart beating again, you see more clearly the path you must take.

How successful you were and how much you wanted to be praised? Why you did it is more important. If you did it out of love for God, after completing that work, you will bow down and give thanks and worship to God. Then you will not envy a king. Simply and directly meeting God is looking only to God in daily life.

Your rights. You truly have the right to be happy, and no matter where you are or what you do, you should receive intercession and prayer. That is the responsibility of a minister who stakes their life.
A woman's heart is easily broken. Because it is precious.

And the Lord said to him, 'What is that in your hand?' And he said, 'A staff.' —Exodus 4:2 1. God does not ask how much I have achieved or been recognized. 2. Even if unimpressive, He asks what you have. 3. Moses was a failed old man with a dry stick. 4. It is not about having much or climbing high to display the Lord's glory. 5. If something you don't have appears to be something you do, there is no need to wear a mask or be boastful. 6. Even a dry life displays power and glory when obedient to the Lord. Moses took the staff of God in his hand. —Exodus 4:20 A dry stick becomes God's staff when held in the Lord's hand. A ring in a department store and a ring given by a loved one are not the same ring. It is the ring 'you' put on. When a meaningless stick I used freely becomes God's staff held by God, I see glory in it. Exodus 4:17 'You shall take this staff in your hand, and with it you shall perform signs.'

The Lord chose you, not because you are more numerous than all peoples, for you are the fewest of all peoples. —Deuteronomy 7:7 I bless you to become the weakest. Then the Lord will appear all the more.

Until we are refined, even with grace, we are more like raw stones than jewels. Angels cannot refine us. That is why He refines us through people. When the interpretation of life changes, the 'enemy' becomes my 'life teacher.'
Rise high so that inferiority complex and victim mentality cannot touch you. At 350km up to 800m.

If you worked hard yet feel emptiness, it is not zeal for the Lord. Had you loved the Lord, you would have abandoned other expectations long ago. If you sought the joy of people, you are not a servant of Christ and should be ashamed.
* Purpose of Immortality What holds me is not the world and people. Only the Lord. That is why life can be beautiful and glorious.

* Focus over Response Repeatedly seeking confirmation of a relationship through the other's response comes from fear. This anxiety stems from 'anxiety about one's own existence.' Not knowing God, we do not know our existence and purpose, and attachment creates vain pursuits that twist our lives.

* Transformation of Soul The soul will not change unless we live out the Word. To live out the Word is not a matter of willpower or compulsion but only through union with Christ. Then it becomes clear where we must start again. The prodigal returning to be united with the Father—repentance is the beginning.

If you do not meet God in worship, like Israel, tribulations will not cease. When people worship God, they also see themselves. When you cannot see yourself, everything appears distorted and the vicious cycle of misunderstanding continues. Worship breaks that cycle. When worship collapses because of busy work, even a passing dog will attack.
Those who believe in Jesus are not hasty. If it is not the Lord's will, they deny even their own emotions and will, and rather rejoice because the desires of the flesh have been destroyed.
Those who believe in Jesus may feel somewhat lonely. Because even in conversation, the focus is on the Lord.
Those who believe in Jesus are not chased. Rather, they live a life of chasing, a life of leading.
In Jesus, there is nothing in vain. All the mistakes I thought were trial and error will become important ministries, and all things will work together for good. In Jesus...

If satisfied with Jesus alone, generosity arises and fear retreats. In Jesus, all evaluations will be reinterpreted. A life of fullness with no lack. One captivated by Jesus is the most beautiful person, and He will vindicate such a person.

Jesus lived as a carpenter until age 30. He must have been a good carpenter. He would have been kind, faithful, and did not overcharge his customers. Coming to serve by lowering Himself to the world, He lived that way even as a worker.
* True Humility Humility is not focusing on yourself but exalting only the Lord.
When you think deeply of Jesus and love Him, you become unaware of how others look at you. In worship and life, the world and I vanish, and only the Lord who redeemed me is seen.

Believing in Jesus does not immediately make you a saint. This is the cause of despair. When Israel defeated Egypt through the ten plagues and crossed the Red Sea, they changed from 'slaves' to 'free people.' But in spirit, 'slave mentality' remains. The 'old self' still writhes alive. That is why wilderness life begins. The Lord calls this wilderness 'church life.' —Acts 7:38

* Same composition, different reason for tears People feel pain from what is within themselves. If pain comes from people, people dwell within; if from worldly conditions, the world fills us. God's person feels pain because of God. That is why His tears become my tears.

The greatest problem of the elder son in Jesus' parable of the prodigal is that he lives in his father's house. He lost his father but did not realize it. While living in his father's house doing his father's work, he lost his father and lost his father's heart and did not know it. He compared and then became angry, judged hastily and then asserted his rights. If you plant beans in spring, you cannot harvest red beans in autumn. If you start from a fundamentally wrong place, no matter how similar it appears, the fruit you bear will be different and you cannot find the Father.
Jesus came to destroy the works of the devil. He called us to save lives and overcome darkness, and paradoxically, this work becomes most powerful when we abandon ourselves.
I am ready Lord, I came to do what I should in the book it is written of me.

There is an old saying that it is not people who lie but money that lies. Though the true heart is not so, harsh reality and circumstances drive people to it. Without spiritual assets, without Jesus, I am easily angered, frustrated, and wounded by small stimuli. Without the risen Jesus, all other problems are merely results of not having Jesus. I pray that the Lord's presence may be with your spirit. May grace be with you. —2 Timothy 4:22

* Infinite Loyalty of a Finite Being That I am finite means I am bound by time and place. I can only work within my limits in the time and place given to me. Conversely, the Lord will work in other realms. If I am faithful unto death, the Lord's omnipotence will accomplish beyond my limitations.

* Your Fear Now Recognized Abraham was a worshipper who built altars everywhere, consulted God's judgment through prayer, and already received the title of a believer. —Genesis 15:6 But only when Abraham did not spare even 'Isaac,' the only son obtained in his old age, did he 'now' confirm that he fears God. Fearing God is not receiving something but obeying when told to let go—cutting off whatever it is and offering it to Him. The Lord said, Do not lay your hand on the boy. Do not do anything to him. Now I know that you fear God, because you have not withheld from me your son, your only son. —Genesis 22:12
* Why Faith Is Not a Show Not being conscious of people, thinking only of the Lord's gaze, and sacrificing unconditionally.

Love is viewing people through a telescope; hatred is viewing through a microscope. Love makes you blind. That is why you can cover sin. Though you give everything, you become the richest person. Before all missions, God commands to love God and love your neighbor. This is the prophet; this is God's person. The white-haired old apostle Peter, who followed Jesus that way and experienced failure, blaming others rather than himself, pleads earnestly to believers living their final days: Above all, love each other deeply, because love covers a multitude of sins. —1 Peter 4:8
* Speak Through the Results of Life Are you not the result of my work in the Lord? —Paul in 1 Corinthians 9:1

* Faith Like Herod Herod was tormented by John the Baptist's blade-like preaching and listened to it. But there was no change in his life. Because he did not want to lose pleasure. Without loss, there is no change. It will surely perish. It is merely religious satisfaction. Reading prayer books instead of praying or receiving evangelism training and just being self-satisfied is the same. Yet when Herod heard him, he was greatly troubled; yet he heard him gladly. —Mark 6:20
Only the life focused on honoring Christ is peaceful. Living and dying are not such issues because they are overwhelmed by more important values.
Without gentleness, you cannot lead a team. That is why God trains us.
You are not called to be perfect. You are standing to serve the perfect Lord.

The king's heart was very grieved, and he went up to the chamber over the gate and wept. And as he went, he said: 'O my son Absalom! My son, my son Absalom! Would I had died instead of you, O Absalom, my son, my son!' —2 Samuel 18:33 Those with the Lord's heart, participate in the Lord's weeping.
If asked why I am in this place now, I would answer Jesus. Since you asked the reason, I will give the reason. Jesus is the only reason for my life.
Do not be lonely; be one who comforts. When you lift your eyes, there are far more people who need you.

More important than appearance is a soft mother's embrace. The ability to see value deeply brings us to glorious places. We buy rice by price, but a farmer reads the story of one grain of rice—whether it is Gyeonggi rice, Chungnam rice, or one that overcame drought. When you know the essence of the Gospel, you read the beginning of salvation where you thought it was the end. The Gospel makes you reject the palace and go to the wilderness.

Joseph went before the king with office at age 30 and died after living 110 years. Had Moses benchmarked Joseph, he would not have left the palace even at 40. Moses spent 40 years in the wilderness and was called only at 80. Do not envy others' lives; live the life God has given you. Even if it does not appear good...
When you look beyond greed and ambition to the Lord, you will surely see the Lord.
Repent of greed and lust and become strong. Become the strongest person looking only to Jesus.

We mistake effectively satisfying much greed as happiness. That is not happiness but sin and death. True happiness is how free you are from sin, which is the same as how successfully you have emptied greed. When the Holy Spirit came, in Acts there was no one who claimed 'my own possessions.' Without the power of the Holy Spirit, there is not even a 1% chance of solving the problems we face.

* Class, Not Zeal There are two ways an elementary student solves problems. One is to work hard. Working hard will raise the grade about 10 points on the midterm. The other is to solve it as a college student. Revival does not come through relative zeal alone. Because that glory is so great that nothing else matters, and it comes when you live a completely different life, pray differently, worship differently toward the goal. Abandon the trivial and dwell in a different class. Brethren, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus. —Philippians 3:13-14

* Life Giving Birth to Life There are those like police, firefighters, and coast guard who save people's lives. Unlike office workers who only touch phones and computers, they sometimes give their lives during duty. To save life, you must risk life. Some believers are such that even without seeing a minister, respect and admiration arise. When you see disciples, you know the teacher.

What we hate hearing is people blaming us. Yet surprisingly, we see and hear it. Because another part of our heart wants it. This contradictory emotion comes from wounds and rejection. When negative feelings creep up, turning them all into excrement at once is knowing Jesus. When you know and experience Jesus, others' evaluations fade, and you see yourself, relationships, and the Lord more clearly. Approaching true reality.

Being loved does not mean monopolizing all attention and affection. It simply means that God's irreplaceable love is poured into earthen vessels like us, making us precious stones, and sometimes we meet people who recognize and respect those gems.

Do you know why we fight with those we love? Because of selfish greed. When trying to satisfy the ego, war never ceases in family, friends, and relationships. To stop this, we must die—accepting that we are already dead. In fact, we have already died with the Lord.
* Love Is Love is not a decision. That is why we cannot do it by our own strength. Love is what fills me completely. That is why it is always on my mind and ultimately moves me.

We are His workmanship —Ephesians 2:10 We are God's masterpiece, God's one-of-a-kind work, God's poem and God's letter. When I become myself, the ability God has given me flows out. Do not become someone else; become yourself. That is the freedom that comes when you discover yourself in God.

We hear Satan's whisper daily telling us to prove ourselves. Even when coming to Jesus, he was tempted to turn stones into bread and prove he was God's Son. What will you do? If you have such power, if it were me, I would pick up a stone, pretend to pray, and then crush Satan's head with it. Apostle Paul said in his letters that he did not preach himself but the Lord. Then the Lord said He also testifies of us before the angels.
We cannot satisfy everyone. That is greed. Jesus did not satisfy everyone either. Do your best, accept the results, and give thanks.

We are called to be light and salt in the world. But we are light and salt serving without light and without name. Those who have pulled out their own greed and character have an overwhelming anointing they cannot bear.

We are the body of Christ. Head and body are never separated. Just as my head is mine, my body is also mine. We are those who participate in God's nature and the fullness of Jesus. That is why the power to rule dwells within us. I pray you never abandon that authority.

* Why Fortune Tellers Demand Payment But Do Not Speak of Repentance Fortune tellers and shamans do not speak of repentance. The characteristic of idol worship is the 'immediacy' of fulfilling the desires I want for some payment—short-term effects and temptations. But it cannot satisfy or fill me; rather, its end is death. Distinguish between bait and true nourishment.

The reason we can walk forward despite the enemy's schemes, fierce challenges, obscuring darkness, and suffocating circumstances is because from the beginning we rejected peace and comfort and started differently. Believing in Jesus means being saved not only from sin but also from relative purposes, reputations, and worldly success. If we can plant the cross in that land and pray for the Holy Spirit to come... then whether we live or die matters little. According to my earnest expectation and hope, I will not be ashamed in anything, but with complete boldness as always, whether by life or by death, I will make Christ honored in my body. For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain. -Philippians 1:20, 21
The common trait of great leaders is character. Image and reputation are what others see in 'me,' while character is 'me' myself. If you cannot give trust, you cannot lead others.

Whether becoming a hypocrite to deceive people or becoming a saint to inspire them, both exercise powerful influence. However, you need not become either a hypocrite or a saint. You need only know the truth. When you know the truth, you can live no other way.
A person who received grace as a gift flows with wise and powerful ruling authority in the Lord to inspire others. -Romans 5:17, reigning as kings

The more harmful food is, the sweeter it tastes. Inferiority, comparison, pride, and rejection are harmful yet impossible to release. However, when you know Christ, you can let go of these things. Before the Lord's honor and beauty, the value of all things is reset to refuse.

Have you ever seen a music critic sing one song properly? We are not called to evaluate and critique but to sing the song of life. From the Garden of Eden to the evil that will continue in the future, what we must hold onto is the 'gospel.' The gospel is enough.
No one in this world confesses that they are jealous. They only say they are executing justice. Jealousy is grieving over another's joy. There are two solutions to resolve this...

Isaiah 14:13-14 reveals the devil's original sin: pride. In that short passage, the personal pronoun 'I' appears five times. Have you met the devil? The devil does not block those heading in the same direction as him. When 'I' comes from my mouth, the devil is at work. When I say I received such treatment, I say how dare anyone do this to me, I cannot stand such things—at that moment, I approach the devil's original sin. It must be 'He' not 'I.' Especially sisters, if you keep emphasizing 'I,' it becomes 'Gaga'—Lady Gaga.
Now I hope you become a person the Lord can entrust authority to with confidence. When you gain power, you must be someone who understands where that authority came from.
You understand but ask how one can live that way. How can you live a different life after receiving a love you don't understand?

Popularity is not necessarily truth. Action movies are so entertaining, but no one watches them again calling them masterpieces. What remains over time is not popularity but life. Life is born again when blood is shed.

Popularity is momentary and fades as quickly as it comes, and money is the same. Only what belongs in Jesus Christ is eternal. There is nothing more uncertain than human relationships according to our will. Do not grieve and rejoice in brief moments according to the Lord's sovereignty and rule; look up to the Lord and move forward. The Lord is eternal.

I saw a famous chef find those who are marginalized and in the shadows on his day off and personally cook for them, enjoying great joy and rest. Stepping away from people who evaluate and assess you, and becoming a source of joy to those who truly need you is also good rest. What is truly difficult is doing nothing meaninglessly. Rest is not doing nothing but being filled with grace and meaning.
The happiest moment in life is not when you are recognized. It is when you lay that recognition at the Lord's feet.
Life suddenly opens to a different path from a small incident. Therefore, every moment is precious like a jewel and cannot be treated carelessly.
Life is a battle of priorities. The source of love and affection determines success or failure. What I do first tells my future.
I compare life to a marathon. I think it's an appropriate comparison, provided that not everyone runs the same course. Live with your own course and your own strengths. Stop comparing anymore...

If you think of life in terms of speed, you become unhappy. But if you think of it in terms of direction, it becomes clear. If the path I walk is the path of truth, no matter how many people rush down the path of desire that others pursue, I will not be shaken. The meaning of life comes not from speed but from direction.
The purpose of life is to fear God and delight in Him forever. -Westminster Shorter Catechism. Forcing delight is a disaster.

Whether you admit it or not, when you stand before the Lord, you are immediately 'a sinner.' Moreover, it does not end there; the Lord calls that extremely wicked sinner a 'fisher of men' and personally leads and transforms him.
Even if I remain lonely in solitude all my life, if I can live for the Lord, that is an inexpressible blessing and great grace to me.
They say the early bird catches the worm. Then someone always says the early worm gets caught. The biggest problem lies in your identity—thinking you are the worm.
To live on your knees while working and studying, life must be extremely simple. Jesus was a layperson who went around with fishermen and tax collectors and prayed according to habit.

Do not find satisfaction in where you belong; God is your Creator. Do not seek meaning in your splendid relationships; God is the One who called you. Do not fear the world or pursue glory; God is the One who sent you.
It is foolish for you to affirm yourself. You must become faith that the Lord affirms. That is a person after God's own heart. The Lord accomplishes His will through such people.

If you do not love and forgive yourself, you cannot lead anyone. As God has received me, I too must receive myself. I hope you become a person of faith who sees God's mercy greater than your own shortcomings.

What you boast of will be taken away. Everything you boast of will be taken away. But if you boast of Jesus, Jesus will be proclaimed. -2 Kings 20:13. When Hezekiah heard the word of the messengers, he showed them all his silver and gold, spices and precious oil, his armor and all that was found in his treasuries. There was nothing in his palace or in all his realm that Hezekiah did not show them.
Position is less important than posture. If there is perseverance and authenticity, people will recognize it, and God will also take notice.

A person who compares themselves to others in the mirror of comparison becomes arrogant or discouraged. But before God's mirror, people become humble. A person with the wisdom not to sin and the wisdom to be humble becomes honored. -Proverbs 1, 2

When you reveal yourself, display yourself, appeal for yourself, and exalt yourself, there is only desolate emptiness. But when I die on the cross, I no longer live in myself, only the Lord lives in me, and I live only by faith in the Lord, then those who evaluated or deliberately ignored me will take notice of the Lord. Because the Lord cannot be hidden. -Galatians 2:20, Psalm 40:3

If you do not deny yourself, you will deny the Lord. When you hold to the Lord, you can deny and renounce yourself. We cannot serve two masters, nor can we straddle both legs, because the gap cannot absolutely be bridged.

A person who sees themselves through their own eyes becomes desperate with people and the world. It means to cling and pester. Because you are anxious about your shortcomings and feel pursued. But a person who dwells in the Lord fears nothing. Where you place your gaze determines all success or failure. But I have calmed and quieted myself, I am like a weaned child with its mother; like a weaned child I am content. -Psalm 131:2
If you work with your mind and popularity, you can do it quickly, but you can only move a few. However, if you work from the heart, you gain everyone's heart and reveal God's work.
Three things that reflect your state: 1. What do you boast of? 2. What causes you pain? 3. What do you cling to? The answers here are what rule me and what I serve.
Look at your desires. Then you will know whom you live for. Those who live for what perishes will be ashamed, and those who live for the eternal One will receive glory.
Do not trust your psychological state; believe the conclusion the Lord gave you through the cross. By believing and accepting that victory, you are victorious.

When you confess through your weakness and insufficiency that you cannot go further down that path, the Lord speaks to feed His sheep. It is not your will, not your zeal, not your determination that sustains you; only I will hold you. -1 Corinthians 15:58. Be those who work more earnestly in the Lord's work... knowing that your labor in the Lord is not in vain.

Not maintaining your own position is sitting in the place of the arrogant. David says in Psalm 1 that such a person has no blessing. When you do not maintain the place the Lord gave you, shame and defilement come. Hold fast to your position.

A person who laments their circumstances, who is discouraged that their appearance is not a jewel, saying 'Others are doing well (comparing), why can't I get ahead?' The gem-like appearance cannot be achieved through cosmetic surgery or upgrading your resume and reversing your life. I just need to become a genuine earthen vessel and focus on the precious Lord. He never demanded I become a gem, nor can I become one. The gem is only Jesus. The secret to overcoming both despair and pride at once is knowing that truth. I am an earthen vessel, the Lord is the gem. -2 Corinthians 4:7. But we have this treasure in jars of clay to show that this all-surpassing power is from God and not from us.

No matter how plausible and persuasive your judgment and thoughts are, the conclusion is that the Lord has chosen me. The Lord's conclusion will make all evaluations and attacks pale in comparison. Be bold.

By observing your thoughts and words, you can see where your life is headed. Especially the first thought when you open your eyes in the morning is important. Because it is your core. A person produces good from the good stored up and evil from the evil stored up. What is in me?
Trying to love with your own strength is arrogance and delusion. How absurd is it for a branch to live independently from the vine?

You need not fear becoming a dropout. God has prepared good works for you long ago. It is good if someone successful prepares for you, but God's preparation is the most beautiful and perfect. We are God's workmanship, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do. -Ephesians 2:10
When you feel you are nobody, rejoice. Now it is time for the Lord to work. Having no goodness or righteousness is where the gospel begins, and that gospel must be held onto today as well.
The greatest reason you are wounded is not people, expressions, or words of rejection. The true reason is the selfishness of protecting yourself.
You are honored even if you are not recognized. So you need not prove or seek confirmation. Recognition of honor comes only from God.

A person who holds onto themselves fears deeply being rejected and having relationships severed, becoming lonely. From birth into this world, almost nothing happens according to my will. What must be clear is that God holds me, and He always gives me the best. When you know and believe that, forced effort disappears and you are freed from clinging. Of course, life becomes abundant.
You complain that you are undervalued or that people do not respect you. The problem is not the troubled people around you but that you have not emptied yourself.
It is easy to stumble over what you do best.
Your true desired goal and destination reveal your identity. Who are you?
When you feel insignificant, look to God who made you. God doesn't make junk.

The natural self has much that must be discarded—foolishness, oversensitivity, things that trouble both me and others. These should be cast off, but it is not easy. Yet when we mature, we put away childish things. Love makes our immature selves complete and gives us the same perspective as the Lord. 1 Corinthians 13:11-12 KJV When I was a child, I spoke as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things. For now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known.
Be free. Failing to be free means infinite retakes of the same test.

He restores our self-worth and opens doors at workplaces in the final hour, and gives bright light in much pain and frustration. Recovery seemed to exist only in books, but it appears in life too. So today I run again, to pray.
Those with low self-esteem are glad when things go well and depressed when they don't. They cannot find happiness in existence, so they try to fill satisfaction with numbers. Our identity is Jesus.

My abilities, surrounding circumstances, rumors, and crises are not really the issue. What matters is that the Lord sent me, and the Lord's hand protects me. So the target I focus on changes. It is only to witness the cross in that land and see God's glory.
Falling often is proof that I rely on my own strength. If I relied on the Lord, I would not fall. Because Jesus, whom I believe in and depend on, never falls.

Animals that cannot lie down even when well are all herbivores. In contrast, strong predators have leisure. Become spiritually strong. Then you will have leisure. We are not predators, but like Psalm 23, we experience making God our Shepherd and lying in green pastures. Notice here—the Shepherd makes the sheep lie down. The Lord's rest is gracious and sometimes half-forced. When your body aches, lie down without moving; there are days of rest.
Living well means not gaining money and honor, but living according to the will the Lord planned.

If I went out to do business and returned without leaving anything behind, then I truly did business well. If I walked the path of faith witnessing only Jesus and nothing remained, then I walked the right path.
After maturity, we put away childish things and what we saw dimly we now see clearly, face to face. Growth means change, and it is achieved when perfect love is fulfilled.

The problem is that Jesus is not in that heart, and a more serious problem is mistaking what isn't there for what is. People produce good from what they have stored up as good, and evil from what they have stored up as evil. If Jesus is present, pain and joy will be transformed and Abraham's deeds will follow—obedience and faith.
It is foolish to waste talent and money. Among these, wasting time is the most foolish act. Because time is life. When time diminishes, life diminishes.
Talent is remembered in people's minds. Consideration remains in people's hearts.

Where your treasure is, there your heart is also. God sees my motives, so He is deeply interested in money matters. Earning money is a technique; spending it is an art. God hopes I am an artist who understands beauty.
If you desire happiness at all, the most important thing is looking in the same direction at the same essential truth—the same Lord, Jesus Christ.

Professional traders analyze, calculate, and compare enormously. Because they want to avoid financial risk. So sinful humans by nature instinctively analyze, calculate, and compare to avoid fear, and soon experience mental breakdown. Such human defense mechanisms are not easily abandoned. They are abandoned only when a greater value is discovered. But what things were gain to me, those I counted loss for Christ. Yea doubtless, and I count all things but loss for the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord: for whom I have suffered the loss of all things, and do count them but dung, that I may win Christ, and be found in him—Philippians 3:7-9.

Even warships sometimes return to port for mooring. They resupply and repair. Why? To go out and fight again. If a ship rests in port and rests at sea, it is not a warship but a cruise ship. Apostle Paul speaks of a 'good fight' lasting a lifetime. We are called to be combative beings. If you believe, you will fight, and if you fight in faith, you will surely win.
Hopeless situations do not change like weather forecasts according to seasons and times. I must change. Repent and be healed. That is the only way.

In my youth, recognized for dedication and loyalty no less than others, one day the Lord gave me a heart to simply wait for Him. Unexpectedly, the Lord came. Repentance burst forth. Though the Lord's presence withdrew long ago, I do not understand why and wonder. Living without shame, not grieving that I do not uphold holiness, but rationalizing instead with the absolute positivity that everything will be well—recovering the cross, faith, and holiness walking together is revival.
In youth I believed there was something I could not lose. But everything except God—any person I depend on—can be lost. So I must depend on the Lord alone and be humble.

The greatest delusion young people have is thinking they will be happy if someone approaches them with nearly perfect love. People become unhappy from the moment they seek to be loved. If you exist in God's love and give love, you become happy. Do not cling to receiving love and loneliness, but receive God's great love and give love that fills.

Come forth as pure gold. There is only One who knows the way I go—God. Only after God has refined me do I become pure gold. Pure gold is valuable not because of its use but because its essence itself is precious. The purpose of refining is to obtain pure gold. When impurities are removed from me, only Jesus remains. More important than Korean church problems or contemporary demands is 'doing your own thing well.' The owner of pure gold is God alone, and pure gold cannot be told to do this or that. But he knows the way that I take, when he has tested me, I will come forth as gold—Job 23:10.

If you truly love, there is no need to hide or exaggerate. Coughing, poverty, and love cannot be hidden. Especially love becomes more evident the more you try to hide it. So she pours perfume on Jesus' feet with tears.

If you truly want to have good faith, you must abandon expectations. Throw away even a hint of hope for recognition and remembrance, and run toward the Lord. This is absolute faith and infinite loyalty.
The most frustrating time is when I cannot go mad because of Jesus. A person of faith has nothing to fear except the absence of Jesus.
What I have deeply learned through long youth ministry is this simple thing: young people are transformed as much as they are loved.
The disciples were persecuted and rejoiced. Being resisted in the world meant they had become worthy of heaven.

Impatience means I have my standards—in other words, my desires. We must put down our standards of seeking recognition from people and God. More than achieving any goal, when I am emptied so God can be revealed through my life, I become known to God. Otherwise, we can do countless works and yet be irrelevant before the Lord.
Nobody respects a gangster just because he drives a luxury car. True human dignity does not come from school, reputation, or class, but from the value of the soul.
Do not seek respect; seek trust. Yet even this is not done by my power. It is achieved only when I look to the Lord and love souls.
A person of faith does not live extraordinarily, but has a clear purpose.

If you grieve because you are not respected or recognized, you do not see God's glory. Agony toward God's kingdom and community testifies that he participates in God's glory. Are you angry or disappointed for human glory? Or are you grieving for the Lord's glory? Lead us to the place the Lord calls.
To be more honest, you are so weak as to be incompetent. But you are strong in the Lord. Infinitely so.
Ultimately, life is a battle at the final station. Either live caught up in the world and go where the world goes, or become obsessed with Jesus and live with Him. We run toward the Lord.

Anyone can like something. But a professional does it with passion until they like what they do. So believers do not fret or mind obstacles and challenges blocking the path of faith. Ignoring and running is ability.

Liking is 'because of what,' and loving is 'in spite of everything.' Just liking thrills, but loving brings tears. Many people 'like' someone for their own happiness. But those who love make you happy because of them.
Liking leads to calculated investment; loving leads to sacrifice with happiness.
Good things are good, but good things that prevent you from approaching the Lord are evil.

There is no clever way to gain good momentum. If I must say, it is the boundless, complete grace the Lord pours out despite my lack of goodness. Next is my becoming good momentum. The Lord gives the momentum source, but remaining a good momentum source is my responsibility.
Everyone hopes and prays to meet a good spouse. If I meet a good spouse, what is the sin of that person meeting unprepared me? Therefore, I must first seek to become a good spouse.
There is absolutely no need to pretend to be a good person. Because in the Lord's eyes, you are sufficiently good and beautiful.
Good morning. You all know I love you, right? Be loved. You were born to be loved.
A good mother is not one with ability to solve everything. She is a mother who grieves she cannot help. Love comes from there.
Good question Q. When your heart and mind differ, does the heart come first or the mind first? A. Faith comes first.

Getting a good car and good house, we cannot find ourselves there. Owning unnecessary things or receiving envious looks does not change the value of my essence. These are all jewelry boxes. But a jewel is a jewel even wrapped in tissue. Even fallen in mud, it is a jewel. So Paul said we hold Jesus, the jewel, in earthen vessels. Do not prove; testify. You will be happy.

It is truly hard to abandon sin. Sin has power beyond human will. But when you truly believe in Jesus, sinning becomes difficult. Because when you truly love Jesus, what is unbreakable becomes breakable. When you love Jesus, everything else can be discarded like dung. But what things were gain to me, those I counted loss for Christ. Yea doubtless, and I count all things but loss for the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord: for whom I have suffered the loss of all things, and do count them but dung, that I may win Christ—Philippians 3:7-9.
Eagles become bolder when storms come. Because they have the vision and wings to fly higher. So they do not react to chicks. So when I see someone reacting, I know who I am. Chicks react to chicks.

Do not try to overcome the limits of sinful nature, the weakness of the flesh, the limits of my character, the power of spiritual trials. These are things humanity has tried but failed to overcome for thousands of years. Rather, look to the Lord and follow Him. If I am where the Lord is, I will soon see myself having overcome limits. My target is not achievement but presence and companionship. I am apprehended by Lord Jesus.

Do not remain fallen in sin, but rise up through love for the Lord. That is truly what the Lord desires, and it is what the devil truly hates. Sin cannot be overcome by determination alone. It is only through the love of Jesus. The sacrifice God seeks is a broken spirit. O God, grant me a broken and contrite heart; You will not despise it. —Psalm 51:17

* The Critical Secret to Relationship Failure Surprisingly, relationships fail because we try too hard. Our voices become tense and our eyes show anxiety. It is human nature to want to eat even a simple bowl of ramen comfortably. Every theatrical performance must eventually have its curtain fall. Life cannot be covered by acting. When we lay hands on a masterpiece, we damage the original. Instead, if you honor yourself and seek to help the person God has given you, you will enter into the amazing blessing of relationships God has prepared. If you long for many things yet cannot love yourself, I urge you to let go and walk away from that hell...

* Why You Should Not Despair of Yourself The reason I am who I am is by God's grace. My despair with myself and living with a mask dishonor the God who created me. If I am God's reason for being, there is no need to live differently or seek attention for a different appearance. When I live as my authentic self, it is the most beautiful life and aligned with God's purpose. If someone dislikes that authentic me, there is no need for me to despair. The desire that all relationships be perfect is greed. Someone who loves my essence is stronger than someone who merely likes me. ^^ That I am who I am is by God's grace. —1 Corinthians 15:10 Even if all the world abandons me, the Lord Jesus will not abandon me. He watches over me to the end. —Hymn 88

Knowing that we are sinners is the first gate of the way of the cross. But we must not stop there. When knowing we are sinners brings the sigh 'What shall I do?', believe in Jesus, and the old self dies on the cross while rising as a new person. To say that evaluation does not change one's essence means becoming a completely new creature. By the Lord's grace.

A deep love is the heart that wants to give but cannot. Many possess much yet cannot love because their hearts are empty. We must sympathize. Love heals. Love makes us believe. Love makes us hear. This is why those who love are happy even with nothing. I pray that young people would feel deep love and practice deep love.

The reason the Lord called me is to do His work. The Lord's work is to believe and to become holy like the Lord. The apostles' devotion to prayer and the Word means that becoming strong in essence is the true solution.

The Lord allowed me to run even when my youth was deeply wounded. The Lord wipes my tears again and allows me to run again for Him. Because He called me, I go forward. The Lord alone is sufficient for me. My longing is to love the Lord more.

If you did what the Lord commanded, you have done very well. In doing so, you may have made mistakes and lacked ability to be perfect in all things, but standing where the Lord said to stand is the beginning of bearing your cross.
Walking the Lord's path does not mean expecting others to follow or support you, but rather you following the Lord. Though that path is narrow and lonely, you walk it with your whole life.
The good thing about abiding in the Lord is that you have nothing more to lose. You who have nothing to lose will continue to be filled.

Paul abandons the seat of an honored rabbi like a straw. Why? Because he saw the true on the Damascus road. Faith is not about choosing not to go; it is about being unable to go. It is because he had never seen the truth that captivates him.
Standing myself before the Lord again and looking only to the Lord is what I have learned until now and the path I must walk going forward.

Lord, whether I live or die is not my concern. Since loving and serving You is my eternal purpose, grant me Your abundant grace and the grace to fulfill this purpose. If my life is long, I will serve and praise You all my life. If my life is short, I will approach Your throne with joy and worship. Following my earnest expectation and hope, being unashamed in all things and confident now as before, whether I live or die, let Christ be honored in my body. —Philippians 1:20
Lord, You know what I truly long for in the end. I desire to be accepted by You.
Do not grieve over the path you could not take; be satisfied walking the path you must walk. Everyone walks, but each has their own path.

When the Lord is said to be faithful, it does not mean it is based on my circumstances or qualifications. He is faithful because He is faithful, and prayer is trusting in His eternally unchanging character and moving forward. Success without Jesus is not success. Therefore, failure in Jesus is not failure. You in Jesus can never fail.

Since this life is temporarily entrusted to us by the Lord, we must live it meaningfully. Humbly and passionately... Jesus gave Himself for us. He died for us so that He might rescue us from all wickedness and cleanse us, making us His people eager to do good works. —Titus 2:14

A person who fears only the Lord has nothing to boast or display. Having nothing to boast or display, they harbor no attachments. Without attachments, they do not cling to position. Because they do not cling, they do not carelessly abandon responsibility.

We must look only to the Lord. We must love the Lord. We must praise only the Lord with all our heart. Those who instead look to people, love people more, and are conscious of people more face judgment. 1 Corinthians 16:22 KJV If anyone does not love the Lord, let that person be cursed. The Lord is coming.
If you depend on and boast of anything more than the Lord, it will be taken from you. You waste time and must start from zero again. Only the Lord is true satisfaction.

For the Lord's sake and His glory, we run forward. Even if everything else crumbles, if that is in the eyes of young people, that is the blessing of the young and hope for this nation and church. All honor, glory, and power belong to the Lord alone.
I live for the Lord, work for the Lord, suffer for the Lord, and will die for the Lord.
I live for the Lord, work for the Lord, suffer for the Lord, and will die for the Lord~
Risk your life for the Lord. Shallow sins and temptations cannot be your match. One who says 'I will die for the Lord' cannot be moved by trivial worldly pleasures. To love Jesus is to die for Jesus.
Wanting too much is called greed. If you are faithful to your original purpose, you will find rest. For Him who came to bear the cross, every moment is a gift and a wonder.

The Lord never commanded you to read the Bible or pray daily. Of course, daily devotion is undoubtedly very beneficial. He only said to bear your cross daily. What is the evidence of bearing your cross daily? It is that there is life transformation around you. For Paul too, what was daily was a cross-bearing life of self-giving. Luke 9:23 Then He said to them all, 'If anyone would come after Me, let him deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow Me.' 1 Corinthians 15:31 Brothers, I assure you by the boasting in Christ Jesus our Lord that I die daily.

The Lord is omnipresent (ubiquitous). That means He is with me now and works to the extent I have surrendered my heart to Him. Joseph, who was conscious of the Lord, overcame the temptation of sin, did not despair, and did not seek revenge.

The Lord humbled Himself and rejoiced, and with that joy came into the world, became a grain of wheat, served until death, and left much fruit. The biblical definition of pride is, as Satan exemplified, 'not keeping one's place.' Unless I deeply root humility and become thoroughly lowered with the Lord as my all, I can never exalt the Lord.
The Lord searches. And He asks. Who am I? Respond to that. Show me through your earnest desire, affection, and choice. As a person thinks in their heart, so is their life.
The Lord searches. He calls me. The Lord is my Lord and the only One worthy of praise.

Knowing we should look to the Lord yet being unable to look is great pain in youth. The reason is simple: it is because I do not regard the values I uphold and cannot lose as 'filth.' Unless you abhor and hate worldly recognition, attention from people, the pride of being successful in relationships, and sinful entertainments that briefly delight me, you cannot serve both these desires and Christ. ...That I may gain Him and be found in Him, having regarded everything else as loss and filth... —This is the crystallization of faith confession of one who lived humbly with the Lord all their life.
When you do not look to the Lord, even with much you feel empty, and though competent you feel only frustration. Let go of everything and look to the beautiful Lord.
To let go of something to hold to the Lord is the greatest investment.

The Lord's call is not to please people or gain popularity. The Lord's call is solely to become holy like His holiness and resemble Christ. (Romans 13:14) Rather, clothe yourselves with the Lord Jesus Christ, and do not think about how to gratify the desires of the flesh.
When I interpret my life with the Lord's eyes, I see opportunities I did not see before.
When interpreted through the Lord's will, despair becomes hope. What the caterpillar calls death, the Lord calls a butterfly.

The Lord's people can never be inherently selfish. Because the gospel is entirely altruistic. Even if an apple tree bears apples, the tree does not eat the apples. Others eat and rejoice. Yet the Owner treasures that tree deeply and sanctifies it further.
One who holds to the Lord's mission is free from the temptation of sin and can keep himself.

Looking only to the Lord's glory and abandoning selfish motives does not happen automatically. The purification time, like the removal of self-ambition from Joseph's dream, varies for each person. Moses spent 40 years as a shepherd, Paul 12 years in the wilderness, Jacob almost a lifetime. I pray you be free from the effort to prove yourself. Jesus could have turned stones into bread to prove Himself, but He relied entirely on the Father's Word and waited only for His evaluation.

When you earnestly long for the Lord's voice, as soon as you open your eyes early in the morning, you think first of the Lord's beauty. Faith is more about the heart than appearance. If you begin the dawn thinking of the Lord, your heart runs following in love for the Lord. See Psalm 119:55.
If my rights are surrendered before the Lord's altar, God will grant incomparably greater privileges.
If the Lord becomes my King and I have decided to praise only Him forever, what more could be a problem? All problems and trials are compressed into one question: 'Who is King?'
Two things the Lord requests: be humble and serve without a name. Look only to the Lord. Amen~
Being called by the Lord alone is infinitely precious. It is truly glorious for me to live in faith with such precious people. Let us glorify the gospel.

A person called by the Lord, who runs forward unconditionally to the Lord's call, who becomes the Lord's joy, for whom no other joy suffices except the Lord, whom the Lord makes alive, who honors only the Lord whether living or dying—may you become such a person even walking this path alone...
Without the 'Lord,' we could never have gone so far. Without 'us,' the Lord would not have accompanied. The Lord and we are one.

Even when you say the Lord is everything, the Lord is never deceived. If it could all be accomplished by words, who couldn't do it? A person for whom the Lord is everything is not disappointed by evaluation. Because the Lord is everything.

I pray the Lord is precious to you. Blessed is the person whose concern and pursuit becomes the Lord. Glorious is the person for whom other values become like rags before the Lord, who is so precious.
To do the work the Lord commanded, for that purpose I am here.

If the Lord has not permitted it, I cannot do anything. If the Lord tells me to guard this position, I will not move and remain faithful even for eternity. Because the Lord is the Lord. When the Lord has not permitted something, it means to stake your life on what He has permitted. I belong to the Lord.
* Performance or Promise? "Among those who put money in the offering box today, this poor widow has put in more than all the others." Faith is not Performance but Promise.

While doing the Lord's work, I feel much lack. At the same time, it is glorious to walk the path of faith with precious people. Meeting you is a great honor to me. Serving beautiful souls is an undeserved honor. My head bows humbly.

* The Heart God does not look at specs or reputation, but at the heart, because He seeks someone after His own heart. This is so that God's will can be fully accomplished on this earth. "I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after my own heart, who will do all my will." -Acts 13:22 "But the Lord said to Samuel, 'Do not look at his appearance or his height, for I have rejected him. The Lord does not see as man sees; man looks at the outward appearance, but the Lord looks at the heart.'" -1 Samuel 16:7
Lord, I came with empty hands and will return with empty hands. Though I possess nothing, Jesus dwells in my heart. Thank You, Lord.

Become an undefeated life. One or two times you may win by luck, many times by skill. But even Olympic gold medalists cannot always win, and in many fields and circumstances, people must be satisfied with merely surviving. However, God's servant David had no one who could oppose him throughout his life, and he was never defeated in all his wars. In terms of probability, it's 0%, and we can call it a miracle. 1. David was a man gripped by calling. When he saw Goliath, he did not go out calculating, but with the zeal and righteous indignation the Lord poured into his heart. God grants power to those who hold fast to their calling. If you do not fulfill your calling, you cannot die. It is not that I hold fast to the calling; the calling holds me and carries me forward.

Sunday Message Part 2 2. David was after God's heart. The Pharisees were rebuked because their hearts were far from God. In the expression of Deacon Stephen, David was the standard of life, one with a matching heart, God's Chaser, after His heart. God is not looking for talented and capable people, but for those who can obey. 3. David was a man of trust. To trust in God appears in worldly terms as abandonment. When he boldly abandoned position, popularity, and people's gaze, God protected him. In contrast, Saul, seeking to preserve his kingship, feared people, oppressed them, and killed them. There is only scattering. David did not take revenge on his enemies. He had all the ability, heart, passion, and infrastructure to build the temple, but he possessed the power of 'stop'—the power of obedience.
Mr. Bean - Part 2 Plain Faith Without MSG (Flavor Enhancer)

I told you on Sunday to be zealous for holiness. In areas where you have no zeal, things go as they may or are done reluctantly. What you pursue can be easily recognized. When you wake up in the morning, whom or what do you think about? If you do not think of Jesus, you are not a servant of Christ.

Sunday Youth Message: Self-esteem - ? Self-esteem comes not from how I see myself or how others see me, but from how God sees 'me.' 1. Be simple. (You must know what you are doing, concentrate, pursue purpose and values require courage) Don't enter wrestling just because you aimed to be MC Kang Ho-dong. Stop the glitter and experience the power of simplicity. A life like decorative flowers wilts. A life conscious of others—someone spending two hours on makeup before coming to church, a life spending 90% of one's ability proving oneself—has no vision. Temperance is the power of the Holy Spirit.
To overcome what you fear most, you need the strongest being. The most feared object is yourself. That is why the strongest Lord holds me.
What remains at the moment of death is only 'relationship with God.' This is called 'righteousness.' It means faith.
For those to whom death becomes a reward, no tribulation or persecution in this world can prevent them. Because what lives within them is not desire and lusts, but Christ, death itself becomes gain.

When surveyed about dreams, more than half of middle and high school students chose employment at famous major companies. We are producing people who march in step with others at similar pace. The greatest advantage of a Christian is nerve. Creativity comes from there. Be bold.
What matters is not being respected, but whether I am honoring the Lord right now.
When the source of joy changes, it becomes holy. When it becomes holy, the subject of joy changes. To indiscriminately pursue pleasure becomes sin.

That I am who I am now is not because of gender, education level, family background, or family history, but solely by God's grace. That means the ability to live more beautifully and excellently already exists within me. Grace does not only mean going to heaven after death. Grace is power, and grace is 'fullness of Jesus,' the Lord giving Himself directly to us.

Whether I am receiving love as vast as a mountain or as tiny as a sesame seed now is because my faults have not been fully exposed, or the other person has loved me including my hidden weaknesses. God's covering of the faults of the deficient and showing them mercy is all His boundless grace.

The people given to me now and the work I serve are the best things given at this moment. God always gives the very best. Realizing this is calling, and those living without calling will live most miserably the life that should be happiest. "And God saw all that he had made, and it was very good." -Genesis 1:31

I can do what I can do right now. If I die and live by Jesus, I will experience many souls coming alive. The problem is not 'others' but 'me.' If I live the life the Lord desires, I will behold the Lord's glory. Rather than trying to satisfy my decaying, sickly self with perishable things, blessed is the person who is satisfied with the Lord alone, the true Truth.

I am the kingdom of God where the Lord's rule comes. Through me, the community I belong to will be blessed, and I am the source of blessing through which the world is blessed. When I advance, God's kingdom comes there.
The true meaning of a leader is that one has learned obedience.
Knowledge puffs up, but love covers a multitude of sins.

When a water main pipe buried underground loses water pressure, underground water seeps through corroded cracks in the pipe. So after the water is cut off, when water supply resumes, muddy water comes out. If I am not filled with Jesus and take no measures, I will become a person supplying muddy water to those expecting living water. Maintain holy pressure. "Therefore, holy brothers and sisters, who share in the heavenly calling, fix your thoughts on Jesus, whom we acknowledge as our apostle and high priest." -Hebrews 3:1

Position and title, past career do not prove who I am. More than knowledge of truth, zeal, and many activities, Jesus valued 'first love' above all. Priority and motive reveal who I am. "Yet I hold this against you: You have forsaken the love you had at first. Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first. If you do not repent, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place." -Revelation 2:4-5
While working and studying, it may feel burdensome to do the Lord's work simultaneously. But later, you realize that living without doing so and enjoying the world is even harder.

If you truly want to save lives, truly want to save lives, then pay the price and unite with the Lord. John the Baptist, the greatest figure throughout the Old Testament, concentrated his possessions and lifestyle in one direction. "John wore clothing made of camel's hair, with a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey." -Mark 1:6
A true Christian realizes that 'holiness like the Lord' is more joyful than 'personal happiness.' Faith is not complicated. Whom do you love more?
True happiness is not human satisfaction, wealth or honor, or the envious gaze of others. Happiness comes from above. It is the grace that makes me who I am.

The real and fake are revealed when damaged, when tested. Sandalwood releases more fragrance when cut. Artificial flowers seem lovely but lack the honey to give and sacrifice from the beginning. What is real does not fade, and its subtle lingering continues.
Deep love makes distance and time irrelevant. What should be broken yet becomes meaningful and continues because of Jesus. Who can separate us from God's love in Christ Jesus?

The characteristic of earthen vessels is that the vessel itself has no intrinsic value, is easily broken, and has no options beyond its sole function of holding. Filling it completely with treasure is victory. Everything else can be entrusted under His sovereignty.
If you cling, you cannot discern. True discernment only comes when you are dead. Before that, everything done is not discernment but noble criticism and prejudice.
Do not cling; concentrate instead. Abandon idols and love the Lord.
Clinging means there is dissatisfaction. Unfulfilled desire. It is unfulfilled. This is the typical symptom that appears when you do not find satisfaction in the Lord and do not live within Him.
Some cling and desire to achieve their will, while others surrender to the cross to gain salvation and life.

Do not be chased. The devil does not chase us; rather, we are called to chase them. We will expose all falsehood and deception and burn them in Jesus' name. "And I tell you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of Hades will not overcome it." -Matthew 16:18 *Hades: the power of death, *gates: defense, *overcome: to be overcome. In the original language, it means aggressively breaking through the gates of demonic forces—not defense but offense.

When you hire a car, you must fill it exactly with what you used. Of course, there is no need to add more, so you fill it just right. But with your own car, you simply fill it full. You are not driving it for just a day or two, right? Fill your soul abundantly with grace and live a life overflowing. Since you will travel this one lifetime anyway...

Just as a warning light comes on when a car has no oil, when the Lord dims in the heart, an emotion warning light comes on. Rather than ignoring or removing those warnings, when pain comes, you must welcome the Lord. Faith is loving Jesus with all your heart. Open your heart and confirm: Is Jesus at the center?

When you become a true worshiper, God responds with 'questions.' What do you want Me to do for you? The thousandth worshiper, Solomon, makes an amazing request. Because through worship, his purpose was purified.
Worldly wisdom teaches to highlight strengths and hide weaknesses. But faith challenges us to boast in weakness instead and testify to God's power being made perfect in it.
When first following Jesus, you fear nothing, and nothing on that path toward the Lord can stop you.

What I learned when I first believed in Jesus was that I must continually advance toward Jesus, the goal. Though my choices are limited and there is some collapse, I must keep rising and running forward. Even if I fall, I fall forward so I can grasp the Lord's feet...
When you go to heaven, you will be surprised. That you are not a victim but have arrived there through the Lord's mercy.

The kingdom of heaven is like this: selling all you own to buy a field where treasure is hidden. People say you are crazy, but it is not madness; it is seeing higher value. The treasure buried in the field (Jesus) is not visible because of other worldly pleasures and desires.

If ten million people call me an angel, who am I? A person. If ten million people call me a devil, who am I? Still a person. Then why am I shaken so much by others' evaluations? Because I am not looking simply at the Lord.

Trying to receive the love of a thousand people alone will leave you lonely and empty. But if one person loves a thousand people, you will be honored and happy. Life is not about accumulating but scattering and giving.

Youth will inevitably fade and disappear. But the impressions and tears of that time remaining in the heart do not disappear. That is why the young must sow. Even if weeping, even if falling and stumbling and being pushed aside, they must crawl and sow. In youth, success is not success and failure is not failure. "Those who sow with tears will reap with songs of joy. Those who go out weeping, carrying seed to sow, will return with songs of joy, carrying sheaves with them." -Psalm 126:5-6

That someone calls me an angel does not make me an angel, and being called a devil does not make me evil. The reason Paul did not care about people's evaluation was because he looked to the only Judge. In fact, people often become indignant not because of justice but because of inferiority within themselves. Different standards do not provoke reaction. "I care very little if I am judged by you or by any human court; indeed, I do not even judge myself..." -1 Corinthians 4:3 -> You do your best. "Therefore judge nothing before the appointed time; wait until the Lord comes..." -1 Corinthians 4:5 -> Do not overstate.
Young people, stand at the forefront. Dedicate yourselves to the breakthrough mission to prepare the way for the Lord's coming.

Not fearing pointed criticism and not finding joy in recognition—forgetting what lies behind and pressing toward the goal for the prize to which God has called us in Christ Jesus. I press on toward the goal for the prize of God's upward calling in Christ Jesus. —Philippians 3:14
The greatest gift for a minister concerning the flock is personal holiness before the Lord.
When you do your best, confidence is born. This is why someone who has read extensively can do better than someone in favorable circumstances.
Loyalty is not selecting people who please me and keeping them by my side. Because we are called to love, we fix our eyes only on the Lord and cover all people with the heart He gives us.
Praise tests a person. You are tested whether you become proud from receiving more praise or jealous from receiving less.
Praise refines a person. God seeks those who, when lifted up, can immediately humble themselves and serve.
