Whether holding the hilt of the sword or the blade in a critical situation, a Christian's attitude is always consistent. We do not become arrogant in favorable circumstances or despair in unfavorable ones. Only the Lord establishes and leads me.

Kathryn Kuhlman, after performing miracles and mighty works, would weep backstage, wrestling with pride. She did this to fight against arrogance. Once you become busy and famous, you can become nothing in heaven. Please humble yourselves.

Runny-nosed children boast about winning jacks and Pokémon cards. As they grow, they boast about computers, mobile phones, and pretty things. But strangely, when adults, they boast about drinking well, mixing drinks skillfully, and miscellaneous information about alcohol. Boasting reveals one's level. Those who boast about alcohol compensate for inferiority complexes about power and status. Woe to those who are mighty at drinking wine and valiant at mixing strong drinks. —Isaiah 5:22. Human nature pursues power. (Der Wille zur Macht) —Nietzsche

Do not struggle from the evaluation and reaction of those who do not know you well. Rather, be happy because of the One who created you, knows you, and calls you. Faith is a battle of sight and focus.

Even if you praise greatly, confess that you love much, and receive much training, if your heart is not continuously drawn to Jesus's presence, you have lost 'that love.' You must realize that loving Jesus is not merely about doing something. Even if our labor is great, without a burning love for the Lord, it is merely emptiness. Moving forward for a deep relationship with the Lord in each moment demands a certain cost from me.

The greatest comfort for a Christian is that the Lord knows my heart. If you do not have a heart willing to lose everything to gain Him alone, then everything becomes a problem and a test. Make your purpose clear; your master will be determined.

When the object changes, the approach changes. Let us look to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith. He, for the joy set before him, endured the cross, scorning its shame, and sat down at the right hand of God's throne. —Hebrews 12:2 → Elementary Internet Version: Let us look to Jesus, the ultimate and final boss of faith. He, for the awesome reward before Him, disregarded the cross and totally ignored the shame, and became the ultimate ruler at the right hand of God's throne.
A Christian is a person who cannot be bought. Money, fame, greed, or lust cannot buy them. Every deal has already been completed by the Lord on the cross. I die with the Lord and live again in Him.
A Christian does not need to pretend to be strong. Once you are found in the Lord, you automatically become strong.
A Christian does not live by competition but by response. Competition requires endless comparison and attack, but prayer saves me and saves others too.

A Christian works by 'identity.' It is not the 'me' I think of but the 'me' whose old self died and was resurrected through the cross. In the Lord, my 'self' never crumbles before any challenge or external pressure. I hope you believe this and hold your head high.
A Christian is not a being with ambiguous words. Forever, I have only one thing to say: the blood of Jesus.
A Christian's true and complete ability is not to point out but to bear the cross at that very place. God's solution is always people. Revival and repentance begin with me.

The way a Christian lives in the world is not the old way. It is not the way of unbelievers. It is not living in crisis, thinking that once you are pushed back, you are finished. In the midst of wolves, we live as sheep. Then you will experience the Lord's rod and staff comforting you. Do not become a wolf; you are a sheep walking with the Lord.

Simply, the Lord gives two ways for a Christian to exercise influence. One is 'holiness' and the other is 'honesty.' Even if 'holiness' collapses in some area, if you at least honestly face it, repentance occurs.
Living truthfully in my own way is far more meaningful and happy than succeeding and living well in someone else's image. It is a blessing that he prospers and I decrease.

The prodigal son leaves home and his father for a better life. Perhaps he rationalized that he could adequately balance the freedom of deviation with a compromised faith. Only in the pigsty does he realize that compromise that began for happiness leads to greater unhappiness. A faith that has lost holiness and saltiness becomes emptier and more distant the more it pursues something. The further you rationalize, the farther you are from the path to return. The Lord does not compromise.

A transparent life has nothing to worry about. There is nothing to calculate. Because as it is, it is sufficiently beautiful, so without adding or subtracting, I race the path the Lord has given and focus toward the goal.

Especially to a mother, the Lord gives an earnest, loving heart for the child because raising a life requires such a great cost and sacrifice. To shed those tears of great sacrifice with joy and not grow weary, 'that love' is necessary. Whoever receives the Father's heart releases heaven's miracles to this earth.

A life that was at peace but somehow becomes fearful, like walking on top of a prison wall, so precarious that one mistake seems to lead to irreversible danger. Safety in this precarious life consists of two things: 1. There is absolutely no condemnation in Jesus, and 2. Firmly believing that truth and having the ability to completely disregard it. This is sufficient.

What is more important than reputation is the Lord's will and help. It is not the world's reputation that matters; history changes because of one prepared person, like Hezekiah. The Lord prepares people and expects from them.

After the exile and return, what was reflected upon through regret was not system improvement. It was that the restoration of worship and people, the community they belong to, and the leaders were important. What must be prioritized even for individuals is believing in the blood of the cross and the holiness of abiding in the Lord. An unexpected breakthrough will come.

To exercise violence means one is weak. Thus, one tries to suppress the other to overcome that inferiority. All harsh words and violence contain the desire to prove oneself. That much means you lack confidence. Those who truly possess authority and power are gentle. Because they no longer need to prove themselves. In the Lord, no further proof is needed; we only testify to the Lord.

The gospel Paul really wanted to convey to the Romans, in terms of today's situation, is this: At a class reunion after a long time, standing next to a classmate boasting that her husband's company has entered the top 100, standing next to a classmate who is congratulated by everyone for her husband becoming a government minister through politics, standing before a classmate beaming with joy that her son or daughter graduated from a prestigious university and received a Ph.D. from Harvard, standing before a pastor's wife classmate grateful to God that her husband became the senior pastor of a large church—I publicly boast that I have wagered my entire life on Jesus Christ who died on the cross. There is nothing to boast of except the cross of Christ.

David, who was after God's own heart—through him God promises to fulfill all His purposes. (Acts 13:22) Is that not marvelous? A person of faith after God's heart who fulfills all His purposes—can I become such a person too? Why not me? David was a man of repentance. In Psalm 51, he sought two things in his repentance, which show what a person after God's heart is like. 1. Do not cast me from Your presence or take Your Holy Spirit from me. I do not desire my honor, money, status, or vested rights. I only want You, Lord. 2. Do not take Your Holy Spirit from me. Your presence is my life. The greatest judgment is Your absence, so I desperately cling to Your presence. Have you stumbled? Become such a person. The cross is the conclusion.
Always, in every moment, there is a question I ask: What do I want that I am here? The answer is always the same: 'I want to see the Lord's glory!'

I am here only because of the Lord. That is why I can overcome all things and am not bothered. Before people's voices and spiritual challenges, and sometimes in situations of collapse I cannot bear, I can still laugh. Because the Lord is my only vision.

What is important before God is what kind of people you are and how you live. The worship you offer must be worship where you seek truth in your spirit. The Father seeks people just like that—people who worship simply and honestly as they are before Him. (John 4:23, The Message Bible)

The only authenticity of belonging to God is 'loving.' When you believe in Jesus, God dwells within you, and the most powerful characteristic of that 'indwelling' is loving. This is what reveals the authenticity of faith. When people ask Jesus about eternal life, He teaches them to love their neighbor. To love is to belong to God. 1 John 4:7... Love belongs to God.
To live a life recognized by God, you must form distinct habits from youth. Daniel makes the resolve not to drink wine from childhood.
Give your heart to God. Due to infinite happiness, you will dislike enjoying other sins. Those who give their whole heart have the happiness of their whole heart.

To know what I respond to, I dig into the roots of my distress. When I actually discover it, it is truly shameful to reveal. A person does not become jealous of a dog. Because the level does not match.

When God designed me, He did not make me so that 'I alone could do well.' I was made to express my highest value when I become one in the Lord, when I pray for others and am prayed for in the Lord. It is rather in becoming a being that can do nothing without 'grace.'

When God called me, He already knew all my foolishness and folly, but He did not despise me; He changed me into wisdom and power. This is entirely so that I become everything in Jesus Christ, put on Christ, manifest Christ, and live by Christ. 'Rather, clothe yourselves with the Lord Jesus Christ, and do not think about how to gratify the desires of the flesh.' —Romans 13:14

If God were to paint our image on a canvas, He would paint it so beautifully. He loved us not because we are without faults but because He loves us; He covers everything else. I hope you will take down the self-portrait you painted and hang the image the Lord has drawn for you. The Lord sees no fault in Jacob nor trouble in Israel. The Lord their God is with them. The shout of the King is among them. —Numbers 23:21 Come now, let us reason together, says the Lord. Though your sins are like scarlet, they shall be as white as snow; though they are red as crimson, they shall be like wool. —Isaiah 1:18
What God desires is not my 'success' but my 'transformation.'

That God distinguished us among all the people of the earth and gave us special grace is His presence with His people. Walking with God, His presence is the greatest sign and blessing. Be holy. By the power of the blood.
When you look only to God, people's evaluation becomes colorless. With one word—'God knows'—you would not even spare your life.

When you look only to God, people's evaluation becomes colorless, and you are freed from all eyes. Receiving recognition does not make you higher, nor does being despised make you lower. Everything comes only from the Lord.

God reveals our hidden darkness through pain and suffering. It is difficult for what is hidden to be revealed, but when we come before the Lord in holiness and completeness, we also cannot avoid it. We become whole to the depth of our pain.
God did not call me to become an outstanding person. He desires me to become a whole person. United in belief and understanding. —Ephesians 4:13

God comes to me when I hit bottom and reminds me of 'my existence.' Then He renews me. When I know who I am, how I was saved, what price was paid, and what love I received, I gain the strength to stand again.

God does not immediately demand that I become a perfect person. That will take much time. He only desires honesty. To be honest, you only need decision. A prostitute and a tax collector can be honest. I am a prostitute. I am a tax collector. But the Pharisee was not and acted hypocritically. Jesus's evaluation separates at that point.

God sees David differently. It is because David's purpose was different from others. Even while being pursued by enemies, and even when he stepped down from the throne and faced the threat of death from his son, what he truly longed for was worship offered in the temple—meeting God Himself. When the object of longing changes, that grace was not withdrawn even after thousands of years.

Before God met David, He had no particular intention to establish a king through that household or send the Messiah. Through Abraham's blessing reaching David, God establishes his descendants as kings through the 'Covenant made with David' and ultimately brings forth the Messiah. I pray that you become a person whose united heart creates thoughts that did not exist before.

God does not evaluate people by the importance of their assigned work. He does not ask which organization you belong to or what title you hold. He desires to see how you respond before Him and what kind of person you stand as.
When God uses a person, He does not assign present work based on past skills. David was young and inexperienced, but he loved the Lord and confessed that He was his strength.
God does not want you to remain as a person whose heart collapses at another's expression. He desires only that you become more sensitive to the Lord's joy.

God created us 'absolutely.' We are not relative beings. Therefore, we must not compare. When you compare at work or school, you suddenly become extremely unhappy. Only the Lord determines my path. Be bold.

God desires that we rule the earth (Genesis 1), cultivate the earth (Chapter 2), and bless the earth (Chapter 12). He also desires that we make disciples and transform the world (Matthew 28). We go deep into the world but are not corrupted; rather, we participate through the power of the Holy Spirit and as experts with biblical principles, called to be God's representatives changing the world. Become a Spirit-filled and wise professional.
God gave us people to love and resources to use. However, people often love material things and use people instead.

God does not fulfill our selfish desires. Jesus came as the Suffering Servant, and though sinless, He came as the Suffering Servant in our place not to wash away sin and sanctify us, but because He is not a servant for us to command as we wish. Rather, we must stand as holy servants serving Jesus. Just as the Lord did, we must be transformed through anointing and purity into a holy life.
God has given us something small but already precious. If we cannot be satisfied with what is ours, we become depressed or angry. Eventually, we fall into sin and face great shame.

God is not troubled by the corruption of institutional systems. He leaves 7,000 who did not bow to Baal and Gideon's 300. God will use many professionals as missionaries. All are priests like kings. If the Lord called you, do not limit yourself by saying you are a layperson.
Seek the Lord so that the dry place becomes holy. When you fill your weaknesses and shortcomings with Jesus, you become spiritually strong.

That God's rule came upon Egypt's court, which did not know God, and Babylon's offices, which served idols, is because there were Joseph and Daniel, moved by God's Spirit and walking with the Lord. People who belong not to the world yet are not separated, who are not corrupted but instead bring transformation. —John 17:14
The tabernacle that houses God, the earthen vessel that houses Jesus, is strong.

A person who truly worships God in spirit becomes a new person. When they see God and behold God's glory, they abandon their sinful perspective and take on the mind that the Lord has. Therefore, 'those who fail in worship' will certainly 'fail in relationships.' Only through worship can you discover your true self and be free from hatred and misunderstanding.
Those with passionate love for God have already built an invisible temple without constructing a physical one. You are the temple. Ephesians 2:22 When people meet you, God's light shines upon them.
God's concern is not the techniques and abilities with which I handle problems but the attitude with which I face them.
A person full of God's power is not hastily chased. Rather, it is a life that gives challenges and pursues.
When you understand God's will, the dry rod-like life in my hand becomes God's staff. Joseph was not so much a prisoner as he was positioned at the shortest distance from Pharaoh's palace.

God's Word prepares me, purifies me, comforts me, transforms me, and empowers me. Thus, it makes me 'God's letter sent to the world.' You are a letter of Christ produced through us, written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts. —2 Corinthians 3:3 You need no introduction or recommendation letter. Look at the morning dews that came with togetherness.

A person who feels God's pain can never go astray. Jonah did not know God's heart toward Nineveh, and the elder brother of the prodigal did not know his father's heart. Ultimately, God's love overcomes all, but there cannot be love that does not understand that pain.

A person who can lose everything is strong. If you resolve to lose everything except Jesus, everything suddenly becomes easy. If you cling, you will be robbed; but if you let go and gain the Lord, He will give you all you need.
God's grace and love do not wound us. That you receive wounds frequently, well, and more excellently than others means that immeasurable love has become exceptionally thin.

Those who know God's character will understand that God never speaks through condemnation. God does not threaten or coerce. The devil tries to deceive us with condemnation, but we are under the Lord's blood.
When you try to live by faith, you truly experience death. You do not pretend to die but die completely and are buried. Then you are born anew.

God's people are never mass-produced. That young Mary did not say to the angel, 'Let me consult with my mother' but readily said 'Amen' means she was a person who had undergone tremendous refinement. Do not marvel only at the conclusion but receive grace within the process.

God's work cannot be done by position, spiritual maturity, or background. Just as animals live by instinct and beasts survive by wildness, Christians live solely by the spirituality of gratitude and awe.

Trying to do God's work by yourself is pride, and leaving your work to God is indulgence. People like this have no repentance. Without repentance, you cannot meet God. Repentance means laying yourself down before the cross.
God called me not to cling but to focus. To anxiously focus on what He has not given is attachment, and to come to Jesus Christ is focus.

Those called by God have respect in relationships and respect those worthy of respect. However, this does not mean seeking people's joy. If you sought people's joy, you would not be God's servant. Will you please people or please God?
The standard by which God uses people is not usefulness or competence but 'self-surrender.'

If God told you one day that He would give you great reward and comfort, your expectations would truly be great. You would freely imagine—nice cars and houses, wonderful spouses, and so on. But if that great reward and comfort were the Lord Himself given to you, would you be disappointed? Or would you rejoice more? If you say 'Ugh!' when He says He will give Himself, you are far away.

Even with much, it is only anxious as if living in another's house. It is because it is not what is given to me but my greed. Believe in and look to Jesus, and the peace of heaven comes. Jesus is the ultimate already fully given to me. The person seeking only the Lord is the most peaceful and strong.

What God desires is not competence. He alone is already ineffably competent. He desires faithfulness. He considers laziness evil. Fulfill your mission. Your mission will hold you and carry you forward.
God gave it. He lent it. When you mistake temporary 'right of use' for eternal 'ownership,' you immediately become unhappy. Ownership makes you compare; use makes you wise.
Hold the heart God seeks. It is a broken heart. The Lord seeks those who know they are spiritually poor and know how to mourn.

'Pride' impersonates, taking glory as if you had done it in God's place, and 'discouragement' falsely reports, denying and not living as if the living God had died. *Violation of the law on identity fraud and false reporting.

Jesus received all authority in heaven and earth, and through the cross, He reclaimed all the rights that Adam lost. Yet because the Lord sits at the right hand of majesty, the rule and governance on this earth must naturally be exercised by us, His body. Rise boldly and exercise the power to reign as kings in life through Jesus. When I call, the Father is bound to answer. That is the covenant, that is the promise, and that is the power of the gospel.
The Lord, greater than the highest heavens, desires my small and lowly heart.

Jesus brought all the blessings of heaven. When His body was broken, the blessings of the kingdom poured out. That God's Son, piercing darkness and coming, and destroying the devil's works, was marked by lowliness—wrapped in swaddling clothes and lying in a manger. The mark by which Christians overcome in the world and disarm the devil is lowliness.

Those who have learned the secrets of heaven never compete. 'Let the lowly believer boast of his high position. But let the rich boast of his low position, because he will pass away like a wild flower.' James 1:9-10

Not stopping is power. Rather than focusing on the many reasons why you should stop, just run toward the goal. Why? Because the goal is Jesus, who from the throne of heaven never stopped reaching down to sinful me.

If a president or king of a nation felt unhappy because he lacked a Chupa Chups lollipop, it would mean he is not a king but a runny-nosed child playing king in an alley. The Bible calls me a priest-king. Do not let deficiency become unhappiness; let level of understanding become the solution.

A rich young man ran away from Jesus. The reason was not because of his weakness but precisely because of his strength. 'The young man heard this and went away sad, because he had great wealth.' —Matthew 19:22

The transformation of one person not only changes a family and friends but also serves as a pillar in the body of the church. Moreover, you will realize that the one person you met once and worship had the highest value to you. That means later you will realize that this worship and co-laborers given to me by measure were truly the worship of my dreams and the people I love, and regret that you could not give more. Therefore, love so hotly that you cannot give more. Now.
Being called a single grain of wheat does not mean you are sent to judge or analyze others. The calling of a grain of wheat is to perish. Poof~

When afraid, you self-defend, and in doing so, through competition, avoidance, jealousy, victim mentality, compulsion, anger, and guilt, defense does not happen but rather causes deep harm. When you die on the cross with Christ, you no longer need to defend yourself, and conversely, weakness and death become beneficial. This happens when you always trust and hope in the Lord.

When you feel endlessly shabby, or when nothing but a sigh comes at 'what can you expect from someone like me?', look to the glorious Lord and that I am in that Lord. If the devil causes you to remember your past, remind the devil of the devil's future.
Every single soul is not just created. I am proud of you.

A working person was employed at an insurance company. Due to severe salary cuts and restructuring, his mind was scattered with worry and anxiety about the future. The world's standard of recession destroyed his peace. A mother named 'Lea Miller' left a memo for her son in such a state: 'There is nothing more precious than Jesus the Lord; it cannot be exchanged for all the riches of this world.' This son, 'Beverly Shears,' received a great shock and turned to compose a hymn: 'There is nothing more precious than Jesus the Lord; it cannot be exchanged for all the riches of this world'... 'Forsaking all the world's pleasures, discarding all the world's boasts, there is nothing more precious than Jesus the Lord; there is only Jesus.' —Hymn 102 When the order (Priority) of God changes to Dog.
GOD to DOG.

Koreans are willing to immigrate for their children's education. Yet they are so busy supporting them that they are stingy with actually spending time with their children and building relationships. It's like cooking rice only to give it to dogs. What is more precious than academic achievement is the child as a living being, in themselves.

There was a time when Korean churches claimed 12 million believers, and now we see 6.5 million. Even that seems somewhat exaggerated. Large churches still endure, but they are easy targets for internet and media criticism. So people say the Korean church is in crisis. Yet I say there is hope. Because God is preparing diaspora youth who have not bowed their knees to Baal. I see the precious blood of the cross in your eyes. I see the hope of the home church. In the bondage of Egypt, I see one Moses. Don't just lament and criticize—become Moses yourself.

Coming to Korea, I think this way: I will not stop but pursue what I have been seized by in Jesus the Lord, straining toward the goal. I abstain from everything else and exercise self-control. When the purpose is clear...

In traditional Korean wedding ceremonies, there is something called 'nap-chae'—a ritual to first inform both families' ancestral spirits when two households unite through marriage. The Israelites, through 400 years of slavery and 40 years in the wilderness, became incompetent wanderers with no competitiveness. To live in Canaan, like in Korean wedding ceremonies, they would have needed to exchange and worship the gods and idols that the inhabitants believed in to enable trade and learn agricultural expertise. But God said, 'Do not live this way.' He wanted them to gather a little less, receive a little less recognition, endure being despised, and stubbornly hold fast only to God's word. The God who rescued them from Egypt and the wilderness would also rescue them in Canaan, and through this, make them a channel of blessing to all peoples. Because Israel feared people, flattered them, and even tolerated the idols they served, Israel perished in Canaan. You must truly believe that when the Lord exalts, none can humble, and when He humbles, none can exalt. The Lord alone is my strength.
I am a person infinitely lacking, a person who needs more forgiveness, a person who desperately needs Jesus.
I should not try to exceed my limits but remain in Jesus. Jesus does not fill in our limits. It is His presence, not achievement.
When chocolate receives sunlight it melts, but mud hardens. Some people grow stronger facing the same trials. Wait just a little longer, until the blade is sharp and gleaming.
I did not believe in Jesus to be happy. Rather, I had to abandon and give up many things. Yet I was truly happy. Because after losing, the true value became clearer. There is nothing but Jesus.

Dropping the burden of devotion may seem to guarantee much productivity. Soon you will see how the leisure gained by abandoning faithfulness disappears so meaninglessly. If performance and efficiency were the goal, God would have made us terminators or cattle. Faith is not efficiency but relationship. Please, stay with the Lord.

When hairstyle changes, a person becomes completely different. Among facial features, the eyes occupy ninety percent. Yet such artificial changes quickly become tedious. True transformation is through the gospel, where Jesus becomes precious and all other values become garbage—this is a revaluation of values.

In modern times, there are countless teachings and many expressions. Each presents their own interpretations and solutions to many difficult problems. How did believers in the past confess and move forward with this?: I will lay down my life for the Lord, I have nothing to boast of—without suffering, I fear nothing in Jesus. Before martyrs, there are no problems. For one who treasures their life, everything is a problem.
Hold on. Keeping your place is holiness.

The best female figure skater in the world today is Kim Yuna. Asada Mao is a good athlete, but she cannot be the best. Why? Because she has the same dream as the world's best, Kim Yuna. 'Let's beat Kim Yuna!'—Kim Yuna; 'Let's beat Kim Yuna!'—Asada Mao. Let each one examine their own work, and then they can take pride in themselves alone, not in another. Galatians 6:4

Joseph's brothers wanted to kill him when they recognized him not as their brother but merely as 'father's son'—'your son.' But when Joseph forgave his wounded brothers as family, God's salvation came upon that man. The people I place beside me, my fellow workers, are like angels sent by God Himself to love. When those people become 'my brother' instead of 'your son,' restoration, healing, and salvation occur. It is not that love comes through forgiveness, but forgiveness comes through love. Love with all your heart the people God has placed beside you. That is what God earnestly desires and requests of me at this very moment. But when this son of yours who has squandered your property with prostitutes comes home, you kill the fattened calf for him!'—Luke 15:30. The older brother's perception of the prodigal. They took the ornamented robe back to their father and said, 'We found this. Examine it to see whether it is your son's robe.'—Genesis 37:32. The brothers' perception of Joseph. He is not ashamed to call them brothers—Hebrews 2:11. Jesus's perception of us.

It is not about being grateful and joyful because circumstances are good. The crucial matter is: for whom am I living? For some, even that precious life is burdensome; for others, even death becomes a gain. This is because to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain.—Philippians 1:21. Because I have set my purpose to live for Christ, death is also beneficial to me.—Easy Bible, Philippians 1:21.

The taste of a ripe persimmon cannot be imitated or forced. I cannot taste it, nor can I give it, yet if I describe it and demand 'Why can't you feel the taste?', I must fake it, and I live tired and powerless. Without receiving supply to experience the taste, I blame myself and fall into condemnation. As taste cannot be imitated, neither can the gospel. When life is clearly supplied, the game is over. We cannot but speak of what we have seen and heard.—Acts 4:20
Do not fear the loss of glamorous relationships. Rather, fear the fading of genuine relationship with God.

I do not wish to torture with false hope in the name of faith. The Lord presents the same level of challenge to those just beginning to follow Him as to those following for a long time. 'Who is your master? Whom do you serve? Who lives in you? Where will your allegiance be?' Faith is surprisingly simple. Only people make it complicated. This happens when what you pursue is not the goal. Sell your possessions and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come and follow me.—Matthew 19:21.
If losing becomes gaining, and even death cannot steal my happiness and joy, it is because the one living in me is not 'me' but 'Jesus Christ.' Simply let go. You will obtain the Lord.
Your heart will crumble if you do not receive confirmation. Do not be afraid; rather, crumble completely to powder. God gives a new heart in that broken place.
If you are swayed by environment, the world, and people, you will live a life without peace. If you seek the approval of people, you are certainly not a servant of Christ.—Galatians 1:10.
There are those who want to be welcomed and those who welcome. They may seem similar, but the results are as different as heaven and earth. Welcome others; you will be welcomed.

The Lord's first sign that repentance and change of direction are needed is when the joy of worship and the willingness of spirit disappear. One who recognizes their condition and feels that the true heavenly joy the Lord gave cannot be exchanged for anything else—that person loves the Lord.
A company moves toward profit. But a believer must make decisions toward drawing closer to the Lord. Woe to those who move to please people and expect their approval.

In a contract composition template at a company, there is 'Party's Bargaining Position'—who is the stronger party. This is very important in the world. Daniel also faced a king holding his life and death, and courtiers systematically plotting his death. Even at eighty-nine, after seventy-five years of public service and four kings served as prime minister, Daniel was attacked with racial discrimination: 'that captive from Judah.' Daniel's method of countering this is 'complete disregard' and serving the Lord as he did. I serve only the Lord!
A life without regret is a well-lived life. To have no regret when standing before the Lord, two things must be clear: one is the goal, the other is running toward it with passion.

People often try to prove their love by giving extravagant gifts or planning various touching events. Yet all these can be easily imitated momentarily. But 'sharing pain' and 'continuously empathizing with that pain without change' cannot be copied. Only when we become one does their pain become my pain.

Often people get unreasonably jealous when others react less than usual or grow close to someone else. It means you are not flying. The airplane's altitude is low, so it is affected by all kinds of flies, mosquitoes, and sparrows. One whose conclusion is the Lord soars through the sky. That's why they are not anxious about earthly affairs.

People often like to divide into sides: my side and enemies, good people and troublesome people. Yet this thinking is wrong from the start. In the Bible, the people Jesus received and befriended had serious character and personal problems, and their occupations were not welcomed. Among them, especially 'I' am revealed as the chief of the most wicked sinners. If I do not appear as a sinner, I have not passed through the cross.
We came from dust, so we will return to dust. There is no need to cling. Only He knows me and will remember me.
When you release the spirit of sacrifice, the spirit of selfishness is ashamed to flee.

If you board a bus written 'Heathrow,' you should go to Heathrow, not the opposite direction to Gatwick. If you have decided to look to the Lord, you must run only toward Him. To aim at something else is deception and theft.
Faith is ignoring the desires standing before me and holding fast to the true reality and evidence. That is Jesus.

Paul frequently uses the expression 'pursue' in all his epistles. Personally, this arises because of two things: direction and passion. When the direction of Jesus and passion combine, you cannot help but pursue. Moreover, I consider everything a loss because knowing my Lord Jesus Christ is of surpassing worth. I press on to take hold of what Christ Jesus has taken hold of me for.—Philippians 3:8-12. When you know Jesus, you press on. Simple, right?

In the world, the strong devour the weak. So many people strive to have sharper claws and stronger fangs. Yet those who follow the Lord do not live fighting others. They live because they hear the Shepherd's voice.

Parting Sentiment: During university, a respected psychology professor once asked me a personal question: 'Wha kind of person do you want to become?' With no hesitation whatsoever, a reflexive answer came out, and I was surprised at myself: 'I want to be a person who brings inspiration.' Five years pass so quickly. I realize deeply that what I said boldly was not as easy as it sounds. Not to mention inspiration, I would be grateful just not to hurt others and to not obstruct the Lord's way. I hope to be happy. So when called, though I lack ability and qualification, I thought it would be meaningful and something to be grateful for—even if I were used as a six-month poker stick for God's glory to the youth. I cooked, I drove, I worshiped, I visited, but rather than thinking of it as service, because I loved, it was rapturous. Laughing together, crying together, burning with care, yet I saw heavenly glory. It seems I loved rather than served. Before being a minister, I was truly happy being one youth among beautiful young people. I tried to give, yet nothing was given, and instead I received abundantly. Turning around and walking in silence, I find myself unexpectedly rich. Still in my returned daily life, today my hands do not take hold of work. Jesus's cross takes priority over everything. Whether breathing, heartbeats, storms and waves, or climbing to shining heights, thinking of and beholding the cross is enough. Being together was happy and truly glorious. Missionary Kim Hwa Seong.

There are two legs, signifying 'balance.' Legs straddling the Lord and the world respectively. The intention is to move to the side without loss if necessary. This is a truly wrong way of thinking. The distance between the Lord and the world is so great—how can you straddle both legs? I express my deepest condolences.

If Noah had mastered wine well, if Samson had handled his weakness with women well, history would truly have changed. People are not tested by strength. Those who handle weakness well are remarkable people. Try to settle the sins you repeatedly fall into. You will become a Samson without the weakness of women. Faith is surprisingly simple. Simple, intense, flexible.

It is fully understandable to be cursed as ignorant and a fanatic. It is natural criticism. Because they do not know the verse below: The resurrection of Christ resets and renews all values. If only for this life we have hope in Christ, we are of all people most to be pitied.—1 Corinthians 15:19.

I must nail to the cross my questions about who I am. On this matter, the truth and conclusion is that it happened by God's grace. I should not take it up again to argue but should be filled with gratitude every moment—gratitude that could never be sufficient. Foolish people forget grace and compare. Do not compare and judge yourself; rather, worship with thanksgiving.

For me, all meaning and value comes only from the Lord. When I regard the other person and circumstances with this truth, I rejoice in the meaning the Lord gave and give Him glory. The Lord alone is my reason. 'Unless it is given to him from heaven, a man can receive nothing.'—John 3:27.
Oh happy day, I will never be the same.

*Don't try to be better rather than to be you, for you are his workmanship. Trying to appear as a somewhat decent person is pointless. From the moment I think too deeply about what others think of me, life is wasted, becomes empty, and secret sin enters. The only word I need to hear is this: 'Well done, good and faithful servant.'

* God never let you down nor you can let Him down. The deepest human wound is disappointing someone I love—disappointing myself. Most painfully, when disappointing God, I hurt deeply and grow angry at myself. I want to throw everything away and disappear into the ground. Yet fundamentally, I cannot disappoint God. I think I have disappointed Him by not holding Him well. Rather, because God held me with His righteous right hand, I am here. The Lord knew my weakness from the beginning, and therefore bore the cross, so He does not become disappointed and never abandons me to fail.

* I see you. A dry well yields nothing, yet we keep going, peering in, returning empty-handed, grieving and feeling lonely—this is humanity. Life is unhappy because what should be present is absent and what should be absent is present. What should be present is Jesus, and what should be absent are many things trying to fill the emptiness—garbage. I am not garbage. The Lord never created garbage.

Paul was a man of power, yet he asked the Thessalonian church to manage and deliver the relief offering he had decided on in an orderly and transparent manner. Why not pray and open the heavenly gates to pour down money? Because the undeniable other expression of miracles is transformation of heart. God's kingdom must advance through sacrifice and love.
I do not crumble. Because I cannot crumble. As long as Jesus, my soul and life, is with me, I pursue toward the goal.

I must forget what I have done because there is an eternal goal. I strive for what I have been seized by Christ to do, yet I have not fully accomplished it. That is why I ceaselessly pursue after Jesus, who is that goal.
The happiest moment for me is when the Lord makes me weep.
I intercede for our young people facing problems as complex as salad. Be courageous.
The unbearable sorrow that prevents me from living a life of comfort and convenience comes from the burning love of Jesus in my heart.
Moses departed beautifully and without shame. Because he lived with loyalty as if each day were his last, fulfilling his calling completely. One who completes their calling perfectly is clean.

There is no blessing upon those who change churches—theirs is the kingdom of heaven; there is no blessing upon those who persistently cleanse others' hearts—they will see God; there is no blessing upon those who pass tests and are promoted; there is no blessing upon those whose true value is recognized—they will inherit the land. When we change our environment and others change, we think we will be happy. But without my impoverished spirit, my sorrow, my peace, and my holiness, it is only emptiness.
We also become like what we love.

All animals boast of things at the physical level. But humans, God's greatest masterpiece, must not boast at such a level. We must not boast of what is mine, but boast of Him. The essence of SNS is, in one word, boasting—what we wear, whom we meet, what we eat, what we see. But we boast only in Jesus. That is what makes us different from animals.

When I am wounded, it is not because that person is malicious, but because of my selfishness. Selfishness is self-deception and delusion—satisfying myself with fantasy rather than truth in order to protect myself. When we know the Father's heart, we choose healing over wounds. We realize that he and she, as well as I, are ultimately objects of salvation.

Whether the southeast wind blows or the northwest wind blows, diffusing the fragrance of Jesus who became a lily among thorns, we simply go forward saying hallelujah and amen. No matter what anyone says, regardless of any misunderstandings, no matter what the world's evaluation is, we simply live that way.

A household head does not collapse and spread out. Because there are dependents. A person of calling does not remain fallen. Because they are one who saves lives. Eventually when we go to heaven, we will rest eternally. We hope you will run for glory.
My worth is solely the worth of Jesus Christ.
As it has always been, as if this moment were the last, we risk our lives.
When there is too much of me inside me, I wander. Do not grieve, but do what the Lord desires. Knowledge alone is insufficient; it requires obedient faith.

Many people pray, longing for encouragement and love from others. As prayer deepens, eventually we come to think that it is okay even if everyone turns away from us. Because one Jesus is enough. After receiving this answer, other relationships also unravel. If we cling, we will lose.
There is no better ministry for me. There exists no better youth group in the world for me. There is no better opportunity for me. Because the Almighty Lord has already given me all these things.

Many people resent their environment and people. They find the cause of unhappiness in others. But this is the result of letting go of what we should hold and holding what we should not. If we become a victim, we are unhappy. Do not become a victim, but become a wounded healer.

One of the most foolish things is to transfer one's own shortcomings to others. Rather than throw stones at others, we should hold the cross and strike our own chest. Living as a disloyal servant with a heart that has been beaten so much it no longer exists.

Do not carry too many burdens. That too is greed. I ask that you carry the burden the Lord has given. Your heart will become gentle and humble, and that burden will be light. We call the courageous abandonment of what God did not give 'peace,' and the firm grasping of what He gave 'boldness.' I am gentle and humble in heart; take my yoke upon you and learn from me, and you will find rest for your souls, for my yoke is easy and my burden is light. -Matthew 11:29

People who like me do so as long as conditions align. Liking is simply being satisfied with conditions that will decay for now. But love is unchanging and moreover accepts and covers countless faults and failures. Loving means deciding to sacrifice for the soul the Lord has given, not for conditions that change and decay. It is a gift poured out from that decision. I love you.
Ultimately, it is living eternally with my Jesus. That is what makes me smile and rise again.
Colleagues do not cooperate, the system does not support, and people misunderstand me without reason. No! My worship has simply collapsed. Worship is the beginning of all restoration.

Rudeness is when the weak pretend to be strong and imitate the strong. A truly strong person discovers themselves in the Lord, and even when lowering themselves, their self-respect is not wounded. They acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty that governs all situations.

When you tell a new employee who has just joined a large company after only doing part-time work to sweep the trash and clean the floor, they feel tremendously hurt pride. However, the company's chairman receives respect and glory when doing so. A strong and honorable person can easily lower themselves to serve, and that becomes glory. But a weak and defensive person cannot lower themselves and considers it shame. A truly strong person does not become angry but serves and can lower themselves. Jesus lowered Himself infinitely and bore the cross.

He sent back twenty thousand men and won with three hundred against an allied army of one hundred thirty-five thousand. More precisely, it was a war that could only be won by sending back twenty thousand men. God used Gideon, like barley bread. Precisely because he was like barley bread, He used him. Many people boast of what is vain and perishable. They boast because the numbers are many and high, but in fact, they are not used because of that. I dreamed a dream: behold, a cake of barley bread tumbled into the camp of Midian, and came to the tent, and struck it so that it fell. It turned it upside down, and the tent collapsed. -Judges 7:13

* Self-diagnosis: Rather than 'Who am I?' we must look at 'What Jesus did on the cross.' Rather than 'What have I done?' we must focus more on 'Who is He?' People who focus on 'themselves' encounter problems in 'relationships.' Either becoming aggressive through inferiority complex or avoiding through victimhood. When the lens is defective, the photograph of the subject appears distorted.

What others evaluate about me is not reality. Whether they praised or blamed, they speak not knowing my true essence well. It is like getting angry at someone for calling an extremely vicious, atrocious criminal a 'needle thief.' People's random rumors and criticism are foolish and narrow-minded, but my sinful nature is more evil than that. Without breaking my idols, no progress is made. I am the chief of sinners.

A proud person is truly unhappy. Because they find the reason things don't work out in others. Being discouraged is not being humble. The more proud and self-centered we are, the lower our self-esteem and the more easily we become discouraged. Because we compare. A person without the Lord at their center tries to confirm themselves through creation. Without dying to self, summoning strength and courage is running in the opposite direction from the destination. See yourself in the Lord. You will become humble. The pride of a man's heart brings him into conflict, but whoever trusts in the Lord, mercy surrounds him. -Proverbs 18:12

If you are disappointed that there is nothing good in me, that means you are proud. A humble person does not expect anything in themselves but rather desires to be dead to self and live only through Jesus. Therefore, they are not frustrated by their condition but rather are fascinated by and rejoice in the Lord.

Whether resting on a peaceful bed forgetting everything or under tremendous pressure in an urgent situation, the Lord desires to confirm that God is your center and that the Lord is the center of your heart. Whether we live or die, we live for the Lord or die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord. -Romans 14:8

* Calling: There is the most meaningful and happy life to which the Lord calls. The conclusion is simple. Either go that path simply or go stumbling backward with blinders in Damascus, suffering needlessly. Ultimately, the ball must go into the goal.

Large numbers are not necessary. Scripture calls boasting in numbers foolish. Gideon's three hundred are warriors, but tens of thousands are merely a crowd. Do not become a crowd but become a warrior. The Lord uses such people.

Do not struggle with emotional difficulties. Lift the eyes of faith and see that it is only one piece among all processes. Emotion is not reality; faith is reality. Circumstances are not evidence; faith is evidence. Be bold.

We mistakenly think that when we achieve something, we can give glory to God. But God is already exalted alone and possesses all authority. What could He possibly need that He would extend His hand to us? The only way to glorify God is for us to be glorified. That happens only by grace, by denying self, and by relinquishing rights. Only then does the Lord receive glory. He has never once received glory through our self-realization. Jesus said these words, and lifted up His eyes to heaven, saying, Father, the hour has come. Glorify Your Son, that Your Son also may glorify You. -John 17:1

Picasso's famous 1943 Bull's Head sculpture. One day Picasso sees a bicycle at a junkyard. What others cannot see, Picasso sees, and he creates this work by attaching handlebars and a saddle. Picasso was from Spain, 'the land of bulls.' When people are immersed, they approach their origin. There he saw the bull from childhood with his own unique vision that others cannot see. When there is immersion, pouring your entire heart to the Lord, you discover the Lord there. Immersion: I do not consider myself to have fully obtained or been perfected, but I press on to lay hold of that for which Christ Jesus laid hold of me. -Philippians 3:12

Because I know better than anyone what is more important, I can abandon what is less important better than anyone else. I do not regard the things I have but run toward the prize of the upward calling of God in Christ. One who discovers the greatest value lives simply.

In the parable Nathan told King David, the method of compensating for the ewe lamb is fourfold according to the law. But David becomes furious and condemns him to death. After the Bathsheba incident, during a year of avoiding repentance, David becomes insensitive to himself and furious toward others. The characteristic of hardness is always finding the cause in others.
If you truly want something, you will pursue it. If you want something but do not pursue it, it means you never truly wanted it. You persistently pursue only what you truly want.
Do not blame others but repent and turn. The problem is in the first person. If I change, it will become a good community.
A simple way to become a leader. Determining to have all-weather faith that is not tested in any situation. A leader has no closed season.

* Reason for shedding blood: We do not understand why God chose to love us. We naturally cannot understand. It is not that He evaluates something and chooses and loves, but rather that He loves and therefore chose. So we often cannot understand even a mother's heart. In our teens, our accessories, celebrities' daily lives, cherished hoodies, friends' evaluations, the frequency and hidden meanings of words in texts seem much more important than a mother's concern. But when you give birth to new life that you would willingly die for, you stay up all night nursing and caring for the sick. That is when you learn that love is sacrifice.

A flower is beautiful because there is a flower within me. But sometimes we must direct our steps toward where flowers are blooming. Because blooming flowers cause flowers to bloom within us as well. Within people there are flowers and there is filth. What blooms is determined by what we encounter. Spend time with good people.

* True repentance: After committing a mistake, the confession 'I will never do that again' is not repentance. We are originally such beings—how can we live otherwise? Rather, 'If the Lord does not bestow forgiveness and help upon me, I have no choice but to live this way. Because I am originally such a being.' After a harrowing realization about ourselves, through the Lord's forgiveness, we make a new beginning.
The devil says, 'I will give you what you so desire.' Tell the devil, 'How will you give me Jesus?' If longing is for the Lord, neither people nor the world can rule over me.

There is a question I am always asked. 'What are you doing now?' And each time, a reflexive confession bursts out: 'I want to see God's glory!' After that question and answer, there is warmth in my heart. It is a blessing to know what I truly want and what alone satisfies me.
I am a sinner and lacking, and I stand on the test block daily. Moreover, I am the chief of sinners and weak, and in every moment I call upon the Lord. Amen, Lord Jesus.
The reason David was strong and recovered quickly can be seen in the Psalms. He continuously confessed and proclaimed to himself, God, and the world.
* Living well: In a distant future, if my daughters ask, I think I will have nothing to show them. But I think I will not be ashamed. Because I have burned it all for the Lord, if nothing remains...

* True purpose: While living in this world, there is no need to become great. If we do, we become strained and suffer side effects. And since we already have glory in the Lord, there is no reason to seek worldly glory. Before the Lord's glory, the things of this world are but fireflies.

* The most pitiful in this world: If a life pursues only what is of this world, we are truly pitiful people. Because of the resurrection, we have the most glorious life. But if life without the hope of resurrection does not become pitiable, there is a problem. That means being Christian in name only. Without resurrection, a life should be ruined. If in Christ we hope only for this life, we of all people are most to be pitied. But now Christ has been raised from the dead, the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep. -1 Corinthians 15:19-20

* The Lord becoming Lord: King Saul lived conscious of others' gaze, clinging, while David looked to the Lord's presence and let go and lived. Though having more, he was anxious for not having enough, and though having nothing, he found peace in grace being poured out. Because their master whom they served was different. What am I pursuing, and what is the motivation? Saul was displeased and very angry at this word, saying, They have ascribed to David ten thousands, and to me they have ascribed thousands. Now what more can he have but the kingdom? -1 Samuel 18:8

* A painful but necessary conversation: When people fall in love, change always occurs. Both they and those around them know this change. To the Ephesian church, which does not compromise and keeps the gospel while enduring persecution, the Lord says there is something to rebuke. That is abandoning their first love. That was most important to the Lord. Going through faith life saying, 'I believe,' and 'I love' while not truly believing or loving is a serious illness. We must become honest and sincere. Look back at how you use your time and where your attention and true desire lie. You may not truly and passionately love the Lord. If you do not have private times of intimacy with the Lord and do not pay the cost of sacrifice, you must acknowledge that love has grown cold. What marriage exists without private times of intimacy? That greatly insults the Bridegroom, Jesus.

Relationships are important in life. But when a relationship takes the Lord's place, it becomes idolatry. A relationship the Lord did not give, or obsession with relationships, is evidence of this. The Lord is not a substitute, and we must gain insight through the joy of the bridegroom's friend that John the Baptist had. Taking the bride belongs to the bridegroom, but the friend celebrates and rejoices in that.

*As a result of prayer, people pray to change situations they cannot handle. However, the more one prays, the more one experiences personal transformation. Ultimately, life is a battle of change, and that change comes through Christ who became life and rose again.

*Victory in Overcoming Trials: The extreme trial that is difficult to endure ceases when you crucify the self on the cross. When you take up the cross and die, the trial ends there. When Judah, who once sold his brother, took responsibility for Benjamin and sought to save his family, all tests ended beautifully. 'Please, let me, your servant, remain as a slave to my lord in place of the boy, and let the boy return with his brothers. For how can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me? I cannot bear to see the misery that would come upon my father.' Genesis 44:33
*God's Offering: God's offering does not complain or feel wronged. Like the silent Lord, it becomes a perfect offering. He did not make excuses.

The purpose of the wilderness and the tabernacle is to prepare a people for God. Preparation means seeking the face of the Lord—becoming a people who do not live by bread alone, but by obedience to God's word.

*The Importance of Encounters: Encounters are truly significant. Through those meetings, we are influenced regarding where we place our values in life, and when the purpose of life is corrected, it determines not only our entire lifetime but also our eternal dwelling. 'What is our hope, our joy, or the crown in which we will glory in the presence of our Lord Jesus when he comes? Is it not you? Indeed, you are our glory and joy.' 1 Thessalonians 2:19-20
One of my missions is to raise people who cannot be satisfied with counterfeits or substitutes. To become Christians who, with that holy discontent, run endlessly for Jesus alone.

What is more important than having more than others or living longer than others is how you use what you have and how you live. What the world calls blessing cannot necessarily be blessing, and conversely, what is called difficulty can become a blessing that draws us closer to God.
All true ministry comes out of intimacy with Jesus

*When you love, you are not afraid and you overcome even fearful things. Ministry and love are synonymous. A minister must be a loving person. A loving person does not squeeze out reluctant service from dryness. Rather, they advance toward souls with overflowing emotion and passion, with God's tears, pouring out their blood. Loving God and souls is the most powerful ministry. 'Simon son of John, do you love me more than these?' 'Yes, Lord, you know that I love you.' Jesus said, 'Feed my lambs.' John 21:15-17

*Time and the Design of Life: Life's priorities determine the quality of my life. Time is equal to everyone, but the content that fills that time is different for each person, and the results differ. When a person's affections change, the quality of their life changes.

Demon possession uses the word 'possession' because Satan coerces. However, the Holy Spirit moves. From this comes a willing spirit. Only humans made in God's image can be given freedom to love the Lord.

When you look at people serving earnestly and worshipping in church, it seems as if they are living well without trouble. But when they return to their own places, they face various problems and live in anxiety and unease. Why? Because they are not confident in God's love. 'For those God foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brothers and sisters.' Romans 8:29. God is the God of impartiality. That means just as God loves His Son Jesus Christ, He loves us, His younger brothers and sisters, equally. God cannot be unjust.

I am not a chef, but I cook; I am not a professional minister, but I minister; I am not a driver, but I drive; and though I am clumsy at love, I love. Why? Because the Lord, who is chief of sinners, loved me first. Oh, one more thing—I am not a writer, but words flow; I am not a poet, but poetry flows. ^^ 'I was neither a prophet nor the son of a prophet. I was a shepherd, and I also took care of sycamore-fig trees.' Amos 7:14
Being exalted does not gain recognition, nor does being lowered make you humble. How I see myself is not really important. What matters is how God sees me and what plans He has for me.

The reason you become aware of another's faults is because you have the same shortcomings within you. Jacob meets Laban and realizes how he deceived his brother to steal the blessing. Then he meets Leah, whom the younger brother deceived into marrying.

If I faithfully uphold the Lord's name where I stand and fade away unknown, I will not regret it. Because He knows me and I know Him. Vain glory passes away, and if I can only seek His glory, that is the eternal inheritance and blessing of this insufficient servant.

When athlete Park Ju-young faced Japan for the Olympic bronze medal, it was not difficult or a desire to sit on the bench and rest. Because the Lord is with me, every event that happens to me is a love story. With only Him, only that one Person present, all interpretations become excellent.
I am infinitely insufficient. There is no excuse for my lack. That is why I need Jesus all the more. Without Him, I cannot take a single step. That is also the greatest blessing.

On a freezing day near zero degrees, I walk hunched up. Then a British person passes by wearing just a reflective safety jacket and a short-sleeved shirt. What? ... The most foolish thing is to imitate what others do. Please dress warmly. And do not live the way the world wants you to. Living someone else's life is a tiring life.
The most childish thing is boasting about money, education, and fame. Rather, boast in your weakness. The Lord will be revealed. God's grace is sufficient for us.
I am not an excellent person. However, I walk this path alongside people I respect and admire.

Serving with dedication in church and offering worship may seem fine, but the goal of God is not in outward service alone. God's fundamental purpose is transformation of character and change of heart. If that doesn't happen, no matter how much you serve in the church and dedicate yourself, you will still face criticism. The purpose is 'repent!'

If I have not yet become 'the glorious person of the Lord,' it is because I have not yet clearly encountered 'the glory of the Lord' or 'the Lord of glory.' Even with strong self-discipline and free will, if you do not encounter and see the Lord's glory, it ends in three days of determination. Those who have seen glory find it harder not to live gloriously.

1. There are those who stay temporarily (leave to remain) and those who are permanent family (citizen). 2. Those who stay leave when their purpose is fulfilled, but family does not leave because love is the purpose. 3. We do not love Jesus to go to heaven; we go to heaven because we love Him. Therefore, heaven without Jesus is also displeasing to me. 4. Those whose purpose is Jesus are not discouraged or broken down. This means we are not beings who seek blessing to live a little better than others and boast about it. 5. Moses said to the Lord, 'If your Presence does not go with us, do not send us up from here. How will anyone know that you are pleased with me and with your people unless you go with us? What else will distinguish me and your people from all the other people on the face of the earth?' Exodus 33:15-16

If someone said they treated the Lord lightly, many would jump up. Few realize that what was done to the least of these and what was done in worship was done to the Lord. 'The Lord Almighty says: When you bring blind animals for sacrifice, is that not wrong? When you bring crippled or diseased animals and offer them as sacrifices, is that not wrong? Try offering them to your governor! Would he be pleased with you? Would he accept you?' says the Lord Almighty. 'Now plead with God to be gracious to us. With such offerings from your hands, will he accept you?' says the Lord Almighty. Malachi 1:8-9. Please honor the Lord.

If you received a bright red rose from someone, your heart would race. But if the person who gave the flower was half-naked with only a raincoat, your mind would race. It is not that good things exist, but that a good Person exists.

I mistake some praises of myself, some strengths that I take pride in, as though they support me to this extent. No. Rather, what I deemed insignificant, what I truly confessed as weakness, supports me. '2 Corinthians 12:10 Therefore I delight in weaknesses, in insults, in hardships, in persecutions, in difficulties. For when I am weak, then I am strong.'

When you feel others moving faster than you, think of God who delivers you in every moment of life. Do not be troubled that others have what you do not have; rather, grieve that you cannot live more for the Lord. At least David, who was pursued by his own son, had this pain. 'Blessed are those whose strength is in you, whose hearts are set on pilgrimage.' Psalm 84:5

Moses was called at eighty, Isaac married in his forties. Joseph faced the threat of murder at thirteen and became a political leader at thirty. It is truly incomprehensible to judge myself by my present appearance in life. I must interpret myself through the Lord's eyes. Those who discern what the Lord's will is do not waste time, and the Lord makes them true light. The time given to me now is the greatest opportunity and place of service. 'Show me, Lord, my life's end and the number of my days; let me know how fleeting my life is.' Psalm 39:4

There are times when He brings you to the bottom. It means to repent and be humble. It is not new disappointment but rather a reconfirmation of what we are by nature—sinful and flawed humans, which we temporarily forgot. That is why we need the Lord more. Absolutely nothing works without Jesus. That is why He took up the cross. Repent, pray, and rise up to run again.
Poor people find everything delicious. Those who are poor in spirit taste the Lord's love in everything and give thanks. That is why they are blessed. When life tastes good, how can one not be happy?

When a person of high status cleans with a dustpan, it is a truly precious act. Yet in the world, a person whose profession is cleaning also cleans in church. No one looks back or notices. They are even more precious. There are often precious people in the world who are excluded from recognized praise.
Sometimes He permits trials that we do not understand. It is when love has become an idol. Faithful obedience overcomes the trial.

When you remove the froth from what all people say, the essence of that complexity boils down to this: 1. Upon closer inspection, the other person's behavior is not desirable. 2. Nevertheless, I responded quite coolly. → Conclusion: I am quite a decent person. What we can understand: Most conversations without God are 1. Lowering others and 2. Elevating myself, and 3. Ultimately craving 'recognition.'

That I stumble into sin proves something. It is the surest evidence that I have lived by my own strength. If my will and strength are strong, I will eventually fall, only a bit later. Do not try to delay failure a little; accept the cross, acknowledge your collapse now, and embrace living with Jesus.

If someone is afraid and panicking, their purpose is 'survival.' But those whose purpose is 'God's glory' are not pursued. Whether they live or die, they are interested in Christ being honored within them. Whether you eat or drink, study or work, do it for the Lord's glory.
You cannot go crazy (reach) unless you go crazy (become insane).

The difference between a bouquet and a potted plant is that a bouquet changes with environment and time, but a potted plant changes through its roots. A life rooted in God may not be flashy at first, but it is the strongest life.
My longings reflect my purpose. Whether it is a perishing purpose or a purpose of high calling can be known by what I long for. My longing is to know the Lord completely, just as the Lord knows me.

Many seek to possess much. In this world today, people believe ownership and accumulation are power. But true power is actually the ability to let go. Those who can let go without hesitation are the strongest.
'He must become greater; I must become less.' John 3:30

Many want their weaknesses to disappear or at least hide them. But when I look only to the Lord in my weakness and hope in Him, the Lord receives glory through my insufficiency. Despite my flawed character and expression, clumsy relationships, and unstable emotions, the Lord wants to be glorified through me. Only when I see the Lord.
It takes about twenty-five years to understand even a little the heart of the mother who gave birth to me. Even if the people I meet do not understand my heart, I should not complain or grumble.

If work I did with joy suddenly feels like a loss, and it feels like a one-sided sacrifice, it is because I am not actually looking at the One who truly suffered the loss and made the sacrifice. He is the very image of God, yet He became a servant and was obedient to death. When perspective changes, you think of recouping. Please keep all your focus on the Lord.
Walking the same path as others at a faster speed does not necessarily guarantee happiness. Only 'He' knows my path. Only 'He' can refine me like fine gold, like a jewel, making me truly 'me.'

When you do not follow your emotions, are not hasty, endure with patience, maintain focus to the end, and wait, opportunity will surely come. Silently preparing a lifetime for that opportunity is what we call skill.

Three years ago, when I returned after a long time and began ministry again, I saw within me a heart that I did not start. It was not my nature but the heart the Lord gave me. 'Love.' The original author of that heart was God. That sinners can embrace God's heart is grace, and because of that boundless grace, I can run today.

I am loved not because I am flawless, but because I am insufficient and greatly need mercy. Therefore, Christians are not beings who hide their faults and wear masks, but transparent people, even if their expression is clumsy. It is all God's grace.

Be free from what others see in you. Also, do not strive to be seen. All our attention should be only on God. That is worship. If I look only at the Lord, people will not see me but will see the God I am looking at. Do not be conscious of people; look to God. You will be free from torment.

After David fell from grace and lost everything, the most painful thing was that he could not go to the Lord's house. That is why he so envied the pilgrims. (Psalm 84) This is one whose heart is aligned with God's.

In the 1960s, the national income was 80 dollars. Now it exceeds 20,000. In just a few decades, it has increased 250 times. Back then, people died from absolute poverty and starvation, but now with relative poverty, people do not starve. Income has increased 250 times, yet we should be ecstatically happy, but it doesn't appear so. Can possession truly bring such happiness? No, it cannot. This is because we understand people as mere flesh. People have spirit and pursue meaning. If we cannot be happy by merely existing, we cannot be happy by possessing either.

To give away gracefully, you must have something to give. Without the premise of 'acquiring,' there is no 'giving away.' It is noble and beautiful to have acquired much and be able to enjoy it, yet give it away and share it with many people. People work, gather, and achieve in order to stay and enjoy what they have, but we must work, gather, and achieve in order to share more, give away more, and depart more.

You must be free from how others see you. Also, you must not strive to be seen by others. All our attention and focus must be only on God; that is worship of life, true worship. If I only look to the Lord, then people will not see me but will see the God I am looking at. If they can see God through you, that is the highest glory.

David does not accept Goliath's evaluation. Unless I accept it, no evaluation belongs to me. If I do not acknowledge it, no one can condemn me. Just as Eliab cannot define David, no one can define me. Goliath: I will make you food for beasts. David: That's funny, you become food, I'm passing. Conclusion: Goliath became food. King Saul was a man defined by the evaluations of women. Therefore, he was captured by the times of his life and jealousy. This is the miserable state of a person whose relationships are shaken. Let us learn to work within good relationships. You will realize that problem-solving is easier than you think.

If you have what appears in advertisements and live as it appears in advertisements and become the person in advertisements, will you be happy? Then you will be ruined. You probably died eaten by worms.

Refusing is faith. Not just anyone can refuse, but when you hold onto Jesus, other things fall to the level of excrement. Not giving away is strange. 'By faith Moses, when he had grown up, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh's daughter, choosing rather to be mistreated along with the people of God than to enjoy the fleeting pleasures of sin. He regarded disgrace for the sake of Christ as of greater value than the treasures of Egypt, because he was looking ahead to his reward.' (Hebrews 11:24-11:26)

Values determine desire. It is so difficult for us to regard gold as stone. Yet a dog simply regards gold as stone. The dog is not mature or prays in tongues a lot. It is because the dog lacks perspective.
If you have much but cannot share, you become a pitiful pauper, and if you are poor but can empty yourself, become even lower, and share, you become rich.

A believer who begrudges prayer time is unworthy of enjoying precious things. They are foolish people who cannot discern what is most important. That is why many people eke out a living like beggars. Please pray. You are royal priests.
It is not a problem to have much money. It is a serious problem when money owns you, even if it is a small amount.

When a landlord is upset, he tells you to vacate. If God were truly upset, He could simply take away the 'breath' and a person would just go. God rejects sin, but He does not reject people. Rather, He loves them so very much. Our conscience testifies to our wrong actions, but the devil accuses by emphasizing that I am a flawed human, a worthless human. When Cain feels rejected, he becomes jealous of Abel and commits murder. We have not been rejected; rather, we have been received. 'You welcome him with rich blessings and place a crown of pure gold on his head.' (Psalm 21:3)
What is before me and what is behind me are truly small matters compared to what is within me.
Do not try too hard to become an overly capable person. Rather, become a fool, weak in the Lord. 'My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness' (2 Corinthians 12:9).

When four-year-old Grace helps mom and dad by placing spoons on the table, my wife and I are truly delighted. We are not happy because Grace lightens mom and dad's work. We are not happy about the child's labor, but about her heart. When you become obsessed with ministry or results, it is a sign that my soul has become impoverished. You try to move someone, or even try to move God. The Lord says this: Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. The Lord did not give us a mission because He lacks labor. He is the One who can make children of Abraham from stones, so our insufficient help is irrelevant. He wants to be 'with me' and wants to have 'fellowship.' Do not become a 'beggar,' become a 'friend.' The Lord desires intimacy. The reason I include my inadequate self in God's plan is the same reason I want to do household chores with our children.

People say the church community is difficult because the saints lack enthusiasm, but that is not true. The real crisis is that faithful people demand compensation and credit for their work. When they go beyond 'ownership mentality' to become the 'owner' themselves and call themselves 'unprofitable servants' as 'profitable servants,' wounds and divisions occur. They do not claim what they have accomplished as theirs, and by not claiming it as theirs, they do not unnecessarily linger, and by not lingering, they do not unnecessarily depart either.
you've turned my shame into joyful praise

For by the grace given me I say to every one of you: Do not think of yourself more highly than you ought, but rather think of yourself with sober judgment, in accordance with the faith God has distributed to each of you. (Romans 12:3) Paul exhorts in Romans by grace, saying 'think with restraint.' If you do not receive God's grace, your thinking itself becomes diseased and corrupted. That is why things fall and collapse. Abandon the thoughts you want to have, receive grace, and think accordingly.

Believing means changing masters. When the master changes, values, worldview, views on relationships, all evaluations change. In particular, the bestowed 'authority' and 'power' are used differently. Before, I focused only on achieving much and rising high, efficiency and effectiveness, but ultimately I can win or lose. Only following the Lord's will is true victory.

Fear exists because you have not surrendered. A person who has given everything they possess to Christ and even given their own life has nothing left, so there is nothing to fear in life. You have already given everything, your life is not your own, and you can live the remaining life by the power the Lord gives, so what more is there to fear?
You are a person who came to love. Therefore, you will not be disappointed in all things, nor will you regret when you depart.

Even with much power and wealth, it is useless without health. In that sense, a greater gift than other gifts is to delight in God. (Nehemiah 8:10) Delighting in God comes from relationship and worship. If the relationship is wrong, you are sick, and if you are sick, what use is walking on water? Are you truly delighting in God at this very moment?
Why not me? Lord, You do everything through me by your faithfulness.
