Animals do not have inner conflict. Because they are made of earth. Angels do not have inner conflict. Because they are made of spirit only. People have inner conflict. Because spirit is breathed into earth. (By design...) When earth and spirit collide, which way I fall determines the success or failure of the battle. That is affection, and it is expressed in my pride, attachment, and suffering.

I need to confirm my calling. If I think about why I was called, I can truly be free, be happy, have meaning, and not be concerned with futility. To fulfill the mission and to be aware that the unprofitable servant has only done what unprofitable servants do.
A person soaked by a downpour is no longer afraid of a drizzle. A person satisfied with the Lord is a person who has nothing left to lose. If you hold onto Jesus, that is sufficient.
When ability is exhausted and strength is gone, when I do not know what to do, at least what I can do is love and forgive. No one can take this away from me in the Lord.

In every game there is a player who wins the game, and in every fight there is a game maker who changes the course. David, a man after God's heart, is such a person. A person whom God seems He could entrust His will and it would be accomplished well, a person who can be trusted to always do His will—you must become such a person.
He must become greater; I must become less. Be joyful if you diminish, then there will be a joy the world does not know. He must become greater; I must become less. John 3:30

Many people rush toward their goals. That is proving that they are decent people. When you try to prove yourself, life does not open its doors. Only when you abandon yourself and seek only the Lord's glory do the doors of life truly open. God does not exalt proud people. For their sake, and also for the sake of God's kingdom...

2 Timothy 2:4 No one serving as a soldier gets entangled in civilian affairs, but rather tries to please his commanding officer. A person of the cross does not dwell on their own problems. They focus entirely on pleasing the One who called them. My pride, my reputation, my honor, my feelings are not the main thing. Fulfilling the Lord's will becomes the desire.
Do not prove yourself but testify to the Lord. God has anointed you and is with you.

[Philippians 1:20] I eagerly expect and hope that I will in no way be ashamed, but will have sufficient courage so that now as always Christ will be exalted in my body, whether by life or by death. [Philippians 1:21] For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain. Paul's concern was not on himself but entirely focused on the Lord. Even more than the matter of life and death, more than the limitations and urgency of his current circumstances, his complete interest and passion were on Christ being testified to, glorified, and honored in his aging body—this was his confession. Who can stop such a person? Even the devil, even death? His confession contained this: 'I have no interest in the desires of the flesh. I do not even dream of being lifted up in the world or being recognized in the church as faithful. I look entirely to the cross and run toward it.' When an apostle who has nailed his ego to the cross burns his life like a torch before the altar as an offering for the Lord, the glory of Christ appears in a way even angels would admire. - From the London to nation by Jacob Kim 11pages

In the last days, many of God's people are needed. Through those who respond in faith and pray in love, the Lord will perform miracles and mighty deeds. I earnestly hope that I become such a designer of God, an engineer of God, a teacher of God, a son of God, a daughter of God.

Living by faith does not mean at the end surrendering your life. At the beginning, you are already dead with nothing left to surrender. What more could there be to long for in heaven besides the Lord?

200 years ago, saying a word that freed slaves meant becoming a madman. 100 years ago, if you gave women the right to vote, you were sent to prison. 50 years ago, crying out for liberation from colonies labeled you a terrorist and you died. What the media says is not all truth. Do not live as they desire, but live as He created you. That is who I am.

When asked about dreams, people say what they will 'become.' That is why when they become what they longed for, they feel empty and fall apart. Dreams are not spoken in nouns but in verbs. It is not 'I will become president,' but 'I will free slaves'—that is a dream.
Canaan's land is blessed not because blessing exists there. When I, rooted in blessing, enter it, that land receives blessing. That is why those who receive me hit the jackpot.
By looking at what I pursue, I can know whom I serve. If I have sought to please people? Or to please God? If I sought the pleasure of people, I am certainly not a servant of God.
Gold belongs to the Lord and silver belongs to the Lord. If I have small tears and love, they all come from the Lord.
If the purpose is Him, everything becomes joy.

To me who wants to accomplish everything easily and go, the Lord gives me time for maturity and ripening, asking me to become a person of authenticity. That time is sometimes difficult, but after being refined, I become like pure gold.

My circumstances are not my choice. However, my attitude is something I can decide. My attitude, not my circumstances, determines my life. A change in constitution takes time and the change is not felt, but in time it will surely bear fruit.
In Him who gives me strength, I can endure all things with gratitude and praise. From the world's perspective, it is an incomprehensible contentment. The world cannot handle such a person.
What the community needs is not a person with capable specs, but a person with whom you could be at peace if trapped together in an elevator.
When everything changes and becomes dim relatively, you must think of True North. When you know true north, other choices become easy.

Look to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith. Faith does not mean believing in my convictions. It does not mean believing that I will marry this year or buy a house. True faith means believing in Jesus. It means that the Jesus I look to is with me.
When the holiness of the Lord overflows within me, it is expressed outward as service. Daniel, the holiest, was also an excellent civil servant.
From his first day at work, Daniel set his heart and lived with restraint according to that heart. A person who has set their heart toward the Lord does not waver but advances with trust.
Whatever you do, do it excellently. Not with eye service, but as unto the Lord, showing kindness to people and acting with integrity.
You do not rise by asserting your rights and oppressing others; rather, you descend. God stands with us in the highest authority when we serve and give ourselves.

We call a person who can discern another's thoughts a Pro. We Christians are the only unique experts in reading God's mind. (Even angels marvel at this.) In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus explained the 'pure in heart' will see God. The only way to read God's mind is not through research or unlawful investigation, but through 'becoming pure.'
Moses fell on his face before slander against himself, but before idolatry he threw down the Ten Commandments and shattered them in anger. Die to yourself and become alive unto God.
Do not lost your simplicity. hold it until Lord come

David did not contend against public misunderstandings about himself. Yet he never compromised and risked his life against any challenge to God's name. A person of the Lord stakes his life for the Lord, not for himself.
The tempter, Satan, in the wilderness comes to Jesus with attractive proposals—using authority to end suffering without difficulty. The good and convenient always oppose the great and the cross.

A woman in LA had her handbag snatched by a robber, yet while bleeding, she would not let go. It was not because of credit cards or designer bags. It was for a few photographs in her phone. She was protecting pictures of her daughter who had passed away from cancer ten weeks prior. Everyone has a core value. For some it is things that perish; for others, things of heaven. In Colossians 1:28-29, Paul says he labors and strives. Proclaiming Him and building up people was his core value. The value for which he would give his life was none other than 'Jesus' and 'souls.' Love what is worth loving. You will have no shame.

That you are a disciple means you are not perfect. You may take several steps forward, then step back several paces. Yet life is not about speed but about maintaining the right direction—that is a blessed life.

Only those who do not wish to experience repeated failure should read this. Those of the flesh can never please God, but those who belong to the Lord become His joy. Not by actions but by position—your position determines who you are. Acting has never overcome Positioning. I am under the blood of the Lord.

I have the appearance of the blood of the cross through the Ebenezer fellowship, the teaching of Pastor Kim Jong-han to see only Jesus, and I follow the path of grace through Director Hong Nan-i of the Gugukkje altar. And now I see, learn from, and become like the youth's love for the Lord.
Rough, unpolished truth is stronger than smoothly spoken lies. An ugly face without a mask is far more beautiful than a masked life.

I will surely disappoint people. That is not something to fear but an opportunity to mature. More fearful than that is fearing what should not be feared. Fear discourages, makes us defensive, paralyzes, and prevents us from living. Jesus also did not satisfy everyone, nor does satisfaction always help.
A leader is one who loves the Lord with all his life and loves souls with tears of compassion, desiring nothing more.
The strongest person is not one who hides his desires, but one who has no desires.
Ephesians 6:10–18 It is about 'us', not 'me', and offers a picture of an army standing and fighting in hand-to-hand combat.
I hope you will stand before the gospel again. Jesus is the gospel. May you become a life that finds answers in Jesus, a life that is satisfied with Jesus alone.

David was left out at a family dinner with honored guests, staying with the sheep outside in the fields. Yet God through Samuel would not allow anything to happen until David came. Do not fall into rejection and discouragement; use those lonely times to become a worshiper who worships the Lord intimately. The breakthrough in life comes from intimacy with the Lord. Do not look to people. Breakthrough flows out of intimacy with God.
It is not important what I can do. What matters is whom and how I believe. God's great business is believing in the Father and the One He sent, Jesus.
The devil says, 'I will give you what you so desire.' Tell the devil, 'How can you give me Jesus?' When my longing is the Lord, people and the world cannot govern me.

Go your own path. That path is neither emotion nor a place to soothe your heart. It is living turned away from the world, separated unto the Lord. When you cut off what you cling to, true value comes alive. Only those who cling will know that this joy and meaning is far more happiness.
This is my highest calling, to love as I am loved.
Your attitude determines your altitude.

To stand before the gospel again, to receive the cross as life, means not living by lust or bloodline, but understanding that 'the Lord alone is sufficient' even if you lose everything. If Jesus is not sufficient, then no matter how you fill every moment with what you so desire, the deep wounds and emptiness in your heart will continue.

Do not allow the things that the enemy brought a year ago, a month ago, or a week ago—things that tempted and distorted the direction of my life—to work today. Resist and declare: I am not that kind of person.

1. Do you think you are closer to God than others? 2. Do you feel spiritually more mature and disciplined than others? 3. Do you think you hold a very important position in God's work? 4. Are you encouraged by the success of your work? Discouraged otherwise? If any of these apply, you are dwelling in self-righteousness and religious zeal. The Bible calls such a person an enemy of the cross. As you read these questions, do you think of many people you know who fit this description? If so, there is a high probability they are truly such people. Humble and proud people hardly know if they are truly like that.
Though darkness is so powerful, there is a power it can never possess. What makes every attack and challenge into nothing is 'love.' This is the motive of every action and word of a Christian.
When you give up what you love, you will discover true love.

This is my desire, to know as I am known, to love as I am loved. This is my an ardent wish, to give my very life An offering of love, a living sacrifice. This is my reward To hear You say, ........ "well done"
What the Lord seeks in leaders is authenticity and perseverance. As time passes, I hope you will serve with the same initial heart.
I was not called for such a thing. I was not called for this either. I was called by the Lord solely to fulfill His will.
Part-time Christianity does not exist. There is only full-time Christianity or non-Christianity.
If every moment were the last, there would be no regrets when you close your eyes. If you leave only love in the world, you will be bold before death.
The only way to be set free from fear is to have the fear of God. The more you are conscious of God, the less you become conscious of yourself.
My weakness will ultimately be helpful to both me and the community. Our disappointments are yet another language of God's calling.
What others have taken from me is not what matters; what remains with me now is more important.

Do not go where your needs are met; go where you are needed. Do not stand as a supporting player in relationships that bring you joy and pleasure; stand in the place where you become the Lord's joy. There you will find true satisfaction and joy in the Lord's gaze.

If I have no love for others, no desire to serve for others, and I'm only concerned about my needs, I should question whether Christ is really in my life. A saved heart is one that wants to serve. Apostle John said "Our love for each other proves that we have gone from death to life"
Maturity requires time. We are so focused on how fast we grow, but the Lord is concerned with how strong we grow.
God, so delighted with David, said He would accomplish all His plans through him. God was so pleased when He saw David's three qualities: gentleness, faithfulness, and his attitude toward authority.

Many people strive to be remembered and exalted, but believers fundamentally live to be forgotten. Holding only to promises, possessing only faith, and living by grace alone—believers in Christ are hidden in Christ, forgotten in the eyes of the world's lusts. Colossians 3:2-3
Do not compare yourself with others. To say 'I am ahead' does not mean I am several steps ahead of others, but that today I have walked further than yesterday's me.
Do not listen to the enemy's accusations. When the devil reminds you of your past, just remind him of his future.
When I give up the rights I rightfully should exercise, God gives me authority, and the devil becomes powerless before abandoned rights.
Trying too hard to do well is greed. What God desires is faithfulness.
When I do not keep my place, I fail at work and leave wounds. Typically, this happens when I pretend to be more than I am. Pride inevitably leaves wounds on its victims.
Pride runs far deeper than what I think of myself. It produces many wounds and bitter roots. Humility begins when I admit that I am not humble.

If I were to list my shortcomings and lacks, I could easily fill several pages of A4 paper in minutes without the devil's help. Having shortcomings in the place God called me to be is God's will; when I endure for the Lord's glory despite my insufficiency, the Lord is glorified and pleased. God did not desire achievement but obedience despite our lack.
Do not be angry about yourself; be righteous for the Lord's name. That is meekness, and God seeks such a person, meets with him, and entrusts His will to him. —From a leaders' meeting
To say 'I love you in the Lord's name' at church is not a polite greeting. It is loving with the heart of Christ.
A pure soul remains pure even without recognition.

The ugly duckling does not seek revenge on the ducks that ostracized it because it has already become a swan. When we love our enemies and pray for them instead, we are called children of God (Matthew 5:45). The swan no longer needs to contend with the ducks. I pray that you soar higher.

When David, living a life of being hunted, came before the king of Gath of the Philistines, he drooled, rubbed his body against the wall, pretended to be mad, and barely escaped to hide in the cave of Adullam. I wonder if that was the very bottom of David's life. Even in the life of David, a great hero in human history, when we examine just one chapter of his era, there are such miserable times.
If you wish to fly like an eagle, you cannot run like an ostrich.
If you base your self-worth on the evaluations others have made about you, you will not have a single joyful day.
The reason I can stand here is because of what Jesus did on the cross. The reason we can serve, though imperfectly, today is not because I live in me, but because Jesus lives in me.

Please entrust to the Lord's hands the timid child within you, the child easily wounded by others' expressions, the child lacking confidence, the child without self-assurance. When the Lord embraces you, healthy self-identity enters within you.
total surrender
How great a misfortune it would be if God is not present where I spend most of my time?
True peace comes when I stop judging myself.
Until genuine gratitude for what I have received from God overflows, envy of others and self-contempt will not cease. What God has given us is so great that human words cannot fully express it.
Standing in my current situation as God desires, confessing and acknowledging that the Lord is the master of my work—this is the worship that pleases God most.
Before asking to see something more mystical and transcendent, pray to see your true self.
Even when every heart is broken and it is hard to breathe, if I can live for the Lord, that is a blessing to me.
Before the devil and the world, I pray you become God's dignity like Job.
Since I have offered my life for the Lord, why should I spare anything else?

From the perspective of an Account or Information Security Auditor, the approver and the requester cannot be the same. It constitutes a violation of regulations. It is itself unlawful for the ego to remain alive and yet judge matters related to oneself. Discernment does not come merely from good intentions but requires becoming a being separated from oneself—a person in whom Jesus lives. 'The spiritual person judges all things, but is not subject to anyone's judgment' (1 Corinthians 2:15).
When you desperately wish to worship and desperately wish to kneel, even if that place is mud, a person who can bow before the Lord in the finest formal attire is beautiful.

When desires are many, the heart is uncomfortable. When you want to give much, the heart is happy. Love is not about necessarily bestowing many things. True love is felt when you wish to give but cannot—in that longing.
A person with unadorned self is different from a person with a results-oriented self that demands proof. They are generous people who can give and receive love regardless of achievement.

People think that living as they please is freedom. They look with disdain upon believers walking the narrow path. That is not freedom but slavery, being dragged by sin. When you become a servant obedient to the Lord, there is true freedom. Being free, you need not become a slave to sin.
Christians who eat the flesh of Christ and drink the blood of Christ no longer focus on themselves. For my flesh is Christ's flesh and my blood is Christ's blood.

Discouragement comes from where you fix your gaze. When facing unchanging objects and problems, when you look at yourself and your surroundings, you naturally become discouraged. I hope you look upon the Lord and His works.
When your heart feels cold, you are wearing different clothes. A person clothed in Jesus is warmed sufficiently by Jesus alone. If you pursue empty things, your heart will continue to feel cold.

The more worthless gems a jewel box contains, the more ornate it becomes. A person who possesses true gems does not compare or disparage their own or others' cases. A person who houses Jesus in an earthen vessel is already precious and needs no proof.
A person who seeks problems in others is foolish. It is like analyzing the cause while standing in a burning house. You must first escape and then search later. There is hope only when I change.
Pride is a choice. It is choosing to deceive your true self. This destroys self-esteem, creates anxiety, makes one prone to hurt, and causes one to hide.
Humility is not self-deprecation. The more humble a person is, the greater sense of self-worth they possess.

A psychology professor in university said that the most childish thing in the world is wanting to be treated with respect because you are older, wealthier, or more educated. The world may accommodate such things, but the church does not. If the Lord does not recognize it, it is nothing. What matters is not how people think but how God evaluates. You are not a mass-produced commodity but a masterpiece irreplaceable by anything else.
When you risk your life, you think it is a crisis situation. It is not. Not risking your life is the true crisis. Stake your life on the gospel that gave its life for you.

A person who has done many things and has much experience does not necessarily become a great leader. A leader is someone who loves the Lord and is so, so happy in Him. A person happy in the Lord is already conveying a great message through their life.

A humble person sees the Lord. Humility means knowing your true nature before the Lord, so naturally a humble person's heart becomes drawn to, sees, and becomes clear about the Lord. The fragrance of Jesus emanates from their life.

Many people live acting. They believe they can be happy. Rather, it is exhausting. They lived with effort, but it is not their life. Be faithful to yourself. You are a precious being, one of a kind in the world, and God's Son gave His life for that preciousness. Do not act; be faithful.
Do not compel; inspire.
Many people believe that if they are diligent, they will live a better life. And they blame themselves for laziness. In reality, it is not laziness but the absence of fire in the heart.
You need to relax and be yourself. God loves you as He is creating you.
If I do not permit it, no one can ruin my life.

Arranged flowers are showy. Yet they are dead. When you live to be seen, it is exhausting, lifeless, meaningless, and unhappy. Do not be conscious of others; look upon the Lord. When you are conscious of others, you must perform; but when you look upon the Lord, you can be boldly simple and truthful. A life of arranged flowers passes away; your life should become one planted by a flowing stream.

What matters more than what you eat is with whom you eat. Where you travel matters less than with whom you travel. Traveling economy class with someone you love is better than flying first class alone. Some people say, 'Why can't we fly first class with someone we love?' That is why people keep their distance.
A person who sees a problem and criticizes is one who has not entered from darkness into light. A person who sees a problem and weeps in prayer is family in God's kingdom.
A leader is a person of influence. In other words, a charismatic person. It means a friendly person. When people see you, you should be someone they want to meet.

What I have done and how much I have done is not important. That is the world's standard. What God desires is not numbers, popularity, or recognition, but that the Lord actually governs through me. Kneeling in my inner chamber is no worldly achievement, but if the Lord is governing, naturally the Lord will be revealed through me.

A person trying to be happy through their reputation, job, bank balance, or circumstances is truly pitiful. It is like being tossed by waves of the sea. Rather than such perishable things, where am I heading? Who am I running toward?—this is more important. Life is not made happy by numbers. It is determined by direction. Only when you understand life by direction does your life begin to be interpreted.

Many kings have fallen because their reign was short. Not because they were weak but because they became too strong and fell. When you try to defend and protect yourself, ultimately the fall is great. A person completely broken before the Lord seeks nothing but the Lord. David did not try to protect himself, so the Lord protected him instead.

It is not a great matter that believers are misunderstood. What is more important is that I do not misunderstand. When I firmly grasp and keep this, I am not deceived by darkness and give no occasion for it. Tragedy occurs when both the other person and I misunderstand simultaneously. A person who believes in the Lord has no need to misunderstand.

David was strong because he desired nothing. A person aiming for worldly goals easily falls into traps. I hope you desire nothing but the Lord. Saul, on the other hand, fears people and oppresses them to protect his kingship. Those who try to keep and those who try to let go live on different levels.
If you know how someone spends their holiday, you know what kind of person they are. Who are you?
If I am not broken, health, family, and church will be broken. If the Lord is the Savior of my spirit, He must also be the King of my self. - From the European Revival Conference -
Do not react with emotion; react with truth. Then you protect yourself and save others. God's righteousness enters in.
You are a one-of-a-kind masterpiece in this world.

Do not merely live day by day, but paint a magnificent self-portrait and live it out. That is the difference between the ordinary and the extraordinary. Resolve and trust to live not as you or others see yourself, but as God sees you. Then miracles happen.

Many people I have met were originally greedy and harsh young people. Now they have become soft and greedy for what is good. They thought they would live independently trusting in themselves, but they have become fools who cannot live without the Lord. That is doing it well. Peter was like that, John was like that, and the Apostle Paul was like that. You will become like that too.

You must have assurance in your heart. Does Jesus truly dwell within me? If not, you must come before the Lord as quickly as possible. You may need to seek counsel from a minister and request prayer. Because this is the most important matter.
Crying is love and bringing joy is love, but true love is becoming a single grain of wheat and denying yourself daily and bearing the cross.

A police car exceeds the speed limit for public safety, or an ambulance rushes and violates traffic laws—they are exempted because it is a life-saving situation. For matters of saving lives, receiving grace, and meeting the Lord, we must always proceed exceptionally.
The most basic, yet most fundamental faith is not being conscious of people's eyes and fixing all of yourself only on God present in that moment.

Lift your eyes and look at the world. What the world values is the fulfillment of desire. When you chase desire, you become entangled in darkness. It is foolish to dwell in darkness and say you cannot see. To those who look only to the Lord, accept death on the cross, and proceed, the world cannot bear their dignity. Therefore, there are two kinds of Christians: those who live pursuing the fulfillment of desire and those who lay themselves down and live.
Rise higher. Evil thoughts cannot follow to a higher place.

When David faced the greatest crisis of his life, being pursued by his own son Absalom, he divided his military into three divisions and delegated them. He even entrusted the final division to Ittai the Gittite. Ittai was a Philistine, an enemy of the Israelites. Even an enemy possessed the ability to move with intimacy in that difficult situation.

When a Christian goes to Starbucks and stirs their coffee, it is parting the Red Sea... [abridged]... I do not love only when I am strong or perfect. There is a saying: 'Women leave when they have nothing to give to men, and men leave when they can gain nothing from women.' Is true love also like this? When have you felt deep love from your parents? Not when they gave something. When they wanted to give something good but had nothing and felt regretful—that is when we feel the greatest love.

The reason God shows me the shortcomings of others is not to point fingers, but rather to cause me to look back at myself. I must remember that I too was once a sharp thorn to others, and I still am sometimes. I hope to become comfort and encouragement with gentleness and authority.
Embrace even a thorny person with a grateful heart. I too was once a sharp thorn to someone.
The greatest problem with leprosy is the inability to feel pain. If there is no hunger, thirst, or pain, one is seriously ill.
The saying 'Even a thorn, when embraced with a aching heart, becomes a rose' means that the embracing is done by me, but the transformation is accomplished by God.
The reason the church can be the church and worship can be worship is nothing but self-denial and the power of the Holy Spirit.
The purpose of advertising is marketing. If you proclaim truth while neither experiencing it nor practicing it, you will experience the most effective countereffect.
The reason I live is because of the Lord.

Love ramen. What devoted servants must be most careful about is having nothing to desire. Seek only the glory of the Lord and know how to step back; where you have disappeared, only the fragrance of Jesus should drift. Fourteen years ago when I left Korea, I said this: With just one bowl of ramen and a Bible a day, you can do anything... Try to love ramen simply; it is far simpler and easier than uncomfortable treatment or elaborate parties. And give all glory only to the Lord. Then you will see the glory of the Lord.

Next week is dawn worship, Wednesday service, evening prayer meeting... It's incredible. All the ministry staff are on retreat and I'm left alone. When I check my gifts, one that shows up is 'backup.'
Loyalty_and_Focus: Fall but Continue - Joseph experienced continuous setbacks, yet persevered without clear metrics or schedules. Human goals bring frustration, but God's character and promises bring hope and joy.

Speak What You Hear - When you try to speak your own words, you become exhausted by production and calculation. Simply speak the Word He freely gives and obey with simplicity. As Jesus said, 'When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and that I do nothing on my own but speak just what the Father has taught me.' When you don't rejoice, it is clear evidence that you have never earnestly and continuously sought the Lord. -August 14, 2014 'Those who love me will be loved by my Father, and I too will love them and show myself to them.' -Proverbs 8:17
Horses and Chariots - The reason God forbade Egypt's horses and chariots is inferiority complex.

Repentance, Not Correction - If you try to change others, you only leave wounds and lose people. The Lord called and established us to humble ourselves most and repent. If I don't live by His Word, no matter how good the message or fitting the logic, no one will be transformed. Living according to His Word is only possible when we think deeply about and behold Jesus. (Hebrews 12:2,3)

* When Frustrated at the Theater - As a child, I went to watch a movie, but it never started—only endless advertisements played. Faith is the same. When we preach without practicing, we live impoverished with nothing to share but grasping at clouds.

* What Matters More Than Projects and Preparation - If the Lord does not lead, human-planned projects will bear no fruit, and if the Lord does not protect, even thorough human preparation will crumble. 'Unless the Lord builds the house, those who build it labor in vain; unless the Lord watches over the city, the watchman stays awake in vain.' -Psalm 127:1
* Calculate Less and Pray More - Calculation shortens, but complex problems will be solved.
* Change - If real change is truly needed, God will transform the authorities He has established. If they refuse to change, He will exalt you in your quiet obedience.

* The Lord's Guarantee - The Lord will surely break down whatever security I subtly rely on and challenge me to depend entirely on Him alone. Therefore, those who trust the Lord last are left with nothing—like salvation obtained through fire.

* Wisdom - When you focus on important things, the rest takes care of itself. Anger has a life cycle until sunset; the mission extends to the kingdom of heaven. Rather than fear or anger, focus on your mission.

* The Source of Power - How much others are with you matters less than whether the Lord is with you. Even with the support of influential people, without Jesus it becomes nothing; even surrounded by millions, victory comes when the Lord is with you.
* Emptiness - This happens when you do God's work without God. In God, no work is ever meaningless.

* The Remnant are Victors, Not the Other Way Around - Good faith, celebrated belief, is not taking pride in people's reputation or becoming the protagonist of testimonies. It is looking only at the Lord and going to the end. Many are praised and recognized, but few who run to the end are common.

* Why Not to Turn Back - Without assurance of His guidance, you should not begin; once committed to obedience, you must stake your life on it to the end. If it is work worthy of your life, it must be more precious than self-satisfaction and self-justification, and that is seeing God's glory. We release everything else and go.

* Alabaster Jar - Life's every moment cannot guarantee the next; each moment is a final moment. Therefore, the faithful pour out everything as if this moment were the final worship. As if today were the last day, as if the last were eternal...

* Where You Stand Now - Moving Worship Christian Organisation - It is not important where or how I stand, but 'whom am I worshiping?' Wherever you exalt the Lord's name, I believe you will see the glory of heaven. The early church saints wore sheepskins and became food for beasts, endured burning and crucifixion, yet boldly commanded death itself because they saw heaven's glory. See God's glory. That is the only answer. 'The Son is the radiance of God's glory and the exact representation of his being, sustaining all things by his powerful word. After he had provided purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty in heaven.' -Hebrews 1:3

* Why We Survive - In crossing the desert, we don't die but live because we find an oasis when we should be dying. Since we are in God's hands, we must persevere to victory. 'I will not die but live, and will proclaim what the Lord has done.' -Psalm 118:17

* The Important Factor - I don't know whether good things will come or difficult things will arrive, but what matters is that we meet them Spirit-filled. He is the defender of the weak, comforter of those in need. Being with the Lord is power.

* The Power to Transform - History has never shown transformation through good speeches. Behind transformation is someone's shed blood. This generation speaks easily and listens easily through social media. There is a misconception that proclaiming something makes you live that way. To truly witness the glory of transformation, you must gladly pay the price. You must speak of living sacrifice. And you must live as you speak. Removing fakeness is a remarkable and great work. Therefore, first deny yourself.
* Are You at Peace Now? - Absolute peace is a believer's most basic duty. Without peace, there is no God, and consequently, you abuse yourself and trouble others.
* The Eternal Gospel - By continually hearing the eternal, glorious gospel of the cross for all, you can live unchangingly and gloriously.

* Paying the Price - It is a tragedy to live only for graduation, employment, marriage, security, and peaceful retirement, then stand before God. More than enjoying much, it is to be held by one and live clearly; rather than drawing more together and clinging to stable life, it is laying down your life to plant the cross on this earth. That is holy consumption.
* The Most Meaningful Work - The seeds sown with tears do not fail because the Lord looks upon them.

* Go and Make Disciples (Matthew 28:19) - The example shown in Acts is that rural areas are served by farmers, schools by teachers, finance by bankers, campuses by students, and law by lawyers, equipped and released without hesitation to serve. 'Go and make disciples' does not mean filling chairs on Sunday with Bible books, but going to parched land, planting crosses, and establishing a mobile church where the Spirit comes and intercedes in prayer. 'The churches in the province of Asia send you greetings. Aquila and Priscilla greet you warmly in the Lord, and so does the church that meets at their house.' -1 Corinthians 16:19 'Give my greetings to the brothers at Laodicea, and to Nympha and the church in her house.' -Colossians 4:15

* Reformed Perspective - Calling Jesus 'Lord' means we are His servants. We are someone's servant, and when we become Jesus' servant, we become citizens of the kingdom. Therefore, you are a 'servant of the Lord,' and where you stand now, you are a pastor. A servant of the Lord is not one who uproots pillars and goes to seminary, but one who cannot help but obey because Jesus is Lord.

* Uncommon Father - Knowledge and titles are glorious but difficult to sacrifice for. Life begets life, and the gospel bears fruit only through shed blood. That is why such work does not happen commonly. 'For though you have countless guides in Christ, you do not have many fathers.' -1 Corinthians 4:15

* Called from Commonness - Peter's occupation was a fisherman catching fish, and his dream was a full catch. Paul's dream was to continue Gamaliel's line as a rabbi. But the Lord called them to be fishers of men, a light to the Gentiles. Because there were people naive enough to stake their lives on that calling, I now believe in Jesus, who became life.

* How to Break a Rule - Rules must be kept, but there are times to break them to save a life. When forced to break a rule, you stake your life to save a life. 'Go, gather together all the Jews that are present in Susa, and fast for me. Do not eat or drink for three days, night or day. I and my attendants will fast as you do. When this is done, I will go to the king, even though it is against the law. And if I perish, I perish.' -Esther 4:16

* Consistency is Power - In novels or movies, we sometimes see ghosts sharing friendship and love, but that is extremely romantic imagination. The forces of darkness have incredible consistency—killing. For 6,000 years, they have never lost focus or compromised. But people so easily stray from Jesus. Consistency is power.

* The Only Competitiveness of Christian Workers - Can't drink, can't flatter well, can't inflate or embellish, and don't join in cursing others behind their backs. The only competitiveness is faithfulness and wisdom. Look at Daniel. 'In every matter of wisdom and understanding about which the king questioned them, he found them ten times better than all the magicians and enchanters in his whole realm.' -Daniel 1:20
* Essence - Resolve to be faithful only to the essence. Things unrelated to faith need not provoke anger.
* What Matters Most - Character matters more than ability. Truth matters more than miracles. It's okay to live slightly lacking. If you stand before the Lord, you are already victorious.

* Redeem the Time - Though we want to redeem the time, we do not have such ability. The only way people can redeem time is to be filled with the Spirit. Those led by the Spirit redeem time and live in victory. 'Be very careful, then, how you live—not as unwise but as wise, making the most of every opportunity, because the days are evil.' -Ephesians 5:15-16

* How to Overcome Mannerism - If you love, you pray. Not praying means not loving. If your heart wants to pray but prayer doesn't happen, that too is not loving. Apostle Paul thought only of Jesus Christ, so through so many years and challenges, he remained unchanged and ran toward the goal.

* Irony - If there is no bread in the bakery and you cannot eat at the restaurant, people will take loans and advertise, but soon such business cannot operate normally. You say believe in life, yet there is no change; you say death is overcome, yet you lose to materialism, desire, jealousy, and envy—that is putting on a show, and God will not support such a show. Just as a company's collapse accelerates without proper accounting and audits, faith too will worsen if we avoid reality and only chatter about words. The key is to stop the show and run to God for refuge. That is the essence. 'The Lord will redeem his servants; no one who takes refuge in him will be condemned.' -Psalm 34:22

* True Faith - I cannot understand the existence of star pastors and influential believers. So everyone vies for success and recognition. Then people's evaluation and gaze become important, and we must receive praise. Noah did not build the ark because his shipbuilding skills were superior. Noah built the ark on a mountain by faith, and the Bible says that came only from the fear of God. If you skillfully build a good ship for yourself based on greed, that is not faith but excrement. True faith comes only when you act in fear of Him. 'By faith Noah, when warned about things not yet seen, in holy fear built an ark to save his family. By his faith he condemned the world and became heir of the righteousness that is in keeping with faith.' -Hebrews 11:7

* Real Danger - Not working hard is not a serious crisis. Not acting in faith is truly dangerous. If you do God's work with a heart of martyrdom, staking your life, but it is your own greed, you commit sin with your life. Please stop worshiping and praying to fulfill desire. Faith is to fear only Him.

* Kingdom Church - The true church is not organization and institution but a community bought with Jesus' blood and conceived by the Spirit. Not a city building banks and anxiously building walls, but a place where Jesus' blood and life flow abundantly and override the waves of sin. The Kingdom Church God desires is an apostolic church focused on the command Jesus gave to His disciples. When you resolve to martyrdom and stake your life on Jesus, the church is established not only in the church but also in the world, on campuses, and even on streets.

* When There Is Much Resolve but No Implementation - Spiritual Neutrality - It is because you try to maintain spiritual neutrality. People are bound to be used. Either as tools of righteousness or in darkness' grasp. Only when used righteously do you avoid being used by darkness.

* The Mobile Church - On a flight to New Orleans, four elderly gentlemen, facing an unexpected delay, delivered emotion through an acapella performance as if it were a scheduled concert. At any time, anywhere, making God's throne and worshiping with all your heart is power and a mobile church. That is why I stand here now, and it is true life as light and salt. When you prepare your lifetime awake for the gospel for the moment, wherever that person is and whatever time and space, you will see God's glory and become a mobile church.

* Golden Hour - 'Golden Hour' refers to the time that determines the survival and prognosis of critically injured emergency patients with circulatory and cardiopulmonary damage. Over the past 10 years, young people of different cultures in this age are rapidly leaving the church. If we miss this final timing now, we will lose a generation, acceleration of decline will intensify, and this will impact the church, home, and society entirely. When there is opportunity to take responsibility, hear the Spirit's voice with the authority given and obey—we can save all and even reclaim what was taken. -responsibility for the golden hour.
* When the Gospel Becomes Power - When you live the gospel, the gospel's power works. The gospel becomes truly gospel as much as I live it out.

* Clear Reason - What perfects people is the Word written by God's inspiration, and what establishes and benefits community are the gifts and callings distributed according to the Spirit's will. We must discover the clear reason why people are not perfected and community is not established. If the cause doesn't change, no matter how much effort, the result won't change.
* How to Hear the Word - If you hear the Word but forget it, Satan snatches it away. Hearing the Word begins with the ears, but it becomes power and manifests when you hear it with your life.
* God's People - God's people are those who draw the joy and life that exist in heaven down to this parched earth.

* Let the Fearful Depart - Those with Him are courageous. Those who hear His voice obey without calculation. 'Now if you are afraid or discouraged, you may leave.' So twenty-two thousand men left, while ten thousand remained. -Judges 7:3
* How to Have a Good Day - Trust that this morning God is worthy of allowing a good day. There can be no good day that I don't believe in.
* Prime Time - Don't regret when things were going well; pray right now. This very moment of prayer will surely become your finest moment.

* When you quit a full-time job, you realize something: time remains abundantly, and you truly recognize your solitude. And you must possess a faith and confession that would have no regret even living such a life your entire life.

* When you trust, obedience becomes easy. One who obeys the Lord's will is a servant who acknowledges and trusts the Lord's sovereignty. When you trust, obedience becomes easy. 'Behold, I am the servant of the Lord; let it be to me according to your word.' And the angel departed from her. -Luke 1:38

* Do not try to keep; worship instead. Those who try to keep will be robbed, and those who try to rob destroy themselves. Therefore, all the victors in Scripture were bleeding worshippers, worshippers who let go.
* Your true faith Saul uses human shortcuts when impatient, but David sings to the Lord in crisis. Their results reflect their choices.
Go, return on your way to the wilderness, you shall anoint -1 Kings 19:15
* Anointing has reason and brings responsibility.
* A life without regret stakes your life.
* Hungry Socrates or satisfied pig? Will you be a hungry Socrates? Or will you be a satisfied pig? I will simply be loyal in the form the Lord called me ^^
* Two reasons for unceasing prayer: because there is a vision, and because of love.
* I am the way. Do not envy what is not my way, but give glory to the path given to me. The path the Lord gave is glorious and also the best way. Hallelujah

* Butterfly effect Saul's falling into the foolish envy of David stemmed from his failure to fully obey in the war with Amalek, and Saul's incomplete obedience began with his foolish act of making sacrifice himself without waiting for Samuel. Present choices are closely related to future choices.

* The person people follow Whether foolish and ignorant Bilee works hard causing problems, or whether a godly and capable person works with resentment, it is all useless. When we fear God, we become true leaders. And he said to his son Solomon, 'Be strong and courageous and do the work. Do not be afraid or discouraged, for the Lord God, even my God, is with you. He will not fail you or abandon you until all the work for the service of the house of the Lord is finished. The divisions of the priests and the Levites are ready for all the service of God's house, and every willing man skilled in any craft will help you with all the work. The officials and all the people will be entirely at your command.' -1 Chronicles 28:20,21
* The person who attracts people The faithful people who followed David did not do so because of higher salaries than Saul's people. Those who obey God have heavenly attraction.

* Last Christmas In those days when the Lord called me, why in that manner, why by that method..., and why did He make me walk such a thorny path..., Though I cannot understand, God is still good, truly good, and faithful. The happiest moment of my life was when God made me weep... That God finds me again. The power of the Lord's blood is sufficient. - May 2007
* Mission A mission is not something someone can order or prevent. It is what one who stands before the Lord does.

* Stewardship and provision If something necessary is lacking, it is for two reasons: either the one who should provide did not provide, or the one who received did not steward it. These are two reasons why Jesus becomes scarce.
* Win like this Strive for victory in the field to which you belong. Not for cheap comfort to survive a day or two, but to share eternal opportunity.
* Why it does not become corrupted Paul preached the cross in all his writings, words, and life. He was not corrupted until his very head was severed. Because he remained close to the cross.

* Champion recognizes champion Those of faith recognize faith, and those of glory recognize glory. If a fleshly person recognizes me, it is a sign I am perishing. Rather, when worldly people hate, resist, and envy me, then I am truly walking the right path. Therefore, lift your head.
* A life without regret Even if losing or losing more, if I returned to that place, I would make the same choice. There is nothing more glorious than the gospel of the cross.

* How to live out your destiny The situation I am facing now was something the Lord knew long ago. If this is the life He has allowed me, then it is my part to live it faithfully and holily. I neither flee from pain nor turn away from the joy He gives, but respond and move forward in faith. Before my form was formed, Your eye saw me; and in Your book all the days ordained for me were written when as yet there was not one of them. -Psalm 139:16

* Great power brings much responsibility If a powerless person has poor character, he causes not many problems. But if a person in high position with great ability is like that, it becomes a serious problem.

* Shame Though the Korean church receives much criticism, when I meet jewel-like faithful pastors, my head bows. With endless shame I pray that such precious souls be entrusted to such honored servants.
* Power The greatest power is the power to be with the Lord. The ability to be lonely for the Lord. Everyone retreats at this stage.
* Be bold This does not mean the situation is okay, but that 'God is with you.'
* Respected leader Whether a person is truly respected can be known not from the person they serve, but from those they governed. This age longs for a leader recognized by those under him.
* True friend The bee that comes to see the flower is the first to leave when the flower withers.
* Why regret It is not regret from failing to win hearts or achieve desired success, but regret from not being given more to the Lord that constitutes true faith.

* Duty of the superior The privilege of the haves is not tyranny. It is the ability to serve that God has lent for a time. Just as water's potential energy changes to produce electricity, God's glory appears in proportion to my humility.
* Obey according to the calling Not coming when called or insisting on going when not called—both are arrogance. God does not look at ability. He makes those He calls able.

* Never said to do well Seeking perfection is seeking to do well. God never said to do well. Rather than being recognized for doing what was not asked and doing it well, He desires faithfulness and diligence in what He commanded. Perfectionism can be a burden to me and a wound to others.

* When greed mixes with gifts, gifts become curses There is a chairman's daughter who has never gotten her hands wet in life, yet uses that great ability and privilege just to reverse-park an airplane, and there are people of faith who, with little strength, faithfully and unknowingly sustain and advance the world. That is the difference.

* Responsibility of those who handle iron and fire Iron sharpens iron and fire kindles fire. If you miss the precious opportunity to meet iron and face fire, all your effort becomes futile. Therefore, the responsibility of those who handle iron and fire is great.
* Change Change is fearful and difficult, but regardless of surface results, it is always valuable when seen from afar.
* Do what you are told Do not be arrogant; humble yourself as Jesus did.

* How He heals When facing trials, you need the intercession of faithful believing friends and leaders. Pray that wounded hearts be healed. 'But I have prayed for you, that your faith may not fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.' (Luke 22:32)

* You are the Lord's moving church I hope you are always awake and prepared by the gospel for this moment your whole life. I hope you bear the cross that should be offered and can shed blood and flowers without hesitation. Wherever there is a people and space with such a person, God's glory will be seen and it will become a moving church.
* More than what I love More than what I love, more than what I bless, God desires to love and bless. That is why He commands us to believe and intercede for those we love.

* To discouraged friends In my youth, despite working hard and doing my best, when results betrayed me, I became discouraged, powerless, and lost motivation. Then I heard God's voice toward me: 'It is not in vain. Much is prepared for you.' - May 1990, Pusan God never makes mistakes.
* Do not be discouraged Discouragement is like an umbrella before a hurricane to protect oneself. What God desires is to have God's perspective and to look to Him. Be bold.

* Success apart from the Word is outstanding evil No matter how good a school you graduated from, how enviable your workplace, how much achievement you are recognized for in your field, or how much success you are praised for in ministry, if you depart from God's Word, you are simply doing what is openly evil.
* Faith initiative Faith begins with responding to the prompting of the Holy Spirit. When you hear His voice today, do not harden your heart.
* Morning prayer Today as well, I rely not on my ability and strength, but on the Lord's mercy and compassion.
* Joy of obedience over pleasure of sin You must become addicted to the peace and joy that obedience brings rather than the empty pleasure that sin and self-gratification give.
* Do the basics The most important thing is to faithfully do the basics. That means focusing solely on the Lord first. When focus is lost, anger, debauchery, and regret follow.
* The ability to start again You simply start again from the basics.
* My will, God's will 2 At first you struggle to abandon your will and choose God's will. Later, when your will becomes one with God's will, that is faith.

* My will, God's will It is commendable to break your will and follow God's will, yet you will still find yourself stumbling. Faith is not about breaking my will, but about yielding space for the Lord to turn my will. Therefore, spiritual maturity is not managing my will through law, but being aligned with the Lord's will through grace.
* Guidance God makes a way in the wilderness where I stand that I could not have anticipated.

* Absence of mission, not communication The confusion arises because the person responsible evades decision and refuses responsibility while the person not responsible insists on deciding. First, clarify the mission. Then everything else will fall into order.

* The reason the world is disappointed in the church is not because the church has abandoned their joy. It is because the church has abandoned God's joy, so they see no difference from themselves and become disappointed. When we fail to become God's servants and become servants of the world, people not only become disappointed but angry. Should I please people or should I please God? If I seek the pleasure of people, I am not a servant of Christ. - Galatians 1:10

* Do not be greedy; be loyal in the margins. Greed is the desire to take what the Lord has not given. That is why people seek others, deceive, and are deceived themselves. To seek what the Lord has given is called a calling, and to move forward with gratitude for it is called loyalty.
* What to do in a rebellious generation: to exalt the Lord in the place where I stand. To exalt the Lord higher than the world's malice.
* Fill your portion. Before hastily judging success or failure of work, quietly fill your portion. Prayer that has been lost, worship that has collapsed, and obedience pushed aside by greed.

* If you do not do what is right, you rot. You must pour out everything in your heart. If you do not pour it out and hide it, it rots and bears the smell and fruit of death. Trust in him at all times, you people; pour out your hearts to him, for God is our refuge. Psalm 62:8
* What I must confirm: It is foolish for me to try to confirm my own future. I am to confirm my faith in Him, and God confirms my entire life.

* True courage: Courage to look only to the Lord and to be forgotten. To let only the Lord receive all glory, honor, and power. Am I now trying to please people or God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a servant of Christ. - Galatians 1:10

* The secret of victory: Not hiding greed and covering it with justification, but revealing weakness and holding to His promise. There was a long war between the house of Saul and the house of David. David grew stronger and stronger, while the house of Saul grew weaker and weaker. - 2 Samuel 3:1
* The reason for stinginess: The reason we cannot intercede and help is not because we lack time. It is because we do not enjoy living eternally with God.

* The reason for boldness: Since there is no clear vision and no self-realization that must happen, there is nothing to fear losing. The Lord's grace has always been sufficient, and it will be sufficient in the future as well.
* It is not bad luck. It is not bad luck but God's love. He interferes with affection, wanting you to be prepared and become more humble.

* Good players keep going. A good player is not one who never gets hit. Even if hit, a good player is one who throws endless punches. Continue God's Word and prayer, repentance and gratitude, regardless of emotion and circumstance.
* Maturity does not happen automatically. Maturity and revival are always accompanied by bloodshed. Where God's glory is, there is a leader who sheds blood.

* When you have lost your taste: There was a time when they sold ten rice cakes in front of the school for 100 won. The only ingredients were gochujang and green onions, but it is difficult to find that taste now. If the taste has changed, you must know the reason. You cannot insist that changed taste is the same taste. The rice cakes do not choose people; people have the right to seek their taste.

* When purpose is lost, there is only coercion. When you become obsessed with what (results), you lose precious people. When you focus only on how (methods) to obtain results and evaluation, you lose essence and identity. When you determine 'why' you do it, people who stake their lives on it will emerge.

* How to give glory to God: To attribute God's glory to results, you can only hold to humanism that disregards means and methods. God's glory is given through attitude, not results, and results are under God's control. Well done, good and faithful servant. Because you have been faithful in small things... - Matthew 25:21
Whatever He has prepared, He did with love. Go in peace.
* The difference in class: You should not boast about not doing wrong or depraved things. Rather, you should truly grieve that you do not feel moved and do not long for Him.
* One question: If later you are criticized and misunderstood for something you did not do, can you think of me and be 'gentle'? - 2011 Staff Meeting
* God's voice: Humble yourself and repent, love with your whole life, be loyal unto death. Then you will have done well.
* Stand on the margins: Many people want to stand at the center, but nobody wants to linger on the periphery. Yet the neglected and shadowed place is the Lord's place.
* Why you must be silent: To acknowledge that God is God and to accomplish God's will, not the will of people.
* The power of the blood: The power of the blood is not a one-time license. It must always be proclaimed, drunk, sprinkled, and applied.
* Ministry is not a picnic: Ministry is not joking around or going on a picnic. It is going to die. The problem is not that it is difficult, but that we begin without resolving to die.

* If you pray, He will work: If I work, only I work. But if I pray, He will work. Fill the necessary and requested portion of prayer. Then you will see the Lord's work in every field. Pray so that the Lord may bless you and your community.
* What I feel when going on a business trip: Prayer is so sweet.
* Receive guidance: To those who become nameless and faceless, looking to the Lord, God gives faithful guidance.
* The reason for anxiety: Because we are attempting things God has not called us to do. For the work God commands, He personally prepares and provides a helping hand, so we do not worry but trust.
* We learn more when we teach: If someone else drives or uses navigation, even after going many times, we do not know that road well. But when I drive myself, then it truly becomes my road.
* A life with no reverse gear: An airplane has no reverse gear. In the sky, reverse is not needed. When you embrace a high calling, you do not go backward.
* When a new country you visit for the first time is not that interesting: Because the nation you already belong to is already sufficiently meaningful.
* A question that might seem slightly irreverent: 'God, what should I use You for?' It does not mean to use God. It means to set aside human methods and truly depend on Him once.

* What can I do?: Rather than asking what I can do, I should ask whether I am entrusting myself to the Lord's hands. If God does not give, I have received nothing. If He does not permit, nothing is accomplished.
* Do not decide arbitrarily: I do not decide on my own will. Because I hate pride. If the Lord decides and guides, He will take responsibility.
Building the virtue of faith.
* The ability to suppress repeated attacks: If the enemy repeatedly challenges me with the same problem, I must also continuously hold more firmly to the Lord's Word. Victory is determined there.
The most precious people: Those who awaken me to God's glory and show me that those ordinary things were not in vain are my driving force.

* The war of concentration: Whether crazy about the world or crazy about Jesus, success or failure is ultimately determined by how much and how intensely you are 'crazy' for someone. That is why you cannot dilute it. The ability is in how to maintain the pure concentrate.
* Choose the cross: You must choose to respond through the cross. Completion, like conception of salvation, is through the cross.
* A life without regret: Living the best moments for eternity.

* Glass becomes diamond: When a relationship of faith begins, it is fragile like glass, but through years of tears and joy, it becomes a beautiful diamond-like driving force. God's people are not easily hastily made.

* The believer's foundation: No matter how sound your own opinion is, respecting God who accomplishes and fulfills the work. Singing in the storm because of God's plan, which is greater than my thoughts.

* Prayer request: Brother Jang Byung-hwa, a member of the youth group, has had his father pass away. At 17:00 today, the life support device will be removed. Please pray for Sister Gang and Brother Byung-hwa.
* The required portion of prayer: He desires us to pray. Because what He wants to give is great and high.
* The rules of the game: Revival, not Survival.
* The foundation of faith: Not taking God lightly.

* The core of disciple training: Disciple training is not a systematized program or a successful event. It is stubbornly and continuously repeating the cross given to me in any circumstance. Continuity and repetition require the courage to stake your life and shed blood.
* When faith is shown: It means when my life's salvation is most needed, when faith is shown to the Lord. Faith appears when the level of prayer is raised.
* Main loyalty and unfailing: Keeping the Lord's Day and faithfully watching the main broadcast.
* The ability to win simply is to hear the gospel daily and confirm it in your life.
* The reason for collapse: If you look really honestly, prayer collapsed, so life collapsed. Gather together and pray continuously without interruption.

* How to reach the summit without giving up: I once climbed Glen Nevis, the highest mountain in Scotland, the highest in Britain. I could never have made it alone, but my eldest daughter Sharon told me not to give up during the 7-hour climb. The ability to complete is achieved through togetherness.

* O sleeper, why do you sleep? "The captain came and said to him, 'What do you mean, sleeper? Arise, call out to your God! Perhaps the God will give a thought to us so that we do not perish.'" - Jonah 1:6

* The conclusion of hardship: Please do not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from God's mouth. If you worry about bread, you lose bread and lose other things too. But if you hold to His presence and fellowship, you live through His word. He humbled you, let you hunger, and fed you with manna that neither you nor your ancestors had known, to make you understand that man does not live by bread alone but by every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord. - Deuteronomy 8:3
* The purpose of the wilderness is to humble me. So when you humble and obey before God and His authority, your problems are no longer problems when you fulfill the purpose.

* It is not because you don't know the answer. You are not lost because you don't know the answer. Storms and wilderness always have reasons and purposes, and when those purposes are fulfilled, they immediately stop. Wanting a solution while refusing to be changed means you haven't humbled yourself further. The highest prayer of those who face tribulation is 'humble me more.' The problem of human life is not that you don't know the answer, but the issue of obedience. Remember that the Lord your God led you all these forty years in the wilderness to humble you and to test you, to know what was in your heart, whether you would keep His commandments or not. He humbled you, let you hunger, and fed you with manna that neither you nor your ancestors had known, to make you understand that man does not live by bread alone but by every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord. - Deuteronomy 8:2-3
* Office is not holiness. A holy office does not automatically make oneself holy. Because it is a place where you hold God as holy, you must be more strict in disciplining yourself.
* The way to keep faith from falling: But I have prayed for you that your faith may not fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers. - Luke 22:32
* The standard of recovery: In the Lord, with the Saints. Remain in the Lord and have fellowship with the saints. Those who are 'in Him' and 'together' are the most powerful.
* The ability not to fear: When you give the first, give beforehand, give gladly, give good things, and willingly give precious things, you will not be afraid.
* An excellent leader: Let go of trying to be excellent and simply do faithfully what the Lord commands. You collapse because you try too hard to be excellent.
* The sin of a Christian: Not being moved. True faith is being moved daily and at every moment through faith.

* Spiritual warfare - How the enemy makes you despise what he's prepared: There is a persistent intention in what happens to us. The enemy's intention is to steal, kill, and destroy. He wants us to respond that way in situations to complain about, relationships to hate, events to be discouraged by, and things to be angry about. We do not have the ability to immediately control circumstances, but we can choose our response. Especially when we let go of our rights and speak with a heart opposite to the situation, the strongholds of darkness begin to crumble.
* I have nothing to give. So I am happy. Silver and gold I do not have, but what I have I give you: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, rise up and walk. - Acts 3:6
* What I like vs. what I should like: If I focus on what I like that God has not given, I miss the very best that God gives. Let go of what prevents the best that God gives from coming.
* Move in a straight line. The answer to life is immediate obedience. Let go of rationalization and masks, abandon shortcuts, and run straight toward the Lord.

* Why we fail in the World Cup: The way to quickly do well in a short time is to change physical condition and tactics. Doing that, you can get through the preliminaries reasonably well. What is really important is that soccer is played with a soccer ball. When the ball doesn't stick to your body, you are anxious and the watching people are always anxious. What is really frustrating is trying to find the cause in non-essentials when you've lost in essentials. It takes a long time and much cost (training) to hold to the essence. What makes foreign coaches (revivalists) somewhat advantageous is that there are fewer distractions when focusing on essentials and it takes less time. In terms of faith, when unity with the ball (the Lord) is achieved, even when defense (trials) is attached, there is room (boldness) and you dominate the game (sovereignty). The essence shown in Acts was the Holy Spirit and the Word, the cross and obedience. For an emergency patient, it is urgent to minimize all unnecessary things and have them meet with a specialist.

* Why is there no power? Because we speak risking our lives and listen reluctantly. Listen while risking your life. If you do not sow seeds weeping, no matter how diligently you go, you will reap nothing.
* Be courageous: It means to act in faith.
* Do you think God is slow? God is not slow; He is slow to anger.
* Do not worry. When you meet something to worry about, do what God has simply prepared. Do what you ought to say and do.

* When you face temptation: Do not think it strange. What should come has come, and you should simply respond as God expects. Give thanks and pray with confidence. My brethren, count it all joy when you fall into various temptations, knowing that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance have its complete work, so that you may be complete and whole, lacking nothing. - James 1:2-4

* The fastest way: In the exodus, the fastest shortcut is the southern route. But God made them enter from the east. As a result, it provided the motive for Normandy and Inchon landing operations—'God's masterpiece.' When immediate paths to work and marriage are not visible and life seems to take too long a detour, you must believe there is a shortcut with God. Those who walk with God experience reversal.

* Life that needs no encouragement (infinite power): There is no need to encourage a mother that she must love her child. It is life. If you move by encouragement and praise, you have not yet overcome being a beginner. We call those who move and are faithful through life, not encouragement and slogans, 'people of mission.'

* Mission over success: Mission is a concept far beyond the issue of success. In worldly eyes, it would be success and completion if refugee Esther became queen. But from there, Esther holds to her mission and advances saying, 'If I perish, I perish.' The question is not 'Is it difficult or easy?' but 'Is it God's will?'
* It is always so. I look only to the Lord who does new things within me.
* Ministry is to stop work, redirect direction, and cause us to run toward that eternal goal.

* The reason for fighting: We fight a good fight. Not only to protect, but even more to take what has been taken. Rather than wavering from relationships and circumstances, we will gain and take. Perfect yourselves in the likeness of victors. You who pray are already victorious.

* The content of faith: When you hear His voice, you must not be stubborn. You must believe that He speaks through me and works through me. Before the Anakites of Canaan, though God was with them, Israel, bound by their own judgment that they were like grasshoppers, denied God's work and were rejected. But Joshua and Caleb, who confessed, 'God uses me and receives glory,' became as they believed. Without faith it is impossible to please God. And those who come to God must believe that He exists and that He rewards those who earnestly seek Him. - Hebrews 11:6
* Everything done not in faith is sin: Not acting in faith means not looking to the Lord. Enthusiasm without looking to the Lord is empty, and passion without focus is only fearful.

* Ministry is Delivery: It is delivering God's grace, that is, His very self and presence. Not information about Him or utility, but being with Him. So the responsibility of the one delivering is to faithfully go between God's post office and the soul's home.
* The reason for fear: You are trying too hard to do well. If you seek to be faithful, you will have peace.
* Why you should neither fear nor be arrogant: Because it is the Lord who uses you. Leave the place where it is not yourself and be faithful only in what the Lord uses.

* The solution in crisis is basic: When you face a crisis, hold to the basics. Do thoroughly again what you were doing, be humble, and a way will appear. Do not compare with others; hold to your Jesus.
* The only way to keep the fire: To sustain it. Persistence is power. Do not let the fire on the altar go out. - Leviticus 6:13

* Praise team attire: 1. Garment of holiness: Psalm 29:2 Ascribe to the Lord the glory due His name; worship the Lord in holy array. 2. Garment of respect: Respect the culture of the congregation and church. If you say personally you have no problem so it's fine, that is immaturity. 3. Garment of manner: Short skirts on the high pulpit; tight shirts that burden like this—even renowned praise teams from free-worship-pursuing foreign countries maintain the most conservative standards when it comes to clothing. 4. Do not be sensitive to trends: 'Everyone wears skinny these days.' Then it would be better to go to karaoke. We must be beautiful not because we follow trends but because we follow mission. You are the face of the church. 5. Prepared clothes over expensive brands: With the same amount of offering, seeing the attitude of giving makes me think God has a special delight in offerings. Check your attire with all your best effort. 6. Wear character: Put on compassion, mercy, humility, gentleness, and patience. - Colossians 3:14
* Guard precious freedom: When I want freedom my own way, I lose precious freedom and protection. Eden was lost because of disobedience.

* A community that prays while looking at people's faces is unexpectedly smooth. Because they do not feel it is worth responding to. But when a praying community stops praying, it gnashes its teeth and rushes. Because it knows that opportunity comes only then.

* When you want to cut the cord: When everything is bothersome and community burdensome, it means you are tempted, and the one tempting you is Satan. That cord you want to cut is ultimately Jesus. God, who pulls you back from the cliff with blood-stained hands, has never thought of giving up even once.
* The major of believers: To love to the end and depart.
* Victory in life: Victory in life is victory in faith. Faith is not a peripheral matter of 'what did you do or not do?' It is the matter of what is the priority and whether you can sustain it.
* Boldness comes from humility: Lack of boldness is because you have lost your place. When you pray, you become bold. Because humility that finds your place comes.

* The way God works: God works through people. Therefore He reveals Himself through obedience to His guidance and word. Then He also works sovereignly on those who do not obey His guidance and word. Respond with obedience.

* A dangerous promise: If there is stability from living a lifetime with someone who doesn't please your heart, and a dangerous promise of going with someone your heart yearns for, which would you choose? Live meaningfully even for a day. Recover and be sustained.

* Dangerous familiarity: Becoming familiar is convenient but also becomes dangerous. Events break out from where you are most confident. Especially in faith, you see this when you lose your initial heart.
* When I am powerless: Now it is time for God to work.

* To friends in temptation: The purpose of temptation is unconditional destruction, and the one causing it is Satan (diabolos). Do the opposite of what he wants. What he wants is for you to not endure, to stop, and to be separated.
* The way to focus and sustain: When you pursue what is high and eternal, that happens. You are captivated by the Lord. So much so that other things seem meaningless...
* Obey, and then you will hear the Lord's voice. When you obey the Word, without regard to people's eyes, you will see God's glory.
* Training is difficult because its purpose is to keep soldiers alive in urgent combat situations, not to let them die.

* Birthday gifts: Giving ten years' worth of gifts all at once does not bring happiness. The reason we wait until the birthday to give a gift we've already purchased is because gifts gain their value through the waiting.
* Relative poverty vs. Absolute satisfaction: Do not grieve over unfulfilled desires that God has not given you. Instead, rejoice in quietly walking the path the Lord has set before you.
* Try to stop me once. The devil may hinder me, but he cannot stop me. Relative hindrances cannot block absolute pursuit.
* Lamentable research: Thinking 'I will do well without kneeling down.'

* True victory: Rather than focusing on the adversary, give your heart to the Lord and refuse to compromise. What is certain is that the adversary will obtain nothing that he desires. This is because Jesus bore the cross.

* The mentor's duty: The mentee already knows where he should go. He merely imagines whether there might be other options instead of taking that path. The mentor's role is to ensure the mentee does not turn away from that path he already knows.
* Worthless things get trampled underfoot. A disciple without self-denial is salt that has lost its taste.

* Why cursing is wrong: One should not curse, but when a person of faith curses, it is especially grave. They share gracious words on social media while denying themselves with curses in actual life, thereby committing the sin of rapidly defiling themselves, God's temple. Be someone whose inside and outside are the same.
* A life without regret: Not a life of fulfilled desires, but a life that delights in the Lord.
