* Crisis: Has there ever been a time when crisis was not a crisis? When things are difficult, they are very difficult, and when they are easy, we become careless and hardship comes. In any case, we carry the cross and go through a land of famine; let us become a single grain of wheat like Jesus.

* Rejoice when facing your limits: There has never been a time without limits, and through those limits we are driven to pray. Pray for revival that cannot be blocked by people, traditions, customs, or prejudices.
By you I can run against a troop, and by my God I can leap over a wall. (2 Samuel 22:30)
* Proclaim boldly without timidity. Evangelism and missions are not begging; they are offering a privilege as a gift.
* Do not imagine; humble yourself. Resolve to humble yourself and serve without imagining. If you imagine, you will collapse, but if you humble yourself and serve, He will build everything up.

* A prayer to begin the day: For this one day, with the breath the Lord has given me, may I be humbled and serve, leaving no memory among people, yet standing worthy before the Lord, fixing my eyes only on Him each moment.
* What is shameful: It is not shameful to be cursed at and criticized. It is shameful to abandon the truth for the sake of being welcomed and recognized.
* A wish: O God, grant that I may serve in humility in places where I am needed, nameless and without recognition.

* Futile attempts: Victorious obedience means laying down my attempts to protect and defend myself and placing my security in the Lord. This is the counter-spirit that smashes Satan's schemes. Do not guard yourself; rather, obey Him.
* The difference between professionals and amateurs: Amateurs win by chance and talk much, but professionals win when they must win. Victory is a battle of priorities.
* Warning signs: Collapse does not happen suddenly. The knees of prayer collapsed first.
* Foundational technique: Do not rush to give answers. Love is patient.
* Day and night: The Lord's work does not distinguish between night and day. The Lord's zeal will make you beautiful.

* Absolute advantage: Do not worry excessively about making mistakes. The Lord's power and grace are greater than my failings. Simply focus your motive on pleasing the Lord. If your motive is good, the Lord takes responsibility for the results.

* Good that works together comes from sovereignty: When evil and good work together to produce good, it is because the Lord is alive and working. Become the hands and feet of the Lord, fulfilling His will and sovereignty.

* Unavoidable sin: A car collision is not a sin. The real cause is driving a vehicle with faulty brakes in reverse on a highway. You should have stopped at the beginning. Both sin and holiness are matters of the first button.
* Do not guess; humble yourself more and serve.

* Charging and discharging: When spiritual supply is weak, discharging of my life occurs. Eventually it becomes a matter of time, but spiritual famine brings powerlessness and an inability to be happy. If the community I belong to provides spiritual charging, that is truly something to be grateful for. But if not, it means I have a calling. If there is no calling yet only spiritual loss (discharging) continues to occur, it means I should move to another community.

* Worship while bleeding: When the spirit of worship is poured out, worshiping becomes the greatest joy. Also, He pours out the spirit of worship on those who worship with all their heart. Therefore, a leader is someone through whom the spirit of worship is poured out, maintaining continuous wholehearted worship in a place where no one else worships.
* Song of victory 3: You must be sensitive to whether you are now charging or discharging. True charging occurs only when a current higher than the battery's capacity flows through it.
* Song of victory 2: The part I do not give and keep hidden becomes a test later. Do not become a weapon of unrighteousness for the flesh; become a weapon of righteousness for righteousness.

* When facing difficulty: Difficulty is something over which I have no choice. That means the Lord has chosen it. That is why I entrust and commit it to Him. Then the Lord rules over it. When facing difficulty, my faith is weak, but I trust my Lord who I trust—Hymn 342.
* When finding answers is difficult: When it is difficult to find answers, it is 'what the Lord is doing.' In such times, only 'entrusting myself to Him' is necessary.
* Disarmament: Laying down my attempts to protect and defend myself and placing my security in the Lord is the counter-spirit that smashes Satan's schemes. Do not guard yourself; obey Him.
* Guidance: There is nothing more empty than seeking guidance without the resolve to lose everything.
* Finishing over starting: What is more important than 'How did I start?' is 'How will I finish?' That is why we pray.

* The power of focus: Truly focusing on valuable work means saying 'Yes' to that one thing. This means having the courage to say 'No' to everything else. Only the courageous can focus. According to my earnest expectation and hope, I do not shame myself in anything, and now as always I am fully bold, so that Christ may be magnified in my body, whether by life or by death. (Philippians 1:20)

* The ability to lose: Jacob, who lived his life as a struggle, gained much and lost much. But when he let go, accepting that he might lose, he lost nothing more and instead recovered what he had lost. The person prepared to lose is the strongest. 'If I am bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.' (Genesis 43:14)

* God guides through loss: Sometimes God speaks through loss. From a human perspective it is regret and sorrow, but in God's kingdom, through that loss as a starting point, He speaks of a great work of God. 'Do not worry about the donkeys you lost three days ago, for they have been found. And to whom is all the desire of Israel turned, if not to you and all your father's family?' (1 Samuel 9:20)
* What I anticipate does not necessarily come to pass. God's way surpasses my expectations. Wisdom is ceasing fantasy and being faithful to the present calling, entrusting everything else to Him.
* Maturity shows consideration: It is stepping away from self-centered interpretations and perspectives. When mature, we put away childish things. That is love and perfection.
* A leader's qualification: Whether you are bearing the cross.
* After ministry, become one who serves in greater humility, become corrupted and dead, ground to powder.
* Always remember and pray.
* A life without regret: Pouring out everything until nothing remains.

* The God who called me: The God who called me did not call me to do this work excellently and earn recognition. We do the work, but the Lord bears responsibility for the results. We only have the responsibility to be faithful unto death. He judged me faithful, putting me into His service. (1 Timothy 1:12)

* How can I not give thanks? My friend was martyred in Iraq ten years ago because he believed in Jesus. If we believe in Jesus unto death, how can we not give thanks for the unavoidable obstacles on that path?
* Focus on what is important: The important thing is not 'Am I receiving people's recognition?' but 'How am I loving the people God has given me?' Do not be afraid; only believe.

* A ship without a captain does not know where to go. Authority was given to take responsibility, but if responsibility is evaded and authority is abandoned, great disaster returns. People die because of people. Beautiful young souls die because of foolish and ignorant people, greedy and irresponsible people. Those of us in ministry do not understand all of God's will. But until sin abounds in this world, having failed to protect young souls, we must beat our chests like our blood was shed, weep bitterly, and pray.

* When you are tempted, it means you are not giving thanks, you are not staying awake even one hour in prayer, you are not loving, and you are thinking entirely self-centeredly. The Lord pleaded that faith would not fail. Please respond with intercession and faith.
* Valuable work is work that saves lives, and therefore it shows us what we must focus on.
* The secret to not being tempted: Hold fast to the truth that God has given you a burning mission and that God is almighty.
* A life of carrying out purpose: It is not for testing, but for saving lives.

* Do not fear bearing witness. That a community has good faith means there are believers of influence within that community. If both you and I follow the world, the gospel does not flow. Do not fear rejection and being ignored; strive to testify to and love Jesus.
* Live by the gospel just once. You will no longer complain and will understand what true victory really is.
* Without the gospel, you suffer shame. Suffering shame is not because you do not know the gospel. It is because you do not live by the gospel.

* The simple and clear reason for no revival: There are many who speak, but there is no one who pays the price. Faith does not become good by itself. There must be testimony, and that testimony is given by martyrs. To desire God's presence and revival without bloodshed?....that is nonsense. Martyr comes from the Greek 'Martus,' meaning 'witness' or 'one who testifies.'
* Draw near. If you draw near to God, God will also draw near to you.

* When you complain, you become paralyzed. If you complain about the government, you cannot work hard. If you complain about your teacher, you cannot study. If you complain about your parents, even eating feels like revenge. When you complain, your life becomes paralyzed. But if you take responsibility, you become a leader, and as a leader, you can raise up life.
* Pray and obey. Pray earnestly and obey according to God's Word. Things that cannot be expressed in words will happen.

* I really want to use you. The reason God humbles you is because God really wants to use you. When you are zealous, you are splashing in the water while swimming, but when you humble yourself, a tsunami arises.
* Love the souls entrusted to you as much as your own pain.

* The death of righteous Abel: The Bible says the reason righteous Abel died is simply because he became someone who offered a better sacrifice and pleased God. (Hebrews 11:4) If he had not pleased God, there would have been no reason to die. His name is Abel, meaning 'vanity,' and though he seemed to fade away in vain, God did not leave it at that, saying 'Though he died, he still speaks by his faith.' (Hebrews 11:4) There are people whose very existence speaks without words, and there are those who send a message through absence rather than presence. Even if given another choice, they do not avoid their fate but eternally testify in faith, 'I will only love and rejoice in God.'
* The blessing of humbling yourself: Those who exalt themselves will be humbled, but those who humble themselves for the Lord will be exalted.
* The reason for being troubled: The devil does not leave you alone and harms your heart by every means and method for one reason: to make you stop looking to the Lord. Do not stop; that is victory.
* Live your own life: The 'I' who loves God is far more important than the world that hates me.

* Why there is peace even on a sinking ship: Whether the ship is sinking or sailing smoothly, the Lord is in all of it. If it sinks, He will make me walk on water, and if it sails smoothly, He will make me praise the Lord. May the great waters not overflow me, nor may the deep swallow me up, nor may the pit close its mouth over me. -Psalm 69:15
* Do not worry about 'What should I do and how?' The Lord asks why you want to do it. If that purpose is pure and clear, the method will naturally become sharp.

* If you love first, the will appears later. God desires that we believe in His faithfulness and love now rather than hastily presume to know God's will. If you understand love, the will becomes visible.

* Being washed by blood is dwelling in light. Even if you fail and fall today, never give up on becoming a good people of God. It is not about having less sin, but those who come with their sin are in fellowship with the light. If we walk in the light as He is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of His Son Jesus will cleanse us from all sin. -1 John 1:7

* Outside the camp: Jesus suffered outside the gate because He came as the Lamb to bring forgiveness of sins and salvation. Therefore, we too must go outside, carrying Jesus' cross to gain life, rejecting worldly glory and human recognition. Therefore let us go to Him outside the camp, bearing His reproach. -Hebrews 13:13

* The reason for being called: You were not called to complain about circumstances and people. You were called to obey and live according to God's Word. Then in this difficult situation, you will experience God working and alive.
* The condition for happiness: A person who has a community where they can receive grace is blessed. If supply and healing happen through community, you have received a great blessing.

* Hill warfare: The fact that the ownership of the hill changes several times a day shows how fiercely the battle is fought, which proves how important that area is. You are fighting an important battle. Trust in the Lord and rise again to fight.

* When your calling is worthy, you will not feel frustrated. You were not called to complain about circumstances. Do what you can do. That is to pray where you stand and live according to the Lord's Word. The Holy Lord rules with sovereignty.
* Do not be deceived; overcome evil with good. If you repay evil with evil, you will fight and perish. Do not be deceived.

* A precious journey: I hope to travel warmly with loved ones. May the coming and going be filled with grace and beauty that the Lord provides, so that we become unforgettable meaning and names to one another, and the companionship of Jesus becomes captivating.

* Decide beforehand: The reason I walk this narrow path even now is because the 'I' of the past decided beforehand, no matter how harsh this path is. Decide beforehand not to turn back. The cross is not a beginning but already a conclusion.

* When there is no strength even to eat - Lethargy: When you lack strength, you need adequate meals, but you also need strength to eat. You gain strength from hearing God's Word, but you need the basic strength to listen. Pray for the strength and ability to listen.

* When disappointed: The disciples on the road to Emmaus were disappointed. Jesus made them burn again. He opened the Scriptures to them. Come to the place of listening. If you do not listen and cannot hear, even with much service, you will become scattered and lethargic.

* Why you feel frustrated even though you are working hard: You are working so hard that it is beyond your strength for true revival, yet somehow you feel frustrated. It is because you are trying to confirm yourself through response and recognition. From the beginning, you have strayed from testifying to the Lord, and though people may not know, God knows.
* If you love: If you love, you will pray. If you do not pray while saying you love, it means you only once wished to love, but you are not actually loving.

* The preacher's preparation: The most important thing is to love. If you do not love, it is useless, and preaching God's Word is not meant to be useless. If you truly want to love, you must love through prayer and live in obedience to God's Word. The crisis of the church is the crisis of the preacher. Therefore, we must become poorer and repent.

* Why you are anxious: Your hopes and expectations are rotten and shameful. What you already know yourself is that such anxious hopes always have a path of resolution. That is a shameful ending. May I not be ashamed of my earnest expectation and hope in anything, and as before, may I be entirely bold now, so that whether I live or die, Christ will be honored in my body. -Philippians 1:20
* For revival to come: Revival comes when those who have tasted revival continuously kneel with holy discontent toward a state without revival. We advance as much as we long for glory.
* Decide your future now: If you desire revival, you wish for the outcome to change. To change the outcome, the cause must be different, which means transforming your present.

* If you believe, it happens simply. Giving excessive meaning to what I cannot do is not the Lord's will. Do not think complicatedly; do faithfully what is permitted now, and the Lord will accomplish what needs to be accomplished.
* God's alternative: In response to seven years of severe famine, what God prepared was Joseph. God's alternative is one faithful person.
* Do what you must do: Do what you must do. Then what needs to happen will happen.

* Sustaining faith: It is like barely paying for cheap treatment with only a fifty-fifty chance of recovery to save on hospital bills. That is not thrift but recklessness. Why do you not hold firmly to the cross so that you are devastated by God's powerful presence?

* To be hated: If Christians are to be hated, let them be hated for the sake of truth. Jesus was too. When you are hated for such reasons, do not be ashamed but rather be proud. The world hates you not, but it hates Me, because I testify that the deeds of the world are evil. -John 7:7

* Radical gospel: If my body is not freed from the sins of the world, it is nonsense to say that I can transform the world. Giving in to the lusts of the flesh and falling into the temptations of the world is because the gospel has been diluted. The gospel is God's Son taking up the rough cross. Living by the worth of that blood is faith.

* When you distinguish time: It is when you give your heart. Do you have such a time, such a secret place where you can give your sincere heart once? If it is rare, it means you are a Christian in name only.
* Keep going, ignoring it to the end: Just do it to the end. Do it as much as the East Sea and Mount Baekdu dry up and wear away. Surely history will happen.
* Today's prayer: Grant me the loyalty to do my best in the work entrusted to me, the trust to entrust what I cannot do to You, and the wisdom to discern to whom all of this belongs.

* Give up good for the best: One well-taken photograph is better than hundreds of millions of poorly taken ones. A thousand keychains are not as good as one key. If we have the Lord, we have done well, and if we obey the Lord, we have done very well.
* Do not worry; pray about everything. The faithful Lord will answer.

* Relationships: It is impossible for me, who finds it difficult to obey God completely, to please imperfect and ever-changing people. If I decide and take responsibility in the Lord, I will have nothing to do but stop worrying and be grateful.
* Unfinished adolescence: Because I am conscious of people rather than the Lord, pretense comes out. When I long only for the Lord, people will also look to the Lord.
* Revelation: The Lord's revelation shows us only one step to obey right now, because that is all I need to see.
* The Path to Glory: Through repeated obedience, we gradually advance to the course we must run and the place of keeping faith.
* Holocaust Faith: Faith stumbles because we stop running. We just need to keep running.
* My Way and God's Way: Do not worry about what you cannot do, but be faithful in what you can do. Then the Lord will add all these things to you.

* Fulfilling Desires Does Not Bring Happiness: David spent much of his youth fleeing through the wilderness and enemy territories. If David had a choice, he would surely prefer to stretch out and sleep at home with his children for just one day. Every great figure in the Bible advanced without knowing their future, but when they looked to and relied only on the Lord, their lives became happy lives of purpose.
* The Necessity of Helplessness: You must experience your own helplessness and hit bottom to learn to depend on the Lord. Only then do you feel that this battle cannot be won by your own zeal.

* Why Faith Is Difficult: Because we do not commit fully. When we are half-hearted, we grasp at people instead. Half-hearted presence, half-hearted prayer, half-hearted ministry is what is difficult. When we truly encounter the Lord, I am changed, and afterward the circumstances change too.

* If Unbearable Reality Causes Anxiety: Believe not only that the Lord has sovereignty over the reality you cannot control and knows and entrusts all your future to Him, but also that He faithfully holds you in this very moment.

* Why God Desires Obedience: Sometimes God calls us to make difficult decisions. He calls us to let go, follow, and obey. Though we want to flee, God does not give up, because He must certainly use us.
* The Sheep Following the Shepherd: The life of a sheep following the shepherd is calm, because there is final victory in following the shepherd.
* How to Do the Lord's Work: The church suffers through capable people. God works through useless servants precisely because He is almighty.
* How to Pray: If you keep failing even as you resolve to pray, command in Jesus's name that the bonds be broken and pray for the ability to pray. You must bring this matter before the cross.

* Faith Is a Choice Against Our Nature: It is the process of discovering and obeying God's will. Sometimes that will goes against our nature. Even if following God's will seems disadvantageous and as if we will die, obedience is always followed by God's righteousness and blessings beyond words. 'For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways, declares the Lord. For as the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways and my thoughts than your thoughts.' Isaiah 55:8-9
* Two Simple Questions Explain Everything: 1. What do I truly desire in my heart? 2. If I truly desire it, do I have the willingness to pay the price gladly?
* I Am What Blocks Me: The being that blocks my way is none other than 'I myself'. When I refuse to pay the price while desiring much, I am a thief and a robber.
* Finishing with Boldness: When you let go and start, you can finish with boldness.
* Coram Deo: Living conscious of God's presence in every area.
* The Healing Camp: A young lion does not eat rotten meat. Even in one healing camp, faith is the perspective that stakes one's life. I look forward to the amazing work the Lord will do.
* Faith Does Not Regret: Because it is something done while feeling the Lord's gaze.
* The Ability to Begin Again: It is confirming God's love and holding to His promises.
* Catch Up If You Can: Ideas and methods can be copied, but passion cannot. Works can be imitated, but dying for souls is not something that can be immediately replicated.
* What God Desires Is Not Reformation: 'He has shown you, O mortal, what is good. And what does the Lord require of you? To act justly and to love mercy and to walk humbly with your God.' Micah 6:8

* Human Joy and God's Joy: Even if a servant of God pursues human joy in the church, they are not God's servant. Even if they pursue God's joy in the world, they are God's servant. What moves me is my master. If what moves me is not the Lord, then such idols must be broken and burned until they no longer tempt me.
* Why the Cross Is Lost: Because there is too much to do, especially too many unnecessary things. Two or three things will suffice, or even one thing is enough.

* Victory Does Not Come to Just Anyone: Victory comes to those who overcome with the strength the Lord gives. If you overcame without the strength the Lord gave, it is not true victory but merely a quarrel.

* If You Pray But Do Not Sow: That is fanaticism. If you sow sincerely but do not pray, that is unbelief. If you both pray and sow sincerely but expect vaguely without God's Word, that is superstition. If God does not think this way yet you firmly believe alone, that is blind faith.
* If You Cannot Let Go: You have no intention of letting go. If you are not prepared to lose, trials and tribulations will continue. Only the Lord will be the reward for those who resolve to lose.
* The Reason for Boldness: I know what I desire, and I also know what I can afford to lose. Those who long for eternity cannot be blocked by anything.
* Be Holy Rather Than Successful: That is true victory.
* If You Truly Want to Save Lives: You must be filled with the Holy Spirit. To try to save lives without being filled is not truly desiring to save lives but merely wishing to save them.
* When Grace Runs Out: People return to what they once enjoyed.

* The Warmth of Complete Devotion: Many people fail to give themselves completely to the Lord because they fear Jesus might send them to a miserable mission field. If on a cold morning today a small bird is huddled half-frozen in a shed, that helpless young bird must come completely under my control so it can warm itself and eat. Would I then crush it in my grip? Or would I provide shelter and warmth, food and protection?
* It Is Promise, Not Talent: If you work by talent, you will be anxious and frustrated, but if you hold to the promise, you will have abundant peace and wholeness.

* True Business Begins After Overcoming Disappointment: Even the faithful servants of God in the Bible experienced severe disappointment when their desire to have others understand their faith and deep spiritual communion was rejected. But only by overcoming this can they participate in the Lord's true business and begin to enjoy the Lord being glorified rather than seeking their own recognition. 'Though my father and mother forsake me, the Lord will receive me.' Psalm 27:10
* What Is Leadership: It is the restraint to clarify what to focus on and the mobilization to move all resources and power toward that goal.
* Why We Do Not Get Drunk: Not to obtain salvation, but because having received salvation, we have no further need.

* Evidence That a Branch Is Not Attached to the Vine: It bears no fruit and withers in powerlessness. The vine's only reason for existence is to bear fruit, and that happens only when it remains completely attached to the vine and receives the farmer's cleansing.
* True Boldness: Lord, do not allow any worldly success that hinders me from drawing near to You.
* If You Cannot Let Go, You Miss What Is Important: True faith means lesser desires are surrendered before the Lord. When we focus simply on the Lord, sin decreases and holy influence strengthens.

* God's Solution: God has never overwhelmed with great numbers. Rather, He has chosen one person and accomplished His will through them. The person God seeks is one who gives themselves simply for the Lord's sake. People are the answer.
* Simply and Greatly: What can be simple, intensive, and flexible is called a retreat.

* Absolute Happiness: Paul says that even loss is gain. Because the original purpose was not to gain. When Christ living within me is my original purpose, loss does not become pity and gain does not become pride. 'For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain.' Philippians 1:21

* Solutions Do Not Bring Happiness: If you have managed to solve every challenge, it means you have become someone who relies greatly on yourself. Rather than seeking solutions, seek the Lord. Through this blocked situation, God becomes God and I die and live through Jesus.

* Right Motivation Rather Than Satisfying Results: When I try to take responsibility for results, the Lord sets limits, because I do not truly know who the master is. The Lord opens the door, and I seek the Lord who opens doors.

* The Opposite of Loyalty Is Not Laziness: The opposite of loyalty is trying to do many things well by oneself. Because God alone makes things excellent, when He calls us, He expects us to be sincere and loyal. Adam tried to become like God, Saul sought recognition from people, and Israel wanted quick settlement in Canaan. When they try to do well to gain recognition, much anxiety pierces them.

* The Advance of Sovereignty: Executing God's will is called loyalty. So when we are loyal without looking at people and in faith, we accomplish the Lord's will and experience His reign. The most advanced version of loyalty is 'love'. Therefore, we call the one who loves perfect. 'Over all these virtues put on love, which binds them all together in perfect unity.' Colossians 3:14. 'Whoever loves others has fulfilled the law.' Romans 13:8

* The Basic of Sovereignty: The authority to execute God's will and rule is called sovereignty. So God's sovereignty is my responding in faith to His will and work. When I respond in faith, God's sovereignty comes. When seeking the Lord's sovereignty, surrender follows, but when sovereignty comes, we receive a better inheritance. 'After this, the word of the Lord came to Abram in a vision: Do not be afraid, Abram. I am your shield, your very great reward.' Genesis 15:1
* A Life Without Regret: If you are in the Lord, even if you stumble somewhat, you should not regret, because all things work together for good.
* Why We Fall Repeatedly: Not because temptation is strong, but because we fear falling. If you do not fear, there is no need to fall.
* The Secret of Victory: If the Lord does not help, we will only do things that lead to ruin.
* Secret of Certain Victory: Love even when angry. This is the most certain way to overcome your adversary.
* You Will Be Tested: You will face trials. That it is a difficult test shows not that you are trying to win, but that you are already a victor being tested.

* How Grateful Are You?: By giving situations where you cannot be grateful, He enables genuine gratitude. If circumstances were favorable, why would faith be necessary? What value would there be in acknowledging the Lord who governs all situations?
* True Faith: It is entrusting your heart and focus to the Lord even in moments when it seems impossible to look toward God.
* What Is Genuine Faith?: Praying before hungry lions.
* The Core: To bear good fruit, simply become a good tree. That is what matters most.
* Every Day Special: Fresh events are sometimes needed, but if you truly love, you can show deeper love than events. A thriving community experiences a retreat every single week.
* Prayer for Revival: Whatever joy of mine that hinders God's revival, let it be uprooted.

* What Must Never Be Done Under Any Circumstance: Never make someone a victim of injustice. Whether through another's arrogance and prejudice or through my own ignorance and bias, God will surely repay it. A sin heavier than David's terrible adultery was the death of a faithful and righteous man. "You struck down Uriah the Hittite with the sword and took his wife to be your own, and you killed him with the sword of the Ammonites." - 2 Samuel 12:9

* Recovery of a Struggling Patriarch: Though providing for family is difficult and work is challenging, a bright child's words, "Dad, I love you," can make a father forget all worries and rise again. What cannot be denied is these young people's sincere confession that they love Jesus—something that cannot be fabricated. I believe this is enough.

* Foolish Actions: The devil's most cunning deceit is the lie that problems should be solved in the flesh. The eternal enemy, Amalek, hides itself and attacks from behind. Trust only in the Lord. You will not make mistakes or have regrets.
* The Most Foolish Thing: Worrying over what cannot be resolved. Entrust it to the Lord and be free and at peace. Everything will work out well.

* A Person Who Lives Only in Jesus, Not in Self: There is conflict in life, but that does not make it evil. However, for a servant to seek human pleasure instead of God's pleasure is evil. "Am I now trying to win the approval of human beings, or of God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a servant of Christ." - Galatians 1:10

* Reason, Not Method: We are fixated on How, but God questions Why. We must sincerely ask why we do it, why we must do it, why we are joyful, why our hearts are hurt. Behind that reason lies the knowledge of who is King and who is Lord. I truly hope God becomes our only reason and motivation. "If we live, we live for the Lord; and if we die, we die for the Lord. So, whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord." - Romans 14:8

* Secret of Receiving Power: It is not to feel, judge, and act by your own will, but to acknowledge and uphold the Lord's sovereignty. Living by your own will makes you a person of flesh; living by God's will makes you a person of the Spirit.
* Grace: That I exist is grace, but that I obey is also grace. It is not I, but through the Lord's grace that I do it.
* Gratitude Leads Beyond Peace to Boldness: Paul was not afraid of loneliness, tribulation, or death. It was because he had already given much thanks.
* An Expectation Without Shame: Embracing the Father's expectation.
* The Power of Gentleness: A person of faith can be gentle because they are a victor. If you cannot bow your head or extend your hand, it is because you are defeated.

* A Life Without Regret: When you fulfill purpose, there is no regret. That purpose is not the fulfillment of your selfish desires or the achievement of your wishes. His purpose is to take up the cross and go there. Therefore, those who uphold His purpose are fearless and bold, without regret.

* Repentance Through Pain: When young people struggle, it pierces the minister's heart with pain. Through that pain, I hear what He speaks: Kindle your zeal! Repent! "Those whom I love I rebuke and discipline."

* The Blood: The only way to overcome sin and temptation is through the power of the blood. Without the blood, the Holy Spirit cannot come. The transformation that gives me daily praise is also the power of the blood. Never overlook the basics. Professionals are always faithful to the fundamentals.

* Tenacious Gratitude Like Paul: A person like Paul, who is goal-oriented and tenacious, finds system criticism and incitement of complaint more appealing than gratitude. That thanksgiving overflows through all his letters and every line because he experienced the secret of gratitude and God, who is its object. Therefore, he is strategically, tenaciously, and awesomely grateful.

* How to Overcome Circumstances and Adversaries: For Paul, who rushed toward only the Lord, people could no longer be a source of pain. Thus, if all you hope for is this life, you are truly pitiful. Look to the Lord and find your answer in Him.
* Reason for Being Able to Entrust: David did not seek revenge because he placed his hope only in the living Lord.

* Why Some Don't Finish in the Spirit: Many people begin in the Spirit but finish in the flesh. It is because they lack the willingness to obey unto death. Jesus emptied Himself, took the form of a servant, and obeyed unto death on the cross. If you cannot die, you cannot accomplish.

* Closer Than Pain: After entering into severe suffering, you realize that Jesus is closer than the suffering itself. That is why tribulation is a blessing. "Before I was afflicted I went astray, but now I obey your word." - Psalm 119:67

* Weak Yet Victorious: Humanity is sinful and bound to fall. People are defeated, but faith wins. "For everyone born of God overcomes the world. This is the victory that has overcome the world, even our faith." - 1 John 5:4

* David's Covenant: If a leader can stand upright, God gives a lamp even in crumbling circumstances. Become an ace like David. "Nevertheless, for the sake of David your servant, the Lord your God will not let the lamp of Israel be extinguished. And He will give you a son to sit on the throne of Israel and to preserve Jerusalem stable." - 1 Kings 15:4

* "In My Opinion..." We speak of our thinking as the best we can offer. That is why we fail. God is the one who turns the worst into the best, not merely the best. When David briefly lost faith and followed the "desires of his heart," wandering in Ziklag for a year and four months, he faced the "Ziklag trial," as difficult as the Absalom incident. Deciding without asking is mostly a comfortable decision, and easy decisions always lead to failure. Do not make easy decisions; take up the cross. "David thought to himself, 'One of these days I will be destroyed by the hand of Saul. The best thing I can do is to escape to the land of the Philistines.'" - 1 Samuel 27:1

* No matter how much turmoil, even if all fall into temptation, if you and I remain unshaken and seek and obey God with all our hearts, He will save all the souls of the community. This is David's covenant. God remembered David and saved Israel.

* Purpose of Prayer: "Simon, Simon, Satan has asked to sift all of you as wheat. But I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith may not fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers." - Luke 22:31-32. Wake up and pray. If you do not want to lose what is truly important...
* Dedication Is Power: They say they cannot dedicate because they lack power. No. They lack power because they do not dedicate.

* Mentor and Mentee: The teacher is a learner. The learner becomes a teacher. In Hebrew, "to learn" is "lamad." When emphasized in the intensive form "limmad," it means "to learn much" and also "to teach." The teacher is one who learns much.

* Gospel of the Cross: Testifying to the cross brings criticism. Through that suffering, the gospel of the cross is testified more powerfully and exercises influence. However, compromise to avoid trouble will bring the death of the church.

* Spiritual Malnutrition: There is nothing more dangerous than trying to live today on yesterday's grace, spiritual experiences, and anointing. It is like suffering from malnutrition while not feeling hunger.
* What Is Love?: If it ends with "Ah, this feels perfect," it will soon crumble. Love is based on understanding and fulfilling responsibility.

* Reason for Not Being Discouraged: While doing your best and giving all your diligence, you are not discouraged. It is because you do not rely on your own diligence but trust only in the Lord. Without grace, diligence is futile.
* Ground for Answered Prayer: Hold fast to the promise and pray based on the Word. The ground is His unchanging faithfulness and unwavering covenant.

* Probability, Not Promise: Abraham does not know where he is going, but advances believing the Word. When you abandon probability—that which is calculable and predictable—and hold fast to the promise, you will see God's glory.
* All In: Hitting halfheartedly causes real pain. If faith is not complete, everyone suffers. The most miserable person is one who has not gone all in. You must believe completely.

* Reason for Moving Forward: If you cannot be bold, you cannot obey; if you cannot obey, you cannot advance. Boldness comes when He is with you. Therefore, the greatest sin is not being filled. That is where sin begins.
* Who Knows?: In the end, it is God who knows and calls you. Only He knows you and calls you. Those who answer His call will shine like the stars of heaven.
* There Is No Discouragement: If I did it by my own ability, there would be discouragement. But as I am led by Him, I simply go forward. The Lord is the conclusion.
* True Happiness: Pursuing your own will brings anxiety and emptiness even when achieved. But pursuing the Lord's will brings no fear and true happiness.
* Prayer is both warfare and the most certain victory. Please kindle the fire of prayer. - Sunday Gathering

If you don't like rice, eat noodles. If you don't like that, eat bread. But if breathing becomes burdensome and your heartbeat becomes tedious, that is a serious problem requiring resolution. These symptoms—breathing and heartbeat—should not be covered with something else; they are real issues. A baby must fall thousands of times before gaining the ability to walk a few steps. If you give a baby a wheelchair because walking is difficult, that person deserves to be cursed. The structure should be such that those with living spirits are moved and those without are saddened. Conversely, if those who are moved are restricted and cynical people prevail, the church suffocates. There are things to compromise on and things not to. It means that when a baby cannot drink milk, juice should not be the alternative.
* It is not that there is no method; it is that the purpose is unclear, so people blame circumstances. For those who pursue the Lord and for communities, there is always wisdom and alternatives.
* When you want to defend yourself, when you want to make excuses, simply remain silent. As Jesus entrusted His soul to the Father and maintained complete silence.
* You Wander: It is not that you don't know the answer. You simply do not act on it. You have long known before the Lord how you should behave.
There's no third service on Sunday due to a distribution failure. We'll manage with what we can put together. Hallelujah.

* When we entrust things to God, we become meek. Meekness is suspending judgment until God works. It is not my thought but the Lord's heart, which I cannot fabricate but is given only to those who wholly trust in the Lord's living presence.

If we cannot let go of what must be released, we cannot pray; if we cannot hold onto what must be held, we cannot obey. Fearing the Lord is to release what must be released and hold onto what must be held.
Peter describes the power of Satan's temptation by comparing it to a hungry lion. Here Satan is called Diabolos, which originally means 'the accuser.'

Peter discovered he was a sinner before the Lord and asked Him to depart. Yet Jesus remained with him instead. Even in the disappointing moment when His foremost disciple denied Him and in his weakened flesh at the end of life, He never left. The Lord does not leave us. Never.
How to live with unchanging faith—keep the flame burning. The power of the Lord's blood is eternal. It becomes what I will say forever.
* The sufficient way to happiness—It is far happier to serve quietly and be misunderstood than to attempt theft and be welcomed as Santa Claus. Happiness comes not from reactions but from existence.
* A leader's qualities—Even when shaken like a small boat in storms and waves, a leader is not shaken. Because he has faith.

* Pray silently with tears. We are not called to gain human approval. We are called only to love. Since this is received from God, receiving God's approval is enough, and we silently pray with tears, trusting that God will take responsibility.

Potiphar's wife tried to harm Joseph and had him put in prison. But even if it weren't for Potiphar's wife, Joseph would have gone to prison. The people and circumstances that tried to harm me ultimately could not harm me, and instead they served to fulfill God's providence. What I am is by God's grace.

* Following desire brings anxiety. Trying to satisfy desire brings anxiety. It is because we steal what was not given to us. Pursuing our calling brings joy. Because it pleases the One who gave us our calling.

Potiphar's wife must have been someone Joseph hated enough to want to kill, at least from Joseph's perspective. Yet without her, Joseph would not have gone to prison or met the chief butler. Potiphar's wife was truly greatly used. What looks like things going wrong in our eyes, the Lord sees all things working together for good. 'The Lord has made everything for its purpose, even the wicked for the day of trouble.' (Proverbs 16:4)

* Good is not always good—There's a saying that good is good. Following this saying brings convenience, avoids criticism, and evades relational risks. But good is not always good. Right is good. To hold onto righteousness, we cannot agree with everything and may become outsiders. Yet the Lord is with the righteous.

Invisible roots determine the freshness of the visible tree. Receiving blessings through parents and authorities whom God appointed to serve is the first step in opening my relationships, ministry, and vision. I hope all young people become wise enough for parents and ministers to bless them fully, and all young people whose life doors are opening will be blessed. The key to life's success lies in the basics.
Even if talent seems insignificant, continuous persistence makes it serve as great power. Persistence is loyalty.

By 'discovering' treasure, fleeting glory becomes 'waste,' exerts influence 'like leaven,' possesses 'vitality' like a mustard seed, and by 'recognizing' the bridegroom's value like the five wise virgins, prepares oil, and like the faithful servant entrusted with talents, does not forget the calling and received grace but 'remembers'—I hope this becomes your life.

Evangelical complex—We preach the gospel while giving bread and find comfort in balanced ministry. True gospel—it is total salvation and quite practical. Believing in Jesus as Savior and cultivating wheat with wisdom and holiness. The Christian's only competitive advantage is holiness and wisdom. Like doves and serpents.
Do not respond from your nature but respond from the Word. When you pause and receive with the Word that contains God's will and desire, you will feel there is almost nothing you cannot receive.

Change in essence without true inward transformation and forced external change is called 'hypocrisy.' Change your inner self, then you will serve the Lord in changed form without external pressure. There is pure gold and there is plating. Do not be faith that is plated; be faith that is pure gold.

Do not be ashamed; rather be confident. The reason is Jesus who paid the price with His blood. 'For this reason I also suffer these things; but I am not ashamed, for I know whom I have believed and I am convinced that He is able to guard what I have entrusted to Him until that day.' (2 Timothy 1:12)
Christianity that has lost focus is more dangerous than immoral heresy. Just as a disease without symptoms is more dangerous.

The One who calls, the One who uses, and the One who prepares are one. When the Lord says He will do it, He does it. That One is the Master. Worry and anxiety are the Master's responsibility; obedience is the servant's. Say the Lord will use you. (Mark 11:3)
I desire to act according to the calling of the One who called me. I came for this purpose.
The Lord who called will surely take responsibility. The Lord does not call and then default. Because His attribute is faithfulness that cannot be measured by the world's credit ratings.

My parents always wanted for me a common life that was slightly superior to others and predictable, something I could boast about to others. But in Jesus, the Lord never lets us live ordinarily. The Lord desires to be the foundation of faith and trust in uncertainty.
Even if I am broken, if God said to stand, I will do it to the end.

If you think you lack, why don't you pray? Before losing hope, try praying. Where I still thought there was no way and where there was no path, He reveals great and secret ways. Those who do not reach their desired wishes or lack sufficient earnestness to express them cannot pray.

* Why it's hard—Because you try too hard. But that area is not given to you. What is given to you is the realm of loyalty. Loyalty is standing your post. To such a loyal one, the grace to do well and to prosper follows accordingly.

Meekness is to be angry at what deserves anger and to entrust what should be entrusted. Only those who are convinced in their bones of the Lord's living presence, only those of faith can take such an attitude.

Certainly at that ball there were many women displaying supreme beauty, but the prince's eyes saw only Cinderella. The ability to look only to Jesus while living in the world, the power of vision comes to those who have deeply experienced the Lord's beauty.

What is certain is that there will always be some lack in every moment of our lives. When that shortage and slow progress are interpreted not as a handicap but as an opportunity, God's glory appears. Rather than the way not being open now, the Lord is giving us a little more time to serve Him and the saints before things become extremely busy. You must become someone who is more faithful and prepared when you have time now.
* Opportunity in the worst situation—Only in the worst situations can we give the highest thanksgiving. The Lord is watching me even now. Be faithful.

Maintaining the heart the Lord gives amid busy work, complex matters, and things worth anger is living by faith. If it is not 'living for myself,' which is Adam's nature, but 'living for the Lord,' we must see everything through the Lord's eyes.
Many fiery trials come, but let us not fear; the Savior's power is great, we shall overcome and have more. Hymn 376.

* Being called and being used—All are called; but not all respond. Even those who respond are not all used. To be used as a righteous instrument in God's kingdom, there must be self-denial and a change of direction through dedication that breaks self. To take up the cross without self-denial...? There is no such deception.

To the disobedient Jonah, 'just then' a ship heading to Tarshish comes and favorable winds blow—this is not God condoning his stubbornness but a warm-up exercise before encountering the storm and entering the fish's belly.

Unbelievers have survival as their goal. If they beg before the fiery furnace, they are people who might survive a little longer. On the other hand, those whose goal is glory advance with faith and the cross. They continue to be used even when they overcome the flames and the nation changes and all rulers are purged.

The gospel must continue to be witnessed not only to unbelievers but to everyone. Inevitably, decline comes when anyone is apart from the gospel. The error of powerlessness is treating the gospel merely as a ticket to heaven. Therefore I am eager to preach the gospel to you also who are in Rome. (Romans 1:15)

* The reason for loyalty—It is to run toward the will of the One who called without giving up. This is possible because He is watching. When you are conscious of God, it becomes loyalty; when you are conscious of people, it becomes hypocrisy.
If you are anxious, please let it go. When you entrust it to the Lord's hands, peace comes, and with that peace comes solution.
Are you anxious? Entrust it to the Lord's hands. You will have peace. Because the Lord's hand holds me.
* What is a leader?—To hold whining children and smile with a red face while not showing the countless arrows pierced into the back.

* The most pitiful person—If we had no resurrection, if we had no cross, we would be the most wronged and pitiful people. God will repay. Therefore we wait quietly, looking to Him. Holding onto the cross of resurrection, weeping and nodding our heads, saying 'I can endure more,' we endure. The Lord lives. If in Christ we have hoped only for this life, we are of all people most to be pitied. (1 Corinthians 15:19)

* Falling away from purpose is corruption. Hold fast to the original purpose for which you were called. When you know the reason and hold onto it and run to accomplish it, you become mature, glorious, and gain life. Fulfilling what you were called to do is your mission.

* Do not be conscious of people. If you are conscious of people, you will 'play act,' and if you are conscious of God, you will 'be loyal.' When I become conscious of God, people will also become conscious of God.

* The dignity of one verse—Tim Tebow, who has the greatest influence in American sports history, presents only one verse: John 3:16. You don't need many verses to obey. One Word changes not just a life but history itself.

* Zero visibility (시계제로) - When you can see nothing, do not be anxious, but trust in the Lord who knows and prepares all things. When flying through darkness, you must ultimately listen only to the Lord's voice and move forward. Life is a competition after all. Go coolly. If God clothes the grass of the field, which exists today and is thrown into the fire tomorrow, will He not much more clothe you, O you of little faith? -Matthew 6:30

* Foundation of Life - If your foundation is possessions, pride, or relationships, you will be shaken. But if your foundation is the Lord, you will not be shaken even when other losses come. Trials will reveal what foundation you have built your life upon. Stand upon the rock. The Lord gives and the Lord takes away; may the name of the Lord be praised. -Job 1:21

* Faith is a choice. Choice changes destiny. Therefore, life can be said to be a collection and continuation of choices. But to make a choice, a comparison of values must occur, and to have the ability for such comparison, one must have discernment. Hebrews says we make such value comparisons by faith. Choosing where I must stand, choosing what I must bear—that is faith, and through it, destiny is changed.
Complaining and blaming are strong yet require little sacrifice—this is our condition today. Ananias and Sapphira were disciplined. It was not a matter of money but a sin of heart and motive.

Unnecessary desires will bring confusion and unnecessary suffering. But great missions will bring obedience and reward. The desires of the flesh are against the Spirit, and the desires of the Spirit are against the flesh, for these are opposed to each other, to keep you from doing the things you want to do. -Galatians 5:17

Even in drought-like death where not a drop of rain falls, the person who prays to God and calls down fire hears the sound of great rain alone beforehand. And they prepare the way for rain to come. They pay a costly price and hear the sound of revival. Elijah said to Ahab, Go up, eat and drink, for there is a sound of rushing rain. -1 Kings 18:41

What is more difficult than criticism is praise. Many great warriors of faith have fallen there. So before the course of praise and acclaim, the graves of many servants of the Lord stand lined up. Beginners struggle to get beyond criticism, but the skilled overcome praise. 'Do not regard man, whose breath of life is in his nostrils; For why should he be esteemed?' -Isaiah 2:22 Stop regarding man, whose breath of life is in his nostrils; For why should he be esteemed?
Although there are many painful things, the reason I laugh is because I have a vision the Lord gave me. -Second evening of 1999 New Year's Conference
Even if the authority is wrong, to ignore or seek revenge against it is to be a person without God in their life. It means being merely a formal servant.

Although nothing remains for me, if the Lord looks upon His servant and gives the last heartbeat of perseverance, I will run again for the Lord's glory. If my strength runs out while running, I will gather my last breath to call upon Jesus. Then I will truly be happy.
When you stand against wind and rain, crowds will follow you. Those who look only to Jesus will gain Jesus' people.

* Power of the Word - The power of God's Word is far stronger than our stubbornness. Man does not interpret the Word; the Word interprets man. If you can hear what you should hear and obey what you should obey, you will not be ashamed. I will not be ashamed when I pay attention to all Your commandments. -Psalm 119:6

* When you endure the wind and rain, crowds will follow. Do not seek great fortune from your current circumstances, but be faithful where the Word tells you to go. The results must be completely entrusted to God.

* When you set priorities right, your realm becomes clear. Do not worry about areas you cannot do. That is not the place granted to you. Things you cannot do are not for you to worry about, but for the Lord to be concerned with. You only need to seek His kingdom and His righteousness.
If you criticize, your heart may feel relieved, but you lose people. If you persuade even though it takes time, they will come to understand, and voluntary participation will follow.

The principle by which airplanes fly is not to focus on the balloon pulling upward or the force supporting from the ground. When you have 'thrust' despite resistance and challenges, resistance actually provides 'lift' so you can fly. The thrust of life is the ability to hold onto the cross. Before the cross, everything becomes a negligible problem.

An airplane does not fly believing in vague possibilities. It flies according to a kind of law, a promise. For an airplane to take off, it needs 300km of thrust and must travel about 1800m. It must dash at certain speeds and for certain distances. People decide their investments by looking at possibilities. But a person of faith holds onto the promise and invests absolute time and concentration instead of inferiority and desires. Today is a day for our Saturday gathering. I want to invest absolute time and concentration. The Lord will intervene.

* A Life Without Regret - Do not worry about results that bring regret; rather, it is far more important that the process and motive are not regrettable. If you fear results, you will always make decisions you regret. If your intention is sincere and whole-hearted, you have done well.

Philippians 1:29 begins, 'It has been granted to you on behalf of Christ...,' and reading up to here, your heart races anticipating what grace God will give. The second half reads: 'not only to believe on him, but also to suffer for him.' Christians have inevitable battles. The greater the battle, the greater the victory. Hallelujah! 'This struggle is not a half-day sporting event where you go out, win easily, and quickly forget. This struggle is a long-term war, fighting to the end against the devil and his forces—a matter of life and death.' (Ephesians 6:12, The Message)

* Practice of Remembrance - In difficult moments, suddenly beautiful memories emerge like faded photographs. 'Will that beauty return when we sat together in a café on a cold winter day, sharing faith amid the aroma of coffee?' Surely it was God's grace that allowed such happiness. And though no one knows, if that happiness is permitted again, it too will be God's grace. The Lord's grace holds me as I go on.
I will advance as a light toward the darkness. If I stumble and fall, another light will advance. Thus we will finally proclaim the Lord's glory and witness all peoples worshipping the Lord.

What matters is not how fast you run, but for whom you run. If the one who gave you talent is the Lord, then when you decide whom to use that talent for, it can be the most beautiful or the most futile thing.

It is correct that we have no control over events. But we can control our own response to them. Responding in the Spirit is excellent. Love, joy, peace... responding to events and people with patience and self-control is a perfect score. But if you are responding by instinct and maintaining an emotional state determined immediately upon the event's occurrence, that is not just a zero score—you are continuing to score zero. If you get a problem wrong on the next test, the teacher will continue to ask that question.
While we live, there are trials. And we will also see people shaken by trials. But the storm blows and dogs bark, yet the train speeds on.

The devil prevents us from advancing at all costs. Mental breakdown comes, hardship is given, relationship difficulties are given. When problems erupt, they have the characteristic of erupting suddenly all at once. Suddenly you get an urgent call, school is happening, and on that day the train moves like a turtle. Inside the train, only I am anxious. On such a day my stomach aches. And there is no bathroom. This is what they said to frighten us: 'Their hands will tire and the work will stop, they cannot complete it.' -Nehemiah 6:9 At this time, you must not be discouraged. Nehemiah just speeds on like this. 'Now strengthen my hands.' That is how he prays, that is how he believes, and that is how he advances. If I prayed for strength, the Lord gave it.

The devil can never overcome us. Why? Because we have *'that love.' No matter how strong the devil is, he cannot overcome our love. He can never *'endure long' because of it. Spiritual warfare is won if you do not give up. The devil cannot endure by nature. *'Love'—here it is: It is not that we have loved God, but that He loved us and sent His Son as the atoning sacrifice for our sins. -1 John 1:10 Love is patient, bearing all things, enduring all things. -1 Corinthians 13:3,7
* A Beautiful Life - Everyone desires the beginning to be beautiful, just like a wedding. But when the process and the ending are beautiful, it is truly beautiful.
Apostle Paul suffered for the Lord and died for the Lord. Why? Because he chose what is most important. What is now important to you?
* True Wisdom - Rather than the wisdom of a loser who wisely gives up, choose the foolishness of a winner who stubbornly perseveres.

* The Boldness of Apostle Paul - I am not much concerned with what people evaluate about me. Even I do not feel the need to be self-reproachful or to fall into self-pity. Why? Because the true evaluation will be done by God on that day. To you or to another person's judgment is a small thing to me. I do not even judge myself, for I am not aware of anything against myself, yet I am not acquitted by this. The only one who judges me is the Lord. -1 Corinthians 4:3,4

In Acts 21, many prophets and servants of the Lord, moved by the Spirit, prophesy of tribulation in Jerusalem and seek to hinder Paul's journey. But in chapter 20, the Spirit has already revealed to Paul. While believers who see only Jerusalem's tribulation try to stop Paul, Paul who has seen 'Rome too' continues to the end. You can advance and be bold as much as you see. Do not see only Jerusalem; see Rome beyond it.

In Acts 27, when all have lost hope of survival in an absolutely critical moment, Paul survives, soldiers want to kill the prisoners lest they escape by swimming, but the centurion, wanting to save Paul, prevents it. Upon arriving on the island, bitten by a viper, Paul does not die. Paul was not afraid. He was certain that neither the storm, nor those trying to kill him, nor the viper would harm him until the time God appointed and His will is accomplished. Discern the Lord's time and will. Discernment is ability.
Stop looking at people's faces; look to God's Word. He will make you wiser and more excellent than your enemies.
What matters is not whether you are recognized by people, but whether you are being used by the Lord.
* If I Protect Myself, It Becomes Sin - If I try to protect myself, it is sin. That is why self-respect becomes sin. On the other hand, when God protects me, it becomes self-esteem.

People experience burning grace, shed tears, and then change. This is called degeneracy. Some degenerate in a month, some in six months, some in years. All things in the world decay. But the Lord is eternal. Those who hold only to the Lord can never change.
* Strong Love - When you become a mother, you become strong. Because you protect and raise life with love. Become strong. Therefore, love.
In an age of infinite competition, when people live by the jungle rules of eating and being eaten, Christians live bearing fruit. That is the purpose of life and what He intends.
* Envy Is Not Loss - Envy is not loss. Fear is loss. Because you lose control. Boldness is not arbitrary conviction; it is believing in God's rule and sovereignty within circumstances.

People yearn for the world's dreams. That is tragedy. To be surrounded by good environment, wealth and comfort, guaranteed security, and to have one's heart gradually harden is not what dreams should be. Stephen's death and Paul's dedication—are these tragedy? Those who gave their lives so that the name of Jesus Christ would be praised among people did not live tragedy but lived glory.
* Results and Process - More important than the result and decision is the process of pursuing it. God speaks much through the process.

People tried to recycle animal remains as animal feed to increase efficiency and felt proud, but through one of history's worst investments, mad cow disease emerged. Yet the Lord recognized Mary with the alabaster jar, who made a holy waste. Waste for the Lord is the highest investment in the world.

* People Imitate - Why? Because they want to gain even a little of the reputation that the original possesses. Marinated ribs seem like they should cost more than fresh ribs, but they cost less. Why do they cost less when more seasoning and effort went into them? Because they are not fresh. That is why seasoning supplements the taste. May you become a person who doesn't need seasoning. May you become a person of faith who recovers the power of essence.
People's imperfect evaluation is less important than the sincere confession I offer to the Lord.
What people own and boast about are all like straw—they burn and disappear. But invisible faith, hope, and love are like jewels—unchanging and will shine in heaven.
Do not be head of people; be a servant of the Lord.
* The Season When Faith Is Needed - Faith is not needed when conditions are good and you say 'Hallelujah~' but in trials when it is truly necessary.
Do not respond to people; respond to God. Then you can walk the path the Lord guides. Do not seek the Lord's will from people's reactions.

* Why It Is Difficult - First, it means you are saved; second, it is because you love those being shepherded; third, it is because you love souls. Without this, there would be nothing fundamentally to worry about.
In moments of loss, a person can be said to truly have faith in what they depend upon. When the Lord permits the pain of loss, I hope you will quietly look upon the Lord.
People desire success, but success is achieved by continuing to fail.
Do not fear that you cannot be a joy to people; strive to be a joy to the Lord. Jesus endured the cross for the joy set before Him and sat at the right hand of God.
Human life is wrinkled by self-pursuit. The iron that can smooth it out is 'obedience.'
Do not become someone who circulates personal information. You will be caught up in needless strife. The Lord has granted us only the distribution of the gospel.

* What will you look upon? Do not doubt God's grace because of circumstances; interpret circumstances through the lens of grace. Faith is interpretation and choice. Interpretation and choice determine destiny.
If human actions are transformed into what pleases the Lord, the difficult tasks you struggled with will be resolved. It will become a miracle.
Whatever people sow, they reap accordingly. Whether others see it or not—the result is the same. Who are you when you are alone?

Although people seem to earn what they work for, it is God who provides. God's principle of giving material and gifts is not based on my ability but on need. When you know God, you understand that all things are accomplished according to His will. Even a bird does not fall unless the Lord wills it.
* Eternally Undefeated (永遠不敗) - No matter how dark it appears, God does not make mistakes, and through His faithfulness, He will be praised among the nations and all peoples. I believe in Him.

If a person wants to do God's will, he will know whether this teaching comes from God or from himself—John 7:17. If you seek to do His will and obey, you will hear the Word and gain discernment. But those who do not obey stumble in darkness because they turn away from the light of truth and 'love darkness rather than light' (John 3:19).

* Faith is interpretation. Faith is knowing that all God has given me is sufficient grace through the small grace I have received. Testing is when I judge myself through a few negative things, thinking that all the grace I received was in vain. Faith and unbelief are separated by only a thin line.
Love necessarily comes with sacrifice.

It is glorious to be called to a place devoid of love, full of hatred and envy, with no worship but only dryness and competition. The ground I tread will be covered by the Lord's precious blood. I believe that.

* Living in fellowship with the Lord means living with Jesus. The reason people live by their own will is that they do not know Jesus well. Those who live with Jesus cannot live by self-centeredness and greed.
Desiring to bless loved ones, I—an insufficient person—become a praying person and become God's servant.

I think of loved ones and intercede for them in prayer. When I pray with love and tears, I believe with faith that God will surely bless them and grant the wonderful blessings He has prepared. God will surely work.
Dear beloved, in God's kingdom there is no wasted time or vain thing. This moment I spend with you is more meaningful than a thousand or ten thousand years.

What blocks the calling is not personal weakness, persecution, or inferiority. It is not health or circumstances either. Only Satan hates and blocks the calling. I pray that you have the wisdom to ignore everything else and fight only that one enemy. I fight only Satan.

In fact, there is no luck or chance in life. There is certainly a purpose He has prepared. Life is walking that path, and sometimes you will pass through places seven times hotter than a furnace of fire, yet His deep purpose will never fade away.

Ministry is not a way to make a living. If the Lord says it is enough, that is the extent of the calling. Neither the 'I' that I evaluate nor the 'I' that others evaluate provides any legitimacy. Only He knows my path and determines my time. Moses, who completed his mission and never entered Canaan, is truly an awesome person.
A minister does not get healed or have problems solved by prayer. The Lord has prepared all healing and resolution and has determined to work through His servants.

To a beloved leader from a minister—A person called is not someone seeking success. Why? Because you have already succeeded in the Lord. The Lord has given me the very best, and if He calls me to endure something, He will supply the strength according to His good pleasure. What we need is absolute trust that doesn't move like a rock.
The best thing I do as a minister is not to hinder or obstruct revival. I think of poet Song Myung-hee's poem: 'The Lord did it.'

King Saul tried to kill David to protect what the Lord had not given him, and he ended up dead himself. David entrusted what God gave him to Him, sang with the Psalms on lonely nights, and the Lord ultimately accomplished it.

After his victory, Saul erected a monument to himself on Mount Carmel, but Samuel erected Ebenezer—'Thus far the Lord has helped us'—between Mizpah and Shen. What you face determines everything about your life.

Ironically, after King Saul achieved a great victory over the Amalekites, he distinctly began his path of decline. From this day on, he never meets the prophet Samuel again. The greatest crisis comes when you think you are succeeding greatly. You can win in war but lose in life.

The most effective way to resist Satan is to keep your own place. Because he is a fallen spirit who originally did not keep his place, he fears believers who faithfully keep theirs. No matter how glamorous and desirable a position is, if it is not the place given to me, it is far better to stay home and cook instant noodles. And those angels who did not keep their own domain but abandoned their proper dwelling, He has kept in eternal chains under gloomy darkness until the judgment of the great day—Jude 1:6.
* This is the time to pray. Elevate your prayer. Then a different class of life unfolds.

As we live, we often feel the desire to be pleasing to people's hearts. A warrior for the kingdom of God is someone whose heart is pleasing to Him. Like David, someone I trust to be pleasing to the Lord and fulfill His will—someone like David who endured much suffering but ultimately abandoned his own will and self-centered thinking to accomplish God's purpose. This age needs such people.
* How grateful I am! That we can repent—how grateful is that? After you have turned back, strengthen your brothers—Luke 22:32.

Life is a struggle for control. Whoever takes control determines victory or defeat. The Lord is not the one who helps us take control of our lives. He is the one who must rightfully take dominion over my life instead of me. This war becomes victory when it changes from my war to the Lord's war.
Life is not becoming increasingly difficult; I am being increasingly prepared to walk the way the Lord desires.

Samson lost a glorious life because he did not pray. He became a slave to the nation he should have conquered. If Samson had prayed from the beginning instead of at life's twilight, Judges 16 would have ended as a greater story.
Samson was physically strong but morally weak. We also have times when we are physically strong but morally weak. That is youth. Strength without restraint invites ruin.
It was not because the other person did wrong but because I needed refining. Look and interpret through the eyes of faith, not wounded emotions. Interpretation is power.
I earnestly pray for all friends who walk around saddened all day by loss, health issues, and relationship problems.
* Going all the way - If you saw possibility through prayer, you must complete it through supplication. Ultimately, isn't everything we start meant to see its end?
* Simple faith - In the end, worldly desires and yearning for God collide. Worldly desire lives a life of envying the world, but yearning for God makes the world shameful. Yearn. You will overcome.
Being bold by observing the situation is not the way. When I am bold, the situation obeys.

* The way to win - In games, there is 'winning momentum.' It means the opportunity to win. The moment I am dying under stress is the golden chance. If I push hard in the final five minutes, I can lead to victory. The most foolish thing is to have captured winning momentum but fail to push through. Then a game that could end in five minutes must start all over again. When you have caught spiritual 'winning momentum,' you must go all the way.
Do not judge and become discouraged by what circumstances lead you to; rather, obey what the Lord leads and find peace. Do not look at circumstances but interpret them with faith.
It is not circumstances that permit us to fulfill the Lord's will. When we fulfill the Lord's will, circumstances change.
The new year has work to do: listen to Jesus' voice, look upon Jesus, take Jesus' hand, and walk on water without hesitation.

* Satan's temptation - With so many problems, he says 'let's take a break for a bit.' He says you've done enough. He says stop for now, pause what you're doing, and get it together. Paul preached the gospel to the guard in prison and strengthened the church through letters. Never stop. 'And my imprisonment has become known throughout the whole imperial guard and to all the rest, in connection with Christ'—Philippians 1:13.
* Why we cannot stop - Tribulation, trials of the heart, wounds, and harsh words and mockery cannot stop believers. It is because Jesus, who dwells within me, not I, presses forward.
Do not live as you are naturally inclined; live as trained.

Vitality is challenged. To not only maintain the grace received at retreats but to strengthen it further requires a price: 1. You must cling to the Lord with your life. The Lord strongly supports those who fear Him. 2. You must bless and forgive. When you bless and pray for one another, the Holy Spirit is moved and present.

* Evasion brings no peace. There is a life of greed and a life of grace. If you live by self-centered ego, when storms come, the collapse is severe and you slip into drowning. Why? Because although you desire peace, you flee from rain and wind and run from circumstances, receiving counterfeit goods and painkillers provided by the world. But the peace Jesus gives is fundamentally different from what the world gives. 'Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your hearts be troubled, neither let them be afraid'—John 14:27.

* Why I am not afraid of trials - Stephen, in the face of death, shone with a face like an angel and gazed toward heaven. The trial did not prevail because he looked only to the Lord. Paul likewise held the cross—the boundless grace of the Lord who offered His Son on the cross—in his heart and breathed his last, so he could run for the Lord without regret. 'Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God'—Acts 7:56.

* It is not about fleeing but overcoming. Storms and tempests are not to be fled but overcome. There is no life without turbulent waves. The intensity and height of rough waves are not what matters; what matters is the grace that overwhelms that wind. When I trust in the Lord's sufficient grace, I am not shaken by every trial that comes and overcome abundantly. 'For God alone, O my soul, wait in silence, for my hope comes from him. He alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress; I shall not be shaken. On God rests my deliverance and my honor; my mighty rock, my refuge is God. Trust in him at all times, O people; pour out your heart before him; God is a refuge for us. Those of low estate are but a breath; those of high estate a delusion; in the balances they go up; they are together lighter than a breath. Put no trust in extortion; set no vain hopes on robbery; if riches increase, set not your heart on them. Once God has spoken; twice have I heard this: that power belongs to God, and that to you, O Lord, belongs steadfast love'—Psalm 62.

* The beginning becomes not half but the whole. God begins a good and righteous work within me and does not abandon it but completes and perfects it. It is not because I am good, but because He who does the work is good. Therefore, regardless of circumstances and feelings, we must trust God's Word and not abandon souls. God's grace is boundless. Being confident of this very thing, that he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it until the day of Jesus Christ. -Philippians 1:6

* The Gospel John Proclaimed John tells unbelievers in the Gospel of John to believe in Jesus. Also, in John's epistles, he exhorts believers to love. In Revelation, he tells those who face the end times to 'overcome.' Believe the Gospel, obtain eternal life, enjoy that eternal life, and be victorious.

* Grace That Transcends Limitations Difficult things are truly disliked but not feared. I am weak, but I see the Lord's grace working everywhere. The Lord's grace is not hindered by my human limitations.

* Are you at peace? You must be at peace. Did Jesus not bear the cross? Whether the path I walk in my lifetime is smooth like a calm river or fearful and difficult with great waves, my soul is always at ease. Though the devil rushes with mouth open to devour us, Jesus is our commander, and in the end we will fight and overcome. -Hymn 413

* The Definition of Being Lifted Up The Lord has exalted you and will continue to exalt you. The true meaning of exaltation is building up souls in a manner like the lowly Jesus. To the extent of being lifted up, there will be a drop in humility.

* How to Give Glory At awards ceremonies, holding trophies and flowers while saying 'glory to God,' or achieving worldly success and going around testimony meetings makes it easy to think we are giving glory to the Lord. But true glory is given when faith does not weaken in circumstances where everything has collapsed. And being not weak in faith, he considered not his own body now dead, when he was about an hundred years old, neither yet the deadness of Sara's womb: He staggered not at the promise of God through unbelief; but was strong in faith, giving glory to God. -Romans 4:19,20
