* When Faith Is Needed Faith is not needed when earning money, passing school entrance exams, or gaining popularity in the world. What is truly needed is when you must 'overcome.' Overcome. Overcome through faith.

* When Everything Collapses If the foundation collapses, what can the righteous do? The unrighteous flee to the mountains. But the righteous, when everything collapses, choose the 'righteous God.' Because He sees me and watches over me. I have put my trust in the Lord; How can you say to my soul, 'Flee as a bird to your mountain'? -Psalm 11:1 If the foundations are destroyed, What can the righteous do? -Psalm 11:3 The Lord is in His holy temple, The Lord's throne is in heaven; His eyes behold, His eyelids test the sons of men. -Psalm 11:4 For the Lord is righteous, He loves righteousness; His countenance beholds the upright. -Psalm 11:7
* Who Lives? For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain. -Philippians 1:21

* God's Crystal Terms and Conditions Your sin stands between us, interrupting our communion. I can no longer bless you while you persist in it. Trust My Spirit to help you, My child! Do not reject God's Word. Complete obedience is power. For this reason the Lord said concerning the king: 'Because you have rejected the word of the Lord, the Lord has also rejected you from being king.' -1 Samuel 15:26

No one wants to marry just to clean house and organize the garden. Because they love, such actions naturally follow as a result. Never work without grace. Whatever you do, if you walk with Him, you will find happiness.

* Burning like flames and scattering... As if every moment were the last, as if every meeting were a farewell, everything must be offered wholeheartedly. Before the Lord without regret; my being is by God's grace, and He alone is the eternal Judge. I am already being poured out as a drink offering, and the time of my departure is at hand. I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith. Finally, there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will give to me on that Day, and not to me only but also to all who have loved His appearing. -2 Timothy 4:6,7,8

Resentment and complaint are truly difficult subjects. If we just pass these two subjects well, world evangelization would have been accelerated by about 1000 years. Let us believe in God and love souls. Then we will at least not be hurt.

* God's Specialty There is no law that says you must die. If you advance prepared to die, God will open a path you never imagined. God's specialty is bringing forth water from the wilderness and creating paths in the desert.
When you succeed, you will feel some envious glances. But when you obey, you will experience the power of the earth shaking and the heavens crying out. Obedience over success.

Do not seek to succeed; seek to obey. It is not about my realization that matters, but the Lord's will that comes first. That is discipleship. If you love one another, by this all will know that you are My disciples. -John 13:35

I am lacking and sorry in much before the saints, but I run today because of the Lord who called me. Until now, I have lived because of the Lord's burning compassion, and if He allows me to run for the Lord and serve souls, it will be because of the Lord's boundless grace. Let us look only to the Lord.

The weapons of the Spirit include honesty, humility, sincerity, grace, love, prayer, obedience, community, healthy dependence, God's Word, and following the guidance of the Holy Spirit. On the other hand, the weapons of the flesh include excessive anger, deception, accusation, shame, hatred, pride, hiding things in secret, becoming self-centered, becoming defensive, and unhealthy attitudes like 'I don't need you.' The spiritual warfare we are engaged in is not 'a half-day sports competition where we briefly go out, win easily, and quickly forget.' This fight is a protracted war—a fight for survival, life or death.' (Ephesians 6:12, The Message)

* Worldly people make choices by avoiding challenges and fear and preferring personal interest. But a person of faith stands where the Lord says to stand, at places considered right despite disadvantages. A person prepared to die has nothing to fear. Yet we are already dead in the Lord and living in new life. Once we cross the line of death, we are not only bold but at peace.

In worldly sports, results are very important. There are cheers from the audience. But for us, process and attitude are more important. Because there is One who is watching. Even if the path appears dark, God does not make mistakes. In the One who strengthens me, I can do all things, and all things work together for good.

A person seeking worldly recognition will be shipwrecked in faith. Worldly recognition is pride and a hard-to-resist great temptation. But one who lives by faith is comforted from heaven. Whatever he faces, he will not fear and will overcome. -Hymn 434

Worldly wisdom calculates. There is always fear of avoiding danger. But a person of faith trusts in the Lord's wisdom and is bold. The original Creator has already finished all calculations before the foundation of the world.

The excessive reactions of the world and people are not a realm given to me. It is not my concern. When you are filled with the Holy Spirit and receive power, it is resolved. It is not for you to know the times or epochs which the Father has fixed by His own authority; but you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you shall be My witnesses both in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and even to the remotest part of the earth. -Acts 1:7,8

We must lay down the advantageous position that worldly people think about and move forward trusting only in the Lord. When I cling more to advantageous ground than to the Lord, God tells me to let go. When we let go of advantage, we hear less of the reputation we so desire; yet the question remains whether the Lord alone is sufficient.

Do not abandon 'calling' while giving up 'ambition' for the world. We do not study and work to enjoy only ourselves. We do it to serve. Reject positions, materials, and qualifications for enjoyment, and obtain materials, positions, and qualifications for service.

The hardest thing in the world is imitation. People who do this well are called comedians. Living as if you have a nature you don't have is tiring work and the life the Lord hates most. Beware of practicing your righteousness before other people in order to be seen by them. -Matthew 6:1

We must live as diligently and fiercely as to achieve success in the world. But claiming rights over that achievement is not the purpose. Because we do not claim rights, we do not become unnecessarily attached, and because we are not attached, we are also not irresponsible. When we walk the path He gave, all things work for good.

I hope we become missionaries who choose victory in the Lord over success in the world. Rather than envying those who build specs through self-development, let us become young people who overcome evil with good through genuine 'ability' where the Lord is with us. The era of competing with shells is passing, and in a world like a live broadcast, let us compete with essence, not lip-sync or marketing.

* Mission Over Efficiency Rather than trying to use my time productively, I must do what I must do. Like breathing and heart beating, those who love God and have received that heart stand where they should stand, do what they should do, and are with whom they should be with. But such a life and decisions often face criticism from lust and worldly values.

* A Brave (?) Answer Without Knowledge Moses says to Pharaoh, 'Let this people go.' Pharaoh naturally did not let them go and was destroyed by ten plagues. Because he did not know God. If you do not know God, you cannot be free, nor can you obey. Pharaoh said, 'Who is the Lord that I should obey His voice to let Israel go? I do not know the Lord, and besides, I will not let Israel go.' -Exodus 5:2

There are friends who let go of good chances in worldly terms to keep their cell meetings. While the minister cannot be responsible for all, because that heart given to God is precious, I earnestly pray that He will repay it with good, even if through fasting.
* Become salt and be dissolved. Deny yourself and bear your cross daily. You will disappear and be forgotten, but the Lord will surely find you.

Somali pirates do not touch American merchant ships. Because they refuse to negotiate with terrorism, they cannot originally receive money. Do not respond to rumors and talk. Then evil powers can no longer exercise power in relationships.
Marginalized, threatened, with lost hope of living, frustrated, and fallen into sin, yet he was a man who trusted in God, a king loved by God's heart. David. Rise up like David.

Sometimes we knowingly want to be deceived. This is called self-pity. True faith is needed here. We must think about who called us at first. 'You must believe that He exists and that He is a rewarder of those who seek Him.'

There is an illusion that we can pursue selfish desires any way we can, find stability, and then raise God's name. God is not a passenger in such success. He seeks and supports those who rely only on His power and advance boldly.

* Just a Moment, Let Me Think About Our Sister~~. What He wants you to think now is not negative scenarios that pollute me. Think and hope for what will go well. Think of possessing God's heart and that His influence will spread and the kingdom of heaven will overflow, and declare it. Surprisingly, I see Christians loosening up difficult situations from small places. Not 'I'll have a hard time meeting that person!' but 'Whether I live or die, Christ in me will be glorified, surely God will help me!'
After praying for the retreat...,

I hope that the Lord will show grace to all the members who prayed and prepared for the retreat, and give them rest. And I earnestly hope that they will run without regret with the grace they have received. In faith, there is no retreat. By running and running, I hope they bloom as a beautiful fragrance like a libation offering before the Lord's altar, poured out like wine.

A Story About the Retreat The church (organizers) is prepared. What about you? There is something in life that must not be missed. You must seize the opportunity the Lord gives you at a time worthy of receiving grace, in the day of salvation.
A student who completes homework and studies perfectly for an exam is not only at peace but confident and enjoys it. Those who achieve their purpose are bold. That is why people in love are bold.

A moment seems like nothing. But for a person of faith, every moment is precious. When we do our best in each moment, relying on the Lord, ordinary hours of trusting the Lord gather to create a glorious day like a giant wave.
There are times when it is difficult to make a decision to obey. There are times when it is fearful to let go of what was familiar. But the Lord says He will walk with us.

* You do not need to send a reminder to the cupbearer to remember your favor. No matter how shabby or meaningless it seems (prison work), if you do your work faithfully, the day will come when Pharaoh is so curious about the dream he had that he dies of curiosity. Prepare for a lifetime for a moment.

Drunkenness and debauchery, the world is full of all kinds of corruption. God prepares new people for new work. Just as Abraham built an altar and called on 'the Lord' in an age without the fear of God, we too must call on the Father's holy name in this London land. The hope of this nation begins with my repentance.
Endless passion—what was it you so longed to see, what made you burn all your youth and never stop running? Jesus, Jesus only.
* Immediate Obedience What is desired now is what is possible. Prayer and thanksgiving, blessing and forgiveness, intercession and love, and looking only to Jesus and running forward.
Do not be easily shaken. Resist challenges fiercely regardless of victory or defeat. Then even if you fall, recovery is easier in the next step.
* Tailor Made Who will tell you to do it? And who will stop you? The calling is individual for each person.

What is important in sports is controlling the flow of the game. King Nebuchadnezzar threatens with a furnace heated seven times hotter, trying to control the situation. What breaks through these worldly challenges is 1. The 'faith' that the Lord rescues, and 2. The 'sacrifice' to remain unchanged even if He does not—the cross. When we have absolute trust and faith in God and bear the cross saying 'if we die, we die,' we experience the Lord overwhelming the enemy and controlling the situation. God has put all things under the authority of Christ and has made him head over all things for the benefit of... God has put everything under the control of Christ. Ephesians 1:22
Victory is determined by how you fill time and empty spaces. Will you fill them with Jesus, or surrender them to laziness and meaninglessness?

We may sing songs of victory and gather the harvest, yet drag ourselves along like defeated soldiers who have no choice. The clear reason is that we serve not only the Lord but harbor vain thoughts, and are filled more with the world than with the Holy Spirit. What is fearful and difficult about offering your life before the Lord?
To avoid regret as time passes, you must live well. Living well means focusing on the Lord and not fearing people.
* The most difficult challenges become tests. That is why God asked Abraham for Isaac, not Sarah.

* The attitude of receiving curses: King Saul feared people, so through religious rituals he was sensitive to the people's responses. But David feared God, so he did not respond to man's curses. David also said to Abishai and all his servants, 'If even my own son seeks my life, what more this Benjamite? The Lord has commanded him to curse me; let him curse.' - 2 Samuel 16:11

Trials and challenges will not cease, and they have never been easy. Yet I am not afraid. What does one who has already died to the cross have to cling to? Even if everything is taken away, truth lives and His kingdom is eternal. Hope is power.

When we are tested, we think it is because the other person is evil or we are weak. This is not true. The fundamental cause of being tested is our failure to believe in the Lord's work. Unbelief is the root cause.

Even if you encounter problems you don't know during a test, set them aside and keep moving forward. We are not called to be perfect, but called to obey. God is not pleased when we retreat and fall into despair. Problems that seemed impossibly difficult in elementary school become easy in middle school.

When you encounter a trial, rejoice. This is because trials of faith produce endurance. When endurance is fully developed, you become complete, equipped with all things, and mature. Endurance without guidance is difficult. That is why we need guidance. When you receive the wisdom and insight that 'last month I was attacked, but this month I will reap the harvest,' you can endure present suffering. Trial produces maturity, and maturity requires wisdom. If you pass through the tunnel, go through it boldly. God will surely reward you. 'My brethren, count it all joy when you fall into various trials, knowing that the testing of your faith produces endurance. But let endurance have its perfect work, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking nothing. If any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all liberally and without reproach, and it will be given to him.' - James 1:2, 5

When you encounter a trial, what the Lord commands is endurance. Endurance makes me complete. Wisdom that looks to the Lord's promises will support endurance. The Lord surely rewards those who wait. 'My brethren, count it all joy when you meet various trials, knowing that the testing of your faith produces endurance. Let endurance have its perfect work, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking nothing. If any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all liberally and without reproach, and it will be given to him.' - James 1:2, 5

* Judge, jury, and audience: An athlete cannot perform excellently if he repeatedly focuses on the audience's gaze or relies on his own feelings. The judge alone makes the judgment. There is nothing more foolish than judging your existence by your own feelings arising from the audience's reaction, and nothing more absurd than abandoning the competition and running away because of it. 'From now on there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will give to me on that Day, and not to me only but also to all who have loved His appearing.' - 2 Timothy 4:8
If trials lead to hope, break through decisively. Tribulation produces endurance, endurance produces character, and character produces hope.

Since the New Year's conference, the messages that keep being drawn are about loyalty, diligence, and dedication. And God will surely remember that labor and loyalty. Let us look only to the Lord and run toward His Word. Let us put aside difficulty and pain and run for the Lord. 'Therefore, my beloved brethren, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that your labor is not in vain in the Lord.' - 1 Corinthians 15:58

Nothing is as fair as faith. Because there is judgment, and moreover, that judgment is just. Faith is not something you cannot do because you lack money, or because you lack air conditioning or even buildings. You cannot do it only because you lack heart. If you have heart, you see the Lord's glory even on a deserted island.

The Christian life is a prayer life. In the end, the struggle is whether you continue prayer. Satan will try to prevent prayer through various other issues. But those who advance toward the goal continue to move toward the place of prayer.

There are many difficult factors and trials in faith. There are two ways to go that path: going while filled, or barely getting by. Environmental problems are solved by being filled, but spiritual poverty cannot be solved by environment. Simply be filled.

* Knowing myself: David was the eighth son, not introduced to honored guests. When his father sent him on errands, he was not trusted enough to be asked for a sign. He habitually heard harsh words from his brother Eliab. He himself confessed that his mother conceived him in sin. Yet he was faithful, steadfast, and bold because he discovered himself in relationship with the Lord. 'Abide in Me, and I in you.' - John 15:7 'When my father and my mother forsake me, then the Lord will take care of me.' - Psalm 27:10
Faith is not something you merely should live like; it is something you cannot help but live like.
Faith is not finished by hitting one home run. We advance day by day between choices. If we live choosing to follow the Spirit, we will not achieve the desires of the flesh or worry from vain losses.

Christians must be flexible. There is nothing worth risking your life for except the Lord, and all things work together for good in Him. If you are easily excited, you will be embarrassed many times later. The Almighty Father accomplishes many beautiful conclusions without my anger.

The reason believers need power is not to rule and oppress like Pharaoh. The world needs money and power to live comfortably and discriminate, but we use power to confront suffering and see the Lord's glory.

* Three ways of love: We must love passionately with all that the Lord has given and allowed to us. Then we must take out one life insurance policy. Insurance pays when things leave. It is to offer unceasing intercession so that the souls we have loved will continue to grow, looking only to the Lord.
In disappointing situations, if you can win and endure ridicule and mockery for the sake of hope, you can receive the grace God wants to give.

Disappointment comes from greed. Greed means you had human expectations. If the Lord did not give it, we have received nothing. If it is the Lord's gift, let us obey and wait. If we use human calculation and methods, like Jacob, our autobiography becomes thick.
If you are disappointed, it means you were impatient. Impatience means you did not trust the Lord and further sought your own glory. Remember this: The Lord never gives His glory to idols.
* However weak, however poor, it is a blessing to have something to offer. Weakness does not prevent helping, nor does lack prevent giving.

Those who have steadfast hearts and trust the Lord are the same person. The Lord upholds and keeps in peace those who offer their hearts to the Lord every moment and second, committing themselves to Him. 'You will keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on You, because he trusts in You.' - Isaiah 26:3
No matter how dark situations appear or how critical the environment seems, advance toward the goal. Never ceasing, with the power to overlook, love, humble yourself.... ♡♡♡♡

No matter how much information you gather or how much you think, you cannot be certain you will make the right decision. That is why you must pray. Those who just follow others have no prayer. A decision without prayer ignores God and denies His image. I hope you become a person whose life the Lord takes responsibility for.
No matter how difficult and hard, even seemingly impossible, believe that God is working through His faithfulness. The Lord never gives up or makes mistakes.
* Beating heart means you have a mission. If you are given time and life, you have God's mission, and that is love. Serve with what the Lord has given.

Abraham was commanded to leave Ur of the Chaldeans and go to Canaan. But he wandered at Haran, a middle point. There he was rebuked by God and finally entered Canaan. He was 75 years old then. Do not wander in Haran. Haran means 'dryness.'

* My purpose is the calling: The one who sent me here is God. What God expects is not the achievement of ambition or showy success. I am not perfect in faith, character, and virtue, and deserve criticism, but I need only to do what Jesus did through the merit of His blood. That is to plant the cross on this earth, baptize in the Lord's name, and cry out for the Holy Spirit to come. Then I will find true happiness.
Not falling completely means being held up and lifted by the Lord's strong right arm. Not falling completely means... Psalm 37

An African chief was given a Toyota 4x4. He boasted of using it amazingly. He listens to music on CD, and when it is hot, he goes inside, turns on the air conditioning and rests. Then he leaves again, walking a long distance. A life without prayer is futile digging. It is amazing that even after experiencing this, he cannot let go of the regret of futile digging. He has been deeply involved in the organization for a long time—this is not a faith life for just a day or two.

Never be defeated by evil. Even if defeated by evil, the Lord will restore again, but now grace must overflow from restoration to transcendence. Not being defeated by evil means not sinning, but rather overcoming evil with good. 'Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.' - Romans 12:21

Evil people seem strong and appear to be succeeding, but before tribulation and the day of judgment, they are like chaff blown away. Because they have no roots. Even if leaves flourish, they will only be blown away at the critical moment. In farming, winnowing throws both grain and chaff into the air. Then the grain remains and the chaff is blown away. The condition of righteousness lies in God's Word.
I do not want to become an evil minister. Neither do I desire to be called a decent minister. Apart from the Lord, I become an empty shell, nothing.

Do not retreat when something seems impossible, but look with faith and attempt it. Even if you lack faith and sink while walking on water, if the Lord holds your hand and you walk, that is enough. Having no failure means you have not attempted anything. Faith is an adventure.
If you pursue only stability, you cannot live the best life but only a second-best life. Please undertake the adventure of faith. ^^

Carry a list of intercession prayers in your inner pocket or handbag. Prayer gains strength through repetition. Accumulated prayer is more useful and powerful than the traditions of prominent families.
* Looking toward the goal, thinking deeply, pursuing and running means that what you are aiming for is so precious that you do not care about other things.
It is regrettable that I cannot give more. When suffering most, we can give most powerfully. When it is darkest, we can seek the Lord most earnestly.
If those who go before are faithful, those who follow will become faithful too. Do not become unfaithful; be faithful.

James said the prayer of a righteous person has great power. If we do not have God's heart within us, even if we pray, we cannot release God's power to work. To pray effectively, we must pray with God's heart and attitude toward that situation and person. Without forgiveness, true blessing and prayer cannot be achieved.
If you disguise ambition as calling, you will compete. If you compete, you cannot obey. Because means and ends become reversed. If you cannot obey, the Lord is not your master.

* Schedule: A Christian famous for piety was revealed to be involved in crime. Moreover, he was caught at the scene attempting rape, received a heavy sentence, and went to prison. While he was serving time, some said the human heart is unknowable, and some said the God he so believed in was fake. His name was Joseph. (Genesis 39) Be patient. God's timetable may differ somewhat from yours.

Suppose one day you receive a text or email saying someone will give you several thousand pounds. How grateful and encouraged would you be? But most such offers are loans where you borrow and must repay double the principal over six years. It seems to solve problems like helping you, but the conclusion is making more money. The Lord does not give cash. The Lord gives promises. Promises bear fruit through prayer and obedience.
Whatever disappointment and failure come, and however long the times of the world's realistic difficulties flow, 'the power of the blood that brings freedom from sin' is 'eternal unchanging truth.'

Sometimes you encounter situations that are like terrible hell. Though it should never happen, if I were taken to such a place, I would look for a guitar as long as permitted, organize worship meetings, and worship the Lord. And after the gatherings, since there is much sulfurous fire there, providing food should not be difficult. Please remember that the Lord's grace is sufficient, that He gives me what I can bear, and that it is clear what I must do wherever I am.

* The tears of blessing are never light. Abraham, the source of blessing, received the glory of becoming God's friend and became the ancestor of all believers today. However, many overlook the tears of blood he must have shed for those four days. 'My son, God will himself provide the lamb for the burnt offering.' - Genesis 22:8

Someone asks like this: 'Even if I cannot climb high, can I not take off one piece of clothing and give it to someone naked?' The answer is this: That is why the Lord tells us to live diligently. Do not merely receive help while stripped naked; rather, help those in need by giving them clothes. Since you are giving anyway, give multiple garments to help them. The Lord does not extinguish the dimly burning wick and does not break the bruised reed. However, at the same time, the Lord does not want me to become a dimly burning wick or a bruised reed. He desires that I be like a torch and like cedar.

* Either-Or 2: If something must be abandoned, then the things I love and cherish so dearly must be abandoned without hesitation to hold onto the Lord. If someone truly loves souls, they will attempt to love them with the highest love. That is choosing 'voluntary self-isolation' called solitude to draw living water from a deep well. Being called as a channel of grace is because holiness is life. Truth is lonely but grows bolder each day. - Rev. Choi Chun-seon
When facing difficult times, though my faith is small, I trust even more in my Lord whom I depend on. - Hymn 342
When enduring through hardship, you will surely overcome. Foolishness is to give up the game that must be won. Giving up is the work of the devil; endurance is the work of the Holy Spirit.
When you think something is difficult, disregard it and attempt it. Make the impossible normal.
An adult can match a child's pace, but a child cannot match an adult's pace. That is why the mature person is a servant and one who understands.
A foolish person regards each moment as meaningless. This means not valuing time. This present moment spent together is more precious than gold. This is because it is life.

Even if awkward, when a meeting and fellowship maintains courtesy toward the other person and is conducted truthfully without shame before the Lord, no regret remains even after parting. As many years pass, even when meeting again by chance on some street or at some place, the heart does not race again; instead, the path I must walk becomes clearer.

* Confirm your faith. The Christian life is not about avoiding storms well but confronting them well. Whatever the world says, holding onto the Lord is true faith, and this is proven only when storms come.

* Life comes with suffering as a basic option. When God does not work according to my will, it is easy to blame others. That is because we are trying to walk in the air apart from reality. Life is not a vacation; it is paying the price and holding onto holiness.
It could be good until yesterday, but today could be different. However, do not be alarmed. Only the righteous live by faith, not by circumstances and evaluations.

A person who faithfully tends his small flock will have the Lord of glory appear to him 'suddenly, unexpectedly, at a time he did not anticipate.' 'And there were shepherds living out in the fields nearby, keeping watch over their flocks at night. An angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them.' - Luke 2:8-9

How much you possess matters little. It matters who holds it. Two small fish and five loaves of bread from a small child are nothing, but in Jesus' hands, they became a miracle that fed five thousand. Please entrust your inadequate life to the Lord. The Lord's grace will be sufficient.

When a mother enters the operating room, children cry. But there is nothing we can do for her. The mother comforts the children and tells them to be good listeners, to care for one another, and that she will return healthy. The night before Jesus went to the cross, He made one request. While washing their feet, He said to cover one another's shortcomings and love one another. 'When I return, by this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another.' That is the Lord's request. Right now, the only way to follow the Lord is to love one another.
* The Final Victory Our victory is not in the world. When we breathe our last and stand before the Lord, the true victory is determined.
When I see various problems, I feel infinitely insufficient. That is why I pray and fall to my knees. Being able to see one's own insufficiency is a blessing. It is because I depend more on the Lord.

If you grasp summer so as not to miss it because summer is good, the Lord cannot give you an abundant autumn. When you let go of earthly things, you receive better heavenly things. That is walking with the Lord.
Ease does not come automatically, nor is it given because circumstances permit. Ease is the spoils of earnest 'spiritual warfare' and the result of 'priorities' that look only to the Lord.

And the Lord said to him, 'What is that in your hand?' And he said, 'A staff.' - Exodus 4:2 1. God does not ask how much I have achieved or whether I am recognized. 2. He asks what you have, even if it is not impressive. 3. Moses was a failed old man who held a dry stick. 4. Having much and climbing high does not display the Lord's glory. 5. If there is nothing and we pretend to have something, there is no need for a mask and no reason to boast. 6. Even a barren life, when obedient to the Lord, reveals power and glory. 'So Moses took the staff of God in his hand.' - Exodus 4:20 A dry stick, when held in the Lord's hands, becomes the staff of God. A ring from a department store and a ring given by a loved one are not the same ring. It is 'the ring you put on me.' When a meaningless stick I used at will becomes the Lord's staff held by God, glory is seen there. Exodus 4:17 'Take this staff in your hand with which you will perform the signs.'

Now the Lord was with Joseph, and he was a man of success. - Genesis 39:2 Joseph's state is slavery. Yet Scripture says he was a man of success. Betrayed and hated, he lost his position and much else, yet he is called a successful person. Rather, his brothers who sold Joseph remain free with nothing to envy, but they became people without success. The reason for success was that God was with him. Emmanuel itself is a blessing. When God is with you, your troubles and storms are not exempted, but rather you experience the Lord's living presence within them.
So the Lord blessed the Egyptian's house because of Joseph; all his household's possessions and fields came under the blessing of the Lord. - Genesis 39:5 Potiphar was blessed because of Joseph.
* Ministry is Motivation. If motivation is lacking, rather than forcing others, this is something I must kneel down and repent of.

* Small obedience, miraculous change. When the church is not following Scripture, if passion is poured into criticism, destruction will accelerate and judgment will not be escaped. If you examine yourself and live in thorough obedience to the Word, you will experience the miracle of the community coming alive.

If you worked hard yet feel empty, that is not zeal for the Lord. If you loved the Lord, you should have laid aside other expectations long ago. If you sought people's joy, you are not a servant of Christ and should be ashamed.
There is no one without passion. The problem is not maintaining it to the end. Jesus never stopped. He advanced to the very end and said 'It is finished' on the cross.

In English, the word 'crazy' sometimes refers to sustained passion in one field. It's something like 'absorbed.' However, the word 'insane' feels like continuously going to a false well where you cannot get living water no matter what. If you are mad about what is vain (insane), turn around and be mad about the true goal (crazy). You cannot reach (reach) it unless you are mad (crazy) about it.

* Life is message. Pray earnestly for your disciples and clearly command them to live a great life. That is 'like your teacher, look only to the Lord and run forward.' All that was beneficial I count as loss compared to knowing Christ. I am finding all my righteousness in Christ. - Philippians 2 'And I pray that your love may abound more and more in knowledge and depth of insight.' - Luke 22:32

In spiritual warfare, persistence regardless of circumstances is the key to victory or defeat. Love is patient. When you persist with love, even the strongest fortress will crumble. Keep going. They can never keep going.

In spiritual warfare, the most awakened person and group are affected first. This is because they are on the front lines. First is the mission field, and second are intercessors. Do not regard trials as strange. They are a wrong fasting period toward a wrong god.
Transforming souls is not my work. I must entrust it sufficiently (completely) to the Lord. That is why I pray and believe.

In the movie A Beautiful Mind, Professor John Nash overcomes what he wants to see and the hallucinations that result, and wins the Nobel Prize. After his acceptance speech, as he and his wife are leaving, three figures from the hallucinations appear. But he does not choose them. Living by faith does not mean being free from all temptations. It simply means not choosing them. Faith is a choice.

There are two ways to go to the neighbor's house. One is to walk straight in a direct line, and the other is to turn around and circle the entire earth. 'I will run to you. Yes, I will run the race until I see your face, not by might, not by power, but by the spirit of God.'
Those who believe in Jesus do not act by force. They act within God's guidance, that is, within His overflowing guidance.
* The Power to Advance. A believer advances not because circumstances permit, but because prayer transforms circumstances. The Lord is wonderful. Pray for great things for great purposes.
Those who believe in Jesus are not impatient. If it is not the Lord's will, they deny even their own emotions and will, and rather rejoice because the flesh's desire has been broken.
I have come this far by Jesus' love. And by Jesus' love, I will go there too.

* When you encounter injustice. Injustice comes anyway. You may be misunderstood about things you did not do and your intentions. There are two choices: if you just get angry, there is no solution and both you and others die; the other is to think of the Lord and His glory within it, endure, and become beautiful. For those who think of the Lord who permitted all this, there will be great glory. 'It was good for me to be afflicted so that I might learn your decrees.' - Psalm 119:71 'If you suffer for doing good and endure it, this is commendable before God.' - 1 Peter 2:19
In Jesus, nothing is meaningless. All the mistakes I thought were trial and error will become important uses, and all things will work together for good. When in Jesus....

* The end of those who look to people. Trying to satisfy people is truly selfish. Pilate knew Jesus was innocent but delivered Him to the cross to satisfy the Jews. Not because he loved the Jews, but only for his own comfort, to satisfy them. Stop caring about people's eyes and surrender to the Lord.

The more Jesus is attacked, the stronger He becomes. This is because from the beginning, faith is depending on the Lord, not on my own strength. This is why He becomes stronger in difficulty and His power becomes stronger when we are weak. Strike a little more until the blade is sharp and shiny.
Jesus does not forcibly create storms. And He will never lead us into them without giving us the power to overcome.

Jesus drew a clear boundary between His disciples and the crowds. For those who love Jesus, for those who follow Jesus, miracles become common sense. 'Whoever has my commands and keeps them is the one who loves me. The one who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I too will love them and show myself to them.' - John 14:21

There are people who insist on walking a path Jesus blocks. They are foolish. There are people who seek Jesus' help on their own path. They are cunning. There are people who walk a path they would not walk if it were not for Jesus. They are people of faith. They will see God's glory.
'I am ready, Lord. I came to do what I should, as it is written of me in the book.'
Do not keep hesitating with 'just in case' about things other than Jesus. If you run 'just as expected' for Jesus alone, you will see glory.

From long ago until now, the Lord has helped us, and He is still helping us now. 'Samuel took a stone and set it up between Mizpah and Shen. He named it Ebenezer, saying, 'Thus far the Lord has helped us.' - 1 Samuel 7:12

When I was 20 years old, with a torn and breaking heart, not knowing what I was hoping for, I went to an all-night prayer meeting, staying up all Friday night. Pastor Kim Jong-han, then 73 years old, spoke unexpectedly around 2 AM and said: 1. I understand your heartache completely. 2. And you walk the path of the Lord. God understands me more than anyone. And He encourages me to walk the Lord's path. A person of faith does not fail the Lord's work because of personal circumstances. For me who has kept this rule for the past 24 years, it is both a rule and the bare minimum courtesy toward the Lord. By faith alone! I can do all things through Him who gives me strength.

The essence of faith is no different now than it was in the past. In the past, if you did not deny the Lord, they would throw you into a lion's den; now, if you hold more affection for something else than the Lord, they threaten you with failure and despair. To reject these things still requires clinging to the cross while shedding blood. Determine to shed blood. Rather, you will have peace.

* Infinite Loyalty of a Finite Being Being finite means being limited by time and place. I can only work within my capacity in the time and place given to me. Conversely, the Lord will work in all other areas. When you are faithful unto death, the Lord's almightiness accomplishes what transcends my limits.
* Philippians Bible Study (Deep and Powerful) - Fellowship preparation in progress

* The Power of Refusal In an evil age, when you refuse comfort and ease and embrace your calling, you can live a life obedient to God's purpose in calling you to this place. We mistakenly think we can follow the Lord's way while also fulfilling our own desires. Blessings come when you refuse your desires. Your wishes are desires; the Lord's wishes are callings.

* Your 'Fear' Now Understood Abraham was a worshipper who built altars everywhere, consulted God through prayer regarding His judgment, and had already received the title of a person of faith (Genesis 15:6). However, only when he did not spare his only son Isaac, whom he obtained in old age, did God 'confirm' that Abraham feared Him. Fearing God is not about gaining something but about cutting away whatever He asks you to abandon. The angel said, 'Do not lay your hand on the boy or do anything to him, for now I know that you fear God, since you have not withheld your son, your only son, from me.' —Genesis 22:12
* Why Faith Is Not a Show It is not being conscious of people but keeping only the Lord's gaze in mind and sacrificing unconditionally.

* Why Change Does Not Come Despite Agreement Though you are troubled and nod your head and listen sweetly, when you finally make a choice, you see that you have not abandoned yourself. It is because from the beginning, you have no intention of giving up the pleasure you enjoy. If you do not obey, the voice will not be heard.
* Fly Rather Than Survive Go beyond merely surviving; soar. Therefore, offer different prayers, different worship, different dedication. That is my calling. We are not just holding on....
* Abundant Supply There is no lack of God's faithful supply for God's work that aligns with God's will.

Before saying there is not enough, try praying first. Retreating by saying there is not enough is the most convenient method but also the worst choice. When there is lack, come to the Lord and entrust yourself to Him. I entrust my barley-bread life to the Lord. Living life without prayer is like a train trying to move without rails laid down.

* The Secret to Determining Life To live a blessed life, you must remove many things. In particular, if you could, you would want to remove the causes of pain and find freedom. One of the greatest pains is the pain of childbirth, yet no one abandons its cause—life itself. Pain is not a problem to be avoided but a work to be completed.

* Winning Hearts Gaining people's hearts is not accomplished by effort alone. It is grace. Therefore, you must do two things: be content and be grateful. If you have more than one thing given to you, it means you have people to serve and work to do.
* Speaking Through the Results of Life Are you not the result of my work in the Lord? —Paul in 1 Corinthians 9:1

* Herod's Kind of Faith Herod was troubled while hearing John the Baptist's sharp, cutting sermon, yet there was no change in his life. It was because he did not want to lose pleasure. Without loss, there is no change. He will surely be destroyed. That is merely religious satisfaction—reading prayer books and substituting them for prayer, or receiving evangelism training and simply satisfying yourself. 'When Herod heard about John, he feared him and protected him, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man. When Herod heard him, he was greatly perplexed; yet he heard him gladly.' —Mark 6:20

* Being Limited Is Excellence When you are swept away by circumstances and situations, you cannot follow God's will. You must discern the Lord's will you should walk and draw lines that you must not cross. Being limited is excellence.

* Good Loss Some people do what they can do, yet their faith declines. They feel the lack and know they must change to live, but looking back, they are always stepping backward. The reason is that they have not determined to let go. A greater value can only be obtained after losing a lesser value. You cannot have both revival and the world. Once you have determined your purpose, you must boldly abandon what needs to be abandoned. Only then will you receive the blessing of all these things being added to you.

* What Must Be Done When Humbled There is something that must be done when you are humbled. Your destiny changes there. Ability is what you must develop during days of obscurity. When you are full and receive attention, sharp spirituality hardly emerges. Give everything and dwell in His presence that cannot be taken away.

Only the Lord sustains me. When I stumble, He still upholds me. 'If your law had not been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction.' —Psalm 119:92 'This is my comfort in my affliction: your promise gave me life.' —Psalm 119:50

A person who sits on five thousand talents and eats alone is not living well. He is merely a foolish rich man, a person living poorly. A person who shares even a single bean is living well. You must become a person of faith who rushes forward without hesitation to save many lives.

* True Gospel The world is not moved by the church's building, high position, or power. It wants to hear the true Gospel. A pastor's wife shares the Gospel with a taxi driver. He is indifferent. But then she adds: 'I am on my way back from the hospital where I received a cancer diagnosis. Driver, please believe in Jesus. There is surely heaven after death. You must believe in Jesus.' The taxi driver listens. The greater glory and influence of believers is not 'seeking blessings' but 'overcoming.' 'He made up his mind...' —Daniel 1:8
If you have sought entirely God's glory and the salvation of souls, you are already a champion. Do not be shaken; look to the Lord alone with boldness.

* Anxiety Am I not aging like this? Will I become a failure, a loser in life? Must I take some action? Worrying that you will become what you do not want to be—that is anxiety. While anxiety is natural for pursuing desires, those who pursue the Lord's will are bold and peaceful because He accomplishes it.
If someone asks why I am in this place now, I will answer: Jesus. Since they asked the reason, I will give the reason. Jesus is the only reason for my life.

If Joseph had believed, 'I believe I will go home this year,' his faith would have been shipwrecked. Joseph believed in God who was with him. Therefore, he could be guided on a path he had never walked. Do not press to accomplish your desires but look back to see if the Lord is with you. The Lord asks you: Where is your faith?

Joseph rejected sexual temptation and willingly went to prison. Moreover, he forgave all his wicked brothers who tried to kill him and made most of his teenage and twenties rot in slavery and prison. If he had lived a life of retaliation, he would have made both himself and others miserable. After being released from prison at age thirty, he lived eighty-one more years and accomplished God's amazing plan. That is the ability to interpret, the ability to discern the Lord's will, the ability to redeem the time, the ability to live wisely to the end, the ability to seize opportunity, and the ability to walk with the Lord. —Ephesians 5:15-17
* Faith Is a Gift Faith is a gift, but it grows when you use it.
There is no luck. There is only thorough guidance. Abandon 'perhaps' and walk the prepared way firmly in prayer. Do not compare or wander.

* Bad Habits When work is busy and tests are imminent, your heart is so preoccupied that you do not say, 'Oh! I forgot to breathe these past few days! I need to breathe quickly.' You do not even forget to breathe; you cannot skip eating at all. When you miss the place of grace because of work, it becomes a habit. You are developing a terrible habit.
The One working among us is God, so I must also walk by the Spirit. That is self-control and focus. Then the desires of the flesh are rejected and God's will is executed.

* Why the Storm Is Fierce If a test is too difficult or too easy, the discrimination to gauge ability falls short. Everyone can take it, but it is never easy, and the higher the level, the higher the difficulty. That is why the wicked cannot endure judgment and fall away, but the blessed and the righteous endure. The storm is fierce because not just anyone is permitted to walk that path, and that place is prepared only for me. 'But the wicked are not so, but are like chaff which the wind drives away. Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the assembly of the righteous.' —Psalm 1:4-5

* We Win Even in Darkness All the people in the Bible did not walk seeing their own success. Though it was dark, they ran to live the life the Lord desired. Those standing in that place are already victors and blessed people. 'Blessed is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked or stand in the way of sinners or sit in the seat of mockers. But his delight is in the law of the Lord, and on his law he meditates day and night. He is like a tree planted by streams of water, which yields its fruit in season and whose leaf does not wither. Whatever he does prospers.' —Psalm 1:1-3

* Not Zeal but Class There are two ways an elementary student solves problems. One is to work hard. Working hard, you might move up ten places on a midterm exam. The other is to solve it after becoming a college student. Revival does not come through relative zeal alone. Because that glory is too great, it comes when you live a completely different life, pray different prayers, and offer different worship, caring nothing for other things and pressing toward the goal. Abandon the insignificant and dwell in a different class. 'Brothers, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.' —Philippians 3:13-14

* Life Giving Birth to Life There are those like police officers, firefighters, and coast guard personnel who save human lives. Unlike those who just touch phones and computers in offices, sometimes they may die on duty. To save life, you must risk your life. When you see a certain believer, you feel respect and admiration without even seeing the minister. You know the teacher through the disciple.

* Faith to Leave What Is Familiar To leave what is familiar and what you depend on, and to be told to go without being shown where to go, means God Himself will guide you to your destination. 'Leave your country, your people and your father's household and go to the land I will show you.' —Genesis 12:1

We are shocked when famous people among us become people of the other world overnight. The trial did not kill those people. Most of them 'voluntarily' gave up in the face of trials and fear. Trials and fear have never voluntarily or actively caused people to be destroyed or die.
That we have collapsed in sin is necessarily proven by one fact: that I have lost sight of the Lord. As long as I keep my eyes on the Lord, even if the enemy comes at me, I will not collapse.

When we pass through a tunnel, the most necessary thing is humility. 'He humbled you, causing you to hunger and then feeding you with manna, which neither you nor your ancestors had known, to teach you that man does not live on bread alone.' —Deuteronomy 8:2
We abandon one day and one week not because the challenge is fierce but because it seems difficult. Even if you fall, get up and keep going. Never abandon looking to the Lord.

Everything we cannot do is not material for despair but 'a matter for prayer.' That is why we must earnestly pray for 'the power of prayer' to be given and guided so that we can 'pray' about the most important thing we cannot do in our own strength. Pray in order to pray.

Even when we face tribulation, we must unflinchingly continue to hold to the truth and advance, because no matter how dark the situation appears, God is the One who hits a home run in the bottom of the ninth with two outs through His great wisdom and power. 'Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest, if we do not give up.' —Galatians 6:9

* God Trying to Help God Trying to help God when you are frustrated with the situation (?) is a serious unbelief that greatly limits God's sovereignty and work. Abraham 'helping' God resulted in Ishmael being born through Hagar, the seed of conflict, and Peter drawing a sword in Gethsemane. (The Lord was probably quite flustered in that urgent moment searching for and reattaching the fallen ear in the middle of the night.)

We hear Satan's whisper every day telling us to prove ourselves. Even when Jesus came, Satan tempted Him to turn stones into bread and prove He is the Son of God. What will you do? If I had such ability, I would pick up a stone, pretend to pray, and then use it to crush Satan's head. The Apostle Paul wrote in his letter that he does not proclaim himself but proclaims the Lord. Then the Lord testifies to us before the angels as well.
We must pray again for the opportunity to give ourselves to the Lord. May we live serving faithfully and die as a sacrifice.

We pray unknowingly, but the Lord works knowingly. We pray inefficiently, but the Lord works with perfect efficiency. We simply raise and extend our hands, but the Lord covers with an enormous hand. We ultimately entrust, but the Lord enters into unimaginable micro-management of that prayer. Lord, take control. Intensely~~~

We are called to be light and salt in the world. But we are light and salt serving without fame and without name. Those who have plucked out their desires and nature have an anointing that cannot be borne.

Sometimes we cannot give a precise answer to why we are here. Despite not volunteering, we fight on the front lines. It is not our decision but the Lord's calling. If He called us, He has certainly given us the ability to bear it.

We cannot arbitrarily second-guess or reject God's method of shaping us. However, within those circumstances, we can choose our heart's attitude toward our God. Do not try to do everything; do what you can do. What more will you worry about?

We were not called to prove our own righteousness on this earth. We are called only to testify to His cross. The Pharisees tried to prove their own righteousness, were enslaved to human approval, and based their lives on it. But the Lord silently bore the cross and fulfilled the Father's will. Do not seek proof to gain recognition; humble yourself and testify. In your youth, it will not be easy to resist the temptation to prove yourself.

We are made by the Lord and are in His hands. Therefore, we do not do things by force. We believe that entrusting ourselves to Him is the most right and good path. The Lord always gives me the best. If He did not give it, it is because it should not be given. What we lack is more beneficial. The Lord boasted of Job's faith to Satan. Through this, Job's trials began. This is why people know that the theme of courage is suffering. The conclusion is that Job is transformed. A fairly good believer becomes a man of faith who sees the Lord himself. It seems like a wager between God and Satan that started the suffering. No, it is the Lord's providence that causes us to abandon the good and advance to the best. Therefore, the theme of Job is grace. Satan's dispute is transformed so that we become like refined gold. The ability to work all things together for good belongs to Him.

* Suffering is a means of differentiation. When an exam is too easy, everyone scores high and cannot be evaluated. The reason suffering is difficult is because it narrows the entrance, and not just anyone enters—it is a place reserved for me alone who received grace. If it is difficult, it is what was prepared for you.

We endure tribulation and overcome challenges. However, enduring and overcoming is not the purpose. Through it, we release God's reign and rule into our domain. You will be reviled and attacked. (Luke 6:26) But the suffering we now experience cannot be compared with the glory, dominion, and rule we will enjoy in the future. (Romans 8:18) To open the Lord's reign in healing, prophecy, love, restoration, politics, economics, and sports, we must fight and endure. We must not give up. After that, God will use us as a light to the nations. (Acts 13:47)

* Recovery vs. Escape: When problems come, some people escape. The easiest decision is the most evil decision. Rather than leaving your place, make the place where you stand full and a place of repentance. The problem is not solved by turning away from it but by facing it together with the Lord.
Most of our failures and mistakes in life can be forgiven. The only fatal and great mistake for a person is giving up—if we do not do that.

* The Power of Giving Everything: Why is there no power? Because we give only a part. Simply give your whole life. When told to give everything, everyone is afraid. They say I cannot travel and there is no fun. That is correct. The travels I want to take will also be unavailable and there will be no fun I seek. Instead, there will be journeys and joy with the Helper the Lord has prepared. Even if we go to the same Scotland, who we go with is more important.

Our spiritual ancestors could not deny the power of the blood of Christ, so they were hidden in caves, imprisoned, and had to give up their lives. Please proclaim that cross and blood again. Since human problems are problems of sin, only the blood can solve them. We have delivered that gospel as our life.

Do you know where crisis comes in our lives? From money, lust, despair, pride, or laziness? The greatest crisis is 'Jesus alone is not enough for me'—in other words, 'lack of faith.' If we are not satisfied with Jesus alone, whatever we do and whatever we have will only be lacking. When lacking, we become greedy; when greedy, we do evil. When God is our Shepherd, there is no lack, and even our cup overflows and pours out—Psalm 23.
Command your destiny to come. Those who have entrusted everything to the Lord have nothing to fear.
Cry out to your destiny: Destiny, come! I am not afraid, and I entrust all things to the Lord.

* How not to be shaken when suffering loss: Sometimes we earn less money and receive lower scores to obey the Word. We suffer loss but must not be shaken. It is easy if we know the conclusion of those who obeyed. Those who live by faith receive from heaven. Whatever we do, God's help is there.

* The Strategy of Faith: We can do many things, but not all are beneficial. If we strategically pour strength into what is most important, the rest will naturally balance out. That is fearing God. Yet without this strategic and meticulous approach, a deaconess in a rural church grasps the important thing all at once. It is truly amazing.

The reason we can walk forward despite the enemy's schemes, fierce challenges, darkness that seems to obscure everything, and suffocating pressure is that we rejected comfort and ease from the beginning. To believe in Jesus means we are also saved from other relative purposes, reputation, and success. If we can plant the cross there and pray for the Holy Spirit to come, then whether we live or die is not much of a concern. According to my eager expectation and hope, I will not be ashamed in anything, but with all boldness, as always, so now also Christ will be exalted in my body, whether by life or by death. For to me, living is Christ and dying is gain. (Philippians 1:20, 21)

Do not live as you wish; live by requesting what you need. Running away and abandoning everything is what we want; receiving God's power and authority in the place where I stand is what is necessary. I hope you become young people who overcome darkness with the sword of the Word.

If you say you desire something but do not ask for it, you do not truly desire it. If you say you love but do not pray, you do not truly love. A life that does not earnestly desire and cannot love fervently is disliked even in worldly enterprises.
The common trait of great leaders is character. Image and reputation are the 'self' others see, while character is the 'self' itself. If you do not give trust, you cannot lead others.

A great life lies in excellent choices. A good choice is not based on your circumstances but responding with faith to a great proposal. Joshua and Caleb passed that difficult test against odds of 1 in a million.

* The Greatest Recovery: God's Word must flow. It must overflow and become flowing Word. When God's Word flows through my life, I am sufficiently happy without filling anything else. Peaceful relationships and the fulfillment of mission happen naturally. The person who fulfills the Lord's will experiences the greatest recovery. It is not that I accomplish the mission; the mission accomplishes me.
* The Greatest Mistake: The greatest mistake is doing nothing to avoid mistakes. If you avoid the challenge, you will truly regret it bitterly.

Answers require time and patience. It is not that God is limited by time; rather, we need more time to empty ourselves further, cut away more, and burn away more in order to become a vessel worthy of receiving the Lord's answer. That time is the Lord's 'faithful care.' If you abandon attachment, you will receive the answer.
When we understand the meaning, we can endure it. We can even rejoice. Meaning gives us the power to love. We bear all things and endure all things.

A doctor tells a patient how serious the situation is before treating. Not because there is no cure, but because he wants the patient not to return to harmful habits and to be managed long-term. God makes us experience the bottom before solving. Solving is not the purpose; transformation is the purpose.
The righteous do not live by emotion. The righteous live only by faith.
May you become young people of power who awaken souls caught in spiritual powerlessness—those seeking devotion without reliance, crowns without suffering, compromising with reality. (Winter 1995)
In this final generation, the person who does not waste time, is not deceived by schemes, and truly accomplishes the Lord's work is one who discerns what the Lord's will is. (Ephesians 5:17)
I bless and pray for everyone laboring, striving, and running the race of faith at this moment. Our good God will give the very best. Let us give thanks.
Stop theoretical talk and live out the Word. (February 26, 2015)

No one can force you to abandon your dream because it is difficult to achieve. Do not fear an unwinnable battle or worry about unbearable pain. Even if you cannot achieve it, keep dreaming. Miracles happen in places no one has looked.

I am delivering a special lecture titled 'The Cross and Life' at this retreat. As I prepare this, this thought comes to me: Stand there together with me. I have decided not to minister alone. Because the Lord told us to stand together. For that ministry, I ask that you pray together and stand together. I will proclaim from that place, but before the Lord, I will stand together with our young people. Please pray. Wednesday at 9 a.m.

The Israelites experienced the miracle of the exodus, drank water from the rock, were baptized in the Red Sea, and ate supernatural food and manna, but they perished. Experience is not everything. Do not complain. Some among them who complained were destroyed by the destroyer. Do not complain like them. (1 Corinthians 10)
Now may you become someone in whom the Lord can trust and entrust authority. When you gain authority, you must be someone who understands where that authority comes from.

Economy is better than economy class, and business is better than economy, but first class is better than business. However, if the airplane is crashing, before the issue of class, fundamentally you must board an airplane that does not crash. Jesus never crashes.
I understand, but how can you live that way? If you have received love that does not make sense, how can you live any other life?
When placed in unfamiliar circumstances, you experience God's work more concretely.

Human will is greed. The Lord's will is mission. If you live as your heart desires, you will pursue what you were not given; if you live as the Spirit desires, you will live grateful for what you were given.

I saw a humanitarian chef rest by going to neglected and marginalized people on his day off and cooking for them personally, enjoying great joy and rest. Stepping out from people who evaluate and measure you, becoming a source of joy to those who truly need you is also a good kind of rest. What is truly difficult is doing nothing meaninglessly. Rest is not doing nothing; it is being filled with grace and meaning.

There are days in life when things do not go my way. On such days, just say, 'Today is a day when things are not going my way.' We do not live by emotion but by faith. David also experienced moments when his heart was discouraged, but he did not allow himself to live that way. Do not hide; reveal and confess. That is normal.

It is not difficult to realize that in life, even with effort, things do not go my way. From the beginning, unexpected lives and encounters await me, and I feel myself drifting away from the line of life I drew. Behind this is the Lord's guidance. Many people in the Bible did not live the lives they wanted or could anticipate; they lived according to the will the Lord wanted to accomplish.
Life is not about building up but about scattering and giving. To give is to open a way. Do not defend; love. Along that way, the life of Jesus will flow.
Life is a battle of priorities. The subject of love and affection becomes the cause of success or failure. What I do first tells of my future.

I compare life to a marathon. I think it is an appropriate comparison, with the premise that not everyone runs the same course. Live in your own course with your own strengths. Do not compare anymore.

If you think of life in terms of speed, you become unhappy. But if you think in terms of direction, it is interpreted. If the path I am going is the path of truth, no matter how many people rush to pursue the path of greed that others seek, I will not be shaken. The meaning of life comes not from speed but from direction.

The reason life's storms grow increasingly severe is that people do not turn back to God. The storm does not stop until Jonah repents. Even if he makes the decision to be thrown into the sea, a fish is prepared in the sea for him. God does not negotiate.
When passing through life's storms, the most important thing is to cast your anchor on Jesus.

At life's halftime, Abraham leaves his stable life and sets out on a journey, not to the 'land shown' but to the 'land to be shown.' The path of vision is certainly not easy. But sadder than that is a life lived in anxious present circumstances for fear of failure, attempting nothing.

Life does not become wonderful in an instant. Just as there is a time and season for all things, wrinkles and gray hair come, and we become wise. Rather than putting my hand to it, it is true wisdom and discernment to yield the place and entrust it to the Lord.
People say that is just how life is. Be clear about this: Life is never like that. God does not leave those with eyes of faith to live only the routine path others take. Rise up high.

When life becomes twisted contrary to our wishes, there is the Lord's wonderful providence within it. Augustine desired to be a rhetorician, Paul aspired to be a famous rabbi, and Joseph longed for the life of a Near Eastern prince. When their lives became twisted, they were startled, but they came to realize that a life touched by God's hand exists within a glorious and wonderful plan. Even when you fail exams, your business fails, and you lose your dwelling place, the Lord will be with you as you confess His faithfulness. Even in circumstances you don't understand, the Lord remains faithful. ㅠㅠ
Life is difficult because we do not shed our blood. Rather, when we willingly shed our blood for Jesus, we experience the greatest happiness and glory.

On the internet, elementary and middle school students spread things everywhere. Those who know better refrain because of defamation laws and related regulations. The Gospel seems similar. Those who insist on being perfect before moving forward accomplish nothing their entire lives. But when we return to our original heart, we simply proclaim and simply come back.
Even if I live in loneliness all my life, if I can live for the Lord, that is an incomparable treasure and a great blessing to me.

In ordinary circumstances, faith is not apparent. In extraordinary situations, in moments of crisis, true faith is revealed when we rely on the Lord and gain strength from Him. When the people's hearts were grieved because of their children and they wanted to stone David, David was greatly distressed, but he strengthened himself in the Lord his God and took courage. -1 Samuel 30:6
To live on your knees while working and studying, your lifestyle must be incredibly simple.
To live on your knees while working and studying, your lifestyle must be incredibly simple. Jesus was a layperson who went around with fishermen and tax collectors, and He prayed according to habit.
Now, Apostolic Beginnings Lesson 8... Perhaps this will be the last Bible study at LG. I pray that the Lord's will may be accomplished. I also request your prayers.

For a person to acknowledge himself is foolish. You must become someone whose faith the Lord acknowledges. That person is one after God's own heart. The Lord accomplishes His will through such people.

Among animals and insects that defend themselves, those with protective coloring change their color according to seasons and environment to protect themselves. However, most do not survive more than a year. But turtles, cranes, and storks that maintain their own color whether it rains or snows live long without changing their garments. If you have faith to discard your protective coloring before God and your brothers and reveal yourself, the problems will begin to melt away. Therefore confess your sins to one another and pray for one another that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective. -James 5:16

Rather than trying to change your position, strive to change your posture. If your posture aligns with the Lord's heart, your position will change by itself. Worship the Lord in the place where He called you, just as you are, and the Lord will see it.
More important than position is posture. Where there is persistence and authenticity, not only do people recognize it, but God also takes notice.
Unless you abandon yourself and your desires, you cannot escape the wilderness.

If you have not lived for yourself but sought only the Lord completely, you can bear criticism. Rather than committing sin and receiving criticism, if you have done wholeheartedly what you ought to do, there is no regret regardless of the results and reactions.
Dedicating and consecrating yourself does not happen through great resolve, but rather becomes a natural footprint of life that follows from simply loving the Lord and remaining in that place.

If you cannot deny yourself, you will deny the Lord. If you hold onto the Lord, you can abandon and deny yourself. We cannot serve two masters, nor is it possible to straddle two sides. Because that gap is absolutely not shared.

A person who relies on himself appears self-confident and assured. Such people are quick to walk the tightrope. Of course they walked the rope because they were confident. But the fall and shame will be severe. The claim that you can obtain both holiness and satisfaction of the flesh is a lie. There is no such thing. Stop walking the tightrope and simply give up and walk the narrow path. Prodigals who have come back halfheartedly tend to walk on that fence like a tightrope walker, while disciples walk the narrow path.
Do not prove yourself, but testify to the Lord. Then He will show you how to act.

When you try to accomplish your own will, you must engage in much struggle and leave many wounds. When you try to accomplish the Lord's will, you must truly become humble and entrust yourself completely.

If you work with your own mind and popularity, you can do things quickly, but you can only move a few people. However, when you work from the heart, you gain everyone's hearts and further reveal God's work.

Do not be disappointed that your expectations and desires are not fulfilled, but rejoice that the Lord's sovereignty is acknowledged. I am not the master; the Lord is the Master and He is responsible.

When you confess that you cannot continue down that path because of your weakness and insufficiency, rather the Lord says in His love, 'Feed My sheep.' It is not your willpower that upholds you, nor your zeal, nor your determination, but only that I will uphold you. -1 Corinthians 15:58 'Become those who labor more abundantly in the work of the Lord... knowing that your labor is not in vain in the Lord.'
Guard your place and do not leave it; be steadfast. The Lord is almighty.

Not guarding your place is sitting in the seat of the arrogant. David says in Psalm 1 that they are completely without blessing. When you do not keep the place the Lord gave you, shame and defilement come upon you. I hope you will maintain your place.

When you feel you are of no significance, rejoice. Now is the time for the Lord to work. Having no goodness or righteousness is the beginning of the Gospel, and that Gospel must still be grasped today.
If you have sought purely the Lord's glory, act boldly. No one can destroy you. Those who look only to the Lord are the ones who truly prevail.
Falling frequently is proof that you are relying on your own strength. If you had relied on the Lord, you would not fall. This is because the Jesus in whom I believe and trust never falls.

Jonah, claiming to fear God, hesitated here and there, and after severe torture, passed through the three-day journey to Nineveh in just one day, speaking the Word in a murmur, yet the people repented. Open your mouth and proclaim the Word whether it is opportune or not. Then the Lord will take care of it.
Pressing forward without hesitation is faith.

There will be small challenges, but there will also be great trials. However, the principle of overcoming them remains constant. Acting by faith. Through Jesus, shame and suffering become inconsequential.
If you are not faithful in small things, the tasks of others, and unrighteous wealth, God cannot use you with His power.

If you are affected by small matters, then you are deliberately ignoring what is truly important. Do not merely imitate; I hope you gain genuine competence. That comes from repeated presence born of repentance.
You should not become too exhausted trying to achieve perfection in small tasks. However small the task, it is the Lord who must do it.
Do not be cunning and regretful, but ask in faith and be bold. Whether it is cunning or faith can be discerned by the degree of 'hastiness.'
Those who listen well can proclaim well. Listening and humility must come before standing up and proclaiming.

Did you rest well? After training retreats where I slept only two hours, I finally rested yesterday with my normal sleep pattern. Now, let's run toward better things, higher things. Here we go ~, One way, Jesus + a pitcher of living water ^^// The young adult ministry continues.
Living well does not mean gaining money and fame, but living according to the Lord's planned will.

If you try to do well, you become tired. Relationships, ministry, work—everything becomes stress. God did not call you to do everything well and satisfy everyone, but said that doing one or two things, or even just one thing, is sufficient. If you do wholeheartedly that one thing the Lord called you to without greed, that is truly doing well. Trying to go too fast and trying to obtain everything is greed. Miracles happen rather when you abandon greed.

A sleepless rabbit never loses to the tortoise in a race. An awakened, running rabbit (the insomniac rabbit) is undefeated. Today I'm making up for what I couldn't do last week at full speed. (fire breathing!!)

Noisy sounds are not heard. When you are too busy to hear the Lord's voice and your attention is elsewhere, He calls you to a loud place. That place is the wilderness. No water, no moisture, no one. There you finally hear the Word, see it, and train to live not by what seems advantageous but by hearing and faith. For we live by faith, not by sight. 2 Corinthians 5:7 'We live not by sight but by faith.'

Brother Jang Byung-hwa's father's funeral service is Tuesday at 5:30 AM departure. Please comment if you are able to attend. The late Elder Jang Yoon-sang, September 16 at 11:00 AM, Durham, St. Joseph's Church
It is said that when you sow with tears, you reap with joy. But when you sow with beautiful people, tears become pearls and labor becomes joy. When you are together, fear disappears.

If you went to do business and returned without leaving anything behind, you have truly done business well. When you walk the path of faith, proclaim only Jesus and leave nothing behind, you have truly walked the right path.

Even with obstacles and storms, your heart toward the Lord cannot stop. We run toward the Lord, toward His Word. As time passes, we will run even more, and at the end of this path we will see the Lord. God will never turn away 'the earnest seeker.' -From Apostolic Beginnings Lesson 6, 'The Cry That Received an Answer'

You should not despair at obstacles, but ask the Lord how you can be faithful in the place where you are and prostrate yourself. You must pray, receive direction, and obey. When you faithfully and sincerely handle even such inadequate work, God's will is accomplished in God's time, by God's method, and in God's way.
More important than having talent, ability, or academic credentials is faith. Look at Jabez. He had nothing, but he believed in the Lord's help.
Wasting talent and money is foolish. Among these, wasting time is the most foolish thing. Because time is life. When time decreases, life decreases.

Where treasure is, there your heart is also. The Lord sees my motivation, so He takes great interest in money matters. Earning money is a technique; using that money is an art. The Lord wants me to be an artist who understands beauty.
When my resolve and plans fail and I become discouraged, only then does the Lord work. When I am weak, I become complete; I am also made strong.
Evening Prayer 7:30 PM @Mission House. For my sake, for the sake of my lips... Lord, be exalted. We will pray simple prayers. Simple prayer means continuing to pray sufficiently and abundantly.
