People keep asking if there is an evening prayer meeting. If you know, why do you keep asking? Even if a nuclear war breaks out tomorrow or a tsunami occurs, we will continue on steadily.
When you pray for two and a half hours in the evening and return home, you will feel there is no time to waste in life. Now expect the anointing upon your studies and future path.

I am a minister. I belong to those who work diligently. However, our young people will find it difficult without me, but they will not perish. Because God is with them. For the righteous live by 'faith' alone.

I do not fail to understand that our friends live in an age where maintaining faith is extremely difficult. There is weakness of the flesh, sinful nature, and the devil's work. But we can overcome. Why? Because we must overcome. Therefore we will overcome, and I will make you overcome.
I am confident that the Lord will give much grace in this training retreat. Because I see the members serving with a heart ready to become martyrs, humbling themselves as they serve.
My heart overflows. Because the gospel of the cross is glorious. Because it is the power of God that gives salvation to all believers, I do not hesitate to proclaim that gospel.
We prepare our food with craftsmanship. With a missionary spirit....
Even if the first half did not please you, do not be discouraged. In the second half, practice self-control.

Do not take off the full armor of God. The enemy gives you good things and satisfaction to strip away your best. Do not abandon the only way to fight an invisible opponent, one you cannot touch with flesh and blood.
Put on the full armor of God. The angels who deliver prayer and my fellow workers will do the same. The answer to prayer will come swiftly.

When you trust the Lord with all your heart, the Lord works. Seeking the Lord with all your heart does not require any qualifications, achievements, or reputation. It is because we are lacking, because the basics are not in place that we seek. When I deeply feel my insufficiency, the work of the Lord begins.

Become a believer who is faithful in all seasons. Regardless of spiritual adversity, looking only to the Lord, unaffected by worldly glory, relationships, responses, circumstances and environments—a faith that ignores and presses on, a person who loves even in darkness with the love of Christ in difficult positions where the devil brings depression and discourages—you who love the Lord are the solution of this age. Acts 13:2 While they were serving the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, 'Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.'

A warship sometimes returns to harbor for mooring. It receives supplies and repairs. Why? To go back out and fight again. If you rest in the harbor and rest at sea, then it is not a warship but a cruise ship. The Apostle Paul says there is a 'good fight' throughout his entire life. We are called to be fighters. If you believe, you will fight, and if you fight with faith, you will surely win.

Proclaim and pray to be a witness daily. From hymn 505, today at the company I have the third lesson of invitation to happiness, Luke 5:1-11 more precious than a full catch. Please pray for me to live a life of proclaiming, praying, and witnessing.
In youth without regret, only for the Lord~~
In youth I believed there was something that could not be lost. But any person and everything we rely on besides God can be lost. That is why we must rely on the Lord alone and be humble.

Even when a young person's heart falls, we pray; even when it seems tomorrow's sun will not rise, we run forward. Living not by circumstances but by Jesus is what Hebrews calls a person of faith that the world cannot contain.

It is wisdom to discard what will decay in order to grasp what is truly precious. Faith is looking only to the Lord and holding fast to the path given to me regardless of response and circumstances. It is loving Jesus. Then you will be free from all trials.
Will you truly serve, love, and worship only Him?

Do you truly believe in Jesus? Then the true meaning of crisis has already been overcome. It is not tomorrow but the Lord's work. As a steward I will work and labor faithfully, but all responsibility and resolution belong to the Lord who is the Owner.

If you truly want to have good faith, you must abandon expectations. Cast away any hope of being recognized and remembered, and run forward for the Lord. This is the way of absolute faith and infinite loyalty.

When it is truly so difficult that you could die, when just moments before you could barely breathe and were nearly at death's door, being able to cry out 'One more time~' is because you believe in the Lord and live for the Lord. Everything resets before the Lord.

I hope there is a decisive faith to skip what is less important in order to speak what is truly important. Jesus had much to rebuke the Jews about, but He spoke what was more important. I have much to say and judge about you, but He who sent me is true, and I speak to the world what I have heard from Him. (John 8:26)

True faith is truly looking to the Lord in the storm. That witnessing the cross experiences fierce resistance from darkness is because the gospel of the cross is the most precious and valuable work. I pray that you become servants of life, your hearts unchanged and clinging only to the cross.

To truly not bring shame to God's name, the community needs much change, and this means there must be greater presence of the Lord. We must seek the Lord's face more. This is especially required of those in ministry.
Do you truly desire to speak and do God's Word? Then change your time. He whom God sent speaks God's words, for God gives the Spirit without measure. John 3:34
Living honestly will usually cost you dearly and cause loss in ordinary times. But in moments of crisis, in moments of absolute necessity, God always upholds the hand of the honest.
Do not extinguish the fire on the altar. - Leviticus 6:12,13
The most valuable life is one that runs for the Lord and through holy consumption leaves not a drop. To be used until nothing remains is truly a glorious life.
Jesus who washed the disciples' feet commanded us to likewise wash the feet of brothers and sisters. Not because their feet are dirty, but it means to wash away their sins and transgressions.

That the disciples abandoned and fled from Jesus was not because they were 'evil' but because they were 'weak.' Not being filled with the Holy Spirit, departing from the place of prayer and the place of grace, is already an extremely wicked sin. After Pentecost, Peter became bold not because circumstances improved but because he was filled.

If you confuse conditions with results, you get no answer. Resolution, fruit, phenomena, and victory are results, not conditions. The condition and cause is Jesus. There must be a cause for there to be a result. A result without a cause is hypocrisy.

What we commonly call success in the Joguk Church is often expressed in numbers. But many souls gathered from different churches do not receive Christ, and even nominal existing church members do not thoroughly submit to God or dedicate themselves wholeheartedly. We should grieve over such success. And we deserve to be wounded.

Due to impatience and anxiety, I had to grab my brother's heel and deceive my father to steal the blessing, and there was only suffering in that life. When I let go of the attempt to make myself happy and the attempt to satisfy and protect myself, true obedience is achieved. A person obedient to God is the most beautiful person.
Not to be anxious is faith. When you resolve 'if I die, I die; if I lose, I lose,' all conditions disappear and the Lord becomes your only support. That is faith.
Do not seek to be respected but to be trusted. Yet even this is not done by my strength. It is only achieved when I look to the Lord and love souls.
A person of faith does not live remarkably but has a clear purpose.
To speak more precisely, Peter doubted but did not sink. Because Jesus caught him as he was sinking. We are insufficient, but when we come to Jesus, the Lord holds us.
Ultimately, life is a fight at the final station. Either live absorbed in the world and go where the world goes, or become obsessed with Jesus and live with Him. We run toward the Lord.

Anyone can do what they like. But a professional pursues their work with passion until they like what they do. Therefore, they are not so concerned about obstacles and challenges that block the path of faith, nor do they mind them. They ignore and run. Ignoring and running is power.
You want to sit a person you like next to you. But a person you love, you want to give them your seat.

It is very difficult to distinguish between what is good and what appears good. But there is a fundamental difference—whether you pay the price. Good-looking faith and good faith can be known through paying the price.

There is no clear way to gain good fellow workers. If I must mention it, it is the boundless and complete grace that the Lord pours out even though there is no goodness in me. Next is becoming a good fellow worker myself. Fellow workers are given by the Lord, but remaining a good fellow worker is my responsibility.
Good choice brings victory in battle. When you choose the cross and nail the flesh, we win righteously in this battle.
People who made a good start fall for a simple reason. Because they are satisfied and compromise. It is like applying the whip to a running horse.
Do not do the ministry and love you want to do, but do it in the way God desires. God's foolishness is wiser than people, and God's weakness is stronger than people. 1 Corinthians 1:25

When a storm comes, the eagle becomes bolder. Because it has the vision and wings to fly higher. Therefore it does not respond to chicks. So when I see a person who responds, I know who I am. A chick responds to chicks.

The secret to overcoming sin and conquering this evil world is to fear the Lord. When you look to the Lord with all your heart always and rely on His blood, your life transforms from one of stumbling into one of praise and testimony.

The only way to overcome sin is to surrender yourself to the Lord. Then you become the Lord's servant and the Lord becomes your Lord, and you live under His grace. Victory in the struggle over who is master means victory in all things. Do you not know that if you present yourselves to anyone as obedient slaves, you are slaves of the one whom you obey, either of sin, which leads to death, or of obedience, which leads to righteousness? - Romans 6:16

Before lamenting that you have fallen into sin, you must repent of not looking entirely to the Lord. Our struggle is not against flesh and blood but against the spiritual forces. Therefore, faith is a battle of perspective.

Trying to overcome sin and temptation is futile. You can never win. It is like keeping garbage and spraying perfume on it. The garbage must be thrown away, and the flesh must be crucified daily on the cross.
If you compromise with sin, even after ten years you will be enslaved to it, but if you begin to fight against sin, there will come a day when you master it.
We can be bold even in the face of sin's challenge when we know and believe that the Lord holds us. The Lord never lets us go. The cross overcomes sin.

Believing what you want to believe is not faith. That is merely superstition. Faith means believing in God's absolute sovereignty and providence and obeying in the present day. When you trust in the Lord's omnipotence, sorrow will be transformed into joy. Joseph named his firstborn Manasseh, saying, 'God has made me forget all my trouble and all my father's household.' And he named the second son Ephraim, saying, 'It is because God has made me fruitful in the land of my suffering.' - Genesis 41:51-52

The Lord called me to do His work. The Lord's work is to believe and to become holy like Him. That the apostles devoted themselves to prayer and the Word means that becoming strong in the essence is the true solution.
The Lord is at my right hand.

In my youth, even when my heart bore deep wounds, the Lord made me able to run. The Lord wipes my tears again and enables me to run for Him again. Because He has called, I go forward. The Lord alone is sufficient. My longing is to love the Lord more.

If you have done what the Lord commanded, you have done well. In the course of obeying, you may make mistakes and lack ability to be perfect in all things, but standing where the Lord says to stand is the beginning of bearing the cross.
Walking the Lord's way does not mean expecting others to follow or support me, but that I follow the Lord. Though that path is narrow and lonely, I walk it with all my life.

The prayer 'Lord, mold me' gives a brief season of sweet romance and then crushes all my stubbornness and passes me through a fiery furnace. If you ask to be molded, then you will be molded. It is the fastest way. For I know the plans I have for you, declares the Lord, plans for welfare and not for evil, to give you a future and a hope. Then you will call upon me and come and pray to me, and I will hear you. When you search for me with all your heart, you will find me. - Jeremiah 29:11, 13
I sow the seed at the Lord's word, but it is God who makes it grow.
Entrust yourself to the Lord's hands. The Lord's promise is eternal. Because He is my reason, I worship in every circumstance.

In the Lord, sad things do not end sadly and fearful things do not end fearfully. In life, people become proud when favored and despair when unfavored, but the faithful trust only in the Lord who knows and vindicates them in every situation. Therefore, we say with boldness, 'The Lord is my helper; I will not fear; what can man do to me?' - Hebrews 13:6
The person who kneels longest before the Lord stands most firmly.
Standing before the Lord and looking only to Him is what I have learned thus far and the path I must walk going forward.
Become a person who kneels long before the Lord. You will stand most firmly.
I hope you are loyal before the Lord and gentle before the saints. God seeks such people, observes them as if writing in a record, and pours out His power upon them.

When I stand before the Lord in endless insufficiency, the Lord gives me mission and new strength. Do not become a person who hides your true self. Whether turned to ash or to rice, when you stand before the Lord in truthfulness, even if it is confessed as a sigh from your lips, believe that the Lord will accomplish it and move forward.

When I am faithful in the small work the Lord gives me, the Lord does great work on His part. Two loaves and five fish are indeed insignificant, but when held in the Lord's hands, they feed five thousand and give life. Sow in good soil with faith.

There was a time when I could do great and small things as I pleased without hearing the Lord's voice. But now is the time to hear that voice, obey it, and move forward. God delights in a heart that seeks and listens, and through His good pleasure, He leads in wonderful ways. So when a faithful person buys an airplane ticket, even unbelievers want to board the same plane.
How do you know if it is a vision from the Lord? Dreams not given by the Lord rot away. Even if you incubate a Tesco egg, it rots, but if you incubate a fertile egg, it produces life.
Being satisfied with the Lord alone is a confession paid for with great cost. It is never cosmetics hiding my selfishness.

Lord, I do not fear as I run toward You. And I am happy because I will live for You and die for You. Looking to You with all my heart, sin does not overcome me. That I can run for You is a glory I cannot bear.
Lord, I have no intention of stopping until I have run my full course. Make me run more. Here I am.

Lord, whether I live or die is not my concern. Because loving and serving You is my eternal purpose, grant me Your sufficient grace and the grace to accomplish that purpose. If my life is long, I will serve and praise You all my days, and if my life is short, I will joyfully approach Your throne and worship. According to my eager expectation and hope, I will not be ashamed of anything, but as always I will be fully bold, so that whether I live or die, Christ will be exalted in my body. - Philippians 1:20
To walk with the Lord, two things must be premises: 1. One is faith and 2. The other is holiness. - Joshua 1:8
When you walk with the Lord, two representative powers appear: 1. You bear much fruit. 2. In the Lord, you can do all things. - John 15:5
Drawing near to the Lord is power. For this, something must be sacrificed. The most critical challenge now is media. Exercise restraint. It is the price to be paid.

Prayer to the Lord means simultaneously believing that He surely hears and answers and being grateful even if He does not. Know that He will surely deliver you from the hand of the king, and even if He does not, you will not bow to their god.

There is time that must be given to the Lord. There is passion that must be given to the Lord. When absolute time and focus are given, that life becomes extraordinary. The prime of life comes. I urge you to run over 300 kilometers, over 1800 meters. Revival begins with me.
Entrust the steering wheel of your life to the Lord. It is never too late now. God is the only one who can bring the best results from the worst.
What the Lord desired of Peter in Galilee was not a detailed account or plausible excuses. It was the confession of a voice hesitant and faltering with lack of confidence: 'I love You, Lord.'

Simply walk by faith in the way the Lord says is right. Even if it is like striking a rock with an egg, do it by faith. Your life should not be one that gambles on probabilities but one that lives by the Word.

There are times when the Lord makes you pour water into a bottomless container. Remember this: it is not the end. Even though pouring water seems to accomplish nothing, during that time muscles develop and your constitution changes. In the Lord's time, explosive revival, recovery, and life's abilities appear.

To say the Lord is faithful does not mean it is based on my circumstances and qualifications. He is faithful because He is faithful, and prayer is relying on that eternally unchanging character and moving forward. Success without Jesus is not success. Therefore, failure within Jesus is not failure. You in Jesus can never fail.

Because this is a life entrusted to you for a time, you must live meaningfully. With humility and passion... Jesus gave Himself for us. He died for us so that He might redeem us from all wickedness and purify us as His people eager to do good works. - Titus 2:14
For the Lord's perfect promise to appear not only in our afterlife but in our present reality, we need faith and absolute obedience to His Word.

A person who fears only the Lord has nothing to boast about or display. Having nothing to boast about or display, they hold no regrets. Without regrets, they do not cling to position. Not clinging, they do not irresponsibly abandon responsibility either.

For the Lord, for the Lord's glory, I run forward. Even if everything crumbles, if it is in the eyes of youth, that is the blessing of the young and there is hope for this nation and church. Let the Lord alone receive honor, glory, and all authority.
I live for the Lord, work for the Lord, labor for the Lord, and will die for the Lord.
I live for the Lord, work for the Lord, labor for the Lord, and will die for the Lord~
Risk your life for the Lord. Shallow sin and temptation cannot match you. Those who say they will die for the Lord cannot be moved by this world's petty pleasures. To love Jesus is to die for Jesus.

The Lord does not solve everything in one go according to my method. He gives gradual recovery through grace that helps in due time. The fact that He does not work according to my method and schedule does not mean the Lord is not working. This is why I am still grateful. Do not be afraid of them, for the Lord your God, who is great and awesome, is among you. The Lord your God will drive out these nations before you, little by little. You will not be allowed to eliminate them all at once, or the wild animals will multiply around you. - Deuteronomy 7:21-22

The Lord never commanded you to read the Bible or pray daily. Of course, practicing devotion daily is undoubtedly very beneficial. He only said to take up your cross daily. What is the evidence of taking up your cross daily? It is that there is a change of life in those around you. For Paul, what he did daily was the life of surrendering himself on the cross. Luke 9:23: And He said to them all, 'If anyone would come after Me, let him deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow Me.' 1 Corinthians 15:31: 'I protest, brothers, by my pride in you, which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die every day.'

The Lord is omnipresent. That means He is with me now and works as much as the place I have yielded in my heart. Joseph, who lived conscious of the Lord, overcame the temptation of sin, was not discouraged, and did not seek revenge.
Wait upon the Lord. And delight yourself in Him. He has promised that He will grant you the desires of your heart.
What happens when you do not wait upon the Lord and do not believe in His work? Answer) You can never hear. You will become parched.

Become a person who follows the Lord. Follow even when afraid, follow even when it is difficult. Even when opinions differ, when the Lord goes, you let go completely without hesitation and follow. This is what it means to be a Christian. The Lord works.

Following the Lord, and especially engaging in ministry, means recognizing loss beforehand. That is why spiritual loss, material expenditure, and the pain of broken relationships are not viewed as fear but as a necessary payment to be made. Those who have decided to follow the Lord do not regard such things as dreadful suffering. Why? Because they have seen the glory of the Lord. Personally, poverty and loneliness are difficult, but not frightening.

If there is no error in your speech, you are truly a complete person. A person without error in speech is at peace. Why? Because they are a grateful person. Gratitude and peace are the same thing. If you have lips of gratitude, you truly become a perfected person. Since we all make many mistakes, if there be one who makes no mistake in speech, he is a perfect man, able also to bridle the whole body. James 3:2
To abandon something in order to hold onto the Lord is the greatest investment.

If you love the Lord completely and truly acted in that love, you will have nothing more to fear. You have done what God commanded, regardless of the outcome. What the Lord desires is not success but obedience.

Those who do not fully trust in the Lord but cling to their own abilities are all oppressed people. Whether they know it or not, they are not free within spiritual oppression, have no peace, and suffer from illness. Let us hold fast to the truth at the cost of our lives, so that darkness will retreat and light will shine.

Depending on the Lord and rising up once more—this is true strength. Never give up! When the people spoke of stoning David because their hearts grieved over their children, David was greatly distressed; but he strengthened himself in the Lord his God. 1 Samuel 30:6
Depending on the Lord and with all my heart, I have preached the gospel. When the opportunity came, I also watched and shared the Bridge video. I entrust this to the Lord.

A person who truly trusts in and prays to the Lord is already blessed in the likeness of the Lord, satisfied, and has received the answer. And I—in righteousness I will see your face; when I awake, I will be satisfied with seeing your likeness. Psalm 17:15

The Lord's call in these days is not to please people or attract popularity. The Lord's call in these days is solely to be conformed to Christ and become holy as He is holy. (Romans 13:14) Rather, clothe yourselves with the Lord Jesus Christ, and do not think about how to gratify the desires of the flesh.

Determining the rights and wrongs of problems is truly foolish. Why? Because what matters more than the surface cause of the problem is my response to it—my faith. The Lord desires to see my faith when the storm arises. Where is your faith? Mark 4:40

This is a time to pray so that the Lord's will is perfectly accomplished in my life. Beginning to pray does not mean starting from tomorrow's dawn prayer, next week's evening prayer, or next month's all-night prayer. If it does, you will never pray in your lifetime. Prayer means praying right now, at this very moment when you are reading these words, right where you are. Only then can you hold onto prayer and accomplish the Lord's will.
Those who hold onto the Lord's mission are freed from the temptation of sin and can guard themselves.

Looking only to the Lord's glory and abandoning selfish desires does not happen automatically. The time of purification, like the removal of self-ambition in Joseph's dreams, is different for each person. Moses spent 40 years as a shepherd, Paul spent 12 years in the wilderness, and Jacob spent almost a lifetime. I pray that you become free from the effort of proving yourself. Jesus could have turned stones into bread and proven Himself, but He relied completely only on the Father's word and waited for His evaluation alone.

Seeking the Lord's glory means having unwavering faith. Even though you have sought the Lord's glory, impossible situations and things you truly wish to avoid may come upon you. Yet without further modification, continuously seeking only the Lord's glory, being able to rejoice and say 'Glory to the Lord' even if you die there—this is faith.

Those who have recognized the Lord's grace, abandoned the world, and returned to Him have one thing they truly regret. They regret returning too late and wasting their years when they were going to return anyway.
Obey the Lord's guidance with courage. You will not die anyway. Rather, you will experience the Lord's work.

When doing the Lord's work, the most important thing is not financial support, gifts, or organization. It is to guard your heart. When you guard your heart, you do not lose heart and persevere, and when you continuously persevere without ceasing, God's glory is revealed. Let us not grow weary while doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up. Galatians 6:9
If my rights are surrendered at the Lord's altar, God will grant me incomparably greater privileges.
I have heard your prayer and seen your tears. 2 Kings 20:5 God hears and sees. It means that when He hears and sees, He accomplishes all things.

There are two ways to receive the Lord's blessing and transformation. It is best to understand through the Word. But even if you do not, do not worry too much. There is the wilderness. If you are thoroughly decomposed in the wilderness for about ten years, you will understand well. Of course, I recommend the former.

There are two ways to respond in faith. One is obedience. It does not happen by birth; even Jesus learned obedience through suffering. The second is trust. Trust without obedience is fanaticism, and obedience without trust is blind faith.
Without the Lord's permission, no one can harm me. But when I react and respond to it myself, my own wickedness will harm me. Persecution rather makes me a precious believer.
The reason the Lord delays the conclusion is to truly give Himself.
When I make a decision that the Lord should make, that is suffering. After being refined, I become faithful unto death in the small work given to me. The Lord is watching.

When the Lord raised Lazarus, when He raised the widow's son in Nain, He not only restored life but also healed the diseases they had suffered from during their lifetime. (There is no such saying as 'Lazarus was raised, but his chronic illness remained.') God's healing, God's restoration, God's life are complete and transcend the conditions of anxiety and worry—His faithfulness. I urge you to trust in the Lord. For I know the plans I have for you, plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future. Jeremiah 29:11

The Lord has sent you.—Mission. Even if it is a narrow path that many have turned away from, He sent you saying this path is the true path, this path is meaningful. Even if you receive no honor your entire life, even if there is no one to prepare warm meals for you, it is the path that Apostle Paul walked. It is the path He called you to in Damascus when He showed you God's glory. It is the path of pouring yourself out like a drink offering before the altar without regret. It is the path that joyfully went even though there was loss and suffering in conforming to God's will. I pray that you walk the path that will be remembered as one who truly loved soul and God, a path worthy to be inscribed on your tombstone.

The Lord calls you.—Calling. The Lord calls you. He calls you to love, to serve, to humbly lower yourself. Even when all things scatter in the distant future and the name and memory you leave vanish, He called you to love toward the Lord with tears eternally. Even today, as you sow seeds and anxiously wait for them to sprout, you are called to entrust even that to the Lord, and to give thanks even if it is not you who gathers the harvest.
Two things the Lord asks of you: Humble yourself and serve with no name. Look only to the Lord. Amen.
Serving the people the Lord has entrusted to you means pouring out sweat, tears, and blood. Then many grains of wheat return to the Lord.

May you become a person the Lord calls, a person who rushes out unconditionally to the Lord's calling, a person who becomes the Lord's joy, a person for whom nothing but the Lord can bring joy, a person the Lord gives life to, a person who honors only the Lord whether living or dying, even if you walk this path alone.
If the Lord had not given life, we could not move even our little finger as we wish. In the Lord, there is no easy work nor difficult work. There is only the work that Jesus living in us does.
Had it not been for 'the Lord,' we could never have gone. Had it not been for 'us,' the Lord would not have walked with us. The Lord and we are one.
Do what the Lord desires. Compared to the glory you will receive and enjoy, the suffering and price you pay now is utterly incomparable.
Do not hesitate in the work the Lord desires.

If the Lord does not give, you have received nothing. Do not grieve, lament, or hope for luck over what you have not received and cannot receive. Be faithful to what is given even though no one looks back at you. The Lord will look back. This is the Lord's strong request this very morning. John answered, 'A person can receive only what is given them from heaven.' John 3:27
If you strive for what the Lord has not given, you are wasting time. It is holy to devote your strength to the place where you should stand and the work you should do. Focus on what the Lord desires.
Rather than seeking and grieving over what the Lord has not given, I hope you will accomplish the Lord's purpose with what He has given. Then He will add all these things to you.

The path the Lord gives is the most blessed path. Forcing yourself to take a path He has not given is called greed, and when greed is satisfied, it seems like it will be pleasant, but it becomes most miserable. The reason I do not become weary even when walking in parched land all day long is that the Lord has become my path.

If the Lord does not guard, the watchman's vigilance is in vain. Relationships you worked on, your career, social recognition—all become futile efforts. You must work hard, but effort alone is not enough. You must entrust yourself to the Lord's keeping.

A life that only opens when the Lord blesses, a life where human cunning does not work, a life where even the probabilities and luck that come so easily to others do not follow, so you have no choice but to entrust and go—this life is blessed.
I am here to do the work the Lord has commanded me to do.
When you do the work the Lord commands, there is nothing to fear or regret. If it is something the Lord permits, even loneliness is precious.
You only need to accept what the Lord has done. Those who stubbornly refuse to accept truth as truth, those who depend on emotion, suffer much.

If the Lord has not permitted it, you can do nothing. If the Lord tells me to guard this place, I will not move and will be faithful even for eternity. Why? Because the Lord is the Lord. The fact that the Lord has not permitted means to stake your life on what He has permitted. I belong to the Lord.
Do not merely be satisfied with what the Lord allows, but be devoted and volunteer to what He leads. That is a joyful life.
Performance or Promise? 'I tell you the truth, this poor widow has put more into the treasury than all the others.' Faith is not Performance but Promise.

While serving the Lord's work, I feel many shortcomings. At the same time, it is glorious to walk the path of faith with precious people. Meeting you is a great honor to me. Serving beautiful souls is an undeserved honor for me. My head bows in humility.

Policy regarding youth is primarily to act according to faith. Do not overextend beyond your strength, but act according to grace. However, there is work I want to do beyond my strength. There is something that requires concentration so intense that sparks fly from your eyes. You must be strong in seizing opportunities. That is saving souls. When the missionaries visit this time, please serve with all your strength and sincerity. I hope it becomes comfort and strength to those who go far away to save souls, fighting death, curses, and spiritual warfare. Those who lay down their lives for the Lord are precious people. Those who serve such people are equally precious. Such dedication is precious, and those who recognize such dedication are precious. Please commit to offerings, dedication, and prayer. As a youth ministry worker, this is my first earnest request. For this occasion, I have often not been actively pursuing other requests. Above all, I challenge you for the sake of souls and yourselves.
Those who look to the Lord receive new strength.

*Faith: True faith is to 'simply' believe in situations where you cannot possibly understand with your head. No matter how much I calculate, there is no answer, but the Lord has already reached His conclusion. He said He would establish an heir through Isaac, yet commanded to offer Isaac as a burnt offering—a contradiction by my ability and logic, but the answer exists only with Him. Abraham, when tested, offered Isaac by faith, so I, having received the promise, offered my only begotten son. For He had already told me, 'Through Isaac your offspring shall be named.' He considered that God was able to raise him even from the dead, from which, figuratively speaking, he did receive him back. —Hebrews 11:17, 19
We run toward the Lord.

There is a saying, 'a whip for a running horse.' Even when the Saturday gathering ministry ends, the Lord's work continues. For those friends who return home, I continue to pray and kneel more deeply so that the Holy Spirit touches them continually, both in words and in thoughts. The Lord is still working, and the minister sees this and runs further.

* Focus: The reason God looks at the heart rather than at qualifications or reputation is to find one whose heart aligns with His. Only then can God's will be fully accomplished on this earth. 'I have found David son of Jesse, a man after my own heart; he will do everything I want him to do.' —Acts 13:22. 'The Lord does not look at the things people look at. People look at the outward appearance, but the Lord looks at the heart.' —1 Samuel 16:7
We run toward the Lord's Word. Powerful prayer like fire and His response.

Seeking to obtain what the Lord has not given, hastening the time the Lord has not appointed—these are all desires. They bring considerable side effects. Such people will have thick autobiographies. Do not steal others' wells; let us become people who find water by digging. God does not abandon us.
To those who live faithfully in the time given, He shows another path.

If Your law had not been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction. I will never forget Your precepts, for by them You have preserved my life. —Psalm 119:92-93. In all difficult valleys and mountains of pain, the only reason they are made level is God's Word. That is the cause of life that makes me live.

Become an undefeated person. One or two times one may win by luck, several times by skill. However, even Olympic gold medalists cannot always win, and in many fields and circumstances, one must be satisfied with merely surviving. Yet God's servant David had no one able to oppose him in his lifetime, and he never lost in any of his many wars. Statistically speaking, it is 0%—a miracle. 1. David was a man seized by his calling. When he saw Goliath, he did not go out calculating, but with a heart and righteous indignation poured out by the Lord. To those who seize their calling, He grants power. One cannot die without fulfilling one's calling. It is not that I seize the calling; rather, the calling seizes me and carries me forward.

Sunday Message Part 2. 2. David's heart was aligned with God's. The Pharisees were rebuked because their hearts were far from God. According to Stephen the deacon's expression, David was a standard-setting life, one whose heart aligned with God—a God's Chaser, after His heart. God does not seek talented and capable people; He seeks obedient people. 3. David was a man of trust. Trusting in God appears in worldly terms as abandonment. When he boldly abandoned position, popularity, and people's eyes, God kept him. Meanwhile, Saul, fearing people to protect his kingship, oppressed and killed them. Only scattering resulted. David did not avenge his enemies. He had the power, heart, passion, and infrastructure to build the temple, yet he possessed the power of obedient 'restraint.'
Though Sunday ministry appears to end, it is not finished. We continue seeking the Lord, seeking still, and do not cease proclaiming and bearing witness. Let us go all the way.
Mr. Bean—Part 2: Plain Faith Without MSG (seasoning).

Sunday Leaders' Meeting: 1 Corinthians 12—Gifts are given for building up. We don't receive gifts for praise. Produce fruit with your gifts. Prophecy is the Word (preaching). Speak prophecies (advice) one by one in order and to gain instruction. Gifts are given to those who earnestly desire them. If love is not the motive, it is useless. Quiet Time around James 2:14—Only through faith do we receive salvation. The 'works' in James represent the matured state of faith. The words 'faith' and 'obedience' are synonymous. Faith is expressed through word and action. If words and actions differ, what do you believe? James found Rahab's righteousness in her works. The author of Hebrews finds Rahab's righteousness in her faith in chapter 11. They see the same thing but express it differently. Why? Because true faith produces the result of obedience. If you confess true faith in words but disobedience comes, that is false faith.

I said on Sunday to be ambitious about holiness. In areas where you are not ambitious, things either happen as they will or are forced. What you pursue can be easily seen. When you wake up in the morning, who and what do you think about? If you don't think of Jesus, you are not a servant of Christ.

If I had died, I would have lived and others would have been transformed. But like the words, it is not so easy. Dying is a power—the power of faith. Without faith that the Lord is the resurrected Lord, one wishes to live, not to die.
Go not as you desire, but as God pours out and as the Lord fills you.
Those who confess 'if I die, I die' are filled by God instead.
Dead tradition is more evil than heresy. —John Stott
For those for whom death becomes a reward, no tribulation or persecution in this world can prevent them. Because what lives within them is not desire and lust but Christ, death too becomes gain.

Paul worked by day and taught by night in Ephesus for three years, weeping. (Acts 20:31) He also served the Lord with burning zeal, possessing aflame truth. (Romans 12:11) Over decades of ministry, he continually served the Lord with a burning heart and ceaseless tears. Why? Simply put, he spent decades in the field as a single day. 'Remember that for three years I never stopped warning each of you night and day with tears.' —Acts 20:31. 'Never be lacking in zeal, but keep your spiritual fervor, serving the Lord.' —Romans 12:11. Day by day, fervent in spirit, with a burning heart in the Spirit... we gather to pray and the truth of the Cross is proclaimed. 7:30 as usual.

In a survey asking middle and high school students about their dreams, more than half mentioned entering a famous large company. We are churning out people who move in step with others at similar speeds. The greatest advantage of Christians is boldness. Creativity comes from there. Be bold.

Lifting dumbbells against gravity is exercise. Muscles are built by overcoming the resistance of gravity. What develops while evading gravity are lumps of yellow fat. Do not fear and avoid tribulation. Rather, find and obtain the treasure hidden within it.
The Lord who looks at the heart is not deceived. When the Father refines me, it is because He is good. The Father's character is the highest good. Therefore, what He does for me is the highest good.

Do it immediately. Whatever God moves you to do, do it immediately. Otherwise, you will quench the Spirit and ultimately face defeat. Pray immediately. Read God's Word immediately. Evangelize immediately. Forgive immediately. And always keep your eyes on the Lord.
The evidence of being 'grateful' now is 'victory.' The evidence of 'victory' is also 'gratitude.'

Being there now is said to be the Lord's grace. It was His intervention and grace that placed you there initially, and now He sustains you with that grace. Not by personal choice or luck, but by God's sufficient grace. Faith is walking the path that cannot be walked without the Lord. Become a worshiper, so that this place where I stand is soaked in the Lord's blood.

The reason you can be bold in all these circumstances right now is because you believe in the Lord's will. So whether you lose, you are not sad, nor do you become arrogant if you gain a little. Those who walk the Lord's path are bold.
Please embrace about three times more than now. Embracing will cause some muscle soreness, but you will experience much more flexible and abundant ministry.

Please intercede for those among us working late at this hour, those members spending nights awake under much pressure from trials, and those members away in distant places for various reasons. When love is the motive, miracles occur. May there be peace within souls and strength within bodies; may there be victory in all works. I bless all of you in the Lord's name.
Those who walk with the Lord now will stand in the very center of the Lord's will tomorrow.

For decades, I have preached the gospel thousands of times. Yet no matter how much I think about it, I preach the same content. 1. Trust in the blood. 2. Look only to the Lord. 3. Seek the Holy Spirit. In the end, the conclusion is the Cross.

That I have done well thus far does not mean my strength was great. Therefore, even becoming experts in our respective fields, we must never treat it casually or lightly. While studying and working, we must earnestly seek the Lord's help and also ask for prayer.

If I have lived by God's grace until now, I must rely on the Lord's grace going forward. Living a life of quickly departing from the Lord when things go well is truly pouring rice into a dog's bowl. Since I will believe anyway, believe well. There is one God, but there are brand names and counterfeits even in faith.

Have you recently made an unfavorable choice for the Lord's sake? Faith is not pursuing 'profit' but following 'righteousness.' Therefore, sometimes it may be 'unfavorable.' Blessed are those persecuted for righteousness' sake.
Even if you shed tears now, please sow seeds. You will surely reap in joy.
Paul was not lonely. Rather than hoping for luck, he kept the Lord's will and His timing in mind.
Now is the time to seek the Lord; break up your fallow ground, and the Lord will come and shower His righteousness upon you. —Hosea 10:12—The Word given at Christmas 1990.
I will not remember past events or think of all I have done before. Before the Lord who makes streams in the desert and opens ways, I will look only to the Lord who works new things within me.

I will not remember past events or think of all I have done before. Before the Lord who makes streams in the desert and opens ways, I will look only to the Lord who works new things within me. The Lord will protect me and uphold me. I know this, believe it, and praise it.

I give thanks for the presence and spirit of worship permitted during these past three nights and four days. What I earnestly desire now, having returned, is the Lord's greater presence. 'Not that I have already obtained all this, or have already arrived at my goal; but I press on to take hold of that for which Christ Jesus took hold of me.' —Philippians 3:12
Lasting faithfulness means bringing my lack of faithfulness to the Lord without deception.
The true meaning of a leader is that he has learned obedience.

Persuade the intellect and understanding follows. Touch emotion and compassion results. Direct the will (concretely) to action and decision follows. Asking without doing these is 'coercion.' In the church, instead of coercion, it is voluntariness; in ministry, it is motivation.

Only persistence bears fruit. What does not persist is merely a waste of time and consumption. Don't pour rice for a dog; hold onto the Lord to the end and by all means bear beautiful fruit. Trials make fruit beautiful.
If you try to keep it, it will be taken from you. If you desire to give, He will give you something better.

Wisdom does not fall from heaven but is the good use of the abilities you have. Using the slingshot you possess (like David) is the greatest wisdom. When God gives grace, He strengthens what you have rather than give you something new. 'The Lord asked him, "What is that in your hand?" "A staff," he replied.' —Exodus 4:2. When a dry staff is held by power, it parts the Red Sea and brings water from a rock.
While working and studying, balancing the Lord's work may feel overwhelming. But later, you realize that living without serving the Lord and enjoying the world feels even harder.

I go to work and come home wanting to be a caring father, and in the evenings I run tirelessly to prayer meetings with young people, and again I work late into the dawn, but what I still desire is to run closer to the Lord. So that no one can stop me.

Proclaiming truth as if spreading fire, being faultless in doctrine, and staking one's life on ministry—but what matters equally is whether my heart is beating because of love for Jesus. If I cannot be moved, it will only be difficult.

True love brings three things: longing to see, a desire to hear his words, and yearning to follow his will. David possessed all three. He longed for God, desired to hear God's Word, and broke his own will to seek God's will.
I will love you enough to tell you the truth. 'Faithful are the wounds of a friend; but the kisses of an enemy are deceitful' (Proverbs 27:6)

If you truly want to save lives, if you truly want to save lives, pay the price and unite with the Lord. John the Baptist, the greatest person throughout the Old Testament, was concentrated in one direction in terms of his possessions and lifestyle. John wore camel's hair and a leather belt around his waist, and ate locusts and wild honey. —Mark 1:6
True faith is an unwavering prayer that does not fade or grow cold even through the weathering of decades. If you pray, believe according to your prayer. It will be so.

True worship is keeping only the glorious Lord in mind regardless of my emotional state and surroundings. Become a worshiper through worship. It is the path to fulfilling life's purpose and the secret to breaking through difficulties. 'By faith Jacob, when he was dying, blessed each of Joseph's sons and worshiped as he leaned on the top of his staff.' —Hebrews 11:21
True courage does not come from personality but from dedication to truth.

True wisdom comes from affection. When you truly desire and love, wisdom that saves comes forth. In these times, what is needed is earnestness and anointing that discovers the way to live even in the most difficult and harsh moments.
Many signals exist in the air, but only the frequency that matches my wavelength is received and responded to. Responding only to the Lord is what is good.

The real and the fake are revealed when they are damaged, when they are tested. Sandalwood adds fragrance when it is cut. Artificial flowers seem plausible, but from the beginning there is no honey to give or sacrifice. The real does not change color, and its subtle lingering continues.

Everyone covets walking on a glamorous red carpet with photo zones rather than muddy roads. But the Lord told us not to take the easy path, the recognized path. Rather, He told us to take the difficult and narrow way. You will feel that those who seek it are significantly few. What is more important than the quality of the road is who is at the end of that road.
You must concentrate. The Lord speaks what is important. If you hold onto it, you will win.
Faith is a battle of response. It is actively responding to God's Word, not to your environment or emotions.

In a hymn, there is a lyric that says, 'Do not lower the sword in your hand before you have gone all the way.' You must push when you need to push. If you don't, later you won't be able to push when you want to. So this week I am pushing harder than last week.

A truly difficult person is a stubborn person. It is the same from the devil's perspective. A person who reports everything to God and consults about all things, a person who reports every single second repeatedly even though the Lord knows everything. Perhaps such a person would be unbearable. The devil: I'm tired too, and now we're even, so let's make a truce, I won't bother you. The believer: Lord! The devil says he's tired too and wants a truce, but he won't leave. Destroy him. Destroy... Destroy... Destroy... Continue to destroy him.
Worldly wisdom teaches to highlight strengths and hide weaknesses. But faith challenges us to boast in weakness and testify to God's power that is made perfect in it.
When I first began to follow Jesus, I was not afraid of anything, and nothing on that path toward the Lord could stop me.

When I first believed in Jesus, what I learned was that I must continually advance toward Jesus who becomes my goal. Though I may not have many choices and there may be some collapse, I must keep getting up and running. Even if I fall, if I fall forward I can grasp Jesus' feet.

When I first evangelize, I proclaim this way: Believe in Jesus! To those who have fought the fight of faith and finished the race, I say this: Believe in Jesus. Looking to Jesus is both the beginning and completion of faith. Looking to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith.

If ten thousand people call me an angel, who am I? A person. If ten thousand people call me a devil, who am I? Still a person. Then why am I shaken so much by other people's evaluation? It is because I am not looking to the Lord simply.

If you try to receive the love of a thousand people alone, you will be lonely and empty. But if you love a thousand people even alone, you will be honorable and happy. Life is not about accumulating but about scattering and giving.

First love, protecting the community with fiery truth, enduring persecution in Jesus' name, and possessing loyalty and faithfulness that would be sorrowful if second, cannot be replaced by anything other than 'love.' More than excellent ministry and faithful dedication, what is required is to recover your 'first love.' That is finding what you lost most importantly, and it is recovered only through repentance, not through events or mood changes. Jesus calls that repentance overcoming. 'I know your deeds, your hard work and your perseverance. I know that you cannot tolerate wicked people, that you have tested those who claim to be apostles but are not, and have found them false. You have persevered and have endured hardships for my name, and have not grown weary. Yet I hold this against you: You have forsaken the love you had at first. Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first. If you do not repent, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place.' —Revelation 2:3,4 'Whoever has ears, let them hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who is victorious, I will give the right to eat from the tree of life, which is in the paradise of God.' —Revelation 2:7

Since it is youth ministry, I try to speak the Word in a more relatable way when I preach. I do not think seriousness and joy are opposites. But the problem is that many friends are crying while I think it's humor and preach the Word. It seems the Lord is not using my jokes. Rather, I see Him working directly. Whatever way Christ is proclaimed, I rejoice and again I rejoice.

Youth will surely pass away and disappear. But the emotion and tears of those days that remain in the heart do not disappear. That is why youth must sow. Even with tears... Even if they fall and stumble and are pushed aside... Still, they must crawl and go and sow. In youth, success is not success and failure is not failure. 'Those who sow with tears will reap with songs of joy. Those who go out weeping, carrying seed to sow, will return with songs of joy, carrying sheaves with them.' —Psalm 126:5,6

Youth ministry pattern: 1. Eat and listen to the Word and pray, then eat and listen to the Word and pray, then sleep and wake again to eat and listen to the Word and pray, then return. 2. Come back and gather and eat like crazy. (Kimchi stew or seafood noodles + etc) 3. And share.

What youth must do is pass through fire, be refined, and be tested. Then you can nail down ambition disguised as calling and offer it to the Lord. That is one of the most essential refinements. What I most painfully reflect on at my age is that I need far more refinement and correction than I thought. I seek the Lord's mercy.

When life disrupts for youth, they become embarrassed. It is because they look only at reality. Reality says 'a loser stays a loser,' but what is needed then is to clearly confirm again where you must go. That is your calling. When your calling is clearer than 'life's disruption,' it becomes the strength to rise again, and God never disappoints those who rise.
Youth, take the lead. Dedicate yourself with the breakthrough mission to prepare the way for the Lord's coming.
Youth must sow even with tears. That is God's will.

In youth, even when praying, it is easy to mistake emotional guidance for the Lord's will. God's guidance is not hasty. Because the Almighty responds and is sure to accomplish it. But emotional guidance and self-rationalization originate from oneself, so they are pursued, desire immediate results, and resort to human schemes. Follow the Lord.
Every day I face my limits. Each time, there is God's voice to me: 'Look only to the Lord.' Not looking to the Lord is a grave sin.
I must hold onto faith every moment. The moment I lose faith, I fall into temptation.

In sixth grade, I lied about my age and attended the church's middle and high school worship service. The older middle school girls treated me as their age and were so kind... Since I had no faith, worship was just a formality and fellowship time was more enjoyable. One Sunday, my friend and I arrived late to the youth worship service as usual, when an older high school student, about seven years our senior, grabbed us and sternly rebuked us: 'You're worshiping God, but you have no sincerity like this...' That day my friend and I mocked like this: 'At least go to a good university, why do you take a year off to prep..., tsk.' That older brother is probably close to fifty by now. I think he must have worried a lot back then. 'That red-faced kid needs to believe in Jesus and become a real person...' 'Brother, I'm sorry. Though I am lacking now, I live with yearning to live for the Lord and to die for the Lord. See you in heaven.'

If 60 elementary school students pray in unity, gather wisdom, encourage each other, and declare that they can do it, and even write in blood, they still cannot solve a calculus problem. Only a third-year high school student can solve it. Do not try to solve the problem; raise your level. The Lord does not directly solve our problems but rather wants us to be elevated. Because of this perspective difference, it is difficult. Have peace of mind. These are problems that will all be solved after graduating from the wilderness school.

I am not afraid of pointed criticism, and recognition does not bring joy, so I only forget what is behind and press toward the goal, the prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus. 'I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.' —Philippians 3:14
The greatest gift: For a pastor, the greatest gift to the flock is personal holiness before the Lord.

Those who have seen the highest glory are not tempted by lesser glory. Those who have experienced the best are not shaken by what is inferior. Experience the glory of the Lord. Then you will not be shaken by emotional reasons, or by comparison and worldly boasting. 'May the Lord cause his face to shine on us, that your way may be known on earth, your salvation among all nations.' —Psalm 67:1 When God's Word runs, glory is experienced. Make the Lord's Word run in your heart.
When you do your best, confidence is born. That is why those who have read everything can do better than those in good circumstances.

There are things that cannot be done even when you do your best. To be precise, rather than saying it failed, it means it did not go as you wished. That means it was permitted under the Lord's sovereignty, and if you combined intent and your best effort, it is something better.

For a soccer player to run on the field without resting on the bench is not a difficult task but something extremely glorious. 'I consider my life worth nothing to me; my only aim is to finish the race and complete the task the Lord Jesus has given me—the task of testifying to the good news of God's grace.' —Acts 20:24

When David was fleeing from Saul, he did not overcome by holding onto the vision of a brilliant throne in the future. He simply endured. Holding onto the Lord, singing with tears as if today were the last.
Loyalty is not choosing people I like and keeping them beside me. Because I was called to love, I look only to the Lord and cover all with the heart the Lord gave me.
Praise refines a person. When lifted up, the Lord is looking for that person who can immediately be lowered and serve.

Whether holding the hilt of the sword or the blade of the sword in an absolutely critical situation, a Christian's attitude is always consistent. Not boasting in advantageous situations and not despairing in disadvantageous situations. Only the Lord establishes me and leads me.
Not great power but patience and courtesy will heal most of our problems. 1 Corinthians 13

'Quo Vadis, Domine?' Where are you going, Lord? This phrase appears twice. Once to Jesus carrying the cross, and the other, not recorded in Scripture but in Roman history, when the risen Jesus appeared before Peter fleeing persecution in Rome carrying the cross. Jesus, through the great surgery of the cross, carried away all our sins and poured out God's grace upon us. There is nothing more we can do for Him. Because the cross alone accomplishes perfect redemption.

The greatest comfort for a Christian is that the Lord knows my heart. If there is not a heart willing to lose everything to gain that one person, everything becomes a problem and everything becomes a trial. Make your purpose clear; who your master is will be determined.
Do not be afraid and be faithful to the end. This is the Lord's Word.
Christians do not avoid culture, art, and business. Rather, we conquer them by faith.
Christians must analyze and calculate thoroughly. However, we must not stop at that limit. Faith is transcending what cannot be done by conditions alone.

Christians work by 'identity.' It is not the 'I' that I think of myself, but the 'I' that the old self died and was resurrected through the cross. In the Lord, my 'I' never collapses under any challenge or external pressure. I hope you believe this and hold your head high.

A Christian's holy fellowship is not like a sales pitch trying to please. It is walking in truth within the Holy Spirit. To some it becomes the aroma of death, and to others the fragrance of Christ that gives life. Proclaim, pray, and be a witness daily.

A Christian's battle is not a battle of blood and flesh, so we do not resolve it by the world's methods. When fighting against all these battles and advancing, the powerful weapons the Lord gives are 'waiving rights' and 'opposing spirit.' When you waive your rights, you gain a stronger spiritual position and obtain what the Lord desires. When stinginess and hatred, anger attack, advance with the opposing spirit of giving and love, gentleness, and God's kingdom will be released in that darkness. I ask that you become a helping hand that assists with the Lord's heart. —Acts 16:10
A Christian's true and complete power is not in pointing out but in bearing the cross in that place. God's solution is always people. Revival and repentance begin with me.
Do not be anxious; do what you are called to do. Then you will experience what the Lord will do.

When Christians say they love but do not pray continuously and unconditionally, it is only interesting to the soul. The beginning and end of love through action and sincerity must be earnest intercession.

I give Christians two simple ways to exercise influence. One is 'holiness' and the other is 'sincerity.' No matter which area holiness has collapsed in, if you at least face it sincerely, repentance will occur.
There is the sound of great rain. It is the sound of great rain being accomplished through our young people.

After a great spiritual victory comes strong spiritual challenges and spiritual depression. Peter chose the path of returning to his old life as a fisherman in that depression, but gained nothing. Trials are overcome by patience. (James 1:3) Patience is thinking of the Lord and waiting for Him, and in that waiting, it is more important than anything that I am waited for, not just others. Especially if I can be patient until I can be changed, I will gain life through the pain of childbirth. Possess your souls through patience. Luke 21:19

When a large car and a small car meet, the small car must back up. The admiral of a great warship can order a blocking vessel to move aside. However, the low-ranking sailor guarding the lighthouse is the only sailor who can refuse it.
A leader is one who worries and prays about others' concerns and problems as if they were his own.
Living truthfully in my own way is far more meaningful and happy than being successful and living well by the appearance of others. It is blessed that he should prosper while I should diminish.

Before Paul's fifth visit to Jerusalem, he received warnings and dissuasion several times from spiritual servants and co-workers of the Lord due to great afflictions and imprisonment, and on the Alexandrian ship he faced several times a situation where hope of survival seemed lost due to a great storm. Yet he remained composed and bold to the end. He knew two secrets: the Lord's time and the Lord's will. Faith is never complicated.

Passing through a tunnel is painful. It is dark and you don't know when it will end. However, refusing and avoiding the tunnel is even greater pain. We do not welcome tunnels, but a tunnel is also the fastest shortcut. Do not refuse the shortcut but run eagerly.
Do you really need to...? In that time, why not look to the Lord one more time?
Hebrews 12:1-3: The life God desires from us is running the race of faith and looking to Jesus, thinking deeply of Him. Then we do well. In such a life there is presence and fruit.

Many have sawed wood with a saw, but no one has sawed sawdust. Because it was already sawed when the wood was cut. A person suffering from the past or losing courage is the same as trying to saw sawdust. Do not saw sawdust; saw wood.

A transparent life has nothing to worry about. There is nothing to calculate. Since what is as it is can be sufficiently beautiful, with nothing to add or subtract, we run the path the Lord has given us with focus on the goal.

The height of waves and the depth of trials are not what matter; what matters is overcoming through faith. Overcoming the world is not success or achievement but our faith. There is no one who has overcome the world except through believing Jesus as Christ, and only faith overcomes all things. Only faith.
Rather than taking the easy path, I hope you walk the path to becoming stronger.

It will truly be a blessing to walk on level ground and till fertile soil. However, it will be great glory even to toil painfully on rocky ground and collapse. The place where the Lord tells us to go and the place where He tells us to stand is the promised land, holy ground.

Using violence means you are weak. That is why you try to suppress the other person to overcome that inferiority. In all violence and abusive language there is a desire to prove oneself. It means you lack that much confidence. Those who truly have authority and power are gentle. There is no longer need to prove themselves. In the Lord, there is no need for further proof, but only to testify of the Lord.
Being tired, exhausted, and in pain is not always disliked. What matters more is for whom it is. If it is for the Lord, go without regret.
In God's kingdom policy, in no case do we respond to Satan's requests. Not even an inch of thought to give. Rather, we advance forward.
When we stand before God without presumption, with sincerity and honesty, we enjoy what God has prepared in His time.
God, grant us strength this day to run for Your glory in this world.
God, give courage to friends who need courage, and give peace to those who need peace. I pray that You will be strength to friends who are struggling at this hour. We need You, Lord, so very much.
God, I desire that the least of us be given grace to pour out all once more.

Without being close to God, without becoming one with Him, truth cannot be accomplished. The only way to become one is through prayer and the Word. If this does not happen, we must stop serving and ministering and remain in the Word and prayer. Sanctified through the Word and prayer. 1 Timothy 4:5
Rather than offering to God something that appears quite good, offer yourself. This is what the Lord desires and is a living sacrifice in spirit.
After receiving a vision from God, what you must do is pay the price for it.
Submit to God and resist the devil. The secret to victory is removing your sandals before the Lord. When rights are waived, the Lord works.

We must earnestly pray that God grant us the gifts and tools we need. However, we do not only hope for and wait for these things, but when we focus on that calling, obey it, and move forward, God is pleased and begins to supply all things. It is the calling, not the tools.
To live a life recognized by God, we must form distinctive habits from our youth. Daniel decided from childhood not to drink wine.

When we receive what did not come from God, we become enslaved. When we receive money and gain power, we are dragged along by the world. If the Lord has not given it, simply refuse it cleanly. Who is tempting you? We are beings who pursue not the riches the world desires but what the Lord desires.

The faith-filled life the Lord encourages is like a race. In the race of faith there are: 1. Putting aside unnecessary burdens, 2. Breaking the chains of persistent sin, 3. Living with focus only on Jesus. We must live simply and with concentration. The more critical the situation, the more difficult the moment, the more we must let go of complexity and do only a few things the Lord tells us to do, thinking only of the Lord and bearing with patience. That is living by faith. Therefore, seeing that we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight and the sin that so easily entangles us, and let us run with endurance the race that is set before us, looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of our faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is seated at the right hand of the throne of God. Hebrews 12:1-2
What God needs in guiding me is not 'my opinion' but prayer and obedience.
God has not answered all my prayers. If He had, I would have married someone else and also would have married several times.

The truth that if God does not help, we can only perish is not an ominous sign but something to rejoice and be glad about. The Lord pushes forward those who seek His face and whose hearts are in harmony with His, with the Lord's biased support.

God leads to peace, peace upon peace those whose hearts and minds are firm, those like a tree planted by streams of water, those who have fixed God's Word in their hearts and rooted themselves in it. Because that person relies only on and hopes in the Lord.
God said this: 'I did not call you to succeed,' 'I called you to obey.' When we obey, even if we do not 'succeed,' we will ultimately 'be victorious.'
May the work God has begun and that path ultimately show us the Lord's glory. I will dwell only in the Lord's tent.

There are times when God pours out an abundance of grace in His Word beyond what is needed for today. When we live life, there are times we must walk through the wilderness for long periods without supply. At that time, we endure like a camel with stored-up Word. A camel traverses the desert for a month on the fat stored in its hump without eating.

God will use our young people. He will awaken the continent and spread revival like a wildfire. He will pour out grace we cannot imagine and have not thought of, and all people will marvel. Human credit will fade and the Lord's sovereignty will come. We must continue to pray with the faith that the Lord will surely work. What we must hold onto is remaining humble to the end and not belonging to Satan. Decide not to respond to darkness and respond with waiving rights and opposing spirit. Continue to pray for one another, and through sacrificial worship and costly sacrifice, become young people who set fires in this land. Even if serving for a day, we desire to live that way.
When God disciplines, it is not to make us successful. His will is that we become holy like Him. If you seek success, the tunnel will be longer, but if you seek holiness, you will soon see light.

I must look at myself. If the Lord blesses immediately, can I see, walk, and give glory to Him? The problem is not the Lord's preparation but my preparation. When I change, the answer automatically occurs.
God understands the depths of my sorrow despite my insufficiency causing difficulty, and He is with me. And holding my hand, He strengthens me and says: You must still walk the Lord's path. Amen.

The One who guides that path is the Lord God Jehovah. Rather than being chased by circumstances and betting on the side with higher odds, when we trust in faith and hope in Him, He permits a sign at the appropriate time. What is important is the preparation of examining ourselves.
If only you look to God, human evaluation becomes colorless. One word—'God knows'—makes even life not precious.
You must become a person who cannot be satisfied by anything other than God. Whether it is a furnace heated seven times hotter or a den of hungry lions, nothing can stop such a person.
I am still thirsty and must continue running; I have no choice but to continue pursuing the Lord.
God is never late, and God never fails.
God disciplines and refines us in the wilderness. What people must do when passing through the wilderness is to remain silent.

Everything is a matter of life. If there is life, one lives like a flame; if there is none, it withers. Jesus declares that He is life. However, it is difficult to meet Him by simply seeking. 'One must seek and seek again.' I have never seen life overflow to a person who does not repeatedly seek the Lord. Faith is simply the power of repeatedly returning to the cross.

God gives unimaginable conflicts and anguish to those He loves most. This is because He desires that we completely let go of everything and look only to the Lord, holding fast to His Word. Though you face tribulation in this world, be courageous. Jesus has overcome the world.

God causes me to abandon things that were beneficial to me. Only when I lack even the carob pods do I barely return to the Father. The reason God drives me so harshly is not that I become an adequate person, but that I become a person of promise. I must leave the nest to enter the promised land. Faith is what is needed when walking between the nest and the promised land.
God did not call me to become outstanding. He desires that I become perfect. To be one in believing and knowing - Ephesians 4:13

God does not immediately demand that I become a perfect person. That will take much time. He only desires honesty. Honesty requires only resolve. Even prostitutes and tax collectors can be honest. I am a prostitute. I am a tax collector. But the Pharisees could not be; they were hypocritical. Jesus' evaluation is determined there.

God is alive and guides us faithfully, but sometimes He gives us confirmation in human ways. One of these is placing a faithful person beside me. When we observe that person of God faithfully looking to the Lord and have fellowship in the gospel, we experience God's encouragement and comfort. Take heart. You are 'the apple of the Lord's eye.' 'The Lord found him in a desert land, in the howling wilderness of the wasteland; He surrounded him, cared for him, protected him as the apple of His eye. Like an eagle that stirs up its nest, that hovers over its young, so the Lord alone guided him, and there was no foreign god with him' - Deuteronomy 32:10-12

God primarily remembers two things: 1. Small things. 2. Seeking His kingdom and His righteousness, not my own desires. If I am faithful in small matters seeking His glory rather than fulfilling my own desires, I will be remembered.

God pours His blessings upon the confession that says 'If it is not Your will, it is okay to give up' and 'Still, I love the Lord.' He exalts the person who can remain humble after being lifted up, who can truly find happiness in fearing the Lord, and through such a person, He will reveal God's glory.

God does not evaluate people by the importance of the work entrusted to them. He does not ask what organization they belong to or what their title is. He only desires to see how they respond before God and how they stand.

God guides all people. If they accept His guidance, He guides based on that obedience; if they do not accept it, He guides based on that refusal. Moreover, despite each person's unfaithfulness and loss of purpose, God accomplishes His purpose and vision that transcend these through God's own methods. Therefore, we must not say we know little, but acknowledge that we know nothing at all and advance by faith.

Before water engulfed the world, God held back for 120 years to give Noah and the wicked world an opportunity to repent. And after Noah's family entered the ark, He waits 7 more days. The rain comes only after those final 7 days have passed.
When God uses people, He does not look at past accomplishments and entrust present work based on that. David was young and inexperienced, but he loved the Lord and confessed that He was his strength.
God does not desire for us to remain as people whose hearts crumble at another's expression. He desires that we become people more sensitive to the Lord's joy alone.
Through all ministry, the life I want to share with young people can be summarized surprisingly simply: 'Preach and pray; every day you will be a witness' - Hymn 505
If, like Lot's wife, you do not turn toward the Lord but repeatedly look back, you will be hardened like a pillar of salt.
God transforms us to use us, and to accomplish that transformation, He blocks the corner I depend on. He makes mink into dog fur.

God causes us to shed tears to mature us, but truly God's heart is even more grieved. You can confirm this by seeing how much more He blesses when training is complete, proportional to the depth of that pain. God is faithful.
God refines us faithfully. When dealing with me, He refines me through difficulty—that is, through what I 'cling to.' If I let go of my attachment, I will quickly pass through the tunnel.
To those who seek first His kingdom and His righteousness, God's sovereignty and relationship with Him, He gives blessings beyond measure.

God does not ask us to take responsibility for results. We lack that ability anyway. He only desires that we walk with the Lord. Looking at results alone, Moses was called too late, traveled in 40 years what should have taken 4 weeks, and ultimately could not enter Canaan. Yet Moses is a glorious person because he obeyed what he was commanded to do.

God gives us limited, not unlimited resources. Not because of lack of power, but because the heart must bleed for the offering to be accepted. Only sacrifices offered with heart-felt devotion are accepted. Is this difficult to understand? If you have nothing to offer, I encourage you to offer your sorrow. Though you have little, I encourage you to offer it with a heart that thinks of and loves the Lord first.
God never says 'I'm sorry' to us. Not because He has bad character or lacks manners, but because He never does anything to apologize for. The Lord is faithful.
When your heart aches on one side, it means you must seek the Lord more. You are a saint because it aches.

God desires partnership with us. Not because He is lacking, but precisely because He is almighty. Partnership requires investment. We make an investment of two fish and a few loaves of bread—an investment that does not affect the big picture—while the Lord makes an investment that feeds 5,000 with leftovers. At humanity's meager dedication, God pours out abundance beyond measure.
God responds even to our groaning because He is merciful. But He does not support all weak people. When weak, if you complain, you will be ruined. Rather, if you praise, He gives you strength.
When God responds, He looks only at faith. Even if everything collapses, you must not back down in faith that believes in God's omnipotence and faithfulness. Overcome by faith.

God is not troubled by the corruption of institutional systems. He reserves 7,000 who did not bow to Baal and 300 of Gideon's prayer warriors. God will use many professional workers as missionaries. All are priests like kings. If the Lord called you, do not limit yourself by saying you are a layperson.
