God loves small us. To those who tell the Lord all the small details, He sends His Word, heals, restores, and strengthens.
God, if needed, changes hardship into miracles or gives strength to overcome, and according to His deep purposes, He also continues to allow it.

God does not forcefully request what is impossible. To the people who complained against Moses and contended with him. What Moses did was only 'inform' God. Laying my limitations before the Lord and obeying His instruction to strike the rock—living this way is truly living well.

When waiting for God, you must give time. Because nothing is gained physically or soulfully in that time, it feels futile to those without faith. Time waiting for the Lord is necessary. Why? Because I, bound to the world and unable to hear the Lord's voice, wait for the anxious waiting of God the Father who waits for me. In reality, it is the Lord waiting for me, not I waiting for the Lord.

God's rule came upon the Egyptian court that did not know God and the Babylonian courts that served idols because Joseph and Daniel, touched by God's Spirit and walking with the Lord, were there. They are not of the world yet not separated from it, unchanged and rather bringing change to others - John 17:14
When a group that loves God, loves the church, and truly loves one another can put their heads together for an appropriate length of time concerning any problem, there is no mountain we cannot move.
If you know God but are not intimate with Him, when facing problems, you will reach for human methods first. Living faith means hoping for what you long for in difficulty.
God's intervention is not hindrance. It is the most glorious path that even angels desire for a human being. God's will is sweet to those who obey.
God's concern is not the technique and ability with which I handle problems, but the attitude with which I face them.
God's kingdom is not overthrown by such things. Believe in the power of the blood and keep going. Another word for faith is disregarding the world.

Executing prematurely before God's time comes cannot produce complete fruit nor build up the community. God does not change or negotiate His complete will and glorious intention because of human impatience. Picking unripe fruit can never satisfy you or others.
A common characteristic that occurs in all people seeking God's will is 'answered prayer.'
Living according to God's Word is our duty, and it is God's responsibility to fill and protect us as we live this way. In conclusion, we need fear nothing.
I hear God's Word because I listen and desire to obey. I do not hear God's Word in that area because I want to cover it up and not obey.
God is most pleased with those who grieve with God's grief.

A person who can lose everything is a strong person. If you resolve to lose everything except Jesus, everything suddenly becomes easy. If you cling, you will be robbed; but if you let go and gain the Lord, He will give you all you need.
God's promise is like investing in a long-term fund, especially when we say all things work together for good. Be patient.

Become God's ace player. Become one whose appearance completely changes the flow of the game. Trust in the Lord who holds and transforms the person you have interceded for and prayed for. Become warriors of faith who, under difficult circumstances and situations, extend their hands, bow their heads, call upon the Lord, and open the new heavens through faith. Send down fire, send Your Holy Spirit's fire upon me.
God's zeal does not rest until the purpose is accomplished. The purpose is greater than any circumstance we will face. God has a plan that transcends the problems we face.

Even when we want to hear God's voice, we cannot hear it clearly. This is because His voice is heard by those who obey. Though I feel I would obey if I heard, Scripture says His voice is heard by the obedient.

God's name is not blasphemed through unbelievers, but through the coldness and hypocrisy of believers. Rather than being angry at those who deny Him, become like David—moved by righteous indignation when God's name is not glorified. Glorify the gospel through your life.
When we try to live by faith, we truly experience death. It is not merely pretending to die, but completely dying and being buried. Then we are born anew.

God's people are never mass-produced. When young Mary heard the angel's words, she did not say 'I will consult with my mother,' but readily said 'Amen'—this shows she was a person who had undergone tremendous refining. Do not merely marvel at the conclusion; receive grace through the process.

God's work cannot be done through position, spiritual experience, or background. As animals live by instinct and beasts survive by nature, Christians live solely by the spirituality of gratitude and awe.

Trying to do God's work yourself is pride; entrusting your own work to God is licentiousness. Such people have no repentance. Without repentance, we cannot meet God. Repentance means laying ourselves down before the cross.
When we do God's work, we receive God's support.
Those who strive to remain within God's domain of rule escape human rule. This was Daniel's experience.
Blessed are those who go to the place God commands. When we cross beyond the beautiful territory God has measured out for us, we become anxious and find no rest.
There are times when God calls again. He desires to rebuild everything and grant strength and ability. Wisdom is knowing that timing perfectly.

If God does not help, no one can help. Even if people help and use tricks, it ultimately ends in tragedy and ruin. Pray and obey so God can help you. 'Go and tell Hezekiah: This is what the LORD, the God of your father David, says: I have heard your prayer and seen your tears; I will add fifteen years to your life. And I will deliver you and this city from the hand of the Assyrian king. I will defend this city.' Isaiah 38:5-6.

That God sometimes permits hardship is grace. For that is when we become strongest. Kneeling in tears, prostrate with face to the ground, we appear weakest, but that moment is when we become most firm and strong. 'For when I am weak, then I am strong.' 2 Corinthians 12:10.

Wait until what God has shown you is accomplished in God's way. Surrender even your self-imposed deadlines and desires. Only then can the Lord work. Wait excellently until it is accomplished in God's way. Please—let the Lord work. Let patience finish its work perfectly, so that you may be mature and complete, lacking nothing. James 1:4.

Those called by God show respect in relationships and esteem those worthy of respect. Yet this is not seeking human approval. If you seek human approval, you are not God's servant. Will you do well for people or for God?
The standard God uses in employing people is not usefulness or capability, but 'surrender.'

Why God regarded Abraham as His friend: When Jesus died on the cross, many angels and saints were with God in heaven, yet none understood God's heart like Abraham. Abraham truly understood and shared the painful heart of having to sacrifice one's beloved son. Abraham and God had shared the same boat, so God unhesitatingly called him His friend. From God's perspective, a 'friend' is one who shares in the pain. Are you sharing in His pain now?

God asks us for something: do not desperately compete with others, trampling them to climb up. It is in His hands to exalt and humble. Rather, He says, 'seek the good of others' (1 Corinthians 10:24). The boldness and leisure to seek others' benefit in a fiercely competitive society is a privilege enjoyed only by those working for God's glory.
God does not want capability. He alone is already infinitely capable. He wants faithfulness. He considers laziness evil. Fulfill your mission. Your mission will carry you forward.

Dwelling on thoughts God did not give wastes time and invites seven worse demons. Your latter condition becomes worse. In faith, being clean means not only being emptied but being filled with obedience and relationship to the Lord.
If you evade the training God gives, growth stops and you eventually return to where you started.

Those who walk the path God gave are not pursued or anxious. Though I cannot know my way, He knows it, guides me, and causes me to emerge as refined gold. Do not align your life with people but with the goal He has set.
You do it well when you do the prayer God commanded, the work He assigned, the meetings He allowed, and the labor He asked. Everything else the Lord will do.
I have come to do Your will, God, as it is written about me in the scroll. Hebrews 10:7.
How to do God's work excellently: They asked Him, 'What must we do to do the works God requires?' Jesus answered, 'The work of God is this: to believe in the one he has sent.' John 6:28-29.

Even for a single day, run at 350 km/h. You will see the Lord's glory. Otherwise, you will crawl on the ground with wings. God called us gloriously. * Airplane takeoff: absolute speed 350 km/h, absolute distance 800+ meters.

There are problems that cannot be solved within a term. Some are only resolved when the year changes or even after graduation. When living through a current term with unresolved problems, what matters is clinging to basics, getting up whenever you fall, and maintaining the direction you must go. In any circumstance, His presence and voice are the unassailable basics.

One comforting truth is that we did not choose Jesus; Jesus chose us and established us. Jesus said the reason was to bear fruit that remains—so that whatever we ask the Father in Jesus' name, we will receive. John 15:16. You who are established, find peace in following the will of your Establisher; rejoice and delight in Him.

Beyond one obstacle stands another. Yet it does not matter because those running for the Lord's glory never quit or stop. Believing in the Lord's omnipotence, I believe we will work together for good.
Not stopping is power. Rather than focusing on many reasons to stop, we simply run toward the goal. For that goal is Jesus, who never stopped from heaven's throne to me, a sinner.
One day's excitement becomes the next day's abandonment and regret; one day's pain of letting go becomes another day's glory returning. Genesis 40:23: ...he forgot about Joseph.
Being called as a grain of wheat does not mean we are sent to judge or analyze others. The grain of wheat's calling is to die. Complete and utter death.
Limits are transcended not by clenching our teeth but by loving the Lord and receiving His grace to govern us.

People say Koreans are good with their hands. I think it is more about desperation. Where there is affection, wisdom comes. It is not whether we can save, but that this is a life we must save, so without hesitation we challenge and never give up.

Korean churches once had 12 million members; now we see 6.5 million. Even that seems somewhat exaggerated. Large churches still endure, but they are frequently pummeled by the internet and media. So people say Korean churches are in crisis. Yet I say there is hope. God is preparing young diaspora who have not bowed to Baal. I see the cross and its blood in your eyes. I see hope for the homeland church. In Egypt's bondage, I see a Moses in one person. Do not lament and criticize only; become Moses yourselves.

Coming to Korea, I think this: I will not stop; grasping what I have been grasped by toward Jesus the goal, I pursue it. Everything else is governed by self-control and sobriety. If the purpose is clear...

Korean traditional marriage ceremonies have 'nap-chae'—a ritual informing both families' spirits before the union. The Israelites were incompetent wanderers through 400 years of slavery and 40 years of wilderness. To live in Canaan, like Korean marriage, they would need to exchange gods and idols of Canaan, enabling trade and farming knowledge. But God says not to live that way. Instead, He wanted them to stubbornly cling to God's Word while harvesting less and receiving less recognition, being disrespected. God, who rescued them from Egypt and the wilderness, would rescue them in Canaan too, making them a channel of blessing to all nations. Because Israel feared people and flattered them, even tolerating their idols, Israel perished in Canaan. We must truly believe: if the Lord exalts, none can humble; if He humbles, none can exalt. Only the Lord is my strength.
Brother Han-seok has been discharged. Yet intercession continues. Victory is assured to the end.
True ability is being able to do something but not doing it. Winning but allowing defeat, restraint is power.

If we believe that all things work together for good, we will know the Lord speaks through even the wounds He permits us. Bearing pain, His Spirit comes to comfort the suffering, and ultimately God's righteousness will be accomplished.

Times and seasons are not our concern. That is the Lord's domain. He will make known and accomplish when the time comes. The essence is receiving the Holy Spirit and becoming a witness. Entrust the timing to the Lord and be filled first.
Core values, passion, and effort are concentrated on the gospel; we run without stopping.
Chocolate melts under sunlight, but mud hardens. Some people grow stronger facing the same trials. Just look a bit more, until the blade is razor-sharp.
Happiness is not a state without pain and sorrow, but a state overwhelmed by meaning and peace. Be bold.
We did not believe in Jesus to be happy. Rather, we had to surrender and give up much. Yet it was truly happy. Once we lost things, we saw true value more clearly. There is nothing but Jesus.

Faith without works is dead faith. Scripture holds a monistic view—when we confess faith, we also possess obedience. Along with the confession of belief, also declare transformation. This is possible only by the Holy Spirit's power.
An empty heart is not filled by social means. It is filled only by the Holy Spirit's fire. Do not waver between here and there; boldly look only to the Lord and move forward.
What we ask of dedicated leaders is not to squeeze harder. The only charge is to draw closer to the Lord and love Him more.

Laying down the burden of dedication might seem to guarantee much productivity. Soon you will see how meaningless the leisure gained by abandoning faithfulness becomes. If performance and efficiency were the goal, God would have made us terminators or livestock. Faith is not efficiency but relationship. Please remain in the Lord.

In modern times, there are many teachings and expressions, each presenting interpretations and solutions to numerous difficulties. How did saints of old confess and move forward? 'I will give my life for the Lord,' with no boasting. Without suffering, no fear. In Jesus. Before martyrs, there are no problems. For those who fear losing their lives, everything is a problem.
Reality may bring tears even while eating, but the Lord makes you dream beyond walls. Confess and declare before the Lord and the world: 'I will not die but live to see the glory of God.'
Hold on. Guarding your position is holy.

Threats and condemnation work because weaknesses have been seized. It is not because of weakness that weaknesses are seized, but because of failure to confess and repent. Those forgiven are not only bold but also at peace.

It is not gratitude and joy because circumstances are good. The key is for whom I am living. This precious life is burdensome for some, and even death becomes a gain for others. This is because living is Christ and dying is gain to me—Philippians 1:21. I have set my purpose to live for Christ, so dying is also gain for me.—Easy Bible Philippians 1:21

Breathing is continuity. A rope is continuity. If even a small gap appears, it becomes meaningless death and separation. Faith is the same. Do not show gaps. It takes years to build, but a day to lose.

In marathons and cycling races, with so many people running, they don't get tangled and fall. The reason is because they have the same goal. If they wanted to go different paths, it would become like an egg scramble from the start. A good community is one that spreads this word through the hearts of all souls and plants it deeply. Persistently, uncompromisingly, continuing to ignore... not that I have already obtained or have already been made perfect, but I press on to lay hold of that for which Christ Jesus has laid hold of me. Brothers, I do not consider myself yet to have laid hold of it. But one thing I do: forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.—Philippians 3:12-14

The taste of ripe persimmon cannot be imitated or forced. If I who have not tasted the persimmon taste myself cannot explain it and demand 'Why don't you feel the taste?' then one must act insincerely, live tiredly and powerlessly. Even though the taste was not experienced due to lack of supply, one falls into self-blame and condemnation. Just as taste cannot be imitated, so it is with the gospel. When life is clearly supplied, the game is over. We cannot help but speak of what we have seen and heard—Acts 4:20 https://youtu.be/WzJad3R29r8
Do not fear that brilliant relationships no longer exist. Rather, you must fear that your sincere relationship with God becomes thin.

I do not wish to torture hope in the name of faith. The Lord gives the same level of challenge to those who follow from the beginning and those who follow long. 'Who is your master? Whom do you serve? Who lives in you? Where will you belong?' Faith is surprisingly simple. It is only people who make it complicated. This happens when what you pursue is not the goal. Sell your possessions and give to the poor, then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.—Matthew 19:21

If you lose yet it becomes a gain, and even death cannot take away your happiness and excitement, it is because the One living in you is not 'me' but 'Jesus Christ.' Simply lose things. You will gain the Lord.

In chemistry, there is a chain reaction. The preceding reaction triggers the following reaction. People typically call this explosion. Iron sharpens iron. When people of faith and answered by Jesus are put together, a chain reaction of revival occurs and it becomes hotter. What kind of trigger do the people beside me now give to me?

Do not be anxious within probability but entrust and rest in the Lord. While the Lord embraced the stubborn prophet Jonah, He prepared a storm, a great fish, the fish's vomit, hot wind, scorching sun, a plant, a worm, and sea wind. Everything is in the Lord's hands. This matter I face is the Lord's best choice for me.
Act with conviction. If conviction is small, act small. If there is much to be done, pray to dwell in much conviction through deeper prostration.
Walking through tribulation humbles me and makes me keep the Lord's statutes.

Not being sober after repentance means not having repented. Those who repent are humble before the Lord and are made sober. You cannot hold the cross while feasting, while not leaving the evil path. The cross is not like a handrail you can grab on a bus. It is received as life.
A company moves toward profit. But believers must make decisions toward drawing closer to the Lord. Those who move to please people and expect it have woe.

In the company's contract compose template, there is Party's Bargaining Position. Who is the stronger party? This is very important in the world. Daniel also had a king who held his life and death, and rivals who eagerly sought his life. Even Daniel, who at 89 years old had served 4 kings and served as prime minister for 75 years in public service, was attacked with racist accusations as 'a captive from Judah.' Daniel's way to counter this is to 'ignore it' and serve the Lord as before. I serve only the Lord!

A company's fatal challenge is not aggressive advertising from competitors but lack of conviction among its employees. The most effective way to deny the Lord is not an atheist who says the Lord does not exist. It is when a believer in God worries.
You must be faithful when in the congregation. But you must be even more faithful where no one worships. Do not seek to fulfill your desires but uphold the faithful will of the Lord.
If there is no regret, you have lived well. When standing before the Lord, two things must be clear to have no regret. One is the goal and the other is running with passion.

Some people charge their phone once and try to use it for a week. After 3 or 4 days they say KakaoTalk doesn't work and Facebook doesn't work. It's not that it doesn't work; it's that it's turned off. Faith is not that it doesn't work; it's that it turns off. That's why you must always be full. Then you overcome sin and overcome temptation.

What is commonly called success is not God's glory; rather, enduring and waiting becomes glory. If my faith does not weaken and I hold to God's promise, I give glory to God. Glory is given through faith, not through fulfillment of desires. Being strengthened in faith without weakening and not doubting God's promise, I give glory to God with firm faith—Romans 4:20

People often say they give glory to the Lord when receiving spots others envy. That's why the Lord doesn't exalt them. He exalts because you give Him glory. When the Lord humbles, it means I was not prepared. It is not glory to succeed and be envied or to speak of God's glory in an interview. Glory can be given when you wait long without response, yet your faith does not weaken and you hold to the promise without doubt. Not just anyone gives glory. (Romans 4:19) Abraham was about a hundred years old—yet his faith did not weaken. (Romans 4:20) Without weakening in faith, not doubting God's promise but being strengthened in faith, he gave glory to God.

You cannot turn a water wheel with water that has already flowed. Yesterday's grace cannot become today's and cannot sustain today with yesterday's manna. Become humble, kneeling people who look to the Lord.

If you boarded a bus written Heathrow, it is right to go to Heathrow. You cannot go to the opposite direction, Gatwick. If you have decided to look to the Lord, you must run toward only the Lord. To pursue other things is fraud and theft.

It is family that gives strength in difficult moments. Looking at today's Path Finder rehearsal, I deeply realize that each member of our family is precious to me. Thinking of precious people changes the heart from wanting to step back to wanting to love unto death. Precious people are given 'strength to start again.' Loss and damage are not costly for those we love.

Paul frequently uses the expression 'pursue' in all his letters. I believe this happens because of two things: direction and passion. When direction of Jesus and passion combine, you cannot help but pursue. Also, I consider everything a loss because knowing my Lord Christ Jesus is of surpassing worth...to lay hold of that for which Christ Jesus has laid hold of me. I press on—Philippians 3:8-12. When you know Jesus, you run. Really simple, right?
Before saying you are tired and will die, please pray. Don't stand on stage untested, lingering on the stairs crying. Compete with your knees and faith. You will really feel it's doable.

Giving up when difficult is the easiest and most convenient choice but also the most proven worst choice. The Lord did not give up on the cross but accomplished it. Saying it is finished means enduring well. When difficult, faith brings victory, and most faith appears in the form of enduring.

People say they will give up because it's hard. Before saying it's hard, you must pray and then speak. That is not being shot down while flying but falling over yourself on the ground. Without prayer, you don't even have the right to say it's hard.

It is the logic of the world that the strong devour the weak. That's why many try to have sharper claws and stronger teeth. But those who follow the Lord do not live fighting others. They live hearing the Shepherd's voice.
Gallop with the whip—to kindle again the gift of God that is in you, I remind you—2 Timothy 1:6

Parting Reflections—A respected psychology professor in college once asked me a personal question. 'What kind of person do you want to become, Hwa-sung?' Without 0.1 seconds of hesitation, a reflexive answer came out, surprising even me. 'I want to be a person who inspires.' I realize how fast five years passes. That was said with spirit, but I deeply realize it is not as easy as words suggest. Forget moving others; not hurting and not blocking the Lord's path, even that feels lucky. I hope to be happy. So when called, though I have no skill and am not qualified, if I can be used as a six-month poker stick for God's glory to young people, I would be grateful and meaningful. I cooked rice, drove, worshiped and visited, but rather than thinking of serving, I was enchanted by love. Laughing together, crying together, even burning with concern, I saw heaven's glory. I think I loved rather than served. I was happy being simply one youth among those beautiful youths before being a minister. I tried to give but gave nothing, and instead received abundantly. Turning back and walking silently, I have unexpectedly become rich. Still in my daily return, today my hands won't take to work. Jesus's cross takes precedence over all things. When breathing, when the heart beats, when storms and waves come, when climbing to brilliant heights, remember and look to the cross. It was happy and truly glorious to be together. Kim Hwa-sung, Missionary
Even if a dog barks, Eurostar runs. When purpose is clear, what is inappropriate and unnecessary can be ignored.

That my faith continues to be challenged means I must respond with faith. That this moment has become a sharp front line where neither can retreat is because this is truly a critical moment. Please respond with faith.
Running away doesn't end the war. You must face the war and win. Then you will not lose life but find more life.

If Noah had mastered alcohol well, if Samson had handled relationships well, history would truly have changed. People are not tested by their strength. A person who handles weakness well is a person of character. Cleanse yourself of the sin you keep falling into. You will become a Samson without relationship problems. Faith is surprisingly simple: simple, intense, flexible.

If you pray for 5 minutes, it seems like 5 minutes of work, and if you pray for 10 minutes, 10 minutes of work. It's not like that. He works in bulk. Low cost, high efficiency. That's why the Bible uses the expression 'storing up.' Romans 2:5
There is no reason to hesitate or waver. If the Lord established it.

*Christian's Appeal—When the church sees structural evil and injustice, there are two ways: simply turn away, incite public opinion, or use physical force. The way the church changes the world is first through prayer. Because the Lord is the Master. While hearing the Lord's Word about how to approach the world, appeal with God's character. During continuous intercessory prayer, specific action is guided. When the Lord speaks and guides, raise your voice. With shame and a loving heart, simultaneously hate sin and not tolerate injustice.

*Cry out and act—There is a time to cry out and then act. If you keep demanding and act, it is forced artificiality. But if you move in response to the Word, it becomes obedient faith. The Lord said to Moses, 'Why are you crying out to me? Tell the Israelites to move on. Raise your staff and stretch out your hand over the sea to divide the water. The Israelites will go through the sea on dry ground.'—Exodus 14:15-16

*God's Sovereignty and My Freedom—When I preach God's sovereignty, I am frequently asked whether it is fatalism. (Do we become robots?) What is clear is that God's sovereignty cannot be resisted by anyone. Some Reformers overemphasized the Lord's sovereignty and fell into fatalistic predestinarianism without missionary work. [Sow righteousness for yourselves, reap the fruit of unfailing love, and break up your unplowed ground; for it is time to seek the Lord, until he comes and showers his righteousness on you.]—Hosea 10:12. When you break yourself and move forward, it is God who gives rain there. To till the land while seeking rain is our task, and to send rain and water the land with righteousness is God's work.

People of faith do not ignore common sense and reality. They simply transcend with faith. I too abandoned the world's pleasures and the world abandoned me, but the work of transforming the world within the world has not ceased. I illumine the world with excellent wisdom and faithfulness.
The strong seem to survive. But the one who survived is strong. Endure. Then you will become strong and return.
Simply cast it away. Cast it to the Lord. And trust the Lord and just go. The Lord is alive.
Do not worry about things you cannot do but obey with what you can do. Go forward with only prayer and thanksgiving.

*Don't try to be better rather than to be you, for you are his workmanship. Attempts to appear somewhat decent are useless. When you think too deeply about how others think of you, life becomes wasted and empty, and secret sin enters. There is only one thing I must hear: 'Well done, good and faithful servant.'

Moses is beautiful because after walking Moses's path and being loyal unto death, he exited solemnly on Mount Nebo. If Moses had deliberately walked Joshua's brilliant path of entering Canaan, it would not only be unbeautiful but also obscure God's glory. My life is most beautiful only when I walk the path the Lord gave me. Walk your path. Even if it seems narrow...

Although Paul was a powerful man, he asked the Thessalonian believers to manage and deliver the relief offering for the poor in an orderly and transparent manner. Why not pray and open the windows of heaven to pour down money? Because another undeniable expression of miracles is the transformation of hearts. God's kingdom must advance through sacrifice and love.

*When unavoidably changing churches: 1. Maintain good relationships if at all possible. 2. Do not act in anger, but act when you can bless. 3. In the process, do not forget that the Lord required my transformation, not the church's transformation. 4. When your calling and your life collide, seek the Lord's will. Is there remaining work to be done, or is my supply of grace urgent right now? 5. At least pray for a place that ensures continuous supply of grace and opportunities to serve. Never be buried among the masses.
I will not collapse. Because I cannot collapse. As long as Jesus, who is my soul and life, is with me, I will pursue toward the goal.

When fasting, it is natural to feel hungry and weak. It feels like you will die. This is especially true for the first couple of days. Your body sends you signals to stop. When you receive such a message and ignore it, peace and new strength come, and you realize it was somewhat exaggerated. When the response of darkness intensifies, think of it as a light matter. Victory is near.
I must forget what I have done because there is an eternal goal. I strive to be apprehended by Jesus, but I have not fully attained. So I press on ceaselessly toward Jesus, who is that goal.
Prayer is difficult. Because I pray too little. Prayer becomes sweet. When you pray much.
When much has collapsed, this is the prayer I offer: God, let me do what I absolutely must do right now. The answer is to fulfill my calling and remain in the Lord.
Only when I cannot lose the Lord even if I lose everything else does He truly remain with me.
The unbearable sorrow that keeps me from living a life of comfort and convenience comes from the burning love of Jesus in my heart.

Moses does not collapse in shame but exits beautifully. Because he remained faithful unto death each day as if it were his last and fulfilled his calling. Those who fully complete their calling are clean.

I hope you do not lament that your hopes are unfulfilled. Coming this far has been immense grace. This is not a cause for discouragement but for thanksgiving. Without the Lord's grace, I could not have taken even one step, and the same will be true going forward. Trust in His faithfulness.

When everything I expect crumbles without exception, the Lord makes me understand something. Especially after observing shallow circumstances and scheming excessively, the Lord's voice that comes is: 'You follow Me.' Abandon trickery and hold fast to your calling.
Regardless of the type of problem, the conclusion is gratitude. You are the victor when you choose gratitude regardless of the emotions you feel and the results.

Those who find faith too difficult are those who cannot let go of too many things. Think about it. How difficult it would be to walk a narrow path while carrying and dragging heavy burdens. Only after laying aside easily entangling sin and heavy burdens can we run the race of faith.
Do not stop. Persistence becomes real power. Continue ceaseless intercession and love.

A dog barks, but the KTX runs. Why? Because its purpose is clear. Those whose purpose is the Lord do not mind reactions and words. Whether living or dying becomes secondary. Because the most important purpose is to glorify Jesus.

The worst thing one can do is advertise and sell a product one does not believe in. There is nothing worse than proclaiming Jesus without being filled with Jesus. That is why being filled with Jesus is the beginning of mission.
The leader tries to do ministry well. If there is no hindrance, revival comes. Let me not be a hindrance but a servant of obedience.

Fasting while roaring and crying out is painful. But the result is transformation of life. When you sow seeds with tears, you will surely reap with joy. That harvest will be returned to you not as wealth but as life.

When eating only manna and water from the rock, the Israelites practiced the most focused faith, but they rapidly became corrupt in Canaan because there was much to lose. Complexity is already a losing game. Keep it simple.
Wake up! Everything is under His control!

Apostle Paul, who was in prison, had an opportunity to be released, but he exchanged that opportunity for the opportunity to stand before Caesar in Rome and preach the gospel (Acts 25). On his voyage to Rome, opposing winds blow. Those who live by the gospel experience the powerful winds of hell. But when the wind of the Holy Spirit blows, everything will be devastated. 'The winds were against us' (Acts 27:4)

Whether southeast winds blow or northwest winds blow, spreading the fragrance of Jesus who became a white lily among thorns, I just go on saying hallelujah and amen. Whatever anyone says, whatever misunderstanding there may be, whatever the world's evaluation, I just live that way.

A head of household does not collapse and spread out. Because there are family members to support. Those called to a mission do not remain fallen. Because they are those who save lives. Anyway, we will rest eternally in heaven. I hope you run for His glory.

Peace comes when you entrust everything with the heart that says, 'It may not happen, and if I lose, I lose; if I die, I die.' Moreover, you become bold. Be strong and courageous before the Lord of peace who superintends all circumstances. Not my will, but let Your will be done, Father.
Miracles happen when you say not 'I must achieve this,' but 'Even if I lose everything, I will hold onto the Lord.' That transformation, that confession bursting forth is worship.
Just as always, as if this is the last time, I stake my life.
If there is too much of myself in me, I wander. Rather than agonizing, do what the Lord desires. Knowing alone is insufficient; it requires the obedience of faith.
It is fine. Because I am in the Lord's hands... I have known the answer from long ago. I just need to do it.
The devil opposes; the Lord is received. I look only to and think only of the Lord 24 hours.

Anyone can be deceived for a while, but not for long. Without intimacy with God, things may go well. Only for a very short time. And the collapse will be severe. Make time for the Lord, speak with Him, and trust Him. That will save me.

I cannot express how much hope the words 'faith wins' bring. The problem is that there are many difficulties and I have no strength, but faith wins, wins for me, draws along weak me, and makes me win. How truly it is that when I believe and rely while advancing without fear, by the power of believing in Lord Jesus, I overcome the whole world—faith wins, faith wins, believing in Lord Jesus, I overcome the whole world (Hymn 357).

Mr. Kim, how are things going? Although we part this way, there will come a day when you say that precisely because of such poignancy at a corner of life, there is now warmth and happiness. First, do your best to take care of what remains. Second, humble your heart to correct the fundamental cause of the distortion. For some, today's careless laughter becomes disaster, while for others, yesterday's tears of blood return as today's humble happiness, so we cannot simply say that now is misfortune. Your children are healthy, your family is well, and you are still quite young, so if you do not give up and sow seeds, you can enjoy pure happiness. And in the Bible there is Proverbs. An easy-to-read collection of brief sayings. Read it daily. http://kcm.co.kr/bible/kor/Pro1.html And I will also pray here. 'Those who sow with tears will reap with songs of joy. Those who go out weeping, carrying seed to sow, will return with songs of joy, carrying sheaves with them' (Psalm 126:5-6). 04 Feb Jacob. From a letter written to one who thought they had lost everything.

Simply put, there are two kinds of trials I endure: trials I can bear, and trials that bring an escape route when I cannot bear them. Believe this truth and be assured and grateful for overcoming trials and victory.

One of the most foolish things is to pass one's own lack onto others. Rather than throwing stones at others, I should hold the cross and strike my own breast. An unfaithful servant living with a heart beaten so much it becomes nothing.

Please value gatherings. From my youth until now, I have never failed to value gatherings. Gathering is loyalty, and God does not use those who are not loyal, no matter how outstanding their abilities and gifts.

Rather than relying on other things, I hope you rely only on the Lord who enables you. Even if circumstances seem difficult and someone you trusted turns away, the Lord permits only manageable trials and provides a way of escape when you cannot bear them. Look to and believe only in the Lord who enables you to endure.

Belief without working is because there is continuous disbelief. There is too large a gap between belief and belief, creating a blank space of actual disbelief. That is why the Bible tells us to always look to Jesus. Sustainable faith is achieved when you fix your gaze on Jesus and think deeply.

Do not carry too many burdens. That too is greed. Please carry the burdens the Lord has given. Your heart will become gentle and humble, and that burden will be light. Boldly abandoning what the Lord has not given is called 'peace,' and holding fast to what He has given until the end is called 'boldness.' I am gentle and humble in heart, so take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy and my burden is light (Matthew 11:29).

What I cannot control is not my domain but God's. That is why Jacob did not devise strategy to fight Esau's four hundred men at the Jabbok River but appealed to God. How simple are obedience and prayer? The simple life the Lord gives. Simple, flexible, intense!

If while riding rides at an amusement park you complain as if the ride will never end, you will look very pathetic. When the time comes, it ends; enjoy while it lasts. The Lord has given only two options: 1. I will permit only trials you can bear. 2. If the trial is unbearable, I will provide a way of escape. Therefore, it is not a time to panic but to advance with thanksgiving and worship.

The only reason a person of faith can muster courage when otherwise impossible is—David was greatly distressed, but he found strength in the Lord his God (1 Samuel 30:6). The Lord says to find strength in Him. And muster courage and turn things around.
The greatest prayer is the confession of loving the Lord and the prayer that His will be accomplished.

Sometimes in living there are times when answers do not come. It is a situation where the heart collapses. What is needed at that time is faith. Disregard and trust the Lord, and continue walking the path you were on. After all, if the Lord does not help, it will not be resolved, so all I do is trust.

What set the three-and-a-half years of discipleship training on fire, made it memorable, returned everything to its place, and made it work was the fullness of the Holy Spirit. When the Word becomes word and faith becomes faith, the Holy Spirit must come.
The problems I face must ultimately be resolved by God, and God works miracles for God's people.

The problem I struggle with is not the real problem. The Lord must intervene to resolve it. God seeks those of undivided heart and grants them power. Therefore, the wise person does not hate the person who is like a thorn to me but finds resolution in my fear of the Lord. The fear of the Lord is resolution.
I trust God who hears my prayers. I believe that the One who moved me to pray will also work. I intercede and bless.

With a clear purpose comes focus. You gain insight to see core values and simply restrain unnecessary things to achieve the goal. Therefore, a person of faith can do all things in Him who gives me strength.

The letters I wrote long ago are still recognizable as mine. When hearing testimonies, I recognize the truth message woven within them. Surely the Word works as truth in that life. That is transformation.
Soldiers train and obey orders. They do not avoid fighting, flee for refuge, or seek retreat when forced to fight. A soldier exists to fight and win. Be steadfast.
This path will not be easier than others. But it will truly be glorious.
Do not stop; continue. Even if you weep, do not rest but sow seeds. The day will surely come when you reap with joy.
David prepared five stones. Why? Because if one missed, he was prepared to throw the other four in rapid succession. Keep going until it is done. Simply ignore the noise and press on.

Whatever work you do, there is no meaningless work in what the Lord permits. Even if no one acknowledges it, in God's sight it is a sacrifice of obedience, a prayer offered with your body, and an exceedingly great reward that He will surely remember. Be faithful.

Please offer the shortest yet most powerful intercessory prayer. Please handle that person. Though prayer is simple, the Lord who applies it is wondrous—turning the whole earth around to transform that human being.

Whether resting on the most comfortable bed after forgetting everything, or in an urgent situation under tremendous pressure, God desires that you confirm that God is the center of my heart and that the Lord is the center of my soul. Whether we live, we live for the Lord; whether we die, we die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord—Romans 14:8
The last words Jesus spoke to His disciples were to watch and pray so as not to enter into temptation, and that He intercedes so that your faith will not fail.
When I let go of holding myself, God holds me. 'See My servant whom I uphold, My chosen one in whom I delight.'

When I peel fruit, it looks shabby, as if a mouse had gnawed at it. But a mother's handiwork reaches the heights of flawless artistry. My inability is my hope. Work entrusted to the Lord is accomplished at the Lord's level.

Faith looks to a better reward. When pursuing better values, choices arise, and within those decisions, destiny changes. The decision of faith accomplishes the intention of the One who sent it and enables the most meaningful life.

* Fixed gaze: You wish to look to the Lord, but for just a moment—really just a moment—very briefly, there is a desire to glance sideways and gaze upon what will perish. Such events never end there. In human history, not once has carelessness ended as mere carelessness. Fixing your gaze requires paying a small price for a few minutes, but if you avoid it, recovery may not come even after years. Look to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of faith.

* The minister's calling: Pray that people live for the Lord even in life and die for the Lord even in death, or intercede that those who are lukewarm and neither hot nor cold will never die gracefully.

* The calling: There is the most meaningful and happy life to which the Lord calls you. The conclusion is simple. Either go that path simply, or become blind on the Damascus road, kicked back by the spear of rebellion, suffering greatly. Either way, the ball will eventually go to the goal.

Numbers are not what is needed. Scripture says it is foolish to boast in numbers. Gideon's three hundred dedicated warriors are mighty men, but tens of thousands are merely a crowd. Do not become a crowd; become a warrior. That is the kind of person the Lord uses.
Faith is invisible. But proclamation and confession cannot be hidden—they are heard. Above all, obedience is so.

When passing through a painful wilderness, you must pray. Pray like this: 'Make me lower still.' This will accomplish the wilderness's purpose most effectively and quickly. (Deuteronomy 8:16) 'He humbled you and let you hunger and fed you with manna...that He might make you know that man does not live by bread alone...that He might humble you and test you, to do you good in the end.'
Tell me what you pray for, not what you want. I am interested in that. Because it will be accomplished.

Faith in Hebrews 11 is seeking a better sacrifice, a better homeland, preferring suffering, a better resurrection, counting greater wealth, and seeking what is better prepared. Faith is having the perspective to compare and staking your life on what is 'more' important. If you cannot see what is better or cannot acknowledge its value, you cannot seek what is better and will only pursue what is visible and perishable. That is truly foolish.—Hebrews 11
I know what I want, therefore I pray again

That I cannot do it by my own strength becomes comfort beyond disappointment. How blessed is this? To rely only on the Lord, not on my own strength. Confess that you cannot and move forward. The Lord will rule directly.
If you do not pray, you become a fraud.

I hope that when you are able to give, you give. Then later, the devotion and sacrifice I offered will become grace that holds me and carries me eternally. Yet to me, by the grace of God I am what I am, and His grace toward me was not in vain. On the contrary, I worked harder than all of them—yet not I, but the grace of God that is with me.—1 Corinthians 15:10

In the word 'opposite,' there is the meaning of an opponent party. However, a person of faith does not deal with opponents. We must always deal with the eternal Father who is our only concern and solution. Then you will experience the Lord's rule over challenges, rumors, and problems whether they have substance or not. God said David was a man after His own heart because he was someone who bowed down and prayed before problems. I love, but they oppose me in return. I only pray.—Psalm 109:4

A person of faith is not pessimistic about this sinful world. Mere criticism without alternatives is meaningless. A thermometer only indicates temperature, but a thermostat actually exerts influence and changes the temperature. Faith is not a thermometer but a thermostat.
Let the Word keep me far from sin. Otherwise, sin will keep the Word far from me.
If you truly want something, you will pursue it. If you want something but do not pursue it, you did not truly want it. Only what you genuinely want will you persistently pursue.
David did not always praise with joy. From truly unspeakable despair, when he determined to praise the Lord, joy burst forth that no one could restrain. Faith is about fixing your heart.
Faith is seeing clearly. Stop wasting time on meaningless things and pour your time and passion into what has value.

When you long for something, you do not give up and passion keeps arising. Like Mary, choose the good part and persistently do not let it be taken. To choose the good part means giving up other things and focusing. When you long, you focus.—From a Sunday leaders' meeting
That the Lord allowed obstacles and challenges means He poured out love. Because without passion and endurance, you cannot break through.

It is not because my will is weak but because I do not love the Lord. If you love the Lord, you must make time and kneel in prayer. Jesus told Peter to watch and pray so as not to enter into temptation. Without prayer, all eating, drinking, and breathing belong to the devil.
A simple way to become a leader: Determine to be all-weather faith that is not tempted in any circumstance. A leader has no off-season.
That I do not feel an answer does not mean God is not working. When I groan, it sounds like thunder to God. So you must keep praying.
The greatest work is bearing the cross.

* When people work hard but do not get the results they expected, they are disappointed and resentful. They thought that if they were sincere and did their best, everything would work out, but it does not. In life, there are things where even your best effort does not yield good results. But you need not fear or despair about this. There is One watching over me now, and there is One who collects my tears in a bottle and records them in His book. When you rely, you do not fear, for there is a human realm and God's realm. 'You have kept count of my tossings; put my tears in Your bottle. Are they not in Your book?'—Psalm 56:8. 'When I am afraid, I put my trust in You.'—Psalm 56:11

I rejected the Lord's calling multiple times until my teens, until one day I met the Lord and turned back. The reason I turned back was that though I am a sinner who crucified Jesus, He loved me and died on the cross for me. I understood how precious this was even at that young age. I could instinctively sense that there would be many challenges in holding to this truth. But in that moment of conversion, I confessed what I truly wanted: 'I will follow only the Lord.'

When I walk through a dry wilderness, when darkness covers me, what I will do is worship the Lord. 'Naked I came from my mother's womb, and naked shall I return. The Lord gave, and the Lord has taken away; blessed be the name of the Lord.'—Job 1:21

* Wrong rights, grateful consideration, benefits taken for granted are actually God's faithful care. Sometimes, in a place called wilderness, He withdraws that care for a time. The person that is 'I' often mistakes 'consideration for which I should be grateful' as 'a right I deserve.' I pray that God's grace overflows to all those who now think of God and endure hardship. Hardship is a reminder that draws us to God.
Hold firm to your faith. The Lord desires this, and ultimately the refining comes to an end, and at the end of that path stands the glorious Lord.

The most powerful life is a life without regret. Whatever happens, whatever tribulation comes, a life that seeks the Lord's glory has no regret. If what you seek is selfish desire, there will be regret and lingering attachment, but if it is the Lord's complete glory, it will suffice.

Not everything I wish for comes to pass. Nor should it. The Lord, who is higher and deeper than I, knows what I truly need. So when I feel 'I am lacking,' the Lord says 'You are sufficient.' When my opinion and the Lord's opinion differ, which is right? 'Jesus guides me on my way as I complete my course; in difficult times He gives me sufficient grace.'—Hymn 434

That the path ahead looks only dark means I have no option to choose. That is not something to be frustrated about but rather a sign that the Lord will guide me. Blessed is the person who is helpless and foolish and has no choice but to trust the Lord.
Why David was strong and recovered quickly can be understood from the Psalms. He constantly confessed and proclaimed to himself, to God, and to the world.
Look only to the Lord, who will comfort my hardened heart and raise me up again.
Many people are satisfied with claiming truth and agreeing with it. Not paying the price and not praying is denying the truth. For life ultimately denies it. A tree is known by its fruit.

In the final age, the posture of the final runner: Being awake, being sober-minded, and praying are the same thing. Love without prayer is a lie. '1 Peter 4:7-8 KJV: The end of all things is near; therefore be of sound judgment and sober spirit for the purpose of prayer. Above all, keep fervent in your love for one another, because love covers a multitude of sins.'

The most powerful faith manual: Simple power messages by each topic, indexing tens of thousands of insights that pierce through worries and difficulties in the world, equipping you with strength.—Faithbook by LFGC Youth
Give me a young person who loves the Lord. Then I will turn this world to the Lord.

* Living by faith: Do not cling to what benefits you, but steadily do what is right. Rather than finding work suited to your ability, seek ability suited to your calling. Worldly work is about investing little and gaining much, but heavenly work is about pouring everything out and looking only to the Lord. (John 1:48) 'Nathanael said to Him, 'How do You know me?' Jesus answered and said to him, 'Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.'

*When my daughters ask me in the distant future about living well, I don't think I will have much to show them. However, I don't think I will be ashamed. Since I have offered it all to the Lord, if nothing remains...,

* The present moment, right now is what matters. You must be happy now, must advance further now, and must walk together with Him now. And you can entrust everything else and move forward. Therefore, do not fear your destiny. It is enough to burn like a flame just living for today. After that, I'm not sure. All honor, glory, and authority belong to the Lord alone.
*Confess like this: 'God, I have collapsed but I will not give up. I will continue to fight. I will continue to seek Your face. I will continue to hold onto the cross.' You are a victor.
* Faith is like dominoes. When one falls, others fall consecutively. Conversely, when one succeeds, revival happens in others. Add the whip to the running horse. Keep going~~~
*Time. Looking back, everyone lacks time. Since time is short anyway, kneel before the Lord. You will experience God, who created time, filling it.

*Yuna Kim's jump. When you overlay Yuna Kim's practice performances and actual competitions, they come out almost identical. Because she practices one jump ten thousand times. We want to win in faith while failing in prayer. Please hold the rope of prayer. An unbroken rope creates unbroken victory in faith. The victory of a day is almost decided in the morning prayer time. Forget what came before and keep going without hesitation.

*Receiving and obeying the mission. God gave the widow of Zarephath the mission to provide for Elijah. Through that mission, she was robbed of her remaining provisions for herself and her son before dying. But that was not taken away; it was sown for the fruit of a great miracle. When you obey the mission, miracles happen.

If you are opposed in any fight, it means you must not give up but continue persistently. For Daniel and his friends, what their enemies really wanted to hinder was worship and prayer, which come from the fear of the Lord. The world is pleased when we live worldly. But when we live by the cross, it resists powerfully. Because revival breaks out.
*God's offering. God's offering is not loud or resentful. Like the silent Lord, it becomes a perfect offering. He did not make excuses.

*Human plans vs. God's guidance. Looking back, I feel that things were not done according to my plan but by His providence. No matter how difficult and dark the circumstances I face, I am in His hands and He will surely lead me to still waters. Even if blocked by guns and swords, the Lord does not give up, and if only He is with me, I tell my fate to come. I am not afraid.
Nevertheless, healing must continue, the cross must continue to be held, looking only to the Lord must continue, and the Word of the gospel must be witnessed without ceasing.

The purpose of the wilderness and the tabernacle is to prepare God's people. Preparation means seeking the Lord's face and becoming a people of obedience, not living by bread alone but by every word that comes from the Lord's mouth.

*In His Time. The Lord has His time and season. Only in His time is everything fully accomplished. The Lord's time is the moment when you acknowledge Jesus as Lord and surrender everything to Him. Then He makes it beautiful and accomplishes it. He has made everything beautiful in its time. Also, he has put eternity into man's heart, yet so that he cannot find out what God has done from the beginning to the end. Ephesians 3:11
One of my missions is to raise up people who cannot be satisfied with counterfeits or substitutes. To become Christians who, with that holy dissatisfaction, run continuously for Jesus alone.

More important than possessing more than others or living longer than others is how you use it and live. What the world calls blessing cannot necessarily be blessing, and conversely, what is called difficulty becomes a blessing that brings us closer to God.

Hearing and being moved is not enough. You must decide and kneel down. All genuine ministry comes from Jesus. It comes from intimacy with Him. That is everything. Even if you sacrifice all else, this is what you must hold onto...,

*What is marriage? It is the pursuit of happiness, stability, and romance. But this never comes automatically. When you first go to work or school, only the first time is thrilling. No worldly achievement automatically guarantees happiness or provides stability. And romance ends with married life. True romance is the fruit that begins for those who have overcome everything.

The silliest thing is to bowl once, score better than expected, think you're talented, and look down on others. Not knowing that day was your best day by accident and thinking romantically. Forget what you have already done and continue running toward the goal. 走馬加鞭 (Keep the whip on the running horse)

*How to overcome fear. The opposite of fear is not courage. It is companionship. When you walk with the Lord, fear will transform into a song. To Joshua, who was trembling without charisma, the Lord said 'meditate on the Word (faith)' and 'obey it (holiness).' Pursue peace and holiness with all people, without which no one will see the Lord—Hebrews 12:14. By faith Enoch was taken up so that he should not see death, and he was not found, because God had taken him. Now before he was taken he was commended as having pleased God—Hebrews 11:5

*When you love, you are not afraid and you overcome even fearful things. Ministry and love are synonymous. A minister must become a loving person. A loving person does not do work wrung out reluctantly from dryness. Rather, they come forth with bursting emotion and passion, and with God's tears, pour out blood for souls. Loving God and souls is the most powerful ministry. Simon, son of John, do you love me?... Lord, you know that I love you. Jesus said, Feed my sheep. John 15:17

*Time and life design. Life's priorities determine the quality of my life. Time is equal for everyone, but the content that fills that time is different for each person, and the results differ. When a person's affection changes, their quality of life changes.

*God's gift. We are not beings who pursue pleasure but beings who ask even small things of the Lord and receive His answers. Do not calculate for the situation you want; pray simply, be grateful for what is given, and live with joy. Then you will enjoy God's gift that cannot be compared with anything.
If I design my future with my own strength, it means I will squander all my assets. God does not show me His plans, but His plan for me is perfect, so investment given to God never fails.

If you despise the authority given to me, you will lose an absolutely large portion of what God has prepared for your life and will surely regret it. Because it is an act of denying the almighty Lord. Please respect those entrusted with the Word and share good things.
When I give up, the Lord accomplishes what I couldn't.

Paul called those with legalism and self-righteousness 'dogs' (Philippians 3:2). The simplest way to say this is: 'Sincerity moves heaven.' No matter how sincere, you cannot move God. Without faith it is impossible to please God, for whoever comes to God must believe that He exists and that He rewards those who seek Him—Hebrews 11:6

The 600,000 Israelites who saw ten plagues and great miracles complained and died in the wilderness. But the descendants who heard the Word and believed at Mount Gerizim and Mount Ebal entered Canaan.
Do not try to move God to fulfill my joy; rather, let the Lord's will become my joy. That is the secret to answering prayer and having a prosperous life.

Even if I crumble away unnoticed where I stand while honoring the Lord's name, I will not regret it. Because He knows me and I know Him. Vain glory fades, and if I can seek only His glory, that is my eternal inheritance and blessing, insufficient though I am.

It is God who blocks my path. Ultimately, it is also God who opens it. In fact, whether the path closes or opens is not so important. Because it doesn't matter whether you meet the one you love outside or inside. I pursue what Christ has grasped me to grasp. Because what matters now is that He is within me and I belong to Him.

When athlete Park Ju-young faced the Japanese team for Olympic bronze, he was not tired or wanting to rest on the bench. Because the Lord is with me, every event that happens to me is a love story. With only that One, that single Person, every interpretation becomes excellent.

When I seek to fulfill my wishes. Those who believe in God as a problem-solver pray faithfully, serve with dedication, but when the course of worldly affairs does not go as they intended, they will surely, in proportion to their desire to achieve their will, intensely doubt God's existence, guidance, and being itself. So he called that place Massah, or Meribah, because the children of Israel contended, or because they tested the Lord, saying, Is the Lord among us or not?—Exodus 17:7

Preaching the Word is a gift. Those who receive the gift do it excellently. Just as those gifted in performance excel at performing. But the Lord does not praise the person for preaching well. Because it is a gift. Ability and eloquent speech do not guarantee that one obeys the Word or that one is Lord when alone. What then is my reward? Just this: that in preaching the gospel I may offer it free of charge, and so not make full use of my right in the gospel—1 Corinthians 9:16–18

When eating kimchi stew, the complaint is always that so little meat is included. What if you put half kimchi and half meat? Why not? When I served it that way, everyone was happy. You must run when you can run. You must give when you can give. You must let go when you can let go. Let's go as far as we can.

To live by faith is to roar against reality that is disconnected from and opposes my vision and continue advancing. That reality is its own matter; I am moved by the heart the Lord has poured into me and persist.

I work diligently. After believing in Jesus, I have done all work faithfully. I come up with ideas, stay awake all night, fast, and do not know fatigue. I mean, those things are relatively easy for me. But the truly difficult, more demanded, and deeper life and ministry is 'waiting.' God causes us to exceed the time limit He has set, the limits of His patience. Then we set an extension. That too must be surrendered. Because true answers come only after passing through the limit of waiting. Many people try to fulfill the vision God gave them through worldly methods. It is because they cannot wait. It looks like success, but it is ultimately failure.

1. There are those who remain (leave to remain) and those who are eternal family (citizen). 2. Those who remain depart when their purpose is fulfilled, but family does not leave because love is their purpose. 3. Not loving Jesus to go to heaven, but because we love Him, we go to heaven too. So heaven without Jesus is not appealing to me either. 4. Those whose purpose is Jesus are not disappointed or broken. We are not beings who seek blessing so we can live a little better than others and boast about it. 5. Moses said to the Lord, 'If Your Presence does not go with us, do not send us up from here. How will anyone know that You are pleased with me and with Your people unless You go with us? What else will distinguish me and Your people from all the other people on the face of the earth?'—Exodus 33:15,16

Following the pillar of cloud is not chasing clouds. The exodus from Egypt and entry into Canaan are not speculation about a nice land. It is the true grace of God, the cross, wanting to be with sinners, and nailing Himself and me to the tree to make us one. So the journey of faith is not the achievement of any status or state, but training in holiness, presence, companionship, and fellowship.

A four-week journey to Canaan took forty years. Canaan, flowing with milk and honey, was not the purpose; God wanted to give Himself. A journey's speed is not about distance but about who walks with you. You gave them the lands of the nations, and they took possession of the fruit of the peoples' labor, that they might keep His statutes and observe His laws. Praise the Lord!—Psalm 105:44,45
Tomorrow at 6—A fiery Saturday gathering. Let's run.

1. All Satan can do is frighten. Because he was completely defeated at the cross. 2. He will try to hide this, but it will all be revealed before the truth. Be confident in the Lord. 3. In accomplishing good, Satan's hindrance is not an obstacle; rather, Satan's interference sometimes works together to accomplish good. 4. Become champions of faith who make Satan repent with gratitude and score own goals.

When praying, some cry out, 'What more do you want me to do?' But looking back, that time will also become the most grateful time. Just as the moment Joseph was sold to Egypt because of his brothers' jealousy became Joseph's most grateful time, this present moment of solitude is the most grateful time.

For those who cannot decide between A or B..., not reaching an answer despite long contemplation is not meaningless. Being able to pray about one problem for months is a blessing. How can a life decided by probability without such years of hardship be the same weight as a life decided through prayer with longing for an answer?

While comparing the lives of David and Saul for a leader's meeting, I came across 1 Chronicles. Though its content is similar to 2 Kings, it was written through Ezra after the return from exile with earnest self-reflection for the restoration of faith alongside the tradition of Judah. The key points are: ① Jerusalem is important. ② Co-workers are important. ③ Worship is important. ④ We must rely on God. What struck me personally was how, through David's foundational work in opening the kingship, it emphasized the historical circumstances of Judah's recovery for future generations. It was about essence, not technique. Before any operational strategy, what matters is living in dependence on the Lord alone.

My prayer is not to be recognized, to succeed, and to accomplish my will. What I earnestly ask for strength is to complete my race that the Lord has granted me and to run as a minister who does not hinder the Lord's revival.

Please entrust everything and go forward. Even if it is regrettable and distressing, even if my plans do not come to fruition, please entrust them to the Lord and go. Then you have done well. Then you have truly done well. If the Lord says that is enough, that is blessing.

1. A Christian living a powerless faith may not experience answered prayer. 2. The Bible says the devil surrenders, but such things rarely seem to happen in reality. 3. Why doesn't the devil surrender? Surrender happens on the battlefield (in the field), not at the desk. 4. Because we do not go to the battlefield, there is no surrender and no power manifests. Just as storing weapons in a warehouse and checking them daily accomplishes nothing, 5. Please do what the Lord wants where the Lord wants. Then the Lord will follow with signs that will surely confirm His Word. Do not become an eloquent expert but become champions who ignite life. Lord, in Your name even the demons submit to us—Luke 10:17. When the apostles went out and preached everywhere, the Lord worked with them, and confirmed His word by the signs that accompanied it—Mark 16:20. Peter and John, speaking boldly in the strength of the Lord, while God did great wonders and signs through the apostles... to give witness to the word of His grace—Acts 14:3

When you feel others are moving faster than you, think of God who rescues you at every moment of life. Do not be troubled that others have what you lack; rather, grieve that you cannot live more for the Lord. At least David, fleeing from his own son, had such pain. Blessed are those whose strength is in You, whose hearts are set on pilgrimage. When they pass through the Valley of Baka, they make it a place of springs—Psalm 84:5
Whether in distress or in joy, I will follow only the Lord; who can stop me? Not even death. Hymn 355

If Moses had built the Tabernacle at age 40, he probably would have reinterpreted God's commands and specifications to make it magnificent. But that would not be the Tabernacle—merely an expensive unsold tent. After the wilderness refinement, Moses obeyed the Lord unconditionally. Listening is power. In Exodus 39 and 40, the phrase 'as the Lord commanded' is repeated 17 times.

When you obey with a dry stick, it becomes God's staff. 'Moses took the staff of God in his hand' (Exodus 4:20). Do not view the things you lack now—career, marriage, academics, achievement—as deficiency. Rather than comparing and becoming miserable, God grants you time to worship, serve, and prepare. A wise woodcutter rests while sharpening his axe. In any case, without the Lord giving, you have nothing. Even when He gives, He gives in His time. When deficiency becomes power, God's glory is revealed. That is the power of interpretation. I pray you gain the Lord's perspective.

Even in the most urgent and pressing situations, you must hear the Lord's voice and hold fast to His Word. Only by maintaining peace in the most confusing circumstances can you respond to the Lord's gentle voice.

There are times when God permits difficult problems beyond my capacity. When all methods and paths are blocked, what God originally desired is for you to weep and fall on your face, clinging to the cross.

You will certainly be disciplined and refined. If not, it means you are not being loved. The Lord will certainly intervene in my rationalizations. And He will not compromise or give up until you obey. Though it may be difficult and tear-filled now, later it will become faith like gold.

Do you labor with joy? If it takes more than two seconds to answer 'yes' to this question, a red light is on. Joy is not calculation or coercion, but obedience as a response before the condition of the Lord's sovereignty. To act in faith means to act in joy. If it was not my choice, obedience suffices. Because He is deeper and higher than me.

Before prayer, there seems to be no path. But when you cry out, even a path that doesn't exist in the sea becomes visible. The ability to see process rather than parts comes after refinement and prayer.
I will not stop. I will not give up. I will not look back. Even if I fall, I will rise again. I will never be defeated; I will be victorious. Because the Lord has shown me His glory.

It is truly precious when a dignified person sweeps with a broom. Yet a person whose profession is cleaning in the world also cleans in the church. No one looks back or gives credit. Such a person is more precious. Often there are precious people excluded from praise that goes unnoticed in the world.
If I cannot fill my time with holiness, the power of sin will dominate. When I serve the Lord with first things, the Lord fills later things as well.
If a marathoner does not run the track with all their strength and still has energy left after the finish line, that is sin.

I do not rejoice when circumstances are favorable. Nor do I become discouraged when circumstances are unfavorable. Because the Lord has prepared and governs all things. My only desire is to be pleasing to the Lord.

'O great mountain, who are you before Zerubbabel? You shall become a plain; and he shall bring forward the top stone amid shouts of 'Grace, grace to it!'' (Zechariah 4:7). To make a great mountain into a plain, humans instinctively dig. That is man's way. But God works by Himself. Not by might nor by power, but by His Spirit. 'And he answered and spoke to me, saying, This is the word of the Lord to Zerubbabel: Not by might, nor by power, but by My Spirit, says the Lord of hosts' (Zechariah 4:6).

There are times when frustration comes like running a race with hands bound behind your back. Spending time this way is a problem for me, but it seems the Lord would suffer great loss. Yet you must enjoy and delight in that time. Sometimes the Lord binds you so He can work quietly. When you cannot move, rejoice only in His presence.

When Abraham was asked to give up his only son Isaac, he fell into a paradoxical dilemma. His actions were two-fold: 1. To obey, and 2. Belief that even in death, God would restore life. That reckless obedience toppled idols and brought blessings beyond calculation.
Sometimes God permits trials that make no sense. It is when love has become an idol. Faithful obedience overcomes the test.

Among David's many achievements, the greatest was not what he accomplished but what he did not build—the Temple. He had the ability, will, justification, and even passion to do everything, but his true blessing was not doing what God told him not to do. Do not cling to justification and desires; recognize the Lord's sovereignty. Active passivity—advancing with passion but knowing that the Lord accomplishes it, thereby entrusting at the decisive moment.
Not my strength, not my ability, not my zeal... 'Therefore a remnant will go out from Jerusalem, and survivors from Mount Zion. The zeal of the Lord Almighty will accomplish this' (Isaiah 37:32 KJV).
Unless you are crazy, you cannot reach.

The difference between a bouquet and a potted plant is that a bouquet changes with environment and time, but a potted plant changes through its roots. A life rooted in God may not be brilliant immediately, but it is the strongest life.
To not fear the future, decide the future now. The surest way to determine the future is to turn only to the Lord who knows my way.
Any society that would give up a little liberty to gain a little security will deserve neither and lose both. - Benjamin Franklin
Many seek to possess much. In this world, people believe ownership and accumulation are power. But true power is actually the ability to let go. One who can let go without regret is the strongest.
I pray that brothers and sisters who are working and studying according to the Lord's call in unbelief and barren land would receive strength and wisdom...
When I set all eyes on the Lord and feel the Almighty God, I experience God's work.

There is glory in sainthood when prayer so overwhelms you that you find yourself in a restroom crying, weeping and crying out to the Lord. A person who has no choice but to call on the Lord regardless of time or place, a person who has no choice but to rely on Him—that person is glorious and strongest. When your eyes are set on the Lord and your heart becomes tender, you taste the sweetness of His presence.

There are tears you can only shed after failing and being rejected. These tears flow inward rather than outward, making it difficult for others to partake. Though tears have similar composition, the reason and meaning differ. In these tears, I am broken, see my limits, and finally see the Lord. God's hammer that breaks my shell seems terrifying, painful, and sad, but after refinement it produces fruit of righteousness and peace. 'No discipline seems pleasant at the time, but painful. Later, however, it produces a harvest of righteousness and peace for those who have been trained by it' (Hebrews 12:11).

In the wilderness there is nothing extra. One robe, one pair of sandals, even daily bread—you must trust in faith for the manna that falls daily. It is grace that without God's help, even one day's basic survival is not granted. Without the Lord, you will do only things worthy of ruin.

Opportunity will surely come. If you waste time before it arrives, when it finally comes you will be unprepared, missing a golden opportunity. Prepare as if today were your last, readying yourself for that moment throughout your lifetime.

A performer on stage is extremely nervous. They practice a piece they have played many times hundreds of times. But the audience is relaxed, simply enjoying. After the performance ends, the performer receives applause, flowers, and glory, but the spectators do not. Exceptionally, there are audience members who worry along with the performer and rejoice when it ends—loved ones like family. When you love, you share hardship and glory without envy.

When you do not act on emotion, are not hasty, endure patiently, maintain focus to the end and wait, opportunity will surely come. Quietly preparing your whole life for that opportunity is called skill.
Even when my chest is heavy and my heart aches, I continue to look to and trust in God because God does not make mistakes. I entrust all this to the Lord. Only the Lord rules over me.

Three years ago when I returned after a long time and began ministry again, I saw within me a heart I did not begin. It was not my nature but a heart the Lord gave me. It was 'love.' The author of that heart was God. It is grace that a sinner can embrace God's heart, and by that boundless grace I can run today.
I believe and rely because there is a Lord who knows all my circumstances. Thus preceding faith is knowing the Lord. Then naturally comes trust.
Just take the only things God allows and do not care others.

Everyone lives different lives, but if one truly believes that the blood of Jesus saved me from death, that weight of grace is the same for me and for others. If the motivation and starting point of our faith and calling is not me but Him, then rather than asking 'Must I really go this far?' I must ask 'What other path would I take besides this?' 'Better is one day in your courts than a thousand elsewhere; I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God than dwell in the tents of the wicked. For the Lord God is a sun and shield; the Lord bestows favor and honor; no good thing does he withhold from those whose walk is blameless' (Psalm 84).
