* What I Recommend to Sinners: To those who repeatedly fall into empty sin never satisfied, I recommend Jesus, the eternal joy. The only higher absolute peace that can break sin's chains.
* Second Law of Spiritual Dynamics: We are most at peace when we humble ourselves before the Lord.

* The Anxiety of Seeking Recognition; The Joy of Being Used: If you seek recognition, you will be fearful and pursued. But if you are satisfied with being used by Him, you will be at peace and complete. What matters is not the size of the role or its results, but Him who uses me. Tell the Lord He will use you.—Matthew 21:3
* Be Courageous: Not that the circumstances are fine, but that 'God is with you.'

* The Reason for Disappointment: What I thought existed no longer exists. But it was never mine to begin with. When I know the Lord's greatness and realize my smallness, I escape disappointment and dwell unafraid.
* Change: Change is frightening and difficult, but when viewed from a distance, regardless of surface results, it is always valuable.
* Have Courage: The precious co-worker and mentor, training and service given to me are permitted by God. The Lord's guidance is without error, and His intention toward me is peace.

When you look to people, you are always being chased. The problem is not being chased but always making bad moves in that situation. If you would only be silent, you would see God's work. But anxiety makes you speak and act wrongly. Look only to the Lord. Then you will be able to be still.

There is no fear in love, and perfect love casts out fear, for fear has to do with punishment. Those who fear have not been made perfect in love. - 1 John 4:18. If you are confident in God's love, you will not fear. Fear is not optional because it is a matter of punishment. It must be banished. By the Lord's love.
If there is sorrow in your heart because you have not been loved, there will be few happy things in your life. Love is given by the Lord, and I love and serve with that love.

* One thing that must be clear: The Lord is the ruler. Seek His kingship—His kingdom, His righteousness—intimacy with the Lord. Those who seek these will have God's peace, while those who seek self-determined decisions and friendship with the world will experience fear.
* How to entrust your worries: Knowing who God is and confirming the Lord before your reality at every moment.

* The harbor of hope and safe place: Relying on what is not believable brings only anxiety. When you place your security in Him and trust in the Lord's work, there is true peace. A peace that can never be taken away.
To love means that one's mere existence becomes a joy.

* When the wind blows, stand on the hill. How wonderful it would be if the wind stopped just because we hide! The Lord tells us to be courageous 366 times in the Bible, every moment. Stand boldly before the fierce, cutting wind that seems to blow you away. Then you will come to know that the Lord is with you. Jesus awoke and rebuked the wind, saying to the sea, 'Peace! Be still!' And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm. - Mark 4:39

To quote what the Lord said in the Sermon on the Mount, those who make communities peaceful are people who live well (blessed people), while those who cause discord and division are failures who do not live well.

* It is not by might, nor by power, but when my strength and will do not work out as I desire, that becomes discouragement. Rather, the greatest consolation and hope come from allowing the Holy Spirit to work it out. Those who seek to accomplish their own will become obsessed, but those who look to the Almighty's grace will dwell in peace.
Do not imagine in anger; instead, have peace in truth. It is not that thing itself, but it is simply what the Lord's providence has permitted for me.

Do not imagine. Imagination is what unbelievers do. Imagination makes us violent. We all get hit by poisoned arrows daily. Do not pull out the arrow and search for the culprit; rather, apply medicine first, detoxify, and bandage the wound. Come to the Lord. Listen to what God says. Do what God desires. Practice obedience to the Lord's voice more than rational calculation. If the Lord says to be angry and seek vengeance, then do so. If not, obey and forgive. The reason I cannot speak of unfairness is because I fear myself. Have gentleness and the eyes to see the whole picture. Then you will be able to state things peacefully. Those who know the end have leisure and peace. There is no complaint or anger without reason. It is too reasonable. But reconsider whether it fulfills 'God's righteousness.' The shortcomings others display are what the Lord shows me for myself. Pray equally for others and for myself, and we will move forward. (Romans 1) The Lord places Saul before me to deal with the Saul within me. Respond with escape, forgiveness, and prayer. Do not resist. The Lord calls us for miracles. Respond in faith and you will see the miracle of transformation. The older I get, the more I realize that everything I thought was right was wrong. I have changed greatly and lived with many misunderstandings. When understanding comes, I can let go. Discern the identity of the enemy. When revealed, it flees. Bring it into the light. Why does persuasion fail and meet rejection? Because we do not pray and ask. Even I cannot treat lightly an agenda that someone has prayed much over and brought forward, because it feels like opposing God. Do not be stingy in expressing love. Especially Gyeongsang people. Not misunderstanding is the highest solution to blocking the enemy's spear. Move forward with a spirit of opposition plus relinquishing rights. You will be victorious. When dealing with an inconsiderate person, look at yourself. Am I myself not without decorum before the Lord? Relationships require patience and continuity. Above all, we must depend on the Lord. The Lord is the master in relationships too. Do not become an unbeliever without faith, but become a believer. There are those who sulk if things are not done their way, and such people are unbelievers. Believe that the Lord is omniscient and omnipotent, and can make the descendants of Abraham through the person you have set over you. David was able to control his reactions because he believed in the living God. Do you live without burden and without lingering resentment? Without burden and lingering resentment, you will perish. Regard others as better than yourself. And relinquish your share. The Lord will repay. Loyalty is important, but if you are loyal without knowing God's secret, you cause ruin. More important than gathering and knowing much is seeing God. That is purity of heart. The greatest crisis in life is when Jesus is not enough. Do not envy those who are doing well in the world. Good packaging does not mean good contents. When you have Jesus, you have the highest value. Do not be too absorbed in gold, silver, and jewels. They are merely paving material and interior decoration for heaven's road. Do not carry too large a brick in your ears.

The Lord touches the broken heart. In the end, it is only He who touches the broken heart. No matter how much humans create atmosphere, match appearances, and arrange words, ultimately healing and restoration occur before His throne. There is no need to be impatient about the Lord's timing, nor any reason to worry about His method. Trust the Lord and look to the Lord.

There is no need to grieve when circumstances and conditions are not favorable. Even if people who believe in Jesus face drought in life, there is no hindrance whatsoever. Why? Because we are 'a tree planted by the stream.'

The most amazing way to reverse situations immediately and truly control the game of life is 'gratitude.' Because God's blessing and rule already come upon the grateful soul. Are you grateful? Rejoice and be glad. It means the Lord's will is being executed.
Do not judge according to circumstances and despair, but obey as the Lord leads and seek peace. Do not look at circumstances but interpret them by faith.

Life moves and expresses itself. The Lord's love is life. That abundant love is limited within us. Unless we give Him freedom to express it, the Lord has ordained to remain limited in us. Rest and enjoyment are the beginning of bearing fruit. The Lord does this.

* You cannot have peace if you avoid. There is a life lived by greed and a life lived by grace. If you live with a self-centered ego, when storms come, you collapse severely and fall into ruin. Why? Because although we desire peace, we are prescribed imitation products and painkillers provided by a world that avoids and escapes when there is wind and rain. But the peace that Jesus gives is fundamentally different from the peace the world gives. 'Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid.' - John 14:27
* Jesus does not cling. Clinging comes when fearful and anxious. Jesus loves without clinging. Because He loves not my outward appearance and image, but my heart.

* It is not to avoid but to overcome. Storms and rough seas are not to be avoided but overcome. There is no life without turbulence. It is not the intensity and height of the rough waves that matter, but the grace that overwhelms those winds. When you believe in the Lord's sufficient grace, you will not be shaken by each trial that comes, but will overcome abundantly. 'My soul is still before God alone; my salvation comes from him. He alone is my rock and my salvation; he is my fortress, I will not be shaken. My soul yearns for God, for the living God. When can I go and meet with God? My tears have been my food day and night. I say to them, 'Where is your God?' These things I remember as I pour out my soul: how I used to go with the multitude, leading the procession to the house of God. He alone is my rock and my salvation; he is my fortress, I will not be shaken.' - Psalm 62

* Do not worry about anything, do not worry about anything, do not worry about anything, do not worry about anything. Rather, in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus. - Philippians 4:6-7

* Are you at peace? You must be at peace. Did Jesus not take up the cross? Whether the path I take all my life is smooth like a calm river or frightful and difficult with great storms, my soul is always at ease. Though the devil comes rushing with mouth open to devour us, Jesus is our commander, and we will fight and win in the end. - Hymn 413

* When you embrace true well-being ambitions, anxiety and fear arise. This causes you to struggle and live a worldly life. But thanksgiving brings peace and rest, and blessing allows you to live a heavenly life.

* Chain reaction: Gratitude comes from a contented heart. If you are grateful, you will not quarrel and will have peace and abundance. So if you are content, grateful, and at peace, you prove that you are in Him who gives you strength. 'I can do all this through him who gives me strength.' - Philippians 4:13

No one wants to get married just to do spring cleaning and garden maintenance. These actions follow as a result of love. I hope you will never work without grace. Whatever you do, if you walk with Him, you will be happy.

Disappointment and grumbling are very difficult subjects. If we could just pass these two subjects well, world evangelization would have been accelerated by about 1000 years. Let us believe in God and love souls. Then at least we will not be hurt.
Do not worry for success; pray to fulfill the Lord's will. The Lord will keep you in peace.
* Heart! Overcome. Ask yourself. Are you afraid? If you are afraid, you must overcome. If you are not afraid, you have already overcome.

People of the world make choices by avoiding challenges and fears and preferring personal interests. But people of faith stand in the place where the Lord says to stand, despite disadvantages, where He considers it right. Those who resolve to die have nothing to fear. Yet we are already people who died in the Lord and live as new creations. Those who cross the line of death are not just bold but at peace.
The world's challenges and darkness of darkness will fade with time. Because they are not truth, they have no lasting power. But the Lord's love is the same yesterday, today, and forever.

If you seek peace while setting your heart on the world, that is greed. When life is complicated and chaotic, it is impossible for prayer to come forth with eyes closed. Choose one. Either choose the world and live in confusion, or look to the Lord, receive power, and live.

Powerful people in the world are weak people. Those outside the gospel are very weak. They worry terribly about car accidents, cancer, and their families. The gospel is a lifelong special contract that covers both this age and the age to come.

Shame is a feeling only humans experience. A dog can be happy, angry, sad, and joyful, but it does not feel embarrassed. Because shame came as a result of sin. Those who experience shame become violent or powerless to the point of giving up life, suffering internal wounds. Only the Lamb has the power to remove shame and instead make us dance in a crown. 'By the blood of Jesus, all my shame has disappeared.' - Hymn 282
A student who has completed homework and studied perfectly for an exam is not just at peace but bold and delighted. Those who achieve their purpose are bold. So those who love are bold.

Trials and challenges will not stop. And they have never been easy. Yet we do not fear. Is one who has already died to the cross going to cling to anything more? Even if everything is taken away, truth lives on and that kingdom will be eternal. Hope is power.
* Free from worry. Only Jesus can touch my heart. It is not a problem to worry about but a problem to be touched. So now let go and be at peace. The Lord is in control.

Believers must be flexible. There is nothing worth risking life for except the Lord, and all things work together for good in Him. If you get excited easily, you will be embarrassed later. The Almighty Father is able to reach many beautiful conclusions without my anger.
If you can overcome disappointing situations and endure criticism and mockery for the sake of hope, you can receive the grace the Lord wants to give.

One with a steadfast heart and one who trusts the Lord are the same person. Every minute and second, those who give and entrust their hearts to the Lord are upheld by Him and kept by His peace. 'You will keep in perfect peace those whose minds are steadfast, because they trust in you.' - Isaiah 26:3
The cross, the cross, beneath its shadow, there is life.
* If there is no peace... it is not the circumstances or people that are the problem; it is that I am not giving thanks. Peace in life and a hellish existence are divided by gratitude alone.

The cross takes precedence over all emotions and precedes all reactions. The cross is the Lord's only and eternal conclusion. Those who live by the cross and die by the cross are free from pain and anxiety, and are truly happy. The cross is not a joke. It is more everything than I ever thought I was everything. Before the cross, all desires are rejected and all heart's requests are removed from priority. The Almighty Holy One's 'I cannot bear to give you up' thrills my heart so I cannot sleep. 'No matter what I do, I will save you.' Before that cross, petty pride, vain human expectations, and rotten recognition all become filth. When I hold that low, rough cross, I become higher; the power of sin cannot reach it and retreats.
The cross does not exempt us from life's storms. Rather, it makes us walk on storms.
Seeing the Lord's beautiful face, my head bows before You. The Lord's hand touches me and comforts my body and soul.
No matter how dark circumstances appear or how critical the environment seems, we move toward the goal. Never stopping, with the power to ignore completely, we love and humble ourselves. ♡♡♡♡
Never being completely overcome is because the Lord holds us with His strong right arm and raises us up. Never being completely overcome is... Psalm 37

* The most peaceful prayer: If filling deficiency would be convenient, another anxiety would ensue. The most peaceful prayer is this: Regarding all things that were beneficial to me as harmful and as filth. Temperance is power.
Rest is the power of restoration. And the qualification to enjoy rest is also power. Rest does not come by circumstances but only by faith. By faith alone.

God's sight and touch remain on the weak. Do not boast of what is perishable; instead, boast in weakness. Then Christ Himself will visit and dwell with you. 'He said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.' Therefore I will boast all the more gladly about my weaknesses, so that Christ's power may rest on me.' - 2 Corinthians 12:9

* Absolute peace: Paul conveys three joys to the Philippians from prison. And he urges them to resist the three opponents of that joy: false love, legalism, and worldly anxiety. If you truly want peace, embrace the mind of Christ, regard what was beneficial as filth, and present everything to God with thanksgiving. That is the theme of Philippians.

* Absolute anxiety: For those who distance themselves from God, are not in worship and the place of grace, and forget Jesus' cross work, peace is not even worth half a farthing. Those without Jesus in their hearts, filled with the world and people, must necessarily be anxious. They must experience panic that shakes them to the roots. Unless there is even a carob pod, the prodigal will not seek home. I pray earnestly that such people will never sleep stretched out and comfortable.
No matter what disappointment and despair come and how much time passes with the world's realistic difficulties, 'the power of the blood that sets free from sin' is 'an eternal unchanging truth.'

There are times when we encounter horrible, hellish situations. It would never be, but if the Lord takes us to such a place, while He permits, we will find a guitar, form a worship gathering, and worship the Lord. And after the meeting, since there is much sulfur fire there, it should not be difficult to provide food. Remember that the Lord's grace is sufficient, He gives what I can bear, and it is clear what I must do wherever I am and whatever I must do.
No matter what problem or challenge comes, there is no one to look to but Jesus. I hope you will look beyond problems and conditions to the living Jesus.

Though awkward, when a meeting and fellowship maintain courtesy toward the other person and are conducted truthfully before the Lord without shame, even after parting, no regret remains. When years pass and we unexpectedly meet again somewhere, in some place, rather than my heart racing again, the path I must take becomes clearer.
Things may seem fine yesterday, but they can fall apart today. Yet do not be dismayed. The righteous live by faith, not by circumstances or evaluations.
How can we not rejoice? That we have the Lord to hope in...
How grateful we should be? That we need not rely on ourselves.
Peace does not come by itself, nor is it given when conditions permit. Peace is the spoils of sincere 'spiritual warfare' and the fruit of prioritizing Jesus alone.
A woman's heart breaks easily. It is because she is precious.

The Lord was with Joseph, so he became a prosperous man in the house of his Egyptian master - Genesis 39:2. Joseph's state was that of a slave. Yet Scripture calls him prosperous. Though betrayed, hated, and having lost his position and much else, he is called a prosperous man. Rather, Joseph's brothers who sold him, though free and seemingly wanting for nothing, became unprosperous. The reason for Joseph's prosperity was God's presence with him. Immanuel itself is a blessing. When God is with us, our tribulations and storms are not exempted; rather, we experience the living presence of the Lord within them.

The Lord is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in him, and he helps me. My heart leaps for joy, and with my song I praise him. - Psalm 28:7. David had gold and silver to rely on, people and power. Yet he relied only on the Lord. There can be no other strange power for those whose God is God. Though the world recommends ever newer and stranger things, there is no other name under heaven given to mankind by which we must be saved but Jesus. Do you love Jesus? Then there is nothing more to seek.

Do not worry. Do not worry, but rather bring your requests to God in prayer and petition with thanksgiving. This is what truth demands. My conviction and dedication alone are insufficient. That is why in Acts 1, the Lord spoke thus: 'But when the Holy Spirit comes upon you...'
Those who believe in Jesus are not hasty. If it is not the Lord's will, they deny even their own emotions and will, and rejoice that the desires of the flesh have been destroyed.
Those who believe in Jesus can be somewhat lonely. Because even in conversation, their focus is on the Lord.

If you are content with Jesus alone, ease comes and fear retreats. In Jesus, all evaluations will be reinterpreted. Life becomes full and lacking in nothing. Those captivated by Jesus are the most beautiful, and He will vindicate such people.

* Why you cannot rejoice: Do not complain about what you lack; give thanks for what you already have and your daily bread. Receive joy as a gift, and other things will be added to you. Give us this day our daily bread... - The Lord's Prayer

Jesus' method of dealing with our fears was not to remove the cause of fear. The Lord says, 'It is I; do not be afraid.' The storm and waves remain, but the Lord is there. When the Lord is present, we become courageous, and the waves naturally calm.

Jesus promised to give a peace unlike the peace the world gives. It is absolute peace. Even when rough waves rage, it is the peace given by the Absolute Sovereign, regardless of the height and depth of those waves. When my thoughts are many within, the Lord upholds me. 'When anxiety was great within me, your consolation brought me joy.' - Psalm 94:19
Jesus does not forcibly create storms. And He will never let us enter them without giving us power to overcome.

Jesus said that apart from Him we can do nothing. So what is there to be disappointed about if we can do nothing? Pride in relying on ourselves and others brings disappointment. The humble are never disappointed.

* Pass through the night this way. You must pray that Jesus becomes light in your darkness. That darkness covers you means you have no other option, and it means the Lord will become your light. When you pass through darkness, the Lord will be your light.
To believe in Jesus is to be at peace. Because we look only to Jesus. Honestly, the word 'believe' is not easily spoken. It means He monopolizes me.
A life focused solely on honoring Christ is at peace. Living and dying become less of an issue because we are overwhelmed by more important values.

Only the Lord upholds me. When I stumble, He still upholds me. If your law had not been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction. - Psalm 119:92. This word is comfort in my trouble: Your promise has preserved my life. - Psalm 119:50

* Anxiety: Am I aging like this? Will I become a loser and failure in life? Don't I need to take some action? Worrying about becoming what you don't want to be—that is anxiety. Anxiety is natural for pursuing desires, but those living out the Lord's will are courageous and at peace because He accomplishes it.

The left-handed judge Othniel killed King Eglon and saved Israel, and the land had peace for forty years. The resolution of life and peace in relationships come from the Lord. A nation, people, or individual becomes peaceful and redeemed when they act in faith.
Desire + Anxiety = Attachment. Purpose + Passion = Focus. Life - Faith = Worry. Life + Faith = Hope. Two Lives = Worry + Hope (Life is either hoping or worrying).

* Why you are troubled: Simply put, you are troubled because you try to decide matters beyond your authority. Your future is not for you to decide, nor can you change people's hearts. If you look to the Lord, who is not limited by circumstances and people, there will be no frustration or regret. Though a man plans his course, it is the Lord who directs his steps. - Proverbs 16:9
We need not be troubled in heart because the Almighty God dwells among us as Immanuel. Since God declared, 'Believe in me, believe in Jesus,' we can come before His throne without any fear.

There is nothing effective we can do about the past we have gone through. When we try to do something with the past, it only gets worse. The only option for the believer facing the past is 'forgiveness.'

We cannot arbitrarily second-guess or reject God's method in forming us. However, we can choose our heart and attitude toward God in that situation. Do not try to do everything; do what you can. What more do you need to worry about?
Cry out to your destiny: Destiny, come! I do not fear, and I entrust all things to the Lord.
Stop complaining. Even if you spent your whole life giving thanks for every moment and expression, it would not be enough. What I have been given is the best thing.

Though the enemy's schemes exist, challenges are fierce, darkness covers and nothing is visible, you are cramped, there is nothing wrong with walking forward. This is because from the beginning you rejected peace and comfort. To believe in Jesus means being saved from other relative purposes, reputation, and success. If you can plant a cross on that land and pray for the Holy Spirit to come... whether you live or die matters little. According to my earnest expectation and hope, that I will not be ashamed, but with all boldness, as always, so now also Christ will be magnified in my body, whether by life or by death. For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain. - Philippians 1:20-21

* How to overcome evil with good: Those who take God's Word as light and lamp are characterized by peace and boldness. They work by the Lord's standard and leave the rest to God regardless of results. It means not using the world's methods but fighting in an entirely different way. It means conquering with love and forgiveness. And it is certain that you always conquer.

* The strongest recovery: God's Word must flow. It must become an overflowing word. When God's Word flows through my life, I am sufficiently happy without filling anything else. Reconciliation in relationships and fulfillment of mission happen naturally. Those who accomplish the Lord's will experience the strongest recovery. I do not accomplish my mission; my mission accomplishes me.

Despair comes when you look only at circumstances and calculate them. The worst way to calculate circumstances is by comparing with others. When you lose faith, situations are always exaggerated and comparisons always end in misery.
When you understand meaning, you can endure. Rather, you can rejoice. Meaning gives the power to love. Love bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
There is no accident in this world. It happens under the Lord's permission. When you think within the Lord's will, all things become opportunities for us.

I saw a world chef on his day off visiting marginalized and shadowed people and cooking food for them with hands, experiencing great joy and rest. Being a joy to those who truly need you, apart from those who evaluate and price yourself, is also good rest. What is truly difficult is doing nothing without meaning. Rest is not doing nothing but being filled with grace and meaning.

In human affairs, there are always misunderstandings. Among them, malicious rumors are hard to bear. Such false rumors disappear within months. If I just do not dig them up and explain... Why? Because Jesus was not crucified because of such false rumors. Jesus' cross was for me, who am infinitely more evil than such malicious rumors.
Even if you remain alone in loneliness all your life, if you can live for the Lord, that is a spirit beyond endurance and an immense blessing.

Work feels frightening not because its size is bigger than you, but because you do not feel God's presence with you. Those for whom God is their recourse before impossible tasks do not step back. Fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my righteous right hand. - Isaiah 41:10

It is our nature to protect ourselves. The more we accomplish and prepare, the stronger this nature becomes. When we have much to hold, desires arise. But what we must know is that 'I cannot protect myself.' Only when the Lord guards me is not 'a hair' harmed. It does not mean life is preserved by having insurance and tapping the bridge carefully. You must trust the Lord.

If you lived not for yourself but sought only the Lord completely, it is fine to hear criticism. Rather than sinning and being criticized, if you have given yourself completely to what must be done, there is no regret regardless of results and response.

Those who see themselves through their own eyes are choked by people and the world. It means they cling and fuss. They are anxious about their inadequacy and are being chased. But those who dwell in the Lord fear nothing. Where you place your gaze determines all success and failure. Truly I have calmed and quieted myself, I am like a weaned child with its mother; like a weaned child I am content. - Psalm 131:2
You should not think of matters beyond your share. It is not your responsibility and only brings bone-eating worry. When you acknowledge the Almighty's sovereignty, you will have peace.

Do not be disappointed that your expectations and hopes are not fulfilled. Rather, rejoice that the Lord's sovereignty is acknowledged. I am not the master; the Lord is the master and takes responsibility.
If you pursue your own desires, you will be hasty and anxious, but if you seek the Lord's will, you will be peaceful and courageous.
Prayer to accomplish the Lord's will rather than fulfill your own desires is the fastest and most peaceful path.

No matter how plausible and persuasive your judgment and thoughts may be, the conclusion is that the Lord has chosen me. The Lord's conclusion will overshadow all evaluations and attacks. Be courageous.
Even if you are not recognized by others, you are still precious. Therefore, there is no need to prove yourself or seek confirmation. Recognition of your worth comes only from God.

Those who cling to themselves greatly fear rejection, broken relationships, and loneliness. From the moment we are born into this world, almost nothing happens according to our will. What must be clear is this: God holds me, and He always gives me the very best. When you know and believe this, forced effort disappears and you become free from attachment. Of course, your life will flourish.
Problems you have overcome and laid before the Lord—the Lord will not bring those difficulties upon you again. Just as you don't retake a course you've already passed.
Those with low self-esteem are happy when things go well and depressed when they don't. Unable to find happiness in their existence, they try to fill the void with numbers. Our identity is Jesus.

Did you rest well? I too have left behind the training sessions where I only slept two hours, and yesterday I finally rested with my normal sleep pattern. Now, let's run toward better things, higher things. Here we go~, One way, Jesus + a cup of living water ^^// Youth group continues.

All animals that cannot lie down when tired are herbivores. In contrast, powerful predators have leisure. Become spiritually strong. Then you will have peace. We are not predators, but like Psalm 23 says, with God as our Shepherd, we experience lying down in green pastures. Notice that the Lord makes the sheep lie down. The Lord's rest is gracious, and sometimes even semi-forceful. Get sick and see—you will have days when you lie still with no choice but to rest.
When you sin, the whole world seems filled with darkness. But when you repent, the Lord shines again.

When you try hard to do well, you become exhausted. Relationships, ministry, work—everything becomes stress. God did not call us to do everything well and satisfy everyone. He said that doing one or two things, or even just one thing, is enough. To do that one thing the Lord called you to without greed is to truly do well. Rushing too quickly and trying to gain everything is greed. Miracles happen when we let go of greed.
Be still and look only to the Lord. In complete silence...
It is said that when you sow with tears, you reap with joy. But when you sow with beautiful people, tears become pearls and labor becomes delight. When we are together, fear disappears.
If you want to be happy at all, the most important thing is to look at one essential thing in the same direction. The same truth, the same Lord, Jesus Christ.
Yesterday's fear becomes my prayer, and a deep sigh becomes my song, because at the foot of the cross I have laid down all my burdens. Hallelujah~♥

Despair is the process toward rest. The greater the despair, the greater the scope of the Lord's work. Despair is seeing your true state and nature; rest is seeing the Lord's righteousness, salvation, grace, and mercy.
Such hopeful words: 'The Lord reigns!' Therefore, you need not worry.
It's truly wonderful, beyond words... looking to the Lord... I am truly happy.
The most frustrating thing is when we cannot reach further because of Jesus. Those of faith need not fear anything except the absence of Jesus.

Because of impatience and anxiety, I had to grab my brother's heel and deceive my father to steal his blessing—a life filled only with suffering. When I lay down my attempts to make myself happy and my efforts to defend and satisfy myself, true obedience is fulfilled. Those who obey God are the most beautiful people.
Do not be hasty. God is still working. Do not let tomorrow's worries dominate me. I am under the blood.

Through Professor John Nash's life, I heard that choices change our lives. But that choice is made possible through the support of love. It is 'power' possible only when Jesus becomes the reason for living. To have spiritual insight, spiritual vision, and discernment regarding choices, you must remove the comfortable and pleasant food easily consumed and the grain. Do not worry; the Lord will boldly remove them. Wow~ Chicken vision: 30cm, Eagle vision: 10.0 - Weekly summary 20130901
There is absolutely no need to pretend to be a good person. Because in the Lord's sight, you are already sufficiently good and beautiful.

Believing what you want to believe is not faith. That is merely superstition. Faith is believing in God's absolute sovereignty and providence, and obeying as you walk forward today. When you trust in the Lord's omnipotence, sorrow will be changed into creativity. Joseph named his firstborn Manasseh, saying, 'God has made me forget all my trouble and all my father's household.' And he named the second son Ephraim, saying, 'It is because God has made me fruitful in the land of my suffering.' Genesis 41:51-52
The benefit of dwelling in the Lord is that you have nothing more to lose. Having nothing left to lose, you will continue to be filled.

In the Lord, sad things do not end in sadness, and fearful things do not end in fear. Life makes us proud when favorable and desperate when unfavorable, but those of faith trust only in the Lord who knows and vindicates them in any circumstance. Therefore we say with confidence, 'The Lord is my helper; I will not be afraid. What can mere mortals do to me?' Hebrews 13:6
Do not grieve over the paths you could not take; be satisfied walking the path you must walk. Everyone walks, but each has their own path.
Give your heart to the Lord moment by moment. Then the Lord will give you His heart. A broken heart is offered, and the Father's heart comes upon it.
That the Lord is almighty means that I am at peace.
'Be at peace in the Lord' is not a mere greeting. Please, do not dwell outside the Lord and become wounded. Achieve wholeness in the Lord. This is my intercession.
If you cannot be happy with the Lord, nothing else has any meaning. Find your answer in the Lord.

The Lord told us not to worry. 'Do not let your hearts be troubled.' These are words He spoke to His disciples as He departed, after they had followed Him for three and a half years, living with total dependence. As the basis for being free from worry, the Lord says, 'You believe in God; believe also in me.' It means faith. Do not seek from afar; believe. Then you will be free from trouble. John 14:1
Having too many desires is called greed. If you are faithful to your original purpose, you will find rest. For the One who came to bear the cross, every moment was a gift and a source of awe.
Sometimes when baseless anxiety comes, there is something to do: grasp Jesus, who is the most certain reason. Faith is a life that transcends feeling.
Anticipate the Lord. And rejoice in Him. Then He has said He will grant the desires of your heart.

The time you look to the Lord is the happiest and most precious time. He calls you out from tribulation to be with Him. Do not worry about tomorrow. Do not fear those who breathe through their nostrils with temporal power. Just focus on the Lord.
When you cannot look to the Lord, though you have much, you feel empty; though you are capable, you feel frustrated. Put down everything and look to the beautiful Lord.

If there is no mistake in your words, you are truly a perfect person. Those without mistakes in their speech are at peace. Why? Because they are thankful people. Thanksgiving and peace are the same thing. If you have lips of gratitude, you have truly become a complete person. 'Anyone who is never at fault in what they say is perfect, able to keep their whole body in check.' James 3:2

If you love the Lord completely and truly act in that love, there is nothing more to fear. Because you have done what God commanded, regardless of the outcome. What the Lord wants is not success but obedience.

Those who do not completely trust in the Lord but cling to their own ability are all oppressed people. Knowingly or unknowingly, they remain under spiritual oppression, without freedom, without peace, and suffering from illness. Hold fast to the truth with your life. So darkness retreats and light shines...

Those who truly trust and pray to the Lord are already blessed, satisfied, and answered in the Lord's likeness. 'And I—in righteousness I will see your face; when I awake, I will be satisfied with seeing your likeness.' Psalm 17:15

It is far better to perish in the Lord without His help in solving problems than to solve them without the Lord. If the Lord does not help, nothing can help. Rather than alleviating symptoms through human methods, atmosphere changes, or pretexts, when the prayer the Lord requests becomes deeper, the problem is solved simply by walking with the Lord and being content. 'Lord, though my heart ached and I prayed, now I am already satisfied in You; do according to Your will. My soul is satisfied because of You.' 'Unless the Lord builds the house, the builders labor in vain. Unless the Lord watches over the city, the watchmen stand guard in vain.' Psalm 127:1 'My lord the king, please help me!' The king replied, 'If the Lord does not help you, how can I?' 2 Kings 6:26-27
When you confirm that nothing can be done your own way apart from the Lord's will, peace comes. Because peace comes only when you let go of greed.
Before the Lord's cross, all problems are small. Rise up and take hold of the cross.

*It is not human error but God's providence. An ambulance passes ahead of all vehicles. It goes ahead of drunk drivers, reckless drivers, inexperienced drivers, and unlicensed vehicles. Because it seeks to save life. It is not error but providence. God is saving me and saving everyone. The purpose of providence is not condemnation but the salvation of life. God seeks to save life— Genesis 45:5,7

The Lord's kindness is truly beautiful. Even if I let go of everything... 'Because your love is better than life, my lips will glorify you. I will praise you as long as I live, and in your name I will lift up my hands.' Psalm 63:3-4
Believe in the Lord's omnipotence and wait silently. Humans always worsen situations due to fear. Yet it is remarkable how God's love and grace overturn those situations.

If the situation you face seems overwhelming, it is a sign to enter into greater grace. In grace, there are no limits and nothing to fear. 'Jesus guides me all my way, granting sufficient grace when facing difficulties'—Hymn 384
The Lord's omnipotence is such great comfort to us.
The Lord's great comfort is the fact that He speaks to us and grants us His presence. With voice and presence, these two things, you have everything.

The reason I can sing that the Lord is fair is not because my vested interests and evaluations have comparative advantage or at least some propriety, but because I have the 'Lord's presence' that others do not have. This enables me to see what others have not seen, hear voices others have not heard, and enjoy love that others rejected. 'I may have no possessions...' —Poet Myung-hee Na

When the Lord raised Lazarus and the widow's son of Nain, He not only restored their lives but also healed the diseases they had suffered during their lifetime. (There is no account of Lazarus being alive but continuing to suffer from his previous ailment.) God's healing, God's restoration, God's life are complete and a faithfulness that transcends the conditions of sorrow and worry. Trust in the Lord. 'For I know the plans I have for you, declares the Lord, plans for welfare and not for evil, to give you a future and a hope.' Jeremiah 29:11
When we say that the Lord is almighty, it means that I am bold and enjoy true peace. Whether we live or die.

Even if you say the Lord is everything, the Lord is never deceived. If everything could be accomplished by words alone, who would fail? A person for whom the Lord is everything is not disappointed by evaluation. Because the Lord is everything.
The Lord is sovereign. That means He solves it.
If the Lord has not given it, you have received nothing. Do not worry about many variables, but seek peace in the Lord. The Lord will never abandon us.
If you do what the Lord commands, there is nothing to fear or regret. If the Lord permits it, even loneliness becomes precious.

Because the Lord is with us, there is nothing to fear. Since it is God's work, you will not grow weary. Because it is for His eternal glory, all other things are secondary. Like a ride at an amusement park..., hold the handrail tightly. Now we run.
If there is someone to whom you can give your shoulder, then no matter what anyone says, that person is successful.

When you take what the Lord has not given, you will be afraid and anxious. You know it will be taken again. When you have what the Lord has given, you will have peace. No one can take away what belongs to the Lord. Not only give thanks for what He has given, but be moved with joy at every moment.
When you seek the Lord's face, God comes with the grace of washing and restoring us. Therefore, you must always seek the Lord in your heart.
Go not as you desire, but as God pours out, as the Lord fills.

Peace does not come from solving the most difficult problem you face now. True peace comes because there is One who is with us even in the storm. Seek to be with One who is greater than your problems.
It is truly joyful to rejoice in what is worthy of rejoicing. But it is truly sad to grieve over what is not worthy of grief. If there is a wound, you cannot respond correctly.

The reason you can be bold in all these circumstances right now is because you believe in the Lord's will. So you are not sad even if you lose, nor do you become proud if you gain a little. Those who walk the Lord's path are bold.

At this very moment, acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty and take by faith His protection and guidance. Even if 'ten thousand may encamp against you,' the Lord, who gives you the peace to 'lie down and sleep' peacefully, will protect and guide you with His sovereignty. —Psalm 3

At this hour, I remember in prayer friends who are earnestly seeking the path of life, friends who cannot sleep and are working and studying, friends who cannot find rest because of many thoughts. Let us pray.

At this time, I ask for your intercession for the members who are working late into the night, for the members who are awake through the night under great pressure from exams, and for the members who have left to distant places for various reasons. When love becomes the motivation, miracles happen. May there be peace in the soul, strength in the body, and victory in the work. In the name of the Lord, I bless all of you.
If you cannot be happy now, you cannot be happy later. If you cannot love now, you will become a person full of hatred later. The Lord always gives me the very best.
Even if you shed tears now, please sow seeds. You will surely reap with joy.
Paul was not lonely. Rather than hoping for luck, he kept in mind God's will and God's timing.

To pray truly, you must invest time in deep fellowship, gathering your heart and abiding in His presence. But at first, this is difficult. It is because it is a spiritual matter. The flesh will not permit it. If we do not spend sufficient time in a secret place, we will be in danger, lose peace, and fail in balanced human relationships. A person who communes with the Lord in a secret place will not lose that light in daily life.

True joy is not acquisition or achievement. It is bearing the cross and looking to the coming joy that lies ahead. That joy enables us to overcome present lack and suffering. 'Who for the joy set before Him endured the cross.' Hebrews 12:2
True rest does not come from comfortable circumstances but from relationships. If there is sin and grace is exhausted, no amount of time and rest will bring rest.
True happiness is not human satisfaction, wealth or honor, or the envious gaze of others. Happiness comes down from heaven. Because it is the grace that makes me who I am.

Real blessing is what the Lord gives. When we say the Lord gives, it means there is a measure and a scope that the Lord gives. If you think you have something you don't have, you will be anxious and tormented. It is greed. Please reject what the Lord has not given. And instead boast in your lack and weakness. He said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for My strength is made perfect in weakness.' 2 Corinthians 12:9
Attachment means there is dissatisfaction. Unfulfilled desire. It is because it is not filled. This is a typical symptom that comes when we are not satisfied with the Lord and do not live in Him.

The reason He comforts me when my heart aches and becomes a good friend when I am lonely is because God has unspeakable reasons. Making me shed tears because I cannot have the blessings that others have is because He wants to give Himself as the true blessing. That is why it hurts. Are you grateful?

The reward for those who accept discipline with a willing heart is the improvement of their relationship with the Lord and the peace that comes from it. No one enjoys the peace of the Lord like those straightened by discipline.
Praise brings joy, and gratitude brings peace. This simplicity is the truth.
It is truly a blessing that the Lord alone is my strength and my shield. Although it is a fierce battlefield, I feel warmth and peace in the presence of the Lord.

When an accuser picks a fight with fear and frustration, softly say this: 'I'm in worship right now!' He who dwells in the secret place of the Most High will abide under the shadow of the Almighty. I will say of the Lord, 'He is my refuge and my fortress, my God in whom I trust.' Surely He will save you from the fowler's snare and from the deadly pestilence. —Psalm 91:1-3

At first you pray not to collapse. It is with the desperation of grasping at straws. But when you encounter the almighty God in the midst of the storm, becoming aware of your own infinite weakness, you think it is fine to collapse. You not only have peace but also become bold.
Well, just whether we live or die.
My heart is not troubled without reason. It is because the Lord has something to teach me.

There are things that do not work out even with your best effort. To be precise, it is not that it didn't work out, but that it didn't work out as you wished. That means the Lord has permitted it under His sovereignty, and if you have paired intention with your best effort, it is something better.

If you do not pray enough, you will end up blaming others. You become sensitive to personality, reactions, and words, and most of all, you cannot commit everything to Him. When you pray, He quiets your soul. Because you come to know who the Master is.

This peace that wells up in my heart, this precious treasure hidden in the depths. Who will come and take my treasure? Be assured, peace, peace comes down from heaven. May the waves of that love cover my soul forever. Hymn 469
Not great power, but gentleness and long-suffering will heal most of our problems. 1 Corinthians 13

The greatest comfort for a Christian is that the Lord knows my heart. If you do not have a heart willing to lose everything to gain Him alone, everything becomes a problem and everything becomes a trial. Be clear about your purpose; it will determine who your master is.
Do not fear, but remain faithful to the end. It is the word of the Lord.
Do not worry; do your part. Then you will experience what the Lord will do.

The way a Christian lives in the world is not the old way. It is not the way of the unbeliever. It does not mean living in crisis where once you are pushed back, you are lost forever. Among wolves and wild beasts, you live as a sheep. Then you will experience the Lord's rod and staff comforting you. Do not become a wolf; you are a sheep walking with the Lord.
Being successful and living well in someone else's image is far less meaningful and happy than living truly in your own image. It is blessed that he should prosper and I should decline.

Before Paul's fifth visit to Jerusalem, he was warned and dissuaded by the Lord's faithful servants and coworkers multiple times because of great tribulations and imprisonments ahead. On an Alexandrian ship, he faced a great tempest and was in situations where hope of survival was lost many times, yet he remained calm and bold to the end. He knew two secrets: the Lord's timing and the Lord's will. Faith is never complicated.

A transparent life has nothing to worry about. There is nothing to calculate. Because what already exists can be sufficiently beautiful in itself, there is nothing to add or subtract; you run the race on the path the Lord has given and focus on the goal.

I especially pray for the coworkers who are preparing and laboring in the Lord's work this Sunday that through the Lord's being Master, He will fully govern all circumstances and ministries with His great hand. Fear, anxiety, and worry will depart, and only what the Lord permits and accomplishes under His sovereignty will come to pass. The Lord desires it far more earnestly than we do.
Bless the commissioned believing disciples with the authority of worship and peace the Lord has given. Then that peace will remain in their cells, relationships, and the people they need to hear.
Do not panic; be grateful. You will enjoy peace in anxiety. In everything, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. Philippians 4:6
May the Lord's peace abundantly overflow in your present life...

Peace is not a circumstance but a power. Only those with authority can have peace. True authority comes from faith that believes and trusts in the Lord. On the contrary, to fear is to strongly testify to God's absence. Like Gideon, I hope we will build an altar to Yahweh and become servants of Shalom who are at peace in worship (in His presence) and proclaim peace. Then Gideon built an altar there to the Lord and called it The Lord is Peace. -Judges 6:24
Peace I leave with you; my own peace I give you. The peace I give is not as the world gives. To give very my life, an offering of love, living sacrifice.
May you be at peace. Because the Lord reigns. When the Lord becomes King, He takes full responsibility for this situation and breaks the yoke with His anointing.

There are no miracles in peace. Miracles come in hospitals, in urgent situations, in desperate crises. There is no problem—only prayer requests. Let prayer work. Let prayer arise. Set fire to prayer. When prayer overflows, peace comes, and when peace comes, answers surely follow. I hope we become people of faith who sweep away the obstacles blocking life with overflowing prayer.
The premise of peace is that Your will be done. -Matthew 6:10

A life that was at peace but for some reason becomes fearful, like walking along a prison wall where one misstep seems irreversible and danger lurks. Safety in such a life is found in: 1. There is absolutely no condemnation in Jesus, and 2. Firmly believing this truth and having the power to ignore it. This is enough.

A victim mentality is victim delusion. Nothing that happens to me occurs without the Lord's permission. What the Lord does not know does not happen to me. Ultimately, a company's personnel authority, a government's power, sometimes unjust oppression and discrimination—none of these can surpass the Lord's omnipotence. Thinking that the harm done to me is greater than the Lord's omnipotence and omniscience is delusion.

I am here only because of the Lord. That is why I can overcome everything and am indifferent to it. Before the voices of people and spiritual challenges, even in situations of collapse that seem unbearable, I can laugh. Because the Lord is my only vision.
God, please give courage to friends who need courage, and give peace to those who need peace. In this moment, I pray You will be strength to those who are struggling. We need the Lord so much.

Please cast away excessive burdens and stress that do not come from God in the name of Jesus. God is the only One who can keep us at peace, and He is the Father who works and accomplishes His will even within that peace.
God leads those whose hearts and minds are steadfast—those like trees planted by streams, whose roots are fixed in His Word—to peace upon peace. Because they rely on and hope in the Lord alone.
When you hope only in God, people's evaluations fade, and you become free from all their eyes. Being recognized does not exalt you, nor does being despised lower you. It comes only from the Lord.
God is your keeper.
God is never sorry with us. Because He is almighty and guards us as the apple of His eye.
God loves us, though we are insignificant. To those who tell everything in detail before the Lord, He sends His Word to heal, restore, and strengthen them.

Those who glorify God enjoy peace even in tribulation. Those who succeed in Ministry to God also succeed in Ministry to People. Priority gives life. That devil comes with open jaws to swallow us, but Lord Jesus is our Commander and will fight to the very end and win. My soul is at peace, my soul, my soul is at peace. -Hymn 46
To live according to God's Word is our duty, and to fill and protect us as we live this way is God's responsibility. In conclusion, we need fear nothing.
We must listen to God's Word because we are existentially sinners and foolish in our actions. Only those who hear my voice will live in peace and without fear of calamity. -Proverbs 1:33
No word brings as much peace and relief as the phrase 'God's sovereignty.' Because the Lord reigns directly, there is nothing to worry about.
It is blessed to go as far as God tells you to go. When you go beyond the beautiful boundary the Lord has measured for you, anxiety arises and rest disappears.

When God designed humans, He did not design them to worry. Naturally, worry that comes to us is not our portion. However, when we worry, the 'purpose has changed,' causing tremendous wear on the product (humanity), and it ultimately breaks. 'Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God' (Philippians 4:6) → All the contents of worry must be used as kindling to burn the altar of prayer. 'But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well' (Matthew 6:33) → When you devote yourself to the Lord's work, worry disappears. Are you worrying? You have extra time.

What humans try to solve that God should work on is called 'worry.' Statistics show that only about 4% of what happens to us can be addressed by us. Ultimately, if something cannot be solved after 15 minutes of worry, you must coolly entrust it to prayer.

Even with much, it feels insecure, as though living in someone else's house. Because it is greed, not what was given to me. If you believe in and hope in Jesus, heavenly peace enters you. Because Jesus is completely the supreme gift given to me. Those who seek only the Lord are the most peaceful and strong.
God gives us salvation and oneness, peace and rest, not because we are good. We are not good but evil, but He is good.
If you don't understand, it causes stress; if you understand, it becomes a blessing.

You must entrust everything to God to work directly and move forward. To do this, you must believe and declare that 'I' have been crucified with Jesus. Surrender seems painful, but it is also the greatest peace.
I hope you become a beautiful relationship blessed by God. Moreover, I hope that parting becomes even more beautiful than that. For Christians, even the place left behind is beautiful.

Those who know the secrets of heaven never compete. 'Let the lowly rejoice in their high position. But let the rich rejoice in their humiliation—for they will pass away like a wild flower.' James 1:9-10

There are problems that are not resolved during a term. Some are resolved only when the year changes or even when graduation arrives. When living through a term while carrying unresolved problems, what matters is holding to the basics, rising every time you fall, and maintaining the direction you must go. In any situation, His presence and voice are basics that cannot be taken away.

One consoling fact is that we did not choose Jesus; Jesus chose and appointed us. Jesus said the reason is to bear fruit, and by bearing fruit always, He appointed us so that whatever we ask the Father in Jesus's name, we will receive it. John 15:16. You who are appointed, be at peace according to the intention of the One who appointed you, rejoice, and delight in Him.

If a president or king of a nation feels unhappy because they lack chewing gum, it means they are not a king but a runny-nosed child playing king in an alley. The Bible calls me a priest-king. I hope deficiency does not become unhappiness, but rather that level (standard) becomes the solution.

A working man was employed at an insurance company. Due to harsh wage cuts and restructuring, his mind was scattered with worry and anxiety about the future. The world's standard of recession destroyed his peace. A mother named 'Leah Miller' left a note for her son: 'There is nothing more precious than Jesus, Lord. Nothing in this world's wealth can compare.' Her son, 'Beverley Shea,' was deeply moved, repented, and composed a hymn: 'There is nothing more precious than Jesus, Lord. Nothing in this world's wealth can compare.' ... 'Abandoning all the world's pleasures, abandoning all the world's boasts, there is nothing more precious than Jesus, Lord; there is nothing but Jesus.' -Hymn 102. When the order (priority) for GOD changes, it becomes DOG.
If you have done everything but it was useless, there is still one thing left undone. That is returning to the Lord. So come home running, His arms are open wide.
Happiness is not a state free from pain and sorrow but a state overwhelmed by meaning and peace. Be courageous.
When accompanied by a strong police officer rather than a vicious thug, we do not fear. Because power overwhelms fear. The greatest fear is myself, and the greatest power is Jesus.

To find rest in a harsh world, you must have competence. Without competence, you are always pursued. You weigh gold for vanity and spend silver on emptiness. This competence does not mean influence in the world. It means intimacy with the Lord, that eternal power. That is true competence.

Blackmail and condemnation work only when your weakness is exposed. It is not weakness that exposes weakness but refusal to confess and repent. Those who are forgiven are not only bold but peaceful as well.

In confusion, give clear wisdom and a spirit of revelation. Give new strength, inspiration, and emotion to tired bodies and minds. Send someone to preach the gospel to family in the homeland, and provide experts and helping hands to solve complicated legal problems and tangled issues. Heal the physical body; especially pour the oil of healing in the knees. Fill the emptiness and purposelessness in the heart with Your truth and meaning. Let us find rest in the Lord without being chased by workplace and visa issues. Give healing to wrists and joints and cover with love. Fill loneliness with Your time, and let us entrust all our work only to You and depend on You. Let us overcome human ambition with calling and work without light or name. Let judgment and condemnation depart and let us serve with love, mercy, and compassion, like You. Reverse our academic situation, let us meet helping hands, work miracles. In situations seemingly impossible by human standards, grant the needed and prepared power. Guide my path in whether to serve You in the UK or Korea. Give me the power to bear this work. Bring harmony among our parents and open the door of economy in our home. Refine us and establish us as leaders serving souls, and arm us. Let anxiety and worry in our hearts melt before Jesus's name. Deliver us from the temptation and greed of sin and satisfy us with You alone. Almighty Lord, pour out power that I can bear. I pray for my friends with a sorrowful heart these days.

Everyone pursues peace, but sometimes uncomfortable feelings arise. This is truly a grace. Because when the signal comes, I can reflect on myself and correct where my focus went wrong. Any focus not aligned with the Lord must vanish.

Do not be anxious in probabilities but trust and find rest in the Lord. While God embraced the stubborn prophet Jonah, He prepared storms, a great fish, the fish's vomit, hot wind, scorching sun, a vine, a worm, and a wind. Everything is in the Lord's hand. This experience I am having is the Lord's greatest choice for me.
If you are swayed by environment, world, and people, you will live without peace. If you seek approval from people, you are not at least a servant of Christ. -Galatians 1:10
I must reach the depth where I can obtain peace. That is losing myself before the Lord's great love in a hidden room where no one is.

When you see what one clings to in the day of tribulation, you know their faith. To fear is evidence of having no life. Those with life do not fear but are confident. Psalm 27:3: Though an army besiege me, my heart will not fear; though war break out against me, even then will I be confident.

Certainty of restoration: Even wrong choices, mistakes, and difficult wounds are surely healed and restored by God's love. Though I walk through a barren path now, I am confident that God will work so that the intercessors for you and the gospel of the cross are not in vain. The evidence of this certainty is the cross. I am convinced that neither death nor life, neither angels nor demons, neither the present nor the future, nor any powers, neither height nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God that is in Christ Jesus our Lord. -Romans 8:38-39

The fatal challenge to a company is not aggressive advertising from competitors but the lack of conviction among employees. The most effective way to deny the Lord is not through atheists who say God does not exist. It is through believers who worry.

Often when the other person responds less than usual or is more familiar with someone else, we overreact unnecessarily. It is a conclusion that we are not flying. The airplane's altitude is low, so we are affected by all kinds of flies, mosquitoes, and sparrows. Those for whom the Lord is the conclusion soar through the sky. That is why they do not obsess over earthly matters.

Have hope. No one can take it from you. Have leisure and humor. If faith is a luxury brand, life too becomes a luxury brand. Even if you set aside what you are being pursued for now, the great work you think of will not happen. Believe in the almighty God and entrust everything to Him.
When you are weary, look to the Lord. The Lord of comfort will give you rest.

* Sunday Leaders Meeting: 1. Life is a matter of fullness. 2. Those who know the future are at peace, even bold. 3. When you know the ending, you can enjoy it playfully. 4. The darker the situation, the more fun it becomes. 5. Believing is seeing what has not yet happened. 6. If you have faith, you are bold and focused. 7. It is not a matter of good character or willpower but a matter of having faith. 8. If you see and receive the conclusion by faith, you cannot live any other way; you live this way inevitably. 9. Those with reliable, certain information change their behavior when they see the result. 10. Focus on Jesus. Always think of Him. 11. Are you lonely? Pray like this: Make me even more lonely. 12. This is the appearance of a spiritual giant and the path of one who laughs at destiny and walks the Lord's way.

When I cannot protect myself, I become bold instead. This is because I have moved beyond worries that can be solved by worrying. Please do not worry, but entrust everything with thanksgiving. That is the intention of the Almighty.
Cast it simply. Cast it to the Lord. And trust the Lord and just go. The Lord is alive.

* God will never let you down. When you believe in Jesus, you will face tribulation in the world. But there is no need to fear. Because Jesus has overcome the world. He has never failed me and never will. All His victories were already accomplished on the cross. That is the conclusion. My conclusion. The conclusion that Jesus foresaw on the cross. This is my destiny.

When you fast, of course you become hungry and weak. You feel like you are about to die. This usually happens for the first couple of days. Your body sends a signal telling you to stop. When you receive such a message and ignore it, peace and new strength come, and you realize it was somewhat exaggerated. If the response from darkness is fierce, think of it lightly. Victory is near.
When I believe in the Lord's omnipotence, peace comes. Because that is when miracles happen. Peace is a great grace given to believers. The Lord is greater than the problem.
What is needed in fear and worry is not logic and persuasion. Even with understanding, anxiety does not disappear. What is truly urgent is intimacy. Oh, the Father is with me! That is enough.

There is no saying: 'Blessed are those who move churches, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven; blessed are those who persistently cleanse others' hearts, for they will see God; blessed are those who pass and get promoted; or blessed are those whose true value is recognized, for they will inherit the land.' Changing environment and changing others may seem like it will bring happiness, but without my poor spirit, my mourning, my peace, my holiness, it is only emptiness.
Do not feel wronged that things are not fair. Rather, be thankful that the Lord is with you. Even with my shortcomings, He has given abundantly.
It is God who holds me. Therefore, do not fear people. It does not come from east or west, but from God alone.

Looking back, I see that I worried and suffered needlessly. Everything is resolved and solved within the Lord's grace, yet I draw tomorrow's concerns forward today. Do not worry beyond earnest prayer. The Lord will accomplish it.
If you desire much, you will have much conflict. If you are satisfied with the Lord's glory alone, life becomes simple and recovery and healing occur.

When you entrust everything with the heart that says 'He may choose not to, I will accept loss if lost, and death if death,' peace comes and you become bold. Before the Lord of peace who controls all circumstances, be strong and courageous. Not according to my will, but according to the Father's will.
It's okay. Because it is in the Lord's hands... I have known the answer from the beginning. I just need to do it.

When you are afraid, rely on the Lord. God, who becomes our refuge, hides me and protects me. The Lord's peace is boundless, so those who trust in the Lord give praise. When I am afraid, I trust in the Lord.

Do not carry too heavy a burden. That too is greed. Please carry the burden the Lord has given you. Your heart will become gentle and humble, and that burden will be light. To abandon what He has not given is called 'peace,' and to hold onto what He has given until the end is called 'courage.' I am gentle-hearted and humble; take my yoke upon you and learn from me, then your hearts will find rest. For my yoke is easy and my burden is light. - Matthew 11:29
Ultimately, it is living forever with my Jesus. That makes me smile and stand up again.
Faith goes beyond regret and brings trust in the Lord's sovereignty and rule, and even gives the amazing peace that He responds. The Lord is still working now. Not by your sight but by your faith.

"Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. Not as the world gives do I give to you" (John 14:27). Only Jesus can give fundamental peace. Only Jesus washes away sins with His blood and reconciles us with God. Those who do not fear God and are not reconciled with Him naturally should be anxious. We do not call a disease without symptoms a blessing. We must pray that those who have left God will never be able to rest. We must pray that they remain unsettled.
When I let go of holding onto myself, God holds onto me. Behold my servant whom I uphold, my chosen one in whom I delight.
If you have entrusted all your works to the Lord, be bold. The Lord will not demand more from there. From there, He takes responsibility.

Do not suffer from emotional problems. Lift the eyes of faith and see that it is only one piece in the entire process. Emotion is not reality; faith is reality. Circumstances are not evidence; faith is evidence. Be courageous.

The fact that I cannot do it with my own strength becomes peace beyond regret. How blessed is that? Relying only on the Lord, not on my strength... Confess that I cannot do it and move forward. The Lord will govern it Himself.
David did not always rejoice and praise. In truly indescribable sorrow, when he decided to praise the Lord, joy and emotion that no one could stop burst forth. Faith is about settling your heart.

* When someone works hard but does not get the scores they expected, people become disappointed and resentful. They thought that if they were sincere and did their best, everything would work out, but it did not. There are things in life where despite giving your best, results are not satisfactory. But you need not fear or be discouraged about this. There is One watching me now and storing my tears in a bottle and recording them in His book. When you rely on Him, you do not fear. There is a realm of humans and a realm of God. 'Count my wanderings; put my tears in your skin bag—are they not in your book?' Psalm 56:8 'When I am afraid, I put my trust in you.' Psalm 56:11

* What is needed after the greatest grace. Mary received an exceedingly great grace worthy of confirmation by an angel. Yet that grace turned into anxiety due to human circumstances and customs. When she met Elizabeth after a long and difficult mountain journey, she received confirmation again in unexpected welcome. 'The mother of my Lord...' What is needed after receiving grace is continuous confirmation through God's people by His Word.

The strongest life is one without regret. Whatever happens, whatever tribulation comes, those who seek the Lord's glory have no regret. If what you seek is selfish desire, you will have much regret and lingering attachment, but if it is the Lord's perfect glory, it will be sufficient.

Not all I desire will be accomplished. And it should not be. The Lord, who is higher and deeper than me, knows what I truly need. That is why what I feel as 'lacking,' the Lord calls 'sufficient.' When my opinion and the Lord's opinion differ, which is correct? Jesus guides me on my way; even when facing difficulties, He gives sufficient grace. - Hymn 434

A newborn baby is helpless. That is why parental care is needed. That is why babies do not worry or be anxious. That is the parents' responsibility. No matter how dark and difficult, when you are confident in the Lord's will and relationship with Him, you can entrust everything and find peace. The fact that I worry and despair is because I do not listen to the Lord's voice and do not know His will. 'My grace is sufficient for you.' - 2 Corinthians 12:9
There are two reasons I do not fear circumstances. One is that things do not go according to my will, and the other is that the Lord governs. Courage comes from focus. Where is your focus?

* The present time is what matters. You must be happy now, improve now, and walk with Him now. And leave everything else and go. Therefore, do not fear destiny. Living just today is enough, burning like a flame. After that, I do not know well. All honor, glory, and all authority belong only to the Lord.

* Let the Lord be Lord. King Saul lived conscious of others' views, clinging to them, but David looked to the Lord's presence, let go, and lived. Though he had more, unable to get more, he was always anxious, but with nothing, yet given grace, he finds peace. Because they served different masters. What am I pursuing and what is my motivation? 'When Saul heard this, he became very angry. "They have credited David with tens of thousands," he said, "but me with only thousands. What more can he get but the kingdom?" - 1 Samuel 18:8
* Good news. The Lord governs even now. In all visible and invisible realms, the Lord is King. This is comfort and joy.

* Reasons to be courageous in tribulation. We suffer in the world, experience indescribable pain and unimaginable tribulation. And not knowing tomorrow's things, we drag our weary bodies to the place of worship. We are happy. Now only the Lord and I exist.

* What people realize in suffering. When people suffer, they realize two things. One is that I cannot control things, and the other is that I must look to the Lord's face. This means the same as letting go and finding rest. Let us look to the Lord's face.

There are times when it is clearly not something to be angry about, yet anger explodes and you are not even given a chance to explain. When you think of the Lord and turn around, it is frustrating and your heart is hurt, but please pour it out to the Lord. 'Dear child, I know your heart and the lordship of this matter belongs to me. I will make it beautiful.' That is why the Psalms end with gratitude along with the pouring out of a wounded heart. I trust in the Lord.

* How to overcome fear. The opposite of fear is not courage. It is companionship. When you walk with the Lord, fear will transform into song. To Joshua, who trembled with no charisma, the Lord said to 'meditate on His Word (faith)' and 'keep and do it (holiness).' Pursue peace and holiness with all people; without these, no one will see the Lord. - Hebrews 12:14 'By faith Enoch was taken from this life, so that he did not experience death... he was commended as a man who pleased God.' - Hebrews 11:5

* Discern this way. The secret to knowing if prayer is answered is whether you can wait. Emotional leading can sound incredibly like the Lord's response. Emotions are that strong. The Lord's response is peaceful, not hurried or driven. Because it is given by the Almighty and all battles belong to God, there is no anxiety and no trying to accomplish it yourself through scheming. If you cannot wait for the Lord's time in peace, it is not an answer. 'May the Lord direct your hearts into God's love and Christ's perseverance.' - 2 Thessalonians 3:5 When you receive guidance, you naturally persevere.

When you see people at church serving earnestly and giving praise, it seems they are living well without trouble, but when they return to their places, they face various problems and live in anxiety and restlessness. This is because they have not confirmed God's love. 'For those God foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son... to be the firstborn among many brothers and sisters.' - Romans 8:29 God is a God of impartiality. That means God loves us, the younger siblings, just as much as He loves His Son, Jesus Christ. God cannot be unfair.

Problems always exist, but do not wait for them to be solved; instead, rejoice always. Because the One closest to us before anyone else is God, we must respond to God first as God's chosen people. 'Rejoice always, pray without ceasing, give thanks in all circumstances.' - 1 Thessalonians 5:16
The most childish thing is to boast of money, education, and honor. Instead, boast in weakness. The Lord will be revealed. The Lord's grace is sufficient for us.

Even this one breath of rest I am taking now is given by the Lord. God has given everything. Without that confidence, I would worry and fear. The One who began is the Lord, and the One who will accomplish is the Lord.

Drinking passion fruit juice on a palm tree beach while watching the waves and tanning does not bring true rest. True rest requires the Lord to be with you. 'The Lord replied, "My Presence will go with you, and I will give you rest." - Exodus 33:14

Pharisees and Sadducees had power in their society. They could mobilize armies. But they could not lay a hand on Jesus, it says. This means they could not arrest Him. Why? Because 'His hour had not yet come.' Jesus taught in front of the temple treasury, yet 'no one seized him, because his hour had not yet come.' - John 8:20 The best bodyguard is God. Do not fear.

Before problems where I am helpless, what I need most is not a skilled person to back me up or luck. It is His voice that changes my fear to peace and makes me know that the Lord is God. 'He said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you."' - 2 Corinthians 12:9 If the Lord is sufficient, then it is sufficient.

Please entrust everything and go. Even if it is frustrating, even if my heart aches, even if my plan does not work out, entrust it to the Lord and go. Then you have done well. Then you have really done well. If the Lord says there, then that is the blessing.

Thoughts of emptiness that suddenly come to mind are because there is no truth in them. Those who believe in God's sovereignty praise the Lord in all circumstances and are not shaken. Do not become empty; discern what the Lord's will is. Those who acknowledge God's sovereignty will receive comfort. 'Therefore do not be foolish, but understand what the Lord's will is.' - Ephesians 5:17
