In the most urgent and pressing situations, you must hear the Lord's Word, His voice, and hold onto it. Only by maintaining peace in the most bewildering situations can you respond to the Lord's gentle voice.

Do not worry too much about things I cannot do, but be glad and thankful for what I can do. Worry is not God's will. 'Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.' - Philippians 4:6-7

Do you labor with joy? To this question, you will eventually answer 'yes,' but if it takes more than 2 seconds, a red light is on. Joy is not calculation and coercion, but obedience in response to the condition of the Lord's sovereignty. To say 'act by faith' is to say 'act with joy.' If it is not my choice, then just obey. Because He is deeper and higher than me.
Do not be afraid; only believe. It means to entrust everything. 'Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you.' - 1 Peter 5:7
Please give up on fixing and resolving. Instead, cultivate the ability to empathize—to rejoice together and weep together. Then you will see more recovery and life. Do not analyze; love.
I do not rejoice in favorable circumstances, nor do I despair in unfavorable ones. This is because the Lord prepares and rules over all things. I desire only to bring joy to the Lord.

There are times when frustration comes, like running a race with hands tied behind your back. As I spend time this way, not only am I a problem, but the Lord would seem to suffer great loss. Yet I must enjoy and delight in that time. Sometimes the Lord binds me up so He may work quietly. When you cannot move, simply rejoice in His presence.

After prayer, your face should show no anxiety. If you fear even after praying, you must seek more earnestly for faith and assurance. Trust in the Lord who rules all tangled situations. Use your faith, and declare what your faith believes, and dwell in peace.
To avoid fearing the future, determine your future now. The most certain way to determine your future is to turn to the Lord alone, who knows your path.
My feet hurt greatly. Yet I am very happy. Because of our young people—that is the blessing of this inadequate one. To meet such precious friends...
Do not fear; only believe. Do not be crushed by problems; break through them with prayer. Jesus said to them, 'Do not be afraid; just believe.' Luke 8:50

There are tears that can only be shed after experiencing failure and rejection. These tears flow inward rather than outward, making it difficult for others to share in them. Though tears have similar composition, their reasons and meanings differ. In these tears, I am broken, I see my limits, and only then do I see the Lord. God's hammer that breaks my shell is terrifying, painful, and sorrowful to behold, but after refining it bears fruit of righteousness and peace. 'No discipline seems pleasant at the time, but painful. Later on, however, it produces a harvest of righteousness and peace for those who have been trained by it.'—Hebrews 12:11

Those who come to deal, bargain, or struggle are tense. Because they must gain more. But those who come to love are at peace. Rather, their original purpose is to give generously and lose. The Lord gave life but obeyed even unto death on the cross without asking for another price in return. This is why power and authority come forth from those who abandon greed, consume everything completely, and leave only an eyeglass case and one worn garment—they have peace. If they truly loved...
Those who trust in His guidance do not panic even when storms arise and rumors of war come. Boldness is not about one's personality; it is about 'what' you believe in.

Be free from what others think of you. Also, do not strive to be seen. All our attention should be only on God. That is worship. If I look only to the Lord, people will not look at me but will see the God I am beholding. Do not be conscious of people; look to God. You will be free from distress.

Wherever I am, my heart is always at ease. The peace given by Lord Jesus always overflows in me. My heart is always at peace, my heart is always calm. Though waves of evil sin come many, my heart is always at peace.—Hymn 408 Do not let circumstances and people rule over you. All these things are like clothes you will shed, and they can neither bring you true joy nor threaten you.

At the most fitting time, the Lord will guide you along the best path. The Good Samaritan must have delayed to help the man beaten by robbers and met whom he was meant to meet. When afraid, rely on the Lord. The Lord is your refuge.

If you try to abandon fear and worry, you become more focused on the problem, bringing greater difficulty. Rather than trying to abandon it, simply hold onto Jesus. When you hold onto greater value, other values become dung. Not treasuring dung and discarding it is not training—it becomes natural. When I die and live in Jesus, that is a victorious life.

You are the reason I live, You are my song in the night. Into your hand I commit. You hold my life in the palm of your hand. I will trust in You, I will worship You forever. Do not be afraid; only believe.

The first gift God gives to those who make their requests known to Him with thanksgiving is peace. Through an irresistible peace that makes them forget even the needs they asked for, He works so that there is much more to give thanks for.
People of faith have margin. Margin is not idleness but giving thanks in crisis and despair. Then the Lord's intervention, which the world cannot take away, will come as peace. The Lord reigns.

There are times when despite effort, despite hard work, the results are not good. What is needed then is to hold onto one thing: the fact that God loves me. Though all heaven and earth change, this truth does not change.
I have relied on Your unfailing love; my heart rejoices in Your salvation.—David, Psalm 13:5
The attitude and confession we hold toward the problem is more important than the size of the problem. Though He is not visible now, He is working.

There are times of worry, distress, sleeplessness, anguish, anger, unforgiveness, trembling, jealousy, desire to give up, and loneliness. This is because I do not know the Lord's will for me. Because I cannot be certain of His will, I can only judge by my own thinking, and because I try to solve life by my own thoughts, I cannot obey. The ship heads for the mountains. For life to be at peace on the right path, I must discern the Lord's will. It means not being foolish (Ephesians 5:17). Therefore, do not be foolish, but understand what the Lord's will is.
Rather than worrying while focusing on struggling matters, concentrate on what is opening and succeeding. Sometimes certain things require time. If you love and know the Lord, He will counsel you.

How can you not have peace when you can pray? To all who meditate on this word today, God's wonderful peace will be with you. 'Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.'—Philippians 4:6-7

In distress, you must look to the Lord's face. Saints who have suffered in the world must be fixed on the Lord of comfort. When sad, He comforts; the Lord of love will surely give rest. 'Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.'—Matthew 11:28

Even if the problem disappears immediately, you can never obtain rest. True peace is not the absence of work and issues but when the Lord gives you rest. The difficulty comes not from many trials but from the absence of true rest. 'Rejoice in the Lord always. I will say it again: Rejoice!...Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.'—Philippians 4:4, 6-7

I am troubled but not unhappy. I am troubled but happy. Because the Lord heals the brokenhearted and gives true rest. 'The Spirit of the Sovereign LORD is on me, because the LORD has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim freedom for the captives...'—Isaiah 61:1
If you seek to be served, you will surely suffer. Rather, if you serve and minister to others, you will be joyful. That joy comes from heaven.

Do not complain by your own calculation, but find peace through obedience to the Lord. The gospel is that the Almighty Lord died on the cross in weakness. 'For the foolishness of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than human strength.'—1 Corinthians 1:25

A lamp's wick can only shine when it continually receives fuel. When the supply is cut off, the wick itself burns up. Rather than working sporadically, when you consistently spend time with the Lord, the thorns are removed and you gain the strength to rest. No burn out.

It does no good to weep over the person who took what was mine, over a merciless past and environment. The world has the ability to take but not the ability to restore. To weep before the Father—that is power.

Treat well those who have shown you kindness. That is God's will and an invitation to peace in life. Even if that person has not been good to you and is of foul character, overcome evil with good. That is our calling. 'While Joseph was there in the prison, the LORD was with him; he showed him kindness and granted him favor in the eyes of the prison warden.'—Genesis 39:21
Decide and confirm that you will not worry about what you will eat or what you will wear. God knows that these things are necessary for you.
If the joy of meeting the Lord is greater than the weight of problems, life cannot help but be happier. There is hope beneath the shadow of the cross.
If pain and conflict remain in your heart, it means I must look only to the Lord more at this very moment.

In the 1960s, the national income was $80. Now it is over $20,000. In just a few decades it has jumped 250 times. Then there were people dying of starvation from absolute poverty, but now people do not starve from relative poverty. If income rises 250 times, one should be faint with happiness, but it does not seem so. Can possessions really bring such happiness? No. It is because people are understood as mere flesh. If a person cannot be happy by existing, they cannot be happy by possessing.
The only competitive advantage for a helpless sheep is 'companionship'—with the Shepherd.
What is before me and what is behind me are really small matters compared to what is within me.

Because my heart aches from rejection and wounds and is troubled by lack and deficiency, the fact that I cannot accept or forgive others does not dilute the Lord's forgiveness. God has already forgiven that person completely. Now I simply need to let go.

I want to determine my happiness by looking at my possessions. I am a person who lives by bread alone. I determine my happiness by grace and mission, by God's fullness. I am a person who lives by the Word. I do not live worrying and anxious about finding needs, but looking forward to the Lord filling me. I pray you become a life pursuing mission, pursuing the Word.

Do not worry or be anxious. The Lord will surely deliver you from the mouths of your professor and your boss. 'I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.'—2 Timothy 4:17
You are a person who came to love. Therefore, you will not be disappointed in all things, nor will you have regrets when you leave.
David was greatly distressed...but David found strength in the LORD his God.—1 Samuel 30:6

One of the two ways the Holy Spirit helps during prayer is by giving peace—strength to pray and confidence in the answer. The other is by creating discomfort and the sense that the prayer will not be answered, redirecting the prayer's course. In the end, we must rely on the warm Holy Spirit who guides from uncomfortable prayer to peaceful prayer.

If I had known the result, I would not have been so excited. A person of faith is someone who knows the result. Be confident in the Lord's will for you in the Lord. Know that 'the plans I have for you are not to harm you but to prosper you.' Without faith, anger, excitement, and despair follow.

When groundless fear rushes in and I stand before anxiety that the worst thing I worry about will come, I pray you awaken to the fact that the Lord's will is being done and that His will is good for me. The Lord knows my path. Please let go of worry and anxiety.
Do not be afraid; only believe.
What I fear determines the direction of my life. God desires that I lay the fear of the future at His feet.

Faith means believing two things: the assurance of companionship—'that He is surely present'—and the assurance of trust and response—'that He rewards those who seek Him.' God chooses the detailed options, and we enjoy the joy.
What a community needs is not a person of impressive competence but a person with whom you can be at peace if stuck in an elevator together.

I hoped for stability but was shaken; I wanted not to be shaken but everything crumbled. Yet in that heartbreaking place, the reason I can still rejoice is that God does not make mistakes. Even in that place you absolutely cannot understand, God 'never' makes mistakes. God is assuredly faithful.
Be bold. I hope you will be as bold as one who has overcome the world. The Lord has overcome the world.

If I frequently become discouraged and frustrated, it is because I am comparing the path itself with others, not looking at the destination. The reason we can rejoice even when we face tribulation, persecution, and suffering is because we possess the ability of overview—seeing the 'whole.' I hope you will be bold, possessing the power of outlook, the ability to look toward the end of the path.
Revealed problems have deep roots. Do not deal with the problem, but look only beyond it to the Lord who stands there. Only the Lord heals me and sets me free from all causes.

When you make a resolution but waver, when you turn toward the Lord but soon look to the world and become fearful, and your heart grows heavy without peace, I hope you will hold fast to the Word of the Lord. The Holy Spirit, the Spirit of truth, sets us free. Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom. 2 Corinthians 3:17

More important than being misunderstood is not misunderstanding others. When that happens, in God's church, the devil may attempt, but ultimately cannot finish. I hope you will become stubborn and obstinate, ignoring any attack. The devil will feel frustrated before such a person and will not live his days—struck down by 'cardiac infarction.' We are under the Lord's precious blood.
I worry much. It is because I hold on. It is not that I hold on, but only the Lord holds all things.

Even after gaining victory in prayer and worship, anxiety can still rush upon you when you return home. This is entirely natural and a normal process of advancing in sanctification. You may waver, but you will not return to your former state. Because you have made peace with God and are changed, you will never collapse. This is because you are under the Lord's precious blood.
Rather than seeking peace by confirming all things, I hope you will find peace by being assured of 'that love' alone. The Lord's love is sufficient.
The strongest person is not one who hides what they desire, but one who desires nothing.
I hope you will be bold. I pray for you.
Without worrying about others' opinions when your flesh desires to make a decision, I hope you will find peace in the choice the Lord desires. That is not bondage but rather freedom.
Do not fear disappointing people. When we became sinners, the Lord loved us, bore the cross, and never disappointed us. Look to the Lord and find peace.
You will hear of wars and rumors of wars, but take heed; the end is not yet. Instead, the Lord told us not to be afraid. We are in the Lord's hands. I desire the Lord's care.
When He tells us to entrust, it is not to burden us, but to add faith to us.
If each moment were the last, there would be no regret when I close my eyes. If what I leave in this world is love alone, I will be bold before death.
When you are in pain, look upon the face of the Lord. Behold the Lord of peace. To all who call upon the Lord's name, the Lord gives strength and protection.
The only way to be freed from fear is to have the fear of God. The more conscious you become of God, the less conscious you become of yourself.
What others have taken from me is not important; what remains with me now is more important.

When I hold onto the unchangeable past, I blame myself, become depressed, and cannot control my emotions. The Lord has never pressed us. Our mistakes are not burdens we can bear. He understands our weaknesses, our loneliness, and our limitations better than anyone. Dear [name], that is not your fault. Your life desperately needs Jesus, and I bore the cross for you.
Meaning is far more important than money. A simple life in the fear of God is better than richness with a ton of headaches.
All things work together for good for those who love God and are called according to His purpose. Our God is a good God, and He has the amazing ability to turn evil into good.

There is one thing I must focus on: God's will and His presence. The remaining things are what God must concern Himself with. Therefore, do not worry about things that have not happened or things that have already happened. Look to the Lord in the present moment.

Take your brother also and go back to that man. May the Almighty God grant you mercy before that man so that he will send back your other brother and Benjamin. If I am bereaved, I am bereaved. (Genesis 43:13-14) After this event, Jacob not only does not lose Benjamin but even experiences Joseph coming back alive. When I let go with the heart that this is the end, the Lord gives me the power to begin again.
If you base your self-worth on the evaluation others have made of you, you will not have a single joyful day.

I hope you will entrust to the Lord's hands the timid child within you, the child wounded by expressions, the discouraged child, the child without confidence. When the Lord embraces it, a healthy self enters within me.
Worry does not solve anything. Anxiety does not improve anything. I hope you will entrust it to the Lord. It is the Lord who works among us.
True peace comes when I let go of judging myself.
If you desire much, your heart is uncomfortable. If you wish to give much, your heart is happy. Love is not only when you give many things. When you long to give but cannot, you feel true love.

Discouragement comes from sight. When you look at unchanging subjects, problems, yourself before them, and your surroundings, you will naturally become discouraged. I hope you will look to the Lord and what the Lord has done.
You who pray and run are not alone right now. Hold the Word and be bold. Intercession continues.

Please do not calculate too much. Even if you do, you cannot understand, and even if you could, because there is no solution, you only become more frustrated. If it is a situation the Lord has permitted, there is surely the Lord's way. Do not panic, but pray.
When your heart aches, that is when the Lord calls you to entrust all to Him.

A person who trembles before a police officer is a criminal. An upright person is rather helped. Before the Lord, a person with greed is always anxious before people and the Lord. But a person with a righteous heart finds comfort. Because they love the Lord, there is nothing to desire or fear.

Many people live as though acting a play. They do so believing they can be happy. Rather, it is tiring. They lived diligently, yet it is not their own life. Be faithful to yourself. You are a precious existence, one of a kind in the world, and for that preciousness, God's Son laid down His life. Do not act; be faithful.
You need to relax and be yourself. God loves us as He is creating us.

There is no anxious bird. God said He loves us more than the birds of the air. With faith and confidence in God's love alone, you will experience that almost every problem is resolved. Why are you afraid?

You must stay awake to pray and pray to stay awake. You must pray whether you can or cannot, in joy or sorrow. You must pray with many words or few words or with no words at all. You will surely receive the answer that brings peace.
An outing is not at all inferior to a prayer meeting; I hoped you would become stylish people with such rest and ease.
Though the gathering has ended, the Lord's touch continues. That is how I will pray.

It seems you must do many things, and you can only stand where everyone desires if you do everything well. That is compulsion. That is deception. Life is not accomplished by effort alone because my life is not mine but the Lord's. When I entrust to the Lord and accept, He shows me secret things I do not know. Martha, Martha, you are worried and upset about many things, but few things are needed—or indeed just one. Mary has chosen what is better, and it will not be taken away from her. When you are busy, mistakes happen.

Many people wander, not knowing the way. But not knowing the way is entirely normal. Abraham did not know the way and followed the Lord. Isaac and Jacob could not predict their lives either. Job 23:10 says only God knows the path I take. That the Lord knows is enough for us to have perfect peace. Trust only in the Lord.
Why do you worry when you can pray? After ministry, even when I go home, intercession continues. My husband has come, but the wife is so busy praying she doesn't even look outside.

Do not fear dissertations or exams. The dissertation a professor requires is something that might appear once in a hundred years if at all—something ideal, and such a dissertation is something the professor themselves has never written. Do not be overwhelmed by exams and essays; rather, become a life that overwhelms.
A song I long to sing, for everyone. Carrying one life in my heart and returning, I entrusted everything to the Lord.
Spiritual_Warfare: Tares and the Cross: We must come before the Lord’s throne every moment to obtain grace in time of need. The enemy, of course, sows tares and desires to stop believers whenever possible. The most effective tare is 'self-defense,' and the weapon that breaks this and allows us to boldly approach the King’s throne is 'self-denial,' that is, the 'Cross.'

Never Tear Down the Altar: 'They have torn down your altars and put your prophets to the death with the sword' - 1 Kings 19:14. 1. It was because of the altar that sinful humans could approach God. 2. Furthermore, the holy God spoke to inadequate humans through the prophets. 3. Tearing down the altar and killing the prophets was a satanic strategy and an intelligent evil seeking a complete (two-way) disconnection from God. 4. Preventing people from coming to the throne of grace through the Cross and blocking them from receiving mercy and grace is exactly what Satan wants. 5. Therefore, any ministry or pursuit lacking the blood of the Cross is equivalent to an 'own goal.'

The Reason for Pain: The reason the heart aches so deeply is because of spiritual lethargy—a state where one only seeks the Lord when forced by such pain. It is the reason a stiff soul, who only had the intent but did not know how to pray, can finally fall on its knees, clutching its chest, and cry out to the Lord with wailing.

The Most Corrupt Nature: Stalin, who purged and killed countless people through the secret police, even had those dozens of secret police directors kill one another through their successors. It is said that while he watched American cowboy movies all night long, he personally decided the ending of every Soviet film. The 'controlling instinct' is the oldest and most corrupt instinct humans possess—the thought of wanting to be God. A person who tries to control situations, organizations, and people always lives in fear and anger because expectations are never fully met. Ultimately, Stalin collapsed from a stroke, was spat upon by his loyal subordinate Beria, and died of suffocation by a pillow. When you let go of the controlling instinct, sin stops. You must entrust everything so that only the Lord's sovereignty rules over you and all things.

When You Feel Infinitely Small: When you feel like a 'nobody' and can only sigh, you must look upon and confirm the glorious Lord and the fact that you are in Him. If Satan reminds you of your past, I hope you will remind Satan of his future.

Addiction to Power: The reason the rational Pilate became irrational and ruthless was to appease the Jews he was trying to control. All twisted desires for power seem to make one a ruler for a moment, but eventually, one becomes a slave to their own greed and is unexpectedly pushed into cruelty. Kim Jong-un of North Korea is in a similar situation. 'Wishing to satisfy the crowd, Pilate released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus flogged, and handed him over to be crucified.' - Mark 15:15

Consistency is Power: In novels or movies, scenes sometimes appear where one shares friendship or love with demons, but that is merely a romantic imagination. The forces of darkness have tremendous consistency, and that is to kill. For the past 6,000 years, they have never lost focus or compromised. Yet, people stray from Jesus so easily. Consistency is power.

When There Are Many Resolutions but No Execution - Spiritual Neutrality: This happens because one tries to maintain spiritual neutrality. A person is bound to be used by something—either as an instrument of righteousness or by the hand of darkness. Only when you are used righteously will you avoid being used by darkness.

How to Listen to the Word: If you only listen to the Word and then forget it, you will lose it to Satan. Listening to the Word begins with the ears, but it manifests as power only when you 'listen' through your life.

Time in the Wilderness: If you thought you would be used but are instead driven into the wilderness, it is because you are trying too hard to teach others. The scope of the wilderness will continue until you become quick to listen and slow to speak.

Discernment Disorder - December War? 1. If a defect appears on a factory line, all other products are also thoroughly inspected. 2. If what you spoke and executed with confidence turns out to be wrong—no matter how idle the words—you must thoroughly reflect and repent. 3. Error is a tendency, and such tendencies are likely to recur. 4. If there is no repentance regarding being more sensitive to the voice of emotions, becoming a prisoner of prejudice, or regarding self-centered thinking and greed, it means the same errors will be fatally repeated in the future. 5. If there is no reflection, as if nothing happened, regarding false eschatology, the 'December war' theory, wrong judgments, taking sides, and the wounds caused by exaggerated expressions and self-centered language, one will not move beyond that spot even after decades. 6. The reason David is great is not because he struck down Goliath, but because of his ability to recover.
Inferiority Complex and Pride: An inferiority complex slanders a loyal person as being proud, while pride ignores a whole person as being inferior.

Bread Comes from the Oven: When delicious bread is baked in an oven, it becomes a finished product ready to be eaten. Yet, everyone prides themselves on being a 'finished product' that has passed through the hot oven, even though they haven't even been kneaded into dough yet.

The Greatest Discipline: I once saw someone who used to serve so wonderfully being put on trial. Through the mouths and bodies of believers, profanity and violence were rampant and the police were called; the victim wept, yet the perpetrator claimed they had done no harm. The greatest discipline among all disciplines is being 'left alone.' It is truly a fearsome thing when greed is disguised as a mission, not realizing how shameful and terrifying it is, or what it is doing to the Kingdom of God and the Korean church.
The War of Priorities: Winning the battle of priorities determines victory or defeat. This is because it reveals what I serve, who I am, and shows where I am currently heading.

A Good Community: When organic matter (animals, plants) is subjected to temperature and pressure underground, it becomes coal. However, if a slightly higher price is paid and it receives higher temperature and pressure, it becomes a diamond. Instead of complaining that souls are not changing, the mission of the earthly church should be to raise the spiritual temperature and pressure.
Both Wash and Burn: It is the Holy Spirit who purifies our thoughts. He both washes and burns.

The Way to Change All: Sin hinders prayer. Prayer also hinders sin. To think one can overcome sin without prayer is an incredibly arrogant thought. This is because delaying a choice automatically becomes choosing sin.

Are You Repenting Today? If God is not in your heart, you will become envious, jealous, hateful, and lustful. You must lay down the 'fruit of the knowledge of good and evil'—which hates having God in the heart—and turn back to God. That is repentance. 'Furthermore, just as they did not think it worthwhile to retain the knowledge of God, so God gave them over to a depraved mind, so that they do what ought not to be done.' - Romans 1:28
The Power to Overpower Recurring Thieves: If the enemy repeatedly challenges me with the same problem, I must also constantly hold faster to the Lord’s Word. Victory or defeat is decided right there.
Secure Peace: To save a soul, you must seek peace. Only when I am at peace can I move others without coercion.

The War of Concentration: Whether you are 'crazy' for the world or 'crazy' for Jesus, ultimately, who you were crazy for and to what extent determines success or failure. That is why you must not dilute it. The power lies in how you maintain the original undiluted solution.

Struggle and the Cross: God did not struggle; He ruled by His sovereignty. The pinnacle of that rule is the Cross. Therefore, a believer lives by the Cross and dies by the Cross. If you live that way and die that way, there is resurrection.
Counter-Spirit: Feelings of rejection and alienation are fought with love and respect.

The Core of Disciple Training: Disciple training is not a systematized program or a successful event. It is the stubborn, continuous repetition of the Cross given to me in any situation. Persistence and repetition require the courage to risk one's life and shed blood.

Healing from Addiction: If you are destroying your life through addiction to fleshly things, it is evidence that you are a spiritual miser who grudges even a single hour of prayer. 'Couldn’t you men keep watch with me for one hour so that you will not fall into temptation?' - Matthew 26:40
When Waking from Sleep: Never overlook the invisible world. No matter what anyone says, the flesh must die and the spirit must live.

How to Effectively Fight Sin: 1. Wash 'sins' (acts) with the blood of Jesus. 2. Crucify the 'sinful nature' on the Cross. 3. Do not tremble in fear of falling into sin, but believe in the Lord who will keep you. 4. Look toward the glory that is incomparable to the pleasures of sin. 5. Always look to the Lord who is with me and who is coming again. 'Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.' - Galatians 5:24

Spiritual Warfare - How to Make the Enemy's Efforts Go to Waste: There exists a persistent 'intention' in the things that happen to us. The enemy’s intention is to steal, kill, and destroy. It wants us to react in that way to situations that warrant complaining, relationships that warrant hate, events that warrant discouragement, and matters that warrant anger. We do not have the power to control the situation immediately, but we can choose our response. Especially when we lay down our rights and declare a heart that is opposite to the situation, the strongholds of darkness begin to crumble.

Devoting Oneself to the Word and Prayer: The idea of trying to replace the Holy Spirit, and being obsessed with the means while losing the true essence, is the cause of stagnation. Mobilizing other non-essentials to solve this becomes a way to mass-produce nominal Christians who have not changed.

The Reason for Failing in the World Cup: The way to do well quickly in a short time is to change physical strength and tactics. If you do that, you can do reasonably well until the preliminaries. What is truly important is that soccer is played with a soccer ball. If the ball doesn't stick to the body, the player is anxious and the citizens watching are always anxious. What is truly frustrating is trying to find the cause in non-essentials after losing in the essentials. Grasping the essentials requires a long time and a high price (training). The reason a foreign coach (revivalist) is somewhat advantageous is that there is less interference and it takes less time to focus on the essentials. In religious terms, if you achieve oneness with the 'ball' (the Lord), you gain composure (boldness) even when the 'defense' (testing) presses you, and you come to dominate the game (sovereignty). The essentials shown in the Book of Acts were the Holy Spirit and the Word, the Cross and obedience. For an emergency patient, the priority is to minimize all distractions and get them to a specialist.
The Reason I Stand Here: Because God has overcome me. 'You deceived me, Lord, and I was deceived; you overpowered me and prevailed. I am ridiculed all day long; everyone mocks me.' - Jeremiah 20:7
When Meeting People Who are Too Beautiful: I find myself hating and distancing myself from sin. This is because a person who is already happy has nothing more to seek.

Drink Living Water, Not Sewage: Things that happen naturally on their own are like food waste given to pigs. To avoid being crushed by problems, you must mobilize power that comes from the outside. There are funds that come only when you pray, people who come only when you pray, and environments that come only when you pray. Also, there are ideas that come only when you pray. This heavenly wisdom provides the clue to solving difficult problems.

The Reason for the Fight: We fight the good fight. We fight not only to protect but even more to take back. We will not be anxious over relationships and situations but will instead take and seize them. Reform yourselves into the appearance of a victor. You, who are praying, are already a winner.
Darkness tries to hide: The fact that He has received glory. This is the fact that darkness wants to deny so badly.
A Fearsome Attack, but a Glorious Turnaround: This is because the God we serve is the only one worthy to receive honor and glory. Bring it on.
The Only Way to Keep the Fire: To persist. Persistence is power. Do not let the fire on the altar go out. - Leviticus 6:13

Two Beings That Remind You: The one who reminds you of the true meaning of the grace you received and the worship you offered is the Counselor, the Holy Spirit. However, the role of evil spirits is to make things feel meaningless and lead you to deny them. Do not be used by the deceiver, but by the One who is the Truth. 'But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you all things and will remind you of everything I have said to you.' - John 14:26

A community that doesn't pray while looking at people's faces is surprisingly smooth. This is because the enemy doesn't feel it is worth responding to. However, when a community that used to pray stops praying, the enemy rushes in with gritted teeth. Because he knows the opportunity is only then.

When You Want to Cut the Cord: When everything is bothersome and the community feels like a burden, it means you have fallen into a test, and the one causing that test is Satan. The cord you want to cut is ultimately Jesus. God, who pulls me from the top of the cliff with blood-stained hands, has never once thought of giving up.

To Friends Under Testing: The purpose of testing is unconditional destruction, and the one who causes it is Satan (Apollyon). Do the opposite of what he wants. What he wants is for you to fail to persist, to stop, and to be separated.
The Reason Training is Hard: To keep you alive and not dead in an urgent combat situation.
Two Stupid Things: The devil who tries to torment you with the same method he used last year, and the foolish one who is still deceived by that same method.
Just Try to Block Me: The devil may hinder me, but he cannot stop me. Relative hindrance cannot stop an absolute pursuit.
Let the Adversary Gain Nothing: You just need to focus on the Lord.

True Victory: Giving your heart to the Lord and not compromising, rather than paying attention to the adversary. What is clear is that the adversary will gain nothing he wants. Because Jesus bore the Cross.
A Storm is to be Overcome: The reason Jesus came walking on the water was not to take me out of the storm. It was to break through it together.
Anyway, You Will be Used: If you are being used by Him, you will be grateful and at peace, but if you are used by 'that guy,' you will complain and hate. There is no middle ground.

Vain Attempt vs. Obedience of Victory: Letting go of the attempt to protect and defend myself and placing my security in the Lord is the counter-spirit that smashes Satan’s schemes. Do not protect yourself, but obey Him.

If You are Immature, You Will Gradually Become Alone: Even as one ages, if the soul is immature, one cannot help but pester for recognition in various ways. Such a person reveals themselves, is sensitive to others' reactions, gives wounds and rubs salt in them, yet possesses a self-centered mindset that treats it as no big deal. Maturity is seeing 'me' and 'you' through the Lord. Empathy is power.

The Work Remaining for a Born-Again Believer: Being born again happens through the imperishable seed, the Word of God, and the Power & Ministry of the Holy Spirit. To the disciples who were already born again and prepared to die, Jesus told them to receive the baptism of the Holy Spirit. One must start from the confession that without the Holy Spirit, I am nothing.

Charging and Discharging: If spiritual supply is weak, a discharge occurs in my life. Then, though it is a matter of time, a spiritual famine occurs, making one lethargic and unable to be happy. If the community I belong to provides spiritual 'charging,' it is something to be truly grateful for, but if not, it means I have a mission. If there is no mission but only continuous spiritual loss (discharging), it means you should move to another community.
Song of Victory 3: One must be sensitive to whether they are currently being 'charged' or 'discharged.' Charging only happens when a current higher than the battery's flows through it.

Song of Victory 2: The parts I do not offer and keep hidden eventually become matters for testing. Do not offer your body to the flesh to become an instrument of unrighteousness, but offer it to righteousness to become an instrument of righteousness.
Disarmament: Letting go of the attempt to protect myself and placing my security in the Lord is the counter-spirit that smashes Satan’s schemes. It means not to protect yourself, but to obey Him.

Thoughts Leading to Death: The mind of the flesh is death. The mind of the flesh refers to self-centered thoughts. This doesn't mean superficially matching appearances while minding others' opinions, but giving up on 'living as myself' and living through Jesus. 'The mind governed by the flesh is death, but the mind governed by the Spirit is life and peace.' - Romans 8:6

When Trying to Have Faith: Whenever you try to have faith, without fail, a powerful challenge comes. That is why thoughts of giving up everything and running away arise. Nevertheless, faith is not believing in those feelings, but still believing in the sovereignty of the Lord who rules.
The Secret to Not Falling into Testing: Hold on to the truth that God is almighty and that He has given you a blood-boiling mission.
Fighting Technique: Fight by faith. You will win by the truth.

Do Not Fear the Evidence: A community having good faith means there are believers in that community who exert influence. If everyone follows the world, the gospel does not flow. Do not fear rejection and disregard; strive to testify of Jesus and love.
Try Living According to the Gospel: You will stop whining and come to know what true victory is.

The Simple and Clear Reason for No Revival: It means there are many people talking, but no one is paying the price. Faith does not improve on its own. There must be evidence, and that evidence is provided by martyrs. Presence and revival without the shedding of blood? That is nonsense. 'Martyr' originates from the Greek 'Martus,' meaning 'witness' or 'one who provides evidence.'
The Secret to Binding the Enemy: Try declaring and confessing with faith. All the negative and dark forces currently stirring will be bound. What you bind on earth is bound in heaven.

The Reason for Picking a Quarrel: The reason the devil does not leave you alone and uses every means and method to hurt your heart and belittle you is for one reason. To get you to stop looking at the Lord and to quit. Do not stop; that is victory.
Do Not Concern Yourself with What Does Not Yield Life: Every outcome is decided in one place:
You must focus only on what yields life.
A Person Whom Threats Do Not Work On: A threat only works when the other person controls something I do not want to lose. Therefore, threats do not work on a Christian.

Two Ways to Rely: There are two ways to rely on the blood. If you rely on it after sinning, it becomes the power that cleanses whiter than snow; if you rely on it before sinning, it becomes the victory that overcomes the test. The blood of the Lord is true power.

True Prayer: You must admit that you cannot say even a single word of sincere prayer without the help of the Holy Spirit, and you must rely on the Holy Spirit. 'In the same way, the Spirit helps us in our weakness. We do not know what we ought to pray for, but the Spirit himself intercedes for us through wordless groans.' - Romans 8:26

High Ground Battle: The fact that the owner of the high ground changes several times a day is proof of how important that area is. You are fighting an important battle. Rely on the Lord and rise again to fight.
You're Venting Your Anger? I'm Praying: Fight with the name of Jesus and a counter-spirit.
Don't Be Deceived, Overcome with Good: If you repay evil with evil, you fight but perish. Do not be deceived.

Situation Reversal: To reverse a situation, you must release a heart that is opposite to the situation. You must release trust in the midst of distrust, love in the midst of hate, and sacrifice in the midst of selfishness.
The Reason for Being Wicked and Lazy: It is because you are possessed by a wicked and lazy spirit. When the Word is not heard and criticism rises within you, receive a serious warning and repent.

When You Don't Even Have Strength to Eat - Stagnation: When you lack strength, you need to eat a proper meal, but you also need strength to be able to eat. You need to hear the Word to gain strength, but you need a basic strength for 'hearing' to be able to hear. Please pray for the strength and ability to hear.

When Disappointed: The disciples going to Emmaus were disappointed. Jesus made them fervent again. It was because He explained the Word to them. Please come to the place of hearing. If you do not hear and it is not heard, eventually, even if you serve a lot, you will become distracted and stagnant.

The Reason for Holiness: A doctor washing their hands cleanly several times before surgery is not trying to be holy alone. It is to save lives. Stay with the Lord and be holy. Only then will life and change come, will they not?

In the Time of Testing: The time of testing is the time given to darkness, so you must lower yourself and pray while waiting for that time of darkness to pass. Because Peter could not stay awake and pray, he went to the wrong place and fell into testing.
Repent Instead of Resenting: One hundred percent of people who resent have no repentance. That is why they perish. Since they look for the cause in others, they cannot essentially repent.

Life Without Seasoning: At a 'healing camp,' someone asked in wonder if it's okay not to add other seasonings or MSG while cooking. If you boil it long enough, the original flavor of the ingredients comes out. A focused life needs no artificial seasonings.
The Necessity of Helplessness: You must experience your own helplessness and hit rock bottom to learn to rely on the Lord. Only then do you feel that your own zeal is useless in this war.

How to Pray: If you keep failing even after resolving to pray, you must command the bonds to be broken in the name of Jesus and pray for the sake of prayer. You must bring this problem before the Cross.

Victory Does Not Come to Just Anyone: Victory comes to the one who wins with the strength the Lord gives. If you 'won' but it wasn't by the strength the Lord gave, it is not true victory but merely a quarrel.
True Spirit: Lord, please do not allow even any success in the world that would hinder me from being close to You.
The Reality of an Angry Person: An angry person, in particular, is not looking into the Lord's eyes. Because a person fixed on the Lord does not react to the greed of the self.
The Reason for Falling Repeatedly: It is not because temptation is strong, but because of the fear of falling. If you do not fear, the need to fall disappears.
The Secret to Absolute Victory: Love when you are angry. It is the most certain way to overpower the adversary.
You Will be Tested: You will receive tests. The reason it is a difficult test is because it is not an attempt to win, but a test that manifests that you are already a victor.

Simply Give Thanks: Yesterday, at the 'Fiery Saturday' meeting, after preaching to overpower the enemy's gate with gratitude and to be prepared to lose everything, my car window was smashed in the parking lot and I lost my LG G2 phone. God is a person with a sense of humor. Thinking simply: 1. He wants to give me a new car window, and 2. He means for me to turn off my phone for a while and hold onto prayer and the Word more. The new car window they installed looks refreshing.

It is Not a Choice, but a Lifeline: Gratitude is not a generous 'option' to be exercised when you feel like it. It is a lifeline you die without. The moment you miss gratitude and start resenting, you fail to see the truth and lose your heart to lies. Gratitude is the perspective that lets God be God and me be me.
Prayer: You can never win without prayer. That also means if you pray, you will surely win. 'But this kind does not go out except by prayer and fasting.' - Matthew 17:21
Ask This Way: Earnestly ask for the courage like a storm and the wisdom to overcome the enemy and the adversary.
The Victory of Faith: It is peace. That comes when you give thanks. Making one complain about situations and people is the work of the devil. Overcome and give thanks.

What You Truly Must Overcome: You must transcend your own cleverly rationalized desires. It is not because temptation is strong, but because you cannot love Jesus that you cannot abandon yourself and are deceived. However, you cannot even deceive the Lord. If you truly love the Lord, you will vomit blood in repentance.

A Foolish Act: The devil's most cunning scheme is the lie that problems should be solved by the flesh. Amalek, the eternal enemy, hides himself and attacks from behind. Rely only on the Lord. You will not make mistakes or have regrets.

Conflict: The flesh constantly demands peace and comfort. But the desire of the Spirit is to make me submit and become holy before the Lord. What you choose when these two desires conflict tells the story of how you will live. It is like the flesh wanting the world's pleasure while my spirit wants to put its face to the cold church floor and pray.

How to Overpower Situations and the Adversary: For Paul, who was racing only toward the Lord, people could no longer be a source of pain. That is why if our hope is only in this life, we are truly pitiful people. Hope in the Lord and let the Lord be your answer.

Weak, but Victorious: Humans are sinners, so they cannot help but crumble. Humans fail, but faith wins. 'For everyone born of God overcomes the world. This is the victory that has overcome the world, even our faith.' - 1 John 5:4

If You Surely Give Thanks and Praise, You Will Certainly Win: The Book of Revelation is marked by many persecutions and tribulations. It also contains the most scenes of praise and worship. Because acknowledging and exalting God in the face of testing and challenges is the most powerful response. Rejoicing in God is our strength. 'The joy of the Lord is your strength.' - Nehemiah 8:10

Pray Based on the Word: Pray by pointing to and holding onto the words of promise and hope. The devil will be stripped bare. 'Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.' - Philippians 4:6-7
Perfect Love Drives Out Fear: Being captured by fear is losing to the devil. Fear is emotionally denying the living presence of God. What kind of answer can there be when you deny God?

The Power That Always Wins: Resolve to be absolutely grateful and absolutely at peace in all things. No adversary will be able to deceive you or stand against you. Love brings about the greatest gratitude and peace.

The Purpose of Prayer: 'Simon, Simon, Satan has asked to sift all of you as wheat. But I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith may not fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.' - Luke 22:31-32 Stay awake and pray. If you do not want to lose what is truly important...

Spiritual Malnutrition: There is nothing more dangerous than trying to live today on yesterday's grace, spiritual experience, and anointing. It is like not feeling hunger even while suffering from malnutrition.
Prayer is combat and the most certain victory. I hope you will kindle the fire of prayer. - Sunday Meeting

If you don't like rice, eat ramen; if you don't like that, eat bread. However, if breathing becomes bothersome and the heartbeat feels tedious, that is a truly serious problem and a matter that must be resolved. These symptoms themselves—breathing and heartbeat—are not problems that can be covered up with something else just because they are difficult. A baby must fall thousands of times to gain the ability to walk even a few steps. If someone gave a baby a wheelchair for life as an alternative because walking is hard, that person would deserve to be cursed. It should be a structure where the person whose spirit is alive is thrilled and the one who isn't feels regret. Conversely, if those who are thrilled are restricted and cynical people prevail, the church suffocates. There are things to compromise on and things not to. I mean that juice should not be an alternative just because a baby cannot drink milk.

I hope you do not strive to defend yourselves. I hope you will yield the space so that the Lord alone may keep us. Our calling is not to defend ourselves but to lay everything down and know the Lord more.

If You Focus on the Problem, Evil Spirits Work: You must have experienced various negative imaginations arising when you are preoccupied with a problem. The moment I try to solve the problem, I become bound to it and start thinking as the evil spirits dictate. The Lord handles the resolution, and I give thanks.
Peter explains the intensity of Satan’s temptation using the analogy of a hungry lion. Here, Satan is called 'Diabolos,' which originally means 'the accuser.'

Prayer: The reason problems run rampant is because of a lack of prayer. You must raise the temperature of prayer until it burns and melts the problems away. When time is given, stop being a slave to useless corruption and cry out in prayer.

2 Peter 2:15-22 Thursday QT. Greed blinds your eyes. Verse 15: Balaam son of Beor is a representative of false teachers. The motivation for that false teaching is the wages of unrighteousness—that is, greed. People do not do things because of conviction; ultimately, they do them because of what they want—greed. Verse 16: Such humans are worse than donkeys. (Even though 4,000 years have passed, the Bible testifies to that notoriety.) Verse 17: The judgment upon them will be truly intense. Verse 18: Their doctrines are 1. subtley boasting, and 2. giving suggestive physical satisfaction. -> Human depravity has a fixed route established since the 'fruit of the knowledge of good and evil.' Once a path is made on a mountain, countless animals and people frequent it. It is truly difficult to erase a path once it is made. Verse 19: Physical pleasure is not freedom but becoming a slave to destruction, a slave to the devil, and a slave to sin, doing everything as commanded. The flesh must die and the spirit must live. (You'll get mind-controlled by a Dark Archon; it feels disgusting.) Verse 20: Knowing the truth and then falling away or apostatizing is no joke. Look at the people around you who betray the faith and consistently engage in depravity with pride. Simply put, if an angel falls, they don't become a semi-angel, they become a devil. Verse 21: 'It would have been better for them not to have known the way of righteousness' is not just in love songs. How severe must the judgment be for such words to be spoken? A foolish person does not know fear. Ignorance makes one brave. Verse 22: Let's not fall into such a dirty proverb while living (the dog, the pig). The Lord lived a life that fulfilled the Word and will of God. Will you follow the dog and pig? Or will you follow the Word and the Spirit? Decide now. * Greed turns a person into a beast and leads to such low-level decisions (encounters, actions, language, choices). What is truly pitiful is falling away from the truth within the church. If you worked and served terrifyingly hard but were led astray by lies, you made the porridge only to give it to the dog. Paul preached the gospel with fear and trembling. If boasting, lust, and jealousy are secretly lurking, you are acting as an enemy of the Cross.
A characteristic of a sick person is striving to prove oneself and always trying to condemn. When caught by the fatal disease called sin, one is bound to become a self-centered person.

When Potiphar's wife tempted him, Joseph refused, saying, 'How then could I do such a wicked thing and sin against God?' In Joseph's eyes of faith, God was visible, but He was not visible to that woman. When He is not visible, one lives recklessly. A person lives by what they gaze upon.
Focused-lost Christianity is more dangerous than immoral heresies. Just as a disease without subjective symptoms is more dangerous.

Partial things are meant to disappear when the complete thing comes. When knowing partially, one condemned, but when knowing the whole, one comes to forgive and have mercy. 'Ah! So that's why, how hard it must have been! It must have hurt a lot!' When one acts up thinking they know a little, they condemn everyone. Satan works through condemnation. However, when the complete person comes, they save life. They forgive. Love is reacting to the whole.
When revival comes, all the thorns of life are pulled out. Revival does not come without prayer, and prayer is done by those whose hearts are dripping with blood.
Anger blocks prayer and wisdom. However, if you pray when angry, He gives wisdom to dominate and settle the situation.

The Ability of Discernment: The most basic thing is that power recognizes power. Elisha's excellence did not start with doing some great work, but from recognizing Elijah. The most fundamental beginning starts from respecting those who should be respected. Discernment is power.
Though fiery trials are many, let us not be afraid; for the Savior's power is great, it will be more than enough to overcome. Hymn 376

Fire overcomes fire. The fire of revival overcomes the fire of war, and the fire of faith overcomes the fire of the flesh. The fire falling on the altar stayed only on the sacrifice, and the wrath poured out on the Cross was borne by Jesus alone. Let us become lives that are lightning rods.

Prayer: Lord, please grant our friends the wisdom to overcome the enemy's schemes, and make them not only at peace but bold in a world that intimidates and threatens. Even at this time, let them believe that there are tears being offered as the incense of prayer and that You are working every moment as they trust in You who hears those prayers; let them believe that He who began a good work in them will carry it on to completion until the day of Christ Jesus. I believe the Spirit of Truth will not leave them as orphans.

Unnecessary desires will be followed by confusion and unnecessary suffering. However, a great mission will be followed by obedience and reward. 'For the flesh desires what is contrary to the Spirit, and the Spirit what is contrary to the flesh. They are in conflict with each other, so that you are not to do whatever you want.' - Galatians 5:17

To live a life without comparison, you must discard the wrong plumb line of the heart. I am a person who lives by receiving answers. Elevating and lowering a person is entirely God's portion. Testing comes to tear down all emotions that did not come from God. It comes within God's permission and providence. God tears down everything and builds anew to heal us. - From Saturday Worship

Praise is even more difficult than criticism. Many great warriors of faith have collapsed there. That is why the path of compliments and praise is lined with the graves of many servants of the Lord. A beginner finds it hard to get past criticism, but a master overcomes praise. 'Stop regarding man, whose breath of life is in his nostrils; for why should he be esteemed?' - Isaiah 2:22

Attitude is More Important than the Problem: When facing a situation, one tries to prove oneself with a human heart and returns the wounded heart, giving and receiving scars. That happens because one does not know the target of the fight, the method of the fight, or God's intention. 'For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the powers of this dark world and against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly realms.' - Ephesians 6:12

An airplane must run 800m at over 350km/h to fly. Without absolute speed and absolute distance, even the best airplane is less convenient than a Tesco cart. How can there be spiritual warfare without the absolute prayer that must be given to the Lord? It's just spiritual child's play. It's as if the airplane is parked in the Tesco parking lot because it can't fly, and the neighborhood kids are throwing stones at it. Do not crawl; soar.

Philippians 1:29: 'For it has been granted to you on behalf of Christ not only to believe in him...' Reading up to here makes the heart race with expectation of what grace God will give. The second half is '...but also to suffer for him.' ㅠㅠ Christians have an inevitable fight. The greater the fight, the greater the victory. Hallelujah. 'This is no afternoon athletic contest that we’ll walk away from and forget about in a couple of hours. This is for keeps, a life-or-death fight to the finish against the Devil and all his angels.' (Ephesians 6:12, The Message)
As long as we live, testing is not absent. We will also see ourselves shaking as we fall into tests. However, the wind and rain blow and the dog barks, but the train ignores it all and runs.

Satan deceives. We resolve, but we are easily deceived. We are easily deceived in situations, in rumors, and in emotions. Only when we hear the voice of the Lord—who knows me better than the subjective confidence I boast of—can we avoid being deceived. Paul exhorts the saints fighting on the final day to put on the belt and sword of truth. All lies will crumble before the truth.

Satan tries to stop us from advancing by any means. He causes 'mental breakdowns,' brings tribulations, and creates difficulties in relationships. Problems have a characteristic of bursting out all at once. Suddenly you get an urgent call and have to go to school, but that day the train decides to play turtle as if on purpose. Only I feel rushed inside the train. And on such a day, the stomach hurts. But there is no bathroom. 'They were all trying to frighten us, thinking,
' - Nehemiah 6:9 You must not be discouraged then. Nehemiah ignores them and goes. 'But I prayed,
' He prays that way, believes that way, and moves forward. If I prayed for strength, He has given it.

Satan can never defeat us. Because we have 'that love.' No matter how strong the devil is, he cannot defeat our love. Because he can never be patient. Spiritual warfare is won if you do not give up. Since Satan has a nature that can never endure. 'This is love: not that we loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son as an atoning sacrifice for our sins.' - 1 John 4:10 'Love... always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres.' - 1 Corinthians 13:3, 7

Satan trying to condemn us through hidden, secret sins is an attempt to say, 'I am a worthless being.' However, those regrettable mistakes of mine actually prove that I am hurt, angry, and especially lonely. Satan takes this and tries to block us from coming before the Lord by making us doubt and hide alone, but the Lord instead loves us more, forgives us, and has mercy on us. What I truly need and the only thing I must have is His love, and He has fully paid the price on the Cross.
Truly Words of Love: Speak the words that Jesus living inside you wants to say to that person.

People live bound to cause and effect—to causal relationships. That is the life of general principles. However, when the Truth and the Spirit come, they shatter all such relationships and lead us to fight against darkness while following the Spirit’s guidance regarding sin, righteousness, and judgment.
Being misunderstood by people is not very important. If I do not misunderstand until the end, the devil will return empty-handed and one more of God's people will be gained. Overcome evil with good.

If you look at people, you are bound to be always chased. Being chased is not the problem; the problem is that you always make a 'bad move' (mistake) in that situation. You would see God's working if you just stayed quiet, but becoming anxious and saying or doing things you shouldn't is the problem. Look only to the Lord. Then you will be able to be still.
If a person's actions change into something that pleases the Lord, things that were difficult to handle will be resolved. It will be a miracle.

What blocks a mission is not one's weakness, persecution, or a sense of disqualification. It is not health or circumstances either. Only Satan hates and blocks the mission. I hope you have the wisdom to ignore everything else and beat up only 'that one guy.' I only beat up Satan.

After achieving a great victory in the war against Amalek, Saul ironically walks a decisive path of decline. From this day forward, he never meets the prophet Samuel again. The greatest crisis comes when you think you have succeeded greatly and are doing well. This means life can crumble even after winning a war.

The most effective way to resist Satan is to keep one's own place. Since he is originally a fallen spirit who did not keep his own place, he fears a believer who loyally keeps theirs. No matter how flashy or enviable a position is, if it is not the place given to me, it is much better to just boil ramen at home. 'And the angels who did not keep their positions of authority but abandoned their proper dwelling—these he has kept in darkness, bound with everlasting chains for judgment on the great Day.' - Jude 1:6

Life is a battle of initiative. Who takes that initiative determines victory or defeat. The Lord is not someone who helps us take the initiative of our lives. He is the one who should rightfully govern my life instead of me. When this war becomes the Lord's war instead of my war, victory is achieved.
When a wounded heart is hidden, it rots and calls for spirits of darkness, but when given to the Lord, it becomes a sacrifice He is pleased with.

Do not be overpowered by the situation; instead, govern the situation. To fully exercise the governance the Lord has delegated, you must overcome the wicked enemy, and that power is governing by 'God's overflowing grace.' From the start, darkness is no match for us.

Simple Faith: Ultimately, the desires of the world and the longing for God are in conflict. The desires of the world lead to a life of envying the world, but the longing for God makes the world feel ashamed. Long for Him. You will win.

How to Win: In matches, there is such a thing as 'momentum.' It means the opportunity to win. That moment when you are exhausted to death is the perfect chance. If you push hard in the last 5 minutes, you can lead the match to victory. The most foolish thing is failing to push even after catching the momentum. Then a match that could have ended in 5 minutes must start all over again. When you catch spiritual 'momentum,' you must go all the way.

마귀의 회유 (The Devil's Conciliation): He says since there are many problems, let's take a break. He says you've done enough, it's sufficient. He says for now, let's stop, suspend the work you are doing, pull ourselves together, and then go. Even in prison, Paul preached the gospel to the palace guard and strengthened the church through letters. I hope you will never stop. 'As a result, it has become clear throughout the whole palace guard and to everyone else that I am in chains for Christ.' - Philippians 1:13
The Reason One Cannot Stop: Neither tribulation, nor testing of the heart, nor scars, nor harsh words and cynicism can stop a saint. Because it is not I, but Jesus living in me who advances.
Do not live just as you were born; live as one who has been trained.
The Kind of Fight: A Christian fights the good fight. A good fight is one that saves lives, looks toward the glory of the conversion of souls, and brings recovery. Any other fight is an evil fight.

Are You at Peace? You must be at peace. Did Jesus not bear the Cross? 'When peace like a river attendeth my way, when sorrows like sea billows roll; whatever my lot, thou hast taught me to say, it is well, it is well with my soul.' Though that devil opens his mouth and rushes to swallow us, Jesus is our captain, so we will fight to the end and win. - Hymn 413

Definition of Being Established: The Lord has established you high and will establish you again. The true meaning of elevating you is to establish souls with an appearance like the lower Jesus. A 'fall' of humility will be created as much as the height you have been raised.
When Faith is Needed: Faith is not needed when making money, passing school entrance exams, or gaining popularity in the world. When it is truly needed is 'when you must win.' Win. Win by faith.

The weapons of the Spirit include honesty, humility, truth, grace, love, prayer, obedience, community, healthy dependence, the Word of God, and following the guidance of the Spirit. On the other hand, the weapons of the flesh include excessive anger, deceit, criticism, shame, hatred, pride, secret hiding, being self-centered, becoming defensive, and a healthy-less 'I don't need you' attitude. The spiritual warfare we are fighting is 'no afternoon athletic contest that we’ll walk away from and forget about in a couple of hours. This is for keeps, a life-or-death fight...' (Ephesians 6:12, The Message)
Heart! Win: Ask yourself. Are you afraid? If you are afraid, you must win. If you are not afraid, you have already won.

A person seeking world recognition will suffer shipwreck in their faith. World recognition is pride and a great temptation that is hard to overcome. But the one who lives by faith receives comfort from heaven. No matter what they encounter, they will not fear and will overcome. - Hymn 434

The world and Satan challenged Jesus regarding His being the Son of God. They told Him to prove it if He could. A life lived while proving oneself is tiring. Since Jesus had the testimony of God the Father, He had no need to prove it to the world. Who I am—my identity—naturally appears if I stay with the Lord. 'Be still, and know that I am God.' I hope you become a life that testifies rather than proves.

One enjoys the thrill, thinking they can open their heart a little to the world and close it immediately if necessary. In the very moment you think that, you become a slave to the devil. It is the devil's typical method. The Bible identifies the problem of lust not as a matter of self-control or ethics, but as a problem of an evil spirit working behind it. Lust is the work of the devil. I remember an old pastor who had been in ministry for a long time once saying that once one becomes addicted to lust, it is difficult to cut it off, and when cutting it off, a heart that risks its life is needed. I think this: it is difficult even with a 'resolution to risk one's life.' It is possible only by the 'law of the Spirit.' (Romans 8:1-4). And I want to testify like this: if you fully rely on the Holy Spirit, it is a war that can sufficiently be won (have courage). Two things must be kept in mind: 1. Discard the vague inference that current sinful tendencies will be tolerated. Lust is a serious crime and you will surely pay a price beyond imagination. You cannot serve it and the Lord at the same time. (John 5:30) 2. Guilt actually calls for more sin. Do not trust your own will, but trust the 'law of the Spirit.' The 'Lord's blood' certainly has power. One certain fact is that 'lust' is like holding a fire in your chest that must be discarded immediately; though it cannot be done by my will and resolution, you must believe that the 'fire of the Holy Spirit' can give more than enough victory.

I hope you will be mission-minded people who choose victory in the Lord over success in the world. I hope you will be young people who overcome evil with good through true 'skill' that the Lord is with, rather than envying those building 'specs' through self-development. The era of competing with shells has passed; I hope you compete with the essence in a world like a live broadcast, not through lip-syncing or marketing.

Mission Over Efficiency: Instead of striving to use my time productively, I must do what I rightfully ought to do. Just as breathing and the heart beating happens, a person who loves God and has received that heart stands where they should stand, does what they should do, and stays with those they should be with. However, such a life and such decisions frequently face criticism from lust and worldly values.
There is a light that drives out the darkness swallowing the world, and if there is a sign of some spiritual work, there is always a leader standing there holding a candle.
Become salt and melt away. Deny yourself and take up your cross daily. You will disappear and be forgotten, but the Lord will surely find you.

Somali pirates do not touch American merchant ships. Because they refuse to negotiate with terrorists, it's fundamentally impossible to get money. Do not react to rumors and talk. Then evil powers will no longer have strength within relationships.
The Greatest Miracle is the Change of the Soul: There must be a longing for God's glory. There is no greater glory than a sinner receiving change. I see God's working through souls.

Hidden evil causes many wounds. If poison is dissolved in medicine, it does the work of saving but also the work of killing as a side effect. You must look back at yourself and lay things down and pray so that the poison that was there before does not work and hide the Lord's glory.
Do not crumble easily. You must resist a challenge intensely regardless of the outcome. Only then is it easy to recover at the next step even if you fall.

In sports, what's important is dominating the flow of the match. King Nebuchadnezzar tries to dominate the situation by threatening with a furnace seven times hotter. Breaking such worldly challenges is done through 1. the 'faith' that the Lord will rescue, and 2. the 'sacrifice'—the Cross—of not changing even 'if He does not.' When you take up the faith of total trust in God and the Cross of 'if I die, I die,' you experience the Lord who overwhelms the enemy and controls the situation. 'God has put all things under the authority of Christ and has made him head over all things for the benefit of... God has put everything under the control of Christ.' - Ephesians 1:22
Victory is determined by how you fill time and empty space. Will you fill it with Jesus? Or will you yield it to laziness and meaninglessness?

You can march as a defeated soldier who is barely dragged along, or you can go while singing a song of victory. The clear reason for the former is failing to serve the Lord alone and harboring vain hearts, being filled with the world rather than the Spirit. For a life that offers itself before the Lord, what is there to fear or find difficult?
Stay awake and pray so that you do not fall into testing.

The life of faith is a life of prayer. Ultimately, the battle is a matter of whether you continue to persist in prayer. Anyway, Satan tries to keep you from praying through various other issues. However, a person pressing toward the goal continues to go to the place of prayer.
The hardest thing in faith is doing nothing. From an unbeliever's perspective, prayer is doing nothing. But if you lift your spiritual eyes, the greatest and biggest work is happening in the air.

Faith does not end just because you hit a home run once. Day by day, it moves forward between choice and choice. If you live while choosing according to the Spirit, you will not fulfill the desires of the flesh or the anxiety caused by vain loss.

A person who has passed through the Cross is bound to seek the Holy Spirit. Because they know by faith they have died, but the gap between reality and the Word is too big. Being filled with the Holy Spirit starts from longing.

An African chief was gifted a Toyota 4-way. He boasted of using it wonderfully. He said he listens to music on CDs and goes inside to turn on the air conditioner to rest when it's hot. And then, he sets off again on the long road by foot. A life that doesn't pray 'shovels' (works in vain). It's amazing that despite having gone through that, one cannot let go of the attachment to shoveling. Even though it's been a long time since one was deeply involved in the organization and it's not a matter of living the faith for just a day or two.

You must never lose to evil. Even if you lose to evil, the Lord will restore you again, but now you must leap from the grace that restores to the grace that overflows. Not losing to evil means not committing sin and instead overwhelming evil with good. 'Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.' - Romans 12:21

Evil must be overcome. However, if you overcome evil with evil, that is not winning, but losing. Evil must be overcome with good. The greatest victory is mercy and forgiveness. That is why the Cross is the greatest victory. 'Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.' - Romans 12:21
Evil must be overcome. It must be overcome with good—the goodness given by the indwelling Holy Spirit.

A Traditional Exam: The traditional test given by the devil is about priorities. It is telling you to turn stones into bread. If the devil tested even the Son of God, would he pass over a weak human? There is a huge difference between 'eating then doing' and 'doing then eating.' Living according to mere desire (profit/利) and seeking His kingdom and His righteousness (righteousness/義) not only has a fundamental difference but will create an eternal difference.

Carry a list for intercessory prayer in your inner pocket or handbag. Prayer gains strength if you do it often. Accumulated prayer is more useful and powerful than the traditions of a prestigious family.

Weaknesses are hidden, and good points are exaggerated. That is the principle of marketing. However, we instead boast of our weaknesses. Through our weaknesses, the Lord’s power flows out. That is why when I am weak, then I am strong.
Darkness removes windows, clocks, and mirrors for a person. It is to prevent them from looking back at themselves. In fact, there are no windows, clocks, or mirrors in casinos.
The strongholds of darkness have crumbled. We have won. 'The great dragon was hurled down—that ancient serpent called the devil, or Satan, who leads the whole world astray.' - Revelation 12:9
If you endure when it is difficult, you will surely win. Foolishness is giving up on a game you are bound to win. Giving up is the work of the devil, and enduring is the work of the Holy Spirit.

Confirm the Faith: The life of faith is not about avoiding the storm well, but about facing it well. Holding onto the Lord no matter what the world says is true faith, and this is only proven when the storm comes.

A Greater Sin: The greatest sin is not being thrilled. Since you aren't thrilled, how can you have the means to overcome the fierce attacks of the world's sin? You must burn bright enough to melt the world's darkness and scorch the temptation of sin.

Ephesians - 4 Things the Secular World Pursues (Ephesians 2:1, 3). Even if the world promises happiness, it deceives and tries to rule over souls (threats + conciliation) through these 4 things: 1. The power of sin - 'you were dead in your transgressions and sins.' 2. Worldly values - 'in which you used to live when you followed the ways of this world.' 3. The work of the devil - 'and of the ruler of the kingdom of the air, the spirit who is now at work in those who are disobedient.' 4. Physical desires - 'gratifying the cravings of our flesh and following its desires and thoughts.' - Ephesians 2:1, 3

The Living Spirit: Man became a living being from the dust through God's breath of life, but returns to dust (flesh) again because of sin. However, when one hears God's voice, they live again and become a person of the Spirit.
The Final Showdown: Our showdown is not in the world. The true outcome is decided when we take our last breath and stand before the Lord.
Composure does not come on its own, nor is it given because circumstances allow it. Composure is the spoil of diligent 'spiritual warfare' and the result of 'priorities' that look only to the Lord.
Fly high so that inferiority complexes and victim mentalities cannot touch you. At 350km up to 800m.

When engaging in spiritual warfare, persisting regardless of the environment becomes the key to victory or defeat. Love is patient. If you persist with love, the fortified stronghold is bound to crumble. Keep going. They can never keep going.
