In spiritual warfare, the most awake person and group are 'affected' first. Because they are at the front lines. First is the mission field and second are the intercessors. There is no need to think of the test as strange. It is a wrong fasting period toward a wrong god.

In the movie A Beautiful Mind, Professor John Nash overcomes the things he wants to see and the hallucinations shown because of them, and wins the Nobel Prize. As he is about to leave with his wife after the acceptance speech, the three figures from his hallucinations are there. But he doesn't choose them. Living by faith is not being free from all temptation. It is simply not choosing it. Faith is a choice.

The moment you do not focus on Jesus and the Word, you have already entered into transgression; you cannot realize it because of pride, your vision is obscured, and like a car with broken brakes, wicked and corrupt thoughts will seethe as a result. 1 Timothy 6:4-5. However, if you become a life for which the Lord alone is sufficient, you will become holy in all these things. 1 Timothy 6:6

Jesus becomes stronger the more He is attacked. Because from the start, faith is about relying on the Lord instead of my own strength anyway. This is why one becomes stronger when it's difficult and why the Lord's power is stronger when I am weak. Strike me a little more, until the edge is sharp and blue.

The reason Jesus came is to destroy the works of the devil. The reason He called us is also to save lives and smash darkness, and this work paradoxically becomes most powerful when we give up on ourselves.

There's an old saying that it's not the person who lies, but the money that lies. It means the original intention is not like that, but the desperate reality and situation drive the person to be that way. If I do not have spiritual assets—Jesus—I will be angry, frustrated, and easily hurt by even small stimuli. Without the resurrected Jesus, all other problems are just byproducts of His absence. 'The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you all.' - 2 Timothy 4:22

What You Must Do When Being Lowered: There is something you must do when you are being lowered. That is where destiny changes. What you must possess in a time of obscurity is skill. When you are well-fed and well-treated, it is difficult for a sharp spirituality to emerge. Dwell in the Presence that cannot be taken away even if everything else is given.
Bad Habits: One doesn't say,
Forget breathing, one can't even skip food. If you miss the place of grace because of work, it becomes a habit. You are growing a truly terrible habit.
This path we walk is not safe. Among many challenges and tests, we sometimes see 'casualties.' However, it is not hopeless. Because His sovereignty exists. Be prepared to die.

Do you know why we fight with the people we love? Because of selfish greed. When we seek to satisfy the self, war never ends in homes, among friends, and in relationships. To stop this, one must die, and dying means accepting that one has already died. In fact, we have already died together with the Lord.

We are shocked when people we thought were famous become people of that other world overnight. It wasn't the trial that came and killed them. Most 'voluntarily' gave up in the face of the trial and fear. Trials and fear have never once spontaneously or actively ruined or killed a person.
The fact that we crumbled under sin proves one thing for certain. The fact that I lost focus on looking at the Lord. As long as I look to the Lord, I do not crumble even if the enemy comes toward me.

Every day we hear the devil's whisper telling us to prove ourselves. He came to Jesus as well, tempting Him to turn stones into bread and prove He was the Son of God. What would you do? If I had such power... I would probably pretend to pray with the stone and then plan to smash the devil's head with it. Apostle Paul said in his letters that he does not preach himself but the Lord. Then the Lord said He would also testify of us before the angels.

A Sin That Doesn't Seem Like Sin, but is Large: The most effective way to teach young people to pray is to enter the fiery pit of prayer together. Also, the Lord gives grace so that if someone says they'll die if they pray, He kindly leads them to a situation where they feel they'll die if they don't pray. Prayer allows one to receive the power of the Holy Spirit, smash the devil's works, and make difficult tasks easy. To stop praying is a sin.

The Reason They Demand a Fee but Do Not Speak of Repentance: Fortune-tellers and shamans do not tell you to repent. Because a characteristic of idolatry is 'immediacy'—fulfilling the desires I want with a certain payable price—it is a short-term effect and temptation. However, it cannot satisfy or fill me, and instead, its end is death. Please distinguish between bait and food.

When the enemy comes toward me, should I attack? Or should I defend? David confessed this way: 'You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies.' - Psalm 23. If one thinks they are being chased and attacked by the enemy, they would just stab and kill Saul when he comes into the cave, but David thought he was under God's guidance. Do not respond to an attack with a counter-attack, but be invited to the dinner the Lord is preparing.

How to Overcome Evil with Good: The characteristics of a person who takes the Word as a 'light' and a 'lamp' are peace and boldness. Because they work according to the Lord's standards and leave the rest to God unconditionally regardless of the outcome. It means using a completely different way to fight than the world's ways. It means winning with love and forgiveness. And it means winning surely and always.

Do not live as you want, but live by requesting what is needed. Throwing everything away and running is what is 'wanted,' and receiving God's power and authority at the place I stand is what is 'needed.' I hope you will be young people who overcome darkness with the sword of the Word.

I hope you will be powerful young people who wake up souls stagnant in spiritual lethargy by compromising with reality, seeking a crown without suffering and commitment without entrustment. - Winter 1995
No one in this world admits they are jealous. They only say they are executing justice. Jealousy is being saddened by another person's joy. The two solutions to resolve this are...

No one can force me to give up my dreams just because they are hard to achieve. I hope you do not fear a fight you cannot win or worry about pain you cannot endure. Also, I hope you dream even if it cannot be achieved. Miracles happen in the place where no one looks.

Isaiah 14:13-14 reveals the original sin of the devil. It is pride. In those short verses, the personal pronoun 'I' appears 5 times. Have you ever met the devil? The devil does not stand in the way of a person going in the same direction as him. When the word 'I' comes out of my mouth, the devil is working. When I say I'm being treated this way, how can they dare do this to 'me,' and 'I' can't stand to see such a thing, I am approaching the original sin of the devil. It must not be 'I,' but 'He.' Especially for sisters (Ladies), if you keep emphasizing 'I' (Na-ga), you'll become 'Ga-ga,' Lady Gaga.

In Isaiah chapter 14, the devil's confession appears. In those short two verses, 'I will' appears 5 times. A hardened heart that says 'I will' is one where the initiative is always with 'me.' When you go out into the wilderness, receive grace, and are broken and shattered, you become a person who follows the Lord's will with a soft heart. Leave those with 'strong teeth' (stubborn talk) alone. All justifications and rationalizations will be shattered in the face of wilderness suffering.

Holy tears that break oneself control fleshly lusts, extinguish the fire of discord, cleanse dirty desires, lower proud eyes, refrain from vain words, pursue intimate fellowship, love silence, and bear the fruit of prayer.

There will be small challenges, but there are also big tests. However, the principle of overcoming them is consistent. It is to act by faith. It is becoming unconcerned with shame and tribulation because of Jesus.

One cannot hear a sound with too much noise. If you are too busy to hear the Lord's voice and your interest is elsewhere, He calls you out to a place where He can be heard loudly. That is the wilderness. That place without water, parched, and with no one. There, finally, the Word is heard and seen, and you train to live not by convenience but by hearing and faith. 'For we live by faith, not by sight.' - 2 Corinthians 5:7 [sic - 2 Cor 5:7]

If a Zerg user decides to do a Lurker timing rush on a somewhat close map, you'll be hit even if you know it's coming. It's easy to hear the devil's voice even when you clearly know it's a deception. Because it is so human, so persuasive. I hope you listen to the Lord's voice.

It's not that I don't understand that our friends live in an era where it is so difficult to keep the faith. There is physical weakness, there is a sinful nature, and Satan works. But you can win. Because you must win. So you will win, and I will make you win.

The enemy has never once been stronger. It's because Israel instead became weaker. Whether you serve God or serve idols is the watershed between peace and disaster. Whether you will worship your emotions, desires, and wounds, or serve the only Lord, is the true war.

Through the blood shed by the Son, Jesus, who was within the perfect love of the Almighty Father, He showed His greatest love. By the power of the blood, the power of sin is shattered, every test is overcome, and one is not deceived by the enemy's lies. The power of the blood is the greatest victory and power, whether on God's side or for man.

Do not take off the full armor of God. The enemy makes you take off your best by giving you good things and satisfaction. Do not give up the only way to fight an invisible opponent—an opponent not touched as flesh and blood.
I hope you put on the full armor of God. The angels delivering the prayers and my fellow workers will do the same. The answer to prayer will be swift.

Become an all-weather believer. One who hopes only in the Lord regardless of spiritual foul weather, regardless of worldly glory, relationships and reactions, or circumstances and environment... A person with the power to ignore it all, a person who makes the devil depressed, a person who melts the dark world with love even in a difficult position where they can't save face—you, who love the Lord, are the solution for this era. Acts 13:2: 'While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said,

Even warships sometimes return to port to dock. They receive supplies and repairs. Because they must go out again to fight. If it rests at the port and rests at sea, it's not a warship but a cruise ship. Apostle Paul says the 'good fight' lasts a lifetime. We were called as combative beings. If you intend to believe, you will fight, and if you fight by faith, you will surely win.

It is wisdom to discard what will rot in order to hold onto what is truly precious. Faith is looking only to the Lord and maintaining the path given to me regardless of reactions and the environment. It is loving Jesus. Then you will be free from all testing.
To be more honest, you are weak enough to be incompetent. But you are strong in the Lord. Even infinitely so...
Ultimately, life is a battle of the destination. Living lost in the world and going where the world goes, or being crazy for Jesus and living with Him. I race toward the Lord.

When passing through the narrow gate, there are times when the gate becomes even narrower. Then, you must lay everything down and hold only onto what is essential. You only need to focus on two things: fearing the Lord and resisting the devil. When fighting, you need the focus of beating up just 'one guy.' 'It seemed good to the Holy Spirit and to us not to burden you with anything beyond the following requirements...' - Acts 15:28

Anyone can do what they like. But a professional does what they do with passion until they like it. So they do not particularly mind or care about the obstacles and challenges blocking the path of faith; they ignore them. Ignoring them and running is power.
Good choices lead to victory in battle. When we choose the Cross and crucify the flesh, we win this battle in a good way.

There may be feelings of frustration, but there is no frustration. In Jesus, everything becomes hope. He is almighty, and as long as I do not permit frustration, despair is impossible in Jesus. I hope you testify of the Lord who works among us by faith to the darkness that makes us see the phenomena. It will retreat in shame.

Sin hinders our spiritual growth. If sin is not governed, spiritual growth stops. If a life of repeatedly committing sin behind the scenes continues even after receiving much grace, it is like pouring water into a broken jar. Come out and pray. So that the Lord can touch you.
When you commit sin, Satan tells you to disappear. But the Holy Spirit tells you to repent.

The secret to overcoming sin and the wicked world is to fear the Lord. If you always hope in the Lord with all your heart and rely on the blood, a life of falling becomes a life of praise and testimony.

The only way to overcome sin is to offer myself to the Lord. Then I become a servant of the Lord, and the Lord becomes my Lord, and I am under grace. If you win the battle of who is the master, you have won everything. 'Don’t you know that when you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, you are slaves of the one you obey—whether you are slaves to sin, which leads to death, or to obedience, which leads to righteousness?' - Romans 6:16

You resolve several times not to sin, but you crumble every time. Being angry and complaining means you have already become the devil's food and are entering the war having lost. 'Refrain from anger and turn from wrath; do not fret—it leads only to evil.' - Psalm 37:8

Before lamenting that you crumbled under sin, you must repent for not having looked at the Lord fully. Our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but a struggle against the spirit. So faith is a battle of perspective.

Trying to overcome sin and temptation by yourself is a vain effort. You can never win. It's the idea of keeping trash and spraying perfume. Trash must be thrown away, and the nature must be crucified daily.
The reason you can be bold despite the challenge of sin is knowing and believing that the Lord holds you. The Lord never loses us. The Cross overcomes sin.

3 Functions of Confession: 1. It lets you see who God is. 2. It lets you see who you are through the Lord's eyes. 3. Because you know God and look at yourself, it binds all tribulations and enemies and leads to victory. Confess without ceasing that you believe in the Lord, love Him, and are loved by Him every moment. That will be your strength and power. Every time the enemy touches a wound, if you confess, it instead becomes an opportunity to draw closer to the Lord. Hallelujah.
There are aspects of the old self that must be shared and resolved only with the Lord. That sin is not 'overcome.' It must die.

Even as it is, there isn't enough time to get close to the Lord; there is no need to take time to meditate on the things the devil does and his filth. Jews look for signs and Greeks look for wisdom, but we preach and focus only on Jesus crucified. If you know Jesus, you already know and discern everything.
Drawing close to the Lord is power. To do that, you must give something up. The most critical challenge right now is media. Be temperate. It is paying the price.

There are times when the Lord tells you to pour water into a jar with a hole in the bottom. Keep this in mind: that is not the end. It seems like nothing is happening even though you pour water, but during that time, your muscles grow and your constitution changes. At the Lord's time, explosive revival, recovery, and life powers manifest.

Try risking your life for the Lord. Half-baked sins and temptations will be no match. A person prepared to die for the Lord cannot be moved by paltry worldly pleasures. Loving Jesus is dying for Jesus.
The Lord is a good Lord. Believe that the Lord is fighting for you. Whether it is pain you chose or pain caused by others, the Lord does not ignore us.

People who do not fully rely on the Lord but hold onto their own abilities are all 'oppressed' people. Whether they know it or not, they are not free under spiritual oppression, are not at peace, and suffer from diseases. Let's hold onto the truth with our lives. So that darkness retreats and light shines...

Relying on the Lord and rising once again is true power. Never give up! 'David was greatly distressed because the men were talking of stoning him; each one was bitter in spirit because of his sons and daughters. But David found strength in the Lord his God.' - 1 Samuel 30:6

Arguing over the rights and wrongs of a problem occurring is truly a foolish act. Because more important than the surface cause of the problem is my reaction to it—that is, faith. The Lord wants to see my faith when the storm rises. 'Where is your faith?' - Mark 4:40
One who holds onto the Lord's mission can be free from the temptation of sin and can keep themselves.
There is a necessary prayer to seek the Lord's presence, and there is a war that is won only when the Lord's presence is there. Now is the time to pray.
The greatest hindrance to relying on and trusting the Lord's omnipotence is not Satan, but the efforts of my self. Believing in the Lord begins from admitting my own powerlessness.

No one can harm me without the Lord's permission. However, when I react and respond to each one, that evil of mine will be the very thing that harms me. Persecution instead makes me a precious believer.

Mainly, the policy regarding youth is to 'do according to faith.' It means not to do it beyond one's strength, but according to grace. However, there are things I want to do beyond my strength. There are things you must focus on so intensely that sparks fly from your eyes. That is saving souls. When the missionaries come this time, please serve them with all your strength and sincerity. I hope you will be a source of comfort and strength to those who are fighting spiritual warfare, death, and curses in distant lands to save souls. People who lay down their lives for the Lord are precious. Those who serve such people are equally precious. Such devotion is precious, and those who recognize such devotion are also precious. I hope you will commit to offerings, service, and prayer. As a youth worker, this is my first strong request. I have often been less than active in other requests just for this day. More than anything, I challenge you for the sake of souls and yourselves.
To win against the most fearful thing, there must be a most powerful being. The most fearful target is my self. That is why the most powerful Lord holds me.

For three years in Ephesus, Paul worked during the day and taught at night, shedding tears. (Acts 20:31) Also, he served the Lord with zeal and a burning truth. (Romans 12:11) He served the Lord with a heart consistently on fire and unceasing tears throughout decades of ministry. Why? Simply put, because he spent decades as if every day was at the 'scene' (field). 'Remember that for three years I never stopped warning each of you night and day with tears.' - Acts 20:31 'Never be lacking in zeal, but keep your spiritual fervor (fervent in spirit - NASB), serving the Lord.' - Romans 12:11 Day by day, fervent in spirit, with a heart burning in the Holy Spirit... we gather to pray and the truth of the Cross is proclaimed. 7:30 as usual.
Rejoicing in things worth rejoicing over is a truly joyful thing. But grieving when it's not something to grieve over is a truly sad thing. If there are scars, one cannot react correctly.

Now is like the time of Elijah; the most wicked king in Israel's history was Ahab. Yet, the most powerful prophet in history was Elijah, from the same era. The darker the age, the more powerful the light that drives out that darkness. Who will become powerful?

Wisdom transcends the world's law of cause and effect. For example, it is realizing the connection between two seemingly unrelated themes: that if you do not honor the Lord, adversaries arise and war never ends. Before looking at the problems in my reality, I must realize the cause in my attitude of fearing the Lord.
You may feel it is overwhelming to do the Lord's work while juggling work and studies. But later, you realize that not living that way and enjoying the world is even harder.

If you truly want to win—while in worldly logic the winner is the influential or successful person—the winning the Lord wants is repentance. The one who repents is the one who wins. 'Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first... To the one who is victorious, I will give the right to eat from the tree of life, which is in the paradise of God.' - Revelation 2:5, 7

To offer true prayer, time for deep fellowship—focusing the heart and staying in His presence—must be invested. However, this is hard at first. It is because it is a spiritual matter. The desires of the flesh do not permit it. Unless we spend sufficient time in the secret place, we fall into danger, lose peace, and are defeated in balanced human relationships. A person who fellowships with the Lord in the secret place will not have that light fade even in daily life.
You must focus. Because the Lord tells you important things. If you hold onto them, you win.
Faith is a battle of reaction. It is reacting actively to God's Word rather than to the environment and emotions.

I hope you are not chased. It's not that the devil is chasing us, but rather we were called to 'chase' those guys. We will expose all lies and schemes and burn them in Jesus' name. [Matthew 16:18] 'And I tell you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates (pylai) of Hades (Hades) will not overcome (katischyō) it.' * Hades: power of death, * Pylai: gate, * Katischyō: to yield. Looking at the original text, it means to aggressively smash the gate of the devil's power. It means to attack, not defend.

In a hymn, there are lyrics that say: 'Before you have gone all the way on your path, do not sheathe the sword in your hand.' When it's time to push, you must push. Otherwise, when you want to push later, you won't be able to. That's why I'm pushing even harder this week than last week.
It's a great relief. Since the Lord alone is my strength and shield. Though it's a brutal battlefield, I feel warmth and peace within the Lord's presence.

A truly difficult person is a stubborn person. It's the same from the devil's perspective. A person who reports and discusses every little thing with God; even though the Lord knows everything without being told, a person who repeatedly reports every single second. Probably, such a person would be unmanageable. Devil:
When the accuser picks a quarrel with fear and frustration, say this quietly:
'Whoever dwells in the shelter of the Most High will rest in the shadow of the Almighty. I will say of the Lord,
Surely he will save you from the fowler’s snare and from the deadly pestilence.' - Psalm 91:1-3
When first following Jesus, the resolution is that I will not fear anything and nothing will stop me on that road toward the Lord.

Almost every problem a young person faces is something that would be solved by being 'filled.' It's not broken; it just turned off. If my inside is not filled with Jesus, it's correct to see it as already being in a severe state of testing. You will sin, hate, become discouraged, and be anxious for no reason.
You must hold onto faith every moment. Because the moment you lose faith for a while, you fall into testing.
Praise tests a person. One is tested on whether they will become proud after receiving more praise or jealous after receiving less praise.

Kathryn Kuhlman would struggle and weep behind the stage after manifesting power and miracles. It was to fight against pride. After becoming busy and famous, one can become a 'nobody' in heaven. I hope you lower yourselves.

Deception and schemes (the devil's scheme) are the devil's main business, and even now, it is a very effective tactic for deceiving the people close to us. The Bible expresses them as flaming arrows. What we need are the shield of faith (trust) and the Spirit of wisdom and revelation. Never be deceived. 'I keep asking that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the glorious Father, may give you the Spirit of wisdom and revelation, so that you may know him better.' - Ephesians 1:17 '...so that you can take your stand against the devil’s schemes' (Ephesians 6:11), 'In addition to all this, take up the shield of faith, with which you can extinguish all the flaming arrows of the evil one' (Ephesians 6:16).

Because the Christian's fight is not one of flesh and blood, it is not solved by worldly methods. The powerful weapons given to us as we resist all these fights are 'renunciation of rights' and 'counter-spirit.' If you renounce your rights, you gain a stronger spiritual position and take what the Lord wants. When stinginess, hate, and anger attack, if you move forward with the counter-spirit of giving, love, and gentleness, the Kingdom of God is released in that darkness. I request that you be a helping hand with the Lord's heart. - Acts 16:10
There is the sound of heavy rain. It is the sound of heavy rain being achieved through our youth.

After a great spiritual victory, a strong spiritual challenge and stagnation come. Peter chose the path of returning to being a fisherman in that stagnation, but gained nothing. Testing is overcome with endurance. (James 1:3) Endurance is thinking of the Lord and waiting, and in that waiting, waiting for oneself is more important than anything else, just as much as waiting for others. Especially, if you can be patient until you can be changed, you will gain life through the pains of childbirth. 'Stand firm, and you will win life.' - Luke 21:19

Saturday Message Summary: James 4:4-10. Do not live oppressed. Many people live losing relationships, peace, and health under oppression. 1. If you become a friend of the world, you become an enemy of God. 2. Submit to the Lord and resist the devil. Then he will flee. 3. If you treat God as honorable, God will also treat you as honorable.

Paul吐露 (expresses) his weakness. It means there is conflict. The fact that there is conflict means two forces are fighting; something I didn't know when in darkness, I came to know once born as a child of light. But it means I have no strength to win this war. So he groans. Then he experiences a turnaround. It is possible because of Jesus. He says that though humans cannot do anything about the body of death, God does it and does it by the Holy Spirit. The Holy Spirit is received through obedience, hearing, and repentance.

The height of the waves or the depth of the test is not what's important, but winning by faith is what matters. The victory that overcomes the world is not success or achievement, but our faith. No one has overcome the world except the one who believes that Jesus is the Christ, and only faith overcomes everything. Only faith.

There is the 'pipe theory.' It is the function of becoming a conduit to deliver important things. However, a Christian must go one step further. Do not stay as a pipe, but become a living branch. If you block bad things and let good things flow, you save everyone and the branch—the self—also lives. The faith of a living branch is not a mechanical religious life. That is why we do not trade with the Lord, but pray.
I hope you walk the road of becoming strong rather than taking the easy road.
I hope you do not become a prisoner and get somewhat comfortable, but instead fight the good fight fiercely. The glory of the saints can never be compared with current suffering.
God's Kingdom policy never responds to Satan's requests under any circumstances. It means there isn't even an inch to give. Instead, it marches forward.
God, if I wait like this... I'm really going to die. God:

A person who does not have regular fellowship with God is potentially someone living as a slave to the flesh. If you receive the gospel as life and fully rely on the power of the blood, it becomes much easier to govern sin.
Submit to God and resist the devil. The secret to victory is taking off your shoes before the Lord. When rights are renounced, the Lord works.

I hope you drive away excessive burdens and stress that do not come from God in the name of Jesus. God is the only one who can make us dwell in peace, and He is the Father who acts and achieves even within that peace.

There are times when God suddenly gives a 'poor heart' (spiritual poverty). At that time, life must be redesigned. You must change to a structure of kneeling and a structure of meeting the Lord intimately. If you miss that moment, you have to wait another 10 years. Iron is changed only when it is struck while heated hot. Please grant me a poor heart.
There are times when God lowers you infinitely to the roots of the mountains under the sea. There, repentance bursts out and the Word begins to be experienced as alive.
You must become a person for whom nothing else but God brings satisfaction. Such a person is someone whom nothing can stop—whether it's a furnace seven times hotter or a den of hungry lions.

God wants to give us unimaginable grace. Because the challenges and tests we must handle are much bigger than our own level. If you fight without receiving grace, you will lose a hundred out of a hundred times, just as a child cannot beat an adult. You cannot even compare in terms of strategy and strength. How would you fight? Grace comes only to the humble.

God is not telling us to fight the enemy in this war, but to fight the idols within us. Instead of being preoccupied with overpowering others, God's intention is for idols to be burned and for broken worship to be restored.
I hope you seek the Lord so that parched places become holy. If you fill your weaknesses and loopholes with Jesus, you become a spiritual powerhouse.

God will grant revival when we become one in the Lord. In human affairs, achieving oneness is truly difficult. The devil never permits it. One certain fact is that I will see the Lord's glory. For the sake of His glory and holiness, God will handle, fix, and raise us up despite our many sins and flaws. So eventually, He will remove the stubborn heart and give the Lord's heart. 'I will give them an undivided heart and put a new spirit in them; I will remove from them their heart of stone and give them a heart of flesh. Then they will follow my decrees and be careful to keep my laws. They will be my people, and I will be their God.' (Ezekiel 11:19-20). Winning is not believing in a feeling, but believing in the Lord's promise. Hang in there.

If you stand in a place where the probability is high instead of standing where you ought to be, you are leaving the place of the Lord's protection. So you come back after being beaten up and depressed. You say you were under spiritual attack. No. You 'ran away from spiritual home' and were punched a few times by the neighborhood punks. Please keep your place.

A person who glorifies God enjoys peace even in the midst of tribulation. A person who succeeds in 'Ministry to God' also succeeds in 'Ministry to People.' Priorities give birth to life. Though that devil opens his mouth and rushes to swallow us, Jesus is our captain, so we will fight to the end and win. It is well with my soul, it is well, it is well with my soul. - Hymn 46
The Son of God came to resist the works of the devil. Let us resist by faith and win.

If you are a person who knows God's character, you will know that God never speaks through condemnation. God does not threaten and He does not coerce. The devil tries to deceive us with a sense of condemnation, but we are under the blood of the Lord.

Dwelling on thoughts God has not given is not only a waste of time, but also inviting seven more evil spirits. That final state becomes worse. Being 'clean' in faith means not only being emptied, but being filled with obedience and relationship with the Lord.
I hope you reject sadness and distress that God has not given. If that's hard, I hope you seek the 'power to ignore.'
God's training ignored leads to growth stopping and eventually returning to where one was.
The brightness of heaven's glory shatters the dark shadows. Darkness has never once overcome the light. Relying on Jesus' merit, we always win.

Jesus brought all the blessings of heaven. When that flesh was broken, the blessings of heaven were poured out. The sign of the Son of God coming through the darkness and destroying the devil's works was the lowering of being wrapped in cloths and lying in a manger. The sign for a Christian to win in the world and disarm the devil is being lowered.

When you feel infinitely small, or when you can only sigh saying 'someone like me' is 'just like that,' you must look at the fact that the glorious Lord and I are in that Lord. If Satan reminds you of your past, I hope you remind Satan of his future.

When returning to Korea, it feels as if my hardened will disappears, and even the air in the atmosphere feels like it's trying to trip me up. Also, it was a downpour of grace here, but you don't understand why it becomes a drizzle there. In Korea, you must pay a price to fill the thirst. In London, there were people paying it instead. You must seek for yourself and prepare for yourself. You realize poignantly that living the faith here was not my strength, but entirely the Lord's grace. However, if you hold onto the Lord with all your heart, you will know He is everywhere. Hold on. And keep going.
If you walk with a policeman stronger than a vicious thug, you are not afraid. Because power has overwhelmed fear. The greatest fear is my self, and the strongest power is Jesus.

You can know a person's faith by seeing what they hold onto in the day of tribulation. Fearing is evidence that there is no life. A person with life is not afraid but is secure. Psalm 27:3: 'Though an army besiege me, my heart will not fear; though war break out against me, even then I will be confident.'

There is no way to live without repentance. There is no presence that comes automatically without the 'labor of seeking and finding' the Lord. Ultimately, it's a battle of 'Do you have Jesus?' vs 'Are you pretending to?' If Jesus is just inside me, the problem ends right there.

It is a night where much repentance happens. Because I am someone entrusted with souls and the power of life is the key. I must be offered more and burned completely. Only the Lord must receive the glory.

In a 'Contract compose template' at the office, there is a 'Party's Bargaining Position.' It's about who is the 'superior' (Gap). In the world, this is very important. Daniel also had a king who held the power of life and death over him, and enemies who systematically sought his life. Even to Daniel, who had been in public service for 75 years across 4 kings and served as prime minister at age 89, they reached the point of racial attacks calling him a 'captive from Judah.' Daniel's way of countering this was 'utterly ignoring' them and serving the Lord as he had always done. I serve only the Lord!

There are people who want to charge their phone once and use it for a week. After 3 or 4 days, they say KakaoTalk and Facebook don't work. It's not that they don't work; they're turned off. Faith is not about 'not working,' but about 'turning off.' So you must always be filled. Only then do you win over sin and win over tests.
If you release a heart for sacrifice, the spirit of selfishness runs away in shame.

The fact that my faith continues to be challenged means I must react with faith. The reason this moment has become a sharp battlefield where neither side can back down is that it is a truly critical moment. I hope you react with faith.
War does not end just because you run away. You must engage in the war and win. Only then will you not lose life, and instead find more life.

When you hate someone to death and lose all faith and grace, and then barely recover after a long time, you realize the cause was not in that person but in yourself. The reason you hated to death was because Satan made you hate to death. Do not be deceived.

Reject Evil Elements and Stay with the Good: After Abraham wins the war, two kings receive him. He accepts the reception of Melchizedek, who serves the same God he serves, but he rejects and refuses the King of Sodom and the culture of Sodom which distorts the truth. Discern so you may accept or refuse.

If you fast, of course you will be hungry and tired. You feel like you're definitely going to die. That's especially true for the first two days or so. The body is sending me a signal to stop. If you ignore (ssang-kka) that message, peace and new strength arrive, and you feel it was somewhat of an exaggeration. If the reaction from darkness is fierce, think of it simply. Victory is near.
Prayer is hard because you do it too little. Prayer becomes sweet when you do it a lot.

There is no saying: 'Blessed are those who moved churches, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven; Blessed are those who persistently clean others' hearts, for they will see God; Blessed are those who passed and were promoted, or those whose value was recognized, for they will inherit the earth.' It seems like you'd be happy if the environment changed and the other person was transformed, but without my poor spirit, my mourning, my peace, and my holiness, it is merely empty.

I feel like my life would be more abundant and efficient if I reduced the time spent praying, praising, and listening to the Word. No. Life does not become abundant; that is why it becomes impoverished.

Apostle Paul, while in prison, had an opportunity to be released, but he changed that opportunity into a chance to stand before the Emperor in Rome and preach the gospel. (Acts 25) On his voyage to Rome, an adverse wind blows. A person living for the gospel faces a powerful wind from hell. But if the wind of the Spirit blows, everything will be devastated. “The winds were against us” (Acts 27:4).

The reason for fasting is not to change the situation. More than anything, it is to change oneself. Fasting is long because one has much to change about themselves. As much as you are changed, the way will also open.
Resist the devil and welcome the Lord. Hope for and think of only the Lord 24 hours a day.

I cannot tell you how much hope the words 'faith wins' give. The problems are very hard and I have no strength, but the message is that faith wins, wins for me, and leads weak me to the place where I win. Relying on it and moving out without fear, 'Faith is the victory that overcomes the world... Faith is the victory, faith is the victory, oh, glorious victory, that overcomes the world' - Hymn 357.

What ignited the 3 and a half years of disciple training, made them remember, returned everything to its place, and made it work was the fullness of the Holy Spirit. For the Word to be the Word and faith to be faith, the Holy Spirit must come.
Soldiers train and obey orders. They don't avoid the fight, run away as refugees, or try to retreat if they end up fighting. A soldier is a being who fights and wins. Be firm.

Ultimately, this world is a battle of governance. The success or failure of my life is also a battle of governance. Governance is over the territory and people where that nation's sovereignty reaches. If God's sovereignty reaches the church but is refused in relationships, change and glory will not manifest. When I transfer my sovereignty to the Lord, true holiness manifests. Acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty.
The last thing Jesus, who truly loved them, said to the disciples was to stay awake and pray so they wouldn't fall into testing, and that He was interceding so their faith wouldn't fail.
I hope you check whether your desires come from the Lord. If your desire is fulfilled, is it really something the devil fears?

Fix Your Eyes: You try to look at the Lord, but there is a desire to glance, just briefly, just for a second, and gaze at things that will rot. The event never ends there. In human history, there has never been a case where being 'off guard' ended with just being off guard. Fixing your eyes is a small price to pay for a few minutes, but if you avoid it, you may not recover for years. I hope you fix your eyes on Jesus, the author and perfecter of our faith.

The Mission of a Minister: To pray so they become people who live for the Lord if they live and die for the Lord if they die, or to intercede so that those who are lukewarm and neither here nor there cannot die comfortably.

Spiritual Child's Play: An airplane is made to fly. But if it loses absolute speed and distance (prayer), it is worse than a bicycle. It's like going grocery shopping at Tesco in an airplane and causing a nuisance with big wings while parking. People say the devil's challenge is so strong they were hit hard and fell into testing and are crashing. How is it a crash when you haven't even flown at the absolute speed of prayer? You're still circling the parking lot and got hit by a stone thrown by the local punks. How is it spiritual warfare when you haven't tried praying for an hour? You've just fallen into spiritual child's play.

The opposite of faith is not doubt. Doubt doesn't do that much harm. The truly dangerous thing is testing God. If you don't have faith, you can just not cross the Red Sea. But trying to cross the Red Sea without faith is testing God. It's not because of a lack of faith, but because of pretending to have faith that one perishes greatly. 'By faith the people passed through the Red Sea as on dry land; but when the Egyptians tried to do so, they were drowned.' - Hebrews 11:29
Wake up. To do that, you must stop rationalizing.
If you pray in the wilderness, change happens. The greatest beneficiary is yourself. Your stubbornness is broken, the Lord is revealed, and you are lowered.

Moses desperately asks for the sins of the people to be atoned for, risking his own soul. But the start of that heart came from God the Father. An intercessor is someone who knows the Father's heart. The answers and frequency of prayer at one's own level do not frighten the devil. When the intercession happens because the Father's heart has been poured in, darkness crumbles and God's will is done on earth as it is in heaven.
Please tell the devil:
If the longing is for the Lord, people and the world cannot govern me.
Confession: Confess like this:
You are a winner.

The Result of Prayer: People pray to change situations they cannot handle. But as you pray more and more, you experience yourself being changed. Ultimately, life is a battle of change, and that change is through Christ who became life and was resurrected.

If you are being checked in some fight, it means you should do it persistently without giving up. What the enemies really wanted to interfere with for Daniel and his friends were worship and prayer, and these came from fearing the Lord. The world likes it when we live as the world does. But when we live by the Cross, it strongly resists. Because revival happens.

Sexual Sin: Sex is God's gift. And a holy gift at that. Because it is holy, it can also become a serious sin and a danger. If sex were not such a beautiful blessing, it wouldn't be dangerous either. Because the Ark of the Covenant in the Most Holy Place was so holy, one was surely destroyed if it was handled incorrectly.

Receiving a title and serving in the church is a noble thing, but God's fundamental purpose is the change of my character—the changing of the fundamental basis of the heart. If that doesn't happen, no matter how much church service or devotion you do, you'll still get 'picked on' (oppressed). The purpose is 'Repent!'

If I haven't become a 'person of the glorious Lord' yet, it's because I haven't clearly faced the 'Lord's glory' or the 'glorious Lord' yet. Even if you mobilize powerful self-control and free will, if you don't face and see the Lord's glory, it eventually ends as a three-day resolution. For a person who has seen the glory, living a life that isn't glorious is actually harder.

1. The most Satan can do is give you a scare. Because he suffered a total defeat at the Cross. 2. He will try to hide this, but it will all be revealed before the truth. Be bold in the Lord. 3. In achieving good, Satan is not a hindrance; instead, Satan's interference sometimes helps by working together for good. 4. I hope you will be warriors of faith who make Satan score an 'own goal' through your gratitude.

1. A Christian might lead a lethargic religious life and not experience answers to prayer. 2. The Bible says the devil submits, but those things seem to rarely happen in reality. 3. Why does the devil not submit? Submission happens on the battlefield (the scene), not at the desk. 4. Since you don't head to the battlefield, there is no submission and power does not manifest. Just as nothing happens if you store weapons in a warehouse and only check them every day... 5. I hope you do what the Lord wants at the place the Lord wants. Then the Lord will surely vouch for His Word through the following signs. Do not become an expert who speaks well, but become warriors who set life on fire. 'Lord, even the demons submit to us in your name.' - Luke 10:17 'Then the disciples went out and preached everywhere, and the Lord worked with them and confirmed his word by the signs that accompanied it.' - Mark 16:20 'So Paul and Barnabas spent considerable time there, speaking boldly for the Lord, who confirmed the message of his grace by enabling them to perform signs and wonders.' - Acts 14:3
What the devil truly wants is for you to focus on the problem instead of the Word.
Do not try to adjust the situation in front of you; you must aim yourself toward the Lord in heaven. Wicked imaginations eat away at life.
I will not stop. I will not give up. I will not look back. Even if I fall, I will rise again. I will never lose and I will win. Because the Lord's glory has been shown.

Everyone is enjoying the party, and I am sitting in the inescapable seat with an awkward smile amidst the loud music. Because of the Father's heart, and with the Father's heart, it hurts so much. Because of the silent cries of the flock He gave me and because of His heart...

Pharaoh oppresses the people by increasing their labor. King Nebuchadnezzar forces a standard that cannot be followed—idolatry—with absolute power. The world tries to bind and drag people with anxiety and fear. So many people live as slaves to injustice and death. However, this does not work on a person who holds onto 'faith hoping for the Lord's help' and the 'renunciation of rights, the Cross, that says even if He does not.' They've misjudged the person. A person standing before the gospel looks the weakest but is stronger than death. (Daniel 3:17) 'If we are thrown into the blazing furnace, the God we serve is able to deliver us from it, and he will deliver us from Your Majesty’s hand.' (Daniel 3:18) 'But even if he does not, we want you to know, Your Majesty, that we will not serve your gods or worship the image of gold you have set up.'

1. It is said that God resists the sin of pride, especially toward a proud person. ('God opposes the proud...' 1 Peter 5:5 [sic - 1 Pet 5:5, 6]) 2. Because this sin was the beginning of the devil. ('I will make myself like the Most High.' Isaiah 14:14) 3. Therefore, a proud person becomes the person most secretly resembling Satan. 4. Pride is Satan. Before humility, all darkness is bound to crumble because of God's great grace. If we are humble, we can make 'Satan' into 'Satang' (candy). Mixed martial arts and boxing athletes learn to take hits first in their initial training. Especially, they learn not to close their eyes when a punch flies in. If you get hit without knowing better, you'll be knocked out. But if you know and take it, it'll be bearable. Various people exist. Understand the other person and take the hit if you must. It goes beyond pain and even starts to feel cute.
Fly high. Wicked thoughts cannot follow to a higher place.

Lacking oil itself is not the problem. The problem is that the gas station is far but the fuel lamp is broken. If it's deficient, you can fill it. It will often be deficient. Never be conceited without the oil of the Holy Spirit. You must fill it when you can.

When you don't pray, you often don't even know how desperately prayer is needed. Once prayer starts, you poignantly realize you cannot live without it. You prayed to get better, and then you realize prayer is needed just to survive.
Throughout our country's 5,000-year history, we lost the nation when we lacked strength. To avoid losing yourself and your friends, there must be revival.
Repentance_and_Restoration: Never, never bury it. When I justify my wounds and greed, the Holy Spirit certainly gives me an uneasy heart. Only a very few do not overlook the conviction of the Holy Spirit and reveal the packaged motives and manipulative depths that try to deceive even myself and God, then repent and turn around. Wounds, greed, and self-defense can all too easily become the names of love, loyalty, and justice. The glory of transformation and revival is uncommon. It requires not the justification of words and deeds, but contrition that reveals hidden fears, anger, and attachments. Walk the narrow path without carelessly burying things. Be thorough about what your motives are. Rather than superficially covering the sky, confess to God who examines the heart. All a man's ways seem right to him, but the Lord weighs the heart. -Proverbs 16:2

Repentance, not correction. When you try to change others, you only leave wounds and lose people. The reason the Lord calls and establishes us is to humble ourselves most and repent. If I do not live according to the Word, no matter how good the message or how fitting the logic, no one will change. Living according to the Word is only possible when you think deeply about and behold Jesus. (Hebrews 12:2,3)

The power to start again. When everything is dark and you do not know what to do, pray for the Lord to become your lamp. Stop self-defense, excuses, and pity, come before the Lord's throne, and pour out your heart, and the Father will heal, restore, and give you the strength to start again. Before Him, there is no word you cannot bring, and no difficult request.

* Weeds and the Cross. To receive grace according to the season, you must come before the Lord's throne at every moment. The enemy certainly sows weeds and would block believers if it could. The most effective weed is 'self-defense,' and the weapon that breaks through this and causes you to approach the King's throne boldly is 'self-denial,' that is, the 'Cross.'

* When annoyed in a movie theater. When I went to watch a movie as a child, the movie never started and only endless advertisements played. Faith is the same—when what we preach we do not practice, and we live poorly with nothing particular to proclaim, we grasp at clouds.

A precious life. How precious is a life lived once? Therefore, our lives should be happy, and to be happy, we must live a meaningful life. We competed fiercely for happiness, but looking back, is life not empty? What is precious and meaningful is not climbing to high places but coming down to low places.

* The power of change. Change begins with the change of people before us. My repentance must come first. 'Change' is not difficult—it is 'impossible.' It is a fruit only the Lord can produce. That is why where there is change and testimony, willing hearts are built. I planted and Apollos watered, but only God gave the growth. So the one who plants and the one who waters are nothing, but only the one who makes things grow is God. The one who plants and the one who waters are one, but each will receive his reward according to his work. We are God's co-workers, and you are God's field and God's building. -1 Corinthians 3:6-9

* The End of 'Abuse of Power.' Haman in Esther was a man who possessed the whole world, but he became enraged because of one Jew. If Haman had not reacted and had let it pass, history would have changed. Therefore, the persistent 'obsession' of those who have more is not authority but 'judgment.' After this, King Ahasuerus promoted Haman son of Hammedatha the Agagite, advancing him and giving him a seat of honor higher than all the other nobles. When Haman saw that Mordecai would not kneel or pay him honor, he was filled with rage. -Esther 3:1, 5

* Drinking living water stops the party. 'Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again,' He said. The water Jesus gives becomes a spring that flows forever. Without an eternal spring, one has no choice but to drink the world's rotten, salty water. Those who drink the world prove they are not drinking Jesus. Stop the pretense of knowing Jesus while being at odds with the world. Be honest: 'I did not have the Lord and that is why.' The Lord will save you.

* Losing greatly for small gain [小貪大失]. A person who once grasped the entire world reacts to the smallest matter and collapses in resentment and anger. Be satisfied with what has been given and give thanks—that would be enough. But pursuing perfection in what has not been given, you gain the small and lose the great. Rather than having more and becoming higher, lowering yourself and giving thanks brings happiness and enables you to make others happy. If you have truly gained what matters most, you must be able to let go of the small. After this, King Ahasuerus promoted Haman son of Hammedatha the Agagite, advancing him and giving him a seat of honor higher than all the other nobles. When Haman saw that Mordecai would not kneel or pay him honor, he was filled with rage. -Esther 3:1, 5 But whatever were gains to me I now consider loss for the sake of Christ. What is more, I consider everything a loss because of the surpassing worth of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord, for whose sake I have lost all things. I consider them garbage, that I may gain Christ and be found in him. -Philippians 3:7-9
* A wrong meeting. When I introduced my best friend to my girlfriend, before I knew it, we were meeting secretly, and it became a 'wrong meeting.' A meeting without God is like that too.

* Appearance for the sake of appearance. Life is far more important than pretext. On a battlefield where bullets rain and shells pour down, it is more important to save and be saved than to look presentable. Hollow splendor and false propriety are not only human desires but constitute sin before the question of life.

* The most fallen nature. Stalin, who purged and killed many people through the secret police, even had his own secret police chiefs killed by their successors. Yet Stalin himself would watch American cowboy movies all night while deciding the endings of all Soviet films himself. The controlling instinct is the oldest and most fallen instinct humans possess—the thought of becoming God. Those who try to control situations, organizations, and people constantly have unmet expectations, so they always fear and rage. In the end, Stalin suffered a stroke, fell into a coma, was betrayed by his aide Berlin, and died suffocated by a pillow. When you abandon the controlling instinct, sin stops. You must entrust only the Lord's sovereignty to rule over you and all things.

* The power of change. There has never been a history of change through fine words. Behind change is someone's shed blood. This generation easily speaks and easily hears through social media. It mistakenly thinks that if one proclaims something, one lives that way, creating an impression. Truly, if you wish to see the glory of transformation, you must willingly pay the price. You must speak of laying down your life. And you must live as you speak. Removing the fake is a great and wondrous work. That is why you first deny yourself.

* Addiction and the Gospel. A person addicted to games cannot quit no matter how hard they try, but if they play a more addictive game, they can quit. Why can't people stop empty addictions? Because they have not found something more fun and interesting. What is not only more powerful than worldly addiction but gives true satisfaction is 'Jesus Christ.' He alone is the only way, truth, and life of this fallen generation. Call upon Jesus. Then you will live. Jesus answered, 'I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.' -John 14:6

* Addiction to power. The reason the rational Pilate became irrational and cruel was to please the Jews he was trying to control. All twisted desires for power seem to make one a ruler for a moment, but ultimately one becomes a slave to one's own greed and, unexpectedly pushed from behind, becomes cruel. North Korea's Kim Jong-un faces a similar situation. So Pilate, wanting to satisfy the crowd, released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus flogged, and handed him over to be crucified. -Mark 15:15

* Thoughts of the flesh without exception will surely kill me. If you live by the flesh, you will surely die, but if you follow the Spirit, you will not gratify the desires of the flesh. Those who are led by the Spirit of God are the children of God. So if you follow your own selfish desires, you will feel empty and ashamed, but if you hold to your calling, glory will come. So I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh. -Galatians 5:16 For those who are led by the Spirit of God are the children of God. -Romans 8:14

* Basic Cross. The reason we cannot remain faithful to the essence alone is because there are so many systems. The late Pastor Ha Yong-jo said, 'To maintain a large organization, you must have systems and structures, and then you inevitably lose the essence.' The purpose and target to maintain from the beginning besides the Cross were wrong.

* Irony. If a bakery has no bread and a restaurant cannot serve food, they will take out loans and advertise, but soon such a business cannot operate normally. If we say people should believe in life yet there is no change, and we say we have overcome death yet we lose to material things, greed, envy, and jealousy, then we are putting on a show, and God will not go along with such a show. Just as a business deepens its collapse when proper accounting and audits are not conducted, faith deepens its collapse when there is only talk and the reality is avoided. The key is to stop the show and run to take refuge in God. That is the essence. The Lord redeems the life of his servants; none of those who take refuge in him will be condemned. -Psalm 34:22

* True faith. I cannot understand the existence of star pastors and influential believers. So everyone, including you and me, strives for success and recognition. Then people's evaluations and gazes inevitably become important, and we must hear that we are doing well. Noah did not build the ark because his shipbuilding skills were superior. Noah built the ark on the mountain by faith, and the Bible says this came only from the fear of God. If one builds a fine ark for oneself based on greed, it is nothing but dung, not faith. True faith comes only when you act in reverence for Him alone. By faith Noah, when warned about things not yet seen, in holy fear built an ark to save his family. By his faith he condemned the world and became heir to the righteousness that is in keeping with faith. -Hebrews 11:7

* True danger. Lack of diligence is not the serious crisis. Not acting in faith is truly dangerous. If you engage in God's work willing to be martyred, staking your life, yet it is for your own greed, then you are committing sin with your life at stake. Please stop worshiping and praying to fulfill your greed. Faith is revering only Him.

* The reason for sin. Sin means missing the mark. There is a problem with the purpose of making God King—with Lordship. So sin always intensifies in excessive ambition for success and desires for self-realization disguised as ministry.

* When there is much determination but no action—Spiritual neutrality. It is because you try to maintain spiritual neutrality. People are inevitably used. Either by the weapons of righteousness or by the hands of darkness. Only when you are rightly used do you escape being used by darkness.

* The power of loss. A gospel that cannot move me is not the gospel. If greed, people, recognition, and relationships move me, then those are my own gospels and what I serve. When you lose them, you see your true nature and understand what your true gospel really was.
* True gospel. Do not try to imitate. Come before the true gospel that can change you.

* Addiction. When you healthily long for the beauty that is your true nature, you escape from wrong and twisted addictions. When the bright moon shines beautifully without shame, you are sufficiently happy without secretly possessing it or being obsessed with it.
* Elite personnel. In God's kingdom, there is no such thing as elite personnel. There are only people who repent.

* Wilderness time. I thought I would be used, but instead driven into the wilderness—this is because I try too hard to teach others. The scope of the wilderness extends to listening quickly and speaking slowly.
* A life without regret. You must stake your life.

* Butterfly effect. Saul falling into the petty jealousy of David was because he did not fully obey in the war against Amalek. Saul's incomplete obedience originated from the foolish act of offering sacrifice without waiting for Samuel. Present choices are closely related to future choices.

* Discernment disorder—December war? 1. When defects appear on a factory line, all other products are thoroughly investigated. 2. No matter what useless words I speak, if what I confidently spoke and implemented is wrong, I must thoroughly examine myself and repent. 3. Error is a tendency, and such tendencies are likely to keep appearing. 4. If there is no repentance regarding heightened sensitivity to emotional voices, being captive to prejudices, selfish thinking and greed, then the same error will repeat fatally in the future. 5. Without reflection as if nothing happened about false eschatology, December war theories, wrong judgments and divisions, exaggerated expressions and self-centered language causing wounds—even after decades, you will not escape your place. 6. The greatness of David lies not in defeating Goliath but in the power to recover.
* What to urge sinners toward. I urge those who repeatedly collapse in empty sin, never satisfying, toward Jesus who is eternal joy. The only higher absolute peace that can break the chains of sin.
* Merry Christmas. The characteristics of distance from Jesus are pride and doubt. The spirit of Christmas is humility and joy. It is because it is the beginning of the gospel.

* Reformation. People say that when Christianity abandons money and becomes honest, recovery happens. We chose these things because we could not be satisfied with the Cross. But if humans cannot be satisfied with the Cross, won't we fill that empty space with more evil and stimulating things? Religious practice without the Cross is merely glorious wickedness.

* The power of the blood. Nothing in this world can wash away sin. Sin will inevitably find us. Only the blood washes away sin. That only the blood can wash it means no matter how terrible the sin, it is washed by the blood.
* Shame. The Korean church receives much criticism, but when I meet precious pastors who are as genuine as jewels, my head bows. With endless shame, I pray to entrust souls to such precious people.
* Nouveau riche. A person who seeks respect before others and excessively scolds subordinates in public places is a pitiful person who must display their power.

* I never told you to do well. Trying to do things perfectly is trying to do them well. God never said to do well. Rather than seeking recognition for work He did not assign and doing it well, He wants you to diligently serve what He has assigned with loyalty. Perfectionism can be a burden to me and a wound to others.

* The greatest discipline. I once saw someone who ministered most excellently being tried in court. Through the mouths and bodies of believers, curses and violence abound, police arrive, but while the victim sheds tears, the perpetrator says they never harmed anyone. The greatest discipline among disciplines is abandonment. How shameful it is, how fearful it is, what the kingdom of God and the Korean church are doing right now—it is truly fearful that greed becomes mistaken for calling.

* The arrogant crown becomes shame. For a person who has not examined himself, a crown becomes shame. Just as ignorance of essence and purpose becomes not only foolish but dangerous, there is no example of gaining glory while ignorant of one's own nature. You manipulate others to go backward and lower them, but ultimately you push your own life backward and lower yourself.

* When greed mixes with gifts, it becomes a curse. There is a chaebol's daughter who never got her hands wet and used such good ability and privilege to back up an airplane. And there are people of faith who, with little strength, faithfully and unknowingly sustain and advance the world.

* Do not judge. Do not judge means do not make judgments without self-reflection or with hypocrisy. (Matthew 7:1-5). A society, community, and individual without self-reflection become terrible. Usually, the power that should be criticized demands silence. Truly, a pure person who needs no criticism is open to any criticism.

* The sickness of perfectionism. It is not that I cannot correct things and therefore cannot return to God. Only the Cross can correct me. When I return to the Father, everything finds its rightful place.
* The way to overcome all sin is prayer. Prayer also hinders sin. Trying to overcome sin without prayer is arrogant thinking. Delaying a choice automatically becomes choosing sin.

* True rest comes from genuine repentance. Even if you change your time, place, and environment for rest, you can never enjoy peaceful rest unless you abandon sin and draw near to God. Thus says the Lord God, the Holy One of Israel: 'In returning and rest you shall be saved; in quietness and in trust shall be your strength.' But you were unwilling. -Isaiah 30:15
* When collapse is severe, do not torment yourself with guilt. Rather, repent that you have not enjoyed the Lord.

* Hospital meal VS. Driver's meal: The difference between the hospital choosing the meal and the driver choosing it creates differences in taste, price, and speed. If I decide, I will fall; if God decides, I will be holy.
* Guidance becomes easier when you abandon greed. It may seem difficult to abandon greed, but you suffer because you do not abandon it. When you abandon greed, guidance becomes far easier.

* Be careful when you have much. When people feel prosperous, they distance themselves from God. Only when we give and give yet feel lack in our hearts do we draw near to Him. Though I call day and night and offer praise, there is only longing in my heart. -Hymn 82

* A good player keeps going. A good player is not one who never gets hit. A good player is one who keeps throwing punches even when hit. Regardless of emotions and circumstances, continue in God's Word, prayer, repentance, and thanksgiving.
* Inducing reactions is evil. Faith is my believing in God and responding, not binding another person. Believing and trusting in God myself—that is faith.
* Inferiority and superiority: Inferiority sees the obedient as arrogant, and arrogance sees the perfect as inferior.
* Luxury Q: God, are the things I desire luxuries? A: Yes, they are. Q: Really? Ah, yes! (tears)
* Change direction: Go in the right direction that God desires, not the good direction you think.
* Class difference: Do not boast that you do not do evil or fallen things. Rather, grieve deeply over what you do not admire and do not long for.
* True discernment: It is not discerning others but examining yourself, humbling yourself, and looking to the Lord.
* God's voice: Humble yourself and repent. Love with your whole life. Be faithful unto death. Then you will have done well.
* The reason for power: Rather than oppressing others with strength, God desires to rule myself with His Word.

* Walk in the light: Living in the light does not mean becoming sterile through goodness. It means coming into the light, confessing sins without hiding, and living transparently. Then He will cleanse us from all unrighteousness. Darkness means living hidden. Repentance is power. If we walk in the light as He is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus His Son cleanses us from all sin. -1 John 1:7

* Do you repent today? Without God in your heart, you will envy, jealousy, hatred, and immorality. You must lay down the tree of knowledge of good and evil, which you hate having God in your heart, and turn to God. That is repentance. And even as they did not like to retain God in their knowledge, God gave them over to a debased mind, to do those things which are not fitting. -Romans 1:28
* Control your desires. No matter how much desire tempts you, confess that only the Lord's will can be accomplished. I belong to the Lord.
* Jesus deficiency: Every problem starts in one place. Especially, the great problem is that where Jesus should be—and should be the source—He is absent.
* Sin and grace: Sin always separates me from God's grace, and God's grace always separates me from sin.
* Evidence of Jesus' absence: When Jesus becomes faint, you will not repent but blame others. Before Jesus, anyone will burst into repentance.

* A broken heart: A broken heart has always existed and will exist in the future. Do not make that broken heart a desire of sin, but offer it as a sacrifice that the Lord desires. The sacrifice the Lord desires is a broken heart.

* Sad masquerade wearing the mask of holiness: The sad masquerade will not end unless you acknowledge that you are existentially a sinner and that plucking a few fruits of sin does not remove the root. This is not a matter of 'willpower' but of 'dying before the gospel.'
* Choose the cross. You must choose to respond with the cross. For both the conception and the completion of salvation are the cross.

* To love more: Those who have been forgiven much love more. Repentance is power. Therefore I say to you, her sins, which are many, are forgiven, for she loved much. But to whom little is forgiven, the same loves little. -Luke 7:47
* God resolves the problem. With complete silence, focus on what you should be grateful for and what you should repent of. Correcting your attitude before God is the true solution.
* Change: Not because of circumstances, but in spite of them... Therefore, all the more, I am sorry and apologetic.
* Prelude to a downpour: What God permits before revival is a poor spirit.

* What everyone needs now: God's peace. For the leader who must make decisions, for those who must obey the Lord's will, for jealousy that must turn away, for viewing people by productivity with vain ambition, for those in faulty systems who blame but need fundamental repentance—when peace comes to all, then the Lord's high purpose becomes visible.
* The one who wins: If you truly want to win, you must forgive.

* Healing from addiction: If you are addicted to fleshly things and destroying your life, this is evidence that you are spiritually stingy, begrudging even an hour of prayer. Could you not watch with Me one hour? -Matthew 26:40
* God's voice: Sometimes God's voice speaks through circumstances. Through this never-satisfying situation and thirst, He reproves you for not praying.

* How to fight sin effectively: 1. Sins are washed by the blood of Jesus. 2. Sinful nature is crucified on the cross. 3. Do not tremble in fear of falling into sin; believe in the Lord who keeps you. 4. Look to the glory incomparable to the pleasure of sin. 5. Always look to the Lord who is with you and will come again. Those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires. -Galatians 5:24

* It's not because you don't know the answer. You don't wander because you don't know the answer. Storm and wilderness always have reason and purpose, and when that purpose is fulfilled, it immediately stops. Wanting resolution while unwilling to change means you have not yet humbled yourself enough. The highest prayer of one who encounters tribulation is 'lower me further.' Life's problems are not because you don't know the answer but because of obedience. Remember that the Lord your God led you all the way in the wilderness these forty years, to humble you and to test you in order to know what was in your heart, whether or not you would keep His commands. He humbled you, causing you to hunger and then feeding you with manna, which neither you nor your ancestors had known, to teach you that man does not live on bread alone but on every word that comes from the mouth of the Lord. -Deuteronomy 8:2-3
* Do not hate yourself; repent. Rather than hating your weakness, you must repent that you have not depended on the Lord above all else.
* Serious disability: In serious illness, there are no warning signs, or despite clearly wandering through death many times, you easily forget and return to old habits.

* Your insufficient state: God does not say, 'Change your behavior and I will accept you.' In the moment you turn around, just as you are, He accepts you. Believers change their lives not to be saved but because they are saved. To the woman caught in adultery, Jesus said, 'I do not condemn you.' And He said, 'Go, and sin no more.' True change is not a few external modifications but a response from a sincere heart to the Lord's love that accepts me as I am. He said, 'I don't condemn you.' 'Go, and sin no more.'

* Do not confirm yourself through ministry. You become a servant of people, not of God. You will frequently experience emptiness and pride. My existence is by God's grace. This means the cross is my identity—the salvation of a sinner like me.
* Move in a straight line. The answer to life is immediate obedience. Lay down rationalization and masks, abandon shortcuts, and run straight to the Lord.

* Be devoted to Word and prayer. Attempting to replace the Holy Spirit, losing the true essence while desperately clinging to what was given as a means—this is the cause of stagnation. Employing another non-essential to solve it becomes the method of producing nominal Christians who have not changed.
* Reject comfort. My worth determines my actions. Refuse and pray.

* Until you let go, He does not give. If He gives before you let go, you experience serious side effects. The means becomes the goal. Then you become a person pursuing desire instead of pursuing God, always riding the rollercoaster of pride and frustration.
* When you meet beautiful people, you discover yourself hating and distancing from sin. The already happy person has nothing more to seek.
* The deep shadow of death: Because you try too hard and want to be praised for doing well. As pride and frustration intersect, you will experience hell. Do not try to do well; love.

* When you abandon pride, you gain self-worth. When you lay down futile and perishable pride and place security in the Lord, you discover that you are truly valuable and loved, and no longer need to cling to or prove your worth in worthless things.

* Handling wounds: Trying to control what you cannot handle creates wounds. Rather than trying to fix others' lives, repent, humble yourself, and seek the Lord's help—that is the first step of recovery.
* Honest evaluation: The Lord performed great deeds not 'because' but 'in spite of.' Therefore, the more grace you receive, the more repentance bursts forth.

* The gospel we proclaimed: The first Word we proclaimed was the cross. The Word we preached again is the cross. The Word we will preach in the future is also the cross. But though we, or an angel from heaven, preach any other gospel to you than what we have preached to you, let him be accursed. -Galatians 1:8

* Definition of heresy: Requiring anything other than the Lord Jesus Christ. But though we, or an angel from heaven, preach any other gospel to you than what we have preached to you, let him be accursed. -Galatians 1:8
* Worshippers: In leading worship, there are two most important things: Yearning and obedience. Yearning is achieved through supplication, and obedience is accomplished through repentance.

* Dangerous Familiarity Familiarity is convenient, but it also becomes dangerous. Incidents often occur where we are most confident. Especially in faith, this happens when we lose our initial passion.
* The Voice of the Lord You will hear it when you abandon lust and selfish desires and seek His kingdom and righteousness.
* Two Foolish Things Being deceived by the same methods the devil used last year—falling for the same trick twice like a fool.

* The reason of moving on When I feel worthless, what I need is to receive the Lord's forgiveness. The fastest way to get rid of "a sense of unworthiness" is to trust in Christ's forgiveness. And He is ready to forgive at all times.

* Why We Must Not Curse We must not curse, but especially when a person of faith curses, while sharing gracious words on social media, they deny it through curses in their actual life, thereby committing the sin of defiling themselves, who are God's temple. Become someone whose inside and outside are the same.

* The Reason for Envious Rage It is because your desires have not been satisfied. That is why people quarrel and even commit murder. You can tell when requests made with lustful intent go unfulfilled by observing the response. The best answer is to seek according to God's will and trust in His methods and timing.

* Anxiety Is Not Because of Mistakes My mistakes or mishearing the Lord's voice do not cause me to lose His guidance or fail. I do not lose the Lord's guidance because of my mistakes or weaknesses, but wrong priorities mislead me. When a wealthy and influential person adopts wrong priorities despite everything appearing perfect, Jesus says, 'What a pity.'
* Repentance I was a far worse son than I knew myself to be. God showed long-suffering love toward the self I did not know. That is why I repent.

* The Wisdom of Untangling a Knotted Thread There are two ways to untangle a knotted thread: cutting it all with scissors, making it unusable, or remarkably finding one strand of the knot and removing it so it becomes whole. Let go of the need to be recognized. The thread of your life will unravel.
* What Is Truly Shameful It is not shameful to be cursed at or criticized. It is shameful to abandon truth in order to be welcomed and recognized.
* The Beginning of Anguish It is difficult because I am trying to become myself by myself. I am what I am by God's grace.
* Warning Signs There is no sudden collapse. It is the knees of prayer that collapsed first.
* The Method of Controlling Sinful Desires It is pouring out your wounded heart to the Lord.
