* If You Are Immature, You Will Become Increasingly Alone If your soul is young even in old age, you can only beg for recognition in various ways, expose yourself, become sensitive to others' reactions, hurt others and pour salt on their wounds while not taking it seriously—all from self-centered thinking. Maturity is seeing both 'I' and 'you' through the Lord. Empathy is a skill.
* Fear In the Garden of Eden, Adam and Eve hide because they are afraid. What they fear most is their naked self being rejected.

* The Person Jesus Disliked Surprisingly, Jesus disliked the Pharisees. What began as a movement of piety to serve the Lord earnestly became boasting about themselves and despising others in arrogance.

* Unavoidable Sin A car collision is not a sin. The real cause is driving a vehicle with faulty brakes in reverse on the highway. The beginning should have been prevented. Both sin and holiness are matters of the first button.

* Running a Good Restaurant A good restaurant is one that offers what can be eaten and must be eaten. However, nowadays many restaurants offer what cannot be eaten and use good interior design, inferior materials, and excessive food additives to increase short-term profits, which is why TV stations go around investigating good restaurants. I think the church can be the same. In terms of supplying, restaurants and churches are the same.
* Song of Victory 2 What I do not offer and hide becomes a test later. Do not offer to the flesh as an instrument of unrighteousness, but offer to righteousness as an instrument of righteousness.
* Song of Victory 1 If you button the first button wrong, it is difficult to fix no matter how well you do later. Sin is decided by the very first action I take. Begin and end everything with prayer.

* Do Not Delay Now Repent and be filled with the Holy Spirit. Cast away all wickedness, call upon the name of Jesus, receive the declaration of being without sin, and like your name in Scripture, you will receive the Holy Spirit as a gift. Do this right now.

* The Power of Simplicity Simplicity is far more difficult than complexity. Clear thinking is not done by just anyone. I urge you to abandon the lie that you are a victim and accept the mission of being a healer. Love is restoration.

* Boasting Is Empty When people boast, they seek to have their existence recognized. But looking back, you realize how empty such boasting is because no one recognizes you through that boasting. Cease from vain boasting and be at peace in humility.
* How to Pass Through the Wilderness Fulfill the purpose of the wilderness. Hear the Word you have not heard before and humble yourself.

* The Sinful Desire to Worry Comfortably Worrying means you are not looking to the Lord. The desire to worry comfortably without looking to the Lord is fallen sinfulness. Rather, when you go all-in with the Lord, you experience peace that the world cannot give.
* Hell in Abundance It is because you cannot give thanks for what is given and try to have what is not given. It is because there is no sense of God's sovereignty that says, 'The Lord has done....'

* The Fundamental Sin It is not fruit theft of eating the fruit of knowledge of good and evil, but 'selfishness'—wanting to be God and refusing to place God in that heart—and 'idolatry' of serving the self. He died for all, so that those who live should no longer live for themselves, but for him who died for them and was raised again. 2 Corinthians 5:15.
* After Ministry, Become Lower in Service and Decay and Die Until You Become Powder.

* Jacob Meets Laban Jacob's relatives all knew how cunning he was, but Jacob himself did not know. Only after meeting his uncle Laban did he realize how painful such behavior was to others. When troubled about relational difficulties, you must see the other person's image within yourself.

* Expanding Perspective Seeing through others' faults is not insight but seeing my own weaknesses in the other person. When vision expands this way, rather than blaming others, you must see the weakness within yourself. An athlete recognizes an athlete.
* A Life Without Regret Pouring out everything with nothing left over.
* The Lord's Sadness He is truly saddened when I grieve over what will decay.
* The Last Worship, The Last Farewell If you think today is the last, you will think more about your own shortcomings than resentments. So even today, let go of your shortcomings and pray.
* It Is Not Unhappiness to Be Ignored You are not unhappy because people ignore you or the world does not give you opportunities. You become unhappy because you turn away from the Lord.
* The Difference Between Saint and Silly A saint applies insight to himself; a fool applies it to others.

* Guard Your Lips If you do not guard your lips, you will labor in vain and end up giving to the dogs. The background of rude language use is self-centered thinking that leaves no room to empathize with others' pain. Ultimately, you will thoroughly reap that fruit and become unwelcome, yet only you remain unaware. Empathy is a skill.
* Cain's Sinful Desire If you hide your wound, your offering will not be accepted. Such sinful desire must be confessed to the Lord and controlled by grace. It is not erased but covered.

* Why Prayer Is Not Answered The first button is wrong, and you pray from a desperate need for something to change, but the prayer is not answered. You have missed one thing. There is an order to that change, and you must change for your environment and relationships to change. Therefore, the first button of answered prayer is praying for the Lord to change you.

* A Ship Without a Captain Does Not Know Where to Go Authority was given to take responsibility, but if you evade responsibility and abandon authority, it returns as great disaster. People die because of people. Beautiful people die because of foolish and ignorant people, greedy and irresponsible people. Even as ministers, we do not understand all of God's will. However, until sin abounds in this world, we who could not protect young souls must strike our chests in sorrow, weep bitterly, and pray as if we ourselves have shed blood.
* What Is the Gospel? The cross testifies to two things: that I am a sinner of the utmost depravity that I cannot wash away, and that I live if I cling to the cross where Jesus shed His blood.
* Without the Gospel, You Suffer Shame You do not suffer shame from not knowing the gospel. You suffer shame because you do not live according to the gospel.
* If You Are Prepared But Still Delay, It Clearly Means You Are Not Prepared Especially your heart itself is not prepared.
* You Can Live By Abandoning the Lord's Will That is called life support. It is a truly regrettable decision. You live like a zombie, and living is not living.

* Taking Delight in the Lord Is the Key to Everything The compulsion to commit sin leaves, and there is the happiness of entering deeply into the presence of the Lord. That means becoming a new being. A thirst that worldly desires and fleshly lust can never satisfy, that only God can fill.

* If You Complain, You Become Paralyzed If you complain about the government, you cannot work hard. If you complain about your teacher, you cannot study. If you complain about your parents, even living feels like revenge. Your life becomes paralyzed when you complain. But if you take responsibility, you become a leader, and if you become a leader, you can raise up life.
* The Environment Is Not Evil It is only that I have not humbled myself, so evil things show a reaction. Humble yourself and pray. Souls will return to the Lord.
* Envy Is Your Standard You mistakenly think that if you acquire certain things, people will envy you. A proper person is not attracted to such things. Only an IQ 10 goldfish responds to earthworms.

* Do Not Just Bail Out Water If a ship is wrecked, you must not just bail out water. First, you must plug the hole. Just bailing out water from an incident caused by lack of anointing is madness. Bailing water, even for a lifetime, does not provide a solution.

* When Emotional Rain Falls, Wait Awhile When emotional rain pours down, vision becomes unclear. If you are not hasty and step back to wait for the rain to weaken, things become clearer, and hidden desires behind hasty judgments come into view. That desire causes you to miss the best while grasping the good, and sometimes it wears a very spiritual appearance.

* If You Cannot Abandon It, Hold On to It When you want to abandon something but cannot, and the knowledge of Jesus becomes elevated, the previously precious thing you could not abandon becomes excrement. Excrement will be discarded even if told not to be.
* It is truth, not a hint. Do not respond to hints; draw your conclusion at the cross. Hints are lies and desires.

* Hidden desires and God's kingdom: Do not be angry that my hidden desires are not fulfilled. Instead, grieve that God's will is not being done on earth. You cannot contain living water in a broken cistern, nor can you make anything other than the Lord into living water.

* Two ways to rely on the blood: There are two ways to rely on the precious blood. If you rely on it after sinning, it becomes the power to make you whiter than snow. If you rely on it before sinning, it becomes victory over temptation. The Lord's blood is true power.

* Being washed by blood is dwelling in light. Even if you fail and fall today, never give up being God's people. It is not those with fewer sins, but those who come with their sins into the light who have fellowship in the light. If He walks in the light, as He is in the light, then we also walk in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus His Son cleanses us from all sin.—1 John 1:7
* Do not be deceived; overcome evil with good. If you repay evil with evil, you fight and perish. Do not be deceived.

* Do not desecrate the temple. If anyone desecrates God's temple, He will destroy that person. Therefore, do not defile yourself, God's temple, through immorality (1 Cor. 3:16), nor despise God's worship (1 Sam. 2:17). If anyone destroys God's temple, God will destroy him; for God's temple is sacred, and you together are that temple.—1 Corinthians 3:17
* Why evil and laziness: Because you are held by an evil and lazy spirit. When you cannot hear God's Word and criticism within you grows fierce, receive the warning seriously and repent.

* The wondrous secret of the blood: The characteristic of those few who speak language well is that they acquire language within context. While Jesus shed the precious blood, I must become like Moses and the Israelites who kept the Passover, sprinkling the blood. I share a secret with you: confessing in detail before the Lord how you feel tempted to certain sins and how you fall is sprinkling the blood. Sprinkle it well. You will be cleansed well.

* God's Lamb comes at the end of Passover as promised, and true Passover is fulfilled. Through that final Passover, redemption is received. Now through Communion, we commemorate and proclaim life until His coming. So the last Passover seat became the first Communion. Now the place of repentance will become the place of victory and life.
* Look much at people and the world. You will discover there is never satisfaction.

* Preparation of the preacher: The most important thing is to love. If you do not love, it is useless, and preaching God's Word is not meant to be useless. If you truly want to love, you must love through prayer and live in obedience to God's Word. The church's crisis is the preacher's crisis. Therefore, we must become poorer and repent further.

* When angry at an oblivious person: You are angrier not at the mistake itself but at the fact that they still do not know it. The problem is that the standard of 'mistake' is too varied, self-centered, and insufficiently shared. Some things are too embarrassing to share. No one can recognize and adjust to this themselves, and the only One who knows is unwilling to correct it. That is why many good people tilt their heads and ask, 'What exactly is the problem?'

* Why you do not speak when disappointed: Most reasons for not explaining when disappointed is that hurting oneself over such a thing seems embarrassingly illogical. Yet anger arises, so you become indignant over other matters. Much of what you feel angry about is not worth anger but rather something you imagined and misunderstood.

* Which is easier?: What we desire—status, honor, health, wealth, and popularity—belongs to what is easy for God. What God desires—that we repent, be contrite, receive forgiveness of sins, and receive grace—is not easy even from God's side.
* Decide the future now: If you desire revival, it means you desire results to change. To change results, the cause must differ, which means transforming your present self.
* The self-deceived: Ultimately, they are those who ask while unwilling to listen. They are unwilling to hear until they get the answer they want.

* Faith sustained by life support: Like barely paying for cheap treatment with only a fifty-fifty chance of survival to save hospital bills, this is not frugality but a great crime. Why do you not cling sufficiently to the cross so that God's powerful presence may level everything?

* The power of the cross: Some say believing in the cross's power is useless and prayer avails nothing. We must ask what and how much they have grasped. The brutal cross solves humanity's greatest enemy—the problem of sin—and as a result, accomplishes salvation. Yet saints crumble over relationships and circumstances. Either they held to something else, or they merely desired to hold; they have not been crucified with Christ.

* Radical gospel: If your body is not free from the world's sins, claiming you can transform the world is nonsense. Surrendering to the flesh's desires and falling to the world's temptations happens because the gospel is diluted. God's Son bore the rugged cross—that is the gospel. Living by the value of that blood price is faith.
* How to restore lost glory: Restore lost prayer.
* Spend time with those whose souls are beautiful. You will lose attachment to sin and desire. How much more if you behold the glorious Lord?
* Be humble: Know that your faith is over-packaged, and tear your heart. When alone, who am I truly?
* Why you cannot admit wrongdoing: Because you are a cowardly chicken without courage.

* When pride is suddenly hurt and you become upset: The real reason you are angry is not that the seemingly unreasonable evaluation of you does not match your self-image, but that it fits perfectly, and that frightens you. Because you cannot affirm your own worth, you react so sensitively to others' evaluations. When God's relationship becomes your standard rather than worldly reputation and success, you become a firm person.

* You must overcome Saul to become David: The real reason you want to intervene, correct, and your insides are turned inside out because you can see someone's intentions so clearly is that you see in that person, like a mirror, the weakness you really want to deny in yourself.

* The leisure of the dead: Those who must not die have no choice but to be defensive. But those already dead are free. In childhood, what is good to oneself is good; in adulthood, what benefits oneself is good; in maturity, what is right is good. If satisfied here, the community is always divided. You must choose what the Lord desires. This is a value that transcends right and wrong.

* Anger: Examining anger closely reveals it is an explosive method of excessively defending oneself against uncontrollable circumstances and one's own helplessness. It is an attachment to appealing one's existence value in unjust treatment and situations where one is not recognized, and an emotional method to eliminate the cause. You can make absurd choices and regret your entire life. Those recognized by God can control emotion and circumstance. Gentleness springs from self-esteem.

* Earthly matters, heavenly matters: For someone to whom this earth's kingdom and its righteousness—heavenly matters—are tedious, yet be promised eternal heaven is absurd. To claim belief without fellowship with Christ is sophistry. Heaven is where only Christ is joy, making it a suitable place for such people. You must repent.
* Do not complain; repent: People who complain are without exception never repentant. Therefore, they perish. Finding the cause in others makes genuine repentance impossible at the root.
* Preparing for healing camp: Lower yourself, repent, and seek the Lord's face. Only then can you live and save all others, and that is the greatest gift.
* What is complex is evil: Paul warns against evil and false teachers who confuse the gospel. Faith is simple. Complexity that prevents clinging to the cross is evil that has lost its essence.
* Repentance springs from honesty about my incompleteness.
* Do not be angry; think: Do not be angry at people; be humble before God who allows this now.
* Amen: Can you become lower and broken so you repent? So I can use you.
* The beauty of turning back: When returning to the root, it is the most human and beautiful thing. Turning back to the Father. It is called repentance.
* The self that blocks me: The one blocking my path is none other than 'me' myself. I, unwilling to pay the price yet full of desires, am a thief and a robber.
* How to return to the Father: Eat the carob pods the pigs eat. Then the desire to return comes. Stop eating the rotten carob and come home running to the Father's house.

* Why you must repent: Because the kingdom of heaven is near. That means settling accounts according to what you have done. Therefore, put off the shameful deeds of darkness and come into the light. The darkness you deal with now will not be mentioned later.
* The greatest work of the Holy Spirit: Making you repent.

* Human joy, God's joy: Seeking human joy in church does not make you God's servant. Seeking God's joy in the world makes you God's servant. What moves me is my master. If what moves you is not the Lord, such an idol must be broken and burned. Until it no longer tempts you...

* Why Sunday worship does not happen: Not because of too much sleep, not because of too much work, not because there is someone you dislike at church. Only because God is not worth two or three hours to you, your relationship has grown distant. God is life.

* True holiness: Great misunderstanding if you think it is praying a bit and reading the Word a bit. True holiness is that what you desire changes. People commonly think that if the method of desire is Christian rather than the object of desire itself, it is holiness.
* If you cannot abandon it, you have no mind to abandon it. If you are not prepared to lose, trials and tribulations will continue. To those ready to lose, the Lord alone will be the reward.
* Have you loved the Lord?: If you love the Lord, you will cease from shameful deeds.

* If you do not change, you will not be changed: Merely nodding in agreement without changing is not faith. Apart from kneeling, calling the Lord's name, repenting, and being filled with the Holy Spirit, the notion of receiving the Lord's guidance and grace while having your heart stolen by the world is not just surprising but shocking.

* Receiving, not rejecting: After the tree of knowledge incident, God drives out Adam and Eve (Gen. 3:24). But looking at verse 4 below, He 'receives' Abel's offering. Between these is the sacrifice of the Lamb.
* If pressed for time: Try fasting. You will discover time is truly long. You are simply wasting time on things that are perishable and useless.
* When grace runs out, you return to what you once enjoyed.
* Life is not romantic: To bloom life from death requires the love, dedication, and sacrifice of one grain dying. If you lack such a heart, it is right to feel moved and sorry at such a sight.

* When Unable to Be Humble: When someone scorns and hates others for not having much or not graduating from a prestigious school, it is because they have no other way to prove their own worth except by condemning and comparing weaknesses. This stems from deep inner inferiority. Humans naturally deny, condemn, and project their own weaknesses when others discover them.

* True Investment: The things worldly people so desperately crave are ultimately not things they can hold onto forever. Yet there exists something that can never be lost. A person who relinquishes fleeting pleasures, self-satisfaction, and material possessions that cannot ultimately be grasped—all for the sake of what can never be lost—is not a foolish person at all.
* If You Cannot Let Go, You Miss What Matters: True faith is when lesser desires are relinquished before the Lord. When you focus simply on the Lord, sin decreases and holy influence grows stronger.

* Solutions Do Not Bring Happiness: If you have solved every challenge with difficulty, it means you have become someone who relies heavily on yourself. Rather than seeking solutions, I urge you to seek the Lord. Through this blocked circumstance, God becomes God, I die, and I live in Jesus.
* Hypocrisy: It appears to boast of the eternal One, but within it embeds and packages one's own corruption.
* Pride: Pride is boasting about what is vain and perishable.

* When You Boast in Vain Things, You Surrender to Emptiness: The irony is that you are afraid of the very things you boast about so much. When you fear something, you serve it. When you boast in vain things, ultimately you cannot bear the disappointment and fear, your heart collapses, and your life crumbles. You must fear only the Lord.

* Seek His Relationship First: The Pharisees excelled in keeping the law but sought to prove themselves first through human relationships, so they were rejected. The tax collectors had many faults, but received mercy through their relationship with the Lord. Happiness and destiny are determined by the priority of relationships. Seek first His kingdom and His righteousness.
* Tyrant and Worshiper: If you pour out your wounded heart to people, you become a tyrant. If you pour it out to God, you become a true worshiper.
* A Life Without Regret: If you are in the Lord, even if you have collapsed somewhat, you must not regret. This is because all things work together for good. (Romans 8:28)

* The Intent of Rebuke: While being cautious about the evil of sin, believe in the power of the Gospel. If this does not happen, you will feel frustrated while repenting. The cross is eternal power for all.
* Repentance: Do not harbor all faults. Confess before the merciful Lord. He will bless you.

* True Salvation Is Needed: Salvation is not when money suddenly comes to you or a worrisome problem is solved. Salvation comes when you receive forgiveness of sins and are forgiven. That is why in collapse, you do not cling to people but come to God.
* Prayer for Revival: Whatever joy of mine hinders God's revival, let it be uprooted.

* What Must Never Be Done Under Any Circumstance: Never make someone suffer unjustly. Whether it is another's arrogance and prejudice or my ignorance and bias, God will surely repay it. David's dreadful sin of adultery was less grave than killing a faithful, righteous man. 'You have struck down Uriah the Hittite with the sword and taken his wife to be your wife, and have killed him with the sword of the Ammonites.' (2 Samuel 12:9)

* What You Must Really Overcome: You must transcend my subtle, rationalized desires. Not because temptation is strong, but because you cannot love Jesus, you do not abandon yourself and are deceived. Yet you cannot deceive the Lord. If you truly love the Lord, you will flee and repent.

* A Person Who Lives Only in Jesus, Not Myself: Life is not without conflict. Conflict is not evil. But it is evil for a servant who should seek God's pleasure to seek people's pleasure. 'Am I now trying to win the approval of human beings, or of God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a servant of Christ.' (Galatians 1:10)
* What Must Be Done When In Pain: You must humble yourself even more. At first you humble yourself as a reaction to suffering, but consequently it becomes humility that gives life.

* A Life Without Regret: When you fulfill His purpose, there is no regret. That purpose is not the realization of your desires or the fulfillment of your wishes. His purpose is to take up the cross and go there. Therefore, those who honor that purpose are fearless and bold, without regret.

* The Subject of Repentance: Let us repent of lacking zeal. Lack of zeal means lack of passion, and lack of passion means not being filled. The greatest sin is not having the Lord and being filled with other things. Therefore, to have zeal and to repent are the same thing. What harms a loving relationship is not shortcoming and mistake, but boredom and indifference. Do not squeeze—overflow. 'Those whom I love I rebuke and discipline. So be earnest and repent.' (Revelation 3:19)

* Repenting Through Pain: Young people's struggles pierce the heart of the minister as pain. Through that pain, I hear what He speaks: 'Be zealous! Repent! Those whom I love I discipline.' (Revelation 3:19)

* The Blood: The only way to overcome sin and temptation is through the power of the Blood. Without the blood, the Holy Spirit cannot come. The daily change that gives me praise is also the power of the blood. Never overlook the basics. Professionals are always faithful to the fundamentals.

* Conflict: The flesh continually demands peace and comfort. But the desire of the Spirit is to make me submit and become holy before the Lord. When these two desires conflict, what you choose determines how you will live. The flesh desired worldly pleasures, but my spirit desired to pray with my face on the cold church floor.

* Foolishness and Wisdom: The foolish blame others, but the wise find the cause in themselves. Just because Saul was evil does not excuse David from his refining. Rather than complaining about others' words and actions, it is wise before the Lord to see your own wrongdoing and repent.

* The Conclusion of Prejudice: A person bound by prejudice against others, no matter how excellent, gifted, or spiritual, is called 'Jonah.' If God prepared 'Jonah' for the repentance of Nineveh where 120,000 children who cannot distinguish left from right live, then for Jonah's stubborn heart to know God's love, He prepared many things: 'the ship bound for Tarshish and its sailors,' 'the great fish,' 'the gourd plant,' 'the hot east wind,' 'the worm,' and more.
* Why Stumbling Is Repeated: Because you cling to a glimmer of hope that you can find happiness in people, and glance at vain things. When your target is clear, the path ahead will be bright.

* Why the Work Begun in the Spirit Cannot Be Completed: Many begin in the Spirit but finish in the flesh. It is because they lack the will to obey unto death. Jesus emptied Himself, took the form of a servant, and obeyed unto death on the cross. If you cannot die, you cannot accomplish.

Do not live a defensive life; live a life that dies on the cross. Saul lived defending himself and destroyed himself, but David lived surrendering himself and became a good shepherd of Israel. When I stop defending myself, the Lord begins to guard me.
I wander. It is not that I do not know the answer. I simply fail to act. You already knew long ago before the Lord how you should live.

There are things that should be abandoned but are not. Pride, covetousness, lust, envy, jealousy, inferiority, anger. These have seeped into the body for a lifetime with subtle charm, parasitize the flesh, and are not abandoned. Yet before the knowledge of Jesus, they all become excrement. Excrement can only be discarded. When you hold only Jesus, faith becomes not 'asceticism' but 'romance.'

* A Sinner Receives Grace: When a sinner believes in Jesus, he is freed from guilt and declared righteous without cost. Yet the sinful nature does not disappear. Therefore, we depend on the blood of the cross and move toward a holy life. When I acknowledge that I am a sinner declared righteous, I realize I am a being in desperate need of compassion and grace. It is not 'improvement' that is needed, but 'boundless grace.'

If You Do Not Let Go, You Suffer: Wanting to have more is greed, and where there is greed, there is distress. True peace comes when you shift focus from pursuing your will to fulfilling the Lord's will.

If You Cannot Let Go of What Should Be Released and Cannot Hold onto What Should Be Held, You Cannot Obey: Letting go of what should be released and holding onto what should be held is fearing the Lord.

When One Sins and Departs from God, Two Phenomena Appear: The joy of salvation disappears and a willing spirit vanishes. (Psalm 51:12) This is because you grieve and quench the Holy Spirit. (Psalm 51:11) Consequently, when holiness is lost, the Lord's presence departs, and no matter how much ministry is done, transformation does not occur. (Psalm 51:13)

The Sinner's Greatest Focus: The sinning person concentrates most on 'themselves'—my problems, my money, my house, my family, my success. When you concentrate like this, problems should be solved, but like tangled thread, they become more knotted. Problems grow bigger the more you look at them. When you behold the Lord in prayer, that huge problem shrinks. The Lord is great. Hold the faith that sees the Lord as great.
* Prayer: Problems flourish because prayer is insufficient. Raise the temperature of prayer to burn and melt the problems. When time is given, stop serving in worthless decay and cry out in prayer.

2 Peter 2:15-22 Thursday Quiet Time: Covetousness Blinds Your Eyes: Verse 15. Balaam son of Beor is the representative of a false teacher. The motive behind his false teaching is unrighteous gain—covetousness. People do what they do not because of conviction but ultimately because of desire and greed. Verse 16. Such people are worse than donkeys. (Four thousand years have passed, and Scripture testifies to their infamy.) Verse 17. The judgment against them is truly terrible. Verse 18. Their doctrine is 1. subtle boasting, and 2. providing sensual bodily satisfaction. Human depravity has a set route after the tree of knowledge. Once a path is made on a mountain, numerous animals and people come and go. It is very difficult to erase a path once made. Verse 19. Carnal pleasure is not freedom but slavery to destruction, slavery to the devil, slavery to sin—doing whatever you are told. The flesh must die and the spirit must live. Verse 20. When you know the truth and fall again into apostasy, it is no joke. Look around at people who betray faith and consistently pursue apostasy with pride. Simply put, when an angel falls, he does not become a semi-angel—he becomes a demon. Verse 21. 'If only I had not known from the beginning' is found not only in love songs. What kind of judgment is it that such words are spoken? A foolish person does not fear. Ignorance is courage. Verse 22. Live so you do not correspond to such a proverb. (Pigs, dogs) The Lord lived a life where God's word and will were fulfilled. Will you follow dogs and pigs? Or will you follow the Word and the Spirit? Decide now. * Covetousness makes people beasts and causes such base decisions (meetings, actions, words, choices). What is truly pitiful is falling into apostasy regarding truth within the church. You work so hard and try so diligently, but if you are deceived by falsehood, it is like cooking rice only to give it to dogs. Paul proclaimed the Gospel with fear and trembling. If boasting, lust, and envy lurk subtly, you are living as an enemy of the cross.

Change is necessary, but criticizing for change has no effect and is truly something you must not do. This is because only specific people can perform brain surgery with a sharp scalpel. Judgment belongs only to God, and if people do it carelessly, they kill life and community. 'I am afraid that when you pull up the weeds, you may uproot the wheat with them. Let both grow together until the harvest.' (Matthew 13:29-30)

Pay Careful Attention When There Is Change: You must discern whether it is change or corruption. When a cynical person is transformed into tearful emotion, that is the work of the Holy Spirit. The opposite is the work of darkness. Regardless of reason, when you move away from God, it borders on corruption.
The Characteristics of a Sick Person: They try hard to prove themselves and always seek to condemn. When infected with the fatal disease of sin, one necessarily becomes self-centered.

The Reason for Going to the Hospital: It is to undergo surgery and remove the root of a serious disease even at great cost. Visiting the hospital shop, having tea with the doctor, and visiting other patients are not fundamental. We must climb onto the operating table, lie down as if dead, and undergo major surgery to live. We must go through the cross. The church must continue the work of being washed in the blood and receiving the Holy Spirit.

The Reason for Going to the Hospital: It is not to do what you want but to endure pain and cure evil disease and save life. The reason for coming to the Lord's community is to be humbled and lowered so that the Lord's life becomes abundant. It is not a place where you hear what you want to hear and see the situation you want to see, but a place where you repent, shed tears, humble yourself, and turn back to the Lord.

* Gifts: If you are ungrateful for the gifts given and treat them carelessly, God will take them away and give them to those who are grateful. The value of a gift is not its face value but the honor of the One who gave it. 'Samuel said to him, "The LORD has torn the kingdom of Israel from you today and has given it to one of your neighbors—to one better than you."' (1 Samuel 15:28)

Departing from the Gospel: It is not just falling into gross depravity and dissolution. If there is no Jesus in your heart and no emotion, then no matter how excellent a religious appearance you maintain, you have already departed. The Lord does not give up on the youth group regarding this fundamental question, and the Lord will use humble youth before this inquiry.

The Most Basic Character of the Gospel: It is grace, not my merit, and transformation. The gospel that cannot transform me is not the gospel. If the Son of God gave His life to save me, such transformation must follow.
The Characteristics of the Gospel: There is joy, only the cross of Jesus Christ is testified, and human merit, righteousness, and pride are fundamentally denied.
Prodigals who have left the Gospel because they hate it need the Gospel again. There is no recovery or salvation apart from Jesus. That is why Christians are persistent—about Jesus.

Change Without Essential Transformation Is Called 'Hypocrisy.' Change the inner person; then without external pressure, you will serve the Lord in a transformed manner. There is genuine gold, but there is also plated gold. Do not be plated faith; be genuine faith.
External Change Without Essential Transformation Is of Two Kinds: One is cosmetic surgery, and the other is hypocrisy. Essential change is only possible through the Gospel of blood. That is grace.

Parents Do Not Teach Their Children Cheating Methods When Teaching How to Get Good Grades: I pray. It is not that I am trying to become an irrefutable spiritual minister. Rather, I am trying not to become an evil minister. Seeking life without prayer is evil.
I look to the Lord because my shortcomings are endless, and I am full of sin and transgressions. He came not to call the righteous, but to call sinners.

Knowledge of the resurrection alone does not transform us; we must experience the risen Lord. When we meet the Lord, open our hearts, and He pours out His Word, our hearts become fervent, and a U-turn in life appears. Transformation comes from faith. For faith is the vision to see greater values and the determination to choose them.
When revival comes, all the thorns of life are pulled out. Revival does not come without prayer, and prayer is what those whose hearts bleed do.

* Both calling and being used require a calling, but not all respond. And even those who respond are not all used. To be used as a weapon of righteousness in God's kingdom, there must be a dedication that breaks self and a change of direction called self-denial. To carry the cross without self-denial...? There is no such falsehood.

When the disobedient Jonah 'happens' to meet a ship going to Tarshish with favorable winds, it is not that God tolerates his stubbornness, but rather a warm-up exercise before encountering the storm and entering the fish's belly.

If you remain unchanged even though uncomfortable, you will stay in the same place. Even if you avoid uncomfortable people you dislike meeting, you will meet another similar person. This is because your spiritual level has become fixed. If you know God and have experienced Him, yet disobey when it matters most, such relational friction will not cease. If there is a gloomy and distressing relationship, at the core of that problem lies my self that has not died. Even a worm wriggles when stepped on. It was not stepped on properly. I cannot kill my ego by myself. The only way is to invite Jesus to the throne of my life. Then Jesus will embrace my afflicted self and go to the cross. There we can feel the Lord's love flowing toward us.
You must be uncomfortable. Very uncomfortable. There must be no peace. Not until you seek Jesus as your center.
Complaining and blaming are strong, but sacrifice is little—this is the image of us today. Ananias and Sapphira were disciplined. It is not about the amount, but the sin of heart and motive.

Unnecessary desires will bring confusion and unnecessary suffering. But great missions will bring obedience and reward. 'For the flesh desires what is contrary to the Spirit, and the Spirit what is contrary to the flesh. They are in conflict with each other, so that you are not to do whatever you want.' - Galatians 5:17
Even if authority is wrongly exercised, to disrespect it or seek revenge means that person has no God in their life. It means being formally obedient only.
Ambition dressed up as vision and calculated kindness... that is Absalom.

When vitamins are deficient, disease develops and death may come. If the Lord becomes my deficiency, far more serious damage and suffering will come. Do not look for solutions in the wrong place; quickly come to your senses and come before the Lord. Otherwise, 'self-pity' will fill the place where the Lord is absent and lead you to resentment and emptiness.
When criticized, acknowledge it. The more your lack and weakness become evident, say that you need the Lord all the more. We are beings who need the Lord's grace every time our heart beats.

How do we discern whether something is criticism or discernment? By cold standards and justification? No. When we criticize, we prove wrongdoing, gain sympathy, and feel pleasure; but when we discern, God's tears form. Criticism is addictive. Pointing out others' flaws does not automatically make me righteous. Rather, I will face merciless judgment.
I came with empty hands, so I will leave with empty hands. I will take only what I loved with tears and labor. To stake your life on something you will not even remember later is foolish.

Satan tries to condemn us through hidden secret sins by attempting to make us believe 'I am worthless.' But those shameful mistakes of mine are actually evidence that I was wounded, angry, and especially lonely. Satan tries to prevent me from coming before the Lord by making me suspicious and hiding alone, but the Lord instead loves me more, forgives me, and has compassion on me. What I truly need and uniquely must have is His love, for He has sufficiently paid the price on the cross.
* The real problem Before God, the problem is not the real problem. The real problem is my attitude toward the problem.
* No need for anger & no right for anger If I am not wrong, I have no need for anger; if I am wrong, I have no right for anger.

When I compare person to person, I become quite a decent person. But before God, no one is free from lust and violence, greed and hatred, jealousy. Standing before Him is faith. That is why repentance breaks out.

If we live choking on people and the world, there is absolutely no peace or satisfaction there. How long will we waver on the scales? How long will we hesitate and struggle? Without knowing Jesus, life becomes eating next to excrement. Even if we live as we want and make effort, we say it doesn't work. Of course it doesn't. Not only will it not work, it will be ruined. What is not yielded to the Lord will be taken away. 'How long will you waver between two opinions? If the Lord is God, follow him; but if Baal is God, follow him.' - 1 Kings 18:21 'Choose for yourselves this day whom you will serve...But as for me and my household, we will serve the Lord.' - Joshua 24:15

People are frustrated. Because they do not speak of the essence. When the gospel of the cross of Christ disappears from saints, they will be very frustrated. If there is one thing the church can be uniquely different from the world about, it is the gospel of the blood of Jesus' cross. Let us repent.

People live bound by cause and effect, by causality. That is the life of general principles. But when truth and the Holy Spirit come, they overturn and break all these relationships, and guided by the Holy Spirit regarding sin, righteousness, and judgment, we fight against darkness and move forward.
People commit sin. They do it to be happy, to have joy. But instead, only deep regret and pain remain. Holiness and purity are power and happiness.
Being misunderstood by people is not very important. If I do not misunderstand until the end, the devil returns empty-handed and God gains one more of His people. Overcome evil with good.
The greatest gift you can give people is forgiveness. Before God opens a new work, He gives the grace of forgiveness.
People may weep and beat their chests, but ultimately they walk in the direction they desire. This is truly sad and heartbreaking.
People are wounded and commit sin. People are especially wounded and commit sin in places without God. Blessed are those who come to the Lord's Holy of Holies and meet the hidden God.

People are deceived because of hidden desires. They deceive others, deceive themselves, and even try to deceive God. When we put forth truth, we see our true form, and we begin to distinguish what to seek and what to lay down.
People become like what they boast of.
Human life is crumpled by self-pursuit. The ironing that can unfold it is 'obedience.'
Whatever a person sows, they reap what they sow. Whether others see it sown or not...the result is the same. When you are alone, who are you?

Humanity fell and became addicted to ruling and possessing what God allowed us—the works of the earth, the works of darkness. What God allows is to temporarily steward and govern, and this is the work of heaven.
Acts without love are hypocrisy. Without love, it is useless and nothing. Even if the world ends, what remains is love, and only that motive will be recognized. Love passionately.

* Jacob's shameless prayer I am a person unworthy of all the grace and love You have shown me, but...deliver me from my brother Esau. - Genesis 32:10,11. 'I work so hard that I am a decent person' and 'though I am false and hypocritical, yet grace is given' have different starting points. The elder brother of the prodigal son falls into 'arrogant delusion' and condemns, while the prodigal becomes a person who receives 'grace beyond measure.' Those forgiven and receiving grace love the Lord more and accept their friend.
When ministry fails, you can see whom you were really pursuing. It becomes clear whom you sought honor, glory, and all authority for.

Ministry is simple; it sums up in one word: 'transformation.' When this 'transformation' happens to newcomers, it becomes 'settlement,' and when it happens to existing believers, it becomes 'revival.' 'Transformation' happens when the church and saints recover 'holiness,' and when 'holiness' increases, 'influence' is born, and ultimately 'transformation' happens. Ultimately, all begins with repentance. Just as the Pyongyang Revival came from the repentance of the prideful missionary Hardy, systematic change is small change, but change in my inner being is a great, earthquake-like change.
* The Lord accomplishes restoration. It is not restored through circumstances and mood changes. Only the Lord restores me. This is because restoration means returning to the Lord.

* Deficiency is because of His loving-kindness. If I lack what I desire, it is because the Lord has not given it, and I should not lament what the Lord has not given. Rather, I should be humble about the fact that I lack the maturity to receive what I desire and my faith is insufficient. The Lord is always good and kind.
In life, there are always things that hurt our hearts. If we leave that hurt as is, it becomes corrupted sin; if we bring it to the Father, it becomes worship that He seeks.
* How grateful am I? How grateful that I can repent? 'But I have prayed for you, Simon, that your faith may not fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.' - Luke 22:32

Life cannot be manipulated. We try to fix some parts and moments to our liking through calculation and pretense, but ultimately, life comes to and returns to the One who created it. With empty hands. I lay down my scheming and receive heavenly wisdom, receive the ability to exercise self-control, labor, and be faithful.
* The way to change the world Do what I must do and entrust what the Lord must do to Him. What I must do is repent and be transformed. Revival begins from my spirit.
When the Lord shines the light of His truth on the dark shadows of life, we ache and try to hide. God is not trying to interfere; He is trying to forgive sins and make us whole.
Samson was physically strong but morally weak. We too have times when we are physically strong but morally weak. That is youth. Strength without self-control invites ruin.

Samson caught three hundred foxes and burned the Philistines' grain, saying, 'This time I will do harm to them without guilt.' When people become angry, they become extremely creative in thinking of ways to take revenge. That is called waste. Be creative in loving.
When you love the other person, you can see your true self. But when you hate, you only see one cruel, wounded person.
Forcing another into your mold and calling it love is violence. When you understand, it makes sense; when you forgive, you can love.
Without understanding the other person, people can become very cruel. Especially to themselves.

Wounds hurt our hearts. A wounded, unhealthy heart produces the fruit of sin. Look back in your memory. When am I weak regarding sin...? Is it not when I am angry, wounded, and lonely, when I am captive to wrong emotions?
When a wounded heart is hidden, it rots and calls forth the spirit of darkness, but when brought to the Lord, it becomes a sacrifice He delights in.

* Those who support slavery - Slavery refers to the barbaric prejudice of determining a person's status based solely on skin color that I prefer or dislike, regardless of the personality and image of God that a person possesses. If I judge souls with prejudice, it is not much different from supporting slavery. Every soul is a life for which Jesus bore the cross.

* The devil's enticement - The devil says there are many problems, so let's take it easy. You've done enough, it's sufficient. He says to stop, pause what you're doing now, tidy up and move on. But Paul preached the Gospel even in prison to the praetorian guard and strengthened the church through letters. Please never stop. For this reason my bonds in Christ have become manifest throughout the whole praetorian guard and to all the rest - Philippians 1:13

* Antitrust Laws - Do not dominate but serve and rule. The motive for monopoly is the selfish pursuit of happiness through domination. It may seem like you'll succeed if you control the market and hegemony, but if anyone—individual, nation, or corporation—reaches a high place and thoughtlessly kicks away the ladder, monopoly and oligopoly ownership does not become happiness but returns as disaster. Ultimately, God did not call us dominators but called us to rule and serve. Those who rule and serve are meek and humble, and achieve their calling rather than ambition and selfishness. I will send foreigners to Babylon to winnow it, and they will empty the land - Jeremiah 51:2. Those who transform the world will be foreigners with different cultures and worldviews.

* The mechanism of forgiveness - Forgiveness comes from memory. It comes from remembering that I was a sinner who had ten thousand talents forgiven. The absence of the joy of salvation, spiritual amnesia, makes people cruel. Should not you also have mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had mercy on you? - Matthew 18:33

* The way to win even when envious - You don't lose because of shame and envy. You lose when you flee. But my righteous one will live by faith; and if he shrinks back, my soul has no pleasure in him - Hebrews 10:38

* God's Crystal Terms and Conditions - Your sin stands between us, interrupting our communion. I can no longer bless you while you persist in it. Trust My Spirit to help you, My child! Please do not reject God's Word. Complete obedience is power. Because the king has rejected the word of the Lord, the Lord has rejected him from being king over Israel - 1 Samuel 15:26

After the Fall through the Tree of Knowledge, fallen humanity is characterized by 'shifting blame' and 'self-centeredness.' That is why even in small matters, we have become beings that pursue victimhood and selfish interests. The cross begins with recognizing one's own sinfulness and repentance, and nailing one's selfish self to the cross.

What appears in those who sinned through the Tree of Knowledge is 'self-seeking.' Because of this, people envy, quarrel, and even murder (James 4:2). Killing the self is not pursuing the Way or gaining enlightenment. If death of the self were possible from humanity's side, Jesus's cross would be meaningless. It is only believing in what God predetermined at the cross. It is not 'doing' but 'taking,' 'enjoying,' 'considering.' Count yourself and believe in the Lord, and it will be so. Look upon, reckon, consider, count. For we know that our old self was crucified with Jesus so that the body of sin might be done away with, that we should no longer be slaves to sin—because anyone who has died has been set free from sin. In the same way, count yourselves dead to sin but alive to God in Christ Jesus - Romans 6:6, 11

When the leading of the Holy Spirit is lost, the characteristic is becoming defensive of oneself. Because you have acted according to your own desires, you have lost freedom. Find out what came before the Holy Spirit—that thing, that event, that person, that desire. Even a driver's license test consistently falls in similar courses.
Live by response, not by achievement. Victory in the world makes people proud, but the response God gives makes us bear the fragrance of the Lord.

There is something impossible in the world that many people continually try to do: serve two masters. There is absolutely no such thing. There may be sincere feelings toward serving two, but there cannot be wholehearted service to two. Ultimately, you end up serving the one who falls.

People think they can open their hearts slightly to the world and close immediately if needed, and they enjoy the thrill. The moment you think such a thought, you become enslaved to the devil. This is the devil's typical method. The Bible, especially regarding the problem of immorality, directly sees it not as a matter of self-control or ethics, but as a problem of evil spirits working behind it. Immorality is the work of the devil. I remember an elderly pastor who had ministered for a long time saying: Once addicted to immorality, it's hard to break free, and when you break free, you need to be prepared to stake your life. I think this way: Even with the resolve to stake your life, it's difficult. It is only possible through 'the law of the Spirit' (Romans 8:1-4). And I want to testify this way: If you fully depend on the Holy Spirit, it is a war you can definitely win (Be courageous). You must remember two things: 1. Abandon vague reasoning that the sinful tendency being committed now will be tolerated. Immorality is a serious crime and you will pay a price beyond imagination. You cannot serve both the Lord and it simultaneously (John 5:30). 2. Guilt begets more sin. Don't trust your own will; believe in 'the law of the Spirit.' 'The blood of the Lord' surely has power. One clear fact is: 'immorality' is harboring fire in your chest that must be abandoned immediately. Though my will and determination cannot do it, you must believe that 'the fire of the Spirit' can give you abundant victory.
The fastest answered prayer in the world... shall we try it? God, deal with me.

The hardest thing in the world is pretending. People who do this well are called comedians. To live as if you are something when your essence is not is tiring and is the life the Lord most dislikes. Beware of practicing your righteousness in front of others to be seen by them - Matthew 6:1

* The difference between angels and devils - When an angel wanted to be worshiped from the position of worshiper, he became the devil. When I avoid the Gospel for the sake of appropriate relationships, it is my nature to be blamed and worshiped being activated, not any higher reason. Open your mouth and just preach!

* Do you think I made a mistake? In the same worship where I receive the same Holy Spirit and praise the One with you as a member, I become irritated and complain. Because there are reasons of my own. At such times, the Lord asks: 'Do you think I made a mistake?' Before you lose something great, hold onto something small. The love the Lord requires is not easy, but it is not impossible either.

He was marginalized, threatened, lost hope in living, experienced frustration, and stumbled through sin, but he was a man who relied on God and was loved by Him, a king after God's heart. David. Rise up like David.

Sometimes you know you're being deceived but would rather be deceived. This is called self-pity. True faith is needed here. You must think about who called you first. 'You must believe that he exists and rewards those who earnestly seek him.'

* Bad choices and good choices - Your happiness or unhappiness is determined by where you invest your life. Don't be swayed by arbitrary feelings and emotions; decide by truth. Then my heart will prevail.
* The greatest miracle is transformation of the heart - There must be a yearning for God's glory. There is no greater glory than a sinner receiving transformation. We see God's works through souls.

A retreat doesn't change me through the power of godliness and provide a transformed environment. There's a world of difference between a place with no temptations and where there's no sin to commit, and the world. To narrow the gap between there and here, internal transformation must take place. Coming to dawn prayer after going to Korea is not because I'm diligent but temporarily because of jet lag. Internal change and being with people of faith become power. Moreover, flee the evil desires of youth, and pursue righteousness, faith, love and peace, along with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart - 2 Timothy 2:22

Drunkenness and debauchery, the world is full of all corruption. God prepares new people for new works. In an age when there was no fear of God, just as Abraham built an altar and called upon 'the Lord' in faith, we too must call upon our Father's holy name in this land of London. The hope of this nation begins with my repentance.

Hidden evil causes much hurt. If poison is dissolved in medicine, it both heals and kills through side effects. I must examine myself and lay bare any poison from the past that is still working, so that I don't obscure the Lord's glory, and intercede.
Envy, jealousy, and pride rob you of joy, block anointing, and prevent growth. Don't respond to people; respond to God. Only God has the authority to exalt and humble. Psalm 75

Reformation of systems and institutions is a fundamental response. But it is not the true cause. Human desire—sin—is the cause. If you don't resolve that fundamental issue, system reform returns to square one. Christians need the discernment to see priority and the patience to push it forward.
You think being tempted means the other person is evil or I'm weak. No. The fundamental cause of temptation is not believing in the Lord's work. Unbelief is the cause.

* Why mental breakdown comes - There are people whose laughter doesn't seem like real laughter but anxiety shows through. Such people will inevitably face mental breakdown soon, lack the ability to explain, so they either go silent or wear a mask and project negative emotions onto others. The further from God, the more one seeks to be vouched for through 'arbitrary satisfaction' and 'relational affirmation,' always anxious. If you truly believe in what Jesus did on the cross, instead of endless mental breakdowns from futile pursuits, you will live in glory with shameless hope. Love never fails. Love never fails - 1 Corinthians 13:8
When we see the stubbornness of believers, what must the church do? Not anger and criticism, but tears. Empathy is power.
Acts of faith can never be boasted about. For example, it's like boasting that you've seen 300 good deeds. If you really want to boast, you should boast that you 'truly loved.'
If you're disappointed, it means you were impatient. Being impatient means you didn't trust the Lord and furthermore sought your own glory. Remember: The Lord will never give His glory to an idol.

The cross takes priority over all emotions and precedes all reactions. The cross is the Lord's sole and eternal conclusion. Those who live by the cross and die by the cross are free from pain and agony and truly happy. The cross is not a joke. It is more comprehensive than everything I thought was everything. Before the cross, all lusts are denied and all heart's requests are removed from priority. The Almighty Holy One refusing to give me up—that makes my heart race and prevents sleep. 'By whatever means, I will save you.' Before that cross, petty pride, hollow human hopes, and rotten affirmations all become excrement. Grasping that low, low, harsh cross makes you higher, so sin's power cannot reach and must retreat.

No matter how thoroughly you sweep with water flowing from garbage, it won't become clean. No matter how much the Lord wants to pour living water, if my vessel remains in the gutter, all filth and poison will flow out through me as well. Leave the place of filth and darkness and come to the place of worship, prayer, and proclamation of the Word.
However excellent a human may be, there is absolutely no possibility. The only alternative is Jesus Christ. Usefulness without Jesus is a prelude to ruin. Romans 3:21

Leaving the Father's bosom to serve idols, you lose your sonship and live a life locked in a pig's sty. People who lose themselves and live locked up boast at best about gathering many carob pods that pigs eat. Don't merely survive; soar like an eagle toward the goal. Don't tear your clothes but tear your hearts, and return to the Lord your God - Joel 2:13. Don't try to appease the Lord through behavioral change; experience the transformation of being—a turning of heart—in returning to the Lord.

When it hurts, you must say it hurts. Even if I was wrong and deserved it, when I cry tears saying it hurts, the Lord binds it up, restores it, and makes it more glorious than before. 'O people, open your hearts at all times...'

You must never be defeated by evil. Even if defeated by evil, the Lord will restore you again, but now you must overflow from the grace of restoration to the grace of breakthrough. Not being defeated by evil means not sinning and instead overwhelming evil with good. Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good - Romans 12:21

Evil must be overcome. But if you overcome evil with evil, that is not victory but defeat. Evil must be overcome with good. The greatest victory is compassion and forgiveness. That is why the cross is the greatest victory. Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good - Romans 12:21
I do not want to become an evil minister. And neither do I hope to be called a decent minister. Apart from the Lord, I become an empty can, nothing.

* The most peaceful prayer - Filling deficiency brings convenience, but other anxieties rush in. The prayer that brings most peace is this: Counting all that was gain to me as loss and as dung... Self-discipline is power.

When the nature of affection changes, it is not living forcibly under the law. Persevering and enjoying are fundamentally different. Giving reluctantly and giving with joy are different. Struggling to forgive and having hatred fundamentally removed are different. Suppressing the urge to sin and coming to hate sin and evil are different. It is not easy to distinguish between those doing things by force and those not. A tip: Those doing by force lack ease and condemn others who don't meet the standard. Jesus never condemned anyone.
* Do you shed tears of mourning? You are truly blessed. Genuine tears are like dynamite that breaks through blocked paths. Blessed are those who mourn - Matthew 5:4

Jacob saw God's way and God's ladder at Bethel when he slept with a stone for a pillow. Without human wisdom, ambition, or attachment, he saw the angels' procession and God's glory. Until we too lay our heads on a stone pillow, we lie and deceive, betray for worldly glory and are betrayed. When tears flow down rough stone pillows, wetting cheeks and falling, that path invisible before, that eternal path, opens.

* Absolute anxiety - For those who distance themselves from God, are not in worship and grace, and have forgotten the merits of Jesus's cross, there is no such thing as peace. If Jesus is not in the heart and the world and people fill it, one must surely be anxious. You must experience panic shaking to the roots. Only when the carob pods are gone does the prodigal seek home. I earnestly pray that such people never stretch out, relax, and sleep peacefully.

* Binary choice 2 - If something must be abandoned, to hold onto the Lord, the things I love and cherish must be abandoned without hesitation. If someone truly loves souls, they will attempt to love with the highest love. That is choosing 'voluntary self-isolation' called solitude to draw living water from a deep well. Being called as a channel of grace means holiness is life. Truth is solitary but grows bolder daily - Chun Chun-seon

* Prayer Vs Sin - Sin hinders prayer. And prayer also hinders sin. The Lord's arm is not too short to save, nor his ear too dull to hear. Rather, your iniquities have separated you from your God; your sins have hidden his face from you, so that he will not hear - Isaiah 59:1-2

I hope we don't pray carelessly and receive guidance carelessly, saying things we shouldn't say causing hurt, or make mistakes through impatience. The greatest mistake is arbitrary interpretation. Comparing and becoming a victim is not the Lord's will. You must look only to the Lord in faith.

Ephesians: Four Things the World Pursues (Eph 2:1,3) Although the world claims to bring happiness, it deceives and seeks to dominate souls through these four things (threats and inducements). 1. The power of sin - You were dead in your trespasses and sins 2. The world's value system - You walked according to the course of this world, following its ways 3. The work of the devil - You followed the prince of the power of the air, the spirit now at work in the sons of disobedience 4. The desires of the flesh - We lived according to the desires of our flesh and indulged in the cravings of our flesh and of our mind - Eph 2:1,3

* The life-giving Spirit From dust, man became a living soul through God's breath; but through sin, he returns to dust again. Yet when he hears God's voice, he is made alive again and becomes a person of the Holy Spirit.
* Ministry is Motivation Ministry is Motivation. If there is no motivation, rather than forcing it upon others, I must bow down and repent.

* Small Obedience, Miraculous Change When the church is not walking according to Scripture, if we pour our passion into criticism, the destruction will accelerate and we cannot escape judgment. When we examine ourselves and live in thorough obedience to the Word, we will experience the miracle of the community coming alive.

If you have worked hard yet feel emptiness, that is not zeal for the Lord. If you had loved the Lord, you should have let go of other expectations long ago. If you sought the joy of people, you are not a servant of Christ and should be ashamed.

In English, the word 'crazy' sometimes refers to persistently pursuing a field with passion - absorbed, so to speak. However, 'insane' feels like constantly going to a false well where you cannot obtain living water. If you are insane about vanity, turn around and be crazy about the true goal. You cannot reach it unless you are crazy.

* Transformation of the Soul If we do not live out the Word, our souls will not change. Living out the Word is not a matter of willpower or compulsion but only through union with Christ. Then it becomes clear where we must begin again. The prodigal returning to be united with his Father—repentance is where it begins.
* Foolish Greed and Wise Hatred The greed of the fool and the hatred of the wise are the same. Both are forced respect and love.

The moment you do not fix your attention on Jesus and His Word, you have already entered into sin, and through pride you cannot perceive truth. Your vision becomes obscured and the brakes malfunction like a broken-down car, resulting in wicked and corrupt thoughts boiling up inside you. 1 Tim 6:4,5 But if you are satisfied with Jesus alone, you will become holy from all of this. 1 Tim 6:6

* The End of Those Who Look to People Trying to satisfy people is truly selfish. Pilate knew Jesus was innocent, but he handed Him over to the cross to satisfy the Jews. Not because he loved the Jews, but only for his own comfort, he tried to please them. Stop worrying about what people think and commit yourself to the Lord.

Believing in Jesus does not immediately make you a saint. That is the cause of despair. When Israel defeats Egypt through ten plagues and crosses the Red Sea, they transition from 'slaves' to 'free people.' However, in their hearts remains the 'mentality of slaves.' The 'old sinful self' still lives and writhes within them. That is why the wilderness journey begins. The Lord calls this (wilderness) 'church life.' - Acts 7:38

Jesus' death followed a public and continuous method of execution. Because the death of God's Son is an enormous declaration against darkness and death that cannot be hidden or bound, and it becomes the power to gain freedom from sin.

The greatest problem of the older brother in Jesus' parable of the prodigal son is that he lives in his father's house. He has lost his father but does not realize it. He has lost his father and lost his father's heart while living in his father's house and doing his father's work, yet he does not know it. He compared and became angry, judged rashly, and then claimed his rights. If you plant beans in spring, you cannot harvest red beans in autumn. If you start from a fundamentally wrong place, no matter how similar it appears, the fruit you bear will be different, and you will not find the Father.

* Why Grace and Mercy Are Needed Although we do our best, it is difficult to deny that through great and small matters, our lack of thoughtfulness and insufficiency can wound and hurt those close to us. Yet I keenly feel the grace that my evaluation of myself is always more generous than the actual me. Even today, I have no choice but to be grateful that I grow up in God's boundless grace and in the compassion of those I love.

* Why We Cannot Change Even When We Agree Although we ponder with concern and listen gladly, when we look at what we ultimately choose, we do not give up ourselves. This is because we have no intention from the start of giving up the pleasures we enjoy. If we do not obey, we will not hear the voice.

* The Purpose of Godliness In the 1990s at Homyeong Mountain Prayer Center in Daegu, the Lord rebuked us severely. He said not to be vain before people with the Lord's blessing. There will be many who view piety as material for profit. That is not the work of the Lord's servant but the sin of becoming master oneself and using God. Truly, woe will come to those who seek the joy of people. No matter how pious they appear, they will be infinitely ashamed before the Lord's judgment seat. Seek the Lord's joy.

* Faith Like Herod Herod was troubled by and yet listened to John the Baptist's sharp sermons. However, there was no change in his life. Because he did not want to lose his pleasures. If you do not lose, you will not change. You will surely be destroyed. That is merely religious satisfaction. It is the same as reading prayer books and prayer books as substitutes for prayer, or receiving evangelism training and merely satisfying oneself. When Herod knew John to be a righteous and holy man and feared him and protected him, and heard his words, he was troubled but listened gladly. - Mark 6:20

* Good Loss There are those who do what they can, yet their faith regresses. They feel the lack, know they must change to survive, but looking back, they are always retreating. The reason is they have not prepared to lose. Better value can only be gained after losing lesser value. You cannot have both revival and the world. Determine your purpose and abandon what must be abandoned decisively. Only then will you receive the blessing of all these things being added to you.

Joseph rejects sexual temptation and willingly goes to prison. He then forgives all the wicked brothers who sought to kill him and squandered most of his teens and twenties as a slave and prisoner. If he had lived a life of retaliation, he would have made himself and others miserable. After being released from prison at age thirty, he lives another eighty-one years fulfilling God's amazing plan. That is the ability to interpret, the ability to discern the Lord's will, the ability to use time wisely, the ability to live the end wisely, the ability to seize opportunities, and the ability to walk with the Lord. (Eph 5:15-17)

I share something curious from the Deuteronomy quiet time content I am studying recently. The Law is not made of one thing. The Law consists of: first, ethical law—law in which God's character and intention are directly manifested (e.g., the Ten Commandments); second, civil law; third, ceremonial law; and fourth, health law. Among these, the laws abolished by the cross are the civil laws of the Israelite nation and the ceremonial laws that foreshadowed Jesus' sacrifice. Civil law is the spatial and temporal application of the Ten Commandments, so the external form specifically explained to Israel no longer needs to be kept by us; however, the principles have not been abolished. Also, the laws that cannot be abolished are the health laws, which are unchanging as long as human cells do not change, and the ethical and moral laws of the Ten Commandments, which are unchanging as long as human nature does not change.

* Limited Opportunities Opportunities are not always available. I have the opportunity to sow love and gain life only while I have life. Believers face judgment for reward; non-believers face judgment for sin. Death comes once to all people, and after that judgment. - Heb 9:27 They were given white robes and told to wait a little longer until the full number of their fellow servants, their brothers and sisters, were killed just as they had been. - Rev 6:11

* Bad Habits We do not say, 'I was so busy with work and exams were right around the corner that my mind was so preoccupied that I forgot to breathe for the last few days! I need to breathe quickly.' We do not even forget to eat, let alone forget to breathe. If we miss the place of grace because of work, that becomes a habit. We are certainly developing a terrible habit.
Repent of greed and lust and become a strong person. Become the strongest person looking only to Jesus.

We mistakenly think that happiness is effectively fulfilling many desires. That is not happiness but sin and death. True happiness is how much we are free from sin, and that is the same as how successfully we have emptied ourselves of greed. When the Holy Spirit came, in Acts there were no longer those who called anything their own. Apart from the power of the Holy Spirit, there is not even a one percent possibility of solving the problems we face.
The One working among us is God, so I too must walk according to the Spirit. That is self-control and concentration. Then the desires of the flesh are rejected and the will of God is executed.
None of us plan to fail. Failure comes gradually and naturally. If we refuse to learn from mistakes, the problems gradually grow larger.

We dislike hearing people blame us. Yet surprisingly we see and hear it. Why? Because in another part of our heart we desire it. Such contradictory emotions come from wounds and rejection. When negative emotions creep up, knowing Jesus turns them all into excrement. Knowing and experiencing Jesus makes the evaluations of others fade away, and we see ourselves, our relationships, and the Lord more CLEARLY. We approach the true reality.

Do you know why we fight with those we love? Because of selfish greed. When seeking to satisfy our ego, war is endless in family, friendships, and relationships. To end this, we must die; and dying means accepting that we have already died. In fact, we have already died with the Lord.

We cannot drive out darkness. Greed and sin are not things we abandon through our effort and determination but are abandoned when we draw near to Jesus. When we seek the Lord who is light, darkness is driven away faster than the speed of light. That will become the strength to rise again from our own failures.
That we have fallen in sin is proven by one certain fact: I have lost sight of the Lord. As long as I keep my eyes on the Lord, even if the enemy comes against me, I will not fall.

There is nothing we can effectively do about the past we have left behind. When we try to do something with the past, it tends to worsen. The believer's only option in dealing with the past is 'forgiveness.'

* Overstepping by Trying to Help God When the situation is frustrating and we try to help God ourselves (?), it is unbelief that severely limits God's sovereignty and work. Abraham helping God results in Ishmael being born through Hagar, a seed of conflict, and Peter drawing a sword in Gethsemane. (The Lord must have been quite flustered in that urgent moment searching for the fallen ear to reattach it. ㅠㅜ)

We are not called to this earth to prove ourselves (our righteousness). We are called only to witness to Him and His cross. The Pharisees tried to prove their righteousness and put their standard in human recognition, but the Lord silently bore the cross and accomplished the Father's will. Do not try to prove and gain recognition, but humble yourselves and testify. In your youth, it will not be easy to resist the temptation to prove yourself.

We are patients with a grave illness. During our great surgery, we can reach near death. It is truly merciless surgery. Through this major operation, we extract the sick self within us. The scalpel and forceps God uses are none other than our authority figures, whose wounds and weaknesses penetrate us so well.

* Recovery vs. Avoidance When facing problems, some people avoid them. The easiest decision is the most evil decision. Rather than leaving your place, make the place where you are full and a place of repentance. The problem is not solved by turning away from it but by facing the Lord.

* The Inerrancy of Scripture vs. My Fallibility There are times when Scripture collides with me. But there is absolutely no possibility that Scripture will change. Since creation, it has never happened, and it will not happen in the future. In the end, what must change is not Scripture but me. God never apologizes or compromises.
Most of our failures or mistakes in life can be forgiven. The most fatal and greatest mistake for humans is giving up; but only if we do not give up.

The land of Canaan with idolatry did not become holy because the land itself was holy. Mount Horeb, which Moses always visited, is not called holy ground because it is a sacred mountain. Though still full of sin and still barren and desolate, it is called holy ground because the Lord's presence is there. When I take off my shoes (abandon my rights) where I stand, God will be with me and rule over that land. In the difficult land where you are now, acknowledge the Lord's lordship. That will be acknowledged through your servantship. The harder it is, the more remove your shoes. And God said, 'Do not come any closer. Take off your sandals, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.' - Ex 3:5
Complaint comes from the victim. But when we pray, complaint can be replaced with forgiveness and blessing. What is desired becomes reality; the victim becomes a beneficiary and further a helper.

Devotion practiced with complaint... exhausts the body, and the Lord may not accept it. Let us do it with joy. Let us live without regret with love and joy as if today is the last day. 2 Cor 9:7 Each of you should give what you have decided in your heart to give, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.

* Why False Prophets Ask for Payment but Not Repentance Fortune tellers and shamans do not ask for repentance. The characteristic of idolatry is 'immediacy'—achieving what one desires in exchange for a set payment; that is, short-term effects and temptations. But one cannot satisfy or fill oneself with that; rather, the end is death. Distinguish between bait and provisions.

Hypocrites mislead people or saints influence people; both exercise powerful influence. But there is no need to become a hypocrite or a saint. Know the answer. Know the truth, and you can only live that way.
Set your minds on things above, not on earthly things. Die to possession and greed; live in prayer and faith.

The less grace there is, the more rules multiply. Among the Pharisees too, there was an abundance of rules about what to do and what not to do. Because they were dry. What comes after dryness is empty formalism. Do you know? Anything done without faith is sin. Even worship and prayer. What matters is not what you do but whether you do it in faith.

The more harmful food is, the sweeter it tastes. Inferiority complex, comparison, pride, rejection are harmful but absolutely unbearable. Yet knowing Christ allows you to let go of these things. Before the Lord's glory and beauty, the value of everything is reset to excrement.

Before a doctor treats a patient, he tells them how serious the condition is. Not because there is no cure but because he wants them to not return to harmful habits and to be managed long-term.
