Before God solves, He allows us to experience rock bottom. Because the goal is not the solution but transformation.
In this world, no one admits they are jealous. They only say they are executing justice. Jealousy is sorrowing at another person's joy. There are two solutions to resolving this....

If I insist on my own desire, only regret will remain; but if I follow the Lord's way, there will be glory. Though greed may yield something, I will ultimately lose everything, and though the Lord's way requires many sacrifices, I will ultimately gain everything.

The Israelites experienced the miracle of the exodus, drank water from the rock, were baptized in the Red Sea, and ate spiritual food—manna—yet they perished. Experience alone is not enough. Do not grumble. Some among them who grumbled were destroyed by the destroyer. Do not grumble as they did. —1 Corinthians 10

No human can ever deceive God. He knows when I sit and when I rise, when I lie down and when I wake. Behind brilliant good deeds, cloaked by twisted self-love and hidden by painful failures, He discerns even the good motives I once had. Ultimately, we all will stand before God.

Popularity does not equate to truth. Action movies are thrilling, but no one watches them again as masterpieces. What endures beyond time is not popularity but life. Life is reborn when blood is shed.

Misunderstandings are inevitable in life. Among them, malicious rumors are hard to bear. Such false rumors disappear within months if I do not rake them up or explain them. Why? Because Jesus did not die on the cross because of such rumors. Jesus' cross was for me, whose wickedness is incomparably greater than any malicious rumor.
If you truly wish to live without regret as you go through life, you must live with love. Without love, you become proud, inflict wounds, and receive wounds. Ultimately, it is a meaningless life.

When passing through life's suffering, pray like this: 'God, please humble me further.' That is the fastest way to pass through the tunnel. Please humble me. God resists the proud and allows no one such a thing. Remember how the LORD your God led you all the way in the wilderness these forty years to humble you and test you, to know what was in your heart, whether or not you would keep His commands. —Deuteronomy 8:2

The reason life's storms intensify is because we do not turn back to God. The storm does not stop until Jonah repents. Even if he decides to be cast into the sea, a fish is prepared in the waters. God never compromises.

Even if you don't want to, if life doesn't go as planned, you look back at yourself. It is God's grace. You must break the cycle of making plans without the Holy Spirit and becoming angry when things don't go your way. Without the Holy Spirit, I am nothing. Let us adventure with the Holy Spirit.

Whether you acknowledge it or not, standing before the Lord means immediately, 'I am a sinner.' Moreover, it does not end there. The Lord calls that extremely wicked sinner a 'fisher of men,' personally leads him, and transforms him.

The stronger a person's self-management, the longer and harder their refining. The 'self-defense' response to protect becomes poison instead. It becomes easy when you acknowledge and entrust yourself before the Holy Spirit.

We live in an age where people condemn far too easily without even understanding. Yet the Lord never entrusted such condemnation and judgment to us. 'I am not in position to say...' Brothers and sisters, do not slander one another. Anyone who speaks against a brother or sister or judges them speaks against the law and judges it. But when you judge the law, you are not keeping it, but sitting in judgment on it. There is only one Lawgiver and Judge, the one who is able to save and destroy. But you—who are you to judge your neighbor? —James 4:10, 11

Among animals and insects that protect themselves, those with camouflage change color with the season and environment to protect themselves. Yet most do not survive even a year and die. However, turtles, cranes, and storks, which keep their own color whether it rains or snows, live long without changing their clothes. If you have faith enough to cast aside protective coloring and reveal yourself before God and your brothers and sisters, problems will begin to melt. Therefore, confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective. —James 5:16

What you boast about will be taken away. Everything you boast about will be taken from you. But if you boast about Jesus, Jesus will be proclaimed. (2 Kings 20:13) When Hezekiah heard the message, he showed the envoys all the treasures in his storehouses—the silver, the gold, the spices, the fine olive oil, his entire armory and everything found among his treasures. There was nothing in his palace or in all his kingdom that Hezekiah did not show them.
If you boast, the Lord will surely visit you again.
You cannot escape the wilderness unless you give up yourself and your desires.

Only those who despair of themselves seek the Holy Spirit. Experiencing the gap between the cross and their own condition, they come before the Lord and cry out. It is Saturday. Let us gather at six o'clock and pray together.

If you have not lived for yourself but sought only the Lord completely, it is fine even if you hear criticism. It is better to be blamed after doing what is right than to live in sin and be blamed. If you have given your full heart to what you should do, there is no regret regardless of the result or response.

Holy tears that break you work to restrain the desires of the flesh, extinguish the fire of discord, cleanse unclean desires, humble proud eyes, refrain from idle words, pursue intimate fellowship, love silence, and bear the fruit of prayer.

A person who looks in a mirror comparing themselves to others becomes either proud or frustrated. But before God's mirror, a person becomes humble. A person with the wisdom not to sin and the wisdom to be humble becomes honored. See Proverbs 1, 2

If you reveal yourself, display yourself, appeal for yourself, and exalt yourself, there is only bitter emptiness. But when you die at the cross, I no longer live but Christ lives in me, when I live only by faith in the Lord, then those who once evaluated me or tried to ignore me will pay attention to the Lord. Because the Lord cannot be hidden. —Galatians 2:20, Psalm 40:3

If you cannot deny yourself, you will deny the Lord. If you hold to the Lord, you can deny and renounce yourself. We cannot serve two masters, nor can we stand with one leg on each side. The gap is absolute and cannot be bridged.

A person who relies on themselves appears confident and sure of themselves. What such people are quick to do is walk the tightrope. Since you are confident, you would have walked the tightrope. But the fall and shame will be great. The claim that you can achieve holiness and satisfy the flesh is a lie. Such things do not exist. Stop walking the tightrope, simply give up, and walk the narrow path. Prodigals who have let go walk the broad path, while disciples walk the narrow path. Half-hearted returning prodigals tend to walk on that fence like a tightrope walker.

You must examine your inner self. There are not many people who are consciously arrogant fools. Pride is strong in the unconscious. Pride is a murderer you don't know. Ambition disguised as calling deceives both you and others, but never deceives the Lord. Let us bow down and melt before the Lord's eyes like flames of fire.

A person who trusts their own mind and stands will stumble with clever tricks. But a person who has honestly confessed their weaknesses receives compassion, and the Word and His presence are granted. Intimacy is power; overcome by intimacy.
Three things reflecting your condition: 1. What do you boast about? 2. What causes you pain? 3. What do you cling to? What answers these questions dominate me and what I serve.

By observing your thoughts and words, you can know where your life is headed. Especially the first thought when you open your eyes in the morning is important. It is your center. A person produces good from the good they have stored up and evil from the evil they have stored up. What is inside you?
The greatest reason you are hurt is not people, their expressions, or words of rejection. The real reason is the selfishness of protecting yourself.

The natural person has much to discard—foolish things, overly sensitive reactions, things that make others suffer because of difference. These must be discarded, but it is not easy. Yet when maturity comes, the deeds of childhood are put aside. Love makes our immature selves complete and gives us the same perspective as the Lord. For now we see only a reflection as in a mirror; then we shall see face to face. Now I know in part; then I shall know fully, even as I am fully known. —1 Corinthians 13:11-12
Be free. The subject in which you failed remains in an endless loop of retakes.

A self-proclaimed God-fearing person—Jonah fumbled about, then after extreme torment, rushed through the three days' journey of Nineveh in one day and muttered the Word. Yet the people repented. Speak the Word in season and out of season. Then the Lord will take care of it.
If you are affected by small things, you are closing your eyes and pretending regarding what truly matters. Don't merely imitate; gain genuine skill. That comes from repentance and repeated presence.

Do not be anxious about wrong outcomes. Rather, the Lord does not condemn but heals the causes behind the wrong outcome—wounds, anger, loneliness, obsession. He came not to call the righteous but sinners. If we were healthy, why would we need Jesus? —Matthew 9:12
When you sin, the whole world is filled with darkness. But when you turn back, the Lord shines again.

Proverbs 6:27 Can a man scoop fire into his lap without his clothes being burned? Forgiveness is the prerequisite of the Lord's love. Prayer that hides sorrow is only frustrating. If you love the Lord, you must forgive and let go so they can walk freely.
'The fool' appears often in Proverbs. It does not refer to someone with low IQ or inflexibility, but to someone so clever that they do not rely on the Lord's Word.

The problem is that Jesus is not in that heart, and more seriously, they mistake what is not there for what is. A person produces good from the good they have stored up and evil from the evil they have stored up. If Jesus is there, suffering and joy will be exchanged, and they will do the works of Abraham. Obedience and faith.

The enemy has never been stronger. Israel has only become weaker. Whether to serve God or serve idols is the watershed between peace and disaster. Whether to worship my emotions, desires, and wounds or serve the only Lord—that is the true war.

Professional traders analyze, calculate, and compare extensively. Why? To avoid financial risk. Because humans are sinners by nature, we instinctively analyze, calculate, and compare to avoid fear, and soon we collapse mentally. Such human defense mechanisms are not easily discarded. They are discarded only when a better value is discovered. But what things were gain to me, these I have counted loss for the sake of Christ. Yet indeed I also count all things loss for the excellence of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord, for whom I have suffered the loss of all things, and count them as rubbish, that I may gain Christ and be found in Him, not having my own righteousness which is from the law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness which is from God by faith. —Philippians 3:7, 8, 9
Even if the first half didn't go as you wished, do not be discouraged. In the second half, be disciplined.
Hopeless situations do not change like weather forecasts with seasons and time. You must change. Repent and be healed. That is the only way.

In my youth, while serving with devotion and faithfulness equal to others, one day the Lord gave me a heart to simply wait for Him. Unexpectedly, the Lord came. Repentance burst forth. Though the Lord's presence withdrew long ago, I am bewildered not knowing why. A shameless life that does not grieve that holiness is not upheld; rationalizing everything will be well through the power of absolute positivity—recovering faith in the cross and holiness is walking together and revival.
If you truly love, you must learn to love and how to love truly. Ignorant love easily becomes rude love. And it always inflicts deep wounds.

What is truly amazing is that when I set aside my pride and acknowledge my dislikes, my shortcomings and failures, the almighty Father's work begins. Under the blood, why would a Christian's failure even become hope? Because the cross is that powerful.
Lust, jealousy, and pride are less sinful than not seeking and calling upon the Lord. If you had sought and called, you would not have borne such fruit of sin. Return to God.

It was not because the disciples' feet were dirty that Jesus washed them. Bathing represents salvation; foot-washing represents repentance for self-committed sin. Those who received salvation need not debate salvation again. It was the Lord's final work. Repentance is important.

The disciples betrayed and abandoned Jesus not because they were 'evil' but because they were 'weak.' Not being filled with the Holy Spirit, leaving the place of prayer and the place of grace was already an extremely wicked sin. After Pentecost, Peter became bold not because circumstances improved but because he was filled.

In Korean churches, what we commonly call success is often expressed in numbers. However, so many souls who gather from other churches do not receive Christ, and even nominal existing members do not thoroughly surrender to God nor devote themselves wholeheartedly. We should mourn such success. And we should be wounded.

Due to impatience and anxiety, I had to grab my brother's heel and deceive my father to seize the blessing. A life bound by such practices held only suffering. True obedience is achieved when you let go of the attempt to make yourself happy, to satisfy and defend yourself. A person obedient to God is the most beautiful person.

Having anxiety means I have my own standards, in other words, my own desires. We must lay down the standard of wanting to be recognized by people and God. When I am surrendered so that God can be revealed through my life rather than accomplishing some purpose, I become known to God. Otherwise, we can do countless things and yet become irrelevant people before the Lord.
Good things are good, but good things that prevent us from approaching the Lord are evil.
Good choices lead to victory in battle. When we choose the cross and crucify the flesh, we win this battle righteously.
The reason people with good beginnings collapse is simple. They become satisfied and compromise. It is like applying the whip to a running horse.

Sin hinders our spiritual growth. If we do not master sin, spiritual growth stops. If we repeatedly receive much grace and then commit sin behind closed doors, it is like pouring water into a broken vessel. Come out and pray. So the Lord can touch you.

It is truly hard to abandon sin. This is because sin has power that exceeds human will. However, when you truly believe in Jesus, committing sin becomes difficult. This is because when you truly love Jesus, what was not broken becomes broken. When you love Jesus, everything else can be discarded like refuse. Yet whatever were gains to me I now consider loss for the sake of Christ. What is more, I consider everything a loss because of the surpassing worth of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord, for whose sake I have lost all things. I consider them garbage, that I may gain Christ and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own that comes from the law, but that which is through faith in Christ—the righteousness that comes from God on the basis of faith. -Philippians 3:7-9
When we commit sin, Satan tells us to disappear. However, the Holy Spirit tells us to repent.

The secret to overcoming sin: 'And the child grew and became strong; he was filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was on him.' -Luke 2:40 The grace of God was upon Jesus! When God's grace is poured upon a person, what happens? The secret is found in Romans 6:14. 'For sin shall no longer be your master, because you are not under the law, but under grace.'

The only way to overcome sin is to surrender myself to the Lord. Then I become the Lord's servant and the Lord becomes my Lord, and I live under grace. If we win the battle over who is master, we have won everything. Do you not know that when you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, you are slaves of the one you obey—whether you are slaves to sin, which leads to death, or to obedience, which leads to righteousness? -Romans 6:16

We resolve many times not to sin, but we collapse repeatedly. When we become angry and complain, we have already become Satan's food and we are defeated in the battle. Refrain from anger and turn from wrath; do not fret—it leads only to evil. -Psalm 37:8

Do not remain lying down after falling into sin, but rise through loving the Lord. That is truly what the Lord wants and what the devil truly hates. Sin cannot be overcome through determination. It is only through the love of Jesus. The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit; a broken and contrite heart you, God, will not despise. -Psalm 51:17

Before lamenting that you have collapsed in sin, you must repent that you did not look fully upon the Lord. Our struggle is not against flesh and blood but against the spiritual forces of evil. Therefore, faith is a battle of perspective.
Being overly faint-hearted before sin and sorrow is excessive and worthless concern. What does that have to do with me? Simply approach the Lord who seeks and calls you.

Even if we gather all sin, sorrow, anger, and hatred and burn them away, we only manage our expressions; the bondage of slavery does not automatically break free. Even if we change environments, due to the 'law of problem preservation,' the conflict in life continues. Become a servant of Jesus, the only truth, then you will truly be free. Who would dare touch a servant who seeks the joy of Jesus? Where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom, and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.

Trying to overcome sin and temptation is futile. We can never win. It is the idea of keeping garbage while spraying perfume. Garbage must be thrown away, and our nature must be crucified on the cross daily.
If we compromise with sin, even after ten years we succumb to it, but if we begin to fight sin, the day will come when we master it.

If sin and repentance repeat, we are always walking in place. If we follow the Spirit, we do not fulfill the desires of the flesh. This is because the Spirit does not permit it and gives us the power to overcome and master. Receive the fullness of the Spirit and the power of self-control.

No one is free from the temptation of sin. That is why we must rely on the blood of the Lord. No sin can be washed away except by the precious blood, and there is no sin that can be washed away except by the blood.
He was a friend of sinners and tax collectors.

If we leave sinners as sinners, none of the faithful people in the Bible would have been saved. When those who stop blaming and take responsibility stand up, we see revival. Wounds call for wounds, and grace produces grace. There is no perpetrator. Everyone is simply in need of forgiveness. In acceptance and forgiveness, there occurs a miraculous transformation of worth beyond worldly measure.

For a sinful person to dwell in God and for that Holy One to dwell in us is impossible. However, the only way is to be nailed to the cross on which He died for me. When we are crucified together with Jesus, an impossible union occurs.

Knowing that we are sinners is the first gate of the way of the cross. But we must not stop there. When we are sinners and sigh, 'What shall we do?', we believe in Jesus and our old self dies on the cross and we are resurrected as a new person. That the nature is not changed by evaluation means becoming a completely new creature. By the grace of the Lord.

Do not be captive to guilt and condemn yourself. If you condemn yourself, you abuse yourself and are cut off from fellowship with the Lord, leading to more serious sins. Do not condemn yourself but repent. We have the potential to sin and we actually do sin. If we sin because we are sinners, it would be despair. However, we are recognized as righteous by the Lord through the blood, yet we sin. Therefore, we rely on the Lord even more and confess. The condemnation of the sinner leads to death, but the repentance of the righteous leads to life.

The statement 'If we are saved once in the Lord, our sins of the past, present, and future are forgiven' does not mean we have obtained a license to sin freely. It means that because I am under grace and not under law, sin can no longer dominate me. -Romans 6:14
Being satisfied with the Lord alone is a confession we pay a great price for. It is never a facade covering my greed.

Lord, I am not afraid to run toward You. Also, I am happy because I will live for You and die for You. Looking fully to the Lord, sin cannot overcome me. It is an unbearable glory to me that I can run for You.

I held fast to a fellow believer who was rapidly drifting from the Lord and preached the gospel. 'You were so moved by God's love, weeping and saying the Lord was good, why have you become like this?' My friend said, 'What has God done for me? He has blocked everything in my life.' From what I could see, what my friend wanted was ambition, desire for relationships that the Lord did not guide, and the success they desired. Poet Song Myung-hee writes a poem one day with the voice of the Lord. How much pain must God's heart have—He gave His only begotten Son, yet humans complain about lacking things. How much pain must the Lord's heart have—He gave Himself, yet people deny and curse Him.
There are aspects of the old self that must be shared only with the Lord and resolved. That sin cannot be overcome. It must die.
Drawing near to the Lord is power. For that, something must be sacrificed. The most critical challenge now is media. Practice self-control. It is a price to pay.
What the Lord wanted from Peter in Galilee was not the full story or persuasive excuses. It was the confession of the hesitant, almost inaudible voice creeping in: 'Lord, you know that I love you.'

When we continue to look only upon the Lord, we realize we do not even have room to criticize others. When we stop blaming and begin with repentance and confession of our own hearts, God's revival comes.
If there is anything we trust in and boast of more than the Lord, it will be taken away. We waste time and must start again from zero point. Only the Lord is true satisfaction.

The Lord never commanded us to read the Bible or pray daily. Of course, having daily devotions is undoubtedly very beneficial. He only commanded us to take up our cross daily. What is the evidence of taking up the cross daily? It is that there is a change of life in those around us. For Paul, what he did daily was the life of the cross, giving himself. -Luke 9:23 Then he said to them all: 'Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross daily and follow me.' -1 Corinthians 15:31 Brothers and sisters, by the boasting about you that I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die every day.

The Lord humbled Himself and was glad, and with that joy He came into the world, became a grain of wheat, died while serving, and left much fruit. The biblical definition of pride is, like Satan did, 'not keeping one's own place.' Unless I deeply root humility and become wholly the Lord's, becoming thoroughly humble, I can never exalt the Lord.

Knowing that we should look upon the Lord but being unable to do so is great pain in our youth. The reason is not complicated. It is because I do not regard what I espouse and cannot be taken away from me as 'refuse.' Unless we regard and hate worldly recognition, human attention, pride in being successful in relationships, and sinful entertaining that momentarily delights us as filthy, we cannot serve both this desire and Christ. ...for whose sake I have lost all things. I consider them garbage, that I may gain Christ and be found in him.... This is the culmination of the faith confession of one who lived humbly with the Lord throughout their life.
When I see the Lord, I kneel and pray and repent even if I am at the company or school. Peter knelt and repented among many colleagues and in his own workplace. Stand before the Lord.
Peace comes when I confirm that nothing can be done as I wish unless it is the Lord's will. Greed must be withdrawn for peace to come.

People who do not see the Lord's love cannot see their own sin either. My sin is not accidental wrongdoing in the past but is inherent in my fundamental attitude of 'self-love.' It is only in the light of that love that I finally come to realize this. So I place my hand over my mouth and sigh, 'Woe to me! I am ruined! I am a man of unclean lips....' This happens when Jesus has brought me to the heart of God. -Isaiah 6:5

Those who understand the Lord's grace, abandon the world, and return to the Lord truly regret one thing. Since they were going to return anyway, they regret returning so late and wasting so much time.

Two ways to receive the Lord's blessing and transformation: It is best to understand through the Word. But do not worry too much if you do not. There is something called the wilderness. If you rot in the wilderness for about ten years, you will understand well. Of course, I recommend the former.

With a hungry belly clenched, I fasted and offered earnest prayers. However, the One who transforms us and meets us is the Lord, and He alone is the Sovereign. Without the Lord's grace, no religious practice is of any use. I once again earnestly pray that the Lord would grant His presence on that day.
Humble yourselves under the mighty hand of God.
When we seek the face of the Lord, God comes with grace to wash us and restore us. Therefore, we must always seek the Lord with our hearts.
True love lays down even pride.

Sunday Message: 1. Do not live as you wish but live a life driven by necessity. There are many pens you want to have, but you only need one when you write. There are many people you want to date, but you can only marry one. Do not live by greed but live being satisfied. 2. God honors those who honor Him and despises those who despise Him. Do not treat God carelessly. Serve with all your heart. Consider who your master is and what is the cause of your actions. 3. There must be lamentation and sorrow. Do not stand by indifferently watching your collapsing life and community. Request God's rule in your workplace, academy, family, and relationships. Those who do not request it have no right to be happy.
Dead tradition is worse than heresy. - John Stott

The way to escape addiction, helplessness, and anger is to become satisfied with the Lord alone. The Lord said to the woman who poured perfume that because her many sins were forgiven, she expressed much love. Kneel humbly and repent so that many sins are forgiven, and you will become one who loves the Lord.
When we intercede, even lightning strikes will cause repentance. That is a miracle.
When the cause of joy changes, it becomes holy. When it becomes holy, the subject of joy changes. Seeking pleasure indiscriminately becomes sin.
If you are now gripped by anguish in your heart, confess like this: I have died. Then you will see what the Lord does.

That which I lament not having now, had God permitted it, would have drawn me closer to sin and farther from God. Rather than lamenting what you lack, give thanks that through this lack you have received the Lord as a gift.

You can do what you are able to do right now. If I die and live through Jesus, you will experience many souls coming to life. The problem is not 'others' but 'me.' If you live the life the Lord desires, you will behold His glory. Do not seek to satisfy your corrupted and ailing self with things that perish, but blessed are those who find satisfaction in the Lord alone, who is the true truth.
All that has sustained me until now is the Lord's great grace. How can I adequately express His boundless love in mere words? As the chief of sinners, I can only bow my head and shed tears.

I will not remember the things of the past, nor will I consider all that was done before. Before the Lord who makes rivers and roads in the desert, I will look only to Him who does new things within me. The Lord will protect me and uphold me. I know this, I believe it, and I praise Him.
True faithfulness means bringing my unfaithfulness honestly before the Lord.
Knowledge puffs up with pride, but love covers a multitude of sins.

Position, titles, and past accomplishments do not define me. Above knowledge of truth and zeal, above much activity, what mattered most to Jesus was 'first love.' My priorities and motives reveal who I am. 'But I have this against you: you have abandoned the love you had at first. Therefore remember from where you have fallen; repent, and do the works you did at first. If not, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place, unless you repent.'—Revelation 2:4-5

Truth stands on truth alone and needs nothing else. Any attempt to employ worldly values and worldly energy alongside it is evil. The Lord has given us sufficient gospel, sufficient Jesus. If we have been zealous for anything other than truth, let us repent and live by Jesus alone as sufficient.

If you truly wish to overcome, know that the world measures victory as the success of the influential and the prospering. But the victory Jesus desires is repentance. The one who repents is the overcomer. Therefore, remember from where you have fallen, repent, and do the works you did at first... To the one who overcomes, I will give the right to eat from the tree of life, which is in the paradise of God.—Revelation 2:5, 7
True rest does not come from comfortable circumstances but from relationship. When sin exists and grace is exhausted, no amount of time and rest will bring peace.
Above all, please forgive this situation. And please lift it up to the Lord. The Lord is almighty. Acknowledge His sovereignty.

True restoration does not begin with organizing your desk or changing your mind. It begins with destroying the idols within me. An idol is whatever you depend on more than God, whatever has greater influence on your decisions. Rather than blaming the nation, the church, or this household, I must first stand right so that all may live. Joshua said to the people, 'Consecrate yourselves, for tomorrow the Lord will perform wonders among you.'—Joshua 3:5

The truly frightening diseases are those with no warning signs. If there is no pain, our members rot away without our knowing. To escape spiritual leprosy, we must experience painfully, brutally piercing suffering in our own flesh. That is a blessing.
When you cling, you cannot discern. True discernment comes only when you have died. Everything done before that point is not discernment but lofty criticism and prejudice.
Do not cling; concentrate. Abandon idols and love the Lord.
Some people cling and pursue their desires; others willingly take up the cross for salvation and life.

The reward of those who gladly accept discipline is the restoration of their relationship with the Lord and the peace that flows from it. No one enjoys the Lord's peace more than those corrected by His discipline.
Talent can destroy oneself. Those who prosper by chance, who succeed without paying the price—these are not to be envied but pitied.

The most foolish man in the world is one who tries to overcome a woman. When nations and peoples perish, hawks raise their vain pride. The Lord's power is not for silencing others but for building them up and yielding to them.

First love, truth ablaze, guards the community; endurance in persecution for Jesus' name; loyalty and faithfulness second to none—yet none of these can substitute for 'love.' What Jesus demands above excellent ministry and faithful devotion is the recovery of 'first love.' This is finding again the most important thing that was lost, and it is recovered only through repentance, not through events or mood changes. Jesus calls this overcoming. 'Yet I have this against you: you have abandoned the love you had at first. Remember therefore from where you have fallen; repent, and do the works you did at first. If not, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place, unless you repent.'—Revelation 2:4-5. 'Let anyone who has ears to hear listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who overcomes, I will give the right to eat from the tree of life that is in the paradise of God.'—Revelation 2:7

What youth must do is pass through fire to be refined and tested. Then we can nail down ambition masquerading as calling and surrender it to the Lord. This is one of the most crucial refinements. What I reflect on most painfully at my age is that I need far more refining and correction than I realized. I plead for the Lord's mercy.

Nearly all problems young people face are solved by fullness. It is not that something is broken; it has simply gone out. If Jesus is not full within me, I am already in serious trial. I will sin, hate, become despondent, and be anxious without cause.
Do not be angry to save face, especially not with family. People matter more than face.

In sixth grade, I lied about my age to attend the youth service. The middle school girls treated me as their peer, and they were so kind... but lacking faith, worship felt like mere ritual, and the fellowship time was more enjoyable. One Sunday, when a friend and I arrived late to service as usual, a senior in his third year of college, about seven years older, grabbed us and rebuked us sternly: 'You come to worship God with such disrespect?' That day, my friend and I mocked him: 'He should just get into a good university instead of retaking; how ridiculous.' That senior must be close to fifty by now. He must have worried greatly then: 'That red-faced kid needs to believe in Jesus and become human...' 'Brother, I am sorry. Though I am still lacking, I now live for the Lord and die for the Lord, yearning earnestly. Let us meet in heaven.'

The savanna lion does not run at just any time. Its burst speed is tremendous, but its massive body cannot sustain a long run like other herbivores. Yet when the chance comes, it gives all it has. And once it catches, it never lets go. Friday evening, revival begins. If you desire to recover your first love and seek a genuine encounter with God, please attend. And please bring other friends too. In the Lord, I love you.

Have you recently heard from someone to repent, recover your first love, and overcome? This is a most clear and irreplaceable message, unable to be substituted by anything else. Moreover, this cannot be compared to mere pleasant words of blessing; it is a matter directly concerning your relationship with Jesus and your life—'where is your attention and affection flowing?'—a matter of life itself. Blessed are those who hear God's voice as they should. Blessed are those who have someone speaking such words. 'Yet I have this against you: you have abandoned the love you had at first. Remember therefore from where you have fallen; repent, and do the works you did at first. If not, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place, unless you repent.'—Revelation 2:4-5

Kathryn Kuhlman, after manifesting power and miracles, would wrestle with tears behind the stage. She fought against pride. Once you become busy and famous, you could become nothing in heaven. Please humble yourself.

Children brag about marbles and Pokémon cards they won. As they grow, they boast of drinking well, mixing drinks skillfully, and trivial drinking knowledge. Strangely, what people boast of reveals their level. Those who boast of drinking compensate for inferiority complexes regarding power and status. 'Woe to those who are mighty at drinking wine and valiant in mixing strong drink!'—Isaiah 5:22. Human nature pursues power. (Der Wille zur Macht)—Nietzsche
Addiction to pleasure buries even your wings.
A Christian is someone who cannot be dealt with by money, honor, greed, or lust. All deals have already been finished by the Lord on the cross. I die with the Lord and live again in Him.

A Christian's struggle is not against flesh and blood, so we do not resolve it by worldly means. The powerful weapons given when standing against all these struggles are 'renouncing rights' and 'countermeasures.' When you renounce your rights, you gain a stronger spiritual position and obtain what the Lord desires. When stinginess and hatred, anger attack, advance with the opposite spirit of generosity, love, and gentleness, and God's kingdom breaks through that darkness. I request that you become a helping hand with the Lord's heart.—Acts 16:10
A Christian's true and complete power is not in pointing out but in bearing the cross right there. God's solution is always people. Revival and repentance begin with me.

The Lord gives us simply two ways for Christians to exercise influence. One is 'holiness' and the other is 'truth.' Even if 'holiness' has crumbled in some area, if you at least face things 'truthfully,' repentance occurs.

The prodigal son could return home only because no one gave him even the carob pods he had been eating. If adequately supplied, he cannot repent until death. Being brought to the corner with not a penny might be a blessing. Have nothing to depend on but the Lord.—Luke 15:11-32

The prodigal becomes entangled with pigs. Yet the fundamental difference between the prodigal and the pigs is that the prodigal has a father to return to, a father to call. A Christian can experience the worst despair. Yet the truth declares, 'There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.'

The prodigal leaves home and father for a better life. Perhaps he rationalized finding a balance between the freedom of transgression and compromised faith. Only in the pigsty does he realize that the compromises made for happiness bring deeper unhappiness—that faith without holiness and salt grows emptier and more distant with every pursuit. The more we rationalize, the farther the way back becomes. The Lord does not compromise.

Paul expressed his weakness—his inner conflict. Conflict means two forces struggling. What was unknown in darkness becomes known upon becoming a child of light. Yet in this war, he has no strength to win. Thus he groans. Then comes the reversal—possible through Jesus. What humans cannot accomplish from their mortal bodies, God accomplishes through the Spirit. The Spirit is received through obedience, listening, and repentance.

Anyone who has sawed wood has sawed wood, but no one has sawed sawdust. Why? Because the dust was made when the wood was cut. Those who grieve or lose courage because of the past are trying to saw sawdust. Do not saw sawdust; saw wood.
Without weeping and change, you are cast out and trampled. Jesus died for my sins, God's love was confirmed at the cross, and the Holy Spirit indwells us.

A victim mentality is delusional. Nothing happens to me without the Lord's permission. The Lord does not miss anything that happens to me. Ultimately, the company's hiring decisions affecting me, government authorities' power, sometimes unjust oppression and discrimination—none can surpass the Lord's omnipotence. Thinking that damage done to me exceeds the Lord's omnipotence and omniscience is delusion.

David, a man after God's heart—God promised to accomplish all His purposes through him. (Acts 13:22) Is this not wonderful? A man of faith after God's heart, accomplishing all His purposes—can I become such a person? Why not me? David was a man of repentance. In Psalm 51, he requested two things in repentance... revealing what a person after God's heart is like: 1. Do not cast me from Your presence or take Your Holy Spirit from me. I do not want my reputation, money, status, or vested interests. I desire only You, Lord. 2. Do not take Your Holy Spirit from me. Your presence is my life. Since the greatest judgment is Your absence, I desperately cling to Your presence. Have you fallen? Become such a person. The cross is the conclusion.
You cannot meet Jesus without standing honest before God as a sinner and a sick person.—Mark 2:17
To truly establish a new relationship with God means establishing a different relationship with sin. How has God taken possession of my heart and worked?
Rather than offering something that looks quite respectable to God, offer yourself. This is what the Lord desires and is the spiritual living sacrifice.
To know what I respond to, dig into the roots of my pain. Upon discovery, I find it shameful to expose. Humans do not become jealous of dogs. The level is incomparable.

Sometimes the Lord suddenly gives a poor spirit. At such times, you must redesign your life. You must change its structure to one of kneeling and intimate encounter with the Lord. If you miss that moment, you must wait another ten years. When iron is hot, it must be struck to be changed. Grant me a poor spirit.

If the Lord were to paint our portrait, He would paint it so beautifully. Not because we lack faults, but because He loves us and covers all else. Let me take down the self-portrait I have painted and hang the portrait the Lord has painted. 'He has not looked on the iniquity of Jacob; He has not seen wickedness in Israel. The Lord his God is with him, and the shout of a king is among them.'—Numbers 23:21. 'Come now, and let us reason together, says the Lord: though your sins are like scarlet, they shall be as white as snow; though they are red like crimson, they shall become like wool.'—Isaiah 1:18
When you concentrate on yourself in prayer and meditation, you discover how much more you need to be refined and corrected than you thought.
God's discipline is not to make us successful. His will is for us to become holy like Him. If we seek success, the tunnel will only grow longer, but if we seek holiness, we will soon see the light.
There are times when God humbles us endlessly, taking us down to the roots of the mountains at the bottom of the sea. There, repentance bursts forth and God's Word begins to be experienced alive.

I must examine myself. If the Lord blessed me immediately, would I be able to see, walk, and give Him glory? The problem is not the Lord's preparation but my preparation. When I am transformed, the answer comes automatically.

Those who rely on something other than God will surely add more suffering to themselves. The Lord is living water and the only solution. Those who pursue possessions, recognition, and bodily satisfaction instead of God will surely add more suffering if they wait a little longer. It is truly foolish. Those who make offerings to other gods will have their suffering increased. I will not drink their blood offerings or call upon their names. Psalm 16:4
God is not deceived by results. Rather, He examines motives. This is why dying leads to living.

God reveals our hidden darkness through pain and suffering. It is difficult for hidden things to be exposed, but it is inevitable when we approach the Lord with holiness and completeness. We become whole to the depth of our pain.

God causes us to abandon what was beneficial to us. Only when even the carob pods are gone do we barely return to our Father. God drives us so harshly not so that we become decent people, but so that we become people of promise. We must leave the nest to enter the land of promise. What is needed when walking between the nest and the promised land is faith.

God comes to us when we hit rock bottom and reminds us of 'my existence.' Then He makes us new. When we know who we are, how we were saved, who paid what price, and what love we received, we gain the strength to rise again.
God does not condemn or accuse us for the faults we confess and lay bare. Rather, He gives us power to break free from them.

God does not immediately demand that we become perfect people. That will take much time. He only desires our honesty. Honesty only requires determination. Even prostitutes and tax collectors can be honest. I am a prostitute. I am a tax collector. But the Pharisees could not do so and were hypocritical. Jesus's evaluation hinged on this.
God awakens our sleeping souls through 'problems.' However, He does not immediately solve those problems. He waits a little longer until we truly desire to change.

God withheld judgment for 120 years to give Noah and the wicked world an opportunity to repent before the waters flooded the earth. And after Noah's family entered the ark, He waits another seven days. Only after those final seven days pass does the rain come.
When we calculate and scheme, God abandons us, but later He surely attends to it. The Lord works with low cost and high efficiency.
If, like Lot's wife, we cannot turn toward the Lord and repeatedly look back, we will harden like a pillar of salt.
A people that does not know gratitude will not perish. They don't even need to strive to perish. This is because they are already perished most greatly.

God sometimes gives us uncomfortable emotions we cannot understand. He is telling us to turn back. Please stop looking at people's faces, stop looking at your circumstances, and look only to the Lord.
God has given us people to love and material to use. However, people often love material and use people.

God is not one who fulfills our desires. Jesus came as a suffering servant, but although Jesus was without sin, He came as a suffering servant in our place to cleanse sin and make us holy, not to be a servant we manipulate as we wish. Rather, we must stand as holy servants following and serving Jesus. Just as the Lord did, we must be transformed into a holy life through anointing and purity.

God is not telling us to fight the enemy in this war, but to fight the idols within us. Rather than striving to suppress others, God wants to burn the idols and restore broken worship. This is the Lord's intention.
God has given us something small but already precious. If we are not satisfied with what is ours, we become depressed or angry. Eventually we fall into sin and face great shame.

Stacking garbage in the living room and swatting flies is not a fundamental solution. Covering with nice blankets or spraying perfume is not a solution either. Garbage must be cleaned away (repentance) and filled with something precious (Jesus). Don't try to get rid of the consequences of sin (insects); just fill yourself with what is good (Jesus).
Those who believe in God's will and forgive receive the power to start again. Not just others but including themselves.
Let the things that break God's heart break my heart.
To hope when there is no hope is faith, but to hope for what we should not hope for is greed.
We hear God's Word. Because we intend to hear and obey it. We do not hear God's Word. It is because in that area we want to cover it up and not obey.

Before God, complaint and greed must cease for truth to be understood. To desire Him Himself more than to gain something. Be still and know that I am God. Psalm 46:10 Keep your heart with all vigilance, for from it flow the springs of life. Proverbs 4:23
There are people who have received God's grace but misuse it in vain. What is the response to abuse? Not judgment and law, but even more overflowing grace.

God's name is not insulted through unbelievers. It is through the indifference and lukewarmness of believers. Do not rage at not being recognized; become like David who burns with righteous indignation seeing God's name not glorified. Glorify the gospel through your life.
When we try to live by faith, we truly experience death. It is not pretending to die but dying completely and being buried. Then we are born anew.

For God to do His work ourselves is pride, and to leave our work to God is licentiousness. Such people have no characteristic of repentance. Without repentance, they cannot meet God. Repentance is laying yourself down before the cross.
If you acknowledge God's sovereignty, you experience the Lord's rule. Then the kingdom of God you go to after death becomes reality before you. Deny yourself and acknowledge Jesus.
If you love the people God sent more than God, He will take them away. Life has priorities. Everything that comes before God will be taken away.
Have the heart God seeks. It is a broken heart. The Lord seeks those who know their spiritual poverty and know how to mourn.
God shows signs and wonders not to fulfill our desires but to make us believe and follow the Lord. Greeks seek wisdom and Jews seek signs, but these are all desires; we hold fast to the cross.

'Pride' is usurping glory as if you did it instead of God, so it is 'impersonation'; 'despair' is denying the living God and reporting as dead without Him living, so it is 'false reporting.' *Violation of laws regarding impersonation, violation of laws regarding false reporting

A rich young man ran away from Jesus. The reason was not because of his shortcomings but rather because of his strengths. 'And when the young man heard this saying, he went away sorrowful: for he had great possessions.' - Matthew 19:22
Each soul we encounter is standing in danger at critical moments. The gospel is needed and prayer is needed. I feel this strongly. And I see recovery happening.
Being called a single grain of wheat does not mean being sent to judge or analyze others. The calling of a grain of wheat is to decay. Decay~

When you feel endlessly lacking, look to the cross. It will give you the strength to start again. You must go down to rise again. You must empty to fly again. When you hit bottom, you can fill up again.
Feeling endlessly lacking is knowing only the Lord can save us and save our members. I hope all will be humbled and lower themselves to reach salvation and forgiveness of sins.

When afraid, we do self-defense, and through competition, avoidance, jealousy, victim mentality, compulsion, anger, and guilt, defense is not achieved but rather deep harm is caused. If we die on the cross with Christ, we no longer need to protect ourselves, and conversely even weakness and death become beneficial. This happens when we always trust and hope in the Lord.

One piece of good news is that the Lord does not condemn us. However, He also does not want us to remain in that sin and spiritual sickness. Don't just watch from across the river; you must be renewed. Jesus said, 'Neither do I condemn you. Go, and sin no more.' - John 8:11

When David sinned, what God sighed about was 'I have already given enough' and 'if you needed it, I would have given more.' Taking what the Lord did not give through human methods is rebellion. Do not rebel but repent and give thanks.
Lacking everything, needing more forgiveness, I am someone who needs Jesus so very much.
If you have done everything and it was of no use, there is still one thing left undone. That is returning to the Lord. So come home running, His arms are open wide.

When a hairstyle changes, you become a completely different person. Of facial features, the eyes take up nine-tenths. However, such artificial changes easily become tiresome. True change is a revaluation of values through the gospel where Jesus becomes precious and other values become garbage.
In modern times, many people compromise. The most serious among them is salvation without repentance. Without holiness, you cannot see the Lord.

Threats and condemnation work because weakness has been grasped. Not because we are weak and grasped by weakness, but because we do not confess and repent. Those forgiven are not only bold but also have peace.
Threats do not change people. Rather, people are changed when forgiven. Neither do I condemn you; go and sin no more.

The dark things that happen in my workplace, home, relationships, and studies are merely signals to return to the essence. It is telling us to step away from peripheral problems and truly stand at the center. Such problems do not occur because of bad 'luck' but because of the absence of 'Jesus.'
Without confirmation, your heart will crumble. Do not be afraid; rather, you must crumble completely into dust. God gives a new heart in that broken place.

There is no way to live without repentance. There is no automatic presence of the Lord without the labor of 'seeking and seeking' Him. Ultimately, it is a battle of whether Jesus exists or merely appears to exist. If Jesus simply dwells within me, the problem ends there.
This is a night of much repentance. Because the one who carries souls is at stake, and the power of life is the key. Everything must be burned up. Only the Lord must receive glory.

Repentance is a change of direction, turning around, and abandoning self-reliance. The essence of repentance is the same, but there is repentance that obtains salvation and repentance that restores the joy of salvation. When I stand in the dark land and repent—not for success and achievement—the Lord receives glory and it becomes holy ground covered by the blood. When you repent, believe in Jesus (be baptized), and receive forgiveness of sins, you receive the Holy Spirit as a gift. - Sunday Leaders Meeting summary

The Lord's first sign that repentance and a change of direction are needed is the disappearance of the joy of worship and a willing spirit. A person who loves the Lord is one who recognizes their condition and feels that the true heavenly joy the Lord gave cannot possibly be exchanged for anything else.

Repent and believe the gospel. - Mark 1:15 The gospel is a promise and covenant, but the prerequisite for believing it is repentance. Here, repentance is not a detailed list of sins or lamentation of circumstances. It is turning around, changing direction, and returning. Above all, it is abandoning 'self-reliance.' You must abandon your trust in yourself to believe the gospel. The prerequisite for the merit of Jesus's blood is nailing down my merit.

If you repent but do not practice restraint, you have not truly repented. A person who repents becomes humble and restrained before the Lord. If you hold a feast while not leaving that evil path, there is no cross you can grasp. The cross is not a handrail you can grab on a bus. It is received as a matter of life.

A repentant heart is not a shameful heart but a good conscience seeking God. Therefore, you call upon the Lord's name, receive baptism, and receive forgiveness of sins. This is a night of deeply feeling oneself to be a sinner.

People commonly like to divide into sides: my side and enemies, good people and troublesome people. But this thinking is wrong from the start. In the Bible, the people Jesus received and befriended had serious character and personality problems, and their occupations were not socially acceptable. Among them, especially 'I' myself am the chief of the most wicked sinners. If I do not appear as a sinner, I have not passed through the cross.
When you release a sacrificial heart, the spirit of selfishness is ashamed and flees.
When times are difficult, pray like this: God, deal with me and humble me further.
Be transformed by the renewal of your mind, so that you may discern what is the good and pleasing and perfect will of God.

Balance means standing with both legs. It refers to legs that rest on both the Lord and the world respectively. The intention is to move to the safer side if necessary. This is truly a wrong thought. How can you imagine standing with both legs when the distance between the Lord and the world is so great? My sincere condolences.

If Noah had mastered alcohol well, if Samson had handled issues with the opposite sex well, history would truly have changed. People are not tested by their strengths. A person who handles their weaknesses well is a true person of character. Clean up the sins you constantly fall into. You will become a Samson without issues with the opposite sex. Faith is surprisingly simple. Simple, intense, flexible.

What do you think would have happened if the rain had not come while David was sinning and he had not seen Bathsheba bathing? He would still sin. Because his state was already a sinful state. Not being filled with the Lord is already a grave sin. Someday the sun will rise, temptations will come, and trials will come. What matters is what fills me. There is no vacuum in the world. Everything becomes filled with something.

When you hate someone to death and lose all faith and grace, and then much later barely recover, you realize the cause was not in that person but in yourself. The reason you hated them to death is because Satan made you hate them to death. Do not be deceived.

I must nail to the cross my questioning of what I have become. The truth and conclusion of this topic is that it all happened by God's grace. Do not bring it up again to argue about it, but let it become a gratitude so profound that even eternal thankfulness would be insufficient. Foolish people forget grace and compare themselves. Do not judge yourself by comparison, but worship with gratitude.

*Don't try to be better rather than to be you, for you are his workmanship. Attempts to appear somewhat acceptable are wasteful. When you think too deeply about what others think of you, life becomes wasted and empty, and secret sins enter. The only word I need to hear is this: 'Good and faithful servant.'

* God never let you down nor you can let Him down. The greatest wound a person experiences is disappointing someone they love. Among all hurts, disappointing God causes such immense pain and anger at oneself. It makes you want to abandon everything and disappear into the ground. But fundamentally, I cannot disappoint God. I think I failed to hold God properly and caused Him disappointment. Rather, God held me with His righteous right hand, and that is why I am here. From the beginning, the Lord knew my weakness, and therefore He bore the cross, and thus He is not disappointed and will never abandon me to failure.

When everything I expect crumbles without fail, there is something He makes me understand. Especially after calculating shrewdly and using shallow tactics, the voice of the Lord that comes is 'You follow me.' Abandon deceit and hold fast to your calling.

For those who find faith too difficult, it is because they are holding onto far too much. Think about it: how difficult it would be to walk a narrow path while carrying and bearing everything. Only after casting off the sin that entangles us and the heavy burden can we run the race of faith.

All animals boast of things at a physical level. But humans, God's greatest masterpiece, should not boast at such a level. I should not boast of my things but boast of Him. The essence of SNS is, in a word, boasting: what you wear, whom you meet, what you eat, what you see. But we boast only in Jesus. That is what makes us different from animals.
My fullness is my mission. That is where it starts. We must be filled with fire that can save dead souls. I am the core problem at the center of all problems.

I am hurt not because that person is vicious but because of my selfishness. Selfishness is self-deception and delusion—satisfying myself with fantasy rather than truth in an attempt to protect myself. When you know the Father's heart, you choose healing over hurt. You realize that both he and she, including myself, are ultimately objects of salvation.
If you have many desires, you will have much conflict. If you are satisfied with only the Lord's glory, life becomes simple and healing and restoration occur.
If there is too much of 'me' within me, I wander. Do not struggle; rather, do what the Lord desires. Knowledge alone is insufficient; it requires obedient faith.
Fasting is not about changing circumstances. Above all, it is about changing yourself. Prolonged fasting is because you must change greatly. As much as you change, the way will open.

Many people blame their circumstances and people. They find the cause of unhappiness in others. But this is the result of letting go of what should be held and holding onto what should not be held. If you become a victim, you will be unhappy. Do not be a victim; become a wounded healer.
It was so difficult because you could not let go. Once you let go, there is nothing more to lose, and from that place, the Lord's miracles occur.

Manager Kim, I hope you are doing well. Though we part this way, I am certain that someday you will say that because of such poignancy in a corner of life, you have the warmth and happiness of today. First, do your best to take care of what remains; second, humble your heart to correct the distorted fundamental cause. For some, today's forced smile becomes a calamity, and for others, yesterday's tears of blood return as today's humble happiness, so we cannot simply say this moment is unhappiness. Your children are healthy, your family is well, and you are still quite young, so if you do not give up and plant seeds, you can enjoy pure happiness. And there is Proverbs in the Bible—an easy-to-read collection of brief sayings. Read it daily. http://kcm.co.kr/bible/kor/Pro1.html And I will also pray here: 'Those who sow in tears will reap with songs of joy. Those who go out weeping, carrying seed to sow, will return with songs of joy, carrying sheaves with them.' - Psalm 126:5-6. February 4, Jacob. From a letter I wrote to someone who thought they had lost everything.

One of the most foolish things is passing your own shortcomings onto others. Rather than throwing stones at others, you must hold the cross and break your own chest. Living as an unfaithful servant with a shattered, disappearing heart.
Colleagues do not cooperate, the system does not support, and people inexplicably misunderstand me, they complain. No! It is only that my worship has collapsed. Worship is where all recovery begins.

A new employee who just joined a large corporation after working only part-time jobs becomes greatly offended when told to clean trash cans and mop floors. However, the president of that company receives respect and glory by doing such things. A strong and honorable person can easily humble themselves to serve, and that becomes glory; but a weak and defensive person cannot humble themselves and views it as shame. A truly strong person does not become angry but serves and can humble themselves. Jesus lowered Himself endlessly and bore the cross.

Even the difficulties I experience and the wounds I receive are not reality. I must first look back to see why such suffering came and who permitted it. The reality is that I have not looked to the Lord properly but am fleeing from Him. The other person has not become my adversary; the Lord has placed a thorn in my side, urging me to repent.

The 'me' that others evaluate is not reality. Whether they praised me or criticized me, they do not truly know my essence. It is like getting angry and saying a desperately wicked criminal is a needle thief. People's random rumors and rebukes are foolish and narrow-minded, but my sinfulness is worse than that. No progress is made without breaking my idols. I am the chief of sinners.

There is a saying that you fall seven times but rise eight. It is the power of positivity. This saying is a calamity for those without Jesus. Before the ten plagues, Pharaoh and the Egyptians without God try again; the prodigal sons with absolute positivity in a pigsty without carob pods—truly the worst scenario being written.

A proud person is truly unhappy. Because they find the reason things do not work out in others. Discouragement is not the same as humility. The more proud and self-centered, the lower one's self-esteem and the easier it is to become discouraged. Because they compare. When the Lord is not at the center, people try to confirm themselves through creation. To gather strength and courage without dying to yourself is to run in the opposite direction from your destination. See yourself in the Lord. You will become humble. Pride goes before a fall, but humility comes before honor. - Proverbs 18:12

Ultimately, this world is a battle over sovereignty. The success or failure of my life is also a battle over sovereignty. Sovereignty is held over the territory and people of that nation. If God's sovereignty extends to the church but is rejected in relationships, transformation and glory do not appear. When I surrender my sovereignty to the Lord, true holiness appears. Acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty.

If you are disappointed that there is nothing good in you, that means you are proud. A humble person does not expect anything from within themselves but desires only to die to self and live through Jesus alone, so they are not discouraged by their appearance but are captivated by and rejoice in the Lord.

He says to be bold. But since I cannot be bold and He keeps telling me to be bold, it is maddening. A student who did not study cannot do well on an exam because they are fundamentally anxious. A student who did not do homework cannot be bold. If the Lord is not within me and is not my master—if the Lord's influence is not in my lifestyle—I cannot be bold. If I stand on a path the Lord does not desire and am bold, that is not boldness but ignorance. A life apart from the Lord's path should be fearful and anxious. Otherwise, how can there even be a hint of repentance?
Wake up. To do so, you must stop rationalizing.

I mistakenly think that when I achieve something, I can give glory to God. But God is already exalted alone and possesses all authority, so what would He lack that He would extend His hand to us? The only way to glorify God is for us to be glorified. That happens only by grace, through the denial of self and the surrender of rights. Only then does the Lord receive glory. He has never received glory through my self-actualization. Jesus spoke these words: 'After Jesus said this, he looked toward heaven and prayed: Father, the hour has come. Glorify your Son, that your Son may glorify you.' - John 17:1
Prayer in the wilderness brings transformation. The greatest beneficiary is yourself. Your stubbornness is broken, the Lord is revealed, and you are humbled.

In the parable Nathan told King David, the way to compensate for the lamb is to repay fourfold according to regulation. But David is enraged and declares the man must surely die. After the Bathsheba incident, David avoided repentance for a year and is insensitive to himself but enraged at others. The characteristic of hardness of heart is always finding the cause in others.
The Word keeps me from sin. Otherwise, sin will keep the Word from me.
Prayer prioritizes restoration over requests. Therefore we first worship and repent, then give thanks and make supplications. ACTS (Adore, Confess, Thanksgiving, Supplement)
Do not blame others but repent and return to God yourself. The problem is first person. If I change, it will become a good community.

A quack explains with difficulty, treats complicatedly, charges much money, and the illness hardly improves. A good doctor diagnoses accurately, explains at the patient's level of understanding, and treats decisively. Let me say it simply: The reason for all my problems is that I do not love the Lord.

There is no other gospel. The later the times, the more people want to receive something special, curious, and a secret technique that will work immediately. No such thing has existed and cannot exist. It is only the gospel of the cross. The faith that moves mountains and the word of angels must surely testify to the gospel: Jesus Christ crucified on the cross. (Gal 1:6-8) 'I am astonished that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you by the grace of Christ and are turning to a different gospel—which is really no gospel at all. Evidently some people are throwing you into confusion and are trying to pervert the gospel of Christ. But even if we or an angel from heaven should preach a gospel other than the one we preached to you, let them be under God's curse!'

Collecting much carob when eating as a pig is not success. Even when those fall and hunger comes, blessed is the one who comes to their senses thinking of the father's house and turns around. Do not slowly perish by merely not dying; awaken to the futility and turn around quickly.

I turned away from the Lord's calling many times until my teens, but one day I met the Lord and returned. The reason I came back was realizing that I am a sinner who crucified Jesus, yet He loved me and died on the cross for me. Even at that young age, I understood how precious this was. I could intuitively sense that holding onto this truth would bring many challenges. However, at the moment of my conversion, I confessed because I wanted to do this: 'I will follow only the Lord.'

Do not desire too many things. That is greed. Greed surely brings forth death. I should be thankful for the handful of air given to me, a Bible, and a heart that can run loving only the Lord. That is enough.

*True Repentance After committing a wrong, saying "I will absolutely never do that again" is not repentance. How can we live differently when we are originally that kind of being? Rather, "If the Lord does not grant me forgiveness and help, I have no choice but to live this way. This is who I originally am." After a painful recognition of oneself, true repentance and a new beginning come through the Lord's forgiveness.
I am a sinner and lacking, so I climb onto the testing platform daily. Moreover, I am the chief of sinners and weak, so I call upon the Lord at every moment. Amen, Lord Jesus~

Many people are satisfied with merely asserting and agreeing with the truth. Not paying the price and not praying is denying the truth. Ultimately, life itself becomes a denial. A tree is known by its fruit.

In the last days, the posture of the final runner. Being awake and being sober-minded, and praying are the same thing. Love without prayer is a lie. 1 Peter 4:7-8 KJV The end of all things is at hand; therefore be serious and watchful unto prayers. And above all things have fervent love among yourselves; for love shall cover the multitude of sins.

In the wilderness, shortcuts do not work. Only the Lord's presence was the way of survival. However, in Canaan, people could survive to some extent through sowing and trading. That is when idols entered. Trying to live well without His presence—that is a great sin.

Do you love me? Peter, who had denied the Lord, hesitantly says he loves Him. Now it is not by his own strength or self-assurance, but only when the Lord's sovereignty permits, and only within His grace—my very being becomes Him—that this love is fulfilled. Therefore, no one can boast. Love is here; it is not that we loved God...

*A Painful but Necessary Story When in a love relationship, change absolutely occurs. You know it, and so do those around you. The church at Ephesus, which does not compromise and keeps the gospel, enduring persecution, hears that the Lord has something to rebuke them about. That is abandoning first love. The Lord considered that most important. Claiming to 'believe' and 'love' while living a faith life is a serious illness. We must be honest and sincere. I urge you to look back at how you spend your time, your interests, and what you truly desire at your core. You may not truly and earnestly love the Lord with a burning passion. If you do not have intimate private times with the Lord and do not pay the price of sacrifice, you must admit that your love has grown cold. What marriage exists in this world without private times of intimacy? That is deeply insulting to your Bridegroom, Jesus.

Relationships are important in life. However, when a relationship takes the place of the Lord, it becomes idolatry. A relationship not given by the Lord, or obsession with relationships, is evidence of this. The Lord is not a supporting character. We should gain insight through the joy of the friend of the bridegroom, which John the Baptist had. Taking the bride is the bridegroom's business, and the friend celebrates and rejoices in it.
*Disarmament Trying to defend oneself is the instinct of sinful humans. When we lay down self-defense and become disarmed, we experience death and the Lord protects us within His grace.
The past can hurt, but as I see it, you can either run from it, or learn from it!

*Victory Over Temptation Extreme and difficult trials cease when the self is crucified on the cross. When you take up the cross and die, the trials end there. Judah, who had sold his brother, took responsibility for Benjamin and sought to save the family when all tests ended beautifully. I pray, let your servant remain as a slave to my lord instead of the boy, and let the boy go back with his brothers. Genesis 44:33

*The Strength to Rise Again The strength to rise again from ruin and weakness comes only through seeking Jesus. It comes by relying on the power of the blood and being filled with the Holy Spirit. Seeking that is what those who rise again must continually pursue. We fell because we took our eyes away from it.

*Pray This Way The reason prayers are not answered is asking wrongly for selfish desires. Such prayers cannot continue and have no power. Do not hope for gain without cost or mere chance. Rather, ask to be given the strength to pay the price. So pray earnestly that the Lord's will be done, not your own greed.
Tears that only the saved can shed are tears of gratitude and repentance.

When you make up your mind to pray, God will come and pour out His heart to you and give you His heart. He will show you the state of the church and yourself. Then He will break you. Only after this breaking, through paying the price of poverty and mourning, will He grant restoration. All works, relationships, and intentions without prayer must collapse. This is why God does not stop Satan's plans—because He loves us so much. Decide your future right now.

Hearing and being moved is not enough. You must decide and kneel. All genuine ministry comes from Jesus. It comes from intimacy with Him. That is everything. Everything else must be sacrificed to hold onto this...

*Sexual Sin Sex is God's gift. A holy gift, at that. Because it is holy, it can also become a grave sin and a danger. If sex were not such a beautiful blessing, it would not be dangerous. The Ark of the Covenant in the Holy of Holies is so sacred that mishandling it surely brought destruction.

Paul called legalists and those who possessed self-righteousness 'dogs.' (Philippians 3:2) To put it most simply, this is 'extreme sincerity moves heaven.' No matter how sincere, you cannot move God. Without faith, it is impossible to please Him, for whoever comes to God must believe that He exists and that He rewards those who earnestly seek Him. Hebrews 11:6

The reason believers keep away from sin is not because they are ethical and strong-willed, but because the glory and love of God they have experienced is so great. Under the blazing sun, a candle must be extinguished. The more you love the Lord, the more you keep away from sin. Because committing sin becomes too difficult. ㅠㅠ

Pray and serve: This is the Lord's voice. Would you date me now: This is the voice of hormones. More important than meeting is parting. Without self-control and restraint, you are being led by desire and emotion, not by the Lord's guidance. God is almighty and can faithfully lead His path without my anxious impatience. Therefore, trading faith for gain must be eliminated. If the same mistake repeats, it is not coincidence but a genuine desire for 'mistake.'
In the wilderness, only God commands us to advance. We move forward as much as we have been transformed. Therefore, I truly desire to become humble so the Lord can work sufficiently.

600,000 Israelites who saw the ten plagues and great miracles complained and died in the wilderness. However, the descendants who heard and believed the Word at Mount Gerizim and Mount Ebal entered Canaan.

When God makes us aware of another's faults, it is because the same defect exists within us. Jacob meets Laban, and the younger brother who deceived his elder brother to steal the blessing meets Leah in marriage.

**God's Solution 1. In the church, many things happen simultaneously. 2. The greatest concern is that people of faith are becoming distant from worship and God. 3. There are many reasons: relationships, heartbreak, self-pity, victim mentality, self-blame, doubt, family, and sometimes when the environment does not cooperate. 4. A competent leader would effectively resolve all these problems. Then they would become a minister sought after by large churches. 5. But the person God desires is not like that. It is someone who reports all problems in detail to the Lord and holds fast to the cross. 6. Even at this very moment, standing on the rock that is struck by the staff, that One sheds the blood of the cross, and His salvation is accomplished in us. (acknowledging it is not my problem) The people quarreled with Moses and said, 'Give us water to drink.' Moses said to them, 'Why do you quarrel with me?' -Exodus 17:2 (crying out to the Lord) Moses cried out to the Lord, saying, 'What shall I do with this people?' -Exodus 17:4 (The Lord accomplishes the salvation of the cross) Take the staff that struck the Nile in your hand and go. I will stand before you there by the rock at Horeb. Strike the rock, and water will come out of it... -Exodus 17:5,6

Proclaiming the Word is a gift. Those who receive the gift do it exceptionally well, just as someone with the gift of music plays well. However, the Lord does not praise preaching the Word well. It is because it is a gift. Power and eloquent speech do not guarantee a life of obedience to the Word or who becomes master when alone. Though I preach the gospel, I have nothing to boast about, because... What then is my reward? Just this: that in preaching the gospel I may offer it free of charge, and so not make use of my right in the gospel. 1 Corinthians 9:16-18

If my life is so full that there is no empty space for the Lord to enter, such possessions and attachments are not blessings but rather curses. Presence must take priority over work. The one who sent me is with me; he has not left me alone, for I always do what pleases him. -John 8:29

Serving with an office in the church is noble work, but God's fundamental purpose is the transformation of my character, a change in my heart's foundation. If that does not happen, no matter how much church service or dedication, you are still 'pestered.' That purpose is 'Repent!'

If there is always someone who upsets you and makes it hard to love them, then you do not yet love God completely. If you cannot love a visible person because you do not like them, you cannot love an invisible God. This is deceiving yourself with beautiful thoughts about God. Whoever claims to love God yet hates a brother or sister is a liar. For whoever does not love their brother and sister, whom they have seen, cannot love God, whom they have not seen. And he has given us this command: Anyone who loves God must also love their brother and sister. -1 John 4:20,21
