At a wedding, one wears their finest clothes. It would be shameful to wear the finest clothes while standing with a dirty body and dirty hands and feet. Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me. -Psalm 51:10

If someone says you have despised the Lord, many would leap up in objection. Not many realize that what is done to the least of these and what is done in worship is done to the Lord. The Lord Almighty says: 'When you bring blind animals for sacrifice, is that not wrong? When you sacrifice lame or diseased animals, is that not wrong? Try offering them to your governor! Would he be pleased with you? Would he accept you?' says the Lord Almighty. 'Now plead with God to be gracious to us. With such offerings from your hands, will he accept you?' -Malachi 1:8,9 I hope you will honor the Lord.

There are times when the Lord brings you down. It is to repent and be humble. It is not a new disappointment but temporarily forgetting that you are originally a human with many sins and shortcomings, then reconfirming it. Therefore, the Lord is more necessary. Absolutely nothing is possible without Jesus. That is why He took up the cross. Repent, pray, rise again, and run forward.
If you cannot fill your time with holiness, the power of sin will dominate. If you serve the Lord with the first things, you will be filled with the Lord for the last things.

When you strip away the froth from what all people say, the gist of that complexity is as follows: 1. Upon further inspection, the other person's such behavior is not desirable. 2. Nevertheless, I responded quite coolly. → Conclusion: I am quite a decent person. What we can understand: Most conversations without God are 1. lowering others, 2. elevating oneself, and 3. ultimately longing for 'recognition.'

That I stumble into sin proves something. It is the surest proof that I lived by my own strength. If my will and strength are strong, I will eventually fall, just a little later. Rather than trying to delay failure, acknowledge my collapse through the cross now and accept living with Jesus.
Not my strength, not my ability, not my zeal.... Isaiah 37:32 KJV For out of Jerusalem shall go forth a remnant, and they that escape out of mount Zion: the zeal of the LORD of hosts shall do this.
If you want a holy generation, become a holy person. Now kneel and pray for the fire from heaven to come down.

If what I did with joy suddenly feels like a loss and a one-sided sacrifice, it is because you are not looking at the One who truly lost and sacrificed. He is the very image of God, yet became a servant and obeyed even unto death. When perspective changes, you realize you break even. Please fix your sight on the Lord.

Among many forms of piety, the reason many do not bear fruit and obscure God's glory is because they excessively enjoy human praise. O Lord, do not give us the glory, do not give it to us, but give glory to your name for the sake of your love and faithfulness. -Psalm 115:1

I received much, yet strangely was not satisfied. Though I drank much, I was not full. Even wearing good name-brand clothes, strangely I was not warm. I earned wages and brought much money home, yet strangely it was like a bag with a hole in it—wealth would not accumulate. It was truly strange. 'You plant much but harvest little, you eat but are never satisfied, you drink but are never full, you put on clothes but are not warm, and the worker earns wages, only to put them in a purse with holes in it.' Haggai 1:6 Why was that? 'You expected much, but see, it turned out to be little. What you brought home, I blew away. Why?' declares the Lord Almighty. 'Because of my house, which remains a ruin, while each of you is busy with your own house.' -Haggai 1:9

That Christ was crucified for me is a fact. But if we stay there, there will be a cycle of repeated transgression and repentance. I must accept and declare in faith that I too was crucified with that cross.

The very thought that I have something to give is arrogance and sin. Peter mistakenly thought he could hold onto his faith. He thought if he risked his life, everything would work out. But after a devastating failure, after being filled with the Holy Spirit, he looks only to Jesus and lives only by Jesus. 'Silver or gold I do not have, but what I do have I give to you...' -Acts 3:6 Woe to those who have nothing but silver and gold to boast of. Because they lack the most important thing—the Lord.

1. God resists pride, especially the proud. (God opposes the proud... 1 Peter 5:6) 2. Because this sin was the beginning of Satan. (I will make myself like the Most High. Isaiah 14:14) 3. Therefore, proud people are secretly the most Satan-like. 4. Pride is Satan. Before humility, through God's great grace, all darkness collapses. If we are humble, we can turn Satan into candy. Young men, in the same way be submissive to those who are older. All of you, clothe yourselves with humility toward one another, because, 'God opposes the proud but shows favor to the humble.' -1 Peter 5:5

Your heart must be broken over what breaks God's heart. You must grieve that you cannot behold the Lord. The Lord seeks such a person. Though tears may have similar composition, you must shed tears from a different source.

If my people, who are called by my name, will humble themselves and pray and seek my face and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven, and I will forgive their sin and will heal their land.

When you lift your eyes, you see many things that strike your heart. This is because He called you to be a servant. David saw the angel striking the people and immediately said to the Lord, 'I have sinned and done wrong. But these sheep, what have they done? Please let your hand fall on me and on my father's house.' —2 Samuel 24:17

The Pharisees hated Jesus and tried to kill Him. This is because they loved themselves more than God. Their failure to recognize Jesus was not a matter of intellect but of the heart. 'How can you believe if you accept praise from one another and do not seek the praise that comes from the only God?' —John 5:44. A self-centered person can never reach the truth.
Do not seek magic but seek mercy. What I need now is for the Lord to have compassion on me. Falling at Jesus' feet to thank him, saying, 'I am a Samaritan.' —Luke 17:16
We do not call it freedom when a fish leaves water. We call it fish stew.

Various kinds of people come to church. And many come with wounded hearts. So church members sometimes display conduct unbecoming of the world. Because people believe and then are transformed, rather than being transformed before believing, we must accept and wait for such people. However, leaders are different. If a leader or a dedicated worshiper behaves this way, it becomes difficult to continue in that position. Because they give stumbling blocks to many people. The One who has been confirmed as holy requires more devotion and holiness from those newly come to church than from established members.

The most fearful thing is not threat or death. It is the withdrawal of the Lord's presence. That is why David in Psalm 51 does not even mention preserving his kingdom, but seeks His presence. He begs not to be cast away. The most foolish person is one who has no fear of the Lord's absence. A person without the Lord's presence sins carelessly. The Lord is so delicate that the moment we speak carelessly and love sin, we do not feel His presence. The presence we feel is not a feeling but the reality of Emmanuel, the Lord with us.

Seeking to solve problems easily is greed. Simply running away from that place, fearing worldly people and selling your faith, trying to console your heart with the world and people, after parting with a loved one, praying like gambling with fifty-fifty odds and betting on the Lord, interpreting yourself and your situation by comparing with others, falling into self-pity and becoming only a victim, in faith seeking to solve everything at once, seeking to solve everything in this place, right now, trying to do too well, the thought of securing money and recognition to have worry-free stability before doing anything, you must fully use the opportunity that God trains you in. You must shed some tears and experience heartache. You must stay in your place and look upon the Lord faithfully and truthfully. Only then will you come out as pure gold. Otherwise you come out unripe. Not pure gold but gold-plated gold, as they say.
My spirituality is revealed through the smallest of actions. But this does not mean to focus on the action. It means to guard the heart that is the root of that action.

The most threatening person is not an unbeliever. It is a person who has received grace but has not been transformed. A person who has heard the Word and missed the opportunity at the seat of grace and remains untransformed becomes Judas Iscariot. If you are convicted but deliberately suppress it, this person does not remain in a neutral zone without being transformed, but rather becomes a persecutor and a pretender. That is a curse. Repentance is conviction and transformation.

I am very displeased with you for presenting your worship to me as something burdensome and difficult, and I will not accept such worship. —Malachi 1:13. The Lord does not accept formal worship that lacks my heart, soul, and intention. The Lord honors those who honor Him and despises those who despise Him. If we go forward not relying on the precious blood and truth but with human skill and strange fire that the Lord has not commanded, then our altar receives no response of God's fire, but is filled with manufactured excitement, division, and temptation. We must be restored to worship in spirit and truth. —John 4:24

College admission is the goal, then employment after admission, then marriage after employment, and then living above others after marriage. It is machinery. If you live with such goals, you live withered and hollow like an empty can. When pierced with a needle, not a drop of blood comes out. When I see someone being pierced with a needle, I wish they would shed tears as if it were themselves.

When he was a shepherd, he defeated the world's mightiest Goliath. But when he became a great king, he stumbled before Bathsheba. When I become the king of my own life and am exalted, it seems strongest, but that is when I am weakest. When you serve God as King, your life becomes firm and strong. *David, who defeated Goliath, was not even recognized in his own household.

A fish saying it wants to be free from water means it wants to be enslaved to land. Sexual freedom means bondage of sexual sin. The sexual fall that many people face is because they have not given themselves to God.

What is obtained through effort is good. But when effort reaches its limit and all that is good crumbles, and you realize that you cannot do it by your own strength, then you enjoy the very best that comes from heaven.

A 'sense of merit' brings excessive self-pride, monopoly of righteousness, the attitude that only we are right and only I am right. We can stumble if we cling to and boast about what we do well. But if you have a 'sense of grace,' then 'the grace of the Lord who saved such a sinner as me' becomes amazing.

Your values determine your greed. It is so difficult for us to regard gold as stone. But a dog just regards gold as stone. Not because the dog is mature or prays in tongues a lot. It is because it has no perspective.
It is not a problem to have much money. But it is a serious problem if money has you, even if you have little money.
If I sweep away a little negativity from my heart and remove a bit more cynicism, I can reach out to more people.

My heart hurts because of rejection and wounds, and I am upset because of lack and deficiency, but because I cannot tolerate or forgive others does not mean I can dilute the Lord's forgiveness. God has already forgiven that person completely. Now I just need to let go. That's it.

If there is bitter envy in your heart, do not boast. —James 3:14. It means if there is hatred and envy, jealousy and an unforgiving heart in your heart, He does not hear your prayers. Even if great history happens, it says it is meaningless. James sounds an alarm bell to those called the famous ones of faith, saying, 'Wake up.' You cannot prevent that envy and jealousy with spiritual achievements, nor can you be acknowledged by God. So if such bitter envy is stirring within you, you should not boast but prostrate yourself on the ground. In me, the representative self-satisfaction and enemy of the cross are envy and jealousy. Recovery and miracles happen only through those who love.

Many people confess with their lips that they fear the Lord while committing sins with their bodies. Even sin committed because the flesh is weak requires repentance, but it is more serious when the conscience is seared so that one does not know they are a sinner and there is no repentance. I hope you do not despise the Lord's long-suffering patience in waiting until you repent. God will surely judge.

The reason the church community is struggling is not because believers lack enthusiasm, it is said, but it is not so. The real crisis is that faithful people demand compensation and merit. When they go beyond 'ownership mindset' to become the 'owner' and 'unprofitable servants' boast of being 'profitable servants,' wounds and division arise. They do not say that their accomplishments belong to them, and by not saying it belongs to them, they do not deliberately linger, and by not deliberately lingering, they do not deliberately leave.

The way to extinguish a raging wildfire is to set a 'backfire.' You intentionally set fire to areas not yet burned so there is nothing more to burn. What is already burned cannot burn further, so it does not progress. If I am completely burned at the altar, temptation subsides at my boundaries. Please accomplish the death of self through the cross.

Animals do not struggle. Because they are made of dust. Angels do not struggle. Because they are made of spirit only. People struggle. Because spirit was breathed into dust. (By design...) When dust and spirit collide, which way I fall determines the success or failure of the struggle. That is affection, and it is expressed in my pride, attachment, and suffering.

It is not that we do not know and therefore cannot do it. It is that we know but do not want to do it. That is pride. If you practice and live even just what you know, the ordinary will become a miracle.

The fire of the Word, the fire of the Spirit, causes people to concentrate. To concentrate means to be able to let go. Only those who have fire can boldly let go. Whether it is something prepared for a long time or precious to me, I can let it go like straw in the face of life and the Lord's glory. You cannot swallow life without spitting out what is rotten.
All I have to offer is an apology and gratitude. 'So you too, when you have done everything you were told to do, should say, "We are unworthy servants; we have only done our duty."' —Luke 17:10
He must become greater; I must become less. John 3:30.
Pride causes wrong judgment and maintains a wrong emotional state, and deceives and is deceived.

Many people rush toward their goals. This is proving that they are respectable people. When you try to prove yourself, life does not open its doors. Only when you abandon yourself and seek only the Lord's glory does life's door finally open. God does not exalt proud people. For their sake, and for the sake of God's kingdom...

The only power given to all believers is to return to the gospel. Not to react according to your own thoughts and feelings, but only to return to the gospel of shed blood. The gospel means turning from self-centered thoughts to Jesus. There is no salvation except in Jesus.

The most ignorant and foolish person is one who flows along indifferently when the blade is pointed at them. How can you know when the blade is pointed at you? If you neglect prayer, you are already standing on the blade's edge. To confess and repent before shame comes is the highest life. Repent.
You must understand what is most important. It is to repent. No one else can repent for you. Only by the grace of the Lord do 'I' do it.

If what I am boasting about now is not the Lord, it is certain proof that there is a 'spiritual problem.' When complete fellowship with the Lord, His Word, and prayer is severed, you are bound to 'subtly' boast in what is vain and will perish. (It is the difference between refinement and proficiency, but in the end, the boast is not about the Lord.) What do you hope others will acknowledge? What do you hope to display? Your boasting reveals your spirituality.

When you see others' faults and criticism arises in your heart, you are full. Being full of worldly desires, you cannot see God. Just as one who suffers bodily hardship ceases from sin, when you bear the Lord's cross, you are simply sorry and grateful.
Moses prostrated himself on the ground against slander directed at him, but before idolatry, he threw down the Ten Commandments, breaking them in anger. Become dead to yourself and alive to God.

In the wilderness, the tempter 'Satan' comes to Jesus with a plausible proposal. Using rights to finish without suffering, doing it roughly. Always the good way (the convenient way) opposes the great way (the cross).

Only those who do not want to experience repeated failure should read this. Those who belong to the flesh can never please God, but those who belong to the Lord become the Lord's joy. Position, not action, determines me. Acting has never once overcome Positioning. I am under the Lord's precious blood.
Speaking smooth lies is weaker than rough truth left unorganized. A face without a mask is far more beautiful than a masked life.
He has set me free from sin. I have overcome temptation and gained victory.

If there is bitter envy in your heart, do not boast. If there is hatred and envy, jealousy and an unforgiving heart in your heart, He does not hear your prayers. The power of the precious blood works through those who love.

Go the way I go for you. That way is neither emotion nor a place to console your heart. It is turning the world behind and living watchfully. When you cut what you hold, true value comes alive. Only those who hold on will know that such joy and meaning are far happier.
I love the Lord who touches our hearts of stone and stands us up again. I only look to the Lord who opens rivers and roads in the desert, who does a new thing in us.

Things that Satan brought a year ago, a month ago, or a week ago, tempting and distorting the direction of my life, let them not work even today. And resist and speak thus: I am not that kind of person...

1. Do you think you are closer to God than others? 2. Do you feel you have superior spiritual maturity and self-control compared to other people? 3. Do you think you hold a very important position in God's work? 4. Are you encouraged by the success of your work? Discouraged in the opposite case? If these apply to you, you are a person who dwells in the religious zeal of justifying yourself. The Bible calls such a person an enemy of the cross. As you read the above questions, do you think many times of someone you know who fits this? If so, there is a high probability that person truly is such. Humble people and proud people do not know well if they truly are such people.
If you give up what you love, you will discover real love.
If you think much has been given up, it means much more still needs to be given up.

If my people, who are called by my name, turn from their evil ways and humbly pray and seek my face, then I will hear from heaven, forgive their sin, and heal their land. Lord, reveal hidden sins, make us humble and lowered, and let our prayers reach before the Lord's throne.

1 Corinthians 13:11 - If your relationship with others is twisted, then you have grown distant from God. Forgiveness and acceptance are not done only for the other person. The greatest beneficiary is yourself.

When you cling to an unchangeable past, you blame yourself, become depressed, and lose control of your emotions. The Lord has never once urged us on, because they are not mistakes we can bear. He understands our limitations, weakness, and loneliness better than anyone. Dear XX, that is not your fault. Jesus is desperately needed in your life, and I took up the cross for you.

The journey of faith has no round-trip ticket. There is only a one-way ticket to the Lord. If you leave room to return to sin, you will surely stumble again. You cannot fight and overcome sin unless you die to yourself.
My sin is washed clean, and a new day is given to me; the Lord's precious blood has power. His love gives me praise every day; it is a marvelous power.
Please never give up and persistently fight against sin. Through the power of the precious blood, failure and frustration are transformed into testimonies and praises.

Seek grace so that you may stand before the Gospel again. Standing before the Gospel does not mean never making mistakes again, but rather not using the Lord for your own desires. Before the Lord who gave me life, I die and the Lord lives.

The person who preaches the Word is a sinner, and those who listen to the Word are also sinners. Although all are sinners, God said that when He washes us with the precious blood and anoints us, the presence of Jesus will appear and we will do even greater works. May you rely on the washing of the precious blood and the sealing of the Holy Spirit today.
When I do not keep my place, I ruin things and leave wounds. Typically, this happens when I am prideful. Pride always leaves wounds on its victims.
Pride runs much deeper than how I think of myself. It creates many wounds and bitter roots. Humility begins when I admit that I am not humble.
The Lord desires a broken heart. Through that broken crack, the Lord's glory will shine even brighter.

To be crucified, I must desire for the Word to become a sword that pierces my heart. Being pierced hurts and causes pain, but only through that piercing does new comfort flow from the blood that flows forth.
Before asking to see something more mystical and transcendent, pray to see the true self.
Changed hearts lead to a changed society.

From the perspective of an Account or Information Security Auditor, the approver and requester cannot be the same. This constitutes a legal violation. If self has not died, it is unlawful to judge matters related to oneself. Discernment is not done with good intentions alone, but requires becoming a being separated from self, someone in whom Jesus lives. "The spiritual person judges all things, but is himself to be judged by no one" - 1 Corinthians 2:15.

The reason continuous suffering keeps coming upon you is because there is an unchanged self within you that must be changed before that suffering, and the Lord persistently demands this change. Respond with transformation before endless suffering.
Trying to solve everything in its place by my own method is excessive greed. The problem is solved, but deeper wounds remain. There is a Lord's timing for all things.
When suffering is too great, you must pray and ask the Lord to humble me further.

Living as one pleases, they think that is freedom. They regard believers walking the narrow path with contempt. That is not freedom but slavery being dragged by sin. When you become a servant obedient to the Lord, there is true freedom. Being free, there is no need to become a slave to sin.
Faith is beholding what should be beheld. Greed is beholding what should not be beheld. Everything not done in faith is sin.
A person who finds the problem in others is foolish. It is like analyzing the cause while the house is burning. You must first escape, then later investigate. Only when I change is there hope.
Pride is a choice. It is the choice to deceive your true self. This destroys self-esteem, makes you anxious, causes you to be easily hurt, and makes you a person who hides.
Serving the Lord in a successful appearance is pride. When you do not rely on the Lord, you are already proud and will collapse.

A person who trembles before a policeman is a criminal. A righteous person rather receives help. A person with greed before the Lord is always anxious before both people and the Lord. But a person with a right heart finds peace. Because you love the Lord, there is nothing to hope for or fear.
If you do not forgive someone, you cannot escape judgment. Neither will the Lord forgive. Now forgive your friends who owe a hundred denarii. Because we have been forgiven ten thousand talents.

Suffering and discipline have nothing in common except that both are very painful. Suffering is for righteousness, thinking of the Lord and enduring, while discipline is the pain of being led by one's own greed. Look into the source of your suffering. Is it greed or righteousness?
Lift yourself up. The grace God has poured out is higher than the sin I committed and Satan's temptation.

Artificial flowers are gorgeous. But they are dead. When you live to show off, you become tired, have no vitality, no meaning, and no happiness. Do not be conscious of others but look to the Lord. If you are conscious of others, you must put on a show, but if you look to the Lord, you can become simply simple and truthful. The life of artificial flowers must go, and you must become a life planted by the stream.

When you examine the root of pain, it aligns with your own greed. We covet what we cannot have and claim rights to what the Lord has not given. We are thieves and greedy. If we are not satisfied with what is given, we always become a glutton never satisfied no matter how much we eat. Nothing can fill me but the splendor of relationships or abundance of material. Tolle lege, tolle lege—"pick it up and read," the words the neighborhood children played with. Augustine read Romans and was transformed. Only the Lord can fill me.

When absorbed in problems, you are always the victim and the other person is the perpetrator. But when you look to the Lord, both you and others live. David took courage by relying on Jehovah. When you see from the Lord's perspective, everyone is a pitiful sinner who needs Jesus.
If I am not broken, health, family, and church are broken. If the Lord is the Savior of my spirit, He must also be King of my self. - From the European Assembly -

The most important thing to be cautious about is becoming accustomed to serving God. Like the first time I met the Lord, amid tears, the world and I must have no separation. And there I must pray that my worship may be revived.

As someone who valued feelings, I say: if you react and go according to feelings and emotions, you will be 'ruined.' Even if contrary to your 'impulsive' heart, you must treat and think according to truth.

Eric, called the god of the guitar, after his son was born, made a covenant before God to stop drugs and debauchery, but repeatedly collapsed. In 1991, his 4-year-old son fell from a 55-story apartment and died, and he donated 7 million pounds to an island to build a drug rehabilitation center. And he wrote this song: When you see me in heaven, will you know my name? Can you hold my hand? I must be strong and carry on, because I know I don't belong here in heaven. Because he did not fall into self-pity, he could continue that life. Self-pity is idolatry and sin. Psalm 62:8. You must come before the Lord constantly and pour it all out. Then you become a healer of wounds.
A mistake made in the latter part of an adventure that seems to be progressing well right now under the guidance received is pride.

Lift your eyes and see the world. What the world values is fulfilling greed. If you chase greed, you are bound to fall into darkness. To dwell in darkness and say you cannot see is foolishness. To those who look only to the Lord, accept death on the cross, and continue on, the world has an honor it cannot bear. So there are two kinds of Christians: those who live pursuing greed and those who lay themselves down.

There are two ways to understand. The first and best way is to understand by hearing the Word. If you refuse to hear and obey the Word, He sends you to the wilderness. Then you will cry and understand with your body. I recommend the former.

A man called Mr. Law, while hungry, received a wonderful menu and was so moved that he almost memorized it. He condemns and despises others who, like himself, do not see the menu. But even when food comes out, if you cry looking at the pictures in the menu, do quiet time, and keep clinging to it, you deserve to be beaten much. The problem is not experiencing the essential finest dish. However, there is no need to convince or force happiness on those who have experienced the finest dish. They are already happy from eating and tasting, and moreover, they will voluntarily seek it again to become happy.

Three million livestock were buried alive due to foot-and-mouth disease. It is said to have started from a farm owner's overseas visit. When sinful Israelites try to live with God, one of two things happens: either obey and receive God's power, or disobey and receive discipline. We are important people, so individual obedience is required.

The reason the Lord allows me to see another person's shortcomings is not to point fingers but rather to look back at myself. I must remember that I was once a sharp thorn to another person, and I often still am. May I become comfort and exhortation with gentleness and authority.

In relationships, conflict mostly stems from each other's weaknesses. If even one side receives the Lord's touch, there is no need for such collision. Our inner self is distorted and painful. My weakness must not wound family and friends. I must come before the Lord, the throne of grace who bears my weakness. The Spirit also helps our weakness... Romans 8:26.

Do not worry about what you will eat, what you will wear, or how things will turn out. Worry accomplishes nothing and instead grinds away faith. If you worry, you are prioritizing food, clothing, and problems over God. Worry is sin.

The most foolish thing is to blame others. Even more foolish is to judge others based on imagination. God's healing and restoration have no side effects. Jesus did not come to blame but rather came to be blamed for us.
Hold the prickly person in your heart with compassion. I too was once such a sharp thorn to someone.

A businessman trying to sell goods advertises greatly and prepares events, but there is no actual product—only brochures and product information distributed. That is fraud. Give what you promised. Doing the Lord's work without being filled with the Holy Spirit makes you the greatest deceiver.
The purpose of advertising is marketing. If you claim to preach truth while you have not experienced it or practiced it, you will experience the most effective backlash.

One of the most foolish things is to condemn others. When you condemn, both the condemned and the one who condemns become miserable together. Instead of spending time blaming others, repent. God will lift you up. Let us go build an altar of prayer.

Not asking about a path you have not traveled means you will lose your way, and not praying about a life you have not lived means wasting time and committing sin. Walk together with Him, and you will not fulfill the desires of the flesh.
Guidance_and_Protection: To live well without regret as time passes, you must live wisely. Living well means focusing on the Lord and not fearing people.
Envy, jealousy, and pride rob you of joy, hinder your anointing, and prevent your growth. Do not respond to people; respond to God. Only God has the authority to exalt and humble. Psalm 75

When taking a test, even if you encounter problems you don't know, you must push through and keep moving forward. We are not called to be perfect but to be obedient. God does not delight in those who retreat and fall into ruin. A problem that seemed impossibly difficult in elementary school becomes easy in middle school.

When you encounter trials, you should consider it joy. This is because the testing of your faith develops perseverance. When perseverance is fully developed, you become complete and mature, lacking nothing. Persevering without guidance is difficult. That is why you need a guide. If you receive wisdom and insight that says, 'Last month I was attacked, but this month I will reap a harvest,' you can endure present suffering. Trial → Maturity; wisdom is necessary for perseverance. If you have passed through the tunnel, pass through boldly. God will surely reward you. 'Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything. If any of you lacks wisdom, you should ask God, who gives generously to all without finding fault, and it will be given to you.' - James 1:2, 5

When you encounter trials, what the Lord commands is perseverance. Perseverance makes me complete. Wisdom that looks to the Lord's promise will help perseverance. The Lord will surely reward those who hope in Him. 'Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything. If any of you lacks wisdom, you should ask God, who gives generously to all without finding fault, and it will be given to you.' - James 1:2, 5

From the New Year's conference onward, the message that keeps coming up is about faithfulness, labor, and dedication. And that labor and faithfulness are surely remembered by God. Look only to the Lord and run toward His Word. Leave behind hardship and pain and run for the Lord. 'Therefore, my dear brothers and sisters, stand firm. Let nothing move you. Always give yourselves fully to the work of the Lord, because you know that your labor in the Lord is not in vain.' - 1 Corinthians 15:58

Having good faith means knowing the Lord more deeply. The God whom David experienced has no misunderstanding about me, knows all things, and it is spiritually impossible to separate from the Lord beyond space and time. In the end, realizing that each moment I breathe is a moment held in the Lord's hand, I come to cherish His Word, examine myself, and seek His guidance.

Disappointment always means you had expectations. If you pursue worthless things, you will surely be disappointed. They wither away quickly. Those who pursue eternal things will obtain unfading glory.

When fighting, to not fight without direction and not make vain attempts at empty air, you must hear the Lord's voice in prayer and ministry and receive His guidance. That is the boundary line that distinguishes worldly zeal from godliness.

No matter how thoroughly you sweep water that flows from garbage, it will not become clean. No matter how much the Lord wants to pour living water, if my vessel remains in a sewer, all kinds of filth and poison will flow out through me. Leave behind the place of filth and darkness and come to the place of worship, prayer, and the proclamation of God's Word.
No matter how dark the circumstances appear or how critical the environment seems, advance toward the goal. Never stop, but continue with the power to love and humble yourself with unwavering love.

No matter how much information you gather or how much you think, you cannot be certain of making the right decision. That is why you must pray. Those who follow others have no prayer. A decision without prayer ignores God and denies His image. I hope you become a person whose life the Lord takes responsibility for.
If your heart is beating, it means you have a calling. If time and life are given to you, you have God's calling, and that calling is love. Please serve with what the Lord has given you.

Abraham was instructed to leave the land of Ur in Chaldea and go to the land of Canaan. However, he wandered at an intermediate point called Haran. There he received God's rebuke and finally entered the land of Canaan. He was 75 years old at that time. Do not wander in Haran. Haran means 'dryness.'

When electricity first came to Ireland, the Irish people were truly amazed and grateful. In the darkness, turning on electric lights and lighting candles was very convenient. It is foolish to live a life that only moves when cornered. Use your faith.

When you show a top-of-the-line Mercedes to an African native, they will be amazed. No matter how hot it is outside with temperatures exceeding 50 degrees, if it is set to 22 degrees, it will always maintain 22 degrees. Also, navigation works on the screen, and when bored, you can watch videos. So that car is parked in front of the chief's house, and people who have walked a long way come in and rest when it is hot and then leave. This is the tragedy of believers who do not understand grace in detail. Grace is not merely comfort of the heart but the actual, concrete power of God working strongly in reality.
Redesign your suffering youth with God's dream and passion. You were not such a person. The unchanging fact is that you are God's own person, whom God Himself called.
Do not lament what has not happened; be grateful for what He has not directed you toward. There is a purpose in it.

Do not back down when things do not work out; instead, look in faith and try. Even if you lack faith and sink while walking on water, the Lord will hold your hand and you can walk. The fact that there is no failure means you have not attempted anything. Faith is an adventure.

A traditional exam that the devil gives is first about priorities. It says to make bread from stones. If the devil tested even the Son of God, would he spare weak humans? There is an enormous difference between 'eating and doing' and 'doing and eating.' There is not only a fundamental difference between simply living according to desires (profit) and seeking His kingdom and His righteousness (justice), but it creates an eternal difference.
If you pursue only stability, you will not live the best life but only the second-best life. Take the risk of faith.
Looking toward the goal, thinking deeply, and pursuing it means that the goal you are aiming for is so precious that you do not care about other things.
If those who go ahead are faithful, those who follow will also become faithful. Do not faint; be faithful.

Are you weak? The moment you look to the Lord, you become spiritually strong. That is why you must always look to the Lord. That is the beginning and that is the completion. Look to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of faith. - Hebrews 12:2

Suppose one day you receive a text or email saying someone will give you several million pounds. How grateful and relieved would you feel? However, most such offers involve borrowing money, and when you repay it, you must pay double the principal over six years—it is a loan. It appears to help and solve problems, but the conclusion is that you must earn much more money. The Lord does not give cash. The Lord gives promises. Promises bear fruit through prayer and obedience.

Before long, people of our time no longer ask, 'Who am I?' They only ask, 'What do others think of me?' It is difficult enough to live your own one-person life, yet people live the lives that others and relatives expect of them. A meaningless life is unhappy. It is not your own life. You must decide whether to see yourself through God or through your possessions.
When you are at a loss as to where to begin, pray like this: 'Lord, take control!'
There is no one to look to but Jesus, no matter what problems or challenges come. I hope you look to the living Jesus, transcending problems and circumstances.

Someone asks like this: 'If I cannot climb very high, cannot I take off one piece of clothing and give it to someone without clothes?' The answer is this: That is why the Lord says to live diligently. Do not go around naked receiving only help; help those who need help. Since you are going to give, give multiple pieces of clothing and help. The Lord does not extinguish a smoldering wick or break a bruised reed. But at the same time, the Lord does not want me to become a smoldering wick or bruised reed. Be a torch and like a cedar.

Do not pray half-heartedly or receive guidance half-heartedly and speak words you should not speak, causing hurt, or act hastily and fail. The greatest mistake is arbitrary interpretation. Comparing and being a victim is not the Lord's will. You must look only to the Lord in faith.

Life carries hardship as a default option. When God does not work according to my will, it is easy to blame others. This is because you are trying to walk in empty air, separated from reality. Life is not a vacation; it is paying the price and holding fast to holiness.

When mom goes into the operating room, the children cry. But there is nothing they can do instead. Mom comforts the children and tells them to obey, care for each other, and she will return in good health. The day before Jesus went to the cross, He made one request. While washing feet, He told us to cover each other's shortcomings and love one another. When I return, you will know you are my disciples by loving one another. That is the Lord's request. Now the only way to follow the Lord is to love one another.
I am praying for essays, jobs, health, visas, finances—especially tuition, relationships, families, marriages, and visions. Be bold.

Ephesians 5:15-17: 'Be very careful, then, how you live—not as unwise but as wise, making the most of every opportunity, because the days are evil. Therefore do not be foolish, but understand what the Lord's will is.' 1. The characteristic of people who live successfully in the last days is living 'wisely.' (verse 15) 2. The word 'wisely' seems sudden, but verse 16 says that 'making the most of every opportunity' is wisdom. Paul's hint is much appreciated! 3. Translating this with the help of NIV closer to the original meaning, it is 'making the most of every opportunity.' Making the most of opportunity is wisdom. Here, opportunity refers to opportunities already given. Not the future. 4. To see opportunity in a life that does not look like opportunity, filled with hardship and difficulty, you must become someone who understands 'what the Lord's will is' (verse 17). 5. The ability to see your life through the Lord's eyes—the highest ability a believer has—the power to 'interpret.'

If you love summer and try to hold onto it so you don't miss it, the Lord cannot give you a abundant autumn. When you let go of earthly things, you receive better heavenly things. That is walking together with the Lord.
Ministry is Motivation. If you are not motivated, you must not force it on others but rather prostrate yourself and repent of what you need to repent.

Small obedience, miraculous change. When the church is not following the Bible, if you pour your passion into criticism, collapse will accelerate and you will not escape judgment. If you look at yourself and live in thorough obedience to God's Word, you will experience the miracle of the community coming alive.
Rise high so that inferiority and victimhood do not touch you. At 350km up to 800m.
A generation giving passion and intellect. I bless you to have burning logic.
There is no one without passion. The problem is not persisting to the end. Jesus never stopped. He advanced until the end and said at the cross, 'It is finished.'

In English, the word 'crazy' sometimes refers to persistently pursuing one field with passion—absorbed, so to speak. But the word 'insane' feels like continually going to a fake well where you cannot obtain living water. If you are mad (insane) for worthless things, turn around and be mad (crazy) for the true goal. You cannot reach (crazy) without being mad (reach).

Your life is your message. Pray earnestly for your disciples and clearly command them to live a great life. That means 'Look to the Lord alone as your teacher and run after Him.' Considering everything useful as loss... to be found in Christ... - Philippians 2. I have prayed that your faith will not fail... - Luke 22:32

Reaction versus focus. Repeatedly seeking to confirm relationships through the other person's response is because of fear. This anxiety stems from 'anxiety about one's own existence.' Not knowing God, you do not know your own existence and purpose; attachment arises, and through vain pursuits, you twist your life. But those who know God through His Word will know their identity and purpose, transcending anxiety.

There are two ways to go to the neighbor's house: one is to walk straight forward, and the other is to walk backward and go around the whole earth. I will run to you. Yes, I will run the race until I see your face, not by might, not by power, but by the Spirit of God.
A person who believes in Jesus does not do anything by force. They act in the Lord's guidance, that is, in abundant guidance.

The moment you do not focus on Jesus and His Word, you have already entered into sin, and because of pride, you cannot understand; your vision is obscured, and like a car with broken brakes, thoughts that are evil and corrupt boil up as a result. - 1 Timothy 6:4-5. However, if your life becomes sufficient with only the Lord, you will become holy from all these things. - 1 Timothy 6:6

The end of those who look to people. Trying to satisfy people is truly selfish. Pilate knew that Jesus was innocent, but he delivered Him to the cross to satisfy the Jews. It was not because he loved the Jews but solely to satisfy them for his own peace. End people-pleasing and turn entirely to the Lord.

The way Jesus deals with our fear was not to remove the cause of fear. The Lord says, 'It is I; do not be afraid.' The storms and waves remain, but the Lord is present. When the Lord is present, we become bold, and the waves naturally become calm.

Believing in Jesus does not immediately make you a saint. That is the cause of despair. When Israel defeats Egypt through ten plagues and crosses the Red Sea, they change from 'slaves' to 'free people.' But their minds still carry 'slave mentality.' The old man's 'bad self' is still alive and squirming. That is why the wilderness journey begins. The Lord calls this (wilderness) 'church life.' - Acts 7:38

Pass through the night this way. You must pray that Jesus becomes light in your darkness. When darkness covers you, it means I have no options, and it means the Lord will become light. When passing through darkness, the Lord will become light for you.

There are people who deliberately walk the path that Jesus blocks. They are foolish. There are people who seek Jesus's help on their own path. They are cunning. There are people who walk a path they would not have taken if not for Jesus. They are people of faith. They will see God's glory.

At twenty years old, with a broken and torn heart, I stayed up all night on a Friday and went to an all-night prayer service, not knowing what I was seeking in this evil world. Around 2 a.m., Pastor Kim Jong-han, who was 73 years old, spoke an unexpected word to me: 1. I understand all your heartache. 2. And you must walk the Lord's path. God understands me more than anyone else. And He encourages me to walk the Lord's path. A person of faith does not fail in the Lord's work due to personal circumstances. For the 24 years I have kept this, it is a rule and the minimum courtesy toward the Lord. By faith alone! In Him who gives strength, I can do all things.
* Philippians Power Study (intense and powerful Bible study) - Fellowship preparation in progress

* Do not be discouraged but fear the Lord. You are discouraged because you relied on yourself. You are separated because you relied on people. You perish because you relied on material things and circumstances. Only those who fear the Lord will shine like the stars in heaven.

* The Power of Rejection In this evil age, when you reject comfort and convenience and embrace your calling, you can live a life of obedience to God's intention in calling you to this place. We deceive ourselves thinking we can walk the Lord's way while simultaneously fulfilling our own desires. Blessing comes when you reject your desires. If your desire is self-seeking, then the Lord's desire is your calling.

* Why Some Don't Change Despite Agreement Though people nod while troubled and listen sweetly, when you observe what they actually choose, they have not abandoned themselves. It is because from the start they never intended to give up the pleasures they enjoy. If you do not obey, you will not hear God's voice.

* The Purpose of Piety In 1990 at the Homyeong Prayer Center in Daegu, the Lord rebuked me severely. He said not to use His blessings for makeup before people. There will be many people who treat piety as material for profit. That is not the Lord's servant becoming the master and using God for themselves—it is sin. Truly there will be woe upon those who seek human pleasure. No matter how pious they appear, they will be infinitely ashamed before the Lord's judgment seat. Seek the Lord's pleasure.
* Abundant Supply God's faithful supply never lacks for God's work that aligns with His will.

* The Secret to Building a Blessed Life To build a blessed life, you must remove many things. Among them especially, if possible, you would want to remove the causes of suffering and be free. One of the greatest sufferings is childbirth pain, yet no one wants to abandon the cause of that pain—life itself. Suffering is not a problem to be avoided but a work to be completed.

* Imagination is free, misunderstanding has a cost Refrain from premature conversation with those who judge others and themselves and decide their attitudes based only on imagination. Eventually they will only believe what they wanted to see and hear, misinterpreting and attacking any goodness. First, look to the Lord and stake your claim.

* Limitation is Excellence If you are tossed about by circumstances and situations, you cannot follow God's will. I must discern the Lord's will I should walk and draw a line I will not cross. Limitation is excellence.
Do not be lonely; be a comforter. Lift your eyes and see that far more people need you.

Left-handed judge Othniel killed King Eglon and saved Israel, and there was peace for forty years. Life's resolution and relational peace are given by the Lord. A nation, people, or individual experiences peace and redemption when they act in faith.

Joseph went before the king in official position at age thirty and lived to be 110 years old. If Moses had benchmarked Joseph, he would not have left the palace even at forty. Moses wandered the wilderness for forty years and was called at eighty. Do not envy others' lives; live the life God has given you. Even if it does not appear good...

If Joseph had believed 'I will return home this year,' his faith would have shipwrecked. Joseph believed in God who was with him. Therefore, he could be led on a path he had never traveled. Do not force your way to achieve your desires; rather, examine whether the Lord is with you. The Lord asks, 'Where is your faith?'

I share what may be curious from recent Deuteronomy quiet times. The law is not made of one thing. The law consists of: first, ethical law—law where God's character and intention are directly revealed (e.g., the Ten Commandments); second, civil law; third, ceremonial law; fourth, health law. Among these, the laws abolished by the cross include Israel's civil law and the ceremonial law that prefigured Jesus's sacrifice. Civil law is the specific application of the Ten Commandments in place and time, so the external form explained concretely to Israel no longer needs to be kept by us, yet the principle has not been abolished. Furthermore, the laws that cannot be abolished include health law, which is unchanging as long as human cells don't change, and the ethical and moral law of the Ten Commandments, which is unchanging as long as human nature doesn't change.

* Limited Opportunity Opportunities are not always available. As long as I have life, I have only the opportunity to sow love and gain life. Believers face judgment for rewards; unbelievers face judgment for sin. It is appointed for people to die once, and after that comes judgment. —Hebrews 9:27 And white robes were given to each of them, and they were told to rest a little longer, until the number of their fellow servants and their brothers and sisters were also completed, who were to be killed as they themselves had been. —Revelation 6:11
There is no such thing as luck. There is only thorough guidance. I hope you will abandon 'perhaps' and steadfastly walk the prepared path through prayer. Do not compare or wander.
Desire + Anxiety = Obsession; Purpose + Passion = Concentration; Life - Faith = Worry; Life + Faith = Hope; 2 Life = Worry + Hope (Life is either hoping or worrying)
Do not seek to fulfill your desires; earnestly pray that God's will be done. The Lord will anoint your prayer. Because it is an intention that receives God's support.

* Reason for Anguish Simply put, the reason you are anguished is because you try to decide things beyond your authority. My future is not for me to decide, and I cannot change people's hearts. If you look to the Lord, who is not limited by circumstances and people, you will have no frustration or regret. Though a man plans his course in his heart, it is the Lord who establishes his steps. —Proverbs 16:9
None of us plan to fail. Failure comes naturally and gradually. If we refuse to learn from our mistakes, problems grow increasingly larger.

Our society is built on human networks of school ties and regional connections. There's a worldly saying, 'a severed tie.' It means the person who looked after your back or the hope you were waiting for no longer exists. We never had a tie to begin with. Because we believe in and depend on a tie that never existed from the start, the Lord is showing us that the tie we now think is broken is not a tie at all. Since it's a tie that never existed, it's good it was broken, and there's no need to tie it again. But the Lord becomes our 'tie.' Moreover, the Lord becomes a 'pillar' incomparable to worldly 'ties.' "The Lord is my rock, my fortress and my deliverer; my God is my rock, in whom I take refuge, my shield and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold." —Psalm 18:2

* Victory Even in Darkness All people in the Bible did not walk having seen their success. Though it was dark, they ran to live the life the Lord desired. The person standing in that place is already a victor and a blessed person. Blessed is the one who does not walk in step with the wicked or stand in the way of sinners or sit in the company of mockers, but whose delight is in the law of the Lord, and who meditates on his law day and night. That person is like a tree planted by streams of water, which yields its fruit in season and whose leaf does not wither—whatever they do prospers. —Psalm 1:1-3

* Faith to Leave the Familiar When He told you to leave the familiar place you relied on and did not tell you where to go but commanded you to go forth, it means God Himself will guide you to your destination. Leave your country, your people and your father's household and go to the land I will show you. —Genesis 12:1

Our greatest mistake is trying to fix others according to our way and our image. Usually, everyone then withdraws and is wounded. Remember: if you were changed, it was not by anyone but the Lord who changed you. (Acts 20:32) Now I commit you to God and to the word of his grace... Commit to the Lord, and the Lord will give you wisdom and guidance.
We give up a day and give up a week not because the challenge is fierce but because it seems difficult. Even if you fall, get up and continue. Never give up looking to the Lord.

Everything we cannot do is not material for despair but a 'subject for prayer.' So for the most important thing we cannot do by our own strength—'praying'—we must earnestly pray for God to give us the 'power of prayer' and guide us. Pray in order to pray.

The reason we must unhesitatingly continue to hold fast to the truth and press forward even when we encounter tribulation is because no matter how dark the situation appears, God is the one who, with His great wisdom and power, gives a home run in the ninth inning with two outs. Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up. —Galatians 6:9
A person we think will be our strength can never truly be our strength. Relying on such a person and going ahead will result only in prolonged regret. Because God cannot bear to see such a thing.

We sometimes cannot give a precise answer to why we are here. Despite not volunteering, we fight on the front lines. That is not our decision but the Lord's calling. If He called us, He certainly gave us the ability to bear it.

* Recovery vs. Escape When facing problems, some people escape. The easiest decision is the most evil decision. Rather than leaving my place, I should make the place I am in full and a place of repentance. When you face the problem not by turning away but by confronting it with the Lord, it is solved.
We do not read the Bible to change God's will toward us. We read the Bible to change our understanding of God.
Most of our failures and mistakes in life can be forgiven us. The most fatal and great mistake for people is giving up, and if you don't hold onto that...
Learn how to love people's hearts, which are wider than the universe and more delicate than flower petals. That is by asking the Lord. Love without understanding is suffering.

* Strategy of Faith Though we can do many things, not all of them are beneficial. If we strategically invest our strength in what is most important, the rest will naturally balance. That is to fear God. Yet without such strategic and careful approach, the elder at a rural church grasps what is important at once. It is truly amazing.

When an enemy comes against me, should I attack or defend? David confessed this way: The Lord spreads a table before me in the presence of my enemies. —Psalm 23 If we think we are being pursued and attacked by enemies, we could stab and kill Saul who entered the cave, but that's all. David thought he was being guided by God. Rather than countering attacks, accept the Lord's invitation to the dinner He has prepared.

* How to Overcome Evil with Good Those who take God's word as light and lamp are characterized by peace and boldness. It is because they work by the Lord's standard and unconditionally entrust the rest to God regardless of results. It means fighting completely differently without using the world's methods. It means winning through love and forgiveness. And you will always win, without fail.

Do not live as you wish but live requesting what you need. Giving up everything and running away is what you want; receiving God's power and authority in the place you stand is what you need. May you become young people who overcome darkness with the sword of God's word.
* The Greatest Mistake The greatest mistake is doing nothing to avoid mistakes. If you avoid the contest, you will truly regret it bitterly.

Hearing a voice does not mean it is all correct. Prophecy should be according to the measure of faith. We know in part and prophesy in part, only for edification. The only One to hold onto is Jesus. Let the speaker be careful and the listener discern.
In this final generation, one who does not waste time, is not deceived by schemes, and truly accomplishes the Lord's work is one who discerns what the Lord's will is. —Ephesians 5:17
There are no coincidences in this world. All things happen under the Lord's permission. When we think within the Lord's will, every event becomes an opportunity for us.

If I try to carry out my own desires, only regret will remain, but if I follow the Lord's path, there will be glory. If I am greedy, I may gain something, but I will eventually lose everything. The Lord's path requires many sacrifices, but instead I will gain all things.

Because something is difficult to accomplish, no one can force me to abandon my dream. Do not fear a battle you cannot win, and do not worry about pain you cannot bear. And even if you cannot accomplish it, I hope you continue to dream. Miracles happen in places no one else has seen.

The Israelites experienced the miracle of the Exodus, drank water from the rock, were baptized in the Red Sea, and ate spiritual food—manna—but they perished. Experience is not everything. Do not grumble. Some of them who complained were destroyed by the destroyer. Do not grumble as some of them did. —1 Corinthians 10
Economy is not better than business, business is not better than first class. But if the airplane is crashing, do not be satisfied with riding the best thing; you must quickly switch.
The best way to respond to incomprehensible moments is to ask the Lord. God, what should I learn through this?
When placed in an unfamiliar environment, you experience God's work more concretely.

Human desire is selfishness. The Lord's will is calling. If I live as my heart desires, I will pursue what He has not given me, but if I live as the Holy Spirit desires, I will live with gratitude for what He has given me.

I urge you to lay down human greed and methods and acknowledge His sovereignty, who has become the reason and praise of my life. After that, He will open the way, accomplish His will, and provide for it.

You may think you are being guided, but it could be a dead end. Paul saw the Macedonian vision and came, but ended up in prison. If the circumstances given to me are a dead end, it is because that experience is the best for me, so He permitted it. Take a moment to reflect in that dead end.

We can easily realize that life does not go according to our will, no matter how hard we try. From the beginning, unexpected lives and encounters are waiting for us, and we feel ourselves drifting further and further from the trajectory of life we have drawn. Behind this is the Lord's guidance. Many figures in the Bible did not live lives they desired or could predict, but lived according to the will the Lord wanted to accomplish.
Life is a struggle of priorities. Whether the subject of love and affection is you determines success or failure. What I do first tells of my future.

I compare life to a marathon. I think it is an appropriate comparison, with the premise that not everyone runs the same course. Live in your own course with your own strengths. Stop comparing anymore.

If you think of life in terms of speed, you become unhappy. However, if you think of it in terms of direction, it becomes clear. If the path I am taking is the path of truth, no matter how many people rush down the path of worldly desires, you will not be shaken. The meaning of life comes not from speed but from direction.

At important moments of life, it is God who opens the door of my life. That God upgrades us to a new life through the blessing of encounters. The ability to hold onto that encounter comes from capability—from sincerity.
When passing through life's storms, the most important thing is to cast your anchor in Jesus.

Abraham at life's halftime leaves a relatively stable life to venture forth not to 'the land shown' but to 'the land to be shown.' The path of vision is certainly not easy. However, a sadder life is one where you do not attempt anything, clinging to an anxious present out of fear of failure.

In the voyage of life, you must follow the light to avoid running aground and reach your destination completely. Yet among people, there are those who follow the lights of other moving ships. That is foolish. That light passes away and cannot be a standard. You must only follow Jesus, the lighthouse of eternal truth who is always present. When God is your standard, the power to overcome the world comes upon you.

Life does not become wonderful in an instant. Just as there is a time and season for all things, wrinkles and gray hair appear and we become wise. Rather than putting my hand to it, it is true wisdom and understanding to yield and entrust things to the Lord's doing.
People say that is how life is. Be assured: life is never that way. God does not leave a person with eyes of faith to live only in the routine path others take. Rise high.

When life does not unfold as we think, even if we are told not to look back at ourselves, we do. It is God's grace. We must break the cycle of making our own plans without the Holy Spirit and becoming angry when things do not go our way. Without the Holy Spirit, I am nothing. Let us adventure with the Holy Spirit.

Elementary and middle school children are spreading things massively on the internet. Those who understand are suffering from defamation lawsuits and related regulations. The gospel seems similar. Those who insist on perfection before moving forward accomplish nothing their whole lives. However, when we return to our original heart, we proclaim and return boldly.

In ordinary circumstances, faith is not evident. In extraordinary situations and moments of crisis, true faith is displayed in relying on the Lord and gaining strength. When the people's hearts grieved because of their children and said to stone David, David was greatly distressed, but he strengthened himself in the Lord his God. - 1 Samuel 30:6

Work does not feel frightening because its size is greater than me. It is because I do not feel God's presence with me. A person for whom God is the solution before impossible work does not step back. 'Do not fear, for I am with you; do not be afraid, for I am your God. I will strengthen you; I will also help you; I will uphold you with My righteous right hand.' - Isaiah 41:10
The early bird catches the worm, they say. Then someone always comes along saying the early worm gets caught. The biggest problem lies in the identity of thinking you are the worm.
Now, Pillar Class 8... I suspect this may be the last Bible study at LG for me. I pray that the Lord's will is accomplished. I also request your prayers.

Do not be satisfied because of where you belong; it is God who created me. Do not find meaning in your glorious relationships; it is God who called me. Do not fear the world or pursue glory; it is also God who sent me.
Without giving up yourself and your desires, you cannot escape the wilderness.

A person who looks at themselves in a mirror comparing themselves to others becomes either arrogant or discouraged. However, before God's mirror, a person becomes humble. A person with the wisdom not to sin and the wisdom to be humble becomes honorable. See Pillar Classes 1 and 2.

A person who relies on themselves seems confident and assured. What such people are good at right away is tightrope walking. Since they are confident, they walked the tightrope. However, their fall and shame will be severe. The claim that you can gain holiness and satisfy the flesh is a lie. Such a thing does not exist. Stop tightrope walking, simply give up, and walk the narrow path. Prodigals who came back awkwardly tend to walk that wall like a tightrope walker.
Do not prove yourself; testify of the Lord. Then He will show you how to act.

If you work by your own mind and popularity, you can do things quickly, but you can only move a few. However, if you work from the heart, you gain everyone's heart and furthermore display God's works.
You must not think of work beyond your measure. It is not my responsibility and only brings corroding anxiety. If you acknowledge the sovereignty of the Almighty, you will have peace.
To fulfill your desires, you will be anxious and worried, but to fulfill the Lord's will, you will instead have peace and boldness.

Not keeping your own place is sitting in the seat of the arrogant. David says in Psalm 1 there is not the slightest blessing. When you cannot keep the place the Lord gave you, disgrace and uncleanness come upon you. Hold fast to your place.

You need not fear becoming a dropout. For God has prepared beforehand deeds for His good purposes. It is fine even if someone doing well helps, but God's preparation is most beautiful and perfect. We are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand so that we should walk in them. - Ephesians 2:10
You think you cannot because you are insufficient. That severely limits the Almighty God. The Lord is listening now and seeking. He is guiding you.
What you truly desire, your destination, tells of your identity. Who are you?
Be free. Failure due to lack of freedom is an infinite repeat retake.

Recovering self-esteem and praying for doors to open at workplaces, bringing bright lights amid great suffering and frustration. Recovery seemed to exist only in books, but it also appears in life. So today too, I run, to pray.

Jonah, claiming to fear God, shuffled around here and there, and after extreme torture, eventually passed through the three-day journey of Nineveh in just one day, speaking God's word in a mutter, yet the people repented. Open your mouth and speak God's word whether in season or out of season. Then the Lord will take care of it.

A small decision changes the course of life's voyage. Therefore, choice determines destiny. Not a decision your flesh desires but a decision not made unless by the Lord—that is 'faith.' A faithful decision is the good that the Almighty Lord thinks. Through that choice, God glorifies you, making your life one of glory.
If you are not faithful in small things, others' things, and dishonest wealth, God cannot use you with power.

If you are affected by small things, then for important matters you have your eyes covered saying 'yes, yes.' Stop imitating and gain genuine skill. That comes from repeated visitation arising from repentance.
Do not be so exhausted trying to be perfect even in small things. No matter how small a task, it must be done by the Lord.
Stop scheming and stop regretting; ask with faith and be bold. Whether it is scheming or faith can be discerned by the degree of 'urgency.'
One who listens well can proclaim well. Listening and humbling oneself come before standing and speaking.

Did you rest well? I too left behind the retreat where I slept two hours at a time and rested yesterday with my usual sleep pattern for once. Now, let us run toward something better, something higher. Here we go, One way, Jesus + a bottle of living water ^^// The youth group continues.

Successful people try to solve things themselves. There are many such people. However, when it is dark and I can do nothing, it means the Lord will do it. When there is no option, rejoice. The only Option, Jesus, will be revealed.
When you do wrong, the whole world is filled with darkness. But when you turn around, the Lord shines again.

Trying to do well exhausts you. Relationships, ministry, work—everything becomes stress. God did not call us to do everything well and satisfy everyone, but said that doing one or two things, or even just one, is enough. If I do that one thing the Lord called me to without greed, I truly do it well. Rushing too quickly and trying to gain everything is greed. Miracles actually happen when you abandon greed.
Proverbs uses the term 'fool' frequently. The fool spoken of there is not one with low IQ or someone inflexible, but rather someone with such a good mind that they do not rely on the Lord's word.

Noise prevents you from hearing. If you are too busy to hear the Lord's voice and your attention is elsewhere, He calls you to a loud place. That is the wilderness. No water, dry and desolate, no one around. Only there does God's word become heard and seen, and you train to live not as you please but by listening and faith. For we walk by faith, not by sight. - 2 Corinthians 5:7

Do not despair at obstacles; ask the Lord how you can be faithful right where you are and prostrate yourself before Him. Pray, receive guidance, and obey. Even in such humble work, if you handle it with faithfulness and sincerity, in God's time, by God's method, God's will is accomplished.
More important than having talent, ability, and education is faith. Look at Jabez—he had nothing, but believed in the Lord's help.
To waste talent and money is foolish. Among them, to waste time is the most foolish thing. Because time is life. When time decreases, life decreases.

Where your treasure is, there your heart is. Because God sees my motives, He is deeply interested in people's money matters. Earning money is a technique; using that money is an art. God hopes that I am an artist who knows beauty.
People keep asking if there's an evening prayer meeting, and even though you know, why do you keep asking? Even if nuclear war breaks out tomorrow or a tsunami strikes, we will continue steadily on.
When you pray for two and a half hours in the evening and return home, you will feel that there is no time to waste in life. Now anticipate the anointing upon your studies and future path.
I am somewhat impulsive. At times, very much so. Because each moment that seems unlikely to return in one's lifetime is precious, and the enlightenment the Lord gives becomes deeply moving.

Over the past 25 years of faith and ministry, I have witnessed many young people lose faith and wander after experiencing grace like heaven. And I have heard the Lord's tearful cry for those young people. In a dormitory room at Seoul Theological Seminary in 1991 and at a prayer retreat in Gyeongnam in 1990. His heart has always been this: 'Has the world truly given you happiness? I do not want to lose you.'
I pray that the Lord would give me the gift of motivation. The time given to me is five minutes.
We prepare food with craftsmanship. With a missionary spirit....
What I earnestly sought in my youth is this: wherever the Lord sends me does not matter, but I will not go like a dry stick. I desire the Lord's anointing. That was my only condition.

When you truly long for something with all your heart, you naturally move to achieve and obtain it. Not praying earnestly for what you desire means you truly do not want it. I urge you to earnestly seek what you now desire and what you wish to see.
If you truly love, you must learn to love and be loved by that person. Ignorant love easily becomes discourteous love. And it inevitably inflicts deep wounds.

I hope you have the faith to skip what is less important in order to speak what is truly important. Jesus had much to rebuke the Jews about, but He spoke of what was more important. 'I have much to say and judge about you, but He who sent me is true, and what I have heard from Him, these things I speak to the world.' (John 8:26)

To truly not bring shame to God's name, the community needs much transformation, which means a greater presence is necessary. We must seek the Lord's face more. This is especially required of those in ministry.
Do you truly wish to speak and practice God's Word? Then change your time. 'He whom God has sent speaks God's words, for God does not give the Spirit by measure.' (John 3:34)
A greater sin than lust, jealousy, and pride is not seeking and calling upon the Lord. Had you sought and called upon Him, there would be no fruit of such sins. Return to God.
Living honestly means you will surely suffer hardship and loss in ordinary times. But in moments of crisis, in times of absolute necessity, God always upholds the hand of the honest.

From my personal experience, God rarely works in the ways we anticipate. When people stand before the Red Sea, they think only a raft is the solution and returning to slavery is the way. No one imagines there is a path through the sea itself. A path that has never existed before—when you pray desperately, the Lord shows it to you. It is a secret, hidden until now from the world. 'Call to Me and I will answer you, and I will tell you great and mighty things, which you do not know.' (Jeremiah 33:3)
The ability to respect one whom you can respect is a gift. Not everyone can do it, nor is it given to everyone. Recognizing one sent by God is great spiritual insight. Be a blessed person. Please....

If you grieve because you are not respected or acknowledged, you do not see God's glory. Agony toward God's kingdom and community testifies that one participates in God's glory. Do you become angry or disappointed for human glory? Or do you grieve for the Lord's glory? Lead us to the place where the Lord calls.
The reason people who had good beginnings collapse is simple. They become satisfied and compromise. It is like applying the whip to a running horse.
Do not do the ministry and love you wish to do, but do it in the way God desires. 'For the foolishness of God is wiser than men, and the weakness of God is stronger than men.' (1 Corinthians 1:25)
A poor leader speaks much. Truly piercing words.

Though I resolve many times not to sin, I repeatedly collapse. To be angry and complain is already to become Satan's food and to lose in war from the start. 'Cease from anger and forsake wrath; do not fret—it only leads to evil.' (Psalm 37:8)

Do not try to overcome the limits of sinful nature, the weakness of the flesh, the limits of your character, or the power of spiritual trials. These are things humanity has tried to overcome for thousands of years but never could. Rather, look to the Lord and follow Him. When I am where the Lord is, I will suddenly find myself beyond my limits. My goal is not achievement but His presence and companionship. 'I have been seized by Christ Jesus.' (Philippians 3:12)
Being overly timid before sin and sorrow comes from excessive and unnecessary concern. What does it have to do with me? Simply come before the Lord who seeks and calls you.

* The Decisive Secret of Relationship Failure. Surprisingly, relationships fail because we try too hard. Tension enters the voice and stiffness appears in the eyes. Even eating ramyeon, people naturally want to eat comfortably. The curtain must eventually fall on any performance. Life cannot be covered by acting. Touching a masterpiece damages the original. Rather, when you honor yourself and seek and help the person God has given you, you will enter into the amazing blessing of relationships God has prepared. If despite desiring much you cannot love yourself, then let go and walk away. From that hell....
Live by faith. Do what He permits, in the way He desires.

The reason the Lord called me is to do the Lord's work. The Lord's work is to believe and to become holy like the Lord. That the apostles devoted themselves to prayer and the Word means that becoming strong in essence is the true solution.
The Lord will speak through His Word. There will come a time when you hear that voice, and when you hear that voice, you will truly live.

There is no one without problems. In that storm, it is a choice of whom you will hold onto. The storm has a purpose, and when that purpose is fulfilled, it will become calm even if you tell it not to.
Standing myself again before the Lord and looking only to Him is what I have learned until now and the path I must walk going forward.
Zeal without the Lord is worse than laziness. What is more important than zeal is the essence. A life where the Lord is the essence is blessed.

There were times when I could do small and great things freely without hearing the Lord's voice. But now is the time to hear that voice, obey, and go.
