God rejoices in a heart that asks and listens, and through His good pleasure, He guides us down amazing paths. That is why when a faithful person buys an airplane ticket, even unbelievers want to take the same flight.
How do you know if it is a vision from the Lord? Dreams not given by the Lord decay and disappear. Infertile eggs rot even when incubated, but fertile eggs bring forth life when incubated.
Lord, I have no intention of stopping until I complete the race You have set before me. Let me run even more. Here I am.
To walk with the Lord, two things must be presupposed: 1. One is faith, and 2. The other is holiness. (Joshua 1:8)
Do not grieve over paths not taken, but be satisfied walking the path you must walk. Everyone walks, but each has their own path.

There is a time that must be given to the Lord. There is a passion that must be given to the Lord. When absolute time and concentration are offered, that life becomes exceptional. The prime of life arrives. Run over 300 kilometers, over 1,800 meters. Revival begins with me.

Looking to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith. (Hebrews 12:2) Not constantly looking to Jesus is already sin and a wound of the heart. Therefore, Jesus is not an event. You must continually cultivate your heart with the word of truth.
What you have not received from the Lord will all fall down and be rejected. No kindness or consideration that does not come through the Lord will bear good fruit.

If the path the Lord has confirmed is right, walk it in faith. Even if it is like striking a rock with an egg, do it in faith. You must not be a person whose life depends on probability, but one who lives by the Word.

When you continually look only to the Lord, you realize there is not even room to criticize others. Revival comes from God through overcoming accusation and through the repentance and confession of my own spirit.

If there is nothing else to rely on but the Lord, it is a blessing. When you have much experience and talent, preconceptions make it difficult to rely on the Lord and hear His voice. Let us become blank pages so that the Lord's insight may be poured out. The Apostle Paul said that in weakness, the Lord's power becomes complete.
'Peace in the Lord' is not a mere greeting. Please, do not be wounded while dwelling outside the Lord, but achieve completeness in the Lord through intercession.
Though the Lord can lead us to success in whatever state we are in, sometimes that success itself can become the greatest obstacle to hearing God's voice. Let us be humble.
The Lord seeks. And He asks: who am I? Respond to that. Show me through your earnest desire and affection, your choice. According to a person's thought, so is their life.
Expect the Lord. And rejoice in Him. Then He said He will grant the desires of your heart.

That time of beholding the Lord is the happiest and most precious time. He desires to call you out of tribulation and be with you. Do not worry about tomorrow, do not fear the temporary power from the nostrils of breath, but simply concentrate on the Lord.
If there is a challenge so great that, being weak and in dark night, I can make it bright with the Lord's gaze—this is the Lord's personal and sincere 'proposal' to encounter me.

To rely on the Lord and stand again is true power. Never give up! When the people grieved because of their children and wanted to stone David, David was greatly distressed, but he found strength in the Lord his God. (1 Samuel 30:6)
When I interpret my life with the Lord's eyes, opportunities I did not see before become visible.

Following the Lord's will is not playing a probability game with greed as your expectation. It is to pray and humbly seek the Lord's guidance. If you walk according to the Spirit, you will not fulfill the desires of the flesh.

For the Lord's will to be fully accomplished in my life, now is the time to pray. Starting prayer does not mean from tomorrow's early morning service, next week's evening prayer, or next month's all-night prayer. Otherwise, you will never pray in your lifetime. The moment you see this text right now, pray right there. Only then can you hold onto prayer and accomplish the Lord's will.
Proclaiming the Lord's Gospel is not a difficult task. It is an impossible task. Without the Lord's guidance and help, it is fundamentally impossible.

When doing the Lord's work, the most important thing is not financial support, gifts, or organization. It is guarding your heart. When you guard your heart, you do not become discouraged and you persevere; and when you continually push forward without ceasing, God's glory appears. Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up. Galatians 6:9
There is a necessary prayer that seeks the Lord's presence, and there is a war that can only be won when the Lord's presence is there. Now is the time to pray.

If the situation you face seems overwhelming, it is a sign to enter into more grace. When you receive grace, there are no limits and nothing to fear. All the way my Savior leads me—what have I to dread, what to fear? I am with Thee, and Thy hand will guide me. Hymn 384

There are two ways to receive the Lord's blessing and transformation. It is best to understand through the Word. But even if that doesn't happen, don't worry too much. There is something called the wilderness. If you rot thoroughly in the wilderness for about ten years, you will understand well. Of course, I recommend the former.

There are two ways to respond with faith. One is obedience. It is not something that happens at birth; even Jesus learned obedience through suffering. The second is trust. Trust without obedience is fanaticism, and obedience without trust is blind faith.
When the Lord says He will answer prayer, it does not mean He will adjust the problem to suit your taste. It means He will examine and handle it with God's wisdom.

The Lord has sent you. —A calling. Even though it is a narrow path that many have turned away from, He has sent you saying that path is a true path, that path is a meaningful path. Even if you are never honored all your life, even if there is no one to prepare a warm meal for you, it is the path that Apostle Paul walked. It is the path where, shown God's glory in Damascus, he was called to come, and he ran it once without regret, pouring himself out before the altar like a libation. It is the path where, trying to conform to God's will, he went joyfully even though there was much loss and suffering. I pray that you walk the path that will be remembered on your tombstone as a person who truly loved souls and God.

When the Lord longs to meet us very much, sometimes He causes us to leave everything. Do not feel lonely being alone, but accept it as the Lord's special date invitation. I hope your heart thrills at meeting the Lord, not people.
Do not hesitate about the work the Lord desires.
Let us remain in the beautiful portion the Lord has appointed. There we experience a covered well and a sealed spring. Let us not covet what He has not given and be grateful for what He has bestowed.

If the Lord does not give, we have received nothing. Do not suffer, despair, and hope in vain for what we cannot receive without receiving. Be faithful to what no one looks at but the Lord gives. The Lord will look upon you. This is the Lord's strong request this very morning. John answered and said unto him, A man can receive nothing, except it be given him from heaven. John 3:27

If you strive for what the Lord has not given, you are wasting time. It is holiness to pour your strength into the place where you should stand and the things you should do. Focus on what the Lord desires.
Rather than suffer seeking what the Lord has not given, accomplish the Lord's will with what the Lord has given. Then He will add all these things to you.

The path the Lord has given is the most blessed path. Forcefully taking a path He has not given is called greed, and though satisfying greed seems joyful, it becomes the most miserable. This is why I do not grow weary walking the barren land all day when the Lord becomes my path.

If the Lord does not keep you, the watchman's vigilance is in vain. The efforts you invested in relationships, your career, social recognition—all become futile labor. You must work hard, but effort alone is not enough. You must commit yourself so that the Lord will keep you.

A life that only opens when the Lord blesses it, a life in which cunning and human schemes do not work, a life in which even the probability and luck that come so easily to others do not follow, and you can only entrust everything and go forward—this life has blessing.
If you accept what the Lord has done, that is all. But people who refuse to accept truth as truth or rely on emotions suffer greatly.
Wait for the Lord; He will renew your strength.

Seeking to obtain what is not given, hastening the time He has not appointed—these are all greed. It means the side effects are considerable. Such people will have thick autobiographies. Do not steal others' wells; let us become people whose land brings forth water when dug. God will not forsake us.
To those who live faithfully in the given time, He shows another path.

If Your law had not been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction. I will never forget Your precepts, for by them You have given me life. Psalm 119:92-93. The only reason that makes flat the valleys of all difficult situations and the mountains of suffering is God's Word. It is the cause of life that makes me live.

Sunday Message Part 2. 2. David was a man after God's heart. The Pharisees were rebuked because their hearts were far from God. According to Deacon Stephen's expression, David was a man of standard, a man of matching heart, God's Chaser, after His heart. God is not seeking talented and capable people but obedient people. 3. David was a life of commitment. Committing to God appears in worldly terms as abandonment. When he boldly abandoned position, popularity, and people's gaze, God kept him. On the other hand, Saul, trying to preserve his kingship, feared people, oppressed them, and killed them. There is only scattering. David did not avenge his enemies. Though he had the ability, will, passion, and infrastructure to build the temple, he had the power of obedience—the ability to 'stop.'
The Sunday ministry seems to have ended, but it has not. We continue to seek the Lord, seek Him further, and do not cease to proclaim and testify. Let us go all the way.

Sunday Leaders Meeting: 1 Corinthians 12. Gifts are given for edification. Gifts are not given for praise. Bear fruit with your gifts. Prophecy is the Word (preaching). Do things in order and one may prophesy (advise) for instruction. Gifts are given to those who eagerly desire them. If love is not the motive, it is unprofitable. Quiet Time James 2:14~. We are saved by faith alone. The works in James are the mature state of faith. The word 'faith' and the word 'obedience' are synonymous. Faith is expressed in word and action. If word and action differ, what do you believe? James found Rahab's righteousness in works. The writer of Hebrews finds Rahab's righteousness in faith in chapter 11. They see the same thing and express it differently. Because true faith produces the result of obedience. If you confess true faith in words but disobedience comes, that is false faith.

Sunday Message: 1. Do not live as you wish but live out of necessity. Though you want many pens, you only need one when writing. Though you want to date many people, you marry only one. Do not live from greed but from being content. 2. God honors those who honor Him and despises those who despise Him. Do not treat God carelessly. Serve with all your heart. Reflect on who your master is and what causes your actions. 3. There must be mourning and sorrow. Do not stand by watching the ruin of your own life and community as if it were someone else's business. In workplace, academy, family, and relationships, request God's reign. Those who do not request it have no right to be happy.

I told you on Sunday to be ambitious about holiness. In areas where you are not ambitious, things happen by themselves or you do them by force. It is simple to see what you pursue. When you wake up in the morning, who or what do you think about? If you do not think of Jesus, you are not a servant of Christ.

Sunday Youth Ministry Message: Self-esteem—? Self-esteem comes not from how I see myself or how others see me, but from how God sees 'me.' 1. Be simple. (You must know what you do, concentration, purpose—pursuing values requires courage.) Do not enter wrestling if you had set MC Kang Ho-dong as your goal. Stop the flashiness and experience the power of simplicity. A life like a flower withers. A life conscious of others—two hours of makeup coming to church, a life spending 90% of ability proving yourself—has no vision. Self-control is the power of the Spirit.

In a survey asking middle and high school students about their dreams, more than half cited getting hired by a famous large company. The system is churning out people who keep pace similar to others at the same speed. The greatest advantage of Christians is audacity. Creativity comes from there. Be bold.

Lifting dumbbells against gravity is exercise. You must overcome gravity's resistance for muscles to form. What forms while avoiding gravity is just yellowish fat lumps. Do not fear and avoid tribulation. Rather, find and obtain the treasure hidden within it.

Evidence that doesn't appear immediately to the eye, given as proof. Only God can give this. Believe in the Almighty who sees results beforehand, makes them proof, brings results, and takes motivation from results. And God said, Certainly I will be with thee: and this shall be a token unto thee, that I have sent thee: When thou hast brought forth the people out of Egypt, ye shall serve God upon this mountain. Exodus 3:12
I ask that you embrace about three times more than now. Though embracing will cause some muscle soreness, you will experience a much more flexible and abundant work of God.

At this very moment, acknowledging the Lord's sovereignty, take by faith His protection and guidance. Though ten thousand may surround me, the Lord who gives the peace to lie down and sleep gives peace; by His sovereignty He will protect and guide you. Psalm 3

At this hour, I do not forget but pray for friends who seriously ask about the path of life, friends who cannot sleep and work and study, friends whose thoughts are many and cannot find rest. Let us pray.
Those who walk with the Lord now will stand in the midst of the Lord's will tomorrow.

Having done well until now does not mean my strength was strong. Therefore, even when becoming an expert in that field, we must never become casual or light about it. While studying and working, we must earnestly seek the Lord's help and also request prayer.
Now is the time to seek the Lord; plow up your fallow ground. Then the Lord will come and shower His righteousness upon you like rain. Hosea 10:12 – Word given on Christmas 1990
I will not remember things past or think of all former things. Before the Lord who makes rivers in the desert and paths—I will look only to the Lord who will do new things within me.

I will not remember things past nor think of all former things. Before the Lord who makes rivers in the desert and paths, I will look only to the Lord who will do new things within me. The Lord will protect me and hold me fast. I know it, believe it, and praise Him.

Even if a map is good, without a compass it is useless; likewise a plan without vision is mere aimless rushing. Vision is consistent while occupation changes. I go toward the vision the Lord has given, yet I have lived in various forms—as a student, engineer, missionary, and so on. Many mistake occupation for vision. Occupation is the Lord's guidance that seems most necessary at that time for fulfilling vision. It could even be a convict or slave—like Joseph.

If you think payment as mere expense, you cannot do business because it seems wasteful. Rather, it is investment. If you know there is someone watching over you with faith, life becomes much simpler and easier than now.
I pray that the Lord's good hand will help bestow the greatest offer on earth.

When you persuade reason, there is understanding. When you empathize with emotion, there is emotion. When you (specifically) act on will, there is decision. Requesting without doing this is 'coercion.' In church, instead of coercion there is spontaneity, and ministry is motivation.

Only persistence bears fruit. What does not persist is mere time-wasting and consumption. Do not throw away what is cooked; holding onto the Lord to the end, bear fruit by all means. Trials must come for fruit to be beautiful.

When a water pipe buried underground has no water, when there is no water pressure within it, groundwater beneath the earth seeps through the corroded cracks in the pipe. So after water stops, when water supply resumes, muddy water comes out. If you are not filled with Jesus and do nothing about it, you will become someone supplying muddy water to people expecting living water. Maintain holy water pressure. Therefore, holy brethren, partakers of a heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our confession, Jesus. Hebrews 3:1

Wisdom does not fall from heaven but is the good use of the ability you have. Using what you have—your slingstone (like David)—is the greatest wisdom. When God gives grace, He strengthens what you have rather than giving something new. 'The Lord said to him, What is that in your hand? And he said, A staff.' Exodus 4:2. When a dry staff is held by power, it divides the Red Sea and brings water from the rock.

Winning in workplace and school is not easy. Especially when it seems your boss or professor holds your life and death in their hands. No matter how big people and problems appear, you must acknowledge that God is almighty. Then the issue becomes merely a small speck of dust.

It is wisdom to let go of what is less important to gain what is truly important. What is truly important is Jesus. When you look more toward Jesus, you will feel that the world's values and disappointments are like morning mist, and your life will become more focused toward the goal. Concentration (wisdom) is power.

Commit your way to the Lord; trust in him and he will do this: He will make your righteousness shine like the dawn, the justice of your cause like the noonday sun. - Psalm 37:5,6 I urge you to entrust even your body and the clothes you are wearing to Him. He will accomplish it.

True wisdom comes from affection. When you truly desire and love, wisdom that gives life emerges. In this age, we need the earnestness and anointing to discover the way to live, even in the most difficult and trying moments.

The truly frightening disease is one with no warning signs. Without pain, we would not notice our bodies rotting away. To escape spiritual leprosy, we must experience pain that is cruel and piercing. That is a blessing.

Everyone longs to walk on a glamorous red carpet with photo zones rather than muddy paths. But the Lord told us not to take the easy path or the path of recognition. Rather, He told us to walk the difficult and narrow path. You will realize that very few seek this path. What matters more than the quality of the road is who awaits at the end of that road.
You must concentrate. The Lord tells you what is important. When you hold onto it, you will have victory.
If what I seek is not the face of the Lord, I must certainly be anxious. I sought empty things through useless methods, so I must surely face frustration.

Just as a car's warning light comes on when there is no oil, when the Lord becomes dim in your heart, the warning light of emotion comes on. Do not ignore or remove those warnings; when pain comes, you must welcome the Lord. Faith is loving Jesus with all your heart. Please open your heart and confirm: Is Jesus at the center?

In a hymn, there are lyrics that say, 'Before you have gone all the way, do not sheathe the sword in your hand.' When it is time to push, you must push. Otherwise, later when you want to push, you cannot. That is why I am pushing harder this week than last week.
It is arrogant for me to try to help or save someone by my own arbitrary thoughts. God is the One who begins, the One who leads, and the One who completes.

When you become a true worshiper, God responds with a question: 'What do you want me to do for you?' The thousandth worshiper, Solomon, makes an amazing request. Why? Because while worshiping, his purpose was purified.

When I first believed in Jesus, what I learned was that I must continually move forward toward Jesus, who is the goal. Though I may not have many choices and there may be some collapse, I must keep getting up and running. Because if I fall forward, I can grasp Jesus' feet...

When evangelizing at first, we say: 'Believe in Jesus!' To those who have fought the good fight of faith and finished the race, we say: 'Believe in Jesus.' Looking to Jesus is both the beginning and the completion of faith. Let us look to Jesus, the author and perfecter of our faith.

The kingdom of heaven is like this: A man sells all his possessions to buy the field where treasure is buried. People say he is crazy, but he is not crazy; he has seen a greater value. The reason the treasure buried in the field (Jesus) is not seen is because of worldly pleasures and greed.

If ten million people call me an angel, who am I? A person. If ten million people call me a devil, who am I? Still a person. Then why are we shaken so much by others' evaluation? Because we are not looking simply to the Lord.

The most foolish man on earth is one who tries to defeat a woman. When nations and peoples perish, hawks push futile pride. The power given by the Lord is not to be used to destroy the other party, but rather to build them up and yield to them.

When young people experience setbacks in life, they are bewildered. That is because they only look at reality. The reality says, 'Those who can't do it can't do it,' but what is needed then is to clearly reconfirm 'where I must go.' That is our calling. When the calling becomes clearer than the setback, it becomes the strength to rise again, and God never disappoints those who rise up.
Young people, stand at the forefront. Dedicate yourselves with the breakthrough calling to prepare the way for the Lord's coming.
Even if young people weep, they must sow. That is God's will.

In the season of youth, it is easy to mistake emotional guidance for God's will even in prayer. God's guidance is not hasty. Because the Almighty responds and we are confident that He will accomplish it. However, emotional guidance and self-justification originate from oneself, so there is urgency, desire to see immediate results, and human schemes. Follow the Lord.
Every day I face my own limitations. Each time, there is God's voice: 'Look only to the Lord.' Not looking to the Lord is a grave sin.

Even if 60 elementary school students pray together, gather wisdom, encourage each other, and declare that they can do it, or even write in blood, they cannot solve calculus problems. Only high school seniors can solve them. Do not try to solve the problem; raise your level. Rather than directly solving our problems, the Lord desires our level to be elevated. It is this difference in perspective that causes difficulty. Be patient. These are problems that will all be resolved when you graduate from the wilderness school.

Sharp criticism does not frighten me and recognition does not delight me; I simply forget what is behind and press on toward the goal, toward the prize to which God has called me in Christ Jesus. I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus. - Philippians 3:14

Have you recently heard from someone to repent, recover your first love, and overcome? This is so clear and cannot be replaced by anything else; it is the most important message. Moreover, this cannot be compared to mere pleasant good wishes; it is a matter directly connected to 'where my attention and affection flow' - our relationship with Jesus and our very life. Blessed are those who hear God's voice that they must hear. Blessed are those who have someone who speaks such words. Yet I hold this against you: You have forsaken the love you had at first. Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first. If you do not repent, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place. - Revelation 2:4,5
The heart does not trouble itself without cause. It is because the Lord has something to teach us.
Praise refines a person. The Lord seeks the person who, when lifted up, can immediately humble themselves and serve others.
When it is dark, I have no options to choose from. I must only make room for the Lord to work and cry out in prayer.

Young children brag about jacks and Pokémon cards they won. As they grow older, they brag about computers, mobile phones, and pretty things. What is strange is that as adults, people brag about drinking well, mixing drinks skillfully, and miscellaneous information about alcohol. Bragging reveals one's level. People who brag about alcohol are compensating for inferiority about power and status. Woe to those who are heroes at drinking wine and champions at blending drinks - Isaiah 5:22. Human nature pursues power. (Der Wille zur Macht) - Nietzsche
For a Christian, there is no crisis. There is only opportunity. An opportunity to concentrate on the Lord...
Do not be afraid and be faithful to the end. This is the word of the Lord.

A Christian must not only be filled with the Spirit of wisdom, understanding, and knowledge, but must also study for skillful work. This Wednesday morning after the holiday, I pray that we may be a light in the world with integrity, wisdom, and excellence.

Among the abilities Christians possess, the remarkable one is the ability to 'interpret.' When you interpret circumstances and life from God's perspective, it does not end in evaluation but returns as a miraculous life. Please have the Lord's eyes.

The way Christians live in the world is not the old way. It is not the way of unbelievers. It is not living in crisis where if you are pushed back once, you are pushed back forever. We are sheep living among wolves. Then you will experience the Lord's rod and staff comforting you. Do not become a wolf; you are a sheep walking with the Lord.

The prodigal son was able to return home because no one was giving him even the carob pods he used to eat. If adequately supplied, he cannot repent until death. It can be a blessing to escort him to a corner with not even a penny. Cause him to look only to the Lord with nothing else to depend on.

When criticism comes, do not regard it as an obstacle but as a signal to move closer to the Lord. Unnecessary action is not the response; moving closer to the Lord is the reason for giving thirst and need.

Passing through a tunnel is a painful thing. It is dark and you do not know how long it will last. But refusing and avoiding the tunnel is even greater pain. We do not welcome the tunnel, but a tunnel is also the shortest shortcut. Do not refuse the shortcut and run hard.

A text, a passing conversation, a smile at the corner of my mouth, my hasty typing, a voice in a phone call that sounds slightly out of breath. I know that even in my heartbeat and every breath, there is the Lord's care and guidance. The Lord is my guide.
Hebrews 12:1-3 The life God desires for us is to run the race of faith and look to Jesus and think deeply of Him. Then you will do well. This kind of life has God's presence and bears fruit.

There are people who have sawed wood with a saw, but there is no one who has sawed sawdust. That is because it was already sawed when cutting the wood. Those who are troubled or lose courage because of the past are like trying to saw sawdust. Do not saw sawdust; saw the wood.
For the believing disciples being sent out, pray for the authority and peace of worship. Then that peace will remain with your cell, relationships, and the people who need to hear it.

What is more important than reputation is the Lord's will and help. The world's reputation is not important, but history changes because of one prepared person, like Hezekiah. The Lord prepares people and expects much from them.

Unsolved problems actually protect me. They cause me to pray and prevent wrong choices through deep providence. The rulers of the Philistines were angry with him; the Philistine commanders said to him, 'Send the man back, that he may return to the place assigned him. He must not go with us into battle, or he will turn against us during the fighting with the heads of these men. Isn't this the David they sang about in their dances: 'Saul has slain his thousands, and David his tens of thousands'?' - 1 Samuel 29:4,5 God protected the future king from becoming an enemy of Israel while in exile through the Philistines' disapproval.

There will be many reversals in God's kingdom. Do not try to correct people's reactions, but when you love and fear the Lord as the center, great and secret things and God's righteousness are accomplished.
When you stand before God without presumption and with integrity and honesty, you enjoy what God has prepared in His time.

It is good to become foolish to enjoy intimacy with God. Because those who are too wise, God gently wants to have time together through the weakness of the flesh. When the signal comes, respond immediately.

We must earnestly pray that God will give us the gifts and tools we need. However, rather than just looking and waiting for these things, when we concentrate on, obey, and move forward with that calling, God is pleased and begins to fill all things. It is the calling, not the tools.
To live a life approved by God, you must form distinguished habits from your youth. Daniel decides from his childhood not to drink wine.

The life of faith that God encourages is like a race. In the race of faith, there are: 1. Removing unnecessary weight, 2. Breaking the chains of persistent sin, 3. Living life focused only on Jesus. You must live simply and with concentration. The more critical the situation, the more difficult the moment, the more you must let go of complexity and patiently do only the few things the Lord commanded while thinking only of Him. That is living by faith. Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a great cloud of witnesses, let us throw off everything that hinders and the sin that so easily entangles. And let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us, fixing our eyes on Jesus, the pioneer and perfecter of faith. For the joy set before him he endured the cross, scorning its shame, and sat down at the right hand of the throne of God. - Hebrews 12:1,2
When God causes me to suffer, it is because He loves me. It is because He has a special word to speak. That word is to love as much as the pain.

The truth that without God's help we will surely perish is not an ominous sign but something to rejoice and be glad about. The Lord pushes forward those who seek His face and are aligned with His heart through the sponsor's biased support.

There are times when God pours out an abundance of His grace beyond what is needed for today. During life's journey, we pass through wilderness where we must walk long periods without provision. Then, like a camel, we endure with the Word stored up. A camel survives a month-long desert crossing on the fat stored in its hump without eating.
God hears and knows everything. If God knows, then God solves it. When we anxiously pursue our own methods, it only makes things worse, not better.

It is the Lord God who guides that path. Rather than being driven by circumstances and betting on the odds, when we trust in faith and hope in Him, He grants us signs at the appropriate time. What matters is preparing to examine ourselves.

God causes me to abandon what has been beneficial to me. Only when even the carob pods are gone do I barely return to the Father. God presses me so severely not because He wants me to become a decent person, but because He wants me to become a person of promise. I must leave the nest to enter the promised land. Faith is what is needed when walking between the nest and the promised land.
God does not condemn or accuse me for the faults I lay bare without hiding. Rather, He gives me the power to break free from them.

God is very much alive and guides us faithfully, yet sometimes He gives people confirmation through human means. One of these is placing a faithful person beside me. When we see that God's person faithfully looks to the Lord and have fellowship in the gospel, we experience God's encouragement and comfort. Be strong. You are 'the apple of the Lord's eye.' The Lord found him in the wasteland, in the howling desert, and guarded him, protected him, and kept him as the apple of His eye. Just as an eagle stirs up its nest and hovers over its young, spreading its wings and catching them on its wings—the Lord alone guided him, and there was no other god with him. —Deuteronomy 32:10-12

God remembers two things chiefly: 1. Small deeds. 2. Seeking His kingdom and His righteousness, not my own desires. If I am faithful in small things for the Lord's glory rather than trying to fill my own desires, I will be remembered.

God guides all people. If they accept His guidance, He guides based on that obedience; if they do not accept it, He guides based on that rejection. Moreover, despite each individual's unfaithfulness and loss of purpose, God accomplishes His purpose and vision that transcends these through His method. That is why we must acknowledge that we do not know a little—we know nothing at all—and move forward in faith.
God found Saul, prepared him, and arranged an unpleasant event to anoint him as king. He lost his precious donkeys. The grace God prepares may not be seen until something is lost.
God does not want us to remain as people whose hearts are shaken by another's expression. He wants us to become people more sensitive to the Lord's joy alone.
God told Abraham to look toward the north, south, east, and west and said He would give him that land. It means He will give as much as Abraham sees. That is why seeing is power.
God speaks to me as much while I am driving as during worship. This is because I spend considerable time driving in my life. Jesus was among the crowds yet always heard His Father's voice.
God abandons us when we calculate and scheme, but He always attends to it later. The Lord works with low cost and high efficiency.
If, like Lot's wife, we do not turn toward the Lord and repeatedly look back, we will harden like a pillar of salt.

God created us 'absolutely.' This means we are not relative beings. That is why we must not compare. If you compare at work or school, you suddenly become very unhappy. Only the Lord determines my path. Be bold.
To those who seek first His kingdom and His righteousness, God's sovereignty and relationship, He gives blessings beyond measure.

God does not ask us to be responsible for results. We do not have that ability. He only wants us to walk with Him. Looking at results alone, Moses was called far too late, took forty years for a journey of four weeks, and ultimately did not enter Canaan. Yet Moses is a glorious person because he obeyed what he was commanded to do.

God wants us to rule the earth (Genesis 1), cultivate the earth (chapter 2), and bless the earth (chapter 12). He also wants us to make disciples and transform the world (Matthew 28). We go deeply into the world but are not corrupted; rather, we participate through the power of the Holy Spirit as experts with biblical principles and are called to change the world as God's representatives. Be filled with the Holy Spirit and become a wise professional.
God sometimes gives us uncomfortable emotions we cannot understand. He is telling us to turn around. Please stop minding people's eyes, stop looking at circumstances, and look only to the Lord.
God knows what is best for our lives. He also knows better than I how to accomplish it.

If I am not in the place where I should be but stand in a place that increases probability, I leave the place of the Lord's protection. So I get beaten and depressed and return. People say I was spiritually attacked. No. I spiritually ran away from home and got beaten by neighborhood thugs. Keep your own place.

God does not ask for something we cannot do by force. To the people who poured out complaints and quarrels against Moses, all Moses did was 'tattle' to God. I lay out my limitations to the Lord and obey what He commands, striking the rock—that is how to truly live well.

Draw near to God and He will draw near to you. (James 4:8) People are transformed by what they draw near to and gain its power. Relationship is ability. I pray that the power of Mary, who drew near to the Lord, will come upon you. The foolish person in the world is one who seeks power in a place without God.

When waiting for God, we must give time. Because we gain nothing physical or soulish in that time, it feels futile to those without faith. We need time to wait for the Lord. This is because we wait for the painful waiting of God the Father, who waits for me who cannot hear the Lord's voice while bound to the world. In reality, it is not I but the Lord who waits for me.

That God's rule came upon the Egyptian palace ignorant of God and the Babylonian court serving idols was because of Joseph and Daniel, moved by God's Spirit and walking with the Lord. They belonged to the world yet were not separated from it; they were not corrupted but rather brought transformation. —John 17:14
To love God, we must become close to God. To become close, we must meet often. Think deeply on Jesus.
Even if we know God but are not intimate with Him, when faced with problems, we tend to turn to human methods first. We live by faith in what we hope for amid difficulties.

4,500 years ago, leaving home and relationships in the Middle East to go to an unknown land was recklessness with nearly 0% probability. The world thinks increasing probability makes things work, but the Lord gives promises. The Lord does not fulfill a promise He has not given just because people increase probability and pressure. Hold fast to the promise.
Where God's holiness and presence are, all creation worships and darkness retreats. There are many problems, but there is one answer. We receive only the Lord.
God's concern is not my skill and ability in handling problems but my attitude toward problems.
God's wonderful deeds come suddenly one day. But the Lord who performs these deeds examines me closely.

Without executing prematurely before God's time arrives, we cannot bear complete fruit or build up the community. God does not change or negotiate His complete will and glorious intention due to human impatience. Picking unripe fruit never satisfies oneself or others.
A common characteristic that happens to all people seeking God's will is 'answered prayer.'
When I understand what God's will is, my dry stick-like life in my hands becomes God's staff. Joseph became a prisoner in the shortest distance from Pharaoh's palace.

Not all of God's will is the same. There is will He 'directs' and will He 'permits.' He directs us to love, accept, and live in love, but when Israel forcibly demanded a king, He permitted it but was uncomfortable. We must not boast about permission. Rather, we must examine ourselves and obey what His directing will is. If we repeatedly pursue permission, we become greedy, but if we follow His directing will, we live an abundant life.
We must listen to God's Word because I am existentially a sinner and foolish in my deeds. Only those who listen to My words will live in safety and be at ease, without fear of harm. —Proverbs 1:33
God's Word gives us the ability to be fully equipped and do good. —2 Timothy 3:17
I hear God's Word. I hear and try to obey. I do not hear God's Word. In that field, I cover it over and do not want to obey.

God's protection and the inheritance I have agreed to receive can be received through promise. But His presence is with those who seek Him earnestly. I will receive an answer, but God may not be there. That is why Moses and Paul so earnestly sought God's face. 'Oh, that I might know Him!' —Philippians 3:10 The Lord said to Moses, 'Leave this place, you and the people you have brought out of Egypt, and go to the land I promised on oath to Abraham, Isaac and Jacob... I will send an angel before you... But I will not go with you, because you are a stiff-necked people.' —Exodus 33:1, 3

God's thoughts are different from human thoughts. While a principle like 'I will focus on the poor neighborhood' is very good, sometimes God may have me go to 'Beverly Hills' contrary to that principle to preach the gospel. No matter how rational and biblical the principle I think is, it must be 'revisable' before the Lord's command. That is humility.

Before God, there is a truth understood only when we stop complaining and desiring. We desire Him Himself rather than gaining anything else. Be still, and know that I am God. —Psalm 46:10 Guard your heart above all else, for it determines the course of your life. —Proverbs 4:23
God's promise is like investing in a long-term fund. This is especially true when we say it works together for good. Be patient.
God's zeal does not rest until the purpose is accomplished. His purpose is greater than any situation we will experience. God has a plan that transcends the problems we face.
God's Voice—Hearing God's voice begins with believing that the faithful and loving God desires to speak to me without fail.

Even when I want to hear God's voice, I do not hear it well. This is because His voice is heard by those who obey. Though I seem like I would obey if I heard, Scripture says the Word is heard by the obedient.

God's intention is peace. It gives us life and causes us to live abundantly. He gave us authority so we would receive such blessings. That is God's law. Making friends with authority is the secret to receiving blessing. God always works through authority.

God's people are never mass-produced. That young Mary did not say to the angel, 'Let me consult with my mother,' but readily said amen means she was a person refined through enormous trials. Do not marvel at conclusions alone but receive grace in the process.
When you do God's work, you receive God's support.
Those who strive to remain within God's domain of rule transcend human rule. Daniel was such a person.

To live according to God's will, you must hear His voice. To live making decisions without prayer means to live by the voice of the world and darkness. If you truly desire to please God and obey Him, it would be strange not to hear His voice.
It is blessed to go where God tells you to go. When you cross beyond the beautiful territory that the Lord has measured with His line, you become anxious and find no rest.
When God called me, it was not to obsess but to focus. Being anxious about what He has not given and focusing on that becomes obsession, while drawing near to Jesus Christ becomes focus.
There are times when God calls you again. He intends to rebuild everything and grant you strength and ability. Wisdom is knowing that timing perfectly.

Please wait until what God has shown you is accomplished by God's method. Please surrender even your arbitrary deadline desires. Only then can the Lord work. Please wait excellently until it is accomplished by God's method. Please—so that the Lord can work. Let patience have its perfect work, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking nothing. —James 1:4

What God desires is not competence. Because He alone already possesses incomparable competence. He desires faithfulness. He considers laziness evil. Fulfill your calling. Your calling will carry you forward.
You may pray contradictory prayers that God cannot answer. Like this: God, give me patience, please~~, right now~~~
If you avoid the training God has given, growth stops and you eventually return to where you started.

Those who walk the path God has given do not run away or panic. Though I cannot know the way I go, He knows and guides me and brings me forth as refined gold. Do not conform your life to people, but align it to the standard He has given.

The way to do God's work well without ignorance—when they asked Him, 'What must we do to do the works of God?' Jesus answered, 'The work of God is this: to believe in the one he has sent.' —John 6:28-29

There are problems that cannot be resolved during one term. Problems that can only be resolved when the year changes and even after graduation. When living through the current term while bearing unsolved problems, what matters is holding onto the basics, getting back up even when you fall, and maintaining the direction you should go. In any circumstance, His presence and voice are unassailable basics.

When you feel endlessly lacking, look to the cross. It will give you the strength to begin again. You must go down to rise again. You must empty to fly again. When you reach the bottom, you rise again.

Korean people are willing to emigrate for their children's education. Yet they are so busy supporting them that they are stingy with being together and building relationships with their children. It is like cooking rice to give to dogs. More precious than academic achievement is the child as life itself.

People say Koreans are skilled with their hands. I think it is more because of desperation. Where there is affection, wisdom descends. Not whether we can save, but because it is simply a life that must be saved, they boldly challenge and do not give up despite being reckless.
Coming to Korea, I think this way: I will not stop but pursue what I have been seized by in Christ Jesus toward the goal. Everything else can be restrained and controlled. If the purpose is clear...
Coming to Korea, I visited the rock where I prayed seeking wisdom in my youth. True wisdom is not acquiring what you do not have but delighting in what you do have.

Returning to Korea, it feels like all my hardened determination disappears and even the air in the atmosphere seems to try to knock me down. Also, here it was grace like a shower, but there why does it become a drizzle—I cannot understand. In Korea, you must pay a price to satisfy thirst. In London, there were people who paid instead. You must find it yourself and prepare it yourself. I painfully realize that my faith life here was not my strength but entirely the Lord's grace. But if you hold the Lord with all your heart, you will know that He is everywhere. Hold on. And keep going.

In Korean traditional wedding ceremonies, there is something called 'nap-chae'—a ritual to first inform the household spirits of both families when two families unite through marriage. The Israelites were incompetent drifters through 400 years of slavery and 40 years in the wilderness. To live in Canaan, like Korean weddings, they had to exchange the gods and idols they believed in to enable trade and exchange, and to learn agricultural know-how. But God says not to live that way. He desired that they harvest a little less, receive a little less recognition and endure being disrespected, while stubbornly clinging only to God's Word. God, who delivered them from Egypt and the wilderness, intended to deliver them in Canaan as well and through this make them a channel of blessing to all nations. Because Israel feared people, flattered them, and even tolerated the idols they served, Israel perished in Canaan. You must truly believe that when the Lord exalts, none can humble, and when He humbles, none can exalt. The Lord alone is my strength.

Do not be discouraged that you have nothing particular to do. Can you not find the people connected to you, pray for them, bless them, and forgive them? These abilities come not from my strength and power but from the heart God has poured out.
An empty heart is not filled by the social. It is filled only by the fire of the Holy Spirit. Do not wander between here and there, but go forward boldly, looking only to the Lord.
It is the Lord who fills the empty soul. Even without faith in the Lord, you can feel lonely. This is because it is the process of coming out of darkness into light.

The greatest female figure skater in the world today is Kim Yuna. Asada Mao is also a good skater but cannot be the best. Why? Because her dream is the same as world's best Kim Yuna. 'Let's beat Kim Yuna!'—Kim Yuna. 'Let's beat Kim Yuna!'—Asada Mao. Let each of you examine yourself, so that you may take pride in yourself alone and not compare yourself unfavorably with someone else. —Galatians 6:4

Dark events that happen in my workplace, home, relationships, and studies are just signals to return to essence. Come out of peripheral problems and truly stand at the center. Such problems occur not because of bad luck but because of the absence of Jesus.

Breathing is continuity. A rope is continuity. If even a small gap appears, it becomes meaningless death and separation. Faith is the same. Do not show gaps. It takes years to build but a day to lose.

In marathons or cycling races, many people run yet do not tangle and fall. Why? Because their goal is the same. If they intended to go their separate ways, it would become scrambled like eggs from the start. A good community spreads and roots the following words in the hearts of all souls. Persistently, uncompromisingly, continuing to ignore... 'Not that I have already obtained all this, or have already arrived at my goal; but I press on to take hold of that for which Christ Jesus took hold of me. Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.' —Philippians 3:12-14

You must face the Red Sea to cross it. We prefer a peaceful course without the Red Sea, with no thrills, but the One above thinks very differently. He leads a people needing change to the Red Sea. In the Red Sea, no matter how hard you look, there is no path, but when you pray and believe, there is a path in the middle of the sea.

I do not wish to torture hope in the name of faith. The Lord presents the same level of challenge to those just beginning to follow and those following for a long time. 'Who is your master? Whom do you serve? Who lives in you? Where will your belonging be?' Faith is surprisingly simple. People just complicate it. When what you pursue is not the goal, that happens. Sell your possessions and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me. —Matthew 19:21
To walk in tribulation is to be humbled by the Lord and to keep His law.
When you pray in tribulation, help comes from unexpected places. Sometimes even unbelievers help. You cannot limit the Lord's help.
Companies move toward profit. But believers must make decisions moving closer to the Lord. Those who move to please people and expect it from them will face woe.
Without regret is a life well lived. To have no regret when standing before the Lord, two things must be clear. One is the goal, and the other is running with passion.

You cannot turn a water wheel with water that has flowed by. Yesterday's grace cannot become today's, and yesterday's manna cannot sustain today. I hope you become members who humbly kneel and look to the Lord.

If you boarded a bus written 'Heathrow,' it is right to go to Heathrow. You cannot go to the opposite Gatwick. If you determined to look to the Lord, you must run toward the Lord alone. To aim elsewhere is fraud and theft.
In difficult and seemingly impossible situations, put faith in an egg to break a rock. The situation we face may look difficult, but it is a situation the Lord has allowed.
Not praying because you are tired is like not eating because you are hungry. Breaking the vicious cycle quickly is the secret to happiness quickly.

It is the way of the world that the strong devour the weak. So many people try to acquire sharper claws and stronger fangs. But those who follow the Lord do not live fighting others. Because they hear the Shepherd's voice, they live.
To kindle again the gift of God that is in you, I remind you... —2 Timothy 1:6
Though a poodle barks, the Eurostar runs. When the purpose is clear, what is inappropriate and unnecessary can be ignored.

* Sunday Leader Meeting 1. Life is a matter of fullness. 2. Those who know the future are at peace, even bold. 3. When you know the conclusion beforehand, you can enjoy it fully. 4. The darker the situation, the more fun it becomes. 5. Believing is seeing what has not yet come to pass. 6. With faith, you are bold and focused. 7. It is not about having a good personality or a matter of will, but whether you have faith. 8. When you see and accept the conclusion by faith, you cannot help but live that way—not by force. 9. Those with reliable and certain information change their actions upon seeing the result. 10. Focus on Jesus. Always think of Him. 11. Lonely? Pray this way. Make me even lonelier. 12. This is the appearance of a spiritual giant and the path of one who mocks fate and walks the Lord's way.
Be transformed by the renewing of your mind so that you may discern what God's will is—His good, pleasing and perfect will.
Running away does not end the war. You must face the war and win. Only then can you preserve life and find even more life.

* Christian's Appeal When the church sees structural evil and injustice, there are two ways: either turn a blind eye, incite public opinion, or resort to physical force. The first way the church changes the world is through prayer. Because the Lord is the Master. Then, while listening to the Lord's Word on how to approach the world, we must make an appeal with God's character. In the midst of continuous intercession, specific actions are guided. When the Lord speaks and guides, raise your voice. While feeling sorry and having a loving heart, simultaneously hate sin and not tolerate injustice.

* Cry out and Act There are times to cry out and times to act. If you shout demands and act, it is forced artificiality, but if you move in response to the Word, it becomes obedient faith. The Lord said to Moses, 'Why are you crying out to me? Tell the Israelites to move on. Raise your staff and stretch out your hand over the sea to divide the water so that the Israelites can go through the sea on dry ground.' —Exodus 14:15-16

* God's Sovereignty and My Freedom When I preach God's sovereignty, I receive many questions about whether it is fatalism. (Do we become robots?) What is clear is that God's sovereignty cannot be resisted by anyone. Some Reformers over-emphasized the Lord's sovereignty and fell into fatalistic predestination, not doing missions. 'Sow righteousness and reap the fruit of unfailing love, and break up your unplowed ground; for it is time to seek the Lord, until he comes and showers his righteousness on you.' —Hosea 10:12 Breaking yourself and moving forward—it is God who gives rain there. Breaking up the ground while seeking rain is our part, and raining down to water the land with righteousness is God's work.

People of faith do not ignore common sense and reality. They simply transcend it by faith. I have abandoned the world's pleasures and the world has abandoned me, but the work of transforming the world within the world does not stop. I illuminate the world with excellent wisdom and faithfulness.

There is a saying about a 'kite with a broken string.' It refers to a situation where the person or force that supported you has fallen from power and can no longer provide convenience. Simply put, you have been separated. These strings must break, and breaking them is actually a blessing. You must not be separated from Jesus, the vine. Become a kite with a broken string that descends and becomes a branch attached to the Lord.

"He whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for God gives the Spirit without limit" - John 3:34. What is needed now is not earthly logic but God's Word, and what I need is the unlimited outpouring of the Holy Spirit.

Praying only for the problem when you encounter it is the faith of a child. Behind the problem stands God. If you pray for His will to be fulfilled, you will experience the solution through the Lord's way within it. You must ask like this: 'God, what should I learn through this problem, and what purpose should I accomplish for You?'

Not everything that is spoken is conveyed. The Lord must do it. Therefore, the principle of ministry is that prayer comes first. Before leaving this city, you must first be filled with the Holy Spirit.

The reason Moses is beautiful is because he walked Moses' path, remained loyal unto death, and then humbly departed at Mount Nebo. If Moses had walked Joshua's glorious path of entering Canaan, it would not have been beautiful, and it would have obscured God's glory. My life is most beautiful only when I walk the path the Lord has given me. Walk your own path. Even if it appears narrow.

Paul was a man of power, yet he asked the Thessalonian church members to manage and deliver the relief funds designated for their care in an orderly and transparent manner. Why not pray to open heaven's windows and pour out money? Because miracles' undeniable other expression is transformation of spirit. God's kingdom must advance through sacrifice and love.

*When unavoidably changing churches: 1. Maintain good relationships if you possibly can. 2. Do not act in anger; act when you can bless. 3. In doing so, do not forget that He asked for my change rather than the church's change. 4. When your calling and your life collide with each other, seek the Lord's will. Is there remaining work, or is my supply of grace urgent right now? 5. At least pray for a place where continuous supply of grace and opportunity to serve are secured. Never be buried among the masses.

I must forget what I have done because there is an eternal goal. I strive for what I have been seized by in Christ Jesus, but I have by no means fully achieved it. Therefore, I continually pursue toward Jesus, who is that goal.
When much has collapsed, this is the prayer: God, let me do what I must do right now. The answer is to fulfill my calling and remain in the Lord.

When what I expect inevitably collapses, there is something He makes me realize. Especially after examining shallow circumstances and scheming excessively, the Lord's voice that comes is 'You follow me.' Abandon schemes and hold fast to your calling.
The reason you must be bold the more urgent things become is that only then will you hear the Lord's subtle voice and receive guidance and wisdom that strikes at the core of the problem.
It is God who upholds me. Therefore, do not fear people. It does not come from east or west, but only from God.
I pray because I know what I desire and how earnestly I desire it.
When a leader tries to do ministry well, it will revive just by not hindering. Let me not become a hinderer but a servant of obedience.

When Israel ate only manna and water from the rock, they had the most concentrated faith, but in Canaan they rapidly corrupted because there was much to be lost. If it is complicated, the game is already lost. Keep it simple.

Many people blame their environment and people. They find the cause of unhappiness in others. But this is the result of letting go of what should be grasped and grasping what should not be held. If you become a victim, you will be unhappy. Do not become a victim but become a wounded healer.

Anyone can be deceived for a short time, but not for long. If there is no intimacy with God, things will go well. Only for a very short time. And the collapse will be severe. Please spend time with the Lord, talk with Him, and trust. That is what will keep me alive.

Mr. Kim, have you settled in well? Although we part this way, I am confident there will come a day when you say that because of such poignancy at some corner of your life, there is now this warmth and happiness. First, do your best to take care of what remains, and second, humble your heart and correct the fundamental cause of the distortion. For some, today's hollow laughter becomes a disaster, and for others, yesterday's tears of blood return as today's humble happiness, so we cannot simply say this moment is unhappiness. Your children are healthy, your family is well, and you are still quite young, so if you do not give up and sow seeds, you will enjoy clean happiness. And in the Bible there is Proverbs—an easy-to-read collection of aphorisms. Read it daily. http://kcm.co.kr/bible/kor/Pro1.html And I will also pray here: 'Those who sow with tears will reap with songs of joy. Those who go out weeping, carrying seed to sow, will return with songs of joy, carrying sheaves with them' - Psalm 126:5-6. February 4. From Jacob. From a letter written to someone who thought they had lost everything.

Belief without working is because you are not continually believing. Because there is too much gap between one belief and another, there is actually a void of not believing. That is why the Bible says to always look at Jesus. Sustainable faith is achieved when your gaze is fixed on Jesus and you think deeply.

The only reason a person of faith can muster courage when they seemingly cannot - 'David greatly distressed himself, but David encouraged himself in the Lord his God' - 1 Samuel 30:6. He tells us to rely on God. And to muster courage and turn things around.
To beautifully fulfill the Lord's will for me, I must pray to pray. Because there is One who guides and helps prayer. Powerful prayer begins there.

There is a saying that even if you fall seven times, you get up eight times. That is the power of positivity. This saying is a disaster for those without Jesus. Pharaoh and the Egyptians without God before ten plagues, prodigal sons with absolute positivity rising up from pig sties without carob pods to try again—they truly write the worst scenarios.

The problem I am struggling with is not the real problem. The Lord must intervene to resolve it. God seeks those of united heart and gives them power. Therefore, the wise person does not hate a thorny person but finds resolution in their fear of God. Fear is the solution.

If the purpose is clear, you become focused. You develop discernment to see core values, and to achieve your purpose, you simply practice restraint from unnecessary things. Therefore, a person of faith can do all things in Him who gives me strength.
Soldiers train and obey orders. It is not about avoiding or fleeing battle or reluctantly fighting only to retreat. A soldier exists to fight and win. Be steadfast.

A proud person is truly unhappy. Because they find the reason why things do not work out in others. Being discouraged is not the same as being humble. The more proud and self-centered, the lower one's self-esteem and the more prone to frustration. Because they compare. When the Lord is not at the center, a person tries to confirm themselves through creation. If self is not dead, summoning strength and courage is running in the opposite direction from the destination. See yourself in the Lord. You will become humble. 'Pride goes before destruction, a haughty spirit before a fall' - Proverbs 18:12.
David prepared five smooth stones. Why? Because if one missed, he was ready to throw the other four in rapid succession. If you keep doing it, it will work. Just skip it and go.
Faith is having expectation and coming before the Lord. But the most excellent stage is laying down our will to God's will.
Check whether your desire comes from the Lord. If your desire is fulfilled, will the devil truly fear it?

Faith looks to a better reward. When pursuing better value, choice occurs, and within that decision, destiny changes. A faith-based decision fulfills the intention of the One who sent us and allows us to live the most meaningful life.
If you have entrusted all your works to the Lord, be bold. The Lord does not ask for more there. From there, He takes responsibility.

*Fixed Gaze. You want to look at the Lord, but just for a moment, really just a moment, very briefly you glance sideways with a desire to gaze upon what will perish. No event ever ends there. In human history, there has never been a single example of carelessness ending in carelessness. Fixing your gaze is paying a small price for a few minutes, but if you avoid this, it may not recover even after many years. Look to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith.

*Calling. There is the most meaningful and happy life to which the Lord has called you. The conclusion is simple. Either walk that path simply, or like in Damascus, go blind, be driven by a goad, and suffer greatly. No matter how many times the ball bounces, it eventually goes into the goal.

*Spiritual Play. Airplanes are made to fly. But if you lose absolute speed and distance (prayer), you become worse than a bicycle. Going grocery shopping at Tesco, you zoom around in an airplane and cause trouble with your big wings when parking. The devil's challenge is so strong that you take one big hit, enter temptation, and make a fuss about crashing. You never flew at prayer's absolute speed, so what crash are you talking about? You're still spinning in the parking lot, hit by stones thrown by neighborhood kids. You haven't prayed for an hour; what spiritual warfare are you in? You've simply entered spiritual play.

*Be courageous, He says. But you cannot be courageous and He keeps saying be courageous, so it's maddening. A student who hasn't studied will not do well on the exam; fundamentally there is anxiety. A student who hasn't done homework cannot be courageous. If the Lord is not within me and is not my master (if the Lord has no influence on my lifestyle), I cannot be courageous. If you stand on a path the Lord does not desire and are courageous, that is not courage but ignorance. A life turned away from the Lord's path should fear and be anxious. Only then is there any repentance at all, isn't it?
If the destination is the same, you will eventually meet everyone. Those who put the Lord first and move toward the Lord will ultimately become excellent companions for one another.

Faith, according to Hebrews 11, is offering a better sacrifice, seeking a better country, preferring suffering, a better resurrection, regarding greater wealth, and seeking something better prepared. Faith is having the discernment to compare and staking your life on what is 'more' important. If you cannot see something better or recognize its value, you cannot seek something better and will pursue only what decays before your eyes. That is truly foolish. - Hebrews 11.

Picasso's famous 1943 sculpture Bull's Head. One day Picasso sees a bicycle at a junkyard. What others cannot see, Picasso sees, and he creates this work by attaching handlebars and a saddle. Picasso was from 'the land of bulls,' Spain. When a person is 'immersed,' they draw near to their origin. From there, with only their own perspective that others cannot see, they saw bulls as they did in childhood. When there is 'immersion' pouring out my heart completely to the Lord, there I discover the Lord. Immersion: 'Not that I have already obtained this or am already perfect, but I press on to make it my own, because Christ Jesus has made me his own' - Philippians 3:12.

Because I know better than anyone what is more important, I can discard what is less important better than anyone. I do not regard the things I have and pursue the prize of the upward calling of God in Christ Jesus. The person who discovers the greatest value lives simply.

In the word 'opponent,' there is the meaning of the person you oppose (opponent party). However, a person of faith does not oppose the opponent. We must always deal with the eternal Father, who is our only concern and solution. Then you will experience the Lord's dominion over people, rumors, and challenges with or without substance. God said David was a man after His own heart. This was because he was someone who fell on his face in prayer before problems and pleaded. 'I am a man of prayer' - Psalm 109:4.

A person of faith is not cynical, saying this is a sinful world. Criticism without alternatives is meaningless. A thermometer only indicates temperature, but a thermostat actually exercises influence and changes the temperature. Faith is not a thermometer but a thermostat.
If you truly want something, you will pursue it. If you want something but do not pursue it, it was not what you truly wanted. You will continually pursue only what you truly want.

Daniel and his friends were said to have ten times better wisdom in their first job interview and test. Among them, Daniel was the only one who received the ability to interpret dreams and visions. At first, Daniel probably did not understand. Why did he have this ability to interpret that seemed so useless to him? Later, when he solved King Nebuchadnezzar's dream, he would have thanked God. God who poured out talent, trained him, and prepared him. 'O my God, I thank and praise you, for you have given me wisdom and power. Now you have shown me what we asked of you, for you have made known to us the king's matter' - Daniel 2:23.
There is no separate method of prayer; just try it. If there is a method of prayer, try it right now. Then you will receive the guidance of the Holy Spirit.
Faith is insight. Stop wasting time on useless things and pour your time and passion into valuable work.

If you long for it, you do not give up and passion continues. Like Mary, choose the better part and do not let it be taken away. Choosing the better part means giving up other things and focusing. If you long, you focus. - From Sunday leader meeting.
A simple way to become a leader. In any situation, commit to becoming an all-weather faith that is not tempted. Leaders have no off-season.

It is not success to gather many carob pods for pigs to eat. Even greater is the one who, when even those fall short and hunger comes, remembers his father's house and turns back. Avoid slowly perishing to near-death; rather, realize the emptiness and quickly turn back.
*Prayer Request. There is a King's Cross Church meeting today. Please pray that the Lord's will be accomplished.

When my heart is tested and heavy concerns arise, the Lord is with me at such times, always helping me. Always the Lord loves me and always gives me new life. As I await the promise of glory, I look only to the Lord. - Hymn 456. Do not lament that darkness is dark, but look to the Lord and obtain hope. The Lord is with us with immeasurable love and grace.

The reason a flower is beautiful is because there is a flower within me. Yet sometimes I must direct my steps toward where flowers bloom. For blooming flowers cause flowers within me to bloom as well. Within people there is both a flower and filth. What responds depends on what we encounter. Be with good people.

*Difficult Decision, Easy Process. In life, there come times when we must make important decisions. It is necessary to entrust these decisions to the Lord. For a person of faith, what matters more than the result of a decision is the process of deciding. If the Lord is almighty, He will be present even in my insufficient decision and work together for good. If Pilate had entrusted his authority to judge to Jesus, the true Judge, Jesus would have said, "Thus it is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness" (Matt 3:16) and returned the authority to him. However, even the same decision produces different eternal influence when the process differs.

A newborn baby is helpless. Therefore, parental care is necessary. Thus a baby does not worry or be anxious. That is the parents' responsibility. No matter how dark and difficult, if I am certain of the Lord's will and relationship, I can entrust everything and find peace. My worry and despair come from not hearing the Lord's voice and not knowing His will. My grace is sufficient for you. - 2 Corinthians 12:9

There is a saying to leave when applauded. That is selfish thinking. A person of mission does not leave for personal gain but for glory. Glory means not my will but His will, the will of the One who is glorified.

Seeing the path ahead as only dark means I have no options to choose. This is not something to be frustrated about; it means the Lord will guide me. A person who is incompetent and foolish and has no choice but to depend on the Lord is blessed.

*God's Voice. God, who spoke through prophets in former times, speaks through His Son in these last days. Jesus is God's love and His word. To those who behold Jesus, God speaks through His Son. In the past God spoke to our ancestors through the prophets in various ways and at various times; in these last days He has spoken to us through His Son, whom He appointed heir of all things and through whom He also made the worlds. - Hebrews 1:1

The strongest faith manual. Simple power messages by topic. Tens of thousands of insights that pierce through worries and difficulties in the world, equipping you with strength. - Faithbook by LFGC Youth

*Living by Faith. Do not cling to what benefits you, but quietly do what is right. Rather than finding work suited to your ability, seek ability suited to your calling. In worldly matters, invest little and keep much, but in heavenly work, you pour out everything and look only to the Lord. (John 1:48) Nathanael said to Him, 'How do you know me?' Jesus answered him, 'Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.'
Past can hurt, but as I see it, you can either run from it, or learn from it!

*God's Anointing. Without God's anointing, we cannot raise up the Lord's glory in this darkening and increasingly evil world. Before going out into the world, the only condition we must earnestly request is 'the Lord's anointing.' When we request this anointing, the Lord also sets conditions for us: 1. Removing sandals from our feet and entrusting ourselves to the Lord (acknowledging His sovereignty) and 2. Remaining in the Lord's presence (the mystery of the cross, union with Him). Living without this is just toiling in vain.

*Those Who Receive Their Calling and Obey. God gave the widow of Zarephath the mission to provide for Elijah. Through that mission, before she died, she was deprived of the remaining food for her and her son. But that was not deprivation; it was planted for the fruit of a great miracle. When we obey our calling, miracles happen.

*Human Plans vs. God's Guidance. Looking back, I realize that things did not happen as planned but according to His providence. No matter how difficult and dark a situation I face, I am in His hands and He will surely lead me to waters of rest. No matter how much guns and swords block the way, the Lord never gives up, and if only He is with me, I can tell fate itself to come. I am not afraid.

I am sometimes startled and shocked by what happens to me. When unexpected circumstances come to a believer in God, I must not fix my gaze externally but on the Lord within me. Now is the time to pray. He went a little farther and fell on His face and prayed, saying, 'My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass from me; nevertheless, not as I will, but as You will.' - Matthew 26:39

*In His Time. The Lord has His time and hour. All things are perfectly accomplished only in His time. The Lord's time—the moment you acknowledge Jesus as Lord and give Him everything. Then He makes it beautiful and accomplishes it. He has made everything beautiful in its time. Also, he has put eternity into man's heart, yet so that he cannot find out what God has done from the beginning to the end. - Ephesians 3:11

When you determine to pray, God will come and pour out His heart and give you His heart. He will show you the state of the church and yourself. Then He will break you. Only after that breaking, through the price of poverty and mourning, will He grant recovery. Every work, relationship, and intention without prayer must collapse. This is why God does not stop Satan's plan—because He loves us so much. Determine your future right now.

*The Most Delicious Ramen. Kokko? Nagasaki? Shin Ramyun? Ansang? Neoguri? Ramen eaten when hungry? A chopstick-full borrowed when someone else makes it! What kind of ramen it is does not matter. It is when my hunger, my craving for ramen, gratitude for being given a chopstick, and fullness from that chopstick exist. It is not good because it is good; my impoverished spirit causes me to pursue what is best.

*Sexual Sin. Sex is God's gift. Moreover, a holy gift. Because it is holy, it can also become a serious sin and a danger. If sex had not been such a beautiful blessing, it would not have been dangerous. The Ark of the Covenant in the sanctuary is so holy that when mishandled, destruction surely came.

The most foolish thing is to bowl for the first time, score better than expected, think you have talent, and despise others. Not knowing that day was a fluke, the best you've done, thinking romantically. Forget what you have already done and continue running toward the goal. 走馬加鞭 (Continue forward with urgency).

*Faith is a Matter of Gaze. The majesty of a sunrise by the sea captures attention so completely that nothing else comes into view. Good faith means that the frequency, absolute time, and depth of beholding the Lord is different. Looking at the bronze serpent (not touching it) gives life. Looking only to the Lord, other things do not come into view. That is why we overcome. All human problems arise from where our gaze is fixed. Stepping away from the cross means already sinning. Let us look to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of faith. - Hebrews 12:2

*Time and Life's Design. Life's priorities determine the quality of my life. Time is equal for everyone, but the content that fills that time is different for each person, and the results differ. When a person's affections change, the quality of their life changes.
If I design the future by my own strength, it means I will squander all my assets. God does not reveal His plan to me, but His plan for me is so complete that investment entrusted to God never fails.

If I disrespect the authority the Lord has given me, I will lose an absolutely large portion that God has prepared in life and will certainly regret it. This is because it is denying the almighty Lord. Please respect those entrusted with His word and share good things.

Pray and serve: This is the Lord's voice. Now, let's date: This is the voice of hormones. More important than meeting is parting. Without self-control and self-restraint, you are being guided not by the Lord's guidance but by greed and emotion. Because God is almighty, He can faithfully guide His way without my anxiety. Therefore, trading faith for profit must be eliminated. If the same mistake repeats, it is not coincidence but a genuine desire for that 'mistake.' In the wilderness, only God commands us to advance. We advance as much as we are transformed. So I really desire your humility, so the Lord can work sufficiently.

Moses saw the Israelites' problem as God's problem. A problem merely provides an occasion. It is an occasion and opportunity to take the correct stance before God.
