The people contended with Moses, saying, 'Give us water that we may drink.' And Moses said to them, 'Why do you contend with me?' - Exodus 17:2

Living by faith means roaring in the face of reality that contradicts and opposes my vision, and continuing to move forward. That reality is its own matter; I am moved by the heart the Lord has poured into me and persist.

On a day so cold it's almost zero degrees, I walk hunched up. But then a British person passes by in just a reflective safety jacket and a t-shirt. What is this? ...., The most foolish thing is to do what others do. Please dress warmly. And do not live as the world wants you to. Living someone else's life is a tiring life.
Faith is not pursuing your own plans but following in response to the Lord's call.

Ultimately, wisdom can be said to be the ability to draw out core values. This can be called insight. What is unseen to others is seen to those with the Lord's perspective. It is not so much that you are brave and bold, but because you see that the One with us is greater than they are, you are bold.

The Pharisees and Sadducees held power in their society. So they could mobilize armies. But they could not lay hands on Jesus. This means they could not arrest Him. Because 'His hour had not yet come.' Though Jesus taught in front of the temple treasury, still no one arrested Him, because 'His hour had not yet come.' - John 8:20. The greatest bodyguard is God. Do not be afraid.

To those who cannot decide between A or B... Even after long deliberation with no answer, this is not meaningless. The ability to pray about one problem for months is a blessing. How can a life of agonizing indecision that chooses by probability be of equal weight to a life that prays and decides, even without answer?

My prayer is not asking to be recognized, to succeed, and to accomplish my will. What I earnestly ask strength for is so that I can complete the race that the Lord has given me and run as a worker who does not hinder the Lord's revival.

When you feel others moving faster than you, remember God who has rescued you at every moment of life. Rather than being troubled that others have what you do not have, you should grieve that you cannot live more for the Lord. At least David, chased by his own son, had such pain. Blessed are those whose strength is in you, in whose hearts are the highways to Zion. - Psalm 84:5

Moses was called at eighty; Isaac married in his forties. Joseph faced a death threat at thirteen and became a statesman at thirty. It is incomprehensible to measure myself by my present circumstances. I must interpret myself through the Lord's eyes. To those who discern what the Lord's will is, let them not waste time but become a true light. The time given to me now is the greatest opportunity and place of service. O LORD, let me know my end and what is the measure of my days; let me know how fleeting my life is. - Psalm 39:4

When going an unfamiliar road, to save time, you must use navigation. To save time, we must discern what the Lord's will is. Redeem the time, because the days are evil. Do not become foolish but become wise and discern what the Lord's will is.

If Moses had built the tabernacle at forty, he probably would have reinterpreted God's commands and instructions and built it grandly. But that would not be the tabernacle but just an expensive, unsold tent. After refining in the wilderness, Moses obeyed the Lord unconditionally. Listening is power. Exodus 39-40, the phrase repeated 17 times: 'As the LORD commanded.'

If you take a dry stick and obey, it becomes the Lord's staff. And Moses took the staff of God in his hand. - Exodus 4:20. What I lack now—occupation, marriage, studies, achievements—should not be seen as deficiency. Rather than comparing and becoming miserable, the Lord is allowing me time to worship, serve, and prepare. A wise woodcutter sharpens his axe even while resting. In any case, if the Lord does not give, nothing has been received. Even if He gives, He gives in His time. When deficiency becomes ability, God's glory appears. That is the ability to interpret. Please have the Lord's perspective.

I will come down to deliver them out of the hand of the Egyptians and bring them up out of that land. - Exodus 3:8. Now therefore, come, I will send you to Pharaoh so that you may bring My people, the children of Israel, out of Egypt. - Exodus 3:10. 1. God declares that it is He who will lead the people out of Egypt. 2. God also says it is Moses who will lead the people out. 3. God's declaration in verse 8 and God's command in verse 10 seem like contradictions that cannot be harmonized. 4. This is possible because God must be in Moses, and Moses must be in God—perfect union. 5. Verse 12 expresses it this way: And God said, 'Surely I will be with you.' - Exodus 3:13. How can God be with us? Because of the merit of the blood. Brothers, since we have confidence to enter the holy place by the blood of Jesus....
In the most urgent and pressed situation, you must hear and cling to the Lord's word and voice. Only in the most shocking situation, maintaining peace, will you respond to the Lord's gentle voice.
There are times when the Lord permits difficulties I cannot bear. When every method and path is blocked, what God originally wanted was for me to shed tears, fall prostrate, and cling to the cross.
What the devil truly wants is for you to focus on the problem rather than the word.
Do not try to control the situation before me; I must aim myself toward the Lord in heaven. Evil thoughts gnaw away at life.
Only when I wholly seek the Lord and concentrate on Him is passion supplied. Gaze is strength and ability.
Before prayer, there seems to be no path. But when we cry out, even a path that did not exist in the sea becomes visible. The ability to see process rather than parts comes after refining and prayer.

When seeing only results and parts, despair comes. But the Lord makes you see the whole. Before the world's request to settle and maintain the status quo, I hope you have the faith-perspective to see glory even in suffering. The Lord moves us to where He establishes us by methods we do not know. Joseph → Egypt (low-cost, high-efficiency, shortest route).
Rejection is one of the good things God does to send me to exactly where I am needed. Without darkness, day does not brighten.

Most people go with the higher probability. Following probability means delaying the time of failure a bit. Rather, those who wake up early are blessed. They realize they do not live by bread alone but must live by the word.
To not fear the future, decide the future now. The surest way to decide the future is to turn only to the Lord who knows my path.

When the Word is proclaimed, believe and obey immediately. Simple obedience brings forward progress in life. As you advance a little, the next path will also become slightly more visible. Even a thousand-mile journey is completed step by step in this way.

In David's later years, God grants blessings without anxiety. This is because there is no longer a need to face failure and confirm 'what am I pursuing.' The wisest and best path is complete submission before the Lord. It will be the fastest path.
Decide and fix in your heart not to worry about what you will eat or what you will wear. God knows that these things are necessary for you.

The strongest person is not one with great means, methods, and qualifications, but rather one with a powerful purpose in life. That purpose is God's glory. An absolute strong purpose becomes an absolutely strong life. May you be empowered, holding Jesus.

Just as an eagle stirs up its nest and hovers over its young, spreading its wings to catch them and carrying them on its wings—Deuteronomy 32:11. If the nest becomes stable, the eagle will continue to stay there, and then the eaglet becomes a chicken. Do not become a chicken-brain; become a magnificent eagle.
Mary chose what is better, and it will not be taken away from her. The gifts and opportunities God gives must never be taken away. Those who do not lose out are those who win.
I ask that you pray to be used more powerfully in the work you are now entrusted with. That is wisdom.

In the last days, Satan, like a roaring lion, seeks someone to devour. But he only mimics a roaring lion; he is not a lion. The true lion is Jesus, the 'Lion of the tribe of Judah' (Revelation 5:5). Trust in the Lord's name and be bold.

How to break a rock with an egg—why not me? 1. Motivation is ministry. 2. Wise ministry consists of passion, desire, perseverance, flexibility, and elasticity.—Do not stop. 3. Fight a winning war.—General Yi Sun-sin (Brightness, the Battle of Hansan—utilization of asymmetric forces, the tortoise and the hare). 4. Even a nuclear bomb starts with a small button. The fuse—the Jasmine Revolution. 5. System is clothing.—Give it a bath, then the clothes will change. 6. Love is study. The skill of love; ignorant love is rude love and becomes a wound, not love—e.g., Erich Fromm. 7. The world is fast, but the church is real time. 8. The people were stirred up—were they motivated? 9. We love the church.—What is the alternative to criticism? 10. Believe in the Lord's power and almightiness. 11. The power of Saturday gatherings—it comes from prayer. 12. Do not cease praying.—The Almighty intervenes in war. 13. When we boast, a negative wound always follows—Shepherd David's success, King David's failure. Now wrapping up...

Though there are people you must encounter and face repeatedly, if you fix your gaze on God, the sole object of worship, with all your thoughts, emotions, and will, people may first see you, but eventually they will see what the person before them is looking at—our Lord God.
Do not be overcome by your level or circumstances. Do not fix your gaze on your inadequate self, but on my wholeness. Fix all your gaze on me.

The leader of the buffalo herd is the buffalo that smells water well. A leader is someone who has the ability to set direction. What matters is whether the direction of my life aligns with my life's purpose—my mission and the Gospel. Rather than shouting, build up your character.

When I look at the same place God looks, God fills all my needs with His power. A person who does the Lord's work for the Lord's glory receives the Lord's support. Let this mind be in you, which was also in Christ Jesus.—Philippians 2:5.

Just as my laughter is not laughter, what appears now is not everything. The end is not the end; the Lord will surely evaluate and repay. 2 Thessalonians 1:6-10: 'God is just: He will pay back trouble to those who trouble you and give relief to you who are troubled, and to us as well. This will happen when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven in blazing fire with his powerful angels. He will punish those who do not know God and do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus. They will be punished with everlasting destruction and shut out from the presence of the Lord and from the majesty of his power.'

If God desired obedience tomorrow, He would not have spoken today but would have spoken tomorrow. We delay uncomfortable words from God. Sometimes God's intervention stirs up all my plans. This is not a cause for discouragement but a reason to leap for joy. There is something to hope for there.

If you are so busy that you cannot spare time for the people you love, then you are doing more unnecessary work than what the Lord asked. Be less greedy and guard love. Loving the precious people given to you—that is what God desired.

Leviticus 22:22. When you reap the harvest of your land, do not reap to the very edges of your field or gather the gleanings of your harvest. Leave them for the poor and the foreigner. I am the Lord your God. Help when you are able to help. What is remarkable is that God protects the dignity of the poor in this method of giving. Do not give and take credit; it does not matter who picks it up.
For a helpless sheep, the only competitiveness is 'accompaniment'—with the shepherd.

'Buy the truth and do not sell it; get wisdom, discipline and understanding' (Proverbs 23:23). Purchasing truth is a blessing. You must meet good leaders, belong to a good community, and this is fulfilled when you serve that community blessedly. However, more important than buying truth is not selling it. Disobeying truth for worldly gain, betraying your own conscience and following desires—these are selling truth. Distorting Scripture to defend and justify yourself is also selling truth. God's truth includes concrete wisdom, discipline, and understanding for living a successful and meaningful life. Above all, we must prioritize meditating on and studying truth in our lives to live as the Creator intended. Each new day promises success only to those armed with truth to live the specific hours of a new life.

Since the end of all things is near, therefore be of sound mind and sober in spirit for prayer. Above all, love each other deeply, because love covers a multitude of sins.—1 Peter 4:7. In a game, the final round is truly fierce. Athletes pour out all remaining strength to gain even one more point. Scripture tells us we are running the final round. In this final age, we must become the final runner and run with all our might. Running with all our might as a Christian means truly finishing a war already won. Be of sound mind and have wisdom and self-control; be alert and pray; above all, with love that breaks down all schemes, save souls.

* We respond today (fight back). Tonight 9:30 PM at the Missionary Training Center. Content: Prayer that breaks through challenges. Focus: Break through, Prayer, Healing, Response to the challenge. Plan: Tonight only.

Many people confess with their lips that they fear the Lord while committing sin with their bodies. Even sin committed because of weak flesh requires repentance, but it is more serious when the conscience is seared and one does not know one is a sinner, so there is no repentance. I hope you do not despise the Lord's patience and forbearance until repentance. God will surely judge.

How can they believe in the one they have not heard? And how can they hear without someone preaching to them? Romans 10:14. You cannot believe what you do not know. If there is no pathway (understanding) to enter the grace God gives, God's promise in Scripture brings no benefit to me.

1 Timothy 6:8. But if we have food and clothing, we will be content with that. 6:9. People who want to get rich fall into temptation and a trap and into many foolish and harmful desires that plunge men into ruin and destruction. Weeds do not require much. Do not become a weed; become faithful warriors fulfilling the Lord's will.
Walk in the Word without fear. Let Scripture be my conclusion rather than what my senses and worldly rumors say. You will not be shaken.
I will not stop running my course until I finish it.

When fellowship begins, whether we like it or not, the hundredth day of our meeting comes. This is called chronos. However, when fellowship deepens and one day we confess love, it is not related to such mechanical time. That day of confession is called kairos. God will establish you with His love and power on the day prepared and suitable for you (kairos) rather than on the day I am old (chronos). Chronos comes naturally, but kairos must be prepared.

It is not that you cannot do it because you do not know. It is because you know but do not want to do it. That is pride. If you practice and live even what you know, the ordinary will become a miracle.

You need to confirm your calling. If you think about why you were called, you can truly be free, happy, meaningful, and not care about vanity. To understand that fulfilling your calling and having something to say means you have only done the work of an unprofitable servant.
Do what you can do, and then the Lord will do what is fitting.

Every game has a player who makes the team victorious, and every fight has a game-maker who changes the tide. David, a man after God's own heart, is such a person. You must become someone God can trust to accomplish His will, someone who will always do His will.

2 Timothy 2:4. A soldier on active duty does not entangle himself in the affairs of this life, so that he may please the one who enlisted him. A person of the cross does not dwell on his own problems. He focuses all his attention on pleasing the One who called him. My self-esteem, my reputation, my dignity, my emotions are not the main thing. Fulfilling the Lord's will becomes the desire.
If there is anything that frightens me, please rebuke me. If the path I walk is the Lord's path, who can hinder me? When He rebukes and scolds, darkness and fear retreat.
Many failures occur because we start and pray. We should pray before preparing. Then God will teach us. God is not someone who supplements us; He is the One who makes us start rightly.

Regarding the gifts, order, and immorality facing the Corinthian church, Apostle Paul speaks of our dim lives as if looking in a mirror. When we come face to face with the Lord, everything becomes clear, and we will know as the Lord knows. Look only to the Lord. Then He will give you the spirit of wisdom and revelation, and grant you compassion, forgiveness, and unity.

Two hundred years ago, saying you would free slaves meant you became a madman. One hundred years ago, giving women voting rights meant being sent to prison. Fifty years ago, crying out for freedom from colonization was labeled terrorism and resulted in death. What the media says is not all truth. Do not live as they want, but live as He created you. That is who I am.

When asked about dreams, people say they want to 'become' something. So they become that longed-for something and become hollow and reckless. Dreams are not spoken in nouns but in verbs. It is not 'I will become president' but 'I will free slaves'—that is a dream.
What I fear determines the direction of my life. God desires that you place the fear of the future at His feet.

But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.—Matthew 6:33. A person spends their resources and energy according to whose evaluation they care about. If you respond to God, you will try to align with God.

Let all who seek the Lord rejoice and be glad in him! Let those who love his salvation always say, 'The Lord is great!'—Psalm 40:16. You cannot know someone by their smooth words. You can know a person's relationship with God by how they face problems. In the midst of problems, who is the Lord to you?

I am not in this place because an American visa did not work out, because my family could not push me from home, because I lacked ability or missed the timing. Joseph was a slave and a prisoner, yet he was there by the Lord's will. When you understand the Lord's will (Ephesians 5:17), you begin to see true opportunity.
When other paths are blocked, it means the path of prayer is opened. If there is no one to meet, it is something the Lord desires to show you.

To those whose everything has crumbled and lost hope to live, as a minister, I would exhort this: even if you die, give what little remains to the Lord. Then the Lord will handle it, whether making gruel or rice with it. This is what a minister can say. Cast a vote for advice worth getting stoned for. Elijah asked the widow, who said she would eat and die, for her last meal.

If you meet a bear, you will be panic-stricken, cry out for your legs to save you, and run away. But to a hunter, a bear is tuition for his children, rent, the ability to eat out with family for the first time in a while, and income that allows him to replace an old car. Become a hunter of trials.
Do not try to do everything well. That is all greed. Just try to fulfill the Lord's will. Everything is included in that.
When everything changes relatively and becomes dim, you must think of True North. When you know true north, other choices become easy.
From his first day at work, Daniel made up his mind and lived with self-discipline according to that resolve. A person whose heart is fixed on the Lord advances without wavering, in trust.

It is important to live steadily. A young person's life that does not lose heart even without great changes or visible responses will suddenly experience transformation. When that change and opportunity come, grasping it is skill. Therefore, for young people, meetings and gatherings are important.

An unidentified comet approaching at terrifying speed from a distant star, or news flooding the headlines when we wake up does not give us true crisis. The real crisis is when 'Jesus alone is not enough.' When Jesus is not enough, we resort to our own strength, and we use strength we should not use. Then we lose friends, we lose fellow believers, and we lose family.

If I am often discouraged and frustrated, it is because I am comparing the path itself with others beside me rather than looking at the destination at the end of the road. The reason we can rejoice even amid tribulation, persecution, and suffering is because we have the ability of overview—seeing the whole picture. I encourage you to be bold with the authority of outlook, the ability to gaze upon the end of the road.

Which is a better road—a 16-lane motorway or a narrow path? We cannot say based on this alone. Because the destination is not shown. Comparing roads themselves is foolish. What matters is where that path leads and whom we will meet.

David was greatly distressed, but he strengthened himself in the Lord his God... David inquired of the Lord, saying, 'Shall I pursue this raiding party? Will I catch them?' The Lord answered him, 'Pursue them, for you will surely overtake them and recover everything.' When we are together with the Lord, we become bold. When I am together with you whom I love, I fear nothing. We will recover everything that was taken and find even more. The Lord will repay us more than the tears we have shed. When Lazarus died, there was great sorrow, but when he was raised, greater glory of the Lord was revealed. Before the Lord who raises the dead, I ask that all of us rise up. Now, it's our turn!

'Do not be afraid, for those who are with us are more than those who are with them.' Then he prayed, 'Lord, please open his eyes so he may see.' And the Lord opened the servant's eyes, and he looked and saw the hills full of horses and chariots of fire all around Elisha.—2 Kings 6:16-17 Prayer is the royal seal that summons the horses and chariots of fire from heaven. Not to pray is to despise God. Those who pray are protected and surrounded by the chariots of fire from heaven. Prayer warriors have their eyes opened to see heaven's chariots of fire. Are you today spiritually surrounded and under attack, facing trials and hardships? Pray with your life on the line. Then you will behold the heavenly chariots of fire surrounding you.

You make a resolution but waver; you turn to the Lord but soon look to the world and fear, and your heart becomes heavy without peace. I urge you to hold fast to the Lord's Word. The Spirit of truth, the Holy Spirit, will set you free. 'The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.'—2 Corinthians 3:17

Even if you are afraid and feel unable, even if you hold your head in anguish and suffering, if the Lord says to follow, you follow. Though you are tattered and broken, if the Lord says to go, you go. Though you cannot, you obey. If you cannot follow, it is not a matter of compromise but of tearing your heart.

Pharaoh's suggestions to Moses were to do moderately, not go too far, and let only the men go. If you give one thing, he will demand another. Many spiritual giants who overcame persecution and suffering collapse because of the weapon of 'compromise.' All that remains is a magnificent tomb. I urge you to breakthrough and never settle. If the Lord says to go to the cross, cursed by the Jews, you go.

Being a disciple means you are not perfect. You take a few steps forward and then a few steps backward. Yet life is not about speed but about maintaining the right direction—and that is a blessed life.
On the path you walk, you will meet your beloved Lord.

Being sown among thorns means hearing the word but having it choked by worldly cares and the deceitfulness of wealth, so it bears no fruit—Matthew 13:22. Rushing and worrying suffocate the word you have received and prevent it from bearing fruit. I plead with you, do not worry or rush. The Lord is still working. Approaching the altar before Samuel comes is the greatest mistake.

I will surely disappoint people at times. That is not something to fear; it is an opportunity for maturity. What is more fearful is to fear what we should not fear. Fear makes us despondent, defensive, and paralyzed, preventing us from truly living. Even Jesus did not satisfy everyone, and satisfaction is not always helpful.
The problem I face is understood through the character of God that I have known beforehand. The more intimate I am with God, the more I can see His will.

Do not be satisfied with reactions but be assured by faith. Faith is the 'substance' of things hoped for, things not yet seen, things that do not appear as reactions. Those who live by reactions will suffer.
May God's wonderful providence that makes a way in the wilderness work all the more through my older brother.

David was excluded while out in the field with the sheep on the evening of a family banquet with honored guests. Yet God, through Samuel, would not let anything happen until David came. Do not fall into rejection and discouragement. Use the lonely time to become a worshipper who worships the Lord intimately. The breakthrough in life flows from intimacy with the Lord. Do not look to people. Breakthrough flows out of intimacy with God.

Are you frustrated that even after praying, the Lord has not changed your circumstances? The Lord too is frustrated that people cannot let go and cling to things. Surrender and obedience must come before change and resolution.
The reason a chicken cannot fly is because its vision is short. More important than wings is sight. If we cannot see and hear, we cannot move forward either.
Always choose what your flesh desires without being self-conscious. Rather, I hope you find peace in making the choice the Lord desires. That is not bondage but true freedom.

When your heart is troubled, it is the Lord's kind signal to return to the basics. To face hardship while looking only to the Lord is the blessing a believer should embrace, rather than finding peace while committing lawlessness.

If you build a relationship on emotion, it will not last long before divorce, shipwreck, estrangement, or even becoming enemies. But if you build it on promise, though emotions fade, the promise is eternal, and within trust and security, you can create new emotions and have a genuine relationship. God's intention and God's will is the promise.
When you respond to the high calling, the lower problems are naturally resolved.
Time and seasons are set by God. What people must do is be faithful. Respond not to problems or conflicts but to the Lord.
I am not called for that. I am not called for this either. I am called by the Lord solely to fulfill His will.
When standing at a crossroads and unsure which path to take, if our desire is to reach a quick conclusion, the Lord prepares a third way—the mature path of waiting.
If worrying does not help, come to pray. Solving people's problems through fleshly means only causes side effects. Solve life's problems spiritually.

Yes or No—we want results immediately. But the Lord speaks precious words we need to hear through the process. If we skip the process, even if we receive the best result, we cannot enjoy it. What a caterpillar calls death, God calls a butterfly.
When Ruth acts in faith, she happens to enter Boaz's field. When Boaz sees Ruth in the field, he thinks, 'She's really different.' Expect God's guidance and provision. Do not doubt the Lord anymore.
We listen to God; we do not listen out for God.
My weakness will ultimately be helpful to both me and the community. Our disappointment is God's calling in another language.
Do not envy or covet the opportunities that come to others. The opportunity prepared for you will surely come someday. Opportunity is given to those who prepare, not to those who are jealous.

Do not go where your needs are merely met; go where you are needed. In relationships that bring you joy and pleasure, do not stand as a supporting actor but stand as a source of the Lord's joy. There will be true satisfaction and joy in receiving the Lord's gaze.
To understand the members better and know their hearts, we can develop better policies and ideas. The Lord, who expects excellence in leadership, pays attention to faithfulness.
I pray that we be brought again before the Gospel and serve with God's heart and tears. May the Lord's will be accomplished.

There is one thing I must focus on: God's will and His presence. The rest is what God must care about. Therefore, I must not worry about things that have not happened or things that have already passed. I hope you look to the Lord in this present moment.

Martha was busy with her work for the Lord, and her heart was anxious; she even gave instructions to Jesus. In the moment she sought to accomplish her own will through the Lord's work, it came. Jesus, knowing Martha's heart well, speaks to her: 'Martha, Martha, you are worried and upset about many things, but few things are needed—or indeed only one. You have lost your joy in the midst of much work.' Jesus remains Lord in every situation. If Martha had asked Jesus for help, I know the Jesus I know would surely have helped her with compassion, tying on an apron. Though He was a carpenter, He would have taken care of that kitchen and worked while never ceasing to teach so that all could hear...
Do not be angry at me, but be righteous with zeal for the Lord's name. That is gentleness, and God seeks such people, meets them, and entrusts His will to them.—From a leaders' gathering
If you are in distress, humble yourself. The fastest way to pass through life's tunnel is to humble yourself.
If you want to soar like an eagle, you cannot run like an ostrich.
If you succeed without prayer, you will be ruined because of that success.

If your situation is impossible and beyond what I can bear, it is time for the Lord to work. It has been so difficult to receive the Almighty Lord as Lord of my life that I have come all this way, in a roundabout journey, to stand before the Lord.
If God is not present in the place where I spend most of my time, what a great misfortune that would be!

He is a God of relationship. Love is not just making time but sharing that time together. In the last days, many Marthas will fall. I hope you find balance. Do not forget Mary, who always sat at the Lord's feet.
When you encounter problems or trials, pray that the Lord's perspective be given to you.
Standing where you want to go is suffering. Stand where you are sent.
Trying to solve everything in its place by your own method is excessive greed. You may solve the problem but leave a greater wound. There is a time of the Lord for everything.
God's providence sometimes comes through our inconvenient situation

Discouragement comes from your gaze. When you focus on unchanging circumstances, problems, yourself, and your surroundings, discouragement is inevitable. I urge you to look upon the Lord and His works.
We do not always learn good things only from good leaders. We can certainly receive good lessons from poor leaders as well.
A person who finds problems in others is foolish. It is like analyzing the cause while a house is burning. You must first escape, then investigate later. There is hope only when I change.

I will honor those who honor me, and I will despise those who despise me. Not responding in faith is despising God. There is no one who despises God and is happy or successful. Those with misplaced priorities appear advantaged but are actually the most disadvantaged.
Even when going to unfamiliar places or embarking on a journey, there is one thing you must do: look to the Lord with all your heart. This is what God always desires.
Many people believe and live by the idea that diligence brings a better life, and they blame themselves for laziness. In reality, it is not laziness but the absence of fire in the heart.
To those who are faithful to emotions, I say this: thoughts determine emotions.
If you surrender leadership to God, He can help you.

Cut flowers are gorgeous, but they are dead. Living for appearances is exhausting, lifeless, meaningless, and unhappy. Do not be conscious of others; look to the Lord. When conscious of others, you must perform, but when looking to the Lord, you can become boldly simple and truthful. A life of cut flowers must pass away, and your life must become one planted by the stream.

Even if you hit a wrong chord while playing guitar, it does not greatly harm the praise. However, if the rhythm is off, it is tribulation for the player and torture for the listener. When the Lord pushes, you must run with Him. When the Lord pulls, you must cling tightly. Those who cannot do this suffer in their hands and feet, and remain unchanged.
The loneliest moment is when you must make a decision.

Lot was a righteous man, but he was not sensitive enough to God's voice. Not all righteous people are the same, and not all faith is the same. Had Lot been more awake in Sodom and Gomorrah and heard the Lord's voice, there would have been greater salvation than the destruction that delayed his escape.

Leaders must gain authority through power. Authority is increased in those who pray more and obey the Word. The Lord commanded Joshua, who had no experience, to meditate on Scripture and obey. Even without charisma, He gives power to the obedient.

[Acts 9:11-12] The Lord said to him, 'Go to the street called Straight and inquire at the house of Judas for a man of Tarsus named Saul, for he is praying. I have shown him a vision of a man named Ananias coming in and placing his hands on him so that he might regain his sight.' When Paul the Apostle was converted and praying, it was none other than a faithful disciple named Ananias on Straight Street who delivered the answer. At the laying on of hands, God's prepared will and answered prayer were accomplished as a miracle. When we seek together with one accord, the kingdom of heaven unfolds in this parched land. When we extend our hands to bless, the will of heaven is fulfilled.

When absorbed in problems, you always become the victim and the other person becomes the perpetrator. However, when you look to the Lord, both you and others are saved. David took 'courage' by relying on the Lord. When you see through the Lord's perspective, everyone is a pitiful sinner and simply a person who needs Jesus.

Those who seek happiness through their reputation, occupation, bank balance, or circumstances are truly pitiful. It is like being tossed about by ocean waves. Rather than these perishable things, it is more important where I am heading and whom I am running toward. Life is not made happy by numbers. It is determined by direction. Only when you understand life by direction does your life begin to be interpreted.
Do not try to solve problems; listen to the Lord's voice. When absorbed in problems, you see only your own emotions and desires. Jesus is the solution to all problems.

Leaders Meeting Part 2 Deuteronomy Devotion (Thursday) 1. These are regulations concerning war. 2. The leader's role is to bless and declare. 3. This is the only ancient law recording exemptions from military service. The Code of Hammurabi. In ancient Near Eastern law, conscription exists with no exceptions. 4. That God's law has exemptions from military conscription is because war does not belong to humans but to God. - Do not calculate too much. 5. A person with a negative heart must not be appointed, no matter how talented and gifted.

There are two methods of closing. The easy way is to barely finish by running away; the way God desires is to receive power and depart at the Lord's time. Let us abandon the prejudice that only the easy path is the way to live. When the Lord's sovereignty is declared and His time is fulfilled and you complete your portion of transformation, the Lord will set you on high. I hope you become young people held in the Lord's hands, dividing the Red Sea of dark sin.

If you had calculated, you should not have come this way. Because you walked in faith without using your mind, you have come this way. You thought people live by bread alone and money alone, but you must realize you live by the Lord's Word. Furthermore, you must commit yourself to that Word.
Do not respond with emotion; respond with truth. Then you can protect yourself and save others. God's righteousness enters in.

Do not just live day by day; draw a beautiful self-portrait and live it. That is the difference between the ordinary and the extraordinary. Determine and trust to live as God sees you, not as you and others see yourself. Then miracles will occur.
Even if that path is lonely and narrow, do not fear. When you follow the Lord, that narrow path contains God's glory.

A police car violates speed limits for public safety, and an ambulance rushes and violates regulations without liability. This is because it is a life-saving situation and exemptions are granted. You must always proceed exceptionally in the work of saving lives, receiving grace, and meeting the Lord.

When you contemplate the source of suffering, there is greed there. We are here not because we are the most excellent in this field, but because the Lord called us. Do what the Lord has guided and be faithful to what He has permitted. Do not excessively desire to do well. Remember the Lord's tears for that position. The Lord did not call based on ability; He makes those He calls able.
When you place your dreams on the Lord's altar, He returns them with the power of resurrection as pure and selfless visions.

Regarding the retreat on the 19th, please prepare the attendance list and fasting prayer list. I pray that on that day you will hear the Lord's voice and things that were stopped will move forward beautifully like a ship gliding.
The most basic, yet most fundamental faith is not being conscious of people's gaze and fixing all of yourself only on God present in that moment.
A common mistake made in the latter half of an adventure that appears to be going well is pride.

The Principle of the Magi: The Magi from different countries, each following their own route and the star, ultimately meet before baby Jesus. God calls many people through different methods for His kingdom and glory.

There is no revolutionary way to learn to walk. There is no need for other aids or wheelchairs. Within a mother's warm support, you just need to fall many times. Then you will walk and run. Furthermore, you will help your younger siblings.

Many people wander, not knowing where to go. However, not knowing the way is completely normal. Abraham did not know the way but followed the Lord; Isaac and Jacob could not predict their lives. In Job 23:10, it says only God knows the way I go. It is enough that the Lord knows. Please trust only in the Lord.

There are two ways to understand. Understanding by hearing the Word is the best way. If you refuse to hear the Word and obey, He sends you to the wilderness. Then you understand through tears and suffering. I recommend the former.

A man called 'Mr. Law,' hungry, received a wonderful menu and was so moved he almost memorized it. He condemns and despises others like himself who have not seen the menu. However, if when food comes, you look at pictures in the menu, shed tears, do devotion, and continue clinging to it, you must be beaten much. The problem is not experiencing the essence—the finest cuisine. However, there is no need to convince those who have experienced the finest cuisine or force their happiness. For having eaten and tasted it, they are already happy and will naturally seek it again.

Do not fear papers or exams. The paper a professor requires is something that might appear once in a hundred years if at all—something ideal that even professors have never written. Do not be overwhelmed by tests and essays; rather, become a person who overwhelms.

If it is too precious, you cannot use it. British Wedgwood, Chinese Tang Dynasty teacups, and expensive Japanese tea utensils only remain on display shelves because they are too precious to use. Most are broken when carelessly knocked over by juniors during moving. Then no matter how much you curse the junior, it is useless. Serve with precious things. It is recorded in heaven.

Often, when you do not pray, you do not realize how desperately prayer is needed. Once prayer begins, you realize you cannot live without it. You start praying to get better, then realize you need prayer to survive.

Daniel was a civil servant. Not a 9th or 7th grade official but the Prime Minister of the world's greatest superpower. Yet he prayed toward the temple three times a day, and everyone knew he was praying. Are you busy? Then you have one more reason to pray.
I often spoke of 'fulfilling my calling' as a prayer request. I truly wish I would fulfill my calling.

When sailing at sea, if you look at nearby waves, you run aground on the sea. However, if you look at a lighthouse or at least use the North Star as a reference point, you can move forward without wavering. If you do not look only to the Lord but gaze upon people and circumstances, you will be discouraged, disappointed, tempted, downhearted, and angry. By asking what the title of your suffering is, you can know what you are gazing upon.
Be careful of someone who says, 'I will take care of everything.' God does not work so carelessly that we would trust in a person. If you rely on people, you will certainly fall.

One of the most foolish things is to criticize others. When you criticize, both the criticized and the critic become miserable. Instead of spending time criticizing others, repent. God will lift you up. Let us go to build an altar of prayer.

There are two ways to solve problems. If you solve it with tricks, you suffer greatly and return to the same place after forty years, but if you solve it through prayer, you fulfill God's will and become glorious. Experience the Lord's glory.

Not asking about a path you have not traveled means you will lose your way; not praying about a life you have not lived means wasting time and sinning. Walk together, then you will not gratify the desires of the flesh.

Next week is amazing—dawn worship, Wednesday service, evening prayer meeting. The ministry workers' retreat, everyone is leaving and I'm alone. When checking gifts, one thing that comes out is 'substitute.'
Discernment_and_Choice: Never, never just go along with the crowd. When we justify our wounds and greed, the Holy Spirit inevitably gives us an uneasy heart. Only a very few do not overlook this prick of the Holy Spirit, but instead expose and repent of their packaged motives and manipulative inner selves that try to deceive even God and themselves. Wounds, greed, and self-defense can easily take on the names of love, loyalty, and justice. The glory of transformation and revival is not common. This is because there must be a contrition that reveals hidden fear, anger, and obsession, rather than the justification of words and actions. Walk the narrow path of not just blending in. Thoroughly examine what your true motive is. [cite: 3] Rather than clumsily trying to cover the heavens, confess to God who examines the heart. 'All a person’s ways seem pure to them, but motives are weighed by the Lord' - Proverbs 16:2. [cite: 4]
Conflicting questions: 'How much longer?' vs 'Who do you say I am?' If the focus is not aligned, life cannot move forward.

Be filled before you take action—the power of decision. Acts is also called the Acts of the Holy Spirit. If you decide and act based only on emotion and logic, you will face shame. You must be filled before you act.

Basics. The reason we cannot remain faithful only to the essence of the cross is that there are too many institutions. The late Pastor Ha Yong-jo said, 'To maintain a large organization, you must have institutions and systems, and then you inevitably lose the essence.' This is because the object and purpose of what one tries to maintain, other than the cross, was wrong from the beginning. [cite: 4]
Genuine wisdom. True wisdom is putting God first in your life. That is why the fear of the Lord is said to be the beginning of wisdom. [cite: 5]
How to see my essence. When you stand alone before the Lord, many filters are stripped away, and your true essence begins to be seen.
The reason for a lack of trust: It is because one uses the head more than the heart.

Discernment Disorder - The December War? [cite: 5] 1. If a defect is found on a factory line, all other products are thoroughly investigated. [cite: 6] 2. Whatever useless words you have spoken and executed with confidence, if they were wrong, you must thoroughly reflect on yourself and repent. [cite: 7] 3. Error is a tendency, and such tendencies are highly likely to continue appearing. 4. If there is no repentance for being more sensitive to the voice of emotion, for being a prisoner of prejudice, or for self-centered thinking and greed, it means the same fatal errors will be repeated in the future. [cite: 8] 5. If there is no reflection on wounds caused by false eschatology, the 'December War' theory, wrong judgments, taking sides, exaggerated expressions, and self-centered language—as if nothing happened—you will remain in the same place even after decades. [cite: 9] 6. The reason David is great is not because he struck down Goliath, but because of his ability to recover. [cite: 10]
Inferiority and Pride. Inferiority slanders a loyal person as being proud, and pride ignores a whole person as being inferior.

When greed is mixed with spiritual gifts, they become a curse. [cite: 10] There are those like a chairman's daughter who, having never dipped a finger in water, uses her great ability and privilege merely to reverse an airplane; and there are people of faith who, even with little strength, faithfully sustain and advance the world in secret. [cite: 11]

Do not judge. [cite: 11] Do not judge means do not offer hypocritical criticism or criticism without self-reflection (Matthew 7:1-5). A society, community, or individual without reflection becomes horrific. [cite: 12] In most cases, the power that deserves criticism enforces silence. Conversely, a pure person who needs no criticism is open to any critique. [cite: 13]

Mountains are mountains, and waters are waters. [cite: 13] The ability to see a mountain as a mountain and water as water is no small thing. [cite: 14] Even those who pride themselves on knowing the Gospel often fail to see believers as believers and the church as the church. [cite: 15] This is because believers and the church are inherently new creatures that are only visible when I die and live through Jesus. [cite: 16]
Priority War. Winning the battle of priorities determines victory or defeat. [cite: 16] This is because it reveals what I serve, who I am, and where I am going right now. [cite: 17]

Do the basics. [cite: 17] The most important thing is to be faithful to the basics. That means starting by focusing only on the Lord. [cite: 18] When focus is blurred, one becomes angry, dissipated, and full of regret. [cite: 19]
What is needed now: God's wisdom, God's guidance, God's discernment, God's passion, and God's character.
The reason for collapse: The reason one collapses in public is because of issues with God, who sees in secret.

When you lose your saltiness. There was a time when flour tteokbokki in front of schools was 10 won for 10 pieces. [cite: 19] Only gochujang and green onions were added, but that taste is hard to find now. If the taste has changed, you must know why. [cite: 20] You cannot insist that a changed taste is the same as the original. Tteokbokki does not choose the person; the person has the right to seek out the taste. [cite: 21]

Do not be swayed by judgment. [cite: 21] It is not in vain. If I do not even judge myself, what does the judgment of others matter? The only one who judges me is God, the eternal Judge. [cite: 22] 'I care very little if I am judged by you or by any human court; indeed, I do not even judge myself' - 1 Corinthians 4:3. [cite: 23]
Luxury Q: Lord, are these various things I desire a luxury? [cite: 23] A: They are a luxury. Q: Oh? Ah... yes! (sob) [cite: 24]
True Discernment of Spirits. It is not about discerning others, but looking back at oneself, humbling oneself, and looking toward the Lord.

How to avoid mistakes. A drunk person must never take the wheel. [cite: 24] Unless you are filled with the Holy Spirit, you should not make decisions. You should find it unbearable to do something while not being filled with the Spirit. [cite: 25] This is because it only leaves wounds. [cite: 26]
Eyes to see the truth. Truth is meant to be seen within the peace that God gives.

The blessing of an uncomfortable heart. An uncomfortable heart, along with peace, is the other wheel of God's guidance. [cite: 26] It is the red light I can enjoy when I am awake and alive. [cite: 27]

Spiritual Warfare - How to let the enemy do the work for nothing. There is a persistent intention in what happens to us. [cite: 27] The enemy's intention is to steal, kill, and destroy. He wants us to react poorly in complaining situations, hateful relationships, discouraging events, and anger-inducing matters. [cite: 28] We do not have the immediate power to control the situation, but we can choose our response. [cite: 29] Especially when we lay down our rights and declare with a heart opposite to the situation, the strongholds of darkness begin to crumble. [cite: 30]

What you like vs. what you should like. If you focus on what you like that God has not given, you will fail to receive the best that God truly gives. [cite: 30] Lay down that which prevents God's best from coming. [cite: 31]

Two beings that remind us. The one who reminds us of the true meaning of the grace we received and the worship we offered is the Counselor, the Holy Spirit; however, the role of evil spirits is to make us feel it is meaningless and to deny it. [cite: 31] Do not be used by the deceiver, but by the One who is the Truth. 'But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you all things and will remind you of everything I have said to you' - John 14:26. [cite: 32]

Respect the decision. [cite: 32] This is because one takes responsibility for their own decision. Destiny is determined by decisions. Therefore, decisions are made by faith. [cite: 33] You must not make decisions when you are not filled with the Spirit or when you are angry. [cite: 34]

The reason you cannot let go. If you cannot see, you cannot discard and you cannot leave. [cite: 34] Pray that the Spirit of wisdom and revelation may be poured out. Then you will grow distant from unnecessary things. [cite: 35]

Dangerous familiarity. Becoming familiar is convenient, but it also means it becomes dangerous. [cite: 35] Incidents always break out in places where one is overconfident. Especially in faith, this is seen when one loses their initial heart. [cite: 36]

Freedom of Expression and Protection of Privacy. The secret to harmonizing these two wisely is to adjust the intensity and leave some empty space. [cite: 36] Like a song without a rest, a melody made only of forte, or a letter without spaces, suffocating interference—like hitting the predicted comments on every photo and post—exhausts people and will eventually leave you alone. [cite: 37] Observing deeply and expressing restraint at the peak of inspiration is like listening to a beautiful melody. [cite: 38] 'Like apples of gold in settings of silver is a ruling rightly given' - Proverbs 25:11. [cite: 39]

The reason it is hard: It is because the purpose has missed the mark. [cite: 39] Even if the purpose is right, the reason it is hard is because there is another motive behind the right purpose. [cite: 40]
The difference between a pro and an amateur. An amateur wins by chance and talks a lot, but a pro wins when they must win. [cite: 40] Victory is a battle of priorities. [cite: 41]

Do not ride the 'some' (flirt with triviality). Do not react to things that are neither fact nor truth. [cite: 41] It is because your foundation as a 'precious being' and the truth are being shaken. [cite: 42]

This week's leader meeting preview: Faith Algebra. Believing + Truth = Faith. Believing + Desire = ? [cite: 42] Believing + Feeling = ? Believing + Mind = ? Believing + Truth - reality = ? Believing + Heart = ? [cite: 43] Believing + .... ... Try to prepare. [cite: 44]

Let's be a 'Good Restaurant.' A good restaurant is one that serves what can and should be eaten. [cite: 44] However, these days, because many places serve inedible things to increase short-term profits—using nice interiors but poor ingredients and excessive food additives—TV shows go around searching for 'good restaurants.' [cite: 45] I believe the church can be the same. In terms of supply, restaurants and churches are alike. [cite: 46]

Jacob meets Laban. [cite: 46] Everyone else knew how cunning Jacob was, but he alone did not know. Only after meeting his uncle Laban did he realize how painful such actions are to others. [cite: 47] When contemplating difficulties in relationships, you must see the reflection of that other person within yourself. [cite: 48]

Expanding vision. Seeing through others' flaws is not insight; it is because you see your own internal weaknesses in the other person. [cite: 48] When your vision expands like that, you should look at the vulnerability within yourself rather than criticizing others. [cite: 49] It takes a player to recognize a player. [cite: 50]

Wrong faith. Believing what you want to believe without reality testing is called fanaticism. [cite: 50] Vague devotion without the object of God is called superstition. Through faith, we come to know Him; through faith, we make righteous choices; and through faith, we obtain peace and salvation. [cite: 51]

Guard your lips. If you do not guard your lips, you end up doing all the hard work just for someone else to take the credit. [cite: 51] The background of using rude language is a self-centered mind that has no room to empathize with the pain of others. [cite: 52] Eventually, you alone will be unaware that you are reaping those fruits and becoming unwelcome. [cite: 53] Empathy is power. [cite: 54]

Unpermitted happiness. No matter how happy it looks, if it is not your place, do not stand there. [cite: 54] This is because a sharp blade is hidden there, rather than true happiness. Unpermitted happiness is the same as permitted misfortune. [cite: 55]
If you procrastinate despite being prepared, it clearly means you are not ready. [cite: 55] Especially, it means your heart is not right. [cite: 56]

Envy is the level. People mistakenly think that if they possess certain things, others will envy them. [cite: 56] A proper person is not attracted to such things. Only a goldfish with an IQ of 10 reacts to an earthworm. [cite: 57]

When a shower of emotions falls, just wait. [cite: 57] When a shower of emotions pours down, it is hard to see ahead. If you do not rush but take a step back and wait for the shower to subside, things become clearer, and the greed hidden behind hasty judgments is revealed. [cite: 58] That greed makes you miss 'the best' while trying to grab 'the good,' and sometimes it appears in a very religious form. [cite: 59]
It is truth, not suggestion. [cite: 59] Do not react to suggestions, but come to a conclusion through the cross. Suggestions are lies and greed. [cite: 60]

The reason for being evil and lazy: It is because you are gripped by an evil and lazy spirit. [cite: 60] When the Word is not heard and criticism rises within you, take it as a serious warning and repent. [cite: 61]

When angry at a clueless person: You are more angry that they still don't know it than you are at the mistake itself. [cite: 61] The problem is that the standard for that 'mistake' is so varied and self-centered, and often not sufficiently shared. The fact that it's a bit embarrassing to share also plays a part. [cite: 62] No one can realize this and adjust for you on their own; the only One who knows has no intention of just 'matching' your standard. [cite: 63] That is why many well-meaning people tilt their heads and ask, 'What on earth is the problem?' [cite: 64]
Decide the future now. If you want revival, it means you desire a change in the outcome. [cite: 64] To change the outcome, the cause must change, which means transforming your present. [cite: 65]

Judgment and gossip always go looking for their owner. [cite: 65] To not be judged, you must not judge. Gossip destroys faith and makes the little ones stumble, leading oneself to judgment; but mercy triumphs over and boasts against judgment. [cite: 66] What you prided as a cool-headed and firm personality is actually the reef that shipwrecks your life. [cite: 67]
A good thing is not always 'The Best.' [cite: 67] Good things sometimes hinder the best. To hold onto the best, you must let the good things flow away. [cite: 68]
The self-deceiver: The person who asks but won't listen anyway. [cite: 68] Because they won't try to hear until they hear the answer they want. [cite: 69]
Humble yourself. It is knowing your faith has been over-packaged and tearing your heart. [cite: 69] Who am I really when I am alone? [cite: 70]
Today's Prayer: Grant me the loyalty to do my best in the tasks entrusted to me, the trust to leave what I cannot do to You, and the wisdom to discern to whom all these things belong.

You must cross Saul to become David. [cite: 70] The reason you want to interfere and fix someone, and the reason your stomach turns because you see right through their intentions, is because your own weaknesses—which you really want to deny—are seen in that person like a mirror. [cite: 71]

How to hear God's voice: I'll tell you the most certain way. [cite: 71] Obey. Then you will hear it more clearly and in detail. 'Anyone who chooses to do the will of God will find out whether my teaching comes from God or whether I speak on my own' - John 7:17. [cite: 72]

Complexity is evil. [cite: 72] Paul warns against evil and false teachers who complicate the Gospel. Faith is simple. The complexity that prevents one from holding onto the cross is an evil that has lost the essence. [cite: 73]
Two simple questions explain everything. [cite: 73] 1. What do I truly want in my heart? 2. If I truly want it, am I willing to pay the price? [cite: 74]

Faith and 'being nice' are different. [cite: 74] Thinking that just being nice is faith is a delusion. Not being based on a perception of reality is fanaticism. [cite: 75] Faith is not ignoring or disregarding reality, but transcending it. Above all, focus shifted away from Jesus is nothing but perishable things, no matter how much zeal and devotion you have. [cite: 76]
The reason for repeatedly falling: It is not because the temptation is strong, but because you are afraid of falling. [cite: 76] If you do not fear, the need to fall disappears. [cite: 77]
Ask like this: Earnestly ask for courage like a storm and wisdom to overcome the enemy and adversary.
A minister is one who knows the truth better than anyone and thus fears God doubly more on their own.

If you look at the motive, you won't be deceived. [cite: 77] Seeing the appearance is sense, and seeing the result is logic. But seeing the motive is love. That is why God looks at the motive. [cite: 78] 'People look at the outward appearance, but the Lord looks at the heart' - 1 Samuel 16:7. [cite: 79]

God's vision prevents sin. God used Abigail to stop David's anger. [cite: 79] Abigail shows David, who was tied to and looking at the problem only through his own eyes, God's perspective. Look down through God's eyes. [cite: 80] David said to Abigail, 'Praise be to the Lord, the God of Israel, who has sent you today to meet me' - 1 Samuel 25:32. [cite: 81]

Foolishness and Wisdom. A foolish person blames others, but a wise person finds the cause within themselves. [cite: 81] Just because Saul is evil does not mean David's discipline is automatically waived. Rather than resenting others' words and actions, seeing one's own wrongdoing and repenting is being wise before the Lord. [cite: 82]

'In my opinion...', we call our own thoughts our best effort. [cite: 82] That is why we perish. God is the One who changes not the 'best,' but the worst into the 'highest.' [cite: 83] When David briefly lost faith and followed 'the thoughts of his heart' to stay in Ziklag of the Philistines for 1 year and 4 months, he faced the 'test of Ziklag,' which was as difficult as the Absalom incident. [cite: 84] Deciding without asking is mostly a comfortable and easy decision, and easy decisions always lead to ruin. Do not make easy decisions; carry the cross. [cite: 85] David thought to himself, 'One of these days I will be destroyed by the hand of Saul. The best thing I can do is to escape to the land of the Philistines' - 1 Samuel 27:1. [cite: 86]

Why did Snow White's stepmother, the wicked queen, die so miserably? [cite: 86] It was because she was deceived by the mirror. She judged herself based on a condition compared to the teenage Snow White. When looking at a loved one, you do not hold up a mirror; you look with the heart. [cite: 87] If you judge people based on conditions, you will not believe even if the Son of God becomes incarnate and performs miracles before your eyes. [cite: 88] 'You judge by human standards; I pass judgment on no one' - John 8:15. [cite: 89]

Even when criminals gather, there can be truth; even when only saints gather, there can be pettiness. [cite: 89] Cutting in line at church is evil, but being considerate of a new family member is good; encouraging and building up a brother or sister is good, but if it makes others feel alienated, it is evil. [cite: 90] Good faith is not extreme. That is why we are not the judges. Only the Lord judges. [cite: 91]
Peter explains the intensity of Satan's temptation using the analogy of a hungry lion. [cite: 91] Here, Satan is called 'Diabolos,' which originally means 'the accuser.' [cite: 92]

2 Peter 2:15-22 Thursday QT. Greed blinds your eyes. Verse 15. [cite: 92] Balaam son of Beor is the representative of false teachers. The motive of that false teaching is the wages of unrighteousness, namely, greed. [cite: 93] People do not do things because of conviction; ultimately, they do them because of what they want—greed. Verse 16. Such people are worse than donkeys. [cite: 94] (Even after 4,000 years, the Bible testifies to that infamy.) Verse 17. The judgment upon them will be incredible. Verse 18. [cite: 95] Their doctrine is 1. subtle boasting and 2. providing suggestive physical satisfaction. -> Human depravity has a set route since the fruit of knowledge. [cite: 96] Once a path is made on a mountain, countless animals and people frequent it. It is very hard to remove a path once it is made. Verse 19. [cite: 97] Physical pleasure is not freedom, but becoming a slave to destruction, the devil, and sin, doing whatever they command. The flesh must die and the spirit must live. [cite: 98] (You get mind-controlled by a Dark Archon; it feels terrible.) Verse 20. If you know the truth and then fall away or apostatize, it's no joke. [cite: 99] Look at those around you who betray faith and proudly persist in depravity. Simply, if an angel falls, they don't become a semi-angel; they become a demon. Verse 21. [cite: 100] 'It would have been better for them not to have known' is not just in love songs. How severe must that judgment be for such words to be spoken? A foolish person does not know fear. [cite: 101] Ignorance makes one brave. Verse 22. Let's not fall into such proverbs in life (pigs, dogs). The Lord lived a life that fulfilled the word and will of God. [cite: 102] Will you follow dogs and pigs? Or will you follow the Word and the Spirit? Decide now. [cite: 103] * Greed turns people into beasts and leads to such low-level decisions (encounters, actions, language, choices). [cite: 104] What is truly pitiful is to fall away regarding the truth within the church. You worked and served so hard, but if you are deceived by falsehood, you've done all the work just for the 'dog' to take it. [cite: 105] Paul preached the Gospel with fear and trembling. If boasting, lust, and jealousy are subtly lurking, you are acting as an enemy of the cross. [cite: 106]

When you change, you must pay attention. [cite: 106] You must gauge whether it is a transformation or a deterioration. A cynical person changing into one moved by tears is the work of the Holy Spirit; the opposite is the work of darkness. [cite: 107] Regardless of the reason, if you grow distant from God, it is close to deterioration. [cite: 108]

When Potiphar's wife tempted him, Joseph refused, saying, 'How then could I do such a wicked thing and sin against God?' [cite: 108] In Joseph's eyes of faith, the visible God was present, but He was not visible to that woman. If God is not seen, one lives recklessly. [cite: 109] A person lives by what they gaze upon. [cite: 110]

Potiphar's wife must have been someone Joseph hated enough to want to kill. [cite: 110] At least in Joseph's situation, that is so. But without her, he could not have gone to prison or met the cupbearer. [cite: 111] Potiphar's wife was indeed someone used greatly. Through one's own eyes, it's just things getting tangled, but through the Lord's eyes, all things work together for good. [cite: 112] 'The Lord works out everything to its proper end—even the wicked for a day of disaster' - Proverbs 16:4. [cite: 113]
A Christianity that has lost its focus is more dangerous than an immoral heresy. [cite: 113] Just as a disease without symptoms is more dangerous. [cite: 114]

Partial things are meant to disappear when the perfect comes. [cite: 114] When we knew partially, we condemned; but when we know the whole, we forgive and show mercy. 'Ah! So that's why... how hard it must have been! It must have hurt a lot!' [cite: 115] When we act out because we think we know a little, everyone condemns. Satan works through condemnation. But when a perfect person comes, they save life. They forgive. [cite: 116] Love is reacting to the whole. [cite: 117]

Discernment is not easy. It requires capacity. The greatest capacities for discernment are love and passion. [cite: 117] These are what the Lord considers primarily when He looks at us, and that is why God accepts us, gives us opportunities, and never gives up. [cite: 118] Therefore, one who has the Lord's heart does not give up and forgives until the end. [cite: 119]

The power of discernment: The most basic thing is that power recognizes power. [cite: 119] Elisha's excellence did not start from doing some great work, but from recognizing Elijah. The most fundamental start begins with respecting those worthy of respect. [cite: 120] Discernment is power. [cite: 121]

This is an era where discernment is needed. Without discernment, we lose the most precious things. [cite: 121] Discernment is not about accumulating more knowledge or being articulate. True discernment is respecting the Lord in every area. [cite: 122] If you give up your own things to seek God's glory, you will see clearly what the Lord desired. [cite: 123] 'The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge, but fools despise wisdom and instruction' - Proverbs 1:7. [cite: 124]

Unbelief is also a kind of faith. [cite: 124] It is having excessive trust in things that are negative and not the truth. Every thought that does not come from the Lord must be discarded. [cite: 125] When the Spirit of Truth comes, true freedom follows. [cite: 126]

Unbelievers say: The Bible is a contradiction. Yes! That is true. To an elementary student, calculus is a contradiction. [cite: 126] Arbitrary interpretation and a victim mentality come from immaturity. [cite: 127]

Faith is a choice. Choice changes destiny. [cite: 127] So life can be called a collection and a continuation of choices. But to make a choice, a comparison of values must occur, and to have that ability to compare, one must have insight. [cite: 128] Hebrews says that such value comparisons are made by faith. Choosing where I should stand and what I should endure—that is faith, and through it, destiny is changed. [cite: 129]

Our appearance today is one of being strong in complaint and criticism while weak in sacrifice. [cite: 129] The couple Ananias and Sapphira were disciplined. It was not a matter of the amount, but a sin of the heart and motive. [cite: 130]

Whether criticized or praised, it is hard to say that is my essence. [cite: 130] Conversely, the same is true of the judgment I perform regarding others. We know in part and speak in part. Only in the Lord do I know who I am. [cite: 131] I am one loved by God and cleansed by the blood of His Son. [cite: 132]
Ambition packaged as vision and calculated kindness... that is Absalom.

How do we distinguish between criticism and discernment? [cite: 132] Cold standards and legitimacy? No. When you criticize, you prove a fault, gain empathy, and feel pleasure; but when you discern, God's tears well up. Criticism is addictive. [cite: 133] Pointing out others' flaws does not automatically make you righteous. On the contrary, you will face judgment without mercy. [cite: 134]
The ultimate fool: Restricting truly precious things with truly trivial ones.
Advice to reject and advice to accept: Advice without affection and advice with affection.

Satan deceives. [cite: 134] We make resolutions, but we are easily deceived. We are deceived in situations, in rumors, and in emotions. When we hear the voice of the Lord, who knows us better than our own assertive subjectivity, we can avoid being deceived. [cite: 135] In Ephesians, Paul advises believers fighting in the last days to put on the sword and belt of truth. [cite: 136] Before truth, all lies will crumble. [cite: 137]

In Acts 27, in a life-or-death situation where all hope of being saved was lost, they survived. The soldiers thought it best to kill the prisoners lest they swim away and escape, but the centurion, wanting to spare Paul's life, blocked their plan. After reaching the island, Paul was bitten by a viper but did not die. [cite: 137] Paul was not afraid. He was certain that until the time permitted by God and until God's purpose was fulfilled, neither the storm, nor people trying to kill him, nor the viper would harm him or cause his death. [cite: 138] Discern the Lord's time and will. Discernment is power. [cite: 139]

Do not let your heart ache because of people and circumstances, but I hope you have a broken heart together with the things that break God's heart. [cite: 139] Our purpose is not people or circumstances. [cite: 140]

People deceive for their hidden desires. [cite: 140] They deceive others, themselves, and even try to deceive God. When you lay things out with sincerity, you see your true self, and what to seek and what to lay down begins to be distinguished. [cite: 141]

'Anyone who chooses to do the will of God will find out whether my teaching comes from God or whether I speak on my own' - John 7:17. When you try to do His will and intend to obey, the Word is heard and you discern. [cite: 141] However, those who do not obey will stumble in the darkness. This is because they live 'loving darkness rather than light' (John 3:19) and distancing themselves from the light of truth. [cite: 142]

Love is sacrifice. [cite: 142] However, you must discern it from an investment. Love is a purpose in itself. That is why Jesus is not an 'event.' [cite: 143] Every moment, in every breath, you must remain in Jesus Christ. [cite: 144]

Reason not to be envious. Do not envy the prosperity of the wicked. Do not react to wrong intentions. [cite: 144] Do not let your heart be hurt by vain boasting or matters that God has not given you to be concerned with. [cite: 145] That is why the mission-driven person who knows the truth is free. [cite: 146]
It is not that the other person was wrong, but that I needed discipline. [cite: 146] Look and interpret with eyes of faith, not wounded emotions. Interpretation is power. [cite: 147]

A person who calculates situations with petty tricks, or makes guesses and imaginations while being led by emotions, is untrustworthy. [cite: 147] Become a 'fool' who knows nothing but Jesus. You will become the wisest person. [cite: 148]

Interpretation is faith. [cite: 148] It is not that circumstances are harassing me, but that I am interpreting them that way. [cite: 149] Fallen leaves drop without thought, but they become romance to some and sorrow to others. [cite: 150]
If you are not filled with the Spirit, you move by arbitrary interpretation. [cite: 150] Typically, you see small things as big and stake your life on unimportant things. [cite: 151]
The world makes us hold onto 'good' things. [cite: 151] Thus, it makes us miss 'the best.' Things that look good are perishable, but the best things are eternal. [cite: 152]

Redeeming the time is making the most of every opportunity. [cite: 152] To make the most of opportunities, you must discern God's will. God's will is hidden in success. The foolish rich man perished because he could not discern it. God's will is also hidden in failure. [cite: 153] Through it, Peter meets Jesus personally. [cite: 154]
Desire speaks of faith. [cite: 154] What do you truly want? [cite: 155]
Sometimes it's a problem if a desire is fulfilled, and sometimes it's a problem if it's not. [cite: 155] The core is where that desire came from. [cite: 156]

Bad choices and good choices. Whether you will be happy or miserable is determined by where you bet your life. [cite: 156] Do not be swayed by arbitrary feelings and emotions, but decide based on the truth. Then my heart will win. [cite: 157]
Leftover water from the retreat, I'll use it for the next one. [cite: 157] I could start a water business. [cite: 158]

Reforming systems and institutions is a fundamental response. However, that is not the true cause. Human greed—sin—is the cause. [cite: 158] Unless that fundamental is resolved, system reform will return to the starting point. [cite: 159] Christians need the insight to see the priority and the patience to push it through. [cite: 160]
Watch and pray so that you do not fall into temptation.

'Each one must give as he has decided in his heart, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.' (2 Corinthians 9:7)
Jesus, who is the Lion of the tribe of Judah and also a Lamb, heard all the criticism directed at Him, but was firmly angry at challenges to the Father.

Food that is harmful is sweeter. Inferiority, comparison, pride, rejection are harmful but never let go. However, when you know Christ, you can let go of these things. Before the Lord's honor and beauty, the value of all things is reset to filth.

Have you seen a music critic properly sing even one song? We are not called to evaluate and criticize but to sing of life. From the Garden of Eden and continuing in the future, what we must hold onto against sin is the 'gospel.' The gospel is sufficient.
I give thanks and honor and glory to the Lord who has given us such lovely members and hears our prayers. Working Lord! I love you.

Put down human greed and methods, and I urge you to acknowledge the sovereignty of Him who is the reason and praise of my life. After that, He will open the way, accomplish His will, and provide for it.

Popularity is momentary and leaves as quickly as it comes, and money is the same. Only what belongs in Jesus Christ is eternal. Nothing works according to my will like relationships with people. Do not mourn and rejoice for a moment by the Lord's sovereignty and rule, but lift your eyes and look to the Lord as you go forward. The Lord is eternal.
The happiest moment in life is not when you are recognized. It is when you lay that recognition at the feet of the Lord.
The purpose of life is to fear God and delight in Him forever (Westminster Shorter Catechism). It is a disaster to force joy.
When work is finished, I should gather with young people like morning dew and celebrate Christmas.

Rather than trying to change your position, strive to change your posture. When your posture aligns with the Lord's heart, your position will change naturally. Worship the Lord as you are in the place He called you, and the Lord will see.
Dedicating yourself in service does not come from great resolve, but is simply the mark of a life that follows from loving the Lord and remaining in that place.
Blessed is the person whose heart centers on praising the Lord. Because the Lord receives only that praise.
We will be broadcasting the LFGC Youth Mini-retreat @korea on Youtube shortly. I'll share the link when it starts. It should be around 12:30 in UK time.
I become silent and look only to the Lord. In complete silence...

Though there are obstacles and storms, my heart toward the Lord cannot stop. I run toward the Lord and toward His Word. As time passes, I will run even more, and at the end of this path I will see the Lord. God never turns away from the 'passionate seeker.' - From Acts Chapter 6 and Answered Cries

I arrived well hehe. I must have overdone it on Sunday because I got body aches and when I woke up, Monday was almost over;;ㅋㅋ Still, worship and prayer meeting on Sunday were so good!! It would be nice to go on Saturday too..ㅋㅋ I wish I could;;ㅠ
Evening prayer 7:30 PM @Mission Hall. Because of me, because of my lips... Lord, be exalted. We will pray simple prayers. Simple prayer means praying sufficiently and continuously.
Before the Almighty Father, open your mouth and cry out in prayer.
In your youth without regret, only for the Lord~~

In my youth, while serving with dedication and faithfulness no less than others, one day the Lord gave me a heart to simply wait for Him. Unexpectedly, the Lord came. Repentance burst forth. Though it has been long since the Lord's presence withdrew, I wonder why. A life that knows no shame, not grieving over failing to uphold holiness, but rather rationalizing with the power of absolute positivity believing all will be well—recovering faith in the cross and holiness are what accompany and bring revival.

No one thinks it wasteful to make reservations, search for good restaurants, and prepare to spend sweet time with someone you truly love. The most valuable thing in the world is prayer. Worldly people never understand it and cannot understand it. Because it is spoken on the premise of the Lord being alive.

If you truly love, there is no need to hide or exaggerate. A cough and poverty and love cannot be hidden. Especially love becomes more evident the more you try to hide it. That is why we pour ointment on Jesus' feet with tears.
It's truly wonderful, beyond words... looking to the Lord... is truly happiness.

If we are truly not to bring shame to God's name, the community needs much change, which means there must be a greater presence of the Lord. We must seek the Lord's face more. This is especially required of those in ministry.
Do not quench the fire on the altar - Leviticus 6:12,13
Rejoicing in unconditionally loving the Lord so deeply, being captivated by His beauty—this is faith.
