We cannot find ourselves in obtaining a nice car or a nice house. Possessing useless things or receiving envious glances does not change the value of my essence. Those things are all jewelry boxes. But a jewel is a jewel even when wrapped in tissue. Even if it falls in mud, it is a jewel. So Paul said we hold Jesus, the jewel, in earthen vessels. Do not prove yourself; testify. You will be happy.
The Lord's blood is powerful. There is nothing more to say beyond that. Under heaven, no other name has been given to mankind by which we can be saved.
There are many reasons to give my heart to the Lord.
There are many reasons to give my heart to the Lord. Even one reason among them is sufficient to give all my heart.

Three functions of confession: 1. It reveals who God is. 2. It shows me who I am through the Lord's eyes. 3. Knowing God and seeing myself, it binds all troubles and enemies and brings victory. Every moment, unceasingly confess that you believe in and love the Lord and are loved by Him. That will be your strength and power. Each time the enemy touches a wound, confessing actually becomes an opportunity to draw closer to the Lord. Hallelujah
How can I be silent since the Lord has saved me?
Entrust yourself to the Lord's hands. The Lord's promise is eternal. Since He is my reason, I worship Him in any circumstance.
The power to come before the Lord and worship in the temple comes only by relying on God's love. Worship without love is only empty. - Psalm 5:7
I hope you will be faithful before the Lord and gentle before the saints. God seeks such people, observes them as if writing in a ledger, and pours out power upon them.
You have turned my shame into joyful praise

If you cannot maintain a personal encounter with the Lord, secret time becomes a time of sin. But for those who have deep encounters with the Lord, secret time becomes the deepest and most noble time of grace.

There is time that must be given to the Lord. There is passion that must be given to the Lord. When absolute time and focus are offered, that life becomes extraordinary. The greatest prime of life comes. Run more than 300 kilometers, more than 1,800 meters. Revival begins with me.

Looking to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith. - Hebrews 12:2 Not continually looking to Jesus is already sin and a wound of the heart. That is why Jesus is not an event. You must continually prepare your heart with the truth of the Word.

I desire that the Lord give me a burning thirst. Such a thirst that nothing else satisfies, such a longing that counterfeits cannot solve, such a desperate yearning that only the Lord can fill—grant me such a heart. I love You, Lord.

Let us look only to the Lord. Even now the Lord surveys the whole earth to display His power to those who look only to Him (those who turn their whole heart to Him, Acts 16:9). Dead fish are swept away by the currents. But living fish swim against the current. I pray that all who pray would have the strength to swim against the tide that all the world follows, the strength to live against history, the strength to live against the values of the age.

When you continually look only to the Lord, you realize you don't even have the leisure to criticize others. Revival comes from God through repentance and confession of my own spirit beyond condemnation.

You must look only to the Lord. You must love the Lord. You must praise the Lord alone completely. With all your heart. Woe to those who look to people, love people more, and are more conscious of people. 1 Corinthians 16:22 KJV If anyone does not love the Lord, let him be cursed. The Lord is coming.
For the Lord I live, for the Lord I work, for the Lord I suffer, and for the Lord I will die~
The Lord gave us holy hands. He gave them to bless. He also gave us holy ground. To worship by treading upon it. Even in this dark world, bless and worship.

The Lord humbled Himself and rejoiced, and with that joy came to the world, became a grain of wheat, died serving, and left much fruit. The biblical definition of pride is, as Satan did, 'not keeping one's place.' Unless I deeply root humility and become wholly the Lord's, humbling myself thoroughly, I can never exalt the Lord.
The Lord seeks. The Lord calls me. The Lord is my Lord and the only One worthy of praise.

The time of looking to the Lord is the happiest and most precious time. He wants to call us out from trouble and be with us. Don't worry about tomorrow; don't fear the temporary power that is breathing through his nostrils; just focus on the Lord.
When you cannot look to the Lord, though you have much you are empty, though you are capable you are only frustrated. Let everything go and look to the beautiful Lord.
When I see the Lord, even at work or school I kneel and pray and repent. Peter knelt and repented in the presence of many colleagues in his workplace. Stand before the Lord.
You must serve with the Lord's heart and the Lord's love. Only then does the Lord alone receive glory.
I see the greatest strength in those for whom there is nothing to hope for but the Lord's love. Beyond strength, it evokes admiration. That is faith. It is nothing but that.
I desire to seek the Lord's face and bow down completely.

When you fully seek the Lord's glory, you become bold and free. Entrust such people with human rights, property rights, and spiritual authority. Do not fear what is insignificant. I pray you would become a life burning yourself on the altar so that the Lord alone receives glory.

When you long for the Lord's voice, opening your eyes early in the morning makes you think of the Lord's beauty first. Faith is more about the heart than appearance. If you open the dawn with thoughts of the Lord, your heart will be running in love for Him. See Psalm 119:55.

The Lord's kindness is truly beautiful. It is so wonderful that I would gladly lay everything down. Since His kindness is better than life, my lips will praise the Lord. Therefore all my life I will bless the Lord, and by His name I will lift up my hands. Psalm 63:3-4.
When my rights are surrendered before the Lord's altar, God will grant me an incomparable greater privilege.

When you acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty, you will see His work. The God who gave His Son through the cross gives us precious people to love and be loved by, work to serve, and even a longing heart we cannot understand. That is why we present our bodies.

The reason we can sing that the Lord is just is not because my vested interests and evaluations have comparative advantage or at least reasonableness, but because there is the Lord's presence that others do not have. This allows us to see what others cannot see, hear voices that have not been heard, and enjoy love that has been rejected. 'I Have No Possessions' - Poet Song Myung-hee.
If the Lord has become my King and I have resolved to praise Him alone forever, what more can be a problem? All problems and trials are compressed into the question: 'Who is King?'

The Lord is calling. Your calling. He calls you to love, to serve, to humbly lower yourself. He called us to love with tears forever in our hearts toward the Lord, even if all things scatter in the distant future and no names and memories remain. Today He has called us to sow seeds and worry whether they will sprout, yet entrust even that to the Lord, and to be able to give thanks even if the one who reaps is not ourselves.
To serve the people the Lord has entrusted is to pour out sweat, tears, and blood. Then many grains of wheat will return to the Lord.
We must run tirelessly so that the Lord is glorified. Because the Lord is so wonderful, so much that we don't know what to do. May our hearts be full of longing for the Lord's house. I love Jesus.

It is right to raise our hands with both hands and praise with the joy the Lord gives, but when we exalt the Lord with all our heart and soul, He gives us boundless joy. When the Holy Spirit comes, He gives joy, power, and a heart of obedience. But if we believe that He will do so and obey and move forward in faith, He will pour out abundantly as well.

I hope the Lord becomes important. Blessed are those whose interest and pursuit become the Lord. It is glorious when someone finds the Lord so precious that other values become like rags before a luxury brand.
Rather than being satisfied with what the Lord allows, I hope you will dedicate yourselves to what He leads and offer yourselves willingly. That is a joyful life.
Running toward the Lord.

To those who long for the Lord's house, the wind of the Holy Spirit blows. The Holy Spirit's wind enables us to pray what we cannot pray, repent of what we cannot repent, forgive what we cannot forgive, and worship as we cannot worship. Not by might, nor by power, but by the Holy Spirit alone.

Paul worked during the day and taught at night for three years in Ephesus while shedding tears. (Acts 20:31) He also served the Lord with burning passion and fervent truth. (Romans 12:11) Throughout decades of ministry, he continuously served the Lord with a burning heart and endless tears. Why? Simply put, because he spent tens of thousands of days in the field as one day. 'Remember that for three years I never stopped warning each of you night and day with tears.' - Acts 20:31. 'Never be lacking in zeal, but keep your fervent spirit' (fervent in spirit - NASB) in serving the Lord - Romans 12:11. Day by day, fervent in spirit, with a burning heart in the Holy Spirit. When we gather to pray, the truth of the cross is proclaimed. 7:30 as usual.
What is important is not being respected, but whether I am honoring the Lord right now.

Being there now is the Lord's grace, He says. Making you be there at first was His intervention and grace, and even now He sustains you by that grace. It is not by personal choice or luck, but by God's sufficient grace that He holds me. Faith is walking the path you cannot walk without the Lord. In this place where I stand, become a worshiper, dyed by the Lord's blood.

I give thanks for the presence and spirit of worship allowed during the last three days and four nights. What I earnestly desire now that I have returned is the Lord's greater presence. 'Not that I have already obtained all this, or have already arrived at my goal; but I press on to take hold of that for which Christ Jesus took hold of me.' Philippians 3:12.

Proclaiming truth like fire, being flawless in doctrine, and risking one's life in ministry are as important as this: Is my heart beating because of love for Jesus? If we cannot be moved, it can only be difficult.

Genuine love is accompanied by three things: the longing to see, the desire to hear his words, and the yearning to follow his will. David had all three. He yearned for God, desired to hear God's Word, and broke his own will to seek God's will.
A true Christian will discover that holiness like the Lord's is happier than 'their own happiness.' Faith is not complicated. Who do you love more?

True worship is to keep only the glorious Lord in your heart regardless of your emotional state and surroundings. Become a worshiper through worship. It is the path to fulfilling life's purpose and the secret to breaking through difficulties. 'By faith Jacob, when he was dying, blessed each of Joseph's sons, and worshiped as he leaned on the top of his staff.' - Hebrews 11:21.

A true man of God prioritizes presence over achievement. Before David faced Goliath, he had the meditation and prayer contained in the humble shepherd's 'Psalms.' Before you open your day today, bow before the Lord and seek Him and His blessing rather than other blessings. God and rejoicing in Him is my strength.

True strength is the ability to care for the weak. When you win, win with your best effort; when you lose, you must know how to lose. The highest level of power is the strength used to give people hope and joy, improve and restore relationships, and serve and build many people.
Countless signals exist in the air, but only the frequencies that match my wavelength are received and responded to. Responding only to the Lord is doing it right.
If what I seek is not the Lord's face, I should certainly be anxious. I have sought in vain what is futile with useless methods, so I must be frustrated.
When you praise, there is joy; when you give thanks, there is peace. This simplicity is truth.

When you become a true worshiper, God responds with a question: What do you want Me to do for you? The thousand-and-first worshiper, Solomon, makes a remarkable request. Why? Because worship purifies purpose.

When the accuser argues with fear and frustration, quietly say this: 'I am worshiping right now!' 'He who dwells in the secret place of the Most High will rest in the shadow of the Almighty. I say to the Lord, 'You are my refuge and my fortress, my God, in whom I trust. Surely He will save you from the fowler's snare and from the deadly pestilence.' - Psalm 91:1-3.

There is the taste of heaven, the joy of heaven, the fun of heaven. That is why we must become those who storm the kingdom, hungry for heaven. If you taste the pleasures of the world, the pleasures of heaven disappear. Those who believe in Jesus go to heaven. That is why there is the joy and taste of believing in Jesus.

The reward in heaven, the revival of the church—truly many important things exist, but among them the most precious longing is 'that I know Jesus.' There are many methods of how to yearn for God, but the most important is simply 'to yearn.'

The lion of the savanna does not run anytime. Though his speed in an instant is incredibly fast, his large body cannot run as long as other herbivores. But when a chance comes, he gives it his all. And once he catches, he never lets go. A revival meeting begins Friday evening. If you want to recover your first love and desire a genuine encounter with God, please definitely attend. And please bring other friends to attend as well. I love you in the Lord.
The greatest gift. The greatest gift a minister can have for his flock is personal holiness before the Lord.

Those who have seen the highest glory are not tempted by lower glory. Those who have experienced the best are not shaken by inferior things. Experience the glory of the Lord. Then you will not be shaken by emotional reasons, comparisons, or worldly pride. 'May the word of the Lord spread rapidly and be honored.' - 2 Thessalonians 3:1. When God's Word runs, you experience glory. Let the Lord's Word run in your heart.

When David was fleeing from Saul, he did not overcome by holding to the vision of a glorious throne in the future. He simply endured. Holding to the Lord, praising with tears as if today were his last.
They say praise makes even whales dance. Love makes flowers bloom even on dry thorns and dead trees. Do not stop at a blossoming life; become a life that makes flowers bloom.

They say praise makes even whales dance. If you want to see a greater miracle than a dancing whale, it is to give yourself and love. On the day before the cross, Jesus prayed that just as He and the Father are one, they too may be one. If we love, forgive, and become one bound by that love, the Lord's miracle has already begun.
Lord, my strength, I love You. - Prayer 7:30.

Even if you praise loudly, confess much love, and receive good training, if your heart is not continuously drawn to Jesus' presence, you have lost 'that love.' You must realize that loving Jesus is not merely doing something. However much we labor, without the burning love toward the Lord, it is mere emptiness. Moving forward for a deep relationship with the Lord at every moment requires a certain payment from me.

When the object changes, the approach changes. Let us fix our eyes on Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith. 'For the joy set before him he endured the cross, scorning its shame, and sat down at the right hand of the throne of God.' - Hebrews 12:2. [Modern version:] Let us fix our eyes on Jesus, the head and the final authority of faith. He endured the cross for the awesome reward ahead of Him, disregarding the shame, and became the king sitting at the right hand of God's throne.
Hebrews 12:1-3. The life God desires of us is to run the race of faith, fix our eyes on Jesus, and deeply contemplate Him. Then we do well. In this life there is presence and fruit.
Pray blessing and peace for the disciples of faith being sent out. Then that peace will remain on your cell, relationships, and the people who need to hear.

Peace is not circumstance but power. Only the powerful can have peace. True power comes from faith and trust in the Lord. Conversely, fear strongly testifies to God's absence. May we be like Gideon, build an altar for the Lord, have peace in worship (His presence), and become servants of Shalom who proclaim peace. 'Gideon built an altar to the Lord there and called it The Lord is Peace.' - Judges 6:24.
'Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives.' To give very my life, an offering of love, living sacrifice.
May you have peace. Because the Lord rules. When the Lord becomes King, He takes complete responsibility for this situation and breaks the yoke with His anointing.

After returning from exile, what was reflected through deep sorrow was not the improvement of systems. It was that worship recovery and people, the communities and leaders they belong to, were important. What should be prioritized for each person is believing in the blood of the cross and holiness in the Lord. Unexpected breakthroughs will come.

In God's kingdom, there will be many reversals. Do not try to fix people's reactions; when you love and revere with the Lord as center, great and secret things and God's righteousness will be accomplished.
Always, at every moment, there is a question asked. What do I desire that I am in this place for? The answer is always the same: 'I want to see the Lord's glory!!'

What matters before God is who you are and how you live. Your worship must be worship where you pursue truth in spirit. The Father seeks precisely those people—those who worship before Him in simplicity and honesty, just as they are. (John 4:23, The Message).
Rather than presenting something that looks quite good to God, present yourself. This is what the Lord desires and is a living sacrifice of the spirit.

If you face opposition in any battle, it means you must not give up but continue persistently. What the enemies truly wanted to hinder for Daniel and his friends were worship and prayer, which come from the fear of the Lord. The world is pleased when we live by its standards. However, when we live by the cross, it resists powerfully. This is because revival breaks forth.

*Reason to Be Bold in Tribulation: We suffer in this world and face indescribable pain and unimaginable tribulation. We do not know what tomorrow holds, yet with weary bodies we come to the place of worship. We are blessed. Now only the Lord and I exist.

*Faith: When the sight is fixed on the majesty of the sunrise at the seashore, other surrounding things do not catch the eye. Having good faith means that the frequency, absolute time, and depth of gazing upon the Lord is exceptional. It was said that when you look at the bronze serpent (without touching it), you live. When you look only at the Lord, nothing else enters your sight. That is how you overcome. All human problems arise from where you fix your gaze. If you turn away from the cross, you have already sinned. Let us fix our eyes on Jesus, the author and perfecter of our faith. - Hebrews 12:2

*Love as the Most Powerful Interpretation: One reason why interpreting the Holy Spirit is difficult is that a lens is needed to see clearly. That crucial lens is the lens of tears shed from loving Jesus most deeply. Then you begin to see God's simple and straightforward love. Love Jesus. Then you will see God. Neither height nor depth, nor any other created thing will be able to separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord. - Romans 8:39 (God's love exists only in Christ Jesus)
O Lord, my strength, I love You.
My desire is to love the Lord more.

At a wedding, we wear our finest clothes. If we stand in the finest clothes with a dirty body and hands and feet, it would be shameful. Create in me a clean heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me. - Psalm 51:10

If someone said they treated the Lord lightly, many people would jump up. Not many understand that what we do for the least of these and what we do in worship, we do unto the Lord. The Lord of hosts says: 'Is it not evil that you offer blind sacrifices? Is it not evil that you offer lame and sick ones? Now offer it to your governor—would he be pleased with you or accept you?' says the Lord of hosts. You ask for God's grace, saying 'Have pity on us,' but since you act this way, will I accept any of you?' - Malachi 1:8-9. I hope you will respect the Lord.

While comparing the lives of David and Saul for a leadership meeting, I encountered 1 Chronicles. Though 1 Chronicles is similar in content to 2 Samuel and 2 Kings, it was written through Ezra after the exile of Judah, with emphasis on the tradition of Judah and profound self-reflection for spiritual recovery. The content emphasizes: ① Jerusalem is important. ② Coworkers are important. ③ Worship is important. ④ We must rely on God. What astonished me personally while reading was that through the foundational work of David opening his kingship, it was an emphasis for future generations to see Judah's recovery in its historical context. It is about essence, not technique. Before operational tactics exist, what matters is a life that relies only on the Lord.
In trouble or in joy, I follow only the Lord; who can stop me? Not even death can stop me. Hymn 355
Passion is supplied only when I seek the Lord alone completely and concentrate on Him. Your gaze is your strength and power.
For You are holy Lord.
If I cannot fill my time with holiness, the power of sin will reign. If I serve the Lord with first things, He fills me with Himself in all things.
What I desperately need is deep fellowship with the Lord. That is most important now.
I love the Lord.
When I offer all my gaze to the Lord and feel the Almighty God, I experience God's work.

If the things I once did joyfully suddenly feel like a loss and as if I sacrificed unilaterally, it is because I am not looking at the One who truly lost and sacrificed. Though He is the very image of God, He became a servant and was obedient unto death. When your focus changes, you remember you broke even. Please fix all your gaze on the Lord.

Among many forms of piety, the reason many do not bear fruit and obscure God's glory is because they enjoy human praise more than necessary. O Lord, do not give glory to us, do not give it to us, but give it to Your name because of Your lovingkindness and truth. - Psalm 115:1

Be free from concern about who sees you. Also, do not strive to be seen. All our attention should be on God alone. That is worship. If I look only at the Lord, people will not see me but will see the God I am looking at. Do not be conscious of people but look upon God. You will be free from distress.

After David fell from grace and lost everything, the most painful thing was that he could not go to the house of the Lord. That is why he so envied the pilgrims. (Psalm 84) This is a person after God's own heart.
Aha~~ Give thanks with a grateful heart Give thanks to the Holy One

** Special Early Morning Prayer and Evening Special Prayer Session ** God is... I hope this becomes a precious time experiencing the living Lord. We begin from 'Friday' this week. Please prepare and pray with a longing heart. Early morning prayer (Monday–Friday) Evening prayer (7:30 Monday–Thursday, Friday special)

To continue in prayer and praise, love and forgiveness is faith. The reason we cannot move mountains is that we do not attempt and do not persist. A single drop of water is a small amount, but when it flows continuously, it pierces rock and fills the sea. Thus respond to challenges and persevere in what God desires. So what? The power to disregard it.

The most important character of a Christian is to resemble God's character. God's character is 'humility'—emptying Himself, taking the form of a servant, and being obedient unto death. The Lord taught His proud disciples servile humility by washing their feet. However, merely knowing this fact does not make us humble. Only when we always stand before the Lord who washed the feet of us, His sons and daughters, can we truly be humble. Before a Great Master, one naturally becomes humble. That Master is Jesus. 'Let this mind be in you which was also in Christ Jesus.' (Philippians 2:5)

When you love someone, it is natural to take interest in that person's concerns, preferences, and efforts. To someone unfamiliar with that field, it may seem like nothing and meaningless, but if it is related to the person you love, you remember and find meaning in it. You can love that person only to the extent you know them. 'Therefore let us know, let us press on to know the Lord; His going forth is established as the morning; and He will come to us like the rain, like the latter rain watering the earth.' - Hosea 6:3

Moses comes before God bearing the problems of Israel. Even in conflict, you can see God's glory coming forth. Do not focus on the conflict but only on the Lord. Then you will see the Lord's glory in dissolving the problem. Jesus said, 'Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind.' This is the first and greatest commandment. - Matthew 22:37

The power that breaks down strong fortresses is prayer, and that prayer becomes more powerful through attitude. When my life becomes God's joy, and when I fear the Lord, 'the will of heaven will be accomplished in this barren land.' If that person's conduct pleases God...
...and in that way, only the Lord is most glorified, only the Lord is most exalted.
Holiness and purity, single-minded determination, sacrifice and service, hope of heaven, loving enemies, unceasing prayer and gratitude—all these things are not done but become.

More important than the most important thing to me is Jesus. No matter how many blessings I receive, they cannot be better than the Lord. If there is no Lord, loneliness is better, and if the Lord distances Himself, it is better that everything stops.

Among Old Testament sacrifices, the burnt offering is completely consumed. When everything is completely burned, glory appears. When the Holy Spirit comes, we live a life of complete combustion. This is because He gives us a true purpose and mission field.
Listen well. With this song, we'll blow the roof off at the retreat.
When faith is inherent and overflows, it is expressed as joy. Romans 5:2. That is why worship contains heavenly joy.

In 1977, Voyager 2 traveled toward the end of the solar system at a speed faster than a bullet, 90,000 miles per hour. In 1989, about 11 years later, it arrived at Neptune and left the solar system. This means that only 958,000 years from now could we see another star. Even within a 1 light-year range. There are 100 million such solar systems in the Milky Way, and there are again 100 billion such galaxies in the universe. The number of stars in the universe is approximately 7 trillion times 10 billion, which is 10 times more than all the grains of sand on Earth. When you hold a handful of sand, it is 10 to the 16th power times more. Yet, our God created all these things in this way. And He made the stars. (Genesis 1:16) Ah, He is so simple. That is the power of the Creator, the Almighty. Such a God loves me and us, and disregarding insults to His divinity, higher than our personality, He paid all the price on the cross. And He desires that we depend on Him with perfect faith.

I am greatly displeased with your worship because you perform it reluctantly and laboriously, and I will not accept it. - Malachi 1:13. Formal worship without my heart, soul, and mind is not accepted by the Lord. The Lord honors those who honor Him and despises those who despise Him. If we do not rely on the blood and truth but go forth with human skill and fire that the Lord did not command, the result is that there is no response of God's fire on our altar, invented excitement runs rampant, and division and temptation arise. We must recover to worship in spirit and truth. - John 4:24
Can you praise the Lord in every circumstance? That is faith.

Though there are many people we must face and encounter frequently, if I fix my gaze on the one true object of worship, God, with all my thoughts, emotions, and will, then people may see me at first, but ultimately they will see the object their companion is looking at (our Lord God).
I'll become even more undignified than this some would say it's foolishness.
A cool person sheds tears endlessly. That is the love and grace of the Lord.
Anyone who loving Jesus, Just click the like button now! oh~ Yeh
It is time to begin the Saturday gathering open to everyone. We long for the Lord's anointing. There is nothing but the Lord.
When I offer all my gaze to the Lord and feel the living God, my life becomes the work of the Lord and God begins to work.

If there is passion to offer the very best to the most precious One, if you desire to move forward as an apostle of love serving with greater preparation, you must pour more time and devotion into prayer meetings before worship. If you truly long for the Lord's glory...

We must be free from concern about who sees us. Also, we should not strive to be seen. All our attention and focus should be on God alone; that is worship of life, true worship. If I look only at the Lord, people will not see me but will see the God I am looking at. If people can see God through you, that is the highest glory.

To leaders and future leaders... Jesus asked Peter about 'love' and said 'feed My sheep.' You must confirm 'love' and connect it to 'feeding the sheep.' When you fail an exam or fail a diet and devour six portions in a frenzy, and someone asks about the result, you don't want to answer and you resent the one who asked. But when an achievement that was impossible your whole life is finally reached, or when you reach an angelic weight, 'staying silent' is almost impossible. People who have experienced fervent worship cannot help but step forward in 'feeding the sheep.' You must start with love for Jesus, with heat. We cannot help but speak what we have seen and heard. What have you heard and seen? Your heart must burn with Jesus to save souls.

The purpose does not change. It will not stop either. Even if breath runs short, I will complete the race set before me. If while running I become too weary to continue, I will crawl to the Lord's altar on my hands and knees and offer myself. And then I will be happy. Because I have seen the glory of the Lord.

Even if you have much power and wealth, it is useless without health. In that sense, a greater gift than other spiritual gifts is delighting in God. (Nehemiah 8:10) Delighting in God comes from relationship and worship. If the relationship is broken, you are sick; and if you are sick, what good is walking on water? Do you truly delight in God at this very moment?

Let all who seek the LORD rejoice and be glad in him; may those who long for his saving help always say, 'The LORD is great!' - Psalm 40:16 You cannot know a person by their smooth words. You can discern a person's relationship with God by how they face their problems. Who is the Lord to you in the midst of your problems?
No matter how much we give, there is always a longing sense of insufficiency.

The heavy darkness of this world and the sins of many souls lead them to death. It is remarkable that even within London, and within the campus, there has never been a proper challenge in the name of the Lord against such corruption taking place. Like King Jehoshaphat and the people of Israel appearing helpless before the Aramean army, when we proclaim and praise the Lord's holy name and move forward, He will reveal who the true Master of this city is.

Proclaim thanksgiving and praise in the midst of discouragement and worry. This becomes my strength and the power that changes circumstances. Since war belongs to God, when we exalt Him, the enemy falls.
I will love thee, O LORD, my strength.
I hope you laugh as you walk through the wilderness. Only the Lord receives my deep tears and blood....

In prayer meetings, I encounter the Lord and see His face. And I receive comfort without judgment and condemnation. I give thanks. Seeing the beautiful face of the Lord, my face bows down. The Lord's two hands touch me and comfort my body and soul.
I love my Savior Jesus more deeply.
A leader is one who loves the Lord with all their life and loves souls with tears of compassion, desiring nothing else.

Faith is yielding your place to the Lord in every impossible and barren situation. And delighting in Him eternally. This morning we cover our members with the precious blood. We bless you in the name of the honored Lord.

David was excluded at the dinner with his family and honored guests, remaining outside with the sheep. Yet God, through Samuel, prevented anyone from proceeding until David came. Do not fall into rejection and discouragement; instead, use lonely times to become a worshiper who worships the Lord intimately. The breakthrough in life lies in intimacy with the Lord. Do not look to people. Breakthrough flows out of intimacy with God.
Come to a conclusion. Only the Lord becomes your conclusion.

This is my desire, to know as I am known, to love as I am loved. This is my ardent wish, to give my very life An offering of love, a living sacrifice. This is my reward To hear You say, '...well done'
What the Lord seeks in leaders is authenticity and perseverance. Though time passes, serve with your first heart.

The Lord drives those who do not pray into situations where they must pray, and those who do not seek the Lord are locked in wells where they can only behold Him. The best position is to be moved with awe and to revere Him where I now stand.

* A unique praise never before heard in the world - 'From Sin Set Free.' Please listen to this praise repeatedly and let it soak into you. And through cell groups, please sing this praise as well. Please pray that the power of the precious blood covers Africa, Asia, and Europe.

In 2007, when Samsung and LG were eagerly pursuing Nokia by building specs and technology, Apple changed direction and charged ahead. Those who understand life through speed are always chasing, panicking, and comparing.
Like Jesus who walked the path of death in silence, speak in complete silence.

Martha was troubled while doing the Lord's work and even gave instructions to Jesus. The moment came when she sought to accomplish her own will for the sake of the Lord's work. Jesus, knowing Martha's heart well, said to her, 'You are worried and upset about many things, but you have no joy in your heart.' Jesus is the Lord in every situation. If Martha had asked Jesus for help, I am sure the Jesus I know would have kindly put on an apron and helped her. Though He was a carpenter, He would have taken responsibility for that kitchen and worked while never ceasing to teach so that all could hear.
You raise me up!
When the church says 'I love you' in the Lord's name, it is not a nice greeting. It is loving with the heart of Christ.
Standing in the place where I am positioned as the Lord desires and confessing and acknowledging that the Lord is the owner of my work—this is the worship that pleases God most.

He is the God of relationship. Love is not just making time; it is spending that time together. In the end times, many Marthas will fall. Seek balance. Do not forget Mary, who was always sitting at the Lord's feet.
What is important in faith is not becoming familiar. Love for the Lord must never become familiar; it must always be new. Otherwise, from that point on, it becomes business.
I give my life for the Lord; what more would I withhold?
When you long to worship earnestly and long to fall on your knees, even if that place is a mud pile, the person who can prostrate before the Lord in the finest formal wear or dress is beautiful.
Christians who have eaten the flesh of Christ and drunk the blood of Christ no longer focus on themselves. For my flesh is the flesh of Christ and my blood is the blood of Christ.
Right now I am enjoying a cup of tea alone and watching a retreat video, immersed in grace. Everyone worships with real passion and whole heart.
Prayer meeting... I present myself before the Lord's altar.

A person who has done many things and has much experience does not necessarily become a great leader. A leader is one who loves the Lord and is so very happy in Him. A person who is happy in the Lord is already conveying a great message through their life.
Whether going to an unfamiliar place or embarking on a journey, there is one thing you must do. That is to look earnestly toward the Lord. That is what God always desires.

Even if I play the wrong chord on the guitar, there is no great problem in worship. But if the rhythm is wrong, it is a trial for the player and torment for the listener. When the Lord pushes, we must run with Him. When the Lord pulls, we must stick closely with Him. Those who cannot do this suffer in their hands and feet and remain unchanged.
When I earnestly seek the Lord, the Lord meets us. Heavenly fire comes and burns our hearts and all hearts. Then the 'passion' that was dead comes back to life.

What I have done and how much I have done is not important. That is the standard of the world. What God desires is not numbers, popularity, or recognition, but that the Lord actually governs through me. Kneeling in my prayer closet may be no achievement in worldly terms, but if the Lord is ruling, then naturally the Lord will be revealed through me.
I do not think of it as deliberately preaching the gospel. Yet if I do not open my mouth, the stones will rise up and cry out. Amen, come quickly, Lord Jesus.
Do not try to solve the problem; instead, listen to the Lord's voice. When preoccupied with problems, you see only your own emotions and desires. Jesus becomes the solution to every problem.

That is why we need fellowship of faith, worship with His presence, and living gospel every day. Rather, we need them every moment. The devil challenges us daily. We cling to the Lord every moment. Forgetfulness of the gospel and grace is why the people who came out of Egypt perished.

What we must guard most carefully is becoming accustomed to serving God. Without a gap between the world and us, we must advance in tears, as when we first saw the Lord. And there we must pray that our worship be revived.

In 2 Timothy, I see the passion of the apostle Paul, whose life, once fading away, again burns as the fragrant offering poured out on the altar. I hope that we will offer our youth and last breath to exalt and love only the Lord. The Lord is worthy enough to receive it.
Faith is thinking of and beholding the Lord even in indescribable busyness and rushes that words cannot capture.

Personally, eating a three-hour dinner when invited to a nice place is far worse than eating instant noodles quickly and leading a three-hour prayer meeting. To pray, you must design your life. From the design and structure, the content and power of that life emerge. I hope young people will stake their lives on heavenly fire.
When I lay my dreams upon the Lord's altar, the Lord returns them to me as a chaste and selfless vision through the power of resurrection.

If something is too precious, it cannot be used. Porcelain from Wedgwood of England, teacups from the Tang Dynasty of China, and expensive tea vessels from Japan remain only on display shelves because they are too precious to use. Most of these things break and fall when moved by a careless junior. Then no matter how much you curse the junior, it is of no use. Serve with precious things. It is recorded in heaven.

The problem is not the absence of oil itself. The problem is the fuel lamp is broken though the gas station is far away. If there is a shortage, refill it. You will often be short. Never be arrogant without the oil of the Holy Spirit. Fill when you can.
The reason the church is the church and worship is worship is only through self-abandonment and the power of the Holy Spirit.
Without awe and wonder, you cannot be bold. Acting boldly without awe is tormenting those around you. Return before the Lord, remember Him, and be moved with wonder.
Gratitude: If there are no mistakes in speech, then one is truly a complete person. A person without mistakes in speech is at peace. Why? Because such a person is grateful. Gratitude and peace are the same thing. If you have lips that give thanks, you become a person who has truly achieved wholeness. Since we all make many mistakes, if there were one without mistakes in speech, he would be a perfect person, able to bridle his whole body. James 3:2
When I am lacking, it means I need the Lord more than the depth of my lack. That is why there is hope even in lack, and that is why I am grateful.

A person of the Lord can never be selfish by nature. Because the Gospel is entirely altruistic. Even when an apple tree bears apple fruit, it is not the tree that eats the apples. Others eat and rejoice. But the Master treasures that tree dearly and makes it ever more clean.

I will make time to hear the Lord's voice. I hope that as we intercede by groups and with a giving heart, we will bless the members. When I find myself living a faith in a place where such prayer requests become unnatural, I will deeply appreciate anew how special God's grace bestowed upon me truly is.

The Lord's loving-kindness is truly beautiful. So much so that it would be good to lay everything down... Your loving-kindness is better than life; therefore my lips will praise You. Therefore I will bless You as long as I live; I will lift up my hands in the name of the Lord. Psalm 63:3-4

Let us remain in the beautiful region that the Lord has appointed for us. There we experience the covered well and sealed spring. Let us not covet what He has not given, but give thanks for what He has bestowed.
Rather than seeking what the Lord has not given and suffering for it, I hope you will accomplish the Lord's will with what the Lord has given. Then He will add all these things to you.

Believe and accept that what the Lord has allowed is the best; and in what you have received, there is not only what is yours, but surely there is also a portion to be shared. Like a small life of five loaves and two fish, when placed in the Lord's hands, it will save many people.

When you take what the Lord has not given, you will be fearful and anxious. Because you know it will be taken again. When you possess what the Lord has given, you will have peace. Because no one can take away what belongs to the Lord. Not only give thanks for what He has given, but be moved with wonder at every moment.
Lord, I came with empty hands and will return with empty hands again. Though I possess nothing, Jesus is in my heart. Thank you, Lord.
Things did not turn out as I had prepared, but I was truly grateful to spend another Christmas with the people I love. It is grace without measure.
The evidence that we are 'grateful' now is 'victory.' And the evidence that we have 'victory' is 'gratitude.'

That which I now lament not having, that which God did not permit—if I had possessed it, I would have been closer to sin and farther from God. Rather than lamenting what I did not possess, be grateful that because I did not, I received the Lord as a gift.

The people given to me now, the work I serve now—these are the very best things given to me at this moment. God always gives the very best. To realize this is mission, and those who live without mission live the most unhappy lives when they should live the happiest. And God saw everything that He had made, and behold, it was very good. Genesis 1:31

Until now, despite my unworthiness, the fact that I have been allowed to proclaim the Lord's name is because of the Lord's burning compassion. If tomorrow I am also allowed to proclaim that exalted name, that too will be because of the Lord's endless grace.
All that I have lived through until now is by the Lord's great grace. How can I express in words the Lord's boundless love? I can only bow my head as the chief of sinners and shed tears.

I give thanks for the presence and spirit of worship that You allowed during these three nights and four days. What I earnestly desire now that I have returned is the Lord's greater presence. Not that I have already attained or have already become perfect, but I press on to lay hold of that for which Christ Jesus has laid hold of me. Philippians 3:12
In faith, we bless our friends. I believe I have such a blessing. Because God loves us and the young people need it. If needed, He will pour it out.

True blessing is what the Lord gives. When the Lord gives, it means there is a measure and a scope that the Lord gives. If you think you have what you do not have and cannot have, you become anxious and tormented. That is covetousness. Please reject what the Lord has not given you. And rather, boast in your deficiency and weakness. He said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for My power is made perfect in weakness.' 2 Corinthians 12:9

When you rent a car, you must fill it up as much as you used it. Of course, there is no need to add more, so you fill it to just right. But with your own car, you simply fill it to full. You are not going to drive it for just a day or two, are you? Your soul must be filled to overflowing with grace. After all, we go through life only once...
When you praise, there is joy; when you are grateful, there is peace. This simplicity is the truth.

I hope you will regard what you have as sufficient. It is not a matter for complaint but for thanksgiving. In the world there is what belongs to Caesar and what belongs to God. But what belongs to Caesar also belongs to God and is only temporarily entrusted. All is the grace of the Lord.
You must offer the first prayer. You must look upon the cross and give thanks. And you must pray for a heart to pray. Prayer is not done as you wish.

Though it may seem shabby and shabby, if the Lord does not give, there is nothing received. Therefore, we must use all that we have received to the fullest. In Him who gives strength, we can do all things.
They say praise makes even whales dance, don't they? Love makes flowers bloom even on dry thorns and dead trees. Do not stop at a blossoming life, but become a life that makes things bloom.

This peace that wells up in my heart, buried deep, is a precious treasure. Who will come and dig out my treasure? I am assured. Peace, peace descends from heaven above. May the waves of that love cover my soul forever. Hymn 469
After Saturday's meeting, one member said with relish, 'This is nice~' It is good and glorious to me that the Lord's grace has been allowed.

Do not panic, but give thanks. You will enjoy peace in the midst of anxiety. Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. Philippians 4:6
God, I desire that even one more time, the extremely small one may pour out everything through Your grace.
God meets my needs. But He does not satisfy my vanity. Regarding what you have as sufficient is the secret to everything.
A people who do not know gratitude will not be destroyed. They do not have to make an effort to be destroyed. Because the greatest thing is that they are already destroyed.
God has given what is small but already precious. If you cannot be satisfied with what is yours, you become depressed or angry. Eventually you fall into sin and suffer great shame.

God's work cannot be done by position, years of faith, or background. Just as animals live by instinct and beasts survive by wildness, Christians live by the spirituality of gratitude and wonder alone.
God has given. He has lent. When you mistake temporary 'right of use' for eternal 'right of ownership,' you become miserable immediately. Ownership causes comparison; use brings wisdom.

If God does not give, there is nothing received. (John 3:27) The Lord always gives the best and good things. Because He is good. Therefore, the people who belong to me and this work are undoubtedly the highest option from heaven.
I am so grateful that God is God. My wish is to love more.

Every single moment is important. Because it is life. When we are together, people are not glorified and Jesus is the protagonist. Because we meet and share life, and because these are truly precious and valuable times that cannot come again, we must be even more grateful.

When David sinned, what God lamented was that 'I have already given enough' and 'if you needed more, I would have given more.' To take what the Lord has not given by human means is rebellion. Do not rebel, but repent and give thanks.
When I came to Korea, I visited the rock where I prayed seeking wisdom in my youth. True wisdom is not acquiring what you do not have, but rejoicing in what you do have.
Thank you for praying for Han-seok-i. He has improved remarkably and we give glory with the word 'miracle.' I am truly grateful. The Lord is wonderful.

I bless the members and leaders who were together. It was a demanding 14-hour gathering, yet thank you for following without complaint. I especially give glory to the Lord for being with us through prayer and talk.
Happy birthday! Enjoy a joyful time with your family~~

Everyone seeks peace, but sometimes uncomfortable feelings arise. This is truly something to be grateful for. When a signal comes, I can look back at myself and see where my focus went wrong. Any focus not aligned with the Lord must vanish entirely.

Building the tabernacle in a barren desert is nearly miraculous. The only materials were various possessions received from the Egyptians in the land of Egypt. What I have been allowed to acquire, what I have been allowed to take—these are not to be satisfied with possession and acquisition, but are given to accomplish 'the Lord's purpose.' Yet more important than the material given is the attitude and heart of the giver.
Any grace I perceive and any love I receive is given only within the Lord's permission. Therefore, I am moved with wonder and gratitude every day.

People say they are too troubled to be grateful. No. Because you are not grateful, there are so many troubles. I desire that you present what you lack and what is deficient with thanksgiving. The most powerful prayer is gratitude that brings things into reality.

You must nail to the cross the question of how I came to be myself. The truth and conclusion of this theme is that it was accomplished by God's grace. Do not take it out again to argue, but every moment should be so moved with wonder that gratitude is insufficient. Foolish people forget grace and compare. Do not judge yourself by comparison, but worship in gratitude.

When I cannot keep myself, I become bold instead. Because I have been freed from worrying about what can be resolved by worry. Do not worry, but entrust yourself with thanksgiving. That is the intent of the Almighty.
Holy_Spirit_and_Anointing: Breaking through fear is not about the problem being too big, but about the absence of God's presence. Worship in His presence breaks through fear.

Never, never bury it. When I justify my wounds and selfish desires, the Holy Spirit surely gives me an uneasy heart. Only a very few people do not overlook the conviction of the Holy Spirit, and instead expose and repent of packaged motives that attempt to deceive even myself and God, and turn away from manipulative schemes. Wounds, selfish desires, and self-defense can all easily become the names of love, loyalty, and justice. The glory of transformation and revival is not common. It is not the legitimacy of words and actions, but the presence of contrition that reveals hidden fears, anger, and attachments that is needed. Walk the narrow path without carelessly burying things. Be thorough about what your motives are. Rather than clumsily blocking the heavens, confess to God who examines the heart. All a man's ways seem right to him, but the Lord examines the heart. -Proverbs 16:2
* In reverence: The Holy Spirit is not a God of convenience, but a God who brings peace.

* An important factor: I do not know whether good things will come or difficulties will arise, but what is important is that at least I face them in the fullness of the Holy Spirit. He is the defender of the weak, comfort those in need. Being with the Lord is power.

* The thoughts of the flesh will, without exception, surely kill me. If I live by the flesh, I will surely die, but if I follow the Holy Spirit, I will not gratify the desires of the flesh. Those who are led by the Spirit of God are God's children. Therefore, if you pursue your own selfish desires, you will be empty and shameful, but if you hold fast to your calling, glory will come. I say, then: Live by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh. -Galatians 5:16 Those who are led by the Spirit of God are the sons of God. -Romans 8:14

* Be filled before you act - The power of decision. The Acts is also called the Acts of the Holy Spirit. If we decide and act based only on emotion and logic, we will be ashamed. We must be filled before we act.

* Redeem the time. We desire to redeem the time, but we do not have such ability. The only way for people to redeem the time is to receive the fullness of the Holy Spirit. When we follow the Holy Spirit, we redeem the time and live a life of victory. So be very careful, then, how you live—not as unwise but as wise, making the most of every opportunity, because the days are evil. -Ephesians 5:15-16

* The golden window. The 'golden window' refers to the critical time when the survival and prognosis of severely injured emergency patients with damage to the circulatory and cardiopulmonary systems is determined. In this era, for 10 years, the generation of young people with different cultures has been rapidly leaving the church. If we miss this last timing now, we will lose a generation, and the decline will accelerate rapidly, which will impact the church, families, and society entirely. When there is an opportunity to take responsibility, if we listen to the voice of the Holy Spirit with the authority given to us and obey, we can not only save all, but also reclaim and bring them back.

* A clear reason: Making people complete is God's inspired Word, and building community and bringing benefit is the work and gifts poured out according to the Holy Spirit's purpose. We must discover the clear reason why people are not becoming complete and communities are not being built. If the cause does not change, no matter how much effort is made, the result will not change.
* Faith initiative: Faith begins from responding to the prompting of the Holy Spirit. When you hear His voice today, do not harden your heart.
* Both wash and burn. It is the Holy Spirit who makes our thoughts clean. He both washes and burns.

* Gifts: A typical misconception about gifts is confusing gifts with God's love. Since gifts strengthen the function of serving the community, they bear abundant fruit when we establish right relationships with believers and unite together.

* How to avoid mistakes: A drunk person should never take the steering wheel. If you are not filled with the Holy Spirit, you should not make decisions. You must not endure doing anything without being filled with the Holy Spirit. It only leaves wounds.

* Gospel witnessing is not difficult. It is impossible. Gospel witnessing is not difficult. It is impossible. It is not an area I can do with my own strength and wisdom. It corresponds to a revelation—doing what God told us to do, proclaiming what He told us to proclaim.

<Three Gratitude Relay - Second Day> 1. Christ lives within me, so I give thanks for all that happens to me. 2. I am grateful that through precious partners I see God's glory. 3. I am grateful for the presence and gathering that would make me happy even if today were the last day. On the second day of the gratitude relay that began with Esther Minji Chung's choice, I will tag Susan Yusun Kim and Koeun Sun. For the next 3 days, please share three gratitude themes and tag two people (you don't have to, though ^_^).

* Two beings who remind us: The Holy Spirit, the Comforter, reminds us of the true meaning of the grace we have received and the worship we have offered, but on the other hand, the evil spirit's role is to make it meaningless and to deny it. Do not be used by the deceiver but belong to the One who is truth. The Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you all things and will remind you of everything I have said to you. -John 14:26

* Respecting decisions: Because they take responsibility for their own decisions. Your decision determines your destiny. Therefore, decisions must be made in faith. You should not make decisions when you are not filled with the Holy Spirit or when you are angry.

* Continuous asking: When you continuously and repeatedly ask the Lord for what you 'previously asked for' and always seek, you become a person of the Holy Spirit. This means becoming a person who does not fear and loves all.

* Why the source sends water: It is not to water the pipes but to quench thirst through the pipes. If you pray for yourself, a small amount of power will appear, but when you seek to save souls, power will be poured out.

* What remains for the born-again believer: Being born again is being made by God's incorruptible Word and the power and ministry of the Holy Spirit. To the disciples who were already born again and had made the decision to die, Jesus commanded them to be baptized with the Holy Spirit. We must start with the confession that there is nothing without the Holy Spirit.

* How to overcome easily: It is the fullness of the spirit of worship. Then worship becomes moving. When worship comes alive, there is no need to force it, and you will experience 'naturally happening' works. When filled, you will become strong.

* Worship bleeding. When the spirit of worship is poured out, worshiping becomes the greatest joy. Also, to those who worship with all their heart, He pours out the spirit of worship. Therefore, a leader is one who, even where no one else worships, continuously worships with all their heart so that the spirit of worship is poured out.

* Do not delay, do it now. You must repent and receive the fullness of the Holy Spirit. When you cast away all wickedness and call upon the name of the Lord Jesus to receive the verdict of being sinless, you will receive the Holy Spirit as a gift, as with your name in the Bible. Do this right now.
* Peace: Peace does not come from me. It is power that descends from above. Therefore, peace is not calculation and reasoning but hearing the Lord's still, small voice.

* True prayer: We must acknowledge that without the help of the Holy Spirit, we cannot pray even one word with sincerity, and we must depend on the Holy Spirit. In the same way, the Spirit helps us in our weakness. We do not know what we ought to pray for, but the Spirit himself intercedes for us through wordless groans. -Romans 8:26
* To love the Lord, you must be completely filled with Jesus without any gaps. To love the Lord, you must be filled with the Holy Spirit.
* The greatest work of the Holy Spirit: Making us repent.

* If you do not change, you will not be changed. Merely nodding in agreement without changing is not faith. Apart from kneeling down, calling upon the name of the Lord, repenting, and receiving the fullness of the Holy Spirit, the idea of being captured by the world while receiving the Lord's guidance and grace is not only surprising but shocking.

* If you truly want to save lives, you must be filled with the Holy Spirit. Trying to save without being filled is not really about saving lives but merely a desire that it would be nice if they were saved.
* Grace that comes to the unqualified: The Holy Spirit comes upon the afflicted.
* Just as there is no soul in a body, if there is no grace in logic, all believers become fighters. They are neither smart nor beautiful, just zombie-like.
* Holiness and the Holy Spirit: We must be sanctified to receive the Holy Spirit, but we also need the grace of the Holy Spirit to be holy. Not by my strength, but only by the power of the Lord.

* The secret of receiving power: It is not feeling, judging, and acting by your own will, but acknowledging and upholding the Lord's sovereignty. If you live according to your own will, you are a person of the flesh; if you live according to God's will, you are a person of the Spirit.

* The blood: The only way to overcome sin and temptation is through the power of the blood. Without the blood, the Holy Spirit cannot come. The transformation that gives me songs of praise daily is also the power of the blood. Never overlook the basics. Professionals are always faithful to the basics.

* Conflict: The flesh continually demands peace and comfort. But the desire of the Spirit is to make me obedient and holy before the Lord. When these two desires conflict, what you choose reveals how you will live. The flesh desired worldly pleasures, but my spirit desired to pray with my face to the cold church floor.

* Why the Holy Spirit does not finish: Many people start with the Holy Spirit but finish with the flesh. Because they have no intention of obeying unto death. Jesus emptied Himself, took the form of a servant, and obeyed unto death on the cross. If you cannot die, you cannot accomplish.

* Why we move forward: If we cannot be bold, we cannot obey; if we cannot obey, we cannot move forward. Boldness comes when He is with us, and therefore the greatest sin is not being filled. Sin begins from there.

When we sin and turn away from God, two phenomena appear. The joy of salvation disappears, and the willing spirit is gone. (Psalm 51:12) This is because we grieve and quench the Holy Spirit. (Psalm 51:11) As a result, when we lose holiness, the Lord's presence disappears, and no matter how much ministry we do, no change occurs. (Psalm 51:13)

When change comes, pay attention. You must assess whether it is true change or corruption. When a cynical person is transformed to tears of emotion, it is the work of the Holy Spirit, and the opposite is the work of darkness. Regardless of the reason, if you move away from God, it approaches corruption.

The reason we go to the hospital is to have surgery and remove the root cause of a serious illness. Going to the hospital cafeteria, having tea with the doctor, and visiting other patients are not fundamental. We are those who must lie on the operating table as if dead and undergo major surgery to live. We must pass through the cross. In the church, we must continue the work of being washed by the blood and receiving the Holy Spirit.
Do not respond with your nature but respond with the Word. When you pause and receive the Word that contains God's will and desire, you will feel that there is almost nothing you cannot receive.

I see young people confessing that clinking glasses, dazzling lights, and intense beats no longer satisfy me. Congratulations. You have experienced God's glory. Now you will always be hungry and thirsty for everything else.

Do not be satisfied with the grace poured out, but desire the grace that wells up and overflows from within me. Do not live on grace received through others, but live with a heart that Jesus alone is sufficient. See you at the Saturday gathering.

Unbelief is also a kind of faith. It is having excessive trust in something negative and not true. All thoughts that do not come from the Lord must be cast away. When the Spirit of truth comes, true freedom comes.

Regarding events, the correct answer is that we have no control. However, we can control our own response. If we respond with the Holy Spirit, we are doing very well. Love, joy, peace... if we are addressing events and people with long-suffering and self-control, that is a perfect score. If you are responding with instinct and maintaining the emotional state decided immediately after the event occurs, it is not just a zero but a continuous zero. If students continue to get the same problem wrong on the next test, the teacher will continue to give that problem.

People live bound by cause and effect, causality. That is life according to general principles. But when truth and the Holy Spirit come, they overturn and break all these relationships, and we are led by the Holy Spirit regarding sin, righteousness, and judgment, fighting against darkness and moving forward.
When a person pours their soul into a work, a masterpiece is created. When the Spirit of God is poured into a person's body, it becomes a masterpiece of God's level. You are God's masterpiece.

Do not do ministry, but do love. The God we believe in is a God of relationship. Rather than doing well, I hope you become a person with the power of His abiding presence, a person who is strong in God's grace.

* It is not force, nor ability, that my efforts and will do not work out that becomes discouraging, but rather it is a great comfort and hope that only the Holy Spirit makes it happen. Those who seek to accomplish their own will will cling, but those who look to the grace of the Almighty will dwell at ease.

When you become a new creation, new desires arise. One of them is the desire to gather together. If you are a Christian, do you hate gathering? You need to be restored. By the bond of peace, maintain the unity the Spirit has made. Ephesians 4:3

Vitality is tested. Maintaining and strengthening the grace received at the retreat requires a price. 1. You must hold onto the Lord with your life on the line. The Lord strengthens those who fear Him. 2. You must bless and forgive. When you bless and pray for one another, the Holy Spirit is moved and present with you.

The weapons of the Holy Spirit include honesty, humility, truthfulness, grace, love, prayer, obedience, community, healthy dependence, God's Word, and following the Holy Spirit's guidance. On the other hand, the weapons of the flesh include excessive anger, deception, accusation, shame, hatred, pride, concealment, self-centeredness, defensiveness, and unhealthy attitudes like 'I don't need you.' The spiritual warfare we are fighting is not 'a half-day sporting event where we go out for a brief moment, win easily, and quickly forget. This fight is a ground war—a matter of life and death.' (Ephesians 6:12, The Message)

When you miss the Holy Spirit's guidance, the characteristic is becoming defensive. Because you acted according to your own desires, you have lost freedom. Find what came before the Holy Spirit—that thing, that event, that person, that desire. Even a driver's license test tends to be failed on similar courses.

When the Holy Spirit comes, He gives us deeper knowledge of Jesus and enables us to testify about Him to the world. The true work of the Holy Spirit leads us to deep knowledge and understanding of Jesus.
When not filled with the Holy Spirit, you are moved by arbitrary interpretation. Typically, you see small things as large and stake your life on what is unimportant.

The world's and people's excessive reactions are not my domain. That is not my concern. These problems are solved when you are filled with the Holy Spirit and receive power. 'It is not for you to know the times or dates the Father has set by his own authority. But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you; and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria...' (Acts 1:7-8)

People think they can open their hearts a little to the world and immediately close it if needed, and they enjoy the thrill. The moment you think this way, you are already enslaved to the devil. This is the devil's typical method. Scripture, particularly regarding sexual immorality, addresses it not merely as a self-control or ethical issue but as a matter of evil spiritual forces working behind the scenes. Sexual immorality is the devil's work. I remember an elderly pastor who had ministered for many years saying that once addicted to sexual immorality, it is difficult to break free, and breaking free requires staking your life on it. I believe this: even with the determination to 'stake your life' it is difficult. Only through 'the law of the Spirit' is it possible (Romans 8:1-4). And I want to testify: if you fully rely on the Holy Spirit, this is a war you can definitely overcome. (Be courageous.) Two things must be remembered: 1. Abandon the vague reasoning that the sinful tendencies now occurring will be tolerated. Sexual immorality is a serious crime and you will inevitably pay a price beyond imagination. You cannot serve the Lord and this at the same time (John 5:30). 2. Guilt invites more sin. Do not trust your own will; believe in 'the law of the Spirit.' 'The blood of the Lord' certainly has power. One clear fact is that 'sexual immorality' is like carrying fire in your chest that must be immediately cast away, and while your will and determination cannot do it, 'the Spirit's fire' can abundantly grant victory. You must believe this.

* Do you think I made a mistake? You grumble and complain against a member of the body who receives the same Holy Spirit in the same worship as me and praises one Lord. There is a reason on your part. At such times, the Lord asks you this: 'Do you think I made a mistake?' Before losing something great, hold onto something small. The love the Lord requires is not easy, but it is not impossible either.

Faith is not finished with one home run. You advance from day to day between choices. If you choose and live following the Holy Spirit, you will not realize the desires of the flesh or the anxiety from vain loss.
If the gospel of the cross is testified and the Holy Spirit can come, whether living or dying, for the glory of the Lord.

A person who has passed through the cross will seek the Holy Spirit. They understand by faith that they have died, but the gap between reality and God's Word is too great. Being filled with the Holy Spirit begins from longing.
You must overcome evil. You must overcome it with good. It is the goodness that the Holy Spirit, dwelling within us, provides.

* No matter how strong and organized the gospel's opponents are, and no matter how difficult our circumstances are relatively and how dark the prospects appear, the gospel has never once been defeated. The Lord's Spirit has come upon me, therefore He has anointed me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim freedom for the prisoners and recovery of sight for the blind, to set the oppressed free, to proclaim the year of the Lord's favor. (Luke 4:18-19)

A change in the orientation of affection is not about forcefully living by the law. Enduring and enjoying are fundamentally different. Giving reluctantly and giving with joy are different. Struggling to forgive and being in a state of hatred are fundamentally difficult things. Suppressing the impulse to sin and coming to hate sin and evil are different. It is not easy to distinguish between those who do things by force and those who do not. One tip: those who do things by force lack leisure and condemn others who do not meet their standards. Jesus never condemned anyone.
When things are difficult, if you endure, you will surely win. Foolishness is giving up a game you will surely win. Giving up is the devil's work; enduring is the Holy Spirit's work.

* The Spirit that gives life. From dust, by God's breath, humanity became a living being, but through sin, returns again to dust (the flesh). Yet when we hear God's voice, we come alive again and become people of the Holy Spirit.

Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. The truth demands this. It is impossible with only my conviction and dedication. That is why the Lord spoke this way in Acts 1: 'But when the Holy Spirit comes on you...'

Two ways to go to the neighbor's house: one is to walk in a straight line, the other is to turn around and go around the world. I will run to you Yes I will run the race until I see your face not by might, not by power, but the spirit of God.

Jesus did not come to improve attitudes or morals. He came to give us, who were helpless slaves of the devil because of the law of sin and death wielded by Satan, the ability to live by God's life. Because of the cross, the Holy Spirit came, and this Comforter gives God's life and the power to live by that life, so He is called the 'Spirit of life' and further 'the law of the Spirit of life' (Romans 8:2). If you dwell in the flesh, you cannot please God, but if you dwell in the Holy Spirit 'in Christ,' you live not by the old sinful life but by God's life and power. Faith is believing that.
Only through that Son can you go to the Father, and only through the Holy Spirit's influence can you believe in the Son. Not by might, nor by power.

You mistakenly think that happiness is effectively fulfilling many desires. That is not happiness but sin and death. True happiness is how free you are from sin, and that is the same as how successfully you have emptied yourself of desires. When the Holy Spirit came, in Acts, there was no one who said 'this is my possession.' Without the Holy Spirit's power, there is not even a 1% possibility of solving the problems we face.
The one working among us is God, so I too must work following the Holy Spirit. That is self-control and concentration. Then the desires of the flesh are rejected and God's will is executed.

We are called as light and salt to the world. Yet we serve as light and salt without light or name. There is an anointing that cannot be borne for those who have offered up their desires and temperament.

* What seems not to be sin but is a great sin. The most effective way to teach young people about prayer is to enter into the fire of prayer together. Also, when the Lord gives grace, He kindly leads a person who says they will die if they do not pray to a situation where they will seem to die if they do not pray. Prayer receives the Holy Spirit's power and strikes down the devil's work and makes difficult tasks easy. Ceasing prayer is sin.

Human will is desire. The Lord's will is mission. If you live as your heart desires, if you pursue what was not given to you, you will live that way. If you live as the Holy Spirit desires, you will live in gratitude for what was given to you.

When life does not go as planned, even if told not to, you will reflect on yourself. That is God's grace. You must break the cycle of making your own plans without the Holy Spirit and becoming angry when they do not work out. Without the Holy Spirit, I am nothing. Let us adventure with the Holy Spirit.

The stronger a person's self-management, the longer and harder the refining. The 'self-defense' reaction to protect becomes poison instead. Before the Holy Spirit, if you acknowledge and entrust, it is easy.

Only those despairing of themselves will seek the Holy Spirit. In the gap between the cross and their own condition, they experience it and come before the Lord crying out. It is Saturday. Let us gather at 6 o'clock to pray.
I believe in Almighty God and in His only Son Jesus Christ. I believe in the Holy Spirit and in the forgiveness of sins and eternal life.

Become a person of faith in all seasons and conditions. Regardless of spiritual adversity, looking only to the Lord, indifferent to worldly glory, relationships and reactions, circumstances and environment—faith with the ability to ignore. A person whom the devil tries to depress but who melts the dark world with love from a difficult position with poor posture. You who love the Lord are the solution of this age. Acts 13:2 'While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, 'Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.'
When young, there was one unceasing petition: It did not matter where the Lord sent me, but I did not want to go like a dry stick. I wanted the Lord's anointing. That was the only condition.
Do you truly want to speak and do God's Word? Then change your time. One sent from God speaks God's Word; and God gives the Spirit without limit. (John 3:34)

The disciples betrayed and fled from Jesus not because they were 'evil' but because they were 'weak.' Being not filled with the Holy Spirit, leaving the place of prayer and the place of grace, is already an extremely wicked sin. After Pentecost, Peter's boldness came not from advantage but from being filled.

When passing through the narrow gate, sometimes the gate becomes even narrower. Then you must lay down everything and hold only to what is essential. Fearing the Lord and resisting the devil—focus only on these two. In battle, you need the concentration to defeat one enemy. The Holy Spirit and we agreed that it was right to impose no further burden on you than these essentials. (Acts 15:28)
When you commit sin, Satan says to run away. But the Holy Spirit tells you to repent.

The secret to overcoming sin. 'And the child grew and became strong; he was filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was on him.' (Luke 2:40) The grace of God was upon Jesus! What happens when God's grace is poured out upon a person? The secret is in Romans 6:14: 'For sin shall no longer be your master, because you are not under the law, but under grace.'

Even if you gather all sin and sorrow, anger and hatred and burn them away, you only manage your expression; bondage does not automatically break. Even if you change your environment, due to 'the law of conservation of problems,' conflict in life continues. Become a servant of Jesus, the only Truth, and you will truly be free. Who will carelessly touch a servant seeking the joy of Jesus? Where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom, and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.

If sin and repentance keep repeating, you are always walking in place. If you walk following the Holy Spirit, you will not fulfill the desires of the flesh. The Holy Spirit does not permit it and gives power to overcome and rule. Receive the fullness of the Holy Spirit and the power of self-control.
The ability to come before the Lord and worship in the temple is only by relying on God's love. Worship without love is only empty. (Psalm 5:7)

Following the Lord's will is not playing a probability game with the expectation of desire. It is to pray and quietly seek the Lord's guidance. If you walk following the Holy Spirit, you will not fulfill the desires of the flesh.
Proclaiming the Lord's gospel is not a difficult task. It is an impossible task. Without the Lord's guidance and help, it is impossible from the beginning.
The Lord's great comfort is that He speaks to us and grants us His presence. These two things—His voice and presence—if you have them, you have everything.

It is true to lift both hands and praise with the joy the Lord gives, but if you truly exalt the Lord with all your heart, He gives immeasurable joy. When the Holy Spirit comes, He gives joy, power, and a heart of obedience, but if you believe with faith that He will do so, obey and move forward, He also pours out abundantly.
