There is a saying 'the horse's vigor is amplified.' It means adding a whip to a running horse. Even after Saturday gathering ministry ends, the Lord's work continues. Among the friends who returned home, the Holy Spirit continues to touch, and in their words and thoughts He comes, so we pray more and prostrate ourselves more. The Lord continues to work now, and the servant sees it and runs harder.

The wind of the Holy Spirit blows upon those who long for the Lord's temple. The wind of the Holy Spirit enables prayer I cannot pray, repentance I cannot repent, forgiveness I cannot forgive, and worship I cannot offer. Only by the Holy Spirit, not by might or power...

Sunday Youth Group Message: Self-esteem—not how I see myself, not how others see me, but self-esteem comes from how God sees 'me.' 1. Be simple. (Know what you are doing, concentration, purpose requires courage to pursue value)—Do not aim for MC Kang Ho-dong and enter into wrestling. Stop the glamour and experience the power of simplicity. A flowery life withers. A life conscious of others—someone who spends two hours doing makeup to come to church, a person using 90% of their ability to prove themselves—has no vision. Self-control is the power of the Holy Spirit.

When called to intercede, the Lord pours out a heart of compassion. Because prayer is done with a father's heart. God's heart toward us, weak and insufficient, is 'compassion.' An intercessor is one who shares a heart with God. That is why they must be a person who loves. Burning love...

Do it immediately. Whatever God prompts, do it immediately. Otherwise, you will quench the Holy Spirit and ultimately be defeated. Pray immediately. Read God's Word immediately. Evangelize immediately. Forgive immediately. And always look to the Lord.
It is time to begin our Saturday gathering, open to all. We long for the Lord's anointing. There is nothing but the Lord.

Though problems and wandering persist, so do the work of the Holy Spirit and our maturation. Do not panic at what cannot be done, but give thanks for what is being poured out. Rain and wind do not stop the KTX; it speeds through and continues forward. We will never stop before problems and challenges. We solve while running and proclaim the gospel while running.

The fire of God's Word and the fire of the Holy Spirit cause a person to focus. To focus means to be able to let go. Only those who carry fire can boldly let go. Regardless of how long something has been prepared or how precious it is to me, before life and the Lord's glory, it becomes like chaff that can be cast away. We cannot swallow life without spitting out what is rotten.

In prayer, the Holy Spirit offers two kinds of help: one is to give us the strength to pray and assurance of answer through peace when we pray; the other is to redirect our prayer through unease and the feeling that we will not receive an answer. Ultimately, we must rely on the warm Holy Spirit who leads us from uncomfortable prayer to peaceful prayer.

1 Timothy 4:5 says we are sanctified through the word and prayer. A life with nothing to throw away, a life received with thanksgiving—this comes from the work of sanctification unfolded through God's Word and prayer. Hold fast to God's Word and commit yourselves to prayer.
Do not testify of yourself, but testify of the Lord. God anoints you and is with you.

We are still thirsty, pour your spirit out Lord. Do not be satisfied at this level. God prepares greater clouds and rain. What I, as a minister, now intercede for is not a prayer for God to use us in the European continent, but that in the midst of our youth being refined through praise before God who is already using us, we remain humble and give glory to the Lord to the end. I pray that our youth will become those who hold fast to Jesus alone and seek the Lord's joy. They will become magnificent young people who remove their crowns and lay them before the Lord.

Regarding the gifts, order, and immorality facing the Corinthian church, Apostle Paul speaks of our dim lives as if looking in a mirror. When we meet the Lord face to face, everything becomes clear, and we know as the Lord knows. Look only to the Lord. Then the Lord will give you the spirit of wisdom and revelation, and will give you compassion, forgiveness, and unity.

In Matthew 7 and Luke 11, Jesus commonly speaks of praying and asking. However, Luke contains a verse absent in Matthew: God will give to those who ask. But doesn't it say, 'How much more will the Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask Him?' God can give all things and will give them. But what He truly desired to give was 'Himself'—the love of giving Himself. I pray that you become young people who grow strong in that love.

Though you resolve and turn to the Lord, soon you gaze at the world, fear weighs your heart, and peace is lost. Hold to the Lord's Word. The Spirit of truth, the Holy Spirit, sets you free. 'Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.' 2 Corinthians 3:17

When you receive grace while praying and joyfully ask repeatedly, you receive answers. Conversely, if your heart grows heavy while praying and you give up after trying a few times, you receive almost no answer. Selfish prayers for personal gain cannot be made with the joyful, repeated anointing of prayer. Abandon prayers of the soul and offer prayers of the spirit.

The one who proclaims God's Word is a sinner, and those who hear the Word are also sinners. Though all are sinners, God said that when He washes us in the blood and anoints us, the presence like Jesus appears, and He will do greater works. Rely today on the cleansing of the precious blood and the sealing of the Holy Spirit.

Are you frustrated and disappointed? When I have something urgent to resolve but cannot resolve it, first I am frustrated, and then disappointed in God. Paul said the Holy Spirit comes and causes us to call God 'Abba.' Beyond complex understanding, the most important request is to call 'Abba' and run into His arms. Then you experience God who comes to find you.

For me to be nailed, I must desire that the Word become a sword and pierce within me. Being pierced is anguishing and painful, but only in that piercing, from the blood flowing from it, comes new comfort poured out.
Because God gives us the Holy Spirit and the Holy Spirit fills our hearts with God's love, we can feel this warm love anywhere, in our hearts. Romans 5:5
Right now I am alone with a cup of tea and watching retreat videos, being immersed in grace. Everyone truly sings with passion and whole heart...
The day has brightened. Pour out grace and power. Hallelujah.
If I earnestly seek the Lord, He meets us. The fire from heaven comes and burns our hearts and all hearts. Then the 'passion' that was dead comes alive again.
The Holy Spirit comes to give us freedom where that deep sorrow becomes great joy, at the prayer meeting.

In relationships, conflict mostly stems from each other's weakness. If even one is touched by the Lord, there is no need for such collision. Our inner nature is distorted and wounded. Before the throne of grace, the Lord who bears our weakness, we must come so that our weakness does not wound our family and friends. The Holy Spirit also helps us in our weakness. Romans 8:26

The problem is not the absence of oil itself. The problem is that the gas station is far but the fuel lamp is broken. If short, fill it. You will often run short. Never be arrogant without the anointing oil of the Holy Spirit. You must fill when you can.

A businessman wanting to sell merchandise prepares enormous advertising and events, but has no actual product and only distributes brochures and product information—that is fraud. Give what you promised. Working for the Lord without being filled with the Holy Spirit becomes the greatest fraud.
The reason the church is a church and worship is worship is nothing but self-denial and the power of the Holy Spirit.
Sovereignty: * Faith - To live by faith means to live with decision. Especially, it begins with believing in God's existence and character, so from my circumstances and my problems, I step out and address them with the Lord's eyes and the Lord's character. There should be only the Lord who redeemed me, and I should not exist.

* Reality - To small people living fiercely in reality, in the harsh reality and fierce competition, God called young men like Gideon the coward—who had no choice but to be cowardly to survive—'mighty man of valor.' The reason Gideon, who was anxious about work, relationships, and the future, can be a mighty man of valor is one thing only: because God desired to be with him. Judges 6:12 - 'And the angel of the Lord appeared unto him, and said unto him, The Lord is with thee, thou mighty man of valour.'

* How to Break the Rule - Rules must be kept, but there are times when they are broken to save life. When rules must be broken to save a life, it is done with life at stake to save life. 'Go, gather together all the Jews that are present in Shushan, and fast ye for me, and neither eat nor drink three days, night or day: I also and my maidens will fast likewise; and so will I go in unto the king, which is not according to the law: and if I perish, I perish.' - Esther 4:16
* Fear Not - People fear that they will become weak. But they do not know that when they become weak is the only time the Lord works and makes them strong.

* The Reason for Sin - Sin means missing the mark, deviating from the purpose. It is a state where there is a problem with the purpose of making God King, the Lordship. That is why sin always intensifies from excessive desire for success and the desire for self-realization disguised as ministry.

* The Condition for Happiness - You must stop defining your happiness by another's reaction. Furthermore, you must stop imagining based on that reaction. To do so is neither truthful fact nor God's intention.

* Believe in the Lord - Believe in the Lord. If you have faith, you can obey. Before His sovereignty, which is wiser than my decisions and wiser than my thoughts, I will entrust everything and dwell in peace.

* The Problem of Relationships - What is absolutely necessary in the problem of relationships is margin, and that margin comes when you know, experience, and taste God. Meditating on His character, His ability, and His works. 'O taste and see that the Lord is good: blessed is the man that trusteth in him. O fear the Lord, ye his saints: for there is no want to them that fear him. The young lions do lack, and suffer hunger: but they that seek the Lord shall not want any good thing.' - Psalm 34:8-10
* Looking Back - Looking back, you will realize that everything you took for granted was grace.

* Butterfly Effect - Saul's falling into the senselessness of envying David was because he did not obey completely in the war against Amalek, and Saul's incomplete obedience originated from the foolish act of sacrificing on his own without waiting for Samuel. Present choices are closely related to future choices.
* God sent me here, He will send me there. But don't feel badly, don't blame yourselves for selling me. God was behind it. God sent me here ahead of you to save lives - Genesis 45:5

* Last Christmas - At that time when the Lord called me, why in that way, why by that method..., and why did He make me walk such a thorny path..., though I cannot understand, still God is good, truly good, and faithful. The happiest moment in my life was when God made me weep..., and that God seeks me again. The power of the Lord's blood is sufficient. - May 2007
* Do Not Fear What You Will Lose - The Lord both gives and takes away, but all this is so that you gain eternal things eternally. That is why my soul is safe in the Lord.

* Freedom of Expression - On the surface, it may seem that people are suffering, the situation is evil, and luck is bad, but the true and genuine meaning is that God's hand is working and intervening.

* The Anxiety of Seeking Recognition, The Joy of Being Used - If you seek recognition, you will be afraid and chased. But if you are content to be used by Him, you will have peace and completeness. What matters is not the size of the position or the result, but the One who wants to use me. Let the Lord use you. - Matthew 21:3

* How to Live Out Your Destiny - This situation I am experiencing now is something the Lord knew long ago. If this is the life He has granted me, it is our part to live it faithfully and holily. We do not avoid suffering nor turn away from the joy He gives, but we respond in faith and move forward. 'Thine eyes did see my substance, yet being unperfect; and in thy book all my members were written, which in continuance were fashioned, when as yet there was none of them.' - Psalm 139:16
* Be Bold - Not that the situation is fine, but that 'God is with you.'

* The Greatest Discipline - I once saw someone who had been doing ministry so excellently put on trial. Through the mouths and bodies of believers, curses and violence abounded, and police were dispatched, yet the victim shed tears while the perpetrator denies having caused harm. Among disciplines, the greatest discipline is to be abandoned. How shameful it is, how fearful it is, what is happening now to the Kingdom of God and the Korean church, it is truly fearful that greed masquerades as calling.
* It is the Lord who causes us to pray, and it is the Lord who causes us to worship, and it is the Lord who created us, and it is the Lord who sends us, and it is the Lord who calls us.

* The Reason for Disappointment - What I thought existed no longer exists. But it was never with me from the beginning. When I know the greatness of the Lord and realize my smallness, I escape disappointment and remain at ease without fear.
* Understanding Reality - 'Let their God Jehovah be served. Does not the king still know that Egypt is ruined?' - Exodus 10:7

* Blessing and Curse - When revelation is heard, people live, but when revelation ceases, people quickly become corrupt. The gospel person and the proclamation of the gospel are blessing. In the days of Zechariah who clearly knew God's revelation, he sought God, and while he sought the Lord, God prospered him. 2 Chronicles 26:5

* To Discouraged Friends - In my youth, even though I worked hard and did my best, when the result betrayed me, I became discouraged, powerless, and lost motivation. At that time I heard God's voice to me: 'It was not in vain. There is much prepared for you.' - May 1990, Busan. God never makes a mistake.
* Not My Will But God's Perfect Will Be Done.
* Have Courage - The precious co-worker and mentor, the training and service given to me are permitted by God. The Lord's guidance is without error, and His intention toward me is peace.
* Do Not Be Discouraged - Discouragement is like an umbrella in front of a typhoon of self-protection. What God desires is the Lord's perspective and looking upon the Lord. Be bold.

* Empty-Handed Prayer - Even if I have nothing, the Lord is before me, and though I know nothing, the Lord knows all. When I am discouraged, the Lord's good hand is with me, and when I do not know where to go, the Lord becomes the way.
* My Will, God's Will 2 - At first, I struggle to abandon my will and choose God's will. Later, faith is when my will becomes one with God's will.

* My Will, God's Will - It is commendable to break my will and follow God's will, but you will still find yourself stumbling. Faith is not so much about me breaking my will but about yielding my place so that the Lord turns my will. That is why spiritual maturity is not about managing my will through law but about becoming aligned with the Lord's will through grace.
* His Guidance - God makes a way in the wilderness where I stand that I could not have anticipated.
* Earnest Prayer - Rather than doing what I want to do, cause me to think what the Lord desires.
* Comfort - I know nothing, but the Lord knows all things.

* It is not the absence of communication but the absence of calling. The person responsible avoids decision-making and does not take responsibility, while the person who should not make decisions insists on making them, and thus confusion arises. First clarify your calling. Then all else will fall into order.
* Believe and Expect God's Work - When God stirs the teacup, a spiritual tsunami arises in us. May you enjoy peace in the Lord's work.
* If You Boast, You Will Lose - If you boast, even what you have will be taken away, and if you give thanks, even what was lacking will be added.
* What I Must Affirm - It is foolish for me to try to affirm my future. I affirm my faith toward Him, and God affirms my entire life.
* How to Secure Safety - Rather than getting a guarantee from people, it is to lay everything before God and entrust it to Him.

* Spiritual Matrix - Reformed since AD 30 every 500 years. Experience - Truth = Mysticism. Greed + Law = Authoritarianism. Greed + Comparison = Jealousy, Envy. Faith - Grace = Legalism. Faith - Belief = Humanism. Example + Influence = Change. Instruction + Management = Labor. Company = Management + Investment. Church = Spirit + Sacrifice. Bank = Money + Time. Church = Jesus + Time. Tear down and rebuild. - John 2:19

* True Rest Comes from True Repentance - True rest comes from true repentance. Even if you change time, place, and environment for the sake of rest, if you do not forsake sin and draw near to God, you can never enjoy true peaceful rest. 'For thus saith the Lord God, the Holy One of Israel; In returning and rest shall ye be saved; in quietness and in confidence shall be your strength: and ye would not.' - Isaiah 30:15

* The Secret of Victory - It is not hiding greed and beautifying it with pretext, but laying bare weakness and holding to the promise. 'Now there was long war between the house of Saul and the house of David: but David waxed stronger and stronger, and the house of Saul waxed weaker and weaker.' - 2 Samuel 3:1

* The Reason for Boldness - There is no self-realization that I clearly long for or must achieve, so there is nothing to fear losing. The Lord's grace has always been sufficient, and it will be sufficient in the future as well.
* When I Do Not Know Where to Go - When I do not know where to go, I become grateful. This is evidence that He knows my path, calls me, and guides me.

* Driver's Diner vs. Hospital Food - The difference between the hospital choosing the meal and the driver choosing it creates differences in taste, price, and speed. If I decide, I will fall into corruption, but if God decides, it will be holy.
* It Is Not Bad Luck - It is not bad luck but the Lord's love. He intervenes with affection, desiring that I be prepared and become more humble.
* God's Guidance - I do not need to be perfect to receive the perfect guidance of God. Since we are infinitely insufficient, the perfect One guides us abundantly.
* God Is the Reason - It is difficult because I try to see God through results. Results are a tool to humble me, and God is my reason.

* Do Not Be Swayed by Judgment - It is not in vain. I do not judge myself, so what is the significance of others' judgment? The only one who will judge me is the eternal Judge, God. 'But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged of you, or of man's judgment: yea, I judge not mine own self.' - 1 Corinthians 4:3
* Change Direction - Go not in the good direction that I think, but in the right direction that God desires.
* Stand at the Margins - Many want to stand at the center, but none want to linger at the edges. Yet the place of neglect and shadow is the Lord's place.
* The Reason for Giving Power - Rather than oppressing others with power, He desires that I rule myself with His Word.
* One reason to be silent: To acknowledge God as God and to accomplish God's will rather than human will.
* Control your desires. No matter how much desire tempts you, confess that ultimately only the Lord's will can be accomplished. Because I belong to the Lord.

* When you pray, He will work. If I work, only I will work, but if you pray, He will work. Fill the measure of prayer that is needed and requested. Then you will see the Lord's work in every area. Pray so that the Lord may bless you and your community.

* God uses the overwrite button rather than delete. God's work is not to remove or exempt suffering. Suffering simply remains, but He displays great glory and overwhelms suffering with glory. Romans 8:18 'I consider that our present sufferings are not worth comparing with the glory that will be revealed in us.'

* What can I do? Rather than asking what I can do, you must ask whether you are entrusting it to the Lord's hands. If God does not give, nothing is received, and if He does not permit, nothing is accomplished.
* Do not decide arbitrarily. I do not decide according to my own heart. Because I dislike arrogance. When the Lord decides and guides, He will take responsibility.

* What God does not do: How can anyone do what God does not do? Ministry is doing what the Lord has done and shown. Do not do what is good and desirable, but do the righteous work of the Father. The Son can do nothing by himself unless he sees the Father doing it. John 5:19

* Gospel witness is not difficult. It is impossible. Gospel testimony is not difficult. It is impossible. It is not an area you can accomplish with your own strength and wisdom. It corresponds to revelation—proclaiming what God commanded you to proclaim.

* Why doesn't He give cash? Good promises seem to be given, but they cannot be immediately converted to cash. What He truly wants to give is God Himself, and that is the cross. After these things the word of the Lord came to Abram in a vision, saying, 'Do not fear, Abram. I am your shield; your reward shall be very great.' Genesis 15:1

* Why does God permit live variety broadcasts of collapse? Because in that collapse, He wants you to see yourself and lift your eyes to look to the Lord. He desires that you truly experience the Lord in unspeakable anguish.

* Struggle and the cross: God does not struggle but rules through sovereignty. The pinnacle of His rule is the cross. Therefore, believers live by the cross and die by the cross. If you live that way and die that way, there is resurrection.
* God solves problems. With complete silence, focus on what you should thank for and what you should repent of. Correcting your attitude before God is the true solution.

* The believer's foundation: No matter how valid your own opinion is, respect God who accomplishes and completes the work. Singing in the storm because of God's plan greater than my thoughts is the foundational attitude.

* The rest God gives: 'You can do well without me. I am God and I rule.' The reason you do not starve even when you rest is not because of your labor—what you thought was your accomplishment was actually God's boundless grace. If you want genuine rest, you must have genuine faith.
* What you must remember: Remember the Lord's omnipotence. The Lord rules.
* Fear of loss: Worrying about losing things is unbelief. The tree of knowledge of good and evil, which suggests 'this was originally mine,' is at the starting line.
* Mistakes and wrong calls are part of the game. Mistakes and wrong calls are part of the game, but for those who love God who works all things together for good, there is hope and boldness.

* To the question 'What are you doing here now?': You need not be obsessed with influencing the world and people. Simply become intimate with God. That is the true purpose. Do not grieve over not being first; abide beside the Lord and become beautiful. You will become even stronger. O Lord, You have searched me and known me. You know when I sit down and when I rise up; You understand my thought from afar. You scrutinize my going out and my lying down, and are intimately acquainted with all my ways. Even before there is a word on my tongue, behold, O Lord, You know it all. Psalm 139:1-4
* The purpose of the wilderness is to humble me. So when I humble myself and obey before God and His authority, my problem is no longer a problem because the purpose is accomplished.

* What is truly important: If what you think is important always changes, it means it was never important. By worldly standards it may be dismissed, but what is eternal in that kingdom is what is truly important. Even simply immigrating to another country changes one's life.
* What God does: To rough, weak, and struggling me, what God says is 'I will use you.'
Time is in His Hand

* Spiritual fishing: Do not fish for circumstantial evidence when anxious. Take one puzzle piece and move forward even without understanding. The land the Lord gives is not land you have confirmed but land He will show. The Lord said to Abram, 'Go forth from your country, and from your relatives and from your father's house, to the land which I will show you.' Genesis 12:1

* Why teams fail in the World Cup: The quick way to do well in a short time is to change fitness and tactics. Doing this allows you to barely get through the qualifying rounds. What is truly important is that soccer is played with a soccer ball. If the ball doesn't stick to your body, you feel insecure and the watching people are always anxious. What is really frustrating is trying to find the cause in non-essentials when the loss comes from essentials. Grasping the essence requires long time and great cost (training). What makes foreign coaches (revival speakers) somewhat advantageous is less distraction in focusing on essentials and less time required. In spiritual terms, when unity between the ball (the Lord) and oneself is achieved, even when defense (temptation) is attached, composure (boldness) is created and the game is dominated (sovereignty). The essentials shown in Acts were the Holy Spirit and the Word, the cross and obedience. For emergency patients, the urgent priority is to minimize all unnecessary things and connect them with a specialist.

* He does not give until you let go. If He gives before you let go, you experience serious side effects. Means become the end. This makes you a person pursuing desire instead of pursuing God, and you constantly ride the roller coaster of pride and frustration.

* The reason I stand here: Because God has overcome me. O Lord, You have deceived me and I was deceived; You are stronger than I and have prevailed. I have become a laughingstock all day long; everyone mocks me. Jeremiah 20:7
* Do you think God is dragging His feet? God is not dragging His feet; He is slow to anger.

* Do not envy their exclusive league. The reason Jesus came to this barren land as an unimpressive branch is to bring life to the marginalized who cannot join 'their exclusive league.' True influence that breaks the gates of hell and rescues souls comes from renouncing rights and acknowledging sovereignty. It means take up the cross and go to the barren land.

* What becomes visible when you humble yourself: When you humble yourself and look, you will discover meaning in many things that seemed meaningless. Because when you humble yourself, you see the traces and touch of God's loving hand. So when you become low, you become far more glorious than when you are exalted.

* When you encounter a problem: That a problem is difficult is only when I try to solve it. The problem must be solved from heaven. I do not have the ability, responsibility, or choice to solve the problem. I only choose my response to the problem and present this to God in faith.

* A life that needs no encouragement (infinite power): You do not need to encourage a mother to love her child. Because it is life. If you are moved by encouragement and praise, you have not yet left the beginner stage. A person moved by life, not encouragement and slogans, and who serves faithfully is called a servant of the mission.

* Mission over success. Mission is a concept far transcending the matter of success. By worldly standards, a refugee Esther becoming queen is success and completion, but Esther grasps the mission from there and says 'if I perish, I perish' and goes forward. The question is not 'Is it difficult or easy?' but 'Is it God's will?'
* Look back. The fact that I came this far is because of the Lord's encouragement and help, and that encouragement was not because of my excellence but because of my weakness.

* When you are lonely, look to the Lord. 'Unattractive appearance and personality, manner of speaking will not be a problem, and neither will unremarkable humble background nor chronic weakness be a problem. Because I made you and will also make you new.'
* Always. I look only to the Lord who will do new things within me.
* Ministry: stops it, changes its direction, and makes it run toward that eternal goal.
* Not opening but being opened: It is not important that I open my life. It is important that the Father opens it.
* Wound handling: You get hurt trying to control what you cannot handle. Rather than trying to fix others' lives, the first step to recovery is to repent, humble yourself, and seek the Lord's help.
* Never be afraid. I will guide you... The situation you fear is not the situation, but the plan I have prepared.
* I will make all things new. You do not become new simply by setting your determination and resolve. You become new when the Lord makes you new. That is why you must seek the Lord's face.
* Honest evaluation: The reason the Lord did great things is not 'because that was the case' but 'in spite of that.' So the more grace you receive, the more repentance flows out.
* Why you should not fear or be arrogant: Because the One who uses you is the Lord. Leave the place where you are not used; simply be faithful in the work the Lord uses you for.
* Terrible attack but glorious reversal. It is because the God we serve is alone worthy of honor and glory. Bring it on
* What I must always remind myself of: My relationship with the Lord, and the position of the Lord and my position.
* Guard this precious freedom: When I desire to be free according to my own heart, I lose precious freedom and protection. Eden was lost because of disobedience.

* When you want to cut the cord: When everything is bothersome and community is burdensome, it means you have been tempted, and the one tempting you is Satan. That cord you want to cut is ultimately Jesus. The God who pulls you back from the cliff with bloodied hands has never once considered giving up.

* How God works: God works through people. Therefore, through obedience to His guidance and Word, He reveals Himself. Then He sovereignly works even toward those who do not obey His guidance and Word. Respond with obedience.

* Choices Without Regret If I sell something worth ten pounds for fifty pounds, the person introducing it should not be begging. Rather, it is showing favor. Throughout my life, I have never met anyone who regretted attending a retreat. That is because it is God's choice.

* The Lord is Greater Than the Continental Plan The Lord's work cannot be limited by anyone. The reason for having a planned continental meeting is for the sake of souls, and ultimately it is God who transforms souls.
* The Ability to Endure While continually gazing upon the beauty of the Lord...
* When I am most weak, God protects me most powerfully.
* Try to stop it once. The devil may hinder me, but he cannot prevent me. Because relative hindrance cannot block absolute pursuit.

* The Reason for Disappointment Losing hope is what we call disappointment. We lose hope because we expect results through calculation and probability. Rather than expecting the result you desire in all these circumstances, if you expect the good God, you will not be attached, will have peace, and will always be victorious.

* Suffering Beyond My Control When I encounter suffering I cannot control, I must remain calm. Since it is happening in a place that is not my domain anyway, the Lord will handle it, and it is the work of the Lord establishing us from the margins to the center.
* The Storm is to be Overcome The reason Jesus walked across the water was not to pull me out of the storm. It was to break through it together with me.
* Transcend Reality and Shatter Delusion What is as it is we call reality, and believing what you want to believe is delusion. Faith is believing in God's sovereignty.
* Boast in Weakness You will experience the sovereignty of the Lord.
* Do Not Be Troubled by Being Misunderstood Do not find it strange to be misunderstood. God never misunderstands.

* The Reason for Burning Anger It is because your desires have not been fulfilled. Therefore people quarrel and even commit murder. Whether a request was made with wrong motives can be seen from the response when what was requested is not granted. The best answer is to ask according to God's will and to trust both the method and the timing.
* Rejoice When Meeting Your Limits There has never been a time without limits, and through that limit we pray. Pray for revival that cannot be blocked by people, tradition, custom, and prejudice.

* Anxiety is Not Because of Mistakes It is not the case that I make a mistake or misheard the Lord's voice and fail to receive the Lord's guidance or experience failure. I do not lose the Lord's guidance because of my mistakes or weakness, but rather wrong priorities mislead me. When a wealthy official who appears to have everything perfect takes on wrong priorities, Jesus says 'what a pity.'

* Futile Attempts Victory Through Obedience Laying down my attempt to protect and defend myself and placing my confidence in the Lord is a contrary spirit that demolishes Satan's schemes. Do not guard yourself but obey Him.
* The True Reason for Anger It is because you are bound by the world's evaluation. Be bound to God.
* Exclusive Authority Until I give permission, no one can condemn or obstruct me.

* Absolute Advantage Do not be overly anxious about making mistakes. The Lord's power and grace are greater than my failings. Simply focus your motivation on pleasing the Lord. If the motive is good, the Lord takes responsibility for the results.

* All Things Work Together for Good Comes from Sovereignty Good and bad things work together for good because the Lord is alive and working. Become someone who becomes the hands and feet of the Lord to accomplish His will and sovereignty.
* True Happiness It is not because I possessed this and that thing I wanted to have. I am happy because the Lord rules.

* Complete Victory If you only feed a boxer just enough to survive and have him fight in the ring, he will surely struggle and return half-dead after each round. If you then counsel him on how to win, that is truly foolish. The ability to sustain fullness is true power.

* When Encountering Difficult Times Difficult times are situations where I have no choice. That means the Lord has chosen them. So I trust and entrust them. Then the Lord rules. When encountering difficult times, though my faith is small, I trust my Lord who still rules even more. —Hymn 342
* Disarmament Laying down my attempt to protect myself and placing my confidence in the Lord is a contrary spirit that demolishes Satan's schemes. Do not guard yourself but obey Him.
* When You Meet Your Limits A limit is an opportunity to raise your class. The method to overcome limits I already know. When your class changes, many difficult problems are resolved.

* The Ability to Lose Jacob, who lived life as a struggle, gained much and lost much. However, when he let go, ready to lose, he no longer lost anything and rather recovered what he had lost. A person prepared to lose is most powerful. If I am to lose my son, I shall lose him. —Genesis 43:14

* God Who Guides Through Loss Sometimes God speaks through loss. From the human perspective it is regret and remorse, but in God's kingdom, that loss becomes the beginning point of great works of God. Do not be concerned about your donkeys that were lost three days ago; they have been found. And for whom is all the desire of Israel? Is it not for you and all your father's household? —1 Samuel 9:20
* What I Anticipate Does Not Necessarily Come to Pass God's way surpasses my expectations. Wisdom is ceasing imagination, being faithful to present duties, and entrusting everything else.
* Not from East and West but from the Lord Do not lose heart when not recognized, nor worry trying to gain recognition. All recognition comes only from the Lord.
* Hell in Abundance Because you cannot thank God for what He gave and seek to have what He did not give. Because there is no sense of God's sovereignty saying 'The Lord has done...'
* The Scope of Grace Is there anything that is not God's grace? So we only give thanks and are content. The One who gives is God and the One who takes away is God.

* The God Who Called Me The God who called me did not call me to do this work excellently and gain recognition. We do the work, but the Lord takes responsibility for the results. We have only the responsibility to be faithful unto death. He counted me faithful and appointed me to His service. —1 Timothy 1:12
* The Lord's Lament When I am grieved over what will perish, He is truly grieved.
* Unhappiness is Not from Being Ignored People ignoring me or the world not giving me opportunity does not make me unhappy. I become unhappy because I turn away from the Lord.
* Cain's Sinful Desire If the wound is hidden, the offering will not be accepted. This sinful desire must be confessed to the Lord and governed by grace. It is not erased but covered.
* It is Foolish to Be Angry at People Whether lowered by God or foolish or absurd, being angry at people is foolish. Whether exalted by God or concerned with people's eyes, that is absurd.
* What to Do When Despairing Thanksgiving. Because God will work. God works with strength when I am weak.

* When Trying to Have Faith Without fail, when trying to have faith, powerful challenges come. So you want to abandon everything and run away. Nevertheless, faith is not believing that emotion but still believing in the Lord's sovereignty that rules over all.

* Unauthorized Happiness However happy it may appear, if it is not my place, I must not stand there. Because a sharp knife is hidden inside, not true happiness. Unauthorized happiness is the same as authorized unhappiness.
* Focus After the gap has left, do not listen to the uncomfortable heart but only look to the Almighty God and make your petitions with thanksgiving. Like the arrow leaving the bow...
* Solution for a Tangled Life Invite the Lord's rule. All filth and darkness will retreat and the Lord's peace will govern.

* What I Cannot Do If the Lord does not permit it, I cannot do anything. So there is no reason for me to be anxious. Rather than worrying about what I cannot reach, I hope you will be grateful for what is now permitted to me. O Lord, my heart is not proud, my eyes are not haughty; I do not concern myself with great matters or things too wonderful for me. —Psalm 131:1
* Two Certain Facts First, life is difficult, and second, God is good.
* I Really Want to Use You The reason God humbles me is because He really wants to use me. When I am zealous, it is like splashing while swimming, but when I am humbled, a tsunami arises.

* Do Not Just Bail Out Water When a ship is wrecked, you must not just bail out water. First, you must plug the hole. Just bailing out water from an accident caused by lack of anointing is madness. Bailing out water, even for a lifetime, does not bring resolution.
* Morning Meditation Be anxious about nothing. The Lord decides and will take responsibility for everything. Tell destiny to come. Do not be afraid.
* Prayer When Anxious May Your will be accomplished, Lord. Your own will brings anxiety.
* A Person Threatened is Powerless Threat works when the other person controls what I do not want to lose. Therefore, threat does not work against Christians.

* The Reason for Peace Even in a Sinking Ship Whether the ship sinks or sails smoothly, the Lord is with me in all this. If it sinks, He will make me walk on water, and if it sails smoothly, He will make me praise the Lord. Do not let the great waters overflow me or the deep swallow me up, and do not let the pit close its mouth over me. —Psalm 69:15
* Do Not Worry Saying 'What Should I Do and How?' The Lord asks why you want to do it. If that purpose is holy and clear, the method will naturally become sharp.

* Do not be anxious. If you truly believe that the Lord accomplishes all things in the end, there is nothing to be anxious about or collapse. Anxiety arises because selfish desires are not being fulfilled. What is not the Lord's will is displeasing even if given freely, but what the Lord desires is good even if you serve as a gatekeeper.

* Hidden desires and God's kingdom - Do not become angry over your hidden desires remaining unfulfilled, but grieve over heaven's will not being accomplished on earth. You cannot contain living water in a broken cistern, nor can you make anything other than the Lord serve as living water.

* The reason for His calling - You were not called to complain about circumstances and people. You were called to obey the Word and live accordingly. Then you will experience God working and alive in the midst of this difficult situation.

* High ground battles - The fact that the ownership of the high ground changes several times a day, showing how fierce the battle is, proves how important that region is. You are fighting an important battle. Trust in the Lord and rise again to fight.

* When aligned with calling, there is no frustration - I did not call you to complain about circumstances. Do what you are able to do. That is praying where you stand and living according to the Lord's Word. The holy Lord will rule with His sovereignty.
* The future is decided now - Decide your future now through Jesus. Those whose futures are determined are those who clearly desire it now.
* Two remarkable premises of His guidance - One is that I cannot understand it, and the other is that despite this, His guidance transcends my thoughts and understanding, always being good.

* Why not to be deceived by disappointment - Since I am not another and another is not me, it is more important to trust the Almighty Lord who permits this condition given to me than the condition itself. Do not concern yourself so much with what is not truth. Hold fast to the Lord, the eternal Truth, and you can be free from attachments that will perish.

* Which is easier for whom? - What we desire is position, honor, health, material things, and popularity. These belong to the easy side for God. What God desires is our repentance, contrition, forgiveness of sins, and receiving grace. This is not easy even from God's side.

* Belief makes things simply accomplished - Assigning excessive meaning to what I cannot do is not the Lord's will. Do not think complicatedly; faithfully do what is now permitted, and the Lord will accomplish what needs to be accomplished.
* God's alternative - Regarding the severe seven-year famine, what God prepared was Joseph. God's alternative is one faithful person.
* Acknowledge the Lord - Then the Lord's presence comes and He rules with His sovereignty.

* The freedom of the dead - Those who must not die become defensive. But those already dead are free. In childhood, what is good to oneself is good; in adulthood, what profits oneself becomes good; and in maturity, what is right becomes good. If satisfied here, the community is always divided. You must choose what the Lord desires. This is a value that transcends right and wrong.

* Anger - Examining anger closely, it is an explosive method of excessively defending oneself against uncontrollable circumstances and personal helplessness. In situations of unfair treatment and lack of recognition, when unable to control it, it is an attachment to appeal one's existence and an emotional method to eliminate the cause. Such foolish choices can lead to lifelong regret. Those recognized by God can control their emotions and circumstances. Gentleness comes from self-worth.

* Forsake good for the best - One good photo is better than hundreds of millions of poor ones. One key is better than a thousand keychains. Serving the Lord is doing well, and obeying the Lord is doing very well.

* Do not be bound - Let the Lord's blessings flow abundantly within you; according to the giver's abundance and mercy, you too must be generous and let go, releasing freely. Then He will pour out more new grace and blessings.
* The Lord's intervention - For the Lord to intervene, you must restrain your words, be humble, and lower yourself in gentleness. The Lord's sovereignty will rule.
* Revelation - The Lord's revelation shows only the one step you must obey immediately. This is because that is all you need to see.
* Why confirmation is unnecessary - Since the Lord rules, there is no need for confirmation from the weak.
* Reversal - Humanity is inherently evil. Yet rejoice! The Lord is good and His kindness has no end.
* One cause of all problems - Not seeking the face of God. The reason why living and dying are not problems is that the Lord's love compels us. -2 Corinthians 5

* How to stand as the Lord's servant - God's plenipotentiary ambassador accomplishes the Lord's will and has peace and boldness. But if seeking my own purpose and glory, I become anxious and fearful. My righteousness is like filthy rags.
* Why we cling - What the Lord has not given, we cling to. Being anxious, we snatch and quarrel. What the Lord has allowed, we can enjoy with thanksgiving and peace.
* My way and God's way - Do not worry over what you cannot do; be faithful in what you can do. Then all these things the Lord will add to you.

* Fulfilling desires does not bring happiness - David spent much of his youth being chased in the wilderness and enemy territory. If David had a choice, certainly it would be to sleep with his children at home with legs stretched out for even one day. All the great people in the Bible moved forward not knowing their future, but when they looked to and trusted only the Lord, their lives became happy lives fulfilling purpose.
* Do not be angry; think about it - Do not be angry at people; humble yourself before God who permits this now.

* Why the future is not fearful but anticipated - The reason for fear is not from future uncertainty but from evidence of not being completely united with the Lord. When you pray earnestly to unite with the Lord, rather the providence the Lord will accomplish becomes anticipated. The Lord cannot do what is impossible, and we know there is no plan He cannot accomplish. -Job 42:2
* The necessity of helplessness - When you experience your own helplessness and hit bottom, you learn to depend on the Lord. Only then do you feel this battle makes my zeal useless.
* Those who look only to God need not... -

* If unbearable reality makes you anxious, know that regarding reality beyond your control, the Lord has sovereignty and knows and entrusts all your future to Him. You must not only believe this but also believe that He faithfully holds you even in this very moment.

* Why God desires obedience - Sometimes God asks you to make difficult decisions. This is letting go, following, and obeying. Though you want to escape, God does not give up. He must use you by all means.
* Humble yourself and glory comes - Lower yourself again and again, and the Lord enters. Only when the Lord shines does it become glorious. Before the Lord, the world's boasting is like a foul rag.
* How to do the Lord's work - The church suffers because of capable people. God works through unprofitable servants because He Himself is almighty.
* I need to know only one thing - Because the Lord knows all things.

* Why repent - Because the kingdom of heaven is near. That means settling accounts according to what was done. Therefore, put off the shameful deeds of darkness and come into the light. Darkness dealt with now will not be mentioned later.

* The wicked perish in an instant - Hophni and Phinehas perished in one day. This was because they were corrupt and wicked. The reason they became so was that they 'did not know God.' Those who do not know God and do not seek to know about God are those who perish.

* Victory does not come to just anyone - Victory comes to those who overcome with the strength the Lord gives. If you overcome without the Lord's strength, it is not true victory but merely a quarrel.
* Reasons for boldness - I am bold because I know what I desire and because I know what I can lose. Those who long for eternity, nothing can stand in their way.

* What's the matter? - Wherever you go in the world and live, people live the same way. Obsessing over location or having illusions means your current life is truly difficult. Wherever the Lord leads is the best place.

* The warmth of complete dedication - Many people do not fully give themselves to the Lord because they fear Jesus will send them to a miserable mission field. If on a cold morning today a small frozen bird were huddled in a half-frozen barn, that helpless little bird must come into my complete control so I can warm its body, give it food. Would I then squeeze and crush it in my hand? Or would I provide shelter, warmth, food, and protection?

* True investment - What people in the world so desperately crave ultimately cannot be held to the end. But on the other hand, there is also something that can never be lost. Those who let go of pleasures, self-satisfaction, and material things that they ultimately cannot hold to the end for what can never be lost are not foolish people.
* What 'do not fear' means - God's repeated saying 'do not fear' is not merely offering comfort. It also offers solution. That solution is giving Himself.

* What are you afraid of? - What you fear reveals what you boast in and depend on. Those who fear the Lord have nothing to fear. The treasure and peace deeply buried in the heart cannot be taken away.

* What you hope for determines quality - If what you hope for is only this life, you are truly a pitiable person. Everything will disappear. Long for the eternal. Only Jesus and what is given to Jesus is eternal. If in Christ all we hope for is only this life, we are the most pitiable among all people. -1 Corinthians 15:19

* God's solution - God has never overwhelmed by many numbers. Rather, He chose one person to accomplish His will. The person God seeks is one who simply gives oneself for the Lord. The person is the alternative.

* Absolute happiness - Paul says that losing things is profitable. Originally, the purpose was not gain. When Christ, who lives in me, is my original purpose, loss does not become pity and gain does not become pride. 'For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain.' -Philippians 1:21

* Right motive over satisfying results - When I try to be responsible for results, the Lord sets a boundary. This is because I do not know who the true Master is. The door is opened by the Lord, and I seek the Lord who opens the door.

* The advance of sovereignty - Executing God's will is called faithfulness. So when you are faithful in faith not looking to people, you accomplish the Lord's will and experience His rule. The most advanced version of faithfulness is 'love.' Therefore, those who love are called perfect. Over all these things, put on love, which binds everything together perfectly. -Colossians 3:14 Whoever loves others has fulfilled the law. -Romans 13:8

* The basic of sovereignty - The authority to execute God's will and rule is called sovereignty. So God's sovereignty is my faithfully responding to His will and works. When you respond in faith, God's sovereignty comes. When you seek the Lord's sovereignty, sacrifice follows, but when sovereignty comes, you receive a better inheritance. Afterward, the word of the Lord came to Abram in a vision, saying, 'Fear not, Abram, I am your shield and your exceedingly great reward.' -Genesis 15:1

* When you boast in vanity, you surrender to emptiness. The irony is that you fear the very thing you are so proud of. When you fear something, you serve it. If you boast in vain things, ultimately you cannot bear disappointment and fear, your heart collapses, and your life crumbles. You must fear only the Lord.

* The burning bush is not consumed. Fire was kindled to the bush, but it was not burned away. God does not use us as a source to be depleted. Rather, He desires to be with us and to manifest His own glory. Therefore, when you remove your rights and the spirit of doubt before the Lord, God's holiness and glory are revealed.
* A life without regret is found in the Lord. Even if something has collapsed somewhat, if you are in the Lord, you must not regret. This is because all things work together for good.

* Prophesy: 1. My small flutter of wings becomes a tsunami in heaven that covers the world. Do not be discouraged. 2. The One who gave me femininity and charm is God, and when I believe in this, I become most peaceful and beautiful. Do not comfort yourself by looking at yourself and calling your desire humble; long for the most beautiful desire that the Lord gives. 3. My weakness does not cancel God's promise. Do not know yourself through others; when you see yourself through the Lord's eyes, you need not hide in a cave. 4. God, who led me to Him in pain and gave peace, makes me comfort the brokenhearted. 5. The 'margin' He gives me now is to give thanks and to serve. Through me, the community receives blessing—this becomes the most glorious life. 6. That I am is by God's grace and guidance, and the Lord's intention is not calamity but peace. Do not be hasty; entrust it. In the collapse where no answer comes, ask, 'Come, Holy Spirit.'
* The secret of victory: If the Lord does not help, we only do things that lead to destruction.

* How grateful can we be? He gives us situations we cannot give thanks for so that we learn genuine gratitude. If circumstances were merely fine, why would faith be necessary, and what good would it be to acknowledge the Lord who governs circumstances?

* Give thanks simply. At a prayer meeting yesterday, after preaching that we suppress the enemy's gate through thanksgiving and prepare to lose everything, the glass of my car parked in the lot was broken and I lost my LG G2 phone. God has a sense of humor. If I think simply: 1. He wants to replace my car glass, and 2. He means for me to turn off my phone for a while and pray and hold to His Word more. The newly installed car glass looks refreshing.

* Make faith your conclusion. The one as insignificant as a flea on Israel or like a dead dog becomes an incomparable great king. Because he was anointed. The valleys before you will be filled and the mountains will be made low. Whom has the king of Israel come out to pursue? Whom are you chasing? A dead dog or a flea? - 1 Samuel 24:14

* Life is interpretation. What matters is faith—through what will you interpret yourself and your life? When work is hard and paths are blocked, even the fathers of faith fall into doubt and act shamefully. You must discover the Lord's intention in the grace He has given. Faith is interpreting your life knowing His intention toward you is not calamity but peace.
* To rule, you must be the owner. Assembling pieces of all good and bad things to create good is not merely difficult—it is impossible. It is only possible through the Lord's governance.

* Why we lack even when we hoard excessively: There is a necessity that even when we hoard excessively we become poor, and when we scatter and give to the needy we become rich. This is because God lives and rules.

* If you do not understand, acknowledge and give thanks. It is natural not to know where to begin. That is the realm the Lord will govern. What I need is not a temporary painkiller but the Lord's sovereignty. Therefore, do not complain but give thanks.
* He notices inadequate me. Among the multitude, even among VIPs, the Lord notices inadequate me. God is interested in the one who is anointed.

* What you must never do regardless of circumstances: Do not make someone feel wronged. Whether it is another's arrogance and prejudice or my own ignorance and bias, God will surely repay it. David's terrible sin of adultery is lighter than killing a faithful righteous man. You struck down Uriah the Hittite with the sword and took his wife to be your own wife—2 Samuel 12:9
* The victory of faith: The victory of faith is the victory of perspective. Interpret this situation with the Lord's perspective. What a caterpillar calls death, God calls a butterfly.
* The most foolish thing: Worrying about what cannot be resolved. Entrust it to the Lord and be free and at peace. Everything will work out.

* Reason, not method: We cling to How, but God questions Why. Ask genuinely why you do something, why you must do it, why you are glad, why your heart is broken. This is because you know who is King and who is Master behind that reason. I truly hope God alone becomes your reason and motive. Whether we live or die, we live or die for the Lord; therefore whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord—Romans 14:8

* The secret of receiving power: Not feeling, judging, and acting from your own will, but acknowledging and honoring the Lord's sovereignty. Living by your own will makes you a person of the flesh; living by God's will makes you a person of the Spirit.

* God's perspective stops sin. God stops David's anger through Abigail. Abigail, bound by her own perspective of the problem, shows David God's perspective. Look down with God's eyes. David said to Abigail, Praised be the Lord, the God of Israel, who has sent you today to meet me—1 Samuel 25:32

* The conclusion of prejudice: A person trapped by prejudice about others, no matter how excellent, gifted, or spiritual, is called 'Jonah.' If God prepared 'Jonah' for the repentance of Nineveh where 120,000 children who cannot tell right from left live, to teach stubborn Jonah God's love, He prepared many things: 'a ship going to Tarshish and sailors,' 'a fish,' 'a plant,' 'a scorching wind,' 'a worm,' and more.

* How to suppress circumstances and enemies: To Paul who rushed only toward the Lord, people could no longer be a source of suffering. Therefore, if all you hope for is this life, you are truly a pitiable person. Look to the Lord and find your answer in Him.
* The reason you can entrust: David did not seek revenge because his hope was only in the living Lord.

* Entrust instead of worrying, and it will be accomplished. When you cannot control today's matters, worrying about tomorrow's is too much. Especially, Jesus Himself said that worrying about food and clothing—matters of livelihood—is unnecessary. Overcoming worry is making requests for everything, and this is driving out fear with 'peace that surpasses all understanding'—Philippians 4:7. Commit your way to the Lord; trust in him and he will do this—Psalm 37:5
* Absolute faith and absolute sovereignty: Do not fear; quote Scripture with confidence and pray with thanksgiving. The Lord's rule cannot be resisted by anyone.
* David's covenant: If only the leader stands correctly, God gives a lamp even in collapsing circumstances. Be an ace like David. The Lord my God kept a lamp for David in Jerusalem—1 Kings 15:4

* No matter how much chaos, even if all fall into temptation, if you and I remain unmoved, seek God with all our hearts and obey, God will save all the souls of the community—this is David's covenant. God remembered David and saved Israel.
* The basis of answer: Hold to the promise and pray based on Scripture. The basis is His unchangeableness and faithfulness that cannot renege.
* There is no disappointment. If I did it myself, there would be disappointment, but living under His guidance, things just proceed. The Lord is the conclusion.
* True happiness: If you pursue your own will, you will be anxious and feel emptiness even if you succeed. If you pursue the Lord's will, you will fear nothing and will be happy.

Do not live a defensive life; live the life of dying at the cross. Saul lived defensively and destroyed himself, while David lived giving himself over and became a good shepherd of Israel. When I give up protecting myself, the Lord begins to protect me.

* When you focus on the problem, evil spirit operates. When you dwell on problems, you experience various negative imaginations. The moment I try to solve the problem, I become bound by it and think as the evil spirit directs. The Lord solves it; I give thanks.
Those who think there is little to learn will collapse greatly. Those who think there is much to learn will have long and prosperous days. God honors the humble. When God exalts, no one can lower.

* You become gentle when you entrust to God. Gentleness is withholding judgment until God works. It is not my thought but the Lord's heart, which I cannot manufacture; it is given only to those who completely trust the Lord's living presence.
The people had King Saul in their hearts. But God had David in His heart. Do not seek support; seek grace.
When standing before Scripture naked as it were, the Bible is not just a thick book but 'God' Himself—you experience this.

* The sinful human is most focused on 'self.' My problems, my money, my house, my family, my success. Focusing thus, problems should be solved, but like tangled thread they become more knotted. Problems grow the more you look at them. Looking to the Lord in prayer makes that big problem small. The Lord is vast. Have faith that sees the Lord as great.
* The courage to stand at the cliff's edge: If it is the Lord who permits the cliff's edge, trust that even if you fall, He beautifully accomplishes His will. Not by my method but by the Lord's will.

* Change is entirely God's sovereign work and miracle. More amazing is that He continually upholds and preserves that change. The deep and broad love and mercy of the Lord, beyond human comprehension.

* Change is necessary, but criticizing for the sake of change is ineffective and truly should not be done. Only those designated can perform brain surgery with a sharp scalpel. Only judgment belongs to God; if humans do it rashly, they kill life and community. Fearing that pulling weeds might uproot grain instead, let both grow together until harvest—Matthew 13
* Boasting at the hospital is foolish. All patients are just patients. If everyone stands before the Lord as someone who needs healing, there will be no pride or comparison.
* Tremendous power: I do not worry about matters I cannot resolve.

* Potiphar's wife tried to harm Joseph and had him put in prison. But even if it were not for Potiphar's wife, Joseph would have gone to prison. The people and circumstances that tried to harm me ultimately could not, and instead they served to fulfill God's providence. That I am is by God's grace.

* Potiphar's wife was someone Joseph would have wanted to kill, at least from his perspective. But without her, he would not have gone to prison nor met the chief cupbearer. Potiphar's wife was truly greatly used. Viewed with my eyes, things seem to go wrong, but viewed with the Lord's eyes, all things work together for good. The Lord has made everything for its proper purpose, even the wicked for the day of disaster—Proverbs 16:4
* A jewel fallen in mud does not lose its value. A person with strong essence is not shaken by any attack. A diamond attacked by glass shards does not become glass. You are a jewel born by the Lord.
* What is seen is not eternal, they say. Though He is not seen, even now He makes a way before you.

* Why are sad premonitions never wrong? Sometimes I am anxious from being too happy. Fear that this goodness might be taken away. Sure enough, sad premonitions are never wrong. When you enjoy the blessings given and give thanks to the Giver, God guarantees the quality and quantity of your gratitude.

* The Gospel is perfect and God is perfect, but I am not, we say. Rather, because of the Gospel's perfection and God's perfection, I become insufficient. It is because the Lord chose me that it happens.

* Gifts: If you do not express gratitude for the gift given and treat it carelessly, God will take it away and give it to one who knows gratitude. The value of a gift is not its face value but the dignity of the Giver. Samuel said to him, The Lord has torn the kingdom of Israel from you today and has given it to one of your neighbors who is better than you—1 Samuel 15:28

* The One who calls, the One who uses, and the One who prepares are one. If the Lord says He will do it, He does it. He is the Master. Worry and anxiety are the Master's responsibility; obedience is the servant's. Say, 'The Lord will use me'—Mark 11:3
* The Lord who called you will surely take responsibility. The Lord does not call and then default. Because faithfulness that cannot be measured by the world's credit rating is His attribute.
When parents shop at the mart, children do not worry about snacks. When parents are wealthy, they do not worry about money for school supplies. When God is Father, a person does not fear the future.
Even if I break, I will do it completely until the day God tells me to stand.

When you feel lack, the easiest thought that comes is that you should leave, that you should let go. However, that is not that you lacked, but rather your original self with no possibility at all is revealed. Originally there was not a shred of possibility, but because you have walked believing in the Lord, you can also walk with the Lord going forward. If you feel lack, you must kneel down and seek mercy.

* Why it is not difficult: When the Lord becomes Lord, God's kingdom is established. Now this concern is not mine but my Lord's. So when seeking His kingdom and His sovereignty, all these things are added to you. Do not worry.

* Why it is difficult: Because you are trying too hard. But that domain was not given to you. What was given to you is the realm of faithfulness. Faithfulness is keeping your place. To such a faithful one, the grace of doing well and prospering follows.
* The opportunity of the worst situation: Only in the worst situation can you give the highest thanksgiving. The Lord is watching me even now. Be faithful.

This is an age that requires discernment. Without discernment, you lose the most precious thing. Discernment is not stacking more knowledge or having eloquent speech. True discernment is honoring the Lord in every area. If you forsake your own things to seek God's glory, you will see clearly what the Lord desires. 'The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge, but fools despise wisdom and instruction.' Proverbs 1:7

The ship going to Tarshish that Jonah the disobedient encounters 'just happens' to pass by, and favorable winds blow—this is not God condoning his stubbornness, but rather a brief warm-up exercise before meeting the storm and entering the fish's belly.

* Mission: My work is not to receive the most votes in a popularity contest. That is why I was not called as an entertainer. Bear contempt, disgrace, and the cross. Let only the Lord receive honor, glory, and all authority.

Turning anxiety into peace comes from standing before the Lord and looking only to Him. Revival overwhelms fear. 'We have no power to face this vast army that is attacking us. We do not know what to do, but our eyes are on you.' All Judah stood before the Lord with their wives, children, and little ones. —2 Chronicles 20:12,13

* The reason for faithfulness: Running toward the purpose of the One who called you without giving up. This is possible because He is watching. Being conscious of God becomes faithfulness; being conscious of people becomes hypocrisy.

Anxious hearts come from unstable positions. If you lift your heart high, somehow you will have no peace. If you lower your center, you will see the Lord in your anxiety. God leads those with steadfast hearts to perfect peace.
Disadvantage comes from an unfair world. But justice and rule come from God. Do not look to people's faces; look only to the Lord.

* Do not be disappointed. As I experienced life's disappointments, I realized something: you must not say that everything is disappointing just because one part is. God is still working and comforting through what we did not expect. He also transforms that disappointing part into glory.
* Seeking what is important: Before seeking the progress of circumstances and the victory of relationships, seek grace first. All good things in various forms are given by God's grace.

* The foundation of life: If it were possessions, self-esteem, or relationships, you would be shaken. But if it is the Lord, you will not be shaken even when other losses come. Trials reveal what foundation you have lived upon. Stand on the rock. 'The Lord gave and the Lord has taken away; may the name of the Lord be praised.' —Job 1:21
Do not compare. What I have is far more than what I should have—it is what the Lord has given me abundantly.

To live a life without comparison, you must abandon the wrong measuring rod of your heart. I am a person living by receiving answers. Exalting and lowering people is entirely God's work. Tests come to tear down all emotions that do not come from God. They come within God's permission and providence. God completely tears us down and rebuilds us anew for our healing. —From Saturday Worship

When you stand firm through wind and rain, a multitude follows. In your circumstances, do not hope for great fortune, but desire faithfulness to where the Word says to go. Results must be entirely entrusted to God.

* When you set priorities right, your territory becomes clear. Do not worry about areas you cannot handle. That is not the place permitted to you. Things you cannot do are not your concern but the Lord's. You need only seek His kingdom and His righteousness.

Just as absolute speed and distance are necessary for an airplane to take off, to expect something without giving absolute time is foolish. If you truly desire it, please give it. If you really want it, I pray you truly give it. There is no other way.
A plane being delayed is not a problem. What is more important is arriving safely at the destination. Walk the path the Lord intends. Do not compare speed; direction is more important than speed.

The principle of how planes fly does not focus on the updraft pulling upward or the force supporting from the ground. When you have 'thrust' despite resistance and challenges, resistance actually provides 'lift' enabling flight. The thrust of life is the ability to hold to the cross. Before the cross, all things become negligible problems.

* Practice of remembrance: In difficult moments, beautiful memories suddenly appear like faded photographs. 'Could that beauty of sitting in a cafe on a cold winter day, sharing faith amidst the aroma of coffee, return again?' Surely God's grace allowed such happiness. And though no one knows, if that happiness is allowed again, that too will be God's grace. The Lord's grace holds me and carries me forward.
What is important is not running fast but for whom you run. If the One who gave you talent is the Lord, when you decide who to use that talent for, it can be the most beautiful or the most futile.

Do not be discouraged by the glittering power, money, and beauty of the world. What Pharaoh held was a scepter made of gold and jewels, but before the dry rod Moses held, it was completely shattered. The power the Lord gives is not wrapped in splendor, but it is incomparably more glorious than Gandalf's staff. The material does not matter; what matters is who holds it.
Throughout life, there will be trials. You will also see being shaken by falling into trials. But though wind blows and dogs bark, the train runs through.

The boldness of Apostle Paul: People's evaluation of me is of little concern. I do not even feel the need to blame myself or fall into self-pity. Why? Because true evaluation will come from God on that day. 'I care very little if I am judged by you or by any human court; indeed, I do not even judge myself. My conscience is clear, but that does not make me innocent. It is the Lord who judges me.' —1 Corinthians 4:3,4

In Acts 21, many prophets and servants of the Lord, moved by the Holy Spirit, prophesy troubles in Jerusalem and try to stop Paul's journey. But Paul has already been revealed by the Holy Spirit in chapter 20. Believers seeing only Jerusalem's troubles stop Paul, but Paul, who has seen 'Rome as well,' goes forward to the end. You progress and can be bold to the extent you see. Do not see only Jerusalem but also Rome beyond.

In Acts 27, when all have lost hope for survival in a desperate situation, soldiers want to kill the prisoners lest they escape by swimming, but the centurion wants to save Paul and prevents their plan. On the island, though bitten by a viper, Paul does not die. Paul was not afraid. He was confident that the storm, those trying to kill him, and the viper would not harm him or cause him to die before the time the Lord permits and His purpose is fulfilled. Discern the Lord's time and purpose. Discernment is ability.
Stop watching people's faces and look to God's Word. He will make you wiser and more excellent than your enemies.

Living as a supporting actor while watching people's expressions is not the life the Lord designed. In your life, only the Lord is Lord, and you live as His masterpiece. The world's interest is in popularity and recognition, but the Lord's interest is in 'relationship.'

Living obsessed with people and the world will never bring peace or satisfaction. How long will you keep weighing and wavering? How long will you hesitate and conflict? Without knowing Jesus, you live eating beside excrement. Living as you wish, as you want, even with effort, it will not work. Of course it will not. Not only will it not work, you will be destroyed. What is not given to the Lord will be taken away. 'How long will you waver between two opinions? If the Lord is God, follow him; but if Baal is God, follow him.' —1 Kings 18:21 'Choose for yourselves this day whom you will serve... But as for me and my household, we will serve the Lord.' —Joshua 24:15
Do not let your heart ache because of people and circumstances; rather, desire to have a broken heart with the Lord over the things that break His heart. Our purpose is not people and circumstances.

People experience fervent grace, shed tears, and then change. We call it deterioration. Some deteriorate in a month, some in six months. Some deteriorate in years. Everything in the world decays. But the Lord is eternal. Those who hold only to the Lord can never change.

People are anxious, so they try to control circumstances. But believers called to guard their hearts and minds and be self-controlled. Then you will see the glory of the Lord who governs and provides for all things.
* Envy is not losing: Envy is not losing. Fear is losing. You lose control. Boldness is not arbitrary conviction but believing in God's rule and sovereignty in circumstances.
* The basis of joy: If it concerns me and my things, it will bring much sorrow. But if it concerns Him and His things, it will bring joy.
* Faith: Seeing the Lord's rule in situations you cannot bear.
Do not react to people; react to God. Then you can walk the path the Lord leads. Do not seek the Lord's will from people's responses.
People will shed tears and beat their chests, but ultimately they will walk the way they desire. This is truly sad and heartbreaking.
In moments of loss, you can truly say what you depend on is what you truly believe. When the Lord permits the pain of loss, I hope you gaze upon the Lord quietly.
People desire success, but success is achieved only when you continue to fail.
* Desires are fulfilled only by grace: Whether it is something miraculous and impossible or something very small and modest, it can only be received through God's sovereignty and grace.

Do not be afraid of disappointing people. Satisfying people is not my work but the Lord's work. Entrust everything to the Lord and be free in truth. Do not try to solve what the Lord will do by scheming. If you go forward with the determination to lose what is lost, you will see the Lord's work.
Woe to those who expect human recognition. Blessing is to those who expect the Lord.

Though people seem to earn what they work for, it is God who gives. God's principle of giving material and gifts is based not on my ability but on my need. When you know God, you understand that all things are fulfilled by His will. Not a bird falls without the Lord's will.
* Eternally undefeated: However dark it appears, God does not make mistakes, and by His faithfulness, He will receive praise among all nations and peoples. I believe in Him.
When people fell, they became addicted to the work of darkness—ruling and possessing what God permitted to us. What God permits us is to steward it temporarily, and this is the work of heaven.

* One thing must be clear: It is that the Lord is the One who rules. The Lord's kingship—His kingdom, intimacy with the Lord—His righteousness. To those who seek all these things, God's peace will come. To those who seek their own arbitrary decisions and friendship with the world, fear will come.
* The way to entrust worries is to know who God is and to confirm the Lord before my present reality at every moment.
Dear beloved, in God's kingdom there is no waste of time and nothing vain. This moment I spend with Him is more meaningful than a thousand years, than ten thousand years.

* The harbor of hope and safe zone When you rely on what is untrustworthy, you cannot help but be anxious. When you place your stability in Him and trust in the Lord's work, there is true peace. A peace that can never be taken away.

* Circumstances cannot be adjusted. They can only be governed. My circumstances cannot be adjusted as I please. I neither have the ability nor the authority to do so. But when I move forward with gratitude, I will experience the Lord's amazing work and wisdom. I believe in the Lord.

In fact, there is no fortune or chance in life. There is always a purpose He has prepared. Life is walking that path, and sometimes I will pass through places seven times hotter than a furnace, yet His deep purpose will not fade away.
The anointing in ministry is often just participating in what the Lord and His co-laborers have done. If it were not for me, what connection does my inadequacy have with the Lord's work?

Ministry is not a livelihood. When the Lord says it is enough, that is the extent of my calling. Neither the 'I' that I evaluate nor the 'I' that others evaluate provides any justification. Only He knows my path and sets my time. Moses, who fulfilled his calling and did not enter Canaan, is truly an impressive person.
It is not that a minister's prayer heals sickness and solves problems. The Lord has prepared healing and resolution, and has determined to work through His servant.

To a minister's beloved leader—A person with a calling is not someone trying to succeed. Because you have already succeeded in the Lord. Trust that the Lord has given you the best and that if there is something He will enable you to handle, He will supply the strength according to His good pleasure. What we need is unwavering trust like a rock.

* Reasons not to envy Do not envy the prosperity of the wicked. Do not react to wrong intentions. Do not be hurt by vain boasting and matters unrelated to you that He has not given you. That is why those who know the truth are set free.
