As a minister, the best thing to do is not to hinder or obstruct revival. I think of poet Song Myeong-hee's poem: 'The Lord did it.'

King Saul tried to kill David to protect what the Lord had not given him, and in the end Saul himself died. David entrusted what God had given him to the Lord, and praised with psalms on lonely nights, and finally the Lord accomplished it for him.
After his victory, Saul set up a monument to himself on Mount Carmel, but Samuel set up Ebenezer (God has helped us thus far) between Mizpah and Shen. Who you face tells everything about your life.
* Recovery is done by the Lord. Recovery does not come from circumstances and mood changes. Only the Lord restores me. Because recovery means returning to the Lord.

* Lack is because of His mercy. If what I desire is not with me, it is something the Lord has not given me. Rather than lamenting about what the Lord has not given me, I should be humble about the fact that I lack the maturity to receive what I desire and that my faith and character are insufficient. The Lord is always good and merciful.

Life cannot be manipulated. I calculate and perform to adjust some parts and moments to my taste, but ultimately life comes before the One who made it and returns to Him. With empty hands. I lay down my cleverness and receive heavenly wisdom, and receive the ability to be self-controlled, work hard, and be faithful.

Life is a struggle for control. Who seizes that control determines victory or defeat. The Lord does not help us seize control of our lives. He is the One who must properly govern my life in my place. When this war becomes the Lord's war instead of my war, I will be victorious.
* The way to change the world is to do what I must do and entrust what the Lord must do. What I must do is repent and be transformed. Revival begins from my spirit.
When the Lord shines the light of His truth on life's dark shadows, people hurt and want to hide. God is not trying to interfere, but to forgive sin and make whole.

* Not by might nor by power, but what is comforting and hopeful is that it becomes possible only by the Holy Spirit, not by my strength and will. Those who try to accomplish their own will will be obsessed, but those who look to the grace of the Almighty will dwell peacefully.
Do not imagine and become angry. Rest in peace upon the truth. It is not that it is such, but it is simply what is permitted for me within the Lord's providence.

The Lord who touches the wounded heart Ultimately, the One who touches a wounded heart is only that One. No matter how humans create an atmosphere, arrange things, and decorate words, healing and recovery ultimately happen before His throne. There is no need to be anxious about the Lord's timing or to worry about the Lord's method. Trust the Lord and hope in the Lord.

Do not be overcome by circumstances, but rather pray that the Lord will govern them. To fully exercise the dominion the Lord has entrusted, I must overcome the evil enemy, and that power is governing through 'God's overflowing grace.' From the beginning, darkness cannot be our adversary.
Being bold while looking at circumstances is not the way. When I am bold, the circumstances obey.

The most amazing way to immediately reverse circumstances and firmly control the game of life is 'gratitude.' Because God's blessing and rule already come upon the grateful soul. Are you grateful? Rejoice and be glad. It means the Lord's will is being executed.
Circumstances do not permit me to accomplish the Lord's will. When I accomplish the Lord's will, circumstances change.
I consider that the present sufferings are not worth comparing with the glory that will be revealed to us. —Romans 8:18

Life moves and expresses itself. The Lord's love is life. That abundant love is limited in us. Unless we give it freedom to express, the Lord has determined to remain limited to us. Rest and enjoyment are the beginning of bearing fruit. The Lord does it.

* Sufficient grace I must receive the grace the Lord gives, not the desires I crave. Do not be discouraged when the desires of the flesh pursued by the world are not fulfilled, but be grateful for the sufficient grace the Lord gives. It is boundless grace. 'Even in difficult times, He gives sufficient grace,' 'From the rock before me, He makes water spring forth,' 'Forever will I sing my hymn, Jesus has led me.' —Hymn 384

* Antitrust Laws—Govern and serve rather than dominate The motive of monopoly is selfish rule, the pursuit of happiness. It seems one will flourish if one dominates the market and hegemony, but when anyone or any nation, company reaches a high place and without hesitation kicks away the ladder, monopoly ownership does not become happiness but returns as disaster. Ultimately, God did not call us dominators but rulers and servants. Those who rule and serve are meek and humble, people who accomplish their calling rather than ambition and selfishness. I will send foreigners to winnow Babylon and empty that land. —Jeremiah 51:2 Those who transform the world will be foreigners with different cultures and worldviews.

* Believe in the Lord's joy, not my joy To please someone, you must know them. What they dislike and what they like. Pleasing God does not mean conforming to my joy, but seeking joy within God's will. Sometimes He even requires the opposite of my joy, which is painful, but ultimately He permits it because it becomes beneficial to me. God will never fail you!

* Overcome rather than escape Do not escape storms and tempests; overcome them. A life without turmoil does not exist. What matters is not the intensity and height of rough waves, but the grace that overwhelms that wind. When I believe in the Lord's sufficient grace, I will not be shaken by every trial that comes and will overcome abundantly. 'My soul, be still in God alone. My salvation comes from Him. My soul, be still in God alone. My hope comes from Him. Only the Lord is my rock, my salvation. Only the Lord is my fortress; I will not be shaken. My soul earnestly longs for the Lord, and my lips will praise the Lord. My soul will joyfully follow the Lord, and I will bless the Lord all my life. Only the Lord is my rock, my salvation. Only the Lord is my fortress; I will not be shaken.' —Psalm 62

* The beginning becomes the whole, not half God initiates a good and righteous work in me and does not abandon it, but accomplishes and completes it. It is not because I am good, but because the One who does it is good. Therefore, we must not abandon the souls and trust the Word regardless of circumstances and feelings. God's grace is boundless. I am confident of this: that He who began a good work in you will carry it on to completion until the day of Christ Jesus. —Philippians 1:6

* Gratitude changes destiny King Saul is tested by David's fame and the people's cheers. Praise refines a person. If, truly if Saul had thanked God for David, that outstanding talent, the course of history, not just his life, would have changed.

* Grace that transcends limits What is hard is truly unpleasant, but not frightening. I am weak, but I see the Lord's grace working everywhere. The Lord's grace is not blocked by my human limitations.

* Reasons for mercy God the Father has compassion on us as a father has compassion on his children. (Psalm 103) Because He remembers. This is because He knows our frame; He remembers that we are dust. —Psalm 103:18

* When everything crumbles If the foundation crumbles, what can the righteous do? The unrighteous flee to the mountains. But the righteous choose the 'righteous God' even when everything is destroyed. Because He sees and keeps me. 'I have taken refuge in the Lord; how then can you say to me: 'Flee like a bird to your mountain'?' —Psalm 11:1 'If the foundations are destroyed, what can the righteous do?' —Psalm 11:3 'The Lord is in His holy temple; the Lord is on His heavenly throne. He observes everyone on earth; His eyes examine them.' —Psalm 11:4 'The Lord is righteous, He loves justice; the upright will see His face.' —Psalm 11:7

* God's specialty There is no law that we must die. When we advance prepared to die, God opens a way we never imagined. God's specialty is bringing forth water in the wilderness and making roads in the desert.
Do not worry about success; pray to accomplish the Lord's will. The Lord will keep you in peace.
When you succeed, you will feel some envious looks. But when you obey, the earth shakes and heaven cries out with power. Obedience over success.

Though I lack much before the saints and feel sorry, I run today because of the Lord who called me. What has kept me alive until now is the Lord's burning compassion, and if the Lord allows me to run for Him and serve souls, it will be because of the Lord's boundless grace. Let us look only to the Lord.

In worldly sports, results are very important. Because there is the audience's cheers. But for us, process and attitude are more important. Because there is One who sees. Even if the path ahead looks only dark, God does not make mistakes. In Him who gives me strength, I can do all things, and all things work together for good.

The excessive reactions of the world and people are not a domain given to me. That is not my concern. It is resolved by being filled with the Holy Spirit and receiving power. 'It is not for you to know the times or dates the Father has set by His own authority. But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you.' —Acts 1:7,8
The world's and darkness's challenges will fade as time passes. Because they are not truth, they have no lasting power. But the Lord's love is the same yesterday, today, and forever.

We must live fiercely and diligently enough to achieve in the world. But the purpose is not to claim rights over that achievement. Because we do not claim rights, we are not particularly attached, and because we are not attached, neither are we irresponsible. When I walk the path He has given, all things work for good.
* The king's gratitude A king is one who has absolute freedom and authority. The most glorious freedom and authority is gratitude. Offer the king's gratitude. The class changes.

Powerful people in the world are weak people. Those outside the gospel are very weak. They are people anxious about car accidents, cancer, and their families. The gospel is a whole-life policy that covers this world and the next.
Even if the whole world disappears, as long as I have the Lord's guidance, that is enough! O fate, come!

Do not ignore something more important for the success the world speaks of. Do not be caught by success, but be a person captivated by the light within me. Though I go slowly, I will not retreat, and ultimately I will become what God dreams of.

Saving time is taking advantage of opportunities. To take advantage of opportunities, I must discern God's will. Success has God's will hidden within it. The foolish rich fail to discern it and perish. God's will is also hidden in failure. Through it, Peter meets Jesus personally.

* An ignorant yet bold(?) answer—Moses tells Pharaoh to let this people go. Pharaoh naturally refuses and is destroyed by ten plagues. Why? Because he did not know God. If you do not know God, you cannot be free, nor can you obey. Then Pharaoh said, 'Who is the Lord, that I should obey his voice and let Israel go? I do not know the Lord, and moreover, I will not let Israel go.' -Exodus 5:2
Belonging soothes our anxiety. Sometimes it also gives us pride. However, God is not concerned with where we belong. Rather, He says what matters is what kind of figure we stand before Him.
Sometimes even when a desire is fulfilled it becomes a problem, and when it is not fulfilled it also becomes a problem. The key is where that desire came from.

There is a misconception that we should achieve our desires by any means necessary, find stability, and then reveal God's name. God does not travel along with such success. He seeks and supports those who rely only on His power and step forward boldly.

* The reason He saves only after we let go—He saves only after we let go. God wanted to give us something incomparably better rather than fulfill our desires. What God truly wanted to give was Himself. The person who can receive the Lord as a gift is one who is prepared even for failure and is satisfied with Him.

You do not need to send a reminder to the chief cupbearer to remember my favor. No matter how shabby or meaningless it may seem (prison work), if you faithfully do my work, there will come a day when Pharaoh's dream becomes so compelling that the cupbearer cannot bear it. Prepare your whole life for that moment.
Envy, jealousy, and pride steal joy, block anointing, and prevent growth. Do not respond to people; respond to God. The authority to exalt and humble belongs to God alone. Psalm 75

* The posture of receiving curses—King Saul was fearful of people, so through religious ritual he was sensitive to the people's reaction. David feared God, so he did not respond to a person's curse. And David said to Abishai and all his servants, 'If even my own son, born of my body, seeks my harm, how much more might this Benjamite? Leave him alone and let him curse, for the Lord has commanded it.' -2 Samuel 16:11

Reform of systems and institutions is a fundamental response. But it is not the true cause. Human greed—sin—is the cause. Unless we address this fundamental issue, systemic reform returns to square one. Christians need the discernment to see priority and the patience to push it through.

* Judge and spectator—If an athlete repeatedly focuses on the spectators' eyes or relies on their own feelings, they cannot perform excellently. Only the judge makes the decision. There is nothing more foolish than judging your own existence by the self-feeling that comes from the audience's reaction, and there is nothing more stupid than giving up the competition and running away because of it. Now a crown of righteousness has been laid up for me, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will award to me on that day—and not only to me, but also to all who have longed for his appearing. -2 Timothy 4:8

From the New Year's conference onward, the Word that continues to be drawn is about faithfulness, labor, and devotion. And that labor and faithfulness are surely remembered by God. Let us look only to the Lord and run toward His Word. Let us leave behind hardship and pain and run for the Lord. Be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that your labor in the Lord is not in vain. -1 Corinthians 15:58
* Worry Free—Only Jesus can touch my heart. It is not a problem to worry about but a problem to be touched. So now let it go and be at peace. The Lord rules.

Believers must be flexible. There is nothing worth risking our lives for except the Lord, and all things work together for good within Him. If you become easily excited, you will have many awkward moments later. Without my anger, the Lord is an all-powerful Father who brings forth many beautiful conclusions.

The reason believers should have power is not to dominate and oppress like Pharaoh. The world needs money and power to live comfortably and discriminate, but we need power to confront suffering and see the Lord's glory.

Disappointment comes from greed. Greed means we had human expectations. If the Lord has not given it, we have received nothing. If it is the Lord's gift, let us obey and wait. When we use human calculation and methods, like Jacob, our autobiography becomes thick.

If you are disappointed, it means you were impatient. Impatience means you did not trust the Lord and, furthermore, sought your own glory. Remember this: the Lord will never give His glory to an idol.

The cross is the highest conclusion given by the Lord. If the trial cannot be nailed to the cross, the Lord would not have commanded it from the beginning. The spirit of the cross is this: 'I cannot bear to watch you die; I will save you at any cost.'
Not falling completely is being held up and raised by the Lord's strong right arm. -Psalm 37
Do not be grieved by the prosperity of the wicked. God does not judge by outer garments but by the love of the heart.

The wicked may appear strong and seem victorious, but before the day of tribulation and judgment, they are like chaff blown by the wind. They have no roots. Even if the leaves are abundant, they will be blown away in a critical moment. In farming, winnowing throws both grain and chaff into the air. Then the grain remains and the chaff is blown away. The condition of the righteous is in God's Word.
Do not regret what did not happen; give thanks for what He did not lead you to. There is meaning in it.

* The gospel's absolute opponent—However strong and organized the opponent, and however difficult our circumstances and gloomy our prospects, the gospel has never been defeated. The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim freedom for the prisoners and recovery of sight for the blind, to set the oppressed free, to proclaim the year of the Lord's favor. -Luke 4:18-19

Jacob lay down with a stone pillow at Bethel and saw God's way, God's ladder. Without human wisdom, ambition, or attachment, he saw the angels in procession and God's glory. We too, until we come to a situation where we must lay with a stone pillow, will lie, deceive, betray, and be betrayed for worldly glory. When rough stone tears flow down our cheeks, that path unseen before, that eternal path opens.
When ambition hides itself as calling, it becomes competition. Competition makes obedience impossible. Why? Because means and ends are reversed. If you cannot obey, the Lord is not your master.

Obtaining the Promised Land is not about entering Canaan and registering property or making good real estate investments. The only ownership of that land comes from a relationship with God. Canaan is a region where rain plays an absolute role. Your relationship with God determines everything about you. Relationship is authority and power.
* Testimony of suffering—The reason your heart hurts as if pierced by a sword is because God created you with honor and is working within you.

* Schedule—A Christian known for his piety was revealed to be involved in a crime. Moreover, he was arrested at the scene of attempted rape and received a heavy sentence in prison. While he was serving time, some said the human heart is completely unknowable, and others said the God he so believed in was fake. His name was Joseph. (Genesis 39) Persevere. God's schedule and my schedule may differ somewhat.

An Englishman was crossing the Atlantic to America in difficult circumstances. This poor man had to go up on deck at mealtimes with only cheese and biscuits to eat alone, and soon even that ran out, leaving him so hungry he could barely get up. The captain asked why he kept coming up on deck at mealtimes, and the man told him he had no money. Captain: 'The passage fare you paid includes all meals.' The Englishman was so delighted that he ran to the dining room and ate. After that meal, he arrived in America. Jesus paid it all on the cross.

One day a dog reads a book called 'The Power of Positivity' and becomes convinced he can fly. He climbs high and jumps off—for a moment it seems like he is flying. Later he wakes up in the ICU of an animal hospital. He thinks, 'If I pray specifically and fast more, it will work.' But it absolutely won't. The only way is to ride like an airplane in his master's arms.
The forces of darkness have collapsed. We have won. The great dragon was hurled down—that ancient serpent called the devil, or Satan, who leads the whole world astray. -Revelation 12:9
When you are in such a difficult moment that you do not know where to begin, pray like this: 'Lord, take control!'

Sometimes you encounter a situation like hell itself. It should never happen, but if He allows you to go to such a place, find a guitar, form a worship group, and worship the Lord. After the gathering, since there is much sulfurous fire there, providing food should not be difficult. Remember that the Lord's grace is sufficient and He gives what you can bear, that you know clearly what you must do wherever you are. 'I can do all this through him who gives me strength.'
When facing hardship, though my faith is small, I rely more on my Lord whom I trust. -Hymn 342

To a shepherd who faithfully tends a small flock, the Lord of glory appears 'suddenly, unexpectedly, at a time not foreseen' and shows Himself. And there were shepherds living out in the fields nearby, keeping watch over their flocks at night. An angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them. -Luke 2:8-9
How grateful I am that I do not have to rely on myself.
How grateful we are to be able to pray! Prayer transcends borders and regions, is not returned like an unwanted gift, and even transcends time.

How much you have matters little. Whose hand you are held in matters. A small child's two fish and five loaves of bread are nothing, but in Jesus' hands they became a miracle that fed five thousand. Entrust your insufficient life to the Lord. His grace will be sufficient.

Ephesians—Four things the world pursues (Ephesians 2:1, 3)—Though the world claims to give happiness, it deceives and tries to dominate souls through these four things (intimidation + inducement). 1. The power of sin—In which you used to live when you followed the ways of this world; 2. The world's value system—following the ruler of the kingdom of the air, the spirit who is now at work in those who are disobedient; 3. The devil's work—As for you, you were dead in your transgressions and sins; 4. The desires of the flesh—We all formerly lived in the passions of our flesh, indulging the cravings of our flesh and following its desires. -Ephesians 2:1, 3
* The final victory—Our victory is not in the world. Our true victory is decided when we take our last breath and stand before the Lord.

The Lord asked him, 'What is that in your hand?' And he said, 'A staff.' -Exodus 4:2 1. God does not ask how much have you achieved? Are you recognized? 2. He asks what you have, even if it is worthless. 3. Moses was a failed old man with a dry stick. 4. Having much and rising high does not manifest the Lord's glory. 5. If you pretend to have what you do not have, there is no need for a mask and no reason for pretense. 6. Even a parched life, when obedient to the Lord, displays His power and glory. Moses took the staff of God in his hand. -Exodus 4:20 A dried stick, when held in the Lord's hand, becomes the staff of God. A ring from a department store and a ring given by a loved one are not the same ring. It is a ring 'you' put on me. When a meaningless stick I used freely becomes the staff God places in my hand, there I see His glory. Exodus 4:17: 'Take this staff in your hand with which you will perform the signs.'
The Lord did not choose you because you are a large nation—indeed, you are the smallest of all nations. -Deuteronomy 7:7 I bless you to become the weakest. Then the Lord will appear more.

The Lord was with Joseph, so he became a successful man in the house of his Egyptian master. -Genesis 39:2 Joseph's status is slavery. Yet Scripture calls him a successful man. Betrayed, hated, having lost his position and much else, he is called successful. In contrast, his brothers who sold him are free and lack nothing seemingly, yet they became unsuccessful. The reason for success was that God was with him. Emmanuel itself is a blessing. When God is with us, our troubles and storms are not exempted; rather, we experience the living presence of the Lord in the midst of them.
The Lord blessed the Egyptian's house because of Joseph, so that the Lord's blessing extended to all his possessions in the house and in the field. Potiphar was blessed because of Joseph.

Until we are tested, even with grace, we are more like rough stone than a gem. An angel cannot test us. So He tests us through people. When the interpretation of life changes, 'the enemy' transforms into 'my life teacher.'
* The purpose of immortality—What sustains me is not the world and people. It is only the Lord. That is why life can be beautiful and glorious.
Transforming souls is not my work. I must entrust it completely to the Lord. That is why we pray and believe.

If you do not meet God in worship, like Israel, troubles never cease. A person can see himself when he worships God. If you cannot see yourself, everything appears twisted and the cycle of misunderstanding continues. Worship breaks that cycle. If worship collapses because work is busy, even a stray dog will attack.
* The power to advance—Believers advance not because circumstances permit but because through prayer they change circumstances. The Lord is wonderful. Pray great things for great purposes.
In Jesus, nothing is ever in vain. All the mistakes I thought were trial and error will become important purposes, and all things will work together for good. When in Jesus....

Jesus becomes stronger the more He is attacked. This is because faith from the beginning is relying on the Lord, not on my own strength. When difficulties come, He becomes stronger, and when I am weak, the Lord's power becomes even stronger. Strike a little more, until the blade becomes razor-sharp.

Jesus promised to give us a peace unlike the peace the world gives. It is absolute peace. No matter how rough the waves are, it is the absolute peace given by the sovereign Lord, regardless of the height and depth of those waves. When my mind is filled with many thoughts, the Lord upholds me. 'When my anxious thoughts multiply within me, Your consolations delight my soul.' -Psalm 94:19
Jesus does not force storms upon us. Neither does He ever lead us into them without giving us the strength to overcome them.

Jesus' death followed a public and continual method of execution. This is because the death of the Son of God is a tremendous declaration against darkness and death that cannot be hidden or bound, and it becomes the power that sets us free from sin.

From ancient times until now, the Lord has helped us, and He still helps us now. Samuel took a stone and set it between Mizpah and Shen, and said, 'Thus far the Lord has helped us.' He called its name Ebenezer. -1 Samuel 7:12

* Infinite Loyalty of a Finite Being To say that I am finite means I am limited by time and place. I can only work within my limits in the time and place given to me. In other words, beyond that realm, the Lord will work. If I remain faithful until death, the Lord's almightiness will accomplish what surpasses my limits.

* Do not be disappointed; fear the Lord instead. The reason for disappointment is that you trusted in yourself. The reason for separation is that you trusted in people. The reason for ruin is that you trusted in material things and circumstances. Only those who fear the Lord will shine like the stars of heaven.

* The Power of Refusal In an evil age, when you refuse comfort and ease and uphold your calling, you can live a life of obedience to God's intention in calling you to this place. We mistake thinking that we can follow the Lord's way while simultaneously satisfying our own desires. You receive blessings when you refuse your desires. If your wish is desire, then the Lord's desire is calling.

* The Purpose of Godliness In 1990, at Homyongsan Prayer Center in Daegu, the Lord rebuked me sternly. He said not to use the Lord's blessings to beautify ourselves before people. Many people will begin to treat godliness as material for profit. That is not serving the Lord, but making oneself the master and attempting to use God—a sin. Truly, woe will come to those who seek the pleasure of people. No matter how godly they appear, they will be infinitely ashamed before the Lord's judgment seat. Seek the Lord's pleasure.
* Powerful Supply There is no deficiency in God's supply for work that aligns with God's will.

Before saying you lack, try praying first. Retreating because of lack is the most convenient method, but also the worst choice. When lacking, come to the Lord and entrust yourself to Him. Life like barley bread, I entrust to the Lord. Living life without prayer is like a train trying to move without rails laid down.

* Anxiety Is this how I will grow old? Will I become a failure and loser in life? Don't I need to take some action? Worrying that I will become something I don't want—that is anxiety. To fulfill desires, anxiety is natural, but those who fulfill the Lord's will are bold and at peace because He accomplishes it.

Joseph went to the king in an official position at age 30 and lived to 110 years old. If Moses had benchmarked Joseph, he would not have left the palace even at age 40. Moses spent 40 years in the wilderness and was called at age 80. Do not envy others' lives; live the life God has given you. Even if it does not appear good....

* Limited Opportunity Opportunity is not always present. There is only the opportunity to sow love and gain life while I have breath. For believers, there is judgment for reward; for non-believers, there is judgment for sin. It is appointed for men to die once, and after this comes judgment. -Hebrews 9:27 They were each given a white robe and told to rest a little longer until the number of their fellow servants and brothers who were to be killed as they had been was completed. -Revelation 6:11
Do not pursue desires, but pray earnestly for God's will to be accomplished. The Lord will anoint your prayer. This is because it is praying for God's support.

* The Reason for Anxiety Simply put, the reason you are anxious is that you are trying to decide matters outside your authority. My future is not for me to decide, and I cannot change people's hearts. If you look to the Lord, who is not limited by circumstances and people, you will have no frustration or regrets. 'Many are the plans in a man's heart, but it is the Lord's purpose that prevails.' -Proverbs 16:9

* Why the Storm is Fierce If a test is too difficult or too easy, its ability to discriminate is reduced. Everyone can take it, but it is never easy, and the higher the standard, the higher the difficulty. Therefore, the wicked cannot endure judgment and fall away, but the blessed and righteous endure. The storm is fierce because not everyone is permitted to walk that path, and that place is prepared only for me. 'Not so the wicked! They are like chaff that the wind blows away. Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the assembly of the righteous.' -Psalm 1:4-5

Our society is a human network society based on school ties and regional connections. As the world says, we are experiencing a 'severed string'—the person who had my back or the hope and expectation I harbored have disappeared. We never had such a string from the beginning. Because we believed in and relied on a string that never existed, the Lord is showing us that the string we think is now severed is not really a string at all. Since it was never there, it was well severed, and there is no need to tie it again. But the Lord becomes our 'string.' Moreover, the Lord becomes a 'pillar' that cannot be compared to the world's 'string.' 'The Lord is my rock, my fortress and my deliverer; my God is my rock, in whom I take refuge. He is my shield and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold.' -Psalm 18:2

* Victory Even in Darkness All the people in the Bible did not walk seeing their own success. They were dark, but they ran to live the life the Lord desired. Those who stand in that place are already victors and blessed. 'Blessed is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked or stand in the way of sinners or sit in the seat of mockers. But his delight is in the law of the Lord, and on his law he meditates day and night. He is like a tree planted by streams of water, which yields its fruit in season and whose leaf does not wither. Whatever he does prospers.' -Psalm 1:1-3
The path we walk is not safe. Amid many challenges and trials, we sometimes see casualties. But it is not hopeless because His sovereignty exists. Be prepared to die.

If we obtain or enjoy anything, it all comes from God. John the Baptist looked to the Lord and said that we cannot receive anything unless God gives it. If we know the source of supply and the reality of success, our attitude will change. Drawing closer to my Lord is the way to victory.
Even when we do not know the Lord well, the Lord knows us very well. 'You have searched me, Lord, and you know me.' -Psalm 139:1

Our greatest mistake is trying to correct others according to our way and our appearance. Then usually all people retreat and are wounded. Remember, if you have been transformed, it is not so much by anyone as by the Lord who transformed you. <Acts 20:32> 'Now I commit you to God and to the word of his grace...' Entrust to the Lord, and He will give you wisdom and guidance.
We give up a day and a week not because the challenge is fierce, but because it seems difficult. Fall and get up and continue. Never give up watching the Lord.

Even when we face tribulation, we must unhesitatingly continue to hold fast to the truth and move forward. This is because no matter how dark circumstances appear, God is one who, with His great wisdom and power, hits a home run in the bottom of the ninth with two outs. 'Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.' -Galatians 6:9

* Even God Trying to Help God Trying to help is beyond the scope When circumstances are frustrating and you try to help God yourself, it severely limits God's sovereignty and work—a serious lack of faith. Abraham 'helping' God resulted in Ishmael's birth through Hagar, a seed of conflict. Peter drawing his sword in Gethsemane is another example. (The Lord must have been quite flustered amidst the urgency, searching for the severed ear in that midnight hour to reattach it.)
A person you think will be strong never becomes truly strong. Relying on such a person and taking action will result only in long regrets. This is because God cannot bear such a sight.

Our lives became so difficult and hard that I prayed with young people for grace and power. Within that short day, God poured out grace that had been prepared from very long ago. I thought I truly loved, but He makes me love more, bury it deeper in my heart, and shed more tears.

When hardship comes, we seek its cause, and when we cannot find it, we resent God. We think God gave the hardship or at least did not prevent it. Then we lose. We are mostly not permitted to know the cause of hardship. We must only come to God who can save me through faith.

We pray without knowing, but the Lord works while knowing. We pray inefficiently, but the Lord works with perfect efficiency. We simply raise and extend our hands, but the Lord covers us with an enormous hand. We ultimately entrust, but the Lord enters with unimaginable micro-management. Lord, please handle it. Deeply~~~

We sometimes cannot give an exact answer to why we are in this place. Despite not volunteering, we fight on the front lines. This is not our decision but the Lord's calling. If He called, He naturally gave us the ability to handle it.

We cannot arbitrarily give advice about or refuse God's method of making us. However, we can decide our heart and attitude toward God in that situation. Don't try to do everything; do what you can. What more should you worry about?

We are made by the Lord and in His hands. So we do not force things. We believe that entrusting to Him is the most right and good way. The Lord always gives me the best. If He did not give it, it is because it should not be given. What is not given is more beneficial. To Satan, the Lord boasted of Job's faith. Because of this, Job's trial began. So people understand that the theme of courage is hardship. The conclusion is that Job changes. A quite decent believer becomes a person of faith who sees the Lord directly. It seems God and Satan made a bet that started Job's suffering. No, it is the Lord's providence that causes us to abandon what is good and move toward what is best. So the theme of Job is grace. Satan's dispute is turned to make a person pure as refined gold. The ability to work all things together for good belongs to Him.

We are patients with a serious illness. During that enormous surgery, we can even come close to death. Truly merciless surgery. Through this major operation, the sick self within us is extracted. The scalpel and forceps God uses are none other than my own authority figure whose wounded heart and weakness pierce so painfully through resemblance.
We must believe and pray that God's kingdom—that is, God's supremely powerful rule—comes upon us and our land. After 14 hours, may you enjoy that rule and presence. The Lord does it.

* Hardship as Discrimination When a college entrance exam is too easy, everyone scores high and cannot be evaluated. The reason hardship is difficult is that it narrows the entrance, and that place is not for everyone but reserved only for me who received grace. If it is difficult, it is prepared.

We endure hardship and overcome challenges. But enduring and overcoming is not the purpose. Through them, we release God's rule and governance into our realm. You will be slandered and attacked. (Luke 6:26) But the suffering received now is incomparable to the glory, dominion, and rule you will enjoy in the future. (Romans 8:18) To release the Lord's rule in healing, prophecy, love, restoration, politics, economics, and sports, we must fight and endure. We must not give up. After that, God will use us as a light to the nations. (Acts 13:47)

* The Inerrancy of Scripture vs. My Fallibility Scripture sometimes conflicts with me. But there is no possibility Scripture will change. Since the beginning of creation, it has never happened, nor will it ever. In the end, what must change is not Scripture but me. God never apologizes or compromises.

Our prayer does not annoy God. Rather, it brings Him glory. The Lord will surely hear our prayer. If Jesus is my Lord, there is no reason He would not listen. When Jesus' name is exalted, God the Father is most pleased, and He will do what pleases Him. 'Whatever you ask in my name, I will do it, so that the Father may be glorified in the Son. You may ask me for anything in my name, and I will do it.' -John 14:13-14

* The Power of Giving Everything Why is there no power? Because you give only part. Simply give your whole life completely. When told to give everything, everyone is afraid. They say you cannot travel and there is no enjoyment. That is right. You will not be able to go the travels you wanted and will not have the enjoyment you wanted. Instead, there will be travels and joy with the pioneer the Lord has prepared. Going to the same Scotland matters more with whom you go.

Canaan, a land of idolatry, became holy not because the land itself is holy. Horeb mountain, where Moses always went, is not called a holy land because it is a sacred mountain. It is still a land full of sin and still barren and dry, but because the Lord's presence dwells there, it is called holy. If I take off my shoes at the place I stand (abandon my authority), God will be with me and rule that land. In the difficult land where you now stand, acknowledge the Lord's lordship. This will be acknowledged through my servantship. The more difficult, the more you should take off your shoes. 'God said, 'Do not come any closer. Take off your sandals, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.'' -Exodus 3:5
Command destiny to come. Those who have entrusted everything to the Lord have nothing to fear.
Cry out to destiny: Destiny, come! I do not fear, and I entrust all matters to the Lord.

* How Not to Waver When Suffering Loss Sometimes we earn less money and receive lower scores to obey Scripture. We suffer loss but must not waver. It is easy if you know the conclusion of those who obeyed. Those who live by faith receive from heaven. Whatever work you do, God's help is there.
Stop complaining. Even if you were grateful for every moment and every expression your whole life, you could not be grateful enough. What I have been given is the best.

When an enemy comes against me, should I attack or defend? David confessed this: 'The Lord spreads a table before me in the presence of my enemies.' -Psalm 23 If you think you are being chased and attacked by enemies, you would stab Saul who came into the cave and be done with it. But David thought he was being led by God. Do not counter-attack; accept the dinner invitation prepared by the Lord.
Jesus, the Lion of the tribe of Judah and yet a Lamb, heard all the accusations against Him, but responded with firm anger to challenges against His Father.
A person who received grace as a gift flows forth with wise and powerful governing ability that influences people in the Lord (Romans 5:17 reigning).

Answers require time and patience. It is not that God is limited by time, but rather that we need more time to let go, to cut away, and to be refined in order to become vessels worthy of receiving the Lord's answer. That time is the Lord's 'faithful care.' If you abandon attachment, you will receive the answer.

A doctor informs a patient of the severity of their condition before treating them. Not because there is no cure, but because he wants the patient not to return to harmful habits and to be managed for the long term. God allows us to experience the bottom before solving the problem. The goal is not the solution but transformation.
There is no coincidence in this world. Everything happens under the Lord's permission. When we think within the Lord's will, all circumstances become opportunities for us.

In Isaiah 14, there is a confession of the devil. In those short two verses, 'I will' appears five times. A stubborn heart that says 'I will do' means the initiative is always in me. When you go to the wilderness, receive grace, break and are shattered, you become soft-hearted and become a person who follows the Lord's will. I ask that you leave those with strong teeth as they are. All justifications and rationalizations will be broken before the hardships of the wilderness.
Now I pray that you become a person whom the Lord can confidently entrust with authority. When you gain authority, you must be one who understands where that authority came from.

Business is better than economy, and first class is better than business. But if the airplane is falling, before worrying about class, you must fundamentally board an airplane that does not fall. Jesus never crashes.

Man can never deceive God. He knows all your sitting and rising, lying down and waking. Behind brilliant good deeds, He discerns the twisted self-love concealed by packaging and the good motives hidden by painful failures. Ultimately, we will all stand before God.
Please abandon human greed and methods, and acknowledge the sovereignty of Him who has become my reason and praise. After that, He will open the way, fulfill His will, and care for us.

Popularity is momentary and disappears as quickly as it comes, and money is the same. Only what belongs to Jesus Christ is eternal. There is nothing more unpredictable than human relationships. In the Lord's sovereignty and reign, do not mourn and rejoice in the brief moment; look up to the Lord and move forward. The Lord is eternal.

You may think you have been guided, but it could be a dead end. Paul saw the Macedonian vision and came, but ended up imprisoned. If the situation given to me is a dead end, it is permitted because that experience is the best for me. Please pause and reflect in that dead end.

There are days in life when things don't go your way. When that happens, you can simply say, 'Today is a day when my will is not being done.' We do not live by emotion but by faith. Even David experienced depression of heart, but he did not let himself live that way. Do not hide it; confess it openly. That is normal.

It is easy to realize that life does not go your way even with effort. From the beginning, unexpected lives and encounters await me, and I feel myself drifting further from the life trajectory I drew. Behind this is the Lord's guidance. Many figures in the Bible did not live the lives they desired or could have anticipated, but lived according to what the Lord wished to accomplish.

At important points in life, it is God who opens the doors of our lives. That God upgrades us to a new life through the blessing of encounters. The ability to hold onto that encounter comes from skill, that is, authenticity.

The reason the storms of life intensify more and more is because we do not turn back to God. The storm does not stop until Jonah repents. Even if he makes the decision to be cast into the sea, a fish is prepared in the sea. God never negotiates.

The voyage of life must follow the light to avoid running aground and reach the destination completely. But among people, there are those who follow the light of another moving ship. That is foolish. That light passes away and cannot be a standard. Only the lighthouse of truth, Jesus, who is always present and eternal, should be pursued. When God is the standard, the power to overcome the world comes.

Life does not become marvelous in an instant. Just as there is a time and season for all things, wrinkles and gray hair appear, and we become wise. Rather than putting my hand to it, it is true wisdom and discernment to make room for and entrust the Lord to do it.

When life becomes twisted contrary to your wishes, there is the Lord's wonderful providence behind it. Augustine sought the life of a rhetorician, Paul that of a renowned rabbi, Joseph that of merely a prince of the Near East. When their lives became twisted, they were alarmed, but came to understand that a life where God intervenes is in a glorious and wonderful plan. Even if you fail an exam, fail in business, or have no place to dwell, the Lord will be with you in your confession that He is faithful. Even in circumstances you do not understand, the Lord is still faithful.

When a task feels frightening, it is not because the size of the task is greater than you. It is because you do not feel the presence of God. A person for whom God becomes their solution before an impossible task does not step back. 'Fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God. I will strengthen you, yes, I will help you, I will uphold you with My righteous right hand.' Isaiah 41:10
Receiving love without qualification is the irresistible grace and mercy of God that cannot be explained. Therefore, we can only be grateful.
Do not be content because of where you belong; God created me. Do not find meaning in your glorious relationships; God called me. Do not fear the world or pursue glory; God also sent me.

It is instinct for a person to try to protect themselves. The more you accomplish and prepare, the stronger this instinct becomes. When there is much to hold onto, greed arises. But what you must know is: 'I cannot keep myself.' Only when the Lord keeps me is 'not a single hair' harmed. This means that life is not preserved by carrying insurance and tapping bridge railings while walking. You must believe in the Lord.

We live in an age where people criticize far too easily without even understanding. Yet the Lord never entrusted such criticism and condemnation to us. 'I am not in position to say..., Brethren, do not speak evil of one another. He who speaks evil of a brother and judges his brother, speaks evil of the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are not an observer of the law but a judge. There is one Lawgiver and Judge, who is able to save and to destroy. Who are you to judge your neighbor?' James 4:11
If you are to boast, boast of weakness. In our weakness, the power of God abounds all the more. In weakness, God's power without impurity becomes complete.
If you boast, the Lord will surely come and find you.

Rather than trying to change your position, strive to change your posture. If your posture pleases the Lord's heart, your position will change of itself. If you worship the Lord just as you are in the place where He has called you, the Lord will see it.

You must look at your inner self. There are not many consciously proud fools. Pride is strong in the unconscious. Pride is a murderer unknown to me. Ambition masquerading as calling deceives both me and others, but the Lord is never deceived. Let us bow down before the Lord's fiery eyes and melt away.

A person who sees themselves through their own eyes becomes choked by people and the world. It means clinging and fretting. Because they are anxious about their insufficient appearance and feel chased. But a person who abides in the Lord fears nothing. Where you place your gaze determines all success and failure. 'I have calmed and quieted my soul like a weaned child with its mother; like a weaned child is my soul within me.' Psalm 131:2
If you try to accomplish your own will, you must struggle greatly and leave many wounds. If you try to accomplish the Lord's will, you must truly humble yourself and be entirely committed.
You must not think of things beyond your measure. It is not your portion and only brings bone-eating worry. If you acknowledge the sovereignty of the Almighty, you will have peace.
Three things that reflect your condition: 1. What do you boast of? 2. What causes you pain? 3. What are you attached to? The answers here rule you and are what you serve.

Do not be disappointed that your expectations and wishes are not fulfilled. Rather, rejoice that the Lord's sovereignty is acknowledged. You are not the master; the Lord is the Master and takes responsibility.
Examine your wishes. Then you will know who you are living for. A person who lives for what perishes will be ashamed, and a person who lives for the Eternal One will be honored.

When you confess through your weakness and inadequacy that you cannot go further down that path, the Lord, on the contrary, says to feed His sheep in that love. It is not your will, nor your zeal, nor your determination that upholds you, but only because I hold you. 'Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that in the Lord your labor is not in vain.' 1 Corinthians 15:58
If you pursue your own desires, you will be anxious and worried, but if you pursue the Lord's will, you will have peace and boldness.
Prayer that the Lord's will be done rather than pursuing your own desires is the quickest and most peaceful way.
Keep your place and do not depart; be vigilant. The Lord is almighty.
No matter how plausible and persuasive your judgment and thoughts are, the conclusion is that the Lord has chosen me. The Lord's conclusion will overshadow all evaluations and attacks. Be bold.

You need not fear becoming a castaway. God has good works prepared long ago for His purposes. Even if a successful person prepares for you, it is good, but God's preparation is most beautiful and perfect. 'For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand, that we should walk in them.' Ephesians 2:10
You think you cannot succeed because you are insufficient. That enormously limits the Almighty God. The Lord is listening now and searching. He is seeking to guide you.
If you have sought only the Lord's glory completely, act boldly. No one can break you down. The one who looks only to the Lord is the one who always wins.
Even if you are not recognized, you are still worthy. Therefore, there is no need to prove yourself or seek confirmation. Recognition of worthiness comes only from God.

A person who holds onto themselves greatly fears rejection, broken relationships, and loneliness. From the moment we are first born into this world, almost nothing goes according to my will. What must be clear is that the one who upholds me is God, and He always gives me what is best. When you know and believe that, 'forcing' disappears and you become free from 'attachment.' Of course, life becomes abundant.

There are times when your work becomes insignificant and your role seems inadequate. Then surely the demon of 'comparison' will come. When a person begins to compare, hell immediately takes hold. At that time, please pray like this: 'God, fulfill Your will toward me. I have come for this purpose, and I desire to fulfill Your plan. A person who fulfills the Lord's will has no need to compare. It is not my work but the Lord's work.'
A problem set free, a problem laid down before the Lord—the Lord will not trouble us with it again. Because there is no need to retake a course that has been passed.

My ability, surrounding circumstances, rumors, and crises are not the main issues. What matters is that the Lord sent me, and therefore the Lord's hand protects me. So the mark we focus on changes. Only to testify of the cross in that land and to see the glory of God.
Do not become too exhausted trying to be perfect in small things. No matter how small the task, it must be done by the Lord.

Successful people try to solve things themselves. There are plenty of such people. But in darkness, when I can do nothing, it means the Lord will do it. When there is no option, rejoice. Jesus, the only option, will be revealed.

Animals that cannot even rest when well are all herbivores. In contrast, strong predators have leisure. Become a spiritual warrior. Then you will have leisure. We are not predators, but like Psalm 23, making God our Shepherd, we experience lying in green pastures. Notice that the Lord makes the sheep lie down. The Lord's rest is gracious and sometimes even semi-compulsory. Get sick. There will be days you lie helplessly and rest.
Living well is not about gaining money and fame, but about living according to the purpose God has planned for us.

Even with disabilities and storms, our hearts toward the Lord cannot stop. We run toward the Lord and His Word. As time passes, we will run even faster, and at the end of this road, we will see the Lord. God never turns away from 'those who pursue Him earnestly.' - From Lesson 6 of Early Church and The Cry That Was Answered
When my determination and plans fail and I become discouraged, then the Lord works. When I am weak, I become complete and strong.
People keep asking if there's an evening prayer meeting. If you know, why do you keep asking? Even if nuclear war breaks out tomorrow or a tsunami hits, we will continue steadily.
I am a minister. I tend to be quite diligent. However, our young people will not perish without me, though it may be difficult. Because God is with us. The righteous live by faith alone.

I do understand that our friends live in an age when it is very difficult to keep the faith. There is physical weakness, sinful nature, and the enemy's work. But we can overcome. Why? Because we must overcome. Therefore, we will overcome and we will make others overcome.

The enemy has never been stronger. Israel has become weaker instead. Whether we serve God or worship idols is the watershed between peace and calamity. Whether we worship our emotions, desires, and wounds, or serve the only Lord—that is the true battle.

When we rely on the Lord with all our heart, the Lord works. To seek the Lord with all your heart requires no qualifications, credentials, or reputation. We seek because we are lacking, because we lack the basics. The Lord's work begins when we deeply feel our insufficiency.

Despair is a process leading to rest. The greater the despair, the greater the scope of the Lord's work. Despair is seeing our true condition and nature, while rest is seeing the Lord's righteousness, salvation, grace, and mercy.
Desperate situations don't change like weather forecasts according to seasons and time. I must change. I must repent and be corrected. That is the only way.
In my youth, I believed there were things I could not lose. But apart from God, every person and everything I depend on can be lost. That is why we must rely only on the Lord and be humble.

Come forth as pure gold. There is only One who knows the way I go—God. Only after God has refined me do I become pure gold. Pure gold is valuable not for its use, but because the substance itself is precious. The purpose of refining is to obtain pure gold. When impurities are removed from me, only Jesus remains. More important than Korean church problems and contemporary demands is 'doing well yourself.' The owner of pure gold is God alone, and once refined, one cannot dictate its use. But he knows the way that I take, when he has tested me, I will come forth as gold - Job 23:10
What a hopeful word—'the Lord reigns!' Therefore, there is no need to worry.

What is truly remarkable is that when I lay down my pride and acknowledge what I don't want to face, my shortcomings and failures, the work of the Almighty Father begins. Under the blood, this is why a Christian's failures become hope.

Do you truly believe in Jesus? Then the crisis in the true sense has already been overcome. It is the Lord's work, not tomorrow's. As a steward, I will be faithful and labor, but all responsibility and resolution belong to the Owner, the Lord.
Information belongs to God.
If you live honestly, ordinarily you will surely face hardship and loss. But in moments of crisis, at critical times, God always takes the hand of the honest.

Israelites whose goal is only milk and honey easily become discouraged and pass responsibility to others. But those whose purpose is God's face are not afraid of serious situations because the Lord is with them; the situation becomes something ordinary to consume. 'Do not fear the people of the land...they are bread for us. Their protection has been removed from them, and the Lord is with us. Do not be afraid of them.' - Numbers 14:8,9 Because He is with us, we are told not to fear.

From my personal experience, God rarely works in the way we expect. When people stand before the Red Sea, they think the only solution is a raft and returning to slavery. No one thought there could be a path within the sea. When we pray desperately, the Lord shows us a path that never existed before—a secret hidden from the world until now. 'Call to Me, and I will answer you, and show you great and mighty things, which you do not know.' - Jeremiah 33:3

If we confuse conditions with results, we get no answer. Resolution, fruit, phenomena, and victory are results, not conditions. Cause and origin are Jesus. There must be a cause for there to be a result. Results without cause are hypocrisy.
Do not be impatient. God is still working. Do not let tomorrow's anxieties rule over me. I am under the blood.
Not being anxious is faith. When we make up our minds—'whether death or loss'—all conditions disappear and only the Lord becomes our reliance. That is faith.

Having anxiety means I have my own standard, in other words, my own desires. We must lay down the standards by which we seek recognition from people and God. Rather than achieving some goal, when I surrender myself so God can be revealed through my life, I become known to God. Otherwise, we may do countless works yet become irrelevant before the Lord.
Seek not to be admired but to be trusted. Yet even this is not done by my strength. It is accomplished only when we look to the Lord and love souls.
To speak more frankly, you are so weak you could be called incapable. But in the Lord, you are strong. Infinitely so.
Do not do the ministry and love you want to do, but do what God desires. God's foolishness is wiser than human wisdom, and God's weakness is stronger than human strength. - 1 Corinthians 1:25

There is discouragement, but no defeat. In Jesus, everything becomes hope. He is almighty, and as long as I do not allow defeat, despair is impossible in Jesus. I pray that we testify to the Lord working among us with faith against the darkness that makes us see only circumstances. It will retreat in shame.

Believing what we want to believe is not faith. That is just superstition. Faith is believing in God's absolute sovereignty and providence and obeying today. When we trust in the Lord's almightiness, sorrow will be transformed into joy. Joseph named his firstborn Manasseh, saying, 'God has made me forget all my trouble and all my father's household.' And he named the second Ephraim, saying, 'God has made me fruitful in the land of my suffering.' - Genesis 41:51-52
I sow according to the Lord's Word, but it is God who makes it grow.
Entrust yourself to the Lord's hands. Let the Lord's hand hold me.
Entrust yourself to the Lord. The Lord's promises are eternal. Because He is my reason, I worship Him in any situation.
Those who bow humbly before the Lord will experience the Lord personally ruling over problems beyond what human words can address. Before the Lord's sovereignty, all problems melt away.

When we stand before the Lord, we discern and understand what His will is. If we see dimly as in a mirror, we are already in deep problems. When I behold the Lord as He has known me, I come to know eternal value, and worldly desires and lusts cannot hold me.
Be faithful before the Lord and gentle before the saints. God seeks such people and observes them as if recording in a ledger, pouring out His power upon them.

When I stand before the Lord with endless insufficiency, the Lord gives me mission and new strength. We must not become people who hide our true selves. Whether death or life, when we stand before the Lord in sincerity, even if it is confessed as a sigh from our lips, we believe that if the Lord acts, it is enough.

When I faithfully do my small work before the Lord, the Lord does great work on His side. Two loaves and five fish are indeed insignificant, but when held in the Lord's hands, they feed five thousand and bring forth life. I pray you plant with faith in good soil.
Paradise without the Lord becomes more tragic than original hell.
Conversation without the Lord is meaningless. Until the Lord intervenes, human life is in a state of closed business with no solution.
Zeal without the Lord is worse than laziness. More important than zeal is the core. A life where the Lord is the core is blessed.

There was a time when I could do small and great things as I pleased without hearing the Lord's voice. But now is the time to listen to that voice and obey. God is pleased with a heart that asks and listens, and through His good pleasure, He leads us in wonderful ways. That is why when a faithful person buys an airplane ticket, even unbelievers want to board the same plane.
Entrust the steering of your life to the Lord. It is not too late even now. God is the only one who can bring the best results from the worst.
Whatever is not received from the Lord will collapse and be rejected. No kindness or consideration that does not come from the Lord will bear good fruit.

There are times when the Lord makes us pour water into a bottomless jar. Remember this: it is not the end. Even though pouring water seems to accomplish nothing, during that time muscles develop and constitution changes. In the Lord's time, explosive revival, recovery, and life's power will appear.

Let us look only to the Lord. Even now the Lord searches the whole earth to demonstrate power to those who look only to Him (those with hearts devoted wholly to him - 2 Chronicles 16:9). Dead fish are swept away by the currents. But living fish swim against the current. I pray that all of you who pray may have the strength to swim against the tide that everyone in the world follows, the strength to live against the current of history, the strength to live counter to the values of this age.
If there is anything I depend on and boast in more than the Lord, it will be taken away. Time will be wasted and I must start over from zero. Only the Lord is true satisfaction.

The Lord does not solve everything at once my way. He gives gradual recovery through grace that helps in due time. The fact that He did not work according to my method and schedule does not mean the Lord is not working. That is why I am thankful still. 'Do not be afraid of them, for the Lord your God, who is great and awesome, is among you. The Lord your God will drive out those nations before you, little by little.' - Deuteronomy 7:21-22
The Lord is Lord over all relationships. I pray you invite the Lord to the center of your life.

The Lord is omnipresent (ubiquitas). That means He is with me now and works in proportion to the place I have given Him in my heart. Joseph, who lived mindfully of the Lord, overcame the temptation of sin, did not become discouraged, and did not seek revenge.
The Lord transforms moments of anguish and sorrow into the revelation of salvation on His level.
We say that prayer is breath. Without the Lord's help, nothing happens naturally. Everything is the Lord's boundless grace. Pray continually—1 Thessalonians 5:16

The Lord gives us the most beautiful places. He has granted us family and companions, gifts and service. He has also given us tears of sorrow and piercing pain. Through these, He will make all things beautiful. The Lord's zeal will surely be fulfilled. Blessed be the Lord who gave us pain and suffering. His lovingkindness is eternal. The lines have fallen to me in pleasant places; indeed, my heritage is beautiful to me—Psalm 16:6
The Lord surely hears our prayers. He knows our hearts and circumstances. Because He created that deep place within us.
The Lord has never taken His eyes off me for a single moment. Even when I was an unbeliever, His eyes were upon me.

Become one who follows the Lord. Follow even in fear, follow even in hardship. Even when opinions differ, if the Lord goes, abandon everything and follow without hesitation. This is what it means to be a Christian. The Lord is at work.
When I interpret my life through the Lord's eyes, I see opportunities that were hidden before.

Without the Lord's help, it is far better to perish in the Lord than to solve problems. If the Lord does not help, nothing can help. We need not ease our symptoms through human methods, change of atmosphere, or pretense. When the prayer the Lord requests deepens, the problem disappears, and it is enough to walk with the Lord. 'Lord, my heart was broken as I prayed, but now I am already satisfied in You. Let Your will be done. My soul is satisfied in You.' 'Unless the Lord builds the house, those who build it labor in vain; unless the Lord watches over the city, the watchman stays awake in vain.'—Psalm 127:1 'My father, how shall I help you? Unless the Lord helps you, what can I do?'—2 Kings 6:26-27
Peace comes when we confirm that nothing can be done according to our wishes except through the Lord's will. Because we find peace only when we set aside our desires.
Those who have truly grasped the Lord's grace, abandoned the world, and returned to Him have one real regret—that they returned so late and wasted so much time.

*That is not human mistake but God's providence. An ambulance surpasses all other vehicles ahead. It passes drunk drivers, reckless drivers, inexperienced drivers, and unlicensed drivers. Because it seeks to save lives. It is not mistake but providence. God is saving me and saving all. Providence's purpose is not condemnation but salvation of life. God saves life—Genesis 45:5, 7
Believe in the Lord's omnipotence and wait quietly. Always humans worsen their situation through fear. Yet how amazing is God's love and grace that overturns such circumstances.
The greatest hindrance to relying on and trusting in the Lord's omnipotence is not Satan but my own self-effort. Trusting the Lord begins with acknowledging my own helplessness.
I have heard your prayer and seen your tears—2 Kings 20:5 God hears and sees. When He hears and sees, He accomplishes all things.

When we acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty, we will see the Lord's work. The God who gave His Son through the cross gives us precious people to love and be loved by, work to serve, and even a longing we do not understand. That is why we offer our bodies.
Without the Lord's permission, no one can harm me. But when I react and respond to each incident, my own evil will harm me. Persecution rather makes me a precious person of faith.
The Lord delays His conclusion for the purpose of truly giving Himself.
When I decide what only the Lord should decide, it is suffering. After refinement, I become faithful to death in the small work the Lord has given me. The Lord is watching.

I can sing that the Lord is just not because my vested interests and evaluations have comparative advantage or at least plausibility, but because of the Lord's presence that others do not have. This allows me to see what others have not seen, hear voices that have not been heard, and enjoy love that has been rejected. Though I have no possessions—'Me' by Song Myung-hee, poet
The Lord's response to prayer does not mean He will adjust problems to my taste. It means He will review and handle them with God's wisdom.
If the Lord is my King and I have resolved to praise only Him forever, what more is a problem? All problems and trials are condensed into the question 'Who is King?'
If the Lord had not given life, we could not move even our little finger as we wish. In the Lord there is neither easy nor difficult work. There is only the work of Jesus living within us.
That the Lord is omnipotent means that I am bold and enjoy true peace. Whether I live or die.
The Lord is sovereign. That means He resolves all things.

If the Lord does not give, we have received nothing. Do not be troubled, agonize, or hope for fortune over what we have not received and cannot receive. Be faithful to what no one looks at but the Lord gives. The Lord will look upon you. This is the Lord's strong request this morning. John answered and said to him, 'A man can receive nothing unless it has been given him from heaven.'—John 3:27
If the Lord has not given something, striving for it wastes time. To pour our effort into the place we must stand and the work we must do is holiness. Focus on what the Lord desires.
If the Lord has not given it, we have received nothing. Do not be troubled by many variables; be at peace in the Lord. The Lord never forsakes us.

The way the Lord gives is the most blessed way. Forcing ourselves to go the way He has not given is called greed. When we satisfy greed, it seems we will be joyful, but we become most miserable. This is why I am not weary even walking all day in a dry land when the Lord is my way.

If the Lord does not guard, the watchman's vigilance is in vain. It means that all our labored relationships, our careers, social recognition, and so on become wasted effort. We must work hard, but effort alone is not enough. We must rely on the Lord to guard us.

A life that is blessed only by the Lord; a life where cleverness and human tricks do not work; a life where the probability and luck that comes so easily to others do not favor us, and we can only entrust and go forward—this life has blessing.
When we do what the Lord commands, there is nothing to fear or regret. If it is what the Lord permits, even loneliness has worth.

Because the Lord is with us, there is nothing to fear. Because it is the work the Lord desires, we will not grow weary. For His eternal glory, all other work is necessarily secondary. Like riding a roller coaster in an amusement park—hold the handle firmly. Now we run forward.

Believe and accept that what the Lord has permitted is the best. And what we have received contains not only what is mine but surely a portion to share. Though our life is like five loaves and two fish, when placed in the Lord's hands, it feeds many.

If the Lord has not permitted it, we can do nothing. If the Lord commands me to keep this place, I will not move and will be faithful forever. Because the Lord is the Lord. That the Lord has not permitted means to stake our life on what He has permitted. I belong to the Lord.
Do not be satisfied with what the Lord allows; dedicate and volunteer for what He leads. This becomes a joyful life.

*Faith—Faith is 'simply' believing in a situation where no amount of logic can compel belief. No matter how much I calculate, there is no answer, but the Lord has already concluded. Though He said He would establish descendants through Isaac, He commanded to offer Isaac as a burnt offering—a contradiction. This is a contradiction in my ability and logic, but only He has the answer. Abraham, when tested, offered Isaac by faith, he who had received the promises offered his only begotten son, of whom it was said, 'In Isaac your seed shall be called,' concluding that God was able even to raise him from the dead, from which he also received him in a figurative sense—Hebrews 11:17, 19

We clenched our hungry bellies, fasted, and prayed earnestly. Yet it is the Lord who transforms and meets us, and He alone is sovereign. Without the Lord's grace, no religious act is of any use. I pray again earnestly that the Lord grant us His presence on that day.

*Heart—The reason God looks at the heart rather than credentials or reputation is to find one whose heart aligns with God's heart. Only then can God's will be fully accomplished on this earth. 'I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after My own heart, who will fulfill all My purpose'—Acts 13:22 'For the Lord does not see as man sees; for man looks at the outward appearance, but the Lord looks at the heart'—1 Samuel 16:7

When you possess what the Lord did not give, you will feel fear and anxiety. Because you know it will be taken again. When you possess what the Lord gave, you will be at peace. Because no one can take what belongs to the Lord. Not only be grateful for what is given, but be moved by gratitude each moment.
Be humble under the mighty hand of the Lord.

Become an undefeated life. Once or twice you might win through luck, several times through skill. But even Olympic gold medalists cannot always win, and in many fields and circumstances, we must be satisfied with barely surviving. But God's man David had no one who could stand against him his whole life, and he never lost in any of his many wars. In terms of probability, this is 0% and can be called a miracle. 1. David was a man gripped by his calling. When he saw Goliath, he did not go out calculating; he went out with the righteous indignation the Lord poured into him. The Lord gives strength to those gripped by their calling. If you do not fulfill your calling, you cannot die. I do not hold my calling; my calling holds me and carries me forward.

Sunday message: 1. Do not live as you wish but as you are needed. Though there are many pens you want to have, when writing you need only one. Though there are many people you want to befriend, there is only one to marry. Live not with greed but with contentment. 2. The Lord honors those who honor Him and despises those who despise Him. Do not treat God carelessly. Serve with all your heart. Ask yourself: Who is your master and what is the cause of your actions? 3. There must be grief and sorrow. Do not stand indifferent like a spectator watching your life and community crumble. Ask for God's rule in your workplace, academy, family, and relationships. Those who do not ask have no right to happiness.
To overcome what we fear most, we need the strongest Being. What we fear most is ourselves. That is why the mightiest Lord holds us.
Go not as I desire but as God pours out, as the Lord fills.
Those who make the confession 'Let me die,' God fills with abundantly.
For those in whom death becomes a reward, no tribulation or persecution in this world can hinder. Because what lives within them is not desire and lust but Christ, death itself is gain.
As you intercede, God Himself is the King over this retreat.
The Lord who sees the heart is not deceived. When the Father cuts me, it is because He is good. The Father's character is the highest good. Therefore, what He does for me is the highest good.

To call 'the unimportant important' and 'the important unimportant' is falsehood and error. In such a place, what matters is truth. Not diluting the 'original power' of Jesus' cross is authority. Grasping the undiluted original—the cross itself—not a watered-down copy, is God's ace card for this dark world. I have nothing to boast of except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. <Galatians 6:14>.

Evidence that seems absurd because it is not visible to the eye right now—only God can give this. Believe in the Almighty who sees the result beforehand, takes it as evidence, and uses the outcome as motivation. God said, 'I will surely be with you. After you have led the people out of Egypt, you will worship God on this mountain. This will be the sign to you that it is I who have sent you.' (Exodus 3:12).
Solving the most difficult problem you face right now does not bring peace. True peace comes because He is with you in the midst of the storm. Seek to be with One who is greater than your problems.
Right now, the evidence of 'gratitude' is 'victory.' And the evidence of 'victory' is 'gratitude.'

That you are there right now is God's grace. That He placed you there at first was His intervention and grace, and even now He holds you by that grace. Not by personal choice or luck, but by God's sufficient grace, He sustains me. Faith is walking that path which you cannot walk without the Lord. At this place where I stand, become a worshiper, dyed with the Lord's blood.

The very thing you now regret not having, had God granted it, you would have drawn closer to sin and further from God. Do not lament what you did not receive; rather, give thanks that because of this, you received the Lord as a gift.

The people given to me now and the work I serve are the best things given to me at this very moment. God always gives the very best. Realizing this is mission, and those who live without mission live the life that should be most happy as the most miserable. God saw all that He had made, and it was very good. - Genesis 1:31.

The reason I can be bold right now in all these circumstances is because I believe in the Lord's will. So even if I lose, I am not sorrowful, and when I gain even a little, I do not become arrogant. Those who walk the Lord's path are bold.

Right now, acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty and receive His protection and guidance by faith. 'Though ten thousand assail me on every side,' the Lord who gives peace to 'lie down and sleep and wake' will protect and guide you by His sovereignty. - Psalm 3.

I am God's kingdom where His reign is established. Through me, the community I belong to will be blessed, and I am the source of blessing through which God blesses the world. When I advance, God's kingdom comes to that place.
If you cannot be happy now, you cannot be happy later. If you cannot love now, later you will become a person full of hatred. The Lord always gives me the very best.

That I have been permitted to proclaim the Lord's name despite my unworthiness was because of the Lord's burning compassion. If tomorrow I am again permitted to proclaim that Most High Name, it will also be because of the Lord's endless grace.

If I have lived by God's grace until now, I must continue to depend on the Lord's grace going forward. Living a life that quickly leaves the Lord when things are manageable is truly cooking rice only to feed it to dogs. Since you will believe anyway, believe well. There is one God, but even in faith there are genuine articles and counterfeits.
Even now, though you may shed tears, sow the seed. You will surely reap with joy.
