Now, as in Elijah's time, Ahab was the most evil king in Israel's history. Yet the most powerful prophet in history was Elijah of that same era. The darker the age, the more powerful is the light that drives out that darkness. Who will become more powerful? 'POST TENEBRAS LUX' - After darkness, light.
What is disaster now will become great blessing when it turns around later.
Now is the time to seek the Lord; break up your unplowed ground, for it is time to seek the Lord, until He comes and showers His righteousness on you. - Hosea 10:12 - A word given at Christmas 1990.

Just as a map without a compass is useless, a plan without vision is nothing but aimless rushing. Vision is consistent, but occupation changes. I move toward the vision the Lord gave me, yet I have lived in many forms—as a student, engineer, missionary, and so on. Many people mistake their occupation for their vision. Occupation is the guidance of God that seems most necessary at that time to fulfill the vision. It may even be as a prisoner or slave, like Joseph.

If you think of payment merely as simple expense, you cannot do business because you feel sorry. Rather, it is investment. When you know by faith that there is One watching over you, life becomes much simpler and easier than it is now.
If you try to keep, you will lose. If you give, He will give you something better.

Wisdom does not fall from heaven; it is using well the ability you have. Using the sling you possess (like David) is the greatest wisdom. When God gives grace, He strengthens what you have rather than giving something new. 'The Lord said to him, What is that in your hand? He said, A staff.' - Exodus 4:2. When a dry staff is held by His power, it parts the Red Sea and brings water from the rock.

Wisdom transcends the world's law of cause and effect. For example, it is understanding that two seemingly unrelated topics—that unless you honor the Lord, adversaries rise up and wars never cease—are intimately connected. Before seeing the problems in my reality, I must discern the cause in the attitude of fearing the Lord.

Winning at work and school is not easy, especially when your boss or professor seems to hold your life and death in their hands. However big the people and problems appear, you must acknowledge that God is all-powerful. Then the issue becomes merely a speck of dust.

Commit your way to the Lord; trust in Him and He will do this: He will make your righteousness shine like the dawn, the justice of your cause like the noonday sun. - Psalm 37:5,6. Entrust even your body and the clothes on your back to Him. He will accomplish it.
Above all, forgive this situation. And lift it up to the Lord. The Lord is all-powerful. Acknowledge His sovereignty.

True recovery does not begin with organizing your desk or changing your mind. It begins with smashing the idols within you. An idol is whatever you depend on more than God, whatever exercises greater influence over your decisions. Rather than this nation, this church, or this household being wrong, I must first stand right, and then all will live. Joshua said to the people, 'Consecrate yourselves, for tomorrow the Lord will do amazing things among you.' - Joshua 3:5.

True blessing is what the Lord gives. When the Lord gives, it means there is a measure and a scope that the Lord provides. If you think you have what you don't have and haven't received, you become anxious and troubled—that is greed. Reject what the Lord has not given. Rather, boast in your lack and weakness. He said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.' - 2 Corinthians 12:9.

The characteristic of an earthen vessel is that the vessel itself has no value, it breaks easily, and most critically, besides its only function of holding, it has no other options. If you fill it with treasure, that is victory. Leave everything else under His sovereignty.
There are those who cling and seek to fulfill their desires, and there are those who surrender themselves to be nailed to the cross instead, achieving salvation and life.

Do not be chased. It is not the devil chasing us; rather, we are called to chase them. We will expose all falsehood and deceit and burn them in Jesus' name. [Matthew 16:18] 'I tell you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of Hades will not overcome it.' * Hades: the power of death, * Gates: doors, * Overcome: to be overcome. Looking at the original language, it means we break down the devil's gates in an offensive manner. It is attack, not defense.
For me to decide on my own to help or relieve someone is arrogance. The One who begins is God, the One who leads is God, and the One who completes is God.

Worldly wisdom teaches methods and techniques for dealing with people. It has temporary effects. But the absolutely critical relationship that determines the victory or defeat of life is established through prayer. If a person pleases God through his conduct, God has the power to reconcile even enemies. God is the owner of all relationships.

Be content with what you have. It is not a matter for complaint but for thanksgiving. In the world there are things that belong to Caesar and things that belong to God. But what belongs to Caesar also belongs to God and is merely entrusted for a time. All is the Lord's grace.
When you go to heaven, you will be greatly surprised. It is that I was not a victim, but rather that I came there by the Lord's mercy.

That others call me an angel does not make me an angel, nor does being called a demon change me into one. The reason Paul did not care about people's judgments was that he fixed his gaze on the only Judge. In fact, the reason people often become heated is not justice but inferiority within themselves. If the level is different, there is no response. 'I care very little if I am judged by you or by any human court; indeed, I do not even judge myself.' - 1 Corinthians 4:3. You do your own thing. Therefore judge nothing before the appointed time until the Lord comes. - 1 Corinthians 4:5. Do not overreach.

When young people experience disruption in life, they are bewildered. It is because they only look at the present reality. Reality says, 'Those who cannot do it cannot do it,' but what is needed then is to clearly reconfirm, 'Where am I called to go?' That is mission. When mission is clearer than 'life's disruption,' it becomes the strength to rise again, and God never disappoints those who have risen.

Sixty elementary school children praying in unity, pooling wisdom, encouraging each other, and proclaiming 'we can do it,' and even writing in blood, cannot solve a calculus problem. Only when you become a high school senior can you solve it. Do not try to solve the problem; raise your level. Rather than solving our problems directly, the Lord desires that our level be raised. This difference in perspective is why it is difficult. Be patient. These are problems that will all be solved when the wilderness school is finished.

Though it appears shabby and shoddy, if the Lord does not give it, I have received nothing. So I must use everything I have received to the fullest. I can do all things in the strength of the One who gives me power.

There are things that do not happen even with your best effort. To be precise, it is not that they did not happen, but that they did not happen as you wished. That means it was permitted under the Lord's sovereignty, and if your intention and best effort were present, it means something better.

If you do not pray sufficiently, you will blame others. You react sensitively to personality, response, and speech, and above all, you do not commit all things to Him. Prayer quiets your soul. Because you come to know who the Master is.

Whether holding the hilt of the sword or the blade in an absolutely critical situation, a Christian's attitude is always consistent. You do not become arrogant in favorable circumstances, nor do you despair in unfavorable ones. Only the Lord establishes me and leads me.
When it is dark, I have no option to choose. I must only make room for the Lord to work and cry out in prayer.

Do not be troubled by the evaluation and reaction of those who do not know you well. Rather, be happy because of the One who made you, knows you, and calls you. Faith is a battle between sight and focus.
For Christians, there is no crisis. There is only opportunity. The opportunity to focus on the Lord.
Do not worry; do your part. Then you will experience what the Lord does.

When a large car and a small car meet, the small car must back up. The admiral of a great warship can command the blocking vessel to move aside. But the ordinary sailor guarding the lighthouse is the only one in the navy who can refuse.

The prodigal could return home at all because even the pods the pigs ate, no one gave him. If you are adequately provided for, you cannot repent until death. Being brought to the end with nothing to depend on can be a blessing. Making you look only to the Lord because you have nothing else to depend on.

Before Paul's fifth visit to Jerusalem, he received multiple warnings and dissuasions from spiritual servants and co-workers of the Lord due to great tribulations and bonds. On an Alexandrian ship, he faced situations where hope of survival disappeared many times due to severe storms, yet he remained composed and bold until the end. This was because he knew two secrets: the Lord's timing and the Lord's will. Faith is never complicated.

When opposition comes, do not regard it as an obstacle but as a signal to redirect toward drawing closer to the Lord. The reason the Lord gives thirst and deficiency is so we respond by drawing near to Him rather than doing meaningless things.

A single text message, a passing conversation, a smile at the corner of my mouth, my hurried typing due to impatience, my somewhat breathless voice in a phone call—I know that even in each of my heartbeats and breaths, there is the Lord's care and guidance. The Lord becomes my guide.

Saturday Message Summary: James 4:4–10. Do not live under pressure. Many people lose relationships, peace, and health under oppression. 1. Befriend the world and become enemies with God. 2. Submit to the Lord and resist the devil. Then you will escape. 3. If you honor God, God will honor you.
The greatest and most beautiful miracle is transformation. Only the Lord does this.

I especially pray for co-workers preparing and laboring in today's Sunday ministry that through the Lord's sovereignty, all situations and work will be fully governed by the Lord's great hand. Fear, anxiety, and worry will depart, and only what the Lord permits and accomplishes under His sovereignty will be fulfilled. The Lord desires it more earnestly than we do.
May you have peace. The Lord reigns. When the Lord becomes King, He takes full responsibility for this situation and breaks the yoke through His anointing.
The premise of peace is that the Lord's will be done. —Matthew 6:10

Walking on level ground and cultivating good soil will bring true blessing. However, even if we toil painfully in a stony field and collapse, it will be great glory. The work the Lord calls us to do and the place He calls us to stand is the promised land, the holy land.

More important than reputation is the Lord's will and help. It is not the world's reputation that matters but history changes because of one prepared person, like Hezekiah. The Lord prepares and expects from people.

To exercise violence is because one is weak. Thus, one subdues the other to overcome that inferiority. All abusive language and violence contain the desire to prove oneself. It means one lacks that much confidence. A person who truly possesses authority and power is gentle. There is no need to prove oneself any longer. In the Lord, no further proof is needed; we only testify of the Lord.
Loose it; if you loose it on earth, it will be loosed in heaven.

An unresolved problem actually protects me. It makes me pray and causes me to avoid wrong choices through deep providence. The lords of the Philistines were angry with him, and the Philistine lords said to him: Send this man back so that he may return to the place the king has assigned to him. He must not go down with us into battle, or he will turn against us during the fighting. How better to regain his master's favor than by handing over the heads of our own men? Isn't this the David they sang about in their dances: 'Saul has slain his thousands, and David his tens of thousands'? —1 Samuel 29:4–5. The one destined to be king was protected through the Philistine officials' opposition from becoming Israel's enemy while in exile.

When storms arise, seek the great. If you fear the Lord, storms will seem like nothing. He said to his disciples, 'Why are you so afraid? Do you still have no faith?' They were terrified and asked each other, 'Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!' —Mark 4:40–41

A sense of victimhood is delusion. Nothing happens to me without the Lord's permission. What the Lord does not know does not happen to me. Ultimately, the company's personnel decisions affecting me, the government's authority, and sometimes unjust oppression and discrimination cannot exceed the Lord's omnipotence. To regard the harm that befell me as greater than the Lord's omnipotence and omniscience is delusion.

God's kingdom will have many reversals. Do not try to correct people's responses but rather love and revere the Lord as the center; then great and mysterious works and God's righteousness will be accomplished.
In God's kingdom policy, Satan's requests are never granted under any circumstances. There is no intention to give even an inch. Rather, we advance forward.

The two pillars of God's temple, Jachin and Boaz. God Himself established them. God alone is our strength. God's church was established by God Himself, and the Lord is its power. Two pillars—the gospel and prayer—are sufficient.
When we stand before God without presuming and with integrity and honesty, we will enjoy what God has prepared in His time.

I am here only because of the Lord. Therefore, I can overcome all things and am unconcerned. Before people's voices and spiritual challenges, even amid situations I cannot bear and am collapsing, I can laugh. The Lord is my only vision.
To truly establish a new relationship with God means to establish a different relationship with sin. How has God taken possession of my heart and worked?
No one has ever given anything to God. All authority originates from God.
Submit to God and resist the devil. The secret to victory is taking off your shoes before the Lord. When rights are surrendered, the Lord works.

Please cast out excessive burdens and stress that do not come from God in the name of Jesus. God is the only One who can cause us to dwell in peace, and He is the Father who works and accomplishes even within that peace.

If we receive what does not come from God, we become enslaved. When we receive money and gain power, we are dragged around by the world. If the Lord has not given it, we can simply refuse it cleanly. Who tempts you? We are beings pursuing not the riches the world desires but what the Lord desires.

When God called me, He knew all my foolishness and foolishness, but He did not despise me. He changed me with wisdom and power. This is entirely so that Jesus becomes all, so we put on Christ, display Christ, and live in Christ. 'Rather, clothe yourselves with the Lord Jesus Christ, and do not think about how to gratify the desires of the sinful nature.' —Romans 13:14
God has not answered all my prayers. If He had, I would have married someone else and married several times over.

The truth that if God does not help, we can only perish is not an ominous sign but something to rejoice and be glad about. The Lord helps those who seek His face and whose hearts align with His by pressing them forward with the host's one-sided support.

There are times when God pours out an excessive measure of grace through His Word. Times when He pours out more than the Word needed just to live today. As we live life, we pass through deserts where we must walk long periods without supply. Then we endure like camels with the stored Word. A camel crosses the desert for a month on the fat stored in its hump without eating.

God will use our young people. He will awaken continents and spread revival like wildfire. He will pour out grace we cannot imagine and never thought of, and all people will marvel. Human credit will disappear and the Lord's sovereignty will come. Continue to pray. Move forward with faith that the Lord will surely work. What we must hold onto is humility to the end and not falling to Satan's deception. Resolve not to respond to darkness and counter with renouncing rights and a contrary spirit. Continue praying for one another and become young people who cast fire on this land through sacrificial worship and costly sacrifice. Even if we labor for a day, we desire to live that way.
God's discipline is not to make us successful. His will is for us to become holy like Him. If we seek success, the tunnel will be longer, but if we seek holiness, we will soon see light.

That God distinguished us among all people under heaven and gave us special grace is His being with that people Himself. Walking with God, His presence is the greatest sign and blessing. Become holy through the power of the blood.
God hears and knows all. If God knows, God will also solve it. If we hastily pursue my methods, we will only worsen, not solve, the problem.
If we only look to God, people's evaluation becomes colorless. 'God knows,' one word makes even life not precious.
When we only look to God, people's evaluations fade and we become free from all eyes. We are not elevated by receiving recognition nor lowered by being despised. It comes only from the Lord.

Anyone who relies on something other than God will surely increase suffering. The Lord is living water and the only solution. Those who pursue possessions and recognition instead of God, and satisfaction of the flesh, will surely increase suffering if they wait a little longer. It is truly foolish. Those who make offerings to other gods will increase suffering. I will not pour out libations of blood to them or take up their names on my lips. —Psalm 16:4
God does not seek the strong. He seeks the obedient. God is already strong. God's power is perfected in my weakness.
God is not deceived by results. Rather, He examines motives. That is why we live by dying.
God is never late and God never fails.
God knows me and knows my circumstances. You can feel it through prayer. Because He has heard, you become confident He will accomplish it.
God meets my needs. But He does not satisfy my vanity. Regarding oneself as content is the secret of all things.
God is the one who keeps you.

God sees David differently. David's purpose differs from others. Even while fleeing from enemies, even when withdrawn from the throne and facing death threats from his son, what he truly longed for was worship in the temple—beholding God. When the object of longing changes, that grace was not withdrawn even after thousands of years.

Before God met David, He had no particular thought of establishing a king and sending the Messiah through that house. Through the covenant made with David, Abraham's blessing reached David, and He used his descendants as kings and ultimately brought forth the Messiah. Become a person who, with united hearts, makes things exist that did not exist.

God pours His blessings over the confession 'If it is not Your will, it is good to give up' and 'Still, I love You, Lord.' He exalts one who can be humble after being elevated, one who can truly be happy by fearing the Lord, and through such a person, He will reveal God's glory.

God guides all people. If people accept God's guidance, He guides based on that obedience; if they do not accept it, He guides based on that refusal. Furthermore, despite each individual's unfaithfulness and loss of purpose, God accomplishes His purpose and vision transcending that in God's way. Therefore, we must acknowledge that we know not at all, not merely that we know little, and move forward in faith.
God awakens our sleeping souls through 'problems.' But He does not solve that problem immediately. He waits a little longer until we truly desire transformation.

Before the water covers the earth, God holds back for 120 years for Noah and the wicked world's opportunity for repentance. And after Noah's household enters the ark, He waits seven more days. Only after these seven days pass does the rain come down.
When God uses people, He does not look at past abilities and assign present tasks based on them. David was young and inexperienced, but he loved the Lord and confessed that He was his strength.

God found Saul, prepared him, and arranged a rather unpleasant event to anoint him as king—the loss of valuable donkeys. The grace that the Lord has prepared may not be visible until something is lost.
God had Abraham look toward all four directions and promised to give him that land. It meant He would give him as much as Abraham could see. Therefore, what you see is ability.
God lets us go when we calculate and scheme, but He will certainly set things right later. This is because the Lord works with low cost and high efficiency.

God has predetermined when, where, and how long we will live. We are within the Lord's care and love. He made every nation of mankind from one man to live over all the earth, and He marked out their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands (Acts 17:26).
God transforms us to use us, and for that transformation, He blocks the corner I trust in. He makes mink into dog hair.

God causes us to shed tears to mature us, but in truth, God's heart aches even more. You can confirm this by seeing that when training is complete, He blesses us according to the depth of that pain. God is faithful.

God created us 'absolutely.' This means we are not relative beings. Therefore, we must not compare ourselves. When you compare at work or school, you suddenly become extremely unhappy. Only the Lord determines my path. Be bold.
God is the One who fills us. However, He is not the One who fills greed, but the One who fills need. The Lord tests us more often through finances than we think. Where is your heart?
To those who seek first His kingdom and His righteousness—God's sovereign rule and relationship—He gives blessings beyond measure.

God does not ask us to be responsible for results. We do not have that ability. He only desires that we walk with Him. Looking at results alone, Moses was called too late, he traveled in forty years a distance that could be covered in four, and ultimately did not enter Canaan. Yet Moses is a glorious person because he obeyed what the Lord commanded.
God never apologizes to us because He is almighty and keeps us as the apple of His eye.
A people who do not know gratitude do not perish. They do not even have to try to perish because they have already perished greatly.

God sometimes gives us incomprehensible uncomfortable emotions. It is His way of saying, 'Turn back.' He is saying, 'Please stop watching people's faces, stop looking at circumstances, and look only at the Lord.'
God does not say sorry to us. Not because of bad character or lack of manners, but because He never does anything for which to apologize. The Lord is faithful.

God wants to give us unimaginable grace. The challenges and trials we must face are far greater than our level. Without receiving grace and struggling, like a child cannot overcome an adult, we will lose every battle. We cannot match in strategy and strength. How can we fight? Grace requires humility.

God wants partnership with us. Not because He is lacking, but because He is almighty. Partnership requires investment. We make an investment of two fish and barley bread—an investment that does not affect the big picture—while the Lord makes an investment where five thousand are fed and much remains. God pours out immeasurable abundance in response to humanity's insignificant dedication.
God knows what is best in our lives. He also knows how to accomplish it better than I do.
When God answers, He only looks at faith. Even if everything collapses, we must not retreat with faith in God's omnipotence and faithfulness. Win through faith.

God is not flustered by the corruption of institutional systems. He preserves seven thousand who did not bow to Baal and Gideon's three hundred. God will use many professionals as missionaries. All are royal priests. If the Lord has called you, do not limit yourself by saying you are a layperson.

God will grant revival when we become one in the Lord. Achieving unity in human affairs is truly difficult. The devil never allows it. One thing is clear: I will see the Lord's glory. God will work with us, heal us, and raise us up for His glory and holiness, though we are full of sin and failures. Therefore, He will remove our stubborn hearts and give us the heart of the Lord. I will give them an undivided heart and put a new spirit in them; I will remove from them their heart of stone and give them a heart of flesh. Then they will follow my decrees and be careful to keep my laws. They will be my people, and I will be their God (Ezekiel 11:19-20). Victory is not believing in feelings but believing in the Lord's promise. Hold on.
If needed, God changes suffering into miracles, gives strength to overcome, or permits it to continue according to His deep purposes.

When I am not in my proper place but stand in a place that increases odds, I leave the place of the Lord's protection. So I get beaten and depressed and return. People say I was under spiritual attack. No, I ran away spiritually and got beaten by neighborhood thugs. Keep your own place.

Draw near to God and He will draw near to you (James 4:8). People are transformed by those whom they draw near to and gain their power. Relationship is ability. I hope the ability of Mary who draws near to the Lord comes upon you. The foolish person in this world is one who seeks power where God is absent.

Forty-five hundred years ago in the Middle East, abandoning home and relationships and going to an unknown land is near zero probability—recklessness. The world thinks that increasing odds makes things happen, but the Lord gives promises. Just because people increase odds and pressure does not mean the Lord fulfills promises He has not made. Hold onto the promise.
God's interference is not hindering. It is the most glorious path that even angels admire for a human being. God's will is sweet to those who obey.
Where God's holiness and presence are, all creation worships and darkness retreats. There are many problems, but there is one answer. Serve only the Lord.
God's wonderful works come suddenly one day. Yet the Lord who performs them scrutinizes me carefully.
A person full of God's power is not hurried or chased. Rather, they are challenged and pursue life.

Hastily implementing before God's time comes cannot produce complete fruit nor build up the community. God does not change or negotiate His perfect will and glorious intention because of human haste. Picking unripe fruit never satisfies me or others.

When you understand what God's will is, the dry rod like my life in my hand becomes God's staff. Joseph became positioned at the shortest distance from Pharaoh's palace rather than becoming a prisoner.

Not all of God's will is the same. There is will that God 'initiates' and will that He 'permits.' He desires to actively love, accept, and live with us, but when Israel insisted on having a king, while He permitted it, His heart was displeased. We must not boast in permission. Rather, we should look back and obey what He initiates. Repeatedly pursuing permission makes you greedy, but following what He initiates makes life abundant.
Living according to God's Word is our duty, and filling and protecting us as we live that way is God's responsibility. In conclusion, we need not fear anything.

God's thoughts are different from human thoughts. While doing some ministry, the principle of 'I will focus on poor neighborhoods' is very good, but sometimes God may have me go to Beverly Hills against that principle and preach the gospel. No matter how rational and biblical my principles are, they must be 'correctable' before the Lord's command. That is humility.

There is truth that becomes clear only when complaints and greed cease before God. That He Himself is given rather than obtaining something. Be still, and know that I am God (Psalm 46:10). Keep a close guard on your mouth and seal knowledge on your lips (Proverbs 5:2).
God's promise is like investing in a long-term fund. This is especially true when we speak of working together for good. Be patient.
God's zeal does not rest until the purpose is achieved. His purpose is greater than any situation we will experience. God has a plan that transcends the problems we face.

God's intention is peace. It gives life and enables abundant living. He gave authorities to bestow such blessing. That is God's law. The secret to receiving blessing is becoming close with authorities. God surely works through authorities.
When you do God's work, you receive God's provision.
When you acknowledge God's sovereignty, you experience the Lord's rule. Then the kingdom of God that is to come becomes reality before me. Deny yourself and acknowledge Jesus.
There is no word that brings more peace and reassurance than 'God's sovereignty.' Because the Lord Himself rules, there is nothing to worry about.
A person who strives to be within God's domain of rule escapes human rule. This was true of Daniel.
God surely accomplishes His will in His own way. Therefore, now is the time to seek the Lord in all my circumstances. I must call upon the Lord and tell Him exactly how I feel in this situation.
There are times when God calls again. He wants to rebuild everything and give strength and ability. Understanding that timing perfectly is wisdom.

If God does not help, no one can help. Even if people help and resort to tricks, it ends in tragedy and destruction. Pray and obey so that God can help you. Tell Hezekiah: This is what the LORD, the God of your father David, says: I have heard your prayer and seen your tears; I will add fifteen years to your life. And I will deliver you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria. I will defend this city (Isaiah 38:5-6).
If you love God's people whom He sends more than God Himself, He takes them away. Life has priorities. Everything that comes before God, He will take away.

Please wait until what God has shown you is accomplished in God's way. Give up even your own arbitrary deadlines of desire. Only then can the Lord work. Wait beautifully until it is accomplished in God's way. Please... that the Lord may work, let patience finish its work, so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything (James 1:4).

When God designed people, He did not design them to worry. Naturally, worry that comes to us is not our portion. But when we worry, our 'purpose changes,' causing tremendous wear on the product (humanity), and ultimately it breaks. Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God (Philippians 4:6). All the contents of worry must become kindling for the altar of prayer. Seek first His kingdom and His righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well (Matthew 6:33). When you work on the Lord's business, worry disappears. Are you worried? You have time left.
When God uses a person, the criterion is not usefulness or competence but 'surrender.'

When humans try to solve what God should work on, we call it 'worry.' Statistics show that we can only respond to about 4% of the situations that come our way. Ultimately, if after worrying for about 15 minutes something doesn't work out, we must coolly entrust it to prayer.

There is something God asks of us: do not compete fiercely with others, trampling them underfoot to climb up. It is His hand that raises up and brings down. Rather than filling our own selfish desires, He says, 'Seek the welfare of others' (1 Corinthians 10:24). In this unlimited competitive society, the audacity and leisure to seek others' welfare is a privilege enjoyed only by those who work for God's glory.
God has given us things. He has loaned them to us. When we mistake temporary 'usage rights' for eternal 'ownership,' we immediately become unhappy. Ownership makes us compare; usage makes us wise.

If God does not give, we have received nothing (John 3:27). The Lord always gives the very best of things. Because He is good. Therefore, the people and works belonging to me are undoubtedly the finest options coming from heaven.

Those who walk the path God has given do not run away or become flustered. Though I cannot know the way I am going, He knows and guides me and brings me forth as refined gold. Do not adjust my life to people but align it to the goal He has given me.
If you do what God commanded you to pray, the work He assigned, the meetings He allowed, and the labor He requested, you are doing well. Everything else will be done by the Lord.

'Pride' is impersonating God by taking the glory as though you did it, making it 'identity fraud,' and 'frustration' is denying the living God and filing a false death report, making it 'filing a false report.' *Violation of identity fraud laws, violation of false reporting laws.

Jesus received all authority in heaven and earth and recovered through the cross all the rights that Adam had lost. Yet because the Lord sits at the right hand of the throne of majesty, we who are His body must naturally exercise dominion and rule on this earth. Rise boldly and exercise the authority to reign as king in life through Jesus. When I call, the Father will answer. That is the covenant, that is the promise, and that is the power of the gospel.

Our Father in heaven~ Our Lord God, worthy of great glory and honor, God present here and now—Almighty Father God, this land of Europe needs Your reign. Only You can heal and restore us. We seek Your face. Come, we beseech You ~ Yours is the kingdom and the power and the glory, Father, forever. Amen *May all members earnestly long and pray that on that day the cloud of God's glory covers our tent.

Those who have come to know the secrets of heaven never compete. 'Let the believer who is brought low boast in being raised up, and the rich in being brought low, because the rich will fade away like a flower of the field' (James 1:9-10).

Even if you live for one day, run at 350 km/h. You will see the Lord's glory. Otherwise, you will fly and crawl on the ground. God has called us gloriously. *The absolute speed at which an airplane takes off is 350 km/h; absolute distance is over 800 meters.

One thing to be grateful for is not that we chose Jesus, but that Jesus chose us and established us. Jesus said the reason is to bear fruit and that by having fruit always, whatever we ask in Jesus' name to the Father we will receive. John 15:16. You who are established, be at peace, rejoice, and delight in the One who established you.

If a president or king of a nation feels unfortunate because he lacks chupachups, then he is not a king but a runny-nosed kid playing king in an alley. The Bible calls me a priest who is king. I hope that lack does not become misfortune, but that level solves the problem.
The excitement of one day becomes abandonment and regret of another day, and the pain of letting go one day returns as glory another day. Genesis 40:23 ...he forgot Joseph.

When afraid, if we defend ourselves through competition, avoidance, jealousy, victimhood, compulsion, anger, and guilt, we do not achieve defense but rather cause deep harm. When we die on the cross with Christ, we no longer need to defend ourselves, and instead even weakness and death become beneficial. This happens when we always trust and hope in the Lord.

When David sinned, God sighed because 'I have already given enough' and 'if you had needed, I would have given more.' Taking what God has not given through human methods is rebellion. Do not rebel but repent and give thanks.
Limitations are not overcome by clenching your teeth but by loving the Lord and receiving His grace to rule over you.

There was a time when we said the Korean church had 12 million believers; now we see 6.5 million. Even that seems somewhat exaggerated. Large churches still hold on, but they are beaten down in the internet and media whenever there's a chance. That is why we say the Korean church is in crisis. Yet I say there is hope. Because God is preparing diaspora youth who have not knelt before Baal. I see the blood of the cross in your eyes. It means I see the hope of the homeland church. In the bondage of Egypt, I see one Moses. Do not lament and criticize only; become Moses yourselves.

When I return to Korea, it seems all my hardened resolve disappears, and even the air in the atmosphere seems to try to knock me over. Also, here it was a downpour of grace, but there why does it become a light drizzle? I do not understand. In Korea, if you want to fill your hunger, you must pay a price. In London, there were people who paid instead. You must find it yourself and prepare yourself. I deeply realize that my faith life here was not my strength but entirely the grace of the Lord. Yet if you hold onto the Lord with all your heart, you will know that He is present everywhere. Hold on. And keep going.

The time and hour are not our concern. That is the Lord's domain, and when the time comes, He makes it known and accomplishes it. Only receiving the Holy Spirit and becoming witnesses is the core. Entrust the time to the Lord and fill up first.

It is not that we give thanks and rejoice because circumstances are good. What matters is for whom I live. That precious life is burdensome for some, while even death becomes a gain for others. This is because 'to live is Christ, and to die is gain' (Philippians 1:21). I have set my purpose to live for Christ, so death too is gain for me. (Easy Bible Philippians 1:21).

Dark things that happen in my workplace, home, relationships, and studies are just a signal to return to the basics. Come out of peripheral problems and really stand at the center. Such problems occur not because of bad luck but because 'Jesus' is absent.

You must go to the Red Sea to cross it. We prefer a peaceful course without thrills and without the Red Sea, but the One above thinks very differently. To a people needing change, He leads them to the Red Sea. Though the Red Sea seems to have no path no matter how well you look, when you pray and believe, there is a path in the middle of the sea.

Do not be anxious within probability but entrust yourself to and depend on the Lord. Even embracing the prophet Jonah, who could grow horns from his buttocks, God prepared storm, great fish, the fish's vomit, hot wind, scorching sun, gourd plant, worms, and sea wind. Everything is in the Lord's hands. This situation I face is the Lord's best choice for me.
If your heart is not confirmed, it will try to collapse. Do not fear but be completely broken and reduced to dust. God gives a new heart in that broken place.
When you pray in tribulation, help comes from unexpected places. Sometimes even non-believers help. You cannot limit the Lord's help.

Making the tabernacle in the desolate desert is nearly miraculous. The only materials were various treasures brought from the land of Egypt from the Egyptians. What I obtained, what I received—it is not to be satisfied with possession and acquisition, but given to accomplish 'the Lord's will.' Yet more important than the material given is the attitude and heart of the giver.

If you repent and do not abstain, you have not truly repented. A repentant person becomes humble before the Lord and abstemious by nature. You cannot hold onto the cross while feasting, while not leaving that evil path. The cross is not like a handrail you can grab on a bus. It is received as life.
Any grace I perceive and any love I receive comes only under God's permission and is given by Him. Therefore, I am deeply grateful and thankful each day.
You must be faithful when in the congregation. Yet in places where no one worships, you must be even more faithful. Do not try to fulfill your own desires but obey the faithful Lord's will.

The humanist's best is 'I will take responsibility for everything.' God did not call us to be responsible. He called us to come to Jesus, who will be responsible for us forever, to depend on Him, and He has given us the position to enable others to fully depend on Him. We cannot be responsible even for ourselves, much less for others. I pray that you entrust everything only to the Lord.

Often, when the other person responds less than usual or becomes close to another, we get excessively worked up beyond necessity. That means you are not flying. The airplane's altitude is low, so you are influenced by all manner of flies, mosquitoes, and sparrows. A person whose conclusion is the Lord soars in the sky. That is why such a person does not fret over earthly affairs.
Because we came from dust, we will return to dust. There is no reason to grip tightly. Only He will know and remember me.

Have hope. No one can take it away. Have leisure and humor. If your faith is a luxury brand, your life becomes a luxury brand too. Even if you set aside for a while the work you are being chased by, the big thing you think about will not happen. Believe in the Almighty God and entrust everything to Him.
In difficult and seemingly impossible situations, put your faith in an egg and break the rock. Though our situation seems hard, it is the situation the Lord has allowed.

There is the both-feet stance, meaning balance. It means feet set on both the Lord and the world. Just in case, you think you could move to the side without loss. That is truly wrong thinking. Given how far apart the Lord and the world are, how can you think you can stand with both feet? I respectfully offer my condolences.

If it hadn't rained when David sinned and he hadn't seen Bathsheba bathing, what would have happened? He would have still sinned. Because his condition was already a sinful state. Not being filled with the Lord is already a great sin. Eventually the sun will rise, temptations will come, and trials will come. What matters is what fills me. There is no vacuum in the world. Something always fills it.

If you pray for 5 minutes, it seems He will work for 5 minutes; if you pray for 10 minutes, He will work for 10 minutes. That is not so. He does it all at once, in bulk. He works with low cost and high efficiency. That is why the phrase 'storing up' appears in the Bible (Romans 2:5).
There is no reason to hesitate or be reluctant. If the Lord has established it.

*God's Sovereignty and My Freedom. When I preach God's sovereignty, I am often asked whether it is fatalism (does it make us robots?). What is clear is that God's sovereignty cannot be resisted by anyone. Some reformers overemphasized the Lord's sovereignty and fell into a fatalistic predestination without doing missions. 'Sow for yourselves righteousness, reap steadfast love; break up your fallow ground, for it is time to seek the Lord, that He may come and rain righteousness upon you' (Hosea 10:12). When you break yourself and go forward, God is the one who gives rain there. Plowing the ground and seeking rain is our task, and pouring rain and soaking the ground with righteousness is God's work.
When I cannot keep myself, I become bold instead. Because I have escaped the worry of worrying about things. Do not worry but entrust with thanksgiving. That is the intention of the Almighty.

For me, all meaning and value come only from the Lord. When I look at the other person and circumstances through that truth, I rejoice in the meaning the Lord gives and give Him glory. Only the Lord is my reason. 'If then you have received anything from heaven, the person is unable to receive anything' (John 3:27).

Praying only for the problem when facing a problem is childish faith. Behind the problem, God is present. If you pray that His will be accomplished, within that you will experience being resolved by the Lord's method. You should ask this way: 'God, what should I learn through this problem, and what will should I accomplish for You?'

*God never let you down nor you can let Him down. The deepest wound for a person is disappointing someone whom I love. Among all, when disappointing God, the pain is so deep and one becomes angry at oneself. One wants to throw everything down and disappear into the earth. Yet fundamentally, I cannot disappoint God. I think I have failed to hold God well and thus disappointed Him. Rather, because God has held me with His righteous right hand, I am here. The Lord did not ignore my weakness from the beginning, which is why He carried the cross, and therefore He is not disappointed and will never abandon me to fail.

*God will never let you down. When you believe in Jesus, you will face tribulations in the world. Yet there is no need to fear. Because Jesus has overcome the world. He has never failed me once and never will. All His victories are already accomplished on the cross. That is the conclusion. My conclusion. The conclusion that Jesus foresaw on the cross. This is my destiny.
I will not collapse. Because I cannot collapse. As long as Jesus, my soul and life, is present, I pursue toward the goal.
Peace comes when I believe in the Lord's almightiness. Because that is when miracles happen. Peace is a great grace given to believers. The Lord is greater than the problem.
Only when I cannot lose the Lord do I truly have His presence.

Do not lament that your hopes are not fulfilled. Getting here was immense grace. It is not a cause for discouragement but for gratitude. Without the Lord's grace, I could not have taken one step, and it will be so in the future. Rely on His faithfulness.
Do not resent that things are unfair; instead, give thanks that the Lord is with you. What you have received is more than you deserve given your insufficiency.
God is the one who upholds me. Therefore, do not fear people. It does not come from the east or west, but from God alone.

Looking back, I realize I worried and suffered needlessly. Everything is resolved and solved within the Lord's grace, yet I anxiously pull future concerns into the present. We must not worry beyond fervent prayer. The Lord accomplishes it.

A dog barks, but the KTX train runs on. Why? Because its purpose is clear. When the Lord is your purpose, you do not concern yourself with others' reactions and words. Whether you live or die becomes secondary. Because glorifying Jesus is the most important purpose.
When you desire many things, you will have much conflict. When you are satisfied only with the Lord's glory, life becomes simple and healing and restoration occur.
Thanksgiving flows naturally because the Lord's grace is sufficient for me. Even if others and the world do not agree, His grace has surely been sufficient for me.

When you entrust everything with the heart that He need not do it, that you will lose if you lose, and that you will die if you die, peace comes and moreover you become bold. Before the Lord of peace who controls all circumstances, be strong and courageous. Not my will but the Father's will be done.
A miracle occurs when you stop demanding that something must happen and instead say you will hold to the Lord even if you lose everything. Such change and such confession breaking forth is worship.
It is well. Because it is in the Lord's hands... I have known the answer from long ago. I need only to do it.
There is no better ministry for me. There is no better youth group in the world for me. There is no better opportunity for me. Because the Almighty Lord has already given me all of these.
It was so difficult because I could not let go. When you let go, there is nothing more to lose, and that is where the Lord's miracle occurs.

Rather than relying on other things, rely only on the Lord who enables you to endure. Even when circumstances seem difficult and someone you trusted turns away, the Lord allows only trials you can bear and provides a way of escape when you cannot endure. Look to and believe only in the Lord who enables you to endure.

What I cannot control is not my domain but God's domain. That is why Jacob did not devise a strategy to fight Esau's 400 men at the Jabbok River but appealed to God. How simple are the things to obey and the things to pray about? The simple life the Lord gives. Simple, flexible, intense!

Faith surpasses frustration and enables us to trust in the Lord's sovereignty and rule, and even gives the wonderful peace of His response. The Lord is still working. Not by your sight but by your faith.

Sometimes as we live, there are times when answers do not come. It is a situation where one's heart collapses. What is needed then is faith. To abandon everything and rely on the Lord, and to continue on the path we were walking. Since nothing is solved unless the Lord helps, all I can do is rely on Him.

Rudeness is the weak pretending to be strong and imitating the strong. A truly strong person finds himself in the Lord, and even when humbled, his self-respect is not hurt, and he acknowledges the Lord's sovereignty that controls circumstances.
We must increasingly request the Lord's intervention, His rule, His handling. So that we may see the Lord's glory in this dark world.

Whether praying at a corner while walking the street or confessing alone at an incredibly busy desk, 'I rely on the Lord!' or earnestly appealing to the Lord's help with only eyes amid unbearable pressure, the Lord sees and hears it all. He cannot turn away from us whom He has redeemed through His Son's cross and blood.

He sent back 20,000 men and with 300 defeated an allied army of 135,000. To be more precise, it was a war that could only be won by sending back 20,000 men. God used Gideon, barley-bread-like. To put it exactly, because he was like barley bread, he was used. Many people boast of what is futile and perishable. They boast because numbers are many or status is high, but the fact is they are not used because of that. I had a dream, and in the dream, a cake of barley bread tumbled into the camp of Midian and came to the tent and struck it so it fell, turned it upside down, and it fell. —Judges 7:13

The difficulties I face and the wounds I receive are not reality. I must first look back at why such pain came and who allowed it. The reality is that I am not looking to the Lord properly but fleeing from Him. It is not the other person who became an opponent, but a thorn the Lord places at my side to call me to repentance.

There is a saying that if you fall seven times, you rise eight times. It is the power of positivity. This saying is a disaster for those without Jesus. Pharaoh and the Egyptians before ten plagues trying again, the prodigal sons in the pig sty with no fruit of the sycamore trying to rise again, truly writing the worst scenario.
The problem I face must ultimately be worked out by God, and God gives His miracles for God's people.

The problem I am struggling with is not the real problem. The Lord must intervene to resolve it. God seeks those of a whole heart and gives them strength. That is why the wise do not hate the thorny person but find resolution in their reverence. Reverence is the resolution.

Rather, give thanks. Had that not happened, you could have suffered less and avoided the uncomfortable truth you had to face. But I would remain a fool still seeing only what I want to see and pursuing only comfort and ease. Especially for this reason, I must give thanks for the Lord's faithfulness and sovereignty who has allowed all of this.

In the end, this world is a battle for sovereignty. The success or failure of my life is also a battle for sovereignty. Sovereignty lies in the territories and people under that nation's rule. If God's sovereignty extends to the church but is rejected in relationships, transformation and glory do not appear. When I yield my sovereignty to the Lord, true holiness appears. Acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty.
Also, for young lambs in difficulty, grant us effective intercessory prayer—rule, O Lord. When the Lord rules over that soul, He brings rivers to the desert and flowers bloom in the wilderness.

Please offer the shortest but most powerful intercessory prayer. Rule over him, O Lord. Prayer is simple, but the Lord who applies it is so wondrous, turning the whole earth around just to make a person.
Faith is to expect and come before the Lord. But the finest stage is to lay down our will to God's will.

I desire that in all circumstances—whether forgetting everything and lying in a peaceful bed or under tremendous pressure in urgent situations—we would know that God is the center of my heart and that the Lord is at the center of my heart. Whether we live, we live for the Lord, and whether we die, we die for the Lord; therefore, whether we live or die, we are the Lord's. —Romans 14:8

At the place where I stand, in the time I pass through, the Lord must be Lord. If the Lord is Lord, I see a dry path in the vast sea and the dry land becomes holy. Remove the sandals from your feet. 'The commander of the Lord's army said to Joshua, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you stand is holy. Joshua did so.' —Joshua 5:15
If I let go of what I am holding to myself, God holds me. 'Behold my servant whom I uphold, my chosen one in whom I delight.'

If I peel a fruit and leave it, it looks as unsightly as if mice had eaten it. But a mother's handiwork is flawless artistry. That I cannot do it is my hope. Work entrusted to the Lord is accomplished at the Lord's level.
If the destination is the same, you will eventually meet. Those who make the Lord first and move toward Him will ultimately become excellent partners for one another.
People of faith confront. Not because they are without fear, but because they know clearly who is to be most feared.

Because they know better than anyone else what is more important, they can discard what is less important better than anyone else. Not regarding the things I have, I press on toward the prize of the upward call in Christ Jesus. The person who has discovered the greatest value lives simply.

That I cannot do it by my own strength surpasses frustration and becomes peace. How blessed is it? To rely only on the Lord, not on my own strength... Confess that you cannot do it and move forward. The Lord will rule directly.

May it be given when I am able to give. Then in the future, the devotion and sacrifice I have offered will become grace and hold me eternally. But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace toward me was not in vain. On the contrary, I worked harder than any of them—yet not I, but the grace of God that is with me. —1 Corinthians 15:10
That the Lord has allowed me challenges and obstacles means He has poured out love. Because passion and perseverance are necessary to break through without them.
That I do not feel His response does not mean God is not working. When I groan, it sounds like thunder to God. Therefore, I must continue to pray.
Fall to your knees, raise your hands high, and cry out: Let the Lord's will be done.

* When someone works hard but does not get results as hoped, people are disappointed and resentful. They thought that if they were sincere and did their best, all would be well, but it is not. There are things in life where you do your best but the results are not satisfactory. However, you need not fear or be discouraged about this. There is One watching me now, and there is One who puts my tears in a bottle and writes them in His book. When you rely on Him, you do not fear. There is the realm of people and the realm of God. 'You have kept count of my tossings; put my tears in your bottle. Are they not in your record?' Psalm 56:8. 'When I am afraid, I put my trust in you.' Psalm 56:11

When I walk through a parched desert, when dark days cover me, what I will do is worship the Lord. 'Naked I came from my mother's womb, and naked shall I return. The Lord gave, and the Lord has taken away; blessed be the name of the Lord.' —Job 1:21
The devil says, 'I will give you what you so desire.' Tell the devil, 'How will you give me Jesus?' If longing is for the Lord, people and the world cannot rule over me.

* Difficult decision, easy process. As we live life, there are times we must make important decisions. It is necessary to entrust this decision to the Lord. For a person of faith, what is more important than the result of the decision is the process of the decision. If the Lord is almighty, He will be with me even in my inadequate decision and work all things together for good. If Pilate had entrusted his judicial authority to Jesus, the true judge, Jesus would have said, 'Thus it is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness' (Matthew 3:16) and returned the judicial authority. But even with the same decision, if the process differs, the eternal impact is different.

Not all that I desire will be accomplished. Nor should it be. The Lord, who is higher and deeper than I, knows what I truly need. That is why feeling 'I am insufficient' is what the Lord calls 'sufficient.' When my opinion and the Lord's opinion differ, which is correct? 'Jesus guides all the way I go; in difficult times He gives sufficient grace.' —Hymn 434

There is a saying to leave when you are applauded. This is selfish thinking. A person of mission does not leave for personal gain but for glory. Glory is not my will but the will of the One who receives glory.

That the path ahead looks only dark means I have no options to choose. This is not something to be frustrated about but to understand that the Lord will lead. Blessed is the person who is helpless and foolish and can only rely on the Lord.
I do not fear circumstances for two reasons. One is that things do not go my way, and the other is that the Lord rules. Boldness comes from focus. Where is your focus?
Look only to the Lord who touches your hardened heart and raises you up again

In the wilderness, shortcuts do not work. Only the Lord's presence was the way to survive. But in Canaan, people could live to some extent through sowing and trading. That is when idols entered. Trying to live well without His presence—that is a great sin.

* The present moment is what matters. The now is important. You must be happy now, advance further now, and walk together now. Leave everything else to Him and go. Therefore, do not fear your fate. Even if you live only for today, burn like a flame bright enough to be satisfied. What comes after, I do not know well. All honor, glory, and authority should be received by the Lord alone.

* True purpose: There is no need to become great while living in this world. If you do, you strain yourself and side effects follow. Besides, there is already glory in the Lord, so there is no reason to seek worldly glory. Compared to the Lord's glory, the things of this world are nothing but fireflies.

* Let the Lord be Lord: King Saul lived conscious of others' eyes, clinging to them. But David looked to the Lord's presence and let go. Though Saul had more, he was always anxious because he could not have even more. Though David had nothing, he has peace through the grace poured out upon him. Their masters were different. What am I chasing, and what is my motive? Saul was displeased and very angry, saying: 'They have credited David with tens of thousands, but me with only thousands. What more can he get but the kingdom?' —1 Samuel 18:8

Do you love Me? Peter, who had denied the Lord, hesitantly says he loves Him. Now it is not by his own strength or guarantee, but only when the Lord's sovereignty permits, only within the grace of the Lord who made me me. Thus no one can boast. Love is here: 'This is how we know that we love the children of God...'

I cannot establish myself, but through the Lord's boundless grace, He makes me look only to Him and ultimately grants me victory. What am I, a sinner? It is not by my strength and ability, but an irresistible grace that comes through the Holy Spirit alone.

Relationships are important in life. But when a relationship takes the place of the Lord, this becomes idolatry. A relationship the Lord did not give, or an obsession with relationships, is the evidence. The Lord is not a supporting actor. We must gain insight through the joy of the bridegroom's friend that John the Baptist had. The bridegroom takes the bride, and the friend celebrates and rejoices in this.

* Faith is like dominoes: Faith is like dominoes. When one falls, others fall in succession. Conversely, when one succeeds, revival occurs in the others. Add the whip to the running horse. Continue~~~
* Time: When you look back, everyone lacks time. Since time is short anyway, kneel before the Lord. You will experience God, who made time, filling it for you.

* Anointing: Without God's anointing, in this dark world, in this increasingly evil world, we cannot lift up the glory of the Lord. Before going out into the world, the only condition we must earnestly request and ask for is 'the Lord's anointing.' When requesting this anointing, the Lord also sets conditions for us. 1. Taking off your sandals and entrusting yourself to the Lord (acknowledging His sovereignty), and 2. Remaining in the Lord's presence (the mystery of the cross, union with Him). Living without this is just suffering uselessly.

* Those who receive and obey their calling: God gave the widow of Zarephath the calling to provide for Elijah. Through that calling, before she died, she had her remaining food for her and her son taken away. But this was not taken away; it was sown for the fruit of a great miracle. When you obey the calling, miracles happen.
* Good news: The Lord still reigns today. In all visible and invisible realms, the Lord is King. This is our comfort and joy.

* Man's plans vs. God's guidance: Looking back, I realize things happened not as I planned but through His providence. No matter how difficult or dark a situation I face, I am in His hands, and He will surely guide me to still waters. No matter how much guns and swords block the way, the Lord never gives up on me. If only He is with me, tell fate to come. I am not afraid.

* What people realize in suffering: When people suffer, they realize two things. I cannot control, and I must look to the Lord's face. This is the same as saying you must entrust and find rest. Let us look to the Lord's face.

When something happens to me that is startling and surprising, I must not keep my gaze on the outside but on the Lord within me. Now is the time to pray. 'Going a little farther, he fell with his face to the ground and prayed, "My Father, if it is possible, may this cup be taken from me. Yet not as I will, but as you will."' —Matthew 26:39

Sometimes there are things that are not worth being angry about at all, yet someone explodes in anger and does not even give a chance to explain. When I think of the Lord and return, my heart is frustrated and hurt, but I hope to pour it out to the Lord. 'My beloved child, I know your heart. The Lordship of this problem belongs to Me. I will make it beautiful.' That is why the Psalms end with gratitude along with the pouring out of a wounded heart. I trust in the Lord.

The purpose of the wilderness and the tabernacle is to make a people prepared by God. Preparation means seeking the Lord's face, and it means becoming a people of obedience—not living on bread alone but living in obedience to God as the Lord.

* In His Time: The Lord has His own time and hour. Everything is fully accomplished only in His time. The Lord's time is the moment you acknowledge Jesus as Lord and give Him everything. Then He makes it beautiful and accomplishes it. He has made everything beautiful in its time. Also, he has put eternity into man's heart, yet so that he cannot find out what God has done from the beginning to the end. —Ephesians 3:11

* Prayers answered, problems solved: Despite much faith and prayer, the working of God may not appear in daily life. When you make Jesus your Lord, miracles begin to happen. Whenever the disciples acknowledged, believed, and confessed that Jesus is Lord, they unfailingly experienced miracles and power. Powerless Christianity appears when we dilute the Lord's Lordship. Remarkably, all questions of Jesus in the four Gospels are compressed into one: 'Who is your Lord?' The stone jars of wine, the five loaves and two fish, the boat in the storm, the sick daughter, the demon-possessed child, the thirsty life—who is truly Lord? Therefore, do not cling to the problem; check who the Lord of the problem is. This is how you can have peace. His mother said to the servants, 'Do whatever he tells you.' —John 2:5

More important than having more than others or living longer than others is how you use what you have and live your life. What the world calls blessing is not necessarily blessing, and what is called difficulty is often the blessing that draws you closer to God.
If I design my future with my own strength, it means I will lose all my assets. God does not tell me the plan, but His plan for me is perfect. Investment surrendered to God never fails.

If I despise the authority given to me by God, I will lose much that God has prepared for my life and will surely regret it. It is an act of denying the Almighty Lord. I ask you to respect those who handle His Word and share good things with them.
When I give up, I will see that the Lord is actually accomplishing it.
In the wilderness, only God commands us to advance. We move forward as much as we have changed. So truly I hope I become humble. So that the Lord can work sufficiently.
It is not recognition when I exalt myself, nor is it humility when I humble myself. How I see myself is not really important. What matters is how God sees me and what plan He has for me.
Do not try to move God to accomplish my joy. Rather, I hope the Lord's will becomes my joy. That is the secret of a life that receives answers and prospers.

It is God who blocks my path. Ultimately, it is God who opens the path. In fact, whether the path is closed or open is not so important. Because it does not matter whether I meet a loved one outside or inside. I pursue what I have been grasped by Christ to grasp—this present moment and the One in me and to whom I belong. That is what matters.

When athlete Park Ju-young faced the Japanese team for an Olympic bronze medal, he would not have found it difficult or wanted to sit on the bench and rest. Because the Lord is with me, every situation in my life is a love story. Only that One, one Person, is with me, so all interpretation becomes excellent.

When I try to fulfill my desires: A person who believes in God as a problem-solver may pray faithfully, serve devotedly, but when worldly affairs do not proceed as they wish, that person will inevitably doubt God's aliveness, His guidance, and His very existence—in proportion to how much they wanted things their way. 'Moses called the place Massah and Meribah, saying, "It is because the Israelites quarreled and because they tested the Lord saying, 'Is the Lord among us or not?'"' —Exodus 17:7

If I try to accomplish my will, I will be frustrated. But for those who accomplish the Lord's will, everything is only prayer and thanksgiving. Leading God's people is different from leading my people. It is to lead His people according to His will. The mission is to report all matters in detail and seek to see the Lord. Therefore, leading God's people in His way is the calling.

Even the one breath I am taking now is given by the Lord. God has given me everything. Without this assurance, I will worry and fear. The One who began is the Lord, and the One who will accomplish is also the Lord.

I work hard. After believing in Jesus, I performed all my work faithfully. I come up with ideas, stay up all night, fast, and never tire. These things are relatively easy for me. But what is truly difficult, more required, and deeper in life and ministry is 'waiting.' God makes you exceed the time limit He set—the limit of your patience. Then you set another extended time. This too must be given up. Why? Because true answers come only after passing through the threshold of waiting. Many people try to accomplish the vision God gave them through worldly methods. It is because they cannot wait. It looks like success, but it is ultimately failure.

Sitting by a palm tree drinking passion fruit juice while watching the waves and getting a tan is not real rest. True rest requires the Lord to be with you. 'The Lord replied, "My Presence will go with you, and I will give you rest."' —Exodus 33:14

A four-week journey to Canaan took forty years. The goal was not Canaan flowing with milk and honey; it was that God wanted to give Himself. A journey's value is not speed but who travels with you. 'And gave them the lands of the nations, and the peoples toiled seized the fruit of their labor—that they might keep his statutes and observe his laws. Praise the Lord.' —Psalm 105:44-45

Ultimately, wisdom is the ability to extract core values. This can be called insight—what is invisible to others but visible to those who have the Lord's perspective. We are bold not because we are brave but because we see that He who is with us is greater than they.

The Pharisees and Sadducees had power in their society. Therefore they could mobilize armies. But they said they could not lay a hand on Jesus. It means they could not arrest Him. Why? Because 'his hour had not yet come' (according to God's plan). Though Jesus taught before the temple treasury, 'no one laid a hand on him, for his hour had not yet come.' —John 8:20 The best bodyguard is God. Do not be afraid.

1. All Satan can do is frighten. Because he was completely defeated at the cross. 2. He will try to hide this, but it will all be revealed before the truth. Be bold in the Lord. 3. In accomplishing good, Satan is not the hindrance; rather, sometimes Satan's interference becomes help in accomplishing good. 4. I hope you become believers of faith who defeat Satan with thanksgiving, causing him to score an own goal.

Before a problem, what I need most is not someone skilled to cover my back or a stroke of luck. What I need is the voice of the One who changes my fear to peace and makes me know that He is God. 'He said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness."' —2 Corinthians 12:9 If the Lord is enough, then that is enough.

While comparing the lives of David and Saul for a leaders' meeting, I came upon 1 Chronicles. Though 1 Chronicles is similar in content to 2 Samuel, it was written after the exile through Ezra with a tradition of Judah and a solemn self-examination for spiritual restoration. The key points are: ① Jerusalem is important. ② Co-workers are important. ③ Worship is important. ④ We must rely on God. What amazed me personally was seeing through David's foundational work in opening his kingdom, the emphasis for future generations viewing Judah's restoration in its historical context. Essence, not technique. The importance of a life that relies only on the Lord before any operational tactics.

I hope you entrust everything and go. I hope that even if you are sorrowful and heartbroken and your plans do not come to pass, you entrust to the Lord and go. Then you have done well. Then you have truly done well. If the Lord says stop there, then there is blessing.

When emptiness suddenly crosses your mind, it is because there is no truth in it. Those who believe in God's sovereignty praise the Lord in all circumstances and are not shaken. Do not despair, but discern what the Lord's will is. Those who acknowledge God's sovereignty will receive comfort. 'Therefore do not be foolish, but understand what the Lord's will is.' —Ephesians 5:17
Whether in pain or joy, I will follow only the Lord. Who can stop me? Death itself cannot stop me. (Hymn 355)

A dry stick, when obeyed, becomes God's staff. 'Moses took the staff of God in his hand.' —Exodus 4:20 The things I lack now—job, marriage, studies, achievements—should not be seen as deficiency. It is not something to despair about or to compare and become miserable. Rather, it is a time given to me to worship, serve, and prepare. A wise woodcutter, even when resting, sharpens his axe while resting. Anyway, without what the Lord gives, I have nothing. Even when He gives, He gives it in His time. When deficiency becomes ability, God's glory appears. That is the power of interpretation. May you have the Lord's perspective.
Do not try to adjust the situation before me. Rather, align yourself with the Lord in heaven. Evil imagination eats away at life.

Do you labor with joy? To this question, you will ultimately answer 'yes,' but if it takes more than two seconds, a red light has turned on. Joy is not calculation or compulsion but obedience as a response to the condition of the Lord's sovereignty. To act in faith is to act in joy. If it was not your choice, then obey. Because He is deeper and higher than you.

Despair comes from seeing only results and parts. But the Lord makes us see the whole. Before the world's request to be content and maintain the status quo, I hope you have the faith perspective to see glory in suffering. The Lord moves us to places He establishes in ways I do not know. Joseph → Egypt (low-cost, high-efficiency, shortest-route move).
I do not rejoice when circumstances are favorable. Neither do I despair when they are unfavorable. Because the Lord prepares and rules. My only desire is to become the Lord's joy.

A mountain I cannot cross with my strength, a valley I cannot fill with my ability—when I make the Lord my Master and invite His presence, He comes by His Spirit and rules everything by His almightiness. The Lord rules.
Rejection is one of the good things God does to send me to the place I am truly needed. Darkness does not show light without showing darkness.
What I have done is not what matters. What matters is in whom I have done it. Everything done in Christ works together for good.

Daniel was a truly faithful servant to his boss, King Nebuchadnezzar. That king had the authority to hold Daniel's life, promotion, and everything else in his hands. Yet before such pressure from his superior, he was free. Even facing threats of entering the lion's den and the fiery furnace, he proceeded with composure, following the truth. Prayer gives us the insight and perspective to see that God is greater than those who appear great in this world.

When I commit my way to God and trust in the Lord in all circumstances, regardless of my situation, He brings the conclusion. 'Commit your way to the Lord; trust in him and he will do this' - Psalm 37:5
Hardship makes people wither, and success makes people arrogant. But the Lord makes people abundant and humble.

Prayer itself does not guarantee God's intervention. After Jacob prayed, he made bribes and divided his people into three groups to diversify risk. This is when God breaks his hip at the Jabbok River and makes him pray again. When one says 'though I die, I will die,' they will surely live.

Risk your life in prayer. It will certainly be answered. A person who has nothing but the Lord will surely receive an answer. When all support is gone and only the Lord remains, that is when your golden season comes.
If you remain faithful only when all conditions are met, faithfulness until death is impossible. In limited circumstances, risk your life. You will see God's glory.

You must be faithful when opportunity is given. If I refuse, God's work does not stop. Not only will the work be accomplished, but I and my household may even be destroyed. 'If you remain silent at this time, relief and deliverance for the Jews will arise from another place, but you and your father's family will perish.' Esther 4:13-14. 'If they keep quiet, the stones will cry out.' Luke 19:40

What is best? What the Lord has permitted me to have. The best clothing is what fits me well, and the best food is what tastes good to me. Even if what God has given me seems shabby, He displays His power through it. David had a slingshot, and Moses had a staff.

I do my best according to God's work that empowers me as much as I am able. Maximized obedience is not by the world's standard but being gripped by God's Word and challenging the world with it. Believing that my destiny is determined in the Word is obedience. 'To this end I strenuously contend with all the energy Christ so powerfully works in me.' Colossians 1:29. I pray you become a bulldozer bound by the Word.

What we realize through that failure is that failure is not failure but a process of becoming bolder and trusting more. When walking through the dark valley of death, worldly people will say 'you failed,' but it is not so. 'Though I walk through the darkest valley, if you are with me, I can surely be victorious!'

When David was anointed, what immediately followed was not wearing a crown, being exalted, or being recognized, but rather being chased into the wilderness and trained through 'failure.' What we realize through that failure is that failure is not failure but a process of becoming bolder and trusting more. When walking through the dark valley of death, worldly people will say 'you failed,' but it is not so. 'Though I walk through the darkest valley, if you are with me, I can surely be victorious!'
I hope you do not fight for my victory but for the Lord's will. I hope you become warriors of faith who can rejoice in defeat for the Lord's will. I must be diminished and the Lord must be exalted.

In David's later years, God grants him a blessing without anxiety. Why? Because there is no longer a need to face failure to confirm 'what am I pursuing.' The wisest and best way is complete obedience before the Lord. It will be the fastest way.

In 1977, Voyager 2 traveled toward the end of the solar system at a speed faster than bullets—90,000 miles per hour. In 1989, about 11 years later, it reached Neptune and left the solar system. This means we can see another star only after 958,000 more years. And that's only within one light-year. There are reportedly 100 million such solar systems in the Milky Way, and there are again 100 billion such galaxies in the universe. The number of stars in the universe is approximately 7 trillion times 10 billion, which is 10 times more than all the grains of sand on earth. When you hold a handful of sand, it's 10 to the 16th power. But our God created all of this. 'He also made the stars' (Genesis 1:16). Ah, how simple He is. That is the power of the Creator, the Almighty. Yet this God loves me and disregards the mockery of a deity higher than our personhood, paying all the price on the cross. And He desires that we trust Him with complete faith.

There is a way to always make the devil a losing business. Every time he challenges, if we cling more to the Lord, we make him lose. Though there is tribulation and suffering, we become bolder each day. Haha~~~
Resolve and confirm not to worry about what to eat or what to wear. God knows that these things are necessary for you.
If I shipwreck in faith, any other success is merely a glorious failure. I trust in the faithful and almighty God. He will uphold me.
