As a shepherd, he defeats Goliath, the world's greatest. But when he becomes a great king, he falls before Bathsheba. When I become the king of my life and am exalted, it seems strongest, but that is when I am weakest. When serving God as King, my life becomes solid and strong. *David, who defeated Goliath, was not even recognized in his own house.

The Parable of the Wicked Tenants (Mark 12:1-9). Jesus revealed the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven and revelations hidden from the foundation of the world through parables. In most of Jesus' parables concerning the kingdom of heaven, Jesus appears as the Messiah. Ultimately, unless you believe Jesus as the Messiah, the secret is not unlocked. The truth that 'Jesus is Christ' is the key to unlocking all these parables. Terms requiring explanation: Vineyard: A representative fruit of Israel. In Israel's dry climate without water, grapes were essential as a drink that preserved water. Unlike other plants, grapes required the farmer's absolute help. Hedge: A fence to protect crops. Rented to farmers: It was customary for the owner to rent the land and receive a portion of the harvest. From this we can see the farmer was not the owner. Text Analysis: 1. What did the man set up when building the vineyard in Jesus' parable? 2. In what business model does a man entrust his vineyard to the farmers? ①Hostile M&A ②Franchise (receiving deposit 5:5) ③Subcontract ④Partnership ⑤Renting 3. What did the owner demand from the farmers? ①Company ownership ②Stock options ③Exclusive contract ④Part of the harvest. Surrounding with a hedge and entrusting the vineyard means it was very specially set apart. God specially distinguished Israel from all nations and peoples, desiring to give them mission and Word, salvation and His own Son, Jesus Christ. He gave them the temple and Jerusalem. When leaving on a long journey and entrusting to the farmers, it was because there was faith and expectation. What God desired from them was only a portion of the harvest. But the wicked tenants found even that difficult to give. 4. What did the farmers do to the servants sent by the owner? 5. Who was the last person sent by the owner? ①Debt collector ②Police man ③Gangsters ④Delinquents ( ) 6. What was the owner's expectation when sending his son? (verse 6) 7. Why did the farmers kill the son? (verse 7) Surprisingly, the reason the farmers killed the son was because he was the heir. They mistakenly thought that if they killed the heir, the vineyard's inheritance would become theirs. Complete day robbers. They should have been grateful just to rent the vineyard, but they neither paid the rent nor killed the son. The owner's response to the son's death was lukewarm, showing God's reluctance to destroy Israel through judgment. The ultimate evil for the farmers was mistaking something for their own. When Adam and Eve desired independence, sin entered humanity. When the Pharisees sought glory among themselves, they crucified the God they claimed to love. Ultimately what they desired was complete destruction—making children of hell. 8. Where was the son thrown after being killed? (verse 8) 9. Who do you think is the other person to whom the owner gives the vineyard after destroying the farmers? (verse 9) Primary meaning of the parable: Secondary meaning: Tertiary meaning: Subject: Jews / Gentiles / Me / Vineyard: Israel / Church / My life / Watchtower: Jerusalem Wall / / Temple: Worship / Spiritual ( ) / Renting: Jewish mission, stewardship / / Journey: First coming of Jesus / Second coming / Death / Things to ponder further: 1. What self-centeredness prevents you from accepting Jesus? (e.g., religious zeal, desire for reward, ambition) 2. What do you think God desires when He gives us the vineyard (our life)? 3. The vineyard (church) belongs to God. Do I let God be the Master and the vineyard be the vineyard?
When I fix all my gaze on the Lord and feel the living God, my life becomes the Lord's work and God begins to work.
Do not be overcome by your level or situation. Fix your gaze not on insufficient you, but on complete Me. Fix all your gaze on Me.
What is achieved through effort is good. But when effort hits its limit and all good things crumble, realizing that our strength cannot do it, we then enjoy the best that comes from heaven.

When I look at the same place God looks, God fills all my needs with His power. A person who does the Lord's work for the Lord's glory receives the Lord's support. 'Let this mind be in you which was also in Christ Jesus.' Philippians 2:5

Just as my smile is not merely my smile, what appears now is not everything. The end is not the end; the Lord will surely evaluate and repay. '2 Thessalonians 1:6-10 God is just: He will pay back trouble to those who trouble you and give relief to you who are troubled, and to us as well. This will happen when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven in blazing fire with his powerful angels. He will punish those who do not know God and do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus.'

The devil's message about material wealth is 'I earned this money' and 'anything can be bought with money.' If God does not give it, you have received nothing, and truly important things in life cannot be bought with money. Eternal values are unseen, and unseen values are lived through unseen faith, hope, and love.
What is before me and what is behind me is truly a small matter compared to what is within me.

The devil came to steal, kill, and destroy. He gives victimhood and alienation, separates from community, blocks spiritual supply through division, and destroys self-worth through accusation. Ultimately what he desires is thorough destruction—making children of hell. But the Son of Man, Jesus, came so that we may have life and have it to the full. There is recovery and reconciliation, peace and joy, forgiveness of sin and the joy of heaven, and the powerful and perfect rule of God is established. What made this possible was not something else but the blood of the cross. 'The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.' John 10:10
I believe in the power of prayer. I believe in the faithful God. I believe in the almighty God. I believe He is one who changes hearts. I believe He is one who performs miracles.

Does my God supply all your needs according to Wall Street, the banking system, the economic situation, and the unemployment rate? No. 'And my God will meet all your needs according to the riches of his glory in Christ Jesus.' Philippians 4:19. Any faith and thought that does not allow God to be God is sin. God alone is truly God.

To believe means to change masters. When the master changes, values, worldview, views on relationships, and all evaluations change. The specially given 'authority' and 'power' are used differently. Previously I concentrated only on achieving much and being elevated, on efficiency and effectiveness, but ultimately I realize I can win or lose. True victory is only following the Lord's will.

The reason fear exists is because you have not surrendered. A person who has given everything to Christ and even his own life has nothing left and therefore has nothing to fear in life. Already given everything, life is not mine, and the remaining life is lived by the strength the Lord gives—what more is there to fear?

Anyone in the world can say 'everything will be okay.' But what a person of faith says is different. 'Everything will be okay. Because...' That 'because' is not just a covering branch but a great tree rooted down to the underground. 'Do not be afraid or discouraged because of this vast army. For the battle is not yours, but God's!' 2 Chronicles 20:15
Do not worry or be anxious. The Lord will surely deliver you from the mouths of your professor and your boss. 'I have been delivered from the lion's mouth.' 2 Timothy 4:17
Depending only on the Lord's faithfulness in situations that seem absolutely impossible—that is faith.

When you begin dating, whether you like it or not, the 100th day comes. This is called chronos. But when the relationship deepens and you confess love, it has nothing to do with such mechanical time. That day of confession is called kairos. God will build me with His love and power not according to how old I am (chronos) but on the day that is prepared and fitting for me (kairos). Chronos comes by itself, but kairos must be prepared for.

If everything is prepared but the Lord delays, that is the surest proof that I am not yet ready. I have not become lower, not humble enough—my greed has not been removed. I must bow down more. I should live bowing down so completely that I would not regret even if I spent my whole life doing so. Then the Lord will work.

In the hands of Moses was a dry staff, but when obedient before God's power, it became mighty. The grace God gives is not a new gift but kindling the gifts I already have. I have this style, this vessel, but when I am held in God's hands, I become most powerful.

David had no choice to change his circumstances. But what David thought about when choosing his life's attitude was God's sovereignty. The person who acknowledges the Lord's sovereignty becomes the most powerful person. 'Psalm 75:6-7 No one from the east or the west or from the desert can exalt themselves. It is God who judges: He brings one down, he exalts another.'
Every day I encounter my limits, and through those limits I meet death. In that death I meet the Lord again. How grateful those limits are.
Do what you are able to do, and the Lord will do what is fitting.

If I had known the outcome, I would not have been so anxious. A person of faith knows the outcome. Be assured in the Lord and of the Lord's will. Know that 'my plans for you are not for disaster but for peace'—without faith we become angry, anxious, and discouraged.

When baseless fear overwhelms me and I stand before anxiety about the worst thing I could worry about happening, may I be reminded that only the Lord's will is accomplished and the Lord's will is good for me. The Lord knows the path I take. Let go of worry and anxiety.
Living by faith does not mean laying down your life at the end. From the beginning you are already dead, with no more life to give. What more is there to hope for besides the Lord in heaven?
What I fear determines the direction of my life. God desires that we lay the fear of the future at His feet.
When other paths are blocked, it means the path to prayer is opened. If there is no one to meet, it is what the Lord wants to show you.
Gold belongs to the Lord, and silver belongs to the Lord. If I have small tears and love, they all come from the Lord.

Weariness and hardship cannot block faith. If what I desire is the Lord, then I desire that 'whether I live or die, only the Lord be exalted within me.' The person who seeks the Lord's will cannot be stopped by the world.
You must understand what is most important. It is repentance. Repentance cannot be done by another. It is only by the Lord's grace that 'I' do it.

Become a hunter of hardships. When you encounter hardship, do not be afraid and retreat, but cry out, 'Hardship, I've met you well!' When you collide with it, it is nothing much. It is not hardship itself that makes it difficult, but our hearts breaking before hardship that makes it hard. In Psalm 119:71, 'It is good for me that I have been afflicted, that I may learn Your statutes.' The person who discovers the hidden meaning within hardship is the one Satan fears most. Before Almighty God the Father, no accident happens to us. It means there is always something I must learn in it.
Do not try too hard to do well. That is all greed. Just seek to fulfill the Lord's will. Everything is contained in that.

I hoped for firmness but was shaken, I wished not to be shaken but everything crumbled. In that heartbreaking place, what allows us to still rejoice is that God does not make mistakes. Even in that place that you utterly cannot understand, God 'never' makes mistakes. God is absolutely faithful.
When we assert our rights and oppress others, we do not rise but rather fall lower. God stands with us in the highest authority when we serve and give.

David did not resist the unjust misunderstandings about himself. However, to any challenge against God's name, he risked his life without hesitation. The Lord's person risks their life for the Lord, not for themselves.
Be assured that He who has made me pray will also accomplish it. Prayer requires no words but lying prostrate before Him in supplication.

Problems appearing large is the work of Satan. When you look to the Lord, problems are not problems but prayer requests and merely tools by which the Lord refines me. We are under the mighty hand of the Lord and under the blood of the Lord.
Exposed problems have deep roots. Do not deal with the problem but look only to the Lord beyond it. Only the Lord can heal me and free me from all causes.
Answer and say the Lord will use it. Luke 19:34
Even a person falling without wings can soar again at that falling speed if they rely on the Lord. - The first law of Spiritual Thermodynamic
It is not I who hold to the Lord but the Lord who holds me, so I do not worry excessively. The only reason I have been able to jump up consistently through the past years is 'Jesus.'

Sown among thorns means hearing the word but being choked by the cares of the world and the deceitfulness of wealth, so bearing no fruit - Matt 13:22. Being hurried and anxious suffocates the word received and prevents it from bearing fruit. Please do not worry or hurry. The Lord is still working. Going to the sacrifice before Samuel comes is the greatest mistake.

And he said, As the Lord lives... - 1 Samuel 26:10. If the Lord truly lives in my life, I can abandon anxiety and look only to the Lord. David received many temptations to take human actions, but trusting in the 'living God,' he ultimately gained victory. Hallelujah~ Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
Holding me is the calling of Almighty God. When calling drives life, everything else opens through the Lord's power.
I worry greatly. Because I am trying to hold on. I am not holding on; only the Lord holds all things.

Most people are frustrated because what they need and what they want do not match. What they want is not what they need, and what they truly need is not what they want. When these two coincide, amazing things happen. Only Jesus can simultaneously fulfill both what I need and what I want.
May God's amazing providence of making paths in the wilderness work even more through my brother.
Reach a conclusion. Only the Lord becomes the conclusion.
I love the Lord who touches our stony hearts and lifts us up again. I look only to the Lord who makes rivers and paths in the desert and does new things within us.
There is a high probability of receiving the greatest wound and disappointment from the person you trust most. Because you should rely only on the Lord.
When you respond to a high calling, lower problems resolve naturally.
Times and seasons are determined by God. What people must do is remain faithful. We must respond to the Lord, not react to problems or conflicts.
You will hear of wars and rumors of wars, but be careful, the end is not yet. Instead, the Lord said do not be afraid. We are in the Lord's hands. We long for the Lord's blessing.
** Prayer Meeting ** While praying and fasting, please list up your prayer requests. I desire that the Lord's sovereignty and grace overflow in all areas as we earnestly lift them before Him.

If you have faith even as small as a mustard seed, you will painfully feel that nothing can be accomplished by my strength. Do not handle relationships directly; it will not work as you wish. Entrust it. The level, frequency, and outcome are all in the Lord's hands. The Lord is faithful.
If you succeeded without prayer, that success will destroy you.

When Ruth acted in faith, she 'happened' to enter the field of Boaz. When Boaz saw Ruth in the field, he said, 'She's really different.' Expect God's guidance and provision. Do not doubt the Lord anymore.

Fear is also a kind of faith. A stubborn belief that something bad will surely happen to me. Believing means not calculating circumstances but going to the Lord and entrusting everything, whether it becomes death or food.

Do not envy or be jealous of opportunities that have come to others. Because opportunities prepared for you will surely come someday. Opportunities are given not to those who envy but to those who prepare.
Meaning is far more important than money. A simple life in the fear of God is better than riches with a ton of headaches.
There are no shortcuts to maturity. While we worry about how fast we grow, God is concerned about how strong we grow. God is never in a hurry but He is always on time! Keep going guys.

God was so pleased with David that He said He would accomplish all His planned work through him. God was so pleased when He saw David's three qualities of heart: gentleness, faithfulness, and his attitude toward authority.

Whether good or bad things happen to me, the Lord's will is that they harmonize and work together so that all situations conclude beautifully. For this to happen, I must love God and be in His calling.

There is something I must concentrate on: God's will and presence. The remaining things are what God must be concerned about. Therefore, I must not worry about things that have not yet happened or things that have already happened. I hope you look to the Lord at this present moment.

Take your brother and go back to that man again. I pray that Almighty God will show you mercy before that man and cause him to send back your other brother and Benjamin. If I am bereaved of my son, then I am bereaved. (Genesis 43:13-14) After this incident, Jacob not only does not lose Benjamin but experiences Joseph coming back to life. When I let go with the heart that I am finished, the Lord gives me the strength to begin again.
The day of decision is dawning.

When God gives hardship, He has determined to save that person. If God intended to destroy, He would not give hardship. Those who will perish He leaves alone. I hope you give thanks in hardship and are transformed. Hold to the word and make the resolve to die, and you will live.

The difference between my desire to have problems solved and God's desire to fundamentally change my life determines the length of my refining period. Ultimately there is only one place to return to, the difference is how much I get beaten along the way. A soccer ball, no matter how much it bounces, eventually goes into the goal.

When we Retreated, 79 cars were needed. While preparing the message, I limited my request for help to those I could contact and the Lord gave me the heart to trust in Him. When we left Wycliffe at the end after arranging everything and checked all the vehicles, there was not one seat left over or lacking. God is high efficiency with low cost.
Worrying does not solve it. Being anxious does not improve it. Please entrust it to the Lord. The one working among us is the Lord.

If my situation is impossible and beyond my capacity, then it is time for the Lord to work. That is why it has been so difficult to receive Almighty God as the Lord of my life, and I have come circuitously before the Lord.
How great a misfortune it would be if God is not present in that place where I spend most of my time.
Abraham, the father of faith, was not great; rather, the God he believed in was great.
Standing in my present circumstances as God desires, confessing and acknowledging that the Lord is the master of my work—this is the worship that pleases God most.

Pray about all the problems you face. Then those problems are not solved by me but by God. Even problems from when I was too young to understand, even problems that existed before I was born—if I lift them up to Him, He solves them all.
...But I have come to this place for this very reason. John 12:27
Trying to solve everything in your own way on the spot is excessive greed. Though the problem may be solved, it leaves deeper wounds. There is the Lord's timing in all things.

People think living as they please is freedom. They look down on believers walking the narrow path as pitiful. That is not freedom but slavery being dragged by sin. When you become a servant obedient to the Lord, you find true freedom. Being free, you need not be a slave to sin.
The portion measured out to me with a line is in a beautiful place; truly, my inheritance is beautiful.

Please do not calculate too much. Even if you do, you cannot know, and even if you think you do, there is no solution, making you even more frustrated. If the situation is one the Lord has allowed, there is surely the Lord's way. Do not panic; pray.
When I serve the Lord humbly, there are many painful things, but I rely all the more on the Lord I can depend on.
Thinking I will serve the Lord in a successful manner is pride. When I do not depend on the Lord, I have already become proud and will fall.
Those who honor me I will honor..., 1 Samuel 2:30

A psychology professor in university said that the most childish thing in the world is seeking respect because you are older, richer, or more educated. The world may play along with it, but the church does not. If the Lord does not acknowledge you, you are nothing. What people think is not what matters; what God evaluates is what matters. You are not a mass-produced commodity but a masterpiece irreplaceable by anything else.
People say the economy is difficult. The most foolish thing among these is killing a thriving economy to try to revive the economy. Let us run so that grace is poured over the crumbs given to me.

Doing what I want to do and believing what I want to believe is not faith. Seeking the face of God is true faith. People wish God would change, but God never changes and is never persuaded. Because He is truth itself.

There is no excuse that we cannot do the Lord's work because we lack money. It makes no sense to say we cannot do the work of the almighty Lord due to shortage. If we abandon human glory and look to the Lord's glory, there is no reason God would not provide.
If I do not allow it, no one can destroy my life.
If you surrender the initiative to God, He can help you.
There are no birds that worry. God said He loves us more than the birds of the air. Through faith and confidence in God's love alone, nearly all problems are resolved. Why do you fear?

How much I have done and what I have accomplished are not important. That is the world's standard. What God desires is not numbers, popularity, or recognition, but that the Lord actually rules over me. My kneeling in a secret place may be no worldly achievement, but if the Lord is ruling, naturally the Lord will be revealed through me.

When you acknowledge Him, God's sovereignty is revealed. Acknowledge the Lord's sovereignty in finances, relationships, academics, at work, in spiritual warfare. If I do not believe in the Lord's sovereignty, I belong to Satan and face condemnation. I urge you to declare that the land we tread is the land of God's sovereignty.

When buried in problems, I always become the victim and the other becomes the perpetrator. But when I look to the Lord, both I and the other are saved. David took 'courage' through the Lord. When seen from the Lord's perspective, everyone is a pitiful sinner who needs Jesus.

A person who seeks happiness through their reputation, occupation, bank balance, or circumstances is truly pitiful. It is like being tossed by ocean waves. Rather than such perishable things, where am I heading? Who am I running toward? That is more important. Life is not made happy by numbers. It is determined by direction. When I understand life by direction, only then does my life begin to be interpreted.

Many kings fell because their days were short. They did not fall from weakness but from becoming too strong. When I try to defend and protect myself, in the end I collapse greatly. A person completely broken before the Lord seeks nothing but the Lord. David was not trying to protect himself, so the Lord protected him instead.

The reason David was strong was because he had nothing to seek. A person aiming for worldly goals easily falls into traps. I hope you seek nothing but the Lord. In contrast, Saul fears people and oppresses them to protect his kingship. Those who try to preserve and those who try to surrender live on different levels.

There are two ways to close. The easy way is to barely finish by running away; the way God desires is to receive power and leave at the Lord's time. Let us abandon the prejudice that only the easy path is the way to live. When the Lord's sovereignty is proclaimed and the Lord's time is fulfilled and I complete my portion of transformation, the Lord sets me on high. I pray we become young people held in the Lord's hand, dividing the Red Sea of dark sin.
If I am not broken, health, family, and church are broken. If the Lord is the Savior of my spirit, He must also be King of my self. - From a European Revival Conference -

Countless invisible messages fill the sky through mobile signals. A Christian's intercessory prayer is even more meaningful and incomparably powerful comfort, and it moves the hand of the Almighty that actually works. One of the most important things is that coverage is actually infinite compared to mobile. ^^

The Principle of the Magi: Wise men from different lands dwelling in different countries each followed different routes following the star, and ultimately they met before the baby Jesus. God calls different people through different ways for His kingdom and His glory.

It seems I must do many things, and I think I can only stand where everyone hopes if I do everything well. That is compulsion. That is deception. Life is not achieved by effort alone. Because my life is not mine but the Lord's. When I entrust and accept from the Lord, He shows me secret things I do not know. Martha, Martha, you are anxious and troubled about many things, but one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is better, which will not be taken away from her. When busy, mistakes come.
A moment before praying, then pray and everything becomes good. The essence comes alive in conversation, and the Lord becomes the master of the home.

Three million livestock were buried alive due to foot-and-mouth disease. It is said to have started from an overseas visit by one farm owner. When sinful Israelites try to live with God, one of two things happens. They either obey and receive God's power, or disobey and receive discipline. We are important people, so individual obedience is required.

Do not worry what you will eat, what you will wear, how things will go. Worry accomplishes nothing and instead devours faith. If you worry, you are valuing food, clothing, and problems more than God. Worry is sin.
The saying that even a thorn becomes a rose if held to your heart means I do the holding but God brings the change.

When sailing at sea, if you look at the nearby waves, you will run aground. But if you fix your gaze on the lighthouse or at least the North Star as your standard, you can move forward without wavering. When you look not at the Lord but at people and circumstances, you will experience despair, disappointment, temptation, discouragement, and anger. By asking what the subject of your suffering is, you can know what you are looking at.
Beware of the person who says, 'I will take care of everything.' God does not work so carelessly that we would depend on people. If you rely on people, you will surely collapse.
It is not boldness that brings blessing. God's blessing is poured out by God. Awake from deep sleep, rise up now, lift your holy hands, and pray.
The soul that prays shall be blessed in all things; since the Lord does not forsake me, even if there is trial, even if life is threatened, I will pray to the end.
During five thousand years of our nation's history, when we lacked strength, we lost our country. To not lose yourself and your friends, there must be revival.

Increase the concentration of prayer. Sometimes God uses our deep pain as a passage for His wonderful plans. Because pain causes us to kneel against the flesh and move toward the seat of earnest prayer that transcends human-level desires and seeks the Lord's face. Even now the Lord searches throughout this world and finds those who cannot live without Him (people of whole heart), pouring not human wishes but God's wishes, and challenges them so that prayer at that level is fulfilled. He does not answer the prayer of one seeking to bear a son, but causes the prayer that brings Samuel, who brings the dawn of history, to be prayed.
Give what you must give; the Lord will give what He will give.
Relationships: * Do not be obsessed with influencing the world and people in response to the question, "What are you doing here right now?" Simply grow intimate with God. That is the true purpose. Do not grieve over not being first; rather, dwell beside the Lord and become beautiful. You will become stronger instead. "O LORD, you have searched me and known me. You know when I sit down and when I rise up; you discern my thoughts from afar. You search out my path and my lying down, and are acquainted with all my ways. Even before a word is on my tongue, behold, O LORD, you know it altogether." —Psalm 139:1-4
* Do not hate yourself; repent. Rather than hating your weakness, you must repent of not relying on the Lord above all else.
* The Standard of Recovery: In the Lord, with the Saints. Dwell in the Lord and commune with the saints. Those who are "in" and "together" are the most powerful.
* The Reason for Generosity: Because you have already received much, you do not cling to greed desperately. It is because you are in the Lord and receiving precious love from those you love.
* Why You Feel Empty Despite Working Hard: All ability, meaning, and the value of your existence come from relationship. Seek Him.
* The Pilgrim: What a pilgrim needs is a good friend—one who comes from God.

* Rejoice Even When You Are Not Seen: A mother is satisfied just seeing delicious food go into her son's or daughter's mouth. If her child looks pretty in a photograph, she is happy even if she herself is not seen. "I have no greater joy than to hear that my children are walking in the truth." —3 John 1:4
* The Church Is Not Far: It is not burdensome to live a life of faith because the church is far away. The church is near. It is only your home that is far.

* The Deep Shadow of Death: It happens because you try too hard to excel and want to be told you do well. As pride and disappointment cross, you will experience hell. Do not try to do well; love instead.
* What You Realize When Becoming One with the Lord: What you thought was burden and weight was actually love.

* Ministry is Delivery: It is delivering God's grace—His very self, His presence. It is not information about Him or usefulness, but being with Him. Therefore, the responsibility of one who delivers is to faithfully go back and forth between God's post office and the souls' homes.
* What I Must Always Remind Myself Of: The relationship between me and the Lord, and the position of the Lord and my position.

* To the Praise Team at Retreat: Show them what kind of God called us, what He has done for us, how we want to respond before that love, how beautiful it is to become one and experience the Lord's waterfall-like presence, and how far the beauty of the One who makes us beautiful reaches. Regardless of the results, let only the Lord shine, and that is the victory.
* The More You Love the Lord, the More You Love Souls: As you draw near to the Lord, there are souls who draw nearer to you.

* Birthday Gift: It does not make you happy to give ten years' worth of gifts all at once. The reason we wait until the birthday to give a gift we prepared in advance is because a gift adds value when you wait for it.
* Repentance: I was a far worse son than the 'me' I knew. God waited patiently and loved the 'me' that only I did not know. Therefore, I repent.
* Being Loved: You were not called to receive the love of people. Because you are one who receives God's love, you are free to love and be loved.
* Fear: In the Garden of Eden, Adam and Eve hid in fear. What they feared most was being rejected in their naked, exposed form.

* True Concern: At this moment, you must think of brothers and sisters who are in tribulation and suffering, and pray earnestly. If you only seek relationships that you want, how are you any better than those who do not believe?

* The Capital of Love: What is needed to love? The answer is 'nothing is needed.' If something else must be added for it to happen, it is a merger and acquisition, not love. Holding only Jesus in your heart with a burning spirit is enough.
* Maturity Shows Consideration: Because you escape self-centered interpretation and perspective. When you grow up, you put away childish things. That is love and completion.

* Jacob Meets Laban: Jacob did not know how cunning he was, though everyone else knew. Only after meeting his uncle Laban did he realize how painful such behavior is to others. When struggling with relational difficulties, you must see another side of yourself in the other person.
* The Lord's Sorrow: When I am grieved about what will perish, He truly grieves.
* You Are Not Unhappy Because You Are Ignored: You are not unhappy because people ignore you or the world does not give you opportunity. You become unhappy because you ignore the Lord.
* What Came Out After Ten Years: "Now I finally understand why worship is so important," said a young man, wiping his tears.

* Guard Your Lips: If you do not guard your lips, you will labor in vain and feed your work to dogs. Rude language stems from self-centered thinking that leaves no room to empathize with others' pain. Ultimately, you reap that fruit thoroughly yourself and fail to be welcomed, and only you do not know it. Empathy is ability.
* Why You Are Not Afraid: Because there is One who walks with you, and your purpose has changed.

* Songs of Ascent—Psalm 131: Looking at the songs of ascent to the temple through David's psalms, there is no content about how to make the bulletin or how to display subtitles. There is simply the peace and longing of a weaned child because God is so good.

* It Seems Extremely Faithful, but It Is Not: It was simply that people were likable and people were good. Faith is not that people are good, but that Jesus is good, and it is good when Jesus is revealed in fellowship because of Jesus. An easy way to know is to ask whom you are witnessing about.
* Draw Near: If you draw near to God, God will also draw near to you.
* Love the souls entrusted to you with all your pain.

* Too Much Concern: Honestly, others are not that interested in me, but I am only worried about others' gaze. You can love your neighbor at the same level as your own body, and others can have peace only when you have peace.
* The Condition of Happiness: A person who has a community that can receive grace is blessed. If supply and healing happen through community, you have received a great blessing.
* God's Special People: A person who knows how much God loves people this way cannot easily despise or hate others. It is because God loved them specially.

* What Was Taken for Granted Was Never Taken for Granted: Be thankful for what you took for granted. You will realize that it was never something to be taken for granted, but kindness that came from love and sacrifice.

* A Precious Journey: I hope to travel warmly with the people you love. May the coming and going be filled with grace and beauty that the Lord fills, and may each become an unforgettable meaning and name to one another, and may the company of Jesus become fascinating.

* God's Voice: The reason people are anxious even in joy and worry does not fade even when they seem at peace is that they do not hear God's voice. Quietness without voice is only the eve of a typhoon. But when you hear His voice, you have peace. "Seek my face," you hear. "When you said, 'Seek my face,' my heart said to you, 'Your face, LORD, do I seek.'" —Psalm 27:8
* If You Love: If you love, you will pray. If you pray and say you love without praying, it means you wanted to love once, not that you are loving.

* If You Really Like a Dress: If you like a dress, you naturally go on a diet. If a dress is good, your body knows it first, and if love is really good, you resemble its object. Therefore, when you love beautifully, the lusts of the flesh are forgotten.
* Life Becomes Difficult Because You Seek to Be Loved: Even though you were sent to give love, you seek to receive it.
* A Precious Person: A precious person leaves a bigger empty space when they depart.

* When You Get Angry at Clueless People: You are more angry at the fact that they still do not know it than at the mistake itself. The problem is that the standard of 'mistake' is too varied and self-centered, and things that are not sufficiently shared. There is also the awkwardness of sharing such things. There is no one who can realize this themselves and adjust to it, and the only One who knows is One without intention to adjust. That is why many good people tilt their heads and say, "What on earth is wrong?"

* Why You Do Not Speak When Disappointed: Most of the time, when you are disappointed, you do not explain because being hurt by such a matter seems embarrassing even in your own thinking. Yet you are still angry, so you vent about other things. There are many things that are not anger-worthy, but things you imagined and misunderstood.
* Me: There is 'me' that I know and others know, 'me' that only I know, and 'me' that others know but I do not. Yet if you know the 'me' that God loves, the other 'me's are merely changing garments.

* When Pride Suddenly Hurts and You Flare Up: The real reason you get angry is not that the seemingly unreasonable evaluation of you does not match you, but that it fits you terrifyingly well. Because you cannot accept your own value, you respond so sensitively to others' evaluations. When your relationship with God becomes the standard rather than worldly reputation and success, you become a firm person.
* One Cause of All Problems: Because you do not seek God's face. The reason death and dying do not become problems is that the Lord's love compels us. —2 Corinthians 5

* One Wish: In His presence, to eternally long for the Lord's beauty and complete union with the Lord. "Seek my face" (Psalm 27). Jesus is "the radiance of the glory of God and the exact imprint of his nature" (Hebrews 1:3). All the apostles' ministry was only to seek His face (2 Corinthians 3:18, 4:1), and Jesus told the crowds who wanted to see the Father, "See Me" (John 14:8-9). Look only to the Lord 24 hours a day. That is all.
* Have a Nice Week: Every week you have the best weekend because you meet truly lovely people.
* How to Return to the Father: Eat the carob pods that pigs eat little by little. Then the heart to return arises. Stop eating the rotten carob pods and come home running to the Father's house.

* The value of the alabaster jar offered by one who loves is not diminished—Kim Yeon-ah. The woman who broke the alabaster jar is criticized, but the value of her love is not diminished. Because the most important One recognizes it. The fact that judges who deny Kim Yeon-ah and those who recognize her are divided like the Red Sea means that Kim Yeon-ah's blood and tears are not tarnished on the gold-leafed medal.
* The most terrifying film—God's people appearing without love.

* The reason Sunday observance fails is not because of too much sleep, nor because of too much work, nor even because there is someone disliked at church. It is simply because the relationship has grown distant—so distant that two or three hours for God seem wasteful. God is Life.

* When you cannot be humble—If you despise and hate someone because they do not possess much or did not graduate from a prestigious school, it is because you have no other way to prove your existence value except to criticize and compare their weaknesses. This stems from deep internal inferiority complex. People tend to vehemently deny, condemn, and impute their own weaknesses when others discover them.

* The ability to remove heavy burdens—Even if you desperately try to remove heavy burdens, they cannot be removed. Heavy burdens are not taken off but exchanged for lighter, easier ones. If success is laboring and bearing heavy burdens to avoid becoming miserable, then the cross gives us the courage to be okay with becoming a humble being in the world. Those who have the courage not to fear becoming miserable also receive the grace of running to the Lord's arms and being held. 'Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy and my burden is light.' Matthew 11:28,30

* The evidence that a branch is not attached to the vine—it bears no fruit and withers powerlessly. The only reason for a grapevine's existence is to bear fruit, and that happens only when it is wholly attached to the vine and receives the farmer's cleansing.
* Life's victory depends on who you connect with. When my soul was weary within me, I remembered the Lord; and my prayer rose to You, into Your holy temple. Jonah 2:7

* The greatest gift and the plot twist—If you didn't expect it but friends remembered your birthday and sang 'Happy Birthday,' you would be moved. But soon after, if they lit candles on a beautiful cake and blessed a fellow believer on the same birthday, it would immediately turn into hell. (Comparing) Your heart would ache so much that tears would come, and you'd rush to the bathroom, when a brother as handsome as Jung Woo-sung secretly places a ring in your hand where only you can see. (Identity) The tears you were holding back become a storm. Because you experience heaven. (Happiness) Happiness is not about Quantity but Quality. Do not expect many meaningless welcomes of lesser importance, but expect the Lord who is truly important to you.

* The advance of sovereignty—Executing God's will is called faithfulness. Therefore, when you are faithful in faith without looking to people, you fulfill the Lord's will and experience His rule. Among the versions of faithfulness, the most advanced one is 'love.' Therefore, those who love are called perfect. 'And over all these virtues put on love, which binds them all together in perfect unity.' Colossians 3:14. 'The one who loves others has fulfilled the law.' Romans 13:8

* Seek His relationship first—The Pharisees excelled in keeping the law but tried to prove themselves first through human relationships, so they were rejected. The tax collectors had many faults but received mercy through their relationship with the Lord. Happiness and destiny are determined by the priority of relationships. Seek first His kingdom and His righteousness.
* True faith—Even when it is extremely difficult to look upon God, it is to surrender your heart and focus to the Lord.
* Special every day—Fresh events are sometimes necessary, but if you truly love, you can show deep love rather than events. A thriving community makes every week a spiritual retreat.

* The Lord's interest—Jesus' interest is 'Who do you say I am?' Therefore, you must be clear about who your master is. You must examine what you are looking at, who fills you, what brings you joy, and what wounds you.

* The recovery of a struggling father—Even when providing for the family is difficult and work at the company is hard, a cheerful child's words 'Dad, I love you' can make him forget all worries and rise up again. What cannot be denied is the genuine confession of these young people that they love Jesus. This is sufficient.

* What really needs to be overcome—You must transcend your own subtle rationalized desires. It is not because temptation is strong; it is because you cannot love Jesus, so you do not abandon yourself and are deceived. But you cannot deceive even the Lord. If you truly love the Lord, you will grieve and repent.

* You are not deceived when you see the motive—Seeing appearance is sensation, seeing results is logic. But seeing motive is love. That is why God sees the motive. 'The Lord does not look at the things people look at. People look at the outward appearance, but the Lord looks at the heart.' 1 Samuel 16:7

* The subject of repentance—Repent for lacking zeal. Lacking zeal means lacking passion, and lacking passion means not being filled. The greatest sin is being without the Lord and filled with other things. Therefore, 'be zealous' and 'repent' mean the same thing. What breaks the relationship of love is not lack and mistakes, but boredom and indifference. Do not squeeze; let it overflow. 'Those whom I love I rebuke and discipline. So be earnest and repent.' Revelation 3:19

* Closer than suffering—After entering into severe suffering, you realize that Jesus is even closer than the suffering itself. That is why affliction is a blessing. 'Before I was afflicted I went astray, but now I obey your word.' Psalm 119:67
* Unity is not just a good method—In faith, becoming one in love is not merely an effective method to achieve a goal but is itself victory and revival.

* Mentor and mentee—The one who teaches is the one who learns. The one who learns becomes the teacher. In Hebrew, 'to learn' is 'lamad.' But when you use the causative intensive form 'limed,' it becomes 'to learn much' and also 'to teach.' The one who teaches learns even more.
* Sorrow gives birth to mercy and compassion—God is great in mercy and boundless in compassion because He experienced sorrow that no one else could have experienced.
* What is love?—If it ends with 'Ah, this is just perfect,' it will soon collapse. Love is based on understanding and fulfilling responsibility.
Hungry people, call our home! Daniel J Kim Young Jae Jo

When you are betrayed, insulted, or treated unfairly, it hurts your heart. But what hurts more is when you do not look to the Lord properly. When such anguish comes, please confess like this: 'I am a person who loves the Lord.'

There are things that should be discarded but are not—pride, greed, lust, envy, jealousy, inferiority, anger. These are ingrained in us for life, possess subtle charm, parasitize the flesh, and are not discarded. But before the knowledge of Jesus, they all become dung. Dung must be discarded. If you only hold onto Jesus, faith becomes not 'asceticism' but 'romance.'

Even when criminals gather, there can be truth; even when only saints gather, there can be pettiness. Cutting in line at church is evil, but consideration for new members is good; encouraging and building up brothers and sisters is good, but if others feel excluded through it, that is evil. Good faith is not extreme. That is why we are not judges. Only the Lord judges.

When people sin and leave God, two phenomena appear. The joy of salvation disappears, and the willing spirit vanishes. (Psalm 51:12) This is because they grieve and quench the Spirit of the Lord. (Psalm 51:11) Consequently, when holiness is lost, the Lord's presence disappears, and no matter how much work is done, no transformation occurs. (Psalm 51:13)

Peter discovered he was a sinner before the Lord and asked Jesus to leave. But Jesus stayed with him instead. Even in the disappointing moment when His own disciple denied him and in his weakened flesh in the twilight of his life, Jesus never once left. The Lord does not leave us. Never.

The invisible roots determine the freshness of the visible tree. Through the parents and authorities given to me to serve, receiving blessings is the first step to unlocking my interpersonal relationships, ministry, and vision. I pray that parents and ministers may fully bless young people with sufficient wisdom so they can, and that all young people whose lives are opening up become people filled with understanding. Success or failure in life is in the basics.
Prodigals who left because they disliked the gospel need the gospel again. There can be no recovery or salvation apart from Jesus. That is why Christians are persistent. When it comes to Jesus.
If it were not for the gospel, it would be merely an event with nothing in common, but before the gospel, the souls standing before it become family.
A life satisfied with the Lord alone without complicated calculations... Blessed are those who long for the Lord's house and are already happy because the Lord is present.
In the insufficient disciples, we ultimately see Jesus. Because Jesus shed His blood and loved them.

If you have the knowledge of resurrection but do not experience the risen Lord, you will not be transformed. When you meet the Lord, open your heart, and He pours out His word, and your heart becomes warm, a U-turn in life appears. Transformation comes from faith. Because faith is the perspective that sees greater value and the decision to choose it.
The clear fact is that as much as you forgive, as much as you love, you can be united with the Lord. There is no other religious activity that can replace this.

Discernment is not easy. It requires capacity. The greatest capacity for discernment is love and passion. What the Lord considers first when looking at us, and therefore God accepts, gives opportunity, and never gives up. Therefore, those with the Lord's heart do not give up and forgive to the end.
You are anxious. Because you have momentarily lost the Lord. Remember the Lord again and intercede for all, and you will find the lost peace. Peace without Jesus is all fake.

If you don't change even though it's uncomfortable, you will remain in the same place. Even if you avoid uncomfortable and unwilling people, you will meet another similar person. This is because your spiritual level is fixed. Even if you know God and have experience, if you do not obey at the critical moment, such relational friction will not cease. If there is a gloomy and frustrating relationship, at the core of that problem lies my self that has not died. Even a worm writhes when stepped on. It's because you didn't step on it properly. I cannot kill my self on my own. The only way is to invite Jesus to the throne of my life. Then Jesus, holding suffering me, goes to the cross. There you can feel the Lord's love flowing toward you.
Though foolish, I am not foolish enough to exchange the Lord's glory for perishable glory. Even if I know nothing else, if I know the Lord, I know what is truly important.

When vitamins are deficient, disease occurs and death may follow. If the Lord becomes a deficiency in me, a far more serious damage and suffering will come. Do not seek solutions in the wrong places; quickly come to your senses and come before the Lord. Otherwise, 'self-pity' will take the place where the Lord is absent and lead you to resentment and emptiness.
Criticism may satisfy the heart momentarily, but you lose people. Though it takes time, persuasion leads to understanding and brings forth willingness.
* The counsel to reject and the counsel to accept—Counsel without affection and counsel with affection.

Living as a supporting actor while watching people's expressions is not the life the Lord designed. In your own life, only the Lord is the Lord, and you live as His masterpiece. The world's interest is popularity and recognition, but the Lord's interest is in 'relationship.'
People cannot reach eternity through meetings where they wear masks and perform. Because a play always ends. The meeting where you are most yourself is a good meeting.

People erase the past and pretend and act as if. In conclusion, it's because they want to hear 'you are a decent person I cannot ignore.' That's why they 'exaggerate' and 'overdo' it. You don't need to exaggerate or pretend to be something you're not. It's because you don't know yourself well. Because you don't know God well, you don't know yourself well. Instead of exaggerating and overdoing it, be loved. I will also love you warmly. Big Hug!! ^^ Happy Valentine's.
* True words of love—Speak the words that Jesus within you wants to say to that person.

People say they solve things through 'conversation' with each other. Misunderstandings occur far more often than things are resolved. When you pray to the Lord, intimate 'conversation' with the almighty Father takes place, and you receive power from heaven.

* The reasons it's difficult—First, it means you are saved; second, because you love those moving forward; and third, because you love souls. Without these, there would be nothing to be fundamentally concerned about.
People are wounded and sin. People especially are wounded and sin where God is absent. Blessed are those who come to the Lord's holy of holies and meet the God who works in secret.
I don't know how happy it is to live loving people. Love makes people happy.
If you harbor sadness and pain from not being loved, you will have few happy moments in your lifetime. Love is given by the Lord, and I am to love and serve with that love.

Love gives us courage. It causes us to challenge ourselves for the person we love. We may fail and fall, but without love we would not attempt such things. Even if things don't go according to plan, the heartfelt regret itself proves we have loved with all our hearts. If we have loved with all our hearts, we will feel neither shame nor fear.
Love is sacrifice. However, it must be distinguished from investment. Love is an end in itself. Therefore, Jesus is not an event. In every moment, with every breath, we must remain in Jesus Christ.

* Living together with the Lord means dwelling with the Lord. The reason people live according to their own desires is because they do not truly know Jesus. Those who dwell with Jesus cannot live selfishly or greedily.

A loving heart is given by God. Therefore, to be loved according to God's will is both a gift and grace. It is not because I am excellent, but because the grace the Lord pours out is deep and great. Therefore, we must truly be grateful and love with that heart.
Wanting to bless the people I love, as an insufficient person, I become a praying person, a servant of God.
To love means that one's very existence becomes joy.
In truth, many people considered bad are not bad—their hearts are wounded. They are friends in great need of much love. Only God's love can establish them and make them whole.

Do not rejoice in your ministry, nor in successful ministry and visible results. Rather, rejoice in eternal relationships and love. We should not rejoice so much in what we could do for God, but rather in what God has done for us. Yet Jesus said: Do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven. —Luke 10:20
Ministry is not something to be forced or squeezed out. As the Lord always did with His disciples, it overflows. The Lord's love compels us.
Do not work at ministry; seek to love. The God we believe in is a God of relationship. Rather than doing well, I hope you become a person of the power of His abiding presence, strong in God's grace.
* The Lord alone brings restoration. Restoration does not come from circumstances or a change of mood. Only the Lord restores me. This is because restoration means returning to the Lord.
To paraphrase what the Lord said in the Sermon on the Mount: a person who makes a community peaceful is one who lives well (blessed), while one who causes discord and division is a failure.

Samson caught three hundred foxes and burned the Philistines' crops, then said: 'Even if I harm them this time, I will have done no wrong.' When people become angry, they become extremely creative in thinking of ways to take revenge. We call that a waste. Be creative in loving.
When you love another person, you can see your true self. But when you hate, you see only a fierce, wounded person.
Loving someone by forcing them into your mold is violence. When you understand and look, understanding comes; when you forgive and look, you can love.

Hurt comes down to one thing: a sense of rejection. However, rejection is not the root cause of hurt. The essential issue is that before the matter of rejection, you have lost the identity of 'how much value I have in the Lord.' The problem is not the environment or people, but not knowing 'who I am in the Lord.' — During a leadership meeting: 'Dear children, I write to you because you have known the Father. Dear parents, I write to you because you have known him who is from the beginning. Dear young people, I write to you because you are strong, and the word of God lives in you, and you have overcome the evil one.' —1 John 2:14

The Lord who touches a wounded heart—Indeed, the only One who touches a wounded heart is He alone. No matter how much humans create atmosphere, arrange things, or compose words, ultimately healing and restoration happen before His throne. There is no need to be anxious about the Lord's timing or concerned about His way. Trust the Lord and wait for Him.

When we become new creations, new desires arise. One of them is a constant desire to gather. If you are a Christian, do you dislike gathering? You must be restored. Make every effort to keep the unity of the Spirit through the bond of peace. —Ephesians 4:3

A thoughtless person chooses gifts by price. A gift is my heart toward the other person. Therefore, a gift reflects the image of the recipient. A single rose means you are beautiful, and a small keychain means I will walk with you. 'It's your favorite SPAM luncheon meat. It's quite expensive compared to others. Good, right?' means 'You are my comrade.'
* Jesus does not cling. Clinging comes from fear and anxiety. Jesus loves without clinging. He loves your heart, not your appearance and image.
* The reason for compassion: God the Father has compassion on us as a father does his children (Psalm 103). He remembers. He knows our makeup and remembers that we are but dust. —Psalm 103:14

* The mechanism of tolerance: Tolerance comes from remembrance. Remembering that I was a sinner forgiven of ten thousand talents. Without the joy of salvation, spiritual amnesia makes people cruel. Should you not have had mercy on your fellow servant just as I had on you? —Matthew 18:33
* Right now: Those who love me will love my love, and those who seek me earnestly will find me. —Proverbs 8:17

* God's Crystal Terms and Conditions: Your sin stands between us, interrupting our communion. I can no longer bless you while you persist in it. Trust My Spirit to help you, My child! Please do not reject God's Word. Complete obedience is power. Because the king rejected the word of the Lord, the Lord rejected him and did not allow him to remain king of Israel. —1 Samuel 15:26

* Those I respect and love—their outstanding ministry and gifts, their position—I simply acknowledge. But for those who love Jesus, I automatically bow my head and they earn my respect. They are so lovable that their shortcomings are covered.

No one gets married wanting to do house cleaning and garden maintenance. Such actions naturally follow because of love. I urge you never to work without grace. Whatever you do, if you walk with Him, you will be happy.

Do not seek to succeed; seek to obey. My fulfillment is not what matters; the Lord's will comes first. That is discipleship. By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another. —John 13:35
When the Holy Spirit comes, He helps us know Jesus better and testifies to the world. The true work of the Holy Spirit leads us to deep knowledge and understanding of Jesus.

Worldly thoughts and human thoughts are non-existent thoughts with no resonance. They are merely thoughts. However, thoughts of Jesus make His presence real and allow us to experience God coming in the midst of His people's longing.

The world seeks success and happiness through much learning and research. What such pursuit overlooks—what is unseen and unheard by worldly eyes—is relationship with God. True victory and happiness is relationship with God. It is the most basic and irreplaceable high calling.

* Do you think I made a mistake? From the same worship and receiving the same Holy Spirit, you are annoyed and complain about a member who praises the same God. There are reasons for it. The Lord asks you: 'Do you think I made a mistake?' Hold onto the small before you lose the great. The love the Lord requires is not easy, but it is not impossible either.

A retreat transforms me through the power of godliness; it does not provide a transformed environment. A place without signals and without occasion for sin is completely different from the world. To minimize the difference between that place and this, inner transformation must occur. Coming to dawn prayer after going to Korea is not because I am diligent but because of temporary jet lag. Inner transformation and fellowship with people of faith become power. Also: Flee the evil desires of youth and pursue righteousness, faith, love and peace, along with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart. —2 Timothy 2:22

* Why He rescues only after we let go: He rescues only after we let go. God wanted to give us incomparably better things rather than fill our desires. What God really wanted to give was Himself. The person who can receive the Lord as a gift is someone who is willing to accept even failure and is satisfied with Him.
When you love, there is no regret, and you become happy.

* Knowing myself: David was the eighth son, not even introduced to important guests. When his father sent him on an errand, he was not trusted enough to be asked for proof. His older brother Eliab habitually spoke harshly to him. He even confessed that his mother conceived him in sin. Yet he was faithful, steadfast, and bold because he discovered himself in relationship with the Lord. Abide in me, and I will abide in you. —John 15:4 Though my father and mother forsake me, the Lord will receive me. —Psalm 27:10

Good faith grows when we know the Lord better. The God David experienced has no misunderstanding of me, knows everything, and leaving the Lord is spiritually impossible beyond space and time. Eventually, realizing that each moment I breathe is held in the Lord's hands, I cherish His Word, examine myself, and seek His guidance.
When we see the stubbornness of believers, what should the church do? Not anger and criticism, but weeping. Empathy is power.

* The way of love 3: I must love fervently with all that is given and permitted to me. And I must take out one life insurance policy. Insurance originally pays out when something is gone. It is to intercede without ceasing so that the souls I have loved can continue to grow, looking only to the Lord.
Seeing the beautiful face of the Lord, my head bows before You. Your hand touches me and comforts my body and soul.

Leaving the Father's embrace to serve idols, we lose our identity as children and live imprisoned in a pig sty. People who have lost themselves and live imprisoned boast at best of gathering much carob pods that pigs eat. Do not merely survive; soar like an eagle toward the goal. Tear your hearts, not your garments, and turn to the Lord your God. —Joel 2:13 Do not try to appease the Lord through changed behavior, but experience the transformation of being that comes from turning to the Lord with a changed heart.
A father need not be socially high or wealthy. Often he is more limited and weak than me. Still, he is a good father to me. Because he became limited and weak for my sake. Do well by your parents.

You're still alive... A while ago, Pastor Jung Nam's family visited and I heard everyone is doing well. Our Sharon has grown a lot; your Sharon must have grown too. Social media doesn't suit my personality, but it's good to see you all living this way.

Treasure the small place of prayer on your knees when you wake in the morning. It is far greater than elaborate eloquence and overflowing knowledge. (At least it was for me.) There is the dignity of the saints. I love the Lord because he has heard my voice and my cry for mercy. Since he turned his ear to me, I will call on him as long as I live. —Psalm 116:1-2

* True love: To be loved is truly wonderful. But when we only receive, we don't fully understand. When we love, we come to understand. When we see how much God the Father loved us—the price He paid—we understand. See what great love the Father has lavished on us, that we should be called children of God! And that is what we are! The reason the world does not know us is that it did not know him. —1 John 3:1
If those who go before are faithful, those who follow will become faithful too. Do not faint; be faithful.

James said that the prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective. If we lack God's heart, even when we pray, God's power cannot be released. To pray effectively, we must pray with God's heart and attitude toward the situation and person. Without forgiveness, true blessing and prayer cannot be realized.

Obtaining the promised land is not about entering Canaan and registering property or investing well in choice land. The only way to become its owner is through relationship with God. Canaan is a region where rain plays an absolutely essential role. Your relationship with God determines everything for you. Relationship is authority and power.

It is said that those who sow with tears will reap with joy. But when you sow together with beautiful people, tears become pearls and labor becomes delight instead. When you are together, there is nothing to fear.
When we mature, we put away childish things and see clearly what we once saw dimly, as face to face. To grow is to change, and this happens when perfect love is achieved.
Talent is remembered in a person's mind. Consideration remains in a person's heart.

Over the past 25 years of faith and ministry, I have witnessed many young people who were in grace like heaven lose their faith and wander. And I have heard the Lord's weeping cry for them. In a Seoul Seminary dormitory room in 1991 and at a prayer center in Gyeongnam in 1990. His heart was always like this: 'Has the world given you true happiness? I do not want to lose you.'

The greatest misconception young people have is that they will be happy if someone loves them perfectly and ideally. People become unhappy from the moment they desire to be loved. When you exist in God's love and give love, you become happy. Do not cling to receiving love and feel lonely, but rather receive God's great love and practice love that gives and fulfills.

If you truly love, there is no need to hide or exaggerate. Coughing, poverty, and love cannot be hidden. Especially love becomes more evident the more you try to hide it. That is why tears flow as precious ointment is poured on Jesus' feet.
If you truly love, you must learn to love and how to love the person you love. Ignorant love easily becomes rude love. And it inevitably causes deep wounds.
A greater sin than lust, jealousy, and pride is not seeking or calling upon the Lord. If you had sought and called, you would not have borne such fruit of sin. Return to God.
What I have deeply understood through long years of youth ministry is simple: young people are transformed as much as they are loved.
Jesus, who washed the disciples' feet, commanded us likewise to wash the feet of our brothers and sisters. Not because their feet were dirty, but to wash away their sins and transgressions.

Through Professor John Nash's life, I heard that choices change lives. But such choice is only possible through the support of love. It is an 'ability' only possible when Jesus becomes the reason for living. To have spiritual discernment, spiritual vision, and wisdom in choice, you must set aside the easy, comfortable, and convenient food and grain that can be eaten without effort. Do not worry; the Lord will boldly remove it. Swift—Chicken vision: 30cm, Eagle vision: 10.0 - Weekly Summary 09/01/2013

What you like is 'because of what,' but what you love is 'in spite of everything.' If you simply like something, it excites you, but if you love, tears flow. Many people 'like' someone for their own happiness. But those who love make others happy because of them.
You want to seat the person you like next to you. But the person you love, you want to give up your seat for.
When you like someone, you make calculated investments. When you love, you sacrifice and yet find happiness.

A good spouse—everyone wishes and prays to meet a good spouse. If I meet a good spouse, what sin has that person committed in meeting me, unprepared? Therefore, I should first seek to become a good spouse myself.
Good morning. You all know that I love you, right? Be loved. You were born to be loved.
A good mother is not one with the ability to solve everything. She is a mother who is heartbroken because she cannot help. That is where love comes from.
Before good food, I think of the people I love. I want to eat good food with good people.
When I see good food, I think of my family. When I see a beautiful view, I think of my parents. When I see our young people, I want to embrace them all. I give you a big hug. God will help.

Do not strive to overcome the limits of sinful nature, the weakness of the flesh, the limits of your character, and the power of spiritual trials. These are things humanity has tried to overcome for thousands of years but could not. Rather, look to the Lord and follow Him. If I am where the Lord is, I will soon find myself beyond limits. My goal is not achievement but His presence and companionship. I am apprehended by Jesus the Lord.
To be overly timid before sin and worry is excessive and unnecessary concern. What has that to do with me? Simply come to the Lord who seeks and calls you.

* The decisive secret of relationship failure—Relationships fail because, surprisingly, people try too hard. Tension enters the voice and tension appears in the eyes. It is human nature to want to eat even a bowl of ramen comfortably. Acting must eventually end. Life cannot be covered by acting. When you touch a masterpiece, you damage the original. Rather, honor yourself and find and help the person given to you, and you will enter the wonderful blessing of relationships that God has prepared. Even if you long for much, if you cannot love yourself, I ask you to let go and walk away. From that hell...

Do not be seized by guilt and condemn yourself. Condemnation leads to self-abuse and breaks fellowship with the Lord, causing you to commit greater sin. Do not condemn; repent instead. We have the possibility of sinning and actually do sin. If we sin because we are sinners, that would be despair. But we are righteous through the blood of the Lord, and yet we sin. That is why we depend on and confess to the Lord even more. The condemnation of sinners leads to death, but the repentance of the righteous leads to life.
The fact that the Lord wants to hear my prayer.

The Lord, in my youth, made it possible for me to run even when my heart was deeply wounded. The Lord allows me to wipe my tears again and run for Him again. Because He called, I come. The Lord alone is sufficient. My longing is to love the Lord more.

It is more blessed to give than to receive—this means I first receive what I give to others. If I want to show mercy, God first shows me mercy, and it must overflow so I can share it with others. This applies to wounds as well. To give a wound, that wound must first dominate me. What will you give and what will you receive?
The ability to come before the Lord and worship in the temple is sustained only by God's love. Worship without love is empty. —Psalm 5:7

When you stand before the Lord, you discern and understand His will. If it is dim like looking in a mirror, you are already in deep trouble. As the Lord knows me, if I look to the Lord, I will know eternal value, and worldly desires and lusts cannot hold me.
A paradise without the Lord becomes more tragic than the original hell.
Conversation without the Lord is meaningless to share. Until the Lord intervenes, life is a state of closure without solution.
When you walk with the Lord, two representative powers appear: 1. You bear much fruit. 2. You can do all things in the Lord. —John 15:5

If you do not maintain a personal encounter with the Lord, private time becomes a time of sin. But for those who have a deep encounter with the Lord, private time becomes the deepest and most precious time of grace.
Give your heart to the Lord at every moment. Then the Lord will give you His heart. It is given with a wounded heart, and the Father's heart comes upon it.
What the Lord desired from Peter in Galilee was not a detailed account or persuasive excuse. It was the confession from hesitant, unsure lips: 'I love You, Lord.'

I wish the Lord would give me a burning thirst. A thirst so intense that nothing else can satisfy it, a heart so earnest that counterfeits cannot solve it, a longing so desperate that only the Lord can fill it. I love You, Lord.
It is a joy to have many people who pray for me. It means I have many loved ones.
'Be at peace in the Lord' is not a mere greeting. Please, do not remain outside the Lord and be wounded, but be made whole in the Lord through intercession.

The Lord said, 'Do not let your hearts be troubled.' These are His words to disciples who followed Him for three and a half years, living in total dependence, as He was about to depart. As the basis for escaping anxiety, the Lord says, 'You believe in God; believe also in me.' It means faith. Do not search far; believe. Then you will be freed from trouble. John 14:1
The Lord is Lord over all relationships. Invite the Lord to the center of your life.
The Lord surely hears our prayers. He knows our hearts and circumstances. For He created that deep heart within us.
The Lord seeks and asks: Who am I? Respond to Him. Show me who you are through your earnest desires, affections, and choices. According to that person's thoughts, so is their life.
The Lord seeks and calls me. The Lord is my Lord and is alone worthy of praise.
The Lord has never taken His eyes off me for a single moment. Even when I was an unbeliever, His eyes beheld me.

To know that you should look to the Lord yet be unable to is a great pain in youth. The reason is not complex. It is because I do not regard what I uphold and cannot lose as 'dung.' Unless I despise and hate worldly recognition, people's attention, pride in relational success, and sinful entertainment that briefly pleases me, I cannot serve both these desires and Christ. ...That I have lost all things and regard them as dung so that I may gain Christ and be found in Him..., —This is the crystallization of faith testimony from one who lived humbly with the Lord all his life.

If you are so weak and your dark night is challenged enough to be made white before the Lord that you have no choice but to look to Him, that is the Lord's personal, intimate 'proposal' to meet with you.
You must serve with the Lord's heart and the Lord's love. Only then does the Lord receive glory.
In those who have nothing but the Lord's love to hope for, I see the greatest strength. Beyond strength, I feel an admiration worth envying. Faith is that. It is nothing but that.

When you long for the Lord's voice, you open your eyes early in the morning and think first of the Lord's beauty. Faith is not about outward appearance but about the heart. If you begin the dawn thinking of the Lord, your heart will run toward loving Him. See Psalm 119:55.

I will spend time listening to the Lord's voice. I hope you will bless your brothers and sisters in groups with interceding hearts and giving hearts. When you live out your faith in a place where such prayer requests become unnatural, you will deeply thank God for the special grace He has given you.
The Lord's great comfort is the fact that He speaks to us and the presence He allows us. With voice and presence, these two things, you have everything.
The reason the Lord delays His conclusion is to truly give Himself to us.
Without the Lord, we could never have had this. Without us, the Lord would not have walked with us. The Lord and we are one.
We must run tirelessly so the Lord is glorified. So much love for the Lord that we don't know what to do, may our hearts be full of longing for the Lord's temple, we love Jesus.

When the Lord so deeply desires to meet us, sometimes He will cause all others to leave. Don't feel lonely when you're alone, but accept it as the Lord's special date invitation. I hope your heart will thrill at meeting the Lord, not people.
If there is someone to whom you can give your shoulder, no matter what others say, that person is a successful person.
True love lays down pride as well.
At the moment of death, only the 'relationship with God' remains. We call this 'righteousness.' It means faith.
Things did not go as prepared, but I was truly grateful to spend yet another Christmas with the people I love. It is boundless grace.

The way to escape addiction, powerlessness, and anger is to become sufficient with the Lord alone. The Lord said to the woman who poured ointment that because many sins were forgiven her, she expressed much love. That you may have many sins forgiven, kneel down and repent humbly; you will become a person who loves the Lord.

When the Lord gives intercession, He pours out compassion. Because prayer is done with the Father's heart. God's heart toward us who are weak and insufficient is 'compassion.' An intercessor is one who shares the heart with God. That's why they must be loving people. Burning love.
Peace does not come from solving the most difficult problem you face right now. True peace comes from His presence with you even in the storm. Seek to be with One greater than the problem.

The fact that I receive great love like a mountain or small love like a sesame seed now is because all my faults have not been exposed or because the other person loved even my hidden weaknesses. That God covers the faults of the insufficient and grants them mercy from others is all His boundless grace. ㅠㅜ
What is needed at this very moment is to confirm the Lord's love for you. Then He touches invisible wounds. Stand straight with sincerity.
Whoever walks with the Lord now will stand in the midst of the Lord's will tomorrow.
All that we have lived until now is by the Lord's great grace. His boundless love, how can I express it in words? I, the chief of sinners, can only bow my head and shed tears.
True faithfulness is bringing my unfaithfulness honestly before the Lord without falsehood.

When you persuade the mind, it is convinced. When you empathize with emotion, it becomes moving. When you (concretely) act with will, it becomes decision. To request without doing these is 'coercion.' In the church, it is voluntariness instead of coercion, and in ministry it is motivation.

Position and title, past career do not prove who I am. More than knowledge about truth and zeal, more than many activities, what was most important to Jesus was 'first love.' Priority and motive tell me who I am. 'But I have this against you: You have abandoned the love you had at first. Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first. If you do not repent, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place.' -Revelation 2:4-5
